eng: The Pharisees and Sadducees came to Jesus. In order to test him they asked him to show them a sign from heaven.
local: Nɩɩ Farasii abi yɛ Sadusi abi ba ba Yesu asɛ ba ba tʋʋ mʋ tɔ kɩɩ, ɩmʋ sʋ ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ ɔ waa asɩn kyinkyinsa kʋ lɩɩ sʋsʋ kaapʋ bamʋ.

eng: But he replied, At evening you say, It will be nice weather because the sky is bright red.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Kyɔwɛ maa kpee ɔ naa tɩyɛ mʋ, fɛn taalɛ fɛn bɩɩ fɛn tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔkɛ kyɔwɛ ii biti ɔ da, lɩɩ fɛɛ abwaarɛ‐wʋlɛ ɩ payɩ kɔɔ.

eng: And in the morning you say, There will be bad weather today because the sky is cloudy. You know how to make sense of the skys appearance. But you are unable to recognize the signs that point to what the time is.
local: Nɩɩ nyenyenfuu mɔɔ fɛn taalɛ fɛn tɔwɛ yɛ, Ndaga bʋlayɩ ii biti ɔ da, lɩɩ fɛɛ abwaarɛ‐wʋlɛ ɩ payɩ nɩɩ i bii tɔ. Fɛn taalɛ fen wu abwaarɛ‐wʋlɛ mʋ kanan mʋ nɩɩ i du, nɩɩ fɛɛ lɛɛ ɩmʋ kasɛ, amaa fɛn mɛɛ taalɛ fɛn bɩɩ nkɛ nɩmʋ tɔ asɩn, kɛ fɛn lɛɛ ɩmʋ kasɛ.

eng: An evil and unfaithful generation searches for a sign. But it wont receive any sign except Jonahs sign. Then he left them and went away.
local: Fanɛ ndaga kakyɩna nɩmʋ tɔ alibi awaapʋ mʋ nɩɩ fan kina Ɩbwaarɛ. Fɛn taasɛ fen biti asɩn kyinkyinsa lɩɩ mɛ asɛ, amaa fan man yɛ fan wu asɩn kyinkyinsa kʋ, amɔɔ ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ waa Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Gyona mʋ. Yesu maa tɔwɛ kanɩn mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ yɛgɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ɔ kyʋn.

eng: When the disciples arrived on the other side of the lake, they had forgotten to bring bread.
local: Abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba maa faa kpee benbe mʋ, ba nyiŋi fɛɛ ba man taa bodobodo.

eng: Jesus said to them, Watch out and be on your guard for the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan kɩɩ danbɩrasa, kɛ fan baa fɛn da Farasii abi yɛ Sadusi abi kɩfɛ mʋ nɩɩ kii tin bodobodo mʋ sʋ.

eng: They discussed this among themselves and said, We didnt bring any bread.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba piili bɛɛ tɔwɛ abaa asɛ yɛ, A maa man bɩya bodobodo mʋ sʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ tɔwɛ kanɩn mʋ.

eng: Jesus knew what they were discussing and said, You people of weak faith! Why are you discussing among yourselves the fact that you dont have any bread?.
local: Yesu maa nyi kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɔwɛ sʋ mʋ ɔ taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fanɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii kɩ man kyɔ! Mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fɛn tɔwɛ, fanɛ kɩ man sʋ bodobodo asɩn?.

eng: Dont you understand yet? Dont you remember the five loaves that fed the five thousand and how many baskets of leftovers you gathered?.
local: Fan san fan man pɩɩta nu asɩn nɩmʋ kasɛ? Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ n daa gyayɩ bodobodo anuu tɔ sa anyɩn ngbɩn nnuu mʋ, fen mee nyiŋi? Agyisan alantan afɩnɛ nɩɩ fan daa tasɛ bɔla mʋ?.

eng: And the seven loaves that fed the four thousand and how many large baskets of leftovers you gathered?.
local: Abɛɛ n maa daa gyayɩ bodobodo asunɔ tɔ sa asa ngbɩn nna mʋ, agyisan alantan afɩnɛ nɩɩ fan daa taasɛ bɔla mʋ?.

eng: Dont you know that I wasnt talking about bread? But be on your guard for the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
local: Mɩnɛ ɩɩ waa sʋ nɩɩ fɛn mɛɛ taalɛ bɩɩ fɛɛ man gyɛ bodobodo asɩn nɩɩ n tɔwɛ? Amaa fan baa fan da fanɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ lɩɩ Farasii yɛ Sadusi abi kɩfɛ tinsa mʋ sʋ.

eng: Then they understood that he wasnt telling them to be on their guard for yeast used in making bread. No, he was telling them to watch out for the teaching of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba lan nu kasɛ fɛɛ man gyɛ kɩfɛ tin agyitɔ mʋ asɩn nɩɩ ɛɛ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ ba baa ba da sʋ, amaa Farasii yɛ Sadusi abi asɩn kaapʋsa nɩɩ ɔ dɩyɛ.

eng: Now when Jesus came to the area of Caesarea Philippi, he asked his disciples, Who do people say the Human One is.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu kpee kabaafʋn mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ tiri Kasariya Filipi kadɛ tɔ mʋ. Maa bʋ tʋtɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ, Anɩmʋ nɩɩ asa ba yɛ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ n gyɛ.

eng: They replied, Some say John the Baptist, others Elijah, and still others Jeremiah or one of the other prophets.
local: Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Akʋ ba yɛ fʋ ɩ gyɛ Gyɔn Okyugyeepu mʋ, nɩɩ akʋ ba yɛ fʋ ɩ gyɛ Eligya, nɩɩ akʋ mɔɔ ba yɛ fʋ ɩ gyɛ Gyeremiya abɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ.

eng: He said, And what about you? Who do you say that I am.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Nɩɩ fanɔɔ fan fa? Anɩmʋ nɩɩ fan yɛ n gyɛ.

eng: Simon Peter said, You are the Christ, the Son of the living God.
local: Nɩɩ Simon Pita lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Fʋ ɩ gyɛ Krisito, Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛ kakpaa mʋ Mu‐Bii.

eng: Then Jesus replied, Happy are you, Simon son of Jonah, because no human has shown this to you. Rather my Father who is in heaven has shown you.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Fʋ Simon, Gyona mu‐bii! Kusee ɩ gyɛ sa fʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ man gyɛ nyɩmɩsa ɩ lɛɛ falɛ kesintin asɩn nɩmʋ kaapʋ fʋ, amaa mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ sʋsʋ mʋ ɩ lɛɛ falɛ asɩn mʋ kaapʋ fʋ.

eng: I tell you that you are Peter. And Ill build my church on this rock. The gates of the underworld wont be able to stand against it.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ n tɔwɛ fʋ yɛ, fʋ gyɛ Pita, nɩɩ kanɩn kɩfʋlɛ maŋa sʋ nɩɩ n biti n pɔyɩ mɛ, Krisito kʋbʋ mʋ, nɩɩ lewu gbaa mɛɛ taalɛ gyii kɩmʋ sʋ.

eng: Ill give you the keys of the kingdom of heaven. Anything you fasten on earth will be fastened in heaven. Anything you loosen on earth will be loosened in heaven.
local: N biti n taa sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii ɩsafwɩɩ kpini sa fʋ. Kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fu kyula kasɛ sʋ nfɩɩ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ gbaa kyula kɩmʋ sʋsʋ. Nɩɩ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fu kina kasɛ sʋ nfɩɩ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ gbaa kina kɩmʋ sʋsʋ.

eng: Then he ordered the disciples not to tell anybody that he was the Christ.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu da mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ amu tɔ yɛ, ba man kan waa tɔwɛ ɔkʋ yɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ Krisito.

eng: From that time Jesus began to show his disciples that he had to go to Jerusalem and suffer many things from the elders, chief priests, and legal experts, and that he had to be killed and raised on the third day.
local: Lɩɩ kanɩn saŋa maŋa ɩɩ sʋ ii kpee mʋ, Yesu piili bugi tɔ ɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ, I tiri fɛɛ n maa kpee Gyerusalem naa gyii awʋrʋfɔ gaa lɩɩ abɩlɩsa, yɛ aseepu‐bɩlɩsa yɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ kpini abaa tɔ. Bee biti ba mɔɔ mɛ, kɛ kakɛ sasapʋ mʋ, n kyiŋi ba nkpa tɔ.

eng: Then Peter took hold of Jesus and, scolding him, began to correct him: God forbid, Lord! This wont happen to you.
local: Nɩɩ Pita taa Yesu kpee ɩkaa sʋ tɔwɛ yii mʋ yɛ, Afuloo! Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ! Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ taa ɩmʋ kpee kata‐kata. Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ mɛɛ yɛ ɩ ba fʋ sʋ kakaakʋ.

eng: But he turned to Peter and said, Get behind me, Satan. You are a stone that could make me stumble, for you are not thinking Gods thoughts but human thoughts.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu biliŋi tɔwɛ Pita yɛ, Sɩtaanɛ, taa lɩɩ mɛ sʋ! Fii kun mɛ ɔkpa nɩn, lɩɩ fɛɛ fʋ nfɛɛrɛ ɩ man gyan Ɩbwaarɛ atɔ sʋ, amɔɔ ɩ gyan nyɩmɩsa lɛɛ sʋ nɩn.

eng: Then Jesus said to his disciples, All who want to come after me must sayno to themselves, take up their cross, and follow me.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ, Nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan ee biti o buu mɛ mʋ, ɩ dagaa maa kina mʋ n‐yɩɩ, kɛ ɔ sʋla mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mʋ oyu‐laasa kɛ o buu mɛ.

eng: All who want to save their lives will lose them. But all who lose their lives because of me will find them.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ mɔlɩgɛ mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mʋ nkpa mʋ, ii biti ɔ pan ɩmʋ, amaa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ pan mʋ nkpa lɩɩ mɛ sʋ mʋ, ii biti o bwii nyɛ mʋ nkpa mʋ.

eng: Why would people gain the whole world but lose their lives? What will people give in exchange for their lives?.
local: Nɩɩ nyɩmɩsa kan nyɛ dulinyaa tɔ kʋtɔ kʋmaa, nɩɩ ɔ pan mʋ nkpa mʋ, mɩnɛ tɔnɔ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ? Abɛɛ mɩnɛ nɩɩ nyɩmɩsa ɩ taalɛ taa kyɛrɛ mʋ nkpa?.

eng: For the Human One is about to come with the majesty of his Father with his angels. And then he will repay each one for what that person has done.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ n biti n baa yɛ Ɔsɛ mʋ bʋnyaa, yɛ mɛ nbɔɔ, kɛ n ba ka ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ kʋkɔ lɩɩ mu kusun mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa mʋ sʋ.

eng: I assure you that some standing here wont die before they see the Human One coming in his kingdom.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin nɩn fɛɛ asa kʋ ba yɩla nfɩɩ, nɩɩ ba mɛɛ yɛ ba wu, naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ bee biti ba wu mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa maa ba yɛ mu kuwura‐gyii mʋ.

eng: A record of the ancestors of Jesus Christ, son of David, son of Abraham.
local: Kanan mʋ nɩɩ Yesu Krisito kakʋʋgɛ tɔ kɩnaana ki du ndee. Yesu Krisito lɩɩ Owura Dawidi kanaan tɔ, nɩɩ Dawidi mɔɔ lɩɩ Abraham kanaan tɔ.

eng: Abraham was the father of Isaac. Isaac was the father of Jacob. Jacob was the father of Judah and his brothers.
local: Abraham mu‐bii ɩ gyɛ Isaki, nɩɩ Isaki mu‐bii ɩ gyɛ Gyekobu, nɩɩ Gyekobu mɔɔ mu‐bii ana ɩ gyɛ Gyuda maa mu‐supu ana.

eng: Judah was the father of Perez and Zerah, whose mother was Tamar. Perez was the father of Hezron. Hezron was the father of Aram.
local: Gyuda mu‐bii ana ɩ gyɛ Peresi maa Sera, nɩɩ bamʋ‐nyi ɩ gyɛ Tama. Peresi mu‐bii ɩ gyɛ Hesiron, nɩɩ Hesiron mɔɔ mu‐bii ɩ gyɛ Ram.

eng: Aram was the father of Amminadab. Amminadab was the father of Nahshon. Nahshon was the father of Salmon.
local: Nɩɩ Ram mu‐bii ɩ gyɛ Aminada, nɩɩ Aminada mu‐bii ɩ gyɛ Naason, nɩɩ Naason mu‐bii ɩ gyɛ Salimon.

eng: Salmon was the father of Boaz, whose mother was Rahab. Boaz was the father of Obed, whose mother was Ruth. Obed was the father of Jesse.
local: Nɩɩ Salimon mu‐bii ɩ gyɛ Bowasi, nɩɩ mu‐nyi ɩ gyɛ Rahabu, nɩɩ Bowasi mu‐bii ɩ gyɛ Obedi, nɩɩ mu‐nyi ɩ gyɛ Rufu, nɩɩ Obedi mu‐bii ɩ gyɛ Gyeese.

eng: Jesse was the father of David the king. David was the father of Solomon, whose mother had been the wife of Uriah.
local: Nɩɩ Gyeese mu‐bii ɩ gyɛ Owura Dawidi. Nɩɩ Owura Dawidi taa ɔkyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ daa ɔ gyɛ Yuriya mʋ‐ka kʋʋgɛ Solomon.

eng: Solomon was the father of Rehoboam. Rehoboam was the father of Abijah. Abijah was the father of Asaph.
local: Solomon mu‐bii ɩ gyɛ Rehobowam, nɩɩ Rehobowam mu‐bii ɩ gyɛ Abigya, nɩɩ Abigya mu‐bii ɩ gyɛ Asa.

eng: Asaph was the father of Jehoshaphat. Jehoshaphat was the father of Joram. Joram was the father of Uzziah.
local: Nɩɩ Asa mu‐bii ɩ gyɛ Gyehosafati, nɩɩ Gyehosafati mu‐bii ɩ gyɛ Gyehoram, nɩɩ Gyehoram mu‐bii ɩ gyɛ Yusiya.

eng: Uzziah was the father of Jotham. Jotham was the father of Ahaz. Ahaz was the father of Hezekiah.
local: Nɩɩ Yusiya mu‐bii ɩ gyɛ Gyotam, nɩɩ Gyotam mu‐bii ɩ gyɛ Ahasi, nɩɩ Ahasi mu‐bii ɩ gyɛ Hesekiya.

eng: Hezekiah was the father of Manasseh. Manasseh was the father of Amos. Amos was the father of Josiah.
local: Nɩɩ Hesekiya mu‐bii ɩ gyɛ Manase, nɩɩ Manase mu‐bii ɩ gyɛ Amon, nɩɩ Amon mu‐bii ɩ gyɛ Gyosiya.

eng: Josiah was the father of Jechoniah and his brothers. This was at the time of the exile to Babylon.
local: Nɩɩ Gyosiya mu‐bii ɩ gyɛ Gyekoniya maa mu‐supu ana. Kanan saŋa maŋa nɩɩ Babilon abi ba kɔ kɩtaa Ɩsɩrayɩ abi waa bamʋ anyɛ mʋ.

eng: After the exile to Babylon: Jechoniah was the father of Shealtiel. Shealtiel was the father of Zerubbabel.
local: Nɩɩ lɩɩ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba yɛgɛ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi mʋ nɩɩ ba kɩtaa kpee Babilon naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba kʋʋgɛ Yesu mʋ, ɩmʋ kakʋʋgɛ tɔ nkyaga sʋ ndee. Gyekoniya mu‐bii ɩ gyɛ Selatiye, nɩɩ Selatiye mu‐bii ɩ gyɛ Serubabeli.

eng: Zerubbabel was the father of Abiud. Abiud was the father of Eliakim. Eliakim was the father of Azor.
local: Nɩɩ Serubabeli mu‐bii ɩ gyɛ Abiyudi, nɩɩ Abiyudi, mu‐bii ɩ gyɛ Eliyakim, nɩɩ Eliyakim mu‐bii ɩ gyɛ Aso.

eng: Azor was the father of Zadok. Zadok was the father of Achim. Achim was the father of Eliud.
local: Aso mu‐bii ɩ gyɛ Sadoki, nɩɩ Sadoki mu‐bii ɩ gyɛ Akim, nɩɩ Akim mu‐bii ɩ gyɛ Eliyudi.

eng: Eliud was the father of Eleazar. Eleazar was the father of Matthan. Matthan was the father of Jacob.
local: Nɩɩ Eliyudi mu‐bii ɩ gyɛ Eleyasa, nɩɩ Eleyasa mu‐bii ɩ gyɛ Matan, nɩɩ Matan mu‐bii ɩ gyɛ Gyekobu.

eng: Jacob was the father of Joseph, the husband of Mary—of whom Jesus was born, who is called the Christ.
local: Nɩɩ Gyekobu mu‐bii ɩ gyɛ Gyosefu, Mɛɛrɩ mu‐kuli mʋ. Nɩɩ Mɛɛrɩ maŋa ɩ kʋʋgɛ Yesu mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ Krisito mʋ.

eng: So there were fourteen generations from Abraham to David, fourteen generations from David to the exile to Babylon, and fourteen generations from the exile to Babylon to the Christ.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ lɩɩ Abraham saŋa naa fʋʋ Owura Dawidi kanaana tɔ kakʋʋgɛ nkyaga sʋ mʋ ɩ gyɛ kudu ana. Nɩɩ lɩɩ Dawidi naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba kɩtaa bamʋ waa anyɛ mʋ kanaana tɔ kakʋʋgɛ nkyaga sʋ ɩ gyɛ kudu ana. Nɩɩ lɩɩ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba yɛgɛ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi lɩɩ Babilon naa fʋʋ Krisito kakʋʋgɛ saŋa mʋ gbaa gyɛ kanaana tɔ kakʋʋgɛ nkyaga sʋ kudu ana.

eng: This is how the birth of Jesus Christ took place. When Mary his mother was engaged to Joseph, before they were married, she became pregnant by the Holy Spirit.
local: Yesu Krisito kakʋʋgɛ asɩn ndee. Ba taa mu‐nyi Mɛɛrɩ sa Gyosefu kekili, amaa pɔyɩ kɛ ba bɩɩ abaa mʋ, ɩ ba fɛɛ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ sʋ ɔtɔ, nɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ɩ ba kanɩn.

eng: Now when Jesus had come down from the mountain, large crowds followed him.
local: Yesu maa kpɩlɩgɛ lɩɩ kɩbɩɩ mʋ sʋ ba mʋ, asa damantɛ ba buu mʋ.

eng: Joseph her husband was a righteous man. Because he didnt want to humiliate her, he decided to call off their engagement quietly.
local: Gyosefu mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ mu‐kuli mʋ gyɛ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ mʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi, nɩɩ maa mee biti fɛɛ ɔ ŋaabaa Mɛɛrɩ asa nsana tɔ sʋ mʋ, ɔ waa mʋ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ ee biti ɔ baala kina mʋ.

eng: A man with a skin disease came, kneeled before him, and said, Lord, if you want, you can make me clean.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔnyɩn kʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ lɔ kibootoo ba muŋa mu amulii sʋ mu ansi tɔ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ nɩɩ ɩ pɩrɛ fʋ mʋ, fɩɩ taalɛ yɛgɛ mɛ n‐yɩɩ ɩ lɔŋɔ sʋ.

eng: As he was thinking about this, an angel from the Lord appeared to him in a dream and said, Joseph son of David, dont be afraid to take Mary as your wife, because the child she carries was conceived by the Holy Spirit.
local: Amaa Gyosefu, maa fɛ falɛ nfɛɛrɛ mʋ, nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ lɛɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ kaapʋ mʋ idee tɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Gyosefu, Dawidi kanaana‐bii, man waa kufuu fɛɛ fʋ mɛɛ yɛ fʋ taa Mɛɛrɩ kili, lɩɩ fɛɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ ɔtɔ.

eng: Jesus reached out his hand and touched him, saying, I do want to. Become clean. Instantly his skin disease was cleansed.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɛɛŋɛ mʋ kɩbaa mata ɔnyɩn mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɩ pɩrɛ mɛ, fʋ n‐yɩɩ ɩ lɔŋɔ sʋ. Opula tɔ nɩɩ mʋ kibootoo mʋ kɩ lʋwɛ.

eng: She will give birth to a son, and you will call him Jesus, because he will save his people from their sins.
local: Ee biti ɔ kʋʋgɛ obii‐nyɩnsa, kɛ fʋ yɩla mʋ kɩtɩɩ yɛ Yesu, kpalɩ fɛɛ mʋ ii biti ɔ ba mɔlɩgɛ mʋ asa lɩɩ bamʋ alibi tɔ.

eng: Jesus said to him, Dont say anything to anyone. Instead, go and show yourself to the priest and offer the gift that Moses commanded. This will be a testimony to them.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Man waa fʋ tɔwɛ ɔkʋ, amaa taa fʋ n‐yɩɩ naa kaapʋ Ɩbwaarɛ Oseepu, kɛ fʋ lɛɛ saraga fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Mosesi nbara ɩ kaapʋ mʋ, kɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ o wu fɛɛ fʋ n‐yɩɩ ɩ lɔŋɔ sʋ.

eng: Now all of this took place so that what the Lord had spoken through the prophet would be fulfilled.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ kpini ɩ ba waa, kɛ ɩ ba tɔ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ bɔla mʋ ɔtɔwɛpʋ sʋ tɔwɛ yɛ.

eng: When Jesus went to Capernaum, a centurion approached.
local: Yesu maa loo Kapanum kadɛ tɔ mʋ, Roma anaakɔpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa kʋ ba mʋ asɛ ba kʋlɛ mʋ yɛ ɔ kyɛ mʋ tɔ.

eng: That day Jesus went out of the house and sat down beside the lake.
local: Kanɩn kakɛ maŋa nɩɩ Yesu lɩɩ kɩkpaara sʋ kpee naa kyɩna ɔbʋn kaa ɛɛ kaapʋ.

eng: Look! A virgin will become pregnant and give birth to a son, And they will call him, Emmanuel.
local: Kibite mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man nyi ɔnyɩn ii biti ɔ nyɛ ɔtɔ kʋʋgɛ obii‐nyɩnsa, kɛ ba tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Emanuweli, kɩmʋ kasɛ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ bʋ anɛ asɛ.

eng: pleading with him, Lord, my servant is flat on his back at home, paralyzed, and his suffering is awful.
local: Ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, mɛ kɩyaafɔlɛ dɛ mɛ kɩkpaara sʋ ɛɛ lɔ mʋgʋlɛ‐mʋgʋlɛ, ɩɩ tɔɔrɔɔ mʋ gaa.

eng: Such large crowds gathered around him that he climbed into a boat and sat down. The whole crowd was standing on the shore.
local: Asa damantɛ ba maa kyaabɔɔ mʋ mʋ, o loo ɔkʋlɩ tɔ naa kyɩna yɛgɛ asa mʋ ba yɩlɛ ɔbʋn‐kaa.

eng: When Joseph woke up, he did just as an angel from God commanded and took Mary as his wife.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ Gyosefu maa kyiŋi mʋ, o kili Mɛɛrɩ taa mʋ kpee mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ sa kɔnɔ yɛ ɔ waa mʋ.

eng: Jesus responded, Ill come and heal him.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ, Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, N biti n naa kyɛ mʋ kʋlɔ.

eng: He said many things to them in parables: A farmer went out to scatter seed.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ taa aŋasan tɔ kaapʋ bamʋ atɔ damantɛ. Ɔ bɔ bamʋ kɩŋasan kʋ yɛ, Ɔdɔɔpʋ kʋ ɩ bʋ tɔ nɩɩ o kpee ɔ naa ŋmatɛ ayu.

eng: But he didnt have sexual relations with her until she gave birth to a son. Joseph called him Jesus.
local: Amaa Gyosefu man dɛ yɛ Mɛɛrɩ halɩɩ naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kʋʋgɛ obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ. Nɩɩ Gyosefu yɩla mʋ kɩtɩɩ yɛ Yesu.

eng: But the centurion replied, Lord, I dont deserve to have you come under my roof. Just say the word and my servant will be healed.
local: Nɩɩ anaakɔpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ lɛɛ mʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ n man dagaa fɛɛ fʋ ba mɛ kɩkpaara sʋ. Amaa dan sa kɔnɔ, mɛ kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ ii biti ɔ kpaarɛ.

eng: As he was scattering seed, some fell on the path, and birds came and ate it.
local: Maa naa ŋmatɛ ɩmʋ mʋ, ɩkʋ ɩ tɩyɛ ɔkpakaa, nɩɩ nbwiibi ba ba tasɛ ɩmʋ gyii.

eng: Im a man under authority, with soldiers under me. I say to one, Go, and he goes, and to another, Come, and he comes. I say to my servant, Do this, and the servant does it.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ngbaa mɛ n bʋ akʋ ɔlʋn kasɛ nɩn, yɛgɛ anaakɔpʋ ba bʋ mɛ kasɛ. N kan tɔwɛ ɔkʋ yɛ, Kpe nfɩɩ, nɩɩ ee kpee. Nɩɩ n tɔwɛ ɔkʋ yɛ, Ba nfɩɩ, nɩɩ ɛɛ ba. Nɩɩ n tɔwɛ mɛ kɩyaafɔlɛ yɛ, Waa ɩnɩmʋ, nɩɩ ɛɛ waa ɩmʋ.

eng: Other seed fell on rocky ground where the soil was shallow. They sprouted immediately because the soil wasnt deep.
local: Nɩɩ ɩkʋ ɩ tɩyɛ afʋlɛ sʋ, katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɩsɛ ɩ man kyɔ. Nɩɩ ɩ kɔyɩ bilen kpalɩ fɛɛ ɩsɛ mʋ maa man bʋnyɛ sʋ.

eng: When Jesus heard this, he was impressed and said to the people following him, I say to you with all seriousness that even in Israel I havent found faith like this.
local: Yesu maa nu kanɩn mʋ, i kyinkyin mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba buu mʋ mʋ yɛ, N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin fɛɛ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi kpini tɔ mʋ, n man pɩɩta wu ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ falɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii damantɛ nɩmʋ kedubi.

eng: But when the sun came up, it scorched the plants, and they dried up because they had no roots.
local: Amaa kyɔwɛ maa ba ɛɛ da mʋ, i fugya ɩmʋ, nɩɩ i wu lɩɩ fɛɛ ɩmʋ ilin ɩ man kyɔ.

eng: I say to you that there are many who will come from east and west and sit down to eat with Abraham and Isaac and Jacob in the kingdom of heaven.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ asa gaalaagaa bee biti ba lɩɩ kyɔwɛ kɩlɩɩ yɛ kyɔwɛ kɩtɩyɛ, kɛ ba maa Abraham maa Isaki yɛ Gyekobu ba ba bʋga kyɩna bamʋ ɩkyɩnakpa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ kuwura‐gyii kensi‐gyii ngyaŋɛ mʋ tɔ.

eng: Other seed fell among thorny plants. The thorny plants grew and choked them.
local: Nɩɩ ɩkʋ mɔɔ ɩ tɩyɛ iwii tɔ, nɩɩ iwii mʋ ɩ dan nyan kɩtaa ɩmʋ.

eng: But the children of the kingdom will be thrown outside into the darkness. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: Amaa fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ naafɔɔ fan gyɛ kuwura‐gyii abi mʋ, bee biti ba taa fanɛ funti kewu tɔ kitentenbiri tɔ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ kusu yɛ anobi keduŋi bʋ.

eng: Other seed fell on good soil and bore fruit, in one case a yield of one hundred to one, in another case a yield of sixty to one, and in another case a yield of thirty to one.
local: Amaa ayu mʋ ɩkʋ ɩ tɩyɛ kasɛ danbɩrasa sʋ, nɩɩ ɩ sɔyɩ amu lala‐lala. Ɩkʋ ɩ gyɛ kɩlɩfa, nɩɩ ɩkʋ ɩ gyɛ adisiye, nɩɩ ɩkʋ mɔɔ adɩsa.

eng: Jesus said to the centurion, Go; it will be done for you just as you have believed. And his servant was healed that very moment.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ anaakɔpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ yɛ, Kpewu! Ii biti ɩ waa sa fʋ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fʋ kɔɔlɛ gyii mʋ. Nɩɩ kanɩn saŋa maŋa tɔ kyɛɛkyɛɛ nɩɩ mʋ kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ nyɛ alanfɩya.

eng: Everyone who has ears should pay attention.
local: Ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ asʋ nɩɩ ee biti o nu mʋ o nu.

eng: Jesus went home with Peter and saw Peters mother-in-law lying in bed with a fever.
local: Yesu maa ba Pita kɩkpaara sʋ mʋ, o wu fɛɛ Pita mʋ‐saa kyɩɩsa dɛ ɛɛ lɔ kibirima.

eng: Jesus disciples came and said to him, Why do you use parables when you speak to the crowds.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba ba mʋ asɛ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ aŋasan tɔ nɩɩ fʋ sʋ fɩɩ kaapʋ asa.

eng: He touched her hand, and the fever left her. Then she got up and served them.
local: Ɔ kɩtaa mʋ kɩbaa tɔ nɩɩ kibirima mʋ kɩ yɛgɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kʋsʋ kɩtaa bamʋ kɩfɔ.

eng: Jesus replied, Because they havent received the secrets of the kingdom of heaven, but you have.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Fanɛɛ fanɛ bɩrɛ ɔkpa bugi sa fanɛ fɛɛ fan bɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ asiri asɩn mʋ, amaa bamɛɛ bamʋ bɩrɛ ɩ man bugi sa bamʋ.

eng: That evening people brought to Jesus many who were demon-possessed. He threw the spirits out with just a word. He healed everyone who was sick.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ kyɔwɛ tɩyɛ mʋ, nɩɩ asa ba taa alɔpʋ kʋkyɔ mʋ nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi ba tɛ bamʋ sʋ mʋ bɩya Yesu. Nɩɩ ɔ sa nbwɩɩ libi mʋ kɔnɔ gya bamʋ kʋsaa alɔpʋ mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kyɛ alɔpʋ mʋ kpini.

eng: For those who have will receive more and they will have more than enough. But as for those who dont have, even the little they have will be taken away from them.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn mʋ, bee biti ba sa mʋ gaa tii sʋ, kɛ ɔ nyɛ kyʋn sʋ. Amaa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man sʋ mʋ, halɩɩ pii kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ mʋ gbaa mʋ, bee biti ba kɔɔlɛ lɩɩ mʋ asɛ.

eng: This happened so that what Isaiah the prophet said would be fulfilled: He is the one who took our illnesses and carried away our diseases.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Isaya tɔwɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ mʋ ɩ ba tɔ. Asɩn mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, Ɔ kɔɔlɛ anɛ alɔ, nɩɩ ɔ taa anɛ alobi sʋla.

eng: This is why I speak to the crowds in parables: although they see, they dont really see; and although they hear, they dont really hear or understand.
local: Kʋtɔ mʋ sʋ nɩɩ n sʋ aŋasan n kaapʋ bamʋ mʋ ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ, Bɛɛ kɩɩ amaa be mee wu, bee nu, amaa be mee nu danbɩrasa abɛɛ be nu ɩmʋ kasɛ.

eng: Now when Jesus saw the crowd, he ordered his disciples to go over to the other side of the lake.
local: Yesu maa wu asa damantɛ ba maa kyaabɔɔ mʋ mʋ, ɔ tɔwɛ mu asunpu mʋ yɛ ba faa kpee benbe.

eng: What Isaiah prophesied has become completely true for them: You will hear, to be sure, but never understand; and you will certainly see but never recognize what you are seeing .
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bɔla mʋ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Isaya sʋ tɔwɛ yɩla mʋ, ɩ ba tɔ asa nɩmʋ sʋ. Ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, Fen biti fan nu kɛ fan nu, amaa fɛn mɛɛ yɛ fan nu kasɛ. Fen biti fan kɩɩ kɛ fan kɩɩ, amaa fɛn mɛɛ yɛ fan wu sɛhn.

eng: A legal expert came and said to him, Teacher, Ill follow you wherever you go.
local: Nɩɩ nbara ɔkaapʋpʋ kʋ ba mʋ asɛ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔkaapʋpʋ n biti n buu fʋ kpee katɩn kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fii kpee mʋ.

eng: For this peoples senses have become calloused, and theyve become hard of hearing, and theyve shut their eyes so that they wont see with their eyes or hear with their ears or understand with their minds, and change their hearts and lives that I may heal them .
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ asa banɩmʋ bamʋ nkɔlɔ ɩ bʋlʋn, nɩɩ ba tii bamʋ asʋ, nɩɩ ba bun bamʋ ansi sʋ. Naafɔɔ bamʋ ansi ii biti i wu, kɛ bamʋ asʋ ii nu, kɛ bamʋ nkɔlɔ tɔ ii nu asɩn kasɛ, kɛ ba bwii ba mɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ kɛ n kyɛ bamʋ alɔ.

eng: Jesus replied, Foxes have dens, and the birds in the sky have nests, but the Human One has no place to lay his head.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Agboogboo ba sʋ ɩbɔ bɛɛ dɛ tɔ, nɩɩ nbwiibi ba sʋ nsʋswaa, amaa mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ bɩrɛ, n man sʋ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ n dɛ fʋʋtɛ.

eng: Happy are your eyes because they see. Happy are your ears because they hear.
local: Kusee ɩ gyɛ sa fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fanɛ ansi ii wu nɩɩ fanɛ asʋ ii nu.

eng: Another man, one of his disciples, said to him, Lord, first let me go and bury my father.
local: Mʋ ɔbɩɩlapʋ kʋ gbaa tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, yɛgɛ n naa pula mɛ‐sɛ pɔyɩ.

eng: I assure you that many prophets and righteous people wanted to see what you see and hear what you hear, but they didnt.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ gaalaagaa yɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ bamʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ mʋ, bee biti gaa fɛɛ ba wu atɔ mʋ nɩɩ fen wu yɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ fen nu falɛ, amaa ba man nyɛ ɩmʋ.

eng: But Jesus said to him, Follow me, and let the dead bury their own dead.
local: Amaa Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Buu mɛ kɛ fʋ yɛgɛ bamʋ nɩɩ be du fɛɛ alewupu mʋ, be kpee naa pula bamʋ alewupu.

eng: Consider then the parable of the farmer.
local: Nbɩɩnbɩɩ fan nu kɩŋasan lɩɩ ayu ɔŋmatɛpʋ mʋ kasɛ.

eng: When Jesus got into a boat, his disciples followed him.
local: Nɩɩ o loo ɔkʋlɩ tɔ nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba buu mʋ loo.

eng: Whenever people hear the word about the kingdom and dont understand it, the evil one comes and carries off what was planted in their hearts. This is the seed that was sown on the path.
local: Ayubi mʋ nɩɩ ɩ tɩyɛ ɔkpakaa mʋ du nɩn fɛɛ, bamʋ nɩɩ bee nu Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ asɩn, nɩɩ ba man nu ɩmʋ kasɛ mʋ, nɩɩ Sɩtaanɛ ɛɛ ba bɛ lɛɛ abi mʋ nɩɩ ba ŋmatɛ mʋ lɩɩ bamʋ nkɔlɔ tɔ.

eng: A huge storm arose on the lake so that waves were sloshing over the boat. But Jesus was asleep.
local: Ba maa gyan ɔbʋn lala mʋ sʋ bee kpee mʋ, opula tɔ nɩɩ afuu lala i tigi yɛgɛ ɩɩ da apaa ii loo ɔkʋlɩ mʋ tɔ. Amaa yɛgɛ Yesu dɛ nsɩɩrɛ.

eng: As for the seed that was spread on rocky ground, this refers to people who hear the word and immediately receive it joyfully.
local: Ayubi mʋ nɩɩ ɩ tɩyɛ afʋlɛ sʋ mɔɔ mʋ, i du nɩn fɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bee nu asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ, nɩɩ ba taa kɔkɔlɔ fuuli kɔɔlɛ ɩmʋ opula tɔ mʋ.

eng: They came and woke him, saying, Lord, rescue us! Were going to drown.
local: Abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba naa kyiŋi mʋ, nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, mɔlɩgɛ anɛ! An biti a muuri.

eng: Because they have no roots, they last for only a little while. When they experience distress or abuse because of the word, they immediately fall away.
local: Amaa ɩ maa man loo bamʋ nkɔlɔ tɔ kyɛɛkyɛɛ sʋ mʋ, asɩn mʋ mɛɛ kyɛɛrɛɛ bamʋ tɔ. Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ awʋrʋfɔ abɛɛ kanyanla dan ɩ ba bamʋ sʋ lɩɩ asɩn mʋ sʋ mʋ, opula tɔ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩyɛ lɩɩ bamʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii tɔ.

eng: He said to them, Why are you afraid, you people of weak faith? Then he got up and gave orders to the winds and the lake, and there was a great calm.
local: Ɔ lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fanɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii kɩ man kyɔ, mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fan sʋ kufuu kanɩn? Nɩɩ ɔ kʋsʋ pʋntɛ afuu yɛ apaa mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɩ waa diin.

eng: As for the seed that was spread among thorny plants, this refers to those who hear the word, but the worries of this life and the false appeal of wealth choke the word, and it bears no fruit.
local: Ayubi mʋ nɩɩ ɩ tɩyɛ iwii tɔ mʋ ɩ gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bee nu asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ, amaa dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ atɔ tɔɔrɔɔsa yɛ kafulee biti bansa mʋ, ɩɩ nyan kɩtaa asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ, nɩɩ ɩ mɛɛ sɔyɩ abi.

eng: The people were amazed and said, What kind of person is this? Even the winds and the lake obey him.
local: Nɩɩ i kyinkyin asa mʋ, nɩɩ ba taasɛ yɛ, Mɩnɛ nyɩmɩsa ndee? Halɩɩ afuu yɛ apaa gbaa ii nu mʋ asɛ.

eng: As for what was planted on good soil, this refers to those who hear and understand, and bear fruit and produce—in one case a yield of one hundred to one, in another case a yield of sixty to one, and in another case a yield of thirty to one.
local: Amaa ayubi mʋ nɩɩ ɩ tɩyɛ kasɛ danbɩrasa tɔ mʋ, ɩ gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bee nu asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ nɩɩ bee nu ɩmʋ kasɛ mʋ, ɩmʋ sʋ bɛɛ waa atɔ danbɩrasa gaa fɛɛ ayubi mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ sɔyɩ abi kɩlɩfa nɩɩ ɩkʋ adisiye nɩɩ ɩkʋ mɔɔ adɩsa mʋ.

eng: When Jesus arrived on the other side of the lake in the country of the Gadarenes, two men who were demon-possessed came from among the tombs to meet him. They were so violent that nobody could travel on that road.
local: Yesu maa fʋʋ ɔbʋn mʋ benbe mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ Gadara ɔsʋwʋlɛ mʋ, anyɩn anyɔ kʋ mʋ nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi ba tɛ bamʋ sʋ mʋ ba lɩɩ nkyan tɔ ba gyaŋa mʋ. Ba kyɩna ba libi gaa fɛɛ ndʋnbwɛ, nɩɩ ɔkʋ mɛɛ taalɛ bɔla kanɩn ɔkpa maŋa sʋ.

eng: Jesus told them another parable: The kingdom of heaven is like someone who planted good seed in his field.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu bɩla bɔ bamʋ kɩŋasan kʋ yɛ, Sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii mʋ du nɩn fɛɛ ɔnyɩn kʋ maa naa ŋmatɛ ayubi danbɩrasa mʋ ndɔɔ tɔ.

eng: They cried out, What are you going to do with us, Son of God? Have you come to torture us before the time of judgment.
local: Ba kuusi yɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ mu‐Bii, mɩnɛ nɩɩ fii biti anɛ asɛ? Fʋ ba nɩn yɛ fʋ ba waa anɛ awʋrʋfɔ pɔyɩ kɛ ɩmʋ saŋa mʋ ɩ fʋʋ, abɛɛ.

eng: While people were sleeping, an enemy came and planted weeds among the wheat and went away.
local: Asa ba maa dɛ lʋwɛ kagyanbwɛ mʋ, ɔdɔɔpʋ maŋa mʋ‐dʋn naa taa afitiri libi ŋmatɛ waa ayu mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ natɛ kyʋn.

eng: Far off in the distance a large herd of pigs was feeding.
local: Akuuri damantɛ kʋ ba yɩlɛ tʋtɔ kɩbaafʋn bee gyii.

eng: When the stalks sprouted and bore grain, then the weeds also appeared.
local: Ayu mʋ maa nyɛ ɩtɔ ɩɩ kʋʋgɛ mʋ, nɩɩ afitiri libi mʋ gbaa ɩɩ dan.

eng: The demons pleaded with him, If you throw us out, send us into the herd of pigs.
local: Nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi mʋ ba kʋlɛ Yesu yɛ, Nɩɩ fʋ kan fii biti fʋ gya anɛ bɩrɛ, yɛgɛ a naa loo akuuri damantɛ nɩmʋ tɔ.

eng: The servants of the landowner came and said to him, Master, didnt you plant good seed in your field? Then how is it that it has weeds.
local: Nɩɩ kadɔɔ wuya mʋ ayaafɔlɛ ba ba mʋ asɛ ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, man gyɛ ayubi danbɩrasa nɩɩ fʋ ŋmatɛ fʋ ndɔɔ mʋ tɔ? Fɩnɛ nɩɩ afitiri libi nɩmʋ ɩ lɩɩ.

eng: Then he said to the demons, Go away, and they came out and went into the pigs. The whole herd rushed down the cliff into the lake and drowned.
local: Ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan natɛ! Nɩɩ ba lɩɩ anyɩn mʋ tɔ naa loo akuuri mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ akuuri damantɛ mʋ kpini ba sɩlɛ kpɩlɩgɛ kidenbi mʋ naa tɩyɛ ɔbʋn lala mʋ tɔ nɩɩ ba wu.

eng: An enemy has done this, he answered. The servants said to him, Do you want us to go and gather them.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Mɛ‐dʋn kʋ ɩ waa kanɩn. Nɩɩ mʋ ayaafɔlɛ mʋ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Fii biti fɛɛ a naa kyiigi ɩmʋ abɛɛ.

eng: Those who tended the pigs ran into the city and told everything that had happened to the demon-possessed men.
local: Nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ kɩɩ akuuri mʋ sʋ mʋ, ba sɩlɛ kpee kadɛ tɔ, naa tɔwɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ waa kpini yɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ waa anyɩn anyɔ mʋ nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi ba tɛ bamʋ sʋ mʋ.

eng: But the landowner said, No, because if you gather the weeds, youll pull up the wheat along with them.
local: Nɩɩ bamʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ mʋ lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ayee, fan yɛgɛ ɩmʋ kikyiigi, lɩɩ fɛɛ fan kan yɛ fen kyiigi afitiri libi mʋ, fen biti fan kyiigi bʋga yɛ ayu mʋ.

eng: Then the whole city came out and met Jesus. When they saw him, they pleaded with him to leave their region.
local: Kanɩn sʋ kadɛ mʋ tɔ asa kpini ba lɩɩ ba naa gyaŋa Yesu. Ba maa wu mʋ mʋ, ba kʋlɛ mʋ yɛ ɔ lɩɩ bamʋ ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ.

eng: Let both grow side by side until the harvest. And at harvesttime Ill say to the harvesters,First gather the weeds and tie them together in bundles to be burned. But bring the wheat into my barn.
local: Fan yɛgɛ ɩmʋ kpini ɩ bʋga dan naa fʋʋ kɩtɩŋɛ saŋa. Kanɩn saŋa maa mʋ, n biti n tɔwɛ atɩŋɛpʋ mʋ yɛ, ba daa kɩkpantɛ afitiri libi mʋ ŋmina ntun‐ntun kɛ ba tuwa ɩmʋ. Kɛ ba laata ayu mʋ naa waa mɛ kɩkyagɩla tɔ. .

eng: He told another parable to them: The kingdom of heaven is like a mustard seed that someone took and planted in his field.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu bɩla bɔ bamʋ kɩŋasan kʋ yɛ, Sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii mʋ du nɩn fɛɛ ɔnyɩn kʋ maa taa masitadi oyu kibi pii naa duu mʋ ndɔɔ tɔ.

eng: Its the smallest of all seeds. But when its grown, its the largest of all vegetable plants. It becomes a tree so that the birds in the sky come and nest in its branches.
local: Halɩɩ oyu maŋa kibi kɩ maa du pii iyu abi kpini tɔ gbaa mʋ, kɩɩ dan kɩ kyɔ apʋnfa kpini, nɩɩ kɩɩ ba waa oyu nɩɩ nbwiibi bɛɛ ba waa bamʋ nsʋswaa kɩmʋ n‐yaabi sʋ.

eng: He told them another parable: The kingdom of heaven is like yeast, which a woman took and hid in a bushel of wheat flour until the yeast had worked its way through all the dough.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu bɩla bɔ bamʋ kɩŋasan yɛ, Sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii mʋ ki du nɩn fɛɛ ɔkyɩɩ kʋ maa taa kɩfa‐tinsa kyagɩlɛ bodobodo inyifo ɩkyɛmɩsɛ ɩlala ɩsa tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ inyifo mʋ i tin nyɛ akyɔ.

eng: Jesus said all these things to the crowds in parables, and he spoke to them only in parables.
local: Aŋasan tɔ nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ ɩnɩmʋ sa sakyɔ mʋ. Ɔ man tɔwɛ sɛhn sa bamʋ yɛgɛ ɩ man gyɛ aŋasan tɔ.

eng: This was to fulfill what the prophet spoke: Ill speak in parables; Ill declare what has been hidden since the beginning of the world.
local: Yesu waa kanɩn nɩn kɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ tɔwɛ yɩla mʋ ɩ ba tɔ. Asɩn mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, Aŋasan tɔ nɩɩ n biti n tɔwɛ bamʋ asɛ, N biti n tɔwɛ bamʋ asɩn baalasa mʋ nɩɩ ɔkʋ man nyi ɩmʋ lɩɩ koo dulinyaa npiili asɛ.

eng: Jesus left the crowds and went into the house. His disciples came to him and said, Explain to us the parable of the weeds in the field.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɩɩ sakyɔ mʋ asɛ nɩɩ ɔ kyʋn kɩkpaara sʋ, nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba ba mʋ asɛ ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Kaapʋ anɛ kɩŋasan mʋ nɩɩ fʋ bɔ lɩɩ ayu yɛ afitiri libi mʋ kasɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, The one who plants the good seed is the Human One.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɔdɔɔpʋ mʋ nɩɩ o duu ayubi danbɩrasa mʋ ɩ gyɛ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ.

eng: The field is the world. And the good seeds are the followers of the kingdom. But the weeds are the followers of the evil one.
local: Ndɔɔ mʋ ɩ gyɛ dulinyaa, nɩɩ ayubi danbɩrasa mʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ asa. Afitiri libi mʋ mɔɔ ɩ gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba gyɛ Sɩtaanɛ asa mʋ.

eng: The enemy who planted them is the devil. The harvest is the end of the present age. The harvesters are the angels.
local: Nɩɩ ɔdʋn mʋ nɩɩ o duu afitiri libi mʋ ɩ gyɛ Sɩtaanɛ. Ayu mʋ kɩtɩŋɛ saŋa mʋ ɩ gyɛ dulinyaa laalaalʋwɛ. Nɩɩ atɩŋɛpʋ mʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ mʋ.

eng: Just as people gather weeds and burn them in the fire, so it will be at the end of the present age.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba kpantɛ afitiri libi mʋ tuwa mʋ, kanɩn nɩɩ ii biti ɩ waa dulinyaa laalaalʋwɛ nɩn.

eng: The Human One will send his angels, and they will gather out of his kingdom all things that cause people to fall away and all people who sin.
local: Mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ, n biti n sun mɛ sʋsʋ nbɔɔ, kɛ ba ba tasɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ pɩna asa nɩɩ bɛɛ waa alibi mʋ, yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa alibi mʋ, lɩɩ mi kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ.

eng: He will throw them into a burning furnace. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: Kɛ ba taa bamʋ tʋʋ waa awʋrʋfɔ fʋlɔn yen‐yen mʋ tɔ, opula mʋ nɩɩ kusu yɛ anobi kiduŋi bʋ.

eng: Then the righteous will shine like the sun in their Fathers kingdom. Those who have ears should hear.
local: Amaa bamʋ nɩɩ bamʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ mʋ bee biti ba ŋalɩgɛ sʋ fɛɛ kyɔwɛ bamʋ‐Sɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ. Ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ asʋ nɩɩ ee biti o nu mʋ o nu.

eng: The kingdom of heaven is like a treasure that somebody hid in a field, which someone else found and covered up. Full of joy, the finder sold everything and bought that field.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu bɩla bɔ bamʋ kɩŋasan kʋ yɛ, Sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii mʋ ki du nɩn fɛɛ atɔ kparɛ fɛɛ suwa mʋ nɩɩ ba taa baala nɩɩ ɔnyɩn kʋ naa benti kasɛ kʋ sʋ. Maa wu ɩmʋ mʋ, nɩɩ o bwii bun ɩmʋ sʋ. Ɩ maa waa mʋ ɔkɔn gaa sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ naa taa mʋ atɔ kpini fɛɛ nɩɩ ɔ naa sɔɔ kanɩn kasɛ maŋa.

eng: Again, the kingdom of heaven is like a merchant in search of fine pearls.
local: Sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii mʋ kɩ bɩla ki du nɩn fɛɛ, ɔyaawʋ‐gyipu maa naa ee biti kigyibi kparɛ mʋ.

eng: When he found one very precious pearl, he went and sold all that he owned and bought it.
local: Nɩɩ maa naa nyɛ kʋkʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ kɩkparɛ mʋ, ɔ naa fɛ mʋ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ mʋ kpini, nɩɩ ɔ naa sɔɔ kɩmʋ.

eng: Again, the kingdom of heaven is like a net that people threw into the lake and gathered all kinds of fish.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu bɩla tɔwɛ yɛ, Sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii mʋ bɩla ki du nɩn fɛɛ, ɩkɩn ɔkɩtapʋ maa tʋʋ mʋ kisewu kɩtaa ɩkɩn kʋkwɩɩ‐kʋkwɩɩ.

eng: When it was full, they pulled it to the shore, where they sat down and put the good fish together into containers. But the bad fish they threw away.
local: Ɩkɩn mʋ ba maa bɔla kisewu mʋ nɩɩ akɩtapʋ mʋ ba bɩɩtɛ kɩmʋ ba ɔlɩɩkaa nɩɩ ba kyɩna tasɛ adanbɩrasa mʋ waa alantan tɔ, nɩɩ ba taa bamʋ nɩɩ ba man bʋrɔn mʋ fɔyɩ.

eng: Thats the way it will be at the end of the present age. The angels will go out and separate the evil people from the righteous people.
local: Kanɩn nɩɩ ii biti ɩ ba waa dulinyaa laalaalʋwɛ mʋ. Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ mʋ bee biti ba ba tasɛ asa libi lɛɛ asa danbɩrasa tɔ.

eng: and will throw the evil ones into a burning furnace. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: kɛ ba taa bamʋ tʋʋ waa awʋrʋfɔ fʋlɔn yen‐yen mʋ tɔ, opula mʋ nɩɩ kusu yɛ anobi kiduŋi bʋ.

eng: Have you understood all these things?Jesus asked. They said to him, Yes.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan nu ɩmʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ fanɛ kpini kasɛ? Nɩɩ ba yɛ, Ɛɛhn, an nu ɩmʋ kasɛ.

eng: Then he said to them, Therefore, every legal expert who has been trained as a disciple for the kingdom of heaven is like the head of a household who brings old and new things out of their treasure chest.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Kanɩn sʋ, nbara ɔkaapʋpʋ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ ba waa ɔbɩɩlapʋ lɩɩ sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii sʋ mʋ, du nɩn fɛɛ kɩkpaara wuya mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ mʋ atɔ kparɛ pʋpwɛ yɛ adɩdaa lɩɩ mu obu to mʋ.

eng: When Jesus finished these parables, he departed.
local: Yesu maa bɔ bamʋ aŋasan anɩmʋ lʋwɛ mʋ, ɔ lɩɩ kadɛ mʋ tɔ.

eng: When he came to his hometown, he taught the people in their synagogue. They were surprised and said, Where did he get this wisdom? Where did he get the power to work miracles?.
local: Nɩɩ o kpee mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mʋ kadɛ tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ naa piili ɛɛ kaapʋ asa bamʋ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa, nɩɩ i kyinkyin bamʋ. Nɩɩ ba taasɛ yɛ, Fɩnɛ nɩɩ ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ nyɛ falɛ kanyaasɩn yɛ asɩn kyinkyinsa nɩmʋ lɩɩ?.

eng: Isnt he the carpenters son? Isnt his mother named Mary? Arent James, Joseph, Simon, and Judas his brothers?.
local: Man gyɛ kapinta mu‐bii mʋ ndee? Man gyɛ mu‐nyi ɩ gyɛ Mɛɛrɩ mʋ? Nɩɩ man gyɛ mu‐supu ana ɩ gyɛ Gyemisi yɛ Gyosefu yɛ Simon yɛ Gyudasi mʋ?.

eng: And his sisters, arent they here with us? Where did this man get all this.
local: Man gyɛ mʋ pɩɩkyɩɩ ana kpini ɩ bʋ ana asɛ nfɩɩ? Nɩɩ fɩnɛ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ falɛ asɩn nɩmʋ kpini lɩɩ.

eng: They were repulsed by him and fell into sin. But Jesus said to them, Prophets are honored everywhere except in their own hometowns and in their own households.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ba kina kɩkɔɔlɛ mʋ. Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ kʋmaa nɩmʋ sʋ bɛɛrɛɛ katɩn kʋmaa, amɔɔ mʋ kadɛ tɔ yɛ mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ abi nkʋn asɛ nɩɩ ɔ man sʋ bɛɛrɛɛ.

eng: He was unable to do many miracles there because of their disbelief.
local: Yesu man waa asɩn kyinkyinsa gaalaagaa tʋtɔ, lɩɩ fɛɛ asa mʋ ba maa man sʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ.

eng: When Jesus finished teaching his twelve disciples, he went on from there to teach and preach in their cities.
local: Yesu maa kaapʋ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ kudu anyɔ mʋ asɛ lʋwɛ mʋ, ɔ lɩɩ tʋtɔ kpee ɔ naa kaapʋ kɛ ɔ tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ ndɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ san Galile ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ mʋ.

eng: Now when John heard in prison about the things the Christ was doing, he sent word by his disciples to Jesus, asking.
local: Saŋa maa tɔ mʋ Gyɔn Okyugyeepu mʋ bʋ obu kitii tɔ. Maa nu kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Krisito ɩɩ waa mʋ, o sun mʋ abɩɩlapʋ.

eng: In those days John the Baptist appeared in the desert of Judea announcing.
local: Kanɩn saŋa maa mʋ, Gyɔn mʋ nɩɩ ee gyee asa Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee mʋ, ba Gyudeya ofuli sʋ ɛɛ tɔwɛ abwaarɛsɩn ɛɛ sa asa.

eng: Are you the one who is to come, or should we look for another.
local: ba naa taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Fʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ anɛ yɛ ee biti ɔ ba mʋ, abɛɛ a kɩɩ ɔpʋpwɛ ɔkpa.

eng: Change your hearts and lives! Here comes the kingdom of heaven.
local: yɛ, Fan yɛgɛ alibi kɩwaa, lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ ki tiri tɔ.

eng: Jesus responded, Go, report to John what you hear and see.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Fen bwii naa tɔwɛ Gyɔn asɩn mʋ nɩɩ fan nu yɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ fan wu.

eng: He was the one of whom Isaiah the prophet spoke when he said: The voice of one shouting in the wilderness, Prepare the way for the Lord; make his paths straight. .
local: Gyɔn nɩmʋ asɩn nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Isaya wʋla kyʋrɔɔ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ mʋ yɛ, Ɔsa kʋ bʋ ofuli sʋ ee kuusi ɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Fan lɔŋɔ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ ɔkpa mʋ, kɛ fan waa ɔkpa mʋ ntintin sa mʋ. .

eng: Those who were blind are able to see . Those who were crippled are walking. People with skin diseases are cleansed. Those who were deaf now hear. Those who were dead are raised up. The poor have good news proclaimed to them .
local: fɛɛ agyaatanapʋ ansi i bugi, nɩɩ ayaa yɛ abaa wusa wuya ana ba kʋsʋ natɛ, nɩɩ abootipu n‐yɩɩ lɔŋɔ sʋ, nɩɩ atikpiri asʋ a tigi, nɩɩ alewupu ba kyiŋi ba nkpa tɔ, nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ Asɩn Danbɩrasa mʋ sa atiripu.

eng: John wore clothes made of camels hair, with a leather belt around his waist. He ate locusts and wild honey.
local: Nyɔɔma ifuu nɩɩ ba taa waa Gyɔn atɔ bunsa, nɩɩ kʋtɔbwaaya ɔwʋlɛ kɩrɩsʋ i ŋmina mʋ kasɩyɛ tɔ. Mʋ agyitɔ‐gyisa mɔɔ ɩ gyɛ ntuntunbi yɛ ŋmasan.

eng: Happy are those who dont stumble and fall because of me.
local: Kusee ɩ gyɛ sa ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man yɛgɛ mɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii.

eng: People from Jerusalem, throughout Judea, and all around the Jordan River came to him.
local: Asa ba lɩɩ Gyerusalem kadɛ tɔ yɛ Gyudeya ɩsʋwʋlɛ sʋ yɛ Gyɔdan kabaafʋn kpini ba mʋ asɛ.

eng: When Johns disciples had gone, Jesus spoke to the crowds about John: What did you go out to the wilderness to see? A stalk blowing in the wind?.
local: Gyɔn Okyugyeepu abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba maa bwii bee kpee mʋ, nɩɩ Yesu piili ɛɛ tɔwɛ Gyɔn n‐yɩɩ sʋ asɩn ɛɛ sa sakyɔ mʋ yɛ, Mɩnɛ nɩɩ fan kpee Gyɔn asɛ kiperi mʋ sʋ fanaa kɩɩ? Fan kpee naa kɩɩ kifitiri mʋ nɩɩ afuu ɩɩ da kɩmʋ ii biliŋi tɔ nɩn?.

eng: As they confessed their sins, he baptized them in the Jordan River.
local: Ba lɛɛ bamʋ alibi tɔwɛ, nɩɩ o gyee bamʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee Gyɔdan ɔbʋn mʋ tɔ.

eng: What did you go out to see? A man dressed up in refined clothes? Look, those who wear refined clothes are in royal palaces.
local: Nɩɩ man gyɛ kanɩn mʋ, mɩnɛ nɩɩ fen kpee naa kɩɩ? Nyɩmɩsa mʋ nɩɩ o bun ataa danbɩrasa mʋ, nɩɩ fan naa kɩɩ? Ayee, asa mʋ nɩɩ bee bun ataa danbɩrasa mʋ, ba tɛ iwurakpa nɩn.

eng: Many Pharisees and Sadducees came to be baptized by John. He said to them, You children of snakes! Who warned you to escape from the angry judgment that is coming soon?.
local: Gyɔn maa wu Farasii yɛ Sadusi abi gaalaagaa ba maa ba mʋ asɛ, kɛ o gyee bamʋ kekyugyee mʋ, ɔ taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fanɛ anansɛ banɩmʋ, anɩmʋ ɩ tɔwɛ waa fanɛ yɛ fan sɩlɛ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ agbʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ ba asa sʋ mʋ tɔ?.

eng: What did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet.
local: Nɩɩ mɩnɛ gbaa nɩɩ fan kpee naa kɩɩ? Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ abɛɛ? Ɛɛhn, n tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ ɔ kyɔ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ.

eng: Produce fruit that shows you have changed your hearts and lives.
local: Fan waa atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa kɛ ɩɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ fan yɛgɛ alibi kɩwaa.

eng: He is the one of whom it is written: Look, Im sending my messenger before you, who will prepare your way before you .
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Gyɔn nɩmʋ asɩn nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ yɛ, N biti n sun mɛ kabɔɔ kɛ ɔ gyankpaa fʋ, mʋ ii biti ɔ ba lɔŋɔ fʋ ɔkpa yɩla fʋ. .

eng: And dont even think about saying to yourselves, Abraham is our father. I tell you that God is able to raise up Abrahams children from these stones.
local: Fan man da fanɛ nkɔlɔ sʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, Anɛ naana ɩ gyɛ Abraham ɩmʋ sʋ an sʋ kamɔlɩgɛ. N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ taalɛ taa abuu nɩmʋ biliŋi anaana‐bii sa Abraham.

eng: I assure you that no one who has ever been born is greater than John the Baptist. Yet whoever is least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu bɩla tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin yɛ, nyɩmɩsa kʋ man bʋ tɔ nɩɩ ba kʋʋgɛ mʋ kasɛ sʋ nfɩɩ, nɩɩ ɔ kyɔ Gyɔn okyugyeepu mʋ. Ɩmʋ kpini gbaa mʋ, ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ pii sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ mʋ kyɔ mʋ.

eng: The ax is already at the root of the trees. Therefore, every tree that doesnt produce good fruit will be chopped down and tossed into the fire.
local: Nbɩɩnbɩɩ bɩrɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla taa kpanfa yɩla iyu mʋ ilin tɔ o gyoo. Nɩɩ oyu kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ mɛɛ sɔyɩ abi mʋ, bee biti ba ŋɛ kɩmʋ tʋʋ kɛ ba taa waa fʋlɔn tɔ.

eng: From the days of John the Baptist until now the kingdom of heaven is violently attacked as violent people seize it.
local: Lɩɩ Gyɔn okyugyeepu saŋa ba lɩɩ ndaga mʋ, bamʋ nɩɩ bamʋ ansi ɩ payɩ yɛ bee biti ba loo sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ mʋ, ba sʋ ɔlʋn yɛ kensi payɩ bee loo kɩmʋ tɔ.

eng: I baptize with water those of you who have changed your hearts and lives. The one who is coming after me is stronger than I am. Im not worthy to carry his sandals. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and with fire.
local: Nkyu nɩɩ mɛɛ mɛ n sʋ n gyee fanɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee kɛ ɩɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ fan yɛgɛ alibi kɩwaa. Amaa ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ mɛ kamaa ɛɛ ba mʋ kyɔ mɛ gaa, nɩɩ n man dagaa gbaa fɛɛ n sʋla mʋ asɩbɩta. Mɛɛ mʋ bɩrɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ yɛ fʋlɔn nɩɩ ee biti ɔ taa gyee fanɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee.

eng: All the Prophets and the Law prophesied until John came.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ mʋ yɛ nbara mʋ, ɩ wʋla tɔwɛ lɩɩ kuwura‐gyii mʋ sʋ naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Gyɔn ba.

eng: The shovel he uses to sift the wheat from the husks is in his hands. He will clean out his threshing area and bring the wheat into his barn. But he will burn the husks with a fire that cant be put out.
local: Ɔ sʋ mʋ kʋtɔ kyaalɛsa mʋ kɩbaa tɔ, kɛ ɔ kyaalɛ ayubi mʋ lɛɛ afʋnfʋntɛɛ mʋ tɔ waa kikyunkun tɔ. Ee biti ɔ taa afʋnfʋntɛɛ mʋ waa fʋlɔn mʋ nɩɩ i mee dun mʋ tɔ.

eng: If you are willing to accept it, he is Elijah who is to come.
local: Nɩɩ fan kan fen biti fan kɔɔlɛ kyula mʋ, Gyɔn ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Eligya mʋ nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ yɩla yɛ ee biti ɔ ba mʋ.

eng: At that time Jesus came from Galilee to the Jordan River so that John would baptize him.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu lɩɩ Galile ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ ba Gyɔdan ɔbʋn mʋ tɔ Gyɔn asɛ, kɛ o gyee mʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee.

eng: Let the person who has ears, hear.
local: Ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ asʋ nɩɩ ee biti o nu mʋ, o nu.

eng: John tried to stop him and said, I need to be baptized by you, yet you come to me.
local: Amaa Gyɔn kpan mʋ n‐yɩɩ kɛ ɔ kyɛɛgɛ Yesu nfɛɛrɛ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Fɛɛ fʋ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ fu gyee mɛ kekyugyee, nɩɩ fʋ ɩ ba mɛ asɛ yɛ n gyee fʋ.

eng: To what will I compare this generation? It is like a child sitting in the marketplaces calling out to others.
local: Mɩnɛ nɩɩ n taalɛ taa kaala ndaga asa? Ba du nɩn fɛɛ nbii ba maa tɛ agya tɔ bɛɛ tɩɩ abaa bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ.

eng: Jesus answered, Allow me to be baptized now. This is necessary to fulfill all righteousness. So John agreed to baptize Jesus.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Yɛgɛ ɩ waa kanɩn nbɩɩnbɩɩ nɩmʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkpa nɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa a maa waa ɩmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ biti mʋ kpini. Nɩɩ Gyɔn kyula gyee Yesu Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee.

eng: We played the flute for you and you didnt dance. We sang a funeral song and you didnt mourn.
local: An fʋlɛ nleebi sa fanɛ, amaa fan man kyaa, nɩɩ an bɩɩ alengyi ɩlʋn, amaa fan man su.

eng: When Jesus was baptized, he immediately came up out of the water. Heaven was opened to him, and he saw the Spirit of God coming down like a dove and resting on him.
local: Gyɔn maa dan gyee Yesu Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee lʋwɛ mʋ, Yesu lɩɩ ɔbʋn mʋ tɔ. Opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ sʋsʋ bugi tɔ, nɩɩ o wu Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ maa kpɩlɩgɛ fɛɛ kuwura‐lɔpɔ ba gyɔŋɔ mʋ sʋ.

eng: For John came neither eating nor drinking, and they say, He has a demon.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Gyɔn maa ba mʋ, o mee gyii nɩɩ o mee nuu, nɩɩ ba yɛ, Nbwɩɩ libi ba tɛ Gyɔn sʋ.

eng: A voice from heaven said, This is my Son whom I dearly love; I find happiness in him.
local: Nɩɩ ɔbʋlɛ kʋ lɩɩ sʋsʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ mɛ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ obii‐bitisa, nɩɩ mʋ ɩ bɔla mi ansi.

eng: Yet the Human One came eating and drinking, and they say, Look, a glutton and a drunk, a friend of tax collectors and sinners. But wisdom is proved to be right by her works.
local: Mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ n maa ba mʋ, n gyii nɩɩ n nuu, nɩɩ ba yɛ, Ee gyii gaa nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ ɔtabʋʋpʋ yɛ lenpoo akɔɔlɛpʋ yɛ alibi awaapʋ bamʋ kyɛmɩnɛ. Amaa nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan o buu Ɩbwaarɛ kanyaasɩn ɩkpa sʋ mʋ, ɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ mʋ kanyaasɩn mʋ gyɛ kesintin.

eng: Then he began to scold the cities where he had done his greatest miracles because they didnt change their hearts and lives.
local: Asa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ ndɛ mʋ nɩɩ Yesu waa mʋ asɩn kyinkyinsa gaalaagaa mʋ, ba man yɛgɛ bamʋ alibi kɩwaa, kanɩn sʋ Yesu tɔwɛ yii bamʋ yɛ.

eng: How terrible it will be for you, Chorazin! How terrible it will be for you, Bethsaida! For if the miracles done among you had been done in Tyre and Sidon, they would have changed their hearts and lives and put on funeral clothes and ashes a long time ago.
local: Korasin abi, fanɛ laakʋ! Betisayida abi, fanɛ laakʋ! Asɩn kyinkyinsa mʋ nɩɩ n waa fanɛ ndɛ nɩmʋ sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɩ daa ɩ gyɛ Tayi maa Sidon nɩn, naafɔɔ ba wʋla dɩla ɩbɔɔta, kɛ ba taa ndaara bun bamʋ amu, kɛ ɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ ba kyɛɛgɛ bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ lɩɩ alibi tɔ.

eng: Be careful that you dont practice your religion in front of people to draw their attention. If you do, you will have no reward from your Father who is in heaven.
local: Fan baa fan da sʋ kɛ fan man waa fanɛ adanbɩrasa asa ansi sʋ, kɛ ba nyɛ wu fanɛ. Nɩɩ fan kan waa kanɩn mʋ, fan mɛɛ yɛ fan nyɛ kʋkɔka lɩɩ fanɛ‐Sɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ sʋsʋ mʋ asɛ.

eng: But I say to you that Tyre and Sidon will be better off on Judgment Day than you.
local: Amaa n tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, Tayi abi yɛ Sidon abi kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ ii biti ɩ baa ɩ bɔ fanɛ lɛɛ kakɛ talɛsa mʋ.

eng: Whenever you give to the poor, dont blow your trumpet as the hypocrites do in the synagogues and in the streets so that they may get praise from people. I assure you, thats the only reward theyll get.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ fʋ kan kɛɛ otiripu atɔ mʋ, man taa da kibonbon fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ nnɔ nnyɔ‐nnyɔ wuya ana mʋ bɛɛ waa kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɩgyaŋɛkpa yɛ agbenbi sʋ kɛ asa ba nyɛ yɩn bamʋ mʋ. N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin fɛɛ ba tɛɛ wʋla nyɛ bamʋ kʋkɔka lʋwɛ.

eng: And you, Capernaum, will you be honored by being raised up to heaven? No, you will be thrown down to the place of the dead. After all, if the miracles that were done among you had been done in Sodom, it would still be here today.
local: Nɩɩ fanɛ Kapanum abi, fen biti fɛɛ ba kʋsaa fanɛ kpee sʋsʋ Ɩbwaarɛ ayɛ nɩn abɛɛ? Ayee, bee biti ba tʋʋ fanɛ waa awʋrʋfɔ fʋlɔn yen‐yen tɔ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ asɩn kyinkyinsa mʋ nɩɩ n waa fanɛ kadɛ tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ɩ daa ɩ gyɛ Sodom nɩɩ n daa waa ɩmʋ nɩn, naafɔɔ kadɛ mʋ san kɩ bʋ tɔ ba lɩɩ ndaga.

eng: But when you give to the poor, dont let your left hand know what your right hand is doing.
local: Amaa nɩɩ fʋ kan kɛɛ atiripu afulee mʋ, man yɛgɛ fʋ‐bɩna kɩbaa ɔ bɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ fu gyisa kɩbaa mʋ waa mʋ.

eng: But I say to you that it will be better for the land of Sodom on the Judgment Day than it will be for you.
local: Amaa n tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, Sodom abi kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ ii biti ɩ baa ɩ bɔ fanɛ lɛɛ kakɛ talɛsa mʋ tɔ.

eng: so that you may give to the poor in secret. Your Father who sees what you do in secret will reward you.
local: kɛ fʋ kakɛɛ ɩ waa asiiri tɔ. Saamʋ fʋ‐Sɛ mʋ nɩɩ ee wu kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ fɩɩ waa asiiri tɔ mʋ, ii biti ɔ ka fʋ kʋkɔ.

eng: At that time Jesus said, I praise you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because youve hidden these things from the wise and intelligent and have shown them to babies.
local: Saŋa maŋa nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ yɛ, Mɛ‐Sɛ, fʋ gyɛ sʋsʋ yɛ kasɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, n yɩn fʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ fʋ taa falɛ asɩn baala anyaasɩnpʋ yɛ anyipu sʋ, nɩɩ fʋ lɛɛ ɩmʋ kaapʋ nbii wuribi.

eng: When you pray, dont be like hypocrites. They love to pray standing in the synagogues and on the street corners so that people will see them. I assure you, thats the only reward theyll get.
local: Nɩɩ fɛn kan fɛn kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ mʋ, fan man waa fɛɛ nnɔ nnyɔ‐nnyɔ wuya ana mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ bee biti kɩyɩlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɩgyaŋɛkpa yɛ agbenbi nkpankyɛɛrɛ bɛɛ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ, kɛ asa ba nyɛ wu bamʋ. N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin fɛɛ, ba wʋla nyɛ bamʋ kʋkɔka lʋwɛ.

eng: Indeed, Father, this brings you happiness.
local: Ɩ gyɛ kanɩn nɩn, nɩɩ kanɩn ɩ gyɛ fʋ kɔkɔlɔ biti.

eng: Then Pharisees and legal experts came to Jesus from Jerusalem and said.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Farasii abi mʋ akʋ yɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ ba lɩɩ Gyerusalem ba Yesu asɛ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ.

eng: But when you pray, go to your room, shut the door, and pray to your Father who is present in that secret place. Your Father who sees what you do in secret will reward you.
local: Amaa nɩɩ fʋ kan fɩɩ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ mʋ, loo fu obu to kɛ fu tii, kɛ fʋ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ sa fʋ‐Sɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔkʋ mee wu mʋ mʋ. Saamʋ fʋ‐Sɛ mʋ nɩɩ ee wu kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ fɩɩ waa asiiri tɔ mʋ, ii biti ɔ ka fʋ kʋkɔ.

eng: My Father has handed all things over to me. No one knows the Son except the Father. And nobody knows the Father except the Son and anyone to whom the Son wants to reveal him.
local: Mɛ‐Sɛ taa kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ waa mɛ abaa tɔ. Ɔkʋ man nyi obii mʋ amɔɔ Ɔsɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔkʋ man nyi Ɔsɛ mʋ amɔɔ Obii mʋ, yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ Obii mʋ ii biti ɔ lɛɛ mʋ kaapʋ bamʋ mʋ.

eng: Why are your disciples breaking the elders rules handed down to us? They dont ritually purify their hands by washing before they eat.
local: Mɩnɛ waa nɩɩ fʋ abɩɩlapʋ be mee buu anɛ anaana tɔ kɩkaapʋ mʋ? Lɩɩ fɛɛ ba mɛɛ fwɩɩ bamʋ abaa pɔyɩ nɩɩ bee gyii.

eng: When you pray, dont pour out a flood of empty words, as the Gentiles do. They think that by saying many words theyll be heard.
local: Nɩɩ fan kan fen kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ mʋ, fan man tɔwɛ ntɔwɛ bʋnswɩɩsa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ man sʋ kasɛ fɛɛ ɩdakpa asunpu lɛɛ mʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ bɛɛ fɛ nfɛɛrɛ nɩn fɛɛ bamʋ kʋkʋlɛ bʋnswɩɩsa mʋ, ii biti ɩ yɛgɛ kɛ ɩ ba tɔ sa bamʋ.

eng: Come to me, all you who are struggling hard and carrying heavy loads, and I will give you rest.
local: Fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fan sʋ nsʋla budusa nɩɩ fan kpɩnɛ mʋ, fan ba mɛ asɛ, kɛ n sa fanɛ ɔfʋʋtɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, Why do you break the command of God by keeping the rules handed down to you?.
local: Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ lɩɩ fanɛ anaana tɔ kɩkaapʋ sʋ, fen mee buu Ɩbwaarɛ kɔnɔsa mʋ.

eng: Dont be like them, because your Father knows what you need before you ask.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan man waa fɛɛ bamʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ fanɛ‐Sɛ nyi kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kii tiri fanɛ pɔyɩ nɩɩ fɛn kʋlɛ mʋ mʋ.

eng: For God said, Honor your father and your mother, and The person who speaks against father or mother will certainly be put to death .
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Bu fʋ‐sɛ yɛ fu‐nyi nɩɩ ɔ bɩla yɛ, Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ‐sɛ abɛɛ mu‐nyi kɔnɔ mʋ, ba mɔɔ mʋ.

eng: Put on my yoke, and learn from me. Im gentle and humble. And you will find rest for yourselves.
local: Fan kyula kɛ fan buu mɛ, kɛ fan kasɛ mɛ tɔ, kɛ fan nyɛ ɔfʋʋtɛ sa fanɛ kala ana, lɩɩ fɛɛ n du bwahn nɩɩ n bɩya mɛ n‐yɩɩ kasɛ lɩɩ mɛ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ.

eng: Pray like this: Our Father who is in heaven, uphold the holiness of your name.
local: Nɩɩ fen biti fan kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ mʋ, falɛ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fan maa tɔwɛ. Anɛ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ fʋ bʋ sʋsʋ, yɛgɛ asa ba bʋnyaa fʋ kɩtɩɩ.

eng: But you say, If you tell your father or mother,Everything Im expected to contribute to you Im giving to God as a gift,then you dont have to honor your father.
local: Amaa fɛn kaapʋ yɛ, nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan tɔwɛ mʋ‐sɛ abɛɛ mu‐nyi yɛ, Kakɛɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ fʋ nyɛ lɩɩ mɛ asɛ mʋ, n taa waa saraga sa Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ.

eng: My yoke is easy to bear, and my burden is light.
local: Nɩɩ fan kan fan buu mɛ mʋ, ɩ man bʋlʋn nɩɩ mɛ kasʋla gbaa man budu.

eng: Bring in your kingdom so that your will is done on earth as its done in heaven.
local: Yɛgɛ fu kuwura‐gyii mʋ kɩ ba, kɛ fʋ yɛgɛ fu kebiti kɩ waa kasɛ sʋ nfɩɩ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ki du sʋsʋ mʋ.

eng: So you do away with Gods Law for the sake of the rules that have been handed down to you.
local: ɩ man bɩla i tiri fɛɛ o buu mʋ‐sɛ yɛ mu‐nyi kɛ ɔ kɩɩ bamʋ sʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ lɩɩ fanɛ anaana tɔ kɩkaapʋ sʋ mʋ, fen mee buu Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn mʋ.

eng: After Jesus was born in Bethlehem in the territory of Judea during the rule of King Herod, magi came from the east to Jerusalem.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba kʋʋgɛ Yesu Krisito Betilehem kadɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ Gyudeya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ mʋ, kanɩn saŋa maa mʋ Herodi ɩ gyɛ owura. Nɩɩ ɩ ba fɛɛ, anyaasɩnpʋ akʋ ba lɩɩ kyɔwɛ ɩlɩɩ ba Gyerusalem kadɛ tɔ.

eng: Hypocrites! Isaiah really knew what he was talking about when he prophesied about you.
local: Fanɛ kebunbun ansi wuya ana! Fanɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bɔla mʋ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Isaya sʋ tɔwɛ mʋ gyɛ kesintin. Ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ.

eng: Then the Spirit led Jesus up into the wilderness so that the devil might tempt him.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Kayaayu mʋ sʋla Yesu kpee ofuli sʋ, kɛ Sɩtaanɛ ɔ naa tʋʋ mʋ tɔ kɩɩ.

eng: Give us the bread we need for today.
local: Sa anɛ kakɛ kʋmaa agyitɔ.

eng: Boarding a boat, Jesus crossed to the other side of the lake and went to his own city.
local: Yesu dii loo ɔkʋlɩ tɔ, faa ɔbʋn mʋ ba mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mʋ kadɛ tɔ.

eng: They asked, Where is the newborn king of the Jews? Weve seen his star in the east, and weve come to honor him.
local: nɩɩ ba taasɛ yɛ, Nkɩɩ kebii mʋ nɩɩ ba kʋʋgɛ mʋ yɛ o be gyii Gyiwu abi kuwura mʋ? An wu mu kikyikpebi maa paatɛ lɩɩ kyɔwɛ kɩlɩɩ, nɩɩ an ba yɛ an be muŋa bʋnyaa mʋ.

eng: This people honors me with their lips, but their hearts are far away from me.
local: Asa nɩmʋ ba sʋ bamʋ nnɔ nɩn bɛɛ bʋnyaa mɛ amaa bamʋ nkɔlɔ ɩ man bʋ mɛ asɛ.

eng: After Jesus had fasted for forty days and forty nights, he was starving.
local: Maa waa noŋmina nkɛ adɩna kakyʋwapɛ yɛ kagyanbwɛ mʋ, akʋn ɩɩ mɔɔ mʋ.

eng: Forgive us for the ways we have wronged you, just as we also forgive those who have wronged us.
local: Taa anɛ alibi kɛɛ anɛ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ anɛ gbaa anɛ an taa bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa anɛ alibi kɛɛ bamʋ mʋ.

eng: People brought to him a man who was paralyzed, lying on a cot. When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the man who was paralyzed, Be encouraged, my child, your sins are forgiven.
local: Nɩɩ asa akʋ ba taa ɔnyɩn mʋgʋlɛ‐mʋgʋlɛ wuya mʋ nɩɩ ɔ dɛ kɩkpaata sʋ bɩya mʋ. Yesu maa wu bamʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii mʋ, ɔ tɔwɛ mʋgʋlɛ‐mʋgʋlɛ wuya mʋ yɛ, Mi‐bii, yɛgɛ fʋ kɔkɔlɔ kɩ dɛ fʋ. N taa fʋ alibi kɛɛ fʋ.

eng: When King Herod heard this, he was troubled, and everyone in Jerusalem was troubled with him.
local: Owura Herodi maa nu falɛ asɩn mʋ ɩɩ tɔɔrɔɔ mʋ gaa yɛ Gyerusalem abi kpini.

eng: Their worship of me is empty since they teach instructions that are human rules .
local: Bee sun mɛ kɩyan nɩn, lɩɩ fɛɛ nyɩmɩsa nbara nɩɩ bɛ sʋ bɛɛ kaapʋ. .

eng: The tempter came to him and said, Since you are Gods Son, command these stones to become bread.
local: Nɩɩ Sɩtaanɛ ba mʋ asɛ ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Nɩɩ fʋ kan fʋ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Mu‐Bii mʋ, tɔwɛ abuu nɩmʋ kɛ i biliŋi agyitɔ.

eng: And dont lead us into temptation, but rescue us from the evil one.
local: Man yɛgɛ a loo kʋtʋʋ tɔ kɩɩ tɔ, amaa lɛɛ anɛ lɩɩ olibilibi mʋ abaa tɔ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ kuwura‐gyii yɛ ɔlʋn, yɛ bʋnyaa ɩ gyɛ fʋ lɛɛ kakpaa.

eng: Some legal experts said among themselves, This man is insulting God.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ nbara akaapʋpʋ mʋ akʋ bɛɛ tɔwɛ abaa asɛ yɛ, Ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ ɩ tɔwɛ nbusaa asɩn ee yii Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: He gathered all the chief priests and the legal experts and asked them where the Christ was to be born.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu abɩlɩsa yɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ ba gyaŋɛ, nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fɩnɛ nɩɩ bee biti ba kʋʋgɛ Krisito abɛɛ Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ mʋ.

eng: Jesus called the crowd near and said to them, Listen and understand.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɩɩ sakyɔ mʋ ba mʋ asɛ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Fan nu mɛ asɛ kɛ fan nu ɩmʋ kasɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, Its written, People wont live only by bread, but by every word spoken by God .
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ba kyʋrɔɔ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlɛsa mʋ tɔ yɛ, Man gyɛ agyitɔ wʋlɛ nkʋn ɩɩ sa nyɩmɩsa nkpa, amɔɔ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kɔnɔ tɔ. .

eng: If you forgive others their sins, your heavenly Father will also forgive you.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ nɩɩ asa ba waa fanɛ alibi nɩɩ fan taa kɛɛ bamʋ mʋ, fanɛ‐Sɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ sʋsʋ mʋ gbaa ii biti ɔ taa kɛɛ fanɛ.

eng: But Jesus knew what they were thinking and said, Why do you fill your minds with evil things?.
local: Yesu maa bɩɩ nfɛɛrɛ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ fɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Mɩnɛ nfɛɛrɛ libi nɩɩ fɛn fɛ fanɛ nkɔlɔ tɔ kanɩn?.

eng: They said, In Bethlehem of Judea, for this is what the prophet wrote.
local: Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ mʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Bee biti ba kʋʋgɛ mʋ Betilehem kadɛ tɔ Gyudeya Ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ kʋ wʋla kyʋrɔɔ yɩla yɛ.

eng: Its not what goes into the mouth that contaminates a person in Gods sight. Its what comes out of the mouth that contaminates the person.
local: Man gyɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kii loo nyɩmɩsa kɔnɔ tɔ mʋ ɩɩ waa mʋ ineesi, amɔɔ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ lɩɩ mʋ kɔnɔ tɔ mʋ ɩɩ waa mʋ ineesi.

eng: After that the devil brought him into the holy city and stood him at the highest point of the temple. He said to him.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Sɩtaanɛ taa mʋ kpee Gyerusalem, kadɛ kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ tɔ naa yɩlɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ ketin tɔ sʋsʋ.

eng: But if you dont forgive others, neither will your Father forgive your sins.
local: Amaa nɩɩ fan kan fan man taa asa alibi kɛɛ bamʋ bɩrɛ, fanɛ‐Sɛ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ taa fanɛ alibi kɛɛ fanɛ.

eng: Which is easier—to say, Your sins are forgiven, or to say, Get up and walk?.
local: Ɩmʋ kɩmɔmɔ ɩ bʋlʋn kɩtɔwɛ? Fɛɛ n tɔwɛ ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ yɛ, N taa fʋ alibi kɛɛ fʋ, abɛɛ n tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Kʋsʋ, kɛ fʋ natɛ.

eng: You, Bethlehem, land of Judah, by no means are you least among the rulers of Judah, because from you will come one who governs, who will shepherd my people Israel.
local: Fʋ Betilehem mʋ nɩɩ fʋ bʋ Gyudeya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ, halɩɩ fʋ maa gyɛ pɩɩ gbaa mʋ, fʋ gyɛ ɔkparɛ lɩɩ fɛɛ fʋ tɔ nɩɩ owura lala mʋ ii biti ɔ lɩɩ, nɩɩ mʋ ee biti ɔ baa ɔ gyɛ fɛɛ nbʋlʋpʋ ɔkpapʋ sa mɛ asa Ɩsɩrayɩ abi.

eng: Then the disciples came and said to him, Do you know that the Pharisees were offended by what you just said.
local: Nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba ba mʋ asɛ nɩɩ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Fu nyi yɛ Farasii abi mʋ ba maa nu falɛ asɩn mʋ ɩ tɔɔrɔɔ bamʋ.

eng: Since you are Gods Son, throw yourself down; for it is written, I will command my angels concerning you, and they will take you up in their hands so that you wont hit your foot on a stone. .
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Nɩɩ fʋ kan fʋ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Mu‐Bii mʋ, fugi tɩyɛ kasɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ba kyʋrɔɔ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ yɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ sa mʋ nbɔɔ kɔnɔ lɩɩ fʋ sʋ, nɩɩ bee biti ba taa bamʋ abaa sɔgɩta fʋ, kɛ fʋ kɩyaa kɩ man nyɛ watɛ abuu. .

eng: And when you fast, dont put on a sad face like the hypocrites. They distort their faces so people will know they are fasting. I assure you that they have their reward.
local: Nɩɩ fan kan waa noŋmina mʋ, fan man waa fanɛ ansi tɔ kʋwɛɛ kuwu fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ nnɔ nnyɔ‐nnyɔ wuya ana bɛɛ waa mʋ. Bɛɛ nyaaŋɛ bamʋ ansi tɔ kɛ asa ba nyɛ wu yɛ ba ŋmina kɔnɔ. N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin fɛɛ ba wʋla nyɛ bamʋ kʋkɔka lʋwɛ.

eng: But so you will know that the Human One has authority on the earth to forgive sins—he said to the man who was paralyzed—Get up, take your cot, and go home.
local: Amaa n biti fan bɩɩ fɛɛ, mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ n sʋ ɔlʋn kasɛ sʋ n taa alibi kɛɛ. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋgʋlɛ‐mʋgʋlɛ wuya mʋ yɛ, Kʋsʋ, taa fʋ kalan kɛ fu kpewu.

eng: Then Herod secretly called for the magi and found out from them the time when the star had first appeared.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Owura Herodi tɩɩ anyaasɩnpʋ mʋ lɩɩ ɩkaa sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ bamʋ kɛ ɔ bɩɩ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba wu kikyikpebi maa lɩɩ mʋ.

eng: Dont judge, so that you wont be judged.
local: Fan man bun kʋpwɛ, kɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ man bun fanɛ gbaa kʋpwɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, Every plant that my heavenly Father didnt plant will be pulled up.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Oyu kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ sʋsʋ i du kɩmʋ mʋ, ee biti o kyiigi kɩmʋ tʋʋ.

eng: He called his twelve disciples and gave them authority over unclean spirits to throw them out and to heal every disease and every sickness.
local: Yesu tɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ kudu anyɔ ba gyaŋɛ, nɩɩ ɔ sa bamʋ ɔlʋn yɛ ba taa gya nbwɩɩ libi, kɛ ba kyɛ alɔ yiri‐yiri kʋmaa nɩmʋ.

eng: Jesus replied, Again its written, Dont test the Lord your God .
local: Yesu bwii lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ba bɩla kyʋrɔɔ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ yɛ, Man tʋʋ fʋ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔ kɩɩ. .

eng: Now when Jesus saw the crowds, he went up a mountain. He sat down and his disciples came to him.
local: Yesu maa wu sakyɔ mʋ, o dii kpee kɩbɩɩ‐bi kʋ sʋ naa kyɩna kɩmʋ sʋ, nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba ba mʋ asɛ tʋtɔ.

eng: When you fast, brush your hair and wash your face.
local: Amaa nɩɩ fʋ kan ŋmina kɔnɔ mʋ, fwɩɩ fu ansi tɔ, sʋswa nfɔ kɛ fʋ sɛyɩ fu kumu tɔ.

eng: The man got up and went home.
local: Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ kʋsʋ nɩɩ o kpewu.

eng: At that time Jesus went through the wheat fields on the Sabbath. His disciples were hungry so they were picking heads of wheat and eating them.
local: Saŋa maŋa nɩɩ Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba naa alʋn kadɔɔ tɔ kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ kʋ. Nɩɩ akʋn a mɔɔ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ba piili bɛɛ gyayɩ alʋn mʋ ɩkʋ bɛɛ wɛ.

eng: He sent them to Bethlehem, saying, Go and search carefully for the child. When youve found him, report to me so that I too may go and honor him.
local: Nɩɩ o sun bamʋ Betilehem tɔwɛ yɛ, Fan kpee naa kɩɩ danbɩrasa biti kebii mʋ. Nɩɩ fan kan wu mʋ mʋ fan ba tɔwɛ mɛ, kɛ ngbaa mɛ n kpee naa muŋa bʋnyaa mʋ.

eng: Youll receive the same judgment you give. Whatever you deal out will be dealt out to you.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ fɛn bɔla sʋ fen bun fanɛ bɩrɩsa ana kʋpwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ gbaa ii biti ɔ bɔla sʋ bun ngbaa fanɛ kʋpwɛ. Nɩɩ kʋtɔ kaalasa mʋ nɩɩ fan taa kaala sa fanɛ bɩrɩsa ana mʋ, kanɩn nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ kaala sa fanɛ.

eng: Leave the Pharisees alone. They are blind people who are guides to blind people. But if a blind person leads another blind person, they will both fall into a ditch.
local: Fan man tʋwɛ bamʋ, ba du nɩn fɛɛ agyaatanapʋ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ gyankpaa agyaatanapʋ nɩn. Nɩɩ gyaatanapʋ kan ɛɛ gyankpaa gyaatanapʋ mʋ, bamʋ kpini bee biti ba tɩyɛ ɔbɔ tɔ.

eng: Here are the names of the twelve apostles: first, Simon, who is called Peter; and Andrew his brother; James the son of Zebedee; and John his brother.
local: Abɩɩlapʋ kudu anyɔ mʋ atɩɩ ndee. Ɔgyankpaapʋ mʋ ɩ gyɛ Simon maa mu‐supu Andiruwu, yɛ Gyemisi maa mu‐supu Gyɔn, Sebedi mu‐bii ana.

eng: Then the devil brought him to a very high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms of the world and their glory.
local: Sɩtaanɛ bɩla taa mʋ kpee kɩbɩɩ lala kʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kaapʋ mu kuwura‐gyii kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ yɛ ɩmʋ bʋnyaa mʋ.

eng: He taught them, saying.
local: Nɩɩ o piili ɛɛ kaapʋ bamʋ ɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ.

eng: Then you wont look like you are fasting to people, but only to your Father who is present in that secret place. Your Father who sees in secret will reward you.
local: Saamʋ, asa ba mɛɛ yɛ ba wu fɛɛ fu ŋmina kɔnɔ, amɔɔ fʋ‐Sɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔkʋ mee wu mʋ mʋ nkʋn. Fʋ‐Sɛ mʋ nɩɩ ee wu kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ fɩɩ waa asiri tɔ, ii biti ɔ ka fʋ kʋkɔ.

eng: When the crowds saw what had happened, they were afraid and praised God, who had given such authority to human beings.
local: Sakyɔ mʋ ba maa wu ɩnɩmʋ mʋ ɩ waa bamʋ kufuu, nɩɩ ba yɩn Ɩbwaarɛ fɛɛ maa taa kanɩn ɔlʋn sa nyɩmɩsa.

eng: When the Pharisees saw this, they said to him, Look, your disciples are breaking the Sabbath law.
local: Farasii abi mʋ ba maa wu ɩnɩmʋ mʋ, ba tɔwɛ Yesu yɛ, Kɩɩ! Mɩnɛ ɩ waa sʋ nɩɩ fʋ abɩɩlapʋ bɛɛ waa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ man dɛ ɔkpa kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ.

eng: When they heard the king, they went; and look, the star they had seen in the east went ahead of them until it stood over the place where the child was.
local: Ba maa nu Owura mʋ asɛ lʋwɛ nɩɩ ba maa sʋ bee kpee mʋ, opula tɔ nɩɩ ba bwii wu kikyikpebi mʋ nɩɩ ba daa wu kyɔwɛ kɩlɩɩ mʋ, nɩɩ kɩ gyankpaa bamʋ kpee naa yɩlɛ opula mʋ nɩɩ kebii mʋ bʋ mʋ sʋsʋ kyɛɛkyɛɛ.

eng: Why do you see the splinter thats in your brothers or sisters eye, but dont notice the log in your own eye?.
local: Mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fii wu keyuubi mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ fʋ bɩrɩsa kinsi tɔ, nɩɩ fu mee wu kagyakpoti mʋ nɩɩ kɩ dɛ fu kinsi tɔ?.

eng: Then Peter spoke up, Explain this riddle to us.
local: Nɩɩ Pita tɔwɛ yɛ, Lɛɛ kɩŋasan maŋa kasɛ kaapʋ anɛ.

eng: Philip; and Bartholomew; Thomas; and Matthew the tax collector; James the son of Alphaeus; and Thaddaeus.
local: yɛ Filipu maa Batolome, yɛ Tomasi maa Matiwu, lenpoo ɔkɔɔlɛpʋ mʋ, yɛ Gyemisi Alifeyo mu‐bii, yɛ Tadiyosi.

eng: He said, Ill give you all these if you bow down and worship me.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Nɩɩ fʋ kan muŋa sun mɛ mʋ, n biti n taa ɩnɩmʋ kpini sa fʋ.

eng: Happy are people who are hopeless, because the kingdom of heaven is theirs.
local: Kusee ɩ gyɛ sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba taa bamʋ n‐yɩɩ tʋʋ Ɩbwaarɛ nkʋn sʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii mʋ kɩ gyɛ bamʋ lɛɛ.

eng: Stop collecting treasures for your own benefit on earth, where moth and rust eat them and where thieves break in and steal them.
local: Fan man kʋʋla kapatɩyɛ yɩla kasɛ sʋ nfɩɩ sa fanɛ n‐yɩɩ, nfɩɩ nɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩtabɩrɩta yɛ nkandɩra ii nyita ɩmʋ, nɩɩ ayu bee loo bee yuuri ɩmʋ.

eng: As Jesus continued on from there, he saw a man named Matthew sitting at a kiosk for collecting taxes. He said to him, Follow me, and he got up and followed him.
local: Yesu maa lɩɩ tʋtɔ ee kpee mʋ, o wu lenpoo ɔkɔɔlɛpʋ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Matiwu maa tɛ lenpoo ɔkɔɔlɛkpa. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Buu mɛ, nɩɩ Matiwu kʋsʋ buu mʋ.

eng: But he said to them, Havent you read what David did when he and those with him were hungry?.
local: Yesu lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Fan man pɩɩta kalɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Owura Dawidi maa mʋ kamaa tɔ abi ba waa saŋa mʋ nɩɩ akʋn a mɔɔ bamʋ mʋ nɩn?.

eng: When they saw the star, they were filled with joy.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba wu kikyikpebi mʋ, bamʋ ansi i gyii naa kyʋn sʋ.

eng: How can you say to your brother or sister, Let me take the splinter out of your eye, when theres a log in your eye?.
local: Nɛnɛ sʋ nɩɩ fɩɩ taalɛ tɔwɛ fʋ bɩrɩsa yɛ yɛgɛ n lɛɛ keyuubi fu kinsi tɔ, yɛgɛ kagyakpoti dɛ ngbaa‐gbaa fu kinsi tɔ?.

eng: Jesus said, Dont you understand yet?.
local: Yesu taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Ɩmʋ sʋ fanɛ nfɛɛrɛ ɩ man pɩɩta bugi?.

eng: Simon the Cananaean; and Judas, who betrayed Jesus.
local: yɛ Simon mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Seloti mʋ, yɛ Gyudasi Isikariyoti, mʋ nɩɩ ɔ ba lɛɛ Yesu sa mʋ.

eng: Jesus responded, Go away, Satan, because its written, You will worship the Lord your God and serve only him .
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Sɩtaanɛ, natɛ lɩɩ mɛ asɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ba kyʋrɔɔ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ yɛ, Muŋa sun fʋ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ, nɩɩ mʋ nkʋn nɩɩ fu sun. .

eng: Happy are people who grieve, because they will be made glad.
local: Kusee ɩ gyɛ sa bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ bɔɔta tɔ, lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti o yuula bamʋ nkɔlɔ.

eng: Instead, collect treasures for yourselves in heaven, where moth and rust dont eat them and where thieves dont break in and steal them.
local: Amaa fan kʋʋla kapatɩyɛ yɩla sʋsʋ sa fanɛ n‐yɩɩ, katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɩtabɩrɩta yɛ nkadʋrɔ ɩ mɛɛ taalɛ nyita ɩmʋ, nɩɩ ayu ba mɛɛ taalɛ loo naa yuuri ɩmʋ.

eng: As Jesus sat down to eat in Matthews house, many tax collectors and sinners joined Jesus and his disciples at the table.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba kpee Matiwu kɩkpaara sʋ bee gyii mʋ, lenpoo akɔɔlɛpʋ gaalaagaa yɛ alibi awaapʋ ba ba, nɩɩ ba maa bamʋ kpini ba bʋga gyii.

eng: He went into Gods house and broke the law by eating the bread of the presence, which only the priests were allowed to eat.
local: O loo kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ naa taa bodobodo mʋ nɩɩ ba lɛɛ saraga sa Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ, nɩɩ maa mʋ asa mʋ ba wɛ. Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ nbara tɔ mʋ ɩ man dɛ ɔkpa fɛɛ Dawidi maa bamʋ ba waa kanɩn, amɔɔ Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu nkʋn.

eng: They entered the house and saw the child with Mary his mother. Falling to their knees, they honored him. Then they opened their treasure chests and presented him with gifts of gold, frankincense, and myrrh.
local: Ba maa loo kɩkpaara mʋ sʋ mʋ, ba wu kebii mʋ maa mu‐nyi Mɛɛrɩ, nɩɩ ba kyʋn muŋa sun mʋ. Nɩɩ ba saŋɛ bamʋ ɩsʋla nɩɩ ba lɛɛ suwa yɛ tulale mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ firanki‐ɩnsɛnsɩ yɛ tulale mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ mɩɩrɛ kɛɛ mʋ.

eng: You deceive yourself! First take the log out of your eye, and then youll see clearly to take the splinter out of your brothers or sisters eye.
local: Fʋ nnɔ nnyɔ‐nnyɔ wuya, gyankpaa lɛɛ kagyakpoti mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ fu kinsi tɔ mʋ, pɔyɩ kɛ fʋ lan wu danbɩrasa, kɛ fʋ lɛɛ keyuubi mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ fʋ bɩrɩsa kinsi tɔ mʋ.

eng: Dont you know that everything that goes into the mouth enters the stomach and goes out into the sewer?.
local: Fan man nyi yɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ kii loo nyɩmɩsa kɔnɔ tɔ mʋ, ii kpee mʋ ɔtɔ tɔ nɩn nɩɩ ɩɩ lɩɩ mʋ ɔpɩɩ tɔ?.

eng: Jesus sent these twelve out and commanded them, Dont go among the Gentiles or into a Samaritan city.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu sun abɩɩlapʋ kudu anyɔ mʋ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan man kpee bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ kɩbaafʋn abɛɛ Samariya abi kadɛ kʋ tɔ.

eng: The devil left him, and angels came and took care of him.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Sɩtaanɛ yɛgɛ mʋ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ ba ba kyɛ Yesu tɔ.

eng: Happy are people who are humble, because they will inherit the earth.
local: Kusee ɩ gyɛ sa bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ bɩya bamʋ n‐yɩɩ kasɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ ii biti ba gyii kasɛ mʋ fɛɛ bamʋ kapatɩyɛ.

eng: Where your treasure is, there your heart will be also.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ fʋ kapatɩyɛ bʋ mʋ, tʋtɔ nɩɩ fʋ nfɛɛrɛ gbaa ɩ bʋ.

eng: But when the Pharisees saw this, they said to his disciples, Why does your teacher eat with tax collectors and sinners.
local: Farasii abi ba maa wu kanɩn mʋ, ba taasɛ Yesu abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ, Mɩnɛ ɩ waa sʋ nɩɩ fanɛ ɔkaapʋpʋ maa lenpoo akɔɔlɛpʋ yɛ alibi awaapʋ bɛɛ bʋga bee gyii.

eng: Or havent you read in the Law that on the Sabbath the priests in the temple treat the Sabbath as any other day and are still innocent?.
local: Abɛɛ fan man pɩɩta kalɛ Mosesi nbara mʋ tɔ wu fɛɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu ba maa sun kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ, kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ mʋ, bee nyita nbara, amaa ɔkʋ man bun bamʋ kʋpwɛ?.

eng: Because they were warned in a dream not to return to Herod, they went back to their own country by another route.
local: Kamaa tɔ mʋ Ɩbwaarɛ da bamʋ amu tɔ idee tɔ yɛ ba man bɩla bwii kpee Owura Herodi asɛ, ɩmʋ sʋ ba bɔla ɔkpa pʋpwɛ sʋ bwii kpee bamʋ ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ.

eng: Dont give holy things to dogs, and dont throw your pearls in front of pigs. They will stomp on the pearls, then turn around and attack you.
local: Fan man taa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ki du kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ sa igyoonɔ, lɩɩ fɛɛ bee biti ba biliŋi yɩlɛ fanɛ sʋ duŋi fanɛ waa kyabɩlɛ‐kyabɩlɛ. Nɩɩ fan man taa fanɛ atɔ kparɛ tʋʋ sa akuuri, kɛ be kyigya ɩmʋ sʋ nyita.

eng: But what goes out of the mouth comes from the heart. And thats what contaminates a person in Gods sight.
local: Amaa atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ lɩɩ mʋ kɔnɔ tɔ mʋ lɩɩ mʋ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ nɩn, ɩnɩmʋ ɩɩ waa nyɩmɩsa ineesi.

eng: Go instead to the lost sheep, the people of Israel.
local: Amaa fan kpee Ɩsɩrayɩ abi mʋ nɩɩ ba du fɛɛ nbʋlʋpʋ fɔyɩsa mʋ asɛ.

eng: Now when Jesus heard that John was arrested, he went to Galilee.
local: Yesu maa nu yɛ ba kɩtaa Gyɔn tii obu mʋ, o bwii kpee Galile.

eng: Happy are people who are hungry and thirsty for righteousness, because they will be fed until they are full.
local: Kusee ɩ gyɛ sa bamʋ nɩɩ bamʋ ansi ɩɩ payɩ ɩkpa kikyiigi sʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ yɛgɛ ba ŋmɛ lɩɩ bamʋ atɔ bitisa tɔ.

eng: The eye is the lamp of the body. Therefore, if your eye is healthy, your whole body will be full of light.
local: Nyɩmɩsa ansi ɩ gyɛ mʋ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ mʋ fɩtɩla. Nɩɩ fu ansi ɩ kan ɩ bʋrɔn bɩrɛ, fʋ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ mʋ kpini gyɛ ɩŋmaŋɛsa nɩn.

eng: When Jesus heard it, he said, Healthy people dont need a doctor, but sick people do.
local: Yesu maa nu kanɩn mʋ, ɔ lɛɛ Farasii abi mʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Man gyɛ alanfɩya wuya nɩɩ ɔkyɛpʋ tiri sa, amɔɔ ɔlɔpʋ nkʋn.

eng: But I tell you that something greater than the temple is here.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ fɛɛ ɔkʋ bʋ nfɩɩ ɔ kyɔ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ.

eng: When the magi had departed, an angel from the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream and said, Get up. Take the child and his mother and escape to Egypt. Stay there until I tell you, for Herod will soon search for the child in order to kill him.
local: Ba maa kyʋn kamaa mʋ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ ba lɛɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ kaapʋ Gyosefu idee tɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Kʋsʋ taa kebii mʋ maa mu‐nyi sɩlɛ kpee Egyipiti naa kyɩna tʋtɔ naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ n biti n tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ fan lɩɩ tʋtɔ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ Herodi ɩɩ kɩɩ ee biti kebii mʋ ɔ mɔɔ.

eng: Ask, and you will receive. Search, and you will find. Knock, and the door will be opened to you.
local: Fan kʋlɛ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ, ee biti ɔ sa fanɛ. Fan kɩɩ biti Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ, fen biti fan wu. Fan da mʋ kebunɔ, ee biti o tigi sa fanɛ.

eng: Out of the heart come evil thoughts, murders, adultery, sexual sins, thefts, false testimonies, and insults.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ nɩɩ nfɛɛrɛ libi fɛɛ, nyɩmɩsa kɩmɔɔ, yɛ kɩlɩɩ okuli abɛɛ ɔkaa kamaa, yɛ kɩkyɩɩ yɛ kɩnyɩn biti kɩgyagaa, yɛ keyu, yɛ antɩŋɛsa kɩtɩn, yɛ kɩtɔwɛ asɩn libi gyaga asa sʋ.

eng: As you go, make this announcement: The kingdom of heaven has come near.
local: Fan kpee naa tɔwɛ bamʋ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ yɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ ki tiri tɔ.

eng: He left Nazareth and settled in Capernaum, which lies alongside the sea in the area of Zebulun and Naphtali.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ Nasareti kpee Kapanum kadɛ tɔ naa kyɩna. Kadɛ maŋa bʋ Galile ɔbʋn lala ɔkaa, Sebulun maa Nafitali kabaafʋn mʋ.

eng: Happy are people who show mercy, because they will receive mercy.
local: Kusee ɩ gyɛ sa bamʋ nɩɩ bee wu bamʋ bɩrɩsa ana kʋwɛɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti o wu kʋwɛɛ.

eng: But if your eye is bad, your whole body will be full of darkness. If then the light in you is darkness, how terrible that darkness will be!.
local: Amaa nɩɩ fu ansi ɩ kan ɩ man bʋrɔn bɩrɛ, fʋ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ mʋ kpini bʋ kitentenbiri tɔ nɩn. Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ fɩtɩla mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ fʋ tɔ mʋ gyɛ kitentenbiri mʋ, ii biti ɩ waa kitentenbiri tumm.

eng: Go and learn what this means: I want mercy and not sacrifice . I didnt come to call righteous people, but sinners.
local: Fan naa kɩɩ biti Asɩnkyan yɩlasa kasɛ. Asɩn mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, Abaa kʋwɛɛ kuwu nɩɩ mɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ n biti, man gyɛ atɔbwaaya kɩtaa lɛɛ saraga sa mɛ. Nɩɩ Yesu kyaga sʋ yɛ, N man ba nɩn kɛ n ba tɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ kɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ, amɔɔ alibi awaapʋ.

eng: If you had known what this means, I want mercy and not sacrifice , you wouldnt have condemned the innocent.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ yɛ, Man gyɛ atɔbwaaya kɩtaa lɛɛ saraga sa mɛ nɩɩ n biti, amaa kuwu abaa kʋwɛɛ nɩɩ n biti. Nɩɩ fan kan nu asɩn nɩmʋ kasɛ nɩn, naafɔɔ fen mee bun asa mʋ nɩɩ ba man waa alibi mʋ kʋpwɛ.

eng: Joseph got up and, during the night, took the child and his mother to Egypt.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔ kʋsʋ taa kebii mʋ maa mu‐nyi kagyanbwɛ nɩɩ ba sɩlɛ kpee Egyipiti.

eng: For everyone who asks, receives. Whoever seeks, finds. And to everyone who knocks, the door is opened.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kʋlɛ ɛɛ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ mʋ, ɛɛ nyɛ, nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kɩɩ ee biti mʋ, ee wu, nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ da kebunɔ mʋ, ee tigi ɛɛ sa mʋ.

eng: These contaminate a person in Gods sight. But eating without washing hands doesnt contaminate in Gods sight.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ waa nyɩmɩsa ineesi, amaa nɩɩ nyɩmɩsa mɛɛ fwɩɩ mʋ abaa nɩɩ ee gyii mʋ mɛɛ waa mʋ ineesi.

eng: Heal the sick, raise the dead, cleanse those with skin diseases, and throw out demons. You received without having to pay. Therefore, give without demanding payment.
local: Fan kan kpee mʋ fan kyɛ alɔpʋ, kɛ fan kyiŋi alewupu, kɛ fan yɛgɛ abootipu n‐yɩɩ ɩ lɔŋɔ sʋ, kɛ fan gya nbwɩɩ libi lɩɩ asa sʋ. Ba taa ɔlʋn maŋa kɛɛ fanɛ nɩn, ɩmʋ sʋ fanɛ gbaa fanɛ fan taa kɛɛ.

eng: This fulfilled what Isaiah the prophet said.
local: Ɩ waa kanan nɩn kɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Isaya tɔwɛ lɩɩ ndɛ maŋa sʋ mʋ ɩ ba tɔ. Asɩn mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ.

eng: Happy are people who have pure hearts, because they will see God.
local: Kusee ɩ gyɛ sa bamʋ nɩɩ ineesi kʋ man bʋ bamʋ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ, lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ ii biti ba wu Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: No one can serve two masters. Either you will hate the one and love the other, or you will be loyal to the one and have contempt for the other. You cannot serve God and wealth.
local: Ɔkʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔkʋ mɛɛ taalɛ sun anyɩrɩpɛ anyɔ. Ee biti o kisi ɔkʋ kɛ ɔ nyɛ kebiti sa ɔkʋ, abɛɛ ee biti ɔ nyɛ saŋa sa ɔkʋ kɛ ɔ yɛgɛ ɔkʋ. Fan mɛɛ taalɛ sun Ɩbwaarɛ maa afulee.

eng: At that time Johns disciples came and asked Jesus, Why do we and the Pharisees frequently fast, but your disciples never fast.
local: Kakaakʋ Gyɔn Okyugyeepu abɩɩlapʋ ba ba taasɛ Yesu yɛ, Mɩnɛ ɩ waa sʋ nɩɩ anɛ yɛ Farasii abi mʋ a ŋmina kɔnɔ, nɩɩ fʋ abɩɩlapʋ bɩrɛ be mee ŋmina kɔnɔ.

eng: The Human One is Lord of the Sabbath.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ, ɩ gyɛ kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa mʋ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ.
