eng: He stayed there until Herod died. This fulfilled what the Lord had spoken through the prophet: I have called my son out of Egypt .
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba kyɩna naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Herodi wu. Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ bɔla mʋ ɔtɔwɛpʋ sʋ tɔwɛ mʋ ba tɔ. Ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, Lɩɩ Egyipiti tɔ nɩɩ n tɩɩ Mi‐bii.

eng: Who among you will give your children a stone when they ask for bread?.
local: Fanɛ anɩmʋ nɩɩ mu‐bii ɩɩ kʋlɛ mʋ bodobodo kɛ ɔ taa kubuu sa mʋ?.

eng: From there, Jesus went to the regions of Tyre and Sidon.
local: Yesu lɩɩ tʋtɔ, nɩɩ o kpee Tayi maa Sidon ɔsʋwʋlɛ kɩbaafʋn.

eng: Workers deserve to be fed, so dont gather gold or silver or copper coins for your money belts to take on your trips.
local: Fan man taa afulee kʋ waa fanɛ igyifa tɔ.

eng: Land of Zebulun and land of Naphtali, alongside the sea, across the Jordan, Galilee of the Gentiles.
local: Sebulun ɔsʋwʋlɛ yɛ Nafitali ɔsʋwʋlɛ, ndɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ kii kpee ɔpʋ asɛ, ndɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ Gyɔdan benbe mʋ, bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi nɩɩ ba bʋ Galile.

eng: Happy are people who make peace, because they will be called Gods children.
local: Kusee ɩ gyɛ sa bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ bɩya keyuula asa nsana, lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ tɩɩ yɛ Mu‐bii ana.

eng: Therefore, I say to you, dont worry about your life, what youll eat or what youll drink, or about your body, what youll wear. Isnt life more than food and the body more than clothes?.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ n tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, fan man yɛgɛ fanɛ nkpa yɛ agyitɔ mʋ nɩɩ fen biti fan gyii, abɛɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ fen nuu, abɛɛ fanɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ yɛ atɔ bunsa asɩn ɩ tɔɔrɔɔ fanɛ. Nkpa ɩ man kyɔ agyitɔ abɛɛ? Nɩɩ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ mɔɔ man kyɔ atɔ bunsa?.

eng: Jesus responded, The wedding guests cant mourn while the groom is still with them, can they? But the days will come when the groom will be taken away from them, and then theyll fast.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Nɛnɛ sʋ nɩɩ kɩkyɩɩfɔ tɔ afɔ bee biti ba bɔɔta tɔ, yɛgɛ ɔkyɩɩfɔ mu‐kuli mʋ bʋ bamʋ asɛ? Ɩ mɛɛ ba kanɩn. Amaa saŋa kʋ ii biti ɔ ba nɩɩ bee biti ba taa ɔkyɩɩfɔ mu‐kuli mʋ lɩɩ bamʋ asɛ. Kanɩn saŋa maŋa nɩɩ bee biti ba ŋmina kɔnɔ.

eng: Jesus left that place and went into their synagogue.
local: Yesu maa lɩɩ tʋtɔ ee kpee mʋ, ɔ naa loo bamʋ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa.

eng: When Herod knew the magi had fooled him, he grew very angry. He sent soldiers to kill all the children in Bethlehem and in all the surrounding territory who were two years old and younger, according to the time that he had learned from the magi.
local: Owura Herodi maa bɩɩ fɛɛ anyaasɩnpʋ mʋ ba pɩna mʋ mʋ, ɔ nyɛ agbʋ gaa. Nɩɩ ɔ sa kɔnɔ yɛ ba mɔɔ nbii‐nyɩnsa‐bii mʋ nɩɩ ba man pɩɩta kyʋn nsu nnyɔ sʋ, nɩɩ ba bʋ Betilehem yɛ ndɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ kyaabɔɔ kɩmʋ mʋ kpini. Ɔ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ba waa kanɩn nɩn lɩɩ fɛɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ anyaasɩnpʋ mʋ ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ ba wu kikyikpebi mʋ, ii biti ɩ waa fɛɛ nsu nnyɔ.

eng: Or give them a snake when they ask for fish?.
local: Abɛɛ nɩɩ ɛɛ kʋlɛ mʋ ɔkɩn nɩɩ ɛɛ taa kɩnansɛ ɛɛ sa mʋ?.

eng: A Canaanite woman from those territories came out and shouted, Show me mercy, Son of David. My daughter is suffering terribly from demon possession.
local: Nɩɩ Kakanaan‐kyɩɩ kʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ tʋtɔ ba mʋ asɛ ɛɛ saawʋ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, Dawidi kanaana‐bii, wu mɛ kʋwɛɛ! Nbwɩɩ libi ba tɛ mi kebii kyɩɩsa‐bi sʋ bɛɛ tɔɔrɔɔ mʋ gaa.

eng: Dont take a backpack for the road or two shirts or sandals or a walking stick.
local: nɩɩ fan man taa taakpe abɛɛ kaalɛ nyɔsapʋ, abɛɛ asɩbɩta, abɛɛ kikpebi, kpee ɔkpa maŋa. Osunpu dagaa fɛɛ ba sa mʋ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ki tiri mʋ.

eng: the people who lived in the dark have seen a great light, and a light has come upon those who lived in the region and in shadow of death.
local: asa mʋ nɩɩ ba tɛ kitentenbiri tɔ mʋ, ba wu kɩŋmaŋɛsa kparɛ mʋ. Asa mʋ nɩɩ ba tɩyɛ lewu kitentenbiri ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ mʋ, kɩŋmaŋɛsa kɩ lɩɩ bamʋ sʋ.

eng: Happy are people whose lives are harassed because they are righteous, because the kingdom of heaven is theirs.
local: Kusee ɩ gyɛ sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba nyanla bamʋ fɛɛ bamʋ ɩkpa maa kyiigi Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ sʋ mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii mʋ kɩ gyɛ bamʋ lɛɛ.

eng: Look at the birds in the sky. They dont sow seed or harvest grain or gather crops into barns. Yet your heavenly Father feeds them. Arent you worth much more than they are?.
local: Fan kɩɩ nbwiibi mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ afuu tɔ mʋ, be mee duu nɩɩ ba mɛɛ tɩŋɛ, nɩɩ ba mɛɛ kʋʋla agyitɔ bɛɛ gyaga agyan sʋ, amaa fanɛ‐Sɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ sʋsʋ mʋ ɩɩ sa bamʋ agyitɔ. Fanɛ asɩn ɩ man tiri ɩ kyɔ bamʋ nɩn abɛɛ?.

eng: No one sews a piece of new, unshrunk cloth on old clothes because the patch tears away the cloth and makes a worse tear.
local: Yesu bɩla tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔkʋ mɛɛ tɩn kikyibi pʋpwɛ taa baata tii kaalɛ dɩdaa ɔkyatɛkpa ɔbɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ kan waa kanɩn mʋ kikyibi pʋpwɛ mʋ kii biti kɩ kyatɛ lɩɩ, kɛ ɔkyatɛkpa mʋ kɩɩ yɛlɩgɛ tii sʋ.

eng: A man with a withered hand was there. Wanting to bring charges against Jesus, they asked, Does the Law allow a person to heal on the Sabbath.
local: Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn kʋ bʋ tʋtɔ nɩɩ mʋ kɩbaa ki wu. Asa kʋ ba bʋ tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba maa biti ɔkpa ba nyɛ pwɩɩ Yesu sʋ mʋ, ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɩ dɛ ɔkpa fɛɛ ba kyɛ alɔpʋ kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ abɛɛ.

eng: This fulfilled the word spoken through Jeremiah the prophet.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ bɔla mʋ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Gyeremiya sʋ tɔwɛ mʋ ba tɔ. Ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ.

eng: If you who are evil know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your heavenly Father give good things to those who ask him.
local: Nɩɩ fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fan gyɛ alibi awaapʋ mʋ gbaa fen nyi atɔ danbɩrasa kɩtaa sa fanɛ‐bii ana mʋ, mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ fanɛ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ sʋsʋ mʋ mɛɛ yɛ o kyula sa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kʋlɛ mʋ atɔ danbɩrasa mʋ?.

eng: But he didnt respond to her at all. His disciples came and urged him, Send her away; she keeps shouting out after us.
local: Yesu man bugi kɔnɔ tɔwɛ mʋ sɛhn. Nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba ba mʋ asɛ ba kʋlɛ mʋ yɛ, Tɔwɛ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ ɔ natɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɛɛ saawʋ ee tii anɛ asʋ.

eng: Whatever city or village you go into, find somebody in it who is worthy and stay there until you go on your way.
local: Kadɛ lala abɛɛ kadɛ‐bi kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fan loo kɩmʋ tɔ mʋ, fan biti ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ mʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi, kɛ fan kyɩna mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ fen biti fan ba lɩɩ tʋtɔ.

eng: From that time Jesus began to announce, Change your hearts and lives! Here comes the kingdom of heaven.
local: Lɩɩ kanɩn saŋa maŋa nɩɩ Yesu piili ɛɛ tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ yɛ, Fan yɛgɛ alibi kɩwaa, lɩɩ fɛɛ sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii mʋ ki tiri tɔ.

eng: Happy are you when people insult you and harass you and speak all kinds of bad and false things about you, all because of me.
local: Kusee ɩ gyɛ sa fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ asa bɛɛ saalɛ, nɩɩ bɛɛ nyanla fanɛ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩn antɩŋɛsa bɛɛ tɔwɛ asɩn libi bɛɛ gyaga fanɛ sʋ, fɛɛ fan maa gyɛ mɛ abɩɩlapʋ sʋ.

eng: Who among you by worrying can add a single moment to your life?.
local: Fanɛ tɔ ɔmɔɔ nɩɩ asɩn ɩ maa tɔɔrɔɔ mʋ sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɛɛ taalɛ taa kerefi kʋlʋn tii mʋ nkpa sʋ?.

eng: No one pours new wine into old wineskins. If they did, the wineskins would burst, the wine would spill, and the wineskins would be ruined. Instead, people pour new wine into new wineskins so that both are kept safe.
local: Kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ ɔkʋ mɛɛ taa nta pʋpwɛ waa kʋtɔbwaaya ɔwʋlɛ taakpe dɩdaa tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ kan waa kanɩn, nɩɩ nta mʋ ɩɩ bɩlɛ mʋ, ii biti ɩ kyatɛ taakpe dɩdaa mʋ nyita, kɛ nta mʋ ɩ kyɛɛgɛ fɔyɩ. Amaa bɛɛ taa nta pʋpwɛ waa taakpe pʋpwɛ tɔ, kɛ ɩmʋ anyɔ mʋ ɩ man nyita.

eng: Jesus replied, Who among you has a sheep that falls into a pit on the Sabbath and will not take hold of it and pull it out?.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Nɩɩ ɩ kan ba fɛɛ fanɛ tɔ ɔkʋ sʋ kabʋlʋpʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɩyɛ kɩkyabɔ tɔ kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ mʋ, ɔ mɛɛ lɛɛ mʋ lɩɩ kɩmʋ tɔ abɛɛ?.

eng: A voice was heard in Ramah, weeping and much grieving. Rachel weeping for her children, and she did not want to be comforted, because they were no more.
local: Ba nu kusu ɩlʋn kʋ lɩɩ Rama kadɛ tɔ, ɩɩ saawʋ yɛgɛ ɩ sʋ kʋwɛɛ, Raheli ɛɛ sawʋ ee su mu‐bii ana sʋ. Asa ba tɔwɛ yuula mʋ kɔkɔlɔ kpɩnɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ mu‐bii ana ba wu.

eng: Therefore, you should treat people in the same way that you want people to treat you; this is the Law and the Prophets.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fen biti asa ba waa sa fanɛ mʋ, fanɛ gbaa fanɛ fan baa fɛn waa kanɩn fɛn sa bamʋ. Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ Mosesi nbara yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ kɩkaapʋ mʋ.

eng: Jesus replied, Ive been sent only to the lost sheep, the people of Israel.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɩsɩrayɩ abi mʋ nɩɩ ba du fɛɛ nbʋlʋpʋ fɔyɩsa mʋ, nkʋn asɛ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sun mɛ.

eng: When you go into a house, say, Peace.
local: Nɩɩ fan kan loo mʋ sʋ mʋ, fan kʋlɛ keyuula sa kɩkpaara mʋ.

eng: As Jesus walked alongside the Galilee Sea, he saw two brothers, Simon, who is called Peter, and Andrew, throwing fishing nets into the sea, because they were fishermen.
local: Yesu maa kyɩna ɔ naa Galile ɔpʋ sʋ mʋ, o wu ɩkɩn akɩtapʋ anyɔ, Simon mʋ nɩɩ ba bɩla bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Pita yɛ mu‐supu Andiruwu yɛgɛ bɛɛ tʋʋ kɩsawu ɔpʋ mʋ tɔ bɛɛ kɩtaa ɩkɩn.

eng: Be full of joy and be glad, because you have a great reward in heaven. In the same way, people harassed the prophets who came before you.
local: Fan yɛgɛ ɩ waa fanɛ ɔkɔn kɛ fanɛ ansi i gyii, lɩɩ fɛɛ fanɛ kʋkɔka lala kɩ dɛ ki gyoo fanɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ayɛ. Kpalɩ fɛɛ kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ ba daa nyanla Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba wʋla gyankpaa fanɛ ba mʋ.

eng: And why do you worry about clothes? Notice how the lilies in the field grow. They dont wear themselves out with work, and they dont spin cloth.
local: Nɩɩ mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ atɔ bunsa asɩn ɩ tɔɔrɔɔ fanɛ? Fan kɩɩ kanan mʋ nɩɩ iyu ntʋtʋlɛ ɩ dan mʋ. I mee sun kusun nɩɩ ɩ mɛɛ lʋʋ atɔ bunsa ɩ sa ɩmʋ n‐yɩɩ.

eng: While Jesus was speaking to them, a ruler came and knelt in front of him, saying, My daughter has just died. But come and place your hand on her, and shell live.
local: Yesu maa san ɔ maa sʋ ɛɛ tɔwɛ ɩnɩmʋ mʋ, nɩɩ Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa ɔbɩlɩsa kʋ ba Yesu asɛ, ba muŋa mu ansi tɔ kʋlɛ mʋ yɛ, Mi‐bii‐kyɩɩsa dan wu nɩmʋ. Amaa dan waa aniya ba taa fʋ kɩbaa mata mʋ, ee biti o kyiŋi lɩɩ lewu tɔ ba nkpa tɔ.

eng: How much more valuable is a person than a sheep! So the Law allows a person to do what is good on the Sabbath.
local: Nɩɩ nyɩmɩsa kyɔ kabʋlʋpʋ naa ban sʋ falɛ mʋ, ɩmʋ sʋ ɩ dɛ ɔkpa nbara mʋ tɔ fɛɛ a waa adanbɩrasa kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ sa asa.

eng: After King Herod died, an angel from the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph in Egypt.
local: Owura Herodi kelewu kamaa mʋ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ lɛɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ kaapʋ Gyosefu idee tɔ Egyipiti ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ.

eng: Go in through the narrow gate. The gate that leads to destruction is broad and the road wide, so many people enter through it.
local: Fan bɔla kebunɔ pii mʋ tɔ loo. Lɩɩ fɛɛ kebunɔ yɛlɩgɛsa mʋ ɔkpa gbaa yɛlɩgɛ yɛ kɩnatɛ sʋ, nɩɩ kii kpee kinyita kakpaa tɔ nɩn, nɩɩ asa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ bɔla bee loo kɩmʋ tɔ mʋ ba kyɔ.

eng: But she knelt before him and said, Lord, help me.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ ba muŋa mu ansi tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, kyɛ mɛ tɔ.

eng: If the house is worthy, give it your blessing of peace. But if the house isnt worthy, take back your blessing.
local: Nɩɩ kɩkpaara mʋ sʋ abi ba kan kɔɔlɛ fanɛ mʋ, fan yɛgɛ fanɛ keyuula mʋ ki sii kɩkpaara mʋ sʋ. Amaa nɩɩ ba man kan kɔɔlɛ fanɛ bɩrɛ, fan yɛgɛ fanɛ keyuula ki bwii ba fanɛ asɛ.

eng: Come, follow me, he said, and Ill show you how to fish for people.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan ba buu mɛ, kɛ n waa fanɛ asa akɩtapʋ.

eng: You are the salt of the earth. But if salt loses its saltiness, how will it become salty again? Its good for nothing except to be thrown away and trampled under peoples feet.
local: Fan du nɩn fɛɛ nfɔlɛ sa asa dulinyaa tɔ. Amaa nɩɩ nfɔlɛ kan pan ɩmʋ ɔkɔn mʋ, mɩnɛ nɩɩ fɩɩ bɩla taalɛ taa waa ɩmʋ kɛ ɩ waa ɔkɔn? Ɩ man bɩla ɩ sʋ kyaarɛ, amɔɔ ba taa kyɛɛgɛ fɔyɩ kɛ asa ba natɛ sʋ.

eng: But I say to you that even Solomon in all of his splendor wasnt dressed like one of these.
local: Amaa n tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, halɩɩ Owura Solomon maa mʋ atɔ bunsa danbɩrasa kpini mʋ, ɩ man bʋrɔn fʋʋ ntʋtʋlɛ mʋ.

eng: So Jesus and his disciples got up and went with him.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ Yesu kʋsʋ buu mʋ, nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ gbaa ba buu mʋ kpee.

eng: Then Jesus said to the man, Stretch out your hand. So he did and it was made healthy, just like the other one.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ ɔnyɩn mʋ yɛ, Tɛɛŋɛ fʋ kɩbaa mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɛɛŋɛ kɩmʋ, opula tɔ nɩɩ kɩ nyɛ ɔlʋn fɛɛ kɩbanban mʋ.

eng: Get up, the angel said, and take the child and his mother and go to the land of Israel. Those who were trying to kill the child are dead.
local: nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Kʋsʋ taa kebii mʋ maa mu‐nyi bwii kpee Ɩsɩrayɩ ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba daa bee biti ba mɔɔ kebii mʋ ba wu.

eng: But the gate that leads to life is narrow and the road difficult, so few people find it.
local: Amaa ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ kii kpee nkpa tɔ mʋ bɩrɛ kɩ bɔntɛ, nɩɩ kɩmʋ kebunɔ gbaa man yɛlɩgɛ, nɩɩ asa mʋ nɩɩ bee wu kɩmʋ bɛɛ bɔla mʋ ba man kyɔ.

eng: He replied, It is not good to take the childrens bread and toss it to dogs.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɩ man dagaa ba maa taa nbii agyitɔ tʋʋ sa igyoonɔ.

eng: If anyone refuses to welcome you or listen to your words, shake the dust off your feet as you leave that house or city.
local: Nɩɩ kadɛ kʋ abɛɛ kɩkpaara kʋ asa ba man kan kɔɔlɛ fanɛ abɛɛ ba man nu fanɛ asɛ mʋ, fan kpɩkpaa fanɛ ayaa tɔ ɩsɛ wʋrɩgɛ bamʋ sʋ, kɛ fan lɩɩ tʋtɔ.

eng: Right away, they left their nets and followed him.
local: Nɩɩ opula maŋa tɔ ba yɛgɛ bamʋ asawu mʋ, nɩɩ ba buu mʋ.

eng: You are the light of the world. A city on top of a hill cant be hidden.
local: Fan du nɩn fɛɛ ɩŋmaŋɛsa sa asa dulinyaa tɔ. Kadɛ lala mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyan kɩbɩɩ sʋ mʋ mɛɛ taalɛ baala.

eng: If God dresses grass in the field so beautifully, even though its alive today and tomorrow its thrown into the furnace, wont God do much more for you, you people of weak faith?.
local: Nɩɩ falɛ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩɩ lɔŋɔ afitiri mʋ nɩɩ ndaga ɩ bʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔkɛ bɛɛ taa ɩmʋ funti waa fʋlɔn tɔ mʋ, mɩnɛ waa nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ sa fanɛ atɔ bunsa kʋkyɔ? O, fanɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii kɩ man kyɔ?.

eng: Then a woman who had been bleeding for twelve years came up behind Jesus and touched the hem of his clothes.
local: Ba maa kpee mʋ, ɔkyɩɩ kʋ mʋ nɩɩ nkalan ɩɩ da mʋ nsu kudu anyɔ mʋ, ba bɔla Yesu kamaa nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ kɩbaa mata Yesu kaalɛ anobi.

eng: The Pharisees went out and met in order to find a way to destroy Jesus.
local: Amaa Farasii abi mʋ ba lɩɩ kpee naa ŋmina kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba nyɛ ɔkpa mɔɔ Yesu.

eng: Joseph got up, took the child and his mother, and went to the land of Israel.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔ kʋsʋ taa kebii mʋ maa mu‐nyi kpee Ɩsɩrayɩ ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ.

eng: Watch out for false prophets. They come to you dressed like sheep, but inside they are vicious wolves.
local: Fan baa fan da atɩŋɛsa atɔwɛpʋ sʋ. Ansi sʋ mʋ be du bɔhn fɛɛ nbʋlʋpʋ mʋ, amaa bamʋ nkɔlɔ tɔ mʋ, be du nɩn fɛɛ agboogboo mʋ nɩɩ ba nyɛ agbʋ.

eng: She said, Yes, Lord. But even the dogs eat the crumbs that fall off their masters table.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, ɩ bʋ tɔ kanɩn, amaa igyoonɔ mʋ gbaa bee gyii agyitɔ wuribi mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ lɩɩ bamʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ ana iteeburu sʋ ɩɩ tɩyɛ kasɛ mʋ.

eng: I assure you that it will be more bearable for the land of Sodom and Gomorrah on Judgment Day than it will be for that city.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin fɛɛ, kakɛ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti o be gyii asa asɩn mʋ, Sodom maa Gomora abi kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ gbaa ii biti ɩ baa ɩ bɔ bamʋ lɛɛ.

eng: Continuing on, he saw another set of brothers, James the son of Zebedee and his brother John. They were in a boat with Zebedee their father repairing their nets. Jesus called them and.
local: Maa kyaga sʋ ee kpee mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ naa wu ɔkʋ maa mu‐supu, bamʋ ɩ gyɛ Gyemisi maa Gyɔn, Sebedi mu‐bii ana. Ba maa bamʋ‐sɛ Sebedi ba bʋ ɔkʋlɩ tɔ bɛɛ lɔŋɔ bamʋ asawu. Nɩɩ Yesu tɩɩ bamʋ.

eng: Neither do people light a lamp and put it under a basket. Instead, they put it on top of a lampstand, and it shines on all who are in the house.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ mee tuwa fɩtɩla, kɛ ɔ taa waa kolobi tɔ. Amɔɔ ɔ taa gyaga fɩtɩla ɔgyagakpa kɛ kɩ baa kɩ kɩɩ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ tɔ kɩkpaara mʋ sʋ.

eng: Therefore, dont worry and say, What are we going to eat? or What are we going to drink? or What are we going to wear.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan man tɔɔrɔɔ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ fɛn taasɛ yɛ, Mɩnɛ nɩɩ an biti a gyii? abɛɛ Mɩnɛ nɩɩ an biti a nuu? abɛɛ Mɩnɛ nɩɩ an biti a bun.

eng: She thought, If I only touch his robe Ill be healed.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ fɛ mʋ nfɛɛrɛ tɔ yɛ, Nɩɩ n dan nyɛ mata mʋ kaalɛ gbaa, n biti n nyɛ alanfɩya.

eng: Jesus knew what they intended to do, so he went away from there. Large crowds followed him, and he healed them all.
local: Yesu maa bɩɩ ɩnɩmʋ mʋ ɔ lɩɩ kanɩn opula maŋa, nɩɩ asa gaalaagaa ba buu mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ lɔ mʋ alɔ kpini.

eng: But when he heard that Archelaus ruled over Judea in place of his father Herod, Joseph was afraid to go there. Having been warned in a dream, he went to the area of Galilee.
local: Amaa maa nu yɛ Akelasi i yii mʋ‐sɛ Herodi ayaa tɔ ee gyii kuwura Gyudeya mʋ, kufuu kɩ kɩtaa mʋ yɛ tʋtɔ kikpe. Ɩbwaarɛ maa da mu kumu tɔ idee tɔ sʋ mʋ, o kpeli kpee Galile kɩbaafʋn.

eng: You will know them by their fruit. Do people get bunches of grapes from thorny weeds, or do they get figs from thistles?.
local: Bamʋ atɔ waasa nɩɩ fen biti fan taa bɩɩ bamʋ. Kididobi oyu sʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩyɛ kimango abi abɛɛ? Abɛɛ kɩkpalɛ oyu sʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩyɛ akutu?.

eng: Jesus answered, Woman, you have great faith. It will be just as you wish. And right then her daughter was healed.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɔkyɩɩ, fʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii kɩ kyɔ gaa! Kanan mʋ nɩɩ fii biti mʋ ɩ waa kanɩn sa fʋ. Nɩɩ opula maa tɔ kebii kyɩɩsa‐bi mʋ nyɛ alanfɩya.

eng: Look, Im sending you as sheep among wolves. Therefore, be wise as snakes and innocent as doves.
local: Fan kɩɩ! N sun fanɛ nɩn fɛɛ nbʋlʋpʋ n kpee akuntun tɔ. Fan baa fan sʋ kanyaasɩn fɛɛ anansɛ, yɛgɛ fan du bwahn fɛɛ awura‐lɔpɔ.

eng: immediately they left the boat and their father and followed him.
local: Opula maŋa tɔ ba yɛgɛ ɔkʋlɩ mʋ yɛ bamʋ‐sɛ nɩɩ ba buu mʋ.

eng: In the same way, let your light shine before people, so they can see the good things you do and praise your Father who is in heaven.
local: Ɔkpa kʋlʋn maŋa sʋ fan yɛgɛ fanɛ ɩŋmaŋɛsa ɩ baa ɩ kɩɩ tɔ sa asa, kɛ ba wu fanɛ asun danbɩrasa mʋ nɩɩ fɛn waa mʋ, kɛ ba yɩn fanɛ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ sʋsʋ mʋ.

eng: Gentiles long for all these things. Your heavenly Father knows that you need them.
local: Bamʋ nɩɩ be mee nyi Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ falɛ atɔ kpini sʋ nɩɩ bamʋ ansi ɩ payɩ. Nɩɩ fanɛ‐Sɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ sʋsʋ mʋ nyi fɛɛ falɛ atɔ nɩmʋ maa tiri fanɛ.

eng: When Jesus turned and saw her, he said, Be encouraged, daughter. Your faith has healed you. And the woman was healed from that time on.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu biliŋi nɩɩ o wu ɔkyɩɩ mʋ, tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Mi‐bii‐kyɩɩsa, yɛgɛ fʋ kɔkɔlɔ kɩ dɛ fʋ. Fʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ fʋ nyɛ alanfɩya. Nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ nyɛ alanfɩya opula maŋa tɔ.

eng: But he ordered them not to spread the word about him.
local: Amaa ɔ da bamʋ amu tɔ yɛ ba man tɔwɛ ɔkʋ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ.

eng: He settled in a city called Nazareth so that what was spoken through the prophets might be fulfilled: He will be called a Nazarene.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ naa kyɩna Nasareti kadɛ mʋ tɔ. Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ tɔwɛ lɩɩ kebii mʋ sʋ mʋ ba tɔ. Asɩn mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, Bee biti ba tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Kanasareti‐nyɩn.

eng: In the same way, every good tree produces good fruit, and every rotten tree produces bad fruit.
local: Kanɩn nɩɩ oyu danbɩrasa ɩɩ sɔyɩ abi danbɩrasa, nɩɩ oyu libi mɔɔ ɩɩ sɔyɩ abi libi.

eng: Jesus moved on from there along the shore of the Galilee Sea. He went up a mountain and sat down.
local: Yesu lɩɩ tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔ naa ɔ naa Galile ɔpʋ mʋ ɩkaa sʋ. Nɩɩ o dii kpee kɩbɩɩ sʋ naa kyɩna.

eng: Watch out for people—because they will hand you over to councils and they will beat you in their synagogues.
local: Fan baa fan da sʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ asa kʋ bee biti ba kɩtaa fanɛ kpee asɩn ogyikpa, kɛ ba tɩŋɛ fanɛ Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɩgyaŋɛkpa.

eng: Jesus traveled throughout Galilee, teaching in their synagogues. He announced the good news of the kingdom and healed every disease and sickness among the people.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu natɛ kyaabɔɔ Galile ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ ɛɛ kaapʋ asa asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ sʋ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa kʋmaa nɩmʋ tɔ yɛgɛ ɛɛ kyɛ alɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ.

eng: Dont even begin to think that I have come to do away with the Law and the Prophets. I havent come to do away with them but to fulfill them.
local: Fan man fɛ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ n ba nɩn kɛ n ba lɛɛ Mosesi nbara mʋ yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ kɩkaapʋ mʋ lɩɩ tɔ. N man ba nɩn kɛ n ba lɛɛ ɩmʋ lɩɩ tɔ, amaa n ba nɩn kɛ bamʋ kɩkaapʋ mʋ ɩ ba tɔ.

eng: Instead, desire first and foremost Gods kingdom and Gods righteousness, and all these things will be given to you as well.
local: Amaa fan gyankpaa kɩɩ biti Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ ɩkpa yɛ mʋ ɩkpa kikyiigisa mʋ, kɛ ɔ taa atɔ nɩmʋ kpini tii sʋ sa fanɛ.

eng: When Jesus went into the rulers house, he saw the flute players and the distressed crowd.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Yesu naa loo ɔgyaŋɛkpa ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ, nɩɩ o wu nleebi afʋlɛpʋ keli mʋ tɔ yɛ asa damantɛ mʋ nɩɩ bee su mʋ.

eng: so that what was spoken through Isaiah the prophet might be fulfilled.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ waa nɩn kɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bɔla mʋ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Isaya sʋ tɔwɛ mʋ ɩ ba tɔ. Ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ.

eng: A good tree cant produce bad fruit. And a rotten tree cant produce good fruit.
local: Oyu danbɩrasa mɛɛ taalɛ sɔyɩ abi libi, nɩɩ oyu libi mɛɛ taalɛ sɔyɩ abi danbɩrasa.

eng: Large crowds came to him, including those who were paralyzed, blind, injured, and unable to speak, and many others. They laid them at his feet, and he healed them.
local: Asa damantɛ ba ba mʋ asɛ, nɩɩ ba bɩya alɔpʋ fɛɛ, ayaa yɛ abaa wusa wuya ana, yɛ agyaatanapʋ, yɛ aboobi, yɛ atikpiri, yɛ alɔpʋ gaalaagaa, nɩɩ ba taa bamʋ naa yɩla mu ansi tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ kyɛ bamʋ.

eng: News about him spread throughout Syria. People brought to him all those who had various kinds of diseases, those in pain, those possessed by demons, those with epilepsy, and those who were paralyzed, and he healed them.
local: Mʋ kɩtɩɩ ɩ yaasɛ loo Siriya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ kpini, nɩɩ asa ba taa alɔpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ alɔ yiri‐yiri bɩya mʋ. Akʋ bamʋ n‐yɩɩ tɔ ɩɩ kɔ bamʋ gaa, nɩɩ akʋ mɔɔ nbwɩɩ libi bɛɛ tɔɔrɔɔ bamʋ, akʋ bɛɛ lɔ kigbingbin, nɩɩ akʋ mɔɔ ba gyɛ amʋgʋlɛ‐mʋgʋlɛ, nɩɩ Yesu kyɛ bamʋ kpini.

eng: I say to you very seriously that as long as heaven and earth exist, neither the smallest letter nor even the smallest stroke of a pen will be erased from the Law until everything there becomes a reality.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin yɛ, naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ sʋsʋ yɛ kasɛ ii biti i muuri mʋ, halɩɩ nbara mʋ tɔ kʋkyʋrɔɔ‐bi pii gbaa, abɛɛ ɩmʋ kitobi mɛɛ yɛ ɩ kɔɔgɛ sʋ, amɔɔ kʋtɔ kʋmaa ii biti ɩ ba tɔ.

eng: Therefore, stop worrying about tomorrow, because tomorrow will worry about itself. Each day has enough trouble of its own.
local: Kanɩn sʋ fan man tɔɔrɔɔ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ lɩɩ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ii biti ɩ ba ɔkɛ. Fan yɛgɛ ɔkɛ atɔ ɩ kɩɩ ɩmʋ gbaa‐gbaa ɩmʋ asɩn tirisa sʋ.

eng: They will haul you in front of governors and even kings because of me so that you may give your testimony to them and to the Gentiles.
local: Lɩɩ mɛ sʋ, bee biti ba kpiya fanɛ awura yɛ alʋlʋnpʋ ansi tɔ, kɛ fan nyɛ ɔkpa tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ lɩɩ mɛ sʋ sa bamʋ, yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ.

eng: He said, Go away, because the little girl isnt dead but is asleep; but they laughed at him.
local: ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ ɔ lɩɩ nfɩɩ, kebii mʋ man wu nɩn, ɔ dɛ nsɩɩrɛ nɩn. Amaa ba ŋmasɛ Yesu.

eng: Look, my Servant whom I chose, the one I love, in whom I find great pleasure . Ill put my Spirit upon him, and hell announce judgment to the Gentiles.
local: Kɩɩ! Mɛ kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ nɩɩ n lɛɛ mʋ ndee, mʋ, nɩɩ n sʋ kebiti sa nɩɩ ɔ bɔla mi ansi. N biti n taa mɛ Kayaayu waa mʋ tɔ, kɛ ɔ tɔwɛ asɩn kyiigisa sa ndɛndɛmandɛ kpini.

eng: Every tree that doesnt produce good fruit is chopped down and thrown into the fire.
local: Oyu kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ mɛɛ sɔyɩ abi danbɩrasa mʋ, bɛɛ ŋɛ kɩmʋ nɩn tʋʋ, kɛ ba taa waa fʋlɔn tɔ.

eng: So the crowd was amazed when they saw those who had been unable to speak talking, and the paralyzed cured, and the injured walking, and the blind seeing. And they praised the God of Israel.
local: Asa damantɛ mʋ ba maa wu anaamuu ba maa tɔwɛ, yɛ boobi yɛ ayaa yɛ abaa wusa wuya ana ba maa natɛ, yɛ agyaatanapʋ ansi ɩ maa bugi mʋ, i kyinkyin bamʋ, nɩɩ ba yɩn Ɩsɩrayɩ abi Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: Large crowds followed him from Galilee, the Decapolis, Jerusalem, Judea, and from the areas beyond the Jordan River.
local: Asa damantɛ ba lɩɩ Galile yɛ Dekapoli yɛ Gyerusalem yɛ Gyudeya yɛ Gyɔdan benbe kpini ba buu mʋ.

eng: Therefore, whoever ignores one of the least of these commands and teaches others to do the same will be called the lowest in the kingdom of heaven. But whoever keeps these commands and teaches people to keep them will be called great in the kingdom of heaven.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o kina kibuu nbara mʋ tɔ kipii, nɩɩ ɛɛ kaapʋ asansa yɛ ba waa kanɩn mʋ, ee biti ɔ baa ɔ gyɛ ɔsa pii Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ. Amaa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o buu nbara mʋ, nɩɩ ɛɛ kaapʋ asansa gbaa yɛ ba buu ɩmʋ mʋ, ee biti ɔ baa ɔ gyɛ nyɩmɩsa kparɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ.

eng: Whenever they hand you over, dont worry about how to speak or what you will say, because what you can say will be given to you at that moment.
local: Nɩɩ ba kan kpiya fanɛ asɩn ogyikpa mʋ, fan man yɩyɛɛ lɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ fen biti fan tɔwɛ, abɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fan tɔwɛ mʋ sʋ. Saŋa mʋ kan fʋʋ mʋ, fen biti fan nyɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ fan tɔwɛ bamʋ.

eng: Therefore, you will know them by their fruit.
local: Kanɩn sʋ fen biti fan bɩɩ antɩŋɛsa atɔwɛpʋ mʋ lɩɩ bamʋ awaasa sʋ.

eng: After he had sent the crowd away, Jesus went in and touched her hand, and the little girl rose up.
local: Ba maa gya asa damantɛ mʋ lɩɩ kamaa tɔ mʋ, Yesu loo kebii kyɩɩsa‐bi mʋ asɛ, nɩɩ ɔ kɩtaa mʋ kɩbaa tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ kʋsʋ kyɩna.

eng: He wont argue or shout, and nobody will hear his voice in the streets.
local: Ɔ mɛɛ yɛ o gyii ɩmɔɔrɛ abɛɛ ɔ pʋntɛ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ natɛ waa ɔlanba agbenbi sʋ.

eng: Now Jesus called his disciples and said, I feel sorry for the crowd because they have been with me for three days and have nothing to eat. I dont want to send them away hungry for fear they wont have enough strength to travel.
local: Yesu tɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba mʋ asɛ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Asa nɩmʋ asɩn ɩ sʋ mɛ kʋwɛɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ba maa bʋ mɛ asɛ ndaga nkɛ nsa ndee ba man sʋ agyitɔ ba gyii. N mee biti fɛɛ ba taa akʋn kpewu, man gyɛ kanɩn bee biti ba lɩɩ tɩyɛ ɔkpa tɔ.

eng: I say to you that unless your righteousness is greater than the righteousness of the legal experts and the Pharisees, you will never enter the kingdom of heaven.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ n tɔwɛ fanɛ fɛɛ, nɩɩ fanɛ ɩkpa ɩ man kyiigi ɩ kyɔ nbara akaapʋpʋ yɛ Farasii abi mʋ, fan mɛɛ yɛ fan nyɛ loo Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ kakaakʋ.

eng: News about this spread throughout that whole region.
local: Nɩɩ falɛ asɩn nɩmʋ yaasɛ kpee kanɩn ɔsʋwʋlɛ maŋa sʋ kpini.

eng: You arent doing the talking, but the Spirit of my Father is doing the talking through you.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ man gyɛ fanɛ gbaa‐gbaa ii biti fan tɔwɛ, amaa fanɛ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu mʋ ii biti ɔ bɔla fanɛ sʋ tɔwɛ.

eng: He wont break a bent stalk, and he wont snuff out a smoldering wick, until he makes justice win.
local: Ɔ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ gya oyu‐bi nyɔnsa tɔ tii sʋ lʋwɛ nɩɩ o mee dun fɩtɩla mʋ nɩɩ kɩ man kɩɩ tɔ mʋ. Ee biti ɔ yɩlɛ tɔ kɛ ɔ yɛgɛ asɩn kyiigisa mʋ i gyii sʋ.

eng: His disciples replied, Where are we going to get enough food in this wilderness to satisfy such a big crowd.
local: Mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Fɩnɛ nɩɩ an biti a nyɛ agyitɔ kɩkyɔ afitiri nɩmʋ tɔ sa asa damantɛ banɩmʋ.

eng: Not everybody who says to me, Lord, Lord, will get into the kingdom of heaven. Only those who do the will of my Father who is in heaven will enter.
local: Man gyɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ tɩɩ mɛ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ, ii biti o loo sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ, amɔɔ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ waa mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ sʋsʋ mʋ kebiti mʋ nkʋn.

eng: You have heard that it was said to those who lived long ago, Dont commit murder, and all who commit murder will be in danger of judgment.
local: Yesu bɩla tɔwɛ yɛ, Fan nu Mosesi nbara mʋ maa tɔwɛ adɩdaapʋ mʋ yɛ, Man kan mɔɔ nyɩmɩsa. Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ mɔɔ nyɩmɩsa mʋ, bee biti ba bun mʋ kʋpwɛ.

eng: As Jesus departed, two blind men followed him, crying out, Show us mercy, Son of David.
local: Yesu maa lɩɩ tʋtɔ ee kpee mʋ, nɩɩ agyaatanapʋ anyɔ akʋ ba buu mʋ, bee kuusi bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Dawidi kanaana‐bii, wu anɛ kʋwɛɛ.

eng: Brothers and sisters will hand each other over to be executed. A father will turn his child in. Children will defy their parents and have them executed.
local: Kanɩn nkɛ maa tɔ mʋ, nyɩmɩsa ii biti o gyii mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mu‐supu kadɛ‐boyi, kɛ ba mɔɔ mʋ, kɛ ɔsɛ gbaa ɔ waa obii kanɩn. Nbii bee biti ba kʋsʋ yɩlɛ akʋʋgɛpʋ ana sʋ, kɛ ba yɛgɛ kɛ ba mɔɔ bamʋ.

eng: And the Gentiles will put their hope in his name.
local: Nɩɩ asa kpini bee biti ba taa bamʋ tamaa tʋʋ mʋ nkʋn sʋ.

eng: Jesus said, How much bread do you have? They responded, Seven loaves and a few fish.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Bodobodo afɩnɛ nɩɩ fan sʋ? Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, An sʋ asunɔ yɛ ɩkɩn‐bi gbɛrɛɛ kʋ.

eng: On the Judgment Day, many people will say to me, Lord, Lord, didnt we prophesy in your name and expel demons in your name and do lots of miracles in your name.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ kakɛ talɛsa maŋa kan fʋʋ mʋ, asa gaalaagaa bee biti ba tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, an bɔla fʋ kɩtɩɩ sʋ tɔwɛ abwaarɛsɩn sa asa, nɩɩ lɩɩ fʋ kɩtɩɩ tɔ sʋ mʋ an gya nbwɩɩ libi, nɩɩ an waa asɩn kyinkyinsa gaalaagaa.

eng: But I say to you that everyone who is angry with their brother or sister will be in danger of judgment. If they say to their brother or sister, You idiot, they will be in danger of being condemned by the governing council. And if they say, You fool, they will be in danger of fiery hell.
local: Amaa n tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ agbʋ waa mʋ tɔmaa mʋ, bee biti ba bun mʋ kʋpwɛ. Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ saalɛ mʋ tɔɔmaa yɛ ɔ man bʋ sɛhn tɔ mʋ, ee biti ɔ nyɛ asɩn Gyiwu kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ abɩlɩsa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ Sanhediri mu ansi. Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ saalɛ mʋ tɔɔmaa yɛ, Fʋ omelensipu nɩmʋ mʋ, ii biti o loo awʋrʋfɔ fʋlɔn yen‐yen tɔ.

eng: When he came into the house, the blind men approached him. Jesus said to them, Do you believe I can do this? Yes, Lord, they replied.
local: Yesu maa naa loo katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛ mʋ, nɩɩ agyaatanapʋ anyɔ mʋ ba kpee mʋ asɛ, nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan kɔɔlɛ gyii fɛɛ n taalɛ kyɛ fanɛ? Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɛɛhn, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, an kɔɔlɛ gyii.

eng: Everyone will hate you on account of my name. But whoever stands firm until the end will be saved.
local: Asa kpini bee biti ba kisi fanɛ lɩɩ mɛ sʋ, amaa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taalɛ yɩlɛ tɔ kenken naa fʋʋ laalaalʋwɛ mʋ, ii biti ɔ nyɛ kamɔlɩgɛ.

eng: They brought to Jesus a demon-possessed man who was blind and unable to speak. Jesus healed him so that he could both speak and see.
local: Kakaakʋ asa akʋ ba taa ɔnyɩn kʋ bɩya Yesu. Ɔ gyɛ gyaatanapʋ yɛ naamuu lɩɩ fɛɛ nbwɩɩ libi ba tɛ mʋ sʋ. Yesu kyɛ mʋ, nɩɩ mu ansi i bugi nɩɩ ɔ bɩla ɛɛ taalɛ ɛɛ tɔwɛ.

eng: He told the crowd to sit on the ground.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ asa damantɛ mʋ yɛ ba kyɩna kasɛ.

eng: Then Ill tell them, Ive never known you. Get away from me, you people who do wrong.
local: Amaa n biti tɔwɛ tigi bamʋ yɛ, Man nyi fanɛ. Fanɛ alibi awaapʋ banɩmʋ, fan lɩɩ mɛ sʋ natɛ.

eng: Therefore, if you bring your gift to the altar and there remember that your brother or sister has something against you.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ, nɩɩ fʋ kan bɩya fʋ saraga kɛ fʋ ba lɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kaswɩɩ sʋ, nɩɩ fu nyiŋi fɛɛ fʋ waa fʋ bɩrɩsa alibi kʋ mʋ.

eng: Then Jesus touched their eyes and said, It will happen for you just as you have believed.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔ mata bamʋ ansi tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Ɩ waa sa fanɛ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fanɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii ki du mʋ.

eng: Whenever they harass you in one city, escape to the next, because I assure that you will not go through all the cities of Israel before the Human One comes.
local: Nɩɩ ba kan nyanla fanɛ kadɛ kʋ tɔ mʋ, fan sɩlɛ lɩɩ tʋtɔ kpee kadɛ kʋ tɔ. N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin fɛɛ, fɛn mɛɛ yɛ fan sɩlɛ kanɩn kyaabɔɔ Ɩsɩrayɩ ndɛ mʋ kpini sʋ, pɔyɩ kɛ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ n ba.

eng: All the crowds were amazed and said, This man couldnt be the Son of David, could he.
local: Nɩɩ ii kyinkyin asa mʋ kpini, nɩɩ ba taasɛ abaa yɛ, Ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ Dawidi kanaana‐bii mʋ do.

eng: He took the seven loaves of bread and the fish. After he gave thanks, he broke them into pieces and gave them to the disciples, and the disciples gave them to the crowds.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ taa bodobodo asunɔ mʋ yɛ ɩkɩn‐bi gbɛrɛɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taa afwaala sa Ɩbwaarɛ, nɩɩ ɔ gyayɩ‐gyayɩ ɩmʋ tɔ taa sa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ba mɔɔ bamʋ ba taa sa asa mʋ.

eng: Everybody who hears these words of mine and puts them into practice is like a wise builder who built a house on bedrock.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ee nu mɛ asɩn nɩmʋ, nɩɩ ee buu ɩmʋ mʋ, du nɩn fɛɛ ɔnyaasɩnpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ pɔyɩ mʋ kɩkpaara gyaga kɩfʋlɛ sʋ mʋ.

eng: leave your gift at the altar and go. First make things right with your brother or sister and then come back and offer your gift.
local: yɛgɛ fʋ saraga yɩla tʋtɔ kɛ fu bwii kpee, kɛ faa fʋ bɩrɩsa mʋ fan lɔŋɔ abaa nsana, pɔyɩ kɛ fʋ bwii ba lɛɛ fʋ saraga mʋ sa Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: Their eyes were opened. Then Jesus sternly warned them, Make sure nobody knows about this.
local: Nɩɩ bamʋ ansi i bugi. Nɩɩ Yesu da bamʋ amu tɔ kenken yɛ, Fan man tɔwɛ ɩnɩmʋ sa ɔkʋ.

eng: Disciples arent greater than their teacher, and slaves arent greater than their master.
local: Ɔbɩɩlapʋ man kyɔ mʋ ɔkaapʋpʋ, nɩɩ kɩnyɛ man kyɔ mʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ.

eng: When the Pharisees heard, they said, This man throws out demons only by the authority of Beelzebul, the ruler of the demons.
local: Amaa Farasii abi mʋ ba maa nu ɩnɩmʋ mʋ, ba tɔwɛ nɩn yɛ, Nbwɩɩ libi kumu mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ Beelisebu mʋ ɩɩ sa ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ ɔlʋn, nɩɩ ɛɛ taalɛ ɛɛ gya nbwɩɩ libi ɛɛ kʋsʋ asa sʋ kanɩn mʋ.

eng: Everyone ate until they were full. The disciples collected seven baskets full of leftovers.
local: Bamʋ kpini ba gyii nɩɩ ba ŋmɛ. Nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba taasɛ ɩmʋ agyisan wuribi mʋ bɔla alantan asunɔ.

eng: The rain fell, the floods came, and the wind blew and beat against that house. It didnt fall because it was firmly set on bedrock.
local: Bʋlayɩ da nɩɩ ɩbʋn ɩ bɔla, nɩɩ afuu lala ɩ da obu mʋ, amaa kɩ man tɩyɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ba pɔyɩ kɩmʋ gyaga kɩfʋlɛ sʋ.

eng: Be sure to make friends quickly with your opponents while you are with them on the way to court. Otherwise, they will haul you before the judge, the judge will turn you over to the officer of the court, and you will be thrown into prison.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan samaa fʋ asɩn ogyikpa mʋ, sun mata mʋ pɔyɩ asɩn kigyii kakɛ mʋ kɩ fʋʋ. Amaa nɩɩ ɩ man kan ba kanɩn mʋ, ee biti ɔ taa fʋ sa asɩn ogyipu mʋ, kɛ mɔɔ mʋ ɔ taa fʋ waa abu tiipu ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ abaa tɔ kɛ o tii fʋ obu.

eng: But they went out and spread the word about him throughout that whole region.
local: Amaa ba lɩɩ tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba yaasɛ asɩn nɩmʋ lɩɩ Yesu sʋ kanɩn ɔsʋwʋlɛ maŋa sʋ kabaafʋn kpini.

eng: Its enough for disciples to be like their teacher and slaves like their master. If they have called the head of the house Beelzebul, its certain that they will call the members of his household by even worse names.
local: Ɩ dagaa ɩ maa kʋkwɛ ɔbɩɩlapʋ fɛɛ maa ba waa fɛɛ mʋ ɔkaapʋpʋ. Nɩɩ kɩyaafɔlɛ mɔɔ ɔ ba waa fɛɛ mʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ. Nɩɩ ba kan bɛɛ tɩn asɩn bɛɛ gyaga mɛ, kɩkpaara sʋ ɔbɩlɩsa sʋ yɛ n gyɛ Beelisebu mʋ, kanɩn bɩrɛ bee biti ba taa atɩɩ libi gyaga fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fan gyɛ mɛ kɩkpaara sʋ abi mʋ ɩ kyɔ mɛ lɛɛ mʋ.

eng: Because Jesus knew what they were thinking, he replied, Every kingdom involved in civil war becomes a wasteland. Every city or house torn apart by divisions will collapse.
local: Yesu bɩɩ bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Nɩɩ Kuwura‐gyii kʋmaa tɔ asa ba kan barɩgɛ tɔ bɛɛ kɔ yɛ abaa mʋ, kɩ mɛɛ kyɛɛrɛɛ nɩɩ kɩ tɩyɛ. Nɩɩ kadɛ abɛɛ kɩkpaara sʋ abi ba kan barɩgɛ tɔ bɛɛ kɔ yɛ abaa mʋ, kadɛ abɛɛ kɩkpaara maŋa kii biti ki boyi.

eng: Four thousand men ate, plus women and children.
local: Anyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba gyii agyitɔ mʋ ba gyɛ ngbɩn nna, yɛgɛ ba man karagɛ akyɩɩ yɛ nbii tii sʋ.

eng: But everybody who hears these words of mine and doesnt put them into practice will be like a fool who built a house on sand.
local: Amaa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ee nu mɛ asɩn nɩmʋ, nɩɩ o mee buu ɩmʋ mʋ, du nɩn fɛɛ omelensipu mʋ nɩɩ ɔ pɔyɩ mu obu gyaga kusipu sʋ.

eng: I say to you in all seriousness that you wont get out of there until youve paid the very last penny.
local: N tɔwɛ fʋ kesintin fɛɛ, fii biti fʋ baa fʋ bʋ tʋtɔ naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ fii biti fʋ‐ka fʋ kʋkɔ mʋ lʋwɛ.

eng: As they were leaving, people brought to him a man who was demon-possessed and unable to speak.
local: Agyaatanapʋ anyɔ mʋ ba maa kpee mʋ, asa akʋ ba bɩya ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi ba tɛ mʋ sʋ nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ taalɛ ɛɛ tɔwɛ.

eng: Therefore, dont be afraid of those people because nothing is hidden that wont be revealed, and nothing secret that wont be brought out into the open.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan man sɩlɛ asa. Kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ki bun sʋ mʋ, ii biti i bugi sʋ, nɩɩ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ baala mʋ, ii biti ɩ lɩɩ ifuli.

eng: If Satan throws out Satan, he is at war with himself. How then can his kingdom endure?.
local: Nɩɩ ɩ kan ɩ gyɛ kesintin fɛɛ Sɩtaanɛ ɩɩ gya Sɩtaanɛ mʋ, ɩɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ ɔ barɩgɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ tɔ kɩnyɔ nɩn. Ɩmʋ sʋ nɛnɛ nɩɩ mu kuwura‐gyii mʋ kɩ bɩla taalɛ yɩlɛ?.

eng: After dismissing the crowds, Jesus got into the boat and came to the region of Magadan.
local: Yesu maa kala sakyɔ mʋ lʋwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ o loo ɔkʋlɩ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ faa kpee Magadan kɩbaafʋn.

eng: The rain fell, the floods came, and the wind blew and beat against that house. It fell and was completely destroyed.
local: Bʋlayɩ da nɩɩ ɩbʋn ɩ bɔla, nɩɩ afuu lala ɩ da nɩɩ obu mʋ lɩɩ tɩyɛ ɔlʋn tɔ birimm.

eng: You have heard that it was said, Dont commit adultery.
local: Fan nu Mosesi nbara mʋ maa tɔwɛ yɛ, Man waa kanyɩn biti abɛɛ kɩkyɩɩ biti kɩgyagaa.

eng: When Jesus had thrown out the demon, the man who couldnt speak began to talk. The crowds were amazed and said, Nothing like this has ever been seen in Israel.
local: Yesu maa dan gya nbwɩɩ libi lɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ piili ɛɛ tɔwɛ. Nɩɩ ɩ waa asa damantɛ mʋ kɩyan, nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, A man pɩɩ wu ɩnɩmʋ kudubi Ɩsɩrayɩ ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ.

eng: What I say to you in the darkness, tell in the light; and what you hear whispered, announce from the rooftops.
local: Asɩn kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ fanɛ kitentenbiri tɔ mʋ, fan tɔwɛ ɩmʋ kakyʋwapɛ, nɩɩ asɩn kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ n kuli waa fanɛ asʋ tɔ mʋ, fan da ɩmʋ kibonbon.

eng: And if I throw out demons by the authority of Beelzebul, then by whose authority do your followers throw them out? Therefore, they will be your judges.
local: Fan yɛ Beelisebu ɩɩ sa mɛ ɔlʋn nɩɩ n sʋ n gya nbwɩɩ libi n kʋsaa asa sʋ. To, kanɩn bɩrɛ anɩmʋ ɩɩ sa fanɛ abɩɩlapʋ ɔlʋn nɩɩ bɛɛ taalɛ bɛɛ gya nbwɩɩ libi bɛɛ kʋsaa asa sʋ? Fanɛ gbaa‐gbaa fanɛ abɩɩlapʋ ii biti ba bun fanɛ kʋpwɛ fɛɛ fan man sʋ kesintin.

eng: When Jesus finished these words, the crowds were amazed at his teaching.
local: Yesu maa tɔwɛ asɩn nɩmʋ lʋwɛ mʋ, mʋ kɩkaapʋ mʋ i kyinkyin sakyɔ mʋ gaa.

eng: But I say to you that every man who looks at a woman lustfully has already committed adultery in his heart.
local: Amaa mɛ ɩ tɔwɛ fanɛ nɩn fɛɛ, ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ waa mʋ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ ɔ dɩn nyɛ maa mʋ ba bʋga nɩn, ɔ tɩyɛ waa kɩkyɩɩ biti alibi mʋ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ.

eng: Dont be afraid of those who kill the body but cant kill the soul. Instead, be afraid of the one who can destroy both body and soul in hell.
local: Fan man sɩlɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ mɔɔ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ nkʋn, yɛgɛ ba mɛɛ taalɛ mɔɔ ɔkala. Amaa fan sɩlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ taalɛ nyita ɔkala yɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ, Sɩtaanɛ fʋlɔn tɔ mʋ.

eng: But if I throw out demons by the power of Gods Spirit, then Gods kingdom has already overtaken you.
local: Amaa nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu nɩɩ n sʋ n gya nbwɩɩ libi n kʋsaa asa sʋ mʋ, kanɩn bɩrɛ fan bɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ kɩ wʋla kɩ bʋ fanɛ asɛ.

eng: But the Pharisees said, He throws out demons with the authority of the ruler of demons.
local: Amaa Farasii abi mʋ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, Nbwɩɩ libi ɔbɩlɩsa ɔlʋn nɩɩ ɔ sʋ ɛɛ gya nbwɩɩ libi falɛ.

eng: because he was teaching them like someone with authority and not like their legal experts.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ mʋ kɩkaapʋ mʋ tɔ sʋ ɔlʋn, nɩɩ ɩ man du fɛɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ lɛɛ mʋ.

eng: And if your right eye causes you to fall into sin, tear it out and throw it away. Its better that you lose a part of your body than that your whole body be thrown into hell.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ fu gyisa kinsi kii biti kɩ yɛgɛ kɛ fʋ waa alibi mʋ, kyɔlɛ kɩmʋ fɔyɩ. Ɩ bʋrɔn sa fʋ fɛɛ fʋ maa pan fʋ katɩn kʋ, ɩ kyɔ fɛɛ ba maa taa fʋ n‐yɩɩ kpini naa tʋʋ awʋrʋfɔ fʋlɔn yen‐yen tɔ.

eng: Arent two sparrows sold for a small coin? But not one of them will fall to the ground without your Father knowing about it already.
local: Man gyɛ kobo kʋlʋn nɩɩ bɛɛ sʋ bɛɛ sɔɔ alangba anyɔ, amaa bamʋ tɔ ɔkʋlʋn mɛɛ yɛ ɔ tɩyɛ kasɛ sʋ wu, yɛgɛ fanɛ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ man sa ɔkpa.

eng: Can people go into a house that belongs to a strong man and steal his possessions, unless they first tie up the strong man? Then they can rob his house.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu bɩla kyaga sʋ yɛ, Ɔkʋ mɛɛ taalɛ loo ɔlʋlʋnpʋ kʋ kɩkpaara sʋ naa swii mʋ kapatɩyɛ, amɔɔ ɔ gyankpaa ŋmina mʋ yɩla pɔyɩ kɛ ɔ lan taalɛ naa swii mʋ kapatɩyɛ lɩɩ kɩkpaara mʋ sʋ.

eng: Jesus traveled among all the cities and villages, teaching in their synagogues, announcing the good news of the kingdom, and healing every disease and every sickness.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu natɛ ndɛ lala yɛ ndɛ‐bii tɔ kpini kyaabɔɔ, yɛgɛ ɛɛ kaapʋ bamʋ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɩgyaŋɛkpa mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɛɛ tɔwɛ bamʋ asɩn danbɩrasa lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɛɛ kyɛ alɔ yiri‐yiri kʋmaa nɩmʋ.

eng: And if your right hand causes you to fall into sin, chop it off and throw it away. Its better that you lose a part of your body than that your whole body go into hell.
local: Nɩɩ fu gyisa kɩbaa kii biti kɩ yɛgɛ kɛ fʋ waa alibi mʋ, tɩn kɩmʋ fɔyɩ. Ɩ bʋrɔn sa fʋ fɛɛ fʋ maa pan fʋ katɩn kʋ, ɩ kyɔ fɛɛ ba maa taa fʋ n‐yɩɩ kpini naa tʋʋ awʋrʋfɔ fʋlɔn yen‐yen tɔ.

eng: Even the hairs of your head are all counted.
local: Halɩɩ fanɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ kumu tɔ inyini gbaa, Ɩbwaarɛ nyi ɩmʋ kɔnɔ.

eng: Whoever isnt with me is against me, and whoever doesnt gather with me scatters.
local: Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man tii mɛ sʋ mʋ, ii kisi mɛ nɩn, nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ kʋʋla ɛɛ kyɛ mɛ tɔ mʋ, ɛɛ yaasɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ n kʋʋla mʋ nɩn.

eng: Now when Jesus saw the crowds, he had compassion for them because they were troubled and helpless, like sheep without a shepherd.
local: Yesu maa wu sakyɔ mʋ, bamʋ asɩn ɩ waa mʋ kʋwɛɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ bɛɛ yɩyɛɛ nɩɩ ba man nyi kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba waa. Be du nɩn fɛɛ nbʋlʋpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba man sʋ ɔkpapʋ.

eng: It was said, Whoever divorces his wife must give her a divorce certificate.
local: Nɩɩ Mosesi nbara mʋ ba bɩla tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o kina mʋ‐ka mʋ, dagaa maa kyʋrɔɔ kekina ɔwʋlɛ sa mʋ.

eng: Dont be afraid. You are worth more than many sparrows.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan man waa kufuu, lɩɩ fɛɛ fan sʋ kyaarɛ fan kyɔ alangba damantɛ.

eng: Therefore, I tell you that people will be forgiven for every sin and insult to God. But insulting the Holy Spirit wont be forgiven.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ, n tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa alibi abɛɛ ɔ tɔwɛ yii Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ taa kɛɛ mʋ. Amaa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yii Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ taa kɛɛ mʋ.

eng: Then he said to his disciples, The size of the harvest is bigger than you can imagine, but there are few workers.
local: Kanɩn sʋ ɔ tɔwɛ sa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ, Kesintin tɔ mʋ, ayu kɩtɩŋɛ mʋ kyɔ, amaa kusun awaapʋ mʋ ba du gbɛrɛɛ falɛ.

eng: But I say to you that whoever divorces his wife except for sexual unfaithfulness forces her to commit adultery. And whoever marries a divorced woman commits adultery.
local: Amaa mɛ ɩ tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o kina mʋ‐ka nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ yɛ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ lɩɩ mʋ kamaa mʋ, ɔ yɛgɛ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ waa alibi kekili tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o kili ɔkyɩɩ maŋa gbaa mʋ waa alibi kekili tɔ.

eng: Therefore, everyone who acknowledges me before people, I also will acknowledge before my Father who is in heaven.
local: Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ lɛɛ ɛɛ tɔwɛ asa tɔ yɛ n gyɛ mʋ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ mʋ, kanɩn nɩɩ ngbaa mɛ n biti n lɛɛ mʋ kaapʋ mɛ‐Sɛ ansi tɔ sʋsʋ.

eng: And whoever speaks a word against the Human One will be forgiven. But whoever speaks against the Holy Spirit wont be forgiven, not in this age or in the age that is coming.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ asɩn kʋ yii mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ taa kɛɛ mʋ, amaa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ asɩn yii Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ bɩrɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ taa kɛɛ mʋ nbɩɩnbɩɩ saŋa nɩmʋ tɔ abɛɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ii biti ɩ ba mʋ.

eng: Therefore, plead with the Lord of the harvest to send out workers for his harvest.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan kʋlɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, kadɔɔ wuya mʋ kɛ o sun kusun awaapʋ kpee mʋ ayu kɩtɩŋɛ mʋ tɔ.

eng: Again you have heard that it was said to those who lived long ago: Dont make a false solemn pledge, but you should follow through on what you have pledged to the Lord.
local: Fan bɩla nu Mosesi nbara mʋ maa tɔwɛ adɩdaapʋ mʋ yɛ, Man bɔ ntan kɛ fʋ laaŋɛ, amaa ɩ dagaa fʋ maa waa kanan mʋ nɩɩ fʋ bɔ ntan sa Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ.

eng: But everyone who denies me before people, I also will deny before my Father who is in heaven.
local: Amaa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o kina mɛ asa tɔ yɛ n man gyɛ mʋ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ mʋ, ngbaa mɛ n biti, n kina mʋ mɛ‐sɛ ansi tɔ sʋsʋ.

eng: Either consider the tree good and its fruit good, or consider the tree rotten and its fruit rotten. A tree is known by its fruit.
local: Nɩɩ fʋ kan kyula yɛ oyu kʋ abi ɩ gyɛ abi danbɩrasa mʋ, oyu maa gbaa gyɛ oyu danbɩrasa nɩn, amaa nɩɩ fʋ kan kina yɛ oyu kʋ abi man gyɛ abi danbɩrasa bɩrɛ, oyu maa gbaa man gyɛ oyu danbɩrasa. Lɩɩ fɛɛ oyu abi sɔyɩsa nɩɩ bɛɛ sʋ bɛɛ bɩɩ kɩmʋ.

eng: But I say to you that you must not pledge at all. You must not pledge by heaven, because its Gods throne.
local: Amaa nbɩɩnbɩɩ mɛ ɩ tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, fan man bɔ ntan kpini‐kpini. Fan man taa sʋsʋ bɔ ntan, lɩɩ fɛɛ tʋtɔ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gya.

eng: Children of snakes! How can you speak good things while you are evil? What fills the heart comes out of the mouth.
local: Fanɛ asa libi fɛɛ anansɛ, nɛnɛ nɩɩ fɛn taalɛ tɔwɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋrɔn, yɛgɛ fanɛ atɔ waasa gyɛ alibi? Lɩɩ fɛɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ nyɩmɩsa kɔkɔlɔ tɔ mʋ, kɩmʋ ɩɩ lɩɩ mʋ kɔnɔ tɔ.

eng: You must not pledge by the earth, because its Gods footstool. You must not pledge by Jerusalem, because its the city of the great king.
local: Fan man taa kasɛ bɔ ntan, lɩɩ fɛɛ tʋtɔ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ayaa ɔgyagakpa. Fan man taa Gyerusalem kadɛ bɔ ntan, lɩɩ fɛɛ kɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ Owura kparɛ mʋ kadɛ lala.

eng: Dont think that Ive come to bring peace to the earth. I havent come to bring peace but a sword.
local: Fan man fɛ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ n ba nɩn kɛ ɩ bɩya keyuula dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ. Man gyɛ keyuula nɩɩ n bɩya, amaa keluu nɩɩ n bɩya.

eng: Good people bring out good things from their good treasure. But evil people bring out evil things from their evil treasure.
local: Nyɩmɩsa danbɩrasa bɩrɛ, atɔ danbɩrasa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ mʋ tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ɛɛ lɛɛ ɛɛ tɔwɛ, nɩɩ nyɩmɩsa libi mɔɔ, atɔ libi mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ mʋ tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ɛɛ lɛɛ ɛɛ tɔwɛ.

eng: And you must not pledge by your head, because you cant turn one hair white or black.
local: Man taa fu kumu bɔ ntan, lɩɩ fɛɛ fʋ mɛɛ taalɛ waa kinyinibi kʋlʋn kifufuli abɛɛ kilenbiri.

eng: Ive come to turn a man against his father, a daughter against her mother, and a daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law .
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ n ba nɩn, kɛ nbii‐nyɩnsa ba kʋsʋ yɩlɛ bamʋ‐sɛ ana sʋ, kɛ nbii‐kyɩɩsa gbaa ba kʋsʋ yɩlɛ bamʋ‐nyi ana sʋ, kɛ asaa‐kyɩɩsa ba kʋsʋ yɩlɛ bamʋ‐saa ana sʋ.

eng: I tell you that people will have to answer on Judgment Day for every useless word they speak.
local: Amaa n tɔwɛ fanɛ fɛɛ, katɔwɛ gyaga kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔsa kʋmaa nɩmʋ tɔwɛ mʋ, ee biti ɔ lɛɛ kɩmʋ kɔnɔ Ɩbwaarɛ ansi tɔ, kakɛ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ talɛ asa mʋ.

eng: Let your yes mean yes, and your no mean no. Anything more than this comes from the evil one.
local: Amaa fan yɛgɛ fanɛ katɔwɛ kɩ baa kɩ gyɛ Ɛɛhn abɛɛ ayee. Nɩɩ fʋ kan bɔ ntan kʋ tii sʋ mʋ, ɩ lɩɩ Sɩtaanɛ asɛ nɩn.

eng: Peoples enemies are members of their own households .
local: Kɛ nyɩmɩsa mʋ dʋn ana ba baa ba gyɛ mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ asa.

eng: You have heard that it was said, An eye for an eye and a tooth for a tooth .
local: Nɩɩ fan bɩla nu Mosesi nbara maa tɔwɛ yɛ, Nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan boyi ɔkʋ kinsi mʋ, ɩ dagaa be boyi ngbaa mʋ lɛɛ. Nɩɩ ɔkʋ kyiigi ɔkʋ kinyi mʋ, ɩ dagaa be kyiigi ngbaa mʋ lɛɛ.

eng: By your words you will be either judged innocent or condemned as guilty.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ fʋ ntɔwɛ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ yɩlɛ sʋ talɛ fʋ fɛɛ fu gyii asʋ abɛɛ fu gyii kʋpwɛ.

eng: Those who love father or mother more than me arent worthy of me. Those who love son or daughter more than me arent worthy of me.
local: Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti mʋ‐sɛ abɛɛ mu‐nyi ɔ kyɔ mɛ mʋ, ɔ man dagaa maa waa mɛ obuupu. Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti mu‐bii‐nyɩnsa abɛɛ ɔkyɩɩsa ɔ kyɔ mɛ mʋ, man dagaa maa waa mɛ obuupu.

eng: But I say to you that you must not oppose those who want to hurt you. If people slap you on your right cheek, you must turn the left cheek to them as well.
local: Amaa mɛ ɩ tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan waa fʋ alibi mʋ, man taa alibi ka mʋ kʋkɔ. Nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan kpa fʋ ɔtan gyisa sʋ mʋ, biliŋaa bɩna sʋ sa mʋ kɛ ɔ kpa tii sʋ.

eng: At that time some of the legal experts and the Pharisees requested of Jesus, Teacher, we would like to see a sign from you.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ nbara akaapʋpʋ akʋ yɛ Farasii abi akʋ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔkaapʋpʋ an biti fɛɛ fʋ waa asɩn kyinkyinsa kaapʋ anɛ.

eng: Those who dont pick up their crosses and follow me arent worthy of me.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man taa mu oyu laasa sʋla buu mɛ mʋ, man dagaa maa waa mɛ obuupu.

eng: When they wish to haul you to court and take your shirt, let them have your coat too.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan taa fʋ kpee asɩn ogyikpa kɛ ɔ kɔɔlɛ fʋ kaalɛ mʋ, taa fʋ balɩmaasʋ tii mʋ sʋ.

eng: But he replied, An evil and unfaithful generation searches for a sign, but it wont receive any sign except Jonahs sign.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ndaga kakyɩna nɩmʋ tɔ alibi awaapʋ yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ be mee sun Ɩbwaarɛ ɩɩ kɩɩ bee biti asɩn kyinkyinsa. Amaa baa mɛɛ nyɛ ɩmʋ, amɔɔ sɩnkaala mʋ nɩɩ ɩ ba Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Gyona sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ n biti n waa kaapʋ bamʋ.

eng: Those who find their lives will lose them, and those who lose their lives because of me will find them.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti mʋ nkpa mʋ kɩbaa sʋ mʋ, ee biti ɔ pan ɩmʋ, amaa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ pan mʋ nkpa lɩɩ mɛ sʋ mʋ, ee biti ɔ nyɛ ɩmʋ.

eng: Just as Jonah was in the whales belly for three days and three nights , so the Human One will be in the heart of the earth for three days and three nights.
local: Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Gyona gyii nkɛ nsa ibiri ɔkɩn lala ɔtɔ tɔ mʋ, kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ gbaa n biti n gyii nkɛ nsa ibiri kasɛ tɔ.

eng: When they force you to go one mile, go with them two.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan nyan fʋ, fɛɛ fʋ sʋla mʋ ɩsʋla kpiya mʋ mayɩlɩ kʋlʋn mʋ, sʋla ɩmʋ kpiya mʋ ɩmayɩlɩ anyɔ.

eng: Those who receive you are also receiving me, and those who receive me are receiving the one who sent me.
local: Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ fanɛ mʋ, kɔɔlɛ mɛ nɩmʋ, nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ mɛ mʋ, kɔɔlɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ o sun mɛ nɩmʋ.

eng: Give to those who ask, and dont refuse those who wish to borrow from you.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan ba kʋlɛ fʋ kʋtɔ kʋ mʋ, sa mʋ. Abɛɛ nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan ba yɛ fʋ paala mʋ kʋtɔ kʋ mʋ, sa mʋ.

eng: The citizens of Nineveh will stand up at the judgment with this generation and condemn it as guilty, because they changed their hearts and lives in response to Jonahs preaching. And look, someone greater than Jonah is here.
local: Kakɛ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ talɛ asa kpini mʋ, Ninewe abi bee biti ba kʋsʋ, kɛ ba bun ndaga kakyɩna nɩmʋ tɔ asa kʋpwɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Gyona naa tɔwɛ bamʋ Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn mʋ ba yɛgɛ alibi kɩwaa. Amaa mɛ, mʋ nɩɩ n kyɔ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Gyona mʋ n bʋ nfɩɩ.

eng: Those who receive a prophet as a prophet will receive a prophets reward. Those who receive a righteous person as a righteous person will receive a righteous persons reward.
local: Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ lɩɩ maa gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ sʋ mʋ, ee biti ɔ nyɛ kʋkɔka mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ ka mʋ ɔtɔwɛpʋ mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ mʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi lɩɩ maa gyɛ ɩkpa kyiigisa wuya sʋ mʋ, ee biti ɔ nyɛ kʋkɔka mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ ka ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ mʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi mʋ.

eng: You have heard that it was said, You must love your neighbor and hate your enemy.
local: Fan nu Mosesi nbara mʋ maa bɩla tɔwɛ yɛ, Fan nyɛ kebiti sa fanɛ tɔɔmaa ana, kɛ fan kisi fanɛ‐dʋn ana.

eng: The queen of the South will be raised up by God at the judgment with this generation and condemn it because she came from a distant land to hear Solomons wisdom. And look, someone greater than Solomon is here.
local: Nɩɩ kakɛ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ talɛ asa kpini mʋ, Seeba Owura‐kyɩɩsa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ kata‐kata mʋ, ii biti ɔ kʋsʋ yɩlɛ kɛ o bun ndaga kakyɩna nɩmʋ tɔ asa kʋpwɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ lɩɩ koo kata‐kata ba nu Owura Solomon kanyaasɩn kɩkaapʋ mʋ. Amaa mɛ mʋ nɩɩ n kyɔ Solomon mʋ n bʋ nfɩɩ.

eng: I assure you that everybody who gives even a cup of cold water to these little ones because they are my disciples will certainly be rewarded.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sa mɛ abɩɩlapʋ nɩmʋ tɔ ɔkʋ nkyu yuulisa kawɛ tɔ, lɩɩ fɛɛ maa gyɛ mɛ ɔbɩɩlapʋ sʋ mʋ, n tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin fɛɛ, ɔ man yɛ ɔ pan mʋ kʋkɔka kpaa.

eng: But I say to you, love your enemies and pray for those who harass you.
local: Amaa mɛ ɩ tɔwɛ fan yɛ, fan nyɛ kebiti sa fanɛ‐dʋn ana, kɛ fan kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ kʋlɛ sa fanɛ anyanlapʋ.

eng: When an unclean spirit leaves a person, it wanders through dry places looking for a place to rest. But it doesnt find any.
local: Yesu kyaga sʋ yɛ, Nɩɩ kayaayu libi dan kʋsʋ nyɩmɩsa sʋ mʋ, kɩ naa kɩ naa ofuli sʋ nɩn kii biti ɔkyɩnakpa. Amaa nɩɩ kɩ man kan nyɛ mʋ.

eng: so that you will be acting as children of your Father who is in heaven. He makes the sun rise on both the evil and the good and sends rain on both the righteous and the unrighteous.
local: kɛ fan baa fan du fɛɛ fanɛ‐Sɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ sʋsʋ mʋ. Mʋ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ mʋ kyɔwɛ ɩɩ lɩɩ ɛɛ sa alibi awaapʋ yɛ adanbɩrasa awaapʋ, nɩɩ ɛɛ yɛgɛ bʋlayɩ ɩɩ da ɛɛ sa bamʋ nɩɩ bamʋ ɩkpa ii kyiigi, yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bamʋ ɩkpa ɩ man kyiigi.

eng: Then it says, Ill go back to the house I left. When it arrives, it finds the place vacant, cleaned up, and decorated.
local: kɩ tɔwɛ sa kɩmʋ n‐yɩɩ yɛ, N biti n bwii kpee mɛ kɩkpaara mʋ nɩɩ n lɩɩ mʋ sʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ kan ba fʋʋ nɩɩ o wu fɛɛ sɛhn man bʋ kɩmʋ tɔ, yɛgɛ kɩ lɔŋɔ kɩ dɛ yɛ ɔdan mʋ.

eng: If you love only those who love you, what reward do you have? Dont even the tax collectors do the same?.
local: Nɩɩ fan kan fan sʋ kebiti sa bamʋ nɩɩ bee biti fanɛ nkʋn mʋ, mɩnɛ kʋkɔka nɩɩ fen biti fan nyɛ? Man gyɛ kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ alibi awaapʋ gbaa bɛɛ waa nɩn abɛɛ?.

eng: Then it goes and brings with it seven other spirits more evil than itself. They go in and make their home there. That person is worse off at the end than at the beginning. This is the way it will be also for this evil generation.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɛɛ lɩɩ kpee kɛ ɔ naa taa mʋ tɔɔmaa ana n‐yaayu libi asunɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba libi ba kyɔ mʋ tii mʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋ, kɛ ba kpee naa kyɩna tʋtɔ. Saamʋ, kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ daa o du mʋ, ii biti ɩ baa ɩ kyɔ kanɩn. Kanɩn nɩɩ ii biti ɩ baa i du sa ndaga kakyɩna kanɩmʋ tɔ asa libi.

eng: And if you greet only your brothers and sisters, what more are you doing? Dont even the Gentiles do the same?.
local: Nɩɩ fan kan fɛn fwaala fanɛ tɔmaa ana nkʋn mʋ, mɩnɛ kʋtɔ nɩɩ fan waa fan kyɔ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ ɩɩ waa? Man gyɛ kanɩn nɩɩ ɩdakpa asunpu gbaa bɛɛ waa nɩn?.

eng: While Jesus was speaking to the crowds, his mother and brothers stood outside trying to speak with him.
local: Yesu maa san ɔ maa sʋ ɛɛ tɔwɛ ɛɛ sa sakyɔ mʋ, mu‐nyi yɛ mu‐supu ana ba ba yɩlɛ nbwama bee biti fɛɛ ba tɔwɛ mʋ asɛ.

eng: Therefore, just as your heavenly Father is complete in showing love to everyone, so also you must be complete.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ɩ dagaa fanɛ kɩbaabʋtɔ maa du kyɛɛkyɛɛ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fanɛ‐Sɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ sʋsʋ mʋ du kyɛɛkyɛɛ mʋ.

eng: Someone said to him, Look, your mother and brothers are outside wanting to speak with you.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ tɔwɛ Yesu yɛ, Fu‐nyi yɛ fu‐supu ana ba yɩlɛ nbwama bee biti fɛɛ ba tɔwɛ fʋ asɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, Who is my mother? Who are my brothers.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Anɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ mi‐nyi, nɩɩ anɩmʋ ana ɩ gyɛ mu‐supu ana.

eng: He stretched out his hand toward his disciples and said, Look, here are my mother and my brothers.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔ tɛɛŋɛ mʋ kɩbaa kaapʋ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ, tɔwɛ yɛ, Fan kɩɩ! Mi‐nyi yɛ mi‐supu ana ndee.

eng: Whoever does the will of my Father who is in heaven is my brother, sister, and mother.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ waa mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ sʋsʋ mʋ kɔkɔlɔ biti mʋ, ɩ gyɛ mi‐supu yɛ mɛ‐pɩɩkyɩɩ yɛ mi‐nyi.

eng: Six days later Jesus took Peter, James, and John his brother, and brought them to the top of a very high mountain.
local: Nkɛ nsiye kamaa tɔ mʋ, Yesu taa Pita yɛ Gyemisi maa mu‐supu Gyɔn, nɩɩ ba dii kpee kɩbɩɩ lala kʋ sʋ bamʋ nkʋn.

eng: He was transformed in front of them. His face shone like the sun, and his clothes became as white as light.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu kyɛɛgɛ bamʋ ansi sʋ. Mu ansi sʋ ɩɩ ŋalɩgɛ fɛɛ kyɔwɛ, nɩɩ mʋ atɔ bunsa ɩ kyɛɛgɛ waa parɛparɛ ɩɩ ŋalɩgɛ sʋ.

eng: Moses and Elijah appeared to them, talking with Jesus.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba wu Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ anyɔ, Mosesi maa Eligya ba lɩɩ opula tɔ bɛɛ tɔwɛ Yesu asɛ.

eng: Peter reacted to all of this by saying to Jesus, Lord, its good that were here. If you want, Ill make three shrines: one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.
local: Nɩɩ Pita tɔwɛ Yesu yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, ɩ bʋrɔn fɛɛ a maa bʋ nfɩɩ. Nɩɩ fʋ kan fii biti mʋ, n biti n yii abu asa, fʋ lɛɛ kʋkʋ, Mosesi lɛɛ kʋkʋ nɩɩ Eligya lɛɛ kʋkʋ.

eng: While he was still speaking, look, a bright cloud overshadowed them. A voice from the cloud said, This is my Son whom I dearly love. I am very pleased with him. Listen to him.
local: Pita maa san ɔ maa sʋ ɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ kabwaarɛ‐wʋlɛ ŋalɩgɛsa ba bun bamʋ sʋ. Nɩɩ ɔbʋlɛ kʋ tɔwɛ kabwaarɛ‐wʋlɛ mʋ tɔ yɛ, Ɔnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ Mi‐Bii‐nyɩnsa mʋ nɩɩ n sʋ kebiti sa, nɩɩ mʋ ɩ bɔla mi ansi. Fan nu mʋ asɛ.

eng: Hearing this, the disciples fell on their faces, filled with awe.
local: Abɩɩlapʋ asa mʋ ba maa nu falɛ mʋ, kufuu kɩ kɩtaa bamʋ gaa, nɩɩ ba kyʋn bun kasɛ.

eng: But Jesus came and touched them. Get up, he said. Dont be afraid.
local: Amaa Yesu ba bamʋ asɛ taa mʋ kɩbaa mata bamʋ, tɔwɛ yɛ, Fan kʋsʋ, fan man waa kufuu.

eng: When they looked up, they saw no one except Jesus.
local: Ba maa kʋsʋ kɩɩ mʋ, ba man wu ɔkʋ, amɔɔ Yesu nkʋn.

eng: As they were coming down the mountain, Jesus commanded them, Dont tell anybody about the vision until the Human One is raised from the dead.
local: Ba maa kpɩlɩgɛ kɩbɩɩ mʋ sʋ bɛɛ ba mʋ, Yesu da bamʋ amu tɔ yɛ, Fan man tɔwɛ ɔkʋ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ fan wu mʋ, naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ n kyiŋi lɩɩ lewu tɔ.

eng: The disciples asked, Then why do the legal experts say that Elijah must first come.
local: Nɩɩ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba taasɛ Yesu yɛ, Mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ nbara akaapʋpʋ mʋ bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ ɩ dagaa Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Eligya maa gyankpaa ba pɔyɩ.

eng: Jesus responded, Elijah does come first and will restore all things.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Kesintin nɩn, ɩ dagaa Eligya maa gyankpaa ba, kɛ ɔ lɔŋɔ kʋtɔ kʋmaa yɩla o gyoo.

eng: In fact, I tell you that Elijah has already come, and they didnt know him. But they did to him whatever they wanted. In the same way the Human One is also going to suffer at their hands.
local: Amaa n tɔwɛ fanɛ nɩmʋ, Eligya tɛɛ wʋla ba nɩɩ asa ba man bɩɩ mʋ, amaa ba waa mʋ kanan kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ bee biti. Nɩɩ kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ gbaa ii biti o gyii awʋrʋfɔ bamʋ abaa tɔ.

eng: Then the disciples realized he was telling them about John the Baptist.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba nu kasɛ nɩn fɛɛ, Gyɔn okyugyeepu asɩn nɩɩ Yesu ɩɩ tɔwɛ ɛɛ sa bamʋ kanɩn.

eng: When they came to the crowd, a man met Jesus. He knelt before him.
local: Ba maa bwii ba sakyɔ mʋ asɛ mʋ, ɔnyɩn kʋ ba Yesu asɛ ba muŋa mu ansi tɔ.

eng: saying, Lord, show mercy to my son. He is epileptic and suffers terribly, for he often falls into the fire or the water.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, wu mi‐bii‐nyɩnsa kʋwɛɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ gyɛ kigbingbin wuya nɩɩ ee gyii awʋrʋfɔ gaa. Ɛɛ taalɛ ɛɛ tɩyɛ fʋlɔn tɔ abɛɛ nkyu tɔ.

eng: I brought him to your disciples, but they couldnt heal him.
local: N taa mʋ ba fʋʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ asɛ, amaa ba man taalɛ kyɛ mʋ.

eng: Jesus answered, You faithless and crooked generation, how long will I be with you? How long will I put up with you? Bring the boy here to me.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, O, fanɛ ndaga kakyɩna nɩmʋ tɔ asa mʋ nɩɩ fan man sʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii, nɩɩ fanɛ ɩkpa ɩ man kyiigi! Nkɛ nfɩnɛ nɩɩ n biti n gyii fanɛ asɛ? Nɛnɛ nɩɩ n biti n bɩla nyɛ kenyiita sa fanɛ? Fan taa kebii mʋ bɩya mɛ nfɩɩ.

eng: Then Jesus spoke harshly to the demon. And it came out of the child, who was healed from that time on.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu pʋntɛ kabwɩɩ libi mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ kɩ kʋsʋ kebii‐nyɩnsa mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ opula maŋa tɔ ɔ nyɛ alanfɩya.

eng: Then the disciples came to Jesus in private and said, Why couldnt we throw the demon out.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba ba Yesu asɛ bamʋ nkʋn ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ an man taalɛ gyan kabwɩɩ libi mʋ lɩɩ kebii‐nyɩnsa mʋ sʋ.

eng: Because you have little faith, he said. I assure you that if you have faith the size of a mustard seed, you could say to this mountain, Go from here to there, and it will go. There will be nothing that you cant do.
local: Yesu lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Lɩɩ fɛɛ fanɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii kɩ maa man kyɔ mʋ sʋ. N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin tɔ fɛɛ, nɩɩ fan kan fan sʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii pii fɛɛ masitadi oyu kibi gbaa mʋ, fɛn taalɛ tɔwɛ kɩbɩɩ nɩmʋ yɛ, Kʋsʋ lɩɩ nfɩɩ kpee falɛ kɛ kɩ kʋsʋ. Sɛhn man bʋ tɔ nɩɩ fɛn mɛɛ taalɛ waa.

eng: When the disciples came together in Galilee, Jesus said to them, The Human One is about to be delivered over into human hands.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ kpini ba ba Galile ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ mʋ, Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Bee biti ba lɛɛ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ waa asa abaa tɔ.

eng: They will kill him. But he will be raised on the third day. And they were heartbroken.
local: Bee biti ba mɔɔ mɛ, kɛ kakɛ sasapʋ mʋ, n kyiŋi ba nkpa tɔ. Abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba maa nu kanɩn mʋ, bamʋ nkɔlɔ i nyita bamʋ gaa.

eng: When they came to Capernaum, the people who collected the half-shekel temple tax came to Peter and said, Doesnt your teacher pay the temple tax.
local: Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba maa ba Kapanum kadɛ tɔ mʋ, kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ tʋʋtʋʋ akɔɔlɛpʋ mʋ ba ba Pita asɛ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Fanɛ ɔkaapʋpʋ ɩɩ ka kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ tʋʋtʋʋ mʋ.

eng: Yes, he said. But when they came into the house, Jesus spoke to Peter first.What do you think, Simon? From whom do earthly kings collect taxes, from their children or from strangers.
local: Nɩɩ Pita lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɛɛhn, ɛɛ ka. Pita maa ba kɩkpaara sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ Yesu daa kɩtaasɛ yɛ, Simon fʋ nfɛɛrɛ ɩ gyɛ nɛnɛ? Bamɔɔ asɛ nɩɩ dulinyaa tɔ awura bɛɛ kɔɔlɛ bamʋ kagya kasɛ tʋʋtʋʋ? Bɛɛ kɔɔlɛ bɛɛ lɩɩ bamʋ gbaa‐gbaa bamʋ‐bii ana asɛ nɩn abɛɛ afɔ asɛ.

eng: From strangers, he said. Jesus said to him, Then the children dont have to pay.
local: Pita lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Bɛɛ kɔɔlɛ bɛɛ lɩɩ afɔ asɛ nɩn. Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ Pita yɛ, Kanɩn bɩrɛ ndɛ‐bii ba man tii sʋ.

eng: But just so we dont offend them, go to the lake, throw out a fishing line and hook, and take the first fish you catch. When you open its mouth, you will find a shekel coin. Take it and pay the tax for both of us.
local: Amaa a maa mee biti a maa waa bamʋ agbʋ sʋ mʋ, kpee ɔpʋ tɔ naa tʋʋ fʋ darʋwa. Ɔkɩn mʋ nɩɩ fii biti fʋ daa kɩkɩta mʋ, bugi mʋ kɔnɔ, fii biti fu wu kederebi mʋ nɩɩ kii biti kɩ fʋʋ maa fʋ tʋʋtʋʋ. Kɛ fʋ taa naa ka bamʋ.

eng: At that time Herod the ruler heard the news about Jesus.
local: Saŋa maa tɔ nɩɩ owura Herodi mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kɩɩ Galile sʋ mʋ nu asɩn lɩɩ Yesu sʋ.

eng: He said to his servants, This is John the Baptist. Hes been raised from the dead. This is why these miraculous powers are at work through him.
local: nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ kamaa abi mʋ yɛ, N kɔɔlɛ gyii fɛɛ Gyɔn okyugyeepu mʋ i kyiŋi lɩɩ lewu tɔ ba, ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ falɛ ɔlʋn ɛɛ waa asɩn kyinkyinsa nɩmʋ.

eng: Herod had arrested John, bound him, and put him in prison because of Herodias, the wife of Herods brother Philip.
local: Herodi nɩmʋ wʋla yɛgɛ nɩɩ ba kɩtaa Gyɔn ŋmina taa mʋ tii obu, lɩɩ Herodiya mʋ nɩɩ naafɔɔ ɔ daa ɔ gyɛ Herodi maŋa mu‐supu Filipu mʋ‐ka mʋ sʋ.

eng: Thats because John told Herod, Its against the law for you to marry her.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Gyɔn daa kyɩna ɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɩ man dɛ ɔkpa fɛɛ fʋ kɔɔlɛ fu‐supu mʋ‐ka Herodiya kili.

eng: Although Herod wanted to kill him, he feared the crowd because they thought John was a prophet.
local: Naafɔɔ Herodi daa ee biti ɔ mɔɔ Gyɔn nɩn, amaa ɔ kyɩna ɔ sɩlɛ asa mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ Gyiwu abi ba wu fɛɛ Gyɔn gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ nɩn.

eng: But at Herods birthday party Herodias daughter danced in front of the guests and thrilled Herod.
local: Amaa kakaakʋ Herodi maa kyɩna ee gyii mʋ kakɛ kʋʋgɛsa akɛ kinsigyi mʋ, Herodiya mu‐bii kyɩɩsa kyaa sa bamʋ, nɩɩ ɩ waa Herodi ɔkɔn naa kyʋn sʋ.

eng: Then he swore to give her anything she asked.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ bɔ ntan tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kan tɔwɛ yɛ ee biti mʋ, ee biti ɔ sa mʋ.

eng: At her mothers urging, the girl said, Give me the head of John the Baptist here on a plate.
local: Nɩɩ mu‐nyi tɔwɛ waa mʋ yɛ ɔ naa tɔwɛ Herodi yɛ, Sa mɛ Gyɔn Okyugyeepu kumu tangɩran tɔ nfɩɩ nbɩɩnbɩɩ nɩmʋ.

eng: Although the king was upset, because of his solemn pledge and his guests he commanded that they give it to her.
local: Asɩn mʋ ɩ tɔɔrɔɔ Owura Herodi gaa, amaa lɩɩ mʋ ntan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bɔ, yɛ mʋ kinsigyi tɔ afɔ mʋ sʋ mʋ, ɔ sa kɔnɔ yɛ ba waa kanɩn sa kebii kyɩɩsa‐bi mʋ.

eng: Then he had John beheaded in prison.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ yɛgɛ ba naa tɩn Gyɔn kumu obu tiisa mʋ tɔ.

eng: They brought his head on a plate and gave it to the young woman, and she brought it to her mother.
local: Ba bɩya Gyɔn kumu mʋ tangɩran tɔ ba sa kebii kyɩɩsa‐bi mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taa kɩmʋ naa sa mu‐nyi.

eng: But Johns disciples came and took his body and buried it. Then they went and told Jesus what had happened.
local: Nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba ba taa mʋ kubuni naa pula. Nɩɩ ba naa tɔwɛ Yesu.

eng: When Jesus heard about John, he withdrew in a boat to a deserted place by himself. When the crowds learned this, they followed him on foot from the cities.
local: Yesu maa nu kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ waa mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taa ɔkʋlɩ lɩɩ tʋtɔ mʋ nkʋn kpee opula mʋ nɩɩ i du surum. Sakyɔ mʋ ba maa nu kanɩn mʋ ba taa ayaa tɔ lɩɩ ndɛ sʋ ba mʋ asɛ.

eng: When Jesus arrived and saw a large crowd, he had compassion for them and healed those who were sick.
local: Yesu maa kpɩlɩgɛ lɩɩ ɔkʋlɩ mʋ tɔ nɩɩ o wu asa damantɛ mʋ, bamʋ asɩn ɩ waa mʋ kʋwɛɛ nɩɩ ɔ kyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ lɔ mʋ.

eng: That evening his disciples came and said to him, This is an isolated place and its getting late. Send the crowds away so they can go into the villages and buy food for themselves.
local: Ɩmʋ kalaatɩpwɛ asɛ mʋ, mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba ba mʋ asɛ ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Opula nɩmʋ bʋ kata yɛ kadɛ, nɩɩ kyɔwɛ mɔɔ tɛɛ lʋwɛ. Yɛgɛ sakyɔ mʋ ba kpee ndɛ‐bi‐ndɛ‐bi tɔ kɛ ba nyɛ naa sɔɔ agyitɔ gyii.

eng: But Jesus said to them, Theres no need to send them away. You give them something to eat.
local: Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɩ man dagaa fɛɛ ba kpee. Fanɛ nɩn fan sa bamʋ agyitɔ kɛ ba gyii.

eng: They replied, We have nothing here except five loaves of bread and two fish.
local: Nɩɩ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Bodobodo anuu yɛ ɩkɩn ɩnyɔ nkʋn nɩɩ an sʋ nfɩɩ.

eng: He said, Bring them here to me.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan taa ɩmʋ bɩya mɛ nfɩɩ.

eng: He ordered the crowds to sit down on the grass. He took the five loaves of bread and the two fish, looked up to heaven, blessed them and broke the loaves apart and gave them to his disciples. Then the disciples gave them to the crowds.
local: Ɔ tɔwɛ asa mʋ yɛ ba kyɩna afitiri sʋ. Ɔ taa bodobodo anuu mʋ yɛ ɩkɩn ɩnyɔ mʋ, kɩɩ sʋsʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa afwaala sa Ɩbwaarɛ. Nɩɩ ɔ kpɛɛ bodobodo mʋ tɔ taa sa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ, nɩɩ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ mɔɔ ba taa sa asa mʋ.

eng: Everyone ate until they were full, and they filled twelve baskets with the leftovers.
local: Bamʋ kpini ba gyii ŋmɛ, nɩɩ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba ba tasɛ awuribi mʋ nɩɩ ba gyii san mʋ bɔla alantan kudu anyɔ.

eng: About five thousand men plus women and children had eaten.
local: Asa mʋ nɩɩ ba gyii bodobodo mʋ kɔnɔ ɩ waa fɛɛ anyɩn ngbɩn nnuu, yɛgɛ ba man kalɛ akyɩɩ yɛ nbii gbaa tii su.

eng: Right then, Jesus made the disciples get into the boat and go ahead to the other side of the lake while he dismissed the crowds.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ kamaa mʋ opula tɔ nɩɩ Yesu yɛgɛ nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba loo ɔkʋlɩ tɔ gyankpaa mʋ faa kpee benbe, nɩɩ ɔ sa sakyɔ mʋ ɔkpa yɛ ba natɛ.

eng: When he sent them away, he went up onto a mountain by himself to pray. Evening came and he was alone.
local: Maa yɛgɛ nɩɩ ba kyʋn mʋ, o dii kpee kɩbɩɩ sʋ mʋ nkʋn ɔ naa kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ. Kalaatɩpwɛ asɛ mʋ, mʋ nkʋn ɩ bʋ tʋtɔ.

eng: Meanwhile, the boat, fighting a strong headwind, was being battered by the waves and was already far away from land.
local: amaa saŋa maa tɔ mʋ ɔkʋlɩ mʋ tɛɛ wʋla fʋʋ nkyu nsana, nɩɩ afuu a maa waa mʋ, nɩɩ apaa a daa a kyenkpen ɔkʋlɩ mʋ.

eng: Very early in the morning he came to his disciples, walking on the lake.
local: Ɩ ba fʋʋ kɩbwayɩkɛ sʋ kerefi asa naa fʋʋ kerefi asiye nsana mʋ, Yesu natɛ nkyu sʋ ee kpee mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ asɛ.

eng: When the disciples saw him walking on the lake, they were terrified and said, Its a ghost! They were so frightened they screamed.
local: Ba maa wu maa naa nkyu sʋ ɛɛ ba mʋ, bamʋ nkɔlɔ ɩ ŋmaa bamʋ. Nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, Obuni nɩn. Nɩɩ ba kuusi lɩɩ fɛɛ kufuu kɩ kɩtaa bamʋ.

eng: Just then Jesus spoke to them, Be encouraged! Its me. Dont be afraid.
local: Amaa Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ opula tɔ yɛ, Fan nyɛ nkɔlɔ, mɛ nɩn. Fan man sɩlɛ kufuu.

eng: Peter replied, Lord, if its you, order me to come to you on the water.
local: Nɩɩ Pita lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, nɩɩ ɩ kan ɩ gyɛ fʋ mʋ, yɛgɛ n natɛ nkyu mʋ sʋ ba fʋ asɛ.

eng: And Jesus said, Come. Then Peter got out of the boat and was walking on the water toward Jesus.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Ba! Nɩɩ Pita lɩɩ ɔkʋlɩ mʋ tɔ ɔ naa nkyu mʋ sʋ ee kpee Yesu asɛ.

eng: But when Peter saw the strong wind, he became frightened. As he began to sink, he shouted, Lord, rescue me.
local: Amaa maa wu apaa mʋ, kufuu kɩ kɩtaa mʋ, nɩɩ o piili ee munla, nɩɩ o kuusi yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, mɔlɩgɛ mɛ.

eng: Jesus immediately reached out and grabbed him, saying, You man of weak faith! Why did you begin to have doubts.
local: Opula tɔ nɩɩ Yesu tɛɛŋɛ mʋ kɩbaa kɩtaa mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Fu kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii man kyɔ. Mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fʋ man kɔɔlɛ gyii.

eng: When they got into the boat, the wind settled down.
local: Ba maa dii loo ɔkʋlɩ mʋ tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ afuu mʋ ɩ yɛgɛ.

eng: Then those in the boat worshipped Jesus and said, You must be Gods Son.
local: Nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ ɔkʋlɩ mʋ tɔ mʋ, ba muŋa sun mʋ nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, Kesintin, fʋ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mu‐Bii nɩn.

eng: When they had crossed the lake, they landed at Gennesaret.
local: Ba maa faa ɔbʋn mʋ, nɩɩ ba naa dan Kanesareti ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ.

eng: When the people who lived in that place recognized him, they sent word throughout that whole region, and they brought to him everyone who was sick.
local: Nɩɩ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ tʋtɔ mʋ ba maa bɩɩ Yesu mʋ, ba sun tɔwɛ ndɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ kyaabɔɔ bamʋ mʋ kpini. Nɩɩ asa ba taa bamʋ alɔpʋ kpini ba mʋ asɛ.

eng: Then they begged him that they might just touch the edge of his clothes. Everyone who touched him was cured.
local: Nɩɩ ba kʋlɛ mʋ yɛ, ɔ dan yɛgɛ kɛ alɔpʋ mʋ ba dan taa bamʋ abaa mata mʋ kaalɛ kɔnɔ‐bi, nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba mata maŋa kpini ba nyɛ alanfɩya.

eng: At that time the disciples came to Jesus and asked, Who is the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.
local: Kanɩn saŋa maŋa tɔ mʋ, abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba ba Yesu asɛ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Anɛ tɔ mʋ, anɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔkparɛ sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ.

eng: Then he called a little child over to sit among the disciples.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɩɩ kebii pii kʋ ba yɩlɛ bamʋ nsana‐nsana.

eng: and said, I assure you that if you dont turn your lives around and become like this little child, you will definitely not enter the kingdom of heaven.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin nɩn fɛɛ, fan dan fan man kyɛɛgɛ kɛ fan waa fɛɛ nbii pii‐pii mʋ, fɛn mɛɛ yɛ fan loo sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ kakaakʋ.

eng: Those who humble themselves like this little child will be the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bɩya mʋ n‐yɩɩ kasɛ fɛɛ kebii pii nɩmʋ mʋ, ɩ gyɛ ɔkparɛ sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ.

eng: Whoever welcomes one such child in my name welcomes me.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ kebii pii fɛɛ ɔnɩmʋ mɛ kɩtɩɩ tɔ mʋ, ɔ kɔɔlɛ mɛ nɩmʋ.

eng: As for whoever causes these little ones who believe in me to trip and fall into sin, it would be better for them to have a huge stone hung around their necks and be drowned in the bottom of the lake.
local: Amaa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ kan bɔla mʋ sʋ, kɛ nbii mʋ nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ mɛ gyii falɛ tɔ ɔkʋ waa alibi mʋ, ɩ bɔ sa mʋ fɛɛ ba taa kubuu lala ŋmina mʋ ɔbʋlɛ tɔ naa tʋʋ ɔpʋ bunkyinsa tɔ.

eng: How terrible it is for the world because of the things that cause people to trip and fall into sin! Such things have to happen, but how terrible it is for the person who causes those things to happen!.
local: Dulinya nɩmʋ tɔ asa, bamʋ laakʋ! Lɩɩ fɛɛ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩyɛ bamʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ gyii tɔ mʋ, ɩ nyɛ akyɔ. Falɛ atɔ ii biti ɩ ba, amaa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ bɔla mʋ sʋ ba mʋ, mʋ laakʋ.

eng: If your hand or your foot causes you to fall into sin, chop it off and throw it away. Its better to enter into life crippled or lame than to be thrown into the eternal fire with two hands or two feet.
local: Nɩɩ fʋ kɩbaa abɛɛ fʋ kɩyaa ɩɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ fɩɩ waa alibi mʋ, tɩn kɩmʋ tʋʋ. Ɩ bʋrɔn sa fʋ fɛɛ fʋ maa taa kɩbaa abɛɛ kɩyaa dɩyɛ loo nkpa kakpaa tɔ, ɩ kyɔ fʋ maa sʋ abaa anyɔ yɛ ayaa anyɔ kɛ ba taa fʋ tʋʋ fʋlɔn mʋ nɩɩ ki mee dun kakaakʋ mʋ tɔ.

eng: If your eye causes you to fall into sin, tear it out and throw it away. Its better to enter into life with one eye than to be cast into a burning hell with two eyes.
local: Nɩɩ fu kinsi ɩɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ fʋ waa alibi mʋ, kyɔlɛ kɩmʋ tʋʋ. Ɩ bʋrɔn sa fʋ fɛɛ fʋ maa taa kinsi kʋlʋn loo nkpa kakpaa tɔ, ɩ kyɔ fʋ maa sʋ ansi anyɔ kɛ ba taa fʋ tʋʋ awʋrʋfɔ fʋlɔn yen‐yen tɔ.

eng: Be careful that you dont look down on one of these little ones. I say to you that their angels in heaven are always looking into the face of my Father who is in heaven.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan baa fan da sʋ kɛ fan man kɩɩ apii‐pii mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ fɛɛ ɔ man gyɛ sɛhn. N tɔwɛ fanɛ fɛɛ bamʋ nbɔɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ sʋsʋ mʋ, ii wu mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ sʋsʋ mu ansi tɔ saŋa kʋmaa.

eng: What do you think? If someone had one hundred sheep and one of them wandered off, wouldnt he leave the ninety-nine on the hillsides and go in search for the one that wandered off?.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn kʋ kan ɔ sʋ nbʋlʋpʋ kɩlɩfa, nɩɩ bamʋ tɔ ɔkʋlʋn fɔyɩ mʋ, mɩnɛ nɩɩ fii nyi maa biti ɔ waa? Ɔ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ yɛgɛ adɩkpanɔ yɛ akpanɔ mʋ kɩbɩɩ ɔbʋlɛ tɔ, kɛ ɔ naa biti ɔkʋlʋn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ fɔyɩ mʋ abɛɛ?.

eng: If he finds it, I assure you that he is happier about having that one sheep than about the ninety-nine who didnt wander off.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin tɔ nɩɩ fɛɛ, nɩɩ ɔ kan wu mʋ mʋ, mu ansi ii biti i gyii mʋ sʋ, ɩ kyɔ adɩkpanɔ yɛ akpanɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba man fɔyɩ mʋ.

eng: In the same way, my Father who is in heaven doesnt want to lose one of these little ones.
local: Kanɩn ɔkpa maŋa sʋ nɩɩ fanɛ‐Sɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ sʋsʋ mʋ gbaa mee biti fɛɛ apii‐pii nɩmʋ tɔ ɔkʋlʋn maa fɔyɩ.

eng: If your brother or sister sins against you, go and correct them when you are alone together. If they listen to you, then youve won over your brother or sister.
local: Nɩɩ fu‐supu abɛɛ fʋ‐daa kan waa fʋ alibi mʋ, kpee mʋ asɛ naa lɛɛ mu alibi mʋ kaapʋ mʋ fanɛ asa nyɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ kan nu kasɛ sa fʋ mʋ, fʋ bɩla bɩya mʋ ba ɔkpa danbɩrasa mʋ sʋ nɩmʋ.

eng: But if they wont listen, take with you one or two others so that every word may be established by the mouth of two or three witnesses .
local: Amaa nɩɩ ɔ man kan nu sa mʋ, biti nyɩmɩsa kʋlʋn abɛɛ asa anyɔ tii fʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋ kɛ fen kpee mʋ asɛ. Saamʋ, asɩn mʋ ɩ nyɛ yɩlɛ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ ɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɩ dagaa asɩn kʋmaa ɩ nyɛ asa anyɔ abɛɛ asa asá be gyii ɩmʋ adansɛ.

eng: But if they still wont pay attention, report it to the church. If they wont pay attention even to the church, treat them as you would a Gentile and tax collector.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ man kan bɩla nu sa bamʋ mʋ, taa asɩn mʋ naa tʋʋ Krisito kʋbʋ mu ansi tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ bɩla kina kunu sa bamʋ bɩrɛ, taa mʋ fɛɛ ɔdakpa osunpu abɛɛ apoo lenpoo ɔkɔɔlɛpʋ.

eng: I assure you that whatever you fasten on earth will be fastened in heaven. And whatever you loosen on earth will be loosened in heaven.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin fɛɛ, kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fan ŋmina kasɛ sʋ nfɩɩ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti o ŋmina kɩmʋ sʋsʋ, nɩɩ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fan kina kasɛ sʋ nfɩɩ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti o kina kɩmʋ sʋsʋ.

eng: Again I assure you that if two of you agree on earth about anything you ask, then my Father who is in heaven will do it for you.
local: N bɩla n tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, nɩɩ fanɛ tɔ anyɔ ba kan waa kɔnɔ kʋlʋn kʋlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ bee biti kʋtɔ kʋ mʋ, Mɛ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ sʋsʋ mʋ ii biti ɔ waa sa bamʋ.

eng: For where two or three are gathered in my name, Im there with them.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ katɩn kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ asa anyɔ abɛɛ asa asá ba gyaŋɛ mɛ kɩtɩɩ tɔ mʋ, n bʋ bamʋ asɛ.

eng: Then Peter said to Jesus, Lord, how many times should I forgive my brother or sister who sins against me? Should I forgive as many as seven times.
local: Nɩɩ Pita ba Yesu asɛ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, ɩlɩɩ ɩfɩnɛ nɩɩ mɛ‐bɩrɩsa waa mɛ alibi nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ n taa kɛɛ mʋ? Ilii isunɔ abɛɛ.

eng: Jesus said, Not just seven times, but rather as many as seventy-seven times.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ayee, man gyɛ ɩlɩɩ isunɔ, amaa adusunɔ kanɩn ɩlɩɩ isunɔ.

eng: Therefore, the kingdom of heaven is like a king who wanted to settle accounts with his servants.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii mʋ du nɩn fɛɛ owura kʋ maa biti mʋ ayaafɔlɛ ba ba kaapʋ mʋ akɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ mʋ.

eng: When he began to settle accounts, they brought to him a servant who owed him ten thousand bags of gold.
local: Maa piili ɛɛ taasɛ mʋ akɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ba taa ɔnyɩn kʋ bɩya mʋ. Ɔnyɩn mʋ sʋ owura mʋ kʋkɔ afulee nderebi ngbɩn‐ngbɩn.

eng: Because the servant didnt have enough to pay it back, the master ordered that he should be sold, along with his wife and children and everything he had, and that the proceeds should be used as payment.
local: Maa man taalɛ ka mʋ, nɩɩ mʋ owura mʋ yɛ, ba taa maa mʋ‐ka yɛ mu‐bii ana yɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ mʋ naa fɛ kɛ ba ba ka kʋkɔ mʋ.

eng: But the servant fell down, kneeled before him, and said, Please, be patient with me, and Ill pay you back.
local: Nɩɩ kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ kyʋn muŋa mu amulii sʋ kʋlɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɔbɩlɩsa, nyɛ kenyiita sa mɛ, n biti n ka fʋ kʋkɔ maŋa kpini.

eng: The master had compassion on that servant, released him, and forgave the loan.
local: Nɩɩ kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ asɩn ɩ waa mʋ owura mʋ kʋwɛɛ, nɩɩ ɔ taa kʋkɔ mʋ kpini kɛɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ɔ natɛ.

eng: When that servant went out, he found one of his fellow servants who owed him one hundred coins. He grabbed him around the throat and said, Pay me back what you owe me.
local: Amaa kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ maa lɩɩ mʋ, ɔ naa wu mʋ bɩrɩsa kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ nɩɩ mɔɔ mʋ ɔ sʋ mu afulee nderebi kɩlɩfa. Nɩɩ ɔ naa kɩtaa mʋ ɔbʋlɛ tɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Ka mɛ kʋkɔ mʋ nɩɩ fʋ sʋ mɛ.

eng: Then his fellow servant fell down and begged him, Be patient with me, and Ill pay you back.
local: Nɩɩ mʋ bɩrɩsa kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ muŋa mu amulii sʋ kʋlɛ mʋ yɛ, Nyɛ kenyiita sa mɛ, n biti n ka kʋkɔ mʋ kpini.

eng: But he refused. Instead, he threw him into prison until he paid back his debt.
local: Amaa o kina mʋ bɩrɩsa maŋa kɩkʋlɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ naa tii obu naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ nyɛ afulee ba ka mʋ kʋkɔ mʋ.
