eng: When his fellow servants saw what happened, they were deeply offended. They came and told their master all that happened.
local: Mʋ bɩrɩsa ayaafɔlɛ mʋ nɩɩ ba san mʋ ba maa wu kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa mʋ bɩrɩsa mʋ, ɩ tɔɔrɔɔ bamʋ gaa nɩɩ ba naa tɔwɛ asɩn mʋ kpini sa bamʋ owura mʋ.

eng: His master called the first servant and said, You wicked servant! I forgave you all that debt because you appealed to me.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ owura mʋ tɩɩ mʋ kɩyaafɔlɛ maŋa ba nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Fʋ gyɛ kɩyaafɔlɛ kumu tɔ ɔlʋn wuya. N taa fʋ kʋkɔ kpini nɩmʋ kɛɛ fʋ, kpalɩ fɛɛ fʋ maa kʋlɛ mɛ sʋ.

eng: Shouldnt you also have mercy on your fellow servant, just as I had mercy on you.
local: Ɩ man dagaa fʋ gbaa fʋ maa wu fʋ bɩrɩsa mʋ kʋwɛɛ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ngbaa mɛ n wu fʋ kʋwɛɛ mʋ.

eng: His master was furious and handed him over to the guard responsible for punishing prisoners, until he had paid the whole debt.
local: Tʋtɔ mʋ ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ nyɛ agbʋ gaa, nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ naa tii obu yɛ ba nyanla mʋ naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ ka kʋkɔ mʋ kpini.

eng: My heavenly Father will also do the same to you if you dont forgive your brother or sister from your heart.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Falɛ nɩɩ Mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ sʋsʋ mʋ ii biti ɔ waa fanɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ, amɔɔ fʋ kan taa fʋ bɩrɩsa alibi kɛɛ mʋ lɩɩ fʋ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ mʋ.

eng: When Jesus finished saying these things, he left Galilee and came to the area of Judea on the east side of the Jordan.
local: Yesu maa tɔwɛ ɩnɩmʋ lʋwɛ mʋ ɔ lɩɩ Galile kpee Gyudeya kɩbaafʋn, Gyɔdan ɔpʋ mʋ benbe.

eng: Large crowds followed him, and he healed them.
local: Asa damantɛ ba buu mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kyɛ bamʋ alɔ tʋtɔ.

eng: Some Pharisees came to him. In order to test him, they said, Does the Law allow a man to divorce his wife for just any reason.
local: Nɩɩ Farasii abi akʋ ba ba mʋ asɛ ba ba tʋʋ mʋ tɔ kɩɩ. Ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɩ dɛ ɔkpa sa ɔnyɩn fɛɛ ɔ yɩlɛ asɩn kʋmaa nɩmʋ sʋ kina mʋ‐ka.

eng: Jesus answered, Havent you read that at the beginning the creator made them male and female?.
local: Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Fan man karagɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ wu fɛɛ, Lɩɩ npiili asɛ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ waa bamʋ ɔnyɩn yɛ ɔkyɩɩ nɩn abɛɛ?.

eng: And God said, Because of this a man should leave his father and mother and be joined together with his wife, and the two will be one flesh .
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Lɩɩ falɛ sʋ, ɔnyɩn ii biti ɔ yɛgɛ mu‐nyi yɛ mʋ‐sɛ, kɛ maa mʋ‐ka ba bʋga kɛ bamʋ anyɔ ba waa kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ kʋlʋn.

eng: So they are no longer two but one flesh. Therefore, humans must not pull apart what God has put together.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ba man bɩla ba gyɛ anyɔ, amaa ba gyɛ nyɩmɩsa kʋlʋn. Kanɩn sʋ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ taa bʋga waa kʋkʋlʋn mʋ, ɔkʋ ɔ man barɩgɛ ɩmʋ tɔ.

eng: The Pharisees said to him, Then why did Moses command us to give a divorce certificate and divorce her? .
local: Nɩɩ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ Mosesi sa kɔnɔ yɛ ɔnyɩn ɩ taalɛ sa mʋ‐ka kekili kikina ɔwʋlɛ kɛ o kina mʋ.

eng: Jesus replied, Moses allowed you to divorce your wives because your hearts are unyielding. But it wasnt that way from the beginning.
local: Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Mosesi sa fanɛ ɔkpa yɛ fan kina fanɛ‐ka ana, lɩɩ fɛɛ fanɛ nkɔlɔ ɩ bʋlʋn nɩn. Amaa man gyɛ falɛ nɩɩ ɩ kyɩna i du lɩɩ npiili asɛ mʋ.

eng: I say to you that whoever divorces his wife, except for sexual unfaithfulness, and marries another woman commits adultery.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin yɛ, nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ yɛ ɔkyɩɩ lɩɩ mu‐kuli kamaa, nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ kina mʋ naa kili ɔkyɩɩ pʋpwɛ mʋ, ɔnyɩn mʋ nyita kekili.

eng: His disciples said to him, If thats the way things are between a man and his wife, then its better not to marry.
local: Mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Nɩɩ ɩ kan ɩ gyɛ kanɩn nɩɩ i du okuli maa ɔkaa nsana mʋ bɩrɛ, nɩɩ nyɩmɩsa man kili gbaa ɩ bɔ.

eng: He replied, Not everybody can accept this teaching, but only those who have received the ability to accept it.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Man gyɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ ɩ taalɛ kɔɔlɛ falɛ asɩn kyula, amɔɔ bamʋ nɩɩ ba taa asɩn mʋ sa bamʋ nkʋn.

eng: For there are eunuchs who have been eunuchs from birth. And there are eunuchs who have been made eunuchs by other people. And there are eunuchs who have made themselves eunuchs because of the kingdom of heaven. Those who can accept it should accept it.
local: Anyɩn akʋ ba bʋ tɔ nɩɩ be mee taalɛ kili, lɩɩ fɛɛ kanɩn nɩɩ ba kʋʋgɛ bamʋ, nɩɩ akʋ mɔɔ asa ɩ waa bamʋ kanɩn, nɩɩ akʋ mɔɔ ba kina kekili lɩɩ sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii mʋ kusun sʋ. Ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ taalɛ kɔɔlɛ kɩkaapʋ nɩmʋ kyula mʋ, ɔ kɔɔlɛ.

eng: Some people brought children to Jesus so that he would place his hands on them and pray. But the disciples scolded them.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ asa kʋ ba taa nbii wuribi ba Yesu asɛ yɛ ɔ taa mʋ abaa gyaga bamʋ sʋ, kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ sa bamʋ. Amaa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba gya asa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ bɩya bamʋ mʋ.

eng: Allow the children to come to me, Jesus said. Dont forbid them, because the kingdom of heaven belongs to people like these children.
local: Yesu tɔwɛ yɛ, Fan yɛgɛ nbii wuribi mʋ ba ba mɛ asɛ, fan man tii bamʋ ɔkpa, lɩɩ fɛɛ sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii mʋ kɩ gyɛ sa asa mʋ nɩɩ be du fɛɛ nbii‐wuribi mʋ lɛɛ nɩn.

eng: Then he blessed the children and went away from there.
local: Maa taa mʋ abaa gyaga bamʋ sʋ lʋwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ tʋtɔ kyʋn.

eng: A man approached him and said, Teacher, what good thing must I do to have eternal life.
local: Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn kʋ ba Yesu asɛ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɔkaapʋpʋ, mɩnɛ kʋtɔ danbɩrasa nɩɩ ɩ dagaa n maa waa kɛ n nyɛ nkpa kakpaa.

eng: Jesus said, Why do you ask me about what is good? Theres only one who is good. If you want to enter eternal life, keep the commandments.
local: Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ fɩɩ taasɛ mɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋrɔn sʋ? Ɔkʋ man gyɛ ɔdanbɩrasa, amɔɔ Ɩbwaarɛ nkʋn. Nɩɩ fii biti fʋ nyɛ nkpa kakpaa mʋ, buu Mosesi nbara mʋ.

eng: The man said, Which ones? Then Jesus said, Dont commit murder. Dont commit adultery. Dont steal. Dont give false testimony .
local: Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ taasɛ yɛ, Ɩmɔɔ? Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Man mɔɔ, man lɩɩ fʋ‐ka abɛɛ fu‐kuli kamaa, man yuuri, man yɩlɛ antɩŋɛsa kamaa.

eng: Honor your father and mother, and love your neighbor as you love yourself .
local: buu fʋ‐sɛ yɛ fu‐nyi, kɛ fu biti fʋ bɩrɩsa fɛɛ fʋ n‐yɩɩ. .

eng: The young man replied, Ive kept all these. What am I still missing.
local: Nɩɩ kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, N buu ɩnɩmʋ kpini. Mɩnɛ kʋtɔ ɩ san mɛ.

eng: Jesus said, If you want to be complete, go, sell what you own, and give the money to the poor. Then you will have treasure in heaven. And come follow me.
local: Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Nɩɩ fʋ kan fii biti yɛ fʋ baa fu du kyɛɛkyɛɛ bɩrɛ, kpee naa fɛ fʋ atɔ kpini kɛ fʋ taa afulee mʋ sa atiripu, fii biti fʋ nyɛ kapatɩyɛ sʋsʋ, kɛ fʋ lan ba buu mɛ.

eng: The kingdom of heaven is like a landowner who went out early in the morning to hire workers for his vineyard.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu bɩla tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii mʋ ki du nɩn fɛɛ ɔnyɩn kʋ maa kʋsʋ kakaakʋ nyenyenfuu, ɔ naa biti asa kɛ ba sun kusun mʋ ndɔɔ tɔ.

eng: But when the young man heard this, he went away saddened, because he had many possessions.
local: Ɔnyɩn mʋ maa nu falɛ mʋ ɩ tɔɔrɔɔ mʋ gaa, nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ tʋtɔ kyʋn lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ sʋ atɔ gaa.

eng: After he agreed with the workers to pay them a denarion, he sent them into his vineyard.
local: Maa naa nyɛ bamʋ mʋ, nɩɩ maa bamʋ ba tɔwɛ kyula abaa yɛ ee biti ɔ ka bamʋ kederebi kʋlʋn kakɛ kʋlʋn ibiri mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taa bamʋ kpee mʋ ndɔɔ tɔ.

eng: Then Jesus said to his disciples, I assure you that it will be very hard for a rich person to enter the kingdom of heaven.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ, N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin yɛ, ɩ bʋlʋn sa atɔ wuya fɛɛ o loo sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ.

eng: Then he went out around nine in the morning and saw others standing around the marketplace doing nothing.
local: Kyɔwɛ maa waa fɛɛ kerefi akpanɔ mʋ, ɔ bɩla kpee kadɛ mʋ kɩgya asɛ naa wu akʋ ba maa yɩlɛ ba mɛɛ waa sɛhn.

eng: In fact, its easier for a camel to squeeze through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter Gods kingdom.
local: N bɩla n tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, ɩ bʋpɔɔ sa nyɔɔma fɛɛ o loo baasa ɔbɔ tɔ ɩ kyɔ atɔ wuya o loo Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ.

eng: He said to them, You also go into the vineyard, and Ill pay you whatever is right.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fanɛ gbaa fanɛ fan kpee naa waa kusun mɛ ndɔɔ tɔ, kɛ n ka fanɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ dagaa.

eng: When his disciples heard this, they were stunned. Then who can be saved? they asked.
local: Abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba maa nu kanɩn mʋ, i kyinkyin bamʋ gaa nɩɩ ba taasɛ abaa yɛ, Kanɩn bɩrɛ anɩmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ mɔlɩgɛ nɩmʋ.

eng: And they went. Again around noon and then at three in the afternoon, he did the same thing.
local: Nɩɩ ba kpee. Kyɔwɛ amu nsana yɛ kerefi asa tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ bɩla taa akʋ kpee kanɩn.

eng: Jesus looked at them carefully and said, Its impossible for human beings. But all things are possible for God.
local: Yesu kɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Nyɩmɩsa asɛ bɩrɛ ɩnɩmʋ ɩ mɛɛ taalɛ waa, amaa Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ bɩrɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ ɩ taalɛ waa.

eng: Around five in the afternoon he went and found others standing around, and he said to them, Why are you just standing around here doing nothing all day long.
local: Kalaatɩpwɛ sʋ kyɔwɛ maa tɩyɛ ɛɛ mata afatɛɛ tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ bɩla kpee naa wu akʋ ba maa yɩlɛ ba mɛɛ waa sɛhn. Nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Mɩnɛ ɩ waa sʋ nɩɩ fan yɩlɛ nfɩɩ kakɛ ibiri nɩɩ fan man sʋ kusun.

eng: Then Peter replied, Look, weve left everything and followed you. What will we have.
local: Pita lɛɛ mʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Kɩɩ! A maa yɛgɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ an buu fʋ falɛ, mɩnɛ nɩɩ an biti a nyɛ.

eng: Because nobody has hired us, they replied. He responded, You also go into the vineyard.
local: Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ mʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɔkʋ man taa anɛ yɛ a naa sun sa mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Kanɩn bɩrɛ fanɛ gbaa fanɛ fan ba kyʋn kpee naa waa kusun mɛ ndɔɔ tɔ.

eng: Jesus said to them, I assure you who have followed me that, when everything is made new, when the Human One sits on his magnificent throne, you also will sit on twelve thrones overseeing the twelve tribes of Israel.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin yɛ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kan bun dulinyaa pʋpwɛ, nɩɩ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa kyɩna mɛ bʋnyaa kuwura‐gya mʋ sʋ, fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fan buu mɛ mʋ gbaa, fen biti fan kyɩna nwuragya kudu anyɔ sʋ, kɛ fan gyii Ɩsɩrayɩ nbunɔ kudu anyɔ mʋ sʋ kuwura.

eng: When evening came, the owner of the vineyard said to his manager, Call the workers and give them their wages, beginning with the last ones hired and moving on finally to the first.
local: Kalaatɩpwɛ maa fʋʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ndɔɔ wuya mʋ tɔwɛ asunpu mʋ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ yɛ, Tɩɩ asunpu mʋ, kɛ fʋ ka bamʋ kʋkɔ. Piili lɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba sii kamaa ba mʋ sʋ naa fʋʋ bamʋ nɩɩ ba gyankpaa ba mʋ.

eng: And all who have left houses, brothers, sisters, father, mother, children, or farms because of my name will receive one hundred times more and will inherit eternal life.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ yɛgɛ akpaara, abɛɛ pɩɩkyɩɩ, abɛɛ mʋ‐sɛ, abɛɛ mu‐nyi, abɛɛ mu‐bii ana, abɛɛ adɔɔ lɩɩ mɛ sʋ mʋ, ee biti ɔ nyɛ ɩmʋ ɩlɩɩ kɩlɩfa, kɛ ɔ bɩla nyɛ nkpa kakpaa.

eng: When those who were hired at five in the afternoon came, each one received a denarion.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔ yɛgɛ nɩɩ asunpu mʋ nɩɩ ba kpee ndɔɔ mʋ tɔ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ kyɔwɛ mata afatɛɛ tɔ mʋ, ba ba kɔɔlɛ kederebi kʋlʋn‐kʋlʋn mʋ nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ kakɛ ibiri kʋkɔka.

eng: But many who are first will be last. And many who are last will be first.
local: Amaa asa gaalaagaa mʋ nɩɩ ba gyankpaa mʋ, bee biti be sii kamaa, nɩɩ asa gaalaagaa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ kamaa mʋ, bee biti ba ba gyankpaa.

eng: Now when those hired first came, they thought they would receive more. But each of them also received a denarion.
local: Ɩ maa fʋʋ agyankpaapʋ mʋ sʋ mʋ, be nyi fɛɛ bee biti ba nyɛ ba kyɔ kamaa tɔ wuya ana mʋ, amaa ngbaa bamʋ kederebi kʋlʋn‐kʋlʋn maŋa nɩɩ ba nyɛ.

eng: When they received it, they grumbled against the landowner.
local: Ba maa kɔɔlɛ kederebi kʋlʋn‐kʋlʋn mʋ, nɩɩ be piili bɛɛ kɔlɩtɛ bɛɛ sa ndɔɔ wuya mʋ yɛ.

eng: These who were hired last worked one hour, and they received the same pay as we did even though we had to work the whole day in the hot sun.
local: Bamʋ nɩɩ ba sii kamaa ba falɛ bɩrɛ, saŋa gbɛrɛɛ nɩɩ ba sun kusun, yɛgɛ anɛɛnɛ bɩrɛ an yɩlɛ kyɔwɛ kenken nɩmʋ sʋ waa kusun ibiri. Amaa ana bamʋ kpini nɩɩ fʋ ka kyɛɛkyɛɛ.

eng: But he replied to one of them, Friend, I did you no wrong. Didnt I agree to pay you a denarion?.
local: Nɩɩ kadɔɔ wuya mʋ lɛɛ bamʋ ɔkʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Kyɛmɩnɛ, n man sisii fʋ. Man gyɛ kederebi kʋlʋn kʋkɔka nɩɩ maa fʋ an tɔwɛ kyula kakɛ ibiri kusun?.

eng: Take what belongs to you and go. I want to give to this one who was hired last the same as I give to you.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ taa fu kederebi natɛ. Kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɩ pɩrɛ mɛ yɛ n ka asii‐amaapʋ mʋ, kanɩn nɩɩ maŋa nɩɩ n biti n ka fʋ gbaa.

eng: Dont I have the right to do what I want with what belongs to me? Or are you resentful because Im generous.
local: N man sʋ ɔkpa fɛɛ n waa mɛ kɔkɔlɔ biti lɩɩ mɛ afulee sʋ abɛɛ? Abɛɛ fʋ sʋ kayɩɩ basa lɩɩ mɛ kɩdanbɩrasa kɩwaa sʋ nɩn? .

eng: So those who are last will be first. And those who are first will be last.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Asii‐amaapʋ bee biti ba biliŋi agyankpaapʋ kɛ agyankpaapʋ ba biliŋi asii‐amaapʋ.

eng: As Jesus was going up to Jerusalem, he took the Twelve aside by themselves on the road. He told them.
local: Yesu maa kpee Gyerusalem mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ kudu anyɔ mʋ lɩɩ ɩkaa sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ.

eng: Look, we are going up to Jerusalem. The Human One will be handed over to the chief priests and legal experts. They will condemn him to death.
local: Fan nu! An kpee Gyerusalem nɩmʋ, tʋtɔ nɩɩ bee biti ba naa lɛɛ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ sa aseepu bɩlɩsa yɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ. Kɛ ba bun mɛ lewu kʋpwɛ.

eng: They will hand him over to the Gentiles to be ridiculed, tortured, and crucified. But he will be raised on the third day.
local: kɛ ba taa mɛ waa bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ abaa tɔ, kɛ ba waa mɛ ɩbasasɩn kɛ ba tɩŋɛ mɛ, kɛ ba da mɛ mata oyu laasa sʋ. Amaa nkɛ nsa akɛ mʋ, n biti n kyiŋi lɩɩ lewu tɔ.

eng: Then the mother of Zebedees sons came to Jesus along with her sons. Bowing before him, she asked a favor of him.
local: Nɩɩ Sebedi mʋ‐ka maa mu‐bii ana anyɩnsa anyɔ mʋ ba ba Yesu asɛ, nɩɩ bamʋ‐nyi ba muŋa Yesu ayaa tɔ kʋlɛ mʋ kʋtɔ kʋ.

eng: What do you want? he asked. She responded, Say that these two sons of mine will sit, one on your right hand and one on your left, in your kingdom.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Mɩnɛ nɩɩ fii biti? Nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Kyula sa mɛ fɛɛ, mi nbii nnyɔ nɩmʋ, ɔkʋ ɔ kyɩna fu‐gyisa sʋ kɛ ɔkʋ mɔɔ ɔ kyɩna fʋ‐bɩna sʋ fu kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ.

eng: Jesus replied, You dont know what youre asking! Can you drink from the cup that Im about to drink from? They said to him, We can.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan man nyi kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ fɛn kʋlɛ falɛ. Fɛn taalɛ gyii awʋrʋfɔ mʋ nɩɩ n biti n gyii mʋ? Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɛɛhn, an taalɛ.

eng: He said to them, You will drink from my cup, but to sit at my right or left hand isnt mine to give. It belongs to those for whom my Father prepared it.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fɛn taalɛ gyii awʋrʋfɔ mʋ nɩɩ n biti n gyii mʋ, amaa n man sʋ ɔkpa fɛɛ n lɛɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ kyɩna mi‐gyisa sʋ abɛɛ mɛ‐bɩna sʋ. Ɩkyɩnakpa ɩnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ sa bamʋ nɩɩ mɛ‐Sɛ wʋla lɔŋɔ yɩla sa bamʋ mʋ.

eng: Now when the other ten disciples heard about this, they became angry with the two brothers.
local: Abɩɩlapʋ kudu mʋ nɩɩ ba san mʋ, ba maa nu ɩnɩmʋ mʋ, ba nyɛ agbʋ waa Gyemisi maa mu‐supu Gyɔn.

eng: But Jesus called them over and said, You know that those who rule the Gentiles show off their authority over them and their high-ranking officials order them around.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɩɩ bamʋ ba bʋga nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fen nyi fɛɛ dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ agyankpaapʋ mʋ bɛɛ kaapʋ bamʋ ɔlʋn asa sʋ, nɩɩ alʋlʋnpʋ gbaa bɛɛ kaapʋ bamʋ ɔlʋn asa sʋ.

eng: But thats not the way it will be with you. Whoever wants to be great among you will be your servant.
local: Amaa fanɛɛ fanɛ tɔ bɩrɛ, ɩ man dagaa ɩ maa waa kanɩn. Nɩɩ fanɛ tɔ ɔkʋ kan ii biti kɩbɩlɩsa mʋ, ɩ dagaa maa waa fanɛ kɩyaafɔlɛ.

eng: Whoever wants to be first among you will be your slave.
local: nɩɩ fanɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti fɛɛ ɔ waa ɔgyankpaapʋ mʋ, ɩ dagaa maa waa fanɛ kɩnyɛ.

eng: just as the Human One didnt come to be served but rather to serve and to give his life to liberate many people.
local: fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa gbaa n man ba nɩn kɛ ba sun mɛ, amaa n ba nɩn kɛ n ba sun, kɛ n taa mɛ nkpa kɛɛ, kɛ ɩ ka asa gaalaagaa alibi kʋkɔ.

eng: As Jesus and his disciples were going out of Jericho a large crowd followed him.
local: Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba maa lɩɩ Gyeriko kadɛ tɔ mʋ, asa gaalaagaa ba buu bamʋ.

eng: When two blind men sitting along the road heard that Jesus was passing by, they shouted, Show us mercy, Lord, Son of David.
local: Tʋtɔ mʋ, agyaatanapʋ anyɔ kʋ ba tɛ ɔkpakaa, nɩɩ ba nu fɛɛ Yesu ɩɩ kyʋn mʋ, ba kuusi tɩɩ mʋ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, Dawidi kanaana‐bii, wu anɛ kʋwɛɛ.

eng: Now the crowd scolded them and told them to be quiet. But they shouted even louder, Show us mercy, Lord, Son of David.
local: Nɩɩ asa mʋ ba pʋntɛ bamʋ sʋ yɛ ba bun bamʋ nnɔ sʋ, amaa ba kuusi kenken yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, Dawidi kanaana‐bii, wu anɛ kʋwɛɛ.

eng: Jesus stopped in his tracks and called to them. What do you want me to do for you? he asked.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu yɩlɛ nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ bamʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Mɩnɛ nɩɩ fen biti fɛɛ n waa sa fanɛ.

eng: Lord, we want to see, they replied.
local: Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, an biti fɛɛ anɛ ansi i bugi nɩn.

eng: Jesus had compassion on them and touched their eyes. Immediately they were able to see, and they followed him.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu wu bamʋ kʋwɛɛ nɩɩ ɔ mata bamʋ ansi. Opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ bamʋ ansi i bugi nɩɩ ba buu Yesu.

eng: Then Jesus spoke to the crowds and his disciples.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ kʋbʋ damantɛ yɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ.

eng: The legal experts and the Pharisees sit on Moses seat.
local: Nbara akaapʋpʋ yɛ Farasii abi mʋ ii yii anɛ naana Mosesi ayaa tɔ bɛɛ lɛɛ nbara mʋ kasɛ.

eng: Therefore, you must take care to do everything they say. But dont do what they do.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ fan waa mʋ, fan nu sa bamʋ kɛ fan waa. Amaa fan man waa atɔ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa mʋ, kpalɩ fɛɛ be mee buu atɔ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɔwɛ fanɛ mʋ sʋ.

eng: For they tie together heavy packs that are impossible to carry. They put them on the shoulders of others, but are unwilling to lift a finger to move them.
local: Bee ŋmina ɩsʋla budusa nɩn bɛɛ sa asa yɛ ba sʋla, amaa bamʋ gbaa‐gbaa bamʋ bɩrɛ, ba mɛɛ yɛ ba taa bamʋ kɩbaa‐bi kyɛ asa mʋ tɔ kɛ ba sʋla.

eng: Everything they do, they do to be noticed by others. They make extra-wide prayer bands for their arms and long tassels for their clothes.
local: Nɩɩ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa mʋ, bɛɛ waa kɛ asa ba nyɛ wu bamʋ. Nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ abwaarɛsɩn gyaga ɔwʋlɛ sʋ, nɩɩ ba taa ɩmʋ mata bamʋ asɩkpɩɩ yɛ abaa sʋ, nɩɩ ba waa bamʋ ɩkaalɛ nnɔ ayolobi bʋnswɩɩsa.

eng: They love to sit in places of honor at banquets and in the synagogues.
local: Nɩɩ ba kpee kigyii nsarɛ tɔ abɛɛ kabwaarɛ kʋlɛ ɩgyaŋɛkpa tɔ mʋ, ɩkyɩnakpa kparɛ‐kparɛ nɩɩ bee biti ba kyɩna.

eng: They love to be greeted with honor in the markets and to be addressed as Rabbi.
local: Nɩɩ bee biti fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyaŋa bamʋ kadɛ tɔ mʋ, ɔ gyɔŋɔ fwaala bamʋ, nɩɩ ba bɩla bee biti asa ba tɩɩ bamʋ yɛ Akaapʋpʋ.

eng: But you shouldnt be called Rabbi , because you have one teacher, and all of you are brothers and sisters.
local: Amaa fanɛɛ fanɛ bɩrɛ, fan man yɛgɛ ba tɩɩ fanɛ yɛ, Akaapʋpʋ, kpalɩ fɛɛ fan sʋ Ɔkaapʋpʋ kʋlʋn nɩn, fanɛ kpini fan gyɛ asupu yɛ adaa nɩn.

eng: Dont call anybody on earth your father, because you have one Father, who is heavenly.
local: Fan man tɩɩ ɔkʋ yɛ Ɔsɛ dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ, lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɔsɛ kʋlʋn nɩɩ fan sʋ, nɩɩ mʋ ɩ bʋ sʋsʋ mʋ.

eng: Dont be called teacher , because Christ is your one teacher.
local: Abɛɛ fan man yɛgɛ ba tɩɩ fanɛ ɔkʋ yɛ Ɔkaapʋpʋ, kpalɩ fɛɛ Ɔkaapʋpʋ kʋlʋn nɩɩ fan sʋ, nɩɩ mʋ ɩ gyɛ Krisito mʋ.

eng: But the one who is greatest among you will be your servant.
local: Ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ ɔbɩlɩsa fanɛ tɔ mʋ, ɩ dagaa maa gyɛ fanɛ kɩyaafɔlɛ.

eng: All who lift themselves up will be brought low. But all who make themselves low will be lifted up.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kʋsaa mʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋsʋ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ bɩya mʋ kasɛ, nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bɩya mʋ n‐yɩɩ kasɛ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ kʋsaa mʋ sʋsʋ.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You shut people out of the kingdom of heaven. You dont enter yourselves, and you wont allow those who want to enter to do so.
local: Fanɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ yɛ Farasii abi, fan gyɛ kebunbun ansi wuya ana, fanɛ laakʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ fen tii asa ɔkpa kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba man nyɛ loo sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ. Fanɛ gbaa‐gbaa fanɛ fen mee loo to, nɩɩ fɛn mɛɛ yɛgɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bee biti ba loo mʋ, ba nyɛ loo to.

eng: How terrible it will be for you, legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You travel over sea and land to make one convert. But when theyve been converted, they become twice the child of hell you are.
local: Fanɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ yɛ Farasii abi, fan gyɛ kebunbun ansi wuya ana, fanɛ laakʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ fɛn natɛ nkyu sʋ yɛ ndɛ‐ndɛ sʋ, kɛ fɛn nyɛ kyɛɛgɛ nyɩmɩsa kʋlʋn ɔ ba sun Ɩbwaarɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fen sun mʋ. Amaa nɩɩ fan kan nyɛ mʋ mʋ, fɛn waa ɔsa mʋ ɔ dagaa awʋrʋfɔ fʋlɔn yen‐yen tɔ ɩlɩɩ ɩnyɔ fɛɛ fanɛ.

eng: How terrible it will be for you blind guides who say, If people swear by the temple, its nothing. But if people swear by the gold in the temple, they are obligated to do what they swore.
local: Fanɛ akaapʋpʋ mʋ nɩɩ fen du fɛɛ agyaatanapʋ nɩɩ fɛn gyankpaa asa mʋ, fanɛ laakʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ fɛn kaapʋ yɛ, Nɩɩ ɔkʋ taa kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ bɔ ntan mʋ, ɩ man gyɛ sɛhn, amaa nɩɩ ɔ kan taa kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ suwa bɔ ntan mʋ, ɩ dagaa maa buu ntan mʋ sʋ.

eng: You foolish and blind people! Which is greater, the gold or the temple that makes the gold holy?.
local: Fanɛ agyaatanapʋ melensipu! Suwa mʋ ɩ kyɔ abɛɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ? Nɩɩ man gyɛ suwa mʋ maa bʋ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ sʋ nɩɩ suwa mʋ biliŋi Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ abɛɛ?.

eng: You say, If people swear by the altar, its nothing. But if they swear by the gift on the altar, they are obligated to do what they swore.
local: Nɩɩ fan bɩla tɔwɛ yɛ, Nɩɩ ɔkʋ taa saraga kaswɩɩ bɔ ntan mʋ, ɩ man gyɛ sɛhn, amaa nɩɩ ɔ kan taa kakɛɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyan saraga kaswɩɩ mʋ sʋ bɔ ntan mʋ bɩrɛ, ɩ dagaa maa buu ntan mʋ sʋ.

eng: At that time the kingdom of heaven will be like ten young bridesmaids who took their lamps and went out to meet the groom.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu bɩla bɔ kɩŋasan kʋ yɛ, Kanɩn saŋa maŋa mʋ sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii mʋ ii biti ɩ baa i du nɩn fɛɛ, abite kudu mʋ nɩɩ ba taa bamʋ ɩfɩtɩla lɩɩ kyʋn ba naa gyaŋa ɔkyɩɩfɔ mu‐kuli.

eng: You blind people! Which is greater, the gift or the altar that makes the gift holy?.
local: Fanɛ agyaatanapʋ banɩmʋ! Kakɛɛ mʋ ɩ kyɔ abɛɛ saraga kaswɩɩ mʋ? Nɩɩ man gyɛ kakɛɛ mʋ maa gyan saraga kaswɩɩ mʋ sʋ nɩɩ kɩ ba biliŋi Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ abɛɛ?.

eng: Now five of them were wise, and the other five were foolish.
local: Bamʋ tɔ anu ba gyɛ amelensipu nɩɩ anu mɔɔ ba gyɛ anyaasɩnpʋ.

eng: Therefore, those who swear by the altar swear by it and by everything thats on it.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa saraga kaswɩɩ bɔ ntan mʋ, ɔ taa kaswɩɩ mʋ maa kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyan kɩmʋ sʋ mʋ bɔ ntan nɩmʋ.

eng: The foolish ones took their lamps but didnt bring oil for them.
local: Nɩɩ amelensipu anu mʋ ba maa dan taa bamʋ ɩfɩtɩla mʋ, ba man taa fɩtɩla nfɔ kɩtaa tii sʋ.

eng: Those who swear by the temple swear by it and by everything thats part of it.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ bɔ ntan mʋ, ɔ taa kɩmʋ yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ kɩmʋ tɔ mʋ bɔ ntan nɩmʋ.

eng: But the wise ones took their lamps and also brought containers of oil.
local: Amaa anyaasɩnpʋ anu mʋ bɩrɛ ba kɩtaa fɩtɩla nfɔ ɩpʋrʋntʋwa tɔ tii sʋ.

eng: Those who swear by heaven swear by Gods throne and by the one who sits on it.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa sʋsʋ bɔ ntan mʋ, ɔ taa sʋsʋ kuwura‐gya yɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛ kɩmʋ sʋ mʋ bɔ ntan nɩmʋ.

eng: When the groom was late in coming, they all became drowsy and went to sleep.
local: Ɔkyɩɩfɔ mu‐kuli mʋ maa man ba bilen sʋ mʋ, nsɩɩrɛ ɩ kɩtaa bamʋ, nɩɩ ba dɛ.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You give to God a tenth of mint, dill, and cumin, but you forget about the more important matters of the Law: justice, peace, and faith. You ought to give a tenth but without forgetting about those more important matters.
local: Fanɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ yɛ Farasii abi, fan gyɛ kebunbun ansi wuya ana, fanɛ laakʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ fɛn barɩgɛ fanɛ ɩpʋ atɔ, fɛɛ akatɛ, yɛ gaabu, yɛ akolonku ntun kudu‐kudu, nɩɩ fɛn taa ɩmʋ ketun kʋlʋn‐kʋlʋn fɛn sa Ɩbwaarɛ, amaa fɛn mɛɛ tʋwɛ asɩn kaapʋsa kparɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ nbara mʋ tɔ, fɛɛ kigyii asɩn ɩmʋ ɔkpa sʋ, kʋwɛɛ kuwu, yɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii danbɩrasa. Falɛ atɔ nɩmʋ ɩ dagaa fan maa waa, yɛgɛ fɛn taa ntun kudu sʋ kutun kʋlʋn mʋ fɛn sa Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: But at midnight there was a cry, Look, the groom! Come out to meet him.
local: Ɩ maa ba fʋʋ kiyiridi nsana mʋ, nɩɩ ɔkʋ kuusi tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔkyɩɩfɔ mu‐kuli mʋ ɩɩ ba! Fan lɩɩ ba gyaŋa mʋ.

eng: You blind guides! You filter out an ant but swallow a camel.
local: Fanɛ agyaatanapʋ agyankpaapʋ! Fɛn taalɛ fen wu kisusonbi kɛ fen fura mʋ fɔyɩ, amaa fɛn mɩyɛ nyɔɔma.

eng: Then all those bridesmaids got up and prepared their lamps.
local: Nɩɩ abite kudu mʋ kpini ba kʋsʋ diyaa bamʋ ɩfɩtɩla.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You clean the outside of the cup and plate, but inside they are full of violence and pleasure seeking.
local: Fanɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ yɛ Farasii abi, fan gyɛ kebunbun ansi wuya ana, fanɛ laakʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ fɛn fwɩɩ fanɛ ɩlapɛ yɛ nwɛ‐bi nmaa kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ, yɛgɛ ɩmʋ ɩtɔ tɔ ɩ gyɛ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ fan taa kensi payɩ libi yɛ kekisi bɔla ɩmʋ.

eng: But the foolish bridesmaids said to the wise ones, Give us some of your oil, because our lamps have gone out.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ amelensipu mʋ ba tɔwɛ anyaasɩnpʋ mʋ yɛ, Fan sa anɛ fanɛ nfɔ mʋ ɩkʋ, kpalɩ fɛɛ anɛ ɩfɩtɩla tɔ nfɔ ɩ lʋwɛ.

eng: Blind Pharisee! First clean the inside of the cup so that the outside of the cup will be clean too.
local: Fanɛ Farasii agyaatanapʋ! Fan gyankpaa fwɩɩ nwɛ‐bi yɛ ɩlapɛ mʋ ɩtɔ tɔ, kɛ ɩmʋ nmaa gbaa ɩ lɩɩ kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ.

eng: But the wise bridesmaids replied, No, because if we share with you, there wont be enough for our lamps and yours. We have a better idea. You go to those who sell oil and buy some for yourselves.
local: Nɩɩ anyaasɩnpʋ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ayee! Anɛ gbaa anɛ lɛɛ ɩ man kyɔ kɛ ɩ fʋʋ ana fanɛ. Amɔɔ, fen kpee bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ fɛɛ mʋ asɛ kɛ fan naa sɔɔ.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You are like whitewashed tombs. They look beautiful on the outside. But inside they are full of dead bones and all kinds of filth.
local: Fanɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ yɛ Farasii abi, fan gyɛ kebunbun ansi wuya ana, fanɛ laakʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ fan du nɩn fɛɛ nkyan mʋ nɩɩ ba pɔyɩ nɩɩ ba fuuli ɩmʋ sʋ parɛparɛ, nɩɩ ɩ bʋrɔn yɛgɛ ɩmʋ ɩtɔ tɔ bɩrɛ, abuni awuya yɛ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ gyɔɔ ɩ bɔla ɩmʋ tɔ.

eng: But while they were gone to buy oil, the groom came. Those who were ready went with him into the wedding. Then the door was shut.
local: Amaa ba maa kpee ba naa sɔɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩfɔ mu‐kuli mʋ ba. Nɩɩ anyaasɩnpʋ anu mʋ nɩɩ ba kʋʋla ba gyoo mʋ mʋ, ba nyɛ buu mʋ loo kɩkyɩɩfɔ kensi‐gyii kɩkpaara mʋ sʋ. Nɩɩ ba kɩtaa ɔpʋnʋ mʋ tii.

eng: In the same way you look righteous to people. But inside you are full of pretense and rebellion.
local: Kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ fan du. Asa ansi sʋ mʋ, fan gyɛ ɩkpa kyiigisa wuya ana, amaa fanɛ nkɔlɔ tɔ mʋ, fan gyɛ kebunbun ansi yɛ kumu tɔ ɔlʋn wuya ana nɩn.

eng: Later the other bridesmaids came and said, Lord, lord, open the door for us.
local: Kamaa tɔ abite amelensipu anu mʋ ba maa ba mʋ, ba tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔbɩlɩsa! Ɔbɩlɩsa! Tigi ɔpʋnʋ mʋ sa anɛ kɛ a loo.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You build tombs for the prophets and decorate the graves of the righteous.
local: Fanɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ yɛ Farasii abi, fan gyɛ kebunbun ansi wuya ana, fanɛ laakʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ fɛn pɔyɩ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ mʋ nkyan, nɩɩ fan lɔŋɔ ɩkpa kyiigisa wuya ana nkyan sʋ yɛ ɔdan.

eng: But he replied, I tell you the truth, I dont know you.
local: Amaa ɔ lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin fɛɛ, n man nyi fanɛ! .

eng: You say, If we had lived in our ancestors days, we wouldnt have joined them in killing the prophets.
local: Nɩɩ fɛn tɔwɛ yɛ, Nɩɩ an daa an bʋ tɔ anɛ naana ana saŋa mʋ nɩn, naafɔɔ a mɛɛ yɛ a tii bamʋ sʋ kɛ a mɔɔ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ mʋ.

eng: Therefore, keep alert, because you dont know the day or the hour.
local: Yesu lʋwɛ yɛ, Ɩmʋ sʋ fɛn baa fan da sʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ fan man nyi kakɛ abɛɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ n biti n ba.

eng: You testify against yourselves that you are children of those who murdered the prophets.
local: Fanɛ gbaa‐gbaa fanɛ ii gyii kesintin lɩɩ asɩn nɩmʋ sʋ yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba mɔɔ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ mʋ, bamʋ‐bii ana ɩ gyɛ fanɛ.

eng: The kingdom of heaven is like a man who was leaving on a trip. He called his servants and handed his possessions over to them.
local: Yesu bwii bɔ kɩŋasan kʋ yɛ, Sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii mʋ ii biti ɩ baa i du fɛɛ ɔnyɩn kʋ maa kpee ɔkpa, nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ mʋ ayaafɔlɛ taa mʋ kapatɩyɛ waa bamʋ abaa tɔ.

eng: Go ahead, complete what your ancestors did.
local: Kanɩn bɩrɛ fan tiisi lʋwɛ fanɛ naana ana alibi kusun mʋ.

eng: To one he gave five valuable coins, and to another he gave two, and to another he gave one. He gave to each servant according to that servants ability. Then he left on his journey.
local: Ɔ sa bamʋ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ ɔlʋn du. Ɔ sa ɔkʋ afulee ngbɩn nnuu, nɩɩ ɔ sa ɔkʋ mɔɔ afulee ngbɩn nnyɔ, lan sa ɔkʋ mɔɔ afulee kagbɩn kʋlʋn, nɩɩ ɔ kyʋn mʋ ɔkpa.

eng: You snakes! You children of snakes! How will you be able to escape the judgment of hell?.
local: Fanɛ anansɛ yɛ anansɛ bamʋ‐bii ana! Nɛnɛ nɩɩ fɛn taalɛ sɩlɛ lɩɩ kɩpwɛ kibun yɛ awʋrʋfɔ fʋlɔn yen‐yen nɩmʋ tɔ?.

eng: After the man left, the servant who had five valuable coins took them and went to work doing business with them. He gained five more.
local: Nɩɩ kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ afulee ngbɩn nnuu mʋ taa piili kusun opula tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ agyaga ngbɩn nnuu tii sʋ.

eng: Therefore, look, Im sending you prophets, wise people, and legal experts. Some of them you will kill and crucify. And some you will beat in your synagogues and chase from city to city.
local: Kanɩn sʋ, n biti n sun Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ yɛ anyaasɩnpʋ yɛ akaapʋpʋ fanɛ asɛ. Amaa fen biti fan mɔɔ bamʋ akʋ, kɛ fan da akʋ mata iyu laasa sʋ, kɛ fan dayɩ akʋ fanɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa, kɛ fan gya bamʋ lɩɩ ndɛ sʋ kpee ndɛ sʋ.

eng: In the same way, the one who had two valuable coins gained two more.
local: Kanɩn nɩn, nɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ ngbɩn nnyɔ mʋ gbaa taa waa kusun, nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ ngbɩn nnyɔ agyaga tii sʋ.

eng: Therefore, upon you will come all the righteous blood that has been poured out on the earth, from the blood of that righteous man Abel to the blood of Zechariah the son of Barachiah, whom you killed between the temple and the altar.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ lɩɩ ɩkpa kyiigisa wuya ana mʋ nɩɩ fanɛ asa ba kyɩna mɔɔ mʋ kpini, ii biti ɩ ba fanɛ sʋ. Lɩɩ ɩkpa kyiigisa wuya Abeli naa fʋʋ Barakiya mu‐bii Sakariya mʋ nɩɩ fanɛ Gyiwu abi ba daa mɔɔ mʋ, kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ maa saraga kaswɩɩ nsana mʋ.

eng: But the servant who had received the one valuable coin dug a hole in the ground and buried his masters money.
local: Amaa kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ kagbɩn kʋlʋn mʋ bɩrɛ, taa mʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ afulee mʋ naa kuu pula.

eng: I assure you that all these things will come upon this generation.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin fɛɛ, ɩnɩmʋ kpini kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ ii biti ɩ ba ndaga kakyɩna nɩmʋ tɔ asa sʋ.

eng: Now after a long time the master of those servants returned and settled accounts with them.
local: Ɩ maa kyɛɛrɛɛ kamaa tɔ mʋ, bamʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ mʋ naa bwii ba, nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ bamʋ yɛ ba ba kaapʋ tɔ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba nyɛ agyaga tii afulee mʋ sʋ.

eng: Jerusalem, Jerusalem! You who kill the prophets and stone those who were sent to you. How often I wanted to gather your people together, just as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you didnt want that.
local: Oo! Gyerusalem asa! Fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fɛn mɔɔ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ, nɩɩ fɛn tʋʋ abuu fɛn mɔɔ bamʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sun fanɛ asɛ mʋ. N kpan mɛ n‐yɩɩ saŋa gaalaagaa fɛɛ n bʋga fanɛ‐bii ana, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ kakyaasɛ‐nyi ɩɩ bʋga mu‐bii ana ɛɛ waa mʋ atɛɛ tɔ mʋ, amaa fen mee kyula.

eng: The one who had received five valuable coins came forward with five additional coins. He said, Master, you gave me five valuable coins. Look, Ive gained five more.
local: Kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sa mu afulee ngbɩn nnuu mʋ loo ba, nɩɩ ɔ taa ngbɩn nnuu tii sʋ sa mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Mɛ‐nyɩrɩpɛ, fʋ taa ngbɩn nnuu nɩn sa mɛ. Kɩɩ, n nyɛ agyaga ngbɩn nnuu tii sʋ.

eng: Look, your house is left to you deserted.
local: Fanɛ kɩɩ, Ɩbwaarɛ lɩɩ fanɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ki biliŋi kɩtakpan.

eng: His master replied, Excellent! You are a good and faithful servant! Youve been faithful over a little. Ill put you in charge of much. Come, celebrate with me.
local: Mʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ mʋ yɩn mʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, Fʋ waa kusun, fʋ gyɛ kɩyaafɔlɛ danbɩrasa yɛ kesintin gyipu. Fʋ gyii kesintin lɩɩ atɔ pii anɩmʋ sʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ n biti n taa atɔ damantɛ waa fʋ abaa tɔ kɛ fʋ kɩɩ sʋ. Ba kɛ a bʋga gyii kensi‐gyii mʋ nɩɩ n gyii mʋ.

eng: I tell you, you wont see me until you say, Blessings on the one who comes in the Lords name .
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ nɩn yɛ fan man bɩla yɛ fan wu mɛ naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ fen biti fan tɔwɛ yɛ, Kusee ɩ gyɛ sa ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ ba Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kɩtɩɩ tɔ mʋ. .

eng: The second servant also came forward and said, Master, you gave me two valuable coins. Look, Ive gained two more.
local: Nɩɩ kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sa mʋ ngbɩn nnyɔ mʋ gbaa ba, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Mɛ‐nyɩrɩpɛ, fʋ taa ngbɩn nnyɔ nɩɩ waa mɛ abaa tɔ, kɩɩ, n nyɛ agyaga ngbɩn nnyɔ tii sʋ.

eng: His master replied, Well done! You are a good and faithful servant. Youve been faithful over a little. Ill put you in charge of much. Come, celebrate with me.
local: Mʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ mʋ yɩn mʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, Fʋ waa kusun, fʋ gyɛ kɩyaafɔlɛ danbɩrasa yɛ kesintin gyipu. Fʋ gyii kesintin lɩɩ atɔ pii anɩmʋ sʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ n biti n taa atɔ damantɛ waa fʋ abaa tɔ kɛ fʋ kɩɩ sʋ. Ba kɛ a bʋga gyii kensi‐gyii mʋ nɩɩ n gyii mʋ.

eng: Now the one who had received one valuable coin came and said, Master, I knew that you are a hard man. You harvest grain where you havent sown. You gather crops where you havent spread seed.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ kagbɩn kʋlʋn mʋ ba, nɩɩ ɔ yɛ, Mɛ‐nyɩrɩpɛ, n nyi fʋ asɩn ɩ maa bʋlʋn, nɩɩ fɩɩ tɩŋɛ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ fʋ man duu, nɩɩ fɩɩ kʋʋla fɩɩ waa ɩbɔɔta tɔ, ɩmʋ nɩɩ fʋ man kyaalɛ.

eng: So I was afraid. And I hid my valuable coin in the ground. Here, you have whats yours.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ n sʋ kufuu nɩɩ n taa afulee mʋ nɩɩ fʋ taa waa mɛ abaa tɔ mʋ, naa kuu ɩmʋ pula. Kɩɩ, fu afulee ndee.

eng: His master replied, You evil and lazy servant! You knew that I harvest grain where I havent sown and that I gather crops where I havent spread seed?.
local: Nɩɩ mʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ mʋ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Fʋ gyɛ kɩyaafɔlɛ libi yɛ ɔwʋlɛgyɔpʋ. Fu nyi n maa tɩŋɛ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ n man duu, nɩɩ n kʋʋla n waa ɩbɔɔta tɔ, katɩn mʋ nɩɩ n man kyaalɛ.

eng: In that case, you should have turned my money over to the bankers so that when I returned, you could give me what belonged to me with interest.
local: Kanɩn bɩrɛ, naafɔɔ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ fʋ taa mɛ afulee mʋ naa yɩla afulee ɔyɩlɛkpa, kɛ n maa bwii ba falɛ mʋ, n nyɛ mɛ afulee kibi yɛ agyaga tii sʋ.

eng: Therefore, take from him the valuable coin and give it to the one who has ten coins.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ tɔwɛ mʋ ayaafɔlɛ mʋ yɛ, Fan kɔɔlɛ afulee kagbɩn kʋlʋn mʋ lɩɩ mʋ asɛ, kɛ fɛn taa sa ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ ngbɩn kudu mʋ.

eng: Those who have much will receive more, and they will have more than they need. But as for those who dont have much, even the little bit they have will be taken away from them.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ mʋ, bee biti ba sa mʋ tii sʋ, kɛ ɔ nyɛ naa bansʋ, amaa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man sʋ, nɩɩ halɩɩ gbɛrɛɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ gbaa mʋ, bee biti ba kɔɔlɛ lɩɩ mʋ asɛ.

eng: Now take the worthless servant and throw him out into the farthest darkness. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: Fan taa kɩyaafɔlɛ gyaga mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man sʋ kyaarɛ falɛ, naa tʋʋ kitentenbiri tumm mʋ tɔ. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ sawʋ kɛ o duŋi mʋ anobi. .

eng: Now when the Human One comes in his majesty and all his angels are with him, he will sit on his majestic throne.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu bɩla tɔwɛ yɛ, Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa n biti n ba yɛ mɛ bʋnyaa, maa sʋsʋ nbɔɔ kpini mʋ, n biti n kyɩna mɛ kuwura‐gya mʋ sʋ bʋnyaa tɔ.

eng: All the nations will be gathered in front of him. He will separate them from each other, just as a shepherd separates the sheep from the goats.
local: N biti ɩ bɩya ndɛndɛmandɛ kpini ba gyaŋɛ mi ansi tɔ, nɩɩ n biti n barɩgɛ asa mʋ tɔ ntun nnyɔ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ atɔbwaaya ɔkpapʋ ɩɩ barɩgɛ nbʋlʋpʋ ɛɛ lɩɩ nbwɛ tɔ mʋ.

eng: He will put the sheep on his right side. But the goats he will put on his left.
local: N biti n taa nbʋlʋpʋ yɩlɛ mɛ gyisa sʋ, kɛ n taa nbwɛ mɔɔ yɩlɛ mɛ bɩna sʋ.

eng: Then the king will say to those on his right, Come, you who will receive good things from my Father. Inherit the kingdom that was prepared for you before the world began.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ mɛ, mʋ nɩɩ n gyɛ Owura mʋ n biti n tɔwɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba yɩlɛ mɛ gyisa sʋ mʋ yɛ, Fanɛ ɩ gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ mɛ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ waa kusee mʋ, fan ba kuwura‐gyii mʋ nɩɩ ɔ yɩla sa fanɛ lɩɩ koo saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tʋʋ dulinyaa mʋ.

eng: I was hungry and you gave me food to eat. I was thirsty and you gave me a drink. I was a stranger and you welcomed me.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ akʋn ɩ maa sʋ mɛ mʋ, fanɛ ɩ sa mɛ agyitɔ nɩɩ n gyii, bʋlɩfɔ maa sʋ mɛ mʋ, fanɛ ɩ sa mɛ nkyu nɩɩ n nuu, n maa gyɛ ɔfɔ mʋ, fanɛ ɩ kɔɔlɛ mɛ fanɛ ayɛ.

eng: I was naked and you gave me clothes to wear. I was sick and you took care of me. I was in prison and you visited me.
local: N maa man sʋ atɔ bunsa mʋ, fanɛ ɩ sa mɛ nɩɩ n bun, n maa lɔ mʋ, fanɛ ɩ kɩɩ mɛ sʋ, n maa bʋ obu tiisa tɔ mʋ, fanɛ ɩ bɔɔ mɛ sʋ.

eng: Then those who are righteous will reply to him, Lord, when did we see you hungry and feed you, or thirsty and give you a drink?.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ bamʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi mʋ bee biti ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, saŋa mɔɔ nɩɩ an wu fʋ akʋn ɩ maa sʋ fʋ, nɩɩ an sa fʋ agyitɔ nɩɩ fu gyii, abɛɛ bʋlɩfɔ maa sʋ fʋ, nɩɩ an sa fʋ nkyu nɩɩ fʋ nuu?.

eng: When did we see you as a stranger and welcome you, or naked and give you clothes to wear?.
local: Saŋa mɔɔ nɩɩ an wu fʋ maa gyɛ ɔfɔ, nɩɩ an kɔɔlɛ fʋ ba anɛ ayɛ, abɛɛ atɔ bunsa ɩ maa tiri, nɩɩ an sa fʋ nɩɩ fʋ bun?.

eng: When did we see you sick or in prison and visit you.
local: Saŋa mɔɔ nɩɩ an wu fʋ maa lɔ, abɛɛ fʋ maa tii obu tiisa tɔ nɩɩ an naa bɔɔ fʋ sʋ.

eng: Then the king will reply to them, I assure you that when you have done it for one of the least of these brothers and sisters of mine, you have done it for me.
local: Nɩɩ mɛ, mʋ nɩɩ n gyɛ Owura mʋ, n biti n lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin fɛɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fan waa sa mɛ asa, halɩɩ bamʋ tɔ opii gbaa mʋ, mɛ nɩɩ fan waa sa.

eng: Then he will say to those on his left, Get away from me, you who will receive terrible things. Go into the unending fire that has been prepared for the devil and his angels.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ n biti n tɔwɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba yɩlɛ mɛ bɩna sʋ mʋ yɛ, Fan taa lɩɩ mɛ sʋ, fanɛ nɩɩ mɛ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ nnɔ waa mʋ. Fan natɛ loo awʋrʋfɔ fʋlɔn yen‐yen mʋ nɩɩ ii mee dun, nɩɩ mɛ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ dɛɛla yɩla Sɩtaanɛ maa mʋ nbɔɔ mʋ tɔ.

eng: I was hungry and you didnt give me food to eat. I was thirsty and you didnt give me anything to drink.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ akʋn ɩ maa sʋ mɛ mʋ, fan man sa mɛ agyitɔ nɩɩ n gyii, bʋlɩfɔ maa sʋ mɛ mʋ, fan man sa mɛ nkyu nɩɩ n nuu.

eng: I was a stranger and you didnt welcome me. I was naked and you didnt give me clothes to wear. I was sick and in prison, and you didnt visit me.
local: N maa gyɛ ɔfɔ mʋ, fan man kɔɔlɛ mɛ fanɛ ayɛ, atɔ bunsa ɩ maa tiri mɛ mʋ, fan man sa mɛ nɩɩ n bun, n maa lɔ, abɛɛ n maa bʋ obu tii tɔ mʋ, fan man ba bɔɔ mɛ sʋ.

eng: Then they will reply, Lord, when did we see you hungry or thirsty or a stranger or naked or sick or in prison and didnt do anything to help you.
local: Ngbaa bamʋ bee biti ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, saŋa mɔɔ nɩɩ an wu akʋn ɩ maa sʋ fʋ, abɛɛ bʋlɩfɔ maa sʋ fʋ, abɛɛ fʋ maa gyɛ ɔfɔ, abɛɛ atɔ bunsa ɩ maa tiri fʋ, abɛɛ fʋ maa lɔ, abɛɛ fʋ maa bʋ obu tii tɔ, nɩɩ an man kyɛ fʋ tɔ.

eng: Then he will answer, I assure you that when you havent done it for one of the least of these, you havent done it for me.
local: Nɩɩ mɛ, mʋ nɩɩ n gyɛ Owura mʋ n biti n lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin nɩn fɛɛ, kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fan kina kɩwaa sa mɛ asa, halɩɩ bamʋ tɔ opii gbaa mʋ, mɛ nɩɩ fan kina kɩwaa sa.

eng: And they will go away into eternal punishment. But the righteous ones will go into eternal life.
local: Tʋtɔ mʋ, bee biti ba naa loo kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ kakpaa tɔ, amaa ɩkpa kyiigisa wuya ana bɩrɛ bee biti ba kyɩna nkpa kakpaa tɔ.

eng: When Jesus finished speaking all these words, he said to his disciples.
local: Yesu maa tɔwɛ ɩnɩmʋ kpini lʋwɛ mʋ, ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ.

eng: You know that the Passover is two days from now. And the Human One will be handed over to be crucified.
local: Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fen nyi mʋ, ɩ san nkɛ nnyɔ kɛ kanyaŋɛ nbunɔ kensi kigyii saŋa mʋ ɔ fʋʋ. Saŋa maa nɩɩ bee biti ba lɛɛ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ sa, kɛ ba da mɛ mata oyu laasa sʋ.

eng: Then the chief priests and elders of the people gathered in the courtyard of Caiaphas the high priest.
local: Kanɩn saŋa maŋa nɩɩ aseepu bɩlɩsa yɛ asa mʋ abɩlɩsa ba naa sarɛ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ Kayifasi mʋ lenbu tɔ.

eng: They were plotting to arrest Jesus by cunning tricks and to kill him.
local: naa ŋmina kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba taa kanyaasɩn ɔkpa sʋ kɩtaa Yesu kɛ ba mɔɔ mʋ.

eng: But they agreed that it shouldnt happen during the feast so there wouldnt be an uproar among the people.
local: Amaa ba tɔwɛ yɛ, A man waa kanɩn kensi‐gyii saŋa nɩmʋ tɔ, nɩɩ man gyɛ kanɩn mʋ, sʋgʋrɛ‐sʋgʋrɛ ii biti ɔ ba kadɛ mʋ tɔ.

eng: When Jesus was at Bethany visiting the house of Simon, who had a skin disease.
local: Yesu maa kyɩna ɔ bʋ Betani ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Simon, nɩɩ ɔ kyɩna ɔ gyɛ bootipu mʋ ayɛ mʋ.

eng: a woman came to him with a vase made of alabaster containing very expensive perfume. She poured it on Jesus head while he was sitting at dinner.
local: nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ kʋ taa tulale mʋ nɩɩ kɩ sʋ ɔgyalɩn pʋrʋntʋwa bɔla, ba mʋ asɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛ ee gyii mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ kyɛɛgɛ tulale mʋ wʋrɩgɛ Yesu kumu tɔ.

eng: Now when the disciples saw it they were angry and said, Why this waste?.
local: Yesu abɩɩlapʋ ba maa wu kanɩn mʋ, ɩ basa bamʋ nɩɩ ba taasɛ yɛ, Mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ ii nyita tulale maa kanɩn?.

eng: This perfume could have been sold for a lot of money and given to the poor.
local: Naafɔɔ bɛ taalɛ fɛ tulale nɩmʋ nyɛ afulee damantɛ kɛ ba taa ɩmʋ sa atiripu.

eng: But Jesus knew what they were thinking. He said, Why do you make trouble for the woman? Shes done a good thing for me.
local: Yesu maa bɩɩ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fɛn tɔɔrɔɔ ɔkyɩɩ maŋa? Kʋtɔ danbɩrasa nɩɩ ɔ waa sa mɛ falɛ.

eng: You always have the poor with you, but you wont always have me.
local: Atiripu mʋ bɩrɛ ba bʋ fanɛ asɛ saŋa kʋmaa, amaa mɛɛmɛ bɩrɛ n mɛɛ yɛ n kyɩna fanɛ asɛ saŋa kʋmaa.

eng: By pouring this perfume over my body shes prepared me to be buried.
local: Maa kyɛɛgɛ tulale nɩmʋ wʋrɩgɛ mɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ mʋ, ɛɛ lɔŋɔ mɛ n‐yɩɩ ɛɛ yɩla mi kepula.

eng: I tell you the truth that wherever in the whole world this good news is announced, what shes done will also be told in memory of her.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin nɩn fɛɛ, dulinyaa tɔ katɩn kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ, bee biti ba tɔwɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa falɛ taa nyiŋi mʋ.

eng: Early in the morning all the chief priests and the elders of the people reached the decision to have Jesus put to death.
local: Kayɩ maa kɛ nyenyenfuu mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu abɩlɩsa mʋ kpini yɛ asa mʋ abɩlɩsa mʋ ba bʋga ŋmina yɛ ba mɔɔ Yesu.

eng: Then one of the Twelve, who was called Judas Iscariot, went to the chief priests.
local: Nɩɩ abɩɩlapʋ kudu anyɔ mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ, mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ Gyudasi Isikariyoti mʋ kpee aseepu abɩlɩsa mʋ asɛ.

eng: They bound him, led him away, and turned him over to Pilate the governor.
local: Ba ŋmina mʋ, nɩɩ ba taa mʋ kyʋn, nɩɩ ba taa mʋ waa ɔsʋwʋlɛ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ Paleti abaa tɔ.

eng: and said, What will you give me if I turn Jesus over to you? They paid him thirty pieces of silver.
local: naa taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, N kan lɛɛ mʋ sa fanɛ mʋ, mɩnɛ nɩɩ fen biti fan sa mɛ? Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba karagɛ afulee nderebi adɩsa sa mʋ.

eng: When Judas, who betrayed Jesus, saw that Jesus was condemned to die, he felt deep regret. He returned the thirty pieces of silver to the chief priests and elders, and.
local: Gyudasi mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ mʋ sa mʋ, maa wu fɛɛ ba bun Yesu lewu kʋpwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ o nu mʋ n‐yɩɩ, nɩɩ o bwiiyaa gbiti nderebi adɩsa mʋ naa sa Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu abɩlɩsa yɛ kadɛ abɩlɩsa mʋ.

eng: From that time on he was looking for an opportunity to turn him in.
local: Lɩɩ kanɩn saŋa maŋa taa kyʋn mʋ, Gyudasi piili ee biti ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bɔla sʋ lɛɛ Yesu sa.

eng: said, I did wrong because I betrayed an innocent man. But they said, What is that to us? Thats your problem.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, N waa alibi fɛɛ n maa lɛɛ ɔmʋnɩ ɔ man waa alibi akʋ mʋ sa fanɛ. Nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ man gyɛ anɛ asɩn. Fʋ nkʋn asɩn nɩn.

eng: On the first day of the Festival of Unleavened Bread, the disciples came to Jesus and said, Where do you want us to prepare for you to eat the Passover meal.
local: Bodobodo mʋ nɩɩ kɩ man sʋ kɩfɛ tinsa kigyii kakɛ gyankpaasa mʋ, Yesu abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba ba mʋ asɛ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Fɩnɛ nɩɩ fii biti fɛɛ a naa lɔŋɔ yɩla, kɛ a naa gyii Kanyaŋɛ nbunɔ kesu kensi‐gyii mʋ.

eng: Judas threw the silver pieces into the temple and left. Then he went and hanged himself.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ Gyudasi funti afulee mʋ waa kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kyʋn. Nɩɩ ɔ naa kyaga mʋ n‐yɩɩ ɔfɛ.

eng: He replied, Go into the city, to a certain man, and say, The teacher says,My time is near. Im going to celebrate the Passover with my disciples at your house.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan kpee kadɛ mʋ tɔ ɔnyɩn kʋ asɛ naa tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɔkaapʋpʋ mʋ yɛ, Mɛ saŋa tiri tɔ. N biti fɛɛ n maa mɛ abɩɩlapʋ an biti a ba gyii Kanyaŋɛ nbunɔ kensi‐gyii mʋ fʋ ayɛ. .

eng: The chief priests picked up the silver pieces and said, According to the Law its not right to put this money in the treasury. Since it was used to pay for someones life, its unclean.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu abɩlɩsa mʋ ba tasɛ nderebi mʋ, nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, Nbara mʋ ɩ mɛɛ sa ɔkpa fɛɛ a taa afulee nɩmʋ bʋga yɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ afulee mʋ tɔ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɩ gyɛ nkalan afulee nɩn.

eng: The disciples did just as Jesus instructed them. They prepared the Passover.
local: Nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba kpee naa waa kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ba waa Kanyaŋɛ nbunɔ kensi‐gyii mʋ agyitɔ mʋ yɩla.

eng: So they decided to use it to buy the potters field where strangers could be buried.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ba tɔwɛ kyula abaa nɩɩ ba taa afulee maŋa naa sɔɔ nlobi apɔyɩpʋ kʋ kasɛ, kɛ ba baa bee pula afɔ‐kyilim tʋtɔ.

eng: That evening he took his place at the table with the twelve disciples.
local: Ɩ maa fʋʋ kalaatɩpwɛ sʋ mʋ, Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ kudu anyɔ mʋ, ba tɛ bee gyii.

eng: Thats why that field is called Field of Blood to this very day.
local: Kanɩn sʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ tʋtɔ yɛ, Nkalan kasɛ ba lɩɩ halɩɩ ndaga.

eng: As they were eating he said, I assure you that one of you will betray me.
local: Ba maa gyii mʋ nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin fɛɛ, fanɛ tɔ ɔkʋ ii biti ɔ lɛɛ mɛ sa.

eng: This fulfilled the words of Jeremiah the prophet: And I took the thirty pieces of silver, the price for the one whose price had been set by some of the Israelites.
local: Ɩmʋ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Gyeremiya tɔwɛ mʋ ba tɔ. Ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, Ɩsɩrayɩ abi ba taa gbiti nderebi adɩsa mʋ, ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ afulee mʋ nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ kyula yɛ ba taa sɔɔ mʋ mʋ, kɔnɔ.

eng: Deeply saddened, each one said to him, Im not the one, am I, Lord.
local: Nɩɩ bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ tɔ ii nyita bamʋ gaa, nɩɩ ba piili bɛɛ taasɛ mʋ kʋkʋlʋn‐kʋkʋlʋn yɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, kesintin tɔ, man gyɛ mɛ nɩɩ fʋ dɛɛ.

eng: and I gave them for the potters field, as the Lord commanded me .
local: Nɩɩ ba taa ɩmʋ sɔɔ nlobi apɔyɩpʋ kʋ kasɛ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ sa kɔnɔ mʋ.

eng: He replied, The one who will betray me is the one who dips his hand with me into this bowl.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɔmʋ nɩɩ maa mʋ abaa ɩ bʋga ii loo lapɛ kʋlʋn tɔ mʋ, ii biti ɔ lɛɛ mɛ sa.

eng: Jesus was brought before the governor. The governor said, Are you the king of the Jews? Jesus replied, Thats what you say.
local: Saŋa maŋa yɛgɛ Yesu yɩlɛ ɔsʋwʋlɛ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ Paleti ansi tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Fʋ ɩ gyɛ Gyiwu abi owura lala mʋ? Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɛɛhn, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fʋ tɔwɛ mʋ.

eng: The Human One goes to his death just as it is written about him. But how terrible it is for that person who betrays the Human One! It would have been better for him if he had never been born.
local: Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ ɩ tɔwɛ yɛ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa n biti n wu mʋ, kanɩn nɩɩ ii biti ɩ waa. Amaa kanɩn nyɩmɩsa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ lɛɛ mɛ sa mʋ, laakʋ. Nɩɩ ba man da kʋʋgɛ mʋ gbaa nɩn, naafɔɔ ɩ bɔ.

eng: But he didnt answer when the chief priests and elders accused him.
local: Amaa saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu abɩlɩsa yɛ kadɛ mʋ abɩlɩsa bɛɛ pwɩɩ mʋ mʋ, ɔ man lɛɛ kɔnɔ.

eng: Now Judas, who would betray him, replied, Its not me, is it, Rabbi? Jesus answered, You said it.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Gyudasi mʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ lɛɛ mʋ sa mʋ gbaa taasɛ Yesu yɛ, Ɔkaapʋpʋ mɛ nɩɩ fʋ dɛɛ? Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɛɛhn, kanɩn nɩɩ fʋ tɔwɛ maa.

eng: Then Pilate said, Dont you hear the testimony they bring against you.
local: Nɩɩ Paleti taasɛ yɛ, Fʋ man nu asɩn pwɩɩsa mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ bɛɛ pwɩɩ fʋ mʋ.

eng: While they were eating, Jesus took bread, blessed it, broke it, and gave it to the disciples and said, Take and eat. This is my body.
local: Ba maa gyii mʋ Yesu taa bodobodo, nɩɩ ɔ fwaala Ɩbwaarɛ nɩɩ ɔ gyayɩ kɩmʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ taa sa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, Fan kɔɔlɛ kɛ fan gyii, kɩnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ mɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ mʋ.

eng: But he didnt answer, not even a single word. So the governor was greatly amazed.
local: Amaa Yesu man lɛɛ kɔnɔ lɩɩ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ sʋ bɛɛ pwɩɩ mʋ mʋ tɔ kʋkʋlʋn gbaa. Nɩɩ i kyinkyin Paleti gaa.

eng: He took a cup, gave thanks, and gave it to them, saying, Drink from this, all of you.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ taa kɔɔpʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ fwaala Ɩbwaarɛ, nɩɩ ɔ taa sa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, Fanɛ kpini fan kɔɔlɛ kɛ fan nuu lɩɩ kɩmʋ tɔ.

eng: It was customary during the festival for the governor to release to the crowd one prisoner, whomever they might choose.
local: Nɩɩ ɩ wʋla ɩ dɛ fɛɛ, kesu kɔnɔ kanyaŋɛ nbunɔ kensi‐gyii kʋmaa nɩmʋ tɔ mʋ, Roma abi ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ mʋ, ɩɩ lɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba tii bamʋ obu mʋ tɔ ɔkʋlʋn mʋ nɩɩ sakyɔ mʋ ii biti mʋ sa bamʋ.

eng: This is my blood of the covenant, which is poured out for many so that their sins may be forgiven.
local: Kɩnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ mɛ nkalan mʋ nɩɩ ki ŋmina Ɩbwaarɛ katɔwɛ kyula pʋpwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɩ kyɛɛgɛ wʋrɩgɛ sa asa gaalaagaa alibi kɩtaa kɛɛ sʋ.

eng: At that time there was a well-known prisoner named Jesus Barabbas.
local: Kanɩn saŋa maŋa mʋ, kumu tɔ ɔlʋn wuya kʋ kyɩna o tii obu nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Barabasi.

eng: I tell you, I wont drink wine again until that day when I drink it in a new way with you in my Fathers kingdom.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ nɩn yɛ, n man bɩla yɛ n nuu falɛ nta nɩmʋ naa fʋʋ kakɛ mʋ nɩɩ n maa fanɛ an biti a nuu nta pʋpwɛ mɛ‐Sɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ mʋ.

eng: When the crowd had come together, Pilate asked them, Whom would you like me to release to you, Jesus Barabbas or Jesus who is called Christ.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ sakyɔ mʋ ba maa gyaŋɛ mʋ, Paleti taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Anɩmʋ nɩɩ fen biti n lɛɛ sa fanɛ? Barabasi, abɛɛ Yesu mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ Krisito mʋ.

eng: Then, after singing songs of praise, they went to the Mount of Olives.
local: Nɩɩ ba maa bɩɩ ɔlʋn lʋwɛ mʋ, ba kʋsʋ kyʋn olifu kɩbɩɩ sʋ.

eng: He knew that the leaders of the people had handed him over because of jealousy.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ o nyi ɩ maa gyɛ kayɩɩ basa sʋ, nɩɩ ba taa Yesu waa mʋ abaa tɔ.

eng: Then Jesus said to his disciples, Tonight you will all fall away because of me. This is because it is written, I will hit the shepherd, and the sheep of the flock will go off in all directions .
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Kagyanbwɛ nɩmʋ fanɛ kpini fen biti fan sɩlɛ yɛgɛ mɛ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ yɛ, N biti n mɔɔ nbʋlʋpʋ ɔkpapʋ mʋ, kɛ nbʋlʋpʋ mʋ ba yaasɛ tɔ.

eng: While he was serving as judge, his wife sent this message to him, Leave that righteous man alone. Ive suffered much today in a dream because of him.
local: Paleti maa san ɔ tɛ asɩn kigyii kagya sʋ mʋ, mʋ‐ka sun ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Man waa ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ, mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man waa alibi akʋ kʋtɔ kʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ n wu awʋrʋfɔ mɛ idee tɔ ndaga gaa lɩɩ mʋ sʋ.

eng: But after Im raised up, Ill go before you to Galilee.
local: Amaa nɩɩ n kyiŋi lɩɩ lewu tɔ mʋ, n biti n gyankpaa fanɛ kpee Galile.

eng: But the chief priests and the elders persuaded the crowds to ask for Barabbas and kill Jesus.
local: Amaa Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu bɩlɩsa yɛ kadɛ abɩlɩsa mʋ ba tɔwɛ waa sakyɔ mʋ yɛ, ba kʋlɛ yɛ bee biti Barabasi, kɛ ɔ yɛgɛ ba mɔɔ Yesu.

eng: Peter replied, If everyone else stumbles because of you, Ill never stumble.
local: Nɩɩ Pita tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Halɩɩ nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ sɩlɛ yɛgɛ fʋ gbaa, mɛɛ mɛ n mɛɛ yɛ n sɩlɛ yɛgɛ fʋ.

eng: The governor said, Which of the two do you want me to release to you? Barabbas, they replied.
local: Nɩɩ Ɔsʋwʋlɛ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Bamʋ anyɔ nɩmʋ tɔ mʋ, ɔmɔɔ nɩɩ fen biti fɛɛ n lɛɛ mʋ sa fanɛ? Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Barabasi.

eng: Jesus said to him, I assure you that, before the rooster crows tonight, you will deny me three times.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ mʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, N tɔwɛ fʋ kesintin yɛ, kagyanbwɛ nɩmʋ pɔyɩ kɛ kyaasɛ ɔ bʋn mʋ, fii biti fu kina mɛ ɩlɩɩ ɩsa.

eng: Pilate said, Then what should I do with Jesus who is called Christ? They all said, Crucify him.
local: Nɩɩ Paleti taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Mɩnɛ nɩɩ n waa Yesu mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Krisito mʋ? Nɩɩ bamʋ kpini ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Da mʋ mata oyu laasa sʋ.

eng: Peter said, Even if I must die alongside you, I wont deny you. All the disciples said the same thing.
local: Nɩɩ Pita lɛɛ mʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Halɩɩ bee biti ba mɔɔ n maa fʋ gbaa n mɛɛ yɛ n tɔwɛ kanɩn kpini‐kpini. Nɩɩ abɩɩlapʋ abanban mʋ gbaa ba tɔwɛ kanɩn.

eng: But he said, Why? What wrong has he done? They shouted even louder, Crucify him.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ bɩla taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Mɩnɛ sʋ? Mɩnɛ alibi nɩɩ ɔ waa? Amaa ba lɔŋɔ kuusi kenken tii sʋ yɛ, Da mʋ mata oyu laasa sʋ.

eng: Then Jesus went with his disciples to a place called Gethsemane. He said to the disciples, Stay here while I go and pray over there.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba kpee katɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ Gesemani, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan kyɩna nfɩɩ kɛ n kpee falɛ naa kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ.

eng: Pilate saw that he was getting nowhere and that a riot was starting. So he took water and washed his hands in front of the crowd. Im innocent of this mans blood, he said. Its your problem.
local: Paleti maa wu fɛɛ ɔ mɛɛ bɩla taalɛ waa asɩn mʋ sɛhn mʋ, yɛgɛ ɩ sʋ sʋgʋrɛ‐sʋgʋrɛ ɩɩ ba mʋ, ɔ taa nkyu fwɩɩ mʋ abaa sakyɔ mu ansi tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Mɛ abaa ɩ man tii kanyɩn nɩmʋ lewu sʋ, ɩ gyɛ fanɛ asɩn nɩn.

eng: When he took Peter and Zebedees two sons, he began to feel sad and anxious.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ taa Pita maa Sebedi mu‐bii ana anyɔ buu mʋ n‐yɩɩ kpee. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ mʋ kɔkɔlɔ nyita mʋ.

eng: All the people replied, Let his blood be on us and on our children.
local: Asa mʋ kpini ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Yɛgɛ mu lewu kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ ɩ baa ɩ gyan anɛ yɛ anɛ‐bii ana sʋ.

eng: Then he said to them, Im very sad. Its as if Im dying. Stay here and keep alert with me.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Mɛ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ ɩɩ kɔ mɛ lewu‐lewu, halɩɩ ii biti ɩ mɔɔ mɛ. Ɩmʋ sʋ fan kyɩna nfɩɩ tii mɛ sʋ kɛ fan man dɛ.

eng: Then he released Barabbas to them. He had Jesus whipped, then handed him over to be crucified.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ taa Barabasi sa bamʋ. Amaa ɔ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ba tɩŋɛ Yesu awulibi, nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ sa yɛ ba naa da mʋ mata oyu laasa sʋ.

eng: Then he went a short distance farther and fell on his face and prayed, My Father, if its possible, take this cup of suffering away from me. However—not what I want but what you want.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ o nin kpee ansi tɔ gbɛrɛɛ naa kyʋn bun ɛɛ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ yɛ, Mɛ‐Sɛ, nɩɩ ii biti ɩ waa mʋ, lɛɛ awʋrʋfɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ ba mɛ sʋ falɛ lɩɩ mɛ sʋ. Amaa yɛgɛ ɩ waa fu kebiti, man gyɛ mi kebiti.

eng: The governors soldiers took Jesus into the governors house, and they gathered the whole company of soldiers around him.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ mʋ anaakɔpʋ ba taa Yesu loo bamʋ opula, nɩɩ ba tɩɩ anaakɔpʋ mʋ kpini ba kyaabɔɔ Yesu.

eng: He came back to the disciples and found them sleeping. He said to Peter, Couldnt you stay alert one hour with me?.
local: Nɩɩ o bwii ba abɩɩlapʋ asa mʋ asɛ ba tu ba maa dɛ nsɩɩrɛ, nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ Pita yɛ, Amɔɔ fɛn mɛɛ taalɛ kyɩna yɛ mɛ kerefi kʋlʋn yɛgɛ fan man dɛ?.

eng: They stripped him and put a red military coat on him.
local: Ba bugi mʋ kaalɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ba taa kaalɛ pɩpɛɛ bʋnswɩɩsa kʋ ba bun mʋ.

eng: Stay alert and pray so that you wont give in to temptation. The spirit is eager, but the flesh is weak.
local: Fan kyɩna fan man dɛ, yɛgɛ fan kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ, kɛ fan man tɩyɛ kʋtʋʋ tɔ kɩɩ tɔ. Fanɛ n‐yaayu ii biti ɩ waa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋrɔn, amaa fanɛ n‐yɩɩ‐wʋlɛ pɔɔ.

eng: They twisted together a crown of thorns and put it on his head. They put a stick in his right hand. Then they bowed down in front of him and mocked him, saying, Hey! King of the Jews.
local: nɩɩ ba taa iwii bɔɔ fɛɛ kuwura‐wʋrʋ gyaga mu kumu sʋ, nɩɩ ba taa owura kikpebi waa mʋ gyisa kɩbaa tɔ, nɩɩ ba muŋa mu ansi tɔ nɩɩ ba ŋmasɛ mʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, Gyiwu abi Owura! An muŋa fʋ.

eng: When they approached Jerusalem and came to Bethphage on the Mount of Olives, Jesus gave two disciples a task.
local: Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba maa tiri Gyerusalem tɔ, nɩɩ ba fʋʋ Betifage kadɛ tɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ Olifu kɩbɩɩ‐bi mʋ asɛ mʋ, Yesu sun mʋ abɩɩlapʋ anyɔ.

eng: A second time he went away and prayed, My Father, if its not possible that this cup be taken away unless I drink it, then let it be what you want.
local: Yesu bɩla bwii kpee ɩlɩɩ nyɔsapʋ naa kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ yɛ, Mɛ‐Sɛ, nɩɩ ɩ mɛɛ yɛ ɩ waa fʋ fɛɛ fʋ lɛɛ falɛ awʋrʋfɔ kɔɔpʋ nɩmʋ lɩɩ mɛ sʋ, nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ n gyii awʋrʋfɔ maa mʋ, yɛgɛ fu kebiti kɩ waa kanɩn.

eng: After they spit on him, they took the stick and struck his head again and again.
local: Ba tʋʋ nkyɔlɛ wʋrɩgɛ mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ owura kikpebi mʋ da mu kumu tɔ.

eng: He said to them, Go into the village over there. As soon as you enter, you will find a donkey tied up and a colt with it. Untie them and bring them to me.
local: nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fen kpee kadɛ‐bi mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ fanɛ ansi tɔ mʋ, fen biti fen wu kuruma kʋ maa kyaa tʋtɔ maa mu‐bii. Fan saŋɛ bamʋ bɩya mɛ.

eng: When they finished mocking him, they stripped him of the military coat and put his own clothes back on him. They led him away to crucify him.
local: Ba maa ŋmasɛ mʋ lʋwɛ mʋ, ba bugi kaalɛ pɩpɛɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ba bwii taa mʋ atɔ bunsa mʋ bun mʋ. Nɩɩ ba taa mʋ bee kpee ba naa da mʋ mata oyu laasa sʋ.

eng: Again he came and found them sleeping. Their eyes were heavy with sleep.
local: Maa bɩla bwii ba abɩɩlapʋ asa mʋ asɛ mʋ, o be tu bamʋ ba maa dɛ nsɩɩrɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ba man taalɛ galɛ nsɩɩrɛ.

eng: If anyone says anything to you, say that their master needs them. He sent them off right away.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan taasɛ fanɛ kʋtɔ kʋ mʋ, fan tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu ii biti bamʋ. Nɩɩ ee biti ɔ yɛgɛ fan taa bamʋ bɩya mɛ.

eng: As they were going out, they found Simon, a man from Cyrene. They forced him to carry his cross.
local: Ba maa naa bee kpee mʋ, ba gyaŋa Simon Kesirene‐nyɩn, nɩɩ ba nyan mʋ yɛ ɔ sʋla Yesu oyu laasa mʋ.

eng: But he left them and again went and prayed the same words for the third time.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu bɩla yɛgɛ bamʋ nɩɩ o kpee naa kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ kɩsasapʋ ɛɛ tɔwɛ asɩn kʋlʋn maŋa.

eng: Now this happened to fulfill what the prophet said.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ waa kanɩn nɩn kɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bɔla mʋ ɔtɔwɛpʋ sʋ tɔwɛ yɩla mʋ ɩ ba tɔ. Asɩn mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ.

eng: Then he came to his disciples and said to them, Will you sleep and rest all night? Look, the time has come for the Human One to be betrayed into the hands of sinners.
local: Nɩɩ o bwii ba mʋ abɩɩlapʋ asɛ ba taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan san fan dɛ fɛn fʋʋtɛ nɩn? Fan kɩɩ, saŋa mʋ fʋʋ fɛɛ ba taa mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ waa alibi awaapʋ abaa tɔ.

eng: When they came to a place called Golgotha, which means Skull Place.
local: Ba ba fʋʋ opula mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ Goligota .

eng: Say to Daughter Zion, Look, your king is coming to you, humble and riding on a donkey, and on a colt the donkeys offspring. .
local: Tɔwɛ Siyon abii mʋ yɛ, Fan kɩɩ, fanɛ owura ɩɩ ba fanɛ asɛ, O du bɔhn nɩɩ ɔ tɛ kuruma sʋ, kesintin tɔ ɔ tɛ kuruma kadanba sʋ. .

eng: they gave Jesus wine mixed with vinegar to drink. But after tasting it, he didnt want to drink it.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba sa Yesu nta mʋ nɩɩ ba taa kɩfɛ bʋlɩlɩsa kʋ kyagɩlɛ tɔ, maa daa kɩɩ mʋ, nɩɩ o kina kinuu.

eng: Get up. Lets go. Look, here comes my betrayer.
local: Fan kʋsʋ kɛ a kpee. Fan kɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ lɛɛ mɛ sa mʋ tiri tɔ.

eng: The disciples went and did just as Jesus had ordered them.
local: Mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba naa waa fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Yesu sa bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ ba waa mʋ.

eng: After they crucified him, they divided up his clothes among them by drawing lots.
local: Ba maa da mʋ mata oyu laasa sʋ lʋwɛ mʋ, ba tʋʋ nbii barɩgɛ mʋ atɔ bunsa tɔ.

eng: While Jesus was still speaking, Judas, one of the Twelve, came. With him was a large crowd carrying swords and clubs. They had been sent by the chief priests and elders of the people.
local: Maa san ɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ kudu anyɔ mʋ tɔ, ɔmʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Gyudasi mʋ ba tʋtɔ. Asa damantɛ akʋ ba buu mʋ, yɛgɛ ba sʋ isakunde yɛ agulungu. Aseepu bɩlɩsa yɛ asa mʋ abɩlɩsa i sun bamʋ.

eng: They brought the donkey and the colt and laid their clothes on them. Then he sat on them.
local: Ba taa kuruma yɛ mu‐bii mʋ ba, nɩɩ ba taa bamʋ atɔ bunsa gyaga bamʋ sʋ, nɩɩ Yesu dii kyɩna kadanba mʋ sʋ.

eng: They sat there, guarding him.
local: Nɩɩ ba kyʋn kyɩna bee kun mʋ.

eng: His betrayer had given them a sign: Arrest the man I kiss.
local: Gyudasi mʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ lɛɛ Yesu sa mʋ, wʋla tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ n dan naa baari mʋ tɔ mʋ, mʋ nɩmʋ, fan kɩtaa mʋ.

eng: Now a large crowd spread their clothes on the road. Others cut palm branches off the trees and spread them on the road.
local: Asa damantɛ ba taa bamʋ atɔ bunsa tʋʋ kigbenbi sʋ, nɩɩ akʋ mɔɔ ba kyatɛ iyu n‐yaabi wʋrɩgɛ kigbenbi mʋ sʋ.

eng: Just then he came to Jesus and said, Hello, Rabbi. Then he kissed him.
local: Ba maa fʋʋ tʋtɔ mʋ, nɩɩ opula tɔ Gyudasi kpee Yesu asɛ nɩɩ ɔ yɛ, Ɔkaapʋpʋ, n fwaala fʋ. Nɩɩ o baari Yesu tɔ.

eng: They placed above his head the charge against him. It read, This is Jesus, the king of the Jews.
local: Ba kyʋrɔɔ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba taa bun mʋ kʋpwɛ mʋ gyaga mu amu sʋ yɛ, ƆNƖMƲ Ɩ GYƐ YESU, GYIWU ABI OWURA MƲ.

eng: The crowds in front of him and behind him shouted, Hosanna to the Son of David! Blessings on the one who comes in the name of the Lord! Hosanna in the highest.
local: Asa damantɛ mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ mu ansi tɔ mʋ, yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba buu mʋ mʋ bee kuusi bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Hosana ɩ gyɛ sa Owura Dawidi kanaana‐bii mʋ. Kusee ɩ gyɛ sa ɔmʋnɩ ɛɛ ba Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ kɩtɩɩ mʋ sʋ! Hosana ɩ gyɛ sa Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ sʋsʋ.

eng: But Jesus said to him, Friend, do what you came to do. Then they came and grabbed Jesus and arrested him.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ mʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Kyɛmɩnɛ, waa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ fʋ ba kɩmʋ sʋ mʋ. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ asa mʋ ba kyʋn naa kɩtaa Yesu tɔ kinkin.

eng: They crucified with him two outlaws, one on his right side and one on his left.
local: Ba da bamʋ nɩɩ bee yuuri ɔlʋn sʋ anyɔ akʋ iyu laasa sʋ tii mʋ sʋ. Ɔkʋ bʋ mʋ gyisa sʋ nɩɩ ɔkʋ mɔɔ bʋ mʋ bɩna sʋ.

eng: And when Jesus entered Jerusalem, the whole city was stirred up. Who is this? they asked.
local: Yesu maa loo Gyerusalem kadɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ, kadɛ mʋ kpini kɔɔlɛ ɔlanba, nɩɩ asa bɛɛ taasɛ yɛ, Anɩmʋ nɩn.

eng: One of those with Jesus reached for his sword. Striking the high priests slave, he cut off his ear.
local: Tʋtɔ mʋ, asa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Yesu asɛ mʋ ɔkʋ bɩɩtɛ mʋ sakunde lɛɛ, nɩɩ ɔ ŋɛ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ kɩyaafɔlɛ kʋsʋ tɩn.

eng: Those who were walking by insulted Jesus, shaking their heads.
local: Asa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ kyʋn mʋ, bɛɛ sʋ asebi bɛɛ wʋrɩgɛ mʋ sʋ yɛgɛ bee fi‐fi bamʋ amu.

eng: The crowds answered, Its the prophet Jesus from Nazareth in Galilee.
local: Nɩɩ sakyɔ mʋ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɔnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ Yesu, Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ Nasareti kadɛ tɔ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ Galile kɩbaafʋn mʋ.

eng: Then Jesus said to him, Put the sword back into its place. All those who use the sword will die by the sword.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Bwiiyaa fʋ sakunde mʋ waa kɩmʋ kikufe tɔ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kɩtaa sakunde ɛɛ kɔ mʋ, sakunde lewu nɩɩ ee biti o wu.

eng: and saying, So you were going to destroy the temple and rebuild it in three days, were you? Save yourself! If you are Gods Son, come down from the cross.
local: yɛgɛ bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Fʋ mʋ nɩɩ fʋ tɔwɛ yɛ fii biti fʋ boyi kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ, kɛ fʋ pɔyɩ kɩmʋ nkɛ nsa mʋ, mɔlɩgɛ fʋ n‐yɩɩ! Nɩɩ fʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mu‐Bii‐nyɩnsa mʋ, kpɩlɩgɛ lɩɩ oyu laasa mʋ sʋ.

eng: Then Jesus went into the temple and threw out all those who were selling and buying there. He pushed over the tables used for currency exchange and the chairs of those who sold doves.
local: Yesu loo kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ opula, nɩɩ ɔ gya asa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ fɛ, yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ sɔɔ mʋ kpini lɩɩ tʋtɔ. Ɔ bala afulee akyɛrɛpʋ mʋ iteeburu yɛ alɔpɔ afɛpʋ mʋ ibenkyii bun.

eng: Or do you think that Im not able to ask my Father and he will send to me more than twelve battle groups of angels right away?.
local: Fʋ man nyi fɛɛ n taalɛ tɩɩ mɛ‐Sɛ, kɛ ɔ yɛgɛ sʋsʋ nbɔɔ ngbɩn‐ngbɩn kudu anyɔ ba ba opula nɩmʋ tɔ ba kyɛ mɛ tɔ abɛɛ?.

eng: In the same way, the chief priests, along with the legal experts and the elders, were making fun of him, saying.
local: Kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu abɩlɩsa yɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ yɛ abɩlɩsa mʋ ba ŋmasɛ mʋ.

eng: He said to them, Its written, My house will be called a house of prayer. But youve made it a hideout for crooks.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Ba kyʋrɔɔ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ, Bee biti ba tɩɩ mɛ kɩkpaara yɛ kabwaarɛ kʋlɛkpa, amaa fan taa tʋtɔ biliŋi bamʋ nɩɩ bee yuuri ɔlʋn sʋ ɔbaalakpa.

eng: But if I did that, how would the scriptures be fulfilled that say this must happen.
local: Amaa nɩɩ ɩ kan waa kanɩn mʋ, nɛnɛ nɩɩ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlɛsa mʋ tɔ yɛ ɩ dagaa ɩ maa waa mʋ, ɩ ba tɔ.

eng: He saved others, but he cant save himself. Hes the king of Israel, so let him come down from the cross now. Then well believe in him.
local: Ba tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔ mɔlɩgɛ akʋ, amaa ɔ mɛɛ taalɛ mɔlɩgɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ. Mʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩsɩrayɩ owura! Fan yɛgɛ ɔ kpɩlɩgɛ lɩɩ oyu laasa mʋ sʋ ba nbɩɩnbɩɩ, kɛ a kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii.

eng: People who were blind and lame came to Jesus in the temple, and he healed them.
local: Agyaatanapʋ yɛ ayaa yɛ abaa wusa wuya ana ba ba mʋ asɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ kyɛ bamʋ alɔ.

eng: Then Jesus said to the crowds, Have you come with swords and clubs to arrest me, like a thief? Day after day, I sat in the temple teaching, but you didnt arrest me.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ sakyɔ mʋ yɛ, Fan taa isakunde yɛ agulungu fan ba kɩtaa mɛ fɛɛ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kʋsʋ yɩlɛ kɩbanɛ sʋ. Kakɛ kʋmaa n kyɩna kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ tɔ n kaapʋ, fan man kɩtaa mɛ.

eng: He trusts in God, so let God deliver him now if he wants to. He said, Im Gods Son.
local: Ɔ taa mʋ n‐yɩɩ tʋʋ Ɩbwaarɛ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mu‐Bii mʋ. Fan yɛgɛ a kɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ mɔlɩgɛ mʋ nbɩɩnbɩɩ nɩmʋ.

eng: But when the chief priests and legal experts saw the amazing things he was doing and the children shouting in the temple, Hosanna to the Son of David! they were angry.
local: Amaa Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu abɩlɩsa yɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ ba maa wu atɔ kyinkyinsa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa, yɛ nbii mʋ kikuusi kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Hosana ɩ gyɛ sa Dawidi mʋ kanaana‐bii mʋ, nɩɩ ba nyɛ agbʋ.

eng: But all this has happened so that what the prophets said in the scriptures might be fulfilled. Then all the disciples left Jesus and ran away.
local: Amaa ɩnɩmʋ kpini ɩ waa nɩn kɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ ba kyʋrɔɔ yɩla mʋ ɩ nyɛ ba ɩmʋ tɔ. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ kpini ba sɩlɛ yɛgɛ mʋ.

eng: The outlaws who were crucified with him insulted him in the same way.
local: Ɔkpa kʋlʋn maŋa sʋ nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ bee yuuri ɔlʋn sʋ, mʋ nɩɩ ba da bamʋ mata iyu laasa yɛ mʋ mʋ, gbaa ba taa asebi wʋrɩgɛ mʋ sʋ.

eng: They said to Jesus, Do you hear what these children are saying? Yes, he answered. Havent you ever read, From the mouths of babies and infants youve arranged praise for yourself? .
local: Nɩɩ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Fu nu asɩn mʋ nɩɩ nbii mʋ bɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ? Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ Ɛɛhn, fan man pɩɩta kalɛ lɩɩ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ yɛ, Fʋ kaapʋ nbii yɛ nbii wuribi yɛ ba yɩn fʋ. .

eng: Those who arrested Jesus led him to Caiaphas the high priest. The legal experts and the elders had gathered there.
local: Bamʋ nɩɩ ba kɩtaa Yesu mʋ ba taa mʋ kpee Kefasi, oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ, katɩn mʋ nɩɩ nbara akaapʋpʋ yɛ asa mʋ abɩlɩsa ba gyaŋɛ mʋ.

eng: From noon until three in the afternoon the whole earth was dark.
local: Lɩɩ kyɔwɛ amu nsana naa fʋʋ kalaatɩpwɛ kerefi asa tɔ mʋ, kitentenbiri bun ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ kpini.

eng: Then he left them and went out of the city to Bethany and spent the night there.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ tʋtɔ kpee Betani kadɛ tɔ nɩɩ ɔ dɛ tʋtɔ.

eng: Peter followed him from a distance until he came to the high priests courtyard. He entered that area and sat outside with the officers to see how it would turn out.
local: Amaa Pita buu bamʋ kɩyaa‐kɩyaa, halɩɩ ba naa fʋʋ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ. Nɩɩ Pita sii kyɩna kɩkpaara mʋ akɩɩsʋpʋ mʋ asɛ, kɛ o wu kanan mʋ nɩɩ ii biti ɩ naa lʋwɛ.

eng: At about three Jesus cried out with a loud shout, Eli, Eli, lama sabachthani , which means, My God, my God, why have you left me.
local: Nɩɩ kerefi asa maŋa tɔ mʋ, Yesu kuusi kenken yɛ, Eli, Eli lamaa sabatani? Ɩmʋ kasɛ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, Mɛ‐Sɛ, mɛ‐Sɛ, mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fu kina mɛ.

eng: Early in the morning as Jesus was returning to the city, he was hungry.
local: Lɩɩ kaakɛ nyenyenfuu Yesu maa bwii ee kpee Gyerusalem mʋ, akʋn ɩɩ mɔɔ mʋ.

eng: After hearing him, some standing there said, Hes calling Elijah.
local: Asa mʋ nɩɩ ba yɩlɛ tʋtɔ mʋ, ba maa nu asɩn mʋ, nɩɩ akʋ ba yɛ, Ɛɛ tɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Eligya.

eng: The chief priests and the whole council were looking for false testimony against Jesus so that they could put him to death.
local: Aseepu bɩlɩsa yɛ Gyiwu abi abɩlɩsa amu‐amu ngyaŋɛ mʋ kpini ba tɔwɛ kɔnɔ waa Yesu bee biti antɩŋɛsa ba tɩn gyaga mʋ sʋ, kɛ ba nyɛ ɔkpa mɔɔ mʋ.

eng: He saw a fig tree along the road, but when he came to it, he found nothing except leaves. Then he said to it, Youll never again bear fruit! The fig tree dried up at once.
local: Maa wu figi oyu maa yɩlɛ ɔkpa kaa mʋ o kpee kɩmʋ asɛ, amaa ɔ man wu sɛhn kɩmʋ sʋ amɔɔ afatɛɛ nkʋn. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ kɩmʋ yɛ, Fʋ man bɩla yɛ fʋ sɔyɩ abi kpini‐kpini! Opula tɔ nɩɩ oyu mʋ wʋlɛ.

eng: One of them ran over, took a sponge full of vinegar, and put it on a pole. He offered it to Jesus to drink.
local: Opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ bamʋ tɔ ɔkʋ sɩlɛ naa taa ɔkyɛɛ dufudufu taa naa nyɔ nta nyaŋɛsa kʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ taa ŋmina oyu sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kʋsaa naa tii Yesu kɔnɔ yɛ o nuu.

eng: When the disciples saw it, they were amazed. How did the fig tree dry up so fast? they asked.
local: Abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba maa wu kanɩn mʋ, i kyinkyin bamʋ. Nɩɩ ba taasɛ yɛ, Nɛnɛ sʋ nɩɩ figi oyu mʋ wʋlɛ bilen kanɩn.

eng: They didnt find anything they could use from the many false witnesses who were willing to come forward. But finally they found two.
local: Antɩŋɛsa adansɛ agyipu gaalaagaa ba ba tɔwɛ, amaa ba man nyɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba taa gyaga mʋ sʋ. Kamaa tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ anyɩn anyɔ akʋ ba lɩɩ ba yɩlɛ tɔwɛ yɛ.

eng: But the rest of them said, Lets see if Elijah will come and save him.
local: Asansa mʋ ba yɛ, Nbɩɩnbɩɩ fan yɛgɛ mʋ, kɛ a kɩɩ fɛɛ Eligya ee biti ɔ ba mɔlɩgɛ mʋ abɛɛ.

eng: Jesus responded, I assure you that if you have faith and dont doubt, you will not only do what was done to the fig tree. You will even say to this mountain, Be lifted up and thrown into the lake. And it will happen.
local: Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin yɛ, nɩɩ fan kan kɔɔlɛ mɛ gyii, nɩɩ fan man gyii ɩmɔɔrɛ mʋ, man gyɛ yɛ fen biti fan taalɛ waa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ waa figi oyu nɩmʋ nkʋn. Amaa fɛn taalɛ tɔwɛ kɩbɩɩ nɩmʋ yɛ, Kʋsʋ lɩɩ nfɩɩ naa tɩyɛ ɔpʋ tɔ, kɛ ɩ waa kanɩn.

eng: who said, This man said, I can destroy Gods temple and rebuild it in three days.
local: Ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, N taalɛ boyi kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ nɩmʋ, kɛ n taa nkɛ nsa bwii pɔyɩ kɩmʋ. .

eng: Again Jesus cried out with a loud shout. Then he died.
local: Yesu maa bɩla kuusi kenken mʋ, ɔ tɩn ɔŋɛɛ.

eng: If you have faith, you will receive whatever you pray for.
local: Nɩɩ fan kan kɔɔlɛ mɛ gyii mʋ, fen biti fan nyɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fan kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ tɔ mʋ.

eng: Then the high priest stood and said to Jesus, Arent you going to respond to the testimony these people have brought against you.
local: Nɩɩ Oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ kʋsʋ yɩlɛ nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ Yesu yɛ, Fʋ mɛɛ yɛ fʋ lɛɛ kɔnɔ lɩɩ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ anyɩn nɩmʋ ba sʋ bɛɛ gyaga fʋ sʋ falɛ.

eng: Look, the curtain of the sanctuary was torn in two from top to bottom. The earth shook, the rocks split.
local: Kanɩn saŋa maŋa kɩtaa kunsa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ barɩgɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ, kyatɛ tɔ anyɔ lɩɩ sʋsʋ ba yii kasɛ. Nɩɩ kasɛ gyingyen, nɩɩ afʋlɛ ɩ baaŋɛ tɔ.

eng: When Jesus entered the temple, the chief priests and elders of the people came to him as he was teaching. They asked, What kind of authority do you have for doing these things? Who gave you this authority.
local: Yesu loo kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ. Maa bʋ tʋtɔ ɛɛ kaapʋ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu abɩlɩsa yɛ kadɛ mʋ abɩlɩsa ba ba mʋ asɛ, nɩɩ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Mɩnɛ ɔlʋn nɩɩ fʋ sʋ fɩɩ waa atɔ nɩmʋ? Nɩɩ anɩmʋ ɩ sa fʋ falɛ ɔlʋn.

eng: But Jesus was silent. The high priest said, By the living God, I demand that you tell us whether you are the Christ, Gods Son.
local: Amaa Yesu man tɔwɛ sɛhn. Nɩɩ Oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ bwii tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, N bɔ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛ kakpaa mʋ ntan yɛ tɔwɛ anɛ. Fʋ ɩ gyɛ Krisito, Ɩbwaarɛ mu‐bii mʋ.

eng: and the bodies of many holy people who had died were raised.
local: Nkyan i bugi‐bugi tɔ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba wu mʋ, gaalaagaa ba kyiŋi ba nkpa tɔ.

eng: Jesus replied, I have a question for you. If you tell me the answer, Ill tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ngbaa mɛ n biti n taasɛ fanɛ asɩn kʋ, nɩɩ fan kan lɛɛ mɛ kɔnɔ mʋ, n biti n tɔwɛ fanɛ ɔlʋn mʋ nɩɩ n sʋ n waa ɩnɩmʋ.

eng: You said it, Jesus replied. But I say to you that from now on youll see the Human One sitting on the right side of the Almighty and coming on the heavenly clouds .
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɩmʋ nɩɩ fʋ tɔwɛ mʋ. Amaa n tɔwɛ fanɛ nɩn yɛ, lɩɩ ndaga ɩ sʋ ii kpee mʋ, fen biti fan wu mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ n maa tɛ Ɔlʋnpʋ Owura lala mʋ gyisa sʋ, nɩɩ fen biti fan bɩla wu maa lɩɩ abwaarɛ‐wʋlɛ tɔ sʋsʋ n ba.

eng: After Jesus resurrection they came out of their graves and went into the holy city where they appeared to many people.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Yesu kyiŋi lɩɩ lewu tɔ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba kyiŋi lɩɩ lewu tɔ mʋ, ba tii Yesu sʋ kpee kadɛ kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ asa gaalaagaa ba wu bamʋ.

eng: Where did John get his authority to baptize? Did he get it from heaven or from humans? They argued among themselves, If we say from heaven, hell say to us, Then why didnt you believe him.
local: Gyɔn kekyugyee mʋ, fɩnɛ nɩɩ kɩ lɩɩ? Ɩ kyɩna lɩɩ sʋsʋ nɩn abɛɛ ɩ lɩɩ asa asɛ nɩn? Ba tɔwɛ bamʋ wʋlɛ‐wʋlɛ tɔ yɛ, Nɩɩ an kan tɔwɛ yɛ, Kɩ lɩɩ sʋsʋ nɩn, ee biti ɔ taasɛ anɛ yɛ, Nɩɩ mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fan man kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii.

eng: Then the high priest tore his clothes and said, Hes insulting God! Why do we need any more witnesses? Look, youve heard his insult against God.
local: Nɩɩ Oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ kɩtaa mʋ kaalɛ kyatɛ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Nbusaa asɩn! A man bɩla an biti adansɛ kʋ. Fan nu nbusaa asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ.

eng: When the centurion and those with him who were guarding Jesus saw the earthquake and what had just happened, they were filled with awe and said, This was certainly Gods Son.
local: Anaakɔpʋ kɩlɩfa ɔbɩlɩsa yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ mʋ asɛ bee kun Yesu mʋ, ba maa wu kasɛ maa gyingyen, yɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ waa maŋa kpini mʋ, kufuu kɩ kɩtaa bamʋ gaa, nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, Kesintin tɔ, ɔ kyɩna ɔ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mu‐Bii nɩn.

eng: But we cant say from humans because were afraid of the crowd, since everyone thinks John was a prophet.
local: Amaa an kan tɔwɛ yɛ, Kɩ lɩɩ asa asɛ mʋ, an sɩlɛ asa mʋ kufuu, lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ kpini ba kɔɔlɛ gyii fɛɛ Gyɔn kyɩna ɔ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ nɩn.

eng: What do you think? And they answered, He deserves to die.
local: Nɛnɛ nɩɩ fɛn fɛ kɩmʋ nfɛɛrɛ? Nɩɩ asa mʋ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɩ dagaa maa wu.

eng: Many women were watching from a distance. They had followed Jesus from Galilee to serve him.
local: Akyɩɩ gaalaagaa ba bʋ tʋtɔ ba yɩlɛ kata‐kata bɛɛ kɩɩ. Bamʋ ɩ kyɩna ba gyɛ akyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ ba buu Yesu lɩɩ Galile nɩɩ ba kyɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ.

eng: Then they replied, We dont know. Jesus also said to them, Neither will I tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ba lɛɛ Yesu kɔnɔ yɛ, A man nyi. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Kanɩn bɩrɛ ngbaa mɛ, n mɛɛ yɛ n tɔwɛ fanɛ ɔlʋn mʋ nɩɩ n sʋ n waa ɩnɩmʋ.

eng: Then they spit in his face and beat him. They hit him.
local: Nɩɩ ba tʋʋ nkyɔlɛ waa mu ansi tɔ nɩɩ ba dayɩ mʋ, nɩɩ akʋ mɔɔ ba kpa mʋ ɩtan.

eng: Among them were Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James and Joseph, and the mother of Zebedees sons.
local: Bamʋ tɔ akʋ ɩ gyɛ Mɛɛrɩ Magidalen mʋ, yɛ Gyemisi maa Gyosefu bamʋ‐nyi Mɛɛrɩ, yɛ Sebedi mu‐bii ana bamʋ nyi.

eng: Jesus returned to the synagogue. A man with a withered hand was there.
local: Yesu bwii kpee kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa. Ɔnyɩn kʋ mʋ nɩɩ mʋ kɩbaa ki wu mʋ bʋ tʋtɔ.

eng: What do you think? A man had two sons. Now he came to the first and said, Son, go and work in the vineyard today.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Ɩmʋ nɩɩ n biti n tɔwɛ falɛ, nɛnɛ ɩ gyɛ fanɛ nfɛɛrɛ? Ɔnyɩn kʋ ɩ kyɩna ɔ bʋ tɔ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ nbii‐nyɩnsa anyɔ. O kpee ɔgyankpaapʋ mʋ asɛ naa tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Mi‐bii, ndaga kpee mɛ kadɔɔ mʋ tɔ naa sun kusun.

eng: and said, Prophesy for us, Christ! Who hit you.
local: Nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Krisito, nɩɩ fʋ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ mʋ, bɩɩ tɔwɛ anɛ, ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ da fʋ falɛ.

eng: That evening a man named Joseph came. He was a rich man from Arimathea who had become a disciple of Jesus.
local: Ɩ maa fʋʋ kalaatɩpwɛ sʋ mʋ, atɔ wuya kʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ Arimatiya kadɛ tɔ, nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Gyosefu, nɩɩ mʋ gbaa mʋ gyɛ Yesu ɔbɩɩlapʋ mʋ ɔkʋ.

eng: Wanting to bring charges against Jesus, they were watching Jesus closely to see if he would heal on the Sabbath.
local: Asa akʋ mɔɔ ba bʋ tʋtɔ nɩɩ bee biti kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba nyɛ asɩn akʋ taa pwɩɩ Yesu. Ɩmʋ sʋ ba tɛ bee gyoo fɛɛ Yesu ii biti ɔ kyɛ ɔnyɩn mʋ kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ, abɛɛ ɔ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ kyɛ mʋ.

eng: No, I dont want to, he replied. But later he changed his mind and went.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, N mɛɛ yɛ n kpee. Amaa kamaa tɔ mʋ ɔ kyɛɛgɛ mʋ nfɛɛrɛ nɩɩ o kpee.

eng: Meanwhile, Peter was sitting outside in the courtyard. A servant woman came and said to him, You were also with Jesus the Galilean.
local: Pita maa tɛ kɩkpaara mʋ, nɩɩ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ kibite kʋ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Ngbaa fʋ, fu tii Yesu, Kagalile‐nyɩn nɩmʋ sʋ.

eng: He came to Pilate and asked for Jesus body. Pilate gave him permission to take it.
local: O kpee Paleti asɛ naa kʋlɛ Yesu kubuni, nɩɩ Paleti sa kɔnɔ yɛ ba taa sa mʋ.

eng: He said to the man with the withered hand, Step up where people can see you.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ ɔnyɩn mʋ yɛ, Kʋsʋ yɩlɛ bamʋ ansi tɔ nfɩɩ.
