eng: The father said the same thing to the other son, who replied, Yes, sir. But he didnt go.
local: Nɩɩ ɔsɛ mʋ kpee mu‐bii banban mʋ asɛ naa tɔwɛ mʋ kanɩn asɩn kʋlʋn maŋa. Nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Nsɛ, n biti n kpee. Amaa ɔ man kpee.

eng: But he denied it in front of all of them, saying, I dont know what you are talking about.
local: Amaa Pita kyɛɛ bamʋ kpini ansi tɔ yɛ, Asɩn mʋ nɩɩ fɩɩ tɔwɛ falɛ, n man nyi ɩmʋ sʋ gbaa kpini‐kpini.

eng: Joseph took the body, wrapped it in a clean linen cloth.
local: Gyosefu taa kibuni mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taa kɩkalala pʋpwɛ maalɛ mʋ.

eng: Then he said to them, Is it legal on the Sabbath to do good or to do evil, to save life or to kill? But they said nothing.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ asa mʋ yɛ, Kɩmɔmɔ nɩɩ anɛ nbara mʋ ɩɩ sa ɔkpa fɛɛ a waa kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ? Adanbɩrasa abɛɛ alibi? A mɔlɩgɛ asa abɛɛ a mɔɔ? Asa mʋ ba man taalɛ tɔwɛ sɛhn.

eng: Which one of these two did his fathers will? They said, The first one. Jesus said to them, I assure you that tax collectors and prostitutes are entering Gods kingdom ahead of you.
local: Bamʋ anyɔ maŋa, anɩmʋ ɩ waa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ mʋ‐sɛ ii biti? Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɔgyankpaapʋ mʋ. Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin yɛ, apoo lenpoo akɔɔlɛpʋ yɛ agyamilo‐kyɩɩ bɛɛ gyankpaa fanɛ bee loo Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ.

eng: When he went over to the gate, another woman saw him and said to those who were there, This man was with Jesus, the man from Nazareth.
local: Maa lɩɩ kpee kewusunɔ mʋ, nɩɩ kibite kʋ mɔɔ bɩla wu mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba yɩlɛ tʋtɔ mʋ yɛ, Ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ daa o tii Yesu Kanasareti‐nyɩn mʋ sʋ nɩn.

eng: and laid it in his own new tomb, which he had carved out of the rock. After he rolled a large stone at the door of the tomb, he went away.
local: nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ pula mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mʋ kakyan pʋpwɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ ŋɛ kɩfʋlɛ waa ɔbɔ yɩla mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ o gbiliti kubuu lala kʋ tii kakyan mʋ kɔnɔ nɩɩ ɔ natɛ kyʋn.

eng: Jesus responded by speaking again in parables.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu bɩla taa aŋasan tɔ tɔwɛ sa asa mʋ yɛ.

eng: Looking around at them with anger, deeply grieved at their unyielding hearts, he said to the man, Stretch out your hand. So he did, and his hand was made healthy.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu kɩɩ bamʋ kyaabɔɔ agbʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɩ kɔ mʋ gaa lɩɩ bamʋ kʋsʋ tɔ ɔlʋn sʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ ɔnyɩn mʋ yɛ, Tɛɛŋɛ fʋ kɩbaa. Nɩɩ ɔ tɛɛŋɛ mʋ kɩbaa, nɩɩ ki kyiŋi.

eng: For John came to you on the righteous road, and you didnt believe him. But tax collectors and prostitutes believed him. Yet even after you saw this, you didnt change your hearts and lives and you didnt believe him.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Gyɔn ba fanɛ asɛ nɩn yɛ ɔ ba kaapʋ fanɛ ɩkpa kyiigisa ɔkpa, nɩɩ fan man kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii, amaa lenpoo akɔɔlɛpʋ yɛ agyamilo‐kyɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii. Nɩɩ halɩɩ fan maa wu ɩnɩmʋ kamaa tɔ mʋ, fan man yɛgɛ alibi kɩwaa kɛ fan kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii.

eng: With a solemn pledge, he denied it again, saying, I dont know the man.
local: Nɩɩ Pita bɔ ntan kyɛɛ yɛ, N man nyi ɔnyɩn maŋa gbaa kpini‐kpini.

eng: Mary Magdalene and the other Mary were there, sitting in front of the tomb.
local: Mɛɛrɩ Magidalen maa Mɛɛrɩ banban mʋ, be sii bɛ tɛ, yɛgɛ bamʋ ansi ɩ kaapʋ kakyan mʋ.

eng: The kingdom of heaven is like a king who prepared a wedding party for his son.
local: Sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii mʋ du nɩn fɛɛ, owura kʋ maa waa kɩkyɩɩfɔ kensi‐gyii lala sa mu‐bii‐nyɩnsa.

eng: At that, the Pharisees got together with the supporters of Herod to plan how to destroy Jesus.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Farasii abi mʋ ba lɩɩ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ba maa Herodi kamaa abi ba naa waa kɔnɔ kʋlʋn ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ ba taa kɛ ba mɔɔ Yesu.

eng: Listen to another parable. There was a landowner who planted a vineyard. He put a fence around it, dug a winepress in it, and built a tower. Then he rented it to tenant farmers and took a trip.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan bɩla nu kɩŋasan kʋ. Ɔnyɩn kʋ kyɩna dɔɔ oyu sɔyɩ abisa. Nɩɩ o ŋmina iyu kyaabɔɔ kɩmʋ, nɩɩ o kuu wayɩn ɔnyɩyɛkpa ɔbɔ kʋdɔɔ mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ pɔyɩ opula mʋ nɩɩ akɩɩsʋpʋ bee dii bɛɛ kɩɩ kʋdɔɔ mʋ sʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ taa kʋdɔɔ mʋ waa paa agyipu kʋ abaa tɔ, nɩɩ o kpee ɔsʋwʋlɛ kʋ sʋ.

eng: A short time later those standing there came and said to Peter, You must be one of them. The way you talk gives you away.
local: Ɩ maa waa gbɛrɛɛ mʋ, nɩɩ anyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba yɩlɛ tʋtɔ mʋ ba tɔwɛ Pita yɛ, Kesintin tɔ mʋ, fʋ gyɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ fʋ katɔwɛ tɔ ɩɩ kaapʋ kanɩn.

eng: The next day, which was the day after Preparation Day, the chief priests and the Pharisees gathered before Pilate.
local: Ɩmʋ lɩɩkaakɛ maa gyɛ Kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ mʋ, aseepu bɩlɩsa yɛ Farasii abi mʋ ba kpee Paleti asɛ naa tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ.

eng: He sent his servants to call those invited to the wedding party. But they didnt want to come.
local: Nɩɩ o sun mʋ ayaafɔlɛ yɛ ba kpee naa tɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ɔ da kɩbaa tɩɩ bamʋ yɛ ba be kensi‐gyii mʋ tɔ, amaa ba kina kɩ ba.

eng: Jesus left with his disciples and went to the lake. A large crowd followed him because they had heard what he was doing. They were from Galilee.
local: Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba lɩɩ ɩkaa sʋ kpee Galile ɔbʋn ɔkaa, nɩɩ asa damantɛ ba lɩɩ Galile yɛ Gyudeya ɩsʋwʋlɛ sʋ ba buu mʋ.

eng: When it was time for harvest, he sent his servants to the tenant farmers to collect his fruit.
local: Kɩtɩŋɛ saŋa maa fʋʋ mʋ, nɩɩ o sun mʋ ayaafɔlɛ yɛ ba kpee paa agyipu mʋ asɛ naa kɔɔlɛ mu ogyikpa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ nyɛ mʋ bɩya mʋ.

eng: Then he cursed and swore, I dont know the man! At that very moment the rooster crowed.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Pita tɔwɛ nnɔ gyaga mʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ bɔ ntan tɔwɛ yɛ, N man nyi ɔnyɩn maa kpini‐kpini. Opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ kyaasɛ bʋn.

eng: They said, Sir, we remember that while that deceiver was still alive he said, After three days I will arise.
local: Ɔbɩlɩsa! An nyiŋi fɛɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔpɩnapʋ mʋ tɛ nkpa tɔ mʋ, ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Nkɛ nsa kamaa mʋ, n biti n kyiŋi lɩɩ lewu tɔ ba nkpa tɔ.

eng: Again he sent other servants and said to them, Tell those who have been invited,Look, the meal is all prepared. Ive butchered the oxen and the fattened cattle. Now everythings ready. Come to the wedding party.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔ bɩla sun ayaafɔlɛ akʋ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan naa tɔwɛ bamʋ nɩɩ n da kɩbaa tɩɩ mʋ fɛɛ n kʋʋla lʋwɛ. N mɔɔ ɩnaatɛ gbelu yɛ akpensen‐kpesen mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ nfɔ, nɩɩ n kʋʋla kʋtɔ kʋmaa lʋwɛ. Fan ba kɩkyɩɩfɔ kensi‐gyii mʋ tɔ.

eng: Judea, Jerusalem, Idumea, beyond the Jordan, and the area surrounding Tyre and Sidon.
local: Akʋ ba lɩɩ Gyerusalem kadɛ lala yɛ ɩsʋwʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ Idumiya, yɛ Gyɔdan ɔbʋn kyɔwɛ kɩlɩɩ yɛ Taayi maa Sidon ndɛ lala mʋ abaafʋn. Asa damantɛ ba ba Yesu asɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ba nu kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ waa.

eng: But the tenant farmers grabbed his servants. They beat some of them, and some of them they killed. Some of them they stoned to death.
local: Nɩɩ paa agyipu mʋ ba kɩtaa mʋ ayaafɔlɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ba dayɩ ɔkʋ, nɩɩ ba mɔɔ ɔkʋ, nɩɩ ba twɩɩ ɔsasapʋ mʋ abuu.

eng: Peter remembered Jesus words, Before the rooster crows you will deny me three times.And Peter went out and cried uncontrollably.
local: Nɩɩ Pita nyiŋi asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Yesu daa tɔwɛ mʋ mʋ, fɛɛ Pɔyɩ kɛ kyaasɛ ɔ bʋn mʋ, fii biti fu kina mɛ ɩlɩɩ ɩsa. Nɩɩ Pita lɩɩ ɩkaa sʋ naa su gaa.

eng: Therefore, order the grave to be sealed until the third day. Otherwise, his disciples may come and steal the body and tell the people, Hes been raised from the dead. This last deception will be worse than the first.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ wʋla sa kɔnɔ kɛ asa ba naa kyɩna kun mʋ kakyan mʋ naa fʋʋ kakɛ sasapʋ mʋ. Nɩɩ man gyɛ kanɩn mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ bee biti ba be yuuri mu kibuni mʋ, kɛ ba tɔwɛ asa yɛ o kyiŋi lɩɩ lewu tɔ. Nɩɩ ɩ kan ba kanɩn mʋ, mʋ npɩna mʋ ii biti ɩ ŋara ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɔ wʋla pɩna falɛ.

eng: But they paid no attention and went away—some to their fields, others to their businesses.
local: Amaa asa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ da kɩbaa tɩɩ mʋ, ba man tʋwɛ mʋ. Akʋ ba kyʋn bamʋ ndɔɔ ana tɔ, nɩɩ akʋ mɔɔ ba kyʋn asun tɔ.

eng: Jesus told his disciples to get a small boat ready for him so the crowd wouldnt crush him.
local: Sakyɔ mʋ sʋ mʋ, Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ ba lɔŋɔ ɔkʋlɩ yɩla mʋ, kɛ ba man nyɛ nin tiri mʋ tɔ.

eng: Again he sent other servants, more than the first group. They treated them in the same way.
local: Nɩɩ o bwii sun mʋ ayaafɔlɛ akʋ nɩɩ ba kyɔ bamʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyankpaa sun mʋ, nɩɩ paa agyipu mʋ ba waa bamʋ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba waa abanban mʋ.

eng: Pilate replied, You have soldiers for guard duty. Go and make it as secure as you know how.
local: Nɩɩ Paleti lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Fan taa anaakɔpʋ, kɛ ba naa yɩlɛ kun kakyan mʋ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fen nyi fɛɛ fɛn taalɛ waa mʋ.

eng: The rest of them grabbed his servants, abused them, and killed them.
local: Nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba san mʋ mɔɔ ba kɩtaa owura mʋ ayaafɔlɛ mʋ dayɩ nɩɩ ba mɔɔ bamʋ.

eng: Finally he sent his son to them. They will respect my son, he said.
local: Laalaalʋwɛ mʋ, o sun mu‐bii‐nyɩnsa bamʋ asɛ tɔwɛ yɛ Bee biti ba taa bɛɛrɛɛ sa mi‐bii.

eng: He had healed so many people that everyone who was sick pushed forward so that they could touch him.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ kyɛ asa damantɛ, nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ san bɛɛ lɔ mʋ kpini, bee nin bee tiri mʋ, kɛ ba nyɛ mata mʋ.

eng: Then they went and secured the tomb by sealing the stone and posting the guard.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ba kpee kakyan mʋ asɛ naa waa kʋtɔ kʋ tarɩ kubuu mʋ, nɩɩ ba lɛɛ akɩɩsʋpʋ yɩla yɛ ba kun kɩmʋ.

eng: The king was angry. He sent his soldiers to destroy those murderers and set their city on fire.
local: Owura mʋ nyɛ agbʋ gaa. Nɩɩ o sun mʋ anaakɔpʋ, nɩɩ ba naa mɔɔ kanɩn amɔɔpʋ maŋa nɩɩ ba tuwa bamʋ kadɛ.

eng: But when the tenant farmers saw the son, they said to each other, This is the heir. Come on, lets kill him and well have his inheritance.
local: Amaa paa agyipu mʋ ba maa wu mu‐bii maa ba mʋ, ba tɔwɛ abaa yɛ, Ɔnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ kapatɩyɛ ogyipu mʋ. Fan ba kɛ a mɔɔ mʋ kɛ a taa mʋ kapatɩyɛ mʋ.

eng: Whenever the evil spirits saw him, they fell down at his feet and shouted, You are Gods Son.
local: Nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi wuya ana ba kan wu mʋ mʋ, bɛɛ lɩɩ bɛɛ tɩyɛ mu ansi tɔ, kɛ ba kuusi tɔwɛ fɛɛ, Fʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Mu‐Bii mʋ.

eng: Then he said to his servants, The wedding party is prepared, but those who were invited werent worthy.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ ayaafɔlɛ yɛ, N lʋwɛ kɩkyɩɩfɔ kensi‐gyii agyitɔ mʋ, amaa bamʋ nɩɩ n da kɩbaa tɩɩ mʋ, ba man tʋwɛ, ɩmʋ sʋ ba man dagaa agyitɔ mʋ kigyii.

eng: They grabbed him, threw him out of the vineyard, and killed him.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ba kɩtaa kebii mʋ, bɩɩtɛ mʋ lɩɩ kadɔɔ mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ba mɔɔ mʋ.

eng: But he strictly ordered them not to reveal who he was.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu ɩ gya bamʋ fɛɛ ba man tɔwɛ ɔkʋ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ.

eng: Therefore, go to the roads on the edge of town and invite everyone you find to the wedding party.
local: Kanɩn sʋ fan loo kadɛ tɔ kpee agbenbi sʋ, naa tɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fen wu mʋ ba kɩkyɩɩfɔ kensi‐gyii mʋ tɔ.

eng: Jesus went up on a mountain and called those he wanted, and they came to him.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu kpee kɩbɩɩ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ asa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti, nɩɩ ba ba mʋ asɛ.

eng: When the owner of the vineyard comes, what will he do to those tenant farmers.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ, nɩɩ kadɔɔ wuya mʋ kan ba mʋ, mɩnɛ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ waa paa agyipu mʋ.

eng: Then those servants went to the roads and gathered everyone they found, both evil and good. The wedding party was full of guests.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ayaafɔlɛ mʋ ba lɩɩ kpee agbenbi sʋ yɛ katɩn kʋmaa natɛ tɩɩ ɔsa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ba wu, asa adanbɩrasa yɛ alibi kpini bʋga tɔ, nɩɩ asa ba ba bɔla kɩkyɩɩfɔ lenbu mʋ.

eng: They said, He will totally destroy those wicked farmers and rent the vineyard to other tenant farmers who will give him the fruit when its ready.
local: Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɩ waa kanan kʋmaa ee biti ɔ mɔɔ kanɩn asa libi maŋa, kɛ ɔ taa kadɔɔ mʋ sa paa agyipu pʋpwɛ kʋ, mʋ nɩɩ bee biti ba baa bɛɛ sa mʋ mʋ ogyikpa lɩɩ adɔɔ‐gyitɔ mʋ tɔ kɩtɩŋɛ saŋa.

eng: He appointed twelve and called them apostles. He appointed them to be with him, to be sent out to preach.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ bamʋ tɔ asa kudu anyɔ, nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ bamʋ fɛɛ asunpu. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, N lɛɛ fanɛ fɛɛ fan baa fan bʋ mɛ asɛ, kɛ n sun fanɛ fan naa tɔwɛ abwaarɛsɩn.

eng: Now when the king came in and saw the guests, he spotted a man who wasnt wearing wedding clothes.
local: Amaa saŋa mʋ nɩɩ owura mʋ ba kɩɩ lenbu mʋ tɔ mʋ, o wu ɔnyɩn kʋ maa man bun kɩkyɩɩfɔ tɔ atɔ bunsa.

eng: Jesus said to them, Havent you ever read in the scriptures, The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone. The Lord has done this, and its amazing in our eyes?.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan man pɩɩta kalɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ yɛ, Kubuu mʋ nɩɩ apɔyɩpʋ ba kina mʋ, kɩmʋ ɩ ba biliŋi kɩbɔntɔnbʋlɔntɔ kubuu kparɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ sʋ obu mʋ tɔ. Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ ɩ waa ɩnɩmʋ, nɩɩ i kyinkyin anɛ.

eng: and to have authority to throw out demons.
local: N biti n sa fanɛ ɔlʋn mʋ nɩɩ fen biti fan taalɛ taa gya nbwɩɩ libi.

eng: He said to him, Friend, how did you get in here without wedding clothes? But he was speechless.
local: Nɩɩ owura mʋ taasɛ ɔnyɩn mʋ yɛ, Kyɛmɩnɛ, nɛnɛ nɩɩ fʋ waa fʋʋ nfɩɩ yɛgɛ fʋ man bun kɩkyɩɩfɔ atɔ bunsa? Amaa ɔnyɩn mʋ man taalɛ bugi kɔnɔ.

eng: Therefore, I tell you that Gods kingdom will be taken away from you and will be given to a people who produce its fruit.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ n tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ taa mu kuwura‐gyii mʋ lɩɩ fanɛ asɛ, kɛ ɔ taa sa asa mʋ nɩɩ bee biti ba waa mu kebiti mʋ.

eng: He appointed twelve: Peter, a name he gave Simon.
local: Asa kudu anyɔ mʋ nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ mʋ atɩɩ ndee. Simon .

eng: Then the king said to his servants, Tie his hands and feet and throw him out into the farthest darkness. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ owura mʋ tɔwɛ mʋ ayaafɔlɛ yɛ, Fan ŋmina mʋ abaa yɛ ayaa, kɛ fan lɛɛ mʋ tʋʋ nbwama kitentenbiri mʋ tɔ, tʋtɔ nɩɩ kusu yɛ anobi kiduŋi bʋ. .

eng: Whoever falls on this stone will be crushed. And the stone will crush the person it falls on.
local: Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɩyɛ kubuu nɩmʋ sʋ mʋ, ii biti o boyi tɔ buriburi, amaa ɔmʋ nɩɩ kɩ lɩɩ tɩyɛ mʋ sʋ mʋ, ii biti ɔ patɩyɛ tɔ.

eng: James and John, Zebedees sons, whom he nicknamed Boanerges, which means sons of Thunder.
local: Sebedi mu‐bii ana Gyemisi yɛ Gyɔn, .

eng: Many people are invited, but few people are chosen.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lʋwɛ yɛ, Ba tɩɩ asa gaalaagaa, amaa gbɛrɛɛ falɛ nɩɩ ba lɛɛ.

eng: Now when the chief priests and the Pharisees heard the parable, they knew Jesus was talking about them.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu abɩlɩsa yɛ Farasii abi ba maa nu Yesu aŋasan mʋ, ba bɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ asɩn nɩɩ ɛɛ tɔwɛ.

eng: Then the Pharisees met together to find a way to trap Jesus in his words.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ kamaa mʋ, Farasii abi ba naa gyaŋɛ ŋmina kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋʋ mʋ ɔdʋʋ lɩɩ mʋ asɩn tɔwɛsa tɔ.

eng: and Andrew; Philip; Bartholomew; Matthew; Thomas; James, Alphaeus son; Thaddaeus; Simon the Cananaean.
local: Bamʋ akʋ ɩ gyɛ Andiruwu yɛ Filipu yɛ Batolome yɛ Matiwu yɛ Tomasi yɛ Alifeyo mu‐bii Gyemisi yɛ Simon Seloti .

eng: They sent their disciples, along with the supporters of Herod, to him. Teacher, they said, we know that you are genuine and that you teach Gods way as it really is. We know that you are not swayed by peoples opinions, because you dont show favoritism.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba sun bamʋ abɩɩlapʋ yɛ owura Herodi kamaa tɔ abi kpee Yesu asɛ. Ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɔkaapʋpʋ an nyi fʋ maa gyɛ kesintin gyipu, nɩɩ fɩɩ kaapʋ asa Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn bɔla kesintin ɔkpa sʋ, yɛgɛ fʋ mɛɛ tʋwɛ lɩɩ nfɛɛrɛ mʋ nɩɩ asa bɛɛ fɛ, nɩɩ fʋ mɛɛ kɩɩ nyɩmɩsa kɩkparɛ sʋ.

eng: They were trying to arrest him, but they feared the crowds, who thought he was a prophet.
local: Ba biti ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ ba taa kɩtaa mʋ, amaa ba sɩlɛ sakyɔ mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ asa mʋ be nyi maa gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ nɩn.

eng: and Judas Iscariot, who betrayed Jesus.
local: yɛ Gyuda Isikariyoti, mʋ nɩɩ ɔ ba lɛɛ Yesu sa mʋ.

eng: So tell us what you think: Does the Law allow people to pay taxes to Caesar or not.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ an biti a nu lɩɩ fʋ asɛ yɛ, ɩ dɛ ɔkpa fɛɛ a ka lenpoo sa Roma abi Owura Kasaro, abɛɛ a man ka.

eng: Jesus entered a house. A crowd gathered again so that it was impossible for him and his followers even to eat.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu bwii kpee kewu tɔ. Nɩɩ sakyɔ bɩla ba mʋ asɛ, halɩɩ maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba man nyɛ saŋa kɛ ba gyii.

eng: Knowing their evil motives, Jesus replied, Why do you test me, you hypocrites?.
local: Amaa Yesu maa wʋla o nyi bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ libi sʋ mʋ, ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fanɛ kebunbun ansi wuya ana! Mɩnɛ waa sʋ nɩɩ fɛn sʋʋ mɛ ɔdʋʋ?.

eng: When his family heard what was happening, they came to take control of him. They were saying, Hes out of his mind.
local: Mʋ kʋʋpʋ ana ba maa nu falɛ mʋ, ba sun ba naa kɩtaa mʋ ba, lɩɩ fɛɛ ba nu asa bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ mʋ nfɛɛrɛ i nyita.

eng: Show me the coin used to pay the tax. And they brought him a denarion.
local: Fan taa kederebi mʋ nɩɩ fɛn sʋ fɛn ka lenpoo mʋ kaapʋ mɛ. Nɩɩ ba taa kederebi mʋ ba sa mʋ.

eng: The legal experts came down from Jerusalem. Over and over they charged, Hes possessed by Beelzebul. He throws out demons with the authority of the ruler of demons.
local: Nɩɩ nbara akaapʋpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba lɩɩ Gyerusalem mʋ akʋ ba yɛ, Yesu sʋ Beelisebu mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ nbwɩɩ libi kumu mʋ ɔlʋn. Mʋ ɩɩ sa mʋ ɔlʋn nɩɩ ɛɛ taalɛ ɛɛ gya nbwɩɩ libi falɛ.

eng: Whose image and inscription is this? he asked.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Anɩmʋ foto yɛ kɩtɩɩ ɩ gyan kɩmʋ sʋ falɛ.

eng: When Jesus called them together he spoke to them in a parable: How can Satan throw Satan out?.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ Yesu tɩɩ bamʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ kɩŋasan tɔ yɛ, Ɔkpa mɔɔ sʋ nɩɩ Sɩtaanɛ ɩ taalɛ gya Sɩtaanɛ?.

eng: Caesars, they replied. Then he said, Give to Caesar what belongs to Caesar and to God what belongs to God.
local: Ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Owura lala Kasaro. Ɩmʋ sʋ, Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ Kasaro lɛɛ mʋ, fan ka sa Kasaro, nɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ mʋ, fan taa sa Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: A kingdom involved in civil war will collapse.
local: Nɩɩ ɔsʋwʋlɛ asa ba kan ba barɩgɛ tɔ bɛɛ kɔ yɛ abaa mʋ, ɔsʋwʋlɛ maŋa i boyi.

eng: When they heard this they were astonished, and they departed.
local: Ba maa nu falɛ sʋ mʋ, ɩ waa bamʋ kɩyan, nɩɩ ba yɛgɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ba natɛ kyʋn.

eng: And a house torn apart by divisions will collapse.
local: Nɩɩ kɩkpaara sʋ asa ba kan bɛɛ barɩgɛ tɔ bɛɛ kɔ yɛ abaa mʋ, kɩkpaara maŋa i boyi.

eng: That same day Sadducees, who deny that there is a resurrection, came to Jesus.
local: Kanɩn kakɛ kʋlʋn maŋa mʋ Sadusi abi akʋ ba ba Yesu asɛ. Sadusi abi ba gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ kaapʋ yɛ kikyiŋi ba nkpa tɔ kʋ man bʋ tɔ.

eng: If Satan rebels against himself and is divided, then he cant endure. Hes done for.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ Sɩtaanɛ kuwura‐gyii kɩ kan kɩ barɩgɛ tɔ kɩɩ kɔ yɛ abaa mʋ, kɩɩ tɩyɛ kakpaa.

eng: They asked, Teacher, Moses said, If a man who doesnt have children dies, his brother must marry his wife and produce children for his brother.
local: Ba ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɔkaapʋpʋ, anɛ naana Mosesi kɩkaapʋ mʋ tɔ mʋ, ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, nɩɩ ɔnyɩn kan kili nɩɩ ɔ man kʋʋgɛ nɩɩ o wu mʋ, ɩ dagaa mu‐supu maa bwii kili okulapu‐kyɩɩ mʋ, kɛ ba kʋʋgɛ nbii sa olewupu mʋ.

eng: No one gets into the house of a strong person and steals anything without first tying up the strong person. Only then can the house be burglarized.
local: Ɔkʋ mɛɛ taalɛ loo ɔlʋnpʋ kɩkpaara sʋ naa swii mʋ atɔ natɛ. Amɔɔ ɩ dagaa maa gyankpaa ŋmina mʋ pɔyɩ, kɛ ɔ nyɛ ɔkpa loo mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ yuuri mʋ atɔ.

eng: Now there were seven brothers among us. The first one married, then died. Because he had no children he left his widow to his brother.
local: To! Ɔnyɩn kʋ maa mu‐supu ana asunɔ ba kyɩna ba bʋ anɛ asɛ nfɩɩ. Ɔbɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ kili nɩɩ o wu, yɛgɛ ba man kʋʋgɛ nbii, nɩɩ mu‐supu mʋ taa okulapu‐kyɩɩ mʋ kili.

eng: I assure you that human beings will be forgiven for everything, for all sins and insults of every kind.
local: N gyii fanɛ kesintin fɛɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ ɩɩ taa nyɩmɩsa alibi kʋmaa yɛ mʋ kɩtɔwɛ yii mʋ kʋmaa ɛɛ kɛɛ.

eng: The same thing happened with the second brother and the third, and in fact with all seven brothers.
local: Kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ ɔnyɔsapʋ mʋ yɛ ɔsasapʋ gbaa ba kili mʋ, halɩɩ naa fʋʋ osunɔsapʋ mʋ.

eng: But whoever insults the Holy Spirit will never be forgiven. That person is guilty of a sin with consequences that last forever.
local: Amaa nyɩmɩsa kɩtɔwɛ yii Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ bɩrɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ taa kɛɛ mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ waa alibi mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dɛ kakpaa nɩn.

eng: Finally, the woman died.
local: Laalaalʋwɛ mʋ, ɔkyɩɩ mʋ gbaa ba wu.

eng: He said this because the legal experts were saying, Hes possessed by an evil spirit.
local: Yesu tɔwɛ ɩnɩmʋ nɩn, lɩɩ fɛɛ akʋ bɛɛ yɛ ɔ sʋ nbwɩɩ libi.

eng: At the resurrection, which of the seven brothers will be her husband? They were all married to her.
local: Saamʋ, kakɛ lala mʋ nɩɩ asa bee biti ba kyiŋi lɩɩ lewu tɔ ba nkpa tɔ mʋ, anyɩn asunɔ banɩmʋ tɔ mʋ, anɩmʋ mʋ‐ka ɩ gyɛ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ? Lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ kpini ba kili mʋ.

eng: His mother and brothers arrived. They stood outside and sent word to him, calling for him.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu mu‐nyi yɛ mu‐supu ana ba ba yɩla nbwama, nɩɩ ba sun mʋ asɛ yɛ ba tɩɩ mʋ.

eng: Jesus responded, You are wrong because you dont know either the scriptures or Gods power.
local: Yesu lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Fan fɔyɩ ɔkpa! Lɩɩ fɛɛ fan maa man nu Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ kasɛ, nɩɩ fan man nyi Ɩbwaarɛ ɔlʋn mʋ sʋ.

eng: A crowd was seated around him, and those sent to him said, Look, your mother, brothers, and sisters are outside looking for you.
local: Saŋa maŋa mʋ, asa ba kyɩna kyaabɔɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Kɩɩ fu‐nyi yɛ fu‐supu ana ba bʋ nbwama bee biti fʋ.

eng: At the resurrection people wont marry nor will they be given in marriage. Instead, they will be like angels from God.
local: Kpalɩ fɛɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ alewupu bee biti ba kyiŋi ba nkpa tɔ mʋ, asa ba mɛɛ yɛ ba kili, amaa bee biti ba baa ba du nɩn fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ sʋsʋ mʋ.

eng: He replied, Who is my mother? Who are my brothers.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Anɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ mi‐nyi yɛ mu‐supu ana.

eng: As for the resurrection of the dead, havent you read what God told you.
local: Amaa lɩɩ alewupu kikyiŋi ba nkpa tɔ asɩn mʋ, fan man kalɛ wu asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ sa fanɛ mʋ nɩn?.

eng: Looking around at those seated around him in a circle, he said, Look, here are my mother and my brothers.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ kɩɩ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba tɛ kyaabɔɔ mʋ gyaŋɛ, nɩɩ ɔ yɛ, Fan kɩɩ! Banɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ Mi‐nyi yɛ mi‐supu ana.

eng: Im the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob? He isnt the God of the dead but of the living.
local: Ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Mɛ ɩ gyɛ Abraham Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ Isaki Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ Gyekobu Ɩbwaarɛ. Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ kaapʋ nɩn fɛɛ, asa banɩmʋ ba maa wu gbaa mʋ ba san ba gyɛ asa kɩɩsa Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ, nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ bamʋ Ɩbwaarɛ, man gyɛ alewupu lɛɛ.

eng: Whoever does Gods will is my brother, sister, and mother.
local: Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ waa Ɩbwaarɛ kebiti mʋ, mʋ ɩ gyɛ mi‐supu, yɛ mɛ‐daa, yɛ mɛ‐pɩɩkyɩɩ yɛ mi‐nyi.

eng: Now when the crowd heard this, they were astonished at his teaching.
local: Sakyɔ mʋ maa nu falɛ mʋ, mʋ kɩkaapʋ mʋ ɩ waa bamʋ kɩyan.

eng: When the Pharisees heard that Jesus had left the Sadducees speechless, they met together.
local: Amaa Farasii abi mʋ ba maa nu fɛɛ, Yesu man yɛgɛ nɩɩ Sadusi abi mʋ ba nyɛ bugi kɔnɔ mʋ, ba tɩɩ abaa gyaŋɛ.

eng: One of them, a legal expert, tested him.
local: Nɩɩ bamʋ tɔ ɔkʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ nbara onyipu mʋ taa asɩn taasɛsa sʋʋ Yesu ɔdʋʋ.

eng: Teacher, what is the greatest commandment in the Law.
local: Ɔ taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɔkaapʋpʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ nbara mʋ tɔ mʋ, kɩmɔmɔ ɩ gyɛ kɩkparɛ.

eng: He replied, You must love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your being, and with all your mind.
local: Yesu lɛɛ mʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Taa fʋ kɔkɔlɔ yɛ fʋ kala yɛ fʋ nfɛɛrɛ kpini nyɛ kebiti sa fʋ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: This is the first and greatest commandment.
local: Kɩnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ nbara mʋ tɔ kɩgyankpaasa yɛ kɩkparɛ.

eng: And the second is like it: You must love your neighbor as you love yourself .
local: Nɩɩ kɩnyɔsapʋ mʋ du nɩn fɛɛ kɩmʋ. Ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, Nyɛ kebiti sa fʋ tɔmaa fɛɛ fʋ n‐yɩɩ.

eng: All the Law and the Prophets depend on these two commands.
local: Mosesi nbara mʋ kpini yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ asɩn kaapʋsa mʋ ɩ yɩlɛ nbara nnyɔ nɩmʋ sʋ nɩn.

eng: Now as the Pharisees were gathering, Jesus asked them.
local: Farasii abi ba maa ba gyaŋɛ mʋ, Yesu taasɛ bamʋ yɛ.

eng: What do you think about the Christ? Whose son is he? Davids son, they replied.
local: Mɩnɛ nfɛɛrɛ nɩɩ fan sʋ lɩɩ Krisito mʋ sʋ? Anɩmʋ kanaana‐bii ɩ gyɛ mʋ? Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Owura Dawidi kanaana‐bii.

eng: He said, Then how is it that David, inspired by the Holy Spirit, called him Lord when he said.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Ɩ waa nɛnɛ sʋ nɩɩ Kayaayu tɔ Dawidi tɩɩ mʋ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ? Lɩɩ fɛɛ Dawidi tɔwɛ yɛ.

eng: The Lord said to my lord, Sit at my right side until I turn your enemies into your footstool?.
local: Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ tɔwɛ mɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ yɛ, kyɩna mɛ kɩbaa gyisa sʋ, naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ n biti n taa fʋ dʋn ana waa fʋ ayaa kasɛ. .

eng: If David calls him Lord, how can he be Davids son.
local: Nɩɩ Owura Dawidi maa tɩɩ Krisito yɛ, Mɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ mʋ, nɛnɛ sʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ bɩla taalɛ waa mʋ kanaana‐bii.

eng: Nobody was able to answer him. And from that day forward nobody dared to ask him anything.
local: Bamʋ ɔkʋ man taalɛ bugi kɔnɔ tɔwɛ asɩn akʋ, nɩɩ lɩɩ kanɩn kakɛ maŋa taa kyʋn, ɔkʋ man bɩla taalɛ yɩlɛ tɔ taasɛ mʋ asɩn‐taasɛsa kʋ.

eng: Now Jesus left the temple and was going away. His disciples came to point out to him the temple buildings.
local: Yesu maa lɩɩ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ ee kpee mʋ, mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba ba mʋ asɛ bɛɛ kaapʋ mʋ kanan mʋ nɩɩ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ ibu mʋ i du.

eng: He responded, Do you see all these things? I assure that no stone will be left on another. Everything will be demolished.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Yɛ fan wu atɔ nɩmʋ kpini? N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin yɛ halɩɩ kubuu kʋlʋn gbaa mɛɛ yɛ o sii kɩmʋ bɩrɩsa sʋ, kɛ ba man nin kɩmʋ tʋʋ.

eng: Now while Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives, the disciples came to him privately and said, Tell us, when will these things happen? What will be the sign of your coming and the end of the age.
local: Yesu maa tɛ olifu kɩbɩɩ mʋ sʋ mʋ, mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ nkʋn ɩ ba mʋ asɛ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Tɔwɛ anɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɩnɩmʋ ii biti ɩ ba? Nɩɩ mɩnɛ ii biti ɩ baa ɩ gyɛ fʋ kɩba mʋ yɛ laalaalʋwɛ mʋ sɩnkaala.

eng: Jesus replied, Watch out that no one deceives you.
local: Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Fan baa fan da sʋ, kɛ ɔkʋ ɔ man pɩna fanɛ.

eng: Many will come in my name, saying, Im the Christ. They will deceive many people.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ asa gaalaagaa bee biti ba ba mɛ kɩtɩɩ tɔ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, Mɛ, ɩ gyɛ Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ mʋ, kɛ ba pɩna asa gaalaagaa.

eng: You will hear about wars and reports of wars. Dont be alarmed. These things must happen, but this isnt the end yet.
local: Fen biti fan nu ɩkɔ sʋ kusakusa, kɛ fan nu ɩkɔ asɩn kata‐kata, amaa fan man yɛgɛ fanɛ nkɔlɔ ɩ ŋmaa fanɛ. Kanɩn atɔ maŋa ɩ dagaa ɩ maa waa, amaa ɩ mɛɛ kaapʋ fɛɛ laalaalʋwɛ mʋ tɛɛ fʋʋ.

eng: Nations and kingdoms will fight against each other, and there will be famines and earthquakes in all sorts of places.
local: Ndɛndɛmandɛ bee biti ba kɔ yɛ ndɛndɛmandɛ, kɛ kuwura‐gyii kɩ kɔ yɛ kuwura‐gyii. Akʋn lala yɛ kasɛ kigyingyen ii biti ɩ baa ɩ bʋ katɩn kʋmaa.

eng: But all these things are just the beginning of the sufferings associated with the end.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ kpini i du nɩn fɛɛ keduŋidi gyankpaasa.

eng: They will arrest you, abuse you, and they will kill you. All nations will hate you on account of my name.
local: Nɩɩ bee biti ba taa fanɛ sa kɛ ba nyanla fanɛ kɛ ba mɔɔ fanɛ. Nɩɩ ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ asa kpini bee biti ba kisi fanɛ lɩɩ mɛ sʋ.

eng: At that time many will fall away. They will betray each other and hate each other.
local: Kanɩn saŋa maŋa asa gaalaagaa bee biti ba biliŋi lɩɩ kɩkɔɔlɛ mɛ gyii tɔ, kɛ ba lɛɛ abaa sa kɛ ba kisi abaa.

eng: Many false prophets will appear and deceive many people.
local: nɩɩ antɩŋɛsa atɔwɛpʋ gaalaagaa bee biti ba ba pɩna asa gaalaagaa.

eng: Because disobedience will expand, the love of many will grow cold.
local: Lɩɩ kanan mʋ nɩɩ alibi kɩwaa ɩ lɔŋɔ tii sʋ sʋ mʋ, kɩnyɛ kebiti sa abaa ii biti ɩ ba kasɛ.

eng: But the one who endures to the end will be delivered.
local: amaa ɔmʋnɩ ɔ yɩlɛ tɔ kenken naa lʋwɛ mʋ, ii biti ɔ nyɛ kamɔlɩgɛ.

eng: This gospel of the kingdom will be proclaimed throughout the world as a testimony to all the nations. Then the end will come.
local: Nɩɩ asa bee biti ba tɔwɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn danbɩrasa nɩmʋ lɩɩ mu kuwura‐gyii mʋ sʋ dulinyaa tɔ kpini, kɛ ndɛndɛmandɛ kpini ba nu ɩmʋ, pɔyɩ kɛ laalaalʋwɛ mʋ ɔ baa.

eng: When you see the disgusting and destructive thing that Daniel talked about standing in the holy place.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ fan kan wu kʋtɔ kisisa mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ bɩya alibi kɩ maa yɩlɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Daneli wʋla tɔwɛ yɩla lɩɩ koo dɩdaa mʋ.

eng: then those in Judea must escape to the mountains.
local: saamʋ bamʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Gyudeya mʋ ba sɩlɛ kpee abɩɩ tɔ.

eng: Those on the roof shouldnt come down to grab things from their houses.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan ɔ bʋ obu ketin tɔ mʋ, ɔ man kpɩlɩgɛ ba kasɛ naa taa kʋtɔ kʋ lɩɩ kɩkpaara sʋ.

eng: Those in the field shouldnt come back to grab their clothes.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan ɔ bʋ ndɔɔ tɔ mʋ, ɔ man bwii kpee naa taa mʋ kaalɛ.

eng: How terrible it will be at that time for women who are pregnant and for women who are nursing their children.
local: Kanɩn nkɛ maŋa tɔ mʋ, ɩtɔ wuya ana yɛ atalapʋ, bamʋ laakʋ.

eng: Pray that it doesnt happen in winter or on the Sabbath day.
local: Fan kʋlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ fɛɛ fanɛ kɩkʋsʋ sɩlɛ ɩnɩmʋ ɩ man ba waa awɔɔ saŋa, abɛɛ Kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ.

eng: There will be great suffering such as the world has never before seen and will never again see.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ kanɩn saŋa maa awʋrʋfɔ ii biti ɩ baa ɩ bʋlʋn, nɩɩ awʋrʋfɔ kʋ man pɩɩ ba kanɩn lɩɩ koo dulinyaa npiili asɛ, nɩɩ ɩkʋ man bʋ tɔ ɩ bɩla ba kanɩn.

eng: If that time werent shortened, nobody would be rescued. But for the sake of the ones whom God chose, that time will be cut short.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ man kan tɩn nkɛ mʋ sʋ bɩrɛ, naafɔɔ ɔkʋ man bʋ tɔ ɔ nyɛ kamɔlɩgɛ, amaa lɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ mʋ sʋ mʋ, ee biti ɔ tɩn nkɛ mʋ sʋ.

eng: Then if somebody says to you, Look, heres the Christ, or Hes over here, dont believe it.
local: Kanɩn saŋa maŋa nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan tɔwɛ fʋ yɛ, Kɩɩ, Krisito mʋ bʋ nfɩɩ abɛɛ Mʋ ɩ bʋ falɛ mʋ, man kɔɔlɛ ɩmʋ gyii.

eng: False christs and false prophets will appear, and they will offer great signs and wonders in order to deceive, if possible, even those whom God has chosen.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ antɩŋɛsa Krisito yɛ antɩŋɛsa Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ bee biti ba ba, kɛ ba waa ɩsɩnkaala lala yɛ adangana kɛ ɩ nyɛ taalɛ pɩna bamʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ mʋ gbaa.

eng: Look, Ive told you ahead of time.
local: Fan kɩɩ, n gyankpaa n tɔwɛ fanɛ ɩnɩmʋ nɩn pɔyɩ kɛ ɩ ba.

eng: So if they say to you, Look, hes in the desert, dont go out. And if they say, Look, hes in the rooms deep inside the house, dont believe it.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, Krisito mʋ ɩ lɩɩ ɔ bʋ keperi sʋ falɛ mʋ, fan man kpee tʋtɔ, abɛɛ ɔkʋ kan tɔwɛ yɛ, Krisito mʋ baala nfɩɩ mʋ, fan man kɔɔlɛ gyii.

eng: Just as the lightning flashes from the east to the west, so it will be with the coming of the Human One.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ, kanan mʋ nɩɩ bʋlayɩ ɩ wɛɛ ɩsɛɛ lɩɩ kyɔwɛ kɩlɩɩ, nɩɩ bee wu kɩmʋ halɩɩ kyɔwɛ kɩtɩyɛ mʋ, kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ kɩba ii biti ɩ baa i du.

eng: The vultures gather wherever theres a dead body.
local: Katɩn kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ kʋtɔ belenbuni ɔsa bʋ mʋ, tʋtɔ maŋa nɩɩ agyinbwii bɛɛ lantɛ.

eng: Now immediately after the suffering of that time the sun will become dark, and the moon wont give its light. The stars will fall from the sky and the planets and other heavenly bodies will be shaken.
local: Saŋa maŋa awʋrʋfɔ mʋ kamaa mʋ, opula tɔ nɩɩ kyɔwɛ ii biti o bii tɔ, nɩɩ ɔbwaayɩ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ baa ɔ kɩɩ tɔ, nɩɩ akyikpebi ii biti ɩɩ lɩɩ sʋsʋ tɩyɛ, kɛ atɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ sʋsʋ mʋ i gyingyen.

eng: Then the sign of the Human One will appear in the sky. At that time all the tribes of the earth will be full of sadness, and they will see the Human One coming in the heavenly clouds with power and great splendor.
local: Kanɩn saŋa maŋa mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ sɩnkaala ii biti ɩ lɩɩ sʋsʋ, nɩɩ ndɛndɛmandɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ kasɛ sʋ mʋ bee biti ba bɔɔta tɔ, fɛɛ ba maa wu mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ n maa lɩɩ abwaarɛ‐wʋlɛ tɔ sʋsʋ n ba yɛ ɔlʋn yɛ bʋnyaa lala mʋ.

eng: He will send his angels with the sound of a great trumpet, and they will gather his chosen ones from the four corners of the earth, from one end of the sky to the other.
local: Nɩɩ n biti n sun mɛ nbɔɔ, kɛ ba fʋlɛ nbɩlɛ kenken, kɛ ba bʋga bamʋ nɩɩ n lɛɛ mʋ lɩɩ dulinyaa abɔntɔnbʋlɔn kpini.

eng: Learn this parable from the fig tree. After its branch becomes tender and it sprouts new leaves, you know that summer is near.
local: Nbɩɩnbɩɩ, fan bɩɩla asɩn lɩɩ figi oyu mʋ asɛ. Nɩɩ ɩmʋ n‐yaabi ɩ kan ɩɩ pɔrɔgɛ nɩɩ ɩɩ lɛɛ afatɛɛ pʋpwɛ mʋ, fɛn bɩɩ fɛɛ kɩbɩlɛ saŋa tiri tɔ nɩmʋ.

eng: In the same way, when you see all these things, you know that the Human One is near, at the door.
local: Kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ fan wu ɩnɩmʋ kpini mʋ, fan bɩɩ fɛɛ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa kɩba mʋ ki tiri tɔ, fɛɛ n ba.

eng: I assure you that this generation wont pass away until all these things happen.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin fɛɛ ndaga kakyɩna nɩmʋ tɔ asa ba mɛɛ yɛ ba wu lʋwɛ, pɔyɩ kɛ falɛ asɩn nɩmʋ kpini ɩ waa.

eng: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will certainly not pass away.
local: Sʋsʋ yɛ kasɛ ii biti ɩ kyʋn, amaa mɛ asɩn ɩ man bʋ tɔ ɩ kyʋn.

eng: But nobody knows when that day or hour will come, not the heavenly angels and not the Son. Only the Father knows.
local: Amaa lɩɩ kakɛ mʋ abɛɛ kerefi mʋ nɩɩ ɩnɩmʋ ii biti ɩ waa mʋ ɔkʋ man nyi. Sʋsʋ nbɔɔ ba man nyi, halɩɩ mɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ mu‐Bii mʋ gbaa man nyi, amɔɔ Ɔsɛ mʋ nkʋn.

eng: As it was in the time of Noah, so it will be at the coming of the Human One.
local: Kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɩ kyɩna i du Nowa saŋa mʋ, kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ ii biti ɩ baa i du mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ kɩba saŋa.

eng: In those days before the flood, people were eating and drinking, marrying and giving in marriage, until the day Noah entered the ark.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ pɔyɩ kɛ nkyu i muuri dulinyaa mʋ, asa ba kyɩna ba maa sʋ bee gyii yɛgɛ bee nuu, anyɩn yɛ akyɩɩ bee kili, naa fʋʋ kakɛ mʋ nɩɩ Nowa loo ɔkʋlɩ mʋ tɔ.

eng: They didnt know what was happening until the flood came and swept them all away. The coming of the Human One will be like that.
local: yɛgɛ ba man nyi kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kii biti kɩ ba, halɩɩ nkyu ɩ ba taa bamʋ kpini kyʋn. Kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ ii biti ɩ waa mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa kɩba mʋ saŋa.

eng: At that time there will be two men in the field. One will be taken and the other left.
local: Anyɩn anyɔ bee biti ba baa ba bʋ ndɔɔ tɔ, n biti n taa ɔkʋ kɛ n yɛgɛ ɔkʋ.

eng: Two women will be grinding at the mill. One will be taken and the other left.
local: Akyɩɩ anyɔ bee biti ba baa bɛɛ kɔyɩ kubuu sʋ, n biti n taa ɔkʋ yɛgɛ ɔkʋ.

eng: Therefore, stay alert! You dont know what day the Lord is coming.
local: Kanɩn sʋ fan baa fan da sʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ fan man nyi kakɛ mʋ nɩɩ mɛ, fanɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ mʋ ii biti ɔ ba.

eng: But you understand that if the head of the house knew at what time the thief would come, he would keep alert and wouldnt allow the thief to break into his house.
local: Fan baa fen nyi ɩnɩmʋ fɛɛ, nɩɩ kɩkpaara wuya dɩn o nyi kagyanbwɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ oyu ii biti ɔ ba nɩn, naafɔɔ ɔ dɩn kyɩna kun, kɛ oyu mʋ ɔ man ba nyɛ boyi loo mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ yuuri.

eng: Therefore, you also should be prepared, because the Human One will come at a time you dont know.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ fanɛ gbaa fanɛ fan kʋʋla fan gyoo, lɩɩ fɛɛ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa n biti n ba saŋa mʋ nɩɩ fan man kɩɩ mɛ ɔkpa.

eng: Who then are the faithful and wise servants whom their master puts in charge of giving food at the right time to those who live in his house?.
local: Anɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ kɩyaafɔlɛ kesintin gyipu yɛ kanyaasɩn wuya? Mʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ mʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ taa mʋ waa mʋ ayaafɔlɛ mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ kɩkpaara sʋ mʋ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ fɛɛ ɔ sa bamʋ agyitɔ saŋa kyɛɛkyɛɛ tɔ.

eng: Happy are those servants whom the master finds fulfilling their responsibilities when he comes.
local: Ii biti ɩ baa ɩ bʋrɔn sa kanɩn kɩyaafɔlɛ maŋa fɛɛ mʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ maa ba tu mʋ yɛgɛ ɛɛ waa kanɩn.

eng: I assure you that he will put them in charge of all his possessions.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin yɛ, mʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ mʋ ii biti ɔ taa mʋ kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ waa mʋ abaa tɔ.

eng: But suppose those bad servants should say to themselves, My master wont come until later.
local: Amaa a taa fɛɛ kanɩn kɩyaafɔlɛ maŋa libi nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Mɛ‐nyɩrɩpɛ ii biti ɔ kyɩna tʋtɔ kyɛɛrɛɛ.

eng: And suppose they began to beat their fellow servants and to eat and drink with the drunks?.
local: kɛ o piili ɛɛ dayɩ mʋ bɩrɩsa ana ayaafɔlɛ mʋ, yɛgɛ ee gyii ee nuu maa atabʋʋpʋ.

eng: The master of those servants will come on a day when they are not expecting him, at a time they couldnt predict.
local: Kanɩn kɩyaafɔlɛ maŋa mʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ ii biti ɔ ba kakɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man kɩɩ mʋ ɔkpa, yɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man nyi sʋ.

eng: He will cut them in pieces and put them in a place with the hypocrites. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: Ee biti ɔ bɩɩtɛ kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ kʋsʋ gaa, kɛ ɔ gya mʋ naa waa kebunbun ansi wuya opula, katɩn mʋ nɩɩ bee biti ba baa bɛɛ saawʋ yɛgɛ bee duŋi bamʋ anobi mʋ.

eng: After the Sabbath, at dawn on the first day of the week, Mary Magdalene and the other Mary came to look at the tomb.
local: Kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ kamaa tɔ, ɩmʋ Kwasida akɛ nyenyenfuu mʋ, Mɛɛrɩ Magidalen maa Mɛɛrɩ banban mʋ, ba kpee naa kɩɩ kakyan mʋ.

eng: Look, there was a great earthquake, for an angel from the Lord came down from heaven. Coming to the stone, he rolled it away and sat on it.
local: Opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ kasɛ ki gyingyen gaa. Lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ kpɩlɩgɛ lɩɩ sʋsʋ ba gbiliti kubuu mʋ nɩɩ ki tii kakyan mʋ kɔnɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kyʋn kyɩna kɩmʋ sʋ.

eng: Now his face was like lightning and his clothes as white as snow.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ ɩɩ ŋalɩgɛ sʋ fɛɛ bʋlayɩ maa wɛɛ ɩsɛɛ, nɩɩ mʋ atɔ bunsa mɔɔ ii fuuli parɛparɛ fɛɛ ateebi.

eng: The guards were so terrified of him that they shook with fear and became like dead men.
local: Nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ bee kun kakyan mʋ, ba maa wu kabɔɔ mʋ, kufuu kɩ kɩtaa bamʋ, nɩɩ ba kyɩkyaa tɩyɛ fɛɛ asa wusa mʋ.

eng: But the angel said to the women, Dont be afraid. I know that you are looking for Jesus who was crucified.
local: Nɩɩ kabɔɔ mʋ tɔwɛ akyɩɩ mʋ yɛ, Fan man waa kufuu, lɩɩ fɛɛ n nyi fan maa kɩɩ fen biti Yesu mʋ nɩɩ ba da mʋ mata oyu laasa sʋ mʋ nɩn.

eng: He isnt here, because hes been raised from the dead, just as he said. Come, see the place where they laid him.
local: Ɔ man bʋ nfɩɩ, o kyiŋi lɩɩ lewu tɔ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ wʋla tɔwɛ mʋ. Fan ba kɩɩ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba daa taa mʋ yɩla mʋ.

eng: Now hurry, go and tell his disciples, Hes been raised from the dead. Hes going on ahead of you to Galilee. You will see him there. Ive given the message to you.
local: Fan bwii bilen naa tɔwɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ, O kyiŋi lɩɩ lewu tɔ ba nkpa tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ gyankpaa fanɛ ee kpee Galile ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ fen biti fan wu mʋ. Fan nu, n tɔwɛ fanɛ nɩmʋ.

eng: With great fear and excitement, they hurried away from the tomb and ran to tell his disciples.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ akyɩɩ mʋ, ba kpala lɩɩ kakyan mʋ asɛ, ba sʋ kufuu, yɛgɛ bamʋ ansi i gyii. Nɩɩ ba sɩlɛ ba naa tɔwɛ Yesu abɩɩlapʋ mʋ.

eng: But Jesus met them and greeted them. They came and grabbed his feet and worshipped him.
local: Opula tɔ nɩɩ Yesu gyaŋa bamʋ ɔkpa tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ yɛ, N fwaala fanɛ. Nɩɩ ba nin tiri mʋ tɔ, kɩtaa mʋ ayaa tɔ muŋa sun mʋ.

eng: Then Jesus said to them, Dont be afraid. Go and tell my brothers that I am going into Galilee. They will see me there.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan man waa kufuu! Fan kpee naa tɔwɛ mɛ bɩrɩsa ana yɛ ba kpee Galile ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ, tʋtɔ nɩɩ bee biti ba wu mɛ.

eng: Now as the women were on their way, some of the guards came into the city and told the chief priests everything that had happened.
local: Akyɩɩ mʋ ba maa san ba bʋ ɔkpa tɔ bee kpee mʋ, kakyan mʋ akunpu mʋ akʋ ba kpee Gyerusalem kadɛ tɔ naa buu sa aseepu abɩlɩsa kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ waa.

eng: They met with the elders and decided to give a large sum of money to the soldiers.
local: Aseepu bɩlɩsa yɛ abɩlɩsa ba maa ba gyaŋɛ mʋ, ba ŋmina yɩla kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba waa. Ɩmʋ sʋ ba sa anaakɔpʋ akunpu mu afulee kʋkyɔ.

eng: They told them, Say that Jesus disciples came at night and stole his body while you were sleeping.
local: lan tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan tɔwɛ yɛ, Mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ɩ ba kagyanbwɛ ba yuuri Yesu kibuni mʋ taa kyʋn, saŋa mʋ nɩɩ an dɛ nsɩɩrɛ mʋ.

eng: And if the governor hears about this, we will take care of it with him so you will have nothing to worry about.
local: Nɩɩ falɛ asɩn nɩmʋ kan naa fʋʋ Ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ Paleti asɛ mʋ, anɛ ii biti a tɔwɛ yɩlɛ fanɛ kamaa, kɛ ɔ man waa fanɛ sɛhn.

eng: So the soldiers took the money and did as they were told. And this report has spread throughout all Judea to this very day.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ anaakɔpʋ mʋ ba kɔɔlɛ afulee mʋ, nɩɩ ba waa fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ ba waa mʋ. Nɩɩ falɛ asɩn nɩmʋ ɩ yaasɛ loo Gyiwu abi mʋ tɔ ba lɩɩ halɩɩ ndaga.

eng: Now the eleven disciples went to Galilee, to the mountain where Jesus told them to go.
local: Nɩɩ abɩɩlapʋ kudu ɔkʋ mʋ ba kpee kɩbɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ Galile ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ, katɩn mʋ nɩɩ Yesu wʋla tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ ba kpee mʋ.

eng: When they saw him, they worshipped him, but some doubted.
local: Ba maa wu Yesu mʋ, ba muŋa sun mʋ, nɩɩ bamʋ akʋ ba gyii ɩmɔɔrɛ yɛ man gyɛ mʋ nɩn.

eng: Jesus came near and spoke to them, Ive received all authority in heaven and on earth.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu nin tiri bamʋ, tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ taa sʋsʋ yɛ kasɛ sʋ ɔlʋn kpini sa mɛ.

eng: Therefore, go and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan kpee dulinyaa tɔ katɩn kʋmaa, naa waa asa kpini mɛ abɩɩlapʋ. Fan gyee bamʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee, lɩɩ Ɔsɛ yɛ Obii yɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ kɩtɩɩ tɔ.

eng: teaching them to obey everything that Ive commanded you. Look, I myself will be with you every day until the end of this present age.
local: kɛ fan kaapʋ bamʋ kɛ ba buu kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ n sa fanɛ kɔnɔ yɛ fan waa mʋ. Fan nyiŋi fɛɛ n bʋ fanɛ asɛ saŋa kʋmaa naa fʋʋ dulinyaa laalaalʋwɛ.

eng: The beginning of the good news about Jesus Christ, Gods Son.
local: Asɩn danbɩrasa lɩɩ Yesu Krisito, Ɩbwaarɛ Mu‐Bii mʋ sʋ mʋ ndee.

eng: happened just as it was written about in the prophecy of Isaiah: Look, I am sending my messenger before you . He will prepare your way.
local: Asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ i piili fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Isaya wʋla kyʋrɔɔ yɩla mʋ. Ɔ kyʋrɔɔ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla tɔwɛ Mu‐Bii Yesu Krisito mʋ yɛ, Kɩɩ, n biti n sun mɛ kabɔɔ kɛ ɔ gyankpaa fʋ, mʋ ii biti ɔ ba lɔŋɔ fʋ ɔkpa mʋ.

eng: a voice shouting in the wilderness: Prepare the way for the Lord; make his paths straight .
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Isaya bɩla kyʋrɔɔ lɩɩ Gyɔn sʋ yɛ, Ɔnyɩn kʋ bʋ ofuli sʋ ee kuusi ɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Fan lɔŋɔ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ ɔkpa mʋ, kɛ fan kyiigi mʋ ɩkpa‐bi mʋ. .

eng: John the Baptist was in the wilderness calling for people to be baptized to show that they were changing their hearts and lives and wanted God to forgive their sins.
local: Asɩn mʋ ɩ ba kanɩn. Ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ Gyɔn maa ba ɔ naa ofuli sʋ ee gyee asa Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee, yɛgɛ ɛɛ tɔwɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn ɛɛ sa asa yɛ, Fan yɛgɛ alibi kɩwaa, kɛ n gyee fanɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee, kɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ taa fanɛ alibi kɛɛ fanɛ.

eng: Everyone in Judea and all the people of Jerusalem went out to the Jordan River and were being baptized by John as they confessed their sins.
local: Kesintin mɔɔ, asa gaalaagaa ba lɩɩ Gyudeya ɩsʋwʋlɛ sʋ yɛ Gyerusalem kadɛ lala mʋ tɔ bee kpee Gyɔn asɛ ofuli maŋa sʋ, bɛɛ tɔwɛ alibi mʋ nɩɩ ba waa, yɛgɛ Gyɔn i gyee bamʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee Gyɔdan ɔbʋn mʋ tɔ.

eng: John wore clothes made of camels hair, with a leather belt around his waist. He ate locusts and wild honey.
local: Nyɔɔma ifuu nɩɩ ba taa lʋʋ Gyɔn kaalɛ bunsa, nɩɩ atɔbwaaya ɔwʋlɛ mɔɔ nɩɩ ɔ kyaŋɛ waa mʋ kɩrɩsʋ. Mʋ agyitɔ gyisa mɔɔ ɩ gyɛ ntuntunbi yɛ ŋmasan.

eng: He announced, One stronger than I am is coming after me. Im not even worthy to bend over and loosen the strap of his sandals.
local: Gyɔn ɩɩ tɔwɛ asa mʋ yɛ, Ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ mɛ kamaa ɛɛ ba mʋ, kyɔ mɛ gaa, nɩɩ n man dagaa gbaa fɛɛ n muŋa lɛɛ mʋ asɩbɩta.

eng: I baptize you with water, but he will baptize you with the Holy Spirit.
local: Mɛɛ mɛ, nkyu nɩɩ n sʋ n gyee fanɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee, amaa mɛɛ mʋ bɩrɛ, ee biti ɔ taa Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ ee gyee fanɛ.

eng: About that time, Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee, and John baptized him in the Jordan River.
local: Tʋtɔ mʋ, Yesu lɩɩ Nasareti kadɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ Galile kasɛ sʋ mʋ ba Gyɔn asɛ. Nɩɩ Gyɔn gyee mʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee Gyɔdan ɔbʋn mʋ tɔ.

eng: While he was coming up out of the water, Jesus saw heaven splitting open and the Spirit, like a dove, coming down on him.
local: Yesu maa dan lɩɩ ɔbʋn mʋ tɔ yɩlɛ mʋ, o wu sʋsʋ maa bugi tɔ, nɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ kpɩlɩgɛ lɩɩ sʋsʋ fɛɛ kuwura‐lɔpɔ ba gyɔŋɔ Yesu sʋ.

eng: And there was a voice from heaven: You are my Son, whom I dearly love; in you I find happiness.
local: Nɩɩ ɔbʋlɛ tɔwɛ lɩɩ sʋsʋ yɛ, Fʋ ɩ gyɛ mɛ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ obii bitisa. Nɩɩ fʋ ɩ bɔla mi ansi.

eng: At once the Spirit forced Jesus out into the wilderness.
local: Opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ yɛgɛ nɩɩ Yesu kpee ofuli sʋ.

eng: He was in the wilderness for forty days, tempted by Satan. He was among the wild animals, and the angels took care of him.
local: Yesu kyɩna ofuli maŋa sʋ nkɛ adɩna. Maa bʋ tʋtɔ mʋ, Sɩtaanɛ naa tʋʋ mʋ tɔ kɩɩ. Maa nfatabwɛ nkʋn ɩ bʋ tʋtɔ. Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ ba ba kyɛ mʋ tɔ.

eng: After John was arrested, Jesus came into Galilee announcing Gods good news.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba kɩtaa Gyɔn tii obu kamaa mʋ, Yesu kpee Galile kasɛ sʋ, ɔ naa ɛɛ tɔwɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ ɛɛ sa asa.

eng: saying, Now is the time! Here comes Gods kingdom! Change your hearts and lives, and trust this good news.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ ki tiri tɔ. Ɩmʋ sʋ fan yɛgɛ alibi kɩwaa kɛ fan kɔɔlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ gyii.

eng: As Jesus passed alongside the Galilee Sea, he saw two brothers, Simon and Andrew, throwing fishing nets into the sea, for they were fishermen.
local: Yesu maa naa Galile ɔbʋn kaa mʋ, o wu ɩkɩn akɩtapʋ anyɔ akʋ, Simon maa mu‐supu Andiruwu, ba maa tʋʋ kisewu ɔbʋn mʋ tɔ.

eng: Come, follow me, he said, and Ill show you how to fish for people.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan ba buu mɛ, kɛ n waa fanɛ asa akɩtapʋ.

eng: Right away, they left their nets and followed him.
local: Opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ ba yɛgɛ bamʋ asewu mʋ yɩla, nɩɩ ba buu Yesu.

eng: After going a little farther, he saw James and John, Zebedees sons, in their boat repairing the fishing nets.
local: Yesu maa bɩla kpee mu ansi tɔ gbɛrɛɛ mʋ, ɔ bɩla wu Gyemisi maa mu‐supu Gyɔn, Sebedi mu‐bii ana, ba bʋ bamʋ ɔkʋlɩ tɔ bɛɛ lɔŋɔ bamʋ asewu.

eng: At that very moment he called them. They followed him, leaving their father Zebedee in the boat with the hired workers.
local: Yesu maa dan wu bamʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ bamʋ. Nɩɩ ba bwiiyaa bamʋ‐sɛ Sebedi maa mʋ ayaafɔlɛ yɛgɛ ɔkʋlɩ tɔ, nɩɩ ba natɛ buu Yesu.

eng: Jesus and his followers went into Capernaum. Immediately on the Sabbath Jesus entered the synagogue and started teaching.
local: Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba natɛ ba Kapanum kadɛ tɔ. Kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ maa fʋʋ mʋ, Yesu kpee kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa naa kaapʋ bamʋ abwaarɛsɩn.

eng: The people were amazed by his teaching, for he was teaching them with authority, not like the legal experts.
local: Asa mʋ ba maa nu mʋ kɩkaapʋ mʋ, ɩ waa bamʋ kɩyan, lɩɩ fɛɛ mʋ kɩkaapʋ mʋ sʋ ɔlʋn, nɩɩ kɩ kwɩɩ yɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ mʋ lɛɛ mʋ.

eng: After a few days, Jesus went back to Capernaum, and people heard that he was at home.
local: Ɩ man gyii nkɛ nkʋ nɩɩ Yesu bwii kpee Kapanum, nɩɩ asa ba nu fɛɛ ɔ baa ɔ bʋ kɩkpaara sʋ.

eng: Suddenly, there in the synagogue, a person with an evil spirit screamed.
local: Bamʋ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa mʋ tɔ mʋ, ɔnyɩn kʋ mʋ nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi ba tɛ mʋ sʋ mʋ, kuusi kenken taasɛ Yesu yɛ.

eng: So many gathered that there was no longer space, not even near the door. Jesus was speaking the word to them.
local: Nɩɩ asa kʋkyɔ ba ba bɔla tʋtɔ, nɩɩ ɔkpa man bɩla ɔ bʋ tɔ, halɩɩ kebunɔ gbaa, yɛgɛ Yesu ɩ tɔwɛ bamʋ abwaarɛsɩn.

eng: What have you to do with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are. You are the holy one from God.
local: Yesu, Kanasareti‐nyɩn, mɩnɛ nɩɩ fii biti anɛ asɛ nfɩɩ? Fʋ ba kɛ fʋ ba nyita anɛ nɩn, abɛɛ nɛnɛ? N nyi ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ fʋ gyɛ. Fʋ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Nyɩmɩsa kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ.

eng: Some people arrived, and four of them were bringing to him a man who was paralyzed.
local: Nɩɩ asa ana akʋ ba sʋla mʋgʋlɛ‐mʋgʋlɛ‐nyɩn boobi kʋ bɛɛ bɩya Yesu.

eng: Silence! Jesus said, speaking harshly to the demon. Come out of him.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu pʋntɛ kabwɩɩ libi mʋ sʋ yɛ, Laatɔ, kɛ fʋ kʋsʋ ɔnyɩn mʋ sʋ.

eng: They couldnt carry him through the crowd, so they tore off part of the roof above where Jesus was. When they had made an opening, they lowered the mat on which the paralyzed man was lying.
local: Amaa sakyɔ sʋ mʋ, ba man nyɛ ɔkpa, nɩɩ ba taa ɔnyɩn mʋ dii obu mʋ sʋsʋ, katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba taa ɩsɛ nyalɛ munla mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ba foti ɔbɔ opula mʋ nɩɩ Yesu bʋ mʋ kyɛɛkyɛɛ, nɩɩ ba kpɩlɩgaa ɔlɔpʋ mʋ, yɛgɛ ɔ dɛ mʋ kalan sʋ ba yɩla kasɛ.

eng: The unclean spirit shook him and screamed, then it came out.
local: Nɩɩ kabwɩɩ libi mʋ ki gyingyen ɔnyɩn mʋ, nɩɩ o kuusi kenken kʋsʋ ɔnyɩn mʋ sʋ.

eng: When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralytic, Child, your sins are forgiven.
local: Yesu maa wu kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ ɔnyɩn mʋ yɛ, Mi‐bii, n taa fʋ alibi kɛɛ fʋ.

eng: Everyone was shaken and questioned among themselves, Whats this? A new teaching with authority! He even commands unclean spirits and they obey him.
local: Kanɩn asɩn nɩmʋ ɩ waa asa mʋ kɩyan, nɩɩ bɛɛ taasɛ abaa yɛ, Mɩnɛ asɩn ndee? Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ bɩla ɩ gyɛ kɩkaapʋ pʋpwɛ kʋ nɩn? Kɩɩ, ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ sʋ ɔlʋn mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ pʋntɛ nbwɩɩ libi sʋ nɩɩ bee nu mʋ asɛ.

eng: Some legal experts were sitting there, muttering among themselves.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ akʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba tɛ tʋtɔ mʋ bɛɛ fɛ nfɛɛrɛ bamʋ amu tɔ yɛ.

eng: Right away the news about him spread throughout the entire region of Galilee.
local: Tʋtɔ mʋ, Yesu asɩn nɩmʋ a tɛɛ naa nu Galile ɔsʋwʋlɛ mʋ kpini gyaŋɛ.

eng: Why does he speak this way? Hes insulting God. Only the one God can forgive sins.
local: Mɩnɛ kʋtɔ nbusaa asɩn nɩɩ ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ ɩ tɔwɛ falɛ? Anɩmʋ ɩɩ taalɛ taa alibi ɛɛ kɛɛ tii Ɩbwaarɛ sʋ.

eng: After leaving the synagogue, Jesus, James, and John went home with Simon and Andrew.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba lɩɩ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa mʋ tɔ kpee Simon maa Andiruwu kɩkpaara sʋ, nɩɩ Gyemisi maa Gyɔn gbaa ba tii sʋ.

eng: Jesus immediately recognized what they were discussing, and he said to them, Why do you fill your minds with these questions?.
local: Opula tɔ nɩɩ Yesu bɩɩ mʋ nfɛɛrɛ tɔ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ fɛ, nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ fɛn fɛ kanɩn nfɛɛrɛ?.

eng: Simons mother-in-law was in bed, sick with a fever, and they told Jesus about her at once.
local: Simon mʋ‐saa‐kyɩɩsa ɩɩ lɔ kibirima ɔ dɛ kalan sʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ Yesu maa loo kɩkpaara mʋ sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ba buu sa mʋ yɛ ɔkyɩɩ maŋa ɩɩ lɔ.

eng: Which is easier—to say to a paralyzed person, Your sins are forgiven, or to say, Get up, take up your bed, and walk?.
local: Ɩmʋ ɩmɔɔ ɩ bʋsɩn kɩtɔwɛ? Fɛɛ n tɔwɛ mʋgʋlɛ‐mʋgʋlɛ mʋ yɛ, N taa fʋ alibi kɛɛ fʋ, abɛɛ n tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Kʋsʋ kɛ fʋ taa fʋ kalan natɛ.

eng: He went to her, took her by the hand, and raised her up. The fever left her, and she served them.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu loo ɔkyɩɩ mʋ asɛ obu‐to, kɩtaa mʋ kɩbaa tɔ, kʋsaa mʋ kyɩna. Nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ nyɛ alanfɩya, nɩɩ ɔ waa agyitɔ kɩtaa bamʋ kɩfɔ.

eng: But so you will know that the Human One has authority on the earth to forgive sins—he said to the man who was paralyzed.
local: N biti n kaapʋ fanɛ yɛ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ n sʋ ɔlʋn mʋ nɩɩ n taalɛ taa alibi kɛɛ dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ. Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋgʋlɛ‐mʋgʋlɛ mʋ yɛ.

eng: That evening, at sunset, people brought to Jesus those who were sick or demon-possessed.
local: Kalaatɩpwɛɛ maŋa kyɔwɛ maa dan ɛɛ tɩyɛ mʋ, nɩɩ asa ba taa alɔpʋ yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi ba tɛ bamʋ sʋ bɩya Yesu.

eng: Get up, take your mat, and go home.
local: Mɛ ɩ tɔwɛ fʋ fɛɛ, kʋsʋ taa fʋ kalan kɛ fu kpewu.

eng: The whole town gathered near the door.
local: Kadɛ mʋ asa kpini ba ba gyaŋɛ kɩkpaara maŋa kebunɔ.

eng: Jesus raised him up, and right away he picked up his mat and walked out in front of everybody. They were all amazed and praised God, saying, Weve never seen anything like this.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ kʋsʋ taa mʋ kalan waa bilen kpewu, yɛgɛ asa ba kɩɩ mʋ, nɩɩ i kyinkyin bamʋ gaa, nɩɩ ba yɩn Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ fɛɛ, An man pɩɩta wu falɛ asɩn nɩmʋ kakaakʋ.

eng: He healed many who were sick with all kinds of diseases, and he threw out many demons. But he didnt let the demons speak, because they recognized him.
local: Yesu kyɛ asa gaalaagaa alobi, nɩɩ ɔ gya nbwɩɩ libi lɩɩ asa gaalaagaa sʋ. Nbwɩɩ libi mʋ maa nyi Yesu sʋ mʋ, ɔ man sa bamʋ ɔkpa yɛ ba tɔwɛ kʋtɔ kʋ.

eng: Jesus went out beside the lake again. The whole crowd came to him, and he began to teach them.
local: Yesu bwii kpee Galile ɔbʋn‐kaa, nɩɩ asa damantɛ ba ba mʋ asɛ, nɩɩ o piili ɛɛ kaapʋ bamʋ.

eng: Early in the morning, well before sunrise, Jesus rose and went to a deserted place where he could be alone in prayer.
local: Kyaasɛ gyankpaasa maa dan bʋn mʋ, nɩɩ Yesu kʋsʋ lɩɩ kɩkpaara kpee opula mʋ nɩɩ ki du surum, kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ kʋlɛ.

eng: As he continued along, he saw Levi, Alphaeus son, sitting at a kiosk for collecting taxes. Jesus said to him, Follow me. Levi got up and followed him.
local: Maa kyʋn ee kpee mʋ, o wu Alifeyo mu‐bii Lewi maa tɛ lenpoo ɔkɔɔlɛkpa. Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Ba buu mɛ. Nɩɩ Lewi kʋsʋ buu mʋ.

eng: Simon and those with him tracked him down.
local: Tʋtɔ mʋ, Simon maa mʋ bɩrɩsa ana ba naa bee biti Yesu.

eng: Jesus sat down to eat at Levis house. Many tax collectors and sinners were eating with Jesus and his disciples. Indeed, many of them had become his followers.
local: Ɩmʋ kamaa tɔ, Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba tɛ bee gyii Lewi kɩkpaara sʋ, nɩɩ lenpoo akɔɔlɛpʋ yɛ alibi awaapʋ damantɛ ba buu mʋ, nɩɩ bamʋ gaalaagaa ba be tii mʋ sʋ bee gyii agyitɔ.

eng: When they found him, they told him, Everyones looking for you.
local: Ba maa naa wu mʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ naa ee biti fʋ.

eng: When some of the legal experts from among the Pharisees saw that he was eating with sinners and tax collectors, they asked his disciples, Why is he eating with sinners and tax collectors.
local: Nɩɩ nbara akaapʋpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba gyɛ Farasii abi mʋ, ba maa wu fɛɛ Yesu maa alibi awaapʋ yɛ lenpoo akɔɔlɛpʋ ba maa gyii mʋ, ba taasɛ yɛ, Mɩnɛ kʋtɔ sʋ nɩɩ maa falɛ asa ɩ tɛ bee gyii.

eng: He replied, Lets head in the other direction, to the nearby villages, so that I can preach there too. Thats why Ive come.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu biliŋi tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan yɛgɛ kɛ a kpee ndɛ‐bii mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ tiri nfɩɩ mʋ, kɛ n nyɛ naa tɔwɛ abwaarɛsɩn tʋtɔ. Ɩnɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ n lɩɩ sʋsʋ ba kasɛ sʋ nfɩɩ.

eng: When Jesus heard it, he said to them, Healthy people dont need a doctor, but sick people do. I didnt come to call righteous people, but sinners.
local: Yesu nu kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Bamʋ nɩɩ ba mɛɛ lɔ mʋ, be mee biti ɔkyɛpʋ, amɔɔ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ lɔ. N man ba nɩn kɛ n ba tɩɩ adanbɩrasa awaapʋ, amɔɔ alibi awaapʋ.

eng: He traveled throughout Galilee, preaching in their synagogues and throwing out demons.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔ natɛ Galile ɩsʋwʋlɛ mʋ kpini gyaŋɛ ɛɛ tɔwɛ abwaarɛsɩn bamʋ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɩgyaŋɛkpa, yɛgɛ ɛɛ gya nbwɩɩ libi ɛɛ lɩɩ asa sʋ ɛɛ sa bamʋ.

eng: Johns disciples and the Pharisees had a habit of fasting. Some people asked Jesus, Why do Johns disciples and the Pharisees disciples fast, but yours dont.
local: Gyɔn Okyugyeepu abɩɩlapʋ yɛ Farasii abi bee ŋmina kɔnɔ. Nɩɩ asa akʋ ba kpee Yesu asɛ naa taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ Gyɔn Okyugyeepu abɩɩlapʋ yɛ Farasii abi abɩɩlapʋ bee ŋmina kɔnɔ, nɩɩ fʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ bɩrɛ be mee ŋmina.

eng: A man with a skin disease approached Jesus, fell to his knees, and begged, If you want, you can make me clean.
local: Tʋtɔ mʋ, bootipu kʋ ba muŋa Yesu ayaa tɔ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ fʋ kɩpɩrɛ tɔ mʋ, fɩɩ taalɛ yɛgɛ mɛ n‐yɩɩ ɩ lɔŋɔ sʋ.

eng: Jesus said, The wedding guests cant fast while the groom is with them, can they? As long as they have the groom with them, they cant fast.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Kɩkyɩɩfɔ tɔ asa bɛɛ taalɛ ŋmina kɔnɔ yɛgɛ ɔkyɩɩfɔ mu‐kuli san ɔ bʋ bamʋ asɛ? Ɩ mɛɛ yɛ ɩ waa kanɩn. Fɛɛ ɔkyɩɩfɔ mu‐kuli san bʋ bamʋ asɛ falɛ.

eng: Incensed, Jesus reached out his hand, touched him, and said, I do want to. Be clean.
local: Nɩɩ mʋ asɩn ɩ waa Yesu kʋwɛɛ, nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ kɩbaa mata mʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɩ gyɛ mɛ kɩpɩrɛ tɔ, ɩmʋ sʋ fʋ n‐yɩɩ ɩ lɔŋɔ sʋ.

eng: But the days will come when the groom will be taken away from them, and then they will fast.
local: Amaa saŋa kʋ ɩɩ ba nɩɩ bee biti ba taa ɔkyɩɩfɔ mu‐kuli mʋ lɩɩ bamʋ asɛ. Kanɩn saŋa maŋa tɔ nɩɩ bee biti ba ŋmina kɔnɔ.

eng: Instantly, the skin disease left him, and he was clean.
local: Opula maŋa tɔ, nɩɩ kibootoo kɩ yɛgɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ alanfɩya.

eng: No one sews a piece of new, unshrunk cloth on old clothes; otherwise, the patch tears away from it, the new from the old, and makes a worse tear.
local: Ba mɛɛ taa kikyibi pʋpwɛ bɛɛ mata kaalɛ dɩdaa ɔbɔ, lɩɩ fɛɛ kii biti kɩ kyatɛ kaalɛ mʋ tii sʋ.

eng: Sternly, Jesus sent him away.
local: Pɔyɩ kɛ Yesu ɔ yɛgɛ ɔ natɛ mʋ, ɔ da mu kumu tɔ yɛ.

eng: No one pours new wine into old leather wineskins; otherwise, the wine would burst the wineskins and the wine would be lost and the wineskins destroyed. But new wine is for new wineskins.
local: Kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ ɔkʋ mɛɛ taa nta pʋpwɛ waa kʋtɔbwaaya ɔwʋlɛ taakpe dɩdaa tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ kan waa kanɩn, nɩɩ nta mʋ ɩɩ bɩlɛ mʋ, ii biti ɩ kyatɛ taakpe dɩdaa mʋ nyita, kɛ nta mʋ ɩ kyɛɛgɛ fɔyɩ. Amaa bɛɛ taa nta pʋpwɛ waa taakpe pʋpwɛ tɔ, kɛ ɩmʋ anyɔ mʋ ɩ man nyita.

eng: saying, Dont say anything to anyone. Instead, go and show yourself to the priest and offer the sacrifice for your cleansing that Moses commanded. This will be a testimony to them.
local: Man kan waa kɛ fʋ tɔwɛ falɛ asɩn nɩmʋ sa ɔkʋ. Amaa taa fʋ n‐yɩɩ naa kaapʋ Ɩbwaarɛ oseepu, kɛ ɔ taa fʋ atɔ waa saraga sa fʋ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Mosesi kaapʋ yɩla mʋ, kɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ o wu fɛɛ fʋ kʋlɔ mʋ kɩ lʋwɛ.

eng: Jesus went through the wheat fields on the Sabbath. As the disciples made their way, they were picking the heads of wheat.
local: Kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa kʋ, nɩɩ Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba naa alʋn kadɔɔ kʋ tɔ, nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba piili bɛɛ gyayɩ alʋn mʋ ɩkʋ.

eng: Instead, he went out and started talking freely and spreading the news so that Jesus wasnt able to enter a town openly. He remained outside in deserted places, but people came to him from everywhere.
local: Amaa ɔnyɩn mʋ dan lɩɩ kpee katɩn kʋmaa nɩmʋ ɛɛ da kibonbon ɛɛ sa asa yɛ Yesu ɩ kyɛ mʋ kibootoo. Ɩmʋ sʋ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ Yesu man bɩla ɛɛ nyɛ ee loo kadɛ kʋ tɔ tentengilen. O kpee katɩn mʋ nɩɩ i du diin, yɛgɛ asa bɛɛ lɩɩ katɩn kʋmaa nɩmʋ bɛɛ ba mʋ asɛ.

eng: The Pharisees said to Jesus, Look! Why are they breaking the Sabbath law.
local: Nɩɩ Farasii abi mʋ ba yɛ, Kɩɩ, mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ fʋ abɩɩlapʋ bɛɛ waa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ man dɛ ɔkpa kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa.

eng: He said to them, Havent you ever read what David did when he was in need, when he and those with him were hungry?.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Fan man pɩɩta kalɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Owura Dawidi maa mʋ kamaa tɔ abi ba waa saŋa mʋ nɩɩ akʋn a mɔɔ bamʋ mʋ nɩn?.

eng: During the time when Abiathar was high priest, David went into Gods house and ate the bread of the presence, which only the priests were allowed to eat. He also gave bread to those who were with him.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Abiyata kyɩna ɔ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ, Owura Dawidi loo Kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa obu mʋ tɔ naa taa bodobodo mʋ nɩɩ ba taa lɛɛ saraga sa Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ wɛ, nɩɩ ɔ sa mʋ kamaa tɔ abi, yɛgɛ ɩ man dɛ ɔkpa fɛɛ ba wɛ ɩmʋ, amɔɔ aseepu mʋ nkʋn.

eng: Then he said, The Sabbath was created for humans; humans werent created for the Sabbath.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ fɛɛ, Nyɩmɩsa kɩdanbɩrasa sʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ waa kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa mʋ, man gyɛ nyɩmɩsa nɩɩ ba waa sa kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa mʋ sa.

eng: This is why the Human One is Lord even over the Sabbath.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ n kyɔ kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa mʋ gbaa.

eng: Jesus began to teach beside the lake again. Such a large crowd gathered that he climbed into a boat there on the lake. He sat in the boat while the whole crowd was nearby on the shore.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu bɩla piili ɛɛ kaapʋ Galile ɔbʋn kaa, nɩɩ asa damantɛ ba ba kyaabɔɔ mʋ bɛɛ nu mʋ asɛ. Sakyɔ sʋ mʋ, o loo ɔkʋlɩ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyan nkyu sʋ ɔbʋn kaa, yɛgɛ asa ba yɩla kidenbi sʋ.

eng: He said many things to them in parables. While teaching them, he said.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ taa aŋasan tɔ kaapʋ bamʋ atɔ damantɛ. Mʋ kɩkaapʋ tɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ.

eng: Listen to this! A farmer went out to scatter seed.
local: Fan nu, ɔdɔɔpʋ kʋ ɩ kyɩna kpee ɔ naa ŋmatɛ ayu.

eng: As he was scattering seed, some fell on the path; and the birds came and ate it.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ ŋmatɛ ayu mʋ, ɩmʋ ɩkʋ ɩ tɩyɛ ɔkpa kaa, nɩɩ nbwiibi ba ba tasɛ gyii.

eng: Other seed fell on rocky ground where the soil was shallow. They sprouted immediately because the soil wasnt deep.
local: Nɩɩ ɩkʋ ɩ tɩyɛ fʋlɛ sʋ, katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɩsɛ ɩ man bʋnyɛ, ɩmʋ sʋ ɩ kɔyɩ bilen.

eng: When the sun came up, it scorched the plants; and they dried up because they had no roots.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ kyɔwɛ maa da mʋ, ɩ bwɩɩ, nɩɩ i wu lɩɩ fɛɛ ɩ man sʋ ilin.

eng: Other seed fell among thorny plants. The thorny plants grew and choked the seeds, and they produced nothing.
local: Nɩɩ ɩkʋ ɩ tɩyɛ iwii tɔ, nɩɩ iwii mʋ ɩ dan nyan ɩmʋ, nɩɩ ɩ man talɛ sɔyɩ abi.

eng: Other seed fell into good soil and bore fruit. Upon growing and increasing, the seed produced in one case a yield of thirty to one, in another case a yield of sixty to one, and in another case a yield of one hundred to one.
local: Nɩɩ ɩkʋ mɔɔ ɩ tɩyɛ kasɛ danbɩrasa tɔ. Nɩɩ ɩ kɔyɩ dan nɩɩ ɩ sɔyɩ abii. Ɩkʋ ɩ sɔyɩ abi adɩsa, ɩkʋ mɔɔ adisiye, nɩɩ ɩkʋ mɔɔ kɩlɩfa.

eng: He said, Whoever has ears to listen should pay attention.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lʋwɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ asʋ nɩɩ ee biti o nu mʋ, o nu.

eng: When they were alone, the people around Jesus, along with the Twelve, asked him about the parables.
local: Ɩ maa ba san Yesu nkʋn mʋ, mʋ abɩɩlapʋ kudu anyɔ yɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba nu mʋ asɩn tɔwɛsa mʋ akʋ ba ba taasɛ mʋ kɩŋasan mʋ kasɛ.

eng: He said to them, The secret of Gods kingdom has been given to you, but to those who are outside everything comes in parables.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fanɛ bɩrɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ asiri asɩn kaapʋ fanɛ, amaa bamʋ nɩɩ ba man tii fanɛ sʋ bɩrɛ mʋ, aŋasan tɔ nɩɩ bɛɛ sʋ bɛɛ kaapʋ bamʋ.

eng: This is so that they can look and see but have no insight, and they can hear but not understand. Otherwise, they might turn their lives around and be forgiven.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ, bee biti ba kɩɩ kɛ ba kɩɩ, ba mɛɛ yɛ ba wu, bee biti ba nu, amaa ba mɛɛ yɛ ba nu kasɛ. Nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ kanɩn sʋ, naafɔɔ ba tee bwii ba Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ, kɛ ɔ taa bamʋ alibi kɛɛ bamʋ. .

eng: Dont you understand this parable? Then how will you understand all the parables?.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan man nu kɩŋasan nɩmʋ kasɛ? Nɛnɛ nɩɩ fen biti fan nu kɩŋasan kʋ kasɛ?.

eng: The farmer scatters the word.
local: Ɔdɔɔpʋ mʋ ɩ gyɛ abwaarɛsɩn ɔtɔwɛpʋ.

eng: This is the meaning of the seed that fell on the path: When the word is scattered and people hear it, right away Satan comes and steals the word that was planted in them.
local: Asa akʋ ba du nɩn fɛɛ ayu mʋ nɩɩ ɩ tɩyɛ ɔkpa kaa mʋ. Ba dan bee nu abwaarɛsɩn mʋ, yɛgɛ Sɩtaanɛ ɩ ba lɛɛ abwaarɛsɩn mʋ lɩɩ bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ tɔ.

eng: Heres the meaning of the seed that fell on rocky ground: When people hear the word, they immediately receive it joyfully.
local: Akʋ mɔɔ ba du nɩn fɛɛ abi mʋ nɩɩ ɩ tɩyɛ afʋlɛ sʋ mʋ. Ba dan nu abwaarɛsɩn mʋ, bɛɛ taa kensi‐gyii kɔɔlɛ ɩmʋ.

eng: Because they have no roots, they last for only a little while. When they experience distress or abuse because of the word, they immediately fall away.
local: Amaa ɩ maa mee loo bamʋ nkɔlɔ kasɛ tɔ sʋ mʋ, ɩ mɛɛ kyɛɛrɛɛ bamʋ tɔ. Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ awʋrʋfɔ abɛɛ kanyanla dan ba bamʋ sʋ lɩɩ abwaarɛsɩn sʋ mʋ, bɛɛ yɛgɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kesun.

eng: Others are like the seed scattered among the thorny plants. These are the ones who have heard the word.
local: Akʋ mɔɔ ba du nɩn fɛɛ abi mʋ nɩɩ ɩ tɩyɛ iwii tɔ mʋ. Kanɩn asa mʋ bee nu abwaarɛsɩn.

eng: but the worries of this life, the false appeal of wealth, and the desire for more things break in and choke the word, and it bears no fruit.
local: Amaa kakyɩna kanɩmʋ tɔ tɔɔrɔɔ yɛ kefulee biti kensi payɩ yɛ dulinyaa atɔ kebiti libi, ɩ ba nyan abwaarɛsɩn mʋ, nɩɩ ɩ man taalɛ sɔyɩ abi.

eng: The seed scattered on good soil are those who hear the word and embrace it. They bear fruit, in one case a yield of thirty to one, in another case sixty to one, and in another case one hundred to one.
local: Nɩɩ akʋ mɔɔ ba du nɩn fɛɛ abi mʋ nɩɩ ɩ tɩyɛ ɩsɛ danbɩrasa tɔ mʋ. Ba dan nu abwaarɛsɩn mʋ, bɛɛ kɔɔlɛ ɩmʋ kɛ bɛɛ sɔyɩ abi. Akʋ bɛɛ sɔyɩ adɩsa, akʋ adisiye, nɩɩ akʋ gbaa kɩlɩfa.

eng: Jesus said to them, Does anyone bring in a lamp in order to put it under a basket or a bed? Shouldnt it be placed on a lampstand?.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu bɩla bɔ kɩŋasan kʋ yɛ, Be mee tuwa fɩtɩla kɛ ba taa waa kolobi tɔ, abɛɛ ba taa waa npaa kasɛ. Bee tuwa fɩtɩla ba sʋ bɛɛ gyaga kɩmʋ ɔgyagakpa nɩn.

eng: Everything hidden will be revealed, and everything secret will come out into the open.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ baala mʋ, kii biti kɩ lɩɩ ifuli, kɛ kɩmʋ nɩɩ kii bun sʋ mʋ kii biti ki bugi tɔ.

eng: Whoever has ears to listen should pay attention.
local: Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ asʋ mʋ o nu.

eng: He said to them, Listen carefully! God will evaluate you with the same standard you use to evaluate others. Indeed, you will receive even more.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ fɛɛ, Fan fɛ nfɛɛrɛ danbɩrasa lɩɩ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ fan nu mʋ sʋ. Kʋtɔ kaalasa mʋ nɩɩ fʋ taa kaala sa fʋ tɔɔmaa mʋ, kɩmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ gbaa ii biti ɔ kaala sa fʋ, kɛ ɩ ŋara fʋ lɛɛ mʋ.

eng: Those who have will receive more, but as for those who dont have, even what they dont have will be taken away from them.
local: Ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ mʋ, ee biti ɔ nyɛ tii sʋ, nɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ man sʋ mʋ, halɩɩ pii mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ mʋ, bee biti ba kɔɔlɛ lɩɩ mʋ asɛ.

eng: Then Jesus said, This is what Gods kingdom is like. Its as though someone scatters seed on the ground.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ fɛɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii du nɩn fɛɛ ɔnyɩn kʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ ŋmatɛ ayu mʋ ndɔɔ tɔ.

eng: then sleeps and wakes night and day. The seed sprouts and grows, but the farmer doesnt know how.
local: Ɛɛ dɛ kɛ o kyiŋi kagyanbwɛ yɛ kakyʋwapɛ, yɛgɛ ɔ man nyi kanan mʋ nɩɩ ayu mʋ ɩ waa nɩɩ ɩ kɔyɩ nɩɩ ɩ dan.

eng: The earth produces crops all by itself, first the stalk, then the head, then the full head of grain.
local: Kasɛ mʋ ɩ yɛgɛ ayu mʋ ɩɩ dan nɩɩ ɩɩ kɔyɩ. Oyu mʋ ɩ gyankpaa kɔyɩ kɛ ɩ nyɛ ɔtɔ kɛ ɩ kʋʋgɛ abi.

eng: Whenever the crop is ready, the farmer goes out to cut the grain because its harvesttime.
local: Ɩ dan mʋ, nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ ɩ taa deebi naa tɩŋɛ ɩmʋ lɩɩ fɛɛ ɩmʋ saŋa fʋʋ.

eng: He continued, Whats a good image for Gods kingdom? What parable can I use to explain it?.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ fɛɛ, Mɩnɛ kʋtɔ nɩɩ an biti a taa Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ waa sɩnkaala? Mɩnɛ kɩŋasan nɩɩ an biti a taa kaapʋ tɔ?.

eng: Consider a mustard seed. When scattered on the ground, its the smallest of all the seeds on the earth.
local: Ki du nɩn fɛɛ sinapi oyu kibi atɔ duusa tɔ kpini kibi pii, nɩɩ bee duu kɩmʋ.

eng: but when its planted, it grows and becomes the largest of all vegetable plants. It produces such large branches that the birds in the sky are able to nest in its shade.
local: Nɩɩ ba kan duu kɩmʋ mʋ, kɩ kɔyɩ, nɩɩ kɩɩ dan kɩ gyɔɔ apʋnfa kpini, nɩɩ kɩɩ waa n‐yaabi lala halɩɩ nbwiibi ba ba waa nsʋswaa kɩmʋ onyin tɔ.

eng: With many such parables he continued to give them the word, as much as they were able to hear.
local: Aŋasan tɔ falɛ nɩɩ Yesu taa tɔwɛ bamʋ abwaarɛsɩn. Ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ kanan mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ taalɛ nu kasɛ.

eng: He spoke to them only in parables, then explained everything to his disciples when he was alone with them.
local: Asɩn akʋ ɩ man bʋ tɔ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ, nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ kɩŋasan. Amaa saŋa kʋmaa nɩɩ ɩ ba san maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ nkʋn mʋ, ɛɛ lɛɛ ɩmʋ kasɛ ɛɛ kaapʋ bamʋ.

eng: Later that day, when evening came, Jesus said to them, Lets cross over to the other side of the lake.
local: Kakɛ maŋa kalaatɩpwɛɛ asɛ nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ fɛɛ, Fan yɛgɛ a faa kpee benbe.

eng: They left the crowd and took him in the boat just as he was. Other boats followed along.
local: Nɩɩ ba loo ɔkʋlɩ mʋ nɩɩ Yesu tɛ kɩmʋ tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ba yɛgɛ sakyɔ mʋ ɔbʋn kaa, ɩkʋlɩ ɩkʋ gbaa i tii bamʋ sʋ.

eng: Gale-force winds arose, and waves crashed against the boat so that the boat was swamped.
local: Ba maa kpee mʋ, nɩɩ afuu i tigi kɔnɔ kʋlʋn, nɩɩ apaa ɩ waa nɩɩ ɩ da nkyu ɩ waa ɔkʋlɩ mʋ tɔ naa san gbɛrɛɛ kɛ ɔkʋlɩ mʋ ɔ bɔla.

eng: But Jesus was in the rear of the boat, sleeping on a pillow. They woke him up and said, Teacher, dont you care that were drowning.
local: Amaa Yesu taa mu kumu gyaga keputu sʋ dɛ nsɩɩrɛ ɔkʋlɩ kɔnɔ. Nɩɩ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba kyiŋi mʋ, nɩɩ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, a maa biti a wu mʋ, ɩ man tiri fʋ.

eng: He got up and gave orders to the wind, and he said to the lake, Silence! Be still! The wind settled down and there was a great calm.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu kʋsʋ yɩlɛ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ afuu mʋ fɛɛ, Waa diin. Nɩɩ katɩn kʋmaa waa diin.

eng: Jesus asked them, Why are you frightened? Dont you have faith yet.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ, Mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ fan sʋ kufuu? Fan man sʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii nɩn.

eng: Overcome with awe, they said to each other, Who then is this? Even the wind and the sea obey him.
local: Nɩɩ kufuu kɩ kɩtaa bamʋ gaa nɩɩ ba taasɛ abaa yɛ, Mɩnɛ Nyɩmɩsa ndee? Halɩɩ afuu yɛ apaa gbaa i nu mʋ asɛ.

eng: Jesus and his disciples came to the other side of the lake, to the region of the Gerasenes.
local: Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba faa kpee Galile ɔbʋn benbe, Gerasene kabaafʋn.

eng: As soon as Jesus got out of the boat, a man possessed by an evil spirit came out of the tombs.
local: Yesu maa dan ɛɛ lɩɩ ɔkʋlɩ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔnyɩn kʋ mʋ nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi ba tɛ mʋ sʋ mʋ, lɩɩ nkyan tɔ ba gyaŋa mʋ.

eng: This man lived among the tombs, and no one was ever strong enough to restrain him, even with a chain.
local: Ɔnyɩn maŋa tɛ nkyan mʋ tɔ nɩn. Ɔkʋ man bɩla taalɛ da mʋ ɩgbɛɛ.

eng: He had been secured many times with leg irons and chains, but he broke the chains and smashed the leg irons. No one was tough enough to control him.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ saŋa kʋmaa nɩɩ ba da mʋ ayaa yɛ abaa ɩgbɛɛ mʋ, ɛɛ tɩŋɛ ɩmʋ nɩn. Nɩɩ ɔkʋ man bʋ tɔ kɛ ɔ taalɛ gyii mʋ sʋ.

eng: Night and day in the tombs and the hills, he would howl and cut himself with stones.
local: Ɔ naa ɔ naa nkyan yɛ abɩɩ tɔ kyʋwapɛ yɛ kagyanbwɛ, nɩɩ ɛɛ taa afʋlɛ ɛɛ kyaŋɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ yɛgɛ ee kuusi.

eng: When he saw Jesus from far away, he ran and knelt before him.
local: Maa wu Yesu kata mʋ, ɔ sɩlɛ naa gyaŋa mʋ, nɩɩ o muŋa mu ansi tɔ.

eng: shouting, What have you to do with me, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? Swear to God that you wont torture me.
local: Nɩɩ o kuusi kenken tɔwɛ yɛ, Yesu, fʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ Owura lala Mu‐Bii. Maa fʋ an sʋ mɩnɛ? Bɔ Ɩbwaarɛ fɛɛ fʋ mɛɛ yɛ fʋ tɔɔrɔɔ mɛ.

eng: He said this because Jesus had already commanded him, Unclean spirit, come out of the man.
local: Ɔnyɩn mʋ tɔwɛ kanɩn nɩn lɩɩ fɛɛ Yesu wʋla tɔwɛ nbwɩɩ libi mʋ yɛ, Nbwɩɩ libi, fan lɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ tɔ.

eng: Jesus asked him, What is your name? He responded, Legion is my name, because we are many.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Fʋ kɩtɩɩ? Nɩɩ ɔ yɛ, Bɛɛ tɩɩ mɛ yɛ Kʋkyɔ, lɩɩ fɛɛ an kyɔ.

eng: They pleaded with Jesus not to send them out of that region.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ mata sʋ ɛɛ kʋlɛ Yesu gaa fɛɛ ɔ man gya nbwɩɩ libi mʋ lɩɩ ɔsʋwʋlɛ maŋa tɔ.

eng: A large herd of pigs was feeding on the hillside.
local: Akuuri damantɛ akʋ ba bʋ tiri tʋtɔ kɩbɩɩ kʋ sʋ bee gyii.

eng: Send us into the pigs! they begged. Let us go into the pigs.
local: Nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi mʋ ba kʋlɛ Yesu yɛ, Yɛgɛ a naa loo akuuri mʋ tɔ.

eng: Jesus gave them permission, so the unclean spirits left the man and went into the pigs. Then the herd of about two thousand pigs rushed down the cliff into the lake and drowned.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu sa bamʋ ɔkpa, nɩɩ ba lɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ tɔ naa loo akuuri mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ akuuri mʋ kpini bamʋ kɔnɔ ɩ waa fɛɛ ngbɩn nyɔ mʋ, ba sɩlɛ kpɩlɩgɛ kɩbɩɩ mʋ ogee tɔ naa loo nkyu tɔ muuri.

eng: Those who tended the pigs ran away and told the story in the city and in the countryside. People came to see what had happened.
local: Nɩɩ asa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ kɩɩ akuuri mʋ sʋ mʋ, ba sɩlɛ kpee kadɛ tɔ yɛ ndɔɔ ana tɔ naa tɔwɛ asa. Nɩɩ asa ba ba kɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ waa.

eng: They came to Jesus and saw the man who used to be demon-possessed. They saw the very man who had been filled with many demons sitting there fully dressed and completely sane, and they were filled with awe.
local: Ba maa ba Yesu asɛ mʋ, ba wu ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ daa ɔ sʋ nbwɩɩ libi mʋ, bun ɩkaalɛ danbɩrasa ɔ tɛ, nɩɩ mʋ nfɛɛrɛ tɔ ɩ dɛ. Nɩɩ kufuu kɩ kɩtaa bamʋ.

eng: Those who had actually seen what had happened to the demon-possessed man told the others about the pigs.
local: Bamʋ nɩɩ ba taa bamʋ ansi wu kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Yesu waa mʋ, ba tɔwɛ ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi ba tɛ mʋ sʋ, yɛ akuuri mʋ asɩn sa asa.

eng: Then they pleaded with Jesus to leave their region.
local: Nɩɩ asa mʋ ba yɛ Yesu ɔ lɩɩ bamʋ ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ.

eng: While he was climbing into the boat, the one who had been demon-possessed pleaded with Jesus to let him come along as one of his disciples.
local: Yesu maa loo ɔkʋlɩ tɔ mʋ, ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi ba daa ba tɛ mʋ sʋ mʋ, kʋlɛ mʋ yɛ ɔ yɛgɛ ɔ buu mʋ.

eng: But Jesus wouldnt allow it. Go home to your own people, Jesus said, and tell them what the Lord has done for you and how he has shown you mercy.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu kina, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Bwii kpee kewu naa tɔwɛ fʋ asa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ waa sa fʋ, yɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ o wu fʋ kʋwɛɛ.

eng: The man went away and began to proclaim in the Ten Cities all that Jesus had done for him, and everyone was amazed.
local: Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ lɩɩ tʋtɔ naa natɛ kabaafʋn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ Ndɛ Kudu mʋ sʋ kpini, ɛɛ tɔwɛ asa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Yesu waa sa mʋ. Nɩɩ i kyinkyin bamʋ nɩɩ ba nu mʋ kpini.

eng: Jesus crossed the lake again, and on the other side a large crowd gathered around him on the shore.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu bwii loo ɔkʋlɩ tɔ faa kpee ɔbʋn mʋ benbe, nɩɩ asa kʋkyɔ ba ba sarɛ kyaabɔɔ mʋ.

eng: Jairus, one of the synagogue leaders, came forward. When he saw Jesus, he fell at his feet.
local: Nɩɩ Gyarosi mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa abɩlɩsa mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ ba tʋtɔ. Maa wu Yesu mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɩyɛ mʋ ayaa tɔ.

eng: and pleaded with him, My daughter is about to die. Please, come and place your hands on her so that she can be healed and live.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ kʋlɛ mʋ gaa yɛ, N kʋlɛ fʋ nɩn, mi kebii kyɩɩsa‐bi ɛɛ lɔ ee biti o wu, ba taa fʋ abaa gyaga mʋ sʋ kɛ ɔ nyɛ alanfɩya.

eng: So Jesus went with him. A swarm of people were following Jesus, crowding in on him.
local: Nɩɩ maa Yesu ba yii sʋ bee kpee, yɛgɛ sakyɔ ba mili Yesu sʋ ba buu mʋ.

eng: A woman was there who had been bleeding for twelve years.
local: Ɔkyɩɩ kʋ nɩɩ nkalan ɩɩ da mʋ, nsu kudu anyɔ, bʋ sakyɔ mʋ tɔ.

eng: She had suffered a lot under the care of many doctors, and had spent everything she had without getting any better. In fact, she had gotten worse.
local: Ɔkyɩɩ mʋ nyita mu afulee mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ kpini akyɛpʋ asɛ kpɩnɛ, fɛɛ naafɔɔ kʋlɔ mʋ kɩ bɔɔsɛ mʋ, amaa kʋlɔ mʋ kɩ san kii tii sʋ.

eng: Because she had heard about Jesus, she came up behind him in the crowd and touched his clothes.
local: O nu Yesu sʋ nɩɩ o loo sakyɔ mʋ tɔ ba Yesu kamaa.

eng: She was thinking, If I can just touch his clothes, Ill be healed.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ fɛ nfɛɛrɛ mu kumu tɔ yɛ, Naafɔɔ n daa nyɛ mata mʋ kaalɛ gbaa nɩn, n biti n nyɛ alanfɩya.

eng: Her bleeding stopped immediately, and she sensed in her body that her illness had been healed.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ mata Yesu kaalɛ, nɩɩ nkalan mʋ ɩ tɩn opula tɔ. Nɩɩ o wu mʋ n‐yɩɩ tɔ fɛɛ mʋ awʋrʋfɔ kpini ɩ kyʋn.

eng: At that very moment, Jesus recognized that power had gone out from him. He turned around in the crowd and said, Who touched my clothes.
local: Opula tɔ nɩɩ Yesu bɩɩ fɛɛ ɔlʋn kʋ lɩɩ mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ o biliŋi kɩɩ sakyɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ yɛ, Anɩmʋ ɩ mata mɛ kaalɛ.

eng: His disciples said to him, Dont you see the crowd pressing against you? Yet you ask, Who touched me.
local: Nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba ba sa mʋ kɔnɔ fɛɛ, Fu wu kanan mʋ nɩɩ sakyɔ mʋ mili fʋ sʋ, nɩɩ fʋ taasɛ yɛ, Anɩmʋ ɩ mata mɛ kaalɛ? .

eng: But Jesus looked around carefully to see who had done it.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu biliŋi ɔ dan san ɛɛ kɩɩ ee biti ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ mata mʋ kaalɛ mʋ.

eng: The woman, full of fear and trembling, came forward. Knowing what had happened to her, she fell down in front of Jesus and told him the whole truth.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ maa wu kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ waa mʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taa kufuu kyɩkyaa natɛ ba Yesu ansi tɔ, ba muŋa mʋ ayaa tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ asɩn mʋ kesintin kpini tɔwɛ mʋ.

eng: He responded, Daughter, your faith has healed you; go in peace, healed from your disease.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ fɛɛ, Mi‐bii, fʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii kɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ fʋ nyɛ alanfɩya, kpewu kɔkɔlɔ yuuli sʋ, fʋ awʋrʋfɔ ɩ lʋwɛ.

eng: While Jesus was still speaking with her, messengers came from the synagogue leaders house, saying to Jairus, Your daughter has died. Why bother the teacher any longer.
local: Yesu maa san ɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ nbɔɔ ba lɩɩ ɔbɩlɩsa Gyarosi kɩkpaara sʋ ba tɔwɛ Gyarosi yɛ, Fu kebii kyɩɩsa‐bi mʋ wu, mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ fɩɩ san fɩɩ tɔɔrɔɔ ɔkaapʋpʋ mʋ.

eng: But Jesus overheard their report and said to the synagogue leader, Dont be afraid; just keep trusting.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ mu ansi yɛgɛ bamʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ Gyarosi yɛ, Man waa kufuu, dan kɔɔlɛ gyii kanɩn.

eng: He didnt allow anyone to follow him except Peter, James, and John, James brother.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ man bɩla yɛgɛ ɔkʋ buu mʋ tii Pita yɛ Gyemisi yɛ mu‐supu Gyɔn sʋ.

eng: They came to the synagogue leaders house, and he saw a commotion, with people crying and wailing loudly.
local: Ba maa fʋʋ Gyarosi kɩkpaara sʋ mʋ, ba wu asa ba maa waa ɩlawʋ bɛɛ sawʋ kenken.

eng: He went in and said to them, Whats all this commotion and crying about? The child isnt dead. Shes only sleeping.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu loo taasɛ bamʋ fɛɛ, Mɩnɛ nɩɩ fɛn waa kanɩn? Mɩnɛ kusu nɩɩ fen su kanɩn? Kebii mʋ man wu, ɔ dɛ nsɩɩrɛ nɩn.

eng: They laughed at him, but he threw them all out. Then, taking the childs parents and his disciples with him, he went to the room where the child was.
local: Nɩɩ asa mʋ ba ŋmasɛ mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ gya bamʋ kpini lɩɩ kewu, nɩɩ maa kebii mʋ mʋ‐sɛ yɛ mu‐nyi yɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ asa mʋ ba loo obu mʋ nɩɩ kebii kyɩɩsa‐bi mʋ dɛ kɩmʋ tɔ mʋ.

eng: Taking her hand, he said to her, Talitha koum , which means, Young woman, get up.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ kɩtaa kebii kyɩɩsa‐bi mʋ kɩbaa tɔ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Talita kuum. Kɩmʋ kasɛ ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ, Kebii kyɩɩsa‐bi, n yɛ fʋ kʋsʋ.

eng: Suddenly the young woman got up and began to walk around. She was years old. They were shocked!.
local: Opula tɔ nɩɩ kebii mʋ kʋsʋ, nɩɩ ɔ natɛ. Kebii mʋ gyii nsu kudu anyɔ. Nɩɩ asɩn mʋ i kyinkyin bamʋ gaa.

eng: He gave them strict orders that no one should know what had happened. Then he told them to give her something to eat.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu sa bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ ba man kan tɔwɛ ɔkʋ kanɩn asɩn nɩmʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ fɛɛ ba sa kebii mʋ agyitɔ kɛ o gyii.

eng: In those days there was another large crowd with nothing to eat. Jesus called his disciples and told them.
local: Kanɩn nkɛ maŋa tɔ mʋ, sakyɔ damantɛ kʋ bɩla ba sarɛ Yesu asɛ. Nɩɩ ba maa man sʋ agyitɔ akʋ ba gyii mʋ, Yesu tɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba mʋ asɛ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ.

eng: I feel sorry for the crowd because they have been with me for three days and have nothing to eat.
local: Asa nɩmʋ asɩn ɩ sʋ mɛ kʋwɛɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ndaga nkɛ nsa ndee ba maa bʋ mɛ asɛ, nɩɩ ba man sʋ agyitɔ akʋ ba gyii.

eng: If I send them away hungry to their homes, they wont have enough strength to travel, for some have come a long distance.
