local: Nɩɩ n kan tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ ba bwii kpewu mʋ, bee biti ba naa kirin‐kirin ɔkpa tɔ, lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ akʋ ba lɩɩ kata‐kata.

eng: His disciples responded, How can anyone get enough food in this wilderness to satisfy these people.
local: Nɩɩ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba taasɛ Yesu yɛ, Fɩnɛ nɩɩ an biti a nyɛ agyitɔ kiperi kɩnɩmʋ sʋ sa asa damantɛ nɩmʋ.

eng: Jesus asked, How much bread do you have? They said, Seven loaves.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Bodobodo afɩnɛ nɩɩ fan sʋ? Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Asunɔ nɩɩ an sʋ.

eng: He told the crowd to sit on the ground. He took the seven loaves, gave thanks, broke them apart, and gave them to his disciples to distribute; and they gave the bread to the crowd.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu yɛgɛ nɩɩ asa mʋ ba kyʋn kyɩna kasɛ. Nɩɩ ɔ taa bodobodo asunɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ fwaala Ɩbwaarɛ nɩɩ ɔ kpɛɛ‐kpɛɛ ɩmʋ tɔ taa sa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ ba barɩgɛ tɔ sa asa mʋ ba gyii, nɩɩ ba barɩgɛ tɔ sa bamʋ.

eng: They also had a few fish. He said a blessing over them, then gave them to the disciples to hand out also.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu bɩla taa ɩkɩn gbɛrɛɛ mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ mʋ, fwaala Ɩbwaarɛ bɩla sa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ yɛ ba barɩgɛ ɩmʋ tɔ sa asa mʋ.

eng: They ate until they were full. They collected seven baskets full of leftovers.
local: Nɩɩ asa mʋ kpini ba gyii ŋmɛ. Nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba tasɛ‐tasɛ bamʋ agyisan mʋ bɔla alantan asunɔ.

eng: This was a crowd of about four thousand people! Jesus sent them away.
local: Asa mʋ nɩɩ ba gyii kanɩn agyitɔ maŋa mʋ, ba gyɛ asa ngbɩn nna. Ɩmʋ kamaa tɔ, nɩɩ Yesu yɛgɛ bamʋ, nɩɩ ba kpewu.

eng: then got into a boat with his disciples and went over to the region of Dalmanutha.
local: Opula maŋa tɔ, nɩɩ Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba loo ɔkʋlɩ tɔ faa kyʋn Dalimanuta ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ.

eng: The Pharisees showed up and began to argue with Jesus. To test him, they asked for a sign from heaven.
local: Farasii abi akʋ ba ba Yesu asɛ ba gyii mʋ ɩmɔɔrɛ, kɛ ba nyɛ wu mʋ kilibi. Nɩɩ ba tʋʋ Yesu tɔ kɩɩ yɛ ɔ waa sʋsʋ adangana akʋ kaapʋ bamʋ.

eng: With an impatient sigh, Jesus said, Why does this generation look for a sign? I assure you that no sign will be given to it.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ o muusi, nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ fanɛ nbɩɩnbɩɩ asa fen biti adangana fen wu? N biti n tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin yɛ, n mɛɛ yɛ n waa kanɩn adangana maŋa kaapʋ fanɛ kpaa.

eng: Leaving them, he got back in the boat and crossed to the other side of the lake.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu yɛgɛ bamʋ tʋtɔ naa loo mʋ ɔkʋlɩ tɔ faa kyʋn benbe.

eng: Jesus disciples had forgotten to bring any bread, so they had only one loaf with them in the boat.
local: Yesu abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba tan sʋ fɛɛ ba maa man taa bodobodo kʋkyɔ, amaa bodobodo kukuli kʋlʋn ɩ bʋ bamʋ asɛ ɔkʋlɩ mʋ tɔ.

eng: He gave them strict orders: Watch out and be on your guard for the yeast of the Pharisees as well as the yeast of Herod.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan kɩɩ danbɩrasa kɛ fan baa fan da fanɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ, lɩɩ Farasii abi yɛ Herodi asa bamʋ kɩfɛ mʋ nɩɩ kii tin bodobodo mʋ.

eng: The disciples discussed this among themselves, He said this because we have no bread.
local: Nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba piili bɛɛ tɔwɛ abaa yɛ, A maa man lɛɛ bodobodo kʋkyɔ sʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ tɔwɛ kanɩn mʋ abɛɛ.

eng: Jesus knew what they were discussing and said, Why are you talking about the fact that you dont have any bread? Dont you grasp what has happened? Dont you understand? Are your hearts so resistant to what God is doing?.
local: Yesu maa bɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ fɛn tɔwɛ fanɛ kɩman sʋ bodobodo kʋkyɔ mʋ asɩn? Ɩmʋ sʋ, fan man pɩɩta bɩɩ mɛ awaasa kasɛ? Mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fanɛ nfɛɛrɛ ɩ mɛɛ taalɛ ɩ kɩtaa atɔ bilen kanɩn?.

eng: Dont you have eyes? Why cant you see? Dont you have ears? Why cant you hear? Dont you remember?.
local: Fan sʋ ansi, fan man kɩɩ? Nɩɩ fan sʋ asʋ, fan mee nu? Fen mee nyiŋi asɩn nɩn abɛɛ?.

eng: When I broke five loaves of bread for those five thousand people, how many baskets full of leftovers did you gather? They answered, Twelve.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ n taa bodobodo anuu barɩgɛ sa asa ngbɩn nnuu nɩɩ ba gyii mʋ, alantan afɩnɛ nɩɩ fan tasɛ agyisan mʋ bɔla? Nɩɩ ba yɛ, Alantan kudu anyɔ.

eng: And when I broke seven loaves of bread for those four thousand people, how many baskets full of leftovers did you gather? They answered, Seven.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu bɩla taasɛ yɛ, N maa taa bodobodo asunɔ barɩgɛ tɔ sa asa ngbɩn nna nɩɩ ba gyii mʋ, alantan afɩnɛ nɩɩ fan tasɛ agyisan mʋ bɔla? Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Alantan asunɔ.

eng: Jesus said to them, And you still dont understand.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu yɛ, Ɩnɩmʋ kpini kamaa mʋ, fan man taalɛ bɩɩ ɩmʋ kasɛ.

eng: Jesus and his disciples came to Bethsaida. Some people brought a blind man to Jesus and begged him to touch and heal him.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba kpee Betisayida kadɛ tɔ, nɩɩ asa akʋ ba taa gyaatanapʋ kʋ ba kʋlɛ Yesu fɛɛ ɔ taa mʋ kɩbaa mata mʋ.

eng: Taking the blind mans hand, Jesus led him out of the village. After spitting on his eyes and laying his hands on the man, he asked him, Do you see anything.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu kɩtaa gyaatanapʋ mʋ kɩbaa tɔ, taa mʋ lɩɩ kadɛ mʋ nbwama. Nɩɩ Yesu tʋʋ nkyɔlɛ waa mu ansi tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ abaa gyaga mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Fɩɩ wu atɔ.

eng: The man looked up and said, I see people. They look like trees, only they are walking around.
local: Nɩɩ gyaatanapʋ mʋ diyaa mu ansi sʋsʋ, nɩɩ ɔ yɛ, N wu asa ba maa naa, nɩɩ ba du fɛɛ iyu ɩ maa naa mʋ.

eng: Then Jesus placed his hands on the mans eyes again. He looked with his eyes wide open, his sight was restored, and he could see everything clearly.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu bɩla taa mʋ abaa gyaga ɔnyɩn mu ansi sʋ, nɩɩ mu ansi mʋ i bugi, nɩɩ mu ansi mʋ ɩ nyɛ alanfɩya, ɩɩ kɩɩ danbɩrasa.

eng: Then Jesus sent him home, saying, Dont go into the village.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ gyaatanapʋ mʋ yɛ, Kpewu! Amaa man bɩla bwii kpee kadɛ mʋ tɔ.

eng: Jesus and his disciples went into the villages near Caesarea Philippi. On the way he asked his disciples, Who do people say that I am.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba kpee ndɛ‐bii mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ Kasariya Filipi kadɛ mʋ asɛ. Ba maa kpee mʋ, nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ yɛ, Anɩmʋ nɩɩ asa ba yɛ n gyɛ.

eng: They told him, Some say John the Baptist, others Elijah, and still others one of the prophets.
local: Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Akʋ ba yɛ fʋ ɩ gyɛ Gyɔn okyugyeepu mʋ, akʋ mɔɔ ba yɛ fʋ ɩ gyɛ Eligya, nɩɩ akʋ mɔɔ ba yɛ fʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ.

eng: He asked them, And what about you? Who do you say that I am? Peter answered, You are the Christ.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ yɛ, Nɩɩ fanɛ mɔɔ, fan yɛ anɩmʋ nɩɩ n gyɛ? Nɩɩ Pita lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Fʋ ɩ gyɛ Krisito, Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ mʋ.

eng: Jesus ordered them not to tell anyone about him.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu gya bamʋ yɛ ba man kan tɔwɛ ɔkʋ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ.

eng: Then Jesus began to teach his disciples: The Human One must suffer many things and be rejected by the elders, chief priests, and the legal experts, and be killed, and then, after three days, rise from the dead.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu piili ɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ, Ɩ dagaa fɛɛ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa maa wu awʋrʋfɔ gaa, kɛ Gyiwu abɩlɩsa yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu abɩlɩsa yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ ba kina mɛ. Bee biti ba mɔɔ mɛ, kɛ nkɛ nsa kamaa mʋ, n biti n kyiŋi lɩɩ lewu tɔ ba.

eng: He said this plainly. But Peter took hold of Jesus and, scolding him, began to correct him.
local: Amaa Yesu maa bugi mʋ asɩn mʋ tɔ pensen kaapʋ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ, nɩɩ Pita tɩɩ mʋ lɩɩ ɩkaa sʋ ɛɛ tɔwɛ yii mʋ.

eng: Jesus turned and looked at his disciples, then sternly corrected Peter: Get behind me, Satan. You are not thinking Gods thoughts but human thoughts.
local: Amaa Yesu, biliŋi kɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ Pita yɛ, Lɩɩ mɛ sʋ, fʋ Sɩtaanɛ nɩmʋ. Fʋ nfɛɛrɛ ɩ man lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ, ɩ lɩɩ nyɩmɩsa asɛ nɩn.

eng: After calling the crowd together with his disciples, Jesus said to them, All who want to come after me must say no to themselves, take up their cross, and follow me.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɩɩ sakyɔ mʋ yɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba ba mʋ asɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti fɛɛ o buu mɛ mʋ, ɩ dagaa fɛɛ o kina mʋ n‐yɩɩ, kɛ ɔ sʋla mʋ oyu laasa kɛ o buu mɛ.

eng: All who want to save their lives will lose them. But all who lose their lives because of me and because of the good news will save them.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti fɛɛ o kun mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mʋ nkpa mʋ, ee biti ɔ pan nkpa mʋ. Amaa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ pan mʋ nkpa lɩɩ mɛ yɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ sʋ mʋ, ee biti ɔ nyɛ mʋ nkpa.

eng: Why would people gain the whole world but lose their lives?.
local: Nɩɩ fʋ kan nyɛ dulinyaa tɔ kʋtɔ kʋmaa, nɩɩ fʋ pan fʋ nkpa mʋ, mɩnɛ nɩɩ fʋ nyɛ?.

eng: What will people give in exchange for their lives?.
local: Abɛɛ mɩnɛ nɩɩ nyɩmɩsa ɩ taalɛ ɔ taa kyarɛ mʋ nkpa?.

eng: Whoever is ashamed of me and my words in this unfaithful and sinful generation, the Human One will be ashamed of that person when he comes in the Fathers glory with the holy angels.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan waa ipeeli lɩɩ mɛ yɛ mɛ kɩkaapʋ sʋ, nbɩɩnbɩɩ kakyɩna nɩmʋ tɔ abi mʋ nɩɩ alibi yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kɩman‐sɩlɛ kɩ bʋ bamʋ tɔ falɛ mʋ, kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ kan ba yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ bʋnyaa yɛ mʋ sʋsʋ nbɔɔ mʋ, ngbaa mɛ ii biti ɩ baa ɩ sʋ mɛ ipeeli sa kanɩn ɔsa maŋa.

eng: Jesus continued, I assure you that some standing here wont die before they see Gods kingdom arrive in power.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu bwii kyaga sʋ yɛ, N tɔwɛ fanɛ fɛɛ akʋ ba bʋ fanɛ tɔ nfɩɩ nɩɩ ba mɛɛ yɛ ba wu lewu, amɔɔ ba wu Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ kɩ maa ba ɔlʋn sʋ.

eng: Six days later Jesus took Peter, James, and John, and brought them to the top of a very high mountain where they were alone. He was transformed in front of them.
local: Nkɛ nsiye kamaa tɔ mʋ, Yesu taa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ asa, Pita yɛ Gyemisi yɛ Gyɔn tii mʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋ dii kɩbɩɩ lala kʋ kpee, nɩɩ ba bʋ tʋtɔ bamʋ nkʋn. Nɩɩ Yesu kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ kyɛɛgɛ bamʋ ansi sʋ.

eng: and his clothes were amazingly bright, brighter than if they had been bleached white.
local: Nɩɩ mʋ ɩkaalɛ bunsa gbaa ɩ lɔŋɔ fuuli parɛparɛ ɩ ŋalɩgɛ sʋ. Ɔkʋ man bʋ dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ nɩɩ ɛɛ taalɛ fwɩɩ kaalɛ kɛ ki fuuli parɛparɛ kanɩn kudubi.

eng: Elijah and Moses appeared and were talking with Jesus.
local: Opula maŋa tɔ mʋ, ba ba kɩɩ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ asa dɩdaa anyɔ, Eligya yɛ Mosesi, ba lɩɩ ba, nɩɩ ba maa Yesu bɛɛ tɔwɛ.

eng: Peter reacted to all of this by saying to Jesus, Rabbi, its good that were here. Lets make three shrines—one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.
local: Nɩɩ Pita tɔwɛ Yesu yɛ, Ɔkaapʋpʋ, ɩ bʋrɔn gaa fɛɛ a maa bʋ nfɩɩ. Ɩmʋ sʋ, yɛgɛ kɛ a yii abu asa, fʋ lɛɛ kʋkʋ, Eligya lɛɛ kʋkʋ, Mosesi lɛɛ kʋkʋ.

eng: He said this because he didnt know how to respond, for the three of them were terrified.
local: Nɩɩ kufuu kɩ kɩtaa Pita maa abɩɩlapʋ banban mʋ gaa, nɩɩ ba man bɩla be nyi kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ.

eng: Then a cloud overshadowed them, and a voice spoke from the cloud, This is my Son, whom I dearly love. Listen to him.
local: Nɩɩ kabwaarɛ wʋlɛ ba bun bamʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔbʋlɛ kʋ lɩɩ abwaarɛ‐wʋlɛ mʋ tɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ mi‐Bii‐nyɩnsa mʋ nɩɩ n sʋ kebiti sa. Fan nu mʋ asɛ.

eng: Suddenly, looking around, they no longer saw anyone with them except Jesus.
local: Opula maŋa tɔ, abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba maa kɩɩ kyaabɔɔ mʋ, ba man bɩla wu ɔkʋ bamʋ asɛ, amɔɔ Yesu nkʋn.

eng: As they were coming down the mountain, he ordered them not to tell anyone what they had seen until after the Human One had risen from the dead.
local: Ba maa kpɩlɩgɛ kɩbɩɩ mʋ sʋ bɛɛ ba mʋ, nɩɩ Yesu da bamʋ amu tɔ yɛ, Fan man tɔwɛ ɔkʋ yɛ ɔkʋ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ fan wu, naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ n bwii kyiŋi lɩɩ lewu tɔ mʋ.

eng: So they kept it to themselves, wondering, Whats this rising from the dead.
local: Nɩɩ ba nu sa mʋ, amaa bamʋ wʋlɛ‐wʋlɛ ɩɩ taasɛ abaa yɛ, Kikyiŋi lɩɩ lewu tɔ nɩmʋ kasɛ kaapʋ nɛnɛ.

eng: They asked Jesus, Why do the legal experts say that Elijah must come first.
local: Nɩɩ ba taasɛ Yesu yɛ, Mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ nbara akaapʋpʋ mʋ bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ ɩ dagaa Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Eligya maa gyankpaa ba pɔyɩ.

eng: He answered, Elijah does come first to restore all things. Why was it written that the Human One would suffer many things and be rejected?.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɩ gyɛ kesintin fɛɛ Eligya ii biti ɔ gyankpaa ba, kɛ ɔ ba lɔŋɔ kʋtɔ kʋmaa kɩnɩmʋ. Amaa mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ yɛ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ n biti n wu awʋrʋfɔ gaa kɛ asa ba kina mɛ?.

eng: In fact, I tell you that Elijah has come, but they did to him whatever they wanted, just as it was written about him.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ Eligya tee bwii ba, nɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ asa bee biti mʋ, ba tɛɛ taa waa mʋ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba wʋla kyʋrɔɔ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ lɩɩ mʋ sʋ mʋ.

eng: When Jesus, Peter, James, and John approached the other disciples, they saw a large crowd surrounding them and legal experts arguing with them.
local: Ba maa kpɩlɩgɛ kɩbɩɩ mʋ sʋ bɛɛ ba abɩɩlapʋ asansa mʋ asɛ mʋ, ba wu sakyɔ damantɛ kʋ maa yɩlɛ kyaabɔɔ bamʋ, yɛgɛ ba maa nbara akaapʋpʋ mʋ bee gyii ɩmɔɔrɛ.

eng: Suddenly the whole crowd caught sight of Jesus. They ran to greet him, overcome with excitement.
local: Asa mʋ ba maa wu Yesu maa ba mʋ, i kyinkyin bamʋ, nɩɩ ba sɩlɛ kpee mʋ asɛ naa fwaala mʋ.

eng: Jesus asked them, What are you arguing about.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Mɩnɛ ɩmɔɔrɛ nɩɩ fanaa bamʋ fen gyii mʋ.

eng: Someone from the crowd responded, Teacher, I brought my son to you, since he has a spirit that doesnt allow him to speak.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ lɛɛ kɔnɔ lɩɩ sakyɔ mʋ tɔ yɛ, Ɔkaapʋpʋ, kabwɩɩ libi kʋ tɛ mi‐bii sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ taalɛ ɛɛ tɔwɛ, nɩɩ n taa mʋ ba fʋ asɛ.

eng: Wherever it overpowers him, it throws him into a fit. He foams at the mouth, grinds his teeth, and stiffens up. So I spoke to your disciples to see if they could throw it out, but they couldnt.
local: Ɩ dan kan kyɩna mʋ sʋ de, ɩ sʋ mʋ ɩ tʋʋ kasɛ, yɛgɛ mʋ kɔnɔ tɔ kii fuu afʋfʋʋ, yɛgɛ ɛɛ wɛ mʋ anyi tɔ, nɩɩ ee kirin. N ba kʋlɛ fʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ ba kʋsaa kanɩn nbwɩɩ libi maŋa lɩɩ mi‐bii mʋ sʋ, amaa ba man taalɛ.

eng: Jesus answered them, You faithless generation, how long will I be with you? How long will I put up with you? Bring him to me.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, O! Fanɛ ndaga kakyɩna nɩmʋ tɔ asa mʋ nɩɩ fan man sʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii. Nɛnɛ nɩɩ fen biti yɛ n nyɛ kenyiita sa fanɛ? Fan taa kebii mʋ bɩya mɛ nfɩɩ.

eng: They brought him. When the spirit saw Jesus, it immediately threw the boy into a fit. He fell on the ground and rolled around, foaming at the mouth.
local: Ba maa taa kebii mʋ bɩya mʋ, nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi mʋ maa wu Yesu mʋ, opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ ɩ taa kebii mʋ tʋʋ kasɛ fɛɛ kigbingbin. Ɔ dɛ ee gbiliti yɛgɛ mʋ kɔnɔ tɔ kii fuu afʋfʋʋ.

eng: Jesus asked his father, How long has this been going on? He said, Since he was a child.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ kebii mʋ mʋ‐sɛ yɛ, Kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɩ waa mʋ falɛ mʋ, ɩ tɛɛ kyɛɛrɛɛ do? Nɩɩ mʋ‐sɛ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Lɩɩ koo mu kebii tɔ nɩɩ ɩ waa mʋ kanɩn.

eng: It has often thrown him into a fire or into water trying to kill him. If you can do anything, help us! Show us compassion.
local: Ɩ taalɛ ɩ taa mʋ ɩ tʋʋ fʋlɔn yɛ nkyu tɔ ii biti ɩ mɔɔ mʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ, nɩɩ fɩɩ taalɛ waa kʋtɔ kʋ mʋ, wu anɛ kʋwɛɛ kɛ fʋ mɔlɩgɛ anɛ.

eng: Jesus said to him, If you can do anything? All things are possible for the one who has faith.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ mʋ‐sɛ yɛ, Fʋ yɛ nɩɩ n biti n taalɛ mʋ? Nu kasɛ fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gyii mʋ, kʋtɔ kʋmaa kii biti kɩ waa sa mʋ.

eng: At that the boys father cried out, I have faith; help my lack of faith.
local: Kebii mʋ mʋ‐sɛ taa kensi payɩ kʋlɛ Yesu yɛ, N kɔɔlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gyii, amaa kyɛ mɛ tɔ kɛ n nyɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii tii sʋ.

eng: Noticing that the crowd had surged together, Jesus spoke harshly to the unclean spirit, Mute and deaf spirit, I command you to come out of him and never enter him again.
local: Yesu maa wu sakyɔ mʋ maa nyɛ akyɔ ii tii sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ pʋntɛ nbwɩɩ libi mʋ sʋ tɔwɛ ɩmʋ yɛ, Fanɛ naamuu yɛ akpawu nbwɩɩ libi, n yɛ fan kʋsʋ lɩɩ mʋ sʋ nbɩɩnbɩɩ nɩmʋ, kɛ fan man bɩla bwii ba mʋ sʋ kakaakʋ.

eng: After screaming and shaking the boy horribly, the spirit came out. The boy seemed to be dead; in fact, several people said that he had died.
local: Nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi mʋ ba nyaŋɛ tɔ muusi tɔ taa kebii mʋ tʋʋ ɔlʋn fɛɛ kigbingbin, nɩɩ ba kʋsʋ mʋ sʋ. Kebii mʋ dɛ fɛɛ obuni, nɩɩ asa mʋ ba yɛ o wu.

eng: But Jesus took his hand, lifted him up, and he arose.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu kɩtaa kebii mʋ kɩbaa tɔ kʋsaa mʋ yɩlɛ mʋ ayaa sʋ.

eng: After Jesus went into a house, his disciples asked him privately, Why couldnt we throw this spirit out.
local: Ɩmʋ kamaa tɔ, nɩɩ Yesu loo kɩkpaara sʋ, nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ nkʋn ba naa taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Mɩnɛ ɩ waa sʋ nɩɩ anɛɛnɛ a man taalɛ kʋsaa nbwɩɩ libi mʋ lɩɩ kebii mʋ sʋ.

eng: Jesus answered, Throwing this kind of spirit out requires prayer.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Sɛhn man bʋ tɔ nɩɩ ɩ taalɛ gya ɩnɩmʋ ana kudubi, amɔɔ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ nkʋn.

eng: From there Jesus and his followers went through Galilee, but he didnt want anyone to know it.
local: Ɩmʋ kamaa nɩɩ Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba lɩɩ tʋtɔ bɔla Galile ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ. Nɩɩ Yesu mee biti fɛɛ ba bɩɩ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ.

eng: This was because he was teaching his disciples, The Human One will be delivered into human hands. They will kill him. Three days after he is killed he will rise up.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ kanɩn saŋa maŋa mʋ ɛɛ kaapʋ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ nɩn. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Bee biti ba lɛɛ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ sa asa, kɛ ba mɔɔ mɛ, amaa nkɛ nsa kamaa tɔ mʋ, n biti n kyiŋi ba nkpa tɔ.

eng: But they didnt understand this kind of talk, and they were afraid to ask him.
local: Maa tɔwɛ bamʋ kanɩn mʋ, ba man nu ɩmʋ kasɛ, nɩɩ ɩ sʋ bamʋ kufuu fɛɛ ba taasɛ mʋ ɩmʋ kasɛ.

eng: They entered Capernaum. When they had come into a house, he asked them, What were you arguing about during the journey.
local: Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba kpee Kapanum kadɛ lala tɔ. Ba maa bʋ kɩkpaara sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan maa naa ɔkpa tɔ fɛn ba mʋ, mɩnɛ ɩmɔɔrɛ nɩɩ fen gyii mʋ.

eng: They didnt respond, since on the way they had been debating with each other about who was the greatest.
local: Ba laatɔ nɩn ba man lɛɛ kɔnɔ, lɩɩ fɛɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba naa ɔkpa tɔ bee kpee mʋ, bee gyii abaa ɩmɔɔrɛ yɛ ɔmɔmɔ ɩ gyɛ bamʋ tɔ ɔlala.

eng: He sat down, called the Twelve, and said to them, Whoever wants to be first must be least of all and the servant of all.
local: Yesu maa kyʋn kyɩna mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ kudu anyɔ mʋ ba mʋ asɛ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Nɩɩ fanɛ tɔ ɔkʋ kan ee biti fɛɛ o biliŋi ɔlala mʋ, ɔ waa mʋ n‐yɩɩ kalʋwɛ yɛ kɩyaafɔlɛ sa mʋ tɔɔmaa ana.

eng: Jesus reached for a little child, placed him among the Twelve, and embraced him. Then he said.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu taa kebii kʋ ba yɩlɛ bamʋ ansi tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ taa kebii mʋ abaa tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ.

eng: Whoever welcomes one of these children in my name welcomes me; and whoever welcomes me isnt actually welcoming me but rather the one who sent me.
local: Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kan kɔɔlɛ kebii kɩnɩmʋ dubi mɛ kɩtɩɩ tɔ sʋ mʋ, mɛ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ, nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kan kɔɔlɛ mɛ mɔɔ mʋ, man gyɛ mɛ nkʋn nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ, amaa ɔ kɔɔlɛ ɔmʋnɩ o sun mɛ mʋ gbaa.

eng: John said to Jesus, Teacher, we saw someone throwing demons out in your name, and we tried to stop him because he wasnt following us.
local: Nɩɩ Gyɔn tɔwɛ Yesu yɛ, Ɔkaapʋpʋ, an wu ɔnyɩn kʋ maa sʋ fʋ kɩtɩɩ ɛɛ kʋsaa nbwɩɩ libi ɛɛ lɩɩ asa sʋ, yɛgɛ ɔ man tii anɛ sʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ an gya mʋ yɛ ɔ man bɩla waa kanɩn.

eng: Jesus replied, Dont stop him. No one who does powerful acts in my name can quickly turn around and curse me.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan man gya kanɩn ɔsa maŋa. Ɔsa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ waa asɩn kyinkyinsa lɩɩ mɛ kɩtɩɩ tɔ mʋ, ɩmʋ kamaa mʋ ɔ mɛɛ bɩla taalɛ tɔwɛ asɩn kʋ nyita mɛ kɩtɩɩ.

eng: Whoever isnt against us is for us.
local: Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o mee nyita anɛ kusun mʋ, o tii anɛ sʋ nɩmʋ.

eng: I assure you that whoever gives you a cup of water to drink because you belong to Christ will certainly be rewarded.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin yɛ ɔkʋ kan sa fanɛ nkyu nuusa lɩɩ fan maa gyɛ mɛ lɛɛ sʋ mʋ, ee biti ɔ nyɛ ɩmʋ kʋkɔka.

eng: As for whoever causes these little ones who believe in me to trip and fall into sin, it would be better for them to have a huge stone hung around their necks and to be thrown into the lake.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan yɛgɛ nɩɩ nbii mʋ nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ mɛ gyii falɛ tɔ ɔkʋ kan waa alibi mʋ, kanɩn odunwura maŋa, nɩɩ ba kan taa kɩfʋlɛ‐buu lala ŋmina kyaga mʋ ɔbʋlɛ tɔ taa mʋ tʋʋ ɔpʋ tɔ gbaa mʋ, ɩ bɔ.

eng: If your hand causes you to fall into sin, chop it off. Its better for you to enter into life crippled than to go away with two hands into the fire of hell, which cant be put out.
local: Nɩɩ fʋ kɩbaa kɩ kan kɩɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ fɩɩ waa libi mʋ, tɩn kɩmʋ fɔyɩ. Ɩ bɔ sa fʋ fɛɛ fʋ maa taa kɩbaa dɩyɛ loo Ɩbwaarɛ ayɛ, ɩ kyɔ fɛɛ fʋ maa taa abaa anyɔ naa loo awʋrʋfɔ fʋlɔn mʋ nɩɩ i mee dun kpaa tɔ.

eng: If your foot causes you to fall into sin, chop it off. Its better for you to enter life lame than to be thrown into hell with two feet.
local: Nɩɩ fʋ kɩyaa kɩ kan kɩɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ fɩɩ waa alibi mʋ, tɩn kɩmʋ fɔyɩ. Ɩ bɔ sa fʋ fɛɛ fʋ maa gyɛ boobi nɩɩ fu loo Ɩbwaarɛ ayɛ, ɩ kyɔ fɛɛ fʋ maa taa ayaa anyɔ naa loo awʋrʋfɔ fʋlɔn tɔ.

eng: If your eye causes you to fall into sin, tear it out. Its better for you to enter Gods kingdom with one eye than to be thrown into hell with two.
local: Nɩɩ fu kinsi kɩ kan kɩɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ fɩɩ waa alibi mʋ, kyɔlɛ kɩmʋ tʋʋ. Ɩ bɔ sa fʋ fɛɛ fʋ maa taa kinsi kʋlʋn kpee Ɩbwaarɛ ayɛ, ɩ kyɔ fɛɛ fʋ maa taa ansi anyɔ naa loo awʋrʋfɔ tɔ kakpaa.

eng: Thats a place where worms dont die and the fire never goes out .
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ, asʋnswɛɛ mʋ nɩɩ be mee wu, nɩɩ fʋlɔn mʋ gbaa mee dun kakaakʋ.

eng: Everyone will be salted with fire.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ taa fʋlɔn bɔɔlɛ ɔkʋmaa kɛ ɔ waa kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ nfɔlɛ bɛɛ bɔɔlɛ inun nɩɩ ɩ waa kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ pɔyɩ nɩɩ ba sʋ bɛɛ waa saraga bɛɛ sa Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ.

eng: Salt is good; but if salt loses its saltiness, how will it become salty again? Maintain salt among yourselves and keep peace with each other.
local: Nfɔlɛ ɩ bʋrɔn, amaa nɩɩ nfɔlɛ kan fɔyɩ ɩmʋ ɔkɔn bɩrɛ, nɛnɛ nɩɩ fɛn bɩla taalɛ waa mʋ kɛ ɩ nyɛ ɩmʋ ɔkɔn? Ɩmʋ sʋ fan yɛgɛ fanɛ awaasa ɩ baa ɩ bʋkɔn fɛɛ nfɔlɛ, kɛ fan kyɩna yɛ abaa kayɩɩ yuuli tɔ.

eng: Jesus left that place and came to his hometown. His disciples followed him.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɩɩ tʋtɔ bwii kpee mʋ gbaa‐gbaa kadɛ tɔ, nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba buu mʋ kpee.

eng: On the Sabbath, he began to teach in the synagogue. Many who heard him were surprised. Where did this man get all this? Whats this wisdom hes been given? What about the powerful acts accomplished through him?.
local: Kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ, o kpee kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa ɛɛ kaapʋ. Nɩɩ mʋ kɩkaapʋ mʋ ki kyinkyin asa mʋ nɩɩ ba nu mʋ gaa, nɩɩ ba taasɛ abaa yɛ, Fɩnɛ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ kɩkaapʋ nɩmʋ lɩɩ? Fɩnɛ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ kanyaasɩn nɩmʋ lɩɩ halɩɩ ɛɛ waa asɩn kyinkyinsa anɩmʋ?.

eng: Isnt this the carpenter? Isnt he Marys son and the brother of James, Joses, Judas, and Simon? Arent his sisters here with us? They were repulsed by him and fell into sin.
local: Man gyɛ kapinta mʋ ndee? Man gyɛ Mɛɛrɩ mu‐bii mʋ nɩn? Man gyɛ mu‐supu ana ɩ gyɛ Gyemisi yɛ Gyosefu yɛ Gyuda yɛ Simon? Man gyɛ anaa mʋ‐pɩɩkyɩɩ ana ɩ bʋ nfɩɩ? Nɩɩ ba kina kɩkɔɔlɛ mʋ.

eng: Jesus said to them, Prophets are honored everywhere except in their own hometowns, among their relatives, and in their own households.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ kʋmaa nɩmʋ sʋ bɛɛrɛɛ katɩn kʋmaa, amɔɔ mʋ kadɛ tɔ yɛ mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ abi nkʋn asɛ nɩɩ ɔ man sʋ bɛɛrɛɛ.

eng: He was unable to do any miracles there, except that he placed his hands on a few sick people and healed them.
local: Kanɩn sʋ mʋ, ɔ man taalɛ waa adangana tʋtɔ kʋkyɔ, amɔɔ asa kalɩsa akʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ abaa gyaga bamʋ sʋ nɩɩ ba nyɛ alanfɩya.

eng: He was appalled by their disbelief. Then Jesus traveled through the surrounding villages teaching.
local: Nɩɩ i kyinkyin mʋ gaa lɩɩ fɛɛ asa mʋ ba man kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii. Nɩɩ Yesu kpee ndɛ‐bii mʋ nɩɩ ɩ maa mʋ kadɛ, ɛɛ kaapʋ bamʋ abwaarɛsɩn.

eng: He called for the Twelve and sent them out in pairs. He gave them authority over unclean spirits.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ kudu anyɔ mʋ, o sun bamʋ anyɔ‐anyɔ, nɩɩ ɔ sa bamʋ ɔlʋn yɛ ba taa gya nbwɩɩ libi.

eng: He instructed them to take nothing for the journey except a walking stick—no bread, no bags, and no money in their belts.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ fɛɛ, Fan maa kpee falɛ, fan man taa sɛhn, amɔɔ fanɛ akpebi nkʋn. Fan man taa agyitɔ abɛɛ adiikun abɛɛ afulee waa fanɛ igyifa tɔ.

eng: He told them to wear sandals but not to put on two shirts.
local: Fan waa asɩbɩta, amaa fan man taa ɩkaalɛ ɩkʋ kɩtaa tii sʋ.

eng: He said, Whatever house you enter, remain there until you leave that place.
local: Kɩkpaara kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ fanɛ mʋ, fan kyɩna tʋtɔ naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ fen biti fan lɩɩ kadɛ mʋ tɔ.

eng: If a place doesnt welcome you or listen to you, as you leave, shake the dust off your feet as a witness against them.
local: Nɩɩ fan kan kpee kadɛ kʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔkʋ man kɔɔlɛ fanɛ abɛɛ nu fanɛ asɛ mʋ, nɩɩ fan kan fɛn lɩɩ kanɩn kadɛ maŋa tɔ mʋ, fan kpɩkpaa fanɛ ayaa tɔ ɩsɛ wʋrɩgɛ tʋtɔ, kɛ ɩmʋ ii biti i gyii bamʋ.

eng: So they went out and proclaimed that people should change their hearts and lives.
local: Nɩɩ ba kpee naa tɔwɛ abwaarɛsɩn sa asa yɛ ba yɛgɛ alibi kɩwaa.

eng: They cast out many demons, and they anointed many sick people with olive oil and healed them.
local: Nɩɩ ba gya nbwɩɩ libi damantɛ kʋsʋ asa sʋ, nɩɩ ba sʋswa alɔpʋ damantɛ olifu nfɔ nɩɩ ba nyɛ alanfɩya.

eng: Herod the king heard about these things, because the name of Jesus had become well-known. Some were saying, John the Baptist has been raised from the dead, and this is why miraculous powers are at work through him.
local: Owura Herodi nu kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kii kpee sʋ kpini, lɩɩ fɛɛ Yesu kɔɔlɛ kɩtɩɩ katɩn kʋmaa. Akʋ ba tɔwɛ yɛ Gyɔn okyugyeepu mʋ ɩ bɩla kyiŋi, ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ taalɛ ɛɛ waa kanɩn asɩn kyinkyinsa.

eng: Others were saying, He is Elijah. Still others were saying, He is a prophet like one of the ancient prophets.
local: Akʋ mɔɔ ba yɛ ɔ gyɛ Eligya nɩn, nɩɩ akʋ mɔɔ ba yɛ ɔ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ dɩdaa mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ nɩn.

eng: But when Herod heard these rumors, he said, John, whom I beheaded, has been raised to life.
local: Herodi maa nu Yesu asɩn mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ yɛ, Ɔ gyɛ Gyɔn okyugyeepu nɩn. Mɛ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ba tɩn mu kumu, amaa o bwii ba nkpa tɔ.

eng: He said this because Herod himself had arranged to have John arrested and put in prison because of Herodias, the wife of Herods brother Philip. Herod had married her.
local: Herodi ɩ daa yɛgɛ nɩɩ ba kɩtaa Gyɔn okyugyeepu, nɩɩ ba taa agbeeleebi da mʋ, nɩɩ ba taa mʋ tii obu. Kʋtɔ mʋ sʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa kanɩn mʋ ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ, mʋ‐ka Herodiya mʋ nɩɩ o kili mʋ, ɔ kɔɔlɛ mʋ lɩɩ mu‐supu Filipu asɛ nɩn.

eng: but John told Herod, Its against the law for you to marry your brothers wife.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ saŋa kʋmaa Gyɔn okyugyeepu ɩɩ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ ɩ man dagaa maa kɔɔlɛ mu‐supu mʋ‐ka kili.

eng: So Herodias had it in for John. She wanted to kill him, but she couldnt.
local: Kanɩn sʋ nɩɩ Gyɔn okyugyeepu nyɛ kilibi Herodiya asɛ, nɩɩ ee biti ɔ mɔɔ mʋ, amaa ɔ man taalɛ.

eng: This was because Herod respected John. He regarded him as a righteous and holy person, so he protected him. Johns words greatly confused Herod, yet he enjoyed listening to him.
local: Herodi sɩlɛ Gyɔn okyugyeepu, lɩɩ fɛɛ o nyi maa gyɛ nyɩmɩsa danbɩrasa yɛ nyɩmɩsa kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ, ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ o kun mʋ. Herodi ii biti Gyɔn abwaarɛsɩn kunu, amaa saŋa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o nu ɩmʋ mʋ ɩɩ tɔɔrɔɔ mʋ gaa.

eng: Finally, the time was right. It was on one of Herods birthdays, when he had prepared a feast for his high-ranking officials and military officers and Galilees leading residents.
local: Herodiya ba nyɛ mu kebiti saŋa mʋ nɩɩ mu‐kuli Herodi i gyii mʋ kakɛ kʋʋgɛsa akɛ. Owura Herodi dɩŋa agyitɔ, nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ kɩbanɛ abɩlɩsa yɛ anaakɔpʋ abɩlɩsa yɛ Galile abɩlɩsa kparɛ‐kparɛ yɛ ba be gyii bamʋ ansi.

eng: Herods daughter Herodias came in and danced, thrilling Herod and his dinner guests. The king said to the young woman, Ask me whatever you wish, and I will give it to you.
local: Nɩɩ Herodiya mu‐bii kyɩɩsa lɩɩ ba kyaa, nɩɩ i gyii Herodi yɛ mʋ afɔ ansi gaa. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ kebii mʋ yɛ, Taasɛ mɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fii biti kɛ n sa fʋ.

eng: Then he swore to her, Whatever you ask I will give to you, even as much as half of my kingdom.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ bɔ ntan yɛ, Kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fii biti mʋ n biti n sa fʋ, halɩɩ mi kuwura‐gyii mʋ kɩbaafʋn gbaa.

eng: She left the banquet hall and said to her mother, What should I ask for? John the Baptists head, Herodias replied.
local: Nɩɩ kebii mʋ lɩɩ ɩkaa sʋ naa taasɛ mu‐nyi kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ lɩɩ Owura Herodi asɛ. Nɩɩ ɔ yɛ, Tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ fii biti Gyɔn okyugyeepu mu kumu.

eng: Hurrying back to the ruler, she made her request: I want you to give me John the Baptists head on a plate, right this minute.
local: Nɩɩ kebii mʋ waa bilen bwii kpee Owura Herodi asɛ naa tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, N biti Gyɔn okyugyeepu kumu tangɩran sʋ nbɩɩnbɩɩ nɩmʋ.

eng: Although the king was upset, because of his solemn pledge and his guests, he didnt want to refuse her.
local: Nɩɩ Owura Herodi kɔkɔlɔ ki nyita mʋ gaa, amaa ɔ man taalɛ kina lɩɩ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛɛ bɔ ntan mʋ afɔ ansi tɔ sʋ mʋ.

eng: So he ordered a guard to bring Johns head. The guard went to the prison, cut off Johns head.
local: Nɩɩ o sun ɔnaakɔpʋ kʋlʋn yɛ ɔ naa bɩya Gyɔn kumu. Nɩɩ o kpee obu tiisa mʋ tɔ naa tɩn mʋ Gyɔn kumu.

eng: brought his head on a plate, and gave it to the young woman, and she gave it to her mother.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ taa gyaga tangɩran sʋ taa ba sa kebii kyɩɩsa‐bi mʋ. Nɩɩ kebii mʋ taa naa sa mu‐nyi.

eng: When Johns disciples heard what had happened, they came and took his dead body and laid it in a tomb.
local: Gyɔn okyugyeepu abɩɩlapʋ ba maa nu kanɩn mʋ, nɩɩ ba ba taa mu kibuni sansa naa pula.

eng: The apostles returned to Jesus and told him everything they had done and taught.
local: Yesu asunpu mʋ ba bwii ba Yesu asɛ ba tɔwɛ mʋ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba waa yɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ ba kaapʋ.

eng: Many people were coming and going, so there was no time to eat. He said to the apostles, Come by yourselves to a secluded place and rest for a while.
local: Lɩɩ kanan mʋ nɩɩ asa kʋkyɔ bɛɛ ba Yesu asɛ bee bwii sʋ mʋ, ba man nyɛ saŋa ba gyii. Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ fɛɛ, Fan yɛgɛ a lɩɩ ɩkaa sʋ kpee opula mʋ nɩɩ asa ba man bʋ, kɛ fan nyɛ saŋa fʋʋtɛ gbɛrɛɛ.

eng: They departed in a boat by themselves for a deserted place.
local: Nɩɩ ba loo ɔkʋlɩ tɔ bamʋ nkʋn bee kpee katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔkʋ man bʋ mʋ.

eng: Many people saw them leaving and recognized them, so they ran ahead from all the cities and arrived before them.
local: Amaa asa damantɛ ba wu bamʋ, nɩɩ ba bɩɩ bamʋ, nɩɩ ba waa bilen taa ayaa tɔ sɩlɛ lɩɩ ndɛ mʋ tɔ kpini gyankpaa naa gyoo bamʋ.

eng: When Jesus arrived and saw a large crowd, he had compassion on them because they were like sheep without a shepherd. Then he began to teach them many things.
local: Yesu maa lɩɩ ɔkʋlɩ tɔ, nɩɩ o wu sakyɔ mʋ, bamʋ asɩn ɩ kɩtaa mʋ kʋwɛɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ba du nɩn fɛɛ nbʋlʋpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba man sʋ ɔkpapʋ. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ o piili ɛɛ kaapʋ bamʋ atɔ damantɛ.

eng: Late in the day, his disciples came to him and said, This is an isolated place, and its already late in the day.
local: Ɩ maa sʋ ii kpee kalaatɩpwɛɛ asɛ mʋ, Yesu abɩɩlapʋ ba ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Kyɔwɛ tɛɛ ɛɛ lʋwɛ. Nɩɩ nfɩɩ mɔɔ waa opula mʋ nɩɩ ɩ waa ɩkaa sʋ, nɩɩ sɛhn man bʋ tɔ.

eng: Send them away so that they can go to the surrounding countryside and villages and buy something to eat for themselves.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ yɛgɛ ba kpee ndɔɔ ana tɔ yɛ ndɛ‐bii mʋ nɩɩ ɩ maa anɛ mʋ naa sɔɔ kʋtɔ kʋ gyii.

eng: He replied, You give them something to eat. But they said to him, Should we go off and buy bread worth almost eight months pay and give it to them to eat.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fanɛ gbaa‐gbaa fan sa bamʋ kʋtɔ kʋ kɛ ba gyii. Nɩɩ ba taasɛ Yesu yɛ, Fii biti fɛɛ a taa gbiti nderebi anyɔ naa sɔɔ bodobodo sa bamʋ.

eng: He said to them, How much bread do you have? Take a look. After checking, they said, Five loaves of bread and two fish.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Bodobodo afɩnɛ nɩɩ fan su? Fan naa kɩɩ. Ba maa naa kɩɩ mʋ, nɩɩ ba ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Bodobodo inuu yɛ ɩkɩn ɩnyɔ nɩɩ an sʋ.

eng: He directed the disciples to seat all the people in groups as though they were having a banquet on the green grass.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ ba yɛgɛ asa mʋ ba kyɩna kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ afitiri bunboli sʋ.

eng: They sat down in groups of hundreds and fifties.
local: Nɩɩ asa mʋ ba kyɩna kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ buu abaa, akʋ kɩlɩfa akʋ mɔɔ adunuu.

eng: He took the five loaves and the two fish, looked up to heaven, blessed them, broke the loaves into pieces, and gave them to his disciples to set before the people. He also divided the two fish among them all.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu taa bodobodo inuu yɛ ɩkɩn ɩnyɔ mʋ, nɩɩ o diyaa mu ansi kɩɩ sʋsʋ, nɩɩ ɔ fwaala Ɩbwaarɛ. Nɩɩ ɔ taa sa abɩɩlapʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ba barɩgɛ sa asa mʋ kpini. Nɩɩ ɔ bɩla barɩgɛ ɩkɩn mʋ tɔ sa asa mʋ kpini.

eng: Everyone ate until they were full.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ gyii naa fʋʋ mʋ ɔkaa.

eng: They filled twelve baskets with the leftover pieces of bread and fish.
local: Nɩɩ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba tasɛ bodobodo yɛ ɩkɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɩ san mʋ waa alantan kudu anyɔ tɔ, nɩɩ ɩ bɔla.

eng: About five thousand had eaten.
local: Anyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba gyii agyitɔ mʋ ba gyɛ ngbɩn nnuu.

eng: Right then, Jesus made his disciples get into a boat and go ahead to the other side of the lake, toward Bethsaida, while he dismissed the crowd.
local: Opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ Yesu yɛgɛ nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba taa ɔkʋlɩ gyankpaa mʋ, bee kpee Betisayida mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ benbe. Nɩɩ mɔɔ mʋ lɛɛ asa mʋ ɔkpa.

eng: After saying good-bye to them, Jesus went up onto a mountain to pray.
local: Maa kala bamʋ lʋwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ o kpee kɩbɩɩ sʋ, ɛɛ kʋlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: Evening came and the boat was in the middle of the lake, but he was alone on the land.
local: Ɩ maa ba fʋʋ kalaatɩpwɛɛ asɛ mʋ, abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba fʋʋ nkyu mʋ nsana, yɛgɛ Yesu nkʋn mɔɔ bʋ kidenbi sʋ.

eng: He saw his disciples struggling. They were trying to row forward, but the wind was blowing against them. Very early in the morning, he came to them, walking on the lake. He intended to pass by them.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu wu fɛɛ kɩpalɛ mʋ ki nu abɩɩlapʋ mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ apaa mʋ ɩ gyaŋa bamʋ. Kɩbwaayɩkɛ sʋ mʋ, Yesu natɛ nkyu sʋ naa fʋʋ bamʋ asɛ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ kyʋn bamʋ sʋ.

eng: When they saw him walking on the lake, they thought he was a ghost and they screamed.
local: Amaa ba wu maa naa nkyu mʋ sʋ, be nyi fɛɛ obuni nɩn, nɩɩ ba waa ɩlawʋ.

eng: Seeing him was terrifying to all of them. Just then he spoke to them, Be encouraged! Its me. Dont be afraid.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba wu Yesu mʋ, kufuu kɩ kɩtaa bamʋ kpini gaa. Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ asɛ opula tɔ yɛ, Fan nyɛ nkɔlɔ, fan man waa kufuu, mɛ nɩn.

eng: He got into the boat, and the wind settled down. His disciples were so baffled they were beside themselves.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu loo bamʋ asɛ ɔkʋlɩ mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ afuu mʋ a yɔɔ opula tɔ. Nɩɩ i kyinkyin abɩɩlapʋ mʋ gaa.

eng: Thats because they hadnt understood about the loaves. Their minds had been closed so that they resisted Gods ways.
local: lɩɩ fɛɛ ba man taalɛ nu kanan mʋ nɩɩ Yesu sa anyɩn ngbɩn nnuu agyitɔ mʋ kasɛ. Nɩɩ ɩ kɩtaa bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ gaa.

eng: When Jesus and his disciples had crossed the lake, they landed at Gennesaret, anchored the boat.
local: Nɩɩ ba faa kpee benbe ɔsʋwʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ ba tɩɩ yɛ Kanesareti, nɩɩ ba taa bamʋ ɔkʋlɩ dan.

eng: and came ashore. People immediately recognized Jesus.
local: Ba maa dan bɛɛ lɩɩ ɔkʋlɩ tɔ mʋ, asa ba bɩɩ Yesu.

eng: and ran around that whole region bringing sick people on their mats to wherever they heard he was.
local: Nɩɩ asa ba sɩlɛ lɩɩ ɔsʋwʋlɛ maŋa sʋ kpini bɛɛ ba Yesu asɛ, nɩɩ ba taa alɔpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba dɛ ɩkalan sʋ bɩya mʋ.

eng: Wherever he went—villages, cities, or farming communities—they would place the sick in the marketplaces and beg him to allow them to touch even the hem of his clothing. Everyone who touched him was healed.
local: Katɩn kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ Yesu kpee ndɛ‐bii abɛɛ ndɛ lala tɔ yɛ ndɔɔ ana tɔ mʋ, asa bɛɛ kpiya alɔpʋ agya tɔ yɛgɛ bɛɛ kʋlɛ mʋ yɛ ɔ yɛgɛ ba mata mʋ kaalɛ. Bamʋ nɩɩ ba mata mʋ kaalɛ kpini mʋ, ba nyɛ alanfɩya.

eng: The Pharisees and some legal experts from Jerusalem gathered around Jesus.
local: Farasii abi yɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ mʋ akʋ, mʋ nɩɩ ba lɩɩ Gyerusalem ba Yesu asɛ mʋ, ba sarɛ kyaabɔɔ mʋ.

eng: They saw some of his disciples eating food with unclean hands. They were eating without first ritually purifying their hands through washing.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba wu fɛɛ Yesu abɩɩlapʋ ba tɛ bee gyii agyitɔ yɛgɛ ba man fwɩɩ bamʋ abaa kanan mʋ nɩɩ bamʋ adɩdaasɩn ɩɩ kaapʋ mʋ.

eng: The Pharisees and all the Jews dont eat without first washing their hands carefully. This is a way of observing the rules handed down by the elders.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Farasii abi mʋ yɛ Gyiwu abi kpini ba buu bamʋ naana ana kɩkaapʋ dɩdaa mʋ nɩn, ba mɛɛ yɛ ba gyii agyitɔ, amɔɔ ba fwɩɩ bamʋ abaa kanan mʋ nɩɩ bamʋ adɩdaasɩn mʋ ɩɩ kaapʋ.

eng: Upon returning from the marketplace, they dont eat without first immersing themselves. They observe many other rules that have been handed down, such as the washing of cups, jugs, pans, and sleeping mats.
local: Nɩɩ ba kan lɩɩ kɩgya tɔ ba mʋ, ba mɛɛ yɛ ba gyii, amɔɔ ba fwɩɩ bamʋ n‐yɩɩ pɔyɩ. Nɩɩ ba bɩla ba sʋ ikisii lɩɩ ɩkɔɔpʋ yɛ npulii yɛ ɩtasa atɔ kɩfwɩɩ sʋ.

eng: So the Pharisees and legal experts asked Jesus, Why are your disciples not living according to the rules handed down by the elders but instead eat food with ritually unclean hands.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ, nɩɩ Farasii abi mʋ yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ mʋ ba taasɛ Yesu yɛ, Mɩnɛ kʋtɔ sʋ nɩɩ fʋ abɩɩlapʋ bɩrɛ be mee buu anɛ naana ana kɩkaapʋ dɩdaa mʋ, nɩɩ ba mɛɛ bamʋ bɩrɛ ba sʋ abaa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ man du kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ bee gyii agyitɔ.

eng: He replied, Isaiah really knew what he was talking about when he prophesied about you hypocrites. He wrote, This people honors me with their lips, but their hearts are far away from me.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bɔla mʋ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Isaya sʋ nɩɩ ɔ kyʋrɔɔ yɩla lɩɩ fanɛ sʋ mʋ, ɩ gyɛ kesintin. Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Fan sʋ fanɛ nnɔ fɛn tɔwɛ yɛ fen sun mɛ, yɛgɛ ɩ man lɩɩ fanɛ nkɔlɔ tɔ.

eng: Their worship of me is empty since they teach instructions that are human words.
local: Kɩyan nɩɩ fen sun mɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ nyɩmɩsa ɩkpa nɩɩ fɛn sʋ fɛn kaapʋ fɛɛ mɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ. .

eng: You ignore Gods commandment while holding on to rules created by humans and handed down to you.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu kyaga sʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, Fan kina Ɩbwaarɛ nbara mʋ, nɩɩ fan buu nyɩmɩsa lɛɛ.

eng: Jesus continued, Clearly, you are experts at rejecting Gods commandment in order to establish these rules.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu bɩla tɔwɛ yɛ, Fan sʋ kanyaasɩn ɩkpa libi mʋ nɩɩ fɛn sʋ fɛn taa Ɩbwaarɛ nbara yɩlɛ ɩkaa sʋ, kɛ fan nyɛ buu nyɩmɩsa kɩkaapʋ sʋ.

eng: Moses said, Honor your father and your mother, and The person who speaks against father or mother will certainly be put to death .
local: Mosesi kaapʋ yɛ, Taa bɛɛrɛɛ sa fʋ‐sɛ yɛ fu‐nyi, nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ nnɔ wʋrɩgɛ mʋ‐sɛ abɛɛ mu‐nyi sʋ mʋ, ba mɔɔ mʋ.

eng: But you say, If you tell your father or mother,Everything Im expected to contribute to you is corban .
local: Amaa fanɛɛ fanɛ, fɛn yɛ nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan tɔwɛ mʋ‐sɛ abɛɛ mu‐nyi yɛ, Kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ naafɔɔ fii biti fʋ nyɛ lɩɩ mɛ asɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ Koriban. .

eng: then you are no longer required to care for your father or mother.
local: Kanɩn sʋ mʋ, ɔ sʋ ɔkpa fɛɛ o kina kɩkyɛ mʋ‐sɛ yɛ mu‐nyi tɔ.

eng: In this way you do away with Gods word in favor of the rules handed down to you, which you pass on to others. And you do a lot of other things just like that.
local: Kanɩn kɩkaapʋ mʋ nɩɩ fan sʋ fɛn kaapʋ asa falɛ ɩɩ yɛgɛ abwaarɛsɩn mʋ ii biliŋi kɩyan. Atɔ damantɛ fɛɛ ɩnɩmʋ nɩɩ fɛn waa.

eng: Then Jesus called the crowd again and said, Listen to me, all of you, and understand.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu bɩla tɩɩ asa ba sarɛ mʋ asɛ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ o nu mɛ asɛ, kɛ ɔ bɩɩ asɩn nɩmʋ kasɛ.

eng: Nothing outside of a person can enter and contaminate a person in Gods sight; rather, the things that come out of a person contaminate the person.
local: Sɛhn man bʋ tɔ nɩɩ ɩɩ lɩɩ kewu ii loo nyɩmɩsa tɔ nɩɩ ɩɩ waa mʋ ineesi, amɔɔ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ lɩɩ mʋ tɔ mʋ ɩɩ waa mʋ ineesi.

eng: After leaving the crowd, he entered a house where his disciples asked him about that riddle.
local: Yesu maa lɩɩ asa mʋ asɛ kpee kɩkpaara sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba taasɛ mʋ kɩŋasan mʋ kasɛ.

eng: He said to them, Dont you understand either? Dont you know that nothing from the outside that enters a person has the power to contaminate?.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ bamʋ fɛɛ, Ɩmʋ sʋ, fanɛ gbaa fen mee nu asɩn kasɛ bilen? Fan man nyi fɛɛ kʋtɔ kʋ man bʋ tɔ kɩɩ lɩɩ kewu nɩɩ kii loo nyɩmɩsa tɔ nɩɩ kɩɩ waa mʋ ineesi.

eng: Thats because it doesnt enter into the heart but into the stomach, and it goes out into the sewer. By saying this, Jesus declared that no food could contaminate a person in Gods sight.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ kii loo nyɩmɩsa tɔ mʋ, ki mee kpee mʋ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ, amɔɔ mʋ ɔtɔ tɔ, kɛ kɩ bɩla bɔla lɩɩ mʋ n‐yɩɩ tɔ.

eng: Its what comes out of a person that contaminates someone in Gods sight, he said.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu kyaga sʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, Kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ lɩɩ nyɩmɩsa tɔ mʋ, kɩmʋ ɩɩ waa mʋ ineesi.

eng: Its from the inside, from the human heart, that evil thoughts come: sexual sins, thefts, murders.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ lɩɩ nyɩmɩsa tɔ, yɛ mʋ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ nɩɩ nfɛɛrɛ libi yɛ kɩbawʋlɛ kigyii, yɛ keyu, yɛ asa kɩmɔɔ yɛ kɩlɩɩ okuli abɛɛ ɔkaa kamaa.

eng: adultery, greed, evil actions, deceit, unrestrained immorality, envy, insults, arrogance, and foolishness.
local: yɛ kensi payɩ libi yɛ kumu tɔ ɔlʋn, yɛ kapɩna asa, yɛ kɩgyagaa, yɛ kayɩɩ basa yɛ nyɩmɩsa kinyita yɛ alala‐sɩn yɛ kimelensi.

eng: All these evil things come from the inside and contaminate a person in Gods sight.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ kpini ɩɩ lɩɩ nyɩmɩsa kɔkɔlɔ tɔ nɩn, nɩɩ ɩɩ waa mʋ ineesi.

eng: Jesus left that place and went into the region of Tyre. He didnt want anyone to know that he had entered a house, but he couldnt hide.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɩɩ Galile kpee Taayi katɩnɛ. Nɩɩ o loo kɩkpaara kʋ sʋ, ɔ man daa ee biti fɛɛ ɔkʋ o wu mʋ, amaa ɔ man taalɛ baala mʋ n‐yɩɩ.

eng: In fact, a woman whose young daughter was possessed by an unclean spirit heard about him right away. She came and fell at his feet.
local: Ɔkyɩɩ kʋ mʋ nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi ba tɛ mu‐bii sʋ mʋ nu Yesu sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ ba mʋ asɛ bilen ba muŋa mʋ ayaa tɔ.

eng: The woman was Greek, Syrophoenician by birth. She begged Jesus to throw the demon out of her daughter.
local: Ɔkyɩɩ mʋ man gyɛ Gyiwu obii, ɔ lɩɩ Fonesiya mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ Siriya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ mʋ nɩn. Nɩɩ ɔ kʋlɛ Yesu yɛ ɔ gya nbwɩɩ libi mʋ lɩɩ mu kebii kyɩɩsa‐bi mʋ sʋ sa mʋ.

eng: He responded, The children have to be fed first. It isnt right to take the childrens bread and toss it to the dogs.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ yɛ, Ɩ dagaa nbii mʋ ba maa gyii ŋmɛ pɔyɩ. Ɩ man bʋrɔn fɛɛ ba taa nbii agyitɔ sa igyoonɔ.

eng: But she answered, Lord, even the dogs under the table eat the childrens crumbs.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ sa kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, ɩ gyɛ kesintin nɩn, amaa nbii ba kan bee gyii agyitɔ nɩɩ ɩkʋ ɩ lɩɩ tɩyɛ kasɛ mʋ, igyoonɔ bɛɛ tasɛ bee gyii.

eng: Good answer! he said. Go on home. The demon has already left your daughter.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu sa ɔkyɩɩ mʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Lɩɩ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fʋ sa mɛ kɔnɔ sʋ mʋ, bwii kpewu, nbwɩɩ libi mʋ ba lɩɩ fu kebii kyɩɩsa‐bi mʋ tɔ.

eng: When she returned to her house, she found the child lying on the bed and the demon gone.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ bwii kpewu naa wu kebii mʋ maa dɛ npaa sʋ yɛgɛ nbwɩɩ libi mʋ ba tɛɛ wʋla lɩɩ mʋ tɔ.

eng: After leaving the region of Tyre, Jesus went through Sidon toward the Galilee Sea through the region of the Ten Cities.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɩɩ Taayi katɩnɛ, nɩɩ ɔ bɔla Sidon kpee Galile ɔbʋn‐kaa naa fʋʋ Dekapoli ɩsʋwʋlɛ sʋ.

eng: Some people brought to him a man who was deaf and could hardly speak, and they begged him to place his hand on the man for healing.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ asa akʋ ba taa kpawu kʋ nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ taalɛ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ ɔ dan ba Yesu asɛ ba kʋlɛ mʋ yɛ ɔ taa mʋ abaa gyaga mʋ sʋ sa bamʋ.

eng: Jesus took him away from the crowd by himself and put his fingers in the mans ears. Then he spit and touched the mans tongue.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu taa kpawu mʋ lɩɩ asa tɔ kpee ɩkaa sʋ, taa mʋ abaa‐bi waa mʋ asʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ tʋʋ nkyɔlɛ nɩɩ ɔ mata mʋ kanandʋlʋ.

eng: Looking into heaven, Jesus sighed deeply and said, Ephphatha, which means, Open up.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu diyaa mu ansi kɩɩ sʋsʋ, nɩɩ o muusi. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ ɔnyɩn mʋ yɛ, Ɩfata. Kɩmʋ kasɛ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, Bugi tɔ.

eng: At once, his ears opened, his twisted tongue was released, and he began to speak clearly.
local: Opula tɔ nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ asʋ a tigi, nɩɩ ee nu katɔwɛ, nɩɩ ɛɛ taalɛ ɛɛ tɔwɛ danbɩrasa.

eng: Jesus gave the people strict orders not to tell anyone. But the more he tried to silence them, the more eagerly they shared the news.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ asa mʋ yɛ ba man kan tɔwɛ sa ɔkʋ. Amaa kɩgya damantɛ nɩɩ ɔ gya bamʋ mʋ, saamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ lɔŋɔ bɛɛ tɔwɛ bee tii sʋ.

eng: People were overcome with wonder, saying, He does everything well! He even makes the deaf to hear and gives speech to those who cant speak.
local: Nɩɩ i kyinkyin asa mʋ gaa nɩɩ ba yɛ, Ɔ waa kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ danbɩrasa. Ɔ yɛgɛ nɩɩ akpawu bee nu, nɩɩ anaamuu bɛɛ tɔwɛ.

eng: Jesus spoke to them in parables. A man planted a vineyard, put a fence around it, dug a pit for the winepress, and built a tower. Then he rented it to tenant farmers and took a trip.
local: Yesu bɔ bamʋ kɩŋasan yɛ, Ɔnyɩn kʋ kyɩna dɔɔ wayɩn kʋdɔɔ kʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ pɔyɩ kɩbandɩyɛ kyaabɔɔ kɩmʋ, nɩɩ o kuu wayɩn ɔnyɩyɛkpa ɔbɔ kʋdɔɔ mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ pɔyɩ opula bʋnswɩɩsa mʋ nɩɩ akɩɩsʋpʋ bee dii bɛɛ kɩɩ kʋdɔɔ mʋ sʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ kadɔɔ maŋa waa paa agyipu kʋ abaa tɔ, nɩɩ o kpee ɔkpa.

eng: When it was time, he sent a servant to collect from the tenants his share of the fruit of the vineyard.
local: Kɩtɩŋɛ saŋa maa fʋʋ mʋ, nɩɩ o sun mʋ kɩyaafɔlɛ kʋ paa agyipu mʋ asɛ, kɛ ɔ naa kɔɔlɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ mʋ lɛɛ mʋ bɩya mʋ.

eng: But they grabbed the servant, beat him, and sent him away empty-handed.
local: Amaa paa agyipu mʋ ba kɩtaa mʋ kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ, dayɩ mʋ bwiiyaa mʋ abaakpan.

eng: Again the landowner sent another servant to them, but they struck him on the head and treated him disgracefully.
local: Nɩɩ kadɔɔ wuya mʋ bwii sun ɔkʋ, nɩɩ paa agyipu mʋ ba dayɩ mʋ paatɛ mu kumu gyila mʋ ipeeli.

eng: He sent another one; that one they killed. The landlord sent many other servants, but the tenants beat some and killed others.
local: Kadɔɔ wuya mʋ bɩla sun ɔkʋ, nɩɩ paa agyipu mʋ ba dayɩ mʋ mɔɔ. Ɔ bɩla sun gaalaagaa, nɩɩ ba dayɩ akʋ, nɩɩ ba mɔɔ akʋ mɔɔ.

eng: Now the landowner had one son whom he loved dearly. He sent him last, thinking, They will respect my son.
local: Ɔkʋlʋn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ dan san kɛ o sun mʋ ɩ gyɛ mu‐bii mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ kebiti sa. Laalaalʋwɛ mʋ o sun mʋ nɩɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔ maa gyɛ mi‐bii gbaa‐gbaa falɛ bɩrɛ, bee biti ba taa bɛɛrɛɛ sa mʋ.

eng: But those tenant farmers said to each other, This is the heir. Lets kill him, and the inheritance will be ours.
local: Amaa paa agyipu mʋ ba tɔwɛ abaa nɩn yɛ, Ɔnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ kapatɩyɛ ogyipu mʋ. Fan ba kɛ a mɔɔ mʋ, kɛ kapatɩyɛ mʋ ki biliŋi anɛ lɛɛ.

eng: They grabbed him, killed him, and threw him out of the vineyard.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ba kɩtaa mu‐bii mʋ, nɩɩ ba mɔɔ, lɛɛ mʋ lɩɩ kadɔɔ mʋ tɔ.

eng: So what will the owner of the vineyard do? He will come and destroy those tenants and give the vineyard to others.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Ɩnɩmʋ falɛ kudubi mʋ, mɩnɛ nɩɩ kadɔɔ wuya mʋ ii biti ɔ waa? Nɩɩ ba yɛ, Ee biti ɔ ba mɔɔ paa agyipu mʋ kpini, kɛ ɔ taa kadɔɔ mʋ waa paa agyipu pʋpwɛ abaa tɔ.

eng: Havent you read this scripture, The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu yɛ, Fan man pɩɩta kalɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ? Asɩn mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, Kibuu mʋ nɩɩ apɔyɩpʋ ba kina mʋ, kɩmʋ ɩ ba biliŋi kɩbɔntɔnbʋlɔntɔ kubuu kparɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ sʋ obu mʋ tɔ.

eng: The Lord has done this, and its amazing in our eyes? .
local: Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ ɩ waa ɩnɩmʋ, nɩɩ i kyinkyin anɛ. .

eng: They wanted to arrest Jesus because they knew that he had told the parable against them. But they were afraid of the crowd, so they left him and went away.
local: Gyiwu abɩlɩsa mʋ ba paa Yesu kɩkɩta, kpalɩ fɛɛ ba bɩɩ yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ Yesu bɔ kanɩn kɩŋasan maŋa. Amaa ba sɩlɛ sakyɔ mʋ, ɩmʋ sʋ ba yɛgɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kyʋn.

eng: They sent some of the Pharisees and supporters of Herod to trap him in his words.
local: Farasii abi yɛ owura Herodi asa akʋ ba ba Yesu asɛ ba sʋʋ mʋ ɔdʋʋ lɩɩ mʋ asɩn tɔwɛsa sʋ.

eng: They came to him and said, Teacher, we know that youre genuine and you dont worry about what people think. You dont show favoritism but teach Gods way as it really is. Does the Law allow people to pay taxes to Caesar or not? Should we pay taxes or not.
local: Ba ba mʋ asɛ ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɔkaapʋpʋ, an nyi fʋ maa gyɛ kesintin ogyipu, nɩɩ fʋ mɛɛ tʋwɛ lɩɩ nfɛɛrɛ mʋ nɩɩ asa bɛɛ fɛ, nɩɩ fʋ mɛɛ kɩɩ nyɩmɩsa kɩkparɛ sʋ, amaa fɩɩ kaapʋ asa Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn bɔla kesintin ɔkpa sʋ. An biti a nu lɩɩ fʋ asɛ yɛ, ɩ dɛ ɔkpa fɛɛ a ka lenpoo sa Roma abi Owura lala Kasaro?.

eng: Since Jesus recognized their deceit, he said to them, Why are you testing me? Bring me a coin. Show it to me.
local: Ɩ dagaa fɛɛ a ka abɛɛ a man ka? Amaa Yesu wʋla o nyi ba maa gyɛ kebunbun ansi wuya ana nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Mɩnɛ ɩ waa sʋ nɩɩ fan sʋʋ mɛ ɔdʋʋ? Fan sa mɛ kefulee nderebi kɛ n kɩɩ.

eng: And they brought one. He said to them, Whose image and inscription is this? Caesars, they replied.
local: Ba maa sa mʋ kefulee nɩɩ ɔ kɩɩ mʋ, ɔ taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Anɩmʋ foto yɛ kɩtɩɩ ɩ gyan kefulee mʋ sʋ falɛ? Nɩɩ ba yɛ, Owura lala Kasaro lɛɛ.

eng: Jesus said to them, Give to Caesar what belongs to Caesar and to God what belongs to God. His reply left them overcome with wonder.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ Yesu yɛ, To! Kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ Kasaro lɛɛ mʋ, fan ka sa mʋ, nɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mɔɔ lɛɛ mʋ, fan taa sa mʋ. Nɩɩ Yesu asɩn a waa bamʋ kanankʋ.

eng: Sadducees, who deny that there is a resurrection, came to Jesus and asked.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Sadusi abi mʋ nɩɩ ba yɛ kelewu kyiŋi man bʋ tɔ mʋ, ba ba Yesu asɛ ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ.

eng: Teacher, Moses wrote for us that if a mans brother dies , leaving a widow but no children, the brother must marry the widow and raise up children for his brother.
local: Ɔkaapʋpʋ, Mosesi kyʋrɔɔ nbara ɩnɩmʋ sa anɛ yɛ, nɩɩ ɔnyɩn kan wu yɛgɛ mʋ‐ka, nɩɩ maa mʋ ba man kʋʋgɛ nbii mʋ, mu‐supu ɩ taalɛ taa okulapu‐kyɩɩ maŋa kili kɛ ba kʋʋgɛ nbii sa olewupu mʋ.

eng: Now there were seven brothers. The first one married a woman; when he died, he left no children.
local: Ɔnyɩn kʋ maa mu‐supu ana asunɔ ɩ kyɩna ba bʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔdaa mʋ kili ɔkyɩɩ, nɩɩ o wu, yɛgɛ maa mʋ‐ka mʋ ba man kʋʋgɛ.

eng: The second married her and died without leaving any children. The third did the same.
local: Osupu mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyan sʋ taa okulapu‐kyɩɩ maŋa kili, nɩɩ ngbaa mʋ wu, yɛgɛ maa ɔkyɩɩ mʋ ba man kʋʋgɛ. Ɔsasapʋ maa bɩla taa mʋ, kʋkʋlʋn maŋa nɩn.

eng: None of the seven left any children. Finally, the woman died.
local: Bamʋ asunɔ maŋa kpini ba kili ɔkyɩɩ mʋ gyaŋɛ yɛgɛ bamʋ ɔkʋ maa ɔkyɩɩ mʋ ba man kʋʋgɛ. Laalaalʋwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ gbaa wu.

eng: At the resurrection, when they all rise up, whose wife will she be? All seven were married to her.
local: Nbɩɩnbɩɩ nɩmʋ, bamʋ asunɔ kpini ba maa kili mʋ falɛ, nɩɩ kelewu kyiŋi kakɛ mʋ, bamʋ tɔ ɔmɔɔ mʋ‐ka ii biti ɩ baa ɩ gyɛ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ.

eng: Jesus said to them, Isnt this the reason you are wrong, because you dont know either the scriptures or Gods power?.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Man gyɛ fan maa man nyi abwaarɛsɩn abɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔlʋn mʋ sʋ nɩɩ fan fɔyɩ falɛ?.

eng: When people rise from the dead, they wont marry nor will they be given in marriage. Instead, they will be like Gods angels.
local: Ɩmʋ kasɛ ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ kelewu kyiŋi saŋa mʋ, asa ba man bɩla yɛ ba kili, bee biti ba biliŋi fɛɛ sʋsʋ nbɔɔ mʋ nɩn.

eng: As for the resurrection from the dead, havent you read in the scroll from Moses, in the passage about the burning bush, how God said to Moses, I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob?.
local: Nbɩɩnbɩɩ, lɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ bee biti ba kyiŋi lɩɩ lewu tɔ mʋ, fan man pɩɩ kalɛ Mosesi ɔwʋlɛ tɔ lɩɩ kanan mʋ nɩɩ o wu fʋlɔn maa dɛɛ apʋnfa mʋ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Mɛ ɩ gyɛ Abraham Ɩbwaarɛ, Isaki maa Gyekobu Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ.

eng: He isnt the God of the dead but of the living. You are seriously mistaken.
local: Ɔ gyɛ asa kɩɩsa Ɩbwaarɛ nɩn, man gyɛ alewupu lɛɛ. Fan fɔyɩ kesintin sʋ.

eng: One of the legal experts heard their dispute and saw how well Jesus answered them. He came over and asked him, Which commandment is the most important of all.
local: Nbara ɔkaapʋpʋ kʋ bʋ tʋtɔ nɩɩ o nu ɩmɔɔrɛ mʋ nɩɩ bee gyii. O wu fɛɛ Yesu lɛɛ Sadusi abi mʋ kɔnɔ danbɩrasa. Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔ ba mʋ asɛ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Nbara mʋ kpini tɔ mʋ, kɩmɔɔ kɩ gyɛ kɩkparɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, The most important one is Israel, listen! Our God is the one Lord.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Nbara mʋ tɔ kɩkparɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ kɩnɩmʋ mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, Oo! Ɩsɩrayɩ nu! Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ anɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ ɩ gyɛ ɔkʋlʋn nɩn.

eng: and you must love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your being, with all your mind, and with all your strength.
local: Taa fʋ kɔkɔlɔ yɛ fʋ kala yɛ fʋ nfɛɛrɛ yɛ fʋ ɔlʋn kpini nyɛ kebiti sa fʋ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: The second is this, You will love your neighbor as yourself . No other commandment is greater than these.
local: Kɩnyɔsapʋ mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, Biti fʋ bɩrɩsa fɛɛ fʋ n‐yɩɩ! Nbara kʋ n man bɩla ɔ bʋ tɔ nɩɩ ɩ kyɔ ɩnɩmʋ.

eng: The legal expert said to him, Well said, Teacher. You have truthfully said that God is one and there is no other besides him.
local: Nɩɩ nbara ɔkaapʋpʋ mʋ tɔwɛ Yesu yɛ, Fʋ waa atɔ gaa, Ɔkaapʋpʋ! Ɩ gyɛ kesintin fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ nkʋn ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kʋ man bɩla ɔ bʋ tɔ.

eng: And to love God with all of the heart, a full understanding, and all of ones strength, and to love ones neighbor as oneself is much more important than all kinds of entirely burned offerings and sacrifices.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan ɔ taa mʋ kɔkɔlɔ yɛ mʋ nfɛɛrɛ yɛ mʋ ɔlʋn nyɛ kebiti sa Ɩbwaarɛ, nɩɩ o biti mʋ‐bɩrɩsa fɛɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ mʋ, ɩ bʋrɔn ɩ kyɔ fɛɛ ɔ maa waa saraga yɛ kakɛɛ sa Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: When Jesus saw that he had answered with wisdom, he said to him, You arent far from Gods kingdom. After that, no one dared to ask him any more questions.
local: Yesu maa wu fɛɛ ɔnyɩn mʋ lɛɛ kɔnɔ kanyaasɩn tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Fʋ man bʋ kata yɛ sʋsʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ. Ɩnɩmʋ kamaa mʋ, ɔkʋ man bɩla nyɛ kɔkɔlɔ taasɛ Yesu asɩn kʋ.

eng: While Jesus was teaching in the temple, he said, Why do the legal experts say that the Christ is Davids son?.
local: Yesu maa kaapʋ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ yɛ, Nɛnɛ sʋ nɩɩ nbara akaapʋpʋ mʋ bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ Krisito mʋ ii biti ɔ lɩɩ Dawidi kanaana tɔ?.

eng: David himself, inspired by the Holy Spirit, said, The Lord said to my lord, Sit at my right side until I turn your enemies into your footstool.
local: Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ yɛgɛ nɩɩ Dawidi tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ ɩ tɔwɛ mɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ yɛ, Kyɩna mɛ gyisa sʋ naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ n biti n taa fʋ dʋn ana kpini waa fʋ ayaa kasɛ.

eng: David himself calls him Lord, so how can he be Davids son? The large crowd listened to him with delight.
local: Dawidi gbaa‐gbaa ɩ tɩɩ mʋ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ. Ɩmʋ sʋ, nɛnɛ sʋ nɩɩ Krisito mʋ ii biti ɔ lɩɩ Dawidi kanaana tɔ? Nɩɩ sakyɔ damantɛ mʋ kpini ba nu Yesu asɛ yɛ kinsigyi.

eng: As he was teaching, he said, Watch out for the legal experts. They like to walk around in long robes. They want to be greeted with honor in the markets.
local: Yesu maa san ɛɛ kaapʋ bamʋ mʋ, ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Fan baa fan da nbara akaapʋpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba bun bamʋ ɩbalɩmasʋ ba naa bee biti afwaala yɛ bɛɛrɛɛ agya tɔ mʋ sʋ.

eng: They long for places of honor in the synagogues and at banquets.
local: yɛgɛ bɛɛ kɩɩ bɛɛ lɛɛ ɩkyɩnakpa kparɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa yɛ kensi‐gyii ipula mʋ.

eng: They are the ones who cheat widows out of their homes, and to show off they say long prayers. They will be judged most harshly.
local: Bɛɛ naa bee puni akulapu‐kyɩɩ bee gyii bamʋ atɔ, yɛgɛ bɛɛ yɩlɛ asa tɔ bɛɛ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ bʋnswɩɩsa. Bee biti ba nyɛ kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ gaa ɩ kyɔ asa kpini.

eng: Jesus sat across from the collection box for the temple treasury and observed how the crowd gave their money. Many rich people were throwing in lots of money.
local: Kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ, Yesu kyɩna yɛgɛ mu ansi ɩɩ kɩɩ afulee kɩdakaa katɩnɛ, nɩɩ ɛɛ kɩɩ asa ba maa sʋ afulee bɛɛ waa tɔ. Afulee wuya ana gaalaagaa ba waa afulee gaa.

eng: One poor widow came forward and put in two small copper coins worth a penny.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ okulapu‐kyɩɩ tiripu kʋ taa nderebi anyɔ ba waa tɔ.

eng: Jesus called his disciples to him and said, I assure you that this poor widow has put in more than everyone whos been putting money in the treasury.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba sarɛ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin fɛɛ, okulapu‐kyɩɩ tiripu nɩmʋ waa afulee kɩdakaa mʋ tɔ ɔ kyɔ asa mʋ kpini.

eng: All of them are giving out of their spare change. But she from her hopeless poverty has given everything she had, even what she needed to live on.
local: lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba waa gaa‐gaa mʋ, ba gyii san ɩkʋ yɩla bamʋ ibu to nɩn, amaa okulapu‐kyɩɩ tiripu nɩmʋ bɩrɛ, mu afulee kpini mʋ nɩɩ ɔ dan ɔ sʋ mu obu to mʋ kpini‐kpini nɩɩ ɔ taa ba waa tɔ falɛ.

eng: At daybreak, the chief priests—with the elders, legal experts, and the whole Sanhedrin—formed a plan. They bound Jesus, led him away, and turned him over to Pilate.
local: Lɩɩkaakɛ nyenyenfuu, nɩɩ aseepu bɩlɩsa yɛ Gyiwu abɩlɩsa yɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ yɛ kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ abɩlɩsa mʋ kpini, ba gyaŋɛ nɩɩ ba taa nfɛɛrɛ kʋlʋn. Nɩɩ ba ŋmina Yesu nɩɩ ba taa mʋ kyʋn naa sa Paleti mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ Roma obii, nɩɩ ɛɛ kɩɩ Ɩsɩrayɩ ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ mʋ.

eng: Pilate questioned him, Are you the king of the Jews? Jesus replied, Thats what you say.
local: Nɩɩ Paleti taasɛ Yesu yɛ, Fʋ ɩ gyɛ Gyiwu abi owura mʋ? Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Kanan mʋ nɩɩ fʋ tɔwɛ mʋ, kanɩn nɩn.

eng: The chief priests were accusing him of many things.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ mɔɔ ba pwɩɩ Yesu lɩɩ asɩn gaalaagaa sʋ.

eng: Pilate asked him again, Arent you going to answer? What about all these accusations.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ ɔbɩlɩsa Paleti bɩla taasɛ Yesu yɛ, Fʋ mɛɛ yɛ fʋ lɛɛ kɔnɔ? Nɩɩ asɩn damantɛ mʋ nɩɩ ba pwɩɩ fʋ lɩɩ ɩmʋ sʋ falɛ fa.

eng: But Jesus gave no more answers, so that Pilate marveled.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu kina kɩlɛɛ kɔnɔ. Nɩɩ ɩ waa Paleti kanankʋ.

eng: During the festival, Pilate released one prisoner to them, whomever they requested.
local: Kesu kʋmaa, ngyaŋɛ nbunɔ saŋa mʋ, ɔbɩlɩsa Paleti ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ bɛɛ lɛɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba tii obu mʋ tɔ ɔkʋlʋn mʋ nɩɩ sakyɔ mʋ bee biti mʋ sa bamʋ.

eng: A man named Barabbas was locked up with the rebels who had committed murder during an uprising.
local: Kanɩn saŋa maŋa tɔ mʋ, ba tii ɔnyɩn kʋ obu, bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Barabasi. O tii bamʋ nɩɩ ba man biti kɩbanɛ nɩɩ ba kɔ keluu mɔɔ nyɩmɩsa mʋ sʋ nɩn.

eng: The crowd pushed forward and asked Pilate to release someone, as he regularly did.
local: Nɩɩ asa mʋ ba ba Paleti asɛ, ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, ɔ waa sa bamʋ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ wʋla ɛɛ waa mʋ.

eng: Pilate answered them, Do you want me to release to you the king of the Jews.
local: Nɩɩ Paleti taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fen biti fɛɛ n yɛgɛ Gyiwu abi owura mʋ.

eng: He knew that the chief priests had handed him over because of jealousy.
local: Paleti dan o nyi kesintin fɛɛ aseepu abɩlɩsa ba taa Yesu ba sa mʋ lɩɩ kayɩɩ basa.

eng: But the chief priests stirred up the crowd to have him release Barabbas to them instead.
local: Amaa aseepu bɩlɩsa mʋ ba fun waa asa mʋ yɛ ba kʋlɛ Paleti kɛ ɔ yɛgɛ Barabasi sa bamʋ, man gyɛ Yesu.

eng: Pilate replied, Then what do you want me to do with the one you call king of the Jews.
local: Nɩɩ Paleti bɩla bwii taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Nɩɩ mɩnɛ nɩɩ fen biti fɛɛ n waa ɔmʋ nɩɩ fɛn tɩɩ yɛ Gyiwu abi owura mʋ.

eng: They shouted back, Crucify him.
local: Nɩɩ ba kuusi yɛ, Da mʋ oyu laasa sʋ mɔɔ mʋ.

eng: Pilate said to them, Why? What wrong has he done? They shouted even louder, Crucify him.
local: Nɩɩ Paleti taasɛ yɛ, Nɩɩ mɩnɛ kilibi nɩɩ ɔ dan waa nɩn? Nɩɩ ba kuusi kenken tii sʋ yɛ, ba da mʋ oyu laasa sʋ.

eng: Pilate wanted to satisfy the crowd, so he released Barabbas to them. He had Jesus whipped, then handed him over to be crucified.
local: Paleti mɔɔ bwii ee biti ɔ waa asa mʋ kɔkɔlɔ biti sʋ, nɩɩ o tigi Barabasi sa bamʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ba taa tɩŋɛ Yesu, nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ waa bamʋ abaa tɔ yɛ ba naa da mʋ mata oyu laasa sʋ.

eng: The soldiers led Jesus away into the courtyard of the palace known as the governors headquarters, and they called together the whole company of soldiers.
local: Nɩɩ anaakɔpʋ mʋ ba taa Yesu naa loo Ɔbɩlɩsa Paleti lenbu tɔ. Nɩɩ ba tɩɩ bamʋ tɔɔmaa ana asansa.

eng: They dressed him up in a purple robe and twisted together a crown of thorns and put it on him.
local: Nɩɩ ba bun Yesu kɩrɩman‐kyu kɩtaa fɛɛ awura lɛɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ba taa iwii waa kuwura‐wʋrʋ bun mʋ.

eng: They saluted him, Hey! King of the Jews.
local: Nɩɩ ba piili bɛɛ fwaala mʋ bɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Gyiwu abi owura mʋ, nyɛ nkpa bʋnswɩɩsa.

eng: Again and again, they struck his head with a stick. They spit on him and knelt before him to honor him.
local: Nɩɩ ba taa oyu bɛɛ da mu kumu tɔ, nɩɩ bɛɛ tʋʋ nkyɔlɛ ba bun mʋ, nɩɩ ba gya muŋa bamʋ ayaa muli sʋ sa mʋ fɛɛ ba maa sun owura mʋ.

eng: When they finished mocking him, they stripped him of the purple robe and put his own clothes back on him. Then they led him out to crucify him.
local: Ba maa tɛɛ waa Yesu ahin‐sɩn lʋwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ba bugi bamʋ kɩrɩman‐kyu kɩtaa mʋ, nɩɩ ba taa Yesu gbaa‐gbaa lɛɛ bun mʋ. Nɩɩ ba taa mʋ kyʋn ba naa da mʋ oyu laasa mʋ sʋ.

eng: Simon, a man from Cyrene, Alexander and Rufus father, was coming in from the countryside. They forced him to carry his cross.
local: Ba maa kpee mʋ, ba gyaŋa Simon mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ Siren ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ, ɛɛ ba kadɛ lala mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ anaakɔpʋ mʋ ba nyan mʋ yɛ ɔ sʋla Yesu oyu laasa mʋ. Simon nɩmʋ mu‐bii ana ɩ gyɛ Alekisanda maa Rufusi.

eng: They brought Jesus to the place called Golgotha, which means Skull Place.
local: Nɩɩ ba taa Yesu kpee katɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ Goligota mʋ. Goligota kasɛ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, Kumu kawɛ opula.

eng: They tried to give him wine mixed with myrrh, but he didnt take it.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba taa kɩfɛ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ mɩɩrɛ waa nta tɔ sa Yesu yɛ o nuu. Amaa Yesu kina ɔ man nuu.

eng: They crucified him. They divided up his clothes, drawing lots for them to determine who would take what.
local: Nɩɩ ba da Yesu oyu laasa sʋ. Ba maa biti ba barɩgɛ Yesu ɩkaalɛ tɔ mʋ, ba tʋʋ nbii barɩgɛ mʋ ɩkaalɛ mʋ tɔ sa bamʋ n‐yɩɩ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa gyii mʋ.

eng: It was nine in the morning when they crucified him.
local: Nyenyenfuu, ɩ waa fɛɛ kerefi akpanɔ saŋa, nɩɩ ba da Yesu mata oyu laasa mʋ sʋ.

eng: The notice of the formal charge against him was written, The king of the Jews.
local: Nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba taa bun Yesu kʋpwɛ mʋ taa da mata mu amu sʋ yɛ, ƆNƖMƲ Ɩ GYƐ GYIWU ABI OWURA MƲ.

eng: They crucified two outlaws with him, one on his right and one on his left.
local: Kanɩn saŋa maŋa mʋ, ba da bamʋ nɩɩ bee yuuri ɔlʋn sʋ anyɔ akʋ iyu laasa sʋ Yesu asɛ. Ɔkʋ bʋ mʋ gyisa sʋ, nɩɩ ɔkʋ mɔɔ bʋ mʋ bɩna sʋ.

eng: People walking by insulted him, shaking their heads and saying, Ha! So you were going to destroy the temple and rebuild it in three days, were you?.
local: Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ ba ɛɛ kyʋn tʋtɔ gbaa bɛɛ sʋ asebi bɛɛ wʋrɩgɛ Yesu sʋ, yɛgɛ bee fi‐fi bamʋ amu bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, To, yɛ fʋ yɛ fii biti fu boyi kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ kɛ fʋ bɩla bwii pɔyɩ kɩmʋ nkɛ nsa akɛ mʋ?.

eng: Save yourself and come down from that cross.
local: Kanɩn bɩrɛ kpɩlɩgɛ lɩɩ oyu laasa mʋ sʋ kɛ fʋ mɔlɩgɛ fʋ n‐yɩɩ.

eng: In the same way, the chief priests were making fun of him among themselves, together with the legal experts. He saved others, they said, but he cant save himself.
local: Kanɩn nɩɩ aseepu bɩlɩsa yɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ mʋ gbaa ba ŋmasɛ Yesu nɩn. Bɛɛ tɔwɛ abaa yɛ, Ɛɛ mɔlɩgɛ asa amaa ɔ mɛɛ taalɛ mɔlɩgɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ.

eng: Let the Christ, the king of Israel, come down from the cross. Then well see and believe. Even those who had been crucified with Jesus insulted him.
local: Nɩɩ ba yɛ, Fʋ nyɛ nɩɩ fʋ gyɛ Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ yɛ ɔ ba mɔlɩgɛ asa, nɩɩ fʋ bɩla fʋ gyɛ Gyiwu abi owura mʋ de, kpɩlɩgɛ lɩɩ oyu laasa mʋ sʋ, saamʋ an nyɛ kɔɔlɛ fu gyii. Asa anyɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba da bamʋ iyu laasa mʋ sʋ gbaa bɛɛ saalɛ Yesu.

eng: From noon until three in the afternoon the whole earth was dark.
local: Kyɔwɛ maa fʋʋ amu nsana mʋ, nɩɩ kitentenbiri ki bun ɔsʋwʋlɛ mʋ kpini sʋ naa fʋʋ kalaatɩpwɛ asɛ.

eng: At three, Jesus cried out with a loud shout, Eloi, eloi, lama sabachthani, which means, My God, my God, why have you left me.
local: Ɩ maa fʋʋ kerefi asa kalaatɩpwɛɛ asɛ mʋ, nɩɩ Yesu kuusi kenken yɛ, Eli, Eli, lamaa sabatani? Ɩmʋ kasɛ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, Mɛ Ɩbwaarɛ, mɛ Ɩbwaarɛ, mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fʋ yɛgɛ mɛ kanɩn.

eng: After hearing him, some standing there said, Look! Hes calling Elijah.
local: Asa mʋ nɩɩ ba yɩlɛ tʋtɔ mʋ i nu ɩnɩmʋ, nɩɩ ba yɛ, Fan nu! Ɛɛ tɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ Ɔtɔwɛpʋ Eligya.

eng: Someone ran, filled a sponge with sour wine, and put it on a pole. He offered it to Jesus to drink, saying, Lets see if Elijah will come to take him down.
local: Nɩɩ bamʋ ɔkʋ sɩlɛ naa taa ɔkyɛɛ dufudufu nyɔ nta nyaŋɛsa tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ taa ŋmina oyu sʋ kʋsaa naa tii Yesu kɔnɔ yɛ o nuu. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Fan yɛgɛ a kɩɩ fɛɛ Eligya ii biti ɔ ba kpɩlɩgɛ mʋ oyu laasa mʋ sʋ.

eng: But Jesus let out a loud cry and died.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu sawʋ kenken, nɩɩ o wu.

eng: The curtain of the sanctuary was torn in two from top to bottom.
local: Nɩɩ kɩtaa kunsa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ barɩgɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ ɔtɔ tɔ mʋ kyatɛ tɔ kɩnyɔ lɩɩ sʋsʋ ba yii kasɛ.

eng: When the centurion, who stood facing Jesus, saw how he died, he said, This man was certainly Gods Son.
local: Nɩɩ anaakɔpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ yɩlɛ tʋtɔ mʋ wu kanan mʋ nɩɩ Yesu sawʋ nɩɩ o wu mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Kesintin, ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Mu‐Bii nɩn.

eng: Some women were watching from a distance, including Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of James and Joses, and Salome.
local: Akyɩɩ akʋ ba yɩlɛ kata bɛɛ kɩɩ. Bamʋ tɔ akʋ ɩ gyɛ Mɛɛrɩ Magidalen yɛ Mɛɛrɩ, Gyemisi pii asɛ maa Gyosefu bamʋ‐nyi, yɛ Solome.

eng: When Jesus was in Galilee, these women had followed and supported him, along with many other women who had come to Jerusalem with him.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Yesu bʋ Galile mʋ, ba buu mʋ, nɩɩ ba kyɛ mʋ tɔ. Akyɩɩ gaalaagaa mʋ nɩɩ ba buu mʋ ba Gyerusalem mʋ gbaa ba yɩlɛ tʋtɔ.

eng: Since it was late in the afternoon on Preparation Day, just before the Sabbath.
local: Kanɩn kakɛ maŋa kɩ gyɛ kakɛ kʋlasa gyoo kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa mʋ. Kyɔwɛ maa kpee ɔ naa tɩyɛ mʋ.

eng: Joseph from Arimathea dared to approach Pilate and ask for Jesus body.
local: nɩɩ Gyosefu mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ Arimatiya mʋ naa ba. Ɔ gyɛ nyɩmɩsa kparɛ nɩn, nɩɩ o tii kadɛ abɩlɩsa mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ sʋ bɛɛrɛɛ asa asɛ. Nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ee gyoo Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ kɩba saŋa mʋ. Nɩɩ Gyosefu waa kɔkɔlɔ kpee Paleti asɛ naa kʋlɛ Yesu kibuni mʋ.

eng: Pilate wondered if Jesus was already dead. He called the centurion and asked him whether Jesus had already died.
local: Paleti maa nu fɛɛ Yesu tɛɛ wʋla wu mʋ, nɩɩ i kyinkyin mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ anaakɔpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Yesu tee wu.

eng: When he learned from the centurion that Jesus was dead, Pilate gave the dead body to Joseph.
local: Paleti maa nu anaakɔpʋ bɩlɩsa asɛ yɛ ɩ gyɛ kanɩn mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ sa ɔkpa yɛ Gyosefu ɔ naa taa Yesu kebuni mʋ naa pula.

eng: He bought a linen cloth, took Jesus down from the cross, wrapped him in the cloth, and laid him in a tomb that had been carved out of rock. He rolled a stone against the entrance to the tomb.
local: Nɩɩ Gyosefu naa sɔɔ taafuli, nɩɩ ɔ naa saatɛ Yesu, taa taafuli mʋ maalɛ mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ naa pula kakyan mʋ nɩɩ ba ŋɛ kɩfʋlɛ tɔ waa ɔbɔ mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ Gyosefu gbiliti kubuu lala tii kakyan mʋ kɔnɔ.

eng: Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of Joses saw where he was buried.
local: Nɩɩ Mɛɛrɩ Magidalen yɛ Gyose mu‐nyi Mɛɛrɩ ba yɩlɛ kɩɩ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba taa Yesu naa pula mʋ.

eng: Jesus left that place and went beyond the Jordan and into the region of Judea. Crowds gathered around him again and, as usual, he taught them.
local: Yesu maa lɩɩ tʋtɔ mʋ, o kpee Gyudeya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ faa Gyɔdan ɔbʋn mʋ kpee benbe. Nɩɩ sakyɔ damantɛ kʋ kɩ ba kyaabɔɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kaapʋ bamʋ abwaarɛsɩn fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ wʋla ɛɛ waa mʋ.

eng: Some Pharisees came and, trying to test him, they asked, Does the Law allow a man to divorce his wife.
local: Tʋtɔ mʋ Farasii abi akʋ ba ba sʋʋ Yesu ɔdʋʋ sɩntaasɛ tɔ yɛ, Ɩ dɛ ɔkpa sa ɔnyɩn fɛ o kina mʋ‐ka.

eng: Jesus answered, What did Moses command you.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu bwii taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Mɩnɛ nbara nɩɩ Mosesi kaapʋ fanɛ.

eng: They said, Moses allowed a man to write a divorce certificate and to divorce his wife.
local: Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Mosesi sa ɔkpa fɛɛ ɔnyɩn ɔ kyʋrɔɔ kekina ɔwʋlɛ sa mʋ‐ka kɛ o kina mʋ.

eng: Jesus said to them, He wrote this commandment for you because of your unyielding hearts.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fanɛ nkɔlɔ ɩ maa bʋlʋn sʋ nɩɩ Mosesi kyʋrɔɔ kanɩn nbara mʋ sa fanɛ.

eng: At the beginning of creation, God made them male and female .
local: Naafɔɔ lɩɩ koo npiili asɛ, atɔ kʋtʋʋ tɔ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ waa bamʋ ɔnyɩn yɛ ɔkyɩɩ.

eng: Because of this, a man should leave his father and mother and be joined together with his wife.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ ɔnyɩn ii biti ɔ yɛgɛ mʋ‐sɛ yɛ mu‐nyi, kɛ ɔ taa mʋ n‐yɩɩ mata mʋ‐ka.

eng: and the two will be one flesh. So they are no longer two but one flesh.
local: kɛ bamʋ asa anyɔ ba biliŋi nyɩmɩsa kʋlʋn, yɛgɛ ba man bɩla ba gyɛ asa anyɔ, amaa ba gyɛ ɔkʋlʋn nɩn.

eng: Therefore, humans must not pull apart what God has put together.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ, ɩmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ taa waa kʋkʋlʋn mʋ, ɔkʋ man kan barɩgɛ ɩmʋ tɔ.

eng: Inside the house, the disciples asked him again about this.
local: Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba maa loo kɩkpaara sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba lɔŋɔ taasɛ mʋ asɩn lɩɩ kekili mʋ asɩn sʋ.

eng: He said to them, Whoever divorces his wife and marries another commits adultery against her.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kan kina mʋ‐ka nɩɩ ɔ naa kili ɔkyɩɩ pʋpwɛ mʋ, o nyita kekili, nɩɩ ɔ waa mʋ‐ka mu alibi.

eng: and if a wife divorces her husband and marries another, she commits adultery.
local: Kanɩn ɔkpa kʋlʋn sʋ nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ kan kina mu‐kuli naa kili ɔnyɩn pʋpwɛ mʋ, o nyita kekili.

eng: People were bringing children to Jesus so that he would bless them. But the disciples scolded them.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ asa akʋ ba taa bamʋ nbii wuribi bɛɛ ba Yesu asɛ yɛ ɔ taa mʋ kɩbaa gyaga bamʋ sʋ, yɛgɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ bɛɛ gya bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ bɩya nbii mʋ.

eng: When Jesus saw this, he grew angry and said to them, Allow the children to come to me. Dont forbid them, because Gods kingdom belongs to people like these children.
local: Yesu maa wu ɩmʋ kanɩn mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ agbʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan yɛgɛ nbii wuribi mʋ ba ba mɛ asɛ, kɛ fan man tii bamʋ ɔkpa, lɩɩ fɛɛ sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii mʋ kɩ gyɛ sa asa mʋ nɩɩ ba du fɛɛ nbii wuribi mʋ nɩn.

eng: I assure you that whoever doesnt welcome Gods kingdom like a child will never enter it.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man kɔɔlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ fɛɛ kebii mʋ, ɔ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ nyɛ loo kɩmʋ tɔ.

eng: Then he hugged the children and blessed them.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ bamʋ puga, taa mʋ abaa gyaga‐gyaga bamʋ sʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa bamʋ kusee.

eng: As Jesus continued down the road, a man ran up, knelt before him, and asked, Good Teacher, what must I do to obtain eternal life.
local: Yesu maa dan yii ɔkpa mʋ sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔnyɩn kʋ sɩlɛ ba muŋa mu ansi tɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɔkaapʋpʋ danbɩrasa, mɩnɛ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa n maa waa kɛ n nyɛ nkpa kakpaa.

eng: Jesus replied, Why do you call me good? No one is good except the one God.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ fʋ tɩɩ mɛ ɔdanbɩrasa? Ɔkʋ man gyɛ ɔdanbɩrasa, amɔɔ Ɩbwaarɛ nkʋn.

eng: You know the commandments: Dont commit murder. Dont commit adultery. Dont steal. Dont give false testimony . Dont cheat. Honor your father and mother .
local: Fu nyi Ɩbwaarɛ nbara mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, Man mɔɔ nyɩmɩsa, man kan lɩɩ fʋ‐ka abɛɛ fu‐kuli kamaa, man yuuri, man tɩn antɩŋɛsa, man puni fʋ tɔɔmaa, taa bɛɛrɛɛ sa fʋ‐sɛ yɛ fu‐nyi. .

eng: Teacher, he responded, Ive kept all of these things since I was a boy.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɔkaapʋpʋ, n wʋla n buu ɩnɩmʋ kpini lɩɩ koo mi kibii tɔ.

eng: Jesus looked at him carefully and loved him. He said, You are lacking one thing. Go, sell what you own, and give the money to the poor. Then you will have treasure in heaven. And come, follow me.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu kɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ diin, mʋ asɩn ɩ pɩrɛ mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɩ san fʋ kʋtɔ kʋlʋn, kpee naa fɛ fʋ kapatɩyɛ kpini, kɛ fʋ taa afulee mʋ sa atiripu. Ɩmʋ ii biti ɩ yɛgɛ fʋ nyɛ ɩmʋ kʋkɔka, kɛ fʋ ba buu mɛ.

eng: But the man was dismayed at this statement and went away saddened, because he had many possessions.
local: Ɔnyɩn mʋ maa nu kanan mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ nyaŋɛ mu ansi tɔ taa kɔkɔlɔ nyita kpewu, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ gyɛ atɔ wuya gaa nɩn.

eng: Looking around, Jesus said to his disciples, It will be very hard for the wealthy to enter Gods kingdom.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu kɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ tɔ kyaabɔɔ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Ɩ bʋlʋn gaa sa atɔ wuya ana fɛɛ ba loo Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ.

eng: His words startled the disciples, so Jesus told them again, Children, its difficult to enter Gods kingdom!.
local: Asɩn mʋ i kyinkyin abɩɩlapʋ mʋ. Amaa Yesu bɩla tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Mi‐bii ana, yɛ fan wu kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ kiloo bʋlʋn.

eng: Its easier for a camel to squeeze through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter Gods kingdom.
local: Ɩ bʋpɔɔ sa nyɔɔma fɛɛ ɔ taalɛ loo baasa ɔbɔ tɔ bʋlɛ, ɩ kyɔ atɔ wuya o loo Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ.

eng: They were shocked even more and said to each other, Then who can be saved.
local: Asɩn nɩmʋ ɩ dɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ gaa, bɛɛ taasɛ abaa yɛ, Anɩmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ mɔlɩgɛ.

eng: Jesus looked at them carefully and said, Its impossible with human beings, but not with God. All things are possible for God.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu kɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ surum, nɩɩ ɔ yɛ, Nyɩmɩsa asɛ nɩɩ ɩnɩmʋ ɩ mɛɛ taalɛ waa, man gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ. Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ bɩrɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ ɩɩ taalɛ waa.

eng: Peter said to him, Look, weve left everything and followed you.
local: Nɩɩ Pita tɔwɛ yɛ, An bwiiyaa kʋtɔ kʋmaa yɛgɛ nɩɩ an baa an buu fʋ.

eng: Jesus said, I assure you that anyone who has left house, brothers, sisters, mother, father, children, or farms because of me and because of the good news.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ yɛ, N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin yɛ ɔkʋ man pɩɩ yɛgɛ mʋ kɩkpaara yɛ mʋ‐daa ana yɛ mu‐supu ana yɛ mʋ‐pɩɩkyɩɩ ana mu‐nyi yɛ mʋ‐sɛ yɛ mu‐bii ana yɛ mʋ ndɔɔ ana, lɩɩ nmaa mɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ sʋ.

eng: will receive one hundred times as much now in this life—houses, brothers, sisters, mothers, children, and farms —and in the coming age, eternal life.
local: nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ yɛ o bwii nyɛ kɩkpaara yɛ mʋ‐daa ana yɛ mu‐supu ana yɛ mʋ‐pɩɩkyɩɩ ana mu‐nyi yɛ mʋ‐sɛ yɛ mu‐bii ana yɛ mʋ ndɔɔ ana kpini kɩlɩfa sʋ. Ee biti ɔ nyɛ kekisi ndaga kakyɩna nɩmʋ tɔ bɩrɛ, amaa ee biti ɔ ba nyɛ nkpa kakpaa.

eng: But many who are first will be last. And many who are last will be first.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu bɩla tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Agyankpaapʋ gaalaagaa, bee biti ba be biliŋi asii‐amaapʋ, kɛ asii‐amaapʋ ba be biliŋi agyankpaapʋ.

eng: Jesus and his disciples were on the road, going up to Jerusalem, with Jesus in the lead. The disciples were amazed while the others following behind were afraid. Taking the Twelve aside again, he told them what was about to happen to him.
local: Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba naa bee kpee Gyerusalem nɩɩ Yesu ɩ gyankpaa bamʋ bee kpee. Asɩn mʋ i kyinkyin abɩɩlapʋ mʋ, nɩɩ kufuu kɩ sʋ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba buu bamʋ mʋ. Nɩɩ Yesu bɩla lɛɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ kudu anyɔ mʋ ɩkaa sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kii biti ki tu mʋ.

eng: Look! he said. Were going up to Jerusalem. The Human One will be handed over to the chief priests and the legal experts. They will condemn him to death and hand him over to the Gentiles.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ, An kpee Gyerusalem, nɩɩ bee biti ba lɛɛ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ sa Gyiwu aseepu bɩlɩsa yɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ. Bee biti ba bun mɛ lewu kʋpwɛ, kɛ ba taa mɛ waa bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi abaa tɔ.

eng: They will ridicule him, spit on him, torture him, and kill him. After three days, he will rise up.
local: Kanɩn asa maŋa bee biti ba waa mɛ yɛrɛyɛrɛ, kɛ ba tʋʋ nkyɔlɛ bun mɛ, kɛ tɩŋɛ mɛ awʋlɛ‐bi kɛ ba mɔɔ mɛ. Amaa nkɛ asá kamaa mʋ, n biti n kyiŋi ba nkpa tɔ.

eng: James and John, Zebedees sons, came to Jesus and said, Teacher, we want you to do for us whatever we ask.
local: Nɩɩ Sebedi mu‐bii ana Gyemisi maa Gyɔn, ba ba Yesu asɛ ba kʋlɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɔkaapʋpʋ, an kʋlɛ fʋ yɛ fʋ waa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ an biti a kʋlɛ fʋ falɛ mʋ sa anɛ.

eng: What do you want me to do for you? he asked.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Mɩnɛ nɩɩ fen biti fɛɛ n waa sa fanɛ.

eng: They said, Allow one of us to sit on your right and the other on your left when you enter your glory.
local: Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Nɩɩ fʋ kan ba kyɩna fʋ kuwura‐gya, fʋ bʋnyaa kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ mʋ, fʋ yɛgɛ anɛ ɔkʋ ɔ kyɩna fu gyisa sʋ, kɛ ɔkʋ mɔɔ ɔ kyɩna fʋ‐bɩna sʋ.

eng: Jesus replied, You dont know what youre asking! Can you drink the cup I drink or receive the baptism I receive.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan man nyi kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ fɛn kʋlɛ falɛ. Fɛn taalɛ gyii awʋrʋfɔ mʋ nɩɩ n biti n gyii falɛ, nɩɩ kekyugyee mʋ nɩɩ bee biti ba gyee mɛ mʋ, fɛn taalɛ gyee kanɩn kekyugyee maŋa.

eng: We can, they answered. Jesus said, You will drink the cup I drink and receive the baptism I receive.
local: Nɩɩ ba kyula yɛ, An taalɛ. Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fen biti fan gyii awʋrʋfɔ mʋ nɩɩ n biti n gyii falɛ, kɛ fan gyee mɛ kekyugyee mʋ nɩɩ n gyee mʋ.

eng: but to sit at my right or left hand isnt mine to give. It belongs to those for whom it has been prepared.
local: Amaa ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ kyɩna mɛ gyisa sʋ abɛɛ mɛ bɩna sʋ bɩrɛ, man gyɛ mɛ ii biti n sa. Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ taa sa bamʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɔŋɔ yɩla mʋ.

eng: Now when the other ten disciples heard about this, they became angry with James and John.
local: Yesu abɩɩlapʋ kudu mʋ nɩɩ ba san mʋ, ba maa nu asɩn mʋ, ba nyɛ agbʋ waa Gyemisi maa Gyɔn.

eng: Jesus called them over and said, You know that the ones who are considered the rulers by the Gentiles show off their authority over them and their high-ranking officials order them around.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu tɩɩ bamʋ kpini sarɛ mʋ asɛ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fan nyi mʋ, bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi bamʋ agyankpaapʋ mʋ, bɛɛ kaapʋ bamʋ ɔlʋn asa sʋ, nɩɩ bamʋ alʋlʋnpʋ gbaa bɛɛ kaapʋ ɔlʋn asa sʋ.

eng: But thats not the way it will be with you. Whoever wants to be great among you will be your servant.
local: Amaa fanɛ bɩrɛ, fan man waa kanɩn. Ɔmʋ nɩɩ ee biti fɛɛ ɔ waa nyɩmɩsa kparɛ mʋ, o biliŋi kɩyaafɔlɛ sa mʋ tɔɔmaa ana.

eng: Whoever wants to be first among you will be the slave of all.
local: Nɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ waa ɔgyankpaapʋ mʋ, ɩ dagaa maa waa fɛɛ kɩnyɛ sa ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ.

eng: for the Human One didnt come to be served but rather to serve and to give his life to liberate many people.
local: Halɩɩ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa gbaa n man ba yɛ asa ba sun mɛ nɩn. N ba n ba sun asa nɩn, kɛ n taa mɛ nkpa kɛɛ, kɛ ɩ ka asa gaalaagaa alibi kʋkɔ.

eng: Jesus and his followers came into Jericho. As Jesus was leaving Jericho, together with his disciples and a sizable crowd, a blind beggar named Bartimaeus, Timaeus son, was sitting beside the road.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba kpee Gyeriko kadɛ tɔ. Maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ yɛ kʋbʋ damantɛ ba maa lɩɩ kadɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ, ba wu gyaatanapʋ kʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Batimayu, maa tɛ ɔkpa kaa ɛɛ kʋlɛ.

eng: When he heard that Jesus of Nazareth was there, he began to shout, Jesus, Son of David, show me mercy.
local: Gyaatanapʋ mʋ maa nu fɛɛ Yesu Kanasareti‐nyɩn mʋ ɩ kyʋn mʋ, nɩɩ o kuusi tɩɩ mʋ yɛ, Yesu, Dawidi kanaana‐bii, wu mɛ kʋwɛɛ.

eng: Many scolded him, telling him to be quiet, but he shouted even louder, Son of David, show me mercy.
local: Maa kuusi kenken sʋ mʋ, asa ba pʋntɛ mʋ sʋ yɛ o bun mʋ kɔnɔ sʋ, yɛgɛ ɛɛ lɔŋɔ ee kuusi kenken ɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Dawidi kanaana‐bii, wu mɛ kʋwɛɛ.

eng: Jesus stopped and said, Call him forward. They called the blind man, Be encouraged! Get up! Hes calling you.
