local: Nɩɩ Yesu yɩlɛ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Fan tɩɩ mʋ sa mɛ. Nɩɩ ba tɩɩ mʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, Yɛgɛ fʋ kɔkɔlɔ kɩ yuuli fʋ, Yesu yɛ fʋ ba.

eng: Throwing his coat to the side, he jumped up and came to Jesus.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ kʋsʋ fugi yii mʋ ayaa sʋ ŋmanyan mʋ kɩtaa tʋʋ ɩkaa sʋ nɩɩ o kpee Yesu asɛ.

eng: Jesus asked him, What do you want me to do for you? The blind man said, Teacher, I want to see.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Mɩnɛ nɩɩ fii biti yɛ n waa sa fʋ? Nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɔkaapʋpʋ, n biti n baa n kɩɩ nɩn.

eng: Jesus said, Go, your faith has healed you. At once he was able to see, and he began to follow Jesus on the way.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Natɛ! Fʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii kɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ fʋ nyɛ alanfɩya. Opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ mu ansi mʋ a bugi, nɩɩ o buu Yesu kpee ɔkpa tɔ.

eng: Many people have already applied themselves to the task of compiling an account of the events that have been fulfilled among us.
local: Mɛ‐kyɛmɩnɛ Tiyofolisi, asa damantɛ ba taa tʋʋ bamʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋ kyʋrɔɔ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɩ waa anɛ tɔ mʋ yɩla.

eng: They used what the original eyewitnesses and servants of the word handed down to us.
local: Ba kyʋrɔɔ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ asa ba wu yɛ ansi, lɩɩ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Yesu asɩn mʋ i yii kasɛ, nɩɩ ba da ɩmʋ kibonbon tɔwɛ anɛ mʋ, yɩla.

eng: Now, after having investigated everything carefully from the beginning, I have also decided to write a carefully ordered account for you, most honorable Theophilus.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ bɛɛrɛɛ wuya Tiyofolisi, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ mɛ gbaa‐gbaa mɛ n loo asɩn mʋ tɔ kɩɩ lɩɩ ɩmʋ npiili mʋ, ɩ dagaa fɛɛ n kyʋrɔɔ ɩmʋ kɩgyasʋ‐kɩgyasʋ kyɛɛkyɛɛ sʋ sa fʋ.

eng: I want you to have confidence in the soundness of the instruction you have received.
local: Kɛ fʋ nyɛ bɩɩ asɩn mʋ kesintin fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba kaapʋ fʋ mʋ.

eng: During the rule of King Herod of Judea there was a priest named Zechariah who belonged to the priestly division of Abijah. His wife Elizabeth was a descendant of Aaron.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Herodi gyɛ owura Gyudeya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔnyɩn kʋ bʋ tɔ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Sakariya. Ɔ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu kʋbʋ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ kɩmʋ yɛ Abigya mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ. Nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ‐ka Elisabeti, nɩɩ ngbaa mʋ lɩɩ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ Aron kɩnaana tɔ.

eng: They were both righteous before God, blameless in their observance of all the Lords commandments and regulations.
local: Sakariya maa Elisabeti kakyɩna tɔ ɩkpa i kyiigi Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ, nɩɩ ba buu Ɩbwaarɛ nbara yɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ mʋ nnɔsa mʋ ɩ kaapʋ mʋ.

eng: They had no children because Elizabeth was unable to become pregnant and they both were very old.
local: Ba bɩlɛ, amaa ba man kʋʋgɛ kebii, lɩɩ fɛɛ Elisabeti maa gyɛ ogbentipu sʋ.

eng: One day Zechariah was serving as a priest before God because his priestly division was on duty.
local: Saŋa kʋ ɩ ba fʋʋ aseepu kʋbʋ mʋ nɩɩ Sakariya bʋ kɩmʋ tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ bee sun Ɩbwaarɛ ansi tɔ.

eng: Following the customs of priestly service, he was chosen by lottery to go into the Lords sanctuary and burn incense.
local: Nɩɩ ba tʋʋ nbii lɛɛ mʋ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ aseepu ɩkpa ɩ kaapʋ mʋ, fɛɛ o loo kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ naa tuwa nlaa.

eng: All the people who gathered to worship were praying outside during this hour of incense offering.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ o loo ee tuwa nlaa mʋ, yɛgɛ asa damantɛ ba bʋ kewusunɔ bɛɛ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ.

eng: An angel from the Lord appeared to him, standing to the right of the altar of incense.
local: Nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ kabɔɔ ba mʋ asɛ, ba yɩlɛ nlaa kaswɩɩ mʋ gyisa sʋ, katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ee tuwa nlaa mʋ.

eng: When Zechariah saw the angel, he was startled and overcome with fear.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Sakariya wu kabɔɔ mʋ, mʋ kɔkɔlɔ kɩ ŋmaa mʋ, nɩɩ ɩ kɩtaa mʋ kufuu.

eng: The angel said, Dont be afraid, Zechariah. Your prayers have been heard. Your wife Elizabeth will give birth to your son and you must name him John.
local: Amaa nɩɩ kabɔɔ mʋ tɔwɛ Sakariya yɛ, Man waa kufuu, Ɩbwaarɛ nu fʋ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ. Fʋ‐ka Elisabeti ii biti ɔ kʋʋgɛ Obii‐nyɩnsa kɛ fʋ yɩla mʋ kɩtɩɩ yɛ Gyɔn.

eng: He will be a joy and delight to you, and many people will rejoice at his birth.
local: Lɩɩ mʋ sʋ mʋ, fanɛ ansi ii biti i gyii, kɛ asa damantɛ gbaa ansi i gyii lɩɩ mʋ kakʋʋgɛ sʋ.

eng: for he will be great in the Lords eyes. He must not drink wine and liquor. He will be filled with the Holy Spirit even before his birth.
local: Ee biti ɔ waa nyɩmɩsa kparɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ansi tɔ. Ɔ man kan waa nuu nta abɛɛ nta bʋlʋnsa. Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ biti o loo bɔla mʋ tɔ, lɩɩ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ mu‐nyi ɔtɔ tɔ mʋ.

eng: He will bring many Israelites back to the Lord their God.
local: Nɩɩ ee biti ɔ yɛgɛ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi gaalaagaa ba nyɛ nfɛɛrɛ kɩkyɛɛgɛ, kɛ ba bwii ba Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ bamʋ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ.

eng: He will go forth before the Lord, equipped with the spirit and power of Elijah. He will turn the hearts of fathers back to their children, and he will turn the disobedient to righteous patterns of thinking. He will make ready a people prepared for the Lord.
local: Fu kebii Gyɔn nɩmʋ ii biti ɔ taa Kayaayu tɔ ɔlʋn gyankpaa Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Eligya. Ee biti ɔ lɔŋɔ asɛ yɛ nbii bʋga tɔ, kɛ ɔ taa bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa kʋsʋ tɔ ɔlʋn mʋ, ba ba nyɛ kanyaasɩn kɛ bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ ɩ ba Ɩbwaarɛ ɩkpa kyiigisa mʋ sʋ. Ee biti ɔ waa ɩnɩmʋ lɔŋɔ asa mʋ gyoo Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ.

eng: Zechariah said to the angel, How can I be sure of this? My wife and I are very old.
local: Nɩɩ Sakariya taasɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ yɛ, Nɛnɛ nɩɩ n biti n waa bɩɩ fɛɛ ɩ gyɛ kesintin? Lɩɩ fɛɛ n bɩlɛ, nɩɩ mɛ‐ka gbaa bɩlɛ yɛ kebii kɩnyɛ.

eng: The angel replied, I am Gabriel. I stand in Gods presence. I was sent to speak to you and to bring this good news to you.
local: Nɩɩ kabɔɔ mʋ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Mɛ ɩ gyɛ Gabireli mʋ nɩɩ n yɩlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ansi tɔ sʋsʋ mʋ. Nɩɩ o sun mɛ yɛ n ba tɔwɛ fʋ asɩn danbɩrasa nɩmʋ.

eng: Know this: What I have spoken will come true at the proper time. But because you didnt believe, you will remain silent, unable to speak until the day when these things happen.
local: Kpalɩ fɛɛ fʋ maa man kɔɔlɛ mɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ii biti ɩ ba ɩmʋ saŋa tɔ kyɛɛkyɛɛ gyii sʋ mʋ, n tɔwɛ fʋ yɛ fʋ man bɩla yɛ fʋ taalɛ tɔwɛ sɛhn, naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ mɛ katɔwɛ yɩla mʋ kii biti kɩ ba tɔ.

eng: Meanwhile, the people were waiting for Zechariah, and they wondered why he was in the sanctuary for such a long time.
local: Saŋa kʋlʋn maa tɔ, yɛgɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ kewusunɔ mʋ, bɛɛ fɛ nfɛɛrɛ kʋtɔ mʋ sʋ nɩɩ Sakariya kyɛɛrɛɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ.

eng: When he came out, he was unable to speak to them. They realized he had seen a vision in the temple, for he gestured to them and couldnt speak.
local: Nɩɩ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ obu mʋ tɔ mʋ, ɔ man bɩla ɛɛ taalɛ ɛɛ tɔwɛ bamʋ asɛ, nɩɩ ba bɩɩ fɛɛ, o wu ansi sʋ kɩlɛɛkaapʋ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ abaa nɩɩ o sii ɔ sʋ ɛɛ tɔwɛ bamʋ asɛ.

eng: When he completed the days of his priestly service, he returned home.
local: Sakariya maa sun mu kiseepu kusun lʋwɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ, ɔ lɩɩ tʋtɔ kpewu mʋ ayɛ.

eng: Afterward, his wife Elizabeth became pregnant. She kept to herself for five months, saying.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ kamaa mʋ, nɩɩ mʋ‐ka Elisabeti nyɛ ɔtɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ n‐yɩɩ baala kɩkpaara sʋ kanɩn ɩbwaayɩ inuu.

eng: This is the Lords doing. He has shown his favor to me by removing my disgrace among other people.
local: Nɩɩ Elisabeti yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ ɩ waa ɩnɩmʋ sa mɛ. O su mɛ kʋwɛɛ, nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ ipeeli lɩɩ mɛ sʋ lɩɩ mɛ tɔɔmaa ana tɔ.

eng: When Elizabeth was six months pregnant, God sent the angel Gabriel to Nazareth, a city in Galilee.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Elisabeti sʋ mʋ ɔtɔ ɩbwaayɩ isiye mʋ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sun mʋ kabɔɔ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Gabireli kpee Nasareti kadɛ tɔ, mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ Galile ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ mʋ.

eng: to a virgin who was engaged to a man named Joseph, a descendant of Davids house. The virgins name was Mary.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ sun mʋ kpee kibite mʋ nɩɩ o mee nyi ɔnyɩn nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Mɛɛrɩ mʋ asɛ. Ba taa mʋ sa ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Gyosefu kekili, nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ Dawidi kɩnaana tɔ.

eng: When the angel came to her, he said, Rejoice, favored one! The Lord is with you.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ kpee mʋ asɛ naa fwaala mʋ yɛ, Faa kumu ɔkɔn! Ɩbwaarɛ bʋ fʋ asɛ, nɩɩ ɔ waa fʋ kusee gaa.

eng: She was confused by these words and wondered what kind of greeting this might be.
local: Nɩɩ ɩ kɩtaa Mɛɛrɩ nfɛɛrɛ lɩɩ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ kabɔɔ mʋ tɔwɛ mʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɛɛ fɛ kanɩn afwaala mʋ nfɛɛrɛ.

eng: The angel said, Dont be afraid, Mary. God is honoring you.
local: Nɩɩ kabɔɔ mʋ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Mɛɛrɩ man waa kufuu, lɩɩ fɛɛ fʋ nyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ kabwaala.

eng: Look! You will conceive and give birth to a son, and you will name him Jesus.
local: Fii biti fʋ nyɛ ɔtɔ kɛ fʋ kʋʋgɛ Obii‐nyɩnsa, kɛ fʋ yɩla mʋ kɩtɩɩ yɛ Yesu.

eng: He will be great and he will be called the Son of the Most High. The Lord God will give him the throne of David his father.
local: Ee biti ɔ waa nyɩmɩsa kparɛ kɛ ba tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Owura lala Mu‐Bii. Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ taa Yesu mʋ naana Dawidi kuwura‐gya mʋ sa mʋ.

eng: He will rule over Jacobs house forever, and there will be no end to his kingdom.
local: Nɩɩ ee biti o gyii Ɩsɩrayɩ abi sʋ kuwura‐gyii naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ man sʋ ɔkaa.

eng: Then Mary said to the angel, How will this happen since I havent had sexual relations with a man.
local: Nɩɩ Mɛɛrɩ taasɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ yɛ, Nɛnɛ nɩɩ ɩnɩmʋ ii biti ɩ bɔla sʋ waa, yɛgɛ n gyɛ kibite mʋ nɩɩ n man nyi ɔnyɩn.

eng: The angel replied, The Holy Spirit will come over you and the power of the Most High will overshadow you. Therefore, the one who is to be born will be holy. He will be called Gods Son.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ lɛɛ mʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ ii biti ɔ ba fʋ sʋ, kɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Owura lala mʋ ɔlʋn o bun fʋ sʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ bee biti ba tɩɩ obii kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ nɩɩ fii biti fʋ kʋʋgɛ mʋ mʋ, yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mu‐bii.

eng: Look, even in her old age, your relative Elizabeth has conceived a son. This woman who was labeled unable to conceive is now six months pregnant.
local: Fʋ kʋʋpʋ Elisabeti mʋ nɩɩ asa bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ ogbentipu nɩɩ ɔ waa kakyɩɩ bɩlɩsa gbaa mʋ, sʋ obii‐nyɩnsa‐bii ɔtɔ ɩbwaayɩ isiye.

eng: Nothing is impossible for God.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ kʋtɔ kʋ kɩ man bʋ tɔ yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mɛɛ taalɛ waa.

eng: Then Mary said, I am the Lords servant. Let it be with me just as you have said. Then the angel left her.
local: Nɩɩ Mɛɛrɩ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, N gyɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ nɩn, yɛgɛ ɩ waa kanɩn sa mɛ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fʋ tɔwɛ mʋ. Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ lɩɩ tʋtɔ kyʋn.

eng: Mary got up and hurried to a city in the Judean highlands.
local: Ɩmʋ nkɛ gbɛrɛɛ kamaa mʋ, Mɛɛrɩ kʋʋla mʋ n‐yɩɩ ɛɛ waa bilen o kpee kadɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ abɩɩ tɔ Gyudeya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ.

eng: She entered Zechariahs home and greeted Elizabeth.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ naa loo Sakariya kɩkpaara sʋ naa fwaala mʋ‐ka Elisabeti.

eng: When Elizabeth heard Marys greeting, the child leaped in her womb, and Elizabeth was filled with the Holy Spirit.
local: Elisabeti maa nu Mɛɛrɩ afwaala mʋ, kebii mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ mʋ ɔtɔ tɔ mʋ fitii. Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ kɩ maa loo bɔla Elisabeti mʋ.

eng: With a loud voice she blurted out, God has blessed you above all women, and he has blessed the child you carry.
local: nɩɩ ɔ pʋntɛ kenken tɔwɛ yɛ, Akyɩɩ kpini tɔ mʋ, fʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ waa kusee kʋkyɔ, nɩɩ kusee bun fu kebii mʋ nɩɩ fii biti fʋ kʋʋgɛ mʋ sʋ.

eng: Why do I have this honor, that the mother of my Lord should come to me?.
local: Mɩnɛ kusee lala nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ taa bwaala mɛ yɛ mɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ mu‐nyi ɔ ba bɔɔ mɛ sʋ?.

eng: As soon as I heard your greeting, the baby in my womb jumped for joy.
local: N maa dan nu fʋ afwaala mʋ, kebii mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ mɛ ɔtɔ tɔ mʋ taa kensi‐gyii fitii.

eng: Happy is she who believed that the Lord would fulfill the promises he made to her.
local: Kusee ɩ gyɛ fʋ lɛɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ fʋ maa kɔɔlɛ gyii fɛɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ fʋ mʋ, ii biti ɩ ba tɔ kesintin.

eng: Mary said, With all my heart I glorify the Lord!.
local: Nɩɩ Mɛɛrɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, N yɩn Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ lɩɩ mɛ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ.

eng: In the depths of who I am I rejoice in God my savior.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ mɛ Ɔmɔlɩgɛpʋ sʋ mʋ, mi ansi i gyii lɩɩ mɛ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ.

eng: He has looked with favor on the low status of his servant. Look! From now on, everyone will consider me highly favored.
local: Kpalɩ fɛɛ mɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ kibite mʋ nɩɩ n man bʋ sɛhn tɔ mʋ, mɛ asɩn i tiri Ɩbwaarɛ. Nɩɩ lɩɩ ndaga sʋ i kpee mʋ, ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ ii biti ɔ tɩɩ mɛ yɛ kumu ɔkɔn wuya.

eng: because the mighty one has done great things for me. Holy is his name.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɔlʋnpʋ lala Ɩbwaarɛ waa atɔ kparɛ sa mɛ. Mʋ kɩtɩɩ ɩ gyɛ Kɩkyɩrɛkyɩrɛ.

eng: He shows mercy to everyone, from one generation to the next, who honors him as God.
local: Nɩɩ asa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ba sɩlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ, ee wu bamʋ kʋwɛɛ, san sa abii san yɛ anaana.

eng: He has shown strength with his arm. He has scattered those with arrogant thoughts and proud inclinations.
local: Ɔ taa mʋ kɩbaa bʋlʋnsa mʋ waa asɩn kyinkyinsa, nɩɩ ɔ yaasɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ kʋsaa bamʋ n‐yɩɩ bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ tɔ.

eng: He has pulled the powerful down from their thrones and lifted up the lowly.
local: Ɔ kʋsaa awura‐gyipu bamʋ ngya sʋ, amaa nɩɩ o diyaa abʋlʋpʋ mʋ sʋsʋ.

eng: He has filled the hungry with good things and sent the rich away empty-handed.
local: Ɔ yɛgɛ nɩɩ atiripu ba nyɛ atɔ danbɩrasa mʋ nɩɩ i tiri bamʋ. Amaa atɔ wuya ana bɩrɛ, o bwiiyaa bamʋ yɛ abaa kpan.

eng: He has come to the aid of his servant Israel, remembering his mercy.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ nyiŋi mʋ asa Ɩsɩrayɩ abi sʋ, nɩɩ o wu bamʋ kʋwɛɛ kyɛ bamʋ tɔ.

eng: just as he promised to our ancestors, to Abraham and to Abrahams descendants forever.
local: fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɩla anɛ naana ana agyankpaapʋ mʋ. Ɔ tɔwɛ Abraham yɛ ee biti o wu maa mʋ kanaana tɔ abi kpini kʋwɛɛ.

eng: Mary stayed with Elizabeth about three months, and then returned to her home.
local: Nɩɩ Mɛɛrɩ kyɩna Elisabeti asɛ ɩ waa fɛɛ ɩbwaayɩ ɩsa pɔyɩ nɩɩ o bwii kpewu.

eng: When the time came for Elizabeth to have her child, she gave birth to a boy.
local: Elisabeti kakɛ kʋʋgɛsa maa fʋʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kʋʋgɛ kebii‐nyɩnsa‐bii.

eng: Her neighbors and relatives celebrated with her because they had heard that the Lord had shown her great mercy.
local: Nɩɩ Elisabeti mʋ tɔɔmaa ana yɛ mʋ kʋʋpʋ ana ba maa nu kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ wu mʋ kʋwɛɛ mʋ, nɩɩ bamʋ kpini ba bʋga gyii bamʋ ansi.

eng: On the eighth day, it came time to circumcise the child. They wanted to name him Zechariah because that was his fathers name.
local: Ɩmʋ kakɛ buruwasapu mʋ ba gyaŋɛ, kɛ ba tɩn kebii mʋ oduu. Naafɔɔ bee biti ba taa mʋ‐sɛ kɩtɩɩ Sakariya mʋ yɩla mʋ.

eng: But his mother replied, No, his name will be John.
local: Amaa nɩɩ mu‐nyi kina kɩtɩɩ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ yɛ, Ba tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Gyɔn.

eng: They said to her, None of your relatives have that name.
local: Nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ Elisabeti yɛ, Fan man sʋ ɔkʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ kanɩn kɩtɩɩ fanɛ kanan tɔ.

eng: Then they began gesturing to his father to see what he wanted to call him.
local: Nɩɩ ba waa abaa tɔwɛ mʋ‐sɛ Sakariya asɛ, kɛ ba bɩɩ kɩtɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ ba taa yɩla kebii mʋ.

eng: After asking for a tablet, he surprised everyone by writing, His name is John.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ba taa kɩgyantan‐bi sa mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ kyʋrɔɔ sʋ yɛ, Mʋ kɩtɩɩ ɩ gyɛ Gyɔn. Nɩɩ i kyinkyin bamʋ gaa.

eng: At that moment, Zechariah was able to speak again, and he began praising God.
local: Opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ Sakariya kɔnɔ bugi, nɩɩ mʋ kanandʋlʋ gbaa saŋɛ, nɩɩ o piili ɛɛ tɔwɛ ɛɛ yɩn Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: All their neighbors were filled with awe, and everyone throughout the Judean highlands talked about what had happened.
local: Nɩɩ kufuu kɩ kɩtaa mʋ tɔɔmaa ana mʋ kpini, nɩɩ asɩn nɩmʋ ɩ yalagɛ ndɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ abɩɩ tɔ Gyudeya Ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ.

eng: All who heard about this considered it carefully. They said, What then will this child be? Indeed, the Lords power was with him.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o nu kanɩn asɩn mʋ ɩɩ fɛ kɩmʋ nfɛɛrɛ, nɩɩ ɛɛ taasɛ yɛ, Mɩnɛ nɩɩ kanɩn kebii nɩmʋ ba ɔ ba waa? Lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kɩbaa kɩ gyan mʋ sʋ.

eng: Johns father Zechariah was filled with the Holy Spirit and prophesied.
local: Nɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ bɔla kebii mʋ mʋ‐sɛ Sakariya tɔ, nɩɩ ɛɛ tɔwɛ abwaarɛsɩn yɛ.

eng: Bless the Lord God of Israel because he has come to help and has delivered his people.
local: Fan yɛgɛ a yɩn anɛ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ ba nɩn ɔ ba kyɛ mʋ asa tɔ kɛ ɔ mɔlɩgɛ bamʋ.

eng: He has raised up a mighty savior for us in his servant Davids house.
local: Lɩɩ mʋ kɩyaafɔlɛ Dawidi kanan tɔ mʋ, ɔ lɛɛ Ɔmɔlɩgɛpʋ Lʋnpʋ sa anɛ.

eng: just as he said through the mouths of his holy prophets long ago.
local: Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ ba wʋla tɔwɛ yɩla fɛɛ.

eng: He has brought salvation from our enemies and from the power of all those who hate us.
local: ee biti ɔ ba mɔlɩgɛ anɛ lɩɩ anɛ dʋn ana yɛ anɛ akisipu kpini abaa tɔ.

eng: He has shown the mercy promised to our ancestors, and remembered his holy covenant.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ yɛ ee biti o wu anɛ naana ana kʋwɛɛ kɛ o nyiŋi mʋ katɔwɛ kyula kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ.

eng: the solemn pledge he made to our ancestor Abraham. He has granted.
local: Katɔwɛ kyula nɩmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bɔ ntan sa anɛ naana Abraham yɛ.

eng: that we would be rescued from the power of our enemies so that we could serve him without fear.
local: ee biti ɔ kɔɔlɛ anɛ lɩɩ anɛ akisipu abaa tɔ, kɛ ɔ yɛgɛ a nyɛ ɔlʋn sun mʋ yɛgɛ a man sʋ kufuu.

eng: in holiness and righteousness in Gods eyes, for as long as we live.
local: Kɛ a ba an gyɛ asa kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ kɛ anɛ ɩkpa i kyiigi, mʋ Ɩbwaarɛ ansi tɔ lɩɩ anɛ kakyɩna kpini.

eng: You, child, will be called a prophet of the Most High, for you will go before the Lord to prepare his way.
local: Nɩɩ Sakariya tɔwɛ mu‐bii Gyɔn yɛ, Mi‐bii, bee biti ba tɩɩ fʋ yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Ɔlʋnpʋ mʋ ɔtɔwɛpʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ fii biti fʋ gyankpaa Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ naa lɔŋɔ mʋ ɔkpa.

eng: You will tell his people how to be saved through the forgiveness of their sins.
local: Fii biti fʋ ba yɛgɛ mʋ asa ba bɩɩ fɛɛ ɩ lɩɩ alibi kɩtaakɛɛ sʋ nɩɩ bee biti ba nyɛ kamɔlɩgɛ.

eng: Because of our Gods deep compassion, the dawn from heaven will break upon us.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ maa wu anɛ kʋwɛɛ sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ yɛgɛ nɩɩ Ɔmɔlɩgɛpʋ mʋ ba anɛ asɛ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ kyɔwɛ ɩ lɩɩ sʋsʋ ba ɛɛ da kasɛ mʋ.

eng: to give light to those who are sitting in darkness and in the shadow of death, to guide us on the path of peace.
local: Kɛ ɩ ŋmaŋɛ sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ kitentenbiri mʋ nɩɩ kɩ sʋ bamʋ kii kpee lewu tɔ, kɛ ɩ ŋmaŋɛ tɔ kaapʋ anɛ ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ sʋ i kpee keyuula tɔ.

eng: The child grew up, becoming strong in character. He was in the wilderness until he began his public ministry to Israel.
local: Nɩɩ kebii mʋ dan nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu tɔ ɔlʋn, nɩɩ ɔ kyɩna ofuli sʋ naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ kaapʋ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi.

eng: In those days Caesar Augustus declared that everyone throughout the empire should be enrolled in the tax lists.
local: Kanɩn nkɛ maŋa tɔ mʋ, Owura Kasaro Agusitu sa kɔnɔ yii sʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ Roma kuwura‐gyii kasɛ mʋ kpini ɔ naa kyʋrɔɔ mʋ kɩtɩɩ kɛ ba kalɛ mʋ.

eng: This first enrollment occurred when Quirinius governed Syria.
local: Kɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ asa kɩkalɛ gyankpaasa mʋ nɩɩ ba waa Roma kuwura‐gyii tɔ, saŋa maŋa tɔ nɩɩ Kwirinisi gyɛ Siriya ɔsʋwʋlɛ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ mʋ.

eng: Everyone went to their own cities to be enrolled.
local: Kanɩn sʋ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ kpee mʋ kadɛ tɔ kɛ ɔ naa kyʋrɔɔ mʋ kɩtɩɩ.

eng: Since Joseph belonged to Davids house and family line, he went up from the city of Nazareth in Galilee to Davids city, called Bethlehem, in Judea.
local: Nɩɩ Gyosefu gbaa kʋsʋ Nasareti kadɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ Galile ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ, kpee kadɛ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ kɩmʋ yɛ Betilehem mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ Gyudeya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ. Betilehem ɩ gyɛ kadɛ mʋ nɩɩ owura Dawidi lɩɩ. Gyosefu kpee Betilehem kpalɩ fɛɛ ɔ lɩɩ Dawidi kebunɔ nɩn.

eng: He went to be enrolled together with Mary, who was promised to him in marriage and who was pregnant.
local: Gyosefu taa Mɛɛrɩ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ yɛ ee biti o kili mʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ba kpee Betilehem ba naa kyʋrɔɔ bamʋ atɩɩ. Kanɩn saŋa maŋa mʋ Mɛɛrɩ sʋ ɔtɔ.

eng: While they were there, the time came for Mary to have her baby.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Betilehem mʋ, nɩɩ Mɛɛrɩ saŋa kʋʋgɛsa kɛ fʋʋ.

eng: She gave birth to her firstborn child, a son, wrapped him snugly, and laid him in a manger, because there was no place for them in the guestroom.
local: Nɩɩ Mɛɛrɩ kʋʋgɛ mu‐bii‐nyɩnsa gyankpaasa. Nɩɩ ɔ taa akyibi mili kebii mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ dɩɩla kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ atɔbwaaya bee gyii mʋ tɔ. Kpalɩ fɛɛ afɔ ba maa ba kɩkyɔ sʋ mʋ Mɛɛrɩ maa Gyosefu ba man nyɛ opula afɔ obu mʋ tɔ.

eng: When Jesus and his followers approached Jerusalem, they came to Bethphage and Bethany at the Mount of Olives. Jesus gave two disciples a task.
local: Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba maa tiri Gyerusalem, nɩɩ ba ba fʋʋ Olifu kɩbɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ maa Betifage maa Betani mʋ, Yesu sun mʋ abɩɩlapʋ anyɔ.

eng: Nearby shepherds were living in the fields, guarding their sheep at night.
local: Saŋa maŋa tɔ, nbʋlʋpʋ akpapʋ akʋ ba bʋ kadɛ mʋ afitiri tɔ bɛɛ kɩɩ bamʋ nbʋlʋpʋ sʋ kagyanbwɛ.

eng: saying to them, Go into the village over there. As soon as you enter it, you will find tied up there a colt that no one has ridden. Untie it and bring it here.
local: nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan kpee kadɛ‐bi mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ fanɛ ansi tɔ, fan maa dan fɛn fʋʋ mʋ, fen biti fan wu kekuruma‐bi mʋ nɩɩ ba man pɩɩ dii mʋ maa kyaa, kɛ fan saŋɛ mʋ bɩya mɛ nfɩɩ.

eng: The Lords angel stood before them, the Lords glory shone around them, and they were terrified.
local: Nɩɩ opula maa tɔ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ kʋ kɩ ba bamʋ asɛ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bʋnyaa mʋ ŋmaŋɛ tɔ kyaabɔɔ bamʋ. Nɩɩ kufuu kɩ kɩtaa bamʋ.

eng: If anyone says to you, Why are you doing this? say, Its master needs it, and he will send it back right away.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan taasɛ fanɛ kʋtɔ mʋ sʋ nɩɩ fan saŋɛ mʋ mʋ, fan tɔwɛ odunwura maŋa yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ ii biti mʋ, amaa ee biti o bwiiyaa mʋ ba nbɩɩnbɩɩ nɩmʋ. .

eng: The angel said, Dont be afraid! Look! I bring good news to you—wonderful, joyous news for all people.
local: Amaa nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan man waa kufuu. N bɩya fanɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ nɩɩ ii biti a waa asa kpini ɔkɔn.

eng: They went and found a colt tied to a gate outside on the street, and they untied it.
local: Kesintin, nɩɩ ba naa wu kekuruma‐bi mʋ maa kyaa kigbenbi sʋ kɩkpaara kʋkʋ kebunɔ. Ba maa saŋɛ mʋ mʋ.

eng: Your savior is born today in Davids city. He is Christ the Lord.
local: Ndaga kakɛ nɩmʋ ba kʋʋgɛ Ɔmɔlɩgɛpʋ mʋ Dawidi kadɛ tɔ. Mʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ mʋ.

eng: Some people standing around said to them, What are you doing, untying the colt.
local: nɩɩ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba yɩlɛ tʋtɔ mʋ ba taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ fɛn saŋɛ kekuruma‐bi mʋ.

eng: This is a sign for you: you will find a newborn baby wrapped snugly and lying in a manger.
local: Falɛ nɩɩ fen biti fan wu kesintin mʋ. Fen biti fan wu fɛɛ ba maa taa akyibi mili kebii pʋpwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ dɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ atɔbwaaya bee gyii mʋ tɔ.

eng: They told them just what Jesus said, and they left them alone.
local: Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Yesu dan tɔwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ asa mʋ ba yɛgɛ ba taa natɛ.

eng: Suddenly a great assembly of the heavenly forces was with the angel praising God. They said.
local: Opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ kʋbʋ lala kʋ ba lɩɩ sʋsʋ ba tii kabɔɔ mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ba yɩn Ɩbwaarɛ bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ.

eng: They brought the colt to Jesus and threw their clothes upon it, and he sat on it.
local: Ba taa kekuruma‐bi mʋ bɩya Yesu, taa bamʋ ataa gyaga kuruma mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ Yesu dii kyɩna sʋ.

eng: Glory to God in heaven, and on earth peace among those whom he favors.
local: Bʋnyaa bʋ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ sʋsʋ mʋ asɛ. Kɛ keyuula ki bun bamʋ nɩɩ ba bɔla mu ansi mʋ kasɛ sʋ.

eng: Many people spread out their clothes on the road while others spread branches cut from the fields.
local: Asa gaalaagaa ba taa bamʋ ataa tʋʋtʋʋ ɔkpa tɔ, nɩɩ asansa mɔɔ ba ŋɛlɛ afatɛɛ wʋrɩgɛ ɔkpa mʋ tɔ.

eng: When the angels returned to heaven, the shepherds said to each other, Lets go right now to Bethlehem and see whats happened. Lets confirm what the Lord has revealed to us.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ ba lɩɩ bamʋ asɛ kpee sʋsʋ mʋ, nbʋlʋpʋ akpapʋ mʋ ba tɔwɛ abaa yɛ, Fan yɛgɛ a kpee Betilehem naa kɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ waa nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ anɛ mʋ.

eng: Those in front of him and those following were shouting, Hosanna! Blessings on the one who comes in the name of the Lord!.
local: Asa mʋ nɩɩ ba gyankpaa yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba buu kamaa mʋ kpini bee kuusi yɛ, Hosana! Kusee ɩ gyɛ sa ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ naa Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ kɩtɩɩ tɔ ɛɛ ba mʋ!.

eng: They went quickly and found Mary and Joseph, and the baby lying in the manger.
local: Kanɩn sʋ nɩɩ ba kpaala kpee nɩɩ ba naa wu Mɛɛrɩ maa Gyosefu yɛ kebii pʋpwɛ mʋ maa dɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ atɔbwaaya bee gyii tɔ mʋ.

eng: Blessings on the coming kingdom of our ancestor David! Hosanna in the highest.
local: Kusee ɩ gyɛ sa anɛ‐Sɛ Owura Dawidi kuwura‐gyii mʋ nɩɩ kɩ ba mʋ! Hosana ɩ gyɛ sa anɛ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ sʋsʋ mʋ.

eng: When they saw this, they reported what they had been told about this child.
local: Nbʋlʋpʋ akpaapʋ mʋ ba maa naa wu kebii mʋ, nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ asa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ ba tɔwɛ bamʋ lɩɩ kebii mʋ sʋ mʋ.

eng: Jesus entered Jerusalem and went into the temple. After he looked around at everything, because it was already late in the evening, he returned to Bethany with the Twelve.
local: Yesu maa fʋʋ Gyerusalem mʋ, ɔ naa loo kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ, nɩɩ ɔ kɩɩ kʋtɔ kʋmaa kyaabɔɔ. Amaa kpalɩ fɛɛ kyɔwɛ maa tɛɛ lʋwɛ sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ taa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ kudu anyɔ mʋ kpee Betani.

eng: Everyone who heard it was amazed at what the shepherds told them.
local: Nɩɩ i kyinkyin bamʋ nɩɩ ba nu asɩn mʋ nɩɩ atɔbwaaya akpaapʋ mʋ ba tɔwɛ mʋ.

eng: The next day, after leaving Bethany, Jesus was hungry.
local: Lɩɩkaakɛ, ba maa lɩɩ Betani bɛɛ ba mʋ, akʋn a kɩtaa Yesu.

eng: Mary committed these things to memory and considered them carefully.
local: Nɩɩ Mɛɛrɩ taa asɩn mʋ kpini waa mʋ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ, ɛɛ fɛ ɩmʋ nfɛɛrɛ.

eng: From far away, he noticed a fig tree in leaf, so he went to see if he could find anything on it. When he came to it, he found nothing except leaves, since it wasnt the season for figs.
local: Maa kɩɩ kata‐kata mʋ, o wu figi oyu maa pɔrɔgɛ kɩ yɩlɛ, nɩɩ o kpee kɩmʋ asɛ naa kɩɩ fɛɛ ɩ sɔyɩ abii, amaa afatɛɛ nkʋn nɩn, kpalɩ fɛɛ ɩmʋ saŋa sɔyɩsa man pɩɩ fʋʋ.

eng: The shepherds returned home, glorifying and praising God for all they had heard and seen. Everything happened just as they had been told.
local: Nɩɩ nbʋlʋpʋ akpaapʋ mʋ ba maa bwii bee kpee mʋ, yɛgɛ bɛɛ bɩɩ ɩlʋn bɛɛ yɩn Ɩbwaarɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ba nu nɩɩ ba wu mʋ, ɩ bɔla sʋ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ kɩ tɔwɛ bamʋ mʋ.

eng: So he said to it, No one will ever again eat your fruit! His disciples heard this.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ figi oyu mʋ yɛ, Ɔkʋ man bɩla yɛ o gyii fʋ abii mʋ kakaakʋ! Mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba nu kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ oyu mʋ.

eng: When eight days had passed, Jesus parents circumcised him and gave him the name Jesus. This was the name given to him by the angel before he was conceived.
local: Kebii mʋ maa fʋʋ nkɛ nburuwa akɛ kɛ ba tɩn mu oduu mʋ, nɩɩ ba yɩla mʋ kɩtɩɩ yɛ Yesu, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ wʋla tɔwɛ yɩla pɔyɩ nɩɩ ba nyɛ mʋ ɔtɔ mʋ.

eng: They came into Jerusalem. After entering the temple, he threw out those who were selling and buying there. He pushed over the tables used for currency exchange and the chairs of those who sold doves.
local: Ba maa fʋʋ Gyerusalem mʋ, Yesu kpee kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ obu mʋ tɔ naa, nɩɩ o piili ɛɛ gya atɔ afɛpʋ yɛ asɔɔpʋ. Nɩɩ o biliŋaa‐biliŋaa afulee akyarɩpʋ iteeburu yɛ awura‐lɔpɔ afɛpʋ ngya taŋa‐taŋa.

eng: When the time came for their ritual cleansing, in accordance with the Law from Moses, they brought Jesus up to Jerusalem to present him to the Lord. .
local: Nɩɩ saŋa mʋ maa fʋʋ fɛɛ Gyosefu maa Mɛɛrɩ ba fwɩɩ bamʋ n‐yɩɩ ineesi, fɛɛ kanɩn mʋ nɩɩ Mosesi nbara mʋ ɩ kaapʋ mʋ, kanan sʋ ba taa kebii mʋ kpee Gyerusalem ba naa taa mʋ waa Ɩbwaarɛ abaa tɔ.

eng: He didnt allow anyone to carry anything through the temple.
local: Nɩɩ o tii ɔkpa fɛɛ ɔkʋ ɔ man bɩla taa kʋtɔ kʋ bɔla kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ opula.

eng: Its written in the Law of the Lord, Every firstborn male will be dedicated to the Lord.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ba kyʋrɔɔ yɩla Ɩbwaarɛ nbara mʋ tɔ yɛ, Ɩ dagaa fɛɛ ba taa kebii‐nyɩnsa gyankpaasa kʋmaa nɩmʋ naa sa Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: He taught them, Hasnt it been written, My house will be called a house of prayer for all nations? But youve turned it into a hideout for crooks .
local: Nɩɩ ɔ lan kaapʋ bamʋ yɛ, Ba kyʋrɔɔ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ yɛ, Bee biti ba tɩɩ mɛ kakpaara yɛ kabwaarɛ kʋlɛkpa sa dulinyaa tɔ asa kpini. Amaa fan taa kɩmʋ biliŋaa bamʋ nɩɩ bee yuuri ɔlʋn sʋ ɔbaalakpa.

eng: They offered a sacrifice in keeping with whats stated in the Law of the Lord, A pair of turtledoves or two young pigeons .
local: Nɩɩ Mɛɛrɩ maa Gyosefu ba naa lɛɛ saraga fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ nbara ɩ kaapʋ mʋ. Nbara mʋ ɩ kaapʋ yɛ, .

eng: The chief priests and legal experts heard this and tried to find a way to destroy him. They regarded him as dangerous because the whole crowd was enthralled at his teaching.
local: Aseepu bɩlɩsa yɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ ba nu kanɩn asɩn mʋ, nɩɩ ba piili bee biti ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ ba taa mɔɔ mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ mʋ kɩkaapʋ ki kyinkyin asa damantɛ mʋ kpini, nɩɩ ɩnɩmʋ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ba sɩlɛ mʋ.

eng: A man named Simeon was in Jerusalem. He was righteous and devout. He eagerly anticipated the restoration of Israel, and the Holy Spirit rested on him.
local: Kanan saŋa mʋ ɔnyɩn kʋ bʋ Gyerusalem nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Simeyon. Ɔ gyɛ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ mʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi nɩɩ ee sun Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ kɔkɔlɔ kʋlʋn, nɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ kɩ bɔla mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ o gyoo saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ bɩya Ɩsɩrayɩ abi kamɔlɩgɛ.

eng: When it was evening, Jesus and his disciples went outside the city.
local: Kalaatɩpwɛɛ asɛ nɩɩ Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba lɩɩ kadɛ lala mʋ tɔ.

eng: The Holy Spirit revealed to him that he wouldnt die before he had seen the Lords Christ.
local: Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ kɩ lɛɛ kaapʋ mʋ fɛɛ, ɔ mɛɛ yɛ o wu, amɔɔ o wu Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ mʋ pɔyɩ kɛ o wu.

eng: Early in the morning, as Jesus and his disciples were walking along, they saw the fig tree withered from the root up.
local: Lɩɩkaakɛ nyenyenfuu ba maa naa ɔkpa tɔ mʋ, ba wu figi oyu mʋ kɩ maa wu naa fʋʋ kɩmʋ kasɛ.

eng: Led by the Spirit, he went into the temple area. Meanwhile, Jesus parents brought the child to the temple so that they could do what was customary under the Law.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Mɛɛrɩ maa Gyosefu ba taa kebii mʋ kpee kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ tɔ, kɛ ba naa waa mu kumu sʋ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ nbara ɩ kaapʋ mʋ, nɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ kpiya Simeyon kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ.

eng: Peter remembered and said to Jesus, Rabbi, look how the fig tree you cursed has dried up.
local: Nɩɩ Pita nyiŋi kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ waa, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ Yesu yɛ, Ɔkaapʋpʋ, kɩɩ figi oyu mʋ nɩɩ fʋ tɔwɛ nnɔ mʋ, kɩ maa wu.

eng: Simeon took Jesus in his arms and praised God. He said.
local: Nɩɩ Simeyon kɔɔlɛ kebii mʋ kɩtaa mʋ abaa tɔ nɩɩ ɔ yɩn Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ yɛ.

eng: Jesus responded to them, Have faith in God!.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ mʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Kɔɔlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gyii.

eng: Now, master, let your servant go in peace according to your word.
local: Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ fʋ yɛgɛ nɩɩ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ fʋ tɔwɛ mʋ ɩ ba tɔ, kanɩn sʋ nbɩɩnbɩɩ fɩɩ taalɛ yɛgɛ mɛ, fʋ kɩyaafɔlɛ n wu kayɩɩ yuuli tɔ.

eng: I assure you that whoever says to this mountain, Be lifted up and thrown into the sea—and doesnt waver but believes that what is said will really happen—it will happen.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin yɛ, ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ kɩbɩɩ kɩnɩmʋ yɛ, Taa fʋ n‐yɩɩ naa tʋʋ ɔpʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ waa sika‐sika mʋ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ gyii fɛɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ falɛ kii biti kɩ waa mʋ, ii biti i waa kanɩn nɩn sa mʋ.

eng: because my eyes have seen your salvation.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ mi ansi i wu Ɔmɔlɩgɛpʋ mʋ nɩɩ fʋ sa asa mʋ.

eng: Therefore I say to you, whatever you pray and ask for, believe that you will receive it, and it will be so for you.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ n tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, nɩɩ fan kan kʋlɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa fanɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ tɔ mʋ, fan kɔɔlɛ gyii fɛɛ fan tɛɛ nyɛ kɩmʋ, nɩɩ fen biti fan nyɛ kɩmʋ.

eng: You prepared this salvation in the presence of all peoples.
local: Mʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɔmʋ nɩɩ fʋ wʋla lɔŋɔ sa asa kpini mʋ.

eng: And whenever you stand up to pray, if you have something against anyone, forgive so that your Father in heaven may forgive you your wrongdoings.
local: Nɩɩ fʋ kan yɩlɛ fɩɩ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ mʋ, taa alibi kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan waa fʋ mʋ kɛɛ mʋ, kɛ fʋ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ sʋsʋ mʋ gbaa ɔ taa fʋ lɛɛ kɛɛ fʋ.

eng: Its a light for revelation to the Gentiles and a glory for your people Israel.
local: Mʋ ɩ gyɛ kaŋmaŋɛ mʋ nɩɩ kii biti kɩ yɛgɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi mʋ ba bɩɩ fʋ ɔkpa mʋ, kɛ kɩ yɛgɛ fʋ asa, Ɩsɩrayɩ abi gbaa ba nyɛ bʋnyaa.

eng: Jesus and his disciples entered Jerusalem again. As Jesus was walking around the temple, the chief priests, legal experts, and elders came to him.
local: Nɩɩ ba bɩla bwii naa fʋʋ Gyerusalem. Tʋtɔ mʋ, Yesu maa naa kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu bɩlɩsa, yɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ, yɛ abɩlɩsa ba ba mʋ asɛ.

eng: His father and mother were amazed by what was said about him.
local: Asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Simeyon tɔwɛ lɩɩ kebii mʋ sʋ mʋ, i kyinkyin mu‐nyi yɛ mʋ‐sɛ.

eng: They asked, What kind of authority do you have for doing these things? Who gave you this authority to do them.
local: Nɩɩ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Mɩnɛ ɔlʋn nɩɩ fʋ sʋ fɩɩ waa ɩnɩmʋ kpini? Anɩmʋ ɩ sa fʋ kanɩn ɔlʋn maŋa.

eng: Simeon blessed them and said to Mary his mother, This boy is assigned to be the cause of the falling and rising of many in Israel and to be a sign that generates opposition.
local: Nɩɩ Simeyon waa Mɛɛrɩ maa Gyosefu kusee, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ Mɛɛrɩ yɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ kebii nɩmʋ nɩn, yɛ kpalɩ mʋ sʋ mʋ, Ɩsɩrayɩ abi gaalaagaa bee biti ba tɩyɛ, kɛ akʋ mɔɔ ba nyɛ kamɔlɩgɛ. Mʋ ii biti ɔ lɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn mʋ kaapʋ, kɛ asa ba gyii ɩmʋ ɩmɔɔrɛ.

eng: Jesus said to them, I have a question for you. Give me an answer, then Ill tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, N biti n taasɛ fanɛ asɩn, nɩɩ fan kan lɛɛ kɔnɔ kyɛɛkyɛɛ sa mɛ bɩrɛ, n biti n tɔwɛ fanɛ ɔlʋn kʋlʋn mʋ nɩɩ n sʋ n waa ɩnɩmʋ kpini.

eng: so that the inner thoughts of many will be revealed. And a sword will pierce your innermost being too.
local: Kɛ asa gaalaagaa nfɛɛrɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ bamʋ amu tɔ mʋ ɩ lɩɩ ifuli. Kɛ fʋ Mɛɛrɩ mɔɔ, fʋ kayɩyɛɛ kii biti kɩ waa fɛɛ deebi bʋyɛsa nɩɩ ba taa yii fʋ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ mʋ.

eng: Was Johns baptism of heavenly or of human origin? Answer me.
local: Gyɔn kekyugyee mʋ, ɩ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ abɛɛ nyɩmɩsa asɛ.

eng: There was also a prophet, Anna the daughter of Phanuel, who belonged to the tribe of Asher. She was very old. After she married, she lived with her husband for seven years.
local: Saŋa maŋa mʋ, kakyɩɩ bɩlɩsa kʋ bʋ tɔ, bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Ana nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ. O kili nsu nsunɔ nɩɩ mu‐kuli wu. Mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Fanuweli, nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ Asee kebunɔ.

eng: They argued among themselves, If we say, Its of heavenly origin, hell say, Then why didnt you believe him.
local: Nɩɩ ba yii kasɛ bee gyii abaa ɩmɔɔrɛ yɛ, Nɩɩ an kan lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ mʋ ee biti ɔ taasɛ anɛ yɛ, Mɩnɛ ɩ waa sʋ nɩɩ a man kɔɔlɛ Gyɔn gyii.

eng: She was now an -year-old widow. She never left the temple area but worshipped God with fasting and prayer night and day.
local: Ɔ kyɩna kukula mʋ tɔ naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ o gyii nsu aduburuwa yɛ ana. Saŋa kʋmaa kagyanbwɛ yɛ kakyʋwapɛ ee sun Ɩbwaarɛ. Ɔ mɛɛ lɩɩ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ tɔ. Ɔ bʋ tʋtɔ nɩn ee ŋmina kɔnɔ yɛgɛ ɛɛ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ.

eng: But we cant say, Its of earthly origin. They said this because they were afraid of the crowd, because they all thought John was a prophet.
local: Amaa nɩɩ an kan tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɩ lɩɩ nyɩmɩsa asɛ mɔɔ mʋ. .

eng: She approached at that very moment and began to praise God and to speak about Jesus to everyone who was looking forward to the redemption of Jerusalem.
local: Opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ Ana ba Gyosefu maa Mɛɛrɩ asɛ nɩɩ ɔ fwaala Ɩbwaarɛ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ kebii mʋ asɩn sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba tɛ ba gyoo Ɔmɔlɩgɛpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ ba lɛɛ Gyerusalem abi lɩɩ awʋrʋfɔ tɔ mʋ.

eng: They answered Jesus, We dont know. Jesus replied, Neither will I tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ba sii lɛɛ Yesu kɔnɔ nɩn yɛ, A man nyi. Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Kanɩn bɩrɛ, ngbaa mɛ, n mɛɛ yɛ n tɔwɛ fanɛ ɔlʋn kʋlʋn mʋ nɩɩ n sʋ n waa ɩnɩmʋ kpini.

eng: When Mary and Joseph had completed everything required by the Law of the Lord, they returned to their hometown, Nazareth in Galilee.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Mɛɛrɩ maa Gyosefu ba lʋwɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ dagaa fɛɛ ba waa fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ nbara ɩ kaapʋ mʋ, ba bwii kpewu bamʋ kadɛ tɔ Nasareti mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ Galile ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ mʋ.

eng: The child grew up and became strong. He was filled with wisdom, and Gods favor was on him.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu dan nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ ɔlʋn, nɩɩ kanyaasɩn kɩ bɔla mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabwaala kɩ bʋ mʋ sʋ.

eng: Each year his parents went to Jerusalem for the Passover Festival.
local: Kesu kʋmaa Gyosefu maa Mɛɛrɩ bee kpee Gyerusalem ba naa gyii Kanyaŋɛ nbunɔ kesu kɔnɔ kɔlɔŋɔ.

eng: When he was years old, they went up to Jerusalem according to their custom.
local: Yesu maa gyii nsu kudu anyɔ mʋ, o buu Mɛɛrɩ maa Gyosefu kpee naa gyii kanɩn Kanyaŋɛ nbunɔ mʋ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ bamʋ anaana‐tɔ ɩ kaapʋ mʋ.

eng: After the festival was over, they were returning home, but the boy Jesus stayed behind in Jerusalem. His parents didnt know it.
local: Ba maa gyii bamʋ nkɛ lʋwɛ nɩɩ ba bwii bee kpewu mʋ, Yesu sii kamaa Gyerusalem tɔ yɛgɛ mʋ akʋʋgɛpʋ ana mʋ ba man nyi.

eng: As Jesus left the temple, one of his disciples said to him, Teacher, look! What awesome stones and buildings.
local: Yesu maa dan ɛɛ lɩɩ Kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ, nɩɩ mʋ ɔbɩɩlapʋ kʋ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɔkaapʋpʋ! Kɩɩ abuu danbɩrasa yɛ ibu bʋrɔnsa mʋ nɩɩ ba pɔyɩ falɛ.

eng: Supposing that he was among their band of travelers, they journeyed on for a full day while looking for him among their family and friends.
local: Ba nyi fɛɛ ɔ tii kʋbʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba maa bamʋ ba naa mʋ sʋ nɩn, nɩɩ ba natɛ kakɛ ibiri pɔyɩ nɩɩ ba piili bee biti mʋ bamʋ kʋʋpʋ ana yɛ bamʋ kyɛmɩnɛ ana mʋ tɔ.

eng: Jesus responded, Do you see these enormous buildings? Not even one stone will be left upon another. All will be demolished.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ mʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Yɛ fu wu ibu kparɛ‐kparɛ maŋa? Kibuu kʋlʋn gbaa kɩ mɛɛ yɛ ki sii kɩ gyan kʋkʋ sʋ yɛ be mee nin kɩmʋ tʋʋ.

eng: When they didnt find Jesus, they returned to Jerusalem to look for him.
local: Ba maa man wu mʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ba bwii kpee Gyerusalem ba naa biti mʋ.

eng: Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives across from the temple. Peter, James, John, and Andrew asked him privately.
local: Yesu maa tɛ Olifu kɩbɩɩ‐bi mʋ sʋ yɛgɛ mu ansi ɩɩ kɩɩ Kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ, Pita yɛ Gyemisi yɛ Gyɔn yɛ Andiruwu ba ba mʋ asɛ.

eng: After three days they found him in the temple. He was sitting among the teachers, listening to them and putting questions to them.
local: Kakɛ sasapʋ akɛ mʋ, ba naa wu mʋ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ, maa tɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ mʋ asɛ, yɛgɛ ee nu bamʋ asɛ yɛgɛ ɛɛ taasɛ bamʋ asɩn mʋ kasɛ.

eng: Tell us, when will these things happen? What sign will show that all these things are about to come to an end.
local: Nɩɩ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Saŋa mɔɔ nɩɩ ɩnɩmʋ ii biti ɩ ba? Mɩnɛ sɩnkaala ii biti ɩ waa kaapʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɩnɩmʋ ii biti ɩ ba.

eng: Everyone who heard him was amazed by his understanding and his answers.
local: Bamʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba nu kanan mʋ nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ asɩn mʋ kasɛ mʋ, i kyinkyin bamʋ.

eng: Jesus said, Watch out that no one deceives you.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu piili ɛɛ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan baa fan da sʋ, kɛ fan man yɛgɛ ɔkʋ ɔ pɩna fanɛ.

eng: When his parents saw him, they were shocked. His mother said, Child, why have you treated us like this? Listen! Your father and I have been worried. Weve been looking for you.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ mʋ akʋʋgɛpʋ ba naa wu mʋ mʋ, i kyinkyin bamʋ, nɩɩ Mɛɛrɩ taasɛ Yesu yɛ, Mi‐bii, mɩnɛ nɩɩ fʋ waa anɛ kanɩn? N maa fʋ‐sɛ ansi ɩ payɩ an biti fʋ.

eng: Many people will come in my name, saying, Im the one! They will deceive many people.
local: Asa gaalaagaa bee biti ba ba mɛ kɩtɩɩ tɔ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, Mɛ, ɩ gyɛ Krisito mʋ! kɛ ba pɩna asa gaalaagaa.

eng: Jesus replied, Why were you looking for me? Didnt you know that it was necessary for me to be in my Fathers house.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ fan naa fen biti mɛ? Fan man nyi fɛɛ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ n baa n bʋ mɛ‐sɛ ayɛ.

eng: When you hear of wars and reports of wars, dont be alarmed. These things must happen, but this isnt the end yet.
local: Nɩɩ fan kan nu anaa sʋ yɛ anaa asɩn mʋ, fan man yɛgɛ fanɛ nkɔlɔ ɩ ŋmaa fanɛ. Ɩ dagaa ɩnɩmʋ ɩ maa ba kanɩn, amaa ɩ mɛɛ kaapʋ fɛɛ laalaalʋwɛ mʋ tee tiri tɔ nɩn.

eng: But they didnt understand what he said to them.
local: Amaa ba man nu asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ kasɛ.

eng: Nations and kingdoms will fight against each other, and there will be earthquakes and famines in all sorts of places. These things are just the beginning of the sufferings associated with the end.
local: Ɔsʋwʋlɛ ii biti ɔ kɔ yɛ ɔsʋwʋlɛ, kɛ kuwura‐gyii ɔ kɔ yɛ kuwura‐gyii. Kasɛ kii biti ki gyingyen katɩn kʋmaa nɩmʋ, kɛ akʋn a tɩyɛ. Kanɩn asɩn nɩmʋ kpini i du nɩn fɛɛ ɔkyɩɩ keduŋidi gyankpaasa.

eng: Jesus went down to Nazareth with them and was obedient to them. His mother cherished every word in her heart.
local: Nɩɩ o buu Mɛɛrɩ maa Gyosefu kpee Nasareti, yɛgɛ saŋa kʋmaa ee nu bamʋ asɛ. Amaa mu‐nyi taa asɩn nɩmʋ kpini waa mʋ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ ɛɛ fɛ ɩmʋ nfɛɛrɛ.

eng: Watch out for yourselves. People will hand you over to the councils. You will be beaten in the synagogues. You will stand before governors and kings because of me so that you can testify before them.
local: Fan baa fan da sʋ. Bee biti ba kɩtaa fanɛ kpee asɩn ogyikpa, kɛ ba dayɩ fanɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa. Lɩɩ mɛ sʋ bee biti ba tɩɩ fanɛ alʋnpʋ yɛ awura ansi tɔ, kɛ fan nyɛ ɔkpa tɔwɛ bamʋ mɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ.

eng: Jesus matured in wisdom and years, and in favor with God and with people.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu dan n‐yɩɩ tɔ yɛ kanyaasɩn tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ bɔla Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ asa ansi.

eng: First, the good news must be proclaimed to all the nations.
local: Amaa pɔyɩ kɛ laalaalʋwɛ ɔ ba mʋ, ɩ dagaa fɛɛ ba tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ sa asa yiri kʋmaa nɩmʋ.

eng: When they haul you in and hand you over, dont worry ahead of time about what to answer or say. Instead, say whatever is given to you at that moment, for you arent doing the speaking but the Holy Spirit is.
local: Nɩɩ ba kan kɩtaa fanɛ kpee asɩn ogyikpa mʋ, fan man yɛgɛ ɩ tɔɔrɔɔ fanɛ lɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ fen biti fan naa tɔwɛ mʋ sʋ. Saŋa mʋ kan fʋʋ mʋ, fan tɔwɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ taa waa fanɛ nnɔ tɔ mʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ man gyɛ fanɛ ɩɩ tɔwɛ, amaa Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ ɩɩ tɔwɛ.

eng: Brothers and sisters will hand each other over to death. A father will turn in his children. Children will rise up against their parents and have them executed.
local: Osupu ii biti ɔ ba lɛɛ ɔdaa sa kɛ ba mɔɔ mʋ, kɛ ɔsɛ gbaa ɔ lɛɛ mu‐bii sa. Nɩɩ nbii bee biti ba kʋsʋ yɩlɛ bamʋ akʋʋgɛpʋ sʋ kɛ ba yɛgɛ ba mɔɔ bamʋ.

eng: Everyone will hate you because of my name. But whoever stands firm until the end will be saved.
local: Ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ ii biti o kisi fanɛ lɩɩ mɛ sʋ. Amaa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɩtaa tɔ kenken naa fʋʋ laalaalʋwɛ mʋ, ii biti ɔ nyɛ kamɔlɩgɛ mʋ.

eng: When you see the disgusting and destructive thing standing where it shouldnt be , then those in Judea must escape to the mountains.
local: Kanɩn saŋa maŋa mʋ, nɩɩ fan kan wu ɔdaatapʋ libi‐libi mʋ maa yɩlɛ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ ikisii yɛ kɩsɩkpa sa Ɩbwaarɛ fɛɛ maa yɩlɛ tʋtɔ mʋ, saŋa maŋa mʋ, bamʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Gyudeya mʋ, ba sɩlɛ kpee abɩɩ tɔ.

eng: Those on the roof shouldnt come down or enter their houses to grab anything.
local: Ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ obu to mʋ, ɔ man bɩla bwii loo obu to ɔ naa taa mʋ atɔ kɛ ɔ sɩlɛ.

eng: Those in the field shouldnt come back to grab their clothes.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ ndɔɔ tɔ mʋ, ɔ man bɩla bwii mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ naa taa mʋ atɔ bunsa.

eng: How terrible it will be at that time for women who are pregnant and for women who are nursing their children.
local: Kanɩn nkɛ maŋa tɔ mʋ, ɩ mɛɛ yɛ ɩ baa ɩ bʋrɔn sa akyɩɩ ɩtɔ wuya ana, yɛ akyɩɩ atalapʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ nbii wuribi abaa tɔ mʋ!.

eng: Pray that it doesnt happen in winter.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ kɛ kanɩn asɩn nɩmʋ a man ba kɩkyɛ saŋa.

eng: In those days there will be great suffering such as the world has never before seen and will never again see.
local: Kanɩn saŋa maŋa ii biti ɩ baa i libi ɩ kyɔ awʋrʋfɔ mʋ nɩɩ dulinyaa wʋla wu lɩɩ koo saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tʋʋ dulinyaa ba fʋʋ ndaga saŋa nɩmʋ ii sʋ ii kpee.

eng: If the Lord hadnt shortened that time, no one would be rescued. But for the sake of the chosen ones, the ones whom God chose, he has cut short the time.
local: Amaa nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ man dɩn kɔɔgɛ kanɩn awʋrʋfɔ nkɛ mʋ sʋ nɩn, naafɔɔ ɔkʋ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ nyɛ kamɔlɩgɛ. Amaa lɩɩ mʋ asa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ mʋ sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔgɛ kanɩn nkɛ maŋa sʋ.

eng: Then if someone says to you, Look, heres the Christ, or There he is, dont believe it.
local: Kanɩn saŋa maa mʋ, nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, Fan kɩɩ! Krisito mʋ ndee! abɛɛ Fan kɩɩ! Mʋ ɩ bʋ falɛ mʋ! fan man kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii.

eng: False christs and false prophets will appear, and they will offer signs and wonders in order to deceive, if possible, those whom God has chosen.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ antɩŋɛsa Krisito ana yɛ antɩŋɛsa atɔwɛpʋ bee biti ba ba. Bee biti ba ba waa asɩn kyinkyinsa yɛ adangana pɩna asa. Naafɔɔ nɩɩ ɩ nyɛ waa nɩn, bɛɛ pɩna asa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ mʋ.

eng: But you, watch out! Ive told you everything ahead of time.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan baa fan da sʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ n wʋla tɔwɛ fanɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa pɔyɩ kanɩn nkɛ maŋa ɩ fʋʋ.

eng: In those days, after the suffering of that time, the sun will become dark, and the moon wont give its light.
local: Kanɩn awʋrʋfɔ nkɛ maŋa kamaa mʋ, kyɔwɛ man bɩla yɛ ɔ lɩɩ, ɔbwaayɩ gbaa man bɩla yɛ o fuuli.

eng: The stars will fall from the sky, and the planets and other heavenly bodies will be shaken.
local: Akyikpebi gbaa ii biti ɩ tɩyɛ lɩɩ sʋsʋ ba kasɛ, nɩɩ sʋsʋ ɔlʋn atɔ kpini ii biti i gyingyen.

eng: Then they will see the Human One coming in the clouds with great power and splendor.
local: Kɛ asa kpini ba wu mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ n maa lɩɩ abwaarɛ‐wʋlɛ tɔ n ba yɛ ɔlʋn yɛ bɛɛrɛɛ sʋ.

eng: Then he will send the angels and gather together his chosen people from the four corners of the earth, from the end of the earth to the end of heaven.
local: Kɛ n sun sʋsʋ nbɔɔ dulinyaa tɔ katɩn kʋmaa, kyɔwɛ kɩlɩɩ yɛ kyɔwɛ kɩtɩyɛ, adii yɛ akpɩlɩgɛ, kɛ ba natɛ kʋʋla Ɩbwaarɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ lɩɩ dulinyaa tɔ kpini.

eng: Learn this parable from the fig tree. After its branch becomes tender and it sprouts new leaves, you know that summer is near.
local: Fan yɛgɛ iyu afatɛɛ kɩpɔrɔgɛ ɩ waa asɩn baalasa sa fanɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ fan kan wu iyu ɩ maa pɔrɔgɛ mʋ, fan bɩɩ fɛɛ kɩkyɛ ki tee tiri tɔ.

eng: In the same way, when you see these things happening, you know that hes near, at the door.
local: Kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ fan kan wu fɛɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ fanɛ falɛ kpini ɩ kan ɩ waa mʋ, fan baa fan nyi fɛɛ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa kibwii ba mʋ ki tiri tɔ, nɩɩ i du fɛɛ ɔ yɩlɛ kebunɔ.

eng: I assure you that this generation wont pass away until all these things happen.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin fɛɛ atɔ nɩmʋ kpini ii biti ɩ waa pɔyɩ, kɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba san ba tɛ nkpa tɔ falɛ kpini ba wu.

eng: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will certainly not pass away.
local: Sʋsʋ yɛ kasɛ kpini ii biti ɔ kyʋn, amaa mɛ atɔwɛsa bɩrɛ ɩ mɛɛ yɛ ɩ kyʋn kakaakʋ.

eng: But nobody knows when that day or hour will come, not the angels in heaven and not the Son. Only the Father knows.
local: Ɔkʋ man bʋ tɔ o nyi kanɩn kakɛ abɛɛ saŋa. Sʋsʋ nbɔɔ yɛ mɛ, Obii mʋ gbaa a mee nyi, amɔɔ Ɔsɛ mʋ nkʋn i nyi.

eng: Watch out! Stay alert! You dont know when the time is coming.
local: Fan kɩɩ danbɩrasa yɛgɛ fan da sʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ fen mee nyi saŋa mʋ nɩɩ n biti n ba.

eng: It is as if someone took a trip, left the household behind, and put the servants in charge, giving each one a job to do, and told the doorkeeper to stay alert.
local: Ii biti ɩ waa nɩn fɛɛ nyɩmɩsa maa kpee ɔkpa kata, kɛ ɔ taa kʋtɔ kʋmaa waa mʋ ayaafɔlɛ abaa tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ sa ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ mu kusun sunsa. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ kebunɔ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ yɛ ɔ baa ɔ da sʋ.

eng: Therefore, stay alert! You dont know when the head of the household will come, whether in the evening or at midnight, or when the rooster crows in the early morning or at daybreak.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan baa fan da sʋ, kpalɩ fɛɛ fen mee nyi saŋa mʋ nɩɩ mɛ, fanɛ kɩkpaara wuya mʋ n biti n ba. N taalɛ n ba fɛɛ kalaatɩpwɛ abɛɛ kiyiridi nsana abɛɛ kɩbwayɩkɛ abɛɛ nyenyenfuu.

eng: Dont let him show up when you werent expecting and find you sleeping.
local: Nɩɩ n kan ba purigi fanɛ mʋ, n man ba tu fanɛ fan maa dɛ nsɩɩrɛ.

eng: What I say to you, I say to all: Stay alert.
local: Asɩn mʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ fanɛ falɛ, n tɔwɛ n sa ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ yɛ, Ba fʋ da sʋ! .

eng: It was two days before Passover and the Festival of Unleavened Bread. The chief priests and legal experts through cunning tricks were searching for a way to arrest Jesus and kill him.
local: Ɩ maa san nkɛ nyɔ kɛ Gyiwu abi bee gyii bamʋ kanyaŋɛ nbunɔ yɛ bodobodo mʋ nɩɩ kɩ man sʋ kɩfɛ tinsa mʋ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu bɩlɩsa yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ bee biti ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ ba baala taa kɩtaa Yesu kɛ ba mɔɔ mʋ.

eng: But they agreed that it shouldnt happen during the festival; otherwise, there would be an uproar among the people.
local: Nɩɩ bɛɛ yɛ, Fan man yɛgɛ a waa kanɩn saŋa nɩmʋ tɔ, akʋtɔ akʋ bee biti bɛɛ waa ɩlawʋ.

eng: Jesus was at Bethany visiting the house of Simon, who had a skin disease. During dinner, a woman came in with a vase made of alabaster and containing very expensive perfume of pure nard. She broke open the vase and poured the perfume on his head.
local: Yesu maa bʋ Betani mʋ, nɩɩ o kpee Simon mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ bootipu mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ. Maa tɛ tʋtɔ ee gyii mʋ, nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ kʋ naa taa tuulaale pʋrʋntʋwa tɔ ba mʋ asɛ. Kanɩn tuulaale maŋa sʋ ɔgyalɩn nɩn gaa, lɩɩ fɛɛ nadi nɩɩ ba taa waa ɩmʋ. Nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ tigi pʋrʋntʋwa mʋ kyɛɛgɛ tuulaale mʋ kpini wʋrɩgɛ Yesu kumu tɔ.

eng: Some grew angry. They said to each other, Why waste the perfume?.
local: Nɩɩ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ tʋtɔ mʋ akʋ ba nyɛ agbʋ bɛɛ taasɛ abaa yɛ, Saamʋ maa dan taa tuulaale nɩmʋ kyɛɛgɛ wʋrɩgɛ kɩyan falɛ, mɩnɛ kyaarɛ nɩɩ ɩ sʋ?.

eng: This perfume could have been sold for almost a years pay and the money given to the poor. And they scolded her.
local: Naafɔɔ nɩɩ ba taa tuulaale nɩmʋ fɛ nɩn, ɩ waa fɛɛ nyɩmɩsa kesu laalaamuu kʋkɔka, kɛ baa taa afulee mʋ sa atiripu. Nɩɩ ba nyɛ agbʋ bɛɛ pwɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ.

eng: Jesus said, Leave her alone. Why do you make trouble for her? She has done a good thing for me.
local: Amaa Yesu yɛ, Fan yɛgɛ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ! Mɩnɛ ɩ waa sʋ nɩɩ fɛn pwɩɩ mʋ? Kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa mʋ gyɛ kʋtɔ danbɩrasa sa mɛ.

eng: You always have the poor with you; and whenever you want, you can do something good for them. But you wont always have me.
local: Atiripu mʋ bɩrɛ, ba bʋ fanɛ asɛ saŋa kʋmaa. Kanɩn sʋ, saŋa kʋmaa fɛn taalɛ waa bamʋ adanbɩrasa. Amaa mɛɛ mɛ bɩrɛ, n man yɛ n baa n bʋ fanɛ asɛ saŋa kʋmaa.

eng: She has done what she could. She has anointed my body ahead of time for burial.
local: Ɔkyɩɩ nɩmʋ waa ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ taalɛ nɩn. Ɔ kyɛɛgɛ tuulaale nɩmʋ wʋrɩgɛ mɛ sʋ, ɔ gyankpaa ɛɛ sʋswa mɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ sa mi kepula kakɛ nɩn.

eng: I tell you the truth that, wherever in the whole world the good news is announced, what shes done will also be told in memory of her.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ nɩmʋ, dulinyaa tɔ katɩn kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ bee biti ba tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ, bee biti ba tɔwɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa falɛ taa nyiŋi mʋ.

eng: Judas Iscariot, one of the Twelve, went to the chief priests to give Jesus up to them.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu abɩɩlapʋ mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Gyudasi Isikariyoti kpee Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu bɩlɩsa mʋ asɛ, kɛ ɔ lɛɛ Yesu sa bamʋ.

eng: When they heard it, they were delighted and promised to give him money. So he started looking for an opportunity to turn him in.
local: Kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ mʋ, waa bamʋ ɔkɔn gaa, nɩɩ ba yɛ bee biti ba sa mu afulee. Ɩmʋ sʋ Gyudasi piili ee biti ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bɔla sʋ lɛɛ mʋ sa bamʋ.

eng: On the first day of the Festival of Unleavened Bread, when the Passover lamb was sacrificed, the disciples said to Jesus, Where do you want us to prepare for you to eat the Passover meal.
local: Kakɛ gyankpaasa mʋ nɩɩ bee gyii kanyaŋɛ nbunɔ bodobodo mʋ nɩɩ kɩ man sʋ kɩfɛ tinsa, nɩɩ ba mɔɔ kɩmʋ saraga nbʋlʋpʋ mʋ, nɩɩ Yesu abɩɩlapʋ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Fɩnɛ nɩɩ fii biti a lɔŋɔ sa fʋ, kɛ fu gyii kanyaŋɛ nbunɔ agyitɔ mʋ.

eng: He sent two of his disciples and said to them, Go into the city. A man carrying a water jar will meet you. Follow him.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu sun mʋ abɩɩlapʋ anyɔ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan kpee kadɛ lala mʋ tɔ, fen biti fan gyaŋa kanyɩn kʋ maa sʋ kolobi yɛ nkyu, fan buu mʋ.

eng: Wherever he enters, say to the owner of the house, The teacher asks,Where is my guest room where I can eat the Passover meal with my disciples.
local: naa loo kɩkpaara mʋ nɩɩ ee biti o loo mʋ sʋ, kɛ fan taasɛ kɩkpaara wuya mʋ yɛ, Ɔkaapʋpʋ mʋ yɛ, obu mɔɔ tɔ nɩɩ n maa mɛ abɩɩlapʋ an biti a gyii kanyaŋɛ nbunɔ agyitɔ mʋ.

eng: He will show you a large room upstairs already furnished. Prepare for us there.
local: Kɩkpaara wuya mʋ ii biti ɔ kaapʋ fanɛ obu lala kʋ abansʋrʋ sʋ, mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kʋʋla lɔŋɔ yɩla. Tʋtɔ nɩn fan lɔŋɔ kʋtɔ kʋmaa sa anɛ.

eng: The disciples left, came into the city, found everything just as he had told them, and they prepared the Passover meal.
local: Nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba kpee kadɛ lala mʋ tɔ naa wu kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ mʋ. Nɩɩ ba waa kanyaŋɛ nbunɔ agyitɔ mʋ.

eng: That evening, Jesus arrived with the Twelve.
local: Ɩ maa fʋʋ kalaatɩpwɛ asɛ mʋ, nɩɩ Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ kudu anyɔ mʋ ba ba abansʋrʋ mʋ sʋ.

eng: During the meal, Jesus said, I assure you that one of you will betray me—someone eating with me.
local: Ba maa gyii mʋ, nɩɩ Yesu yɛ, N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin yɛ, n maa fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ an gyii falɛ, fanɛ tɔ ɔkʋ ii biti ɔ lɛɛ mɛ sa.

eng: Deeply saddened, they asked him, one by one, Its not me, is it.
local: Asɩn mʋ a nyita bamʋ nkɔlɔ tɔ, nɩɩ ba piili bɛɛ taasɛ Yesu kʋkʋlʋn‐kʋkʋlʋn yɛ, Ɩ gyɛ mɛ nɩn, abɛɛ.

eng: Jesus answered, Its one of the Twelve, one who is dipping bread with me into this bowl.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Fanɛ kudu anyɔ mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ, ɔmʋ nɩɩ n maa mʋ abaa ɩ bʋga ii loo lapɛ kʋlʋn tɔ mʋ, ii biti ɔ lɛɛ mɛ sa.

eng: The Human One goes to his death just as it is written about him. But how terrible it is for that person who betrays the Human One! It would have been better for him if he had never been born.
local: Mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa n biti n wu fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba wʋla kyʋrɔɔ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ mʋ. Amaa ɔmʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ lɛɛ mɛ sa mʋ, mʋ laakʋ. Naafɔɔ ba daa ba man kʋʋgɛ mʋ gbaa mʋ, ɩ bɔ.

eng: While they were eating, Jesus took bread, blessed it, broke it, and gave it to them, and said, Take; this is my body.
local: Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba maa san bee gyii mʋ, nɩɩ Yesu taa bodobodo, nɩɩ ɔ taa afwaala sa Ɩbwaarɛ, nɩɩ ɔ kpɛɛ bodobodo mʋ tɔ, taa sa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan kɔɔlɛ bodobodo nɩmʋ gyii, ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ mɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ mʋ.

eng: He took a cup, gave thanks, and gave it to them, and they all drank from it.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu taa kɔɔpʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taa afwaala sa mʋ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ. Nɩɩ ɔ taa sa bamʋ kpini yɛ ba nuu.

eng: He said to them, This is my blood of the covenant, which is poured out for many.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ mɛ nkalan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ taa yɩlɛ sa katɔwɛ kyula mʋ, kɛ ɩ kyɛɛgɛ wʋrɩgɛ sa asa gaalaagaa.

eng: I assure you that I wont drink wine again until that day when I drink it in a new way in Gods kingdom.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ n man bɩla yɛ n nuu wayɩn nta nɩmʋ kakaakʋ, naa fʋʋ kakɛ mʋ nɩɩ n biti n bɩla nuu nta pʋpwɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ.

eng: After singing songs of praise, they went out to the Mount of Olives.
local: Nɩɩ ba maa bɩɩ ɔlʋn yɩn Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ, ba lɩɩ kyʋn Olifu kɩbɩɩ mʋ sʋ.

eng: Jesus said to them, You will all falter in your faithfulness to me. It is written, I will hit the shepherd, and the sheep will go off in all directions .
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fanɛ kpini fen biti fan sɩlɛ yɛgɛ mɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ yɛ, N biti n mɔɔ nbʋlʋpʋ ɔkpapʋ mʋ, kɛ nbʋlʋpʋ mʋ ba yaasɛ tɔ. .

eng: But after Im raised up, I will go before you to Galilee.
local: Yesu kyaga sʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, Amaa mi kelewu kyiŋi kamaa mʋ, n biti n gyankpaa fanɛ kpee Galile.

eng: Peter said to him, Even if everyone else stumbles, I wont.
local: Nɩɩ Pita lɛɛ Yesu kɔnɔ ɛɛ, Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ sɩlɛ yɛgɛ fʋ gbaa, mɛɛ mɛ n man yɛ n sɩlɛ yɛgɛ fʋ.

eng: But Jesus said to him, I assure you that on this very night, before the rooster crows twice, you will deny me three times.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ Pita yɛ, N tɔwɛ fʋ kɛ fu nu yɛ, ndaga, pɔyɩ kɛ kyaasɛ ɔ bʋn ɩlɩɩ ɩnyɔ mʋ, fii biti fʋ kyɛɛ ɩlɩɩ ɩsa yɛ fu mee nyi mɛ.

eng: But Peter insisted, If I must die alongside you, I wont deny you. And they all said the same thing.
local: Amaa Pita payɩ ansi yɛ, Halɩɩ ɩ gyɛ yɛ n maa fʋ an biti a wu gbaa mʋ, n mɛɛ yɛ n tɔwɛ yɛ n man nyi fʋ. Kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ abɩɩlapʋ abanban mʋ kpini ba tɔwɛ.

eng: Jesus and his disciples came to a place called Gethsemane. Jesus said to them, Sit here while I pray.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba kpee katɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ Gesemani, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan kyɩna nfɩɩ kɛ n kpee naa kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ.

eng: He took Peter, James, and John along with him. He began to feel despair and was anxious.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu taa Pita yɛ Gyemisi yɛ Gyɔn buu mʋ n‐yɩɩ. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu kɔkɔlɔ nyita mʋ, nɩɩ mʋ n‐yɩɩ gbaa ɩ yɔɔ mʋ.

eng: He said to them, Im very sad. Its as if Im dying. Stay here and keep alert.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Mɛ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ ɩ dɩyɛ mɛ lewu‐lewu. Fan sii nfɩɩ yɛgɛ fan da sʋ.

eng: Then he went a short distance farther and fell to the ground. He prayed that, if possible, he might be spared the time of suffering.
local: Maa kpee ansi tɔ gbɛrɛɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ naa kyʋn tɩyɛ kasɛ kʋlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ, Nɩɩ ɩ nyɛ waa bɩrɛ, falɛ awʋrʋfɔ ɩnɩmʋ ɩ man tu mʋ.

eng: He said, Abba, Father, for you all things are possible. Take this cup of suffering away from me. However—not what I want but what you want.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu kʋlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Nsɛ, kʋtɔ kʋmaa kɩ man bʋlʋn fʋ asɛ. Kɔɔlɛ kanɩn awʋrʋfɔ nɩmʋ lɩɩ mɛ sʋ. Amaa man gyɛ mɛ kɔkɔlɔ biti, amaa fʋ yɛgɛ fʋ kɔkɔlɔ biti kɩ waa.

eng: He came and found them sleeping. He said to Peter, Simon, are you asleep? Couldnt you stay alert for one hour?.
local: Yesu maa bwii kpee mʋ abɩɩlapʋ asɛ mʋ, ɔ naa tu ba maa dɛ nsɩɩrɛ. Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ Pita yɛ, Simon, fʋ dɛ nɩn? Amɔɔ fan mɛɛ bɩla taalɛ kyɩna kerefi kʋlʋn yɛgɛ fan man dɛ?.

eng: Stay alert and pray so that you wont give in to temptation. The spirit is eager, but the flesh is weak.
local: Fan baa fan da sʋ, fan kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ kʋlɛ, kɛ fan man tɩyɛ kʋtʋʋ tɔ kɩɩ tɔ. Kpalɩ fɛɛ fanɛ n‐yaayu bɩrɛ ii biti ɩ waa kʋ tɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋrɔn, amaa fanɛ n‐yɩɩ‐wʋlɛ mɔɔ ɩ pɔɔ.

eng: Again, he left them and prayed, repeating the same words.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu bɩla bwii kpee ɛɛ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ kʋkʋlʋn maŋa.

eng: And, again, when he came back, he found them sleeping, for they couldnt keep their eyes open, and they didnt know how to respond to him.
local: Maa bɩla bwii ba mʋ, o wu ba maa dɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ nsɩɩrɛ ɩɩ sʋ bamʋ. Nɩɩ ba man bɩla be nyi kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ.

eng: He came a third time and said to them, Will you sleep and rest all night? Thats enough! The time has come for the Human One to be betrayed into the hands of sinners.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Yesu naa bwii ba kili sasapʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan san fan dɛ fɛn fʋʋtɛ nɩn? Fan kɩɩ! Saŋa mʋ fʋʋ fɛɛ ba taa mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ waa alibi awaapʋ abaa tɔ.

eng: Get up! Lets go! Look, here comes my betrayer.
local: Fan kɩɩ, ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ lɛɛ mɛ ɛɛ sa mʋ ndee. Fan kʋsʋ kɛ a kpee.

eng: Suddenly, while Jesus was still speaking, Judas, one of the Twelve, came with a mob carrying swords and clubs. They had been sent by the chief priests, legal experts, and elders.
local: Yesu maa dan san ɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ, opula tɔ nɩɩ Gyudasi, abɩɩlapʋ kudu anyɔ mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ, ba fʋʋ tʋtɔ yɛ sakyɔ damantɛ kʋ. Ba sʋ isakunde yɛ agulungu. Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu bɩlɩsa yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ yɛ kadɛ mʋ abɩlɩsa i sun bamʋ.

eng: His betrayer had given them a sign: Arrest the man I kiss, and take him away under guard.
local: Nɩɩ Gyudasi mʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ lɛɛ Yesu sa mʋ, wʋla kaapʋ asa sɩnkaala kʋ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Ɔmʋ nɩɩ n naa baari mʋ tɔ mʋ, mʋ nɩmʋ. Fan kɩtaa mʋ kɛ fan kun mʋ taa natɛ.

eng: As soon as he got there, Judas said to Jesus, Rabbi! Then he kissed him.
local: Gyudasi maa dan fʋʋ Yesu asɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ yɛ, Ɔkaapʋpʋ! Nɩɩ o baari mʋ tɔ.

eng: Then they came and grabbed Jesus and arrested him.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba kɩtaa Yesu ba sʋ mʋ tɔ kinkin.

eng: One of the bystanders drew a sword and struck the high priests slave and cut off his ear.
local: Amaa bamʋ nɩɩ ba yɩlɛ tʋtɔ mʋ, ɔkʋ lɛɛ mu sakunde ŋɛ Ɩbwaarɛ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ kɩnyɛ kʋsʋ tɩn.

eng: Jesus responded, Have you come with swords and clubs to arrest me, like an outlaw?.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Amɔɔ isakunde yɛ ngulungun nɩɩ fɛn taa ba kɛ fan ba kɩtaa mɛ, fɛɛ n maa gyɛ kumu tɔ ɔlʋn oyu?.

eng: Day after day, I was with you, teaching in the temple, but you didnt arrest me. But let the scriptures be fulfilled.
local: Kakɛ kʋmaa, n maa fanɛ nɩn kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ yɛgɛ n kaapʋ, nkɛɛ fan man kɩtaa mɛ. Amaa ɩ dagaa ɩnɩmʋ ɩ waa kanɩn, kɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ mʋ, ɩ ba tɔ.

eng: And all his disciples left him and ran away.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ kpini ba yɛgɛ mʋ sɩlɛ kyʋn.

eng: One young man, a disciple, was wearing nothing but a linen cloth. They grabbed him.
local: Nɩɩ kɩyaafɔlɛ kʋ mʋ nɩɩ o bun kɩkalala nkʋn mʋ o buu Yesu, nɩɩ ba paa yɛ ba kɩtaa mʋ.

eng: but he left the linen cloth behind and ran away naked.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ sɩlɛ kibulunkpan yɛgɛ mʋ kɩkalala mʋ.

eng: They led Jesus away to the high priest, and all the chief priests, elders, and legal experts gathered.
local: Nɩɩ ba taa Yesu kpee oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ kɩkpaara sʋ. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ aseepu bɩlɩsa yɛ kadɛ abɩlɩsa yɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ mʋ kpini ba sarɛ.

eng: Peter followed him from a distance, right into the high priests courtyard. He was sitting with the guards, warming himself by the fire.
local: Nɩɩ Pita buu Yesu kɩyaa‐kɩyaa naa loo oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ kɩkpaara sʋ asɩn ogyikpa tʋtɔ. Nɩɩ o sii kyɩna kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ akɩɩsʋpʋ mʋ asɛ ɛɛ wʋlɛ fʋlɔn.

eng: The chief priests and the whole Sanhedrin were looking for testimony against Jesus in order to put him to death, but they couldnt find any.
local: Nɩɩ aseepu bɩlɩsa mʋ yɛ kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ abɩlɩsa mʋ kpini ba biti asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba tɩn gyaga Yesu sʋ, kɛ ba nyɛ mɔɔ mʋ. Amaa ba kpɩnɛ, ba man nyɛ.

eng: Many brought false testimony against him, but they contradicted each other.
local: Nɩɩ asa gaalaagaa ba tɩn antɩŋɛsa gyaga Yesu sʋ, amaa bamʋ nnɔ tɔwɛsa ɩ man gyɛ kʋlʋn.

eng: Some stood to offer false witness against him, saying.
local: Nɩɩ anyɩn akʋ ba kʋsʋ yɩlɛ tɩn antɩŋɛsa gyaga Yesu sʋ yɛ.

eng: When the Sabbath was over, Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James, and Salome bought spices so that they could go and anoint Jesus dead body.
local: Kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ kamaa tɔ mʋ, Mɛɛrɩ Magidalen yɛ Mɛɛrɩ Gyemisi mu‐nyi yɛ Solome ba bɩya atɔ liifensa mʋ nɩɩ ba sɔɔ mʋ, kɛ ba taa naa sʋswa Yesu kebuni mʋ.

eng: We heard him saying, I will destroy this temple, constructed by humans, and within three days I will build another, one not made by humans.
local: An nu maa tɔwɛ yɛ, N biti n boyi kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ nɩɩ asa ba pɔyɩ mʋ, kɛ nkɛ nsa kamaa mʋ, n biti n pɔyɩ kɩpʋpwɛ mʋ nɩɩ man gyɛ asa ba pɔyɩ kɩmʋ. .

eng: Very early on the first day of the week, just after sunrise, they came to the tomb.
local: Lɩɩkaakɛ, nawɔkyɩ kakɛ gyankpaasa akɛ nyenyenfuu, kyɔwɛ maa dan lɩɩ mʋ, nɩɩ ba kʋsʋ bee kpee Yesu kakyan mʋ asɛ.

eng: But their testimonies didnt agree even on this point.
local: Nkɛɛ bamʋ gbaa‐gbaa bamʋ nnɔ ɩ kwɩɩ yɛ abaa.

eng: They were saying to each other, Whos going to roll the stone away from the entrance for us.
local: Ba maa naa bee kpee ɔkpa tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ba taasɛ abaa yɛ, Anɩmʋ ii biti ɔ naa gbiliti kubuu mʋ lɩɩ kakyan mʋ kɔnɔ sa anɛ? Kpalɩ fɛɛ kubuu mʋ kɩ kyɔ gaa.

eng: Then the high priest stood up in the middle of the gathering and examined Jesus. Arent you going to respond to the testimony these people have brought against you.
local: Nɩɩ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ kʋsʋ yɩlɛ bamʋ kpini ansi tɔ, taasɛ Yesu yɛ, Amɔɔ fʋ man sʋ kɔnɔ fʋ lɛɛ lɩɩ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ bɛɛ pwɩɩ fʋ falɛ.

eng: When they looked up, they saw that the stone had been rolled away.
local: Ba maa kɩɩ mʋ, ba wu fɛɛ kubuu mʋ kɩ tɛɛ wʋla gbiliti kɩ dɛ.

eng: But Jesus was silent and didnt answer. Again, the high priest asked, Are you the Christ, the Son of the blessed one.
local: Amaa Yesu laatɔ ɔ man bugi mʋ kɔnɔ. Nɩɩ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ bɩla bwii taasɛ Yesu yɛ, Fʋ ɩ gyɛ Krisito, Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ mʋ? Fʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ anɛ ayenbi ɩ gyɛ mʋ lɛɛ mʋ Mu‐Bii.

eng: Going into the tomb, they saw a young man in a white robe seated on the right side; and they were startled.
local: Nɩɩ ba loo kakyan mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ba wu kɩyaafɔlɛ kʋ maa bun atɔ fufuli ɔ tɛ kakyan mʋ tɔ gyisa sʋ, nɩɩ bamʋ nkɔlɔ ɩ ŋmaa bamʋ.

eng: Jesus said, I am. And you will see the Human One sitting on the right side of the Almighty and coming on the heavenly clouds.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Mɛ ɩ gyɛ mʋ. Nɩɩ fen biti fan wu mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa n tɛ Ɔlʋnpʋ Ɩbwaarɛ gyisa sʋ abwaarɛ‐wʋlɛ tɔ ɛɛ ba.

eng: But he said to them, Dont be alarmed! You are looking for Jesus of Nazareth, who was crucified. He has been raised. He isnt here. Look, heres the place where they laid him.
local: Nɩɩ kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ yɛ, Fan man yɛgɛ fanɛ nkɔlɔ ɩ ŋmaa fanɛ. N nyi yɛ Yesu mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ Nasareti, mʋ nɩɩ ba da mʋ oyu laasa sʋ mʋ nɩɩ fen biti. Ɔ man bʋ nfɩɩ. O kyiŋi lɩɩ lewu tɔ. Fan kɩɩ, nfɩɩ nɩɩ ba daa taa mʋ yɩla.

eng: Then the high priest tore his clothes and said, Why do we need any more witnesses?.
local: Nɩɩ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ kyatɛ mʋ gbaa‐gbaa ɩkaalɛ, nɩɩ ɔ yɛ, Ɩnɩmʋ bɩrɛ, an man bɩla an biti ɔkʋ ɔ ba tɔwɛ anɛ asɩn pwɩɩsa kʋ lɩɩ mʋ sʋ.

eng: Go, tell his disciples, especially Peter, that he is going ahead of you into Galilee. You will see him there, just as he told you.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan natɛ naa tɔwɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ Pita yɛ, Yesu gyankpaa fanɛ ee kpee Galile. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ fen biti fan wu mʋ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ wʋla tɔwɛ fanɛ mʋ.

eng: Youve heard his insult against God. What do you think? They all condemned him. He deserves to die.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan nu yɛ fanɛ asʋ. Ɔ tɔwɛ nbusaa asɩn yii Ɩbwaarɛ. Kanɩn sʋ fan yɛ a waa mʋ nɛnɛ? Bamʋ kpini ba tɔwɛ yɛ, O gyii kʋpwɛ, nɩɩ ɩ dagaa maa wu.

eng: Overcome with terror and dread, they fled from the tomb. They said nothing to anyone, because they were afraid. .
local: Nɩɩ ba sɩlɛ lɩɩ kakyan mʋ tɔ lɩɩ fɛɛ kufuu kɩ kɩtaa bamʋ, nɩɩ bɛɛ kyɩkyaa. Ba man tɔwɛ ɔkʋ sɛhn kpalɩ fɛɛ kufuu kɩ maa sʋ bamʋ sʋ.

eng: Some began to spit on him. Some covered his face and hit him, saying, Prophesy! Then the guards took him and beat him.
local: Nɩɩ asa mʋ akʋ ba tʋʋ nkyɔlɛ bee bun Yesu, nɩɩ ba bun mu ansi yɛgɛ akʋ mɔɔ bɛɛ da mʋ, nɩɩ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ ɔ bɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ da mʋ? Nɩɩ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ akɩɩsʋpʋ mʋ ba taa mʋ lɩɩ bɛɛ kpa mʋ ɩtan.

eng: They promptly reported all of the young mans instructions to those who were with Peter. Afterward, through the work of his disciples, Jesus sent out, from the east to the west, the sacred and undying message of eternal salvation. Amen. .
local: Yesu maa kʋsʋ lɩɩ lewu tɔ kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ lɩɩkaakɛ nyenyenfuu mʋ, ɔ daa kɩlɛɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ nɩn kaapʋ Mɛɛrɩ Magidalen mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gya nbwɩɩ libi asunɔ lɩɩ mʋ tɔ mʋ.

eng: Meanwhile, Peter was below in the courtyard. A woman, one of the high priests servants, approached.
local: Pita maa dan san ɔ bʋ tʋtɔ mʋ, nɩɩ aseepu ɔbɩlɩsa kɩnyɛ kyɩɩsa kʋ ba tʋtɔ.

eng: She went and reported to the ones who had been with him, who were mourning and weeping.
local: Nɩɩ o kpee naa tɔwɛ mʋ bɩrɩsa ana mʋ nɩɩ ba bɔɔta tɔ ba tɛ bɛɛ sawʋ mʋ.

eng: and saw Peter warming himself by the fire. She stared at him and said, You were also with the Nazarene, Jesus.
local: Maa wu Pita maa san ɛɛ wʋlɛ fʋlɔn mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kɩɩ mʋ surum. Nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ Pita yɛ, Yɛ ngbaa fʋ fu buu Yesu mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ Kanasareti‐nyɩn mʋ nɩn.

eng: But even after they heard the news, they didnt believe that Jesus was alive and that Mary had seen him.
local: Ba maa nu lɩɩ Magidalen asɛ yɛ Yesu tɛ nkpa tɔ nɩɩ o wu mʋ mʋ, ba man kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii.

eng: But he denied it, saying, I dont know what youre talking about. I dont understand what youre saying. And he went outside into the outer courtyard. A rooster crowed.
local: Amaa Pita kyɛɛ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, N man nyi mʋ, nɩɩ n man nyi asɩn mʋ nɩɩ fɩɩ tɔwɛ falɛ kasɛ. Nɩɩ Pita lɩɩ ba lenbu.

eng: After that he appeared in a different form to two of them who were walking along in the countryside.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ kamaa mʋ, Yesu taa ɔkpa pʋpwɛ kʋ sʋ lɛɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ kaapʋ bamʋ tɔ asa anyɔ akʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba naa bee kpee bamʋ kadɛ tɔ.

eng: The female servant saw him and began a second time to say to those standing around, This man is one of them.
local: Nɩɩ kɩnyɛ kyɩɩsa mʋ bɩla wu Pita tʋtɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba yɩla tʋtɔ mʋ yɛ, Ɔnɩmʋ gbaa tii bamʋ sʋ.

eng: When they returned, they reported it to the others, but they didnt believe them.
local: Nɩɩ ba bwii ba tɔwɛ abanban mʋ, amaa ba man kɔɔlɛ bamʋ gyii.

eng: But he denied it again. A short time later, those standing around again said to Peter, You must be one of them, because you are also a Galilean.
local: Nɩɩ Pita bɩla kyɛɛ. Kamaa tɔ mʋ, bamʋ nɩɩ ba yɩlɛ tʋtɔ mʋ ba tɔwɛ Pita yɛ, Kesintin tɔ mʋ, fʋ gyɛ bamʋ tɔ ɔkʋ nɩn. Kpalɩ fɛɛ ngbaa fʋ, fʋ lɩɩ Galile nɩn.

eng: Finally he appeared to the eleven while they were eating. Jesus criticized their unbelief and stubbornness because they didnt believe those who saw him after he was raised up.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ kpini kamaa mʋ, Yesu lɛɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ kaapʋ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ kudu ɔkʋ mʋ, yɛgɛ ba tɛ bee gyii. Yesu pwɩɩ bamʋ lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ nkɔlɔ ɩ maa bʋlʋn gaa, nɩɩ ba man kɔɔlɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba wu mʋ maa tɛ nkpa tɔ mʋ gyii.

eng: But he cursed and swore, I dont know this man youre talking about.
local: Nɩɩ Pita piili ɛɛ tɔwɛ nnɔ ɛɛ gyaga mʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ bɔ ntan tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ fɛn tɔwɛ mʋ asɩn falɛ gbaa, n man nyi mʋ.

eng: He said to them, Go into the whole world and proclaim the good news to every creature.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan kpee dulinyaa tɔ katɩn kʋmaa naa tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ sa daadimaadi.

eng: At that very moment, a rooster crowed a second time. Peter remembered what Jesus told him, Before a rooster crows twice, you will deny me three times. And he broke down, sobbing.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ kyaasɛ bʋn kɩnyɔsapʋ. Nɩɩ Pita nyiŋi asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Yesu wʋla tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Pɔyɩ kɛ kyaasɛ ɔ bʋn ɩlɩɩ ɩnyɔ mʋ, fii biti fʋ kyɛɛ ɩlɩɩ ɩsa yɛ fʋ man nyi mɛ mʋ. Nɩɩ Pita tɩyɛ kusu sʋ ɛɛ sawʋ.

eng: Whoever believes and is baptized will be saved, but whoever doesnt believe will be condemned.
local: Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ gyii, nɩɩ ba gyee mʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee mʋ, ee biti ɔ nyɛ kamɔlɩgɛ. Amaa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man kɔɔlɛ gyii mʋ, bee biti ba bun mʋ lewu kʋpwɛ.

eng: These signs will be associated with those who believe: they will throw out demons in my name. They will speak in new languages.
local: Bamʋ nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ gyii mʋ falɛ ɩsɩnkaala ii biti i buu bamʋ. Lɩɩ mɛ kɩtɩɩ tɔ sʋ mʋ, bee biti ba gya nbwɩɩ libi, kɛ ba tɔwɛ ntɔwɛ pʋpwɛ.

eng: They will pick up snakes with their hands. If they drink anything poisonous, it will not hurt them. They will place their hands on the sick, and they will get well.
local: Fɛɛ nɩɩ ba kan kɩtaa anansɛ tɔ abɛɛ ba kan nuu kʋbwɛ gbaa mʋ, ɩ mɛɛ yɛ ɩ waa bamʋ sɛhn. Bee biti ba taa bamʋ abaa gyaga alɔpʋ sʋ kɛ ba nyɛ alanfɩya.

eng: After the Lord Jesus spoke to them, he was lifted up into heaven and sat down on the right side of God.
local: Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu maa tɔwɛ bamʋ asɛ lʋwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ taa mʋ kpee sʋsʋ naa kyɩna mʋ gyisa sʋ.

eng: But they went out and proclaimed the message everywhere. The Lord worked with them, confirming the word by the signs associated with them.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu abɩɩlapʋ ba kpee katɩn kʋmaa naa tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ, yɛgɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu ɔlʋn tii bamʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ba waa adangana, nɩɩ ɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ bamʋ asɩn danbɩrasa katɔwɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ kesintin.

eng: In the fifteenth year of the rule of the emperor Tiberius—when Pontius Pilate was governor over Judea and Herod was ruler over Galilee, his brother Philip was ruler over Ituraea and Trachonitis, and Lysanias was ruler over Abilene.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Tiberiyu kyɩna ɔ gyɛ Roma ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ owura lala nsu kudu anusapu mʋ, saŋa maŋa tɔ mʋ Pontiyu Paleti ɩ kyɩna ɔ gyɛ Gyudeya Kuwura‐bii, nɩɩ Herodi mɔɔ gyɛ Galile Kuwura‐bii, yɛgɛ mu‐supu Filipu mɔɔ ɛɛ kɩɩ Itureya yɛ Tirakoniti ɩsʋwʋlɛ sʋ, nɩɩ Lisaniya mɔɔ ɩ gyɛ Abilene ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ Kuwura‐bii.

eng: during the high priesthood of Annas and Caiaphas—Gods word came to John son of Zechariah in the wilderness.
local: Kanɩn saŋa maa nɩɩ Anasi maa Kayifasi ba gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ. Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ mʋ asɩn sa Sakariya mu‐bii Gyɔn, saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ ofuli sʋ mʋ.

eng: John went throughout the region of the Jordan River, calling for people to be baptized to show that they were changing their hearts and lives and wanted God to forgive their sins.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ Gyɔn natɛ kyaabɔɔ ɔsʋwʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ maa Gyɔdan ɔbʋn mʋ ɛɛ tɔwɛ abwaarɛsɩn ɛɛ sa asa yɛ, Fan kyɛɛgɛ fanɛ nfɛɛrɛ lɩɩ fanɛ alibi tɔ kɛ fan bwii ba Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ, kɛ fan yɛgɛ ba gyee fanɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee, kɛ fan nyɛ alibi kɩtaa kɛɛ.

eng: This is just as it was written in the scroll of the words of Isaiah the prophet, A voice crying out in the wilderness: Prepare the way for the Lord; make his paths straight.
local: Gyɔn nɩmʋ asɩn nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Isaya wʋla kyʋrɔɔ yɩla mʋ ɔwʋlɛ tɔ mʋ yɛ, Ɔkʋ bʋ ofuli sʋ ee kuusi ɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Fan lɔŋɔ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ ɔkpa, kɛ fan kyiigi mʋ ɩkpa‐bi mʋ.

eng: Every valley will be filled, and every mountain and hill will be leveled. The crooked will be made straight and the rough places made smooth.
local: Kɛ fan tii‐tii igee mʋ, kɛ fan tɩtɛ abɩɩ yɛ abɩɩ‐bi mʋ ba kasɛ waa kyɛɛkyɛɛ. Kɛ fan kyiigi ɩkpa nyɔnsa mʋ, kɛ ɩkpa gogyigogyi mʋ i tii waa tɔlɩtɔlɩ.

eng: All humanity will see Gods salvation. .
local: Kɛ daadimaadi kpini ba wu Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ yɛ ɔ ba mɔlɩgɛ asa mʋ. .

eng: Then John said to the crowds who came to be baptized by him, You children of snakes! Who warned you to escape from the angry judgment that is coming soon?.
local: Nɩɩ Gyɔn tɔwɛ sakyɔ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ ba mʋ asɛ yɛ o gyee bamʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee mʋ yɛ, Fan du fɛɛ anansɛ mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ kʋbwɛ. Nbɩɩnbɩɩ bɩrɛ anɩmʋ ɩ da fanɛ amu tɔ yɛ fan sɩlɛ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ agbʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ fanɛ kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ mʋ mʋ tɔ?.

eng: Produce fruit that shows you have changed your hearts and lives. And dont even think about saying to yourselves, Abraham is our father. I tell you that God is able to raise up Abrahams children from these stones.
local: Fan waa adanbɩrasa mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ fan kyɛɛgɛ fanɛ nfɛɛrɛ lɩɩ fanɛ alibi tɔ ba Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ. Kɛ fan yɛgɛ kɩtɔwɛ yɛ, An lɩɩ Abraham kɩnaana tɔ nɩn, ɩmʋ sʋ Ɩbwaarɛ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ bɩɩtɛ anɛ kʋsʋ. N tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ taalɛ yɛgɛ abuu nɩmʋ a biliŋi Abraham anaana‐bii.

eng: The ax is already at the root of the trees. Therefore, every tree that doesnt produce good fruit will be chopped down and tossed into the fire.
local: Ba tɛɛ wʋla taa kpanfa waa oyu mʋ ilin tɔ, lɩɩ fɛɛ oyu kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ mɛɛ sɔyɩ abi danbɩrasa mʋ, bee biti ba ŋɛ kɩmʋ tʋʋ kasɛ, kɛ ba taa tʋʋ waa fʋlɔn tɔ.

eng: The crowds asked him, What then should we do.
local: Gyɔn maa tɔwɛ kanɩn mʋ, nɩɩ sakyɔ mʋ taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɩmʋ sʋ nɛnɛ nɩɩ an biti a waa.

eng: He answered, Whoever has two shirts must share with the one who has none, and whoever has food must do the same.
local: Nɩɩ Gyɔn lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ ɩkaalɛ ɩnyɔ mʋ, ɔ barɩgɛ tɔ sa mʋ tɔmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man sʋ, nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ agyitɔ gbaa, ɔ waa kanɩn.

eng: Even tax collectors came to be baptized. They said to him, Teacher, what should we do.
local: Lenpoo akɔɔlɛpʋ akʋ gbaa ba ba yɛ o gyee bamʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee. Nɩɩ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɔkaapʋpʋ, nɛnɛ nɩɩ an biti a waa.

eng: He replied, Collect no more than you are authorized to collect.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Fan man kɔɔlɛ afulee kyʋn kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ fan kɔɔlɛ mʋ.

eng: Soldiers asked, What about us? What should we do? He answered, Dont cheat or harass anyone, and be satisfied with your pay.
local: Nɩɩ anaakɔpʋ mʋ akʋ ba ba taasɛ Gyɔn yɛ, Nɛnɛ nɩɩ anɛ gbaa anɛ an biti a waa? Nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Fan man nyan ɔkʋ kɔɔlɛ mu afulee, kɛ fan man pwɩɩ asa mʋ nɩɩ bamʋ asɩn a dɛ ɔkpa, amaa ɩmʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ ka fanɛ mʋ ɩ kʋkwɛ fanɛ.

eng: The people were filled with expectation, and everyone wondered whether John might be the Christ.
local: Ɩ maa waa fɛɛ asa mʋ kpini bɛɛ kɩɩ Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ ɔkpa mʋ, ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ ɩɩ fɛ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ akʋtɔ fɛɛ Gyɔn ɩ gyɛ Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ mʋ.

eng: John replied to them all, I baptize you with water, but the one who is more powerful than me is coming. Im not worthy to loosen the strap of his sandals. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and fire.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ Gyɔn tɔwɛ asa mʋ kpini yɛ, Nkyu nɩɩ n sʋ n gyee fanɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee, amaa ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ kyɔ mɛ gaa mʋ ɩɩ ba. Nɩɩ n man dagaa gbaa fɛɛ n muŋa saŋɛ mʋ asɩbɩta ɩfɛ. Nɩɩ mɛɛ mʋ ii biti ɔ taa Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ yɛ fʋlɔn gyee fanɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee.

eng: The shovel he uses to sift the wheat from the husks is in his hands. He will clean out his threshing area and bring the wheat into his barn. But he will burn the husks with a fire that cant be put out.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ sʋ mʋ kʋtɔ kyaalɛsa mʋ kɩbaa tɔ, ee biti ɔ ba kyaalɛ ayu lɩɩ ayu‐waatɛ tɔ, kɛ ɔ laata ayubi mʋ waa kikyunkun tɔ, kɛ o tuwa ayu‐waatɛ mʋ fʋlɔn mʋ nɩɩ ii mee dun kpaa.

eng: With many other words John appealed to them, proclaiming good news to the people.
local: Ɩkpa damantɛ sʋ nɩɩ Gyɔn taa kɩtaa asa mʋ nkɔlɔ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ sa bamʋ.

eng: But Herod the ruler had been criticized harshly by John because of Herodias, Herods brothers wife, and because of all the evil he had done.
local: Ɩmʋ kamaa mʋ Gyɔn pwɩɩ owura Herodi, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ taa mu‐supu Filipu mʋ‐ka Herodiya kili, yɛ asɩn libi mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa tii sʋ.

eng: He added this to the list of his evil deeds: he locked John up in prison.
local: Amaa alibi mʋ nɩɩ Herodi bɩla waa tii sʋ mʋ ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ kamaa tɔ mʋ ɔ kɩtaa Gyɔn tii obu.

eng: When everyone was being baptized, Jesus also was baptized. While he was praying, heaven was opened.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Gyɔn i gyee asa Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee mʋ, o gyee Yesu gbaa. Nɩɩ Yesu maa kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ mʋ, nɩɩ sʋsʋ bugi tɔ.

eng: and the Holy Spirit came down on him in bodily form like a dove. And there was a voice from heaven: You are my Son, whom I dearly love; in you I find happiness.
local: Nɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ kɩ kpɩlɩgɛ fɛɛ kuwura‐lɔpɔ ba gyɔŋɔ mʋ sʋ. Nɩɩ ɔbʋlɛ kʋ lɩɩ sʋsʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, Fʋ ɩ gyɛ mi‐bii bitisa mʋ, nɩɩ fʋ ɩ bɔla mi ansi.

eng: Jesus was about years old when he began his ministry. People supposed that he was the son of Joseph son of Heli.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Yesu gyii nsu adɩsa mʋ, nɩɩ o piili mu kusun mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sun mʋ yɛ ɔ ba waa mʋ. Nɩɩ asa bɛɛ kɩɩ fɛɛ Yesu gyɛ Gyosefu mu‐bii. Gyosefu mʋ gyɛ Heli mu‐bii.

eng: son of Matthat son of Levi son of Melchi son of Jannai son of Joseph.
local: Nɩɩ Heli mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Matati, nɩɩ Matati mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Lewi, nɩɩ Lewi mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Malikin, nɩɩ Maliki mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Gyanayi, Gyanayi mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Gyosefu kʋ.

eng: son of Mattathias son of Amos son of Nahum son of Esli son of Naggai.
local: Gyosefu nɩmʋ mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Matatiya, Matatiya mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Amosi, nɩɩ Amosi mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Nahum, nɩɩ Nahum mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Isili, nɩɩ Isili mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Nagayi.

eng: son of Maath son of Mattathias son of Semein son of Josech son of Joda.
local: Nɩɩ Nagayi mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Mati, nɩɩ Mati mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Matatiya, nɩɩ Matatiya mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Semen, nɩɩ Semen mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Gyoseki, nɩɩ Gyoseki mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Gyoda.

eng: son of Joanan son of Rhesa son of Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel son of Neri.
