local: Nɩɩ Gyoda mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Gyowanan, nɩɩ Gyowanan mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Resa, nɩɩ Resa mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Serubabeli, nɩɩ Serubabeli mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Selatiye, nɩɩ Selatiye mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Neri.

eng: son of Melchi son of Addi son of Cosam son of Elmadam son of Er.
local: Neri mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Maliki, nɩɩ Maliki mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Adi, nɩɩ Adi mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Kosam, nɩɩ Kosam mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Elimadam, nɩɩ Elimadam mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Eri.

eng: son of Joshua son of Eliezer son of Jorim son of Matthat son of Levi.
local: Nɩɩ Eri mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Gyosuwa, nɩɩ Gyosuwa mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Eliyasa, nɩɩ Eliyasa mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Gyorim, nɩɩ Gyorim mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Matati, nɩɩ Matati mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Lewi.

eng: son of Simeon son of Judah son of Joseph son of Jonam son of Eliakim.
local: Nɩɩ Lewi mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Simeyon, nɩɩ Semiyon mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Gyuda, nɩɩ Gyuda mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Gyosefu, nɩɩ Gyosefu mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Gyonam, nɩɩ Gyonam mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Eliyakim.

eng: son of Melea son of Menna son of Mattatha son of Nathan son of David.
local: Nɩɩ Eliyakim mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Meliya, nɩɩ Meliya mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Mena, nɩɩ Mena mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Matata, nɩɩ Matata mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Natan, nɩɩ Natan mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Dawidi.

eng: son of Jesse son of Obed son of Boaz son of Sala son of Nahshon.
local: Nɩɩ Dawidi mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Gyeese, nɩɩ Gyeese mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Obedi, Obedi mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Bowasi, nɩɩ Bowasi mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Salimon, nɩɩ Salimon mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Naason.

eng: son of Amminadab son of Admin son of Arni son of Hezron son of Perez son of Judah.
local: Nɩɩ Naason mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Aminadabi, nɩɩ Aminadabi mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Ram, nɩɩ Ram mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Hesiron, nɩɩ Hesiron mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Peresi, nɩɩ Peresi mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Gyuda.

eng: son of Jacob son of Isaac son of Abraham son of Terah son of Nahor.
local: Nɩɩ Gyuda mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Gyekobu, Gyekobu mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Isaki, Isaki mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Abraham, nɩɩ Abraham mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Tera, nɩɩ Tera mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Naho.

eng: son of Serug son of Reu son of Peleg son of Eber son of Shelah.
local: Nɩɩ Naho mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Serugi, nɩɩ Serugi mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Rewu, Rewu mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Pelegi, nɩɩ Pelegi mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Ebe, nɩɩ Ebe mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Sela.

eng: son of Cainan son of Arphaxad son of Shem son of Noah son of Lamech.
local: Nɩɩ Sela mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Kenan, nɩɩ Kenan mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Afahadi, nɩɩ Afahadi mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Sem, nɩɩ Sem mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Nowa, nɩɩ Nowa mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Lameki.

eng: son of Methuselah son of Enoch son of Jared son of Mahalalel son of Cainan.
local: Nɩɩ Lameki mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Metusala, Metusala mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Enoki, nɩɩ Enoki mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Gyaredi, nɩɩ Gyaredi mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Mahalale, nɩɩ Mahalale mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Keyinan.

eng: son of Enos son of Seth son of Adam son of God.
local: Nɩɩ Keyinan mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Enosi, nɩɩ Enosi mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Seti, nɩɩ Seti mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Adam. Nɩɩ Adam gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mu‐bii.

eng: Jesus returned from the Jordan River full of the Holy Spirit, and was led by the Spirit into the wilderness.
local: Yesu maa lɩɩ Gyɔdan ɔbʋn mʋ tɔ ba mʋ, yɛgɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ kɩ bɔla mʋ, nɩɩ Kayaayu maŋa yɛgɛ nɩɩ o kpee ofuli sʋ.

eng: There he was tempted for forty days by the devil. He ate nothing during those days and afterward Jesus was starving.
local: Maa bʋ ofuli mʋ sʋ nkɛ adɩna mʋ, nɩɩ Sɩtaanɛ ii kpee ɛɛ tʋʋ mʋ tɔ ɩɩ kɩɩ. Kanɩn nkɛ nɩmʋ kpini mʋ ɔ man gyii sɛhn, ɩmʋ sʋ laalaalʋwɛ mʋ akʋn a kɩtaa mʋ.

eng: The devil said to him, Since you are Gods Son, command this stone to become a loaf of bread.
local: Nɩɩ ɩ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Nɩɩ fʋ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mu‐bii kʋlʋn mʋ de, yɛgɛ kubuu nɩmʋ ki biliŋi bodobodo.

eng: Jesus replied, Its written, People wont live only by bread .
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ ɩɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɩ man gyɛ agyitɔ nkʋn sʋ nɩɩ nyɩmɩsa ii biti ɔ nyɛ nkpa. .

eng: Next the devil led him to a high place and showed him in a single instant all the kingdoms of the world.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ kpee kɩbɩɩ bʋnswɩɩsa kʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taa awuragyi mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ dulinyaa tɔ mʋ kpini kaapʋ mʋ opula tɔ.

eng: The devil said, I will give you this whole domain and the glory of all these kingdoms. Its been entrusted to me and I can give it to anyone I want.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ Yesu yɛ, N biti n sa fʋ awuragyi mʋ ɔlʋn yɛ ɩmʋ bʋnyaa lɩɩ atɔ nɩmʋ kpini sʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɩmʋ kpini ba taa waa mɛ abaa tɔ nɩn, nɩɩ n taalɛ taa sa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ n biti.

eng: Therefore, if you will worship me, it will all be yours.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ fʋ kan muŋa sun mɛ mʋ, ɩmʋ kpini ii biti i biliŋi fʋ lɛɛ.

eng: Jesus answered, Its written, You will worship the Lord your God and serve only him. .
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ ɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, Muŋa fʋ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ, nɩɩ mʋ nkʋn nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ fʋ maa sun. .

eng: The devil brought him into Jerusalem and stood him at the highest point of the temple. He said to him, Since you are Gods Son, throw yourself down from here.
local: Tʋtɔ ɔ nɩɩ bɩla kpiya mʋ Gyerusalem naa yɩlɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ ketin tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Nɩɩ fʋ kan fʋ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mu‐bii kʋlʋn mʋ, yɛgɛ fʋ n‐yɩɩ tɔ lɩɩ tʋtɔ ba tɩyɛ kasɛ.

eng: for its written: He will command his angels concerning you, to protect you.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ ɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti o sun mʋ nbɔɔ kɛ ba be kun fʋ.

eng: and they will take you up in their hands so that you wont hit your foot on a stone. .
local: Bee biti ba sɔgɩta fʋ, kɛ fʋ man tɩyɛ kasɛ kɛ fʋ ayaa a man da abuu.

eng: Jesus answered, Its been said, Dont test the Lord your God. .
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ ɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, Man kan tʋʋ fʋ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔ kɩɩ. .

eng: After finishing every temptation, the devil departed from him until the next opportunity.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tʋʋ mʋ tɔ ɔkpa kʋmaa sʋ kpɩnɛ mʋ, ɔ san yɛgɛ mʋ o gyoo saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ nyɛ mʋ.

eng: Jesus returned in the power of the Spirit to Galilee, and news about him spread throughout the whole countryside.
local: Lɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ ɔlʋn tɔ sʋ mʋ, Yesu bwii kpee Galile ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ, nɩɩ mʋ asɩn ɩ yalagɛ ɩsʋwʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ kyaabɔ tʋtɔ kpini.

eng: He taught in their synagogues and was praised by everyone.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ kaapʋ Abwaarɛsɩn Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɩgyaŋɛkpa, nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ yɩn mʋ.

eng: Jesus went to Nazareth, where he had been raised. On the Sabbath he went to the synagogue as he normally did and stood up to read.
local: Nɩɩ o kpee Nasareti katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba bɩla mʋ mʋ, nɩɩ kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ mʋ, o kpee Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ wʋla ɛɛ waa mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ kʋsʋ yɩlɛ kɛ ɔ kalɛ Abwaarɛsɩn.

eng: The synagogue assistant gave him the scroll from the prophet Isaiah. He unrolled the scroll and found the place where it was written.
local: Nɩɩ ba taa Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Isaya ɔwʋlɛ mʋ bɔɔta mʋ. Maa bugi tɔ mʋ, o biti wu nɩɩ ɔ kalɛ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ yɩla yɛ.

eng: The Spirit of the Lord is upon me, because the Lord has anointed me . He has sent me to preach good news to the poor, to proclaim release to the prisoners and recovery of sight to the blind, to liberate the oppressed.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ kɩ bʋ mɛ asɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ lɛɛ mɛ nɩn fɛɛ n tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ sa atiripu. O sun mɛ yɛ n ba tɔwɛ kamɔlɩgɛ asɩn sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba tii bamʋ ibu mʋ. O sun mɛ yɛ n ba bugi agyaatanapʋ ansi, kɛ bamʋ nɩɩ asa bee sisii bamʋ mʋ, bee biti ba nyɛ bamʋ n‐yɩɩ.

eng: and to proclaim the year of the Lords favor.
local: O sun mɛ yɛ n ba da kibonbon fɛɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kamɔlɩgɛ saŋa mʋ fʋʋ.

eng: He rolled up the scroll, gave it back to the synagogue assistant, and sat down. Every eye in the synagogue was fixed on him.
local: Yesu maa kalɛ lʋwɛ mʋ, ɔ bɔɔ ɔwʋlɛ mʋ bwiiyaa sa ɔmʋnɩ ɛɛ kɩɩ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa mʋ sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ naa kyɩna. Nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ ɔgyaŋɛkpa mʋ kpini ansi i yii mʋ sʋ.

eng: He began to explain to them, Today, this scripture has been fulfilled just as you heard it.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Ndaga Ɩbwaarɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa ɩnɩmʋ ɩ ba tɔ nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ mɛ asɩn fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fan nu n maa kalɛ mʋ.

eng: Everyone was raving about Jesus, so impressed were they by the gracious words flowing from his lips. They said, This is Josephs son, isnt it.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu kɔnɔ tɔ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ i kyinkyin bamʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ tʋtɔ mʋ kpini, nɩɩ ba yɩn mʋ. Nɩɩ ba taasɛ abaa yɛ, man gyɛ Gyosefu mu‐bii mʋ nɩn?.

eng: Then Jesus said to them, Undoubtedly, you will quote this saying to me: Doctor, heal yourself. Do here in your hometown what weve heard you did in Capernaum.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, N nyi fɛɛ fen biti fan bɔ mɛ kɩŋasan nɩmʋ yɛ Ɔkyɛpʋ, kyɛ fʋ n‐yɩɩ. Nɩɩ fen biti fan bɩla tɔwɛ yɛ, Atɔ mʋ nɩɩ an nu fɛɛ fʋ waa Kapanum kadɛ tɔ mʋ, waa ɩmʋ fʋ kadɛ tɔ nfɩɩ.

eng: He said, I assure you that no prophet is welcome in the prophets hometown.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ kʋmaa nɩmʋ man sʋ bɛɛrɛɛ mʋ ayɛ.

eng: And I can assure you that there were many widows in Israel during Elijahs time, when it didnt rain for three and a half years and there was a great food shortage in the land.
local: Nɩɩ n biti fan wu kesintin fɛɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Eligya saŋa mʋ, akulapu‐kyɩɩ ba kyɩna ba kyɔ Ɩsɩrayɩ ɔsʋwʋlɛ mʋ sʋ. Saŋa maŋa tɔ bʋlayɩ yɩlɛ nsu nsa yɛ ɩbwaayɩ isiye nɩɩ akʋn a ba ɔsʋwʋlɛ mʋ kpini sʋ.

eng: Yet Elijah was sent to none of them but only to a widow in the city of Zarephath in the region of Sidon.
local: Amaa Ɩbwaarɛ man sun Eligya bamʋ ɔkʋlʋn gbaa asɛ, amɔɔ okulapu‐kyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ Sarefati kadɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ Sidon kabaafʋn sʋ mʋ nkʋn asɛ.

eng: There were also many persons with skin diseases in Israel during the time of the prophet Elisha, but none of them were cleansed. Instead, Naaman the Syrian was cleansed.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Elisa saŋa mʋ, abootipu ba kyɔ Ɩsɩrayɩ ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ, amaa Elisa man kyɛ bamʋ ɔkʋ, amɔɔ ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Naaman mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ Kesiriya‐nyɩn mʋ nkʋn nɩɩ ɔ kyɛ.

eng: When they heard this, everyone in the synagogue was filled with anger.
local: Asa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ ɔgyaŋɛkpa tʋtɔ mʋ, ba maa nu asɩn nɩmʋ mʋ, nɩɩ agbʋ a kɩtaa bamʋ.

eng: They rose up and ran him out of town. They led him to the crest of the hill on which their town had been built so that they could throw him off the cliff.
local: Nɩɩ ba kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ Yesu lɛɛ kadɛ mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ba taa mʋ dii kpee kɩbɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ bamʋ kadɛ kɩ gyan sʋ mu kumu tɔ, paa fɛɛ ba yɛgɛ mʋ tʋʋ kɩbɩɩ maŋa ogee tɔ.

eng: But he passed through the crowd and went on his way.
local: Amaa ɔ dan natɛ bʋlɛ asa damantɛ mʋ nsana kyʋn.

eng: Jesus went down to the city of Capernaum in Galilee and taught the people each Sabbath.
local: Lɩɩ tʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Yesu kpee Kapanum kadɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ Galile ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ mʋ, o piili ɛɛ kaapʋ asa mʋ.

eng: They were amazed by his teaching because he delivered his message with authority.
local: Mʋ kɩkaapʋ mʋ ki kyinkyin bamʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɩ sʋ ɔlʋn.

eng: A man in the synagogue had the spirit of an unclean demon. He screamed.
local: Nɩɩ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa mʋ tɔ mʋ, ɔnyɩn kʋ kyɩna ɔ sʋ nbwɩɩ libi, nɩɩ kayaayu bʋ mʋ sʋ, maa wu Yesu mʋ, nɩɩ o kuusi kenken yɛ.

eng: Hey! What have you to do with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are. You are the holy one from God.
local: Ndo! Yesu Kanasareti‐nyɩn, mɩnɛ nɩɩ fii biti anɛ asɛ? Fʋ ba fʋ ba nyita anɛ nɩn? N nyi ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ fʋ gyɛ, fʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Nyɩmɩsa Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ.

eng: Silence! Jesus said, speaking harshly to the demon. Come out of him! The demon threw the man down before them, then came out of him without harming him.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu pʋntɛ kabwɩɩ libi mʋ sʋ yɛ, Laatɔ, kɛ fʋ lɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ kabwɩɩ libi mʋ taa kanyɩn mʋ tʋʋ kasɛ bamʋ ansi tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ mʋ tɔ kyʋn yɛgɛ ɔ man taalɛ waa mʋ sɛhn.

eng: They were all shaken and said to each other, What kind of word is this, that he can command unclean spirits with authority and power, and they leave.
local: Nɩɩ ɩ waa asa mʋ kpini kɩyan nɩɩ bɛɛ tɔwɛ abaa yɛ, Mɩnɛ kɩkaapʋ ndee? Ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ taa kɩnyɩn yɛ ɔlʋn ɛɛ pʋntɛ n‐yaayu libi sʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ lɩɩ asa tɔ.

eng: Reports about him spread everywhere in the surrounding region.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu n‐yɩɩ sʋ asɩn mʋ ɩ yɛlɩgɛ kyaabɔɔ ɔsʋwʋlɛ maŋa sʋ kpini.

eng: After leaving the synagogue, Jesus went home with Simon. Simons mother-in-law was sick with a high fever, and the family asked Jesus to help her.
local: Yesu maa lɩɩ Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa obu mʋ tɔ mʋ, o kpee Simon ayɛ. Yɛgɛ Simon mʋ‐saa kyɩɩsa dɛ ɛɛ lɔ kibirima bʋlʋnsa, nɩɩ ba kʋlɛ Yesu yɛ ɔ ba kɩɩ mʋ.

eng: He bent over her and spoke harshly to the fever, and it left her. She got up at once and served them.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ naa yɩlɛ mʋ asɛ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ kibirima mʋ yɛ kɩ lɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ kibirima mʋ kɩ yɛgɛ mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ kɩtaa bamʋ kɩfɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ kʋsʋ kɩtaa bamʋ kɩfɔ.

eng: When the sun was setting, everyone brought to Jesus relatives and acquaintances with all kinds of diseases. Placing his hands on each of them, he healed them.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ kyɔwɛ kpee ɔ naa tɩyɛ mʋ, asa mʋ ba taa alɔpʋ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ lɔ alɔ yiri‐yiri mʋ ba Yesu asɛ. Nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ abaa gyaga bamʋ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ ɔ kyɛ bamʋ kpini.

eng: Demons also came out of many people. They screamed, You are Gods Son. But he spoke harshly to them and wouldnt allow them to speak because they recognized that he was the Christ.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ bɩla gya nbwɩɩ libi lɩɩ asa gaalaagaa tɔ, yɛgɛ bee kuusi kenken bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Fʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Mu‐Bii mʋ. Amaa nɩɩ ɔ pʋntɛ bamʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ man yɛgɛ bamʋ ba tɔwɛ sɛhn, lɩɩ fɛɛ be nyi maa gyɛ Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ mʋ.

eng: When daybreak arrived, Jesus went to a deserted place. The crowds were looking for him. When they found him, they tried to keep him from leaving them.
local: Kayɩɩ maa kɛ mʋ Yesu kpee opula mʋ nɩɩ i du surum, nɩɩ asa mʋ ba lɩɩ ba naa bee biti mʋ. Ba maa ba wu mʋ mʋ, ba man bɩla bee biti fɛɛ ɔ lɩɩ bamʋ asɛ.

eng: But he said to them, I must preach the good news of Gods kingdom in other cities too, for this is why I was sent.
local: Amaa ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Ɩ dagaa fɛɛ n maa tɔwɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ sa ndɛ kʋ asa gbaa, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sun mɛ.

eng: So he continued preaching in the Judean synagogues.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu kyaga sʋ ɛɛ tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɩgyaŋɛkpa, mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ Gyudeya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ kpini.

eng: One day Jesus was standing beside Lake Gennesaret when the crowd pressed in around him to hear Gods word.
local: Kakaakʋ nɩɩ Yesu kpee ɔ yɩlɛ Kanesareti ɔbʋn mʋ nɩɩ ba bɩla bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ Galile mʋ ɔbʋn‐kaa. Nɩɩ asa ba naa kyaabɔɔ mʋ bee nin abaa kɛ ba naa nu abwaarɛsɩn.

eng: Jesus saw two boats sitting by the lake. The fishermen had gone ashore and were washing their nets.
local: Yesu wu ɩkʋlɩ ɩnyɔ ɩ maa dan kebunɔ maŋa yɛgɛ ɩkɩn akɩtapʋ mʋ bɛɛ fwɩɩ bamʋ asawu.

eng: Jesus boarded one of the boats, the one that belonged to Simon, then asked him to row out a little distance from the shore. Jesus sat down and taught the crowds from the boat.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu loo ɔkʋlɩ mʋ kʋkʋ, mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ Simon lɛɛ tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ o nin waa tɔ gbɛrɛɛ. Ɔkʋlɩ maŋa tɔ nɩɩ Yesu kyʋn kyɩna yɛgɛ ɛɛ tɔwɛ kʋbʋ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ sarɛ tʋtɔ mʋ abwaarɛsɩn.

eng: When he finished speaking to the crowds, he said to Simon, Row out farther, into the deep water, and drop your nets for a catch.
local: Yesu maa tɔwɛ abwaarɛsɩn mʋ lʋwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ Simon yɛ Palɛ fʋ ɔkʋlɩ mʋ kpee katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋnyɛ, kɛ faa fʋ tɔɔmaa ana mʋ fan tʋʋ fanɛ asawu kɩtaa ɩkɩn.

eng: Simon replied, Master, weve worked hard all night and caught nothing. But because you say so, Ill drop the nets.
local: Nɩɩ Simon lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Mɛ ɔbɩlɩsa, an tʋʋ nɩn halɩɩ ba kalayɩ, a man kɩtaa sɛhn, amaa nɩɩ fʋ tɔwɛ kanɩn bɩrɛ, n biti n tʋʋ.

eng: So they dropped the nets and their catch was so huge that their nets were splitting.
local: Nɩɩ ba maa tʋʋ asawu mʋ, ba kɩtaa ɩkɩn kɩbansa halɩɩ bamʋ asawu mʋ ɩ san gbaa kyatɛ.

eng: They signaled for their partners in the other boat to come and help them. They filled both boats so full that they were about to sink.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba da abaa tɩɩ bamʋ tɔɔmaa ana mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ ɔkʋlɩ nyɔsapʋ mʋ tɔ yɛ ba ba kyɛ bamʋ tɔ. Ɩkʋlɩ ɩnyɔ mʋ kpini yɛ ɩmaa ɩmʋ ɩkɩn mʋ ɩ san gbaa muuri.

eng: When Simon Peter saw the catch, he fell at Jesus knees and said, Leave me, Lord, for Im a sinner.
local: Simon Pita maa wu kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ waa mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kyʋn muŋa mu amulii sʋ Yesu ansi tɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, lɩɩ mɛ asɛ, n gyɛ alibi ɔwaapʋ nɩn.

eng: Peter and those with him were overcome with amazement because of the number of fish they caught.
local: Lɩɩ ɩkɩn damantɛ mʋ nɩɩ ba kɩtaa mʋ sʋ mʋ, i kyinkyin maa mʋ tɔmaa ana kpini.

eng: James and John, Zebedees sons, were Simons partners and they were amazed too. Jesus said to Simon, Dont be afraid. From now on, you will be fishing for people.
local: Ɩkɩn mʋ kɩkɩta mʋ gbaa ki kyinkyin Gyemisi maa Gyɔn, Sebedi mu‐bii ana mʋ nɩɩ ba maa Simon bee sun kusun mʋ. Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ Simon yɛ, Man waa kufuu, lɩɩ ndaga ɩ sʋ ii kpee mʋ, fii biti fʋ ba fɩɩ kɩtaa asa fɩɩ bɩya mɛ nɩn.

eng: As soon as they brought the boats to the shore, they left everything and followed Jesus.
local: Nɩɩ ba bɩɩtɛ ɩkʋlɩ mʋ ba dan kidenbi sʋ, nɩɩ ba yɛgɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ nɩɩ ba buu Yesu.

eng: Jesus was in one of the towns where there was also a man covered with a skin disease. When he saw Jesus, he fell on his face and begged, Lord, if you want, you can make me clean.
local: Yesu maa bʋ kadɛ kʋ sʋ mʋ, ɔnyɩn kʋ gbaa bʋ tʋtɔ nɩɩ kibootoo kɩtaa mʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋ kpini. Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ o wu Yesu mʋ, o be muŋa mu ansi tɔ kʋlɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, nɩɩ fii biti mʋ, fɩɩ taalɛ yɛgɛ mɛ n‐yɩɩ ɩ lɔŋɔ sʋ.

eng: Jesus reached out his hand, touched him, and said, I do want to. Be clean. Instantly, the skin disease left him.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɛɛŋɛ mʋ kɩbaa mata mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, N biti, lɔŋɔ sʋ. Nɩɩ opula maŋa tɔ kibootoo mʋ kɩ yɛgɛ mʋ.

eng: Jesus ordered him not to tell anyone. Instead, Jesus said, go and show yourself to the priest and make an offering for your cleansing, as Moses instructed. This will be a testimony to them.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu da ɔnyɩn mu kumu tɔ yɛ, Man kan waa tɔwɛ ɔkʋ, amaa taa fʋ n‐yɩɩ kaapʋ Ɩbwaarɛ oseepu mʋ, kɛ fʋ naa lɛɛ saraga fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Mosesi nbara mʋ ɩ kaapʋ mʋ, kɛ ɩ kaapʋ tɔ sa ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ fɛɛ fʋ n‐yɩɩ ɩ lɔŋɔ sʋ.

eng: News of him spread even more and huge crowds gathered to listen and to be healed from their illnesses.
local: Amaa Yesu n‐yɩɩ sʋ asɩn mʋ ɩ lɔŋɔ yalagɛ tɔ, nɩɩ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ asa damantɛ ba be nu mʋ asɛ, nɩɩ ɔ kyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ lɔ mʋ kpini.

eng: But Jesus would withdraw to deserted places for prayer.
local: Amaa saŋa kʋmaa Yesu ɩɩ lɩɩ mʋ nkʋn ee kpee ɛɛ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ, opula mʋ nɩɩ o du surum.

eng: One day when Jesus was teaching, Pharisees and legal experts were sitting nearby. They had come from every village in Galilee and Judea, and from Jerusalem. Now the power of the Lord was with Jesus to heal.
local: Kakaakʋ Yesu maa kaapʋ abwaarɛsɩn mʋ, Farasii abi yɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba lɩɩ Galile yɛ Gyudeya ɩsʋwʋlɛ sʋ yɛ Gyerusalem kadɛ tɔ mʋ ba tɛ tʋtɔ. Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔlɔn mʋ bʋ mʋ asɛ fɛɛ ɔ taa kyɛ alɔpʋ.

eng: Some men were bringing a man who was paralyzed, lying on a cot. They wanted to carry him in and place him before Jesus.
local: Nɩɩ anyɩn akʋ ba sʋla ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ mʋ n‐yɩɩ i wu nɩɩ ɔ dɛ kalan sʋ. Ba kpan bamʋ n‐yɩɩ ba taa mʋ loo kɩkpaara mʋ sʋ, naa yɩla Yesu ansi tɔ.

eng: but they couldnt reach him because of the crowd. So they took him up on the roof and lowered him—cot and all—through the roof tiles into the crowded room in front of Jesus.
local: Lɩɩ sakyɔ mʋ sʋ mʋ, ba man nyɛ ɔkpa ba taa mʋ loo kɩkpaara mʋ sʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ ba dii kpee obu mʋ sʋsʋ, katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba taa ɩsɛɛ nyalɛ munla mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ba foti ɔbɔ kpɩlɩgaa mʋ yɛgɛ ɔ dɛ mʋ kalan sʋ sakyɔ mʋ nsana yɩla Yesu ansi tɔ.

eng: When Jesus saw their faith, he said, Friend, your sins are forgiven.
local: Yesu maa wu kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ mʋ sʋ mʋ, ɔ tɔwɛ ɔnyɩn mʋ yɛ Mɛ kyɛmɩnɛ n taa fu alibi kɛɛ fu.

eng: The legal experts and Pharisees began to mutter among themselves, Who is this who insults God? Only God can forgive sins.
local: Nɩɩ Farasii abi yɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ mʋ ba piili bɛɛ fɛ nfɛɛrɛ bamʋ amu tɔ yɛ Anɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔsa nɩmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ tɔwɛ nbusaa asɩn kanɩn? Anɩmʋ ɩ taalɛ taa alibi kɛɛ tii Ɩbwaarɛ sʋ.

eng: Jesus recognized what they were discussing and responded, Why do you fill your minds with these questions?.
local: Yesu nyi nfɛɛrɛ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ fɛ, nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ bamʋ yɛ Mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fɛn fɛ falɛ nfɛɛrɛ fanɛ nkɔlɔ tɔ?.

eng: Which is easier—to say, Your sins are forgiven, or to say, Get up and walk?.
local: Ɩmʋ kɩmɔɔ ɩ bʋsɩn kɩtɔwɛ. N taa fʋ alibi kɛɛ fʋ, abɛɛ kʋsʋ natɛ?.

eng: But so that you will know that the Human Onehas authority on the earth to forgive sins—Jesus now spoke to the man who was paralyzed,I say to you, get up, take your cot, and go home.
local: N biti fan bɩɩ fɛɛ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa n sʋ ɔlʋn dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ nɩɩ n taalɛ taa alibi kɛɛ. Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔ tɔwɛ ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ mʋ n‐yɩɩ i wu mʋ yɛ, N tɔwɛ fʋ yɛ, Kʋsʋ! Taa fʋ kalan kpewu.

eng: Right away, the man stood before them, picked up his cot, and went home, praising God.
local: Opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ ɔ kʋsʋ yɩlɛ bamʋ ansi tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ kalan nɩɩ ɔ dɛ sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɛɛ yɩn Ɩbwaarɛ ee kpewu.

eng: All the people were beside themselves with wonder. Filled with awe, they glorified God, saying, Weve seen unimaginable things today.
local: Nɩɩ i kyinkyin ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ, nɩɩ ba yɩn Ɩbwaarɛ, nɩɩ ɩ kɩtaa bamʋ kufuu nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, Ndaga an wu asɩn kyinkyinsa.

eng: Afterward, Jesus went out and saw a tax collector named Levi sitting at a kiosk for collecting taxes. Jesus said to him, Follow me.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ kamaa mʋ, Yesu lɩɩ nɩɩ ɔ naa wu lenpoo ɔkɔɔlɛpʋ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Lewi mu kusun obu to. Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Kʋsʋ buu mɛ.

eng: Levi got up, left everything behind, and followed him.
local: Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ kʋsʋ yɛgɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩɩ o buu Yesu.

eng: Then Levi threw a great banquet for Jesus in his home. A large number of tax collectors and others sat down to eat with them.
local: Nɩɩ Lewi dɩŋa agyitɔ kʋkyɔ mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ sa Yesu, nɩɩ lenpoo akɔɔlɛpʋ gaalaagaa yɛ asa akʋ ba bʋga tʋtɔ, nɩɩ ba maa Yesu bee gyii.

eng: The Pharisees and their legal experts grumbled against his disciples. They said, Why do you eat and drink with tax collectors and sinners.
local: Amaa nɩɩ Farasii abi mʋ yɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ mʋ nɩɩ bamʋ gbaa bamʋ gyɛ Farasii abi mʋ bɛɛ kɔlɩtɛ bɛɛ sa Yesu abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ, Mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fanaa lenpoo akɔɔlɛpʋ yɛ alibi awaapʋ fen bʋga fen gyii fen nuu kanɩn.

eng: Jesus answered, Healthy people dont need a doctor, but sick people do.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɔkyɛpʋ man tiri sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ alanfɩya, amɔɔ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ lɔ mʋ nkʋn.

eng: I didnt come to call righteous people but sinners to change their hearts and lives.
local: N man ba yɛ n ba tɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba mʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi nɩn. Amaa n ba nɩn yɛ n ba tɩɩ alibi awaapʋ kɛ ba kyɛɛgɛ bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ ba Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ.

eng: Some people said to Jesus, The disciples of John fast often and pray frequently. The disciples of the Pharisees do the same, but your disciples are always eating and drinking.
local: Nɩɩ asa akʋ ba tɔwɛ Yesu yɛ, Gyɔn okyugyeepu abɩɩlapʋ bee ŋmina kɔnɔ yɛgɛ bɛɛ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ saŋa kʋmaa, kanɩn nɩɩ Farasii abi abɩɩlapʋ mʋ gbaa bɛɛ waa nɩn. Amaa fɛɛ fʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ bɩrɛ ba dan bee gyii bee nuu nɩn saŋa kʋmaa.

eng: Jesus replied, You cant make the wedding guests fast while the groom is with them, can you?.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Fɩɩ taalɛ yɛgɛ kɩkyɩɩfɔ tɔ afɔ ba ŋmina kɔnɔ yɛgɛ ɔkyɩɩfɔ mu‐kuli bʋ bamʋ asɛ?.

eng: The days will come when the groom will be taken from them, and then they will fast.
local: Amaa saŋa kʋ ii biti ɔ ba, kɛ ba taa ɔkyɩɩfɔ mu‐kuli lɩɩ bamʋ asɛ, kanɩn nkɛ maŋa tɔ bɩrɛ bee biti ba ŋmina kɔnɔ.

eng: Then he told them a parable. No one tears a patch from a new garment to patch an old garment. Otherwise, the new garment would be ruined, and the new patch wouldnt match the old garment.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu bɔ bamʋ kɩŋasan kɩnɩmʋ yɛ, Ɔkʋ mɛɛ tɩn kikyibi pʋpwɛ naa baata mata kaalɛ dɩdaa ɔbɔ. Nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan waa kanɩn mʋ, ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ kikyibi pʋpwɛ mʋ ɩ kyatɛ kaalɛ dɩdaa mʋ i tii sʋ nɩn, nɩɩ kikyibi dɩdaa mʋ yɛ kɩpʋpwɛ mʋ ɩ mɛɛ yɛ ɩ baa i duli abaa.

eng: Nobody pours new wine into old wineskins. If they did, the new wine would burst the wineskins, the wine would spill, and the wineskins would be ruined.
local: Kanɩn nɩɩ ɔkʋ mɛɛ taa ɔwʋlɛ dɩdaa ee sun kʋkɔrɛ pʋpwɛ. Nɩɩ ɔ kan waa kanɩn bɩrɛ, ɔwʋlɛ dɩdaa mʋ ii biti ɔ kyatɛ, nɩɩ ɩ mɛɛ yɛ ɩ yɛgɛ kʋkɔrɛ pʋpwɛ mʋ kɩ waa ɔdan.

eng: Instead, new wine must be put into new wineskins.
local: Kanɩn sʋ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ ɔwʋlɛ pʋpwɛ nɩɩ bɛɛ sʋ bee sun kʋkɔrɛ pʋpwɛ.

eng: No one who drinks a well-aged wine wants new wine, but says, The well-aged wine is better.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lan kʋkɔrɛ dɩdaa nɩɩ ɩ dɩn mʋ mʋ, ɔ man bɩla ee biti kʋkɔrɛ pʋpwɛ kɩlan. Ee biti ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Kʋkɔrɛ dɩdaa mʋ kɩ bɔɔ mʋ. .

eng: One Sabbath, as Jesus was going through the wheat fields, his disciples were picking the heads of wheat, rubbing them in their hands, and eating them.
local: Kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ kʋ nɩɩ Yesu naa ayu kʋdɔɔ kʋ tɔ, nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba tɩŋɛ ayu mʋ pʋsa bamʋ abaa tɔ wɛ.

eng: Some Pharisees said, Why are you breaking the Sabbath law.
local: Nɩɩ Farasii abi mʋ akʋ ba taasɛ yɛ, Mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fan waa kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ ikisii.

eng: Jesus replied, Havent you read what David and his companions did when they were hungry?.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Fan man pɩɩta kalɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ, kɛ fan wu kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Dawidi waa saŋa mʋ nɩɩ akʋn a mɔɔ maa mʋ asa mʋ?.

eng: He broke the Law by going into Gods house and eating the bread of the presence, which only the priests can eat. He also gave some of the bread to his companions.
local: Fan man wu kanan mʋ nɩɩ o loo kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa obu to naa taa saraga bodobodo mʋ wɛ, nɩɩ ɔ taa ɩkʋ sa mʋ asa gbaa nɩɩ ba wɛ, yɛgɛ ɩ man gyɛ anɛ nbara sʋ fɛɛ ɔkʋ o gyii ɩmʋ, amɔɔ Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu nkʋn.

eng: Then he said to them, The Human Oneis Lord of the Sabbath.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu bɩla tɔwɛ yɛ, Mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa gbaa n gyɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ sa kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa mʋ.

eng: On another Sabbath, Jesus entered a synagogue to teach. A man was there whose right hand was withered.
local: Kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ kʋ biyo Yesu bɩla kpee Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa ɛɛ kaapʋ. Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn kʋ bʋ tʋtɔ nɩɩ mʋ gyisa kɩbaa ki wu.

eng: The legal experts and the Pharisees were watching him closely to see if he would heal on the Sabbath. They were looking for a reason to bring charges against him.
local: Farasii abi mʋ yɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ mʋ bee biti kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba waa kɛ ba bun Yesu kʋpwɛ. Ɩmʋ sʋ ba tɛ ba gyoo bɛɛ kɩɩ fɛɛ Yesu ii biti ɔ kyɛ mʋ kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ mʋ.

eng: Jesus knew their thoughts, so he said to the man with the withered hand, Get up and stand in front of everyone. He got up and stood there.
local: Amaa Yesu wʋla o n‐yɩɩ bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ fɛ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ mʋ kɩbaa ki wu mʋ yɛ, Kʋsʋ yɩlɛ asa mʋ kpini ansi tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ kʋsʋ yɩlɛ.

eng: Jesus said to the legal experts and Pharisees, Heres a question for you: Is it legal on the Sabbath to do good or to do evil, to save life or to destroy it.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, N taasɛ fanɛ, mɩnɛ kʋtɔ nɩɩ anɛ nbara mʋ ɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ a waa kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ? A waa adanbɩrasa abɛɛ alibi? A mɔlɩgɛ asa nkpa abɛɛ a nyita ɩmʋ.

eng: Looking around at them all, he said to the man, Stretch out your hand. So he did and his hand was made healthy.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ kɩɩ bamʋ kpini kyaabɔɔ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ ɔnyɩn mʋ yɛ, Tɛɛŋɛ fʋ kɩbaa. Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ tɛɛŋɛ mʋ kɩbaa, nɩɩ mʋ kɩbaa mʋ kɩ nyɛ ɔlʋn.

eng: They were furious and began talking with each other about what to do to Jesus.
local: Amaa agbʋ a kɩtaa bamʋ, nɩɩ ba piili bɛɛ bee ŋmina kanan mʋ nɩɩ bee biti ba waa Yesu.

eng: During that time, Jesus went out to the mountain to pray, and he prayed to God all night long.
local: Nkɛ maŋa tɔ mʋ, Yesu kpee abɩɩ tɔ ɔ naa kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ, nɩɩ kagyanbwɛ maŋa kpini mʋ, ɔ bʋ tʋtɔ ɛɛ kʋlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: At daybreak, he called together his disciples. He chose twelve of them whom he called apostles.
local: Nɩɩ kayɩɩ kɩ maa kɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ kpini ba mʋ asɛ, nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ bamʋ tɔ kudu anyɔ, nɩɩ ɔ yɩla bamʋ kɩtɩɩ yɛ asunpu.

eng: Simon, whom he named Peter; his brother Andrew; James; John; Philip; Bartholomew.
local: Bamʋ ɩ gyɛ Simon , yɛ mu‐supu Andiruwu, yɛ Gyemisi maa Gyɔn, yɛ Filipu maa Batolome.

eng: Matthew; Thomas; James the son of Alphaeus; Simon, who was called a zealot.
local: yɛ Matiwu maa Tomasi, yɛ Alifeyo mu‐bii Gyemisi, yɛ Simon mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ Seloti.

eng: Judas the son of James; and Judas Iscariot, who became a traitor.
local: yɛ Gyemisi mu‐bii Gyudasi, yɛ Gyudasi Isikariyoti mʋ nɩɩ ɔ ba lɛɛ Yesu sa mʋ.

eng: Jesus came down from the mountain with them and stood on a large area of level ground. A great company of his disciples and a huge crowd of people from all around Judea and Jerusalem and the area around Tyre and Sidon joined him there.
local: Yesu maa mu asunpu ba kpɩlɩgɛ kɩbɩɩ mʋ sʋ ba kipentenpiren kʋ sʋ. Nɩɩ abɩɩlapʋ gaalaagaa ba be tu mʋ tʋtɔ, nɩɩ asa damantɛ ba lɩɩ Gyudeya yɛ Gyerusalem yɛ ndɛ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ Tayi maa Sidon ɔpʋ nbunɔ mʋ kpini.

eng: They came to hear him and to be healed from their diseases, and those bothered by unclean spirits were healed.
local: Ba ba nu mʋ asɛ kɛ ba nyɛ bamʋ alɔ kɩ kyɛ. Bamʋ nɩɩ n‐yaayu libi bɛɛ tɔɔrɔɔ bamʋ ba nyɛ bamʋ n‐yɩɩ.

eng: The whole crowd wanted to touch him, because power was going out from him and he was healing everyone.
local: Nɩɩ asa mʋ kpini ba kpan bamʋ n‐yɩɩ fɛɛ ba mata mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔlʋn kʋ bʋ mʋ tɔ nɩɩ ɩ lɩɩ ɩ kyɛ bamʋ kpini alɔ.

eng: Jesus raised his eyes to his disciples and said: Happy are you who are poor, because Gods kingdom is yours.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu kɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Amu ɔkɔn wuya ana ɩ gyɛ fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fan gyɛ atiripu, lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ kɩ gyɛ fanɛ lɛɛ.

eng: Happy are you who hunger now, because you will be satisfied. Happy are you who weep now, because you will laugh.
local: Amu ɔkɔn wuya ana ɩ gyɛ fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ akʋn a mɔɔ fanɛ nbɩɩnbɩɩ nɩmʋ mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ yɛgɛ fan ŋmɛ. Amu ɔkɔn wuya ana ɩ gyɛ fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fen su nbɩɩnbɩɩ, lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ yɛgɛ fan ŋmasɛ.

eng: Happy are you when people hate you, reject you, insult you, and condemn your name as evil because of the Human One.
local: Amu ɔkɔn wuya ana ɩ gyɛ fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ asa bee kisi fanɛ, bee kina fanɛ, bɛɛ saalɛ fanɛ, nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩn antɩŋɛsa libi bɛɛ gyaga fanɛ sʋ, lɩɩ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ sʋ.

eng: Rejoice when that happens! Leap for joy because you have a great reward in heaven. Their ancestors did the same things to the prophets.
local: Nɩɩ ba kan waa fanɛ kanɩn mʋ fan yɛgɛ ɩ waa fanɛ ɔkɔn, kɛ fan taa kensi‐gyii kyaa, lɩɩ fɛɛ kɛkɛɛ lala kɩ dɛ sʋsʋ kii gyoo fanɛ. Kpalɩ fɛɛ kanɩn nɩɩ bamʋ naana ana gbaa ba taa waa Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ mʋ.

eng: But how terrible for you who are rich, because you have already received your comfort.
local: Amaa fanɛ atɔ wuya ana, fanɛ laakʋ, fɛɛ fan maa wʋla nyɛ fanɛ ɔkɔn dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ nbɩɩnbɩɩ nɩmʋ.

eng: How terrible for you who have plenty now, because you will be hungry. How terrible for you who laugh now, because you will mourn and weep.
local: Fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fan gyii ŋmɛ nbɩɩnbɩɩ nɩmʋ falɛ mɔɔ, fanɛ laakʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ akʋn ii biti ɩɩ tɩyɛ fanɛ sʋ. Fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fɛn ŋmasɛ nbɩɩnbɩɩ falɛ mɔɔ mʋ, fanɛ laakʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ fen biti fan su kɛ fan bɔɔta tɔ.

eng: How terrible for you when all speak well of you. Their ancestors did the same things to the false prophets.
local: Nɩɩ fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ asa bɛɛ tɔwɛ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ, fanɛ laakʋ. Kanɩn nɩɩ bamʋ naana ana ba tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa lɩɩ atɔwɛpʋ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩn atɩŋɛsa bɛɛ gyaga Ɩbwaarɛ sʋ mʋ.

eng: But I say to you who are willing to hear: Love your enemies. Do good to those who hate you.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu yɛ, Amaa n tɔwɛ fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fen nu mɛ asɛ yɛ, fan biti fanɛ dʋn ana, kɛ fan waa adanbɩrasa sa fanɛ akisipu ana.

eng: Bless those who curse you. Pray for those who mistreat you.
local: Kɛ fan waa bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɔwɛ fanɛ nnɔ mʋ kusee, kɛ fan kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ sa bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ nyanla fanɛ.

eng: If someone slaps you on the cheek, offer the other one as well. If someone takes your coat, dont withhold your shirt either.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan da fʋ ɔtan mʋ, biliŋi kɩnyɔsapʋ mʋ sa mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan kɔɔlɛ fʋ balɩmaasʋ mʋ, yɛgɛ ɔ taa fʋ kaalɛ tii sʋ.

eng: Give to everyone who asks and dont demand your things back from those who take them.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan ba kʋlɛ fʋ kʋtɔ kʋ mʋ, sa mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan taa fʋ kʋtɔ kʋ mʋ, man bɩla naa taasɛ kʋtɔ maŋa sʋ lɩɩ odunwura mʋ asɛ.

eng: Treat people in the same way that you want them to treat you.
local: Kɛ fan waa fanɛ tɔɔmaa ana fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fen biti fɛɛ ba waa sa fanɛ mʋ.

eng: If you love those who love you, why should you be commended? Even sinners love those who love them.
local: Fɛɛ nɩɩ fan kan fen biti bamʋ nɩɩ bee biti fanɛ nkʋn mʋ, mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ bɩla yɩn fanɛ? Halɩɩ alibi awaapʋ gbaa bee biti bamʋ nɩɩ bee biti bamʋ mʋ.

eng: If you do good to those who do good to you, why should you be commended? Even sinners do that.
local: Nɩɩ fan kan waa kɩdanbɩrasa sa bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa fanɛ adanbɩrasa nkʋn mʋ, mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ bɩla yɩn fanɛ? Alibi awaapʋ gbaa bɛɛ waa kanɩn.

eng: If you lend to those from whom you expect repayment, why should you be commended? Even sinners lend to sinners expecting to be paid back in full.
local: Nɩɩ fan kan fɛn paala bamʋ nɩɩ fan kɔɔlɛ gyii fɛɛ bee biti ba ba nyɛ ka fanɛ mʋ, mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ bɩla yɩn fanɛ? Halɩɩ alibi awaapʋ gbaa bɛɛ paala bamʋ tɔmaa ana kɛ bee bwii ka bamʋ afulee maŋa kɔnɔ kyɛɛkyɛɛ.

eng: Instead, love your enemies, do good, and lend expecting nothing in return. If you do, you will have a great reward. You will be acting the way children of the Most High act, for he is kind to ungrateful and wicked people.
local: Amaa fan biti fanɛ dʋn ana kɛ fan waa bamʋ adanbɩrasa. Nɩɩ fan kan paala bamʋ mʋ, fan man fɛ ɩmʋ kɩba ka nfɛɛrɛ. Nɩɩ fɛn waa kanɩn mʋ, fen biti fan nyɛ kɛkɛɛ lala, kɛ fen biti fan baa fan gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Owura lala mʋ nbii ana. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ sʋ kebiti sa asa, nɩɩ ɛɛ waa kɩdanbɩrasa ɛɛ sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba mɛɛ fwaala, nɩɩ ba gyɛ amu tɔ ɔlʋn wuya ana.

eng: Be compassionate just as your Father is compassionate.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan wu asa kʋwɛɛ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fanɛ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gbaa ii wu asa kʋwɛɛ mʋ.

eng: Dont judge, and you wont be judged. Dont condemn, and you wont be condemned. Forgive, and you will be forgiven.
local: Fan man talɛ fanɛ tɔɔmaa ana, kɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gbaa ɔ man talɛ fanɛ. Fan man bun asa kʋpwɛ, kɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gbaa ɔ man bun fanɛ kʋpwɛ. Fan taa kɛɛ abaa, kɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gbaa ɔ taa kɛɛ fanɛ.

eng: Give, and it will be given to you. A good portion—packed down, firmly shaken, and overflowing—will fall into your lap. The portion you give will determine the portion you receive in return.
local: Fan sa, kɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gbaa ɔ sa fanɛ. Ee biti ɔ kaala, bɔla kʋtɔ kaalasa mʋ, kɛ ɔ nyɛɛ sʋ, kɛ o gyingyen kɩmʋ, kɛ ɩ bɔla wʋrɩgɛ fanɛ abaa tɔ. Kʋtɔ kaalasa mʋ nɩɩ fan taa kaala mʋ, kɩmʋ maa nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ taa kaala sa fanɛ.

eng: Jesus also told them a riddle. A blind person cant lead another blind person, right? Wont they both fall into a ditch?.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu bɔ bamʋ kɩŋasan nɩmʋ yɛ, Gyaatanapʋ ɩ taalɛ gyankpaa mʋ tɔɔmaa gyaatanapʋ ɔkpa? Nɩɩ ɩ kan ba kanɩn mʋ, bamʋ asa anyɔ kpini bee biti ba naa lɩɩ tɩyɛ kɩkyabɔ tɔ.

eng: Disciples arent greater than their teacher, but whoever is fully prepared will be like their teacher.
local: Nɩɩ ɔbɩɩlapʋ mɛɛ taalɛ ŋara mʋ ɔkaapʋpʋ, amaa nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bɩla bɩɩ bɩrɛ ɛɛ taalɛ waa fɛɛ mʋ ɔkaapʋpʋ.

eng: Why do you see the splinter in your brothers or sisters eye but dont notice the log in your own eye?.
local: Mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fu wu keyuubi kɩ maa bʋ fʋ tɔɔmaa kensi tɔ, nɩɩ fu mee wu kagyapoti mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ fʋ lɛɛ tɔ?.

eng: How can you say to your brother or sister, Brother, Sister, let me take the splinter out of your eye, when you dont see the log in your own eye? You deceive yourselves! First take the log out of your eye, and then you will see clearly to take the splinter out of your brothers or sisters eye.
local: Mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ fɩɩ taalɛ tɔwɛ fʋ tɔɔmaa yɛ, Mɛ tɔɔmaa, yɛgɛ n lɛɛ keyuubi mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ fu kensi tɔ falɛ! Yɛgɛ fʋ mɛɛ kɩɩ kagyapoti mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ fʋ gbaa‐gbaa fu kensi tɔ? Fʋ kebunbun ansi wuya, gyankpaa lɛɛ kagyapoti mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ fʋ kensi tɔ, pɔyɩ kɛ fʋ taalɛ kɩɩ danbɩrasa lɛɛ keyuubi mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ fʋ tɔɔmaa lɛɛ tɔ.

eng: A good tree doesnt produce bad fruit, nor does a bad tree produce good fruit.
local: Nɩɩ oyu danbɩrasa kʋ man bʋ tɔ kɩ sɔyɩ abi libi, abɛɛ oyu libi mɛɛ sɔyɩ abi danbɩrasa.

eng: Each tree is known by its own fruit. People dont gather figs from thorny plants, nor do they pick grapes from prickly bushes.
local: Oyu kʋmaa nɩmʋ kɩmʋ abi sʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ sʋ bɛɛ bɩɩ kɩmʋ. Nɩɩ ɔkʋ mɛɛ tɩyɛ akutu ɛɛ lɩɩ kɩkpalɛ oyu sʋ, abɛɛ kididobi lɩɩ kimango oyu sʋ.

eng: A good person produces good from the good treasury of the inner self, while an evil person produces evil from the evil treasury of the inner self. The inner self overflows with words that are spoken.
local: Nyɩmɩsa danbɩrasa ɩɩ waa atɔ danbɩrasa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kʋʋla yɩla mʋ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ, nɩɩ nyɩmɩsa libi mɔɔ ɩɩ waa atɔ libi mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kʋʋla yɩla mʋ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ nyɩmɩsa kɔnɔ ɩɩ tɔwɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ mʋ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ nɩn.

eng: Why do you call me Lord, Lord and dont do what I say?.
local: Mɩnɛ ɩ waa sʋ nɩɩ fan tɩɩ mɛ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, yɛgɛ fan mɛɛ waa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ fan waa?.

eng: Ill show what its like when someone comes to me, hears my words, and puts them into practice.
local: Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ ba mɛ asɛ, ba nu mɛ asɩn, nɩɩ ɔ waa ɩmʋ mʋ, n biti n kaapʋ fanɛ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ o duli.

eng: Its like a person building a house by digging deep and laying the foundation on bedrock. When the flood came, the rising water smashed against that house, but the water couldnt shake the house because it was well built.
local: Kanɩn ɔsa maŋa du nɩn fɛɛ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ pɔyɩ kɩkpaara, nɩɩ o kuu loo kasɛ nɩɩ ɔ pɔyɩ ɔpɔrɛ gyankpaasa mʋ gyaga kɩfʋlɛ sʋ. Nɩɩ nkyu n bɔla nɩɩ ɩ da kɩkpaara mʋ, amaa kɩ man gyingyen lɩɩ fɛɛ maa pɔyɩ kɩmʋ danbɩrasa mʋ sʋ.

eng: But those who dont put into practice what they hear are like a person who built a house without a foundation. The floodwater smashed against it and it collapsed instantly. It was completely destroyed.
local: Amaa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o nu mɛ asɩn, nɩɩ ɔ man buu ɩmʋ mʋ, o du nɩn fɛɛ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man kuu ɔbɔ pɔyɩ nɩɩ ɔ pɔ́yɩ́ kɩkpaara mʋ gyaga kusuupu sʋ. Nɩɩ nkyu maa bɔla da kɩkpaara mʋ, nɩɩ kɩ lɩɩ tɩyɛ yaasɛ tɔ kpini‐kpini.

eng: After Jesus finished presenting all his words among the people, he entered Capernaum.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ ɩnɩmʋ lʋwɛ, nɩɩ asa ba nu mʋ katɔwɛ mʋ, o kpee Kapanum kadɛ tɔ.

eng: A centurion had a servant who was very important to him, but the servant was ill and about to die.
local: Roma anaakɔpʋ kɩlɩfa ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ kʋ kyɩna ɔ bʋ tʋtɔ, nɩɩ ɔ sʋ kɩyaafɔlɛ kʋ mʋ nɩɩ ee biti mʋ asɩn, nɩɩ ɛɛ lɔ nɩɩ ee biti o wu.

eng: When the centurion heard about Jesus, he sent some Jewish elders to Jesus to ask him to come and heal his servant.
local: Ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ mʋ maa nu Yesu sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ o sun Gyiwu abi abɩlɩsa akʋ mʋ asɛ, yɛ ɔ ba kyɛ mʋ kɩyaafɔlɛ sa mʋ.

eng: When they came to Jesus, they earnestly pleaded with Jesus. He deserves to have you do this for him, they said.
local: Ba maa ba Yesu asɛ mʋ, ba dan baasɛ kʋlɛ mʋ gaa tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ dagaa fɛɛ fʋ maa kyɛ mʋ tɔ.

eng: He loves our people and he built our synagogue for us.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ sʋ kebiti sa anɛ Gyiwu abi, halɩɩ ɔ pɔyɩ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa sa anɛ.

eng: Jesus went with them. He had almost reached the house when the centurion sent friends to say to Jesus, Lord, dont be bothered. I dont deserve to have you come under my roof.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ Yesu buu bamʋ ee kpee tʋtɔ. Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ o tiri kɩkpaara mʋ tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ Roma anaakɔpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ sun mʋ kyɛmɩnɛ ana ba naa tɔwɛ Yesu yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ man nyanla fʋ n‐yɩɩ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ n man dagaa fɛɛ fʋ ba mɛ kɩkpaara sʋ.

eng: In fact, I didnt even consider myself worthy to come to you. Just say the word and my servant will be healed.
local: Kanɩn gbaa nɩɩ n wu fɛɛ n man dagaa fɛɛ n ba fʋ asɛ. Fɛɛ fʋ dan sa kɔnɔ mɛ kɩyaafɔlɛ ii biti ɔ nyɛ alanfɩya.

eng: Im also a man appointed under authority, with soldiers under me. I say to one, Go, and he goes, and to another, Come, and he comes. I say to my servant, Do this, and the servant does it.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ngbaa mɛ n bʋ ɔbɩlɩsa ɔkʋ kasɛ, nɩɩ anaakɔpʋ ɔkʋ gbaa ba bʋ mɛ kasɛ. Nɩɩ n tɔwɛ nɩmʋ yɛ, Kpe nfɩɩ nɩɩ ee kpee, nɩɩ n bɩla tɔwɛ ɔnɩmʋ yɛ, Ba nfɩɩ, nɩɩ ɛɛ ba, nɩɩ n tɔwɛ mɛ kɩyaafɔlɛ yɛ, Waa falɛ, nɩɩ ɛɛ waa.

eng: When Jesus heard these words, he was impressed with the centurion. He turned to the crowd following him and said, I tell you, even in Israel I havent found faith like this.
local: Yesu maa nu kanɩn mʋ, i kyinkyin mʋ, nɩɩ o biliŋi mu ansi kɩɩ kʋbʋ damantɛ mʋ nɩɩ ba buu mʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin fɛɛ, Ɩsɩrayɩ abi kpini tɔ mʋ, n man pɩɩta wu ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ falɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii damantɛ nɩmʋ kedubi.

eng: When the centurions friends returned to his house, they found the servant restored to health.
local: Nbɔɔ mʋ nɩɩ o sun mʋ ba maa bwii kpewu mʋ, ba naa tu kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ maa nyɛ alanfɩya.

eng: A little later Jesus went to a city called Nain. His disciples and a great crowd traveled with him.
local: Ɩmʋ kamaa mʋ Yesu lɩɩ tʋtɔ kpee kadɛ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ kɩmʋ yɛ Nayin, nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ yɛ asa damantɛ ba buu mʋ.

eng: As he approached the city gate, a dead man was being carried out. He was his mothers only son, and she was a widow. A large crowd from the city was with her.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ o tiri kadɛ mʋ nbwama mʋ, o wu ba maa sʋ obuni ba lɩɩ kadɛ mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ obuni maŋa gyɛ okulapu‐kyɩɩ kebii‐nyɩnsa kʋlʋn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kʋʋgɛ. Nɩɩ asa damantɛ mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ kadɛ mʋ tɔ ba buu okulapu‐kyɩɩ mʋ.

eng: When he saw her, the Lord had compassion for her and said, Dont cry.
local: Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu maa wu okulapu‐kyɩɩ mʋ, mʋ asɩn a kɩtaa mʋ kʋwɛɛ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Man bɩla su.

eng: He stepped forward and touched the stretcher on which the dead man was being carried. Those carrying him stood still. Jesus said, Young man, I say to you, get up.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu taa mʋ kɩbaa naa mata kɩdakaa mʋ, nɩɩ asʋlapʋ mʋ ba sii ba yɩlɛ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Kɩyaafɔlɛ! N tɔwɛ fʋ yɛ, kʋsʋ.

eng: The dead man sat up and began to speak, and Jesus gave him to his mother.
local: Nɩɩ olewupu mʋ kʋsʋ kyɩna, nɩɩ o piili ɛɛ tɔwɛ, nɩɩ Yesu taa mʋ sa mu‐nyi.

eng: Awestruck, everyone praised God. A great prophet has appeared among us, they said. God has come to help his people.
local: Nɩɩ i kyinkyin bamʋ kpini nɩɩ ba yɩn Ɩbwaarɛ. Nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ lala kʋ lɩɩ anɛ sʋ. Ɩbwaarɛ ba ɔ ba kyɛ mʋ asa tɔ.

eng: This news about Jesus spread throughout Judea and the surrounding region.
local: Yesu asɩn nɩmʋ ɩ yalagɛ Gyudeya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ, yɛ ɩsʋwʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dɛ ɩ maa kɩmʋ mʋ.

eng: Johns disciples informed him about all these things. John called two of his disciples.
local: Nɩɩ Gyɔn abɩɩlapʋ ba naa tɔwɛ mʋ Yesu awaasa mʋ kpini. Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔ tɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ anyɔ.

eng: and sent them to the Lord. They were to ask him, Are you the one who is coming, or should we look for someone else.
local: nɩɩ o sun bamʋ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu asɛ yɛ ba naa taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Fʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɔmʋ nɩɩ, Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ ee biti ɔ ba mʋ abɛɛ a gyoo kɩɩ ɔkʋ ɔkpa.

eng: When they reached Jesus, they said, John the Baptist sent us to you. He asks, Are you the one who is coming, or should we look for someone else.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ anyɩn mʋ ba ba Yesu asɛ mʋ ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Gyɔn okyugyeepu i sun anɛ fʋ asɛ yɛ abaa taasɛ fʋ yɛ, Fʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ ee biti ɔ ba mʋ abɛɛ a gyoo kɩɩ ɔkʋ ɔkpa.

eng: Right then, Jesus healed many of their diseases, illnesses, and evil spirits, and he gave sight to a number of blind people.
local: Kanɩn saŋa maa tɔ mʋ Yesu kyɛ asa gaalaagaa alobi, nɩɩ ɔ gya nbwɩɩ libi lɩɩ asa tɔ, nɩɩ o bugi agyaatanapʋ gaalaagaa ansi.

eng: Then he replied to Johns disciples, Go, report to John what you have seen and heard. Those who were blind are able to see. Those who were crippled now walk. People with skin diseases are cleansed. Those who were deaf now hear. Those who were dead are raised up. And good news is preached to the poor.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔ lɛɛ Gyɔn nbɔɔ mʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Fan bwii naa tɔwɛ Gyɔn kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ fan wu yɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ fan nu. Agyaatanapʋ ansi ii bugi, nɩɩ aboobi bɛɛ kʋsʋ natɛ, nɩɩ abootipu bɛɛ nyɛ alanfɩya, nɩɩ akpawu asu ii tigi, nɩɩ alewupu bee kyiŋi bɛɛ ba nkpa tɔ, nɩɩ atiripu bee nu asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ.

eng: Happy is anyone who doesnt stumble along the way because of me.
local: Fan bɩla tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, kusee ɩ gyɛ sa ɔsa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ san ɔ sʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii mɛ tɔ.

eng: After Johns messengers were gone, Jesus spoke to the crowds about John. What did you go out into the wilderness to see? A stalk blowing in the wind?.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Gyɔn nbɔɔ ba lɩɩ mʋ asɛ mʋ, nɩɩ Yesu piili ɛɛ tɔwɛ Gyɔn asɩn ɛɛ sa kʋbʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan maa lɩɩ kpee Gyɔn asɛ ofuli mʋ sʋ mʋ, mɩnɛ nɩɩ fan waa nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ fen kpee fɛn naa kɩɩ? Kʋkyɔ mʋ nɩɩ afuu a da ii fiilaa mʋ?.

eng: What did you go out to see? A man dressed up in refined clothes? Look, those who dress in fashionable clothes and live in luxury are in royal palaces.
local: Nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ kanɩn bɩrɛ mʋ, mɩnɛ nɩɩ fan kpee naa wu? Abɛɛ ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ o bun ɔgyalɩn atɔ? Ayee! Asa mʋ nɩɩ ba bun ɔgyalɩn atɔ nɩɩ ba sʋ kakyɩna danbɩrasa mʋ, ba bʋ awurakpa nɩn.

eng: What did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet.
local: Amaa mɩnɛ nɩɩ fan kpee naa kɩɩ? Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ abɛɛ? Ɛɛhn, n tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin fɛɛ ɔ kyɔ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ.

eng: He is the one of whom its written: Look, Im sending my messenger before you, who will prepare your way before you .
local: Gyɔn nɩmʋ asɩn nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ ɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, N biti n sun mɛ kabɔɔ kɛ ɔ gyankpaa fʋ, mʋ ii biti ɔ lɔŋɔ fʋ ɔkpa gyoo fʋ.

eng: I tell you that no greater human being has ever been born than John. Yet whoever is least in Gods kingdom is greater than he.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ fɛɛ, ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ba kʋʋgɛ mʋ mʋ, man kyɔ Gyɔn, amaa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o du pii sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii tɔ mʋ kyɔ mʋ.

eng: Everyone who heard this, including the tax collectors, acknowledged Gods justice because they had been baptized by John.
local: Asa mʋ kpini yɛ lenpoo akɔɔlɛpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba nu Gyɔn asɩn tɔwɛsa mʋ, ba kɔɔlɛ gyii fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn mʋ i kyiigi, lɩɩ fɛɛ ba yɛgɛ nɩɩ Gyɔn gyee bamʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee.

eng: But the Pharisees and legal experts rejected Gods will for themselves because they hadnt been baptized by John.
local: Amaa Farasii abi yɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ anyipu ba kina nfɛɛrɛ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sʋ sa bamʋ mʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ ba man yɛgɛ nɩɩ Gyɔn gyee bamʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee.

eng: To what will I compare the people of this generation? Jesus asked. What are they like?.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ yɛ, Anɩmʋ nɩɩ n biti n taa ndaga kakyɩna kanɩmʋ tɔ asa kaala. Anɩmʋ ana nɩɩ ba duli?.

eng: They are like children sitting in the marketplace calling out to each other, We played the flute for you and you didnt dance. We sang a funeral song and you didnt cry.
local: Ba du nɩn fɛɛ nbii mʋ nɩɩ ba tɛ kɩgya tɔ bɛɛ tɩɩ abaa bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, An fʋlɛ kensi‐gyii nbɩlɛ sa fanɛ, amaa fan man kyaa, nɩɩ an sawʋ keli alengyi sa fanɛ, amaa fan man su.

eng: John the Baptist came neither eating bread nor drinking wine, and you say, He has a demon.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Gyɔn okyugyeepu maa ba mʋ, ee ŋmina kɔnɔ gaa, nɩɩ ɔ man nuu nta, nɩɩ fan yɛ Ɔ sʋ nbwɩɩ libi.

eng: Yet the Human Onecame eating and drinking, and you say, Look, a glutton and a drunk, a friend of tax collectors and sinners.
local: Nɩɩ Mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa n maa ba mʋ, n gyii nɩɩ n nuu, nɩɩ fan yɛ, N gyɛ ogyipu lala yɛ ɔtabʋʋpʋ, nɩɩ n bɩla n gyɛ lenpoo akɔɔlɛpʋ yɛ alibi awaapʋ bamʋ kyɛmɩnɛ.

eng: But wisdom is proved to be right by all her descendants.
local: Amaa bamʋ nɩɩ bee buu Ɩbwaarɛ kanyaasɩn, lɩɩ bamʋ‐daa sʋ mʋ, ɩɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ kanyaasɩn mʋ bʋrɔn.

eng: One of the Pharisees invited Jesus to eat with him. After he entered the Pharisees home, he took his place at the table.
local: Saŋa kʋ tɔ nɩɩ Kafarasii‐nyɩn kʋ tɩɩ Yesu yɛ ɔ ba kɛ ba naa bʋga gyii. Nɩɩ Yesu kpee mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ nɩɩ ɔ naa kyɩna o gyii.

eng: Meanwhile, a woman from the city, a sinner, discovered that Jesus was dining in the Pharisees house. She brought perfumed oil in a vase made of alabaster.
local: Kanɩn kadɛ maŋa tɔ mʋ ɔkyɩɩ gyaga kʋ bʋ tʋtɔ. Ɔkyɩɩ mʋ maa nu fɛɛ Yesu bʋ Kafarasii‐nyɩn mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ ee gyii mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ bɩya tulale liifensa pʋrʋntʋwa bɔla.

eng: Standing behind him at his feet and crying, she began to wet his feet with her tears. She wiped them with her hair, kissed them, and poured the oil on them.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ ba yɩlɛ Yesu kamaa tiri mʋ ayaa asɛ ee su ansikyu ɛɛ pɔɔla mʋ ayaa. Nɩɩ ɔ taa mu inyini ɛɛ kpayɩ Yesu ayaa sʋ, nɩɩ o munkura ɩmʋ bɛɛrɛɛ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kyɛɛgɛ tulale mʋ wʋrɩgɛ mʋ ayaa sʋ.

eng: When the Pharisee who had invited Jesus saw what was happening, he said to himself, If this man were a prophet, he would know what kind of woman is touching him. He would know that she is a sinner.
local: Nɩɩ Kafarasii‐nyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ Yesu mʋ maa wu ɩnɩmʋ mʋ, ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ nfɛɛrɛ tɔ yɛ, Fɛɛ ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ dɩn dan ɔ gyɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ kesintin nɩn, ɔ dɩn bɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ kʋlʋn mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ mata mʋ falɛ, ɔ dɩn bɩɩ kɩbaabʋtɔ gyaga mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ kɩmʋ tɔ.

eng: Jesus replied, Simon, I have something to say to you. Teacher, speak, he said.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ Simon yɛ, N sʋ kʋtɔ kʋ kɛ n tɔwɛ fʋ. Nɩɩ Simon yɛ, N nu, Ɔkaapʋpʋ, tɔwɛ mɛ.

eng: A certain lender had two debtors. One owed enough money to pay five hundred people for a days work.The other owed enough money for fifty.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu bɔɔ mʋ kɩŋasan yɛ, Anyɩn anyɔ akʋ ɩ naa paala afulee lɩɩ afulee wuya kʋ asɛ. Ɔkʋ paala gbiti nderebi anu, ɔnyɔsapʋ mɔɔ adunuu.

eng: When they couldnt pay, the lender forgave the debts of them both. Which of them will love him more.
local: Bamʋ ɔkʋ man taalɛ nyɛ ɔ naa ka mʋ kʋkɔ. Nɩɩ afulee wuya mʋ taa kɛɛ bamʋ asa anyɔ. Asa anyɔ maŋa tɔ mʋ, anɩmʋ ii biti o biti afulee wuya mʋ asɩn gaa.

eng: Simon replied, I suppose the one who had the largest debt canceled. Jesus said, You have judged correctly.
local: Simon lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, N waa nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ mu afulee akyɔ mʋ. Nɩɩ Yesu yɛ, Fʋ lɛɛ kɔnɔ kesintin.

eng: Jesus turned to the woman and said to Simon, Do you see this woman? When I entered your home, you didnt give me water for my feet, but she wet my feet with tears and wiped them with her hair.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu biliŋi kɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ Simon yɛ, Yɛ fu wu ɔkyɩɩ nɩmʋ? N maa ba fʋ kɩkpaara sʋ mʋ, fʋ man sa mɛ nkyu kɛ n fwɩɩ mɛ ayaa sʋ, amaa ɔkyɩɩ nɩmʋ taa mʋ ansikyu fwɩɩ mɛ ayaa, nɩɩ ɔ taa mu inyini kpayɩ ɩmʋ.

eng: You didnt greet me with a kiss, but she hasnt stopped kissing my feet since I came in.
local: Fʋ man baari mɛ waa mɛ nsa‐nsa, amaa lɩɩ koo saŋa mʋ nɩɩ n ba nfɩɩ mʋ, ɔkyɩɩ nɩmʋ man pɩɩ yɛgɛ mɛ ayaa sʋ kimunkura.

eng: You didnt anoint my head with oil, but she has poured perfumed oil on my feet.
local: Fʋ man taa olifu nfɔ wʋrɩgɛ mi kumu tɔ, amaa ɔkyɩɩ nɩmʋ taa tulale wʋrɩgɛ mɛ ayaa sʋ.

eng: This is why I tell you that her many sins have been forgiven; so she has shown great love. The one who is forgiven little loves little.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ n tɔwɛ fʋ yɛ, kebiti lala mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kaapʋ falɛ sʋ mʋ, ɩɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ ba taa mu alibi damantɛ mʋ kɛɛ mʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ mu alibi a man kyɔ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ kɩtaakɛɛ mʋ, ɛɛ kaapʋ kebiti mʋ gbaa gbɛrɛɛ nɩn.

eng: Then Jesus said to her, Your sins are forgiven.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ yɛ, N taa fu alibi kɛɛ fʋ.

eng: The other table guests began to say among themselves, Who is this person that even forgives sins.
local: Nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba san ba tɛ ogyikpa mʋ bɛɛ tɔwɛ abaa yɛ, Anɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔnɩmʋ, halɩɩ ɔ sʋ alibi ɛɛ kɛɛ.

eng: Jesus said to the woman, Your faith has saved you. Go in peace.
local: Amaa Yesu tɔwɛ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ yɛ, Fʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii kɩ mɔlɩgɛ fʋ. Natɛ yɛ kɔkɔlɔ yuuli.

eng: Jesus called the Twelve together and he gave them power and authority over all demons and to heal sicknesses.
local: Yesu maa tɩɩ abɩɩlapʋ kudu‐anyɔ mʋ ba gyaŋɛ mʋ, ɔ sa bamʋ ɔlʋn nɩɩ ɔ waa bamʋ kɩnyɩn tɔ yɛ ba gya nbwɩɩ libi lɩɩ asa tɔ kɛ ba kyɛ alɔ.

eng: He sent them out to proclaim Gods kingdom and to heal the sick.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ o sun bamʋ yɛ, ba naa tɔwɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ asɩn kɛ ba kyɛ alɔpʋ.

eng: He told them, Take nothing for the journey—no walking stick, no bag, no bread, no money, not even an extra shirt.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan maa kpee falɛ, fan man taa sɛhn tii sʋ. Fan man taa kikpebi abɛɛ adiikun abɛɛ agyitɔ abɛɛ afulee abɛɛ kaalɛ nyɔsapʋ kʋ tii sʋ.

eng: Whatever house you enter, remain there until you leave that place.
local: Nɩɩ kɩkpaara kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fan loo kɩmʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ fanɛ mʋ, fan kyɩna tʋtɔ naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ fen biti fan lɩɩ kadɛ maŋa tɔ.

eng: Wherever they dont welcome you, as you leave that city, shake the dust off your feet as a witness against them.
local: Nɩɩ kadɛ mʋ nɩɩ ba kina fanɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ, nɩɩ fan lɩɩ tʋtɔ fen kpee mʋ, fan kpɩkpaa fanɛ ayaa sʋ apurutiti, kɛ ɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ fan waa mʋ, ɩ man bʋrɔn.

eng: They departed and went through the villages proclaiming the good news and healing people everywhere.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba natɛ ndɛ‐bi‐ndɛ‐bi mʋ sʋ kpini bɛɛ tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ, yɛgɛ bɛɛ kyɛ asa katɩn kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ bee kpee mʋ.

eng: Herod the ruler heard about everything that was happening. He was confused because some people were saying that John had been raised from the dead.
local: Kanɩn saŋa maŋa mʋ Owura Herodi maa nu atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ waa kpini mʋ ɩ tɔɔrɔɔ mʋ gaa. Lɩɩ fɛɛ akʋ bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ Gyɔn okyugyeepu mʋ i kyiŋi ba nkpa tɔ.

eng: others that Elijah had appeared, and still others that one of the ancient prophets had come back to life.
local: Nɩɩ akʋ mɔɔ ba yɛ, olewupu Eligya i bwii kyiŋi ba nkpa tɔ, nɩɩ akʋ mɔɔ ba bɩla tɔwɛ yɛ, atɔwɛpʋ dɩdaa mʋ ɔkʋ i bwii kyiŋi ba nkpa tɔ.

eng: Herod said, I beheaded John, so now who am I hearing about? Herod wanted to see him.
local: Amaa Herodi tɔwɛ yɛ, N yɛgɛ nɩɩ ba tɩn Gyɔn okyugyeepu kumu. Anɩmʋ nɩɩ n bɩla n nu mʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋ asɩn falɛ? Nɩɩ ɔ kpan mʋ n‐yɩɩ fɛɛ o wu Yesu.

eng: When the apostles returned, they described for Jesus what they had done. Taking them with him, Jesus withdrew privately to a city called Bethsaida.
local: Nɩɩ asunpu mʋ ba maa bwii ba mʋ, ba ba tɔwɛ Yesu kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ba naa waa. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔ taa bamʋ nkʋn ba lɩɩ kpee kadɛ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ kɩmʋ yɛ Betisayida mʋ.

eng: When the crowds figured it out, they followed him. He welcomed them, spoke to them about Gods kingdom, and healed those who were sick.
local: Sakyɔ mʋ ba maa nu kanɩn mʋ, ba buu mʋ kpee tʋtɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ bamʋ tɔwɛ kuwura‐gyii asɩn sa bamʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bee biti kɩkyɛ mʋ.

eng: When the day was almost over, the Twelve came to him and said, Send the crowd away so that they can go to the nearby villages and countryside and find lodging and food, because we are in a deserted place.
local: Kyɔwɛ maa biti ɔ lʋwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ abɩɩlapʋ kudu anyɔ mʋ ba ba mʋ asɛ. Nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Yɛgɛ asa mʋ ba kpee ndɛ‐bi‐ndɛ‐bi yɛ ndɔɔ ana mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ tiri nfɩɩ mʋ, kɛ ba naa nyɛ ilookpa yɛ agyitɔ gyii. Lɩɩ fɛɛ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ an bʋ falɛ gyɛ kiyii nsana.

eng: He replied, You give them something to eat. But they said, We have no more than five loaves of bread and two fish—unless we go and buy food for all these people.
local: Amaa Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fanɛ gbaa‐gbaa fan sa bamʋ kʋtɔ kʋ kɛ ba gyii. Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ an sʋ ɩ gyɛ bodobodo anuu yɛ ɩkɩn ɩnyɔ, abɛɛ fii biti fɛɛ a naa sɔɔ agyitɔ sa asa damantɛ banɩmʋ.

eng: Jesus said to his disciples, Seat them in groups of about fifty.
local: . Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ, Fan yɛgɛ asa mʋ ba kyɩna ntun adunuu‐adunuu.

eng: They did so, and everyone was seated.
local: Nɩɩ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba waa kanɩn, nɩɩ ba yɛgɛ asa mʋ kpini ba kyɩna‐kyɩna kasɛ.

eng: He took the five loaves and the two fish, looked up to heaven, blessed them, and broke them and gave them to the disciples to set before the crowd.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu taa bodobodo yɛ ɩkɩn ɩnyɔ mʋ diyaa mu ansi kɩɩ sʋsʋ taa afwaala sa Ɩbwaarɛ. Nɩɩ ɔ kpɛɛ‐kpɛɛ tɔ taa sa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ ba barɩgɛ tɔ sa asa mʋ kɛ ba gyii.

eng: Everyone ate until they were full, and the disciples filled twelve baskets with the leftovers.
local: Asa mʋ kpini ba gyii ŋmɛ, nɩɩ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba tasɛ‐tasɛ agyisan mʋ bɔla alantan kudu anyɔ.

eng: Once when Jesus was praying by himself, the disciples joined him, and he asked them, Who do the crowds say that I am.
local: Kakaakʋ Yesu maa kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ mʋ nkʋn mʋ, nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba ba mʋ asɛ nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Anɩmʋ nɩɩ asa bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, n gyɛ.

eng: They answered, John the Baptist, others Elijah, and still others that one of the ancient prophets has come back to life.
local: Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Akʋ bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ fʋ ɩ gyɛ Gyɔn okyugyeepu, nɩɩ akʋ mɔɔ bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Eligya ɩ gyɛ fʋ, nɩɩ asansa mɔɔ bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, fʋ ɩ gyɛ atɔwɛpʋ dɩdaa mʋ nɩɩ ba wu mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ i bwii ba nkpa tɔ.

eng: He asked them, And what about you? Who do you say that I am? Peter answered, The Christ sent from God.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fanɔɔ fanɛ mɔɔ, anɩmʋ nɩɩ fan yɛ n gyɛ? Nɩɩ Pita lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Fʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔmɔlɩgɛpʋ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ Wʋla lɛɛ mʋ.

eng: Jesus gave them strict orders not to tell this to anyone.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu da bamʋ amu tɔ yɛ ba man kan waa tɔwɛ ɔkʋ.

eng: He said, The Human Onemust suffer many things and be rejected—by the elders, chief priests, and the legal experts—and be killed and be raised on the third day.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ bɩla tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa dagaa maa gyii awʋrʋfɔ gaa, kɛ abɩlɩsa yɛ aseepu bɩlɩsa yɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ kpini ba kina mɛ. Bee biti ba mɔɔ mɛ, amaa nkɛ nsa kamaa mʋ n biti n kyiŋi lɩɩ lewu tɔ ba nkpa tɔ.

eng: Jesus said to everyone, All who want to come after me must say no to themselves, take up their cross daily, and follow me.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ bɩla tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti fɛɛ o buu mɛ mʋ, ɩ dagaa fɛɛ o kina mʋ kɔkɔlɔ biti, kɛ o kukuri mu oyu laasa sʋla buu mɛ kakɛ kʋmaa.

eng: All who want to save their lives will lose them. But all who lose their lives because of me will save them.
local: Ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti fɛɛ ɔ mɔlɩgɛ mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mʋ nkpa mʋ, ii biti ɩ lɩɩ mʋ abaa tɔ, amaa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ nkpa sa lɩɩ mɛ sʋ mʋ, ee biti o bwii nyɛ mʋ nkpa.

eng: What advantage do people have if they gain the whole world for themselves yet perish or lose their lives?.
local: Fɛɛ nɩɩ nyɩmɩsa kan nyɛ dulinyaa tɔ atɔ kpini nɩɩ ɔ pan mʋ nkpa mʋ, mɩnɛ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ?.

eng: Whoever is ashamed of me and my words, the Human Onewill be ashamed of that person when he comes in his glory and in the glory of the Father and of the holy angels.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ sʋ mʋ ipeeli lɩɩ mɛ yɛ mɛ asɩn mʋ sʋ mʋ, kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ ii biti ɩ baa ɩ sʋ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ ipeeli sa kanɩn ɔsa maŋa, lɩɩ kakɛ mʋ nɩɩ maa Mɛ‐Sɛ yɛ mʋ nbɔɔ kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ an biti a ba bʋnyaa tɔ mʋ.

eng: I assure you that some standing here wont die before they see Gods kingdom.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin fɛɛ fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fan yɩlɛ nfɩɩ falɛ tɔ akʋ ba mɛɛ yɛ ba wu lewu, amɔɔ ba dan wu kuwura‐gyii mʋ pɔyɩ.

eng: About eight days after Jesus said these things, he took Peter, John, and James, and went up on a mountain to pray.
local: Yesu maa tɔwɛ asɩn nɩmʋ lʋwɛ nkɛ nsunɔ kamaa mʋ, ɔ taa Pita maa Gyɔn yɛ Gyemisi nɩɩ maa bamʋ ba kpee kɩbɩɩ kʋkʋ sʋ ba naa kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ.

eng: As he was praying, the appearance of his face changed and his clothes flashed white like lightning.
local: Maa san ɔ maa sʋ ɛɛ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ mʋ, nɩɩ mu ansi tɔ a biliŋi, nɩɩ mʋ ɩkaalɛ gbaa ɩ lɔŋɔ fuuli parɛparɛ nɩɩ ɩ ŋalɩgɛ fɛɛ bʋlayɩ maa wɛɛ ɩsɛɛ mʋ.

eng: Two men, Moses and Elijah, were talking with him.
local: Opula maŋa tɔ asa anyɔ akʋ ba ba mʋ asɛ. Bamʋ ɩ gyɛ Mosesi maa Eligya.

eng: They were clothed with heavenly splendor and spoke about Jesus departure, which he would achieve in Jerusalem.
local: Ba taa bʋnyaa sʋ ba Yesu asɛ, nɩɩ maa bamʋ bɛɛ tɔwɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ yɩla fɛɛ ee biti o kpee Gyerusalem naa wu mʋ.

eng: Peter and those with him were almost overcome by sleep, but they managed to stay awake and saw his glory as well as the two men with him.
local: Pita maa mʋ tɔɔmaa ana mʋ ba dɛ nsɩɩrɛ, amaa ba maa kyiŋi mʋ, ba wu Yesu bʋnyaa mʋ yɛ asa anyɔ mʋ ba maa yɩlɛ mʋ asɛ.

eng: As the two men were about to leave Jesus, Peter said to him, Master, its good that were here. We should construct three shrines: one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah—but he didnt know what he was saying.
local: Asa anyɔ mʋ ba maa lɩɩ Yesu asɛ bee kpee mʋ, Pita tɔwɛ Yesu yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, ɩ bʋrɔn gaa fɛɛ a maa bʋ nfɩɩ. Yɛgɛ a waa abu asa, fʋ lɛɛ kʋkʋ. Mosesi lɛɛ yɛ Eligya lɛɛ. .

eng: Peter was still speaking when a cloud overshadowed them. As they entered the cloud, they were overcome with awe.
local: Ɔ dan san ɛɛ tɔwɛ nɩɩ abwaarɛ‐wʋlɛ a ba bun bamʋ sʋ. Nɩɩ ɩ kɩtaa abɩɩlapʋ mʋ kufuu kanan mʋ nɩɩ abwaarɛ‐wʋlɛ mʋ a bun bamʋ sʋ mʋ.

eng: Then a voice from the cloud said, This is my Son, my chosen one. Listen to him.
local: Nɩɩ ɔbʋlɛ kʋ tɔwɛ abwaarɛ‐wʋlɛ tɔ yɛ, Ɔnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ mi‐bii mʋ nɩɩ n wʋla lɛɛ. Fen nu mʋ asɛ.

eng: Even as the voice spoke, Jesus was found alone. They were speechless and at the time told no one what they had seen.
local: Ɔbʋlɛ mʋ maa tɔwɛ lʋwɛ mʋ, ba wu fɛɛ ɩ san Yesu nkʋn nɩn. Nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba yɛgɛ nɩɩ asɩn mʋ a sii yɛ bamʋ nkʋn, ba man tɔwɛ ɔkʋ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba wu saŋa maŋa.

eng: The next day, when Jesus, Peter, John, and James had come down from the mountain, a large crowd met Jesus.
local: Ɩmʋ lɩɩkaakɛ mʋ ba maa lɩɩ kɩbɩɩ mʋ sʋ bɛɛ ba mʋ, sakyɔ damantɛ kʋ ba gyaŋa Yesu.

eng: A man from the crowd shouted, Teacher, I beg you to take a look at my son, my only child.
local: Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn kʋ bʋ sakyɔ mʋ tɔ kuusi tɩɩ yɛ, Ɔkaapʋpʋ! N kʋlɛ fʋ nɩn, kɩɩ mi kebii‐nyɩnsa‐bii nɩmʋ sa mɛ, mʋ nkʋn ɩ dan ɔ gyɛ mi‐bii.

eng: Look, a spirit seizes him and, without any warning, he screams. It shakes him and causes him to foam at the mouth. It tortures him and rarely leaves him alone.
local: Kayaayu libi kʋ ɩɩ taalɛ ɛɛ ba mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ opula tɔ ee kuusi yɛgɛ ɩɩ sʋ mʋ ɩɩ tʋʋ kasɛ, ɩɩ bɩɩtɛ mʋ fɛɛ kabwɩɩ mʋ, yɛgɛ mʋ kɔnɔ tɔ ɩ lɩɩ afʋfʋʋ ɩ taa mʋ i kpii nɩɩ ɩ waa mʋ ɩ kyɛɛrɛɛ pɔyɩ kɛ ɩ yɛgɛ mʋ.

eng: I begged your disciples to throw it out, but they couldnt.
local: Nɩɩ n kʋlɛ fʋ abɩɩlapʋ yɛ ba gya kayaayu libi mʋ lɩɩ mʋ tɔ, amaa ba man taalɛ.

eng: Jesus answered, You faithless and crooked generation, how long will I be with you and put up with you? Bring your son here.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, O! Fanɛ ndaga asa, fan man kɔɔlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gyii, nɩɩ fanɛ ɩkpa ɩ man du kyɛɛkyɛɛ. Nkɛ nfɩnɛ nɩɩ n biti n kyɩna fanɛ asɛ nyɛ kenyiita sa fanɛ? Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ ɔnyɩn mʋ yɛ, Taa fu‐bii mʋ ba nfɩɩ.

eng: While he was coming, the demon threw him down and shook him violently. Jesus spoke harshly to the unclean spirit, healed the child, and gave him back to his father.
local: Nɩɩ kebii mʋ maa ba Yesu asɛ mʋ, nɩɩ kayaayu libi mʋ ba taa mʋ tʋʋ kasɛ nɩɩ i kpii mʋ fɛɛ kigbingbin mʋ. Amaa Yesu pʋntɛ kayaayu libi mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kyɛ kebii mʋ bwiiyaa mʋ sa mʋ‐sɛ.

eng: Everyone was overwhelmed by Gods greatness. While everyone was marveling at everything he was doing, Jesus said to his disciples.
local: Lɩɩ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔlʋn kparɛ mʋ kaapʋ mʋ, ɩ waa asa mʋ kpini kɩyan. Ɩ maa san i kyinkyin asa mʋ lɩɩ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ Yesu waa sʋ mʋ, ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ.

eng: Take these words to heart: the Human Oneis about to be delivered into human hands.
local: Fan kan fanɛ asʋ kɛ fan nu yɛ ɔdan. Bee biti ba lɛɛ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ waa asa abaa tɔ.

eng: They didnt understand this statement. Its meaning was hidden from them so they couldnt grasp it. And they were afraid to ask him about it.
local: Amaa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba man nu ɩmʋ kasɛ. Ɩ gyɛ asɩn baalasa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ man lɛɛ kaapʋ bamʋ, amaa nɩɩ ba sʋ kufuu fɛɛ ba taasɛ mʋ asɩn mʋ kasɛ.

eng: An argument arose among the disciples about which of them was the greatest.
local: Nɩɩ ɩmɔɔrɛ ɩ tɩyɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ nsana fɛɛ bamʋ tɔ mʋ, anɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔlala.

eng: Aware of their deepest thoughts, Jesus took a little child and had the child stand beside him.
local: Amaa Yesu maa bɩɩ nfɛɛrɛ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ fɛ mʋ, ɔ taa kebii pii ba yɩlɛ mʋ asɛ.

eng: Jesus said to his disciples, Whoever welcomes this child in my name welcomes me. Whoever welcomes me, welcomes the one who sent me. Whoever is least among you all is the greatest.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fʋ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fʋ kɔɔlɛ kebii nɩmʋ dubi mɛ kɩtɩɩ tɔ sʋ mʋ, fʋ kɔɔlɛ mɛ nɩmʋ. Nɩɩ fʋ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fʋ kɔɔlɛ mɛ mʋ, fʋ kɔɔlɛ ɔmʋnɩ o sun mɛ mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔmʋnɩ ɔ gyɛ pii fanɛ kpini tɔ mʋ, mʋ ɩ gyɛ fanɛ tɔ ɔlala.

eng: John replied, Master, we saw someone throwing demons out in your name, and we tried to stop him because he isnt in our group of followers.
local: Nɩɩ Gyɔn tɔwɛ sa Yesu yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ! An wu ɔnyɩn kʋ maa sʋ fʋ kɩtɩɩ ɛɛ gya nbwɩɩ libi. Ɩmʋ sʋ an gya mʋ lɩɩ kɩmʋ tɔ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ man tii anɛ sʋ.

eng: But Jesus replied, Dont stop him, because whoever isnt against you is for you.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan man gya mʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ tɔwɛ ee yii fanɛ mʋ, o tii fanɛ sʋ.

eng: As the time approached when Jesus was to be taken up into heaven, he determined to go to Jerusalem.
local: Yesu kibwii kpee sʋsʋ saŋa maa tiri tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ waa mʋ nfɛɛrɛ, nɩɩ o yii sʋ kpee Gyerusalem.

eng: He sent messengers on ahead of him. Along the way, they entered a Samaritan village to prepare for his arrival.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ nbɔɔ sun yɛ ba gyankpaa mʋ kpee Samariya kadɛ‐bi tɔ naa kʋʋla gyoo mʋ.

eng: but the Samaritan villagers refused to welcome him because he was determined to go to Jerusalem.
local: Amaa tʋtɔ asa mʋ ba man kɔɔlɛ mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ba dan be nyi maa kpee Gyerusalem nɩn.

eng: When the disciples James and John saw this, they said, Lord, do you want us to call fire down from heaven to consume them.
local: Nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ anyɔ, Gyemisi maa Gyɔn ba maa wu kanɩn mʋ, nɩɩ ba yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, fii biti fɛɛ a tɩɩ fʋlɔn lɩɩ sʋsʋ ba tuwa bamʋ abɛɛ.

eng: But Jesus turned and spoke sternly to them.
local: Amaa nɩɩ Yesu biliŋi kɩɩ bamʋ, nɩɩ ɔ yɛ, Fanɛ katɔwɛ maŋa man bʋrɔn.

eng: and they went on to another village.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba lɩɩ tʋtɔ taa kpee kadɛ‐bi pʋpwɛ kʋ sʋ.

eng: As Jesus and his disciples traveled along the road, someone said to him, I will follow you wherever you go.
local: Ba maa naa ɔkpa tɔ bee kpee mʋ, nɩɩ ɔnyɩn kʋ tɔwɛ Yesu yɛ, N biti n buu fʋ kpee katɩn kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fii kpee mʋ.

eng: Jesus replied, Foxes have dens and the birds in the sky have nests, but the Human Onehas no place to lay his head.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ ɔnyɩn mʋ yɛ, Agboogboo bɛɛ dɛ ɩbɔ tɔ, nɩɩ nbwiibi ba sʋ nsʋswaa, amaa mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa bɩrɛ n man sʋ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ n dɛ fʋʋtɛ.

eng: Then Jesus said to someone else, Follow me. He replied, Lord, first let me go and bury my father.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu bɩla tɔwɛ ɔnyɩn kʋ mɔɔ yɛ, Buu mɛ. Amaa ɔnyɩn mʋ tɔwɛ Yesu nɩn yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, yɛgɛ n bwii kpee naa pula nsɛ pɔyɩ.

eng: Jesus said to him, Let the dead bury their own dead. But you go and spread the news of Gods kingdom.
local: Amaa Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Yɛgɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba du fɛɛ alewupu, lɩɩ fɛɛ ba man nyi Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ ba kpee naa pula bamʋ alewupu. Kɛ fɛɛ fʋ, fu kpee naa tɔwɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ asɩn.

eng: Someone else said to Jesus, I will follow you, Lord, but first let me say good-bye to those in my house.
local: Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn kʋ mɔɔ bɩla yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ n biti n buu fʋ, amaa yɛgɛ kɛ n bwii kpee naa kala mɛ kɩkpaara sʋ asa kɛ n ba.

eng: Jesus said to him, No one who puts a hand on the plow and looks back is fit for Gods kingdom.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Fʋ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fii biliŋi fɩɩ kɩɩ kamaa mʋ, nɩɩ fʋ nfɛɛrɛ man dɛ katɩn kʋlʋn mʋ, fʋ man dagaa Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii.

eng: After these things, the Lord commissioned seventy-two others and sent them on ahead in pairs to every city and place he was about to go.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ kamaa Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu lɛɛ asa adusunɔ yɛ anyɔ akʋ nɩɩ o sun bamʋ asa anyɔ‐anyɔ, yɛ ba gyankpaa mʋ kpee kadɛ kʋmaa yɛ katɩn kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti o kpee mʋ.

eng: He said to them, The harvest is bigger than you can imagine, but there are few workers. Therefore, plead with the Lord of the harvest to send out workers for his harvest.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ bɔ bamʋ kɩŋasan yɛ, Ndɔɔ tɔ atɔ sɔyɩsa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa a tɩŋɛ mʋ ɩ kyɔ gaa, amaa atɩŋɛpʋ mʋ ba man kyɔ. Fan kʋlɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ kusun mʋ wuya, kɛ ɔ bɩya atɩŋɛpʋ ba tii su kɛ ba ba waa mu kusun mʋ.

eng: Go! Be warned, though, that Im sending you out as lambs among wolves.
local: Fan kpee, n sun fanɛ nɩn fɛɛ nbʋlʋpʋ‐bi ba maa kpee akuntun tɔ.

eng: Carry no wallet, no bag, and no sandals. Dont even greet anyone along the way.
local: Fan man waa fan taa afulee abɛɛ adiikun abɛɛ asɩbɩta, kɛ fan man fwaala ɔkʋ ɔkpa tɔ.

eng: Whenever you enter a house, first say, May peace be on this house.
local: Nɩɩ fan kan loo kɩkpaara kʋmaa sʋ mʋ, fan daa kɩtɔwɛ yɛ, Keyuula kɩ ba kɩkpaara nɩmʋ sʋ.

eng: If anyone there shares Gods peace, then your peace will rest on that person. If not, your blessing will return to you.
local: Nɩɩ keyuula nyɩmɩsa kan ɔ bʋ tʋtɔ mʋ, fanɛ keyuula ii biti ɔ ba mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ kanɩn mʋ, ii biti i bwii ba fanɛ sʋ.

eng: Remain in this house, eating and drinking whatever they set before you, for workers deserve their pay. Dont move from house to house.
local: Fan kyɩna kɩkpaara maŋa sʋ, kɛ fen gyii kɛ fen nuu kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ba sa fanɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ osunpu kʋmaa kʋkɔka dagaa mʋ. Fan man ba fen loo fen loo akpaara‐akpaara sʋ.

eng: Whenever you enter a city and its people welcome you, eat what they set before you.
local: Nɩɩ kadɛ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fan loo kɩmʋ tɔ nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ fanɛ mʋ, fan gyii agyitɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ba sa fanɛ.

eng: Heal the sick who are there, and say to them, Gods kingdom has come upon you.
local: Fan kyɛ alɔpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ kadɛ mʋ tɔ, kɛ fan tɔwɛ ɔkʋmaa yɛ, Nbɩɩnbɩɩ, Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii ki tiri fanɛ tɔ.

eng: Whenever you enter a city and the people dont welcome you, go out into the streets and say.
local: Amaa nɩɩ fan kan loo kadɛ kʋ tɔ nɩɩ ba man kɔɔlɛ fanɛ mʋ, fan lɩɩ kpee kadɛ mʋ agbenbi sʋ kɛ fan tɔwɛ yɛ.

eng: As a complaint against you, we brush off the dust of your city that has collected on our feet. But know this: Gods kingdom has come to you.
local: An kpɩkpaa fanɛ kadɛ tɔ apurutiti mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ anɛ ayaa sʋ mʋ gbaa an wʋrɩgɛ fanɛ sʋ, kɛ ɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ fan waa mʋ, ɩ man bʋrɔn. Amaa fan baa fan nyi fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii ki tiri tɔ.

eng: I assure you that Sodom will be better off on Judgment Day than that city.
local: Nɩɩ n tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, katalɛ lala kakɛ mʋ, kadɛ maa kii biti kɩ nyɛ kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ kɩ kyɔ kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ ba Sodom kadɛ tɔ mʋ.

eng: How terrible it will be for you, Chorazin. How terrible it will be for you, Bethsaida. If the miracles done among you had been done in Tyre and Sidon, they would have changed their hearts and lives long ago. They would have sat around in funeral clothes and ashes.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ yɛ, Korasin abi fanɛ laakʋ, Basayida abi fanɛ laakʋ, adangana mʋ nɩɩ n waa fanɛ asɛ falɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɩ daa ɩ gyɛ Taayi yɛ Sidon nɩɩ n waa kanɩn nɩn, naafɔɔ ba wʋla kyɩna kɛ ba dɩla ɩbɔɔta kɛ ba taa nsɔ bun bamʋ amu kɛ ɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ ba kyɛɛgɛ bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ lɩɩ alibi tɔ.

eng: But Tyre and Sidon will be better off at the judgment than you.
local: Amaa katalɛ lala kakɛ mʋ, Taayi maa Sidon abi kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ mʋ ii biti ɩ baa ɩ bɔɔ fanɛ lɛɛ.

eng: And you, Capernaum, will you be honored by being raised up to heaven? No, you will be cast down to the place of the dead.
local: Fanɛ Kapanum abi, bee biti ba kukuri fanɛ kpee sʋsʋ nɩn abɛɛ? Ayee, bee biti ba bɩya fanɛ kasɛ kamandɛ.

eng: Whoever listens to you listens to me. Whoever rejects you rejects me. Whoever rejects me rejects the one who sent me.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ, Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o nu fanɛ asɛ mʋ, nu mɛ asɛ, nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o kina fanɛ mʋ, o kina mɛ, nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o kina mɛ mʋ, o kina ɔmʋ nɩɩ o sun mɛ mʋ nɩn.

eng: The seventy-two returned joyously, saying, Lord, even the demons submit themselves to us in your name.
local: Abɩɩlapʋ adusunɔ yɛ anyɔ mʋ nɩɩ Yesu sun mʋ ba bwii ba yɛ kensi‐gyii nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, fʋ kɩtɩɩ tɔ sʋ nbwɩɩ libi gbaa ba bɩya bamʋ n‐yɩɩ kasɛ sa anɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, I saw Satan fall from heaven like lightning.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, N wu Sɩtaanɛ maa lɩɩ sʋsʋ ba tɩyɛ kasɛ fɛɛ bʋlayɩ maa wɛɛ ɩsɛɛ mʋ.

eng: Look, I have given you authority to crush snakes and scorpions underfoot. I have given you authority over all the power of the enemy. Nothing will harm you.
local: N sa fanɛ ɔlʋn fɛɛ fen biti fan kyigya anansɛ yɛ ɩkyankpatɛ sʋ, kɛ fan nyɛ ɔlʋn fanɛ dʋn Sɩtaanɛ sʋ, nɩɩ sɛhn mɛɛ yɛ ɔ waa fanɛ.

eng: Nevertheless, dont rejoice because the spirits submit to you. Rejoice instead that your names are written in heaven.
local: Amaa fan man yɛgɛ ɩ waa fanɛ ɔkɔn fɛɛ n‐yaayu libi ba bɩya bamʋ n‐yɩɩ kasɛ sa fanɛ. Amaa fan yɛgɛ ɩ waa fanɛ ɔkɔn fɛɛ ba maa kyʋrɔɔ fanɛ atɩɩ yɩla Ɩbwaarɛ ayɛ.

eng: At that very moment, Jesus overflowed with joy from the Holy Spirit and said, I praise you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because youve hidden these things from the wise and intelligent and shown them to babies. Indeed, Father, this brings you happiness.
local: Kanɩn saŋa maŋa Yesu kɔkɔlɔ ki fuuli mʋ lɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Mɛ‐Sɛ, sʋsʋ yɛ kasɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, n fwaala fʋ lɩɩ fɛɛ fʋ taa atɔ nɩmʋ baala anyaasɩnpʋ yɛ ɩwʋlɛ anyipu sʋ, nɩɩ fʋ lɛɛ kaapʋ nbii wuribi. Nsɛ, fʋ kɩpɩrɛ sʋ nɩɩ fʋ waa ɩnɩmʋ.

eng: My Father has handed all things over to me. No one knows who the Son is except the Father, or who the Father is except the Son and anyone to whom the Son wants to reveal him.
local: Mɛ‐Sɛ taa atɔ nɩmʋ kpini waa mɛ kɩbaa tɔ. Ɔkʋ mee nyi ɔmʋ nɩɩ mɛ, Obii mʋ n gyɛ, amɔɔ Ɔsɛ mʋ nkʋn, nɩɩ ɔkʋ mee nyi Ɔsɛ mʋ, amɔɔ bamʋ nɩɩ n biti n lɛɛ kaapʋ bamʋ mʋ nkʋn i nyi Ɔsɛ mʋ.

eng: Turning to the disciples, he said privately, Happy are the eyes that see what you see.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu biliŋi tɔwɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ nkʋn yɛ, Kumu ɔkɔn ɩ gyɛ sa fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fan wu atɔ mʋ nɩɩ fan wu falɛ.

eng: I assure you that many prophets and kings wanted to see what you see and hear what you hear, but they didnt.
local: N bɩla n tɔwɛ fanɛ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ gaalaagaa yɛ awura, ba daa bee biti ba wu atɔ mu nɩɩ fen wu falɛ amaa ba man wu. Nɩɩ ba daa bee biti ba nu asɩn mʋ nɩɩ fen nu falɛ, amaa ba man nu ɩmʋ.

eng: A legal expert stood up to test Jesus. Teacher, he said, what must I do to gain eternal life.
local: Kakaakʋ nbara ɔkaapʋpʋ onyipu kʋ kʋsʋ ba Yesu asɛ o be nu mʋ kɔnɔ tɔ asɩn. Nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɔkaapʋpʋ, mɩnɛ nɩɩ n biti n waa kɛ n nyɛ nkpa kakpaa.

eng: Jesus replied, What is written in the Law? How do you interpret it.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Nɛnɛ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ? Nɛnɛ nɩɩ fʋ kalɛ ɩmʋ nu kasɛ.

eng: He responded, You must love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your being, with all your strength, and with all your mind, and love your neighbor as yourself .
local: Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Taa fʋ kɔkɔlɔ yɛ fʋ kala yɛ fʋ ɔlʋn yɛ fʋ nfɛɛrɛ kpini biti fʋ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ, kɛ fu biti fʋ tɔɔmaa fɛɛ fʋ n‐yɩɩ.

eng: Jesus said to him, You have answered correctly. Do this and you will live.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Fʋ lɛɛ kɔnɔ kyɛɛkyɛɛ sʋ. Waa ɩmʋ kɛ fʋ nyɛ nkpa.

eng: But the legal expert wanted to prove that he was right, so he said to Jesus, And who is my neighbor.
local: Amaa ɔnyɩn mʋ ii biti ɔ kaapʋ fɛɛ mʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi sʋ mʋ, ɔ taasɛ Yesu yɛ, Anɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ mɛ tɔɔmaa.

eng: Jesus replied, A man went down from Jerusalem to Jericho. He encountered thieves, who stripped him naked, beat him up, and left him near death.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu kɔnɔ kɩlɛɛ tɔ mʋ, ɔ bɔɔ mʋ kɩŋasan yɛ, Kakaakʋ nɩɩ ɔnyɩn kʋ lɩɩ Gyerusalem ee kpee Gyeriko. Nɩɩ ɔ naa tɩyɛ kumu ɔlʋn tɔ ayu abaa tɔ, nɩɩ ba dayɩ mʋ dɩlɩgɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɩ san gbɛrɛɛ kɛ o wu nɩɩ ba yɛgɛ mʋ.

eng: Now it just so happened that a priest was also going down the same road. When he saw the injured man, he crossed over to the other side of the road and went on his way.
local: Ɩ ba fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ oseepu kʋ naa ɔkpa maŋa sʋ ɛɛ ba. Maa wu ɔnyɩn mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kɩlɩgɛ taa ɔkpa mʋ kɩbaafʋn kyʋn yɛgɛ mʋ.

eng: Likewise, a Levite came by that spot, saw the injured man, and crossed over to the other side of the road and went on his way.
local: Kanɩn ɔkpa kʋlʋn maŋa nɩɩ Lewi‐nyɩn kʋ gbaa bɔla ba wu mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kɩlɩgɛ mʋ kyʋn.

eng: A Samaritan, who was on a journey, came to where the man was. But when he saw him, he was moved with compassion.
local: Amaa Kasamariya‐nyɩn kʋ gbaa bɔla ɔkpa maŋa ba lɩɩ mʋ sʋ. Maa ba wu ɔnyɩn mʋ, ɩ kɩtaa mʋ kʋwɛɛ.

eng: The Samaritan went to him and bandaged his wounds, tending them with oil and wine. Then he placed the wounded man on his own donkey, took him to an inn, and took care of him.
local: Nɩɩ Kasamariya‐nyɩn mʋ natɛ kpee mʋ asɛ, nɩɩ ɔ taa wayɩn nta kpayɩ mʋ ɩlɔ ana mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ waa kɩfɛ mʋ ɩlɔ mʋ tɔ nɩɩ o ŋmina ɩmʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ gyaga mʋ kuruma sʋ kpee kɩkpaara mʋ nɩɩ afɔ bee loo kɩmʋ sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kɩɩ mʋ sʋ.

eng: The next day, he took two full days worth of wages and gave them to the innkeeper. He said, Take care of him, and when I return, I will pay you back for any additional costs.
local: Lɩɩkaakɛ, Kasamariya‐nyɩn mʋ maa biti ɔ kʋsʋ kpee mʋ, ɔ lɛɛ afulee sa afɔ kɩkpaara ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Kɩɩ ɔlɔpʋ nɩmʋ sʋ sa mɛ, nɩɩ fu kan nyita afulee akʋ tii ɩnɩmʋ sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ n kan bwii ba mʋ n biti n ba ka fʋ ɩmʋ kpini. .

eng: What do you think? Which one of these three was a neighbor to the man who encountered thieves.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ nbara ɔkaapʋpʋ mʋ yɛ, To, fʋ asɛ mʋ, asa asá nɩmʋ tɔ mʋ, ɔmɔɔ ɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ mʋ tɔɔmaa ɩ gyɛ ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ amu tɔ ɔlʋn ayu ba dayɩ mʋ mʋ.

eng: Then the legal expert said, The one who demonstrated mercy toward him. Jesus told him, Go and do likewise.
local: Nɩɩ nbara ɔkaapʋpʋ onyipu mʋ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɔmʋ nɩɩ o wu ɔnyɩn mʋ kʋwɛɛ mʋ. Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Fʋ gbaa fʋ naa waa kanɩn.

eng: While Jesus and his disciples were traveling, Jesus entered a village where a woman named Martha welcomed him as a guest.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ bee kpee mʋ, ba naa fʋʋ kadɛ‐bi kʋ. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Maata waa mʋ nsa‐nsa taa mʋ kpee mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ.

eng: She had a sister named Mary, who sat at the Lords feet and listened to his message.
local: Maata sʋ osupu mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Mɛɛrɩ. Nɩɩ Mɛɛrɩ naa kyɩna Yesu asɛ ee nu mʋ kɩkaapʋ mʋ.

eng: By contrast, Martha was preoccupied with getting everything ready for their meal. So Martha came to him and said, Lord, dont you care that my sister has left me to prepare the table all by myself? Tell her to help me.
local: Amaa kanɩn asɩn mʋ ɩ tɔɔrɔɔ Maata lɩɩ fɛɛ mʋ nkʋn ɩɩ dɩŋa afɔ mʋ agyitɔ. Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔ ba Yesu asɛ ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Mɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ, ɩ man tiri fʋ fɛɛ mi‐supu maa yɛgɛ mɛ nkʋn n waa kusun mʋ kpini? Tɔwɛ mʋ kɛ ɔ ba kyɛ mɛ tɔ.

eng: The Lord answered, Martha, Martha, you are worried and distracted by many things.
local: Nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu lɛɛ mʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Maata, Maata! Asɩn gaalaagaa ɩ tɔɔrɔɔ fʋ, nɩɩ i tiri fʋ.

eng: One thing is necessary. Mary has chosen the better part. It wont be taken away from her.
local: Amaa kʋtɔ kʋlʋn i tiri. Mɛɛrɩ lɛɛ kɩmʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋrɔn, nɩɩ ba mɛɛ yɛ ba taalɛ kɔɔlɛ ɩmʋ lɩɩ mʋ abaa tɔ.

eng: Jesus was praying in a certain place. When he finished, one of his disciples said, Lord, teach us to pray, just as John taught his disciples.
local: Kakaakʋ nɩɩ Yesu bʋ katɩn kʋ ɛɛ kʋlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ. Maa lʋwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, kaapʋ anɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Gyɔn kaapʋ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ.

eng: Jesus told them, When you pray, say: Father, uphold the holiness of your name. Bring in your kingdom.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan kan fen biti fan kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ mʋ, fan tɔwɛ yɛ, Anɛ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ, yɛgɛ asa ba bʋnyaa fʋ kɩtɩɩ, yɛgɛ fu kuwura‐gyii mʋ kɩ ba kasɛ sʋ nfɩɩ.

eng: Give us the bread we need for today.
local: Kɛ fʋ sa anɛ kakɛ kʋmaa agyitɔ gyisa.

eng: Forgive us our sins, for we also forgive everyone who has wronged us. And dont lead us into temptation.
