local: Taa anɛ alibi kɛɛ anɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ anɛ gbaa anɛ an sʋ an kɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa anɛ alibi mʋ. Kɛ fʋ man yɛgɛ a loo kʋtʋʋ tɔ kɩɩ. .

eng: He also said to them, Imagine that one of you has a friend and you go to that friend in the middle of the night. Imagine saying, Friend, loan me three loaves of bread.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ, A taa fɛɛ fanɛ tɔ ɔkʋ kan kpee mʋ kyɛmɩnɛ asɛ kagyanbwɛ kiyiridi nsana yɛ, Mɛ kyɛmɩnɛ, paala mɛ bodobodo asa.

eng: because a friend of mine on a journey has arrived and I have nothing to set before him.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ mɛ kyɛmɩnɛ kʋ ɩ dan lɩɩ ɔkpa sʋ ba sʋgɛ mɛ sʋ nbɩɩnbɩɩ nɩmʋ. Nɩɩ mɛɛ mɛ n man sʋ sɛhn n sa mʋ kɛ o gyii.

eng: Imagine further that he answers from within the house, Dont bother me. The door is already locked, and my children and I are in bed. I cant get up to give you anything.
local: A taa fɛɛ, mʋ kyɛmɩnɛ mʋ ɔ lɛɛ kɔnɔ obu to yɛ, Man tɔɔrɔɔ mɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ n tɩyɛ tii biri sʋ, nɩɩ maa mi‐bii ana an tɩyɛ dɛ, ɩmʋ sʋ n mɛɛ yɛ n bɩla taalɛ kʋsʋ sa fʋ sɛhn.

eng: I assure you, even if he wouldnt get up and help because of his friendship, he will get up and give his friend whatever he needs because of his friends brashness.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ fɛɛ, nɩɩ ɩ kan ɩ gyɛ kakyɛmɩnɛ nkʋn sʋ mʋ, ɔ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ kʋsʋ sa mʋ sɛhn. Amaa kpalɩ fɛɛ maa man sʋ ipeeli nɩɩ ɔ san ɔ maa sʋ ɛɛ kʋlɛ mʋ, ɩ waa nɛnɛ ee biti ɔ kʋsʋ sa mʋ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ i tiri sa mʋ mʋ.

eng: And I tell you: Ask and you will receive. Seek and you will find. Knock and the door will be opened to you.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ n tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, Fan kyaga sʋ fɛn kʋlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ, ee biti ɔ sa fanɛ, fan kyaga sʋ fen biti, fen biti fan nyɛ, fan kyaga sʋ fɛn da ɔpʋnʋ, ee biti o tigi sa fanɛ.

eng: Everyone who asks, receives. Whoever seeks, finds. To everyone who knocks, the door is opened.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kʋlɛ mʋ, bɛɛ sa mʋ, nɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ee biti mʋ, ɛɛ nyɛ, nɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ da ɔpʋnʋ sʋ mʋ, bee tigi sa mʋ.

eng: Which father among you would give a snake to your child if the child asked for a fish?.
local: Nɩɩ fanɛ tɔ ɔsɛ mɔɔ nɩɩ mu‐bii ɩɩ kʋlɛ mʋ ɔkɩn, nɩɩ ɛɛ taa kɩnansɛ sa mʋ?.

eng: If a child asked for an egg, what father would give the child a scorpion?.
local: Abɛɛ ɛɛ kʋlɛ fʋ kibesi kɛ fʋ kɩtaa kyankpatɩ sa mʋ?.

eng: If you who are evil know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will the heavenly Father give the Holy Spirit to those who ask him.
local: Fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fɛn gyɛ alibi awaapʋ gbaa mʋ, fen nyi atɔ danbɩrasa kɩtaa sa fanɛ‐bii ana. Ɩ waa nɛnɛ sʋ nɩɩ fanɛ‐Sɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ sʋsʋ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ taa mʋ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba kʋlɛ mʋ mʋ.

eng: Jesus was throwing out a demon that causes muteness. When the demon was gone, the man who couldnt speak began to talk. The crowds were amazed.
local: Kakaakʋ Yesu maa gya kabwɩɩ libi mʋ nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ yɛgɛ nyɩmɩsa ɔ tɔwɛ. Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ kabwɩɩ libi mʋ lɩɩ mʋ tɔ mʋ, ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ daa ɔ gyɛ naamuu mʋ, piili ɛɛ tɔwɛ. Nɩɩ i kyinkyin asa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ tʋtɔ mʋ.

eng: But some of them said, He throws out demons with the authority of Beelzebul, the ruler of demons.
local: Amaa asa mʋ tɔ akʋ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, Nbwɩɩ libi kpini kumu mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ Beelisebu mʋ, ɩɩ sa Yesu ɔlʋn nɩɩ ɛɛ taalɛ ɛɛ gya nbwɩɩ libi kanɩn mʋ.

eng: Others were testing him, seeking a sign from heaven.
local: Nɩɩ akʋ mɔɔ ba tʋʋ mʋ tɔ kɩɩ, lan tɔwɛ yɛ, nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ mʋ ɔlʋn maŋa lɩɩ mʋ, ɔ waa asɩn kyinkyinsa kɛ ba kɩɩ.

eng: Because Jesus knew what they were thinking, he said to them, Every kingdom involved in civil war becomes a wasteland, and a house torn apart by divisions will collapse.
local: Amaa Yesu maa wʋla bɩɩ nfɛɛrɛ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ fɛ sʋ mʋ, ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Kuwura‐gyii mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ barɩgɛ tɔ kɩɩ kɔ yɛ abaa mʋ, kɩ mɛɛ yɛ kɩ taalɛ yɩlɛ. Nɩɩ kɩkpaara mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ barɩgɛ tɔ kɩɩ kɔ yɛ abaa mʋ gbaa, kɩ mɛɛ yɛ kɩ taalɛ yɩlɛ.

eng: If Satan is at war with himself, how will his kingdom endure? I ask this because you say that I throw out demons by the authority of Beelzebul.
local: Nɩɩ fɛɛ Sɩtaanɛ gbaa barɩgɛ tɔ kɩɩ kɔ yɛ kɩmʋ n‐yɩɩ mʋ, nɛnɛ nɩɩ kɩmʋ kuwura‐gyii ɩ taalɛ yɩlɛ? Lɩɩ fɛɛ fɛn tɔwɛ yɛ, Beelisebu ɔlʋn nɩɩ n sʋ n gya nbwɩɩ libi sʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ kanɩn.

eng: If I throw out demons by the authority of Beelzebul, then by whose authority do your followers throw them out? Therefore, they will be your judges.
local: Nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ Beelisebu ɔlʋn nɩɩ n sʋ n gya nbwɩɩ libi mʋ, anɩmʋ ɔlʋn nɩɩ fanɛ abuupu bɩrɛ ba sʋ bɛɛ gya nbwɩɩ libi bɛɛ lɩɩ asa tɔ? Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ yɛgɛ kɛ fanɛ gbaa‐gbaa fanɛ abuupu ba talɛ fanɛ fɛɛ fanɛ lɛɛ man dɛ ɔkpa.

eng: But if I throw out demons by the powerof God, then Gods kingdom has already overtaken you.
local: Amaa nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔlʋn nɩɩ n sʋ n gya nbwɩɩ libi n lɩɩ asa tɔ mʋ, kɩmʋ ɩɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii kɩ ba fanɛ asɛ.

eng: When a strong man, fully armed, guards his own palace, his possessions are secure.
local: Fɛɛ nɩɩ nyɩmɩsa bʋlʋnsa wʋla kʋʋla mʋ adʋntɔ ee kun mʋ kɩkpaara bɩrɛ, mʋ kapatɩyɛ kɩ dɛ sa mʋ bwahn.

eng: But as soon as a stronger one attacks and overpowers him, the stronger one takes away the armor he had trusted and divides the stolen goods.
local: Amaa fɛɛ nɩɩ ɔkʋ ɔlʋn ɩ kyɔ mʋ lɛɛ mʋ, nɩɩ o tii mʋ waa tɔ taalɛ mʋ mʋ, ɛɛ kɔɔlɛ mʋ adʋntɔ mʋ nɩɩ mʋ tamaa gyan sʋ mʋ kpini, kɛ ɔ barɩgɛ mʋ kapatɩyɛ mʋ tɔ kɛɛ asa.

eng: Whoever isnt with me is against me, and whoever doesnt gather with me, scatters.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man bʋ mɛ asɛ mʋ, ɛɛ kʋsʋ yɩlɛ mɛ sʋ nɩn, nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ kʋʋla ɛɛ kyɛ mɛ tɔ mʋ, ɩɩ yaasɛ tɔ nɩn.

eng: When an unclean spirit leaves a person, it wanders through dry places looking for a place to rest. But it doesnt find any. Then it says, Ill go back to the house I left.
local: Fɛɛ nɩɩ kabwɩɩ libi kan lɩɩ nyɩmɩsa tɔ mʋ, ee kpee ɛɛ naa ɛɛ kyaabɔɔ ifuli sʋ ee biti ɔfʋʋtɛkpa. Nɩɩ ɔ man kan nyɛ mʋ, ɛɛ tɔwɛ sa mʋ n‐yɩɩ yɛ, N biti n bwii kpee mɛ kɩkpaara mʋ nɩɩ n lɩɩ kɩmʋ sʋ mʋ.

eng: When it arrives, it finds the house cleaned up and decorated.
local: Maa ba mʋ o be wu fɛɛ ba fɔgɛ kɩkpaara mʋ, lɔŋɔ kɩmʋ atɔ kʋmaa yɩla kyɛɛkyɛɛ.

eng: Then it goes and brings with it seven other spirits more evil than itself. They go in and make their home there. That person is worse off at the end than at the beginning.
local: Kɛ ɔ bɩla naa taa nbwɩɩ libi asunɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba libi ba kyɔ mʋ tii mʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋ, kɛ ba kpee naa kyɩna tʋtɔ. Kɛ ɩ yɛgɛ kanɩn nyɩmɩsa maŋa kakyɩna tɔ ki nyita ɩ kyɔ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ daa o du mʋ.

eng: While Jesus was saying these things, a certain woman in the crowd spoke up: Happy is the mother who gave birth to you and who nursed you.
local: Yesu maa tɔwɛ kanɩn mʋ, sakyɔ mʋ tɔ ɔkyɩɩ kʋ diyaa mʋ ɔbʋlɛ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Kusee ɩ gyɛ sa onyi mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kʋʋgɛ fʋ, nɩɩ ɔ sa fʋ mʋ kɩnyapʋ‐kyu mʋ.

eng: But he said, Happy rather are those who hear Gods word and put it into practice.
local: Amaa Yesu lɛɛ mʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Kusee ɩ gyɛ sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba nu abwaarɛsɩn nɩɩ ba buu ɩmʋ mʋ.

eng: When the crowds grew, Jesus said, This generation is an evil generation. It looks for a sign, but no sign will be given to it except Jonahs sign.
local: Asa mʋ ba maa nyɛ akyɔ bee tii sʋ mʋ, Yesu tɔwɛ yɛ, Ndaga asa libi kakyɩna kanɩmʋ tɔ bɛɛ taasɛ bee biti asɩn kyinkyinsa, amaa mɛɛ yɛ n waa sɛhn sa bamʋ, amɔɔ Ɩbwaarɛ Ɔtɔwɛpʋ Gyona lɛɛ mʋ.

eng: Just as Jonah became a sign to the people of Nineveh, so the Human Onewill be a sign to this generation.
local: Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛgɛ nɩɩ Gyona kyɩna ɔ gyɛ sɩnkaala sa Ninewe abi mʋ, kanɩn maŋa sʋ nɩɩ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa gbaa n biti n ba waa Ɩbwaarɛ sɩnkaala sa ndaga asa kakyɩna nɩmʋ tɔ.

eng: The queen of the South will rise up at the judgment with the people of this generation and condemn them, because she came from a distant land to hear Solomons wisdom. And look, someone greater than Solomon is here.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ katalɛ akɛ mʋ, Owura kyɩɩsa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kyɩna lɩɩ akpɩlɩgɛ mʋ, ii biti ɔ kʋsʋ yɩlɛ kɛ o bun ndaga kakyɩna nɩmʋ tɔ asa kʋpwɛ. Kpalɩ fɛɛ ɔ lɩɩ kata‐kata dulinyaa ɔkaa o be nu Solomon mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kyɩna ɔ gyɛ Ɩsɩrayɩ owura mʋ kanyaasɩn. Amaa n tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ n kyɔ Owura Solomon mʋ.

eng: The people of Nineveh will rise up at the judgment with this generation and condemn it, because they changed their hearts and lives in response to Jonahs preaching—and one greater than Jonah is here.
local: Nɩɩ katalɛ akɛ mʋ Ninewe abi mʋ nɩɩ ba daa kyɩna dɩdaa mʋ, bee biti ba kʋsʋ yɩlɛ talɛ fanɛ ndaga asa nɩmʋ tɔ, kɛ ba bun fanɛ kʋpwɛ. Kpalɩ fɛɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Gyona tɔwɛ abwaarɛsɩn sa bamʋ mʋ, ba yɛgɛ alibi kɩwaa. Amaa n tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ n kyɔ Gyona mʋ.

eng: People dont light a lamp and then put it in a closet or under a basket. Rather, they place the lamp on a lampstand so that those who enter the house can see the light.
local: Ɔkʋ mee tuwa fɩtɩla ɔ sʋ ɛɛ yɩla katɩn mʋ nɩɩ kii biti kɩ baala, abɛɛ ɔ taa waa kolobi tɔ. Amaa ɛɛ taa gyaga kɩmʋ ɔgyagakpa nɩn, kɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ bee biti ba ba tʋtɔ mʋ, ɩ baa ɩ kɩɩ bamʋ tɔ.

eng: Your eye is the lamp of your body. When your eye is healthy, your whole body is full of light. But when your eye is bad, your whole body is full of darkness.
local: Fu ansi ɩ gyɛ fɩtɩla sa fʋ n‐yɩɩ. Nɩɩ fu ansi ɩ kan ɩ bʋrɔn mʋ, fʋ n‐yɩɩ kpini gbaa gyɛ fɩtɩla nɩn. Amaa nɩɩ fu ansi ɩ man kan ɩ bʋrɔn mʋ, fʋ n‐yɩɩ kpini gbaa bʋ kitentenbiri tɔ.

eng: Therefore, see to it that the light in you isnt darkness.
local: Kanɩn sʋ kɩɩ sʋ yɛ ɔdan, kɛ fɩtɩla mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ fʋ tɔ mʋ kɩ man biliŋi kitentenbiri.

eng: If your whole body is full of light—with no part darkened—then it will be as full of light as when a lamp shines brightly on you.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ fɛɛ nɩɩ fʋ n‐yɩɩ kpini gyɛ fɩtɩla, nɩɩ ɩmʋ kɩbaafʋn kʋ man gyɛ kitentenbiri mʋ, ii biti ɩ baa ɩ kɩɩ tɔ gaa, fɛɛ fɩtɩla maa kɩɩ tɔ kɩ ŋalɩgɛ fanɛ sʋ mʋ.

eng: While Jesus was speaking, a Pharisee invited him to share a meal with him, so Jesus went and took his place at the table.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ lʋwɛ mʋ, Kafarasii‐nyɩn kʋ tɩɩ Yesu yɛ, ɔ ba kɛ maa mʋ ba bʋga gyii. Ɩmʋ sʋ o kpee nɩɩ maa mʋ ba naa bʋga bee gyii.

eng: When the Pharisee saw that Jesus didnt ritually purify his hands by washing before the meal, he was astonished.
local: Amaa ɩ waa Kafarasii‐nyɩn mʋ kanankʋ, fɛɛ Yesu maa man fwɩɩ mʋ abaa fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ anaana tɔ ɩ kaapʋ mʋ pɔyɩ nɩɩ ee gyii.

eng: The Lord said to him, Now, you Pharisees clean the outside of the cup and platter, but your insides are stuffed with greed and wickedness.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu mʋ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Fanɛ Farasii abi falɛ nɩɩ fen du, fɛn fwɩɩ nwɛ‐bi yɛ ɩlapɛ nmaa, amaa kumu tɔ ɔlʋn yɛ kensi payɩ libi ɩ bɔla fanɛ tɔ.

eng: Foolish people! Didnt the one who made the outside also make the inside?.
local: Fanɛ amelensipu nɩmʋ! Fan man nyi fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ pɔyɩ kɩmʋ kamaa yɛ ɔtɔ tɔ mʋ, kpini asɩn i tiri sa mʋ?.

eng: Therefore, give to those in need from the core of who you are and you will be clean all over.
local: Amaa fan taa ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ fanɛ nwɛ‐bi yɛ ɩlapɛ mʋ tɔ sa atiripu, kɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa kɩnɩmʋ ɩ waa kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ sa fanɛ.

eng: How terrible for you Pharisees! You give a tenth of your mint, rue, and garden herbs of all kinds, while neglecting justice and love for God. These you ought to have done without neglecting the others.
local: Fanɛ Farasii abi fanɛ laakʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ fan barɩgɛ fanɛ adɔɔ‐gyitɔ ntun kudu nɩɩ fan taa ketun kʋlʋn fɛn sa Ɩbwaarɛ. Amaa fan kina fen mee gyii asɩn ɩmʋ ɔkpa sʋ, nɩɩ fen mee biti Ɩbwaarɛ. Ɩ dagaa fɛɛ fan daa kɩwaa ɩnɩmʋ, kɛ fan taa ketun kʋlʋn mʋ sa Ɩbwaarɛ tii sʋ.

eng: How terrible for you Pharisees! You love the most prominent seats in the synagogues and respectful greetings in the marketplaces.
local: Fanɛ Farasii abi fanɛ laakʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ fen biti ɩkyɩnakpa kparɛ‐kparɛ Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɩgyaŋɛkpa, nɩɩ fen biti bɛɛrɛɛ afwaala agya tɔ ipula.

eng: How terrible for you! You are like unmarked graves, and people walk on them without recognizing it.
local: Fanɛ laakʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ fen du nɩn fɛɛ nkyan mʋ nɩɩ ba man daasɛ ɩmʋ fɛɛ nkyan, nɩɩ asa ba naa ɩmʋ sʋ yɛgɛ ba man nyi fɛɛ ɩ gyɛ nkyan.

eng: One of the legal experts responded, Teacher, when you say these things, you are insulting us too.
local: Nɩɩ nbara ɔkaapʋpʋ onyipu kʋ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɔkaapʋpʋ, ɩmʋ nɩɩ fɩɩ tɔwɛ falɛ mʋ, fɩɩ saalɛ anɛ gbaa anɛ tii sʋ.

eng: Jesus said, How terrible for you legal experts too! You load people down with impossible burdens and you refuse to lift a single finger to help them.
local: Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Fanɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ anyipu, fanɛ laakʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ fan taa ɩsʋla budusa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋsɩn kasʋla mʋ sʋla asa. Nɩɩ fanɛ gbaa‐gbaa fanɛ fan mɛɛ taa kɩbaa‐bi kʋlʋn gbaa kyɛ bamʋ tɔ.

eng: How terrible for you! You built memorials to the prophets, whom your ancestors killed.
local: Fanɛ laakʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ fan pɔyɩ nkyan sa Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ, nɩɩ fanɛ naana ana ɩ gyɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba mɔɔ bamʋ mʋ.

eng: In this way, you testify that you approve of your ancestors deeds. They killed the prophets, and you build memorials!.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ɩnɩmʋ ɩ lɛɛ kaapʋ kesintin fɛɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ fanɛ naana ana ba waa mʋ, fan kɔɔlɛ kyula. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ba mɔɔ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ mʋ nɩɩ fanɛ mɔɔ fan pɔyɩ bamʋ nkyan.

eng: Therefore, Gods wisdom has said, I will send prophets and apostles to them and they will harass and kill some of them.
local: Lɩɩ ɩnɩmʋ sʋ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ lɩɩ mʋ kanyaasɩn tɔ yɛ, N biti n sun mɛ atɔwɛpʋ yɛ mi asunpu ba kpee bamʋ asɛ, kɛ ba mɔɔ mɛ asa mʋ akʋ kɛ ba nyanla asansa.

eng: As a result, this generation will be charged with the murder of all the prophets since the beginning of time.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ dɛ sa asa mʋ nɩɩ ba mɔɔ mʋ atɔwɛpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba kyɩna kasɛ sʋ lɩɩ koo dulinyaa npiili mʋ, ee biti ɔ taa kanɩn kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ mʋ bɩɩtɛ ndaga asa libi kakyɩna nɩmʋ tɔ.

eng: This includes the murder of every prophet—from Abel to Zechariah—who was killed between the altar and the holy place. Yes, Im telling you, this generation will be charged with it.
local: Asa mʋ nɩɩ ba mɔɔ lɩɩ Abeli sʋ naa fʋʋ Sakariya mʋ nɩɩ ba mɔɔ mʋ kaswɩɩ yɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ nsana mʋ, n tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin fɛɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ taa amɔɔpʋ mʋ kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ mʋ ɔ bɩɩtɛ fanɛ ndaga kakyɩna tɔ asa libi banɩmʋ.

eng: How terrible for you legal experts! You snatched away the key of knowledge. You didnt enter yourselves, and you stood in the way of those who were entering.
local: Fanɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ anyipu, fanɛ laakʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ fan taa ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ asa bee biti ba bɔla sʋ bɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ baala. Nɩɩ fanɛ gbaa‐gbaa fanɛ fan man naa ɔkpa maa sʋ, nɩɩ fen tii bamʋ nɩɩ bee biti ba natɛ kɩmʋ sʋ mʋ ɔkpa.

eng: As he left there, the legal experts and Pharisees began to resent him deeply and to ask him pointed questions about many things.
local: Yesu maa lɩɩ tʋtɔ mʋ, nɩɩ Farasii abi yɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ ba piili bɛɛ pwɩɩ mʋ kenken, yɛgɛ bɛɛ taasɛ mʋ asɩn lɩɩ atɔ gaalaagaa sʋ.

eng: They plotted against him, trying to trap him in his words.
local: yɛgɛ ba gyoo fɛɛ bee biti ba nyɛ mʋ kɔnɔ tɔ asɩn tɔwɛsa akʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ man dagaa kɛ ba kɩtaa mʋ.

eng: Soon afterward, Jesus traveled through the cities and villages, preaching and proclaiming the good news of Gods kingdom. The Twelve were with him.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ kamaa mʋ, Yesu natɛ ndɛ lala yɛ ndɛ‐bii sʋ ɛɛ tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa lɩɩ kuwura‐gyii mʋ sʋ, yɛgɛ abɩɩlapʋ kudu anyɔ mʋ ba tii mʋ sʋ.

eng: along with some women who had been healed of evil spirits and sicknesses. Among them were Mary Magdalene.
local: Nɩɩ akyɩɩ ɔkʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gya n‐yaayu libi lɩɩ bamʋ tɔ nɩɩ ɔ kyɛ bamʋ alɔ mʋ, gbaa ba buu mʋ. Bamʋ ɔkʋ ɩ gyɛ Mɛɛrɩ mʋ nɩɩ Yesu gya nbwɩɩ libi asunɔ lɩɩ mʋ tɔ mʋ.

eng: Joanna , Susanna, and many others who provided for them out of their resources.
local: yɛ Gyowana mʋ nɩɩ mu‐kuli ɩ gyɛ Kyusa. Mʋ ɩ gyɛ owura Herodi kɩkpaara sʋ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ, yɛ Susana yɛ akʋ. Akyɩɩ anɩmʋ ɩ sʋ bamʋ gbaa‐gbaa atɔ bɛɛ kyɛ Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ tɔ.

eng: When a great crowd was gathering and people were coming to Jesus from one city after another, he spoke to them in a parable.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ asa bɛɛ lɩɩ ndɛ sʋ ndɛ sʋ ba ba gyaŋɛ Yesu asɛ, nɩɩ ba ba nyɛ akyɔ mʋ, Yesu bɔ bamʋ kɩŋasan yɛ.

eng: A farmer went out to scatter his seed. As he was scattering it, some fell on the path where it was crushed, and the birds in the sky came and ate it.
local: Ɔdɔɔpʋ kʋ ɩ kyɩna kpee ɔ naa ŋmatɛ ayu. Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ ŋmatɛ ayu mʋ ɩmʋ ɩkʋ ɩ tɩyɛ ɔkpa tɔ nɩɩ asa ba natɛ ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ nbwiibi ba ba tasɛ gyii.

eng: Other seed fell on rock. As it grew, it dried up because it had no moisture.
local: Nɩɩ ɩkʋ ɩ tɩyɛ afʋlɛ sʋ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɩsɛ ɩ man kyɔ sa ɩmʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ ɩ maa kɔyɩ mʋ, ɩmʋ ilin ɩ man taalɛ kpee kata. Ɩmʋ sʋ kyɔwɛ maa daa ɩmʋ mʋ nɩɩ i fugya.

eng: Other seed fell among thorny plants. The thorns grew with the plants and choked them.
local: Nɩɩ ɩkʋ mɔɔ ɩ tɩyɛ iwii tɔ nɩɩ ɩmaa ɩmʋ ɩ bʋga dan nɩɩ iwii mʋ ɩ dan nyan ɩmʋ.

eng: Still other seed landed on good soil. When it grew, it produced one hundred times more grain than was scattered. As he said this, he called out, Everyone who has ears should pay attention.
local: Nɩɩ abi akʋ mɔɔ ɩ tɩyɛ kasɛ danbɩrasa tɔ nɩɩ ɩ kɔyɩ dan. Nɩɩ ɩ dan sɔyɩ abi kɩlɩfa‐kɩlɩfa. Maa tɔwɛ ɩnɩmʋ lʋwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ o diyaa mʋ ɔbʋlɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ asʋ nɩɩ ee biti o nu mʋ, o nu.

eng: His disciples asked him what this parable meant.
local: Nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba taasɛ mʋ kɩŋasan mʋ kasɛ.

eng: He said, You have been given the mysteries of Gods kingdom, but these mysteries come to everyone else in parables so that when they see, they cant see, and when they hear, they cant understand .
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Fanɛ bɩrɛ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ mu kuwura‐gyii mʋ asiri asɩn kaapʋ fanɛ, amaa asansa bɩrɛ aŋasan tɔ nɩɩ n biti n tɔwɛ sa bamʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ, bee biti ba ba bɛɛ kɩɩ, amaa ba mɛɛ yɛ ba wu, kɛ ba ba bee nu, amaa ba mɛɛ yɛ ba nu kasɛ. .

eng: The parable means this: The seed is Gods word.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ yɛ, Kɩŋasan mʋ kasɛ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, abi mʋ nɩɩ ɔ ŋmatɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ abwaarɛsɩn mʋ.

eng: The seed on the path are those who hear, but then the devil comes and steals the word from their hearts so that they wont believe and be saved.
local: Nɩɩ ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ tɩyɛ ɔkpa tɔ mʋ, ɩ gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bee nu asɩn mʋ, nɩɩ Sɩtaanɛ ɩ lɛɛ ɩmʋ ɛɛ lɩɩ bamʋ nkɔlɔ tɔ, nɩɩ ba mɛɛ taalɛ kɔɔlɛ gyii kɛ ba nyɛ kamɔlɩgɛ.

eng: The seed on the rock are those who receive the word joyfully when they hear it, but they have no root. They believe for a while but fall away when they are tempted.
local: Nɩɩ ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ tɩyɛ afʋlɛ sʋ mʋ, ɩ gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bee nu asɩn mʋ, nɩɩ bɛɛ taa kensi‐gyii kɔɔlɛ ɩmʋ. Amaa asɩn mʋ man loo bamʋ nkɔlɔ tɔ. Bamʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii kɩ mɛɛ kyɛɛrɛɛ, ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ kʋtʋʋ tɔ kɩɩ kɩ kan ba tu bamʋ de, bɛɛ lɩɩ tɩyɛ nɩn.

eng: As for the seed that fell among thorny plants, these are the ones who, as they go about their lives, are choked by the concerns, riches, and pleasures of life, and their fruit never matures.
local: Nɩɩ ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ tɩyɛ iwii tɔ mɔɔ mʋ ɩ yɩlɛ sa bamʋ nɩɩ bee nu asɩn mʋ, amaa lɩɩ kakyɩna tɔ nfɛɛrɛ kɩfɛ, kufulee biti yɛ kensi‐gyii bansa i kun bamʋ, nɩɩ ba mɛɛ taalɛ dan kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii tɔ.

eng: The seed that fell on good soil are those who hear the word and commit themselves to it with a good and upright heart. Through their resolve, they bear fruit.
local: Nɩɩ ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ tɩyɛ kasɛ danbɩrasa tɔ mʋ mɔɔ, ɩ gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba taa kɔkɔlɔ kʋlʋn kɔɔlɛ asɩn mʋ gyii, nɩɩ ba sʋ kenyiita yɩlɛ tɔ kɛ ba waa adanbɩrasa.

eng: No one lights a lamp and then covers it with a bowl or puts it under a bed. Instead, they put it on top of a lampstand so that those who enter can see the light.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu bɩla tɔwɛ bamʋ kɩŋasan kʋ yɛ, Ɔkʋ mee tuwa fɩtɩla kɛ ɔ taa waa kolobi tɔ abɛɛ ɔ taa yii npaa kasɛ, amaa ɛɛ taa ɛɛ gyaga kɩmʋ ɔgyagakpa nɩn, kɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ ba mʋ ba wu fɩtɩla mʋ.

eng: Nothing is hidden that wont be exposed. Nor is anything concealed that wont be made known and brought to the light.
local: Nɩɩ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ baala gbaa kii biti kɩ lɩɩ ifuli, nɩɩ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ asiri atɔ kpini ii biti ɩ lɩɩ ifuli.

eng: Therefore, listen carefully. Those who have will receive more, but as for those who dont have, even what they seem to have will be taken away from them.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan baa fan da sʋ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fen nu asɩn, nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ee nu asɩn kasɛ mʋ, ii biti ɩ sa mʋ tii sʋ, nɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ o mee nu asɩn mʋ kasɛ mʋ, ii biti ɩ kɔɔlɛ ipii mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ mʋ gbaa lɩɩ mʋ asɛ.

eng: Jesus mother and brothers came to him but were unable to reach him because of the crowd.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu mu‐nyi yɛ mu‐supu ana ba ba yɛ ba be wu mʋ, amaa lɩɩ sakyɔ sʋ mʋ, ba man nyɛ nin tiri mʋ.

eng: Someone told him, Your mother and brothers are standing outside, wanting to see you.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔkʋ tɔwɛ Yesu yɛ, Fu‐nyi yɛ fu‐supu ana ba yɩlɛ kewu bee biti fɛɛ ba wu fʋ.

eng: He replied, My mother and brothers are those who listen to Gods word and do it.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ mʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Mi‐nyi yɛ mi‐supu ana ɩ gyɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ bee nu abwaarɛsɩn nɩɩ bɛɛ sʋ bɛɛ waa kusun mʋ.

eng: One day Jesus and his disciples boarded a boat. He said to them, Lets cross over to the other side of the lake. So they set sail.
local: Kakaakʋ nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ, Fan yɛgɛ a faa kpee benbe. Nɩɩ ba loo ɔkʋlɩ tɔ faa kyʋn.

eng: While they were sailing, he fell asleep. Gale-force winds swept down on the lake. The boat was filling up with water and they were in danger.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ palɛ bee kpee mʋ Yesu dɛ nsɩɩrɛ. Nɩɩ afuu lala akʋ a tigi lɩɩ bamʋ sʋ, nɩɩ apaa a da a loo ɔkʋlɩ mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ ɩ san gbɛrɛɛ kɛ ɔkʋlɩ mʋ o muuri, nɩɩ bamʋ nkɔlɔ ɩ ŋmaa bamʋ.

eng: So they went and woke Jesus, shouting, Master, Master, were going to drown! But he got up and gave orders to the wind and the violent waves. The storm died down and it was calm.
local: Nɩɩ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba naa kyiŋi mʋ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ! An biti a sii nkyu tɔ! Nɩɩ ɔ kʋsʋ yɩlɛ, pʋntɛ afuu mʋ yɛ apaa mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɩ waa diin.

eng: He said to his disciples, Where is your faith? Filled with awe and wonder, they said to each other, Who is this? He commands even the winds and the water, and they obey him.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ, Fanɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii bʋ fɩnɛ? Nɩɩ kufuu kɩ kɩtaa bamʋ, nɩɩ i kyinkyin bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ taasɛ abaa yɛ, Nyɩmɩsa mɔɔ ndee nɩɩ ɛɛ sa afuu yɛ apaa kɔnɔ nɩɩ i nu mʋ asɛ.

eng: Jesus and his disciples sailed to the Gerasenes land, which is across the lake from Galilee.
local: Nɩɩ ba palɛ naa fʋʋ Gerasene abi ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ, kɩbaafʋn mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ Galile benbe.

eng: As soon as Jesus got out of the boat, a certain man met him. The man was from the city and was possessed by demons. For a long time, he had lived among the tombs, naked and homeless.
local: Yesu maa dan ɛɛ lɩɩ ɛɛ yɩlɛ ɔbʋn kaa mʋ, nɩɩ Kagerasene‐nyɩn kʋ mʋ nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi ba bʋ mʋ tɔ mʋ ba gyaŋa mʋ. Ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ natɛ kubolonkpan kyɛɛrɛɛ yɛgɛ ɔ mɛɛ kyɩna kɩkpaara sʋ, amɔɔ nkyan tɔ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ.

eng: When he saw Jesus, he shrieked and fell down before him. Then he shouted, What have you to do with me, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? I beg you, dont torture me.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ wu Yesu mʋ, ɔ pʋntɛ tɩyɛ mʋ ayaa tɔ, nɩɩ o kuusi kenken tɔwɛ yɛ, Yesu, sʋsʋ Owura Lala mu‐bii! Mɩnɛ nɩɩ fii biti mɛ asɛ? N kʋlɛ fʋ nɩn, man nyanla mɛ.

eng: He said this because Jesus had already commanded the unclean spirit to come out of the man. Many times it had taken possession of him, so he would be bound with leg irons and chains and placed under guard. But he would break his restraints, and the demon would force him into the wilderness.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Yesu wʋla sa nbwɩɩ libi mʋ kɔnɔ fɛɛ ba lɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ tɔ. Saŋa kʋmaa nɩmʋ nbwɩɩ libi mʋ bɛɛ ba mʋ sʋ. Nɩɩ ɩ kan ba mʋ sʋ mʋ, bɛɛ taa agbeeleebi ŋmina mʋ abaa yɛ mʋ ayaa, yɛgɛ bee kun mʋ. Amaa ɛɛ tɩŋɛ agbeeleebi mʋ nɩɩ ba taa ŋmina mʋ mʋ, nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi mʋ bɛɛ sʋla mʋ kpee ofuli sʋ.

eng: Jesus asked him, What is your name? Legion, he replied, because many demons had entered him.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Nɛnɛ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ fʋ? Nɩɩ ɔ yɛ bɛɛ tɩɩ mɛ yɛ, Kʋbʋ! Lɩɩ fɛɛ nbwɩɩ libi kʋkyɔ ba tɛ mʋ sʋ.

eng: They pleaded with him not to order them to go back into the abyss.
local: Nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi mʋ ba kʋlɛ Yesu yɛ, ɔ man taa bamʋ naa waa ɔbɔ kyinkyingilin mʋ nɩɩ kɩ man sʋ ɔkaa mʋ tɔ.

eng: A large herd of pigs was feeding on the hillside. The demons begged Jesus to let them go into the pigs. Jesus gave them permission.
local: Nɩɩ akuuri damantɛ akʋ ba bʋ kɩbɩɩ mʋ sʋ bee gyii. Nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi mʋ ba kʋlɛ Yesu fɛɛ ɔ yɛgɛ ba naa loo akuuri mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ Yesu sa bamʋ ɔkpa.

eng: and the demons left the man and entered the pigs. The herd rushed down the cliff into the lake and drowned.
local: Nbwɩɩ libi mʋ ba maa lɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ tɔ mʋ, ba naa loo akuuri mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ba sɩlɛ kpɩlɩgɛ kɩbɩɩ mʋ sʋ naa loo ɔbʋn lala mʋ tɔ nɩɩ ba muuri.

eng: When those who tended the pigs saw what happened, they ran away and told the story in the city and in the countryside.
local: Nɩɩ asa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ kɩɩ akuuri mʋ sʋ ba maa wu kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ waa mʋ, ba sɩlɛ kpee kadɛ tɔ yɛ ndɔɔ ana tɔ naa tɔwɛ asa.

eng: People came to see what had happened. They came to Jesus and found the man from whom the demons had gone. He was sitting at Jesus feet, fully dressed and completely sane. They were filled with awe.
local: Nɩɩ asa mʋ ba ba kɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ waa. Ba maa ba Yesu asɛ mʋ, ba wu ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi ba kʋsʋ mʋ sʋ mʋ, o bun kaalɛ ɔ tɛ Yesu ayaa kasɛ yɛgɛ mʋ nfɛɛrɛ tɔ i du kyɛɛkyɛɛ, nɩɩ kufuu kɩ kɩtaa bamʋ.

eng: Those people who had actually seen what had happened told them how the demon-possessed man had been delivered.
local: Bamʋ nɩɩ ba taa bamʋ ansi wu mʋ, ba tɔwɛ asa kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi ba daa ba tɛ mʋ sʋ mʋ nyɛ kɩkyɛ.

eng: Then everyone gathered from the region of the Gerasenes asked Jesus to leave their area because they were overcome with fear. So he got into the boat and returned across the lake.
local: Kanɩn sʋ Gerasene abi mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ kɩbaafʋn maŋa sʋ kpini, ba kʋlɛ Yesu yɛ ɔ lɩɩ bamʋ ɔsʋwʋlɛ maŋa sʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ kufuu kɩ kɩtaa bamʋ. Nɩɩ o loo ɔkʋlɩ tɔ bwii.

eng: The man from whom the demons had gone begged to come along with Jesus as one of his disciples. Jesus sent him away, saying.
local: Pɔyɩ kɛ ba bwii mʋ, ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi ba kʋsʋ mʋ sʋ mʋ kʋlɛ Yesu yɛ ɔ yɛgɛ o buu mʋ. Amaa Yesu kina tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ.

eng: Return home and tell the story of what God has done for you. So he went throughout the city proclaiming what Jesus had done for him.
local: Bwii kpee kewu naa tɔwɛ kʋtɔ lala mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ waa sa fʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔnyɩn mʋ kpee kadɛ mʋ tɔ katɩn kʋmaa nɩmʋ naa tɔwɛ kʋtɔ lala mʋ nɩɩ Yesu waa sa mʋ.

eng: When Jesus returned, the crowd welcomed him, for they had been waiting for him.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Yesu bwii faa kpee ɔbʋn benbe mʋ, sakyɔ mʋ ba waa mʋ nsa‐nsa kɔɔlɛ mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ba wʋla ba tɛ ba gyoo mʋ.

eng: A man named Jairus, who was a synagogue leader, came and fell at Jesus feet. He pleaded with Jesus to come to his house.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Gyarosi, mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ ba tɩyɛ Yesu ayaa kasɛ, nɩɩ ɔ kʋlɛ mʋ yɛ ɔ ba mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ.

eng: because his only daughter, a twelve-year-old, was dying. As Jesus moved forward, he faced smothering crowds.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ mu kebii kyɩɩsa‐bi kʋlʋn mʋ nɩɩ o gyii nsu kudu anyɔ ii biti o wu. Yesu maa yii sʋ ee kpee mʋ, sakyɔ mʋ mili mʋ sʋ ba buu mʋ.

eng: A woman was there who had been bleeding for twelve years. She had spent her entire livelihood on doctors, but no one could heal her.
local: Ɔkyɩɩ kʋ bʋ sakyɔ mʋ tɔ nɩɩ nkalan ɩ da mʋ nsu kudu anyɔ, amaa ɔkʋ man taalɛ kyɛ mʋ.

eng: She came up behind him and touched the hem of his clothes, and at once her bleeding stopped.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ loo sakyɔ mʋ tɔ ba Yesu kamaa ba mata mʋ kaalɛ kɔnɔ‐bi, nɩɩ opula maŋa tɔ mʋ nkalan mʋ ɩ tɩn.

eng: Who touched me? Jesus asked. When everyone denied it, Peter said, Master, the crowds are surrounding you and pressing in on you.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ yɛ, Anɩmʋ ɩ mata mɛ? Bamʋ kpini ba maa kina yɛ ɩ man gyɛ bamʋ mʋ, nɩɩ Pita tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, sakyɔ bee mili fʋ sʋ, bɛɛ mata fʋ.

eng: But Jesus said, Someone touched me. I know that power has gone out from me.
local: Amaa Yesu tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔsa kʋ mata mɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ n wu nɩɩ n bɩɩ fɛɛ ɔlʋn kʋ maa lɩɩ mɛ tɔ.

eng: When the woman saw that she couldnt escape notice, she came trembling and fell before Jesus. In front of everyone, she explained why she had touched him and how she had been immediately healed.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ maa wu fɛɛ ɔ mɛɛ taalɛ taa baala mʋ, ɔ kyɩkyaa ba tɩyɛ Yesu ansi tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ yɩlɛ sakyɔ mʋ tɔ tɔwɛ kʋtɔ mʋ sʋ nɩɩ ɔ mata Yesu, yɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ kɩkyɛ opula maŋa tɔ.

eng: Daughter, your faith has healed you, Jesus said. Go in peace.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ yɛ, Mi‐bii kyɩɩsa, fʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii kɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ fʋ nyɛ alanfɩya, kpewu kayɩɩ yuuli tɔ.

eng: While Jesus was still speaking, someone came from the synagogue leaders house, saying to Jairus, Your daughter has died. Dont bother the teacher any longer.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Yesu san ɛɛ tɔwɛ kanɩn mʋ, nɩɩ kabɔɔ kɩ lɩɩ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa ɔbɩlɩsa Gyarosi kɩkpaara sʋ, ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Man bɩla tɔɔrɔɔ ɔkaapʋpʋ mʋ, fu kebii kyɩɩsa‐bi mʋ wu.

eng: When Jesus heard this, he responded, Dont be afraid; just keep trusting, and she will be healed.
local: Yesu maa nu kanɩn mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ Gyarosi yɛ, Man waa kufuu, fɛɛ fʋ dan nyɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii ee biti ɔ nyɛ alanfɩya.

eng: When he came to the house, he didnt allow anyone to enter with him except Peter, John, and James, and the childs father and mother.
local: Yesu fʋʋ Gyarosi kɩkpaara sʋ mʋ, ɔ man yɛgɛ nɩɩ ɔkʋ buu mʋ loo obu to, amɔɔ Pita maa Gyɔn yɛ Gyemisi yɛ kebii mʋ, mʋ‐sɛ yɛ mu‐nyi nkʋn.

eng: They were all crying and mourning for her, but Jesus said, Dont cry. She isnt dead. Shes only sleeping.
local: Saŋa maa kpini yɛgɛ asa bɛɛ sawʋ bɛɛ waa ɩlawʋ, bee su kebii mʋ keli. Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ yɛ, Fan yɛgɛ kusu, ɔ man wu, ɔ dɛ nɩn.

eng: They laughed at him because they knew she was dead.
local: Nɩɩ asa mʋ ba ŋmasɛ Yesu, lɩɩ fɛɛ be nyi kebii mʋ maa wu.

eng: Taking her hand, Jesus called out, Child, get up.
local: Amaa Yesu kɩtaa kebii mʋ kɩbaa tɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Mi‐bii, kʋsʋ.

eng: Her life returned and she got up at once. He directed them to give her something to eat.
local: Nɩɩ kebii mʋ, mʋ kala bwii ba mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ kʋsʋ yɩlɛ opula maŋa tɔ. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, ba sa kebii mʋ agyitɔ kɛ o gyii.

eng: Her parents were beside themselves with joy, but he ordered them to tell no one what had happened.
local: Nɩɩ ɩ waa kebii mʋ akʋʋgɛpʋ kɩyan, amaa nɩɩ ɔ da bamʋ amu tɔ yɛ ba man waa kɛ ba tɔwɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɩ waa falɛ sa ɔkʋ.

eng: Some who were present on that occasion told Jesus about the Galileans whom Pilate had killed while they were offering sacrifices.
local: Kanɩn saŋa maŋa tɔ asa akʋ ba ba tɔwɛ Yesu yɛ Galile abi akʋ ba taa atɔbwaaya ba bɛɛ lɛɛ saraga bɛɛ sa kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ Paleti yɛgɛ nɩɩ ba mɔɔ bamʋ, nɩɩ bamʋ nkalan ɩ bʋga yɛ atɔbwaaya nkalan maa tɔ.

eng: He replied, Do you think the suffering of these Galileans proves that they were more sinful than all the other Galileans?.
local: Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Fan nyi fɛɛ kanɩn Galile abi maŋa awʋrʋfɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba gyii mʋ, ɩ kaapʋ nɩɩ fɛɛ ba gyɛ alibi awaapʋ ba kyɔ Galile abi kpini?.

eng: No, I tell you, but unless you change your hearts and lives, you will die just as they did.
local: Ayee! N tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ nɩɩ fan man kan lɛɛ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ lɩɩ alibi tɔ bɩrɛ, fanɛ gbaa fanɛ fen biti fan wu fɛɛ bamʋ.

eng: What about those eighteen people who were killed when the tower of Siloam fell on them? Do you think that they were more guilty of wrongdoing than everyone else who lives in Jerusalem?.
local: Abɛɛ asa kudu aburuwa mʋ nɩɩ Silon kɩbansʋrʋ kyɩna tɩyɛ bamʋ sʋ nɩɩ ba wu mʋ, ɩɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ bamʋ ɩ kyɩna waa alibi ba kyɔ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Gyerusalem kpini saŋa maŋa?.

eng: No, I tell you, but unless you change your hearts and lives, you will die just as they did.
local: Ayee! N tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ nɩɩ fan man kan lɛɛ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ lɩɩ alibi tɔ bɩrɛ, fanɛ gbaa fanɛ kpini fen biti fan wu fɛɛ bamʋ.

eng: Jesus told this parable: A man owned a fig tree planted in his vineyard. He came looking for fruit on it and found none.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔ bɔ bamʋ kɩŋasan nɩmʋ yɛ, Ɔnyɩn kʋ ɩ kyɩna duu figi oyu mʋ kʋdɔɔ tɔ, nɩɩ o kpee ɔ naa biti kɩmʋ abi amaa ɔ man nyɛ.

eng: He said to his gardener, Look, Ive come looking for fruit on this fig tree for the past three years, and Ive never found any. Cut it down! Why should it continue depleting the soils nutrients.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kɩɩ kʋdɔɔ mʋ sʋ yɛ, Ɩ waa fɛɛ nsu nsa ndee n maa ba n ba biti abi oyu nɩmʋ sʋ yɛgɛ n mɛɛ nyɛ. Ŋɛ kɩmʋ tʋʋ! Mɩnɛ nɩɩ kɩ yɩlɛ kɩ waa kasɛ nɩmʋ sʋ.

eng: The gardener responded, Lord, give it one more year, and I will dig around it and give it fertilizer.
local: Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ yɛgɛ kɩmʋ kɛ kɩ yɩlɛ kesu kʋlʋn tii sʋ. N biti n kuu kyaabɔɔ kɩmʋ kɛ n waa kɩmʋ atɔbwaaya ibini.

eng: Maybe it will produce fruit next year; if not, then you can cut it down.
local: Nɩɩ kɩ kan sɔyɩ abi kesu kakʋ mʋ, ɩ bʋrɔn, nɩɩ kɩ kan kɩ man sɔyɩ bɩrɛ kɛ fʋ ŋɛ kɩmʋ tʋʋ.

eng: Jesus was teaching in one of the synagogues on the Sabbath.
local: Kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ kʋ Yesu ɩ kaapʋ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa kʋ.

eng: A woman was there who had been disabled by a spirit for eighteen years. She was bent over and couldnt stand up straight.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ kabwɩɩ libi ki biliŋaa mʋ waa boobi nsu kudu aburuwa mʋ bʋ tʋtɔ. Kabwɩɩ libi mʋ bɩɩtɛ mʋ muŋa nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ taalɛ tɛɛŋɛ tɔ kpini‐kpini.

eng: When he saw her, Jesus called her to him and said, Woman, you are set free from your sickness.
local: Yesu maa wu mʋ mʋ, ɔ tɩɩ mʋ ba ansi tɔ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɔkyɩɩ, fʋ nyɛ alanfɩya.

eng: He placed his hands on her and she straightened up at once and praised God.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ abaa gyaga mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ opula maŋa tɔ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ kyiigi tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ yɩn Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: The synagogue leader, incensed that Jesus had healed on the Sabbath, responded, There are six days during which work is permitted. Come and be healed on those days, not on the Sabbath day.
local: Lɩɩ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Yesu kyɛ ɔlɔpʋ kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ mʋ sʋ mʋ, Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ nyɛ agbʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ asa mʋ yɛ, Nkɛ nsiye ɩ gyɛ kusun nkɛ, ɩmʋ sʋ fan ba kanɩn nkɛ maŋa tɔ kɛ ba kyɛ fanɛ alɔ, man gyɛ kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ.

eng: The Lord replied, Hypocrites! Dont each of you on the Sabbath untie your ox or donkey from its stall and lead it out to get a drink?.
local: Nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu lɛɛ mʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Fanɛ kebunbun ansi wuya ana! Fanɛ anɩmʋ ɩ mɛɛ saaŋɛ mʋ naatɛ‐nyɩnsa abɛɛ mu kuruma kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ, nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ sʋ mʋ ee kpee ɛɛ sa mʋ nkyu?.

eng: Then isnt it necessary that this woman, a daughter of Abraham, bound by Satan for eighteen long years, be set free from her bondage on the Sabbath day.
local: Ɔkyɩɩ nɩmʋ gyɛ Abraham kanaana‐bii mʋ nɩɩ Sɩtaanɛ ŋmina mʋ nsu kudu aburuwa. Mɩnɛ waa nɩɩ ɩ man dagaa maa nyɛ kamɔlɩgɛ kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ lɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ki nyita mʋ sʋ.

eng: When he said these things, all his opponents were put to shame, but all those in the crowd rejoiced at all the extraordinary things he was doing.
local: Maa tɔwɛ falɛ lʋwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ipeeli ɩ kɩtaa mʋ akisipu kpini. Amaa asa mʋ bɩrɛ ansi i gyii lɩɩ adangana damantɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa mʋ sʋ.

eng: Jesus asked, What is Gods kingdom like? To what can I compare it?.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ yɛ, Nɛnɛ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ ki du? Mɩnɛ nɩɩ n biti n taa kɩmʋ kaala?.

eng: Its like a mustard seed that someone took and planted in a garden. It grew and developed into a tree and the birds in the sky nested in its branches.
local: Ki du nɩn fɛɛ gowa kibi mʋ nɩɩ ɔdɔɔpʋ taa du mʋ kʋdɔɔ tɔ nɩɩ kɩ dan biliŋi oyu, nɩɩ nbwiibi bɛɛ ba bɛɛ kyɩna kɩmʋ n‐yaabi sʋ.

eng: Again he said, To what can I compare Gods kingdom?.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ bɩlaa bwii taasɛ yɛ, Mɩnɛ nɩɩ n biti n taa Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ kaala?.

eng: Its like yeast, which a woman took and hid in a bushel of wheat flour until the yeast had worked its way through the whole.
local: Ki du nɩn fɛɛ kɩfɛ tinsa mʋ nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ taa bʋga kyagɩlɛ bodobodo nnyifo damantɛ tɔ, kɛ kɩfɛ tinsa mʋ ɩɩ yɛgɛ nnyifo maŋa kpini i tin.

eng: Jesus traveled through cities and villages, teaching and making his way to Jerusalem.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu maa kpee Gyerusalem mʋ, ɔ bɔla ndɛ yɛ ndɛ‐bii mʋ sʋ ɛɛ kaapʋ abwaarɛsɩn.

eng: Someone said to him, Lord, will only a few be saved? Jesus said to them.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, amɔɔ asa gbɛrɛɛ ii biti ba ba nyɛ kamɔlɩgɛ? Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ.

eng: Make every effort to enter through the narrow gate. Many, I tell you, will try to enter and wont be able to.
local: Fan kpan fanɛ n‐yɩɩ kenken kɛ fan bɔla kebunɔ‐bii mʋ tɔ loo to. N tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ asa gaalaagaa bee biti ba kpan bamʋ n‐yɩɩ, amaa ba mɛɛ yɛ ba taalɛ.

eng: Once the owner of the house gets up and shuts the door, then you will stand outside and knock on the door, saying, Lord, open the door for us. He will reply, I dont know you or where you are from.
local: Nɩɩ kɩkpaara wuya kan kʋsʋ tii kebunɔ mʋ, fen biti fan ba yɩlɛ kewusunɔ fen daa ɔpʋnʋ yɛgɛ fɛn kʋlɛ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ tigi kebunɔ sa anɛ. Amaa ee biti ɔ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, N man nyi fanɛ, n man nyi katɩn mʋ nɩɩ fan lɩɩ.

eng: Then you will begin to say, We ate and drank in your presence, and you taught in our streets.
local: Fen biti fan tɔwɛ yɛ, Anaa fʋ ɩ bʋga gyii, nɩɩ an bʋga nuu, nɩɩ fʋ kaapʋ anɛ ndɛ tɔ.

eng: He will respond, I dont know you or where you are from. Go away from me, all you evildoers.
local: Amaa ee biti ɔ tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, N man nyi fanɛ abɛɛ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ fan lɩɩ. Fanɛ alibi awaapʋ kpini fan lɩɩ mɛ asɛ.

eng: There will be weeping and grinding of teeth when you see Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and all the prophets in Gods kingdom, but you yourselves will be thrown out.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ fen biti fan wu Abraham maa Isaki yɛ Gyekobu yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ mʋ kpini, ba maa bʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ fan man nyɛ ɔkpa loo to mʋ, tʋtɔ nɩɩ fen biti fan su kɛ fan wɛ fanɛ anyi tɔ.

eng: People will come from east and west, north and south, and sit down to eat in Gods kingdom.
local: Asa bee biti ba lɩɩ kyɔwɛ kɩlɩɩ yɛ kyɔwɛ kɩtɩyɛ, kɛ akʋ ba lɩɩ adii yɛ akpɩlɩgɛ, kɛ ba ba kyɩna bamʋ ɩkyɩnakpa Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii kensi‐gyii ngyaŋɛ mʋ tɔ.

eng: Look! Those who are last will be first and those who are first will be last.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ kamaa mʋ akʋ, bee biti ba ba gyankpaa, nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba gyankpaa mʋ mɔɔ akʋ bee biti ba sii kamaa.

eng: At that time, some Pharisees approached Jesus and said, Go! Get away from here, because Herod wants to kill you.
local: Saŋa maŋa tɔ nɩɩ Farasii abi akʋ ba ba Yesu asɛ ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Lɩɩ nfɩɩ kpee katɩn kʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ Owura Herodi ii biti fɛɛ ɔ mɔɔ fʋ.

eng: Jesus said to them, Go, tell that fox, Look, Im throwing out demons and healing people today and tomorrow, and on the third day I will complete my work.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Fan kpee naa tɔwɛ kanɩn gboogboo mʋ yɛ, n biti n gya nbwɩɩ libi kɛ n kyɛ asa ndaga yɛ ɔkɛ kɛ kakɛ sasapʋ mʋ n lʋwɛ mɛ kusun.

eng: However, its necessary for me to travel today, tomorrow, and the next day because its impossible for a prophet to be killed outside of Jerusalem.
local: Nɩɩ kanan kʋmaa sʋ mʋ, ɩ dagaa fɛɛ n maa kyaga mɛ ɔkpa sʋ n kpee ndaga yɛ ɔkɛ yɛ kakɛ sasapʋ. Ɩ waa nɛnɛ, Gyerusalem ɩ gyɛ kadɛ mʋ nɩɩ bee biti ba mɔɔ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ.

eng: Jerusalem, Jerusalem, you who kill the prophets and stone those who were sent to you! How often I have wanted to gather your people just as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you didnt want that.
local: O Gyerusalem abi, Gyerusalem abi, fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fan mɔɔ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ, nɩɩ fan tʋʋ bamʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sun fanɛ asɛ mʋ abuu. N kpan mɛ n‐yɩɩ saŋa kʋmaa fɛɛ n kʋʋla fanɛ waa mɛ ɔtɔ tɔ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ kakyaasɛ‐nyi maa bʋga mu‐bii ana mʋ atɛɛ tɔ mʋ. Amaa fan mɛɛ sa mɛ ɔkpa.

eng: Look, your house is abandoned. I tell you, you wont see me until the time comes when you say, Blessings on the one who comes in the Lords name .
local: Kɩɩ, fanɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ kii biti ki sii kɩ dɛ kɩkpan sa fanɛ. N tɔwɛ fanɛ, fanɛ man bɩla yɛ fan wu mɛ kakɛ akʋ, naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ fen biti fan tɔwɛ yɛ, Kusee ɩ gyɛ sa ɔmʋnɩ ɔ ba Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kɩtɩɩ tɔ sʋ.

eng: One Sabbath, when Jesus went to share a meal in the home of one of the leaders of the Pharisees, they were watching him closely.
local: Ɩ baa fɛɛ kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa kʋ nɩɩ Yesu kpee Farasii abi ɔbɩlɩsa kʋ ayɛ ɔ naa gyii, yɛgɛ bɛɛ pɛɛtɛ mʋ fɛɛ ee biti o nyita Gyiwu abi nbara mʋ.

eng: A man suffering from an abnormal swelling of the body was there.
local: Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn kʋ bʋ mu ansi tɔ tʋtɔ, ɛɛ lɔɔ kyankpan.

eng: Jesus asked the lawyers and Pharisees, Does the Law allow healing on the Sabbath or not.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ anyipu mʋ ba maa Farasii abi mʋ yɛ, Ɔkpa dɛ fɛɛ a kyɛ ɔkʋ kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ abɛɛ a man kyɛ.

eng: But they said nothing. Jesus took hold of the sick man, cured him, and then let him go.
local: Amaa ba man tɔwɛ sɛhn nɩɩ Yesu kɩtaa ɔnyɩn mʋ, kyɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ yɛgɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ natɛ.

eng: He said to them, Suppose your child or ox fell into a ditch on the Sabbath day. Wouldnt you immediately pull it out.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fɛɛ nɩɩ fanɛ ɔkʋ sʋ kebii abɛɛ naatɛ‐nyɩnsa nɩɩ ɔ tɩyɛ kitiribo tɔ kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ mʋ, fʋ mɛɛ yɛ fʋ lɛɛ mʋ.

eng: But they had no response.
local: Amaa ba man taalɛ lɛɛ ɩmʋ kɔnɔ.

eng: When Jesus noticed how the guests sought out the best seats at the table, he told them a parable.
local: Yesu maa wu fɛɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba tɩɩ bamʋ ogyikpa mʋ bɛɛ ŋmaarɛ ɩkyɩnakpa kparɛ‐kparɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ bɔ bamʋ kɩŋasan nɩmʋ yɛ.

eng: When someone invites you to a wedding celebration, dont take your seat in the place of honor. Someone more highly regarded than you could have been invited by your host.
local: Fɛɛ nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan tɩɩ fʋ mʋ kɩkyɩɩfɔ tɔ mʋ, man naa kyɩna ɔkyɩnakpa kparɛ, akʋtɔ fɛɛ ɔ tɩɩ ɔsa kparɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bɩla ɔ kyɔ fʋ.

eng: The host who invited both of you will come and say to you, Give your seat to this other person. Embarrassed, you will take your seat in the least important place.
local: Kɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ faa mʋ mʋ, ɔ ba tɔwɛ fʋ yɛ, Taa fʋ kagya sa ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ fii biti fʋ taa ipeeli naa kyɩna katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɩ man sʋ bɛɛrɛɛ.

eng: Instead, when you receive an invitation, go and sit in the least important place. When your host approaches you, he will say, Friend, move up here to a better seat. Then you will be honored in the presence of all your fellow guests.
local: Amaa nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan tɩɩ fʋ mʋ, naa kyɩna katɩn mʋ nɩɩ kɩ man sʋ bɛɛrɛɛ, kɛ ɔ ba tɔwɛ fʋ yɛ, Kyɛmɩnɛ, ba kyɩna kagya kparɛ sʋ nfɩɩ. Saŋa mʋ, fɩɩ nyɛ bɛɛrɛɛ lɩɩ fʋ tɔɔmaa ana mʋ nɩɩ ɔ da kɩbaa tɩɩ mʋ asɛ.

eng: All who lift themselves up will be brought low, and those who make themselves low will be lifted up.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kʋsaa mʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋsʋ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ bɩya mʋ kasɛ, nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bɩya mʋ n‐yɩɩ kasɛ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ kʋsaa mʋ sʋsʋ.

eng: Then Jesus said to the person who had invited him, When you host a lunch or dinner, dont invite your friends, your brothers and sisters, your relatives, or rich neighbors. If you do, they will invite you in return and that will be your reward.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ mʋ mʋ, mɔɔ yɛ, Nɩɩ fʋ kan tɩɩ asa yɛ ba be gyii kakyʋwapɛ abɛɛ kalaatɩpwɛ agyitɔ mʋ, man kan waa fʋ tɩɩ fʋ kyɛmɩnɛ ana abɛɛ fʋ‐daa abɛɛ fʋ kʋʋpʋ ana abɛɛ afulee wuya ana mʋ nɩɩ ba tɛ tiri fʋ mʋ. Nɩɩ fʋ kan waa kanɩn mʋ, akʋtɔ fɛɛ bee biti ba tɩɩ fʋ gbaa fʋ ba bamʋ lɛɛ tɔ kɛ ɩ ka fʋ kʋkɔ.

eng: Instead, when you give a banquet, invite the poor, crippled, lame, and blind.
local: Amaa nɩɩ fʋ kan dɩŋa kensi‐gyii agyitɔ mʋ, tɩɩ atiripu yɛ aboobi yɛ amʋgʋlɛ‐mʋgʋlɛ yɛ agyaatanapʋ.

eng: And you will be blessed because they cant repay you. Instead, you will be repaid when the just are resurrected.
local: kɛ fʋ nyɛ kusee, lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ ɩ mɛɛ taalɛ ka fʋ kʋkɔ. Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ ka fʋ kʋkɔ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ akyɩrɛkyɩrɛpʋ bee biti ba kʋsʋ lɩɩ lewu tɔ mʋ.

eng: When one of the dinner guests heard Jesus remarks, he said to Jesus, Happy are those who will feast in Gods kingdom.
local: Nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ maa bamʋ ba tɛ bee gyii mʋ ɔkʋ maa nu asɩn mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ Yesu yɛ, Kusee ɩ gyɛ nyɩmɩsa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti o gyii Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ.

eng: Jesus replied, A certain man hosted a large dinner and invited many people.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ mʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɔnyɩn kʋ ɩ dɩŋa kensi‐gyii agyitɔ nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ asa damantɛ yɛ ba be gyii.

eng: When it was time for the dinner to begin, he sent his servant to tell the invited guests, Come! The dinner is now ready.
local: Saŋa mʋ maa fʋʋ fɛɛ ba be gyii mʋ, nɩɩ o sun mʋ kɩyaafɔlɛ yɛ ɔ naa tɔwɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ bamʋ mʋ yɛ, ba waa kʋtɔ kʋmaa lʋwɛ, ɩmʋ sʋ ba ba.

eng: One by one, they all began to make excuses. The first one told him, I bought a farm and must go and see it. Please excuse me.
local: Nɩɩ bamʋ kpini ba piili bɛɛ tɔwɛ asɩn bɛɛ lɛɛ bamʋ n‐yɩɩ. Ɔgyankpaapʋ mʋ yɛ, N dan sɔɔ kasɛ nɩn, nɩɩ ɩ daaga fɛɛ n naa kɩɩ kɩmʋ, ɩmʋ sʋ n kʋlɛ fʋ nɩn n mɛɛ yɛ n taalɛ ba.

eng: Another said, I bought five teams of oxen, and Im going to check on them. Please excuse me.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ mɔɔ yɛ, N dan sɔɔ ɩnaatɛ‐nyɩnsa kudu nɩn, nɩɩ n dan kpee n naa taa bamʋ dɔɔ kɩɩ, ɩmʋ sʋ n mɛɛ yɛ n taalɛ ba.

eng: Another said, I just got married, so I cant come.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ mɔɔ bɩla yɛ, N dan kili ɔkyɩɩ nɩn, n mɛɛ yɛ n taalɛ ba.

eng: When he returned, the servant reported these excuses to his master. The master of the house became angry and said to his servant, Go quickly to the citys streets, the busy ones and the side streets, and bring the poor, crippled, blind, and lame.
local: Nɩɩ kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ taa asɩn mʋ ba tɔwɛ mʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ. Nɩɩ kɩkpaara wuya mʋ nyɛ agbʋ, nɩɩ ɔ sa mʋ kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Bilen, kpee kadɛ mʋ tɔ agbenbi sʋ yɛ ɩkpa‐bi naa bɩya atiripu yɛ aboobi yɛ agyaatanapʋ yɛ amʋgʋlɛ‐mʋgʋlɛ wuya ana.

eng: The servant said, Master, your instructions have been followed and there is still room.
local: Ɩ man kyɛɛrɛɛ nɩɩ kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ n waa fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fʋ sa kɔnɔ mʋ, amaa ɩkyɩnakpa san ɩ bʋ tɔ.

eng: The master said to the servant, Go to the highways and back alleys and urge people to come in so that my house will be filled.
local: Nɩɩ ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ bwii tɔwɛ mʋ kɩyaafɔlɛ yɛ, Kpe ɩkpa mʋ nɩɩ ii kpee ndɛ sʋ, yɛ ndɔɔ ana tɔ, kɛ fʋ yɛgɛ ba ba kɛ mɛ kɩkpaara kɩ bɔla.

eng: I tell you, not one of those who were invited will taste my dinner.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ fɛɛ, asa mʋ nɩɩ n tɩɩ bamʋ Sososi mʋ ɔkʋ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ daa mɛ kensi‐gyii agyitɔ mʋ kɩɩ. .

eng: Large crowds were traveling with Jesus. Turning to them, he said.
local: Yesu maa kʋbʋ damantɛ kʋkʋ ba maa naa mʋ, nɩɩ o biliŋi tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ.

eng: Whoever comes to me and doesnt hate father and mother, spouse and children, and brothers and sisters—yes, even ones own life—cannot be my disciple.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan ɛɛ ba mɛ asɛ, nɩɩ o mee biti mɛ ɔ kyɔ mʋ‐sɛ yɛ mu‐nyi, mʋ‐ka yɛ mu‐bii ana, mʋ‐daa ana yɛ mu‐supu ana, apɩɩkyɩɩ yɛ apɩɩnyɩn, halɩɩ mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mʋ nkpa dee, ɔ mɛɛ taalɛ waa mɛ obuupu.

eng: Whoever doesnt carry their own cross and follow me cannot be my disciple.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ sʋla mu oyu laasa, kɛ o buu mɛ mʋ, ɔ mɛɛ taalɛ waa mɛ obuupu.

eng: If one of you wanted to build a tower, wouldnt you first sit down and calculate the cost, to determine whether you have enough money to complete it?.
local: Fɛɛ nɩɩ fanɛ ɔkʋ ii biti ɔ pɔyɩ kɩkpaara lala mʋ, nɛnɛ nɩɩ ɛɛ waa? Pɔyɩ kɛ o piili mʋ ɔ mɛɛ gyankpaa kɩɩ mu afulee mʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ taa pɔyɩ, kɛ o wu fɛɛ mu afulee mʋ ii biti ɩ fʋʋ kɩpɔyɩ mʋ abɛɛ ɩ mɛɛ yɛ ɩ fʋʋ?.

eng: Otherwise, when you have laid the foundation but couldnt finish the tower, all who see it will begin to belittle you.
local: Nɩɩ akʋtɔ ee piili ɔpɔrɛ gyankpaasa, nɩɩ ɔ man taalɛ lʋwɛ mʋ, ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o wu kɩmʋ mʋ, ee biti ɔ ŋmasɛ mʋ yɛ.

eng: They will say, Heres the person who began construction and couldnt complete it.
local: Odunwura nɩmʋ piili kɩkpaara kɩpɔyɩ amaa ɔ man taalɛ pɔyɩ lʋwɛ.

eng: Or what king would go to war against another king without first sitting down to consider whether his ten thousand soldiers could go up against the twenty thousand coming against him?.
local: Abɛɛ owura mɔɔ, nɩɩ maa owura kʋ bee biti ba ba kɔ, nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ kyɩna kɩɩ tɔ yɛ ɛ taalɛ taa mʋ anaakɔpʋ ngbɩn kudu naa gyaŋa owura banban mʋ maa mʋ anaakɔpʋ ngbɩn adʋnyɔ?.

eng: And if he didnt think he could win, he would send a representative to discuss terms of peace while his enemy was still a long way off.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ taalɛ kɔ yɛ mʋ mʋ, ee biti ɔ lɛɛ asa sun ɔbanban mʋ asɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ san ɔ bʋ kata‐kata, kɛ ɔ taasɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba waa kɛ keyuula kɩ ba bamʋ nsana.

eng: In the same way, none of you who are unwilling to give up all of your possessions can be my disciple.
local: Kanɩn ɔkpa kʋlʋn maŋa sʋ, ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man yɛgɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ mʋ, ɔ mɛɛ taalɛ waa mɛ obuupu.

eng: Salt is good. But if salt loses its flavor, how will it become salty again?.
local: Nfɔlɛ ɩ bʋrɔn, amaa nɩɩ nfɔlɛ ɔkɔn kan lʋwɛ mʋ, mɩnɛ kʋtɔ nɩɩ bee biti ba taalɛ taa waa kɛ ɩ bɩla ɩ waa ɔkɔn?.

eng: It has no value, neither for the soil nor for the manure pile. People throw it away. Whoever has ears to hear should pay attention.
local: Ɩ man sʋ kyaarɛ sa kʋtɔ kʋ kɩlɔŋɔ, amɔɔ bɛɛ taa kyɛɛgɛ fɔyɩ. Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ asʋ mʋ, o nu.

eng: All the tax collectors and sinners were gathering around Jesus to listen to him.
local: Kakaakʋ nɩɩ lenpoo akɔɔlɛpʋ yɛ alibi awaapʋ ba ba sarɛ bee nu Yesu asɛ.

eng: The Pharisees and legal experts were grumbling, saying, This man welcomes sinners and eats with them.
local: Nɩɩ Farasii abi yɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ bɛɛ kɔlɩtɛ yɛ, Ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ ɩɩ kɔɔlɛ alibi awaapʋ halɩɩ maa bamʋ bee bʋga bee gyii.

eng: Jesus told them this parable.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu bɔ bamʋ kɩŋasan kɩnɩmʋ yɛ.

eng: Suppose someone among you had one hundred sheep and lost one of them. Wouldnt he leave the other ninety-nine in the pasture and search for the lost one until he finds it?.
local: A taa fɛɛ nɩɩ fanɛ tɔ ɔkʋ kan ɔ sʋ nbʋlʋpʋ kɩlɩfa kɛ bamʋ tɔ ɔkʋlʋn ɔ fɔyɩ. Nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ yɛgɛ adɩkpanɔ yɛ akpanɔ mʋ afitiri tɔ, kɛ ɔ natɛ biti ɔmʋnɩ ɔ fɔyɩ mʋ naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti o wu mʋ?.

eng: And when he finds it, he is thrilled and places it on his shoulders.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ kan wu mʋ mʋ, ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ mu ansi i gyii, kɛ ɔ taa mʋ gyaga mʋ abaakpan sʋ.

eng: When he arrives home, he calls together his friends and neighbors, saying to them, Celebrate with me because Ive found my lost sheep.
local: kɛ ɔ taa mʋ ba kewu. Kɛ ɔ tɩɩ mʋ kyɛmɩnɛ ana yɛ mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ abi sarɛ, kɛ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan ba tii mɛ sʋ kɛ i gyii ansi, lɩɩ fɛɛ n wu mɛ kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ fɔyɩ mʋ.

eng: In the same way, I tell you, there will be more joy in heaven over one sinner who changes both heart and life than over ninety-nine righteous people who have no need to change their hearts and lives.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin, ɔkpa kʋlʋn maŋa sʋ kensi‐gyii kii biti kɩ baa kɩ bʋ sʋsʋ, fɛɛ alibi ɔwaapʋ kʋlʋn maa kyɛɛgɛ mʋ nfɛɛrɛ ɩ kyɔ gaa fɛɛ asa adɩkpanɔ yɛ akpanɔ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ kɩɩ bamʋ n‐yɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi nɩɩ ɩ man tiri fɛɛ ba kyɛɛgɛ bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ.

eng: Or what woman, if she owns ten silver coins and loses one of them, wont light a lamp and sweep the house, searching her home carefully until she finds it?.
local: Abɛɛ a taa fɛɛ ɔkyɩɩ kʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ gbiti nderebi kudu nɩɩ ɩmʋ kʋkʋlʋn fɔyɩ. Nɩɩ o mee tuwa fɩtɩla kɛ ɔ fɔgɛ kɩkpaara kɩɩ biti kɩmʋ naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti o wu kɩmʋ?.

eng: When she finds it, she calls together her friends and neighbors, saying, Celebrate with me because Ive found my lost coin.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ kan wu kɩmʋ mʋ, ɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ kyɛmɩnɛ ana yɛ mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ abi ba sarɛ tɔ, kɛ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan ba tii mɛ sʋ kɛ i gyii ansi, lɩɩ fɛɛ n wu mɛ kederebi mʋ nɩɩ kɩ fɔyɩ mʋ.

eng: In the same way, I tell you, joy breaks out in the presence of Gods angels over one sinner who changes both heart and life.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin fɛɛ, ɔkpa kʋlʋn maŋa sʋ kensi‐gyii ii biti ɩ baa ɩ bʋ Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ asɛ fɛɛ alibi ɔwaapʋ kʋlʋn maa kyɛɛgɛ mʋ nfɛɛrɛ.

eng: Jesus said, A certain man had two sons.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu kyaga sʋ yɛ, Ɔnyɩn kʋ kyɩna ɔ bʋ tɔ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ nbii‐nyɩnsa‐bii anyɔ.

eng: The younger son said to his father, Father, give me my share of the inheritance. Then the father divided his estate between them.
local: Nɩɩ kebii asɛ mʋ tɔwɛ mʋ‐sɛ yɛ, Mɛ‐Sɛ, barɩgɛ fʋ kapatɩyɛ mʋ kpini tɔ kɛ fʋ taa mɛ lɛɛ sa mɛ. Ɩmʋ sʋ, mʋ‐sɛ barɩgɛ kapatɩyɛ mʋ tɔ ntun nyɔ sa bamʋ.

eng: Soon afterward, the younger son gathered everything together and took a trip to a land far away. There, he wasted his wealth through extravagant living.
local: Nkɛ gbɛrɛɛ kamaa tɔ mʋ, kebii asɛ fɛ mʋ kapatɩyɛ mʋ kpini, kʋʋla kʋsʋ loo to kpee ɔsʋwʋlɛ kʋ sʋ kata‐kata. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔ naa gyii mu afulee mʋ kpini kʋsʋbɔ tɔ lʋwɛ.

eng: When he had used up his resources, a severe food shortage arose in that country and he began to be in need.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ o gyii mu afulee mʋ kpini lʋwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ akʋn damantɛ a tɩyɛ ɔsʋwʋlɛ maŋa sʋ kpini, nɩɩ kitiri fufuli kɩ kɩtaa mʋ.

eng: He hired himself out to one of the citizens of that country, who sent him into his fields to feed pigs.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ o kpee naa waa kusun sa kasɛ maŋa sʋ ɔdɛ‐bi kʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ fɛɛ ɔ kɩɩ mu akuuri sʋ.

eng: He longed to eat his fill from what the pigs ate, but no one gave him anything.
local: Ee biti agyitɔ mʋ nɩɩ akuuri bee gyii mʋ kigyii, amaa ɔ man nyɛ.

eng: When he came to his senses, he said, How many of my fathers hired hands have more than enough food, but Im starving to death!.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ mʋ nfɛɛrɛ ɩ ba mʋ tɔ mʋ, ɔ tɔwɛ sa mʋ n‐yɩɩ yɛ, Mɛ‐Sɛ adɔɔpʋ mʋ kpini agyitɔ a gbigi bamʋ, yɛgɛ n bʋ nfɩɩ n biti n wu akʋn lewu.

eng: I will get up and go to my father, and say to him,Father, I have sinned against heaven and against you.
local: N biti n kʋsʋ bwii kpee nsɛ asɛ kɛ n naa tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Nsɛ, n waa Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ fʋ alibi.

eng: I no longer deserve to be called your son. Take me on as one of your hired hands.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ n man bɩla dagaa gbaa fɛɛ fʋ tɩɩ mɛ yɛ fu‐bii, taa mɛ waa fɛɛ fʋ adɔɔpʋ mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ.

eng: So he got up and went to his father. While he was still a long way off, his father saw him and was moved with compassion. His father ran to him, hugged him, and kissed him.
local: Kanɩn sʋ ɔ kʋsʋ yii sʋ bwii ɛɛ ba mʋ‐sɛ asɛ. Amaa saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ dan san ɔ bʋ kata yɛ kewu mʋ, mʋ‐sɛ wu mʋ, nɩɩ o wu mʋ kʋwɛɛ. Nɩɩ ɔ sɩlɛ naa gyaŋa mu‐bii mʋ, nɩɩ o baari mʋ tɔ.

eng: Then his son said, Father, I have sinned against heaven and against you. I no longer deserve to be called your son.
local: Nɩɩ kebii mʋ tɔwɛ mʋ‐sɛ yɛ, Nsɛ n waa Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ fu alibi. Nɩɩ n man bɩla dagaa gbaa fɛɛ fʋ tɩɩ mɛ yɛ fu‐bii.

eng: But the father said to his servants, Quickly, bring out the best robe and put it on him! Put a ring on his finger and sandals on his feet!.
local: Amaa nɩɩ mʋ‐sɛ tɔwɛ mʋ ayaafɔlɛ yɛ, Fan waa bilen! Fan taa kɩtaa kparɛ ba bun mʋ, fan lɛɛ kepini ba waa mʋ kɩbaa‐bi tɔ, kɛ fan waa mʋ asɩbɩta.

eng: Fetch the fattened calf and slaughter it. We must celebrate with feasting.
local: Kɛ fan naa kɩtaa naatɛ bonbi‐nyɩnsa mʋ ba mɔɔ kɛ a gyii, kɛ a kyaa gyii anɛ ansi.

eng: because this son of mine was dead and has come back to life! He was lost and is found! And they began to celebrate.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ mi kebii‐nyɩnsa‐bii nɩmʋ daa wu nɩn, nɩɩ o bwii kyiŋi, ɔ fɔyɩ nɩn, nɩɩ n bwii benti mʋ. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba piili bee gyii bamʋ ansi.

eng: Now his older son was in the field. Coming in from the field, he approached the house and heard music and dancing.
local: Saŋa maa tɔ mʋ yɛgɛ obii bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ bʋ ndɔɔ tɔ. Maa lɩɩ ndɔɔ ɛɛ ba nɩɩ o be tiri kewu tɔ mʋ, o nu ɩlʋn yɛ kakyaa ɩ maa kpira mʋ‐sɛ kɩkpaara sʋ.

eng: He called one of the servants and asked what was going on.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔ tɩɩ mʋ‐sɛ ayaafɔlɛ mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ mʋ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ waa.

eng: The servant replied, Your brother has arrived, and your father has slaughtered the fattened calf because he received his son back safe and sound.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Fu‐supu i bwii ba, nɩɩ fʋ‐sɛ mɔɔ mʋ naatɛ bonbi‐nyɩnsa mʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ fu‐supu bwii ba kewu yɛ kumu nkpa yɛ alanfɩya.

eng: Then the older son was furious and didnt want to enter in, but his father came out and begged him.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔdaa mʋ nyɛ agbʋ yɛ ɔ man bɩla yɛ o loo kɩkpaara sʋ. Kanɩn sʋ mʋ‐sɛ lɩɩ kpee naa tɔwɛ waa mʋ yɛ o loo ba.

eng: He answered his father, Look, Ive served you all these years, and I never disobeyed your instruction. Yet youve never given me as much as a young goat so I could celebrate with my friends.
local: Amaa ɔ lɛɛ mʋ‐sɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Kɩɩ! Nsu ɩnɩmʋ kpini n maa sun fʋ fɛɛ kɩnyɛ. Nɩɩ n man kina fʋ kɔnɔ kunu. Mɩnɛ kʋtɔ nɩɩ fʋ pɩɩta sa mɛ? Fʋ man sa mɛ halɩɩ kabwɩɩ gbaa kɛ maa mɛ kyɛmɩnɛ ana a mɔɔ gyii anɛ ansi.

eng: But when this son of yours returned, after gobbling up your estate on prostitutes, you slaughtered the fattened calf for him.
local: Amaa fu kebii‐nyɩnsa nɩmʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ naa nyita fʋ kapatɩyɛ lɩɩ akyɩɩ gyaga sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ naa bwii ba kewu mʋ, fʋ mɔɔ naatɛ bonbi‐nyɩnsa sa mʋ.

eng: Then his father said, Son, you are always with me, and everything I have is yours.
local: Nɩɩ ɔsɛ mʋ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Mi‐bii, fʋ bʋ mɛ asɛ nfɩɩ saŋa kʋmaa, nɩɩ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ n sʋ mʋ gyɛ fʋ lɛɛ nɩn.

eng: But we had to celebrate and be glad because this brother of yours was dead and is alive. He was lost and is found.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ yɛgɛ ɩ waa anɛ ɔkɔn kɛ a kyaa gyii anɛ ansi, lɩɩ fɛɛ fu‐supu nɩmʋ daa wu nɩn, nɩɩ o bwii kyiŋi, ɔ fɔyɩ nɩn, nɩɩ n bwii benti mʋ. .

eng: Jesus said to his disciples, Things that cause people to trip and fall into sin must happen, but how terrible it is for the person through whom they happen.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ yɛ, Ɩ waa nɛnɛ, atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ nyɩmɩsa ɩ waa alibi mʋ ii biti ɩ ba. Amaa ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ii biti ɩ bɔla mʋ sʋ kɛ ɩ ba mʋ, mʋ laakʋ.

eng: It would be better for them to be thrown into a lake with a large stone hung around their neck than to cause one of these little ones to trip and fall into sin.
local: Fɛɛ nɩɩ mʋ kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, ba taa kubuu lala ŋmina mʋ ɔbʋlɛ tɔ naa tʋʋ ɔpʋ tɔ gbaa mʋ, ɩ bɔ sa mʋ fɛɛ ɩ maa bɔla mʋ sʋ nɩɩ nbii wuribi nɩmʋ ɔkʋ naa waa alibi.

eng: Watch yourselves! If your brother or sister sins, warn them to stop. If they change their hearts and lives, forgive them.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan baa fan da fanɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ. Nɩɩ fu‐supu kan waa fʋ alibi mʋ taasɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kan nu mʋ n‐yɩɩ kasɛ mʋ, kɛ fʋ taa mu alibi kɛɛ mʋ.

eng: Even if someone sins against you seven times in one day and returns to you seven times and says, I am changing my ways, you must forgive that person.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ kan waa fʋ alibi ɩlɩɩ isunɔ kakɛ kʋlʋn, nɩɩ ɔ kan nu mʋ n‐yɩɩ kasɛ ba tɔwɛ fʋ ɩlɩɩ isunɔ yɛ, N man waa danbɩrasa mʋ, taa mu alibi kɛɛ mʋ.

eng: The apostles said to the Lord, Increase our faith.
local: Nɩɩ asunpu mʋ ba tɔwɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ yɛ, Tii anɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ kɛ kɩ nyɛ akyɔ.

eng: The Lord replied, If you had faith the size of a mustard seed, you could say to this mulberry tree, Be uprooted and planted in the sea, and it would obey you.
local: Nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Nɩɩ fan kan fan sʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii pii fɛɛ gowa abi gbaa mʋ, fɛn taalɛ tɔwɛ oyu lala nɩmʋ yɛ, Faa fu ilin kpini fii kyiigi naa yii ɔpʋ tɔ, kɛ i nu fanɛ asɛ.

eng: Would any of you say to your servant, who had just come in from the field after plowing or tending sheep, Come! Sit down for dinner?.
local: Nɩɩ fɛɛ fanɛ ɔkʋ sʋ kɩyaafɔlɛ nɩɩ ɛɛ dɔɔ abɛɛ ɛɛ kɩɩ nbʋlʋpʋ sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kan lɩɩ ndɔɔ tɔ ba mʋ, fɩɩ dan taalɛ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Waa bilen kɛ fʋ ba kyɩna gyii.

eng: Wouldnt you say instead, Fix my dinner. Put on the clothes of a table servant and wait on me while I eat and drink. After that, you can eat and drink?.
local: Ayee! Fii biti fʋ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Dɩŋa mɛ kalaatɩpwɛ agyitɔ ba yii mɛ kɛ n gyii kɛ n nuu. N kan lʋwɛ mʋ, kɛ ngbaa fʋ, fʋ naa gyii kɛ fu nuu.

eng: You wont thank the servant because the servant did what you asked, will you?.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ i tiri fɛɛ ɔ fwaala kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ ɔ waa sa mʋ abɛɛ?.

eng: In the same way, when you have done everything required of you, you should say, We servants deserve no special praise. We have only done our duty.
local: Kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ fanɛ gbaa fanɛ, nɩɩ fan kan waa kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ fan waa mʋ, fan tɔwɛ yɛ, An gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ayaafɔlɛ mʋ nɩɩ a man sʋ kyaarɛ, an dan waa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa a maa waa mʋ nɩn. .

eng: On the way to Jerusalem, Jesus traveled along the border between Samaria and Galilee.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Yesu ii kpee Gyerusalem mʋ, ɔ bɔla Samariya yɛ Galile ɩsʋwʋlɛ ngyaŋɛ nɩn.

eng: As he entered a village, ten men with skin diseases approached him. Keeping their distance from him.
local: Maa kpee kadɛ‐bi kʋ sʋ mʋ, ɔ gyaŋa anyɩn kudu nɩɩ bɛɛ lɔ kibootoo. Nɩɩ anyɩn mʋ ba yɩlɛ kata.

eng: they raised their voices and said, Jesus, Master, show us mercy.
local: nɩɩ ba kuusi kenken yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu, wu anɛ kʋwɛɛ.

eng: When Jesus saw them, he said, Go, show yourselves to the priests. As they left, they were cleansed.
local: Yesu maa wu bamʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ Fan taa fanɛ n‐yɩɩ naa kaapʋ aseepu. Ba maa kpee mʋ nɩɩ ba nyɛ alanfɩya.

eng: One of them, when he saw that he had been healed, returned and praised God with a loud voice.
local: Bamʋ tɔ ɔkʋ, maa wu fɛɛ ɔ nyɛ alanfɩya mʋ, o bwii ba ee kuusi kenken ɛɛ yɩn Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: He fell on his face at Jesus feet and thanked him. He was a Samaritan.
local: Nɩɩ o be muŋa Yesu ayaa tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ fwaala mʋ lɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa sa mʋ mʋ sʋ. Nɩɩ kanɩn kanyɩn maŋa gyɛ Kasamariya‐nyɩn nɩn.

eng: Jesus replied, Werent ten cleansed? Where are the other nine?.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ yɛ, Man gyɛ asa kudu ɩ nyɛ alanfɩya. Fɩnɛ nɩɩ asansa akpanɔ mʋ ba bʋ?.

eng: No one returned to praise God except this foreigner.
local: Mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ kadɛndɛmandɛ nɩmʋ nkʋn ɩ taalɛ bwii ba fwaala Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: Then Jesus said to him, Get up and go. Your faith has healed you.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Kʋsʋ natɛ, fʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii kɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ fʋ nyɛ alanfɩya.

eng: Pharisees asked Jesus when Gods kingdom was coming. He replied, Gods kingdom isnt coming with signs that are easily noticed.
local: Saŋa kʋ nɩɩ Farasii abi ba taasɛ Yesu yɛ, Kakɛ mɔɔ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ kii biti kɩ ba? Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ fɛn mɛɛ yɛ fan taa fanɛ ansi wu kɩ maa ba.

eng: Nor will people say, Look, here it is! or There it is! Dont you see? Gods kingdom is already among you.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ taalɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Kɩɩ kɩmʋ ndee abɛɛ kɩ bʋ falɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura mʋ kɩ bʋ fanɛ tɔ.

eng: Then Jesus said to the disciples, The time will come when you will long to see one of the days of the Human One,and you wont see it.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ, Saŋa kʋ ii biti ɔ ba, nɩɩ fen biti fɛɛ fan wu mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ mɛ nkɛ mʋ kʋkʋ, amaa fan mɛɛ yɛ fen wu.

eng: People will say to you, Look there! or Look here! Dont leave or go chasing after them.
local: Nɩɩ asa bee biti ba tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, Mʋ ɩ bʋ falɛ mʋ abɛɛ Mʋ ndee. Amaa fan man waa fan buu bamʋ.

eng: The Human Onewill appear on his day in the same way that a flash of lightning lights up the sky from one end to the other.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ ɩnɩmʋ lɩɩ fɛɛ, mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa kibwii ba mʋ, ii biti ɩ baa i du nɩn fɛɛ bʋlayɩ maa gyii ɩsɛɛ ŋmaŋɛ sʋsʋ lɩɩ kɩbaafʋn nɩmʋ naa lɩɩ kɩbanban nɩmʋ.

eng: However, first he must suffer many things and be rejected by this generation.
local: Amaa ɩ dagaa fɛɛ n gyankpaa wu awʋrʋfɔ gaa, kɛ kɩnaana nɩmʋ tɔ abi ba kina mɛ.

eng: As it was in the days of Noah, so it will be during the days of the Human One.
local: Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɩ waa anɛ naana Nowa saŋa mʋ, kanɩn nɩɩ ii biti ɩ waa mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa kibwii ba saŋa mʋ gbaa.

eng: People were eating, drinking, marrying, and being given in marriage until the day Noah entered the ark and the flood came and destroyed them all.
local: Anɛ naana Nowa saŋa mʋ, asa ba kyɩna bee gyii yɛgɛ bee nuu, bee kili naa fʋʋ kakɛ mʋ nɩɩ anɛ naana Nowa loo ɔkʋlɩ mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ nkyu ɩ ba muuri bamʋ kpini.

eng: Likewise in the days of Lot, people were eating, drinking, buying, selling, planting, and building.
local: Kanɩn nɩɩ ɩ waa Loti saŋa mʋ, asa ba kyɩna bee gyii yɛgɛ bee nuu, bɛɛ fɛ yɛgɛ bɛɛ sɔɔ, bee duu yɛgɛ bɛɛ pɔyɩ.

eng: But on the day Lot left Sodom, fire and sulfur rained down from heaven and destroyed them all.
local: Amaa kakɛ mʋ nɩɩ Loti lɩɩ Sodom kadɛ tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ fʋlɔn yɛ kuduri lɩɩ sʋsʋ ba dɩyɛ bamʋ kpini.

eng: Thats the way it will be on the day the Human Oneis revealed.
local: Kanɩn nɩɩ ii biti ɩ waa kakɛ mʋ nɩɩ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ n biti n lɛɛ mɛ n‐yɩɩ kaapʋ mʋ.

eng: On that day, those on the roof, whose possessions are in the house, shouldnt come down to grab them. Likewise, those in the field shouldnt turn back.
local: Kanɩn kakɛ maa nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan ɔ bʋ kewusunɔ mʋ, nɩɩ mʋ atɔ a bʋ kɩkpaara mʋ sʋ mʋ, ɔ man bɩla kpee kɩkpaara sʋ naa taa atɔ. Kanɩn ɩ gyɛ sa ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ ndɔɔ tɔ mʋ, ɔ man bɩla bwii kpee kewu kɛ ɔ naa taa kʋtɔ kʋ.

eng: Remember Lots wife!.
local: Fan nyiŋi Loti mʋ‐ka.

eng: Whoever tries to preserve their life will lose it, but whoever loses their life will preserve it.
local: Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti fɛɛ ɔ mɔlɩgɛ mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mʋ nkpa mʋ, ii biti ɩ lɩɩ mʋ abaa tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ nkpa sa lɩɩ mɛ sʋ mʋ, ee biti ɔ nyɛ kɩmʋ.

eng: I tell you, on that night two people will be in the same bed: one will be taken and the other left.
local: Kanɩn kagyanbwɛ maŋa mʋ, asa anyɔ bee biti ba baa ba dɛ kalan kʋlʋn sʋ, kɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ taa ɔkʋ yɛgɛ ɔkʋ.

eng: Two women will be grinding grain together: one will be taken and the other left.
local: Akyɩɩ anyɔ bee biti ba baa bɛɛ kɔyɩ atɔ, kɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ taa ɔkʋ yɛgɛ ɔkʋ.

eng: The disciples asked, Where, Lord? Jesus said, The vultures gather wherever theres a dead body.
local: Nɩɩ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba taasɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu yɛ, Fɩnɛ nɩɩ ii biti ɩ waa. Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Katɩn kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ belenbuni bʋ mʋ, tʋtɔ nɩɩ agyinbwii gbaa ba ba gyaŋɛ.

eng: Jesus was telling them a parable about their need to pray continuously and not to be discouraged.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu bɔ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ kɩŋasan mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ kaapʋ bamʋ fɛɛ, ba ba bɛɛ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ kʋlɛ saŋa kʋmaa, kɛ ba man yɛgɛ bamʋ n‐yɩɩ nnyɔ bamʋ.

eng: He said, In a certain city there was a judge who neither feared God nor respected people.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Asɩn ogyipu kʋ ɩ bʋ kadɛ kʋkʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ man sɩlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ nɩɩ ɔ man taa nyɩmɩsa tɩɩ sɛhn.

eng: In that city there was a widow who kept coming to him, asking, Give me justice in this case against my adversary.
local: Nɩɩ okulapu‐kyɩɩ kʋ gbaa bʋ kadɛ maŋa tɔ, ɔ maa sʋ ɛɛ ba asɩn ogyipu mʋ asɛ ɛɛ kʋlɛ mʋ yɛ, Kyɛ mɛ tɔ kɛ n gyii biri mɛ dʋn ana.

eng: For a while he refused but finally said to himself, I dont fear God or respect people.
local: Ɩ kyɛɛrɛɛ gaa nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ waa sɛhn ɛɛ sa mʋ. Amaa kamaa tɔ mʋ, asɩn ogyipu mʋ tɔwɛ waa mʋ n‐yɩɩ yɛ, Ɩ gyɛ kesintin fɛɛ man sɩlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ nɩɩ man taa nyɩmɩsa tɩɩ sɛhn.

eng: but I will give this widow justice because she keeps bothering me. Otherwise, there will be no end to her coming here and embarrassing me.
local: Amaa lɩɩ kitintin mʋ nɩɩ okulapu‐kyɩɩ nɩmʋ i tintin mɛ falɛ sʋ mʋ, n biti n kɩɩ kɛ n taa mu kesintin sa mʋ. Man gyɛ kanɩn mʋ kɩba mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ ba nfɩɩ falɛ, ii biti ɩ kan sa mɛ kʋlɔ. .

eng: The Lord said, Listen to what the unjust judge says.
local: Nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu tɔwɛ yɛ, Fan nu kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ asɩn ogyipu mʋ nɩɩ o mee gyii kesintin mʋ gbaa tɔwɛ?.

eng: Wont God provide justice to his chosen people who cry out to him day and night? Will he be slow to help them?.
local: Nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ kanɩn bɩrɛ mʋ, mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ mɛɛ yɛ o gyii kesintin sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba gyɛ mʋ lɛɛ mʋ, nɩɩ bee su bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ kakyʋwapɛ yɛ kagyanbwɛ? Abɛɛ ee biti ɔ kyaga sʋ ee kina?.

eng: I tell you, he will give them justice quickly. But when the Human Onecomes, will he find faithfulness on earth.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin yɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ kɩɩ sʋ bilen‐bilen fɛɛ ɔ taa bamʋ kesintin sa bamʋ. Amaa saŋa mʋ nɩɩ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa n kan ba mʋ, n biti n nyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ mɛ gyii kasɛ sʋ.

eng: Jesus told this parable to certain people who had convinced themselves that they were righteous and who looked on everyone else with disgust.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu bɩla bɔ kɩŋasan nɩmʋ sa asa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ kɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi, yɛgɛ bɛɛ kɩɩ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ kɩyan. Ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ.

eng: Two people went up to the temple to pray. One was a Pharisee and the other a tax collector.
local: Asa anyɔ akʋ ba kpee kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ ba naa kʋlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ. Bamʋ tɔ ɔkʋ gyɛ Kafarasii nɩn, nɩɩ ɔkʋ mɔɔ gyɛ lenpoo ɔkɔɔlɛpʋ.

eng: The Pharisee stood and prayed about himself with these words, God, I thank you that Im not like everyone else—crooks, evildoers, adulterers—or even like this tax collector.
local: Nɩɩ Kafarasii‐nyɩn mʋ yɩlɛ mʋ kʋkwɩɩ kʋlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɛɛ yɩn mʋ n‐yɩɩ yɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ, n fwaala fʋ gaa fɛɛ maa n man du fɛɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba san mʋ, n mee yuuri, abɛɛ n mee puni asa abɛɛ n man gyɛ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ n nyita kekili abɛɛ n man du fɛɛ lenpoo ɔkɔɔlɛpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ yɩlɛ nfɩɩ falɛ.

eng: I fast twice a week. I give a tenth of everything I receive.
local: N ŋmina kɔnɔ nkɛ nnyɔ, nkɛ nsunɔ kʋmaa tɔ, nɩɩ n barɩgɛ mɛ atɔ tɔ ntun kudu, nɩɩ n taa ketun kʋlʋn n sa fʋ.

eng: But the tax collector stood at a distance. He wouldnt even lift his eyes to look toward heaven. Rather, he struck his chest and said, God, show mercy to me, a sinner.
local: Amaa lenpoo ɔkɔɔlɛpʋ mʋ bɩrɛ yɩlɛ kata yɛ mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ man diyaa mu ansi kɩɩ sʋsʋ, amaa ɔ taa mʋ kɩbaa gyaga mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ, alibi ɔwaapʋ ɩ gyɛ mɛ, wu mɛ kʋwɛɛ.

eng: I tell you, this person went down to his home justified rather than the Pharisee. All who lift themselves up will be brought low, and those who make themselves low will be lifted up.
local: Ba bwii kpewu. Amaa n tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin fɛɛ, lenpoo ɔkɔɔlɛpʋ mʋ i gyii asʋ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ, man gyɛ Kafarasii‐nyɩn mʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ fanɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kʋsaa mʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋsʋ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ bɩya mʋ kasɛ, nɩɩ fanɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bɩya mʋ n‐yɩɩ kasɛ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ kʋsaa mʋ sʋsʋ.

eng: People were bringing babies to Jesus so that he would bless them. When the disciples saw this, they scolded them.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ asa akʋ gbaa ba sʋ bamʋ nbii wuribi bɛɛ ba Yesu asɛ, kɛ ɔ taa mʋ abaa gyaga bamʋ sʋ. Mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba maa wu kanɩn mʋ, ba gya bamʋ.

eng: Then Jesus called them to him and said, Allow the children to come to me. Dont forbid them, because Gods kingdom belongs to people like these children.
local: Amaa Yesu tɩɩ nbii mʋ ba mʋ asɛ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Fan yɛgɛ nbii mʋ ba ba mɛ asɛ, kɛ fan man tii bamʋ ɔkpa. Lɩɩ fɛɛ banɩmʋ falɛ daa kudubi nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ gyɛ bamʋ lɛɛ.

eng: I assure you that whoever doesnt welcome Gods kingdom like a child will never enter it.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin yɛ, ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ kɔɔlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii fɛɛ kebii mʋ, ɔ mɛɛ yɛ o loo kɩmʋ tɔ.

eng: A certain ruler asked Jesus, Good Teacher, what must I do to obtain eternal life.
local: Nɩɩ Gyiwu abi ɔgyankpaapʋ kʋ taasɛ Yesu yɛ, Ɔkaapʋpʋ danbɩrasa, mɩnɛ nɩɩ n biti n waa kɛ n nyɛ nkpa kakpaa.

eng: Jesus replied, Why do you call me good? No one is good except the one God.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ fɩɩ tɩɩ mɛ yɛ ɔdanbɩrasa? Ɔkʋ man gyɛ ɔdanbɩrasa, amɔɔ Ɩbwaarɛ nkʋn.

eng: You know the commandments: Dont commit adultery. Dont murder. Dont steal. Dont give false testimony. Honor your father and mother. .
local: Fu nyi Ɩbwaarɛ nbara asɩn mʋ nɩɩ kaapʋ yɛ, Man nyita kekili, man mɔɔ nyɩmɩsa, man kan yuuri, man waa nyama yɩlɛpʋ antɩŋɛsa wuya, kɛ fʋ taa bɛɛrɛɛ sa fʋ‐sɛ yɛ fu‐nyi? .

eng: Then the ruler said, Ive kept all of these things since I was a boy.
local: Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, N buu nbara ɩnɩmʋ kpini lɩɩ mi kibii tɔ.

eng: When Jesus heard this, he said, Theres one more thing. Sell everything you own and distribute the money to the poor. Then you will have treasure in heaven. And come, follow me.
local: Yesu maa nu kanɩn mʋ, ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɩ san fʋ kʋtɔ kʋlʋn. Naa fɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fʋ sʋ kɛ fʋ taa afulee mʋ sa atiripu, ii biti ɩ yɛgɛ fu biliŋi atɔ wuya sʋsʋ. Kɛ fʋ ba buu mɛ.

eng: When he heard these words, the man became sad because he was extremely rich.
local: Amaa maa nu falɛ mʋ, ɩ kɔ mʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ gyɛ atɔ wuya gaa.

eng: When Jesus saw this, he said, Its very hard for the wealthy to enter Gods kingdom!.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu kɩɩ mʋ kyiim, tɔwɛ yɛ, I biti ɩ baa ɩ bʋlʋn sa atɔ wuya ana fɛɛ ba yɛgɛ bamʋ n‐yɩɩ kɛ ba buu Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ kɩkaapʋ.

eng: Its easier for a camel to squeeze through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter Gods kingdom.
local: Kesintin ii biti ɩ baa ɩ bʋpɔɔ sa nyɔɔma fɛɛ ɔ taalɛ loo baasa ɔbɔ tɔ bʋlɛ, ɩ kyɔ fɛɛ atɔ wuya maa yɛgɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ kɛ o buu Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ kɩkaapʋ.

eng: Those who heard this said, Then who can be saved.
local: Nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba nu mʋ ba taasɛ yɛ, Kanɩn bɩrɛ anɩmʋ ɩɩ taalɛ nyɛ kamɔlɩgɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, What is impossible for humans is possible for God.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ nyɩmɩsa mɛɛ taalɛ waa mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ bɩrɛ ɛɛ taalɛ waa.

eng: Peter said, Look, we left everything we own and followed you.
local: Nɩɩ Pita tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Kɩɩ! An yɛgɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩɩ an baa an buu fʋ falɛ.

eng: Jesus said to them, I assure you that anyone who has left house, husband, wife, brothers, sisters, parents, or children because of Gods kingdom.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin fɛɛ, ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kʋsʋ yɛgɛ kewu abɛɛ mʋ‐ka abɛɛ mʋ‐daa ana yɛ asupu abɛɛ akʋʋgɛpʋ abɛɛ mu‐bii ana lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii kɩkaapʋ mʋ sʋ mʋ.

eng: will receive many times more in this age and eternal life in the coming age.
local: ee biti ɔ nyɛ falɛ atɔ gaa, nbɩɩnbɩɩ kakyɩna nɩmʋ tɔ yɛ nkpa kakpaa mʋ nɩɩ ii biti ɩ ba kamaa tɔ mʋ.

eng: Jesus took the Twelve aside and said, Look, were going up to Jerusalem, and everything written about the Human Oneby the prophets will be accomplished.
local: Saŋa kʋ Yesu taa abɩɩlapʋ kudu anyɔ mʋ lɩɩ ɩkaa sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, An kpee Gyerusalem kɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ mʋ ba kyʋrɔɔ yɩla lɩɩ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa sʋ mʋ ɩ ba tɔ.

eng: He will be handed over to the Gentiles. He will be ridiculed, mistreated, and spit on.
local: Bee biti ba lɛɛ mɛ sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ kɛ ba waa mɛ yɛrɛyɛrɛ, kɛ ba saalɛ mɛ, kɛ ba tʋʋ nkyɔlɛ wʋrɩgɛ mɛ sʋ, kɛ ba taa awulibi tɩŋɛ mɛ kɛ ba mɔɔ mɛ.

eng: When a crowd of thousands upon thousands had gathered so that they were crushing each other, Jesus began to speak first to his disciples: Watch out for the yeast of the Pharisees—I mean, the mismatch between their hearts and lives.
local: Saŋa maŋa tɔ mʋ, asa mʋ ba maa nyɛ akyɔ bee tii sʋ, halɩɩ bee kyigya abaa ayaa sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ Yesu piili ɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ asɛ, nɩɩ ɔ gyankpaa ɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Fan baa fan da fanɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ lɩɩ Farasii abi kebunbun ansi wuya ana sʋ. Bamʋ akaapʋsa i du nɩn fɛɛ bodobodo kɩfɛ tinsa.

eng: After torturing him, they will kill him. On the third day, he will rise up.
local: Kɛ ɩmʋ kakɛ sasapʋ mʋ kɛ n bwii kyiŋi ba nkpa tɔ.

eng: Nothing is hidden that wont be revealed, and nothing is secret that wont be brought out into the open.
local: Kʋtɔ kʋ man bʋ tɔ nɩɩ kɩ baala yɛgɛ ba mɛɛ yɛ ba bɩɩ, abɛɛ asiri kʋ man bʋ tɔ nɩɩ ɩ mɛɛ yɛ ɩ lɩɩ ifuli.

eng: But the Twelve understood none of these words. The meaning of this message was hidden from them and they didnt grasp what he was saying.
local: Amaa abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba man nu ɩnɩmʋ kasɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ asɩn mʋ kasɛ baala bamʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ba man nyi kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ.

eng: Therefore, whatever you have said in the darkness will be heard in the light, and whatever you have whispered in the rooms deep inside the house will be announced from the rooftops.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ asɩn kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fʋ tɔwɛ kitentenbiri tɔ mʋ, kayɩ kakɛ mʋ ii biti ɩ lɩɩ ifuli, nɩɩ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ fu‐kuli waa kʋsʋ tɔ obu to mʋ, bee biti ba taa ɩmʋ da kibonbon.

eng: As Jesus came to Jericho, a certain blind man was sitting beside the road begging.
local: Yesu maa tiri Gyeriko tɔ mʋ, yɛgɛ gyaatanapʋ kʋ tɛ ɔkpa asɛ ɛɛ kʋlɛ atɔ.

eng: I tell you, my friends, dont be terrified by those who can kill the body but after that can do nothing more.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu kyaga sʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, Mɛ kyɛmɩnɛ ana, fan man sɩlɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ taalɛ mɔɔ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ, nɩɩ ɩmʋ kamaa mʋ ba mɛɛ bɩla taalɛ waa kɩmʋ sɛhn.

eng: When the man heard the crowd passing by, he asked what was happening.
local: Maa nu asa damantɛ ba maa kyʋn mʋ, ɔ taasɛ yɛ, Mɩnɛ ɩɩ waa.

eng: Ill show you whom you should fear: fear the one who, after you have been killed, has the authority to throw you into hell. Indeed, I tell you, thats the one you should fear.
local: Amaa n biti n kaapʋ fanɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa yɛ fan sɩlɛ. Fan sɩlɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ taalɛ mɔɔ fanɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ kɛ ɔ bɩla taa fanɛ waa fʋlɔn tɔ. N tɔwɛ fanɛ fan sɩlɛ mʋ.

eng: They told him, Jesus the Nazarene is passing by.
local: Nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Yesu Kanasareti‐nyɩn mʋ ɩ kyʋn kanɩn maŋa.

eng: Arent five sparrows sold for two small coins?Yet not one of them is overlooked by God.
local: Afɩnɛ ɩ gyɛ langba kɩgya? Kobo anyɔ ɩ taalɛ sɔɔ bamʋ anuu. Amaa n mɛɛ tan bamʋ ɔkʋ sʋ.

eng: The blind man shouted, Jesus, Son of David, show me mercy.
local: Nɩɩ o kuusi tɩɩ yɛ, Yesu, Owura Dawidi kanaana‐bii, wu mɛ kʋwɛɛ.

eng: Even the hairs on your head are all counted. Dont be afraid. You are worth more than many sparrows.
local: Halɩɩ fanɛ kumu tɔ inyini kpini gbaa Ɩbwaarɛ nyi ɩmʋ kɔnɔ. Ɩmʋ sʋ fan man waa kufuu, lɩɩ fɛɛ fan gyɛ akparɩ fan kyɔ alangba ngbɩn‐ngbɩn.

eng: Those leading the procession scolded him, telling him to be quiet, but he shouted even louder, Son of David, show me mercy.
local: Nɩɩ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Yesu ansi tɔ mʋ, ba gya mʋ yɛ o bun mʋ kɔnɔ sʋ, amaa ɔ dan san ɔ maa sʋ ee kuusi kenken yɛ, Owura Dawidi kanaana‐bii, wu mɛ kʋwɛɛ.

eng: I tell you, everyone who acknowledges me before humans, the Human Onewill acknowledge before Gods angels.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ tɔwɛ sakyɔ tɔ yɛ ɔ gyɛ mɛ lɛɛ mʋ, mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa gbaa n biti n tɔwɛ Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ ansi tɔ yɛ ɔ gyɛ mɛ lɛɛ.

eng: Jesus stopped and called for the man to be brought to him. When he was present Jesus asked.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu yɩlɛ nɩɩ ɔ sa kɔnɔ yɛ ba taa mʋ bɩya mʋ. Maa tiri mʋ tɔ mʋ, Yesu taasɛ mʋ yɛ.

eng: But the one who rejects me before others will be rejected before Gods angels.
local: Amaa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o kina mɛ sakyɔ tɔ mʋ, ngbaa mɛ n biti n kina mʋ Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ ansi tɔ.

eng: What do you want me to do for you? He said, Lord, I want to see.
local: Mɩnɛ nɩɩ fii biti fɛɛ n waa sa fʋ? Nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, n biti fɛɛ mi ansi ɩ bɩla bugi.

eng: Anyone who speaks a word against the Human Onewill be forgiven, but whoever insults the Holy Spirit wont be forgiven.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ tɔwɛ asɩn libi ee yii mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ ɩɩ taalɛ taa kɛɛ mʋ, amaa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ tɔwɛ asɩn libi ee yii Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ taa kɛɛ mʋ.

eng: Jesus said to him, Receive your sight! Your faith has healed you.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Fu ansi i bugi! Fʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii kɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ fʋ nyɛ alanfɩya.

eng: When they bring you before the synagogues, rulers, and authorities, dont worry about how to defend yourself or what you should say.
local: Nɩɩ ba kan bɩya fanɛ Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa abɩlɩsa, abɛɛ awura abɛɛ ɔsʋwʋlɛ alʋnpʋ ansi tɔ mʋ, fan man kan fɛ nfɛɛrɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fen biti fan lɛɛ fanɛ asɩn mʋ kɔnɔ, abɛɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ fan tɔwɛ.

eng: At once he was able to see, and he began to follow Jesus, praising God. When all the people saw it, they praised God too.
local: Opula maŋa tɔ mu ansi ɩ bɩla bugi nɩɩ o buu Yesu, yɛgɛ ɛɛ yɩn Ɩbwaarɛ. Nɩɩ asa mʋ kpini ba maa wu kanɩn mʋ, ngbaa bamʋ ba yɩn Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: The Holy Spirit will tell you at that very moment what you must say.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ ii biti ɔ kaapʋ fanɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ fan tɔwɛ saŋa maŋa.

eng: Someone from the crowd said to him, Teacher, tell my brother to divide the inheritance with me.
local: Nɩɩ sakyɔ mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ tɔwɛ Yesu yɛ, Ɔkaapʋpʋ, tɔwɛ sa mɛ‐daa kɛ ɔ yɛgɛ maa mʋ a barɩgɛ kapatɩyɛ mʋ nɩɩ anɛ‐sɛ wu yɛgɛ sa anɛ mʋ.

eng: Jesus said to him, Man, who appointed me as judge or referee between you and your brother.
