local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɔnyɩn, anɩmʋ i yii mɛ asɩn ogyipu abɛɛ kapatɩyɛ ɔbarɩgɛpʋ.

eng: Then Jesus said to them, Watch out! Guard yourself against all kinds of greed. After all, ones life isnt determined by ones possessions, even when someone is very wealthy.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ kyaga sʋ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan kɩɩ danbɩrasa kɛ fan baa fan da fanɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ lɩɩ kensi payɩ libi sʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ nyɩmɩsa nkpa man gyɛ mʋ atɔ damantɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ mʋ.

eng: Then he told them a parable: A certain rich mans land produced a bountiful crop.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ bɔ bamʋ kɩŋasan kɩnɩmʋ yɛ, Afulee wuya kʋ sʋ ndɔɔ nɩɩ kɩ waa agyitɔ gaa.

eng: He said to himself, What will I do? I have no place to store my harvest!.
local: Ɔnyɩn mʋ fɛ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ, Nɛnɛ nɩɩ n biti n waa? N man sʋ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ n taa mɛ agyitɔ anɩmʋ kpini waa.

eng: Then he thought, Heres what Ill do. Ill tear down my barns and build bigger ones. Thats where Ill store all my grain and goods.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Falɛ nɩɩ n biti n waa. N biti n nyita mɛ agyitɔ ɩwaakpa mʋ, kɛ n lɔŋɔ waa ɩmʋ alala‐alala. Kɛ n taa mɛ adɔɔ‐gyitɔ yɛ mɛ atɔ kpini waa tʋtɔ.

eng: Ill say to myself, You have stored up plenty of goods, enough for several years. Take it easy! Eat, drink, and enjoy yourself.
local: Nɩɩ n biti n tɔwɛ sa mɛ n‐yɩɩ yɛ, Fʋ sʋ atɔ damantɛ nɩɩ ɩ dɛ sa fʋ nsu‐nsu. Kanɩn sʋ fʋʋtɛ kɛ fu gyii, kɛ fu nuu kɛ fu gyii fu ansi. .

eng: But God said to him, Fool, tonight you will die. Now who will get the things you have prepared for yourself.
local: Amaa Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Omelensipu nɩmʋ, kagyanbwɛ nɩmʋ fʋ nkpa ii biti ɩ lɩɩ fʋ abaa tɔ. Nɩɩ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ fʋ kʋʋla sa fʋ n‐yɩɩ yɩla mʋ ii biti i biliŋi anɩmʋ lɛɛ? .

eng: This is the way it will be for those who hoard things for themselves and arent rich toward God.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kʋʋla atɔ ɛɛ yɩla ɛɛ sa mʋ n‐yɩɩ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ bɩrɛ ɔ man gyɛ atɔ wuya, falɛ nɩɩ ii biti ɩ waa mʋ.

eng: Then Jesus said to his disciples, Therefore, I say to you, dont worry about your life, what you will eat, or about your body, what you will wear.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ yɛ, Kanɩn sʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, fan man fɛ nfɛɛrɛ lɩɩ kakyɩna kanɩmʋ tɔ atɔ tirisa fɛɛ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ fen biti fan gyii, abɛɛ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ fan nyɛ bun fanɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ.

eng: There is more to life than food and more to the body than clothing.
local: Nkpa i tiri ɩ kyɔ agyitɔ, kanɩn nɩɩ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ gbaa ki tiri kɩ kyɔ atɔ bunsa.

eng: Consider the ravens: they neither plant nor harvest, they have no silo or barn, yet God feeds them. You are worth so much more than birds!.
local: Fan taa fanɛ ansi kɩɩ nbwiibi, ba mɛɛ duu nɩɩ ba mɛɛ tɩŋɛ, nɩɩ ba mɛɛ sʋ abu kɛ ba kʋʋla bamʋ agyitɔ yɩla, amaa Ɩbwaarɛ ɩɩ kɩɩ bamʋ sʋ. Fan man gyɛ akparɩ fan kyɔ nbwiibi?.

eng: Who among you by worrying can add a single moment to your life?.
local: Fanɛ anɩmʋ lɩɩ mʋ kayɩyɛɛ sʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ taalɛ taa kakɛ kʋlʋn tii mʋ nkpa sʋ?.

eng: If you cant do such a small thing, why worry about the rest?.
local: Nɩɩ fan maa mɛɛ taalɛ waa atɔ pii anɩmʋ mʋ, mɩnɛ waa nɩɩ fɛn yɩyɛɛ asansa sʋ?.

eng: Notice how the lilies grow. They dont wear themselves out with work, and they dont spin cloth. But I say to you that even Solomon in all his splendor wasnt dressed like one of these.
local: Fan kɩɩ kanan mʋ nɩɩ iyu ntʋtʋlɛ i du, i mee sun nɩɩ ɩ mɛɛ lʋʋ ataa ɩ sa ɩmʋ n‐yɩɩ. Amaa n tɔwɛ fanɛ fɛɛ, Owura lala Solomon maa mʋ atɔ danbɩrasa kpini gbaa mʋ, ɔ man taalɛ nyɛ atɔ bunsa danbɩrasa fɛɛ ntʋtʋlɛ mʋ.

eng: If God dresses grass in the field so beautifully, even though its alive today and tomorrow its thrown into the furnace, how much more will God do for you, you people of weak faith!.
local: Fanɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii pii wuya ana, nɩɩ ɩ kan ɩ gyɛ falɛ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ i bun afitiri tɔ ntʋtʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ tɔ ndaga kɛ ɔkɛ ba taa tʋʋ fʋlɔn tɔ mʋ, mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ sa fanɛ atɔ bunsa?.

eng: Dont chase after what you will eat and what you will drink. Stop worrying.
local: Fan man yɛgɛ fanɛ nkɔlɔ ɩ baa ɩ gyan atɔ mʋ nɩɩ fen biti fan gyii abɛɛ fan nuu mʋ sʋ, fan man yɛgɛ ɩnɩmʋ ɩ tɔɔrɔɔ fanɛ.

eng: All the nations of the world long for these things. Your Father knows that you need them.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba mɛɛ sun Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ, ɩ fɛ ɩnɩmʋ ana kpini nfɛɛrɛ. Nɩɩ fanɛ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ nyi fɛɛ ɩnɩmʋ ana kpini i tiri fanɛ.

eng: Instead, desire his kingdom and these things will be given to you as well.
local: Amaa fan kɩɩ biti Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ, kɛ ɔ sa fanɛ ɩnɩmʋ kpini tii sʋ.

eng: Dont be afraid, little flock, because your Father delights in giving you the kingdom.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu bɩla tɔwɛ yɛ, Kʋbʋ pii, fan man waa kufuu, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɩ bɔla fanɛ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ansi fɛɛ ɔ waa fanɛ awura‐bii mu kuwura‐gyii tɔ.

eng: Sell your possessions and give to those in need. Make for yourselves wallets that dont wear out—a treasure in heaven that never runs out. No thief comes near there, and no moth destroys.
local: Fan fɛ fanɛ npatɩyɛ kpini, kɛ fan taa afulee mʋ sa atiripu. Fan nyɛ afulee mʋ nɩɩ ɩ mɛɛ yɛ ɩ lʋwɛ mʋ yɩla sʋsʋ, ntɩn mʋ nɩɩ oyu mɛɛ yɛ ɔ taalɛ nin tiri tɔ, nɩɩ tabɩrɩta mɛɛ taalɛ nyita ɩmʋ mʋ.

eng: Where your treasure is, there your heart will be too.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ntɩn mʋ nɩɩ fʋ kapatɩyɛ kɩ bʋ mʋ, tʋtɔ nɩɩ fʋ kɔkɔlɔ gbaa kɩ bʋ.

eng: Be dressed for service and keep your lamps lit.
local: Fan kʋʋla fanɛ n‐yɩɩ kɩrɩsʋ fan gyoo, kɛ fan yɛgɛ fanɛ ɩfɩtɩla ɩ baa ɩ kɩɩ tɔ.

eng: Be like people waiting for their master to come home from a wedding celebration, who can immediately open the door for him when he arrives and knocks on the door.
local: Fɛɛ ayaafɔlɛ ba maa gyoo bamʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ ɔ lɩɩ kɩkyɩɩfɔ tɔ ba, nɩɩ ɔ kan ba da kebunɔ mʋ, kɛ ba nyɛ tigi mʋ bilen.

eng: Happy are those servants whom the master finds waiting up when he arrives. I assure you that, when he arrives, he will dress himself to serve, seat them at the table as honored guests, and wait on them.
local: Ayaafɔlɛ mʋ nɩɩ bamʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ ba tu bamʋ yɛgɛ ba tɛ ba gyoo mʋ, bamʋ ansi ii biti i gyii. N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin fɛɛ ee biti ɔ kʋʋla mʋ n‐yɩɩ kɛ mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mʋ ɔ taa agyitɔ naa yii bamʋ kɛ ba gyii.

eng: Happy are those whom he finds alert, even if he comes at midnight or just before dawn.
local: Ayaafɔlɛ mʋ nɩɩ bamʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ ba tu bamʋ kagyanbwɛ kiyiridi nsana abɛɛ kɩbwayɩkɛ yɛgɛ ba kʋʋla ba gyoo mʋ mʋ, bamʋ ansi ii biti i gyii.

eng: But know this, if the homeowner had known what time the thief was coming, he wouldnt have allowed his home to be broken into.
local: Amaa fan baa fen nyi fɛɛ, nɩɩ kɩkpaara wuya daa o nyi saŋa mʋ nɩɩ oyu ii biti o be yuuri nɩn, naafɔɔ ɔ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ yɛgɛ ba be boyi loo mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ.

eng: You also must be ready, because the Human Oneis coming at a time when you dont expect him.
local: Ɩ dagaa fɛɛ fanɛ gbaa fanɛ fan kʋʋla fanɛ n‐yɩɩ fan gyoo. Lɩɩ fɛɛ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ n biti n ba saŋa mʋ nɩɩ fan mɛɛ fɛ mɛ kɩba mʋ nfɛɛrɛ.

eng: Peter said, Lord, are you telling this parable for us or for everyone.
local: Nɩɩ Pita taasɛ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, anɛ nɩɩ fɩɩ bɔɔ kɩŋasan kanɩmʋ fɩɩ sa abɛɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ.

eng: The Lord replied, Who are the faithful and wise managers whom the master will put in charge of his household servants, to give them their food at the proper time?.
local: Nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Anɩmʋ bɩrɛ ɩ gyɛ kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ kesintin yɛ kanyaasɩn? Mʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋnɩ mʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ taa mʋ ayaafɔlɛ waa mʋ abaa tɔ, yɛ ɔ sa bamʋ agyitɔ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa.

eng: Happy are the servants whom the master finds fulfilling their responsibilities when he comes.
local: Kensi‐gyii ɩ gyɛ sa kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ nɩɩ mʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ ba tu mʋ yɛgɛ ɛɛ waa kanɩn.

eng: I assure you that the master will put them in charge of all his possessions.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin fɛɛ mʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ mʋ ii biti ɔ taa mʋ kapatɩyɛ kpini‐kpini waa mʋ abaa tɔ.

eng: But suppose that these servants should say to themselves, My master is taking his time about coming. And suppose they began to beat the servants, both men and women, and to eat, drink, and get drunk.
local: Amaa a taa fɛɛ nɩɩ kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ kan tɔwɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ fɛɛ, Mɛ‐nyɩrɩpɛ ii biti ɔ kyɛɛrɛɛ! Nɩɩ o piili ɛɛ dayɩ mʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ ayaafɔlɛ yɛ abite, nɩɩ ee gyii nɩɩ ee nuu bʋʋ bɩrɛ.

eng: The master of those servants would come on a day when they werent expecting him, at a time they couldnt predict. The master will cut them into pieces and assign them a place with the unfaithful.
local: kanɩn kɩyaafɔlɛ maŋa mʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ ii biti o be purigi mʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man fɛ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ ee biti ɔ ba, yɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man nyi sʋ. Mʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ mʋ biti ɔ tɩŋɛ mʋ kʋkyʋkyʋʋ, kɛ ɔ bɩɩtɛ mʋ kʋsʋ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ bɩɩtɛ asʋ tɔ ɔlʋn wuya ana kʋsʋ mʋ.

eng: That servant who knew his masters will but didnt prepare for it or act on it will be beaten severely.
local: Kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ nɩɩ o nyi kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ mʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ ii biti, nɩɩ ɔ man kʋʋla mʋ n‐yɩɩ ɔ waa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ee biti mʋ, bee biti ba tɩŋɛ mʋ kʋkyʋkyʋʋ gaa.

eng: The one who didnt know the masters will but who did things deserving punishment will be beaten only a little. Much will be demanded from everyone who has been given much, and from the one who has been entrusted with much, even more will be asked.
local: Amaa ɔmʋnɩ ɔ man nyi nɩɩ ɔ waa atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ mʋ, bee biti ba tɩŋɛ mʋ pii. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ba sa mʋ gaa mʋ, bee biti ba kɔɔlɛ gaa lɩɩ mʋ asɛ. Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ba taa gaa waa mʋ kɩbaa tɔ mʋ, bee biti ba kɔɔlɛ kʋkyɔ lɩɩ mʋ asɛ.

eng: I came to cast fire upon the earth. How I wish that it was already ablaze!.
local: N ba nɩn yɛ n ba yɛgɛ fʋlɔn ɔ dɛɛ kasɛ sʋ, naafɔɔ n daa n biti gbaa fɛɛ fʋlɔn mʋ tɛɛ ɩ dɛɛ.

eng: I have a baptism I must experience. How I am distressed until its completed!.
local: Awʋrʋfɔ lala ii biti i munla mɛ, nɩɩ ɩ tɔɔrɔɔ mɛ gaa fɛɛ ɩ ba kɛ ɩ ba kyʋn.

eng: Do you think that I have come to bring peace to the earth? No, I tell you, I have come instead to bring division.
local: Fen nyi fɛɛ keyuula nɩɩ n bɩya kasɛ sʋ? Ayee, n tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ nbarɩgɛ tɔ nɩɩ n bɩya.

eng: From now on, a household of five will be divided—three against two and two against three.
local: Lɩɩ ndaga ɩ sʋ ii kpee mʋ, kɩkpaara kʋlʋn sʋ mʋ, asa anuu bee biti ba barɩgɛ tɔ yɛ abaa, kɛ asa asá ba kɔ yɛ asa anyɔ, kɛ asa anyɔ ba kɔ yɛ asa asá.

eng: Father will square off against son and son against father; mother against daughter and daughter against mother; and mother-in-law against daughter-in-law and daughter-in-law against mother-in-law.
local: Nbarɩgɛ tɔ ii biti ɩ ba ɔsɛ yɛ obii‐nyɩnsa nsana, obii‐nyɩnsa yɛ ɔsɛ nsana, onyi yɛ obii‐kyɩɩsa nsana, obii‐kyɩɩsa yɛ onyi nsana, ɔsaa‐kyɩɩsa yɛ ɔsaa nsana, ɔsaa yɛ ɔsaa‐kyɩɩsa nsana.

eng: Jesus also said to the crowds, When you see a cloud forming in the west, you immediately say, Its going to rain. And indeed it does.
local: Yesu tɔwɛ sakyɔ mʋ yɛ, Saŋa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fen wu kabwaarɛ wʋlɛ maa gyaga kyɔwɛ kɩtɩyɛ mʋ, opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ fɛn tɔwɛ yɛ, Bʋlayɩ ii biti ɔ daa, nɩɩ ɛɛ daa.

eng: And when a south wind blows, you say, A heat wave is coming. And it does.
local: Nɩɩ afuu a daa a lɩɩ akpɩlɩgɛ mʋ fɛn tɔwɛ yɛ, Kududuu kii biti kɩ waa, nɩɩ ɩ waa.

eng: Hypocrites! You know how to interpret conditions on earth and in the sky. How is it that you dont know how to interpret the present time?.
local: Fanɛ kebunbun ansi wuya ana fɛn taalɛ fɛn kɩɩ kasɛ yɛ sʋsʋ fɛn kaapʋ ɩmʋ kasɛ. Nɩɩ mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fɛn mɛɛ taalɛ bɩɩ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩɩ waa nbɩɩnbɩɩ nɩmʋ kasɛ?.

eng: And why dont you judge for yourselves what is right?.
local: Mɩnɛ waa nɩɩ fɛn mɛɛ taalɛ talɛ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ bɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋrɔn kɛ fan waa?.

eng: As you are going to court with your accuser, make your best effort to reach a settlement along the way. Otherwise, your accuser may bring you before the judge, and the judge hand you over to the officer, and the officer throw you into prison.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan pwɩɩ fʋ, nɩɩ faa mʋ fen kpee asɩn ogyikpa mʋ, kpan fʋ n‐yɩɩ kɛ faa mʋ fan tɔwɛ lɔŋɔ abaa ɔkpa tɔ pɔyɩ kɛ fan fʋʋ tʋtɔ. Nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ kanɩn bɩrɛ, ee biti ɔ taa fʋ naa sa asɩn ogyipu kɛ o bun fʋ kʋpwɛ, kɛ ɔ taa fʋ naa sa obu otiipu, kɛ mɔɔ mʋ ɔ taa fʋ naa tii obu.

eng: I tell you, you wont get out of there until you have paid the very last cent.
local: N tɔwɛ fʋ fɛɛ fii biti fu sii obu maŋa tɔ, naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ fii biti fʋ ka kʋkɔ mʋ kpini‐kpini.

eng: Jesus entered Jericho and was passing through town.
local: Yesu loo Gyeriko kadɛ tɔ bɔla ɛɛ kyʋn.

eng: A man there named Zacchaeus, a ruler among tax collectors, was rich.
local: Ɔnyɩn kʋ kyɩna ɔ bʋ tʋtɔ mʋ kɩtɩɩ ɩ gyɛ Sakiyosi. Ɔ kyɩna ɔ gyɛ lenpoo akɔɔlɛpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa nɩn, nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ atɔ wuya.

eng: He was trying to see who Jesus was, but, being a short man, he couldnt because of the crowd.
local: Ɔ kyɩna ee biti o wu Yesu yɛ mu ansi, amaa maa gyɛ nyɩmɩsa dɛɛ sʋ mʋ, ɔ man taalɛ, lɩɩ sakyɔ mʋ sʋ.

eng: So he ran ahead and climbed up a sycamore tree so he could see Jesus, who was about to pass that way.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔ sɩlɛ kpee ansi tɔ naa dii oyu kʋ kɛ ɔ nyɛ wu Yesu, lɩɩ fɛɛ tʋtɔ sʋ nɩɩ Yesu ii biti ɔ bɔla.

eng: When Jesus came to that spot, he looked up and said, Zacchaeus, come down at once. I must stay in your home today.
local: Yesu maa fʋʋ opula maŋa mʋ ɔ kɩɩ sʋsʋ wu mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Sakiyosi, kpɩlɩgɛ ba bilen. N biti n sʋgɛ fʋ ayɛ ndaga.

eng: So Zacchaeus came down at once, happy to welcome Jesus.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ ɔ kpɩlɩgɛ bilen taa kinsi gyii waa mʋ nsa‐nsa.

eng: Everyone who saw this grumbled, saying, He has gone to be the guest of a sinner.
local: Asa ba wu ɩnɩmʋ nɩɩ ba piili bɛɛ kɔlɩtɛ yɛ, O kpee naa sʋgɛ alibi ɔwaapʋ sʋ.

eng: Zacchaeus stopped and said to the Lord, Look, Lord, I give half of my possessions to the poor. And if I have cheated anyone, I repay them four times as much.
local: Amaa Sakiyosi kʋsʋ yɩlɛ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu yɛ, Kɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, n biti n barɩgɛ mɛ kapatɩyɛ tɔ kɩnyɔ, kɛ n taa kɩbaafʋn sa atiripu. Nɩɩ n kan kɔɔlɛ afulee lɩɩ ɔkʋ asɛ ɩ kyɔ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ n kɔɔlɛ mʋ, n biti n ka mʋ kanɩn kʋkɔ maŋa kɩlɩɩ kɩnaa.

eng: Jesus said to him, Today, salvation has come to this household because he too is a son of Abraham.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Kamɔlɩgɛ kɩ ba fʋ ayɛ ndaga, lɩɩ fɛɛ ngbaa fʋ, fʋ gyɛ Abraham kanaana‐bii nɩn.

eng: The Human Onecame to seek and save the lost.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ n ba nɩn yɛ n ba biti kɛ n mɔlɩgɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba fɔyɩ.

eng: As they listened to this, Jesus told them another parable because he was near Jerusalem and they thought Gods kingdom would appear right away.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ san bee nu falɛ asɩn mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ maa tiri Gyerusalem tɔ mʋ, asa mʋ ba fɛ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ kii biti ki piili opula tɔ, ɩmʋ sʋ ɔ bɔ bamʋ kɩŋasan.

eng: He said, A certain man who was born into royalty went to a distant land to receive his kingdom and then return.
local: yɛ, Ɔsa kparɛ kʋ i kpee kata‐kata kadɛ kʋ, ɔ naa gyii kuwura kɛ o bwii ba.

eng: He called together ten servants and gave each of them money worth four months wages.He said, Do business with this until I return.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔ tɩɩ mʋ ayaafɔlɛ kudu nɩɩ ɔ sa bamʋ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ afulee mʋ nɩɩ i budu, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan taa kanɩn afulee maŋa sun kusun naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ n biti n bwii ba.

eng: His citizens hated him, so they sent a representative after him who said, We dont want this man to be our king.
local: Amaa ɔsʋwʋlɛ mʋ sʋ asa ba kisi mʋ, nɩɩ ba sun gya mʋ sʋ naa tɔwɛ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ee kpee mʋ owura lala mʋ yɛ, A mɛɛ biti ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ o be gyii anɛ sʋ kuwura.

eng: After receiving his kingdom, he returned and called the servants to whom he had given the money to find out how much they had earned.
local: Tʋtɔ abɩlɩsa mʋ ba bun mʋ kuwura bɩrɛ, nɩɩ o bwii ba kewu. Nɩɩ o sun tɩɩ mʋ ayaafɔlɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa afulee mʋ sa bamʋ mʋ, kɛ o wu tɔnɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba nyɛ lɩɩ kanɩn afulee maŋa sʋ.

eng: The first servant came forward and said, Your money has earned a return of one thousand percent.
local: Ɔgyankpaasa mʋ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, fu afulee mʋ, ɩ kʋʋgɛ kanɩn ɩlɩɩ kudu tii sʋ.

eng: The king replied, Excellent! You are a good servant. Because you have been faithful in a small matter, you will have authority over ten cities.
local: Nɩɩ mʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ mʋ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Nsa yɛ kusun, mɛ kɩyaafɔlɛ danbɩrasa. Lɩɩ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fʋ taalɛ gyii atɔ pii nɩmʋ sʋ yɛ kesintin mʋ, n taa ndɛ kudu waa fʋ kɩbaa tɔ yɛ fʋ kɩɩ sʋ.

eng: The second servant came and said, Master, your money has made a return of five hundred percent.
local: Ɔnyɔsapʋ mʋ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ fu afulee mʋ i bwii kʋʋgɛ ɩlɩɩ kunuu tii sʋ.

eng: To this one, the king said, You will have authority over five cities.
local: Nɩɩ mʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, N taa ndɛ nnuu waa fʋ kɩbaa tɔ yɛ fʋ kɩɩ sʋ.

eng: Another servant came and said, Master, here is your money. I wrapped it up in a scarf for safekeeping.
local: Nɩɩ mʋ kɩyaafɔlɛ kʋ gbaa ba tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, fu afulee ndee. N taa kɩmʋ ŋmina kikyibi tɔ taa yɩla katɩnkʋ.

eng: I was afraid of you because you are a stern man. You withdraw what you havent deposited and you harvest what you havent planted.
local: N sɩlɛ fʋ nɩn, lɩɩ fɛɛ fʋ asɩn ɩ bʋlʋn gaa. Nɩɩ fɩɩ taa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ man gyɛ fʋ lɛɛ, nɩɩ fɩɩ kpa atɔ mʋ nɩɩ man gyɛ fu i duu.

eng: The king replied, I will judge you by the words of your own mouth, you worthless servant! You knew, did you, that Im a stern man, withdrawing what I didnt deposit, and harvesting what I didnt plant?.
local: Mʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ mʋ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Fʋ gyɛ kɩyaafɔlɛ kumu tɔ ɔlʋn wuya. Fʋ gbaa‐gbaa fʋ kɔnɔ tɔ asɩn nɩɩ n biti n taa talɛ fʋ. Fu nyi fɛɛ mɛ asɩn ɩ bʋlʋn, nɩɩ n taa atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ mɛ lɛɛ, nɩɩ n kpa atɔ mʋ nɩɩ man gyɛ mɛ i duu.

eng: Why then didnt you put my money in the bank? Then when I arrived, at least I could have gotten it back with interest.
local: Mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fʋ man taa mɛ afulee maŋa naa yɩla afulee ɔyɩlɛkpa kɛ ɩ kʋʋgɛ agyaga sa mɛ? Naafɔɔ n maa ba falɛ kɛ n ba kɔɔlɛ ɩmaa ɩmʋ agyaga.

eng: He said to his attendants, Take his money and give it to the one who has ten times as much.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba yɩlɛ tʋtɔ mʋ yɛ, Fan kɔɔlɛ afulee mʋ lɩɩ mʋ asɛ, kɛ fan taa sa ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ ɩlɩɩ kudu tii sʋ mʋ.

eng: But Master, they said, he already has ten times as much.
local: Nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, ɔ wʋla nyɛ afulee mʋ nɩɩ i budu.

eng: He replied, I say to you that everyone who has will be given more, but from those who have nothing, even what they have will be taken away.
local: Nɩɩ ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, N tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kɩɩ ɩmʋ nɩɩ ba taa waa mʋ abaa tɔ sʋ danbɩrasa mʋ, bee biti ba taa gaa tii mʋ sʋ. Amaa ɔmʋ nɩɩ ba man taa ɩkʋ waa mʋ abaa tɔ kpini‐kpini mʋ, gbɛrɛɛ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ mʋ bee biti ba kɔɔlɛ lɩɩ mʋ asɛ.

eng: As for my enemies who dont want me as their king, bring them here and slaughter them before me.
local: Amaa ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ taa agbʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, Bamʋ nɩɩ ba gyɛ mɛ dʋn ana, nɩɩ be mee biti n maa gyii bamʋ sʋ kuwura mʋ, fan taa bamʋ ba mɔɔ mi ansi tɔ nfɩɩ. .

eng: After Jesus said this, he continued on ahead, going up to Jerusalem.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu maa tɔwɛ ɩnɩmʋ lʋwɛ mʋ, ɔ gyankpaa kʋbʋ mʋ ee kpee Gyerusalem.

eng: As Jesus came to Bethphage and Bethany on the Mount of Olives, he gave two disciples a task.
local: Maa tiri Betifage maa Betani ndɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ Olifu kɩbɩɩ ɔbʋlɛ tɔ mʋ, o sun mʋ abɩɩlapʋ anyɔ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ.

eng: He said, Go into the village over there. When you enter it, you will find tied up there a colt that no one has ever ridden. Untie it and bring it here.
local: Fan kpee kadɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ fanɛ ansi tɔ mʋ. Nɩɩ fan maa dan fen loo kɩmʋ tɔ mʋ, fen biti fan wu kekuruma‐bon kʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɔkʋ man pɩɩta dii mʋ maa kyaa oyu. Fan Saaŋɛ mʋ bɩya mɛ nfɩɩ.

eng: If anyone asks, Why are you untying it? just say, Its master needs it.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan taasɛ fanɛ yɛ, Mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fen saaŋɛ mʋ mʋ? fan tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ ii biti mʋ. .

eng: Those who had been sent found it exactly as he had said.
local: Bamʋ nɩɩ Yesu sun mʋ, ba naa wu mʋ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ mʋ.

eng: As they were untying the colt, its owners said to them, Why are you untying the colt.
local: Ba maa saaŋɛ kuruma mʋ, mʋ wuya ana ba taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fen saaŋɛ kuruma mʋ.

eng: They replied, Its master needs it.
local: Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ ii biti mʋ.

eng: They brought it to Jesus, threw their clothes on the colt, and lifted Jesus onto it.
local: Ba taa mʋ ba sa Yesu, nɩɩ ba taa bamʋ ataa gyaga mʋ sʋ. Nɩɩ ba kyɛ Yesu tɔ nɩɩ o dii kyɩna mʋ sʋ.

eng: As Jesus rode along, they spread their clothes on the road.
local: Maa kpee mʋ, asa ba taa bamʋ ataa tʋʋ ɔkpa tɔ.

eng: As Jesus approached the road leading down from the Mount of Olives, the whole throng of his disciples began rejoicing. They praised God with a loud voice because of all the mighty things they had seen.
local: Yesu maa tiri Gyerusalem opula mʋ nɩɩ ɔkpa ɩɩ kpɩlɩgɛ tɔ ii kpee Olifu kɩbɩɩ mʋ sʋ mʋ, abɩɩlapʋ yɛ kʋbʋ damantɛ ba piili bee kuusi bɛɛ yɩn Ɩbwaarɛ kinsigyi tɔ lɩɩ adangana mʋ nɩɩ ba wu Yesu maa wʋla waa mʋ sʋ.

eng: They said, Blessings on the king who comes in the name of the Lord. Peace in heaven and glory in the highest heavens.
local: Bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ waa Owura mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ ba mʋ kɩtɩɩ tɔ sʋ mʋ kusee. Keyuula kɩ bʋ sʋsʋ. Nɩɩ bʋnyaa ɩ dagaa Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: Some of the Pharisees from the crowd said to Jesus, Teacher, scold your disciples! Tell them to stop.
local: Farasii abi akʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ sakyɔ mʋ tɔ mʋ ba tɔwɛ Yesu yɛ, Ɔkaapʋpʋ, tɔwɛ fu abuupu yɛ ba bun bamʋ nnɔ sʋ.

eng: He answered, I tell you, if they were silent, the stones would shout.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, N tɔwɛ fanɛ kɛ fan nu fɛɛ, nɩɩ ba kan bun bamʋ nnɔ sʋ gbaa, halɩɩ abuu gbaa ii biti i kuusi.

eng: As Jesus came to the city and observed it, he wept over it.
local: Ba maa tiri Gyerusalem tɔ nɩɩ o wu kadɛ mʋ, o su sa kɩmʋ asa.

eng: He said, If only you knew on this of all days the things that lead to peace. But now they are hidden from your eyes.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Nɩɩ fan daa fan nyi ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ kii biti kɩ bɩya fanɛ keyuula ndaga kakɛ nɩmʋ mʋ, naafɔɔ ɩ bʋrɔn. Amaa nbɩɩnbɩɩ fen mee yɛ fen wu ɔkpa mʋ.

eng: The time will come when your enemies will build fortifications around you, encircle you, and attack you from all sides.
local: Kakaakʋ ii biti ɩ ba, kɛ fanɛ akisipu ba kʋsʋ yɩlɛ fanɛ sʋ, kyaabɔɔ fanɛ kɛ ba nyan fanɛ lɩɩ fanɛ abaafʋn kpini.

eng: They will crush you completely, you and the people within you. They wont leave one stone on top of another within you, because you didnt recognize the time of your gracious visit from God.
local: Bee biti ba da fanɛ kasɛ, fanɛ yɛ nbii mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ fanɛ asɛ kpini. Bɛ mɛɛ yɛ ba yɛgɛ fanɛ ɔpɔrɛ kubuu kʋlʋn kɩ gyaga kɩmʋ bɩrɩsa sʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ba fanɛ asɛ mʋ, fan man taalɛ pɛɛsɛ bɩɩ.

eng: When Jesus entered the temple, he threw out those who were selling things there.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu loo kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ opula tɔ, nɩɩ o piili ɛɛ gya bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ fɛ atɔ mʋ.

eng: He said to them, Its written, My house will be a house of prayer, but you have made it a hideout for crooks. .
local: Ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Ba tɔwɛ yɩla Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ yɛ, Mɛ kɩkpaara kii biti kɩ baa kɩ gyɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ kɩkpaara nɩn, amaa fan taa kɩmʋ biliŋi bamʋ nɩɩ bee yuuri ɔlʋn sʋ mʋ ɔbaalakpa. .

eng: Jesus was teaching daily in the temple. The chief priests, the legal experts, and the foremost leaders among the people were seeking to kill him.
local: Kakɛ kʋmaa nɩmʋ ɛɛ kaapʋ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ, amaa Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu bɩlɩsa yɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ yɛ asa mʋ tɔ agyankpaapʋ bee biti fɛɛ ba mɔɔ mʋ.

eng: However, they couldnt find a way to do it because all the people were enthralled with what they heard.
local: Amaa ba man taalɛ nyɛ ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ ba bɔla sʋ kɛ ba waa kanɩn, lɩɩ fɛɛ asa mʋ kpini ba taa bamʋ n‐yɩɩ mata mʋ bee nu mʋ asɛ.

eng: Jesus also said to the disciples, A certain rich man heard that his household manager was wasting his estate.
local: Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ yɛ, Atɔ wuya kʋ ɩ kyɩna ɔ bʋ tɔ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kɩɩ mʋ kapatɩyɛ sʋ. Nɩɩ atɔ wuya mʋ nu fɛɛ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ mʋ ii nyita mʋ kapatɩyɛ.

eng: He called the manager in and said to him, What is this I hear about you? Give me a report of your administration because you can no longer serve as my manager.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔ tɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Mɩnɛ asɩn nɩɩ n nu n lɩɩ fʋ sʋ falɛ? Taa mɛ kapatɩyɛ mʋ nɩɩ n taa waa fʋ kɩbaa tɔ mʋ kpini bɩya mɛ, kɛ fʋ karagɛ ɩmʋ kɔnɔ sa mɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ fʋ man bɩla yɛ fʋ kɩɩ mɛ kapatɩyɛ sʋ.

eng: The household manager said to himself, What will I do now that my master is firing me as his manager? Im not strong enough to dig and too proud to beg.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ mʋ fɛ nfɛɛrɛ yɛ, Nbɩɩnbɩɩ nɛnɛ nɩɩ n biti n waa? Mɛ‐nyɩrɩpɛ ɩɩ kɔɔlɛ mi kusun ɛɛ lɩɩ mɛ asɛ, nɩɩ n man bʋlʋn kɛ n waa kasɛ kukuu kusun, nɩɩ ɩ sʋ mɛ ipeeli yɛ n naa kʋlɛ.

eng: I know what Ill do so that, when I am removed from my management position, people will welcome me into their houses.
local: N nyi kanan mʋ nɩɩ n biti n waa nɩɩ kusun mʋ kɩ kan lɩɩ mɛ kɩbaa tɔ mʋ, asa bee biti ba kɔɔlɛ mɛ bamʋ akpaara sʋ.

eng: On one of the days when Jesus was teaching the people in the temple and proclaiming the good news, the chief priests, legal experts, and elders approached him.
local: Nɩɩ ɩ ba fɛɛ kakaakʋ Yesu bʋ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa kasɔrɔga tɔ ɛɛ kaapʋ asa yɛgɛ ɛɛ tɔwɛ bamʋ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ. Nɩɩ Aseepu abɩlɩsa yɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ yɛ kadɛ tɔ abɩlɩsa gbaa ba tii sʋ, nɩɩ ba ba mʋ asɛ.

eng: One by one, the manager sent for each person who owed his master money. He said to the first, How much do you owe my master.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔ tɩɩ mʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ akɔ wuya ana nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ ɔgyankpaapʋ mʋ yɛ, Mɛ‐nyɩrɩpɛ kʋkɔ afɩnɛ nɩɩ fʋ sʋ.

eng: They said, Tell us: What kind of authority do you have for doing these things? Who gave you this authority.
local: Nɩɩ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Tɔwɛ anɛ, anɩmʋ ɔlʋn sʋ nɩɩ fʋ sʋ fɩɩ waa atɔ nɩmʋ? Anɩmʋ ɩ sa fʋ ɔlʋn maŋa.

eng: He said, Nine hundred gallons of olive oil.The manager said to him, Take your contract, sit down quickly, and write four hundred fifty gallons.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, N sʋ kʋkɔ olifu nfɔ galɔn alɩfa aburuwa. Nɩɩ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ mʋ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Kɔɔlɛ fʋ ɔwʋlɛ, kɛ fʋ kyɩna bilen kɛ fʋ kyʋrɔɔ ana.

eng: He replied, I have a question for you. Tell me.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Ngbaa mɛ n biti n taasɛ fanɛ asɩn kʋ kɛ fan lɛɛ mɛ kɔnɔ.

eng: Then the manager said to another, How much do you owe? He said, One thousand bushels of wheat.He said, Take your contract and write eight hundred.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ ɔnyɔsapʋ mʋ yɛ, Afɩnɛ kʋkɔ nɩɩ fʋ sʋ? Nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Akyembee ɩbɔɔta kagbɩn kʋlʋn. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Kɔɔlɛ fʋ ɔwʋlɛ kɛ fʋ kyʋrɔɔ aduburuwa.

eng: Was Johns baptism of heavenly or of human origin.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee mʋ nɩɩ Gyɔn gyee asa mʋ, kɩ lɩɩ sʋsʋ nɩn abɛɛ kɩ lɩɩ asa asɛ.

eng: The master commended the dishonest manager because he acted cleverly. People who belong to this world are more clever in dealing with their peers than are people who belong to the light.
local: Atɔ wuya mʋ ba yɩn ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ mʋ nɩɩ mu ansi ɩ tɩn fɛɛ maa sʋ kanyaasɩn. Lɩɩ fɛɛ kanɩn nɩɩ dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ asa ba sʋ kensi tɩn kɩkyɔ bɛɛ waa asa ɩ kyɔ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asa ba sʋ bɛɛ waa asa mʋ.

eng: They discussed among themselves, If we say, Its of heavenly origin, hell say, Why didnt you believe him.
local: Nɩɩ bamʋ gbaa‐gbaa bamʋ ba taa kɩmʋ sʋ nfɛɛrɛ nɩɩ ba yɛ, Nɩɩ an kan tɔwɛ yɛ, Kɩ lɩɩ sʋsʋ mʋ, ee biti ɔ taasɛ yɛ, Mɩnɛ ɩ waa sʋ nɩɩ fan man kɔɔlɛ gyii.

eng: I tell you, use worldly wealth to make friends for yourselves so that when its gone, you will be welcomed into the eternal homes.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu kyaga sʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, Fan taa fanɛ dulinyaa tɔ atɔ kyɛ asa tɔ biti akyɛmɩnɛ sa fanɛ n‐yɩɩ, nɩɩ ɩ kan ba fɛɛ atɔ mʋ saŋa ɩ kyʋn mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ kɔɔlɛ fanɛ ba nkpa kakpaa tɔ.

eng: But if we say, Its of human origin, all the people will stone us to death because they are convinced that John was a prophet.
local: Amaa nɩɩ an kan tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɩ lɩɩ asa asɛ mʋ, asa mʋ kpini bee biti ba twɩɩ anɛ abuu, lɩɩ fɛɛ ba kɔɔlɛ gyii fɛɛ Gyɔn gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ nɩn.

eng: Whoever is faithful with little is also faithful with much, and the one who is dishonest with little is also dishonest with much.
local: Ɔmʋ nɩɩ ee gyii kesintin atɔ gbɛrɛɛ sʋ mʋ, ɔ bɩla ee gyii kesintin atɔ kɩkyɔ sʋ. Nɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ o mee gyii kesintin atɔ gbɛrɛɛ sʋ mʋ, kanɩn maa nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ yɛ o gyii kesintin atɔ kɩkyɔ sʋ.

eng: They answered that they didnt know where it came from.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ, ba lɛɛ Yesu kɔnɔ yɛ, An man nyi ntɩn mʋ nɩɩ Gyɔn kekyugyee mʋ lɩɩ.

eng: If you havent been faithful with worldly wealth, who will trust you with true riches?.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ fu mee gyii kesintin dulinyaa atɔ sʋ mʋ, anɩmʋ ii biti ɔ taa Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ man sʋ ɔkaa mʋ waa fʋ abaa tɔ?.

eng: Then Jesus replied, Neither will I tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Kanɩn bɩrɛ, ngbaa mɛ n mɛɛ yɛ n tɔwɛ fanɛ ɔlʋn mʋ nɩɩ n sʋ n waa ɩnɩmʋ kpini.

eng: If you havent been faithful with someone elses property, who will give you your own?.
local: Nɩɩ fʋ mee gyii kesintin ɔkʋ kapatɩyɛ sʋ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ mɛɛ yɛ sa fʋ mʋ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ da yɛ ɔ sa fʋ mʋ.

eng: Jesus told the people this parable: A certain man planted a vineyard, rented it to tenant farmers, and went on a trip for a long time.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu bɔ bamʋ kɩŋasan nɩmʋ yɛ, Ɔnyɩn kʋ dɔɔ wayɩn kʋdɔɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ taa hayɩɩ sa adɔɔpʋ kʋ, nɩɩ o kpee ɔkpa naa kyɛɛrɛɛ.

eng: No household servant can serve two masters. Either you will hate the one and love the other, or you will be loyal to the one and have contempt for the other. You cannot serve God and wealth.
local: Kɩyaafɔlɛ kʋlʋn mɛɛ taalɛ sun anyɩrɩpɛ anyɔ. Nɩɩ ɩ kan ba kanɩn mʋ, ee biti o kisi ɔkʋ kɛ ɔ ba ee biti ɔkʋ, abɛɛ ee biti ɔ baa ee sun ɔkʋ kɛ o kina ɔkʋ. Fɛn mɛɛ taalɛ sun Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ afulee.

eng: When it was time, he sent a servant to collect from the tenants his share of the fruit of the vineyard. But the tenants sent him away, beaten and empty-handed.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ oyu mʋ abi kɩtɩyɛ fʋʋ mʋ, nɩɩ kʋdɔɔ wuya mʋ sun mʋ kɩyaafɔlɛ, kpee ahayɩɩpʋ mʋ asɛ yɛ ba sa mʋ wayɩn oyu abi mʋ ɩkʋ lɩɩ mʋ kʋdɔɔ mʋ tɔ. Amaa ahayɩɩpʋ mʋ ba dayɩ kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ bwiiyaa mʋ abaa kpan.

eng: The Pharisees, who were money-lovers, heard all this and sneered at Jesus.
local: Farasii abi mʋ nɩɩ bee biti afulee gaa ba maa nu ɩnɩmʋ kpini mʋ, ba gbɛɛlɛɛ Yesu.

eng: The man sent another servant. But they beat him, treated him disgracefully, and sent him away empty-handed as well.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ bɩla bwii sun kɩyaafɔlɛ pʋpwɛ, nɩɩ ba bɩla bwii dayɩ mʋ, waa mʋ ipeeli tɔ, bwiiyaa mʋ abaa kpan.

eng: He said to them, You are the ones who justify yourselves before other people, but God knows your hearts. What is highly valued by people is deeply offensive to God.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fanɛ banɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ fɛn waa fɛɛ fanɛ ɩkpa i kyiigi asa ansi tɔ, amaa Ɩbwaarɛ nyi kanan mʋ nɩɩ fanɛ nkɔlɔ tɔ i du. Lɩɩ fɛɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ kʋtɔ kparɛ asa ansi tɔ mʋ, kɩ gyɛ kʋtɔ kisisa sa Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: He sent a third servant. They wounded this servant and threw him out.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ bɩla bwii sun ɔsasapʋ, nɩɩ ba bɩla dayɩ larɩgɛ mʋ, lɛɛ mʋ lɩɩ kʋdɔɔ mʋ tɔ.

eng: Until John, there was only the Law and the Prophets. Since then, the good news of Gods kingdom is preached, and everyone is urged to enter it.
local: Ba tɔwɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ Mosesi nbara yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ mʋ kɩkaapʋ naa fʋʋ Gyɔn saŋa. Lɩɩ kanɩn saŋa maŋa ɩ sʋ ii kpee mʋ, ba tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ kpan mʋ n‐yɩɩ ee loo to.

eng: The owner of the vineyard said, What should I do? Ill send my son, whom I love dearly. Perhaps they will respect him.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ kamaa mʋ, kʋdɔɔ wuya mʋ yɛ, Nɛnɛ nɩɩ n biti n waa? N biti n sun mɛ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ obii, akʋtɔ bee biti ba taa bɛɛrɛɛ sa mʋ.

eng: Its easier for heaven and earth to pass away than for the smallest stroke of a pen in the Law to drop out.
local: Ii biti ɩ baa ɩ bʋpɔɔ sa sʋsʋ yɛ kasɛ ɩ maa nyaa, ɩ kyɔ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ nbara mʋ pii ɩ maa lɩɩ tɔ.

eng: But when they saw him, they said to each other, This is the heir. Lets kill him so the inheritance will be ours.
local: Amaa saŋa mʋ nɩɩ kʋdɔɔ mʋ ahayɩɩpʋ ba wu obii mʋ, ba taa nfɛɛrɛ mʋ sʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ kapatɩyɛ ogyipu mʋ, fan yɛgɛ a mɔɔ mʋ, kɛ atɔ mʋ a biliŋi anɛ lɛɛ.

eng: Any man who divorces his wife and marries another commits adultery, and a man who marries a woman divorced from her husband commits adultery.
local: Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o kina mʋ‐ka nɩɩ ɔ naa kili ɔkyɩɩ kʋ mʋ, o nyita kekili, nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ o kili ɔkyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ mu‐kuli kina mʋ mʋ gbaa ɔ kɔɔlɛ ɔkʋ mʋ‐ka.

eng: They threw him out of the vineyard and killed him. What will the owner of the vineyard do to them?.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba lɛɛ mʋ lɩɩ wayɩn kʋdɔɔ mʋ tɔ nɩɩ ba mɔɔ mʋ. Nbɩɩnbɩɩ, mɩnɛ gbaa nɩɩ oyu kʋdɔɔ wuya mʋ ii biti ɔ waa ahayɩɩpʋ mʋ?.

eng: There was a certain rich man who clothed himself in purple and fine linen, and who feasted luxuriously every day.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu kyaga sʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, Afulee wuya kʋ ɩ kyɩna ɔ bʋ tɔ. Kakɛ kʋmaa ee bun atɔ bunsa danbɩrasa fɛɛ agoo maa kente, yɛgɛ ee gyii mʋ n‐yɩɩ kakɛ kʋmaa.

eng: He will come and destroy those tenants and give the vineyard to others. When the people heard this, they said, May this never happen.
local: Kʋdɔɔ wuya mʋ ii biti ɔ ba mɔɔ ahayɩɩpʋ mʋ, kɛ ɔ taa oyu kʋdɔɔ mʋ sa akʋ. Nɩɩ asa mʋ ba maa nu falɛ mʋ ba tɔwɛ yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ taa kanɩn kpee kata.

eng: At his gate lay a certain poor man named Lazarus who was covered with sores.
local: Odibapu kʋ gbaa kyɩna ɔ bʋ tɔ, nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Lasarusi mʋ nɩɩ mʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋ kpini, ɩ sʋ ɩlɔ. Nɩɩ kakɛ kʋmaa bɛɛ sʋla mʋ ba bɛɛ yɩla atɔ wuya nɩmʋ kebunɔ.

eng: Staring at them, Jesus said, Then what is the meaning of this text of scripture: The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone?.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu kɩɩ bamʋ ansi tɔ, taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Asɩnkyan yɩlasa maa tɔwɛ yɛ, Kubuu mʋ nɩɩ apɔyɩpʋ ba kina mʋ, kɩmʋ ɩ ba biliŋi kɩbɔntɔnbʋlɔntɔ kubuu kparɛ mʋ mʋ, nɛnɛ nɩɩ ɩɩ kaapʋ?.

eng: Lazarus longed to eat the crumbs that fell from the rich mans table. Instead, dogs would come and lick his sores.
local: Nɩɩ ee biti fɛɛ ɔ daa nyɛ agyitɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ lɩɩ atɔ wuya mʋ teeburi sʋ ɩ tɩyɛ kasɛ mʋ gbaa gyii. Halɩɩ igyoonɔ gbaa bɛɛ ba bee nyita mʋ ɩlɔ mʋ tɔ.

eng: Everyone who falls on that stone will be crushed. And the stone will crush the person it falls on.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɩyɛ kubuu maŋa sʋ mʋ, ee biti ɔ gyayɩ tɔ buriburi. Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ kubuu mʋ kɩ tɩyɛ mʋ sʋ mʋ, ee biti ɔ patɩyɛ tɔ.

eng: The poor man died and was carried by angels to Abrahams side. The rich man also died and was buried.
local: Saŋa kʋ tɔ mʋ odibapu mʋ be wu, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ ba taa mʋ kpee Ɩbwaarɛ nyɩmɩsa Abraham asɛ sʋsʋ. Atɔ wuya mʋ gbaa be wu nɩɩ ba pula mʋ.

eng: The legal experts and chief priests wanted to arrest him right then because they knew he had told this parable against them. But they feared the people.
local: Nɩɩ nbara akaapʋpʋ mʋ yɛ aseepu abɩlɩsa mʋ ba bɩɩ fɛɛ kɩŋasan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bɔɔ mʋ i yii bamʋ nɩn. Nɩɩ bee biti ɔkpa ba kɩtaa mʋ opula maŋa tɔ. Amaa ba sɩlɛ asa mʋ.

eng: While being tormented in the place of the dead, he looked up and saw Abraham at a distance with Lazarus at his side.
local: Ɔ naa loo Sɩtaanɛ fʋlɔn tɔ yɛgɛ ɔ bʋ awʋrʋfɔ tɔ. O diyaa mu ansi mʋ, o wu Lasarusi maa tɛ Abraham asɛ kata‐kata.

eng: The legal experts and chief priests were watching Jesus closely and sent spies who pretended to be sincere. They wanted to trap him in his words so they could hand him over to the jurisdiction and authority of the governor.
local: Yɛgɛ abɩlɩsa mʋ ba taa bamʋ ansi yii Yesu sʋ, nɩɩ ba lɛɛ apɛɛtɩpʋ akʋ yɛ ba waa mu ansi sʋ kɩdanbɩrasa. Bɛɛ fɛ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ bee biti ba nyɛ Yesu kɩtaa lɩɩ mʋ asɩn tɔwɛsa sʋ, kɛ ba taa mʋ sa ɔsʋwʋlɛ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ alʋlʋnpʋ.

eng: He shouted, Father Abraham, have mercy on me. Send Lazarus to dip the tip of his finger in water and cool my tongue, because Im suffering in this flame.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ, ɔ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ, Ɔsɛ Abraham, wu mɛ kʋwɛɛ kɛ fʋ sun Lasarusi kɛ ɔ taa mʋ kɩbaa‐bi nyɔ nkyu tɔ, kɛ ɔ taa ba yuula mɛ kanandʋlʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ n gyii awʋrʋfɔ fʋlɔn nɩmʋ tɔ.

eng: They asked him, Teacher, we know that you are correct in what you say and teach. You dont show favoritism but teach Gods way as it really is.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ apɛɛtɩpʋ mʋ ba tɔwɛ Yesu yɛ, Ɔkaapʋpʋ, an nyi fɛɛ fʋ ɩ tɔwɛ nɩɩ fɩɩ kaapʋ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋrɔn, nɩɩ fʋ mɛɛ mɛɛgɛ tɔ, amaa nɩɩ fɩɩ kaapʋ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔkpa mʋ kyɛɛkyɛɛ sʋ.

eng: But Abraham said, Child, remember that during your lifetime you received good things, whereas Lazarus received terrible things. Now Lazarus is being comforted and you are in great pain.
local: Amaa Abraham lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ. Mi‐bii, nyiŋi fɛɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ fʋ kyɩna fʋ tɛ nkpa tɔ kasɛ sʋ mʋ fʋ nyɛ atɔ danbɩrasa nɩɩ Lasarusi mɔɔ nyɛ atɔ libi, amaa nbɩɩnbɩɩ mʋ ee gyii ɔkɔn yɛgɛ fɛɛ fii gyii awʋrʋfɔ.

eng: Does the Law allow people to pay taxes to Caesar or not.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ɩ daaga fɛɛ a ka tʋʋtʋʋ sa Roma Ɩsʋwʋlɛ Owura lala Kayisa, abɛɛ a man ka.

eng: Moreover, a great crevasse has been fixed between us and you. Those who wish to cross over from here to you cannot. Neither can anyone cross from there to us.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ kpini kamaa mʋ, kɩkyɔbɔɔ lala kʋ kɩ dɛ ana fanɛ nsana, nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ lɩɩ nfɩɩ kpee fanɛ asɛ mʋ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ taalɛ, nɩɩ ɔkʋ mɛɛ taalɛ lɩɩ fanɛ asɛ ba anɛ asɛ nfɩɩ.

eng: Since Jesus recognized their deception, he said to them.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu bɩɩ fɛɛ apɛɛtɩpʋ mʋ bɛɛ da mʋ ɔtɔ tɔ nɩn, kɛ ba nyɛ nu mʋ kɔnɔ tɔ asɩn. Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ.

eng: The rich man said, Then I beg you, Father, send Lazarus to my fathers house.
local: Nɩɩ atɔ wuya mʋ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Kanɩn bɩrɛ mɛ‐sɛ n kʋlɛ fʋ nɩn, sun Lasarusi o bwii kpee mɛ‐sɛ kɩkpaara sʋ.

eng: Show me a coin.Whose image and inscription does it have on it? Caesars, they replied.
local: Fan taa nderebi mʋ ba kɛ n kɩɩ. Maa wu kɩmʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ yɛ, Anɩmʋ foto yɛ kɩtɩɩ ɩ gyan kɩmʋ sʋ.

eng: I have five brothers. He needs to warn them so that they dont come to this place of agony.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ, n sʋ mi‐supu ana anuu. Yɛgɛ ɔ naa da bamʋ amu tɔ kɛ ba man ba katɩn mʋ nɩɩ n bʋ n gyii awʋrʋfɔ falɛ.

eng: He said to them, Give to Caesar what belongs to Caesar and to God what belongs to God.
local: Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Owura lala Kayisa lɛɛ nɩn. Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Kanɩn bɩrɛ fan taa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ Owura lala Kayisa lɛɛ mʋ sa mʋ, kɛ fan taa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ mʋ sa Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: Abraham replied, They have Moses and the Prophets. They must listen to them.
local: Abraham lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Mosesi nbara yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ kɩkaapʋ mʋ ɩ bʋ bamʋ asɛ, bɛ taalɛ nu lɩɩ ɩmʋ tɔ.

eng: They couldnt trap him in his words in front of the people. Astonished by his answer, they were speechless.
local: Lɩɩ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ sʋ mʋ, i kyinkyin apɛɛtɩpʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ba laatɔ. Ɩmʋ sʋ ba man taalɛ ba nyɛ mʋ kɔnɔ tɔ asɩn kʋ ba taa kɩtaa mʋ sakyɔ mʋ tɔ.

eng: The rich man said, No, Father Abraham! But if someone from the dead goes to them, they will change their hearts and lives.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Ayee, mɛ‐sɛ Abraham, nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan lɩɩ lewu tɔ naa tɔwɛ bamʋ mʋ, bee biti ba yɛgɛ alibi kɩwaa.

eng: Some Sadducees, who deny that theres a resurrection, came to Jesus and asked.
local: Gyiwu abi kʋbʋ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ Sadusi mʋ nɩɩ ba yɛ kelewu kyiŋi man bʋ tɔ mʋ, akʋ ba ba Yesu asɛ yɛ asɩn taasɛsa. Nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ.

eng: Abraham said, If they dont listen to Moses and the Prophets, then neither will they be persuaded if someone rises from the dead.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Ba kan ba man nu Mosesi nbara yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ kɩkaapʋ mʋ, halɩɩ nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan kyiŋi lɩɩ lewu tɔ gbaa kpee bamʋ asɛ mʋ, ba mɛɛ yɛ ba kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii. .

eng: Teacher, Moses wrote for us that if a mans brother dies leaving a widow but no children, the brother must marry the widow and raise up children for his brother .
local: Ɔkaapʋpʋ, anɛ naana Mosesi kyʋrɔɔ sa anɛ yɛ, nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan wu san okulapu‐kyɩɩ, nɩɩ ba man kʋʋgɛ mʋ, i tiri fɛɛ mu‐supu ɔ taa mʋ kili, kʋʋgɛ nbii sa mʋ‐daa mʋ.

eng: Now there were seven brothers. The first man married a woman and then died childless.
local: To! Anyibii asunɔ akʋ ba kyɩna ba bʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔgyankpaapʋ mʋ kili ɔkyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man kʋʋgɛ nɩɩ o wu.

eng: The second.
local: Nɩɩ ɔnyɔsapʋ mʋ kili okulapu‐kyɩɩ mʋ, nɩɩ mʋ gbaa mʋ wu.

eng: and then the third brother married her. Eventually all seven married her, and they all died without leaving any children.
local: Nɩɩ ɔsasapʋ mʋ gbaa kili mʋ, nɩɩ o wu. Nɩɩ ɩ waa kanan halɩɩ asa asunɔ mʋ kpini ba kili mʋ, yɛgɛ bamʋ tɔ ɔkʋ maa ɔkyɩɩ mʋ ba man kʋʋgɛ.

eng: Finally, the woman died too.
local: Ɩmʋ laalaalʋwɛ mʋ okulapu‐kyɩɩ mʋ gbaa ba wu.

eng: In the resurrection, whose wife will she be? All seven were married to her.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ an biti a taasɛ yɛ fɛɛ nɩɩ kakɛ lala mʋ nɩɩ asa kpini ba kan kyiŋi lɩɩ lewu tɔ mʋ, bamʋ tɔ ɔmɔɔ ii biti ɔ baa ɔ gyɛ mu‐kuli? Lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ asunɔ maŋa kpini ba wʋla kyɩna kili mʋ.

eng: Jesus said to them, People who belong to this age marry and are given in marriage.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Dulinya nɩmʋ tɔ bɩrɛ asa bee kili.

eng: But those who are considered worthy to participate in that age, that is, in the age of the resurrection from the dead, wont marry nor will they be given in marriage.
local: Amaa bamʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wu yɛ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ ba nyɛ kelewu kyiŋi, kɛ ba kyɩna dulinyaa pʋpwɛ tɔ mʋ, ba man bɩla yɛ ba kili.

eng: They can no longer die, because they are like angels and are Gods children since they share in the resurrection.
local: Ba man bɩla yɛ ba wu kakaakʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ bee biti ba baa be du nɩn fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ. Ba gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ nbii lɩɩ fɛɛ ba maa kyiŋi lɩɩ lewu tɔ sʋ.

eng: Even Moses demonstrated that the dead are raised—in the passage about the burning bush, when he speaks of the Lord as the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob .
local: Nɩɩ lɩɩ kelewu kyiŋi asɩn bɩrɛ, Mosesi gbaa kɔɔlɛ gyii. Kanɩn sʋ, saŋa mʋ nɩɩ o wu kɩpʋnfa maa dɩyɛ mʋ, ɔ tɔwɛ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sʋ yɛ, mʋ ɩ gyɛ Abraham maa Isaki yɛ Gyekobu Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ.

eng: He isnt the God of the dead but of the living. To him they are all alive.
local: Ɔ man gyɛ alewupu Ɩbwaarɛ nɩn, amaa ɔ gyɛ asa kɩɩsa lɛɛ nɩn. Lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ansi tɔ mʋ alewupu banɩmʋ ba san ba tɛ nkpa tɔ.

eng: Some of the legal experts responded, Teacher, you have answered well.
local: Nɩɩ nbara akaapʋpʋ mʋ akʋ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔkaapʋpʋ, fʋ tɔwɛ kɩmʋ kyɛɛkyɛɛ.

eng: No one dared to ask him anything else.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ kamaa mʋ ɔkʋ man bɩla taalɛ nyɛ kɔkɔlɔ ɔsɩɩ taasɛ mʋ sɛhn.

eng: Jesus said to them, Why do they say that the Christ is Davids son?.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ taalɛ bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ mʋ gyɛ Owura Dawidi kanaana‐bii?.

eng: David himself says in the scroll of Psalms, The Lord said to my lord, Sit at my right side.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Dawidi gbaa‐gbaa tɔwɛ mʋ Ɩlʋn ɔwʋlɛ mʋ tɔ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ mɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ yɛ, Kyɩna mɛ gyisa sʋ nfɩɩ.

eng: until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet.
local: naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ n biti n taa fʋ dʋn ana ba waa fʋ ayaa ɔgyagakpa. .

eng: Since David calls him Lord, how can he be Davids son.
local: Nɩɩ Dawidi gbaa‐gbaa ɩɩ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ bɩrɛ, nɛnɛ ɩ waa sʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ mʋ kanaana‐bii.

eng: In the presence of all the people, Jesus said to his disciples.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ asa mʋ ba san bee nu mʋ, Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ.

eng: Watch out for the legal experts. They like to walk around in long robes. They love being greeted with honor in the markets. They long for the places of honor in the synagogues and at banquets.
local: Fan kɩɩ fan n‐yɩɩ sʋ danbɩrasa lɩɩ nbara akaapʋpʋ mʋ sʋ. Ba gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bee biti ɩkaalɛ lala kubun bɛɛ kyaabɔɔ tɔ, nɩɩ bee biti fɛɛ ba taa bɛɛrɛɛ fwaala bamʋ agya tɔ, bee biti ba kyɩna ngya kparɛ sʋ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa yɛ ɔgyaŋɛ gyiikpa.

eng: They are the ones who cheat widows out of their homes, and to show off they say long prayers. They will be judged most harshly.
local: Bee pɩna akulapu‐kyɩɩ bɛɛ kɔɔlɛ bamʋ akpaara, nɩɩ bɛɛ yɩlɛ ipula bɛɛ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ bʋnswɩɩsa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ nyɛ pɩna asa fɛɛ ba gyɛ asa danbɩrasa. Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ bɩɩtɛ kanɩn asa maŋa asʋ gaa.

eng: Looking up, Jesus saw rich people throwing their gifts into the collection box for the temple treasury.
local: Yesu maa bʋ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ o diyaa mu ansi wu atɔ wuya ana ba maa sʋ afulee bɛɛ waa kakɛɛ kɩdakaa tɔ mʋ.

eng: He also saw a poor widow throw in two small copper coins worth a penny.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ bɩla wu okulapu‐kyɩɩ otiripu kʋ gbaa maa taa nderebi anyɔ ba waa tɔ.

eng: He said, I assure you that this poor widow has put in more than them all.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu yɛ, N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin fɛɛ, okulapu‐kyɩɩ otiripu nɩmʋ waa tɔ afulee ɔ kyɔ bamʋ kpini.

eng: All of them are giving out of their spare change. But she from her hopeless poverty has given everything she had to live on.
local: Asa banɩmʋ kpini, ba lɛɛ lɩɩ bamʋ afulee gaa mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ mʋ tɔ nɩn. Amaa ɔkyɩɩ nɩmʋ bɩrɛ halɩɩ maa mʋ kitiri kɩnɩmʋ mʋ, ɔ taa mu afulee mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ mʋ kpini ba waa tɔ.

eng: Some people were talking about the temple, how it was decorated with beautiful stones and ornaments dedicated to God. Jesus said.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu abɩɩlapʋ mʋ akʋ bee biri kiligyima bɛɛ lɩɩ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ, kanan mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋrɔn, yɛ abuu danbɩrasa mʋ nɩɩ ba taa pɔyɩ, yɛ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ asa ba kɛɛ nɩɩ ba taa lɔŋɔ obu mʋ sa Ɩbwaarɛ. Amaa Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ.

eng: As for the things you are admiring, the time is coming when not even one stone will be left upon another. All will be demolished.
local: Ɩmʋ nɩɩ fan wu falɛ kpini, saŋa kʋ ii biti ɔ ba yɛgɛ fɛn mɛɛ bɩla yɛ fen wu kubuu kʋlʋn gbaa kɩ maa gyan kʋkʋ sʋ, bee biti ba nɩn kubuu kʋmaa nɩmʋ tʋʋ kasɛ.

eng: They asked him, Teacher, when will these things happen? What sign will show that these things are about to happen.
local: Nɩɩ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɔkaapʋpʋ, saŋa mɔmɔ nɩɩ falɛ atɔ nɩmʋ a biti a waa? Nɩɩ mɩnɛ sɩnkaala ii biti ɩ waa kaapʋ fɛɛ saŋa mʋ fʋʋ fɛɛ kanɩn atɔ nɩmʋ ii biti ɩ waa.

eng: Jesus said, Watch out that you arent deceived. Many will come in my name, saying, Im the one! and Its time! Dont follow them.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Fan baa fan da sʋ, kɛ ɔkʋ ɔ man pɩna fanɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ asa gaalaagaa bee biti ba ba mɛ kɩtɩɩ tɔ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, Mɛ ɩ gyɛ Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ mʋ. Nɩɩ bee biti ba bɩla tɔwɛ yɛ, Laalaalʋwɛ saŋa mʋ tee tiri tɔ. Amaa fan man buu bamʋ.

eng: When you hear of wars and rebellions, dont be alarmed. These things must happen first, but the end wont happen immediately.
local: Fan kan nu kɩnaa yɛ ɩkɔ sʋ mʋ, fan man waa kufuu. Ɩ dagaa fɛɛ falɛ atɔ nɩmʋ ɩ maa gyankpaa ba kanɩn pɔyɩ. Amaa ɩ mɛɛ kaapʋ fɛɛ laalaalʋwɛ mʋ tee tiri tɔ nɩn.

eng: Then Jesus said to them, Nations and kingdoms will fight against each other.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu bɩla kyaga sʋ yɛ, Ɔsʋwʋlɛ ii biti ɔ kɔ yɛ ɔsʋwʋlɛ, kɛ awuragyi yɛ awuragyi ba kɔ yɛ abaa.

eng: There will be great earthquakes and wide-scale food shortages and epidemics. There will also be terrifying sights and great signs in the sky.
local: Kɛ kasɛ ki gyingyen ipula kʋkwɩɩ‐kʋkwɩɩ, kɛ akʋn yɛ alɔ kyaŋɛsa a tɩyɛ katɩn kʋmaa nɩmʋ, kɛ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ a sʋ kufuu yɛ ɩsɩnkaala lala‐lala a lɩɩ sʋsʋ.

eng: But before all this occurs, they will take you into custody and harass you because of your faith. They will hand you over to synagogues and prisons, and you will be brought before kings and governors because of my name.
local: Amaa pɔyɩ kɛ ɩnɩmʋ kpini ɩ ba mʋ, bee biti ba kɩtaa fanɛ kɛ ba nyanla fanɛ. Bee biti ba taa fanɛ kpee kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɩgyaŋɛkpa naa gyii fanɛ asɩn, kɛ ba naa tii fanɛ ibu. Bee biti ba taa fanɛ kpee awura yɛ alʋnpʋ ansi tɔ, lɩɩ mɛ sʋ.

eng: This will provide you with an opportunity to testify.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ ii biti ɩ yɛgɛ kɛ fan nyɛ ɔkpa tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ sa bamʋ.

eng: Make up your minds not to prepare your defense in advance.
local: Amaa fan yɛgɛ ɩ baa ɩ bʋ fanɛ nfɛɛrɛ tɔ fɛɛ, ɩ man dagaa fan yɩyɛɛ lɩɩ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ fen biti fan naa tɔwɛ kɔɔlɛ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ mʋ sʋ.

eng: Ill give you words and wisdom that none of your opponents will be able to counter or contradict.
local: Kpalɩ fɛɛ n biti n sa fanɛ kanyaasɩn, kɛ n kaapʋ fanɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ fen biti fan naa tɔwɛ, yɛgɛ fanɛ akisipu ɔkʋ mɛɛ taalɛ kina abɛɛ o gyii ɩmɔɔrɛ lɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ fan tɔwɛ mʋ sʋ.

eng: You will be betrayed by your parents, brothers and sisters, relatives, and friends. They will execute some of you.
local: Fanɛ akʋʋgɛpʋ abɛɛ adaa yɛ asupu abɛɛ fanɛ kʋʋpʋ ana abɛɛ akyɛmɩnɛ bee biti ba lɛɛ fanɛ sa, kɛ ba mɔɔ fanɛ tɔ akʋ.

eng: Everyone will hate you because of my name.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ ii biti o kisi fanɛ, lɩɩ mɛ sʋ.

eng: Still, not a hair on your heads will be lost.
local: Amaa halɩɩ fanɛ ɔkʋ kinyinibi kʋlʋn gbaa mɛɛ yɛ ɔ fɔyɩ.

eng: By holding fast, you will gain your lives.
local: Fan kan nyɛ nɩɩ fan yɩlɛ fanɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii tɔ kenken mʋ, ii biti ɩ mɔlɩgɛ fanɛ.

eng: When you see Jerusalem surrounded by armies, then you will know that its destruction is close at hand.
local: Nɩɩ fan kan wu fɛɛ anaakɔpʋ ba kyaabɔɔ Gyerusalem kadɛ mʋ, fen biti fan bɩɩ fɛɛ kɩmʋ kinyita ki tiri tɔ.

eng: At that time, those in Judea must flee to the mountains, those in the city must escape, and those in the countryside must not enter the city.
local: Kanɩn sʋ bamʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Gyudeya kasɛ sʋ mʋ, ba lɩɩ sɩlɛ kpee abɩɩ tɔ, nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Gyerusalem kadɛ tɔ mɔɔ ba lɩɩ, nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man bʋ kadɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ, ba man loo Gyerusalem kadɛ mʋ tɔ.

eng: These are the days of punishment, when everything written will find its fulfillment.
local: Kpalɩ fɛɛ ɩnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ nkɛ mʋ, ɩmʋ ii biti ɩ yɛgɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ kpini ɩ ba tɔ.

eng: How terrible it will be at that time for women who are pregnant or for women who are nursing their children. There will be great agony on the earth and angry judgment on this people.
local: Nɩɩ akyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ ɩtɔ yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ nbii‐nyɛpʋsa mʋ laakʋ! Lɩɩ fɛɛ awʋrʋfɔ lala a biti a ba tɩyɛ kasɛ maŋa sʋ, kɛ Ɩbwaarɛ agbʋ lala mʋ ɩ ba Gyiwu abi sʋ.

eng: They will fall by the edge of the sword and be taken away as captives among all nations. Jerusalem will be plundered by Gentiles until the times of the Gentiles are concluded.
local: Isakunde nɩɩ bee biti ba taa mɔɔ Gyiwu abi mʋ akʋ, kɛ ba kɩtaa bamʋ akʋ naa biliŋi anyɛ ndɛndɛmandɛ ɩsʋwʋlɛ sʋ. Nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ bee biti ba taa Gyerusalem waa bamʋ kebiti naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ŋmina sa bamʋ mʋ.

eng: There will be signs in the sun, moon, and stars. On the earth, there will be dismay among nations in their confusion over the roaring of the sea and surging waves.
local: Nɩɩ ɩsɩnkaala ii biti ɩ waa kyɔwɛ yɛ ɔbwaayɩ yɛ akyikpebi tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔpʋ ɩlawʋ yɛ apaa a maa da sʋ mʋ, ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ kufuu kɩ kɩtaa asa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ kasɛ sʋ kpini, kɛ ba waa yɛrɛyɛrɛ.

eng: The planets and other heavenly bodies will be shaken, causing people to faint from fear and foreboding of what is coming upon the world.
local: Nɩɩ ba maa kɩɩ ba gyoo kanɩn atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ii biti ɩ waa falɛ mʋ, kufuu ii biti ɩ yɛgɛ asa ba kirin‐kirin, kpalɩ fɛɛ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ sʋsʋ mʋ kpini ii biti i gyingyen lɩɩ ɩmʋ ipula.

eng: Then they will see the Human Onecoming on a cloud with power and great splendor.
local: Kanɩn saŋa maŋa mʋ bee biti ba wu mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa maa sʋ ɔlʋn yɛ bʋnyaa lala n lɩɩ abwaarɛ‐wʋlɛ tɔ n ba.

eng: Now when these things begin to happen, stand up straight and raise your heads, because your redemption is near.
local: Nɩɩ fan kan wu saŋa mʋ nɩɩ falɛ atɔ nɩmʋ ii biti i piili mʋ, fan kʋsʋ yɩlɛ kɛ fan diyaa fanɛ ansi sʋsʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ fanɛ kamɔlɩgɛ mʋ ki tiri tɔ.

eng: Jesus told them a parable: Look at the fig tree and all the trees.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu bɔɔ bamʋ kɩŋasan nɩmʋ yɛ, Fan kɩɩ kɩdɩnpɔ oyu yɛ iyu mʋ nɩɩ ɩ san mʋ kpini.

eng: When they sprout leaves, you can see for yourselves and know that summer is near.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ fan wu ɩ maa pɔrɔgɛ mʋ, fen nyi fɛɛ kɩkyɛ ki tiri tɔ.

eng: In the same way, when you see these things happening, you know that Gods kingdom is near.
local: Kanɩn ɔkpa maŋa sʋ, nɩɩ fan kan wu atɔ nɩmʋ a maa waa mʋ, fan bɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ ki tiri tɔ.

eng: I assure you that this generation wont pass away until everything has happened.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin fɛɛ, ndaga kakyɩna nɩmʋ tɔ asa ba mɛɛ yɛ ba wu lʋwɛ, pɔyɩ kɛ atɔ nɩmʋ kpini ɩ waa.

eng: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will certainly not pass away.
local: Sʋsʋ yɛ kasɛ kpini ii biti ɩ kyʋn, amaa mɛ kɔnɔ tɔ asɩn mʋ bɩrɛ, ɩ mɛɛ yɛ ɩ kyʋn kpaa.

eng: Take care that your hearts arent dulled by drinking parties, drunkenness, and the anxieties of day-to-day life. Dont let that day fall upon you unexpectedly.
local: Fan baa fan da fanɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ, kɛ fan man taa fan n‐yɩɩ sa kensi‐gyii gaa, yɛ kata‐bʋʋ yɛ kakyɩna tɔ atɔ bitisa kʋkyɔ, kɛ kanɩn kakɛ maŋa kɩ man ba purigi fanɛ fɛɛ ɔdʋʋ maa da kunun mʋ.

eng: like a trap. It will come upon everyone who lives on the face of the whole earth.
local: Kpalɩ fɛɛ ii biti i purigi asa mʋ nɩɩ ba tɛ kasɛ sʋ mʋ kpini.

eng: Stay alert at all times, praying that you are strong enough to escape everything that is about to happen and to stand before the Human One.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ saŋa kʋmaa fan baa fan da sʋ, kɛ fɛn kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ kɛ ɩ lɛɛ fanɛ lɩɩ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ii biti ɩ ba falɛ, kɛ fan taalɛ yɩlɛ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mu ansi tɔ.

eng: Every day Jesus was teaching in the temple, but he spent each night on the Mount of Olives.
local: Kakɛ kʋmaa nɩmʋ Yesu ɩɩ kaapʋ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ kalaatɩpwɛ kʋmaa nɩmʋ mɔɔ ee kpee ɛɛ dɛ Olifu kɩbɩɩ mʋ sʋ.

eng: All the people rose early in the morning to hear him in the temple area.
local: Nɩɩ nyenyenfuu kʋmaa nɩmʋ mɔɔ asa mʋ bee kpee kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ bee nu mʋ asɛ.

eng: The Festival of Unleavened Bread, which is called Passover, was approaching.
local: Nkɛ mʋ nɩɩ Gyiwu abi bee gyii Kesu kɔnɔ kɔlɔŋɔ bodobodo mʋ nɩɩ ba man taa kɩfɛ tinsa waa tɔ mʋ tiri tɔ. Kanɩn saŋa maŋa nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ Kanyaŋɛ nbunɔ mʋ.

eng: The chief priests and the legal experts were looking for a way to kill Jesus, because they were afraid of the people.
local: Nɩɩ aseepu bɩlɩsa yɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ bee biti ɩkpa mʋ nɩɩ ba taa kɩtaa Yesu mɔɔ kɛ ɔkʋ ɔ man wu, lɩɩ fɛɛ ba sɩlɛ sakyɔ mʋ.

eng: Then Satan entered Judas, called Iscariot, who was one of the Twelve.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Sɩtaanɛ taa nfɛɛrɛ libi waa Gyudasi mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ Isikariyoti mʋ tɔ. Ɔ gyɛ asunpu kudu anyɔ mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ.

eng: He went out and discussed with the chief priests and the officers of the temple guard how he could hand Jesus over to them.
local: Nɩɩ Gyudasi kpee aseepu bɩlɩsa yɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa kɩkpaara akunpu bɩlɩsa mʋ asɛ, nɩɩ maa bamʋ ba naa taa nfɛɛrɛ ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ lɛɛ Yesu sa.

eng: They were delighted and arranged payment for him.
local: Nɩɩ ɩ waa bamʋ ɔkɔn, nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ kyula yɛ bee biti ba sa mu afulee.

eng: He agreed and began looking for an opportunity to hand Jesus over to them—a time when the crowds would be absent.
local: Nɩɩ o kyula nɩɩ ee biti ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa lɛɛ Yesu sa bamʋ, kanan mʋ nɩɩ sakyɔ mʋ ba man nyɛ wu.

eng: The Day of Unleavened Bread arrived, when the Passover had to be sacrificed.
local: Kakɛ mʋ nɩɩ Gyiwu abi bee gyii bamʋ Bodobodo mʋ nɩɩ ba man taa kɩfɛ tinsa waa tɔ, yɛ Kanyaŋɛ nbunɔ kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ kɩmɔɔ fʋʋ.

eng: Jesus sent Peter and John with this task: Go and prepare for us to eat the Passover meal.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu sun Pita maa Gyɔn nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan naa lɔŋɔ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ an biti a taa gyii Kanyaŋɛ nbunɔ agyitɔ mʋ.

eng: They said to him, Where do you want us to prepare it.
local: Nɩɩ ba taasɛ Yesu yɛ, Fɩnɛ nɩɩ fii biti fɛɛ a naa lɔŋɔ ɩmʋ yɩla.

eng: Jesus replied, When you go into the city, a man carrying a water jar will meet you. Follow him to the house he enters.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Fan maa dan fen loo kadɛ lala mʋ tɔ mʋ, fen biti fan gyaŋa ɔnyɩn kʋ maa sʋ kolobi yɛ nkyu. Fan buu mʋ loo kɩkpaara mʋ nɩɩ ee loo mʋ sʋ.

eng: Say to the owner of the house, The teacher says to you,Where is the guestroom where I can eat the Passover meal with my disciples.
local: Kɛ fan tɔwɛ kɩkpaara wuya mʋ yɛ, ɔkaapʋpʋ mʋ yɛ, Nkɩɩ afɔ obu mʋ nɩɩ maa mɛ abɩɩlapʋ an biti a kyɩna tɔ gyii Kanyaŋɛ nbunɔ mʋ.

eng: He will show you a large upstairs room, already furnished. Make preparations there.
local: Ee biti ɔ kaapʋ fanɛ obu lala kʋ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ abansʋrʋ mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ lɔŋɔ kɩmʋ tɔ waa kɩmʋ kʋtɔ kʋmaa. Fan lɔŋɔ agyitɔ mʋ yɩla tʋtɔ.

eng: They went and found everything just as he had told them, and they prepared the Passover meal.
local: Ba lɩɩ naa wu atɔ mʋ kpini fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ mʋ. Nɩɩ ba kʋʋla Kanyaŋɛ nbunɔ mʋ.

eng: When the time came, Jesus took his place at the table, and the apostles joined him.
local: Saŋa mʋ maa fʋʋ mʋ, Yesu maa mu asunpu ba naa kyɩna tʋtɔ bee gyii.

eng: He said to them, I have earnestly desired to eat this Passover with you before I suffer.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, naafɔɔ n biti gaa fɛɛ maa fanɛ an gyii Kanyaŋɛ nbunɔ agyitɔ anɩmʋ pɔyɩ kɛ n gyii mɛ awʋrʋfɔ mʋ.

eng: I tell you, I wont eat it until it is fulfilled in Gods kingdom.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ n tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, n man bɩla yɛ n gyii falɛ agyitɔ anɩmʋ, naa fʋʋ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ agyitɔ nɩmʋ kigyii kasɛ ɩɩ kaapʋ ɩ waa Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ.

eng: After taking a cup and giving thanks, he said, Take this and share it among yourselves.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu taa kɔɔpʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taa afwaala sa Ɩbwaarɛ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan kɔɔlɛ kɩnɩmʋ barɩgɛ tɔ sa abaa.

eng: I tell you that from now on I wont drink from the fruit of the vine until Gods kingdom has come.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ n wʋla tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, n man bɩla yɛ n nuu falɛ nta nɩmʋ naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ kɩ ba mʋ tɔ.

eng: After taking the bread and giving thanks, he broke it and gave it to them, saying, This is my body, which is given for you. Do this in remembrance of me.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ taa bodobodo, nɩɩ ɔ fwaala Ɩbwaarɛ, nɩɩ ɔ kpɛɛ kɩmʋ tɔ sa bamʋ, tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Kɩnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ mɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ fanɛ sʋ, n taa lɛɛ saraga. Fan gyii kɩmʋ taa nyiŋi mɛ.

eng: In the same way, he took the cup after the meal and said, This cup is the new covenant by my blood, which is poured out for you.
local: Ɔkpa kʋlʋn maŋa sʋ ba maa gyii kalaatɩpwɛ agyitɔ lʋwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taa kɔɔpʋ sa bamʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, Nta nɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ katɔwɛ kyula pʋpwɛ mʋ nɩɩ n taa mɛ nkalan tii ɩmʋ kɔnɔ, nɩɩ n kyɛɛgɛ wʋrɩgɛ sa fanɛ.

eng: But look! My betrayer is with me; his hand is on this table.
local: Amaa n tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ lɛɛ mɛ sa mʋ, maa mʋ abaa ɩ bʋ agyitɔ nɩmʋ tɔ.

eng: The Human Onegoes just as it has been determined. But how terrible it is for that person who betrays him.
local: Mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ n biti n kpee Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla tɔwɛ yɩla mʋ. Amaa ɔmʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ lɛɛ mɛ sa mʋ, mʋ laakʋ.

eng: They began to argue among themselves about which of them it could possibly be who would do this.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba piili bɛɛ taasɛ abaa bamʋ tɔ ɔmɔɔ ii biti ɔ waa kanɩn asɩn nɩmʋ?.

eng: An argument broke out among the disciples over which one of them should be regarded as the greatest.
local: Nɩɩ kamɔɔrɛ‐gyii kɩ tɩyɛ Yesu abɩɩlapʋ mʋ sʋ lɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ bamʋ tɔ ɔbɩlɩsa.

eng: But Jesus said to them, The kings of the Gentiles rule over their subjects, and those in authority over them are called friends of the people.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Dulinya nɩmʋ tɔ awura bɛɛ nyan bamʋ asa nɩn, amaa bamʋ asa mʋ bɛɛ tɩɩ bamʋ yɛ, Anɛ akyɛtɔpʋ.

eng: But thats not the way it will be with you. Instead, the greatest among you must become like a person of lower status and the leader like a servant.
local: Amaa fan man yɛgɛ fanɛ lɛɛ ɔ baa o du kanɩn. Fanɛ tɔ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ, o biliŋi fɛɛ kebii, kɛ fanɛ ɔgyankpaapʋ mɔɔ o biliŋi fɛɛ fanɛ kɩyaafɔlɛ.

eng: So which one is greater, the one who is seated at the table or the one who serves at the table? Isnt it the one who is seated at the table? But I am among you as one who serves.
local: Ɔmɔmɔ ɩ gyɛ ɔbɩlɩsa? Ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ ba kyɩna nɩɩ ee gyii mʋ, abɛɛ ɔmɔɔ nɩɩ ɛɛ bɩya agyitɔ mʋ? Man gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ ba kyɩna ee gyii mʋ? Amaa mɛɛ mɛ n bʋ fanɛ asɛ n sun fɛɛ kɩyaafɔlɛ.

eng: You are the ones who have continued with me in my trials.
local: Mɛ awʋrʋfɔ tɔ mʋ fan yɩlɛ mɛ kamaa.

eng: And I confer royal power on you just as my Father granted royal power to me.
local: Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ mɛ‐Sɛ sa mɛ ɔkpa fɛɛ n gyii kuwura mʋ, kanɩn ɔkpa maŋa sʋ nɩɩ ngbaa mɛ n sa fanɛ yɛ fan gyii kuwura.

eng: Thus you will eat and drink at my table in my kingdom, and you will sit on thrones overseeing the twelve tribes of Israel.
local: Nɩɩ n kan be gyii kuwura mʋ, n biti n yɛgɛ maa fanɛ an gyii, kɛ a nuu teeburi kʋlʋn sʋ, kɛ fan kyɩna nwuragya sʋ kɛ fan talɛ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi nbunɔ kudu anyɔ mʋ.

eng: Simon, Simon, look! Satan has asserted the right to sift you all like wheat.
local: Yesu tɔwɛ Pita yɛ, Simon, Simon! Nu nfɩɩ! Sɩtaanɛ kʋlɛ ɔkpa Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ, kɛ ɔ bɔla kʋtʋʋ tɔ kɩɩ sʋ kyaalɛ fanɛ fɔyɩ fɛɛ ayu waatɛ mʋ.

eng: However, I have prayed for you that your faith wont fail. When you have returned, strengthen your brothers and sisters.
local: Amaa Simon, n kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ sa fʋ kɛ fʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii kɩ man tɩyɛ. Nɩɩ fʋ kan kyɛɛgɛ fʋ nfɛɛrɛ ba mɛ asɛ mʋ, waa fʋ tɔɔmaa ana abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ɔlʋn tɔ.

eng: Peter responded, Lord, Im ready to go with you, both to prison and to death.
local: Amaa Pita lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, n kʋʋla mɛ n‐yɩɩ fɛɛ obu kitii tɔ gbaa n biti n buu fʋ kpee, nɩɩ halɩɩ lewu gbaa maa fʋ an biti a wu.

eng: Jesus replied, I tell you, Peter, the rooster wont crow today before you have denied three times that you know me.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ yɛ, Pita! Pɔyɩ kɛ kyaasɛ ɔ bʋn kagyanbwɛ nɩmʋ mʋ, fii biti fu kina ɩlɩɩ ɩsa yɛ fʋ man nyi mɛ.

eng: Jesus said to them, When I sent you out without a wallet, bag, or sandals, you didnt lack anything, did you? They said, Nothing.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ, Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ n kyɩna sun fanɛ yɛgɛ fan man sʋ afulee ɔwaakpa yɛ itaakpe yɛ asɩbɩta mʋ, fan pan kʋtɔ kʋ? Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, A man pan sɛhn.

eng: Then he said to them, But now, whoever has a wallet must take it, and likewise a bag. And those who dont own a sword must sell their clothes and buy one.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu yɛ, Amaa nbɩɩnbɩɩ bɩrɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ afulee ɔwaakpa yɛ taakpe mʋ ɔ taa. Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man sʋ sakunde mʋ, ɔ fɛ mʋ balɩmaasʋ kɛ ɔ taa sɔɔ kʋkʋ.

eng: I tell you that this scripture must be fulfilled in relation to me: And he was counted among criminals .Indeed, whats written about me is nearing completion.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ba kyʋrɔɔ mɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ asɩn yɩla yɛ, Ba taa mʋ bʋga alibi awaapʋ tɔ. Ɩmʋ sʋ n tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ kanɩn Asɩnkyan yɩlasa nɩmʋ ɩ ba tɔ kesintin nbɩɩnbɩɩ lɩɩ mɛ sʋ.

eng: They said to him, Lord, look, here are two swords. He replied, Enough of that.
local: Nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, kɩɩ isakunde anyɔ ndee. Nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɩ kʋkwɛ.

eng: Jesus left and made his way to the Mount of Olives, as was his custom, and the disciples followed him.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɩɩ tʋtɔ, nɩɩ maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba kpee olifu kɩbɩɩ mʋ sʋ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba wʋla bɛɛ waa mʋ.

eng: When he arrived, he said to them, Pray that you wont give in to temptation.
local: Ba maa fʋʋ opula maŋa mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ kɛ fan man lɩɩ tɩyɛ kʋtʋʋ tɔ kɩɩ tɔ.

eng: He withdrew from them about a stones throw, knelt down, and prayed.
local: Maa tɔwɛ kanɩn mʋ, nɩɩ o nin lɩɩ bamʋ asɛ gbɛrɛɛ naa yɔɔ muŋa mu amulii sʋ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ yɛ.

eng: He said, Father, if its your will, take this cup of suffering away from me. However, not my will but your will must be done.
local: Nsɛ, nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ fu kebiti mʋ, yɛgɛ awʋrʋfɔ anɩmʋ ɩ kɩlɩgɛ mɛ. Amaa man gyɛ mi kebiti, waa ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ fu kebiti.

eng: Then a heavenly angel appeared to him and strengthened him.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ lɩɩ sʋsʋ ba mʋ asɛ, ba waa mʋ ɔlʋn tɔ.

eng: He was in anguish and prayed even more earnestly. His sweat became like drops of blood falling on the ground.
local: Lɩɩ kayɩyɛɛ yɛ kensi payɩ sʋ mʋ, ɔ taa ɔlʋn kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ, nɩɩ kududuu mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ lɩɩ mʋ sʋ ɩɩ kyɔlɛ ɩ tɩyɛ kasɛ mʋ i du nɩn fɛɛ nkalan.

eng: When he got up from praying, he went to the disciples. He found them asleep, overcome by grief.
local: Maa kʋsʋ mʋ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ tɔ mʋ, o bwii naa tu mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba maa dɛ nsɩɩrɛ lɩɩ bamʋ kɩkpɩnɛ yɛ kɔkɔlɔ nyita sʋ.

eng: He said to them, Why are you sleeping? Get up and pray so that you wont give in to temptation.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fan dɛ nsɩɩrɛ? Fan kʋsʋ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ kɛ fan man lɩɩ tɩyɛ kʋtʋʋ tɔ kɩɩ tɔ.

eng: While Jesus was still speaking, a crowd appeared, and the one called Judas, one of the Twelve, was leading them. He approached Jesus to kiss him.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Yesu san ɛɛ maa sʋ ɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ sakyɔ kʋ ba ba lɩɩ tʋtɔ. Nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ kudu anyɔ mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Gyudasi mʋ, gyankpaa sakyɔ mʋ. Nɩɩ o nin tiri Yesu, nɩɩ ɔ baari mʋ tɔ.

eng: Jesus said to him, Judas, would you betray the Human Onewith a kiss.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ Gyudasi yɛ, Kibiri tɔ nɩɩ fʋ sʋ fɩɩ lɛɛ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa fɩɩ sa.

eng: When those around him recognized what was about to happen, they said, Lord, should we fight with our swords.
local: Tʋtɔ mʋ, Yesu abɩɩlapʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ mʋ asɛ mʋ, ba maa wu kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kii biti kɩ waa mʋ, nɩɩ ba taasɛ Yesu yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ a lɛɛ anɛ isakunde mʋ kɔ.

eng: One of them struck the high priests servant, cutting off his right ear.
local: Opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ ŋɛ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ kɩyaafɔlɛ gyisa kʋsʋ tɩn.

eng: Jesus responded, Stop! No more of this! He touched the slaves ear and healed him.
local: Amaa nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ, Fan yɛgɛ kanɩn kɩwaa. Nɩɩ Yesu taa mʋ kɩbaa mata ɔnyɩn mʋ kʋsʋ nɩɩ kɩ kyaga sʋ.

eng: Then Jesus said to the chief priests, the officers of the temple guard, and the elders who had come to get him, Have you come with swords and clubs to arrest me, as though I were a thief?.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ aseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ yɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa akunpu abɩlɩsa yɛ kadɛ tɔ abɩlɩsa mʋ nɩɩ ba ba kɩtaa mʋ mʋ yɛ, N gyɛ kumu tɔ ɔlʋn oyu nɩn nɩɩ fan sʋ isakunde yɛ agulungu kɛ fɛn ba kɔ yɛ mɛ?.

eng: Day after day I was with you in the temple, but you didnt arrest me. But this is your time, when darkness rules.
local: Kakɛ kʋmaa nɩmʋ maa fanɛ nɩn kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ tɔ, nɩɩ fan man kɩtaa mɛ. Amaa ɩnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ fanɛ saŋa fɛɛ Sɩtaanɛ kitentenbiri tɔ owura maa sun kusun.

eng: After they arrested Jesus, they led him away and brought him to the high priests house. Peter followed from a distance.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba kɩtaa Yesu, nɩɩ ba taa mʋ kpee Ɩbwaarɛ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ. Nɩɩ Pita sii kamaa tɔ o buu bamʋ kɩyaa‐kɩyaa.

eng: When they lit a fire in the middle of the courtyard and sat down together, Peter sat among them.
local: Ba maa naa fʋʋ mʋ, ba dɛɛla fʋlɔn kɩkpaara mʋ nsana bɛɛ wʋlɛ, nɩɩ Pita naa sii kyɩna bamʋ asɛ.

eng: Then a servant woman saw him sitting in the firelight. She stared at him and said, This man was with him too.
local: Nɩɩ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ kibite wu Pita maa tɛ fʋlɔn mʋ asɛ. Ɔ kɩɩ mʋ diin nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ gbaa tii Yesu sʋ.

eng: But Peter denied it, saying, Woman, I dont know him.
local: Nɩɩ Pita kyɛɛ yɛ, Ɔkyɩɩ, n man nyi mʋ kpini‐kpini.

eng: A little while later, someone else saw him and said, You are one of them too. But Peter said, Man, Im not.
local: Ɩ maa waa kesin mʋ, ɔkʋ mɔɔ bɩla wu Pita nɩɩ ɔ yɛ, Ngbaa fʋ, fʋ gyɛ bamʋ tɔ ɔkʋ. Nɩɩ Pita lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɔnyɩn n man tii su.

eng: An hour or so later, someone else insisted, This man must have been with him, because he is a Galilean too.
local: Saŋa gbɛrɛɛ kamaa mʋ, nɩɩ ɔkʋ kyaga sʋ ɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Kesintin, ɩ man sʋ ɩmɔɔrɛ yɛ ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ man tii Yesu sʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ gyɛ Galile obii nɩn.

eng: Peter responded, Man, I dont know what you are talking about! At that very moment, while he was still speaking, a rooster crowed.
local: Nɩɩ Pita tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔnyɩn, n man nyi asɩn mʋ nɩɩ fɩɩ tɔwɛ falɛ sʋ. Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ kanɩn mʋ, nɩɩ kyaasɛ bʋn.

eng: The Lord turned and looked straight at Peter, and Peter remembered the Lords words: Before a rooster crows today, you will deny me three times.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu biliŋi kɩɩ Pita surum. Nɩɩ Pita nyiŋi asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ wʋla tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Pɔyɩ kɛ kyaasɛ ɔ bʋn kɩbwayɩkɛ kɩnɩmʋ mʋ, fii biti fʋ kyɛɛ ɩlɩɩ ɩsa yɛ fʋ man nyi mɛ.

eng: And Peter went out and cried uncontrollably.
local: Nɩɩ Pita lɩɩ naa su gaa.

eng: The men who were holding Jesus in custody taunted him while they beat him.
local: Asa mʋ nɩɩ Yesu bʋ bamʋ abaa tɔ mʋ, bee yooli mʋ yɛgɛ bɛɛ dayɩ mʋ.

eng: They blindfolded him and asked him repeatedly, Prophesy! Who hit you.
local: Ba waa Yesu kebunbun ansi, nɩɩ ba da‐da mʋ ɩtan nɩɩ ba yɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ, kaapʋ anɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ da fʋ.

eng: Insulting him, they said many other horrible things against him.
local: Nɩɩ ba saalɛ mʋ asebi gaalaagaa.

eng: As morning came, the elders of the people, both chief priests and legal experts, came together, and Jesus was brought before their council.
local: Kayɩ maa kɛ mʋ, ɔsʋwʋlɛ abɩlɩsa yɛ aseepu bɩlɩsa yɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ ba ba gyaŋɛ nɩɩ ba taa Yesu ba bamʋ asɩn ogyikpa mʋ.

eng: They said, If you are the Christ, tell us! He answered, If I tell you, you wont believe.
local: Nɩɩ ba yɛ, Tɔwɛ anɛ, fʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ mʋ? Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, N kan tɔwɛ fanɛ gbaa fan mɛɛ yɛ fan kɔɔlɛ gyii.

eng: And if I ask you a question, you wont answer.
local: Nɩɩ n kan taasɛ fanɛ asɩn gbaa, fan mɛɛ yɛ fan lɛɛ mɛ asɩn maŋa kɔnɔ.

eng: But from now on, the Human One will be seated on the right side of the power of God. .
local: Amaa lɩɩ nbɩɩnbɩɩ nɩmʋ ɩɩ sʋ ii kpee mʋ, mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa n biti n baa n tɛ Owura lala Ɔlʋnpʋ Ɩbwaarɛ gyisa sʋ.

eng: They all said, Are you Gods Son, then? He replied, You say that I am.
local: Nɩɩ bamʋ kpini ba taasɛ yɛ, Kanɩn sʋ fʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Mu‐Bii mʋ? Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɩmʋ nɩɩ fan tɔwɛ maŋa.

eng: Then they said, Why do we need further testimony? Weve heard it from his own lips.
local: Nɩɩ ba yɛ, Mɩnɛ asɩn nɩɩ an bɩla an biti a nu tii sʋ? Lɩɩ fɛɛ an nu asɩn mʋ lɩɩ mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mʋ kɔnɔ tɔ.

eng: The whole assembly got up and led Jesus to Pilate and.
local: Nɩɩ kʋbʋ mʋ kpini ba kʋsʋ taa Yesu naa sa ɔsʋwʋlɛ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ Paleti.

eng: began to accuse him. They said, We have found this man misleading our people, opposing the payment of taxes to Caesar, and claiming that he is the Christ, a king.
local: Nɩɩ ba piili bɛɛ pwɩɩ mʋ asɩn nɩmʋ sʋ yɛ, An wu kanyɩn nɩmʋ maa nyita anɛ asa yɛ ba man ka tʋʋtʋʋ sa Owura lala Kayisa. Nɩɩ ɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ n‐yɩɩ yɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ Owura yɛ Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ mʋ.

eng: Pilate asked him, Are you the king of the Jews? Jesus replied, Thats what you say.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Paleti taasɛ Yesu yɛ, Fʋ ɩ gyɛ Gyiwu abi owura mʋ? Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɛɛhn, ɩmʋ nɩɩ fʋ tɔwɛ maŋa.

eng: Then Pilate said to the chief priests and the crowds, I find no legal basis for action against this man.
local: Nɩɩ Paleti tɔwɛ Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu bɩlɩsa mʋ yɛ kʋbʋ damantɛ mʋ yɛ, N man wu ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋ asɩn pwɩɩsa akʋ.

eng: But they objected strenuously, saying, He agitates the people with his teaching throughout Judea—starting from Galilee all the way here.
local: Amaa ba mata sʋ yɛ, Ɔ taa mʋ kɩkaapʋ mʋ tɔwɛ kʋsaa Gyiwu abi kpini nkɔlɔ. O piili lɩɩ Galile nɩn halɩɩ ba lɩɩ nfɩɩ.

eng: Hearing this, Pilate asked if the man was a Galilean.
local: Paleti maa nu Galile kɩtɩɩ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ yɛ, Kanyɩn maa gyɛ Kagalile‐nyɩn nɩn.

eng: When he learned that Jesus was from Herods district, Pilate sent him to Herod, who was also in Jerusalem at that time.
local: Maa bɩɩ fɛɛ Yesu lɩɩ owura Herodi ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ mʋ, ɔ taa mʋ kpiya Herodi. Saŋa maa tɔ yɛgɛ ngbaa mʋ ɔ bʋ Gyerusalem.

eng: Herod was very glad to see Jesus, for he had heard about Jesus and had wanted to see him for quite some time. He was hoping to see Jesus perform some sign.
local: Owura Herodi maa wu Yesu mʋ, mu ansi i gyii gaa, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ wʋla ee biti mʋ kyɛɛrɛɛ yɛ o wu mʋ lɩɩ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ o nu lɩɩ mʋ sʋ mʋ. Ɔ sʋ tamaa fɛɛ o wu mʋ kɛ ɔ waa adangana kɛ o wu.

eng: Herod questioned Jesus at length, but Jesus didnt respond to him.
local: Owura mʋ taasɛ mʋ asɩn taasɛsa kɩkyɔ amaa Yesu man lɛɛ mʋ kɔnɔ.

eng: The chief priests and the legal experts were there, fiercely accusing Jesus.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu bɩlɩsa yɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ mʋ ba maa sʋ ba yɩlɛ bɛɛ pwɩɩ mʋ.

eng: Herod and his soldiers treated Jesus with contempt. Herod mocked him by dressing Jesus in elegant clothes and sent him back to Pilate.
local: Nɩɩ Herodi maa mʋ anaakɔpʋ bee yooli mʋ, nɩɩ ba ŋmasɛ mʋ. Nɩɩ ba taa kaalɛ bʋnswɩɩsa danbɩrasa kʋ bun mʋ, nɩɩ ba bwiiyaa mʋ kpee Paleti asɛ.

eng: Pilate and Herod became friends with each other that day. Before this, they had been enemies.
local: Kanɩn kakɛ maŋa nɩɩ ɔsʋwʋlɛ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ Paleti maa owura Herodi ba biliŋi akyɛmɩnɛpʋ. Naafɔɔ ba kyɩna ba gyɛ adʋn nɩn.

eng: Then Pilate called together the chief priests, the rulers, and the people.
local: Nɩɩ ɔsʋwʋlɛ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ Paleti tɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu bɩlɩsa mʋ yɛ kadɛ abɩlɩsa yɛ asa mʋ kpini ba gyaŋɛ.

eng: He said to them, You brought this man before me as one who was misleading the people. I have questioned him in your presence and found nothing in this mans conduct that provides a legal basis for the charges you have brought against him.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fanɛ ɩ bɩya ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ yɛ, mʋ ɩ bɩya kʋsʋ tɔ ɔlʋn ɔsʋwʋlɛ nɩmʋ sʋ. Amaa n maa mɛɛgɛ mʋ asɩn mʋ tɔ fanɛ ansi tɔ nfɩɩ mʋ, n man wu fanɛ asɩn pwɩɩsa mʋ akʋ mʋ sʋ.

eng: Neither did Herod, because Herod returned him to us. Hes done nothing that deserves death.
local: Owura Herodi gbaa ɔ man wu alibi akʋ lɩɩ mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ o bwiiyaa mʋ bɩya anɛ. Kesintin tɔ mʋ, ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ man waa alibi mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa lewu.

eng: Therefore, Ill have him whipped, then let him go.
local: Kanɩn sʋ n biti n yɛgɛ ba tɩŋɛ mʋ awʋlɛ‐bi kɛ n yɛgɛ mʋ ɔ natɛ.

eng: But with one voice they shouted, Away with this man! Release Barabbas to us. .
local: Nɩɩ kʋbʋ damantɛ mʋ ba kuusi kɔnɔ kʋlʋn sʋ yɛ, Fan taa ɔnyɩn maŋa naa mɔɔ, kɛ fan lɛɛ Barabasi sa anɛ.

eng: Barabbas had been thrown into prison because of a riot that had occurred in the city, and for murder.
local: Barabasi nɩmʋ tɔwɛ kʋsaa asa nkɔlɔ nɩɩ ba kɔ yɛ Roma kɩbanɛ, nɩɩ ɔ bɩla mɔɔ ɔsa kʋ ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ ba tii mʋ obu.

eng: Pilate addressed them again because he wanted to release Jesus.
local: Paleti maa biti yɛ ɔ lɛɛ Yesu sʋ mʋ, o bwii tɔwɛ sa bamʋ.

eng: They kept shouting out, Crucify him! Crucify him.
local: Amaa ba maa sʋ bee kuusi yɛ, Fan da mʋ oyu laasa sʋ, fan da mʋ oyu laasa sʋ.

eng: For the third time, Pilate said to them, Why? What wrong has he done? Ive found no legal basis for the death penalty in his case. Therefore, I will have him whipped, then let him go.
local: Paleti bwii tɔwɛ bamʋ asɛ kɩsasapʋ yɛ, Mɩnɛ nɩn? Mɩnɛ alibi nɩɩ ɔnyɩn maŋa waa? N man wu alibi mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa nɩɩ ɔ dagaa lewu. Kanɩn sʋ n biti n yɛgɛ ba tɩŋɛ mʋ awʋlɛ‐bi kɛ n yɛgɛ mʋ ɔ natɛ.

eng: But they were adamant, shouting their demand that Jesus be crucified. Their voices won out.
local: Amaa ba lɔŋɔ kuusi tɔ kenken fɛɛ ɔkpa kʋmaa sʋ mʋ ba da mʋ oyu laasa sʋ. Nɩɩ bamʋ kekuusi mʋ i gyii sʋ.

eng: Pilate issued his decision to grant their request.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ Paleti waa mʋ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ ɔ waa bamʋ kebiti.

eng: He released the one they asked for, who had been thrown into prison because of a riot and murder. But he handed Jesus over to their will.
local: Kanɩn sʋ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ yɛ ɔ lɛɛ sa bamʋ mʋ, ɔ lɛɛ mʋ sa bamʋ. Mʋ ɩ gyɛ Barabasi mʋ nɩɩ ba tii mʋ obu lɩɩ mʋ kɩtɔwɛ kʋsaa asa nkɔlɔ nɩɩ ba kɔ yɛ kɩbanɛ, yɛ mʋ ɔsa kɩmɔɔ sʋ mʋ. Amaa nɩɩ ɔ taa Yesu sa bamʋ ba naa waa bamʋ kɔkɔlɔ biti.

eng: As they led Jesus away, they grabbed Simon, a man from Cyrene, who was coming in from the countryside. They put the cross on his back and made him carry it behind Jesus.
local: Ba maa sʋ bee kpee mʋ, ba gyaŋa Simon Kakereni‐nyɩn mʋ ɔ lɩɩ kukura ee loo Gyerusalem. Nɩɩ ba nyan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋla oyu laasa mʋ o buu Yesu.

eng: A huge crowd of people followed Jesus, including women, who were mourning and wailing for him.
local: Nɩɩ asa damantɛ kʋ ba buu mʋ, akyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ bee su bɛɛ yɩyɛɛ bɛɛ sa mʋ mʋ gbaa ba tii sʋ.

eng: Jesus turned to the women and said, Daughters of Jerusalem, dont cry for me. Rather, cry for yourselves and your children.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu biliŋi tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Gyerusalem akyɩɩsa, fan man sʋ sa mɛ, fan su sa fanɛ n‐yɩɩ yɛ fanɛ‐bii ana.

eng: The time will come when they will say, Happy are those who are unable to become pregnant, the wombs that never gave birth, and the breasts that never nursed a child.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ saŋa kʋ ii biti ɔ ba, kɛ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, Kusee ɩ gyɛ sa agbentipu yɛ ɩtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ man kʋʋgɛ kɩɩ abɛɛ anyɛpʋ mʋ nɩɩ nbii ba man nyɛpʋ.

eng: Then they will say to the mountains, Fall on us, and to the hills, Cover us.
local: Nɩɩ saŋa maŋa tɔ mʋ, bee biti ba tɔwɛ abɩɩ yɛ, Fan tɩyɛ anɛ sʋ! Kɛ ba tɔwɛ adenbi mɔɔ yɛ, Fan bun anɛ sʋ! .

eng: If they do these things when the tree is green, what will happen when it is dry.
local: Nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ kanɩn nɩɩ ba sʋ bɛɛ waa oyu bunboli mʋ, nɛnɛ nɩɩ bee biti ba waa iyu wʋlɛsa mʋ.

eng: They also led two other criminals to be executed with Jesus.
local: Nɩɩ ba taa asun libi wuya ana anyɔ tii Yesu sʋ ba naa mɔɔ.

eng: When they arrived at the place called The Skull, they crucified him, along with the criminals, one on his right and the other on his left.
local: Ba maa naa fʋʋ opula mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ Kumu Kɩwɛɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ba da Yesu maa asun libi wuya ana mata iyu laasa sʋ. Ɔkʋ bʋ mʋ gyisa sʋ yɛgɛ ɔbanban mʋ mɔɔ bʋ mʋ bɩna sʋ.

eng: Jesus said, Father, forgive them, for they dont know what theyre doing. They drew lots as a way of dividing up his clothing.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ yɛ, Nsɛ, taa bamʋ alibi kɛɛ bamʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ba man nyi kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa. Nɩɩ ba tʋʋ nbii barɩgɛ Yesu atɔ bunsa tɔ.

eng: The people were standing around watching, but the leaders sneered at him, saying, He saved others. Let him save himself if he really is the Christ sent from God, the chosen one.
local: Asa mʋ ba yɩlɛ tʋtɔ ba kɩɩ mʋ halɩɩ Gyiwu abi abɩlɩsa mʋ bɛɛ waa mʋ yɛrɛyɛrɛ bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔ mɔlɩgɛ akʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kan ɔ gyɛ Krisito, Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ mʋ, ɔ mɔlɩgɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ.

eng: The soldiers also mocked him. They came up to him, offering him sour wine.
local: Nɩɩ anaakɔpʋ mʋ gbaa ba ba ŋmasɛ mʋ. Nɩɩ ba sa mʋ nta nyaŋɛsa.

eng: and saying, If you really are the king of the Jews, save yourself.
local: Nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, Nɩɩ fʋ gyɛ Gyiwu abi owura kesintin mʋ, mɔlɩgɛ fʋ n‐yɩɩ.

eng: Above his head was a notice of the formal charge against him. It read This is the king of the Jews.
local: Nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ asɩn kʋ mu amu sʋ yɛ, ƆNƖMƲ Ɩ GYƐ GYUDA ABI OWURA.

eng: One of the criminals hanging next to Jesus insulted him: Arent you the Christ? Save yourself and us.
local: Asun libi wuya ana mʋ nɩɩ ba saa iyu laasa mʋ sʋ mʋ ɔkʋ piili ɛɛ saalɛ Yesu yɛ, Man gyɛ fʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ mʋ? Mɔlɩgɛ fʋ n‐yɩɩ kɛ fʋ mɔlɩgɛ anɛ.

eng: Responding, the other criminal spoke harshly to him, Dont you fear God, seeing that youve also been sentenced to die?.
local: Amaa ɔbanban mʋ pʋntɛ mʋ sʋ yɛ, Fʋ man sɩlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ anɛ kpini an bʋ kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ kʋlʋn tɔ falɛ?.

eng: We are rightly condemned, for we are receiving the appropriate sentence for what we did. But this man has done nothing wrong.
local: Anɛ bɩrɛ ba bɩɩtɛ anɛ kʋsʋ kɩmʋ ɔkpa sʋ, fɛɛ asun libi mʋ nɩɩ an waa mʋ sʋ. Amaa ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ bɩrɛ ɔ man waa alibi akʋ.

eng: Then he said, Jesus, remember me when you come into your kingdom.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Yesu, fʋ kan ba gyii fu kuwura mʋ, fu nyiŋi mɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, I assure you that today you will be with me in paradise.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ mʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, N tɔwɛ fʋ kesintin tɔ yɛ, ndaga kakɛ nɩmʋ fu biti fʋ kyɩna maa mɛ‐Sɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ Paradise tɔ.

eng: It was now about noon, and darkness covered the whole earth until about three oclock.
local: Ɩ kyɩna ɩ gyɛ kyɔwɛ amu nsana tɔ nɩn, nɩɩ kitentenbiri kɩ tɩyɛ ɔsʋwʋlɛ mʋ kpini sʋ naa fʋʋ kakyʋwapɛ kerefi asá saŋa.

eng: while the sun stopped shining. Then the curtain in the sanctuary tore down the middle.
