local: lɩɩ fɛɛ kyɔwɛ yɛgɛ kɩda. Nɩɩ kɩtaa kunsa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ barɩgɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa opula kyɩrɛkyɩrɛkpa Kɩkyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ, kɩ kyatɛ tɔ kɩnyɔ.

eng: Crying out in a loud voice, Jesus said, Father, into your hands I entrust my life . After he said this, he breathed for the last time.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu taa ɔbʋlɛ lala kuusi yɛ, Nsɛ, n taa mɛ kala waa fʋ abaa tɔ. Maa tɔwɛ ɩnɩmʋ lʋwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ mʋ ɔfʋʋtɛ tɩn.

eng: When the centurion saw what happened, he praised God, saying, Its really true: this man was righteous.
local: Anaakɔpʋ kɩlɩfa ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ maa wu atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ waa mʋ, ɔ yɩn Ɩbwaarɛ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Kesintin, ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi.

eng: All the crowds who had come together to see this event returned to their homes beating their chests after seeing what had happened.
local: Asa mʋ nɩɩ ba ba gyaŋɛ tʋtɔ bɛɛ kɩɩ mʋ, ba maa wu atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ waa mʋ, bamʋ kpini ba taa nkɔlɔ nyita sawʋ kpewu.

eng: And everyone who knew him, including the women who had followed him from Galilee, stood at a distance observing these things.
local: Amaa bamʋ nɩɩ be nyi Yesu mʋ, yɛ akyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ ba buu mʋ lɩɩ Galile ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ ba mʋ, ba yɩlɛ kata gbɛrɛɛ bɛɛ kɩɩ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ waa maŋa kpini.

eng: Now there was a man named Joseph who was a member of the council. He was a good and righteous man.
local: Ɔnyɩn kʋ ɩ kyɩna ɔ bʋ tɔ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Gyosefu. Nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ Gyuda kadɛ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ Arimatiya mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ nyɩmɩsa mʋ nɩɩ mʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi nɩɩ ɔ sʋ bɛɛrɛɛ. Nɩɩ o tii Gyiwu abi kpini abɩlɩsa kʋbʋ mʋ sʋ.

eng: He hadnt agreed with the plan and actions of the council. He was from the Jewish city of Arimathea and eagerly anticipated Gods kingdom.
local: Amaa o man kyula bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ yɛ awaasa mʋ. Ɔ kyɩna ee gyoo Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ nɩn.

eng: This man went to Pilate and asked for Jesus body.
local: Nɩɩ o kpee Paleti asɛ naa kʋlɛ Yesu kibuni mʋ.

eng: Taking it down, he wrapped it in a linen cloth and laid it in a tomb carved out of the rock, in which no one had ever been buried.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ saatɛ Yesu kibuni mʋ lɩɩ oyu laasa mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taa kɩtaa fufuli maalɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ naa pula kakyan mʋ nɩɩ ba baaŋɛ kɩfʋlɛ tɔ nɩɩ ba man pɩɩta pula ɔkʋ kɩmʋ tɔ mʋ.

eng: It was the Preparation Day for the Sabbath, and the Sabbath was quickly approaching.
local: Ɩ kyɩna ɩ gyɛ kakɛ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ kʋʋla bamʋ n‐yɩɩ ba gyoo kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ mʋ.

eng: The women who had come with Jesus from Galilee followed Joseph. They saw the tomb and how Jesus body was laid in it.
local: Akyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ ba buu Yesu lɩɩ Galile mʋ, ba buu Gyosefu nɩɩ ba naa wu kakyan mʋ, yɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa Yesu kibuni mʋ pula mʋ.

eng: then they went away and prepared fragrant spices and perfumed oils. They rested on the Sabbath, in keeping with the commandment.
local: Nɩɩ ba kpewu naa kʋʋla atɔ liifensa yɛ ituulale mʋ nɩɩ ba taa ba sʋswa Yesu kibuni mʋ. Amaa ba taa bɛɛrɛɛ sa Mosesi nbara mʋ, nɩɩ ba fʋʋtɛ kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa kakɛ maŋa.

eng: In the beginning was the Word and the Word was with God and the Word was God.
local: Lɩɩ npiili asɛ, pɔyɩ kɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ tʋʋ dulinyaa mʋ, ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kɔnɔ tɔ Asɩn mʋ bʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ Kɔnɔ tɔ Asɩn mʋ maa Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ bʋ tɔ, nɩɩ Kɔnɔ tɔ Asɩn maŋa ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: The Word was with God in the beginning.
local: Lɩɩ koo npiili asɛ mʋ, Kɔnɔ tɔ Asɩn mʋ maa Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ bʋ tɔ.

eng: Everything came into being through the Word, and without the Word nothing came into being. What came into being.
local: Kɔnɔ tɔ Asɩn mʋ sʋ nɩɩ ɔ bɔla tʋʋ kʋtɔ kʋmaa. Sɛhn man bʋ tɔ nɩɩ man gyɛ mʋ kɔnɔ tɔ Asɩn mʋ sʋ nɩɩ ɔ bɔla sʋ tʋʋ kɩmʋ.

eng: through the Word was life, and the life was the light for all people.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ Kɔnɔ tɔ Asɩn mʋ tɔ nɩɩ nkpa ɩ bʋ, nɩɩ nkpa maŋa ɩɩ ŋmaŋɛ tɔ ɩɩ sa asa kpini.

eng: The light shines in the darkness, and the darkness doesnt extinguish the light.
local: Kaŋmaŋɛ mʋ ɩɩ ŋmaŋɛ kitentenbiri tɔ, nɩɩ kitentenbiri mʋ man taalɛ dunla kaŋmaŋɛ mʋ kakaakʋ.

eng: A man named John was sent from God.
local: Ɔnyɩn kʋ kyɩna ɔ bʋ tɔ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sun mʋ, bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Gyɔn.

eng: He came as a witness to testify concerning the light, so that through him everyone would believe in the light.
local: Gyɔn ba nɩn ɔ ba tɔwɛ kesintin asɩn lɩɩ kaŋmaŋɛ mʋ sʋ sa asa, kɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ ɔ nyɛ nu kɛ ɔ kɔɔlɛ gyii.

eng: He himself wasnt the light, but his mission was to testify concerning the light.
local: Man gyɛ Gyɔn gbaa‐gbaa ɩ gyɛ kaŋmaŋɛ mʋ, mɛɛ mʋ ba nɩn kɛ ɔ ba tɔwɛ asɩn lɩɩ kaŋmaŋɛ mʋ sʋ.

eng: The true light that shines on all people was coming into the world.
local: Kesintin kaŋmaŋɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ ŋmaŋɛ sa asa kpini mʋ, kɩɩ ba dulinyaa tɔ.

eng: The light was in the world, and the world came into being through the light, but the world didnt recognize the light.
local: Dulinya nɩmʋ tɔ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kɔnɔ tɔ Asɩn mʋ wʋla ɔ bʋ. Nɩɩ mʋ sʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bɔla tʋʋ dulinyaa mʋ, nkɛɛ dulinyaa asa ba man bɩɩ mʋ.

eng: The light came to his own people, and his own people didnt welcome him.
local: Ɔ ba mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mʋ ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ, amaa mʋ gbaa‐gbaa asa ba man kɔɔlɛ mʋ.

eng: But those who did welcome him, those who believed in his name, he authorized to become Gods children.
local: Amaa asa mʋ nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii mʋ kpini mʋ, ɔ sa bamʋ ɔkpa yɛ ba be biliŋi Ɩbwaarɛ mu‐bii ana.

eng: born not from blood nor from human desire or passion, but born from God.
local: Ba man biliŋi mu‐bii ana bɔla nyɩmɩsa kakʋʋgɛ sʋ, abɛɛ nyɩmɩsa kɔkɔlɔ biti sʋ, abɛɛ ɔnyɩn kebiti sʋ. Amaa Ɩbwaarɛ gbaa‐gbaa ɩɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ba be biliŋi mu‐bii ana.

eng: The Word became flesh and made his home among us. We have seen his glory, glory like that of a fathers only son, full of grace and truth.
local: Nɩɩ Ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Kɔnɔ tɔ Asɩn mʋ ba biliŋi nyɩmɩsa, nɩɩ ɔ ba kyɩna anɛ asɛ kasɛ sʋ. An wu mʋ bʋnyaa kparɛ, mʋ nɩɩ ɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ, mʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Mu‐Bii kʋlʋn kpen mʋ. Nɩɩ kabwaala yɛ kesintin kɩ bɔla mʋ.

eng: John testified about him, crying out, This is the one of whom I said, He who comes after me is greater than me because he existed before me.
local: Mʋ asɩn nɩɩ Gyɔn daa ɛɛ tɔwɛ ɛɛ kaapʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ kɔnɔ kenken yɛ, Ɔnɩmʋ asɩn nɩɩ n wʋla tɔwɛ fanɛ, nɩɩ n tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ mɛ kamaa tɔ ɛɛ ba mʋ kyɔ mɛ, nɩɩ ɔ wʋla ɔ bʋ tɔ pɔyɩ nɩɩ ba kʋʋgɛ mɛ mʋ. .

eng: From his fullness we have all received grace upon grace.
local: Lɩɩ mʋ kabwaala mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bɔla mʋ tɔ gaa sʋ mʋ, anɛ kpini an nyɛ kusee kʋkyɔ‐kʋkyɔ.

eng: as the Law was given through Moses, so grace and truth came into being through Jesus Christ.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ bɔla Mosesi sʋ taa nbara mʋ sa. Amaa kabwaala yɛ kesintin bɩrɛ bɔla Yesu Krisito sʋ nɩn ba.

eng: No one has ever seen God. God the only Son, who is at the Fathers side, has made God known.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ man pɩɩta wu Ɩbwaarɛ kakaakʋ, amɔɔ mu‐bii kʋlʋn mʋ nɩɩ ngbaa mʋ ɔ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ, nɩɩ ɔ bʋ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ mʋ, ɩ lɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kedubi kaapʋ.

eng: This is Johns testimony when the Jewish leaders in Jerusalem sent priests and Levites to ask him, Who are you.
local: Nɩɩ Gyiwu abi alʋnpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Gyerusalem mʋ ba sun Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu yɛ Lewi abi Gyɔn asɛ ba naa taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Fʋ ɩ gyɛ anɩmʋ.

eng: John confessed , Im not the Christ.
local: Nɩɩ Gyɔn man kina kɩtɔwɛ, amaa ɔ tɔwɛ bugi tɔ yɛ, Man gyɛ mɛ ɩ gyɛ Krisito, Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ mʋ.

eng: They asked him, Then who are you? Are you Elijah? John said, Im not. Are you the prophet? John answered, No.
local: Nɩɩ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Kanɩn bɩrɛ, anɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ fʋ? Fʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Eligya mʋ? Nɩɩ ɔ yɛ, Ayee! Man gyɛ mɛ ɩ gyɛ Eligya. Nɩɩ ba bɩla taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Fʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ mʋ nɩɩ an kɩɩ mʋ ɔkpa mʋ? Nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ayee.

eng: They asked, Who are you? We need to give an answer to those who sent us. What do you say about yourself.
local: Ɩmʋ kamaa mʋ ba bɩla taasɛ yɛ, Anɩmʋ bɩrɛ ɩ gyɛ fʋ? Lɛɛ kɔnɔ sa anɛ, kɛ a nyɛ asɩn kʋ naa tɔwɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba sun anɛ mʋ. Ɔsa mɔɔ bɩrɛ gbaa ɩ gyɛ fʋ.

eng: John replied, I am a voice crying out in the wilderness, Make the Lords path straight, just as the prophet Isaiah said.
local: Nɩɩ Gyɔn lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Mɛ ɩ gyɛ ɔbʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ kii kuusi ofuli sʋ yɛ, Fan kyiigi ɔkpa mʋ sa Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ. Falɛ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Isaya kyʋrɔɔ yɩla Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn mʋ tɔ mʋ.

eng: Those sent by the Pharisees.
local: Nɩɩ Farasii abi akʋ ba sun nbɔɔ Gyɔn asɛ.

eng: asked, Why do you baptize if you arent the Christ, nor Elijah, nor the prophet.
local: ba naa taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Fʋ maa man gyɛ Krisito mʋ, abɛɛ Eligya, abɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ mʋ, mɩnɛ ɩ waa sʋ nɩɩ fii gyee asa Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee.

eng: John answered, I baptize with water. Someone greater stands among you, whom you dont recognize.
local: Gyɔn lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, N sʋ nkyu nɩn n gyee asa Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee. Amaa ɔkʋ yɩlɛ fanɛ tɔ nɩɩ fan man nyi mʋ.

eng: He comes after me, but Im not worthy to untie his sandal straps.
local: Mʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ mɛ kamaa tɔ ɛɛ ba mʋ. Amaa n man dagaa gbaa n saŋɛ mʋ asɩbɩta ɩfɛ.

eng: This encounter took place across the Jordan in Bethany where John was baptizing.
local: Asɩn nɩmʋ kpini ɩ waa Betani kadɛ tɔ nɩn. Betani bʋ Gyɔdan ɔbʋn benbe kyɔwɛ ɔlɩɩkpa, katɩn mʋ nɩɩ Gyɔn ii gyee asa Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee mʋ.

eng: The next day John saw Jesus coming toward him and said, Look! The Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world!.
local: Ɩmʋ lɩɩkaakɛ Gyɔn maa wu Yesu maa naa ɛɛ ba mʋ asɛ mʋ, ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Fan kɩɩ, ɔnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kabʋlʋpʋ, mʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ taa mʋ n‐yɩɩ lɛɛ saraga kɛ asa kpini ba nyɛ alibi kɩtaakɛɛ mʋ.

eng: This is the one about whom I said, He who comes after me is really greater than me because he existed before me.
local: Ɔnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ n wʋla n tɔwɛ mʋ asɩn yɛ, Ɔnyɩn kʋ bʋ mɛ kamaa ɛɛ ba, nɩɩ ɔ kyɔ mɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ wʋla ɔ bʋ tɔ pɔyɩ nɩɩ ba kʋʋgɛ mɛ.

eng: Even I didnt recognize him, but I came baptizing with water so that he might be made known to Israel.
local: Mɛ gbaa‐gbaa mɛ bɩrɛ n man n daa n nyi mʋ, amaa kʋtɔ mʋ sʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sun mɛ yɛ n ba gyee asa Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee mʋ, kɛ n ba nyɛ lɛɛ mʋ ifuli kaapʋ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi mʋ.

eng: John testified, I saw the Spirit coming down from heaven like a dove, and it rested on him.
local: Nɩɩ Gyɔn gyii ɩmʋ kesintin tɔwɛ yɛ, N wu Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ kɩ maa kpɩlɩgɛ lɩɩ sʋsʋ fɛɛ kuwura‐lɔpɔ nɩɩ kɩ ba kyɩna sii mʋ sʋ.

eng: Even I didnt recognize him, but the one who sent me to baptize with water said to me, The one on whom you see the Spirit coming down and resting is the one who baptizes with the Holy Spirit.
local: Naafɔɔ ngbaa mɛ n man n wʋla n nyi mʋ, amaa Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ o sun mɛ yɛ n ba gyee asa Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee mʋ, ɩ tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, Ɔmʋ nɩɩ fʋ kan wu mɛ Kayaayu kɩ maa kpɩlɩgɛ lɩɩ sʋsʋ ba kyɩna sii mʋ sʋ mʋ, mʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ taa mɛ Kayaayu mʋ gyee asa.

eng: I have seen and testified that this one is Gods Son.
local: N wu ɩnɩmʋ kpini nɩɩ n gyii ɩmʋ kesintin yɛ ɔnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Mu‐Bii mʋ.

eng: The next day John was standing again with two of his disciples.
local: Lɩɩkaakɛ mʋ, Gyɔn maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ anyɔ ba bɩla ba yɩlɛ tʋtɔ.

eng: When he saw Jesus walking along he said, Look! The Lamb of God.
local: Maa wu Yesu maa naa ɛɛ kyʋn mʋ, ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Fan kɩɩ, Ɩbwaarɛ Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ ndee.

eng: The two disciples heard what he said, and they followed Jesus.
local: Gyɔn abɩɩlapʋ anyɔ mʋ ba maa nu maa tɔwɛ kanɩn mʋ, nɩɩ ba buu Yesu.

eng: When Jesus turned and saw them following, he asked, What are you looking for? They said, Rabbi , where are you staying.
local: Yesu maa biliŋi kɩɩ mʋ, o wu ba maa buu mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Mɩnɛ nɩɩ fen biti? Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Rabi, fɩnɛ nɩɩ fʋ tɛ? .

eng: He replied, Come and see. So they went and saw where he was staying, and they remained with him that day. It was about four oclock in the afternoon.
local: Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan ba kɩɩ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ n tɛ. Ɩmʋ sʋ ba buu mʋ naa wu katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛ. Saŋa maŋa tɔ gyɛ kalaatɩpwɛ sʋ kerefi ana tɔ. Nɩɩ ba kyɩna mʋ asɛ kakɛ maŋa.

eng: One of the two disciples who heard what John said and followed Jesus was Andrew, the brother of Simon Peter.
local: Asa anyɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba nu asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Gyɔn tɔwɛ, nɩɩ ba buu Yesu mʋ, bamʋ tɔ ɔkʋ ɩ gyɛ Simon Pita mu‐supu Andiruwu.

eng: He first found his own brother Simon and said to him, We have found the Messiah .
local: Kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Andiruwu gyankpaa waa ɩ gyɛ yɛ, o biti mʋ‐daa Simon nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, An wu Mesaya mʋ. Mesaya mʋ kasɛ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ yɛ ɔ ba mɔlɩgɛ asa.

eng: He led him to Jesus. Jesus looked at him and said, You are Simon, son of John. You will be called Cephas .
local: Nɩɩ Andiruwu taa mʋ‐daa Simon kpee Yesu asɛ. Yesu kɩɩ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, N nyi fɛɛ fʋ ɩ gyɛ Simon, Gyɔn mu‐bii‐nyɩnsa mʋ. Amaa bee biti ba tɩɩ fʋ yɛ Kefasi.

eng: The next day Jesus wanted to go into Galilee, and he found Philip. Jesus said to him, Follow me.
local: Ɩmʋ lɩɩkaakɛ mʋ, Yesu waa nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ ee biti o kpee Galile ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ. O wu Filipu, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Ba buu mɛ.

eng: Philip was from Bethsaida, the hometown of Andrew and Peter.
local: Filipi lɩɩ Betisayida kadɛ tɔ nɩn tʋtɔ nɩɩ Andiruwu maa Pita gbaa ba lɩɩ.

eng: Philip found Nathanael and said to him, We have found the one Moses wrote about in the Law and the Prophets: Jesus, Josephs son, from Nazareth.
local: Nɩɩ Filipu wu Nataneli nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, An wu ɔmʋ nɩɩ Mosesi wʋla kyʋrɔɔ mʋ asɩn waa Nbara ɩwʋlɛ tɔ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ gbaa ba kyʋrɔɔ mʋ asɩn yɩla mʋ. Mʋ ɩ gyɛ Yesu, Gyosefu nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ Nasareti kadɛ tɔ mʋ mu‐bii.

eng: Nathanael responded, Can anything from Nazareth be good? Philip said, Come and see.
local: Nɩɩ Nataneli taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Amɔɔ, kʋtɔ danbɩrasa kʋ ɩ taalɛ lɩɩ Nasareti kadɛ tɔ? Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Filipu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Ba kɩɩ.

eng: Jesus saw Nathanael coming toward him and said about him, Here is a genuine Israelite in whom there is no deceit.
local: Yesu maa wu Nataneli maa naa ɛɛ ba mʋ asɛ mʋ, ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Kesintin tɔ, Ɩsɩrayɩ ɔnyɩn gbaa‐gbaa ndee, nɩɩ antɩŋɛsa kʋ man bʋ mʋ tɔ.

eng: Nathanael asked him, How do you know me? Jesus answered, Before Philip called you, I saw you under the fig tree.
local: Nɩɩ Nataneli taasɛ Yesu yɛ, Nɛnɛ nɩɩ fʋ waa fii nyi mɛ? Yesu lɛɛ mʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, N wu fʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ fʋ bʋ figi onyin tɔ, pɔyɩ nɩɩ Filipu tɩɩ fʋ mʋ.

eng: Nathanael replied, Rabbi, you are Gods Son. You are the king of Israel.
local: Nɩɩ Nataneli tɔwɛ Yesu yɛ, Ɔkaapʋpʋ, fʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Mu‐Bii mʋ! Nɩɩ fʋ ɩ bɩla fʋ gyɛ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi Owura mʋ.

eng: Jesus answered, Do you believe because I told you that I saw you under the fig tree? You will see greater things than these!.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, N maa tɔwɛ fʋ yɛ, n wu fʋ fʋ maa bʋ figi onyin tɔ sʋ nɩɩ fʋ kɔɔlɛ gyii? Fii biti fʋ ba wu atɔ damantɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ kyɔ ɩnɩmʋ gbaa.

eng: I assure you that you will see heaven open and Gods angels going up to heaven and down to earth on the Human One.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu bwii kyaga sʋ yɛ, N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin yɛ, fanɛ kpini fen biti fan wu sʋsʋ maa bugi tɔ, kɛ Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ ba ba bee dii bɛɛ kpɩlɩgɛ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa sʋ.

eng: On the third day there was a wedding in Cana of Galilee. Jesus mother was there, and.
local: Nkɛ nsa kamaa mʋ, ba kpɩɩ ɔkyɩɩfɔ Kena kadɛ tɔ Galile Ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ. Yesu mu‐nyi bʋ tʋtɔ.

eng: Jesus and his disciples were also invited to the celebration.
local: Nɩɩ ba da kɩbaa tɔ tɩɩ Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ gbaa kɩkyɩɩfɔ mʋ tɔ.

eng: When the wine ran out, Jesus mother said to him, They dont have any wine.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ nta ɩ pwagɛ bamʋ mʋ, Yesu mu‐nyi tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Bamʋ nta ɩ lʋwɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, Woman, what does that have to do with me? My time hasnt come yet.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɔkyɩɩ, man tɔwɛ mɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ n waa. Mɛ saŋa man pɩɩ fʋʋ.

eng: His mother told the servants, Do whatever he tells you.
local: Nɩɩ mu‐nyi tɔwɛ nta ayɛpʋ mʋ yɛ, Kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ fan waa mʋ, fan waa.

eng: Nearby were six stone water jars used for the Jewish cleansing ritual, each able to hold about twenty or thirty gallons.
local: Nɩɩ nkyu afʋlɛ alan asiye ɩ yɩlɛ tʋtɔ, ɩmʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ bɛɛ waa Gyiwu abi anaana‐tɔ ineesi kɩfwɩɩ. Nɩɩ kʋfʋlɛ kɩlan kʋmaa kɩ taalɛ gyii ɩgalɔn adʋnyɔ abɛɛ adɩsa.

eng: Jesus said to the servants, Fill the jars with water, and they filled them to the brim.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ nta ayɛpʋ mʋ yɛ, Fan saa nkyu bɔla alan mʋ kpini. Ɩmʋ sʋ ba saa nkyu bɔla alan mʋ kpini bɩlɩbɩlɩ.

eng: Then he told them, Now draw some from them and take it to the headwaiter, and they did.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ ba mʋ yɛ, Fan nyɛ ɩmʋ ɩkʋ naa sa nta ayɛpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ. Nɩɩ ba waa kanɩn.

eng: The headwaiter tasted the water that had become wine. He didnt know where it came from, though the servants who had drawn the water knew. The headwaiter called the groom.
local: Nta ayɛpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ maa daa nuu nkyu mʋ nɩɩ i biliŋi nta mʋ kɩɩ mʋ, o wu fɛɛ ɩ bʋrɔn. Ɔ man nyi katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɩ lɩɩ, amaa ayɛpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba saa nkyu mʋ bɩrɛ be nyi. Nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ ɔkyɩɩfɔ mu‐kuli mʋ lɩɩ ɩkaa sʋ.

eng: and said, Everyone serves the good wine first. They bring out the second-rate wine only when the guests are drinking freely. You kept the good wine until now.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ ɩ daa kɩlɛɛ nta danbɩrasa pɔyɩ. Nɩɩ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ nuu bʋʋ mʋ, kɛ ɔ lan bɩya nta mʋ nɩɩ ɩ man bʋrɔn mʋ. Amaa fʋ taa ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋrɔn yɩla ba fʋʋ saŋa nɩmʋ.

eng: This was the first miraculous sign that Jesus did in Cana of Galilee. He revealed his glory, and his disciples believed in him.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ asɩn kyinkyinsa gyankpaasa mʋ nɩɩ Yesu waa. Ɔ waa ɩmʋ Kena kadɛ tɔ Galile ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ mʋ bʋnyaa mʋ kaapʋ. Nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii.

eng: After this, Jesus and his mother, his brothers, and his disciples went down to Capernaum and stayed there for a few days.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ kamaa mʋ maa mu‐nyi yɛ mu‐supu ana yɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba lɩɩ tʋtɔ kpee Kapanum kadɛ tɔ. Nɩɩ ba kyɩna tʋtɔ nkɛ nkʋ.

eng: It was nearly time for the Jewish Passover, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Gyiwu abi Kesu kɔnɔ Kanyaŋɛ nbunɔ kɩ san gbɛrɛɛ kɩ fʋʋ mʋ, Yesu kpee Gyerusalem kadɛ tɔ.

eng: He found in the temple those who were selling cattle, sheep, and doves, as well as those involved in exchanging currency sitting there.
local: Maa fʋʋ tʋtɔ mʋ, nɩɩ o kpee kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa opula mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ o wu anyɩn akʋ ba maa fɛ ɩnaatɛ, yɛ nbʋlʋpʋ yɛ awura‐lɔpɔ yɛgɛ akʋ mɔɔ ba tɛ iteeburu asɛ bɛɛ kyɛrɛ afulee.

eng: He made a whip from ropes and chased them all out of the temple, including the cattle and the sheep. He scattered the coins and overturned the tables of those who exchanged currency.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ taa ɔfɛ waa kʋkyʋkyʋʋ gyan bamaa bamʋ ɩnaatɛ yɛ nbʋlʋpʋ lɩɩ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa opula. Nɩɩ ɔ yasɛ bamʋ afulee abi tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ bala bamʋ iteeburu bun.

eng: He said to the dove sellers, Get these things out of here! Dont make my Fathers house a place of business.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ fɛ awura‐lɔpɔ mʋ yɛ, Fan lɛɛ bamʋ lɩɩ nfɩɩ. Fan man taa Mɛ‐Sɛ ayɛ biliŋi kɩgya ogyikpa.

eng: His disciples remembered that it is written, Passion for your house consumes me.
local: Nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba nyiŋi Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, Kebiti mʋ nɩɩ n sʋ sa kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ kii gyii mi kumu.

eng: Then the Jewish leaders asked him, By what authority are you doing these things? What miraculous sign will you show us.
local: Nɩɩ Gyiwu abɩlɩsa mʋ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Mɩnɛ asɩn kyinkyinsa nɩɩ fɩɩ taalɛ waa kaapʋ anɛ, kɛ ɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ fʋ sʋ ɔkpa fʋ waa ɩnɩmʋ.

eng: Jesus answered, Destroy this temple and in three days Ill raise it up.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ kɩŋasan tɔ yɛ Fan boyi kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ nɩmʋ, kɛ n taa nkɛ nsa kʋsaa kɩmʋ yii.

eng: The Jewish leaders replied, It took forty-six years to build this temple, and you will raise it up in three days.
local: Nɩɩ Gyiwu abi mʋ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Nsu adɩna yɛ asiye nɩɩ ba taa pɔyɩ Kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ nɩmʋ, nɩɩ fɛɛ fʋ fʋ yɛ fɩɩ taalɛ taa nkɛ nsa kʋsaa kɩmʋ yii.

eng: But the temple Jesus was talking about was his body.
local: Kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ kɩmʋ asɩn falɛ, ɩ gyɛ mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mʋ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ nɩn, man gyɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ gbaa‐gbaa.

eng: After he was raised from the dead, his disciples remembered what he had said, and they believed the scripture and the word that Jesus had spoken.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kyiŋi mʋ lɩɩ lewu tɔ mʋ, mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba nyiŋi yɛ ɔ wʋla tɔwɛ bamʋ ɩnɩmʋ. Nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ yɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ mʋ gyii.

eng: While Jesus was in Jerusalem for the Passover Festival, many believed in his name because they saw the miraculous signs that he did.
local: Asɩn kyinkyinsa kʋkyɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa Gyerusalem kadɛ tɔ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Gyiwu abi bee gyii bamʋ Kesu kɔnɔ Kanyaŋɛ nbunɔ mʋ, asa gaalaagaa ba wu nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii.

eng: But Jesus didnt trust himself to them because he knew all people.
local: Amaa Yesu bɩrɛ, ɔ man taa mʋ n‐yɩɩ tʋʋ bamʋ sʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ o nyi kanan mʋ nɩɩ asa kpini ba du.

eng: He didnt need anyone to tell him about human nature, for he knew what human nature was.
local: Nɩɩ ɩ man tiri mʋ fɛɛ ɔkʋ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ kanan mʋ nɩɩ asa ba du, lɩɩ fɛɛ o nyi kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ asa nkɔlɔ tɔ.

eng: Very early in the morning on the first day of the week, the women went to the tomb, bringing the fragrant spices they had prepared.
local: Gyiwu abi kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ lɩɩkaakɛ kɩbwayɩkɛ mʋ, nɩɩ akyɩɩ mʋ ba taa atɔ liifensa mʋ nɩɩ ba kʋʋla yɩla mʋ kpee Yesu kakyan asɛ.

eng: They found the stone rolled away from the tomb.
local: Ba naa wu fɛɛ kubuu mʋ nɩɩ ba taa tii kakyan mʋ, kɩ maa gbiliti lɩɩ kɩmʋ kɔnɔ sʋ.

eng: but when they went in, they didnt find the body of the Lord Jesus.
local: Amaa ba maa loo kakyan mʋ tɔ mʋ, ba man wu Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu kubuni mʋ.

eng: They didnt know what to make of this. Suddenly, two men were standing beside them in gleaming bright clothing.
local: Ɩ maa waa bamʋ kanankʋ nɩɩ ba yɩlɛ bɛɛ fɛ ɩmʋ nfɛɛrɛ mʋ, opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ anyɩn anyɔ akʋ ba ba yɩlɛ bamʋ asɛ. Ba bun kɩkalala mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ ŋalɩgɛ sʋ fɛɛ bʋlayɩ maa wɛɛ ɩsɛɛ mʋ.

eng: The women were frightened and bowed their faces toward the ground, but the men said to them, Why do you look for the living among the dead?.
local: Nɩɩ kufuu kɩ kɩtaa akyɩɩ mʋ gaa, nɩɩ ba nyɔn bamʋ amu yii tɔ. Nɩɩ anyɩn anyɔ mʋ ba taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fan baa fen biti nyɩmɩsa kɩɩsa lɩɩ alewupu tɔ.

eng: He isnt here, but has been raised. Remember what he told you while he was still in Galilee.
local: Ɔ man bʋ nfɩɩ, o kyiŋi lɩɩ alewupu tɔ. Fan nyiŋi asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ fanɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ fanɛ asɛ Galile mʋ yɛ.

eng: that the Human One must be handed over to sinners, be crucified, and on the third day rise again.
local: Ɩ dagaa fɛɛ ba lɛɛ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ waa asa libi abaa tɔ, kɛ ba da mɛ oyu laasa sʋ mɔɔ, kɛ nkɛ nsa kamaa mʋ, n kyiŋi lɩɩ lewu tɔ.

eng: Then they remembered his words.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ akyɩɩ mʋ ba nyiŋi mʋ asɩn mʋ.

eng: When they returned from the tomb, they reported all these things to the eleven and all the others.
local: Ba maa lɩɩ kakyan mʋ asɛ ba mʋ, ba tɔwɛ asɩn nɩmʋ kpini sa Yesu abɩɩlapʋ kudu akʋ mʋ yɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba san mʋ kpini.

eng: It was Mary Magdalene, Joanna, Mary the mother of James, and the other women with them who told these things to the apostles.
local: Akyɩɩ maŋa ɩ gyɛ Mɛɛrɩ Magidalen, Gyowana, Gyemisi mu‐nyi Mɛɛrɩ yɛ akyɩɩ akʋ, ɩ tɔwɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ sa abɩɩlapʋ mʋ.

eng: Their words struck the apostles as nonsense, and they didnt believe the women.
local: Amaa abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba man kɔɔlɛ akyɩɩ mʋ asɩn mʋ gyii, lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ asɩn mʋ a du nɩn fɛɛ amelensi‐sɩn mʋ.

eng: But Peter ran to the tomb. When he bent over to look inside, he saw only the linen cloth. Then he returned home, wondering what had happened.
local: Amaa Pita kʋsʋ sɩlɛ kpee kakyan mʋ asɛ. Maa suŋa kɩɩ kakyan mʋ tɔ mʋ, o wu kɩtaa fufuli mʋ nɩɩ ba taa maalɛ mʋ mʋ nkʋn. Nɩɩ ɔ fɛ ɩmʋ nfɛɛrɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ waa nɩɩ o bwii kpewu.

eng: On that same day, two disciples were traveling to a village called Emmaus, about seven miles from Jerusalem.
local: Kanɩn kakɛ maŋa mʋ, mʋ abɩɩlapʋ anyɔ ba kyɩna bee kpee Emawusi kadɛ tɔ. Lɩɩ Gyerusalem kpee tʋtɔ gyɛ ɩmaalɩ asunɔ.

eng: They were talking to each other about everything that had happened.
local: Ba maa kyɩna ba naa bee kpee mʋ bɛɛ tɔwɛ abaa asɛ lɩɩ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ waa mʋ kpini sʋ.

eng: While they were discussing these things, Jesus himself arrived and joined them on their journey.
local: Ba maa tɔwɛ abaa asɛ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa kiligyima lɩɩ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ waa mʋ sʋ mʋ, Yesu gbaa‐gbaa ba bamʋ asɛ yɛgɛ maa bamʋ ba naa.

eng: They were prevented from recognizing him.
local: Amaa Ɩbwaarɛ man yɛgɛ nɩɩ ba bɩɩ mʋ.

eng: He said to them, What are you talking about as you walk along? They stopped, their faces downcast.
local: Ɔ taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Mɩnɛ kiligyima nɩɩ fan naa fen biri kanɩn? Nɩɩ ba sii yɩlɛ nyaŋɛ bamʋ ansi tɔ.

eng: The one named Cleopas replied, Are you the only visitor to Jerusalem who is unaware of the things that have taken place there over the last few days.
local: Bamʋ tɔ ɔmʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Kelopasi mʋ taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Fʋ nkʋn ɩ gyɛ ɔfɔ mʋ nɩɩ fʋ bʋ Gyerusalem nfɩɩ nɩɩ fʋ man nyi asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɩ waa tʋtɔ nkɛ nnyɔ nɩmʋ tɔ.

eng: He said to them, What things? They said to him, The things about Jesus of Nazareth. Because of his powerful deeds and words, he was recognized by God and all the people as a prophet.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Mɩnɛ asɩn? Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Lɩɩ Yesu Kanasareti‐nyɩn mʋ sʋ. Ɔ kyɩna ɔ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ nɩn, nɩɩ ɔlʋn bʋ mʋ atɔwɛsa yɛ mʋ awaasa kpini tɔ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ asa kpini ansi tɔ.

eng: But our chief priests and our leaders handed him over to be sentenced to death, and they crucified him.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu bɩlɩsa yɛ ɔsʋwʋlɛ akɩɩsʋpʋ ba taa mʋ sa yɛ ba mɔɔ nɩɩ ba da mʋ mata oyu laasa sʋ mɔɔ.

eng: We had hoped he was the one who would redeem Israel. All these things happened three days ago.
local: Amaa naafɔɔ an kyɩna an sʋ tamaa fɛɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ ba ɔ ba kɔɔlɛ anɛ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi yɛgɛ. Kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bɩlaa ki tii sʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, ndaga ɩ gyɛ kakɛ sasapʋ nɩɩ asɩn nɩmʋ a maa waa.

eng: But theres more: Some women from our group have left us stunned. They went to the tomb early this morning.
local: Ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ bɩla i tii sʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, anɛ akyɩɩ mʋ akʋ ba yɛgɛ nɩɩ i kyinkyin anɛ. Ba kpee kakyan mʋ asɛ naa loo to nyenyenfuu nɩmʋ.

eng: and didnt find his body. They came to us saying that they had even seen a vision of angels who told them he is alive.
local: amaa ba man wu mu kibuni mʋ. Ba ba tɔwɛ anɛ yɛ ba nyɛ ansi sʋ kɩlɛɛkaapʋ nɩɩ ba wu Ɩbwaarɛ n bɔɔ ba maa tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ Yesu bʋ nkpa tɔ.

eng: Some of those who were with us went to the tomb and found things just as the women said. They didnt see him.
local: Nɩɩ anɛ tɔ akʋ ba kpee kakyan mʋ tɔ nɩɩ ba naa wu kanan mʋ nɩɩ akyɩɩ mʋ ba ba tɔwɛ mʋ, amaa ba man wu mʋ.

eng: Then Jesus said to them, You foolish people! Your dull minds keep you from believing all that the prophets talked about.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Mɩnɛ kimelensi ndee? Mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fanɛ nfɛɛrɛ i du swɩɩ‐swɩɩ kanɩn, nɩɩ fan mɛɛ kɔɔlɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ ba wʋla tɔwɛ yɩla mʋ gyii bilen?.

eng: Wasnt it necessary for the Christ to suffer these things and then enter into his glory.
local: Yɛ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ mʋ o gyii falɛ awʋrʋfɔ pɔyɩ kɛ o loo mʋ bʋnyaa tɔ.

eng: Then he interpreted for them the things written about himself in all the scriptures, starting with Moses and going through all the Prophets.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu maa piili lɩɩ Mosesi nbara yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ kɩkaapʋ mʋ sʋ mʋ, ɔ lɛɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ kpini ɩ tɔwɛ lɩɩ mʋ sʋ mʋ kaapʋ bamʋ.

eng: When they came to Emmaus, he acted as if he was going on ahead.
local: Ba maa tiri kadɛ‐bi mʋ nɩɩ bee kpee mʋ tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ Yesu waa fɛɛ maa biti ɔ kyʋn kadɛ mʋ sʋ.

eng: But they urged him, saying, Stay with us. Its nearly evening, and the day is almost over. So he went in to stay with them.
local: Amaa ba kʋlɛ mʋ gaa tɔwɛ yɛ, Dɛ anɛ asɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ saŋa kyʋn, kyɔwɛ lʋwɛ. Ɩmʋ sʋ o loo naa kyɩna bamʋ asɛ.

eng: After he took his seat at the table with them, he took the bread, blessed and broke it, and gave it to them.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ maa bamʋ ba kyɩna kɛ ba gyii mʋ, ɔ taa bodobodo nɩɩ ɔ fwaala Ɩbwaarɛ, nɩɩ ɔ kpɛɛ kɩmʋ tɔ nɩɩ ɔ taa sa bamʋ.

eng: Their eyes were opened and they recognized him, but he disappeared from their sight.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ bamʋ ansi i bugi nɩɩ ba bɩɩ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ nyaa bamʋ ansi sʋ.

eng: They said to each other, Werent our hearts on fire when he spoke to us along the road and when he explained the scriptures for us.
local: Nɩɩ ba taasɛ abaa yɛ, Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ anaa mʋ an naa ɔkpa tɔ nɩɩ ɛɛ tɔwɛ ɛɛ sa anɛ nɩɩ ɛɛ lɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ ɛɛ kaapʋ anɛ mʋ, anɛ n‐yɩɩ ɩ man dii anɛ.

eng: Jesus learned that the Pharisees had heard that he was making more disciples and baptizing more than John .
local: Ɩ maa ba fɛɛ Yesu bɩɩ fɛɛ Farasii abi mʋ ba nu yɛ ɛɛ nyɛ abɩɩlapʋ nɩɩ ee gyee bamʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee ɔ kyɔ Gyɔn.

eng: They got up right then and returned to Jerusalem. They found the eleven and their companions gathered together.
local: Nɩɩ ba kʋsʋ bwii kpee Gyerusalem bilen. Tʋtɔ maŋa nɩɩ ba naa wu kudu ɔkʋ mʋ yɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ bamʋ asɛ mʋ ba maa gyaŋɛ.

eng: although Jesus disciples were baptizing, not Jesus himself.
local: Kesintin tɔ bɩrɛ, Yesu gbaa‐gbaa man gyee asa Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee, amɔɔ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ nkʋn ii gyee asa.

eng: They were saying to each other, The Lord really has risen! He appeared to Simon.
local: nɩɩ bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɩ gyɛ kesintin nɩn yɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ kyiŋi nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ kaapʋ Simon.

eng: Therefore, he left Judea and went back to Galilee.
local: Yesu maa bɩɩ kanɩn mʋ, ɔ kʋsʋ lɩɩ Gyudeya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ bwii kpee Galile ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ.

eng: Then the two disciples described what had happened along the road and how Jesus was made known to them as he broke the bread.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ asa anyɔ mʋ gbaa ba tɔwɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ waa ɔkpa tɔ yɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba bɩɩ Yesu saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kpɛɛ bodobodo mʋ tɔ mʋ.

eng: Jesus had to go through Samaria.
local: Maa kpee mʋ ɩ dagaa maa bɔla lɩɩ Samariya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ.

eng: While they were saying these things, Jesus himself stood among them and said, Peace be with you.
local: Ba maa san ba yɩlɛ bɛɛ tɔwɛ falɛ asɩn mʋ, nɩɩ Yesu gbaa‐gbaa ba yɩlɛ bamʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Keyuula kɩ ba fanɛ sʋ.

eng: He came to a Samaritan city called Sychar, which was near the land Jacob had given to his son Joseph.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ ba fʋʋ Samariya kadɛ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ kɩmʋ yɛ Sikaa mʋ. Kanɩn kadɛ maŋa kɩ bʋ tiri kasɛ mʋ nɩɩ saŋa mʋ dɩdaa mʋ, Gyekobu taa sa mu‐bii Gyosefu mʋ.

eng: They were terrified and afraid. They thought they were seeing a ghost.
local: Bamʋ nkɔlɔ n ŋmaa bamʋ nɩɩ kufuu kɩ kɩtaa bamʋ, nɩɩ ba fɛ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ ba wu obuni nɩn.

eng: Jacobs well was there. Jesus was tired from his journey, so he sat down at the well. It was about noon.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Gyekobu kitiribo mʋ kɩ bʋ. Yesu maa natɛ nɩɩ i nu mʋ mʋ, ɔ naa kyɩna kitiribo mʋ asɛ. Saŋa maŋa gyɛ kyɔwɛ amu nsana.

eng: He said to them, Why are you startled? Why are doubts arising in your hearts?.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ ɩ tɔɔrɔɔ fanɛ, nɩɩ mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fan san fen gyii ɩmɔɔrɛ fanɛ nfɛɛrɛ tɔ?.

eng: A Samaritan woman came to the well to draw water. Jesus said to her, Give me some water to drink.
local: Yesu maa tɛ tʋtɔ mʋ, nɩɩ Kasamariya‐kyɩɩ kʋ ba ɔ ba saa nkyu, nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Sa mɛ nkyu kɛ n nuu.

eng: Look at my hands and my feet. Its really me! Touch me and see, for a ghost doesnt have flesh and bones like you see I have.
local: Fan kɩɩ mɛ abaa yɛ mɛ ayaa. Man gyɛ mɛ nɩn abɛɛ? Fan mata mɛ kɛ fan kɩɩ, obuni man sʋ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ yɛ awuya, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ n sʋ falɛ.

eng: His disciples had gone into the city to buy him some food.
local: Kanɩn saŋa maa mʋ Yesu abɩɩlapʋ ba kyʋn kadɛ tɔ ba naa sɔɔ agyitɔ.

eng: As he said this, he showed them his hands and feet.
local: Maa tɔwɛ falɛ lʋwɛ mʋ, ɔ taa mʋ abaa yɛ mʋ ayaa kaapʋ bamʋ.

eng: The Samaritan woman asked, Why do you, a Jewish man, ask for something to drink from me, a Samaritan woman.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ lɛɛ Yesu kɔnɔ yɛ, Fʋ gyɛ Gyiwu obii nɩn, nɩɩ mɛɛ mɛ n gyɛ Samariya obii nɩn. Mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ fʋ kʋlɛ mɛ nkyu kɛ fu nuu? Ɔkyɩɩ mʋ tɔwɛ kanɩn lɩɩ fɛɛ Gyiwu abi yɛ Samariya abi ba mɛɛ bʋga lapɛ kʋlʋn.

eng: Because they were wondering and questioning in the midst of their happiness, he said to them, Do you have anything to eat.
local: Ba maa man pɩɩta kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii lɩɩ kensi‐gyii yɛ adangana nɩmʋ sʋ mʋ, ɔ taasɛ bamʋ yɛ Fan sʋ agyitɔ kʋ nfɩɩ fan gyii.

eng: Jesus responded, If you recognized Gods gift and who is saying to you, Give me some water to drink, you would be asking him and he would give you living water.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Fʋ daa fu nyi kakɛɛ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩɩ sa, yɛ mɛ mʋ nɩɩ n kʋlɛ fʋ nkyu falɛ nɩn, naafɔɔ fʋ dɩn kʋlɛ mɛ kɛ n sa fʋ nkyu mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ sa nkpa.

eng: They gave him a piece of baked fish.
local: Nɩɩ ba taa ɔkɩn ŋasɩsa kɩdɩyɛ sa mʋ.

eng: The woman said to him, Sir, you dont have a bucket and the well is deep. Where would you get this living water?.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ taasɛ yɛ, Ɔbɩlɩsa, fʋ man sʋ kuuka, nɩɩ kitiribo mʋ mɔɔ bʋ nyɛ. Fɩnɛ nɩɩ fii biti fʋ nyɛ falɛ nkyu mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ sa nkpa mʋ sa mɛ?.

eng: Taking it, he ate it in front of them.
local: nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ kɩmʋ wɛ bamʋ ansi tɔ tʋtɔ.

eng: You arent greater than our father Jacob, are you? He gave this well to us, and he drank from it himself, as did his sons and his livestock.
local: Anɛ naana Gyekobu ɩ sa anɛ kitiribo maŋa. Mʋ gbaa‐gbaa maa mu‐bii ana yɛ mʋ atɔbwaaya kpini kɩmʋ tɔ nɩɩ ba nuu. Fɩɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ fʋ kyɔ anɛ naana Gyekobu nɩn abɛɛ.

eng: Jesus said to them, These are my words that I spoke to you while I was still with you—that everything written about me in the Law from Moses, the Prophets, and the Psalms must be fulfilled.
local: Ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ n kyɩna n bʋ fanɛ asɛ mʋ, n tɔwɛ fanɛ asɩn nɩmʋ yɛ, asɩn kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ yɩla lɩɩ mɛ sʋ, Mosesi nbara tɔ yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ kɩkaapʋ yɛ Ɩlʋn mʋ tɔ mʋ, ɩ dagaa fɛɛ ɩ maa ba tɔ.

eng: Jesus answered, Everyone who drinks this water will be thirsty again.
local: Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o nuu nkyu nɩmʋ mʋ, bʋlɩfɔ ii biti o bwii kɩtaa mʋ.

eng: Then he opened their minds to understand the scriptures.
local: Nɩɩ o bugi bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ kɛ ba nyɛ nu Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ kasɛ.

eng: but whoever drinks from the water that I will give will never be thirsty again. The water that I give will become in those who drink it a spring of water that bubbles up into eternal life.
local: Amaa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o nuu nkyu mʋ nɩɩ n biti n sa mʋ mʋ, bʋlɩfɔ man bɩla yɛ ɔ kɩtaa mʋ. Kanɩn nkyu maŋa ii biti i biliŋi fɛɛ nkyu mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ kyʋn mʋ tɔ, kɛ kɩɩ sa mʋ nkpa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ man sʋ ɔkaa.

eng: He said to them, This is what is written: the Christ will suffer and rise from the dead on the third day.
local: Ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Asɩn mʋ ɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ ii biti o gyii awʋrʋfɔ kɛ o wu, kɛ o kyiŋi lɩɩ alewupu tɔ kakɛ sasapʋ.

eng: The woman said to him, Sir, give me this water, so that I will never be thirsty and will never need to come here to draw water.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔbɩlɩsa, n kʋlɛ fʋ nɩn, sa mɛ kanɩn nkyu maŋa kɛ bʋlɩfɔ ɔ man bɩla kɩtaa mɛ, kɛ n man bɩla n baa n sa nkyu nfɩɩ.

eng: and a change of heart and life for the forgiveness of sins must be preached in his name to all nations, beginning from Jerusalem.
local: Nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ ba tɔwɛ Asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ sa asa kpini mʋ kɩtɩɩ tɔ, kɛ asa ba kyɛɛgɛ bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ lɩɩ alibi tɔ ba Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ kɛ ɔ taa bamʋ alibi kɛɛ bamʋ. Nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ ba piili asɩn nɩmʋ kɩtɔwɛ lɩɩ Gyerusalem kadɛ tɔ.

eng: Jesus said to her, Go, get your husband, and come back here.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ yɛ, Bwii kpee naa tɩɩ fu‐kuli kɛ fan ba.

eng: You are witnesses of these things.
local: Fanɛ ɩ gyɛ kesintin agyipu lɩɩ atɔ nɩmʋ kpini sʋ.

eng: The woman replied, I dont have a husband. You are right to say, I dont have a husband, Jesus answered.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, N man sʋ okuli. Nɩɩ Yesu yɛ, Fʋ tɔwɛ kesintin fɛɛ fʋ man sʋ okuli.

eng: Look, Im sending to you what my Father promised, but you are to stay in the city until you have been furnished with heavenly power.
local: N biti n bɩya fanɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ nɩɩ mɛ‐Sɛ kyɩna da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ yɛ ee biti ɔ sa fanɛ mʋ. Amaa fan kyɩna kadɛ lala nɩmʋ tɔ naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Kayaayu mʋ ɔlʋn mʋ ii biti ɔ lɩɩ sʋsʋ ba fanɛ sʋ.

eng: Youve had five husbands, and the man you are with now isnt your husband. Youve spoken the truth.
local: Fʋ wʋla kili anyɩn anu nɩɩ fʋ yɛgɛ, nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ faa mʋ fan tɛ nbɩɩnbɩɩ falɛ gbaa, man gyɛ fu‐kuli. Ɩmʋ sʋ ɩmʋ nɩɩ fʋ tɔwɛ falɛ gyɛ kesintin nɩn.

eng: He led them out as far as Bethany, where he lifted his hands and blessed them.
local: Yesu maa taa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ lɩɩ Gyerusalem kadɛ tɔ bee kpee naa tiri Betani kadɛ tɔ. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔ kʋsaa mʋ abaa sʋsʋ nɩɩ, ɔ waa bamʋ kusee.

eng: The woman said, Sir, I see that you are a prophet.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔbɩlɩsa n wu fɛɛ fʋ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ nɩn.

eng: As he blessed them, he left them and was taken up to heaven.
local: Maa dan san ɛɛ waa bamʋ kusee maŋa nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ taa mʋ lɩɩ bamʋ asɛ kpee sʋsʋ.

eng: Our ancestors worshipped on this mountain, but you and your people say that it is necessary to worship in Jerusalem.
local: Anɛ naana ana Samariya abi bɩrɛ kɩbɩɩ nɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ ba daa bɛɛ sun Ɩbwaarɛ, amaa fanɛ Gyiwu abi ɩɩ tɔwɛ yɛ Gyerusalem ɩ gyɛ opula mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ a sun Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: They worshipped him and returned to Jerusalem overwhelmed with joy.
local: Nɩɩ ba muŋa sun mʋ nɩɩ ba taa kensi‐gyii lala bwii kpee Gyerusalem kadɛ tɔ.

eng: Jesus said to her, Believe me, woman, the time is coming when you and your people will worship the Father neither on this mountain nor in Jerusalem.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɔkyɩɩ, kɔɔlɛ mɛ gyii fɛɛ saŋa kʋ ii biti ɔ ba, nɩɩ man gyɛ kɩbɩɩ kɩnɩmʋ sʋ abɛɛ Gyerusalem nɩɩ asa bee biti ba sun Mɛ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ.

eng: And they were continuously in the temple praising God.
local: Nɩɩ ba kyaga sʋ kyɩna kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ bɛɛ yɩn Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: You and your people worship what you dont know; we worship what we know because salvation is from the Jews.
local: Fanɛ Samariya abi ɩ man nyi Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ fen sun danbɩrasa, amaa anɛ Gyiwu abi bɩrɛ an nyi Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ an sun. Lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kamɔlɩgɛ mʋ kɩ bɔla Gyiwu abi sʋ nɩn ba.

eng: But the time is coming—and is here!—when true worshippers will worship in spirit and truth. The Father looks for those who worship him this way.
local: Saŋa kʋ ii biti ɔ ba, nɩɩ kɩ tɛɛ fʋʋ lʋwɛ, nɩɩ kesintin asunpu bee biti ba sun Mɛ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ yɛ kesintin tɔ. Kanɩn asunpu banɩmʋ falɛ nɩɩ Mɛ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ ii biti.

eng: God is spirit, and it is necessary to worship God in spirit and truth.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ gyɛ kayaayu nɩn, nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ bee sun mʋ mʋ, ɩ dagaa fɛɛ ba sun mʋ Kayaayu yɛ kesintin tɔ.

eng: The woman said, I know that the Messiah is coming, the one who is called the Christ. When he comes, he will teach everything to us.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ tɔwɛ Yesu yɛ, N nyi fɛɛ Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ, nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Krisito mʋ, ee biti ba. Nɩɩ ɔ kan ba mʋ, ee biti ɔ lɛɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ tɔ kaapʋ anɛ.

eng: Jesus said to her, I Am—the one who speaks with you.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ yɛ, Mɛ, mʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ fʋ asɛ falɛ, mɛ ɩ gyɛ mʋ.

eng: Just then, Jesus disciples arrived and were shocked that he was talking with a woman. But no one asked, What do you want? or Why are you talking with her.
local: Kanɩn saŋa maŋa nɩɩ Yesu abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba bwii lɩɩ kadɛ tɔ ba. Nɩɩ ba maa wu Yesu maa ɔkyɩɩ mʋ ba maa tɛɛ bɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ, i kyinkyin bamʋ. Amaa bamʋ tɔ ɔkʋ man taalɛ taasɛ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ yɛ, Mɩnɛ nɩɩ fii biti? Abɛɛ ba taasɛ Yesu yɛ, Mɩnɛ ɩɩ waa sʋ nɩɩ fɩɩ tɔwɛ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ asɛ.

eng: The woman put down her water jar and went into the city. She said to the people.
local: Tʋtɔ mʋ, ɔkyɩɩ mʋ yɛgɛ mʋ kɔlɔ‐bi yɩla sɩlɛ kpee kadɛ tɔ naa tɔwɛ asa yɛ.

eng: Come and see a man who has told me everything Ive done! Could this man be the Christ.
local: Fan ba kɩɩ ɔnyɩn kʋ maa tɔwɛ mɛ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ n waa mʋ kpini. Abɛɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ Krisito, Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ mʋ.

eng: They left the city and were on their way to see Jesus.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ asa mʋ ba lɩɩ kadɛ mʋ tɔ bee kpee ba naa kɩɩ Yesu.

eng: In the meantime the disciples spoke to Jesus, saying, Rabbi, eat.
local: Saŋa maa tɔ nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba kʋlɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɔkaapʋpʋ, waa aniya kɛ fu gyii agyitɔ.

eng: Jesus said to them, I have food to eat that you dont know about.
local: Amaa nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ yɛ, N sʋ agyitɔ mʋ nɩɩ n gyii, yɛgɛ fan man nyi ɩmʋ sʋ.

eng: The disciples asked each other, Has someone brought him food.
local: Nɩɩ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba taasɛ abaa yɛ, Abɛɛ ɔsa kʋ wʋla bɩya mʋ agyitɔ nɩn nɩɩ o gyii.

eng: Jesus said to them, I am fed by doing the will of the one who sent me and by completing his work.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Mɛ agyitɔ ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ n maa waa Mɛ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ o sun mɛ mʋ kebiti, kɛ n waa kusun mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sa mɛ mʋ lʋwɛ.

eng: Dont you have a saying, Four more months and then its time for harvest? Look, I tell you: open your eyes and notice that the fields are already ripe for the harvest.
local: Man gyɛ fanɛ kɩŋasan ɩɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɩ san ɩbwaayɩ ɩna kɛ a tɩŋɛ ayu mʋ? Amaa n tɔwɛ fanɛ fɛɛ fan bugi fanɛ ansi kɩɩ adɔɔ mʋ, fen biti fan wu fɛɛ ɩ payɩ fʋʋ kɩtɩŋɛ.

eng: Those who harvest are receiving their pay and gathering fruit for eternal life so that those who sow and those who harvest can celebrate together.
local: Ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ tɩŋɛ mʋ ɩɩ nyɛ mʋ kʋkɔka danbɩrasa, nɩɩ ayu mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kʋʋla ɛɛ yɩlɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa ba nkpa kakpaa tɔ. Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ee duu yɛ ɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ tɩŋɛ mʋ kpini bee biti ba bʋga gyii bamʋ ansi.

eng: This is a true saying, that one sows and another harvests.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔkʋ ii duu nɩɩ ɔkʋ ɩɩ kpa, ɩ gyɛ kesintin.

eng: I have sent you to harvest what you didnt work hard for; others worked hard, and you will share in their hard work.
local: N sun fanɛ ndɔɔ tɔ yɛ fan naa tɩŋɛ ayu mʋ nɩɩ fan man duu. Akʋ ɩ waa ɩmʋ kusun nɩɩ fan ba nyɛ ɩmʋ kʋkɔka.

eng: Many Samaritans in that city believed in Jesus because of the womans word when she testified, He told me everything Ive ever done.
local: Samariya abi gaalaagaa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ kadɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ ba kɔɔlɛ Yesu gyii, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ maa tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔ tɔwɛ mɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ n wʋla waa mʋ sʋ.

eng: So when the Samaritans came to Jesus, they asked him to stay with them, and he stayed there two days.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Samariya abi mʋ ba ba mʋ asɛ mʋ, ba kʋlɛ mʋ yɛ ɔ kyɩna bamʋ asɛ, nɩɩ ɔ kyɩna bamʋ asɛ nkɛ nnyɔ.

eng: Many more believed because of his word.
local: Asa gaalaagaa ba ba kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii tii sʋ lɩɩ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ sʋ.

eng: and they said to the woman, We no longer believe because of what you said, for we have heard for ourselves and know that this one is truly the savior of the world.
local: Nɩɩ asa mʋ ba tɔwɛ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ yɛ, Nbɩɩnbɩɩ bɩrɛ, an kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii, yɛgɛ man gyɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ fʋ tɔwɛ anɛ mʋ sʋ, amaa kpalɩ fɛɛ anɛ gbaa‐gbaa anɛ an nu yɛ anɛ asʋ, nɩɩ an bɩɩ fɛɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ dulinyaa tɔ asa ɔmɔlɩgɛpʋ mʋ.

eng: After two days Jesus left for Galilee. .
local: Yesu maa gyii nkɛ nnyɔ kamaa tɔ mʋ, ɔ lɩɩ tʋtɔ kpee Galile ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ.

eng: Jesus himself had testified that prophets have no honor in their own country.
local: Yesu gbaa‐gbaa ɩ wʋla kyɩna tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ kʋmaa man sʋ bɛɛrɛɛ mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mʋ ayɛ.

eng: When he came to Galilee, the Galileans welcomed him because they had seen all the things he had done in Jerusalem during the festival, for they also had been at the festival.
local: Yesu maa naa fʋʋ Galile mʋ, Galile abi ba kɔɔlɛ mʋ abaa anyɔ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ngbaa bamʋ, saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba kpee Gyerusalem naa gyii Kesu kɔnɔ Kanyaŋɛ nbunɔ mʋ, ba wu kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa tʋtɔ mʋ.

eng: He returned to Cana in Galilee where he had turned the water into wine. In Capernaum there was a certain royal official whose son was sick.
local: Yesu maa bʋ Galile ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ mʋ, o bwii kpee Kena kadɛ tɔ. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔ kyɩna taa nkyu biliŋi nta mʋ. Maa bʋ tʋtɔ kanɩn mʋ, kɩbanɛ ɔbɩlɩsa kʋ mu‐bii‐nyɩnsa dɛ Kapanum kadɛ tɔ ɛɛ lɔ.

eng: When he heard that Jesus was coming from Judea to Galilee, he went out to meet him and asked Jesus if he would come and heal his son, for his son was about to die.
local: Ɔnyɩn mʋ maa nu fɛɛ Yesu lɩɩ Gyudeya ba Galile mʋ, ɔ ba mʋ asɛ ba kʋlɛ mʋ yɛ ɔ ba Kapanum ba kyɛ mu‐bii mʋ nɩɩ ɔ dɛ ee biti o wu mʋ sa mʋ.

eng: Jesus said to him, Unless you see miraculous signs and wonders, you wont believe.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Fanɛ ɔkʋ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ kɔɔlɛ mɛ gyii, amɔɔ fen wu asɩn kyinkyinsa yɛ adangana.

eng: The royal official said to him, Lord, come before my son dies.
local: Nɩɩ kɩbanɛ bɩlɩsa mʋ kʋlɛ Yesu yɛ, Ɔbɩlɩsa, yɛgɛ a kpee mi kebii mʋ asɛ pɔyɩ kɛ o wu.

eng: After this Jesus went across the Galilee Sea .
local: Ɩnɩmʋ kamaa mʋ, Yesu faa Galile ɔbʋn lala mʋ nɩɩ ba bɩla bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ Tiberiya mʋ kpee kɩmʋ benben.

eng: Jesus replied, Go home. Your son lives. The man believed the word that Jesus spoke to him and set out for his home.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ yɛ, Natɛ, fu kebii mʋ mɛɛ yɛ o wu. Ɔnyɩn mʋ kɔɔlɛ Yesu asɩn mʋ gyii, nɩɩ ɔ kɩtaa ɔkpa kyʋn.

eng: A large crowd followed him, because they had seen the miraculous signs he had done among the sick.
local: Nɩɩ asa damantɛ ba buu mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ba wu mʋ asɩn kyinkyinsa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa ɛɛ kyɛ alɔpʋ mʋ.

eng: While he was on his way, his servants were already coming to meet him. They said, Your son lives.
local: Maa kpewu mʋ, mʋ ayaafɔlɛ ba ba gyaŋa mʋ ɔkpa tɔ nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, mu‐bii mʋ nyɛ alanfɩya.

eng: Jesus went up a mountain and sat there with his disciples.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba dii kpee kɩbɩɩ‐bi kʋkʋ sʋ naa kyɩna.

eng: So he asked them at what time his son had started to get better. And they said, The fever left him yesterday at about one oclock in the afternoon.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ bamʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ kebii mʋ nyɛ alanfɩya. Nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Ndee kyʋwapɛ kerefi kʋlʋn saŋa mʋ, nɩɩ kibirima mʋ yɛgɛ mʋ.

eng: It was nearly time for Passover, the Jewish festival.
local: Kanɩn saŋa maŋa mʋ, Gyiwu abi Kesu kɔnɔ Kanyaŋɛ nbunɔ kensi‐gyii mʋ ki tiri tɔ.

eng: Then the father realized that this was the hour when Jesus had said to him, Your son lives. And he and his entire household believed in Jesus.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ bɩɩ yɛ kanɩn saŋa maa kyɛɛkyɛɛ nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Fu‐kebii mʋ mɛɛ yɛ o wu mʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔnyɩn mʋ maa mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ abi kpini ba kɔɔlɛ Yesu gyii.

eng: Jesus looked up and saw the large crowd coming toward him. He asked Philip, Where will we buy food to feed these people.
local: Yesu maa diyaa mʋ ansi kɩɩ mʋ, o wu sakyɔ damantɛ maa ba mʋ asɛ. Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔ taasɛ Filipu yɛ, Fɩnɛ nɩɩ an biti an nyɛ agyitɔ sɔɔ sa asa banɩmʋ kpini kɛ bee gyii.

eng: This was the second miraculous sign Jesus did while going from Judea to Galilee.
local: Yesu maa lɩɩ Gyudeya ba Galile mʋ, ɩnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ asɩn kyinkyinsa nyɔsapʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa.

eng: Jesus said this to test him, for he already knew what he was going to do.
local: Yesu tʋʋ Filipu tɔ nɩn kɩɩ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ tɛɛ wʋla o nyi kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ waa.

eng: Philip replied, More than a half years salary worth of food wouldnt be enough for each person to have even a little bit.
local: Nɩɩ Filipu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Pɔyɩ kɛ a taalɛ nyɛ agyitɔ gbɛrɛɛ‐gbɛrɛɛ sa ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ o gyii mʋ, amɔɔ an biti a nyita ɩbwaayɩ iburuwa kʋkɔka.

eng: One of his disciples, Andrew, Simon Peters brother, said.
local: Yesu abɩɩlapʋ mʋ tɔ ɔmʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Andiruwu, Simon Pita mu‐supu mʋ tɔwɛ yɛ.

eng: A youth here has five barley loaves and two fish. But what good is that for a crowd like this.
local: Kebii‐nyɩnsa‐bii kʋ bʋ nfɩɩ, nɩɩ ɔ sʋ bodobodo pii‐pii anuu yɛ ɩkɩn‐bi anyɔ. Kanɩn agyitɔ pii nɩmʋ, nɛnɛ nɩɩ ii biti ɩ waa kɛ ɩ fʋʋ asa damantɛ banɩmʋ.

eng: Jesus said, Have the people sit down. There was plenty of grass there. They sat down, about five thousand of them.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ, Fan yɛgɛ asa mʋ ba kyɩna‐kyɩna. Yɛgɛ afitiri pɔrɔgɛsa a bʋ tʋtɔ kʋkyɔ, ɩmʋ sʋ asa mʋ kpini ba kyʋn kyɩna. Nɩɩ anyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ tʋtɔ mʋ kɔnɔ ɩ waa fɛɛ ngbɩn nnuu.

eng: Then Jesus took the bread. When he had given thanks, he distributed it to those who were sitting there. He did the same with the fish, each getting as much as they wanted.
local: Ba maa kyɩna kanɩn mʋ, Yesu taa bodobodo mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ fwaala Ɩbwaarɛ, nɩɩ ɔ taa sa yɛ ba barɩgɛ tɔ sa bamʋ. Kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ ɔ waa ɩkɩn mʋ gbaa. Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ nyɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ee biti.

eng: When they had plenty to eat, he said to his disciples, Gather up the leftover pieces, so that nothing will be wasted.
local: Ba maa gyii ŋmɛ mʋ, Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ, Fan tasɛ‐tasɛ agyisan mʋ, kɛ fan man yɛgɛ ɩmʋ ɩkʋ i sii.

eng: So they gathered them and filled twelve baskets with the pieces of the five barley loaves that had been left over by those who had eaten.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ba maa gyii bodobodo pii‐pii anu mʋ, ba tasɛ‐tasɛ ɩmʋ agyisan mʋ kpini bɔla alantan kudu anyɔ.

eng: When the people saw that he had done a miraculous sign, they said, This is truly the prophet who is coming into the world.
local: Asa mʋ ba maa wu asɩn kyinkyinsa mʋ nɩɩ Yesu waa mʋ, ba piili bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Kesintin, ɔnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ ba dulinyaa tɔ mʋ.

eng: Jesus understood that they were about to come and force him to be their king, so he took refuge again, alone on a mountain.
local: Yesu maa bɩɩ fɛɛ, bee biti ba ba taa mʋ ɔlʋn sʋ, kɛ ba naa bun mʋ kuwura mʋ, ɔ bɩla lɩɩ bamʋ tɔ kpee kɩbɩɩ‐bi sʋ mʋ nkʋn.

eng: When evening came, Jesus disciples went down to the lake.
local: Kalaatɩpwɛ asɛ maa fʋʋ mʋ, Yesu abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba kpɩlɩgɛ kpee ɔbʋn lala kɔnɔ.

eng: They got into a boat and were crossing the lake to Capernaum. It was already getting dark and Jesus hadnt come to them yet.
local: Nɩɩ ɩ maa sʋla naa fʋʋ kagyanbwɛ yɛgɛ Yesu man pɩɩta bwii ba bamʋ asɛ mʋ, ba naa loo ɔkʋlɩ tɔ bɛɛ faa bee kpee Kapanum.

eng: The water was getting rough because a strong wind was blowing.
local: Nɩɩ afuu lala a tigi a daa yɛgɛ nkyu mʋ ii wuli tɔ.

eng: When the wind had driven them out for about three or four miles, they saw Jesus walking on the water. He was approaching the boat and they were afraid.
local: Yesu abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba maa palɛ kpee ansi tɔ fɛɛ mayɩlɩ asa abɛɛ ana mʋ, ba wu Yesu maa naa nkyu mʋ sʋ ɛɛ ba bamʋ ɔkʋlɩ mʋ asɛ, nɩɩ kufuu kɩ kɩtaa bamʋ.

eng: He said to them, I Am. Dont be afraid.
local: Amaa Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Mɛ nɩn, fan man waa kufuu.

eng: Then they wanted to take him into the boat, and just then the boat reached the land where they had been heading.
local: Nɩɩ ba maa biti fɛɛ ba taa Yesu waa ɔkʋlɩ mʋ tɔ mʋ, opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ ɔkʋlɩ mʋ dan katɩn mʋ nɩɩ bee biti fɛɛ ba fʋʋ mʋ.

eng: The next day the crowd that remained on the other side of the lake realized that only one boat had been there. They knew Jesus hadnt gone with his disciples, but that the disciples had gone alone.
local: Ɩmʋ lɩɩkaakɛ mʋ, asa damantɛ mʋ nɩɩ ba sii ɔbʋn lala mʋ benben mʋ, ba wu fɛɛ ɔkʋlɩ kʋlʋn ki sii kɩ dan tʋtɔ ndee nɩɩ kɩmʋ nɩɩ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba taa. Amaa Yesu mɔɔ man loo mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ asɛ ɔkʋlɩ mʋ tɔ.

eng: Some boats came from Tiberias, near the place where they had eaten the bread over which the Lord had given thanks.
local: Nɩɩ ɩkʋlɩ ɩkʋ mɔɔ ɩ lɩɩ Tiberiya ba dan tʋtɔ tiri katɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ daa taa afwaala sa Ɩbwaarɛ nɩɩ asa ba gyii agyitɔ mʋ.

eng: When the crowd saw that neither Jesus nor his disciples were there, they got into the boats and came to Capernaum looking for Jesus.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ asa damantɛ mʋ ba maa bɩɩ fɛɛ Yesu abɛɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba man bʋ tʋtɔ mʋ, ba loo ɩkʋlɩ mʋ tɔ kpee Kapanum ba naa bee biti Yesu.

eng: When they found him on the other side of the lake, they asked him, Rabbi, when did you get here.
local: Ba maa naa wu mʋ ɔbʋn mʋ benben mʋ, ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɔkaapʋpʋ, saŋa mɔɔ nɩɩ fʋ fʋʋ nfɩɩ.

eng: Jesus replied, I assure you that you are looking for me not because you saw miraculous signs but because you ate all the food you wanted.
local: Yesu lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin, man gyɛ asɩn kyinkyinsa mʋ nɩɩ fan wu sʋ nɩɩ fan naa fen biti mɛ, amaa lɩɩ fan maa nyɛ agyitɔ gyii ŋmɛ kanan sʋ nɩɩ fen biti mɛ.

eng: Dont work for the food that doesnt last but for the food that endures for eternal life, which the Human One will give you. God the Father has confirmed him as his agent to give life.
local: Fan man waa kusun biti agyitɔ mʋ nɩɩ ii biti i nyita, amaa fan waa kusun biti agyitɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ sa nkpa kakpaa. Mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa n biti n sa fanɛ kanɩn agyitɔ mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ Mɛ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ lɛɛ kaapʋ fɛɛ mʋ kɩbaa kɩ gyan mɛ sʋ.

eng: They asked, What must we do in order to accomplish what God requires.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ba taasɛ Yesu yɛ, Mɩnɛ ii tiri fɛɛ a waa kɛ a taalɛ waa kusun mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti.

eng: Jesus replied, This is what God requires, that you believe in him whom God sent.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Kusun mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti fɛn waa mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, fan kɔɔlɛ mɛ, ɔmʋ nɩɩ o sun mʋ gyii.

eng: They asked, What miraculous sign will you do, that we can see and believe you? What will you do?.
local: Nɩɩ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Mɩnɛ asɩn kyinkyinsa nɩɩ fii biti fʋ waa kɛ an wu kɛ a kɔɔlɛ fu gyii? Mɩnɛ nɩɩ fii biti fʋ waa?.

eng: Our ancestors ate manna in the wilderness, just as it is written, He gave them bread from heaven to eat .
local: Saŋa kʋ tɔ, anɛ naana ana ba gyii agyitɔ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ ɩmʋ yɛ Mana mʋ ofuli sʋ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ ɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔ sa bamʋ agyitɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ lɩɩ sʋsʋ nɩɩ ba gyii. .

eng: Jesus told them, I assure you, it wasnt Moses who gave the bread from heaven to you, but my Father gives you the true bread from heaven.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin, man gyɛ Mosesi ɩ sa fanɛ naana ana agyitɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ lɩɩ sʋsʋ mʋ. Amaa Mɛ‐Sɛ ɩ sa fanɛ naana kesintin agyitɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ lɩɩ sʋsʋ mʋ. Nɩɩ nbɩɩnbɩɩ Mɛ‐Sɛ gbaa ɩɩ sa fanɛ kesintin agyitɔ gbaa‐gbaa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ lɩɩ sʋsʋ mʋ.

eng: The bread of God is the one who comes down from heaven and gives life to the world.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ agyitɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ sa asa mʋ, ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ kpɩlɩgɛ lɩɩ sʋsʋ ba kasɛ sʋ, nɩɩ ɛɛ sa dulinyaa tɔ asa nkpa mʋ.

eng: They said, Sir, give us this bread all the time.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ Yesu yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, lɩɩ ndaga ɩ sʋ ii kpee sa anɛ kanɩn agyitɔ maŋa saŋa kʋmaa.

eng: Jesus replied, I am the bread of life. Whoever comes to me will never go hungry, and whoever believes in me will never be thirsty.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ Mɛ ɩ gyɛ agyitɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ sa nkpa mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ ba mɛ asɛ mʋ, akʋn ɩ mɛɛ yɛ a mɔɔ mʋ kakaakʋ, nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ mɛ gyii mʋ, bʋlɩfɔ mɛɛ yɛ ɩ kɩtaa mʋ.

eng: But I told you that you have seen me and still dont believe.
local: Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ n wʋla tɔwɛ fanɛ mʋ, fan wu mɛ, amaa fɛn mɛɛ kɔɔlɛ mɛ fen gyii.

eng: Everyone whom the Father gives to me will come to me, and I wont send away anyone who comes to me.
local: Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ Mɛ‐Sɛ taa sa mɛ mʋ, ii biti ɔ ba mɛ asɛ. Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ ba mɛ asɛ mʋ, mɛɛ yɛ n gya mʋ.

eng: I have come down from heaven not to do my will, but the will of him who sent me.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ n lɩɩ sʋsʋ ba kasɛ sʋ, kɛ n ba waa ɔmʋ nɩɩ o sun mɛ mʋ kebiti, amaa man gyɛ mɛ gbaa‐gbaa mi kebiti.

eng: This is the will of the one who sent me, that I wont lose anything he has given me, but I will raise it up at the last day.
local: Ɔmʋ nɩɩ o sun mɛ mʋ kebiti ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ, bamʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa bamʋ sa mɛ mʋ kpini, bamʋ tɔ ɔkʋlʋn gbaa ɔ man fɔyɩ. Amaa n biti n kyiŋi bamʋ ba nkpa tɔ kakɛ laalaalʋwɛ mʋ.

eng: This is my Fathers will: that all who see the Son and believe in him will have eternal life, and I will raise them up at the last day.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Mɛ‐Sɛ kebiti ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ, ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o wu mɛ, Mu‐Bii mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ mɛ gyii mʋ ɩɩ nyɛ nkpa kakpaa. Nɩɩ n biti n kyiŋi mʋ lɩɩ lewu tɔ ba nkpa tɔ kakɛ laalaalʋwɛ mʋ.

eng: The Jewish opposition grumbled about him because he said, I am the bread that came down from heaven.
local: Nɩɩ Gyiwu abi mʋ ba piili bɛɛ kɔlɩtɛ bee yii Yesu, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Mɛ ɩ gyɛ agyitɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ lɩɩ sʋsʋ ba kasɛ sʋ mʋ.

eng: They asked, Isnt this Jesus, Josephs son, whose mother and father we know? How can he now say, I have come down from heaven.
local: Nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, Man gyɛ Gyosefu mu‐bii mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Yesu mʋ nɩn? An nyi mʋ‐sɛ yɛ mu‐nyi a! Nɩɩ mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ, ɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, N lɩɩ sʋsʋ nɩn ba? .

eng: Jesus responded, Dont grumble among yourselves.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Fan yɛgɛ fanɛ kɩkɔlɩtɛ mʋ.

eng: No one can come to me unless they are drawn to me by the Father who sent me, and I will raise them up at the last day.
local: Ɔkʋ mɛɛ taalɛ ba mɛ asɛ, amɔɔ Mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ nɩɩ o sun mɛ mʋ, ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ɔ ba, nɩɩ n biti n kyiŋi mʋ ba nkpa tɔ kakɛ laalaalʋwɛ mʋ.

eng: It is written in the Prophets, And they will all be taught by God. Everyone who has listened to the Father and learned from him comes to me.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ Atɔwɛpʋ ba kyʋrɔɔ yɩla yɛ, Ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ ii biti ɔ nyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kɩkaapʋ. Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o nu Mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ asɛ, nɩɩ ɔ bɩɩla lɩɩ mʋ asɛ mʋ, ii biti ɔ ba mɛ asɛ.

eng: No one has seen the Father except the one who is from God. He has seen the Father.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ mɛɛ kaapʋ fɛɛ ɔkʋ tee wu Mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ, amɔɔ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ Mɛ‐Sɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ mʋ nkʋn i wu mʋ.

eng: I assure you, whoever believes has eternal life.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin yɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ mɛ gyii mʋ, sʋ nkpa kakpaa.

eng: I am the bread of life.
local: Mɛ ɩ gyɛ agyitɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ sa nkpa mʋ.

eng: Your ancestors ate manna in the wilderness and they died.
local: Fanɛ naana ana ba gyii agyitɔ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ Mana mʋ ofuli sʋ, amaa ba wu.

eng: This is the bread that comes down from heaven so that whoever eats from it will never die.
local: Amaa agyitɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ lɩɩ sʋsʋ ba kasɛ sʋ mʋ ndee, nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o gyii kanɩn agyitɔ maŋa mʋ, mɛɛ yɛ o wu.

eng: I am the living bread that came down from heaven. Whoever eats this bread will live forever, and the bread that I will give for the life of the world is my flesh.
local: Mɛ ɩ gyɛ agyitɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ sa nkpa nɩɩ n kpɩlɩgɛ lɩɩ sʋsʋ ba kasɛ sʋ mʋ. Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o gyii kanɩn agyitɔ nɩmʋ mʋ, ee biti ɔ kyɩna kakpaa. Nɩɩ agyitɔ mʋ nɩɩ n biti n sa dulinyaa tɔ asa kɛ ba nyɛ nkpa mʋ ɩ gyɛ mɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ.

eng: Then the Jews debated among themselves, asking, How can this man give us his flesh to eat.
local: Gyiwu abi mʋ ba maa nu Yesu asɩn tɔwɛsa kanɩn mʋ, ɩ waa bamʋ agbʋ. Nɩɩ ba piili bee gyii abaa ɩmɔɔrɛ, bɛɛ taasɛ abaa yɛ, Nɛnɛ nɩɩ ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ ii biti ɔ waa taa mʋ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ sa anɛ kɛ a wɛ.

eng: Jesus said to them, I assure you, unless you eat the flesh of the Human One and drink his blood, you have no life in you.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin fɛɛ, fan kan fan man wɛ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ, kɛ fen nuu mɛ nkalan de, fɛn mɛɛ yɛ fan nyɛ nkpa.

eng: Whoever eats my flesh and drinks my blood has eternal life, and I will raise them up at the last day.
local: Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ wɛ mɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ, nɩɩ o nuu mɛ nkalan mʋ, ɔ sʋ nkpa kakpaa, nɩɩ n biti n kyiŋi mʋ ba nkpa tɔ kakɛ laalaalʋwɛ mʋ tɔ.

eng: My flesh is true food and my blood is true drink.
local: Kpalɩ fɛɛ mɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ ɩ gyɛ agyitɔ gbaa‐gbaa mʋ, nɩɩ mɛ nkalan ɩ gyɛ atɔ nuusa gbaa‐gbaa mʋ.

eng: Whoever eats my flesh and drinks my blood remains in me and I in them.
local: Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ wɛ mɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ, nɩɩ o nuu mɛ nkalan mʋ, ɔ bʋ mɛ tɔ nɩɩ ngbaa mɛ n bʋ mʋ tɔ.

eng: As the living Father sent me, and I live because of the Father, so whoever eats me lives because of me.
local: Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ nkpa kumu sun mɛ, nɩɩ lɩɩ mʋ sʋ nɩɩ n sʋ nkpa mʋ, ɔkpa kʋlʋn maŋa sʋ, ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ wɛ mɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ mʋ, ii biti ɔ nyɛ nkpa lɩɩ mɛ sʋ.

eng: This is the bread that came down from heaven. It isnt like the bread your ancestors ate, and then they died. Whoever eats this bread will live forever.
local: Mɛ ɩ gyɛ agyitɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ lɩɩ sʋsʋ ba kasɛ sʋ mʋ. N man du fɛɛ mana agyitɔ mʋ nɩɩ fanɛ naana ana ba gyii, nɩɩ ɩ man sa bamʋ nkpa nɩɩ ba wu mʋ. Amaa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o gyii agyitɔ nɩmʋ bɩrɛ, ee biti ɔ nyɛ nkpa kakpaa.

eng: Jesus said these things while he was teaching in the synagogue in Capernaum.
local: Yesu maa bʋ Kapaniyum, Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa ɛɛ kaapʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ falɛ asɩn nɩmʋ.

eng: Many of his disciples who heard this said, This message is harsh. Who can hear it.
local: Yesu abɩɩlapʋ gaalaagaa ba maa nu ɩnɩmʋ mʋ, ba tɔwɛ yɛ, Falɛ kɩkaapʋ kɩnɩmʋ kɩ bʋlʋn. Anɩmʋ ɩ taalɛ kɔɔlɛ ɩmʋ kyula.

eng: Jesus knew that the disciples were grumbling about this and he said to them, Does this offend you?.
local: Yesu maa bɩɩ fɛɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ bɛɛ kɔlɩtɛ lɩɩ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ sʋ mʋ, ɔ taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Ɩmʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ falɛ ɩ kɩtaa fanɛ nfɛɛrɛ nɩn?.

eng: What if you were to see the Human One going up where he was before?.
local: Kanɩn bɩrɛ nɩɩ fan kan wu mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa n maa bwii n kpee sʋsʋ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ n lɩɩ ba kasɛ sʋ mʋ mɔɔ de?.

eng: The Spirit is the one who gives life and the flesh doesnt help at all. The words I have spoken to you are spirit and life.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu mʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ sa nkpa, amaa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ asa ba sʋ bamʋ ɔlʋn bɛɛ waa mʋ man sʋ tɔnɔ. Asɩn mʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ sa fanɛ mʋ, ɩ lɩɩ Kayaayu tɔ nɩn nɩɩ ɩɩ sa nkpa.

eng: Yet some of you dont believe.Jesus knew from the beginning who wouldnt believe and the one who would betray him.
local: Amaa yɛgɛ fanɛ tɔ akʋ ba mɛɛ kɔɔlɛ bee gyii. Yesu tɔwɛ kanɩn lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ wʋla o nyi lɩɩ npiili asɛ, bamʋ nɩɩ ba man kɔɔlɛ gyii yɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ ba lɛɛ mʋ ɛ sa mʋ.

eng: He said,For this reason I said to you that none can come to me unless the Father enables them to do so.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu kyaga sʋ yɛ, Kpalɩ ɩnɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ n wʋla tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, ɔkʋ mɛɛ taalɛ ba mɛ asɛ mʋ kɩbaa sʋ, amɔɔ Mɛ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ sa mʋ ɔkpa.

eng: At this, many of his disciples turned away and no longer accompanied him.
local: Lɩɩ kanɩn saŋa maŋa mʋ, nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ gaalaagaa ba bwii kamaa, nɩɩ ba man bɩla buu mʋ.

eng: Jesus asked the Twelve, Do you also want to leave.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ kudu anyɔ mʋ yɛ, Fanɛ gbaa fanɛ fen biti fan bwii do.

eng: Simon Peter answered, Lord, where would we go? You have the words of eternal life.
local: Nɩɩ Simon Pita lɛɛ mʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, anɩmʋ asɛ nɩɩ an sʋ an kpee? Fʋ nkʋn ɩ sʋ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ sa nyɩmɩsa nkpa kakpaa.

eng: We believe and know that you are Gods holy one.
local: An kɔɔlɛ gyii nɩɩ an bɩɩ fɛɛ fʋ nkʋn ɩ gyɛ Ɔkyɩrɛkyɩrɛpʋ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ mʋ.

eng: Jesus replied, Didnt I choose you twelve? Yet one of you is a devil.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Man gyɛ fanɛ kudu anyɔ mʋ, nɩɩ n lɛɛ? Amaa fanɛ tɔ ɔkʋ gyɛ Sɩtaanɛ.

eng: He was speaking of Judas, Simon Iscariots son, for he, one of the Twelve, was going to betray him.
local: Gyudasi mʋ nɩɩ mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Simon Isikariyoti mʋ asɩn nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ. Halɩɩ Gyudasi maa gyɛ abɩɩlapʋ kudu anyɔ mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ gbaa mʋ, kamaa tɔ mʋ, mʋ ii biti ɔ ba lɛɛ Yesu sa.

eng: There was a Pharisee named Nicodemus, a Jewish leader.
local: Ɔnyɩn kʋ lɩɩ Farasii kʋbʋ mʋ tɔ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Nikodemusi, ɔ gyɛ Gyiwu abɩlɩsa mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ nɩn.

eng: He came to Jesus at night and said to him, Rabbi, we know that you are a teacher who has come from God, for no one could do these miraculous signs that you do unless God is with him.
local: Mʋ ɩ ba Yesu asɛ kagyanbwɛ kʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ Yesu yɛ, Ɔkaapʋpʋ, an nyi yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ i sun fʋ yɛ fʋ ba kaapʋ anɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋ mɛɛ taalɛ waa asɩn kyinkyinsa mʋ nɩɩ fɩɩ waa falɛ, amɔɔ Ɩbwaarɛ tii mʋ sʋ.

eng: Jesus answered, I assure you, unless someone is born anew, its not possible to see Gods kingdom.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɩɩ kɔnɔ yɛ, N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin yɛ, ɔkʋ mɛɛ taalɛ wu kɛ ɔ bɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ, amɔɔ ɔ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ba bwii kʋʋgɛ mʋ.

eng: Nicodemus asked, How is it possible for an adult to be born? Its impossible to enter the mothers womb for a second time and be born, isnt it.
local: Nɩɩ Nikodemusi taasɛ yɛ, Nɛnɛ nɩɩ ba bɩla yɛ ba kʋʋgɛ nyɩmɩsa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛɛ dan? Ɛ taalɛ bɩla bwii loo mu‐nyi ɔtɔ tɔ kɛ ba kʋʋgɛ mʋ.

eng: Jesus answered, I assure you, unless someone is born of water and the Spirit, its not possible to enter Gods kingdom.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ mʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, N tɔwɛ fʋ kesintin yɛ ɔkʋ man bʋ tɔ ɔ taalɛ loo Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ, amɔɔ ɔ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ba gyee mʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee maa Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ kigyee, kɛ ɔ nyɛ kakʋʋgɛ pʋpwɛ.

eng: Whatever is born of the flesh is flesh, and whatever is born of the Spirit is spirit.
local: Nyɩmɩsa kʋʋgɛ nyɩmɩsa fɛɛ mu keduli nɩn, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu mʋ ɩ kʋʋgɛ nyɩmɩsa fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ keduli nɩn.

eng: Dont be surprised that I said to you, You must be born anew.
local: Man yɛgɛ i kyinkyin fʋ fɛɛ, n maa tɔwɛ fʋ yɛ ɩ dagaa ba bwii kʋʋgɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ.

eng: Gods Spirit blows wherever it wishes. You hear its sound, but you dont know where it comes from or where it is going. Its the same with everyone who is born of the Spirit.
local: Afuu ɩɩ da ii kpee katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ii biti. Fii nu ɩmʋ sʋ, amaa fʋ man nyi katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ lɩɩ yɛ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ii kpee. Kanɩn nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ba kʋʋgɛ mʋ Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu mʋ tɔ mʋ du.

eng: After this Jesus traveled throughout Galilee. He didnt want to travel in Judea, because the Jewish authorities wanted to kill him.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ kpini kamaa mʋ, Yesu naa ɔ naa Galile ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ. Ee mee biti maa loo Gyudeya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ Gyiwu abi abɩlɩsa bee biti ba mɔɔ mʋ.

eng: Nicodemus said, How are these things possible.
local: Nɩɩ Nikodemusi taasɛ yɛ, Nɛnɛ nɩɩ ɩnɩmʋ ɩ taalɛ waa.

eng: When it was almost time for the Jewish Festival of Booths.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Gyiwu abi Kesu kɔnɔ abuu kiyii kensi‐gyii a maa tiri tɔ mʋ.

eng: Jesus answered, You are a teacher of Israel and you dont know these things?.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, N nyi fʋ maa gyɛ Ɩsɩrayɩ ɔkaapʋpʋ kparɛ, nɩɩ fʋ man nu ɩnɩmʋ kasɛ?.

eng: Jesus brothers said to him, Leave Galilee. Go to Judea so that your disciples can see the amazing works that you do.
local: Yesu mu‐supu ana ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɩ dagaa fʋ maa lɩɩ nfɩɩ kpee Gyudeya, kɛ fu abuupu ba naa wu fʋ asɩn kyinkyinsa mʋ nɩɩ fɩɩ waa mʋ.

eng: I assure you that we speak about what we know and testify about what we have seen, but you dont receive our testimony.
local: N tɔwɛ fʋ kesintin yɛ, ɩmʋ nɩɩ an nyi mʋ nɩɩ an tɔwɛ, nɩɩ ɩmʋ nɩɩ an wu mʋ, nɩɩ an lɛɛ an tɔwɛ. Amaa fan mɛɛ kɔɔlɛ anɛ asɩn tɔwɛsa fen gyii.

eng: Those who want to be known publicly dont do things secretly. Since you can do these things, show yourself to the world.
local: Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti yɛ mʋ kɩtɩɩ ki dii mʋ, ɔ mɛɛ waa mʋ asɩn ɛɛ baala. Lɩɩ asɩn kyinkyinsa mʋ nɩɩ fɩɩ waa falɛ mʋ sʋ mʋ, ɩ dagaa fʋ maa lɛɛ fʋ n‐yɩɩ ifuli kaapʋ dulinyaa tɔ asa.

eng: If I have told you about earthly things and you dont believe, how will you believe if I tell you about heavenly things?.
local: N maa tɔwɛ fanɛ kasɛ sʋ asɩn nɩɩ fan maa kɔɔlɛ gyii falɛ mʋ, nɛnɛ nɩɩ fan taalɛ kɔɔlɛ sʋsʋ asɩn gyii?.

eng: His brothers said this because even they didnt believe in him.
local: Halɩɩ mu‐supu ana gbaa ba man kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii.

eng: No one has gone up to heaven except the one who came down from heaven, the Human One.
local: Ɔkʋ man pɩɩta lɩɩ kasɛ sʋ kpee sʋsʋ, amɔɔ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ nɩɩ n lɩɩ sʋsʋ ba kasɛ sʋ mʋ.

eng: Jesus replied, For you, anytime is fine. But my time hasnt come yet.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Mɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa mʋ, man pɩɩ fʋʋ, fanɛɛ fanɛ saŋa kʋmaa bʋrɔn sa fanɛ.

eng: Just as Moses lifted up the snake in the wilderness, so must the Human One be lifted up.
local: Saŋa mʋ dɩdaa mʋ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛgɛ nɩɩ Mosesi taa ɔpɔlɛ ŋalɩgɛ waa kɩnansɛ nɩɩ ɔ maalɛ oyu, kʋsaa kɩmʋ ifuli mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ asa ba kɩɩ nɩɩ ba nyɛ nkpa mʋ, kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ ii tiri fɛɛ ba kʋsaa mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ sʋsʋ.

eng: The world cant hate you. It hates me, though, because I testify that its works are evil.
local: Dulinya asa mɛɛ taalɛ kisi fanɛ. Amaa mɛ nɩɩ bee kisi, lɩɩ fɛɛ n gyii bamʋ kesintin fɛɛ bamʋ asunsunsa ɩ gyɛ alibi.

eng: so that everyone who believes in him will have eternal life.
local: kɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ mɛ gyii mʋ ɔ nyɛ nkpa kakpaa.

eng: You go up to the festival. Im not going to this one because my time hasnt yet come.
local: Fanɛ fanɛ fan kpee kensi‐gyii ngyaŋɛ mʋ tɔ. N mɛɛ yɛ n kpee ngyaŋɛ mʋ tɔ nbɩɩnbɩɩ, lɩɩ fɛɛ mɛ saŋa man pɩɩta fʋʋ.

eng: God so loved the world that he gave his only Son, so that everyone who believes in him wont perish but will have eternal life.
local: Lɩɩ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sʋ kebiti sa dulinyaa tɔ asa gaa sʋ mʋ, ɔ lɛɛ mɛ, Mu‐Bii kʋlʋn kpen mʋ sa, fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ mɛ gyii mʋ, mɛɛ yɛ ɔ fɔyɩ, amaa ee biti ɔ nyɛ nkpa kakpaa.

eng: Having said this, he stayed in Galilee.
local: Maa tɔwɛ ɩnɩmʋ maŋa lʋwɛ mʋ o sii kyɩna Galile.

eng: God didnt send his Son into the world to judge the world, but that the world might be saved through him.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ man sun mɛ, Mu‐Bii mʋ ba dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ, yɛ n ba bun kɩmʋ tɔ asa kʋpwɛ, amaa o sun mɛ nɩn yɛ n ba waa bamʋ ɔmɔlɩgɛpʋ.

eng: However, after his brothers left for the festival, he went too—not openly but in secret.
local: Amaa Yesu mu‐supu ana ba maa lɩɩ kyʋn kensi‐gyii ngyaŋɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ, mʋ gbaa mʋ kpee nɩɩ ɔ man lɛɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ ifuli.

eng: Whoever believes in him isnt judged; whoever doesnt believe in him is already judged, because they dont believe in the name of Gods only Son.
local: Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ mɛ gyii mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ mɛɛ yɛ o bun mʋ kʋpwɛ, amaa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man kɔɔlɛ mɛ gyii mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla bun mʋ kʋpwɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ man kɔɔlɛ mɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ Mu‐Bii kʋlʋn kpen mʋ gyii.

eng: The Jewish leaders were looking for Jesus at the festival. They kept asking, Where is he.
local: Kensi‐gyii ngyaŋɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ Gyiwu abɩlɩsa mʋ bɛɛ kɩɩ bee biti mʋ, yɛgɛ bɛɛ taasɛ asa yɛ, Fɩnɛ nɩɩ ɔnyɩn maŋa bʋ.

eng: This is the basis for judgment: The light came into the world, and people loved darkness more than the light, for their actions are evil.
local: Kanan mʋ nɩɩ kʋpwɛ kubun mʋ kɩ ba mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, ɩŋmaŋɛsa mʋ ɩ lɩɩ sʋsʋ nɩn ba dulinyaa tɔ, amaa asa bee biti kitentenbiri mʋ ba kyɔ ɩŋmaŋɛsa mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ asunsunsa ɩ gyɛ alibi nɩn.

eng: The crowds were murmuring about him. Hes a good man, some said, but others were saying, No, he tricks the people.
local: Sakyɔ mʋ tɔ asa damantɛ bee muni abaa asɛ lɩɩ mʋ sʋ. Akʋ ba yɛ, Ɔ gyɛ nyɩmɩsa danbɩrasa. Nɩɩ akʋ mɔɔ ba yɛ, Ɛɛ pɩna asa nɩn.

eng: All who do wicked things hate the light and dont come to the light for fear that their actions will be exposed to the light.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ waa alibi mʋ ii kisi ɩŋmaŋɛsa mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ ba ɩŋmaŋɛsa mʋ tɔ, kɛ mu alibi ɩ ba lɩɩ ifuli.

eng: No one spoke about him publicly, though, for fear of the Jewish authorities.
local: Amaa ɔkʋ man taalɛ lɩɩ ifuli tɔwɛ Yesu n‐yɩɩ sʋ asɩn, lɩɩ fɛɛ ba maa sɩlɛ Gyiwu abɩlɩsa mʋ sʋ.

eng: Whoever does the truth comes to the light so that it can be seen that their actions were done in God.
local: Amaa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ waa atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dɛ ɔkpa mʋ bɩrɛ, ɛɛ ba ɩŋmaŋɛsa mʋ tɔ, kɛ asa ba nyɛ wu fɛɛ mʋ atɔ waasa mʋ ɩ gyɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti.

eng: Halfway through the festival, Jesus went up to the temple and started to teach.
local: Ba maa waa kensi‐gyii ngyaŋɛ mʋ naa fʋʋ nsana mʋ, Yesu kpee Kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ nɩɩ o piili ɛɛ kaapʋ asɩn.

eng: After this Jesus and his disciples went into Judea, where he spent some time with them and was baptizing.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ kamaa mʋ Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba lɩɩ Gyerusalem kpee Gyudeya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ. Ɔ kyɩna tʋtɔ nkɛ nyɔ kʋ maa bamʋ, nɩɩ o gyee asa Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee.

eng: Astonished, the Jewish leaders asked, Hes never been taught! How has he mastered the Law.
local: Mʋ kɩkaapʋ mʋ i kyinkyin Gyiwu abɩlɩsa mʋ, nɩɩ ba taasɛ yɛ, Nɛnɛ nɩɩ ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ man bɩɩla nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ falɛ kinyi.

eng: John was baptizing at Aenon near Salem because there was a lot of water there, and people were coming to him and being baptized. .
local: Kanɩn saŋa maŋa nɩɩ Gyɔn Okyugyeepu mʋ gbaa bʋ Anon kadɛ mʋ nɩɩ ki tiri Salim mʋ tɔ. Ee gyee asa Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee, lɩɩ fɛɛ nkyu ɩ kyɩna ɩ kyɔ tʋtɔ gaa, nɩɩ asa bee kpee mʋ asɛ yɛgɛ ee gyee bamʋ.

eng: Jesus responded, My teaching isnt mine but comes from the one who sent me.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Mɛ kɩkaapʋ mʋ man gyɛ mɛ gbaa‐gbaa mɛ lɛɛ. Ɩ lɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ o sun mɛ mʋ asɛ nɩn.

eng: John hadnt yet been thrown into prison.
local: Saŋa maŋa, ba man pɩɩta tii Gyɔn obu.

eng: Whoever wants to do Gods will can tell whether my teaching is from God or whether I speak on my own.
local: Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ waa Ɩbwaarɛ kebiti mʋ, ee biti ɔ bu fɛɛ mɛ kɩkaapʋ mʋ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ, abɛɛ n tɔwɛ mɛ gbaa‐gbaa mɛ ɔlʋn sʋ nɩn.

eng: A debate started between Johns disciples and a certain Jew about cleansing rituals.
local: Nɩɩ ɩmɔɔrɛ ɩ tɩyɛ Gyɔn abuupu yɛ Gyiwu nyɩmɩsa kʋ nsana lɩɩ anaana tɔ ineesi kɩfwɩɩ sʋ.

eng: Those who speak on their own seek glory for themselves. Those who seek the glory of him who sent me are people of truth; theres no falsehood in them.
local: Ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mʋ ɔlʋn sʋ mʋ, ɩɩ waa kanɩn nɩn ee biti bʋnyaa ɛɛ sa mʋ n‐yɩɩ. Amaa ɔmʋ nɩɩ ee biti bʋnyaa ɛɛ sa ɔmʋ nɩɩ o sun mʋ mʋ, ɩ gyɛ kesintin wuya, nɩɩ antɩŋɛsa kʋ ɩ man bʋ mʋ tɔ.

eng: They came to John and said, Rabbi, look! The man who was with you across the Jordan, the one about whom you testified, is baptizing and everyone is flocking to him.
local: Nɩɩ Gyɔn abɩɩlapʋ ba ba mʋ asɛ ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɔkaapʋpʋ, ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ an wu mʋ Gyɔdan ɔbʋn benbe mʋ, ɔmʋ nɩɩ fu gyii mʋ asɩn kesintin mʋ gbaa ii gyee asa Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee, yɛgɛ asa kpini bee kpee mʋ asɛ.

eng: Didnt Moses give you the Law? Yet none of you keep the Law. Why do you want to kill me.
local: Mosesi man sa fanɛ nbara mʋ? Amaa fanɛ ɔkʋ man buu nbara mʋ. Mɩnɛ waa nɩɩ fen biti fan mɔɔ mɛ.

eng: John replied, No one can receive anything unless it is given from heaven.
local: Nɩɩ Gyɔn lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ man kan taa kʋtɔ kʋ sa ɔkʋ mʋ, ɔ mɛɛ taalɛ nyɛ kɩmʋ.

eng: The crowd answered, You have a demon. Who wants to kill you.
local: Nɩɩ sakyɔ mʋ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Nbwɩɩ libi ba bʋ fʋ tɔ. Anɩmʋ ana ii biti fʋ kɩmɔɔ.

eng: You yourselves can testify that I said that Im not the Christ but that Im the one sent before him.
local: Fanɛ gbaa‐gbaa fanɛ fen biti fan gyii mɛ kesintin fɛɛ, n wʋla tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, Man gyɛ mɛ ɩ gyɛ Krisito, Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ mʋ. Amaa Ɩbwaarɛ sun mɛ nɩn yɛ n gyankpaa ba lɔŋɔ ɔkpa yɩla mʋ.

eng: Jesus replied, I did one work, and you were all astonished.
local: Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, N maa waa adangana kʋlʋn kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɩmʋ i kyinkyin fanɛ falɛ.

eng: The groom is the one who is getting married. The friend of the groom stands close by and, when he hears him, is overjoyed at the grooms voice. Therefore, my joy is now complete.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ bɔ kɩŋasan yɛ, Ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ ɔkyɩɩfɔ mʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔkyɩɩfɔ mu‐kuli. Ɔkyɩɩfɔ mu‐kuli mʋ kyɛmɩnɛ bɩrɛ ɛɛ yɩlɛ ee gyoo nɩn. Nɩɩ ɔ kan nu mʋ ɔbʋlɛ mʋ, mu ansi i gyii. Kanɩn maŋa sʋ nɩɩ nbɩɩnbɩɩ mi ansi i gyii gaa.

eng: Because Moses gave you the commandment about circumcision , you circumcise a man on the Sabbath.
local: Mosesi sa fanɛ oduu kɩtɩn nbara, yɛgɛ kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ fɛn tɩn kebii oduu. Kesintin tɔ mʋ nbara mʋ man lɩɩ Mosesi asɛ, amaa ɩ lɩɩ anɛ naana ana asɛ.

eng: He must increase and I must decrease.
local: Ɩ dagaa fɛɛ Yesu kɩtɩɩ kɩ ba ki dii ki tii sʋ, kɛ mɛ Gyɔn kɩtɩɩ mɔɔ kɩ ba kasɛ.

eng: If a man can be circumcised on the Sabbath without breaking Moses Law, why are you angry with me because I made an entire man well on the Sabbath?.
local: Nɩɩ fan maa tɩn kebii oduu kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ, kɛ i mee nyita Mosesi nbara mʋ, mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ n kyɛ nyɩmɩsa laalaamuu kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ nɩɩ fɛn nyɛ agbʋ waa mɛ?.

eng: The one who comes from above is above all things. The one who is from the earth belongs to the earth and speaks as one from the earth. The one who comes from heaven is above all things.
local: Ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ sʋsʋ ba mʋ kyɔ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ. Ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ kasɛ sʋ mʋ gyɛ kasɛ nɩmʋ sʋ nyɩmɩsa nɩn, nɩɩ kasɛ nɩmʋ sʋ asɩn nɩɩ ɛɛ tɔwɛ. Nɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ sʋsʋ mʋ kyɔ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ.

eng: Dont judge according to appearances. Judge with right judgment.
local: Fan man kɩɩ nyɩmɩsa awaasa kɛ fan bun mʋ kʋpwɛ, amaa fan kɩɩ asɩn mʋ tɔ danbɩrasa pɔyɩ kɛ fan bun kʋpwɛ.

eng: He testifies to what he has seen and heard, but no one accepts his testimony.
local: Ɩmʋ nɩɩ o wu yɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ o nu mʋ, ɩmʋ kesintin nɩɩ ee gyii mʋ, nkɛɛ ɔkʋ mɛɛ kɔɔlɛ mʋ kesintin kigyii mʋ ee gyii.

eng: Some people from Jerusalem said, Isnt he the one they want to kill?.
local: Nɩɩ Gyerusalem asa akʋ ba piili bɛɛ taasɛ yɛ, Man gyɛ ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ nɩɩ bee biti mʋ yɛ ba mɔɔ mʋ?.

eng: Whoever accepts his testimony confirms that God is true.
local: Amaa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kɔɔlɛ mʋ asɩn mʋ gyii mʋ, ɩmʋ ɩɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gyɛ kesintin wuya.

eng: Here he is, speaking in public, yet they arent saying anything to him. Could it be that our leaders actually think he is the Christ?.
local: Fan kɩɩ, mʋ ɩ yɩlɛ asa tɔ ɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔkʋ mɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ sɛhn. Abɛɛ abɩlɩsa mʋ ba bɩɩ nɩn fɛɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ Krisito, Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ mʋ?.

eng: The one whom God sent speaks Gods words because God gives the Spirit generously.
local: Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sun mʋ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn nɩɩ ɛɛ tɔwɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ sa mʋ Kayaayu mʋ nɩɩ kɩ man sʋ ɔkaa.

eng: We know where he is from, but when the Christ comes, no one will know where he is from.
local: Ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ bɩrɛ an nyi katɩn mʋ nu ɔ lɩɩ. Amaa Krisito, Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ mʋ kan ba mʋ, ɔkʋ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ bɩɩ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ.

eng: The Father loves the Son and gives everything into his hands.
local: Mɛ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ ii biti Mu‐Bii kʋlʋn mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taa kʋtɔ kʋmaa waa mʋ kɩbaa tɔ.

eng: While Jesus was teaching in the temple, he exclaimed, You know me and where I am from. I havent come on my own. The one who sent me is true, and you dont know him.
local: Yesu maa san ɔ bʋ Kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa obu lala tɔ ɛɛ kaapʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ kenken yɛ, Kesintin, fan nyi mɛ, nɩɩ fan nyi katɩn mʋ nɩɩ n lɩɩ. Man gyɛ mɛ gbaa‐gbaa mɛ ɔlʋn sʋ nɩɩ n ba nfɩɩ, amaa ɔmʋ nɩɩ o sun mɛ mʋ gyɛ kesintin wuya nɩɩ fanɛɛ fanɛ fan man nyi mʋ.

eng: Whoever believes in the Son has eternal life. Whoever doesnt believe in the Son wont see life, but the angry judgment of God remains on them.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ Mu‐Bii mʋ gyii mʋ sʋ nkpa kakpaa. Amaa ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ man nu sa Mu‐Bii mʋ, ɔ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ nyɛ nkpa kakpaa, amaa Ɩbwaarɛ agbʋ mʋ ɩ dɛ mʋ sʋ.

eng: I know him because I am from him and he sent me.
local: Amaa mɛɛ mɛ n nyi mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ n lɩɩ mʋ asɛ, nɩɩ mʋ i sun mɛ.

eng: So they wanted to seize Jesus, but they couldnt because his time hadnt yet come.
local: Kanɩn sʋ ba biti yɛ ba kɩtaa mʋ, amaa ɔkʋ man taa mʋ kɩbaa mata mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ mʋ saŋa man pɩɩta fʋʋ.

eng: Many from that crowd believed in Jesus. They said, When the Christ comes, will he do more miraculous signs than this man does.
local: Ɩmʋ kpini gbaa mʋ asa gaalaagaa ba kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii sakyɔ mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ bɛɛ taasɛ yɛ, Nɩɩ Krisito, Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ mʋ kan ba mʋ, ee biti ɔ taalɛ waa asɩn kyinkyinsa ɔ kyɔ ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ waa falɛ.

eng: The Pharisees heard the crowd whispering such things about Jesus, and the chief priests and Pharisees sent guards to arrest him.
local: Farasii abi mʋ ba nu sakyɔ mʋ ba maa muni falɛ asɩn lɩɩ mʋ sʋ. Nɩɩ ba maa Oseepu bɩlɩsa mʋ ba sun Kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa obu lala akɩɩsʋpʋ yɛ ba naa kɩtaa mʋ ba.

eng: Therefore, Jesus said, Im still with you for a little while before I go to the one who sent me.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, N mɛɛ yɛ n bɩla n kyɩna fanɛ asɛ kyɛɛrɛɛ, kɛ n bwii kpee ɔmʋ nɩɩ o sun mɛ mʋ asɛ.

eng: You will look for me, but you wont find me, and where I am you cant come.
local: Fen biti fan kɩɩ biti mɛ amaa fan mɛɛ yɛ fan wu mɛ, nɩɩ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ n bʋ mʋ, fan mɛɛ taalɛ ba tʋtɔ.

eng: The Jewish opposition asked each other, Where does he intend to go that we cant find him? Surely he doesnt intend to go where our people have been scattered and are living among the Greeks! He isnt going to teach the Greeks, is he?.
local: Nɩɩ Gyiwu abɩlɩsa mʋ bɛɛ taasɛ abaa yɛ, Fɩnɛ nɩɩ kanyɩn nɩmʋ ii biti o kpee nɩɩ a man bɩla yɛ a wu mʋ? Ee biti o kpee Giriki abi tɔ, katɩn mʋ nɩɩ anɛ asa ba yaasɛ loo, kɛ ɔ naa kaapʋ Giriki abi mʋ abɛɛ?.

eng: What does he mean when he says, You will look for me, but you wont find me, and where I am you cant come.
