local: Maa tɔwɛ yɛ, Fen biti fan biti mɛ amaa fan mɛɛ yɛ fan wu mɛ, nɩɩ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ n bʋ mʋ fan mɛɛ taalɛ ba tʋtɔ mʋ mɩnɛ nɩɩ ɛɛ kaapʋ.

eng: On the last and most important day of the festival, Jesus stood up and shouted, All who are thirsty should come to me!.
local: Nɩɩ kakɛ laalaalʋwɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ kakɛ kparɛ kensi‐gyii mʋ tɔ mʋ, Yesu kʋsʋ yɩlɛ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ kenken yɛ, Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ bʋlɩfɔ kan ɔ sʋ mʋ mʋ, ɔ ba mɛ asɛ ba nuu nkyu.

eng: All who believe in me should drink! As the scriptures said concerning me, Rivers of living water will flow out from within him. .
local: Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ mɛ gyii mʋ, nkyu mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ sa nkpa mʋ ii biti ɩ baa ɩ lɩɩ mʋ tɔ ɩɩ kyʋn fɛɛ ɔbʋn, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ ɩ tɔwɛ mʋ.

eng: Jesus said this concerning the Spirit. Those who believed in him would soon receive the Spirit, but they hadnt experienced the Spirit yet since Jesus hadnt yet been glorified.
local: Nkyu mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ sa nkpa asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ nfɩɩ mʋ, yɩlɛ sa Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ, nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii mʋ, bee biti ba ba nyɛ. Saŋa maŋa tɔ mʋ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ man pɩɩ ba, lɩɩ fɛɛ Yesu bʋnyaa man pɩɩta lɩɩ ifuli.

eng: When some in the crowd heard these words, they said, This man is truly the prophet.
local: Sakyɔ mʋ ba maa nu maa tɔwɛ falɛ asɩn mʋ, bamʋ akʋ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, Kesintin tɔ, ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ mʋ.

eng: Others said, Hes the Christ. But others said, The Christ cant come from Galilee, can he?.
local: Akʋ ba yɛ, Mʋ ɩ gyɛ Krisito mʋ. Nɩɩ akʋ mɔɔ ba taasɛ yɛ, Nɛnɛ nɩɩ Krisito ii biti ɔ lɩɩ Galile?.

eng: Didnt the scripture say that the Christ comes from Davids family and from Bethlehem, Davids village.
local: Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ ɩ tɔwɛ yɛ Krisito ii biti ɔ lɩɩ Owura Dawidi kanan tɔ, kɛ ba kʋʋgɛ mʋ Betilehem kadɛ tɔ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ Owura Dawidi daa kyɩna mʋ.

eng: So the crowd was divided over Jesus.
local: Lɩɩ kanɩn sʋ nbarɩgɛ tɔ ɩ ba sakyɔ mʋ tɔ lɩɩ Yesu sʋ.

eng: Some wanted to arrest him, but no one grabbed him.
local: Nɩɩ bamʋ akʋ bee biti ba kɩtaa mʋ, amaa ɔkʋ man taa kɩbaa mata mʋ.

eng: The guards returned to the chief priests and Pharisees, who asked, Why didnt you bring him.
local: Nɩɩ Kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa akɩɩsʋpʋ mʋ ba bwii kpee Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu abɩlɩsa yɛ Farasii abi mʋ asɛ, nɩɩ ba taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Mɩnɛ waa nɩɩ fan man kɩtaa mʋ ba.

eng: The guards answered, No one has ever spoken the way he does.
local: Nɩɩ akɩɩsʋpʋ mʋ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Nyɩmɩsa kʋ man pɩɩta tɔwɛ asɩn fɛɛ ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ.

eng: The Pharisees replied, Have you too been deceived?.
local: Nɩɩ Farasii abi mʋ ba taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔ bɩla pɩna fanɛ gbaa fanɛ?.

eng: Have any of the leaders believed in him? Has any Pharisee?.
local: Fan wu kadɛ nɩmʋ tɔ abɩlɩsa abɛɛ Farasii abi mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ maa kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii? Ayee!.

eng: No, only this crowd, which doesnt know the Law. And they are under Gods curse.
local: Amaa sakyɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii mʋ bɩrɛ ba man nyi Mosesi nbara mʋ, ɩmʋ sʋ Ɩbwaarɛ nnɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɩla ii biti ɩ ba bamʋ sʋ.

eng: Nicodemus, who was one of them and had come to Jesus earlier, said.
local: Nikodemusi mʋ nɩɩ ɔ wʋla ba Yesu asɛ kakaakʋ, nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ bamʋ abɩlɩsa mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ mʋ, taasɛ bamʋ yɛ.

eng: Our Law doesnt judge someone without first hearing him and learning what he is doing, does it.
local: Anɛ nbara ɩɩ sa ɔkpa fɛɛ a taalɛ bun nyɩmɩsa kʋpwɛ yɛgɛ a man nu mʋ kɔnɔ tɔ asɩn wu kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa.

eng: They answered him, You are not from Galilee too, are you? Look it up and you will see that the prophet doesnt come from Galilee.
local: Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ mʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ngbaa fʋ, fʋ lɩɩ Galile nɩn? Kɩɩ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ danbɩrasa, fii biti fu wu fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ kʋ man lɩɩ Galile.

eng: They each went to their own homes.
local: Nɩɩ ba kʋsʋ ngyaŋɛ mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ kpee mʋ ayɛ.

eng: As Jesus walked along, he saw a man who was blind from birth.
local: Yesu maa naa ɛɛ kyʋn mʋ, o wu ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ gyaatanapʋ lɩɩ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba kʋʋgɛ mʋ.

eng: Jesus disciples asked, Rabbi, who sinned so that he was born blind, this man or his parents.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu abɩɩlapʋ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɔkaapʋpʋ, mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ ba kʋʋgɛ ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ gyaatanapʋ? Mʋ gbaa‐gbaa alibi sʋ abɛɛ mʋ akʋʋgɛpʋ alibi sʋ.

eng: Jesus answered, Neither he nor his parents. This happened so that Gods mighty works might be displayed in him.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Mʋ kɩgyatana mʋ, ɩ man lɩɩ mu alibi sʋ abɛɛ mʋ akʋʋgɛpʋ alibi sʋ. Amaa ɩ waa kanɩn nɩn kɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ yɛgɛ asa ba wu kanan mʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ waa asɩn kyinkyinsa sa mʋ.

eng: While its daytime, we must do the works of him who sent me. Night is coming when no one can work.
local: Kyɔwɛ maa dan san ɔ bʋ tɔ falɛ bɩrɛ, ɩ dagaa a maa waa ɔmʋ nɩɩ o sun mɛ mu kusun. Kyɔwɛ ii biti ɔ lʋwɛ yɛgɛ ɔkʋ mɛɛ bɩla taalɛ sun kusun.

eng: While I am in the world, I am the light of the world.
local: N maa dan san n bʋ dulinyaa tɔ falɛ bɩrɛ, mɛ ɩ gyɛ dulinyaa tɔ ɩŋmaŋɛsa mʋ.

eng: After he said this, he spit on the ground, made mud with the saliva, and smeared the mud on the mans eyes.
local: Yesu maa tɔwɛ ɩnɩmʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tʋʋ nkyɔlɛ tʋʋ kasɛ nɩɩ ɔ palɛ ɩsɛ, taa tayɩ ɔnyɩn mu ansi sʋ.

eng: Jesus said to him, Go, wash in the pool of Siloam . So the man went away and washed. When he returned, he could see.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ ɔnyɩn mʋ yɛ, Kpe Silom kapatɩyɛ mʋ tɔ naa fwɩɩ fu ansi. . Ɔnyɩn mʋ maa naa fwɩɩ mu ansi bwii ba mʋ, mu ansi mʋ a bugi.

eng: The mans neighbors and those who used to see him when he was a beggar said, Isnt this the man who used to sit and beg.
local: Mʋ bɩrɩsa ana yɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba wʋla be nyi mʋ maa kyɩna ɔkpa asɛ ɛɛ kʋlɛ mʋ, bɛɛ taasɛ abaa yɛ, Man gyɛ ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ ɩɩ kyɩna ɔkpa asɛ ɛɛ kʋlɛ mʋ.

eng: Some said, It is, and others said, No, its someone who looks like him. But the man said, Yes, its me.
local: Nɩɩ bamʋ akʋ ba yɛ, Mʋ nɩn. Nɩɩ akʋ mɔɔ ba yɛ, Man gyɛ mʋ nɩn, o duli mʋ nɩn. Amaa ɔnyɩn mʋ gbaa‐gbaa tɔwɛ yɛ, Mɛ nɩn.

eng: So they asked him, How are you now able to see.
local: Nɩɩ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɩ waa nɛnɛ nɩɩ fu ansi i bugi.

eng: He answered, The man they call Jesus made mud, smeared it on my eyes, and said, Go to the pool of Siloam and wash. So I went and washed, and then I could see.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Yesu mʋ, ɩ palɛ ɩsɛ tayɩ mi ansi sʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ n kpee Silom kapatɩyɛ mʋ tɔ naa fwɩɩ mi ansi. Ɩmʋ sʋ n kpee tʋtɔ naa fwɩɩ mi ansi, nɩɩ opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ mi ansi i bugi.

eng: They asked, Where is this man? He replied, I dont know.
local: Nɩɩ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɔnyɩn mʋ bʋ fɩnɛ? Nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, N man nyi.

eng: Then they led the man who had been born blind to the Pharisees.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba taa ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ mu ansi i bugi mʋ kpee Farasii abi mu ansi tɔ.

eng: Now Jesus made the mud and smeared it on the mans eyes on a Sabbath day.
local: Kanɩn kakɛ mʋ nɩɩ Yesu palɛ ɩsɛ tayɩ kanɩn ɔnyɩn ansi sʋ mʋ, gyɛ kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa nɩn.

eng: So Pharisees also asked him how he was able to see. The man told them, He put mud on my eyes, I washed, and now I see.
local: Nɩɩ Farasii abi mʋ mɔɔ ba taasɛ mʋ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɩ waa nɩɩ mu ansi i bugi. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Ɔ palɛ ɩsɛ nɩn tayɩ mi ansi sʋ, nɩɩ n naa fwɩɩ, nɩɩ mi ansi i bugi.

eng: Some Pharisees said, This man isnt from God, because he breaks the Sabbath law. Others said, How can a sinner do miraculous signs like these? So they were divided.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Farasii abi mʋ akʋ ba yɛ, Ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa kanɩn asɩn maŋa mʋ, man lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ o mee buu kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa nbara mʋ. Amaa akʋ mɔɔ ba yɛ, Nɛnɛ nɩɩ alibi ɔwaapʋ ɩ taalɛ waa asɩn kyinkyinsa falɛ kudubi? Nɩɩ nbarɩgɛ ɩ ba bamʋ nsana.

eng: Some of the Pharisees questioned the man who had been born blind again: What do you have to say about him, since he healed your eyes? He replied, Hes a prophet.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ kpini kamaa mʋ, Farasii abi mʋ ba bwii taasɛ ɔnyɩn mʋ yɛ, Ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ o bugi fu ansi mʋ, mɩnɛ asɩn nɩɩ fʋ sʋ fʋ tɔwɛ lɩɩ mʋ sʋ? Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɔ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ nɩn.

eng: The Jewish leaders didnt believe the man had been blind and received his sight until they called for his parents.
local: Gyiwu abɩlɩsa mʋ be mee biti ba kɔɔlɛ gyii fɛɛ ɔ wʋla ɔ gyɛ gyaatanapʋ, nɩɩ mu ansi i bugi, ɩmʋ sʋ ba sun tɩɩ mʋ akʋʋgɛpʋ.

eng: The Jewish leaders asked them, Is this your son? Are you saying he was born blind? How can he now see.
local: Ba maa ba mʋ, ba taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fanɛ‐bii‐nyɩnsa ndee? Fan yɛ fan kʋʋgɛ mʋ gyaatanapʋ nɩn, nɛnɛ ɩ waa sʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɩɩ nbɩɩnbɩɩ.

eng: His parents answered, We know he is our son. We know he was born blind.
local: Nɩɩ mʋ akʋʋgɛpʋ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, An nyi maa gyɛ anɛ‐bii‐nyɩnsa nɩn, nɩɩ an kʋʋgɛ mʋ gyaatanapʋ nɩn.

eng: But we dont know how he now sees, and we dont know who healed his eyes. Ask him. Hes old enough to speak for himself.
local: Amaa a man nyi kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɩ waa nɩɩ mu ansi ɩ kɩɩ, abɛɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ o bugi mu ansi. Ɔ man bɩla ɔ gyɛ kebii, ɩmʋ sʋ fan taasɛ mʋ gbaa‐gbaa, ɛɛ taalɛ lɛɛ kɔnɔ.

eng: His parents said this because they feared the Jewish authorities. This is because the Jewish authorities had already decided that whoever confessed Jesus to be the Christ would be expelled from the synagogue.
local: Kʋtɔ mʋ sʋ nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ kanɩn mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ ba sɩlɛ Gyiwu abɩlɩsa mʋ. Kpalɩ fɛɛ Gyiwu abɩlɩsa mʋ ba wʋla tɔwɛ yɩla yɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ lɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ Yesu ɩ gyɛ Krisito, Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ mʋ mʋ, bɛɛ gya mʋ ba lɛɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa obu mʋ tɔ.

eng: Thats why his parents said, Hes old enough. Ask him.
local: Kanɩn sʋ nɩɩ mʋ akʋʋgɛpʋ mʋ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔ man bɩla ɔ gyɛ kebii, fan taasɛ mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mu.

eng: Therefore, they called a second time for the man who had been born blind and said to him, Give glory to God. We know this man is a sinner.
local: Gyiwu abɩlɩsa mʋ ba bwii tɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ naafɔɔ ɔ gyɛ gyaatanapʋ mʋ kɩnyɔsapʋ, nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Bɔ Ɩbwaarɛ fɛɛ kesintin nɩɩ fɩɩ tɔwɛ. An nyi fɛɛ ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ o bugi fu ansi mʋ gyɛ alibi ɔwaapʋ nɩn.

eng: The man answered, I dont know whether hes a sinner. Heres what I do know: I was blind and now I see.
local: Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Mɛɛ mɛ man nyi yɛ ɔ gyɛ alibi ɔwaapʋ abɛɛ ɔ man gyɛ. Kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ n nyi mʋ ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ, n kyɩna n gyɛ gyaatanapʋ, amaa nbɩɩnbɩɩ n kɩɩ.

eng: They questioned him: What did he do to you? How did he heal your eyes.
local: Nɩɩ Gyiwu abɩlɩsa mʋ ba bɩla taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Mɩnɛ nɩɩ ɔ waa fʋ? Nɛnɛ nɩɩ ɔ waa kyɛ fu ansi.

eng: He replied, I already told you, and you didnt listen. Why do you want to hear it again? Do you want to become his disciples too.
local: Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, N wʋla tɔwɛ fanɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɩ ba, nɩɩ fan kina kunu. Mɩnɛ ɩ waa sʋ nɩɩ fen biti fan bwii nu kɩnyɔsapʋ? Abɛɛ fanɛ gbaa fanɛ fen biti fɛɛ fan biliŋi mʋ abɩɩlapʋ nɩn.

eng: They insulted him: You are his disciple, but we are Moses disciples.
local: Nɩɩ ba saalɛ mʋ, tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Fʋ ɩ gyɛ mu obuupu, amaa anɛɛnɛ bɩrɛ an gyɛ Mosesi abuupu nɩn.

eng: We know that God spoke to Moses, but we dont know where this man is from.
local: An nyi ɩmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ maa kyɩna tɔwɛ Mosesi asɛ, amaa kanɩn Yesu maŋa bɩrɛ, a man nyi mʋ ɔlɩɩkpa.

eng: The man answered, This is incredible! You dont know where he is from, yet he healed my eyes!.
local: Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Mɩnɛ alɩmankarɛ nɩɩ fɛn tɔwɛ kanɩn! Fan yɛ fan man nyi katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ, amaa mʋ i bugi mi ansi falɛ.

eng: We know that God doesnt listen to sinners. God listens to anyone who is devout and does Gods will.
local: An nyi yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mee nu alibi awaapʋ asɛ. Amaa asa mʋ nɩɩ bee sun Ɩbwaarɛ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa mʋ kɔkɔlɔ biti mʋ, bamʋ asɛ nɩɩ ee nu.

eng: No one has ever heard of a healing of the eyes of someone born blind.
local: Lɩɩ koo saŋa mʋ nɩɩ dulinyaa piili mʋ, a man pɩɩta nu fɛɛ ɔsa kʋ bugi nyɩmɩsa mʋ nɩɩ ba kʋʋgɛ mʋ gyaatanapʋ ansi.

eng: If this man wasnt from God, he couldnt do this.
local: Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ man dɩn lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ mʋ, naafɔɔ ɔ man dɩn taalɛ waa ɩnɩmʋ.

eng: They responded, You were born completely in sin! How is it that you dare to teach us? Then they expelled him.
local: Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Kɩɩ, fʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba kʋʋgɛ fʋ alibi tɔ, nɛnɛ nɩɩ fʋ ii biti fʋ ba kaapʋ anɛ asɩn? Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba gya mʋ lɛɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa obu mʋ tɔ.

eng: Jesus heard they had expelled the man born blind. Finding him, Jesus said, Do you believe in the Human One.
local: Yesu maa nu yɛ Gyiwu abɩlɩsa mʋ ba gya ɔnyɩn mʋ lɛɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa obu mʋ tɔ kanɩn mʋ, ɔ naa wu mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Fɩɩ kɔɔlɛ Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ fii gyii.

eng: He answered, Who is he, sir? I want to believe in him.
local: Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, mʋ ɩ gyɛ anɩmʋ? Tɔwɛ mɛ kɛ n kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii.

eng: Jesus said, You have seen him. In fact, he is the one speaking with you.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Fʋ tɛɛ wʋla wu mʋ, mʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ tɔwɛ fʋ asɛ nbɩɩnbɩɩ falɛ.

eng: The man said, Lord, I believe. And he worshipped Jesus.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, Mɛ‐nyɩrɩpɛ, n kɔɔlɛ fu gyii. Nɩɩ ɔ kyʋn muŋa Yesu ayaa tɔ.

eng: Jesus said, I have come into the world to exercise judgment so that those who dont see can see and those who see will become blind.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ yɛ, N ba dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ kɛ n ba gyii asa asɩn, kɛ agyaatanapʋ ansi i bugi, kɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ kɩɩ mʋ, mɔɔ ba biliŋi agyaatanapʋ.

eng: Some Pharisees who were with him heard what he said and asked, Surely we arent blind, are we.
local: Farasii abi mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ tʋtɔ, nɩɩ ba nu kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ, ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Fɩɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ anɛ gbaa anɛ an gyɛ agyaatanapʋ nɩn abɛɛ.

eng: Jesus said to them, If you were blind, you wouldnt have any sin, but now that you say, We see, your sin remains.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Fan kan fan gyɛ agyaatanapʋ bɩrɛ, naafɔɔ fan mɛɛ yɛ fan gyii alibi kʋpwɛ. Amaa fan maa tɔwɛ yɛ fan kɩɩ falɛ bɩrɛ, fan gyii alibi kʋpwɛ.

eng: And Jesus went to the Mount of Olives.
local: Ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ maa kyʋn mʋ ayɛ mʋ, nɩɩ Yesu lɩɩ tʋtɔ kpee Olifu kɩbɩɩ mʋ sʋ.

eng: Early in the morning he returned to the temple. All the people gathered around him, and he sat down and taught them.
local: Ɩmʋ lɩɩkaakɛ nyenyenfuu mʋ, Yesu bwii ba Kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa obu lala mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ asa ba ba yɩlɛ kyaabɔɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kyʋn kyɩna nɩɩ o piili ɛɛ kaapʋ bamʋ.

eng: The legal experts and Pharisees brought a woman caught in adultery. Placing her in the center of the group.
local: Maa bʋ tʋtɔ mʋ, nɩɩ nbara akaapʋpʋ yɛ Farasii abi ba bɩya ɔkyɩɩ kilipu kʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba kɩtaa mʋ maa ɔnyɩn. Nɩɩ ba taa mʋ ba yɩlɛ bamʋ nsana.

eng: they said to Jesus, Teacher, this woman was caught in the act of committing adultery.
local: Nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ Yesu yɛ, Ɔkaapʋpʋ, ba kɩtaa ɔkyɩɩ nɩmʋ maa ɔnyɩn.

eng: In the Law, Moses commanded us to stone women like this. What do you say.
local: Mosesi nbara mʋ ɩɩ kaapʋ anɛ yɛ a tʋʋ abuu mɔɔ kanɩn akyɩɩ maŋa. Nbɩɩnbɩɩ, nɛnɛ nɩɩ fʋ sʋ fɩɩ tɔwɛ.

eng: They said this to test him, because they wanted a reason to bring an accusation against him. Jesus bent down and wrote on the ground with his finger.
local: Ba taasɛ mʋ asɩn nɩmʋ kɛ ba nu mʋ kɔnɔ tɔ alibi, kɛ ba nyɛ yɩlɛ ɩmʋ sʋ pwɩɩ mʋ. Amaa Yesu nyɔn mu kumu yii tɔ, taa mʋ kɩbaa‐bi ɛɛ kyʋrɔɔ kasɛ.

eng: They continued to question him, so he stood up and replied, Whoever hasnt sinned should throw the first stone.
local: Ba maa maa sʋ bɛɛ taasɛ mʋ mʋ, ɔ kʋsaa mu kumu nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Nɩɩ fanɛ tɔ ɔkʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man pɩɩta waa alibi mʋ, ɔ gyankpaa tʋʋ kubuu da ɔkyɩɩ mʋ.

eng: Bending down again, he wrote on the ground.
local: Nɩɩ o bwii nyɔn mu kumu yii tɔ ɛɛ kyʋrɔɔ kasɛ.

eng: Those who heard him went away, one by one, beginning with the elders. Finally, only Jesus and the woman were left in the middle of the crowd.
local: Nɩɩ ba maa nu kanɩn mʋ, ba piili bɛɛ lɩɩ kʋkʋlʋn‐kʋkʋlʋn. Abɩlɩsa mʋ ba gyankpaa lɩɩ pɔyɩ. Nɩɩ ɩ san Yesu maa ɔkyɩɩ mʋ nkʋn yɛgɛ ɔ yɩlɛ tʋtɔ.

eng: Jesus stood up and said to her, Woman, where are they? Is there no one to condemn you.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu kusaa mu kumu nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ yɛ, Ɔkyɩɩ, ba bʋ fɩnɛ? Ɔkʋ man bʋ tɔ kɛ o bun fʋ kʋpwɛ fʋ.

eng: She said, No one, sir. Jesus said, Neither do I condemn you. Go, and from now on, dont sin anymore.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, ayee, ɔkʋ man bʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Ngbaa mɛ mɛɛ yɛ n bun fʋ kʋpwɛ. Natɛ, lɩɩ ndaga ɩɩ sʋ ii kpee man bɩla naa waa alibi.

eng: Jesus spoke to the people again, saying, I am the light of the world. Whoever follows me wont walk in darkness but will have the light of life.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu bwii tɔwɛ Farasii abi mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ tʋtɔ mʋ yɛ, Mɛ ɩ gyɛ dulinyaa tɔ ɩŋmaŋɛsa mʋ. Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o buu mɛ mʋ, mɛɛ yɛ ɔ natɛ kitentenbiri tɔ, amaa ee biti ɔ nyɛ ɩŋmaŋɛsa mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ sa nkpa mʋ.

eng: Then the Pharisees said to him, Because you are testifying about yourself, your testimony isnt valid.
local: Nɩɩ Farasii abi mʋ ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Nbɩɩnbɩɩ, fii gyii fʋ gbaa‐gbaa fʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋ kesintin nɩn, ɩmʋ sʋ a mɛɛ yɛ a taalɛ kɔɔlɛ fu gyii.

eng: Jesus replied, Even if I testify about myself, my testimony is true, since I know where I came from and where Im going. You dont know where I come from or where Im going.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Halɩɩ nɩɩ n gyii mɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ kesintin gbaa mʋ, mɛ asɩn mʋ ɩ gyɛ kesintin. Lɩɩ fɛɛ n nyi katɩn mʋ nɩɩ n lɩɩ nɩɩ n nyi katɩn mʋ nɩɩ n kpee. Amaa fanɛɛ fanɛ bɩrɛ, fan man nyi katɩn mʋ nɩɩ n lɩɩ, nɩɩ fan man nyi katɩn mʋ nɩɩ n kpee.

eng: You judge according to human standards, but I judge no one.
local: Fanɛ bɩrɛ, nyɩmɩsa ɩkpa sʋ nɩɩ fɛn yɩlɛ sʋ fen gyii asɩn. Mɛɛ mɛ bɩrɛ n mee gyii ɔkʋ asɩn.

eng: Even if I do judge, my judgment is truthful, because Im not alone. My judgments come from me and from the Father who sent me.
local: Amaa nɩɩ n kan gyii asɩn mʋ, mɛ asɩn kigyii kɩ dɛ ɔkpa, lɩɩ fɛɛ man gyɛ mɛ nkʋn ii gyii asɩn mʋ. Amaa maa Mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ nɩɩ o sun mɛ mʋ.

eng: In your Law it is written that the witness of two people is true.
local: Ba kyʋrɔɔ fanɛ Nbara tɔ yɛ, nɩɩ asa anyɔ bee gyii kesintin lɩɩ asɩn kʋ sʋ, nɩɩ bamʋ nnɔ ɩ man kpakyarɛ mʋ, bamʋ asɩn mʋ ɩ gyɛ kesintin.

eng: I am one witness concerning myself, and the Father who sent me is the other.
local: Mɛ gbaa‐gbaa mɛ ii gyii mɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ kesintin, nɩɩ Mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ nɩɩ o sun mɛ mʋ gbaa ii gyii mɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ kesintin.

eng: They asked him, Where is your Father? Jesus answered, You dont know me and you dont know my Father. If you knew me, you would also know my Father.
local: Nɩɩ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Fʋ‐sɛ bʋ fɩnɛ? Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Fan man nyi mɛ, nɩɩ fan man nyi Mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ gbaa. Nɩɩ fan daa fan nyi mɛ nɩn, naafɔɔ fan bɩɩ Mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ gbaa.

eng: He spoke these words while he was teaching in the temple area known as the treasury. No one arrested him, because his time hadnt yet come.
local: Yesu tɔwɛ asɩn nɩmʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kaapʋ asɩn Kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ, katɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ afulee ɔyɩlɛkpa mʋ. Amaa ɔkʋ man kɩtaa mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ mʋ saŋa man pɩɩta fʋʋ.

eng: Jesus continued, Im going away. You will look for me, and you will die in your sin. Where Im going, you cant come.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu bwii tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, N kpee nɩmʋ. Nɩɩ fen biti fan kɩɩ biti mɛ, kɛ fan wu fanɛ alibi tɔ. Nɩɩ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ n kpee mʋ fɛn mɛɛ taalɛ ba tʋtɔ.

eng: The Jewish leaders said, He isnt going to kill himself, is he? Is that why he said, Where Im going, you cant come.
local: Nɩɩ Gyiwu abi mʋ bɛɛ taasɛ abaa yɛ, Maa tɔwɛ yɛ, Katɩn mʋ nɩɩ n kpee mʋ fɛn mɛɛ taalɛ ba tʋtɔ mʋ, ee biti ɔ mɔɔ mʋ n‐yɩɩ nɩn abɛɛ.

eng: He said to them, You are from below; Im from above. You are from this world; Im not from this world.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ kyaga sʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, Fan lɩɩ kasɛ sʋ nfɩɩ nɩn, mɛɛ mɛ n lɩɩ sʋsʋ. Nɩɩ fan gyɛ dulinyaa nɩmʋ lɛɛ nɩn, mɛɛ mɛ bɩrɛ n man gyɛ dulinyaa nɩmʋ lɛɛ.

eng: This is why I told you that you would die in your sins. If you dont believe that I Am, you will die in your sins.
local: Kanɩn sʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, fen biti fan wu fanɛ alibi tɔ mʋ. Nɩɩ fan man kan kɔɔlɛ mɛ gyii yɛ, mɛ ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ yɛ n gyɛ mʋ, fen biti fan wu fanɛ alibi tɔ kesintin.

eng: Who are you? they asked. Jesus replied, Im exactly who I have claimed to be from the beginning.
local: Nɩɩ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Fʋ ɩ gyɛ anɩmʋ? Nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Mɛ ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ n dan wʋla n tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ n gyɛ mʋ.

eng: I have many things to say in judgment concerning you. The one who sent me is true, and what I have heard from him I tell the world.
local: N sʋ asɩn gaa n tɔwɛ lɩɩ fanɛ sʋ, kɛ n taa gyii fanɛ asɩn. Amaa ɔmʋ nɩɩ o sun mɛ mʋ gyɛ kesintin wuya, nɩɩ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ n nu lɩɩ mʋ asɛ mʋ nkʋn nɩɩ n tɔwɛ n sa dulinyaa abi.

eng: They didnt know he was speaking about his Father.
local: Ɩmʋ kpini gbaa mʋ ba man pɩɩta bɩɩ fɛɛ Mʋ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn nɩɩ ɛɛ tɔwɛ bamʋ.

eng: So Jesus said to them, When the Human One is lifted up, then you will know that I Am. Then you will know that I do nothing on my own, but I say just what the Father has taught me.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Nɩɩ fan kan ba kʋsaa mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ sʋsʋ mʋ, tʋtɔ nɩɩ fen biti fan bɩɩ fɛɛ n gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ n wʋla tɔwɛ yɛ n gyɛ mʋ, nɩɩ n mɛɛ waa kʋtɔ kʋ mɛ gbaa‐gbaa mɛ ɔlʋn sʋ, amaa asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Mɛ‐Sɛ kaapʋ mɛ mʋ, ɩmʋ nɩɩ n sʋ n tɔwɛ.

eng: He who sent me is with me. He doesnt leave me by myself, because I always do what makes him happy.
local: Ɔmʋ nɩɩ o sun mɛ mʋ bʋ mɛ asɛ, ɔ man yɛgɛ mɛ nkʋn, lɩɩ fɛɛ saŋa kʋmaa n waa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ pɩrɛ mʋ.

eng: While Jesus was saying these things, many people came to believe in him.
local: Nɩɩ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba nu Yesu maa tɔwɛ ɩnɩmʋ kpini mʋ gaalaagaa ba kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii.

eng: Jesus said to the Jews who believed in him, You are truly my disciples if you remain faithful to my teaching.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii mʋ yɛ, Nɩɩ fan kan fan buu mɛ asɩn kaapʋsa mʋ, fan gyɛ mɛ abɩɩlapʋ kesintin tɔ.

eng: Then you will know the truth, and the truth will set you free.
local: Nɩɩ fen biti fan bɩɩ kesintin mʋ, kɛ kesintin mʋ ɔ yɛgɛ fan nyɛ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ.

eng: They responded, We are Abrahams children; weve never been anyones slaves. How can you say that we will be set free.
local: Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, An gyɛ Abraham kɩnaana tɔ asa nɩn. Nɩɩ anɛɛnɛ bɩrɛ, kakaakʋ a man pɩɩta waa ɔkʋ kɩnyɛ. Nɩɩ mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ fɩɩ tɔwɛ yɛ an biti a nyɛ anɛ n‐yɩɩ.

eng: Jesus answered, I assure you that everyone who sins is a slave to sin.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin, ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ waa alibi mʋ gyɛ kɩnyɛ sa alibi nɩn.

eng: A slave isnt a permanent member of the household, but a son is.
local: Nɩɩ kɩnyɛ man gyɛ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ o tii kɩkpaara abi sʋ, amaa obii bɩrɛ gyɛ kɩkpaara mʋ sʋ obii kakpaa.

eng: After this there was a Jewish festival, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ kamaa mʋ, Yesu kpee Gyerusalem naa gyii Gyiwu abi Kesu kɔnɔ ngyaŋɛ kʋ.

eng: Therefore, if the Son makes you free, you really will be free.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ Mu‐Bii mʋ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ɔkʋ nyɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ mʋ, kesintin tɔ ɔ nyɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ kakpaa.

eng: In Jerusalem near the Sheep Gate in the north city wall is a pool with the Aramaic name Bethsaida. It had five covered porches.
local: Kɩpatɛ kʋ kɩ bʋ Gyerusalem kadɛ tɔ, nɩɩ kɩ bʋ tiri kebunɔ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ nbʋlʋpʋ kebunɔ mʋ. Bɛɛ tɩɩ kapatɩyɛ maa Hibriwu kadɛ tɔ yɛ Betesada. Nɩɩ agyan anu ɩ dɛ kyaabɔɔ kɩmʋ.

eng: I know that you are Abrahams children, yet you want to kill me because you dont welcome my teaching.
local: N nyi fan maa gyɛ Abraham anaana‐bii. Amaa ɩnɩmʋ kpini gbaa mʋ, fen biti fɛɛ fan mɔɔ mɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ fɛn mɛɛ kɔɔlɛ mɛ asɩn mʋ fɛn waa fanɛ nkɔlɔ tɔ.

eng: and a crowd of people who were sick, blind, lame, and paralyzed sat there.
local: Agyan nɩmʋ tɔ nɩɩ alɔpʋ damantɛ fɛɛ agyaatanapʋ, yɛ ayaa yɛ abaa wusa wuya ana, amʋgʋlɛ‐mʋgʋlɛ kpini ba ba tʋtɔ bee gyoo.

eng: Im telling you what Ive seen when I am with the Father, but you are doing what youve heard from your father.
local: Kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Mɛ‐Sɛ kaapʋ mɛ mʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ fanɛ falɛ. Nɩɩ fanɛɛ fanɛ fɛn waa atɔ mʋ nɩɩ fan nu lɩɩ fanɛ‐sɛ asɛ.

eng: A certain man was there who had been sick for thirty-eight years.
local: Ɔnyɩn kʋ bʋ tʋtɔ ɛɛ lɔ nsu adɩsa yɛ aburuwa.

eng: They replied, Our father is Abraham. Jesus responded, If you were Abrahams children, you would do Abrahams works.
local: Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Anɛ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Abraham. Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan daa fan gyɛ Abraham mu‐bii ana kesintin tɔ nɩn, naafɔɔ fen biti fan waa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa mʋ.

eng: When Jesus saw him lying there, knowing that he had already been there a long time, he asked him, Do you want to get well.
local: Yesu maa wu mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ dɛ tʋtɔ lɔ kyɛɛrɛɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Fii biti fɛɛ fʋ nyɛ alanfɩya.

eng: Instead, you want to kill me, though I am the one who has spoken the truth I heard from God. Abraham didnt do this.
local: Amaa n maa tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin mʋ nɩɩ n nu lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ gbaa mʋ, fen biti fan mɔɔ mɛ. Abraham man waa falɛ atɔ.

eng: The sick man answered him, Sir, I dont have anyone who can put me in the water when it is stirred up. When Im trying to get to it, someone else has gotten in ahead of me.
local: Nɩɩ ɔlɔpʋ mʋ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɔbɩlɩsa, nɩɩ kabɔɔ mʋ kan lupu nkyu mʋ tɔ mʋ, n man sʋ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa mɛ loo kapatɩyɛ mʋ tɔ. Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ n kpan mɛ n‐yɩɩ fɛɛ n loo to mʋ, nɩɩ ɔkʋ ɩ daa gyankpaa mɛ loo to.

eng: You are doing your fathers works. They said, Our ancestry isnt in question! The only Father we have is God.
local: Atɔ mʋ nɩɩ fanɛ‐sɛ waa mʋ, nɩɩ fanɛ gbaa fanɛ fɛn waa. Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, A man gyɛ akuyurubi, ɔsɛ kʋlʋn nɩɩ an sʋ, mʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gbaa‐gbaa mʋ.

eng: Jesus said to him, Get up! Pick up your mat and walk.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Kʋsʋ taa fʋ kalan kɛ fʋ natɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, If God were your Father, you would love me, for I came from God. Here I am. I havent come on my own. God sent me.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ daa ɔ gyɛ fanɛ‐Sɛ nɩn, naafɔɔ fen biti fan nyɛ kebiti sa mɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ n lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ nɩn, nɩɩ n ba nfɩɩ. Man gyɛ mɛ gbaa‐gbaa mɛ ɔlʋn sʋ nɩɩ n ba, amaa mʋ i sun mɛ.

eng: Immediately the man was well, and he picked up his mat and walked. Now that day was the Sabbath.
local: Opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ alanfɩya, nɩɩ o kukuri mʋ kalan natɛ. Kanɩn kakɛ maŋa gyɛ Gyiwu abi kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ nɩn.

eng: Why dont you understand what Im saying? Its because you cant really hear my words.
local: Nɩɩ mɩnɛ waa sʋ nɩɩ fen mee nu mɛ asɩn tɔwɛsa mʋ kasɛ? Lɩɩ fɛɛ fɛn mɛɛ taalɛ fen nu mɛ asɩn mʋ.

eng: The Jewish leaders said to the man who had been healed, Its the Sabbath; you arent allowed to carry your mat.
local: Nɩɩ Gyiwu abɩlɩsa mʋ ba tɔwɛ ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ alanfɩya mʋ yɛ, Ndaga gyɛ kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ. Anɛ nbara mʋ ɩ mɛɛ sa ɔkpa yɛ fʋ sʋla fʋ kalan.

eng: Your father is the devil. You are his children, and you want to do what your father wants. He was a murderer from the beginning. He has never stood for the truth, because theres no truth in him. Whenever that liar speaks, he speaks according to his own nature, because hes a liar and the father of liars.
local: Fan gyɛ fanɛ‐sɛ Sɩtaanɛ mu‐bii ana nɩn, ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ fen biti fan waa fanɛ‐sɛ mʋ kɔkɔlɔ biti. Lɩɩ npiili asɛ mʋ kpini nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ ɔmɔɔpʋ, nɩɩ ɔ man buu kesintin, lɩɩ fɛɛ kesintin man bʋ mʋ tɔ. Mʋ lɛɛ kpini ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ ɔ tɔwɛ antɩŋɛsa, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ gyɛ antɩŋɛsa wuya, yɛ antɩŋɛsa wuya ana bamʋ‐sɛ.

eng: He answered, The man who made me well said to me, Pick up your mat and walk.
local: Amaa ɔ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kyɛ mɛ mʋ ɩ tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, Taa fʋ kalan kɛ fʋ natɛ. .

eng: Because I speak the truth, you dont believe me.
local: Amaa mɛɛ mɛ bɩrɛ n gyii fanɛ kesintin nɩn, ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ fɛɛ mɛɛ kɔɔlɛ mɛ fen gyii.

eng: They inquired, Who is this man who said to you, Pick it up and walk.
local: Kanɩn sʋ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Anɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ kanɩn ɔnyɩn maŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ fʋ yɛ fʋ taa fʋ kalan kɛ fʋ natɛ.

eng: Who among you can show Im guilty of sin? Since I speak the truth, why dont you believe me?.
local: Fanɛ tɔ anɩmʋ ɩ taalɛ kaapʋ fɛɛ n gyii alibi kʋpwɛ? Nɩɩ n maa tɔwɛ kesintin mʋ, mɩnɛ waa nɩɩ fɛn mɛɛ kɔɔlɛ fen gyii?.

eng: The man who had been cured didnt know who it was, because Jesus had slipped away from the crowd gathered there.
local: Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ man nyi kanɩn ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kyɛ mʋ mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ asa ba kyɔ nɩɩ Yesu loo asa damantɛ mʋ tɔ kyʋn.

eng: Gods children listen to Gods words. You dont listen to me because you arent Gods children.
local: Ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ mʋ, ii nu asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ. Kʋtɔ mʋ sʋ nɩɩ fen mee nu Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ fan man gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ.

eng: Later Jesus found him in the temple and said, See! You have been made well. Dont sin anymore in case something worse happens to you.
local: Kamaa tɔ mʋ Yesu wu mʋ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Kɩɩ, nbɩɩnbɩɩ fʋ nyɛ alanfɩya, ɩmʋ sʋ yɛgɛ alibi kɩwaa. Nɩɩ man gyɛ kanɩn, ɩmʋ nɩɩ ii biti ɩ ba fʋ sʋ mʋ ii biti ɩ baa ɩ kyɔ ɩnɩmʋ.

eng: The Jewish opposition answered, We were right to say that you are a Samaritan and have a demon, werent we.
local: Nɩɩ Gyiwu abi mʋ mɔɔ ba tɔwɛ Yesu yɛ, Nɩɩ a maa tɔwɛ yɛ fʋ gyɛ Kasamariya‐nyɩn nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi ba tɛ fʋ tɔ mʋ, ɩ man gyɛ kesintin.

eng: The man went and proclaimed to the Jewish leaders that Jesus was the man who had made him well.
local: Nɩɩ Ɔnyɩn mʋ lɩɩ tʋtɔ naa tɔwɛ Gyiwu abɩlɩsa mʋ yɛ Yesu ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ kyɛ mʋ mʋ.

eng: I dont have a demon, Jesus replied. But I honor my Father and you dishonor me.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ Nbwɩɩ libi akʋ ba man tɛ mɛ tɔ, amaa n bʋnyaa mɛ‐Sɛ, yɛgɛ fanɛɛ fanɛ fɛn mɛɛ bʋnyaa mɛ.

eng: As a result, the Jewish leaders were harassing Jesus, since he had done these things on the Sabbath.
local: Lɩɩ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ Yesu waa kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ sʋ mʋ, Gyiwu abɩlɩsa mʋ ba piili bɛɛ tɔɔrɔɔ mʋ.

eng: Im not trying to bring glory to myself. Theres one who is seeking to glorify me, and hes the judge.
local: N mee biti bʋnyaa n sa mɛ n‐yɩɩ. Amaa ɔkʋ bʋ tɔ ee biti kɩmʋ ɛɛ sa mɛ. Mʋ ɩ gyɛ asɩn ogyipu mʋ.

eng: Jesus replied, My Father is still working, and I am working too.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Mɛ‐Sɛ ii sun kusun saŋa kʋmaa, ɩmʋ sʋ ngbaa mɛ n sun kusun.

eng: I assure you that whoever keeps my word will never die.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin yɛ, ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o buu mɛ kɩkaapʋ mʋ, ɔ mɛɛ yɛ o wu kakaakʋ.

eng: For this reason the Jewish leaders wanted even more to kill him—not only because he was doing away with the Sabbath but also because he called God his own Father, thereby making himself equal with God.
local: Lɩɩ ɩnɩmʋ sʋ Gyiwu abi ba payɩ ansi bee biti ɩkpa ba mɔɔ mʋ. Man gyɛ kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ nbara kinyita mʋ wʋlɛ nkʋn sʋ, amaa ɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ mʋ‐Sɛ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ, mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ maa Ɩbwaarɛ kyɛɛkyɛɛ.

eng: The Jewish opposition said to Jesus, Now we know that you have a demon. Abraham and the prophets died, yet you say, Whoever keeps my word will never die.
local: Nɩɩ Gyiwu abi mʋ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, Nbɩɩnbɩɩ an wu kesintin tɔ fɛɛ nbwɩɩ libi ba bʋ fʋ tɔ. Abraham wu, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ mʋ gbaa ba wu. Amaa fʋ, fʋ tɔwɛ yɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o buu fʋ kɩkaapʋ mʋ ɔ mɛɛ yɛ o wu.

eng: Jesus responded to the Jewish leaders, I assure you that the Son cant do anything by himself except what he sees the Father doing. Whatever the Father does, the Son does likewise.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu kyaga sʋ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin yɛ, mɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ Mu‐Bii mʋ n mɛɛ taalɛ waa sɛhn mɛ kɩba sʋ. Kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ n wu Mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ waa mʋ, kɩmʋ nɩɩ ngbaa mɛ n waa.

eng: Are you greater than our father Abraham? He died and the prophets died, so who do you make yourself out to be.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ fii biti fʋ kaapʋ fɛɛ fʋ kyɔ anɛ‐sɛ Abraham abɛɛ? O wu, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ mʋ gbaa ba wu. Anɩmʋ nɩɩ fɩɩ fɛ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ fʋ gyɛ.

eng: The Father loves the Son and shows him everything that he does. He will show him greater works than these so that you will marvel.
local: Mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ sʋ kebiti sa mɛ, nɩɩ ɛɛ kaapʋ mɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ waa mʋ. Mɛ‐Sɛ ii biti ɔ kaapʋ mɛ atɔ kparɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ kyɔ ɩnɩmʋ, kɛ n waa kɛ i kyinkyin fanɛ.

eng: Jesus answered, If I glorify myself, my glory is meaningless. My Father, who you say is your God, is the one who glorifies me.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Nɩɩ n kan sa mɛ n‐yɩɩ bʋnyaa mʋ, mɛ bʋnyaa mʋ gyɛ kɩyan. Amaa mɛ‐Sɛ, mʋ nɩɩ fɛn tɔwɛ yɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ fanɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ, ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ bʋnyaa mɛ.

eng: As the Father raises the dead and gives life, so too does the Son give life to whomever he wishes.
local: Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Mɛ‐Sɛ ii kyiŋi alewupu nɩɩ ɛɛ sa bamʋ nkpa mʋ, kanɩn maŋa sʋ nɩɩ mɛ, Obii mʋ gbaa n sa nkpa mi kebiti tɔ.

eng: You dont know him, but I do. If I said I didnt know him, I would be like you, a liar. But I do know him, and I keep his word.
local: Fan man nyi mʋ, mɛɛ mɛ bɩrɛ n nyi mʋ. Nɩɩ n kan n tɔwɛ yɛ n man n nyi mʋ mʋ, n gyɛ antɩŋɛsa wuya fɛɛ fanɛ, amaa n nyi mʋ, nɩɩ n nu mʋ asɛ.

eng: The Father doesnt judge anyone, but he has given all judgment to the Son.
local: Mɛ‐Sɛ gbaa‐gbaa mɛɛ talɛ ɔkʋ, amaa ɔ taa ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ katalɛ waa mɛ kɩbaa tɔ.

eng: Your father Abraham was overjoyed that he would see my day. He saw it and was happy.
local: Fanɛ‐sɛ Abraham mu ansi i gyii fɛɛ ee biti o wu mɛ kɩba. Nɩɩ o wu mɛ kɩba mʋ, nɩɩ mu ansi i gyii.

eng: so that everyone will honor the Son just as they honor the Father. Whoever doesnt honor the Son doesnt honor the Father who sent him.
local: Mɛ‐Sɛ ii biti asa kpini ba taa bʋnyaa sa mɛ, Obii mʋ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ taa bʋnyaa bɛɛ sa Mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ. Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ taa bʋnyaa sa mɛ, Obii mʋ, ɩɩ kaapʋ nɩn fɛɛ ɔ mɛɛ taa bʋnyaa ɛɛ sa Mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ nɩɩ o sun mɛ mʋ.

eng: You arent even years old! the Jewish opposition replied. How can you say that you have seen Abraham.
local: Nɩɩ Gyiwu abi mʋ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Fʋ man pɩɩta gyii nsu adunuu nɩɩ fʋ yɛ fu wu Abraham.

eng: I assure you that whoever hears my word and believes in the one who sent me has eternal life and wont come under judgment but has passed from death into life.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o nu mɛ asɩn, nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ o sun mɛ mʋ gyii mʋ, sʋ nkpa kakpaa, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ mɛɛ yɛ o bun mʋ kʋpwɛ. Ɔ tɛɛ faa lɩɩ lewu tɔ ba nkpa tɔ.

eng: I assure you, Jesus replied, before Abraham was, I Am.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin yɛ, pɔyɩ kɛ ba kʋʋgɛ Abraham mʋ, yɛgɛ n bʋ tɔ.

eng: I assure you that the time is coming—and is here!—when the dead will hear the voice of Gods Son, and those who hear it will live.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin fɛɛ saŋa kʋ ɩɩ ba, nɩɩ nbɩɩnbɩɩ gbaa ɩɩ tɛɛ ba. Nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba du fɛɛ alewupu lɩɩ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba man nyi Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ sʋ mʋ, bee biti ba nu mɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ Mu‐Bii mʋ ɔbʋlɛ, nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba nu mɛ asɛ mʋ, bee biti ba nyɛ nkpa.

eng: So they picked up stones to throw at him, but Jesus hid himself and left the temple.
local: Ba maa nu falɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ba kukuri abuu yɛ ba tʋʋ mʋ. Amaa Yesu loo asa mʋ tɔ bʋlɛ, nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ Kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ kyʋn.

eng: Just as the Father has life in himself, so he has granted the Son to have life in himself.
local: Fɛɛ Kanan mʋ nɩɩ Mɛ‐Sɛ gyɛ nkpa kumu mʋ, kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ ɔ sa mɛ, Mu‐Bii mʋ ɔkpa fɛɛ n baa n gyɛ nkpa kumu.

eng: He gives the Son authority to judge, because he is the Human One.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ sa mɛ ɔlʋn yɛ n talɛ daadimaadi kʋmaa, lɩɩ fɛɛ mɛ ɩ gyɛ Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa.

eng: Dont be surprised by this, because the time is coming when all who are in their graves will hear his voice.
local: Fan man yɛgɛ ɩnɩmʋ i kyinkyin fanɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ saŋa kʋ ii biti ɔ ba nɩɩ alewupu mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ nkyan tɔ kpini bee biti ba nu mɛ ɔbʋlɛ.

eng: Those who did good things will come out into the resurrection of life, and those who did wicked things into the resurrection of judgment.
local: kɛ ba lɩɩ bamʋ nkyan tɔ ba. Nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba waa adanbɩrasa mʋ, bee biti ba kyiŋi nyɛ nkpa, kɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba waa alibi mʋ, bee biti ba kyiŋi kɛ ba gyii kʋpwɛ.

eng: I cant do anything by myself. Whatever I hear, I judge, and my judgment is just. I dont seek my own will but the will of the one who sent me.
local: Nɩɩ mɛ gbaa‐gbaa mɛ mɛɛ taalɛ waa sɛhn mɛ kɩbaa sʋ. N talɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ n nu lɩɩ Mɛ‐Sɛ asɛ, nɩɩ n gyii asɩn ɩmʋ ɔkpa sʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ n mee biti n waa mɛ gbaa‐gbaa mɛ kɔkɔlɔ biti, amɔɔ ɔmʋ nɩɩ o sun mɛ mʋ lɛɛ.

eng: If I testify about myself, my testimony isnt true.
local: Yesu kyaga sʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, Nɩɩ n gyii mɛ gbaa‐gbaa mɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ asɩn kesintin mʋ, ɩ mɛɛ yɛ ɩ baa i budu.

eng: There is someone else who testifies about me, and I know his testimony about me is true.
local: Amaa ɔkʋ bʋ tɔ nɩɩ ee gyii mɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ asɩn, mʋ ɩ gyɛ mɛ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ, nɩɩ n nyi fɛɛ mɛ n‐yɩɩ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ee gyii mʋ ɩ gyɛ kesintin.

eng: A certain man, Lazarus, was ill. He was from Bethany, the village of Mary and her sister Martha. .
local: Ɔnyɩn kʋ ɩ kyɩna ɔ bʋ tɔ, nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Lasarusi, nɩɩ ɔ dɛ ɛɛ lɔɔ. Mʋ pɩɩkyɩɩ ana ɩ gyɛ Mɛɛrɩ yɛ Mata, nɩɩ bamʋ kpini ba lɩɩ Betani kadɛ tɔ.

eng: You sent a delegation to John, and he testified to the truth.
local: Nɩɩ fanɛ gbaa‐gbaa fan sun kpee Gyɔn asɛ, nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ kesintin mʋ tɔwɛ fanɛ lɩɩ mɛ sʋ.

eng: This was the Mary who anointed the Lord with fragrant oil and wiped his feet with her hair. Her brother Lazarus was ill.
local: Mɛɛrɩ nɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔkyɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ kyɩna ɔ ba kyɛɛgɛ tulale wʋrɩgɛ Yesu ayaa sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ inyini bʋnswɩɩsa mʋ kpayɩ‐kpayɩ ɩmʋ mʋ. Mʋ pɩɩnyɩn ɩ gyɛ Lasarusi mʋ nɩɩ ɔ dɛ ɛɛ lɔ mʋ.

eng: Although I dont accept human testimony, I say these things so that you can be saved.
local: Man gyɛ fɛɛ n biti nyɩmɩsa daadimaadi o gyii mɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ kesintin nɩn, amaa n tɔwɛ asɩn nɩmʋ lɩɩ Gyɔn sʋ nɩn, kɛ fan nu kasɛ kɛ fan nyɛ kamɔlɩgɛ.

eng: So the sisters sent word to Jesus, saying, Lord, the one whom you love is ill.
local: Nɩɩ mʋ pɩɩkyɩɩ ana ba sun Yesu asɛ naa tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, fʋ kyɛmɩnɛ danbɩrasa mʋ nɩɩ fʋ sʋ kebiti sa mʋ mʋ, dɛ ɛɛ lɔ.

eng: John was a burning and shining lamp, and, at least for a while, you were willing to celebrate in his light.
local: Gyɔn kyɩna o du nɩn fɛɛ ɩŋmaŋɛsa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ kyaŋɛ nɩɩ kɩ kɩɩ tɔ, nɩɩ fan gyii fanɛ ansi gbɛrɛɛ ɩmʋ tɔ.

eng: When he heard this, Jesus said, This illness isnt fatal. Its for the glory of God so that Gods Son can be glorified through it.
local: Yesu maa nu ɩnɩmʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Kʋlɔ kɩnɩmʋ man gyɛ lewu kʋlɔ. Ii biti ɩ bɩya Ɩbwaarɛ bʋnyaa nɩn, kɛ mɛ, Mu‐Bii mʋ n nyɛ bʋnyaa lɩɩ ɩmʋ sʋ.

eng: I have a witness greater than Johns testimony. The Father has given me works to do so that I might complete them. These works I do testify about me that the Father sent me.
local: Amaa n sʋ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ii gyii mɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ kesintin, nɩɩ i budu ɩ kyɔ Gyɔn lɛɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ kusun mʋ nɩɩ mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ taa sa mɛ nɩɩ n sun falɛ mʋ, ɩɩ lɛɛ kaapʋ kesintin fɛɛ mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ i sun mɛ.

eng: Jesus loved Martha, her sister, and Lazarus.
local: Yesu sʋ kebiti sa Mata maa Mɛɛrɩ yɛ bamʋ pɩɩnyɩn Lasarusi.

eng: And the Father who sent me testifies about me. You have never even heard his voice or seen his form.
local: Nɩɩ mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ nɩɩ o sun mɛ mʋ gbaa gyii mɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ kesintin. Nɩɩ fan man pɩɩta nu mʋ ɔbʋlɛ abɛɛ fan wu mʋ yɛ ansi.

eng: When he heard that Lazarus was ill, he stayed where he was. After two days.
local: Amaa maa nu fɛɛ Lasarusi ɩɩ lɔ mʋ, o bwii kyɩna katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ mʋ nkɛ nnyɔ tii sʋ.

eng: and you dont have his word dwelling with you because you dont believe the one whom he has sent.
local: nɩɩ mʋ asɩn mʋ a man bʋ fanɛ nkɔlɔ tɔ, lɩɩ fɛɛ fan man kɔɔlɛ mɛ, ɔmʋ nɩɩ o sun mʋ gyii.

eng: he said to his disciples, Lets return to Judea again.
local: Ɩmʋ kamaa mʋ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ yɛ, Fan yɛgɛ a bwii kpee Gyudeya ɔsʋwʋlɛ.

eng: Examine the scriptures, since you think that in them you have eternal life. They also testify about me.
local: Fan payɩ ansi bɩɩla Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ fɛn fɛ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ kanan ɔkpa maŋa sʋ nɩɩ fen biti fan nyɛ nkpa kakpaa, yɛgɛ kanɩn Asɩnkyan yɩlasa maŋa ii gyii mɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ kesintin.

eng: The disciples replied, Rabbi, the Jewish opposition wants to stone you, but you want to go back.
local: Nɩɩ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ. Ɔkaapʋpʋ, nkɛ nnyɔ nɩmʋ tɔ nɩɩ Gyiwu abi bee biti ba tʋʋ fʋ abuu mɔɔ, mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ fɩɩ bɩla fii kpee tʋtɔ.

eng: yet you dont want to come to me so that you can have life.
local: Amaa fan kina mɛ asɛ kɩba, kɛ fan ba nyɛ nkpa.

eng: Jesus answered, Arent there twelve hours in the day? Whoever walks in the day doesnt stumble because they see the light of the world.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Man gyɛ kerefi kudu anyɔ ɩ bʋ kayɩkɛ tɔ? Nyɩmɩsa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ naa kayɩkɛ tɔ mʋ mee siti, lɩɩ fɛɛ kyɔwɛ kɩɩ tɔ ɛɛ sa mʋ katɩn kʋmaa.

eng: I dont accept praise from people.
local: Ayɩn‐bi lɩɩ daadimaadi kʋ asɛ man tiri mɛ.

eng: But whoever walks in the night does stumble because the light isnt in them.
local: Amaa nɩɩ ɔ natɛ kagyanbwɛ bɩrɛ ee siti, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ man sʋ ɩŋmaŋɛsa.

eng: Six days before Passover, Jesus came to Bethany, home of Lazarus, whom Jesus had raised from the dead.
local: Ɩ maa san fɛɛ nkɛ nsiye kɛ ba gyii Kesu kɔnɔ Kanyaŋɛ nbunɔ lala mʋ, Yesu kpee Betani kadɛ tɔ. Tʋtɔ ɩ gyɛ Lasarusi mʋ nɩɩ Yesu daa kyiŋi lɩɩ lewu tɔ mʋ ayɛ.

eng: but I know you, that you dont have Gods love in you.
local: Amaa n nyi fanɛ. N nyi fan maa man sʋ kebiti sa Ɩbwaarɛ fanɛ nkɔlɔ tɔ.

eng: He continued, Our friend Lazarus is sleeping, but I am going in order to wake him up.
local: Maa tɔwɛ ɩnɩmʋ kamaa mʋ, ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Anɛ kyɛmɩnɛ Lasarusi dɛ nsɩɩrɛ nɩn, amaa n biti n kpee tʋtɔ n naa kyiŋi mʋ.

eng: Lazarus and his sisters hosted a dinner for him. Martha served and Lazarus was among those who joined him at the table.
local: Nɩɩ ba waa agyitɔ kɩtaa Yesu kɩfɔ. Mata ɩ waa agyitɔ maŋa, Lasarusi gbaa tii asa mʋ nɩɩ ba maa Yesu bee gyii mʋ sʋ.

eng: The disciples said, Lord, if hes sleeping, he will get well.
local: Nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, nɩɩ ɔ kan dɛ nsɩɩrɛ bɩrɛ, ee biti ɔ nyɛ alanfɩya.

eng: I have come in my Fathers name, and you dont receive me. If others come in their own name, you receive them.
local: N ba mɛ‐Sɛ kɩtɩɩ tɔ, nɩɩ fan man kɔɔlɛ mɛ, amaa nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan ba mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mʋ kɩtɩɩ tɔ mʋ, fan biti fan kɔɔlɛ mʋ.

eng: Then Mary took an extraordinary amount, almost three-quarters of a pound, of very expensive perfume made of pure nard. She anointed Jesus feet with it, then wiped his feet dry with her hair. The house was filled with the aroma of the perfume.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Mɛɛrɩ taa tuulaale liifensa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ sʋ ɔgyalɩn pʋrʋntʋwa bɔla ba, nɩɩ nadi nɩɩ ba taa waa ɩmʋ. Mɛɛrɩ taa ba kyɛɛgɛ wʋrɩgɛ Yesu ayaa sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ inyini bʋnswɩɩsa kpayɩ‐kpayɩ ɩmʋ. Nɩɩ tuulaale maŋa ofen kɔɔlɛ kɩkpaara mʋ sʋ kpini femm.

eng: They thought Jesus meant that Lazarus was in a deep sleep, but Jesus had spoken about Lazarus death.
local: Kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Yesu ɩɩ kaapʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ Lasarusi wu, amaa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ bɩrɛ be nyi nɩn fɛɛ nsɩɩrɛ gbaa‐gbaa asɩn nɩɩ ɔ dɩyɛ.

eng: How can you believe when you receive praise from each other but dont seek the praise that comes from the only God?.
local: Fan maa biti ayɩn‐bi lɩɩ abaa asɛ falɛ, yɛgɛ fɛn mɛɛ kpan fanɛ n‐yɩɩ fan nyɛ ayɩn‐bi lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ nkʋn asɛ mʋ, nɛnɛ nɩɩ fen biti fan waa kɔɔlɛ gyii?.

eng: Judas Iscariot, one of his disciples , complained.
local: Amaa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Gyudasi Isikariyoti bʋ tʋtɔ. Mʋ ɩɩ ba ɔ ba lɛɛ Yesu sa mʋ. Maa wu Mɛɛrɩ maa waa kanɩn mʋ, ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ.

eng: Jesus told them plainly, Lazarus has died.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔ tɔwɛ tigi bamʋ yɛ, Lasarusi wu.

eng: Dont think that I will accuse you before the Father. Your accuser is Moses, the one in whom your hope rests.
local: Fan man fɛ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ n biti n pwɩɩ fanɛ Mɛ‐Sɛ mu ansi tɔ. Fanɛ naana Mosesi mʋ nɩɩ fan taa fanɛ tamaa tʋʋ mʋ sʋ mʋ, mʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ pwɩɩ fanɛ.

eng: This perfume was worth a years wages! Why wasnt it sold and the money given to the poor? .
local: Mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ ba man taa tuulaale nɩmʋ fɛ, kɛ ba nyɛ afulee fɛɛ dinari asa, abɛɛ ɩbwaayɩ kudu kusun osunpu kʋkɔka, kɛ ba taa afulee mʋ kɛɛ atiripu.

eng: For your sakes, Im glad I wasnt there so that you can believe. Lets go to him.
local: Amaa lɩɩ fanɛ sʋ mʋ, mi ansi i gyii fɛɛ n maa man bʋ tʋtɔ nɩɩ o wu. Ɩnɩmʋ ii biti ɩ yɛgɛ kɛ fan nyɛ kɔɔlɛ mɛ gyii. Kanɩn sʋ fan yɛgɛ a kpee mʋ asɛ.

eng: If you believed Moses, you would believe me, because Moses wrote about me.
local: Nɩɩ fan kan kɔɔlɛ Mosesi gyii mʋ, naafɔɔ fen biti fan kɔɔlɛ ngbaa mɛ gyii, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ kyʋrɔɔ mɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ asɩn.

eng: He said this not because he cared about the poor but because he was a thief. He carried the money bag and would take what was in it.
local: Man gyɛ atiripu mʋ asɩn i tiri mʋ sʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ kanɩn, amaa ɔ gyɛ oyu nɩn. Nɩɩ mʋ ɩɩ kɩtaa bamʋ afulee ɔwaakpa mʋ, yɛgɛ ee yuuri afulee mʋ ɩkʋ ee gyii.

eng: Then Thomas said to the other disciples, Let us go too so that we may die with Jesus.
local: Nɩɩ Tomasi mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ Kabwaara‐bii mʋ, tɔwɛ abɩɩlapʋ asansa mʋ yɛ, Fan yɛgɛ anɛ gbaa anɛ a kpee kɛ anaa mʋ a naa wu.

eng: If you dont believe the writings of Moses, how will you believe my words.
local: Amaa nɩɩ fan man kan kɔɔlɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kyʋrɔɔ lɩɩ mɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ mʋ gyii mʋ, nɛnɛ nɩɩ fen biti fan waa kɔɔlɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ falɛ gyii.

eng: Then Jesus said, Leave her alone. This perfume was to be used in preparation for my burial, and this is how she has used it.
local: Amaa Yesu tɔwɛ yɛ, Fan yɛgɛ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ! Ɔ waa ɩmʋ yɩla mi kepula akɛ nɩn.

eng: When Jesus arrived, he found that Lazarus had already been in the tomb for four days.
local: Yesu maa fʋʋ Betani kadɛ tɔ mʋ, o nu yɛ ba tee pula Lasarusi ɩmʋ nkɛ nna.

eng: You will always have the poor among you, but you wont always have me.
local: Saŋa kʋmaa fanaa atiripu anɩmʋ ii biti fan baa fan bʋ tɔ, amaa mɛɛ yɛ n baa n bʋ fanɛ asɛ saŋa kʋmaa.

eng: Bethany was a little less than two miles from Jerusalem.
local: Lɩɩ Betani kpee Gyerusalem man bʋ kata, ɩ waa fɛɛ ɩmaalɩ ɩnyɔ.

eng: Many Jews learned that he was there. They came not only because of Jesus but also to see Lazarus, whom he had raised from the dead.
local: Nɩɩ Gyiwu abi adamantɛ ba maa nu fɛɛ Yesu bʋ Betani kadɛ tɔ mʋ, ba kpee mʋ asɛ tʋtɔ. Man gyɛ Yesu nkʋn sʋ nɩɩ ba kpee tʋtɔ, amaa ba kpee ba naa kɩɩ Lasarusi mʋ nɩɩ Yesu kyiŋi mʋ lɩɩ lewu tɔ mʋ gbaa.

eng: Many Jews had come to comfort Martha and Mary after their brothers death.
local: nɩɩ Gyiwu abi mʋ gaalaagaa ba ba Mata maa Mɛɛrɩ asɛ, ba ba tɔwɛ yuula bamʋ nkɔlɔ lɩɩ bamʋ pɩɩnyɩn kelewu sʋ.

eng: The chief priests decided that they would kill Lazarus too.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu bɩlɩsa ba ŋmina ba mɔɔ Lasarusi gbaa.

eng: When Martha heard that Jesus was coming, she went to meet him, while Mary remained in the house.
local: Mata maa nu fɛɛ Yesu ɩɩ ba mʋ, ɔ lɩɩ naa gyaŋa mʋ ɔkpa tɔ, amaa Mɛɛrɩ bɩrɛ o sii kɩkpaara sʋ.

eng: It was because of Lazarus that many of the Jews had deserted them and come to believe in Jesus.
local: lɩɩ fɛɛ taa lɩɩ Lasarusi kelewu kyiŋi mʋ sʋ mʋ, Gyiwu abi mʋ gaalaagaa bee kina bamʋ abɩlɩsa mʋ, nɩɩ bee kpee bɛɛ kɔɔlɛ Yesu bee gyii.

eng: Martha said to Jesus, Lord, if you had been here, my brother wouldnt have died.
local: Mata maa gyaŋa mʋ mʋ, ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, fʋ daa fʋ bʋ nfɩɩ nɩn, naafɔɔ mɛ pɩɩnyɩn man dɩn wu.

eng: The next day the great crowd that had come for the festival heard that Jesus was coming to Jerusalem.
local: Ɩmʋ lɩɩkaakɛ mʋ, kʋbʋ damantɛ mʋ nɩɩ ba ba bamʋ Kesu kɔnɔ Kanyaŋɛ nbunɔ kensi‐gyii mʋ tɔ mʋ, ba nu fɛɛ Yesu kɩtaa ɔkpa ɛɛ ba Gyerusalem.

eng: Even now I know that whatever you ask God, God will give you.
local: Amaa halɩɩ nbɩɩnbɩɩ gbaa n nyi yɛ, kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fʋ taasɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ, ee biti ɔ waa sa fʋ.

eng: They took palm branches and went out to meet him. They shouted, Hosanna! Blessings on the one who comes in the name of the Lord! Blessings on the king of Israel.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ba taa abɩlafɛ lɩɩ kyʋn, kɛ ba naa gyaŋa mʋ, yɛgɛ bɛɛ yɩn mʋ bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Hosana! Kusee ɩ gyɛ sa ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ naa Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kɩtɩɩ tɔ ɛɛ ba! Kusee ɩ gyɛ sa Ɩsɩrayɩ Owura.

eng: Jesus told her, Your brother will rise again.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Fʋ pɩɩnyɩn ii biti o bwii kyiŋi ba nkpa tɔ.

eng: Jesus found a young donkey and sat on it, just as it is written.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu wu kuruma kadanba kʋ nɩɩ o dii mʋ. Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ ɩ ba tɔ kesintin. Ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ.

eng: Martha replied, I know that he will rise in the resurrection on the last day.
local: Mata lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, N nyi yɛ ee biti o kyiŋi ba nkpa tɔ kakɛ laalaalʋwɛ mʋ, saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti o kyiŋi asa ba nkpa tɔ mʋ.

eng: Dont be afraid, Daughter Zion. Look! Your king is coming , sitting on a donkeys colt .
local: O Siyon abi, fan man waa kufuu, fan kɩɩ, fanɛ owura ɩɩ ba, ɔ tɛ kuruma kadanba sʋ ɛɛ ba.

eng: Jesus said to her, I am the resurrection and the life. Whoever believes in me will live, even though they die.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Mɛ ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ n kyiŋi asa n lɩɩ lewu tɔ, nɩɩ ɛɛ sa bamʋ nkpa mʋ. Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ mɛ gyii mʋ, halɩɩ o wu gbaa ee biti ɔ nyɛ nkpa.

eng: His disciples didnt understand these things at first. After he was glorified, they remembered that these things had been written about him and that they had done these things to him.
local: Kanɩn saŋa maŋa mʋ, mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba man nu ɩnɩmʋ kpini kasɛ. Amɔɔ kamaa tɔ, saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sa mʋ bʋnyaa mʋ, pɔyɩ nɩɩ ba lan nyiŋi fɛɛ ba kyʋrɔɔ asɩn nɩmʋ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɩ ba tɔ kesintin.

eng: Everyone who lives and believes in me will never die. Do you believe this.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛ nkpa tɔ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ mɛ gyii mʋ, ɔ mɛɛ yɛ o wu kakɛ akʋ. Fʋ kɔɔlɛ ɩnɩmʋ gyii.

eng: The crowd who had been with him when he called Lazarus out of the tomb and raised him from the dead were testifying about him.
local: Nɩɩ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Yesu asɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ Lasarusi lɩɩ kakyan tɔ, nɩɩ o kyiŋi mʋ lɩɩ lewu tɔ mʋ, ɩɩ kyaga sʋ tɔwɛ asɩn nɩmʋ katɩn kʋmaa.

eng: She replied, Yes, Lord, I believe that you are the Christ, Gods Son, the one who is coming into the world.
local: Nɩɩ Mata kyula yɛ, Ɛɛhn! Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ n kɔɔlɛ gyii yɛ fʋ ɩ gyɛ Krisito, Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ Mu‐Bii mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ ee biti o sun mʋ ba dulinyaa tɔ mʋ.

eng: Thats why the crowd came to meet him, because they had heard about this miraculous sign that he had done.
local: Kanɩn sʋ nɩɩ asa damantɛ ba lɩɩ ba naa gyaŋa Yesu, lɩɩ fɛɛ ba maa nu asɩn kyinkyinsa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa mʋ sʋ.

eng: After she said this, she went and spoke privately to her sister Mary, The teacher is here and hes calling for you.
local: Maa tɔwɛ ɩnɩmʋ lʋwɛ mʋ, o bwii kpee kɩkpaara sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ naa tɩɩ mu‐supu Mɛɛrɩ lɩɩ ɩkaa sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɔkaapʋpʋ mʋ bʋ nfɩɩ, nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ fʋ sʋ.

eng: Therefore, the Pharisees said to each other, See! Youve accomplished nothing! Look! The whole world is following him.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ Farasii abi ba tɔwɛ abaa yɛ, Fan man wu, a mɛɛ bɩla taalɛ waa sɛhn. Fan kɩɩ, dulinyaa tɔ asa kpini ba biliŋi kpee ba buu mʋ.

eng: When Mary heard this, she got up quickly and went to Jesus.
local: Mɛɛrɩ maa nu kanɩn mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kʋsʋ bilen ee kpee mʋ asɛ.

eng: Some Greeks were among those who had come up to worship at the festival.
local: Nɩɩ Giriki abi mʋ akʋ gbaa ba tii bamʋ nɩɩ ba kpee Gyerusalem mʋ sʋ. Ba naa sun Ɩbwaarɛ Kesu kɔnɔ Kanyaŋɛ nbunɔ kensi‐gyii mʋ tɔ.

eng: He hadnt entered the village but was still in the place where Martha had met him.
local: Yesu man pɩɩta loo kadɛ mʋ tɔ, amaa ɔ san ɔ bʋ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ Mata naa gyaŋa mʋ mʋ.

eng: They came to Philip, who was from Bethsaida in Galilee, and made a request: Sir, we want to see Jesus.
local: Kanɩn asa maŋa ba ba Filipu mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ Betisayida kadɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ Galile ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ mʋ asɛ, ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɔbɩlɩsa, an biti fɛɛ a wu Yesu.

eng: When the Jews who were comforting Mary in the house saw her get up quickly and leave, they followed her. They assumed she was going to mourn at the tomb.
local: Gyiwu abi mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Mɛɛrɩ asɛ kɩkpaara sʋ bɛɛ tɔwɛ bee yuula mʋ kɔkɔlɔ mʋ, ba maa wu kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kʋsʋ bilen lɩɩ kewu mʋ, ba lɩɩ buu mʋ, be nyi fɛɛ ee kpee kakyan mʋ asɛ ɔ naa kyɩna saawʋ.

eng: Philip told Andrew, and Andrew and Philip told Jesus.
local: Nɩɩ Filipu naa tɔwɛ Andiruwu, nɩɩ bamʋ anyɔ ba kpee naa tɔwɛ Yesu.

eng: When Mary arrived where Jesus was and saw him, she fell at his feet and said, Lord, if you had been here, my brother wouldnt have died.
local: Mɛɛrɩ maa fʋʋ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ Yesu bʋ, nɩɩ o wu mʋ mʋ, o muŋa mʋ ayaa tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ fʋ daa fʋ bʋ nfɩɩ nɩn, naafɔɔ mɛ pɩɩnyɩn man dɩn wu.

eng: Jesus replied, The time has come for the Human One to be glorified.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Nbɩɩnbɩɩ, saŋa mʋ fʋʋ fɛɛ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa bʋnyaa lala mʋ ɩ lɛɛ ifuli.

eng: When Jesus saw her crying and the Jews who had come with her crying also, he was deeply disturbed and troubled.
local: Yesu maa wu kanan mʋ nɩɩ Mɛɛrɩ yɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ nɩɩ ba buu mʋ mʋ bee su mʋ, ɩ kɔ mʋ nɩɩ i duŋi mʋ ɔbʋlɛ tɔ.

eng: I assure you that unless a grain of wheat falls into the earth and dies, it can only be a single seed. But if it dies, it bears much fruit.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin, kuyu kibi, nɩɩ kɩ man kan tɩyɛ ɩsɛ tɔ gyɔɔ mʋ, kɩ san kɩ gyɛ kuyubi kʋlʋn. Amaa nɩɩ kɩ kan tɩyɛ ɩsɛ tɔ gyɔɔ, nɩɩ kɩ kɔyɩ bɩrɛ, kɩɩ sɔyɩ abi damantɛ.

eng: He asked, Where have you laid him? They replied, Lord, come and see.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fɩnɛ nɩɩ fan pula mʋ? Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, ba kɩɩ.

eng: Those who love their lives will lose them, and those who hate their lives in this world will keep them forever.
local: Ɔsa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ kebiti sa mʋ nkpa gaa mʋ, ee biti ɔ pan kɩmʋ. Amaa ɔsa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ taa mʋ nkpa ɛɛ tɩɩ sɛhn dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ mʋ, ee biti ɔ lɔŋɔ mʋ n‐yɩɩ yɩla sa nkpa kakpaa.

eng: Jesus began to cry.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu su.

eng: Whoever serves me must follow me. Wherever I am, there my servant will also be. My Father will honor whoever serves me.
local: Ɔsa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ee sun mɛ mʋ, dagaa maa buu mɛ. Nɩɩ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ n bʋ mʋ, tʋtɔ nɩɩ mi osunpu mʋ gbaa ii biti ɔ baa ɔ bʋ. Nɩɩ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ee sun mɛ mʋ, Mɛ‐Sɛ ii biti ɔ sa mʋ bɛɛrɛɛ.

eng: The Jews said, See how much he loved him.
local: Nɩɩ Gyiwu abi mʋ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, Fan kɩɩ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ kebiti sa mʋ.

eng: Now I am deeply troubled. What should I say? Father, save me from this time? No, for this is the reason I have come to this time.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ yɛ, Nbɩɩnbɩɩ, mɛ kɔkɔlɔ man dɛ mɛ. N bɩɩ kʋlɛ Mɛ‐Sɛ kɛ n tɔwɛ yɛ, Nsɛ, mɔlɩgɛ mɛ lɩɩ awʋrʋfɔ mʋ nɩɩ ii biti ɩɩ ba mɛ sʋ falɛ abɛɛ? Ayee, falɛ awʋrʋfɔ anɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ n ba.

eng: But some of them said, He healed the eyes of the man born blind. Couldnt he have kept Lazarus from dying.
local: Amaa bamʋ tɔ akʋ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, Man gyɛ Yesu nɩmʋ i bugi agyaatanapʋ ansi mʋ? Mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ ɔ man taalɛ kɔɔlɛ Lasarusi kɛ ɔ man wu.

eng: Father, glorify your name! Then a voice came from heaven, I have glorified it, and I will glorify it again.
local: Nsɛ, bʋnyaa fʋ kɩtɩɩ! Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔbʋlɛ kʋ lɩɩ sʋsʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, N wʋla bʋnyaa kɩmʋ nɩɩ n biti n bwii bʋnyaa kɩmʋ.

eng: Jesus was deeply disturbed again when he came to the tomb. It was a cave, and a stone covered the entrance.
local: Yesu ɔbʋlɛ tɔ bwii duŋi mʋ, nɩɩ o kpee kakyan mʋ asɛ. Kɩ gyɛ kɩfʋlɛbɔ nɩn, nɩɩ ba taa kubuu tii kɩmʋ kɔnɔ.

eng: The crowd standing there heard and said, Its thunder. Others said, An angel spoke to him.
local: Sakyɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ tʋtɔ mʋ ba maa nu ɔbʋlɛ mʋ, ba tɔwɛ yɛ, Bʋlayɩ pʋntɛ! Nɩɩ akʋ mɔɔ ba yɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ ɩ tɔwɛ mʋ asɛ.

eng: Jesus said, Remove the stone. Martha, the sister of the dead man, said, Lord, the smell will be awful! Hes been dead four days.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan tigi kubuu mʋ lɩɩ tʋtɔ. Nɩɩ Mata, olewupu mʋ pɩɩkyɩɩ mʋ tɔwɛ yɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, ee biti ɔ ba ɛɛ lɩɩsɔn, ndaga nkɛ nna nɩmʋ a maa pula mʋ.

eng: Jesus replied, This voice wasnt for my benefit but for yours.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Ɩ man gyɛ mɛ sʋ nɩɩ ɔbʋlɛ nɩmʋ tɔwɛ, amaa lɩɩ fanɛ sʋ.

eng: Jesus replied, Didnt I tell you that if you believe, you will see Gods glory.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ Mata yɛ, N man tɔwɛ fʋ yɛ nɩɩ fʋ kan kɔɔlɛ gyii mʋ, fii biti fu wu Ɩbwaarɛ bʋnyaa.

eng: Now is the time for judgment of this world. Now this worlds ruler will be thrown out.
local: Nbɩɩnbɩɩ ɩ gyɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti o gyii dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ asa asɩn. Nbɩɩnbɩɩ ɩ gyɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ kʋsaa dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ owura lɩɩ mʋ kagya sʋ. Mʋ ɩ gyɛ Sɩtaanɛ mʋ.

eng: So they removed the stone. Jesus looked up and said, Father, thank you for hearing me.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ba tigi kubuu mʋ. Nɩɩ Yesu kɩɩ sʋsʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Nsɛ, n fwaala fʋ fɛɛ fʋ maa nu mɛ kɩkʋlɛ.

eng: When I am lifted up from the earth, I will draw everyone to me. .
local: Amaa nɩɩ ba kan kʋsaa mɛ sʋsʋ lɩɩ kasɛ sʋ bɩrɛ, n biti n bɩɩtɛ asa kpini ba mɛ asɛ.

eng: I know you always hear me. I say this for the benefit of the crowd standing here so that they will believe that you sent me.
local: N nyi saŋa kʋmaa fii nu mɛ kɩkʋlɛ, amaa asa mʋ nɩɩ ba yɩlɛ nfɩɩ mʋ sʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ kanɩn, kɛ ba nyɛ kɔɔlɛ gyii fɛɛ fu i sun mɛ.

eng: He said this to show how he was going to die.
local: Ɔ tɔwɛ ɩnɩmʋ nɩn kaapʋ tɔ lɩɩ kelewu mʋ nɩɩ ee biti o wu.

eng: Having said this, Jesus shouted with a loud voice, Lazarus, come out.
local: Maa tɔwɛ kanɩn lʋwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ o kuusi kenken yɛ, Lasarusi, lɩɩ ba.

eng: The crowd responded, We have heard from the Law that the Christ remains forever. How can you say that the Human One must be lifted up? Who is this Human One.
local: Nɩɩ sakyɔ mʋ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, An nu lɩɩ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ fɛɛ, Krisito, Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ mʋ, ii biti ɔ kyɩna nkpa tɔ kakpaa. Mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ fɩɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɩ dagaa ba maa kʋsaa Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ sʋsʋ? Anɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ.

eng: The dead man came out, his feet bound and his hands tied, and his face covered with a cloth. Jesus said to them, Untie him and let him go.
local: Nɩɩ olewupu mʋ lɩɩ ba, yɛgɛ taafuli mili mʋ abaa yɛ ayaa sʋ, yɛgɛ kikyibi fufuli ŋmina mu ansi sʋ. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan saŋɛ mʋ kɛ ɔ natɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, The light is with you for only a little while. Walk while you have the light so that darkness doesnt overtake you. Those who walk in the darkness dont know where they are going.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ lɩɩ mʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋ yɛ, Ɩŋmaŋɛsa mʋ ɩ man bɩla yɛ ɩ kyɛɛrɛɛ fanɛ asɛ. Ɩmʋ sʋ fan kyaga sʋ natɛ ɩŋmaŋɛsa mʋ tɔ, kɛ kitentenbiri kɩ man nyɛ loo fanɛ tɔ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ naa kitentenbiri tɔ mʋ man nyi katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ee kpee.

eng: Therefore, many of the Jews who came with Mary and saw what Jesus did believed in him.
local: Gyiwu abi adamantɛ mʋ nɩɩ ba ba Mɛɛrɩ afwaala, nɩɩ ba wu kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Yesu waa mʋ, ba kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii.

eng: As long as you have the light, believe in the light so that you might become people whose lives are determined by the light. After Jesus said these things, he went away and hid from them.
local: Fan maa san fan sʋ ɩŋmaŋɛsa fanɛ asɛ falɛ bɩrɛ, fan kɔɔlɛ ɩmʋ gyii, kɛ fan baa fan gyɛ ɩŋmaŋɛsa mʋ asa. Yesu maa tɔwɛ ɩnɩmʋ kamaa tɔ mʋ, ɔ lɩɩ tʋtɔ naa baala bamʋ sʋ mʋ nkʋn.

eng: But some of them went to the Pharisees and told them what Jesus had done.
local: Amaa bamʋ akʋ ba kpee Farasii abi asɛ naa tɔwɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Yesu waa.

eng: Jesus had done many miraculous signs before the people, but they didnt believe in him.
local: Halɩɩ asɩn kyinkyinsa mʋ nɩɩ Yesu waa asa ansi tɔ kpini falɛ gbaa mʋ, ba san ba mɛɛ kɔɔlɛ mʋ bee gyii.

eng: Then the chief priests and Pharisees called together the council and said, What are we going to do? This man is doing many miraculous signs!.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ aseepu bɩlɩsa yɛ Farasii abi ba tɩɩ kadɛ mʋ tɔ Gyiwu abɩlɩsa amu‐amu ngyaŋɛ. Nɩɩ ba taasɛ abaa yɛ, Mɩnɛ nɩɩ an biti a waa. Fan kɩɩ asɩnkaala yɛ asɩn kyinkyinsa damantɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ ɩɩ waa falɛ.

eng: This was to fulfill the word of the prophet Isaiah: Lord, who has believed through our message? To whom is the arm of the Lord fully revealed?.
local: Asɩn nɩmʋ a waa nɩn, kɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Isaya wʋla tɔwɛ mʋ, ɩ ba tɔ kesintin. Falɛ nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, anɩmʋ ɩ kɔɔlɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ an tɔwɛ mʋ gyii? Nɩɩ asa mɔɔ nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ lɛɛ mʋ ɔlʋn mʋ kaapʋ.

eng: If we let him go on like this, everyone will believe in him. Then the Romans will come and take away both our temple and our people.
local: An kan yɛgɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kyaga sʋ ɛɛ waa kanɩn mʋ, ɔsa kʋmaa nɩmʋ ii biti ɔ kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii, kɛ ɩ yɛgɛ Roma abi ba ba nyita anɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ nɩmʋ kɛ ba mɔɔ anɛ asa.

eng: Isaiah explains why they couldnt believe.
local: Kpalɩ ɩnɩmʋ sʋ mʋ, ba man taalɛ kɔɔlɛ Yesu gyii, lɩɩ fɛɛ Isaya bɩla tɔwɛ mʋ ɔwʋlɛ tɔ katɩn kʋ yɛ.

eng: One of them, Caiaphas, who was high priest that year, told them, You dont know anything!.
local: Nɩɩ bamʋ tɔ ɔkʋ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Kayifasi nɩɩ ɔ kyɩna ɔ gyɛ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ kesu maŋa tɔ mʋ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan man nyi sɛhn!.

eng: He made their eyes blind and closed their minds so that they might not see with their eyes, understand with their minds, and turn their lives around— and I would heal them.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ waa bamʋ agyaatanapʋ, kɛ bamʋ ansi ɩ man wu. Ɔ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ba waa kʋsʋ tɔ ɔlʋn, kɛ bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ tɔ ii mee nu asɩn kasɛ, kɛ ba biliŋi ba mɛ asɛ, kɛ n kyɛ bamʋ.

eng: You dont see that it is better for you that one man die for the people rather than the whole nation be destroyed.
local: Fan man nyi yɛ, ɩ bɔ sa fanɛ fɛɛ nyɩmɩsa kʋlʋn o wu sa asa kpini, ɩ kyɔ fɛɛ anɛ asa kpini ba maa muuri.

eng: Isaiah said these things because he saw Jesus glory; he spoke about Jesus.
local: Isaya gyankpaa wu Yesu bʋnyaa mʋ, ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ ɩnɩmʋ lɩɩ mʋ sʋ mʋ.

eng: He didnt say this on his own. As high priest that year, he prophesied that Jesus would soon die for the nation.
local: Kesintin tɔ man gyɛ mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mu kebiti tɔ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ falɛ asɩn nɩmʋ. Amaa maa gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ kanɩn kesu maŋa tɔ sʋ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɩla yɛ Yesu ii biti o wu sa Gyiwu abi kpini.

eng: Even so, many leaders believed in him, but they wouldnt acknowledge their faith because they feared that the Pharisees would expel them from the synagogue.
local: Halɩɩ ɩ maa ɩmʋ gbaa mʋ, Gyiwu abɩlɩsa mʋ adamantɛ ba kɔɔlɛ Yesu gyii. Amaa lɩɩ Farasii abi mʋ sʋ mʋ, ba man taalɛ lɛɛ bamʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii mʋ ifuli, lɩɩ fɛɛ ba sʋ kufuu fɛɛ bee biti ba gya bamʋ lɩɩ ɔgyaŋɛkpa mʋ tɔ.

eng: and not only for the nation. Jesus would also die so that Gods children scattered everywhere would be gathered together as one.
local: Nɩɩ man gyɛ Gyiwu abi nkʋn, amaa yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mu‐bii ana mʋ nɩɩ ba yaasɛ tɔ ba bʋ dulinyaa tɔ katɩn kʋmaa nɩmʋ mʋ, kɛ ba ba bʋga waa kʋkʋlʋn.

eng: They believed, but they loved human praise more than Gods glory.
local: Kpalɩ fɛɛ abɩlɩsa mʋ bee biti kɩyɩn lɩɩ nyɩmɩsa asɛ ba kyɔ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ, ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa kanɩn.

eng: From that day on they plotted to kill him.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ lɩɩ kanɩn kakɛ maŋa Gyiwu abɩlɩsa ba ŋmina yɛ ba mɔɔ Yesu.

eng: Jesus shouted, Whoever believes in me doesnt believe in me but in the one who sent me.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ kenken yɛ, Ɔsa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ mɛ gyii mʋ, man gyɛ mɛ nkʋn nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ gyii, amaa ɔmʋ nɩɩ o sun mɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ gyii.

eng: Therefore, Jesus was no longer active in public ministry among the Jewish leaders. Instead, he left Jerusalem and went to a place near the wilderness, to a city called Ephraim, where he stayed with his disciples.
local: Lɩɩ kanɩn sʋ Yesu man bɩla lɛɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ ifuli ɛɛ natɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ tɔ. Amaa ɔ lɩɩ Gyerusalem kpee bɛɛ tɩɩ kɩmʋ yɛ Efarim kadɛ mʋ, nɩɩ kɩ bʋ tiri kiperi sʋ mʋ. Nɩɩ maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba naa kyɩna tʋtɔ.

eng: Whoever sees me sees the one who sent me.
local: Ɔsa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o wu mɛ mʋ, o wu ɔmʋ nɩɩ o sun mɛ mʋ.

eng: It was almost time for the Jewish Passover, and many people went from the countryside up to Jerusalem to purify themselves through ritual washing before the Passover.
local: Gyiwu abi Kesu kɔnɔ Kanyaŋɛ nbunɔ kensi‐gyii saŋa maa tiri tɔ mʋ, asa gaalaagaa ba lɩɩ bamʋ ndɛ sʋ kpee Gyerusalem. Ba gyankpaa nɩn ba naa fwɩɩ bamʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋ ineesi fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba wʋla bɛɛ waa, pɔyɩ kɛ bamʋ Kanyaŋɛ nbunɔ kakɛ mʋ kɩ fʋʋ.

eng: I have come as a light into the world so that everyone who believes in me wont live in darkness.
local: Mɛ ɩ gyɛ ɩŋmaŋɛsa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ ba dulinyaa nɩmʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ mɛ gyii mʋ, ɔ man sii kitentenbiri tɔ.

eng: They were looking for Jesus. As they spoke to each other in the temple, they said, What do you think? He wont come to the festival, will he.
local: Asa mʋ bɛɛ kɩɩ bee biti Yesu. Nɩɩ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba yɩlɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ asɛ mʋ, bɛɛ taasɛ abaa yɛ, Mɩnɛ ɩ gyɛ fanɛ nfɛɛrɛ? Fen nyi fɛɛ ee biti ɔ ba Kanyaŋɛ nbunɔ ngyaŋɛ nɩmʋ tɔ.

eng: If people hear my words and dont keep them, I dont judge them. I didnt come to judge the world but to save it.
local: Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o nu mɛ asɩn tɔwɛsa, nɩɩ ɔ man buu ɩmʋ mʋ, man gyɛ mɛ ii biti n bun mʋ kʋpwɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ n man ba nɩn yɛ n ba bun dulinyaa tɔ asa kʋpwɛ, amaa n ba nɩn kɛ n ba mɔlɩgɛ kɩmʋ asa.

eng: The chief priests and Pharisees had given orders that anyone who knew where he was should report it, so they could arrest him.
local: Ɩmʋ gbaa mʋ Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu yɛ Farasii abi mʋ ba wʋla sa kɔnɔ yɛ, ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o wu katɩn mʋ nɩɩ Yesu bʋ mʋ, ɔ ba tɔwɛ bamʋ kɛ ba nyɛ kɩtaa mʋ.

eng: Whoever rejects me and doesnt receive my words will be judged at the last day by the word I have spoken.
local: Ɔsa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o kina mɛ, nɩɩ ɔ man kɔɔlɛ mɛ asɩn mʋ, wʋla nyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ee biti o bun mʋ kʋpwɛ. Nɩɩ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ mʋ ii biti i bun mʋ kʋpwɛ kakɛ laalaalʋwɛ mʋ.

eng: I dont speak on my own, but the Father who sent me commanded me regarding what I should speak and say.
local: Kpalɩ fɛɛ ɩ man gyɛ mɛ gbaa‐gbaa mɛ ɔlʋn tɔ nɩɩ n sʋ n tɔwɛ, amaa mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ nɩɩ o sun mɛ mʋ, sa mɛ kɔnɔsa lɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ sʋ, nɩɩ ɩmʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ.

eng: I know that his commandment is eternal life. Therefore, whatever I say is just as the Father has said to me.
local: Nɩɩ n nyi fɛɛ mʋ kɔnɔsa mʋ ɩɩ sa nkpa kakpaa. Ɩmʋ sʋ asɩn kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ mʋ, ɩ gyɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Mɛ‐Sɛ tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ n tɔwɛ.

eng: I assure you that whoever doesnt enter into the sheep pen through the gate but climbs over the wall is a thief and an outlaw.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin fɛɛ, ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ bɔla nbʋlʋpʋ kubunlun kebunɔ mʋ tɔ ee loo, nɩɩ ɔ naa bɔla opula pʋpwɛ loo to mʋ, gyɛ oyu yɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ee yuuri ɔlʋn sʋ.

eng: The one who enters through the gate is the shepherd of the sheep.
local: Amaa ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ bɔla kebunɔ ee loo mʋ, mʋ ɩ gyɛ nbʋlʋpʋ mʋ ɔkpapʋ.

eng: The guard at the gate opens the gate for him, and the sheep listen to his voice. He calls his own sheep by name and leads them out.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkpapʋ mʋ kan ba mʋ, kebunɔ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ mʋ ii tigi sa mʋ, nɩɩ ee loo to. Ɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ nbʋlʋpʋ mʋ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ maa mʋ kɩtɩɩ, nɩɩ nbʋlʋpʋ mʋ be nyi mʋ ɔbʋlɛ, nɩɩ ɛɛ lɩɩ gyankpaa bamʋ.

eng: Whenever he has gathered all of his sheep, he goes before them and they follow him, because they know his voice.
local: Ɔkpapʋ mʋ maa tɛɛ lɛɛ bamʋ kpini lɩɩ kubunlun mʋ tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ɛɛ kyʋn gyankpaa bamʋ yɛgɛ bee buu mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ be nyi mʋ ɔbʋlɛ.

eng: They wont follow a stranger but will run away because they dont know the strangers voice.
local: Amaa ba mɛɛ yɛ ba buu ɔfɔ, bee biti ba sɩlɛ yɛgɛ mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ba man nyi mʋ ɔbʋlɛ.

eng: Those who heard Jesus use this analogy didnt understand what he was saying.
local: Yesu bɔ bamʋ kɩŋasan kɩnɩmʋ nɩn, amaa ba man nu kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ tɔwɛ bamʋ mʋ kasɛ.

eng: So Jesus spoke again, I assure you that I am the gate of the sheep.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ Yesu bwii tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin fɛɛ mɛ ɩ gyɛ nbʋlʋpʋ kubunlun kebunɔ mʋ.

eng: All who came before me were thieves and outlaws, but the sheep didnt listen to them.
local: Bamʋ nɩɩ ba gyankpaa mɛ ba mʋ kpini, nɩɩ ba waa bamʋ n‐yɩɩ fɛɛ nbʋlʋpʋ akpapʋ mʋ, ba gyɛ ayu yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bee yuuri ɔlʋn sʋ, kanɩn sʋ nɩɩ nbʋlʋpʋ mʋ ba man nu bamʋ asɛ mʋ.

eng: I am the gate. Whoever enters through me will be saved. They will come in and go out and find pasture.
local: Mɛ ɩ gyɛ kebunɔ mʋ, ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bɔla mɛ sʋ loo mʋ, ii biti ɔ nyɛ kamɔlɩgɛ. Mʋ kɩlɩɩ kpee yɛ mʋ kibwii ba mʋ ɛɛ nyɛ agyitɔ o gyii.

eng: The thief enters only to steal, kill, and destroy. I came so that they could have life—indeed, so that they could live life to the fullest.
local: Oyu bɩrɛ, mʋ lɛɛ ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ o be yuuri, kɛ ɔ mɔɔ, kɛ o nyita. Amaa mɛɛ mɛ n ba nɩn kɛ asa ba nyɛ nkpa, kɛ ba nyɛ ɩmʋ kʋkyɔ‐kʋkyɔ.

eng: I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd lays down his life for the sheep.
local: Mɛ ɩ gyɛ nbʋlʋpʋ ɔkpapʋ danbɩrasa mʋ. Mɛ, nbʋlʋpʋ ɔkpapʋ danbɩrasa mʋ n taa mɛ nkpa sa nbʋlʋpʋ mʋ halɩɩ lewu tɔ.

eng: When the hired hand sees the wolf coming, he leaves the sheep and runs away. Thats because he isnt the shepherd; the sheep arent really his. So the wolf attacks the sheep and scatters them.
local: Paa ogyipu bɩrɛ, ɔ man du fɛɛ nbʋlʋpʋ ɔkpapʋ danbɩrasa mʋ. Nbʋlʋpʋ mʋ ba maa man gyɛ mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mʋ lɛɛ sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kan wu fɛɛ kuntun ɩɩ ba mʋ, ɛɛ sɩlɛ yɛgɛ nbʋlʋpʋ mʋ nɩn, kɛ kuntun mʋ ɔ ba kɩtaa bamʋ ɔkʋ kɛ ɔ yaasɛ asansa.

eng: Hes only a hired hand and the sheep dont matter to him.
local: Kʋtɔ mʋ sʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ sɩlɛ ɛɛ yɛgɛ nbʋlʋpʋ mʋ ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ, ɔ gyɛ paa ogyipu nɩn, bamʋ asɩn ɩ man tiri mʋ.

eng: I am the good shepherd. I know my own sheep and they know me.
local: Mɛ ɩ gyɛ nbʋlʋpʋ ɔkpapʋ danbɩrasa mʋ. N nyi mɛ nbʋlʋpʋ, nɩɩ mɛ nbʋlʋpʋ gbaa be nyi mɛ.

eng: just as the Father knows me and I know the Father. I give up my life for the sheep.
local: Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Mɛ‐Sɛ mu nyi mɛ, nɩɩ ngbaa mɛ n nyi Mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ, ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ n biti n taa mɛ nkpa sa mɛ nbʋlʋpʋ mʋ halɩɩ lewu tɔ.

eng: I have other sheep that dont belong to this sheep pen. I must lead them too. They will listen to my voice and there will be one flock, with one shepherd.
local: N san n sʋ nbʋlʋpʋ akʋ nɩɩ mʋ ba man bʋ nbʋlʋpʋ anɩmʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ ii tiri fɛɛ n naa taa bamʋ gbaa ba. Bee biti ba be nu mɛ ɔbʋlɛ, kɛ bamʋ kpini ba ba waa kʋbʋ kʋlʋn yɛ ɔkpapʋ kʋlʋn.

eng: This is why the Father loves me: I give up my life so that I can take it up again.
local: Kʋtɔ mʋ sʋ nɩɩ Mɛ‐Sɛ ii biti mɛ asɩn ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ, n kyula yɛ n taa mɛ nkpa sa halɩɩ lewu tɔ, kɛ n bɩla bwii nyɛ kɩmʋ.

eng: No one takes it from me, but I give it up because I want to. I have the right to give it up, and I have the right to take it up again. I received this commandment from my Father.
local: Ɔkʋ mɛɛ taalɛ kɔɔlɛ mɛ nkpa lɩɩ mɛ abaa tɔ. Amaa mɛ gbaa‐gbaa mi kebiti tɔ nɩɩ n kyula yɛ n wu. N sʋ ɔkpa fɛɛ n taa mɛ nkpa sa halɩɩ lewu tɔ, kɛ n bɩla bwii nyɛ kɩmʋ. Falɛ kɔnɔsa kanɩmʋ nɩɩ Mɛ‐Sɛ sa mɛ yɛ n waa.

eng: There was another division among the Jews because of Jesus words.
local: Asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ falɛ sʋ mʋ, nbarɩgɛ ɩ bɩla bwii ba Gyiwu abɩlɩsa mʋ tɔ.

eng: Many of them said, He has a demon and has lost his mind. Why listen to him.
local: Asa mʋ tɔ gaalaagaa bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Nbwɩɩ libi ba bʋ mʋ tɔ, ɛɛ lɔ ɩbʋn. Mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ fen nu mʋ asɛ.

eng: Others said, These arent the words of someone who has a demon. Can a demon heal the eyes of people who are blind.
local: Amaa bamʋ akʋ mɔɔ ba yɛ, Ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi ba bʋ mʋ tɔ mʋ, mɛɛ tɔwɛ falɛ ntɔwɛ nɩmʋ. Nɛnɛ nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi ɩ taalɛ bugi gyaatanapʋ ansi.

eng: The time came for the Festival of Dedication in Jerusalem. It was winter.
local: Nɩɩ saŋa fʋʋ fɛɛ Gyiwu abi ba gyii Kesu kɔnɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ nyiŋi saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba bugi Kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ Gyerusalem kadɛ tɔ mʋ. Saŋa maŋa ɩ gyɛ awɔɔ tɔ nɩn.

eng: and Jesus was in the temple, walking in the covered porch named for Solomon.
local: nɩɩ Yesu bʋ Kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ, yɛgɛ ɔ naa katɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ Owura Solomon kasɔrɔga mʋ tɔ.

eng: The Jewish opposition circled around him and asked, How long will you test our patience? If you are the Christ, tell us plainly.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Gyiwu abɩlɩsa mʋ ba yɩlɛ kyaabɔɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Saŋa mɔɔ nɩɩ fii biti fɛɛ a gyoo naa fʋʋ, pɔyɩ kɛ a wu asɩn mʋ tɔ kyɛɛkyɛɛ? Nɩɩ fʋ ɩ kan fʋ gyɛ Krisito, Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ mʋ, dan tiisi tɔwɛ anɛ kesintin.

eng: Jesus answered, I have told you, but you dont believe. The works I do in my Fathers name testify about me.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, N wʋla tɔwɛ fanɛ, amaa fan man kɔɔlɛ mɛ gyii. Asɩn kyinkyinsa mʋ nɩɩ n sʋ n waa Mɛ‐Sɛ kɩtɩɩ tɔ mʋ, ɩmʋ ii gyii mɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ kesintin.

eng: but you dont believe because you dont belong to my sheep.
local: Amaa fan man kɔɔlɛ mɛ gyii, lɩɩ fɛɛ fan maa man gyɛ mɛ nbʋlʋpʋ mʋ sʋ.

eng: My sheep listen to my voice. I know them and they follow me.
local: Mɛ nbʋlʋpʋ bee nyi mɛ ɔbʋlɛ, nɩɩ n nyi bamʋ, nɩɩ bee buu mɛ.

eng: I give them eternal life. They will never die, and no one will snatch them from my hand.
local: N sa bamʋ nkpa kakpaa, nɩɩ ba mɛɛ yɛ ba fɔyɩ. Nɩɩ ɔkʋ mɛɛ taalɛ bɩɩtɛ bamʋ kɔɔlɛ lɩɩ mɛ abaa tɔ.

eng: My Father, who has given them to me, is greater than all, and no one is able to snatch them from my Fathers hand.
local: Mɛ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ, mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa bamʋ sa mɛ mʋ, kyɔ kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ, ɩmʋ sʋ ɔkʋ mɛɛ taalɛ bɩɩtɛ bamʋ kɔɔlɛ lɩɩ Mɛ‐Sɛ abaa tɔ.

eng: I and the Father are one.
local: N maa Mɛ‐Sɛ an gyɛ kʋkʋlʋn nɩn.

eng: Again the Jewish opposition picked up stones in order to stone him.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Gyiwu abi ba bɩla naa taa abuu kɛ ba tʋʋ mʋ.

eng: Jesus responded, I have shown you many good works from the Father. For which of those works do you stone me.
local: Amaa nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, N waa asɩn kyinkyinsa damantɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ lɩɩ Mɛ‐Sɛ asɛ kaapʋ fanɛ. Ɩmʋ ɩmɔɔ sʋ nɩɩ fen biti fan tʋʋ mɛ abuu.

eng: The Jewish opposition answered, We dont stone you for a good work but for insulting God. You are human, yet you make yourself out to be God.
local: Nɩɩ Gyiwu abi mʋ ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Man gyɛ asɩn kyinkyinsa damantɛ mʋ nɩɩ fʋ waa sʋ nɩɩ an biti a tʋʋ fʋ abuu. Amaa fʋ tɔwɛ nbusaa asɩn yii Ɩbwaarɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ fʋ gyɛ nyɩmɩsa nɩn, nɩɩ fɩɩ tɔwɛ yɛ fʋ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, Isnt it written in your Law, I have said, you are gods?.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan man nyi ba maa kyʋrɔɔ waa fanɛ Nbara mʋ tɔ yɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ, Fan gyɛ mɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ mɛ kedubi?.

eng: Scripture calls those to whom Gods word came gods , and scripture cant be abolished.
local: An nyi fɛɛ ɔkʋ mɛɛ taalɛ kyɛɛgɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ. Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla tɔwɛ bamʋ nɩɩ mʋ asɩn ɩ ba bamʋ asɛ mʋ yɛ ba gyɛ mu keduli mʋ, mɛɛ mɛ bɩrɛ Mɛ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ lɛɛ mɛ nɩɩ o sun mɛ ba dulinyaa tɔ.

eng: So how can you say that the one whom the Father has made holy and sent into the world insults God because he said, I am Gods Son?.
local: Mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ fɛn yɛ n tɔwɛ nbusaa asɩn yii Ɩbwaarɛ, fɛɛ maa tɔwɛ yɛ, N gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Mu‐Bii mʋ?.

eng: If I dont do the works of my Father, dont believe me.
local: Nɩɩ n mɛɛ kan n waa atɔ mʋ nɩɩ Mɛ‐Sɛ ii biti fɛɛ n waa mʋ, kanɩn bɩrɛ fan man kɔɔlɛ mɛ gyii.

eng: But if I do them, and you dont believe me, believe the works so that you can know and recognize that the Father is in me and I am in the Father.
local: Amaa nɩɩ n kan waa ɩmʋ bɩrɛ, nɩɩ halɩɩ fan man kɔɔlɛ mɛ gyii gbaa mʋ, fan kɔɔlɛ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ n waa mʋ kpini gyii, kɛ fan nyɛ bɩɩ fɛɛ n maa Mɛ‐Sɛ an gyɛ kʋkʋlʋn.

eng: Again, they wanted to arrest him, but he escaped from them.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ asa mʋ ba biti fɛɛ ba kɩtaa mʋ, amaa ɔ bʋlɛ bamʋ tɔ lɩɩ kyʋn.

eng: Jesus went back across the Jordan to the place where John had baptized at first, and he stayed there.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu bwii naa faa Gyɔdan ɔbʋn kpee kɩmʋ benben, opula mʋ nɩɩ Gyɔn kyɩna gyee asa Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee mʋ. Ɔ maa bʋ tʋtɔ mʋ.

eng: Many people came to him. John didnt do any miraculous signs, they said, but everything John said about this man was true.
local: nɩɩ asa damantɛ ba ba mʋ asɛ, nɩɩ bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Halɩɩ Gyɔn maa man waa asɩn kyinkyinsa ɩkʋ gbaa mʋ, kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ lɩɩ ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ sʋ mʋ, kpini gyɛ kesintin.

eng: Many believed in Jesus there.
local: Nɩɩ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ tʋtɔ mʋ gaalaagaa ba kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii.

eng: Before the Festival of Passover, Jesus knew that his time had come to leave this world and go to the Father. Having loved his own who were in the world, he loved them fully.
local: Ɩ maa san gbɛrɛɛ kɛ ba piili Kesu kɔnɔ Kanyaŋɛ nbunɔ kensi‐gyii mʋ, Yesu bɩɩ fɛɛ saŋa fʋʋ fɛɛ ɔ lɩɩ dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ bwii kpee Mʋ‐sɛ asɛ. Maa sʋ kebiti sa mʋ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ sʋ mʋ, ɔ nyɛ kebiti sa bamʋ naa fʋʋ ɔkaa.

eng: Jesus and his disciples were sharing the evening meal. The devil had already provoked Judas, Simon Iscariots son, to betray Jesus.
local: Pɔyɩ kɛ ba piili kalaatɩpwɛ sʋ agyitɔ kigyii mʋ, Sɩtaanɛ wʋla loo Simon mu‐bii, Gyudasi Isikariyoti mʋ tɔ yɛ, ɔ lɛɛ Yesu sa.

eng: Jesus knew the Father had given everything into his hands and that he had come from God and was returning to God.
local: Yesu nyi Mʋ‐sɛ maa taa kʋtɔ kʋmaa waa mʋ kɩbaa tɔ, nɩɩ o nyi maa lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ nɩn, nɩɩ mʋ asɛ nɩɩ ee biti o bwii kpee.

eng: So he got up from the table and took off his robes. Picking up a linen towel, he tied it around his waist.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔ kʋsʋ lɩɩ agyitɔ mʋ asɛ, nɩɩ o bugi mʋ balɩmaasʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taa bɔrɩdɩba dɩla mʋ kasɩyɛ.

eng: Then he poured water into a washbasin and began to wash the disciples feet, drying them with the towel he was wearing.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ kyɛɛgɛ nkyu waa lapɛ gyeesa tɔ, nɩɩ o piili ɛɛ fwɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ayaa sʋ, yɛgɛ ɔ sʋ bɔrɩdɩba mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa maalɛ mʋ kasɩyɛ mʋ ɛɛ kpayɩ bamʋ ayaa sʋ.

eng: When Jesus came to Simon Peter, Peter said to him, Lord, are you going to wash my feet.
local: Maa waa kanɩn naa fʋʋ Simon Pita sʋ mʋ, ɔ taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, fii biti fʋ fwɩɩ mɛ ayaa sʋ nɩn.

eng: Jesus replied, You dont understand what Im doing now, but you will understand later.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Nbɩɩnbɩɩ ɩmʋ nɩɩ n waa falɛ fɩɩ mɛɛ yɛ fʋ nu kasɛ, amaa kamaa tɔ fii biti fʋ ba nu ɩmʋ kasɛ.

eng: No! Peter said. You will never wash my feet! Jesus replied, Unless I wash you, you wont have a place with me.
local: Nɩɩ Pita tɔwɛ Yesu yɛ, Ayee, n mɛɛ yɛgɛ fʋ fwɩɩ mɛ ayaa sʋ kakaakʋ. Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Nɩɩ fʋ man yɛgɛ nɩɩ n fwɩɩ fʋ ayaa sʋ bɩrɛ, fʋ man bɩla fii tii mɛ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ sʋ.

eng: Simon Peter said, Lord, not only my feet but also my hands and my head.
local: Nɩɩ Simon Pita tɔwɛ Yesu yɛ, Kanɩn bɩrɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, man fwɩɩ mɛ ayaa sʋ nkʋn, fwɩɩ mɛ abaa yɛ mi kumu tɔ gbaa tii sʋ.

eng: Jesus responded, Those who have bathed need only to have their feet washed, because they are completely clean. You disciples are clean, but not every one of you.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tee gyee mʋ, mʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋ ɩ man bɩla ɩ sʋ ineesi, ɩmʋ sʋ mʋ ayaa sʋ nkʋn nɩɩ ɛɛ fwɩɩ. Fanɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ ɩ man sʋ ineesi kʋ, amɔɔ fanɛ tɔ ɔkʋlʋn.

eng: He knew who would betray him. Thats why he said, Not every one of you is clean.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ tɛɛ wʋla o nyi ɔmʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ lɛɛ mʋ sa, ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, amɔɔ fanɛ tɔ ɔkʋlʋn ɩ sʋ ineesi.

eng: After he washed the disciples feet, he put on his robes and returned to his place at the table. He said to them, Do you know what Ive done for you?.
local: Maa fwɩɩ bamʋ ayaa sʋ lʋwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ o bwii taa mʋ balɩmaasʋ bun, nɩɩ o bwii naa kyɩna mʋ ɔkyɩnakpa. Nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan nu ɩmʋ nɩɩ n waa sa fanɛ falɛ kasɛ.

eng: You call me Teacher and Lord, and you speak correctly, because I am.
local: Fɛn tɩɩ mɛ yɛ Ɔkaapʋpʋ yɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ kesintin, lɩɩ fɛɛ kanɩn nɩɩ n gyɛ.

eng: If I, your Lord and teacher, have washed your feet, you too must wash each others feet.
local: Nɩɩ mɛ, fanɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ yɛ fanɛ Ɔkaapʋpʋ n fwɩɩ fanɛ ayaa sʋ falɛ, ɩ dagaa fɛɛ fanɛ gbaa fanɛ fan fwɩɩ abaa ayaa sʋ.

eng: I have given you an example: Just as I have done, you also must do.
local: N waa sɩnkaala sa fanɛ, yɛ fanɛ gbaa fanɛ fan waa fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ n waa sa fanɛ falɛ.

eng: I assure you, servants arent greater than their master, nor are those who are sent greater than the one who sent them.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin, kɩyaafɔlɛ mɛɛ taalɛ ɔ kyɔ mʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ, nɩɩ kabɔɔ mɛɛ taalɛ ɔ kyɔ ɔmʋ nɩɩ o sun mʋ mʋ.

eng: Since you know these things, you will be happy if you do them.
local: Nbɩɩnbɩɩ fan maa bɩɩ ɩnɩmʋ falɛ, nɩɩ fan kan waa ɩmʋ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ waa fanɛ kusee.

eng: Im not speaking about all of you. I know those whom Ive chosen. But this is to fulfill the scripture, The one who eats my bread has turned against me.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ yɛ, Man gyɛ fanɛ kpini asɩn nɩɩ n tɔwɛ falɛ. N nyi bamʋ nɩɩ n lɛɛ mʋ. Amaa asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ mʋ, ɩ dagaa ɩ maa ba tɔ. Asɩn mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, Ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ n maa mʋ an bʋga lapɛ kʋlʋn tɔ an gyii mʋ, biliŋi ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ kʋsʋ yɩlɛ mɛ sʋ.

eng: Im telling you this now, before it happens, so that when it does happen you will believe that I Am.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ nɩn pɔyɩ kɛ ɩ ba waa. Nɩɩ ɩ ba mʋ, fen biti fan bɩɩ fɛɛ mɛ ɩ gyɛ Ɔmʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ yɛ n gyɛ mʋ.
