eng: I assure you that whoever receives someone I send receives me, and whoever receives me receives the one who sent me.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin, ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ n sun mʋ mʋ, kɔɔlɛ mɛ gbaa mɛ nɩmʋ, nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ mɛ mʋ, kɔɔlɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ o sun mɛ mʋ gbaa nɩmʋ.

eng: After he said these things, Jesus was deeply disturbed and testified, I assure you, one of you will betray me.
local: Yesu maa tɔwɛ ɩnɩmʋ kpini lʋwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ mʋ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ nyita mʋ. Nɩɩ o bugi tɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin yɛ, fanɛ tɔ ɔkʋ ii biti ɔ lɛɛ mɛ sa.

eng: His disciples looked at each other, confused about which of them he was talking about.
local: Nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba piili bɛɛ kɩɩ abaa ansi tɔ, fɛ nfɛɛrɛ bamʋ tɔ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ dɛɛ.

eng: One of the disciples, the one whom Jesus loved, was at Jesus side.
local: Mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ tɔ ɔkʋlʋn, ɔmʋ nɩɩ Yesu sʋ kebiti sa mʋ mʋ, tɛ ɔ maa Yesu.

eng: Simon Peter nodded at him to get him to ask Jesus who he was talking about.
local: Nɩɩ Simon Pita ŋabɛɛ mu ansi yɛ, Taasɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ yɛ, anɩmʋ asɩn nɩɩ ɛɛ tɔwɛ falɛ.

eng: Leaning back toward Jesus, this disciple asked, Lord, who is it.
local: Maa kpɛɛ Yesu mʋ, ɔ taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, anɩmʋ nɩɩ fʋ dɛɛ.

eng: Jesus answered, Its the one to whom I will give this piece of bread once I have dipped into the bowl.Then he dipped the piece of bread and gave it to Judas, Simon Iscariots son.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, N kan taa agyitɔ waa lapɛ tɔ mʋ, ɔmʋ nɩɩ n biti n taa sa mʋ mʋ, mʋ ɩ gyɛ kanɩn nyɩmɩsa maŋa. Maa kpɛɛ agyitɔ mʋ waa lapɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ, ɔ taa sa Gyudasi Isikariyoti.

eng: After Judas took the bread, Satan entered into him. Jesus told him, What you are about to do, do quickly.
local: Gyudasi maa dan kɔɔlɛ agyitɔ maŋa mʋ, nɩɩ Sɩtaanɛ loo mʋ nfɛɛrɛ tɔ. Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ fii biti yɛ fʋ waa mʋ, waa bilen‐bilen.

eng: No one sitting at the table understood why Jesus said this to him.
local: Bamʋ nɩɩ ba tɛ ogyikpa maŋa kpini, ɔkʋ man nu asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ kasɛ.

eng: Some thought that, since Judas kept the money bag, Jesus told him, Go, buy what we need for the feast, or that he should give something to the poor.
local: Amaa Gyudasi maa gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kɩtaa bamʋ afulee sʋ mʋ, akʋ ba fɛ nfɛɛrɛ yɛ, Yesu sun mʋ yɛ ɔ naa sɔɔ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ii tiri sa Kesu kɔnɔ Kanyaŋɛ nbunɔ agyitɔ mʋ. Nɩɩ akʋ mɔɔ ba fɛ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ o sun mʋ nɩn yɛ ɔ taa afulee naa sa atiripu.

eng: So when Judas took the bread, he left immediately. And it was night.
local: Gyudasi maa dan kɔɔlɛ agyitɔ mʋ lʋwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ kewu kyʋn. Saŋa maŋa gyɛ kagyanbwɛ nɩn.

eng: When Judas was gone, Jesus said, Now the Human One has been glorified, and God has been glorified in him.
local: Gyudasi maa lɩɩ kyʋn mʋ, nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ yɛ, Saŋa fʋʋ fɛɛ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa bʋnyaa ɩ lɩɩ ifuli, kɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gbaa ɔ bɔla mɛ sʋ lɛɛ mʋ bʋnyaa mʋ.

eng: If God has been glorified in him, God will also glorify the Human One in himself and will glorify him immediately.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kan bɔla mɛ sʋ lɛɛ mʋ bʋnyaa mʋ ifuli mʋ, ee biti ɔ bʋnyaa mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ. Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ waa ɩmʋ nbɩɩnbɩɩ nɩmʋ.

eng: Little children, Im with you for a little while longer. You will look for me—but, just as I told the Jewish leaders, I also tell you now—Where Im going, you cant come.
local: Mi‐bii ana, saŋa gbɛrɛɛ ɩ san kɛ n kyɩna fanɛ asɛ. Fen biti fan kɩɩ biti mɛ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ Gyiwu abi abɩlɩsa mʋ, kanɩn nɩɩ n tɔwɛ fanɛ nbɩɩnbɩɩ falɛ yɛ, katɩn mʋ nɩɩ n biti n kpee mʋ, fan mɛɛ yɛ fan taalɛ ba tʋtɔ.

eng: I give you a new commandment: Love each other. Just as I have loved you, so you also must love each other.
local: Nbɩɩnbɩɩ n sa fanɛ kɔnɔsa pʋpwɛ yɛ, fan nyɛ kebiti sa abaa. Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ n nyɛ kebiti sa fanɛ mʋ, kanɩn maŋa sʋ nɩɩ fan nyɛ kebiti sa abaa.

eng: This is how everyone will know that you are my disciples, when you love each other.
local: Nɩɩ fan kan nyɛ kebiti sa abaa mʋ, ɩmʋ ii biti ɩ yɛgɛ asa kpini ba bɩɩ yɛ fan gyɛ mɛ abɩɩlapʋ.

eng: Simon Peter said to Jesus, Lord, where are you going? Jesus answered, Where I am going, you cant follow me now, but you will follow later.
local: Nɩɩ Simon Pita taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ fɩnɛ nɩɩ fii kpee? Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Katɩn mʋ nɩɩ n kpee mʋ fʋ mɛɛ yɛ fʋ taalɛ buu mɛ kpee nbɩɩnbɩɩ, amaa fii biti fu buu mɛ ba kamaa tɔ.

eng: Peter asked, Lord, why cant I follow you now? Ill give up my life for you.
local: Nɩɩ Pita taasɛ mʋ yɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, mɩnɛ waa nɩɩ n mɛɛ taalɛ buu fʋ nbɩɩnbɩɩ. N biti n taa mɛ nkpa taga apʋ sa fʋ.

eng: Jesus replied, Will you give up your life for me? I assure you that you will deny me three times before the rooster crows.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Fii biti fu taa fʋ nkpa taga apʋ sa mɛ kesintin? N tɔwɛ fʋ kesintin yɛ, pɔyɩ kɛ kyaasɛ ɔ bʋn mʋ, fii biti fʋ kyɛɛ ɩlɩɩ ɩsa yɛ fʋ man nyi mɛ.

eng: Dont be troubled. Trust in God. Trust also in me.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ, Fan man yɛgɛ fanɛ nkɔlɔ ɩ ŋmaa fanɛ. Fan kɔɔlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gyii kɛ fan kɔɔlɛ ngbaa mɛ gyii.

eng: My Fathers house has room to spare. If that werent the case, would I have told you that Im going to prepare a place for you?.
local: Ibu damantɛ ɩ bʋ Mɛ‐Sɛ kɩkpaara sʋ. Nɩɩ ɩ daa ɩ man gyɛ kanɩn nɩn, naafɔɔ n tɔwɛ fanɛ. N kpee n naa lɔŋɔ ɔkyɩnakpa sa fanɛ.

eng: When I go to prepare a place for you, I will return and take you to be with me so that where I am you will be too.
local: Nɩɩ n kan kpee naa lɔŋɔ ɔkyɩnakpa sa fanɛ mʋ, n biti n bwii ba taa fanɛ kpee mɛ asɛ, kɛ fan naa kyɩna katɩn mʋ nɩɩ n tɛ mʋ.

eng: You know the way to the place Im going.
local: Fen nyi ɔkpa mʋ, nɩɩ fen nyi katɩn mʋ nɩɩ n kpee mʋ.

eng: Thomas asked, Lord, we dont know where you are going. How can we know the way.
local: Nɩɩ Tomasi tɔwɛ Yesu yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, a man nyi katɩn mʋ nɩɩ fii kpee. Nɩɩ nɛnɛ nɩɩ an biti a waa bɩɩ ɔkpa mʋ.

eng: Jesus answered, I am the way, the truth, and the life. No one comes to the Father except through me.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Mɛ ɩ gyɛ ɔkpa mʋ yɛ kesintin mʋ yɛ nkpa mʋ. Ɔkʋ mɛɛ taalɛ kpee Mɛ‐Sɛ asɛ, amɔɔ ɔ bɔla mɛ sʋ.

eng: If you have really known me, you will also know the Father. From now on you know him and have seen him.
local: Nɩɩ fan kan fan nyi mɛ kesintin tɔ mʋ, naafɔɔ fan nyi Mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ gbaa. Lɩɩ nbɩɩnbɩɩ nɩmʋ ɩɩ sʋ ii kpee mʋ, fen nyi mʋ, nɩɩ fen wu mʋ.

eng: Philip said, Lord, show us the Father; that will be enough for us.
local: Nɩɩ Filipu tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, kaapʋ anɛ Fʋ‐sɛ mʋ, kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ki tiri anɛ nɩn.

eng: Jesus replied, Dont you know me, Philip, even after I have been with you all this time? Whoever has seen me has seen the Father. How can you say, Show us the Father?.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Filipu, amɔɔ fʋ man nyi mɛ, halɩɩ kanan mʋ nɩɩ n kyɩna fanɛ asɛ kyɛɛrɛɛ falɛ? Ɔsa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o wu mɛ mʋ, o wu Mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ. Mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fʋ bɩla fɩɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, Kaapʋ anɛ Fʋ‐sɛ mʋ.

eng: Dont you believe that I am in the Father and the Father is in me? The words I have spoken to you I dont speak on my own. The Father who dwells in me does his works.
local: Abɛɛ fʋ man kɔɔlɛ gyii yɛ n maa Mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ an gyɛ kʋkʋlʋn? Asɩn mʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ fanɛ mʋ man gyɛ mɛ gbaa‐gbaa mɛ lɛɛ. Amaa Mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ mɛ tɔ mʋ ii sun mu kusun.

eng: Trust me when I say that I am in the Father and the Father is in me, or at least believe on account of the works themselves.
local: Kanɩn sʋ fan kɔɔlɛ mɛ gyii fɛɛ n maa tɔwɛ yɛ, n maa Mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ an gyɛ kʋkʋlʋn. Nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ kanɩn gbaa mʋ, fan kɔɔlɛ mɛ gyii lɩɩ asɩn kyinkyinsa mʋ nɩɩ fan wu n maa waa falɛ sʋ.

eng: I assure you that whoever believes in me will do the works that I do. They will do even greater works than these because I am going to the Father.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin, ɔsa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kɔɔlɛ mɛ ee gyii mʋ, ee biti ɔ waa atɔ mʋ nɩɩ n waa mʋ. Ee biti ɔ waa ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ kyɔ falɛ gbaa, lɩɩ fɛɛ n kpee Mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ asɛ nɩn.

eng: I will do whatever you ask for in my name, so that the Father can be glorified in the Son.
local: Nɩɩ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fan kʋlɛ mɛ kɩtɩɩ tɔ sʋ mʋ, n biti n waa sa fanɛ, kɛ mɛ, Obii mʋ n bɩya bʋnyaa sa Mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ.

eng: When you ask me for anything in my name, I will do it.
local: Kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fɛn kʋlɛ mɛ lɩɩ mɛ kɩtɩɩ tɔ sʋ mʋ, n biti n waa sa fanɛ. Yesu da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ yɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ kii biti kɩ ba.

eng: If you love me, you will keep my commandments.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ, Nɩɩ fan kan fan sʋ kebiti sa mɛ mʋ, fen biti fan buu mɛ kɔnɔsa.

eng: I will ask the Father, and he will send another Companion, who will be with you forever.
local: N biti n kʋlɛ Mɛ‐Sɛ kɛ ɔ sa fanɛ Ɔkyɛtɔpʋ pʋpwɛ mʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ baa ɔ bʋ fanɛ asɛ kakpaa.

eng: This Companion is the Spirit of Truth, whom the world cant receive because it neither sees him nor recognizes him. You know him, because he lives with you and will be with you.
local: Mʋ ɩ gyɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ lɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kesintin mʋ ɛɛ kaapʋ. Dulinya abi ba mɛɛ yɛ ba taalɛ kɔɔlɛ mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ba man nyi mʋ, nɩɩ be mee wu mʋ. Amaa fanɛɛ fanɛ fan nyi mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ bʋ fanɛ asɛ, nɩɩ ee biti ɔ baa ɔ bʋ fanɛ tɔ.

eng: I wont leave you as orphans. I will come to you.
local: N mɛɛ yɛ n yɛgɛ fanɛ fɛɛ aŋmengyi, n biti n bwii ba fanɛ asɛ.

eng: Soon the world will no longer see me, but you will see me. Because I live, you will live too.
local: Ɩ san gbɛrɛɛ, dulinyaa abi ba man bɩla yɛ ba wu mɛ. Amaa fanɛɛ fanɛ bɩrɛ fen biti fan wu mɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ n maa tɛ nkpa tɔ falɛ, fanɛ gbaa fanɛ fen biti fan baa fan tɛ nkpa tɔ.

eng: On that day you will know that I am in my Father, you are in me, and I am in you.
local: Kanɩn kakɛ maŋa fen biti fan bɩɩ fɛɛ n maa Mɛ‐Sɛ an gyɛ kʋkʋlʋn, nɩɩ n maa fanɛ gbaa an gyɛ kʋkʋlʋn.

eng: Whoever has my commandments and keeps them loves me. Whoever loves me will be loved by my Father, and I will love them and reveal myself to them.
local: Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ mɛ kɔnɔsa, nɩɩ o buu ɩmʋ mʋ, mʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ kebiti sa mɛ. Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ kebiti sa mɛ mʋ, Mɛ‐Sɛ gbaa ii biti ɔ nyɛ kebiti sa mʋ. Ngbaa mɛ n biti n nyɛ kebiti sa mʋ, kɛ n lɛɛ mɛ n‐yɩɩ kaapʋ mʋ.

eng: Judas asked, Lord, why are you about to reveal yourself to us and not to the world.
local: Nɩɩ Gyudasi tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, mɩnɛ waa nɩɩ fii biti fʋ lɛɛ fʋ n‐yɩɩ kaapʋ anɛ nkʋn, nɩɩ man gyɛ dulinyaa abi mʋ.

eng: Jesus answered, Whoever loves me will keep my word. My Father will love them, and we will come to them and make our home with them.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ kebiti sa mɛ mʋ, ii biti o buu mɛ kɩkaapʋ mʋ. Mɛ‐Sɛ ii biti ɔ nyɛ kebiti sa mʋ, nɩɩ maa Mɛ‐Sɛ an biti a ba mʋ asɛ, kɛ a taa mʋ biliŋi anɛ ayɛ.

eng: Whoever doesnt love me doesnt keep my words. The word that you hear isnt mine. It is the word of the Father who sent me.
local: Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man sʋ kebiti sa mɛ mʋ, mɛɛ yɛ o buu mɛ kɩkaapʋ mʋ. Kɩkaapʋ mʋ nɩɩ fen nu falɛ man gyɛ mɛ gbaa‐gbaa mɛ kɩkaapʋ, ɩɩ lɩɩ Mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ nɩɩ o sun mɛ mʋ asɛ nɩn.

eng: I have spoken these things to you while I am with you.
local: N maa san n bʋ fanɛ asɛ falɛ sʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ fanɛ falɛ asɩn.

eng: The Companion, the Holy Spirit, whom the Father will send in my name, will teach you everything and will remind you of everything I told you.
local: Amaa Ɔkyɛtɔpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ, mʋ nɩɩ Mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ ii biti o sun mɛ kɩtɩɩ tɔ mʋ, ii biti ɔ kaapʋ fanɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa kɛ o nyiŋi fanɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ fanɛ falɛ.

eng: Peace I leave with you. My peace I give you. I give to you not as the world gives. Dont be troubled or afraid.
local: Kayɩɩ‐yuuli kɩ baa kɩ bʋ fanɛ asɛ, mɛ gbaa‐gbaa kayɩɩ‐yuuli nɩɩ n sʋ n sa fanɛ. Amaa ɩ man du fɛɛ dulinyaa kayɩɩ‐yuuli mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ sa mʋ. Fan man yɛgɛ fanɛ nkɔlɔ i nyita, fan man waa kufuu.

eng: You have heard me tell you, Im going away and returning to you. If you loved me, you would be happy that I am going to the Father, because the Father is greater than me.
local: Fan nu n maa tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, N kpee kɛ n bɩla bwii ba fanɛ asɛ. Nɩɩ fɛn kan fen biti mɛ asɩn mʋ, naafɔɔ fanɛ ansi ii biti i gyii fɛɛ n maa kpee Mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ asɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ Mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔkparɛ ɔ kyɔ mɛ.

eng: I have told you before it happens so that when it happens you will believe.
local: N wʋla n tɔwɛ fanɛ ɩnɩmʋ kpini nbɩɩnbɩɩ pɔyɩ kɛ ɩ ba waa, nɩɩ ɩ kan ba waa mʋ kɛ fan kɔɔlɛ mɛ gyii.

eng: I wont say much more to you because this worlds ruler is coming. He has nothing on me.
local: N mɛɛ bɩla yɛ n tɔwɛ fanɛ asɛ kyɛɛrɛɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ dulinyaa tɔ owura ɩɩ ba. Ɔ man sʋ ɔlʋn mɛ sʋ.

eng: Rather, he comes so that the world will know that I love the Father and do just as the Father has commanded me. Get up. Were leaving this place.
local: amaa i tiri fɛɛ n maa waa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Mɛ‐Sɛ sa mɛ kɔnɔ yɛ n waa mʋ, kɛ dulinyaa abi ba bɩɩ fɛɛ n sʋ kebiti sa Mɛ‐Sɛ. Fan ba kɛ a lɩɩ nfɩɩ.

eng: I am the true vine, and my Father is the vineyard keeper.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ, Mɛ ɩ gyɛ wayɩn oyu gbaa‐gbaa mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ sɔyɩ abi, nɩɩ Mɛ‐Sɛ ɩ gyɛ oyu mʋ ɔdɔɔpʋ.

eng: He removes any of my branches that dont produce fruit, and he trims any branch that produces fruit so that it will produce even more fruit.
local: Kayaa‐bi kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ kyaa mɛ sʋ nɩɩ kɩ mɛɛ sɔyɩ abi mʋ, ɛɛ ŋɛ kɩmʋ nɩn ɛɛ fɔyɩ, nɩɩ kayaa‐bi kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ sɔyɩ abi mʋ, ɛɛ kpaalɛ kayaa‐bi mʋ kɛ kɩ nyɛ sɔyɩ abi tii sʋ.

eng: You are already trimmed because of the word I have spoken to you.
local: Lɩɩ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ sa fanɛ sʋ mʋ, fan tɛɛ waa kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ.

eng: Remain in me, and I will remain in you. A branch cant produce fruit by itself, but must remain in the vine. Likewise, you cant produce fruit unless you remain in me.
local: Fan baa fan bʋ mɛ asɛ, kɛ ngbaa mɛ n baa n bʋ fanɛ asɛ. Kɩyaa‐bi nkʋn mɛɛ taalɛ sɔyɩ abi, amɔɔ kɩ kyaa oyu mʋ sʋ. Kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ fanɛ gbaa fanɛ fɛn mɛɛ taalɛ sɔyɩ abi, amɔɔ fan bʋ mɛ asɛ.

eng: I am the vine; you are the branches. If you remain in me and I in you, then you will produce much fruit. Without me, you cant do anything.
local: Mɛ ɩ gyɛ oyu mʋ, nɩɩ fanɛ ɩ gyɛ kɩmʋ n‐yaabi mʋ. Ɔsa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kan ɔ bʋ mɛ asɛ, nɩɩ ngbaa n bʋ mʋ asɛ sʋ mʋ, ee biti ɔ sɔyɩ abi gaa, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ kayaa‐bi kyaa oyu sʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ sɔyɩ abi mʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ fan man tii mɛ sʋ bɩrɛ, fɛn mɛɛ taalɛ waa sɛhn.

eng: If you dont remain in me, you will be like a branch that is thrown out and dries up. Those branches are gathered up, thrown into a fire, and burned.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man kan ɔ bʋ mɛ asɛ bɩrɛ, o du nɩn fɛɛ kayaa‐bi mʋ nɩɩ ba ŋɛ tʋʋ nɩɩ kɩ wʋlɛ. Kanɩn n‐yaabi nɩɩ bɛɛ tasɛ bɛɛ tʋʋ fʋlɔn tɔ, kɛ ɩ dɩyɛ mʋ.

eng: If you remain in me and my words remain in you, ask for whatever you want and it will be done for you.
local: Nɩɩ fan kan fan bʋ mɛ asɛ, nɩɩ mɛ asɩn mʋ bʋ fanɛ tɔ mʋ, kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fen biti, nɩɩ fan kʋlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ, ee biti ɔ waa sa fanɛ.

eng: My Father is glorified when you produce much fruit and in this way prove that you are my disciples.
local: Nɩɩ fan kan sɔyɩ abi gaa, mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ fan gyɛ mɛ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ, ɩmʋ ɩ nyɛ lɛɛ Mɛ‐Sɛ bʋnyaa mʋ ifuli kaapʋ.

eng: As the Father loved me, I too have loved you. Remain in my love.
local: Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ sʋ kebiti sa mɛ mʋ, kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ ngbaa mɛ n sʋ kebiti sa fanɛ. Ɩmʋ sʋ fan kyaga sʋ kyɩna mi kebiti mʋ tɔ.

eng: If you keep my commandments, you will remain in my love, just as I kept my Fathers commandments and remain in his love.
local: Nɩɩ fan kan buu mɛ kɔnɔsa mʋ, n biti n kyaga sʋ nyɛ kebiti sa fanɛ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ n buu Mɛ‐Sɛ kɔnɔsa nɩɩ ɔ sʋ kebiti sa mɛ mʋ.

eng: I have said these things to you so that my joy will be in you and your joy will be complete.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ ɩnɩmʋ nɩn kɛ mi kensi‐gyii ɔ kyɩna fanɛ tɔ, kɛ fanɛ kensi‐gyii mʋ ɔ naa lʋwɛ kyɛɛkyɛɛ.

eng: This is my commandment: love each other just as I have loved you.
local: Mɛ kɔnɔsa ɩ gyɛ yɛ, Fan nyɛ kebiti sa abaa, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ n nyɛ kebiti sa fanɛ mʋ.

eng: No one has greater love than to give up ones life for ones friends.
local: Kebiti kɩ man bʋ tɔ kɩ kyɔ kɩnɩmʋ fɛɛ ɔkʋ maa wu sa mʋ kyɛmɩnɛ ana.

eng: You are my friends if you do what I command you.
local: Nɩɩ fan kan buu mɛ kɔnɔsa mʋ bɩrɛ, fan gyɛ mɛ kyɛmɩnɛ ana nɩn.

eng: I dont call you servants any longer, because servants dont know what their master is doing. Instead, I call you friends, because everything I heard from my Father I have made known to you.
local: N mɛɛ bɩla yɛ n tɩɩ fanɛ mɛ ayaafɔlɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ kɩyaafɔlɛ man nyi kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ mʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ ɩɩ waa. Amaa n tɩɩ fanɛ mɛ kyɛmɩnɛ ana nɩn, lɩɩ fɛɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ n bɩɩla lɩɩ Mɛ‐Sɛ asɛ mʋ, n lɛɛ kaapʋ fanɛ.

eng: You didnt choose me, but I chose you and appointed you so that you could go and produce fruit and so that your fruit could last. As a result, whatever you ask the Father in my name, he will give you.
local: Man gyɛ fanɛ ɩ lɛɛ mɛ. Amaa mɛ ɩ lɛɛ fanɛ, nɩɩ n sun fanɛ yɛ fan naa sɔyɩ abi mʋ nɩɩ ii biti ɩ kyɛɛrɛɛ. Nɩɩ fan kan waa kanɩn mʋ, kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fan kʋlɛ Mɛ‐Sɛ mɛ kɩtɩɩ tɔ mʋ, ee biti ɔ waa sa fanɛ.

eng: I give you these commandments so that you can love each other.
local: Mɛ kɔnɔsa ɩ gyɛ yɛ, fan nyɛ kebiti sa abaa.

eng: If the world hates you, know that it hated me first.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ yɛ, Nɩɩ dulinyaa abi ba kan bɛɛ kisi fanɛ mʋ, fan baa fan nyiŋi fɛɛ mɛ nɩɩ ba daa kikisi.

eng: If you belonged to the world, the world would love you as its own. However, I have chosen you out of the world, and you dont belong to the world. This is why the world hates you.
local: Nɩɩ fan kan fan du fɛɛ dulinyaa tɔ abi bɩrɛ, naafɔɔ dulinyaa abi bee biti ba nyɛ kebiti sa fanɛ fɛɛ kɩmʋ asa. Amaa fan man gyɛ dulinyaa abi. Mɛ ɩ lɛɛ fanɛ lɩɩ dulinyaa abi tɔ. Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ dulinyaa abi bee kisi fanɛ mʋ.

eng: Remember what I told you, Servants arent greater than their master. If the world harassed me, it will harass you too. If it kept my word, it will also keep yours.
local: Fan nyiŋi asɩn mʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ fanɛ mʋ yɛ, Kɩyaafɔlɛ mɛɛ taalɛ ɔ kyɔ mʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ. Ɩmʋ sʋ n maa wu awʋrʋfɔ bamʋ abaa tɔ falɛ, kanɩn nɩɩ fanɛ gbaa fanɛ fen biti fan wu awʋrʋfɔ bamʋ abaa tɔ. Nɩɩ ba kan buu mɛ kɩkaapʋ mʋ, bee biti ba buu fanɛ kɩkaapʋ mʋ gbaa.

eng: The world will do all these things to you on account of my name, because it doesnt know the one who sent me.
local: Lɩɩ mɛ kɩtɩɩ sʋ, nɩɩ bee biti ba waa fanɛ ɩnɩmʋ kpini, lɩɩ fɛɛ ba man nyi Ɔmʋ nɩɩ o sun mɛ mʋ.

eng: If I hadnt come and spoken to the people of this world, they wouldnt be sinners. But now they have no excuse for their sin.
local: Nɩɩ n daa man ba tɔwɛ asɩn mʋ sa bamʋ nɩn, naafɔɔ ba mɛɛ yɛ ba wu alibi kʋpwɛ. Amaa nbɩɩnbɩɩ ba man sʋ kɔnɔ kɩlɛɛ lɩɩ bamʋ alibi sʋ.

eng: Whoever hates me also hates the Father.
local: Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ee kisi mɛ mʋ, ee kisi Mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ gbaa.

eng: If I hadnt done works among them that no one else had done, they wouldnt be sinners. But now they have seen and hated both me and my Father.
local: N ba waa asɩn kyinkyinsa mʋ nɩɩ ɔkʋ man pɩɩ taalɛ waa bamʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ ba daa ba man wu ɩmʋ bɩrɛ nɩn, naafɔɔ ba mɛɛ yɛ ba wu alibi kʋpwɛ. Amaa nbɩɩnbɩɩ ba wu asɩn kyinkyinsa nɩmʋ kpini. Halɩɩ ɩmʋ kpini gbaa mʋ ba kisi maa Mɛ‐Sɛ.

eng: This fulfills the word written in their Law, They hated me without a reason.
local: Amaa ɩnɩmʋ ɩ yɛgɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ ɩ ba tɔ. Asɩn mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, Ba kisi mɛ yɛgɛ ɩ man sʋ kasɛ.

eng: When the Companion comes, whom I will send from the Father—the Spirit of Truth who proceeds from the Father—he will testify about me.
local: Amaa n biti n sun Ɔkyɛtɔpʋ mʋ lɩɩ Mɛ‐Sɛ asɛ, mʋ ɩ gyɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ lɛɛ kesintin lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sʋ ɛɛ kaapʋ.

eng: You will testify too, because you have been with me from the beginning.
local: Nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fanɛ gbaa fanɛ fan gyii mɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ kesintin, lɩɩ fɛɛ fan bʋ mɛ asɛ lɩɩ npiili asɛ.

eng: I have said these things to you so that you wont fall away.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ ɩnɩmʋ kpini nɩn, kɛ fan man nyɛ yɛgɛ fanɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii mʋ tɔ.

eng: They will expel you from the synagogue. The time is coming when those who kill you will think that they are doing a service to God.
local: Bee biti ba gya fanɛ lɩɩ Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa. Saŋa kʋ ii biti ɔ ba, nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan ba mɔɔ fanɛ gbaa mʋ, ee biti ɔ fɛ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ ee sun kusun ɛɛ kyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔ nɩn.

eng: They will do these things because they dont know the Father or me.
local: Bee biti ba waa falɛ atɔ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ba man nyi Mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ba man nyi mɛ.

eng: But I have said these things to you so that when their time comes, you will remember that I told you about them. I didnt say these things to you from the beginning, because I was with you.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ ɩnɩmʋ nɩn, nɩɩ saŋa mʋ kan fʋʋ mʋ, fen biti fan nyiŋi fɛɛ n wʋla da fanɛ amu tɔ. N man tɔwɛ fanɛ ɩnɩmʋ bilen, lɩɩ fɛɛ n san n bʋ fanɛ asɛ.

eng: But now I go away to the one who sent me. None of you ask me, Where are you going.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ yɛ, Nbɩɩnbɩɩ n kpee ɔmʋ nɩɩ o sun mɛ mʋ asɛ nɩn, amaa fanɛ ɔkʋ man taasɛ mɛ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ n kpee.

eng: Yet because I have said these things to you, you are filled with sorrow.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ n maa tɔwɛ fanɛ ɩnɩmʋ kpini sʋ mʋ, fanɛ nkɔlɔ tɔ i nyita.

eng: I assure you that it is better for you that I go away. If I dont go away, the Companion wont come to you. But if I go, I will send him to you.
local: Amaa n tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin, mi kikpe mʋ kɩ gyɛ tɔnɔ sa fanɛ nɩn. Nɩɩ n man kan kpee mʋ, Ɔkyɛtɔpʋ mʋ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ ba fanɛ asɛ. Amaa nɩɩ n kan kpee mʋ, n biti n sun mʋ fanɛ asɛ.

eng: When he comes, he will show the world it was wrong about sin, righteousness, and judgment.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ kan ba mʋ, ee biti ɔ ba yɛgɛ dulinyaa tɔ asa ba bɩɩ fɛɛ ba fɔyɩ ɔkpa lɩɩ bamʋ alibi sʋ, yɛ ɩkpa kikyiigi mʋ sʋ yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn kigyii sʋ.

eng: He will show the world it was wrong about sin because they dont believe in me.
local: Ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ ba fɔyɩ lɩɩ bamʋ alibi sʋ mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ ba mɛɛ kɔɔlɛ mɛ bee gyii.

eng: He will show the world it was wrong about righteousness because Im going to the Father and you wont see me anymore.
local: Ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ ba fɔyɩ lɩɩ ɩkpa kikyiigi sʋ mʋ, ɩ gyɛ yɛ n kpee Mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ asɛ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ fan man bɩla yɛ fan wu mɛ.

eng: He will show the world it was wrong about judgment because this worlds ruler stands condemned.
local: Ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ ba fɔyɩ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn kigyii sʋ mʋ, ɩ gyɛ yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ tɛɛ wʋla bun dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ Owura mʋ kʋpwɛ.

eng: I have much more to say to you, but you cant handle it now.
local: N sʋ asɩn kʋkyɔ n tɔwɛ fanɛ, amaa fan mɛɛ taalɛ kɩtaa ɩmʋ kpini nbɩɩnbɩɩ nɩmʋ.

eng: However, when the Spirit of Truth comes, he will guide you in all truth. He wont speak on his own, but will say whatever he hears and will proclaim to you what is to come.
local: Amaa nɩɩ Kayaayu mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ lɛɛ kesintin ɛɛ kaapʋ kan ba mʋ, ee biti ɔ kaapʋ fanɛ kɛ fan bɩɩ kesintin mʋ kpini. Man gyɛ mʋ gbaa‐gbaa ɔlʋn sʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ tɔwɛ. Amaa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ee nu mʋ nkʋn nɩɩ ɛɛ tɔwɛ, nɩɩ ee biti ɔ tɔwɛ fanɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kii biti kɩ ba.

eng: He will glorify me, because he will take what is mine and proclaim it to you.
local: Lɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ o nu lɩɩ mɛ asɛ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ lɛɛ kaapʋ fanɛ, kɛ ee biti ɔ bɩya mɛ bʋnyaa.

eng: Everything that the Father has is mine. Thats why I said that the Spirit takes what is mine and will proclaim it to you.
local: Kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ Mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ lɛɛ mʋ, gyɛ mɛ lɛɛ nɩn. Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ Kayaayu mʋ ii biti ɔ taa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ mɛ lɛɛ mʋ ba kaapʋ fanɛ.

eng: Soon you wont be able to see me; soon after that, you will see me.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu kyaga sʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɩ san gbɛrɛɛ fan man bɩla yɛ fen wu mɛ, nɩɩ ɩ bɩla waa gbɛrɛɛ fen biti fen wu mɛ.

eng: Some of Jesus disciples said to each other, What does he mean: Soon you wont see me, and soon after that you will see me and Because Im going to the Father?.
local: Tʋtɔ maŋa nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ akʋ bɛɛ taasɛ abaa yɛ, Mɩnɛ nɩɩ mʋ katɔwɛ mʋ ɩɩ kaapʋ? Maa tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɩ san gbɛrɛɛ fan man bɩla yɛ fan wu mɛ, nɩɩ ɩ bɩla waa gbɛrɛɛ, fen biti fan wu mɛ nɩɩ ɔ bɩla bwii tɔwɛ yɛ, N kpee Mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ asɛ mʋ, mɩnɛ nɩɩ ɩnɩmʋ ɩɩ kaapʋ.

eng: What does he mean by soon? We dont understand what hes talking about.
local: Nɩɩ ba maa sʋ bɛɛ taasɛ yɛ, Ɩ san gbɛrɛɛ mʋ, ɩmʋ kasɛ ɩ gyɛ nɛnɛ? A mee nu asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ kasɛ.

eng: Jesus knew they wanted to ask him, so he said, Are you trying to find out from each other what I meant when I said, Soon you wont see me, and soon after that you will see me?.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu bɩɩ fɛɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ bee biti ba taasɛ mʋ asɩn mʋ kasɛ, nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, N maa tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɩ san gbɛrɛɛ fan man bɩla yɛ fen wu mɛ, nɩɩ ɩ bɩla waa gbɛrɛɛ fen biti fan wu mɛ mʋ, ɩmʋ asɩn nɩɩ fɛn taasɛ abaa?.

eng: I assure you that you will cry and lament, and the world will be happy. You will be sorrowful, but your sorrow will turn into joy.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin yɛ, fen biti fan su kɛ fan bɔta tɔ, yɛgɛ dulinyaa abi mɔɔ bee gyii bamʋ ansi. Fanɛ nkɔlɔ ii biti ii nyita fanɛ, amaa fanɛ nkɔlɔ kinyita ii biti ɩ ba biliŋi nkɔlɔ fuuli.

eng: When a woman gives birth, she has pain because her time has come. But when the child is born, she no longer remembers her distress because of her joy that a child has been born into the world.
local: I du nɩn fɛɛ keduŋidi maa kɩtaa ɔkyɩɩ mʋ, ɩ kɔ mʋ lɩɩ fɛɛ mʋ awʋrʋfɔ saŋa fʋʋ. Amaa nɩɩ ɔ kʋʋgɛ kebii mʋ, ɛɛ tan mʋ awʋrʋfɔ mʋ sʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ kʋʋgɛ kebii waa dulinyaa tɔ.

eng: In the same way, you have sorrow now; but I will see you again, and you will be overjoyed. No one takes away your joy.
local: Kanɩn nɩɩ i du sa fanɛ gbaa fanɛ. Nbɩɩnbɩɩ fan bʋ awʋrʋfɔ tɔ. Amaa n biti n bɩla wu fanɛ, kɛ fanɛ ansi i gyii. Nɩɩ ɔkʋ mɛɛ taalɛ lɛɛ kanɩn kensi‐gyii maŋa lɩɩ fanɛ tɔ.

eng: In that day, you wont ask me anything. I assure you that the Father will give you whatever you ask in my name.
local: Nɩɩ kakɛ maŋa kan fʋʋ mʋ, fan man bɩla yɛ fan taasɛ mɛ sɛhn. N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin yɛ Mɛ‐Sɛ ii biti ɔ sa fanɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fan kʋlɛ lɩɩ mɛ kɩtɩɩ tɔ sʋ.

eng: Up to now, you have asked nothing in my name. Ask and you will receive so that your joy will be complete.
local: Halɩɩ ba lɩɩ ndaga fan man pɩɩta kʋlɛ kʋtɔ kʋ lɩɩ mɛ kɩtɩɩ tɔ sʋ. Fan kʋlɛ, fen biti fan nyɛ, kɛ fanɛ kensi‐gyii ɔ nyɛ akyɔ.

eng: Ive been using figures of speech with you. The time is coming when I will no longer speak to you in such analogies. Instead, I will tell you plainly about the Father.
local: Ɩ gyɛ kesintin fɛɛ aŋasan tɔ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ asɩn nɩmʋ. Amaa saŋa kʋ ɩɩ ba nɩɩ n man bɩla yɛ n tɔwɛ fanɛ aŋasan tɔ. N biti n lɛɛ ifuli tɔwɛ fanɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ lɩɩ Mɛ‐Sɛ sʋ.

eng: In that day you will ask in my name. Im not saying that I will ask the Father on your behalf.
local: Kanɩn kakɛ maŋa fen biti fan kʋlɛ Mɛ‐Sɛ lɩɩ mɛ kɩtɩɩ tɔ sʋ. N mɛɛ tɔwɛ nɩn yɛ n biti n yɩlɛ fanɛ ayaa tɔ kʋlɛ Mɛ‐Sɛ sa fanɛ.

eng: The Father himself loves you, because you have loved me and believed that I came from God.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ gbaa‐gbaa sʋ kebiti sa fanɛ, kpalɩ fɛɛ fan sʋ kebiti sa mɛ nɩɩ fan kɔɔlɛ gyii fɛɛ n lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ.

eng: I left the Father and came into the world. I tell you again: I am leaving the world and returning to the Father.
local: N lɩɩ Mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ asɛ ba dulinyaa tɔ. Nɩɩ nbɩɩnbɩɩ n lɩɩ dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ n bwii n kpee Mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ asɛ.

eng: His disciples said, See! Now you speak plainly; you arent using figures of speech.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, Nbɩɩnbɩɩ bɩrɛ, fʋ man bɩla fɩɩ tɔwɛ aŋasan tɔ, fii sii fii bugi tɔ nɩn fɩɩ tɔwɛ.

eng: Now we know that you know everything and you dont need anyone to ask you. Because of this we believe you have come from God.
local: Nbɩɩnbɩɩ an wu fɛɛ fu nyi kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ, nɩɩ ɩ man bɩla i tiri fɛɛ ɔkʋ a taasɛ fʋ kʋtɔ kʋ. Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ an kɔɔlɛ fu gyii yɛ fʋ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, Now you believe?.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Nbɩɩnbɩɩ bɩrɛ fan kɔɔlɛ gyii?.

eng: Look! A time is coming—and is here!—when each of you will be scattered to your own homes and you will leave me alone. Im not really alone, for the Father is with me.
local: Amaa saŋa kʋ ɩɩ ba, nɩɩ kɩ tɛɛ ba gbaa, nɩɩ fanɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ ii biti ɔ yaasɛ tɔ kpee mʋ ayɛ. Fen biti fan yɛgɛ mɛ nkʋn. Amaa ɩ mɛɛ yɛ ɩ san mɛ nkʋn lɩɩ fɛɛ Mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ bʋ mɛ asɛ.

eng: Ive said these things to you so that you will have peace in me. In the world you have distress. But be encouraged! I have conquered the world.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ falɛ asɩn nɩn, kɛ lɩɩ mɛ sʋ mʋ, kɛ fan nyɛ kayɩɩ‐yuuli. Fen biti fan gyii awʋrʋfɔ dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ. Amaa fan nyɛ nkɔlɔ, lɩɩ fɛɛ n kɔ gyii dulinyaa mʋ sʋ.

eng: After he said these things, Jesus went out with his disciples and crossed over to the other side of the Kidron Valley. He and his disciples entered a garden there.
local: Yesu maa kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ lʋwɛ mʋ, maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba faa kɩpantan mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ kɩmʋ yɛ Kidiron kpee kɩmʋ benbe. Kadɔɔ‐bi kʋ kʋbʋ tʋtɔ, nɩɩ ba loo kɩmʋ tɔ.

eng: Judas, his betrayer, also knew the place because Jesus often gathered there with his disciples.
local: Nɩɩ Gyudasi mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ lɛɛ Yesu ɛɛ sa mʋ, nyi tʋtɔ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ, Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ bɛɛ taalɛ bɛɛ gyaŋɛ tʋtɔ.

eng: Judas brought a company of soldiers and some guards from the chief priests and Pharisees. They came there carrying lanterns, torches, and weapons.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ aseepu bɩlɩsa yɛ Farasii abi ba yɛgɛ nɩɩ Gyudasi gyankpaa Roma anaakɔpʋ yɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ akɩɩsʋpʋ, yɛgɛ ba sʋ ɩgyaa yɛ ɩfɩtɩla yɛ adʋntɔ ba kadɔɔ‐bi mʋ tɔ.

eng: Jesus knew everything that was to happen to him, so he went out and asked, Who are you looking for.
local: Yesu maa nyi kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kii biti kɩ ba mʋ sʋ mʋ, ɔ lɩɩ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ tɔ nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Anɩmʋ nɩɩ fen biti.

eng: They answered, Jesus the Nazarene. He said to them, I Am.
local: Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, An biti Yesu Kanasareti‐nyɩn mʋ nɩn. Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Mɛ gyɛ mʋ. Yɛgɛ Gyudasi mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ lɛɛ mʋ sa mʋ yɩlɛ bamʋ asɛ tʋtɔ.

eng: When he said, I Am, they shrank back and fell to the ground.
local: Yesu maa tɔwɛ yɛ, Mɛ ɩ gyɛ mʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ba bwii kamaa tɩyɛ kasɛ.

eng: He asked them again, Who are you looking for? They said, Jesus the Nazarene.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ bɩla taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Anɩmʋ nɩɩ fen biti? Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, An biti Yesu Kanasareti‐nyɩn mʋ nɩn.

eng: Jesus answered, I told you, I Am. If you are looking for me, then let these people go.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, N tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ mɛ ɩ gyɛ mʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ mɛ nɩɩ fen biti bɩrɛ, fan yɛgɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba tii mɛ sʋ falɛ ba natɛ.

eng: This was so that the word he had spoken might be fulfilled: I didnt lose anyone of those whom you gave me.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ waa nɩn, kɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ wʋla tɔwɛ mʋ ɩ ba tɔ. Asɩn mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, N man yɛgɛ bamʋ nɩɩ fʋ taa bamʋ sa mɛ mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ fɔyɩ mʋ.

eng: Then Simon Peter, who had a sword, drew it and struck the high priests servant, cutting off his right ear.
local: Simon Pita sʋ sakunde, nɩɩ ɔ bɩɩtɛ kɩmʋ lɔɔrɛ nɩɩ ɔ ŋɛ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ kɩyaafɔlɛ gyisa kʋsʋ tɩn. Kɩyaafɔlɛ maŋa kɩtɩɩ ɩ gyɛ Malikusi.

eng: Jesus told Peter, Put your sword away! Am I not to drink the cup the Father has given me.
local: Yesu tɔwɛ Pita yɛ, Bwiiyaa fʋ sakunde mʋ waa kɩmʋ kukufee tɔ. Fʋ man nyi fɛɛ i tiri fɛɛ n gyii awʋrʋfɔ mʋ nɩɩ Nsɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ŋmina yɩla mɛ mʋ.

eng: Then the company of soldiers, the commander, and the guards from the Jewish leaders took Jesus into custody. They bound him.
local: Nɩɩ anaakɔpʋ mʋ yɛ bamʋ ɔbɩlɩsa yɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ akɩɩsʋpʋ mʋ ba kɩtaa Yesu, nɩɩ ba ŋmina mʋ.

eng: and led him first to Annas. He was the father-in-law of Caiaphas, the high priest that year. .
local: Nɩɩ ba gyankpaa taa mʋ kpee Anasi asɛ, mʋ ɩ gyɛ Kayifasi mʋ‐saa, mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ kanɩn kesu maŋa.

eng: Caiaphas was the one who had advised the Jewish leaders that it was better for one person to die for the people.
local: Kayifasi maŋa ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ daa tɔwɛ waa Gyiwu abi mʋ yɛ ɩ bɔ fɛɛ nyɩmɩsa kʋlʋn o wu sa asa kpini mʋ.

eng: Simon Peter and another disciple followed Jesus. Because this other disciple was known to the high priest, he went with Jesus into the high priests courtyard.
local: Simon Pita maa ɔbɩɩlapʋ kʋ ba buu Yesu. Fɛɛ ɔbɩɩlapʋ mʋ maa nyi Oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ sʋ mʋ, o buu Yesu loo oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ.

eng: However, Peter stood outside near the gate. Then the other disciple came out and spoke to the woman stationed at the gate, and she brought Peter in.
local: Amaa Pita gyoo kebunɔ. Ɔbɩɩlapʋ mʋ nɩɩ o nyi Oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ, bwii ba tɔwɛ kibite mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kɩɩ kebunɔ mʋ sʋ mʋ asɛ, nɩɩ ɔ taa Pita loo.

eng: The servant woman stationed at the gate asked Peter, Arent you one of this mans disciples? Im not, he replied.
local: Kibite mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɩɩ kebunɔ mʋ taasɛ Pita yɛ, Fʋ man gyɛ Yesu abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ɔkʋ nɩn? Nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ayee, n man gyɛ.

eng: The servants and the guards had made a fire because it was cold. They were standing around it, warming themselves. Peter joined them there, standing by the fire and warming himself.
local: Ɩ kyɩna ɩ gyɛ awɔɔ tɔ nɩn, nɩɩ ayaafɔlɛ yɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ akɩɩsʋpʋ mʋ ba dɩyɛla fʋlɔn bɛɛ wʋlɛ, nɩɩ Pita gbaa ba yɩlɛ bamʋ asɛ ɛɛ wʋlɛ fʋlɔn.

eng: Meanwhile, the chief priest questioned Jesus about his disciples and his teaching.
local: Oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ Anasi taasɛ Yesu lɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kaapʋ mʋ sʋ.

eng: Jesus answered, Ive spoken openly to the world. Ive always taught in synagogues and in the temple, where all the Jews gather. Ive said nothing in private.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Saŋa kʋmaa nɩmʋ sakyɔ tɔ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ asɩn, yɛgɛ n kaapʋ atɔ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɩgyaŋɛkpa yɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ Gyiwu abi kpini bɛɛ gyaŋɛ mʋ. N man tɔwɛ kʋtɔ kʋ baala asiri tɔ.

eng: Why ask me? Ask those who heard what I told them. They know what I said.
local: Mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ fɩɩ taasɛ mɛ falɛ asɩn? Taasɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba nu mɛ asɛ mʋ. Ba nyi kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ.

eng: After Jesus spoke, one of the guards standing there slapped Jesus in the face. Is that how you would answer the high priest? he asked.
local: Yesu maa tɔwɛ kanɩn mʋ, nɩɩ akɩɩsʋpʋ mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ yɩlɛ tʋtɔ mʋ kpa mʋ ɔtan, nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Falɛ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fʋ maa lɛɛ Oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ kɔnɔ.

eng: Jesus replied, If I speak wrongly, testify about what was wrong. But if I speak correctly, why do you strike me.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Nɩɩ n kan tɔwɛ asɩn akʋ nɩɩ ɩ man bʋrɔn mʋ, tɔwɛ asɩn maŋa kɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ o nu. Amaa nɩɩ mɛ asɩn mʋ ɩ gyɛ kesintin mʋ, mɩnɛ waa nɩɩ fʋ da mɛ ɔtan.

eng: Then Annas sent him, bound, to Caiaphas the high priest.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔbɩlɩsa Anasi yɛgɛ nɩɩ ba taa Yesu yɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba ŋmina mʋ mʋ naa sa Kayifasi, Oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ.

eng: Meanwhile, Simon Peter was still standing with the guards, warming himself. They asked, Arent you one of his disciples? Peter denied it, saying, Im not.
local: Simon Pita san ɔ yɩlɛ ɛɛ wʋlɛ fʋlɔn, nɩɩ ɔkʋ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Fʋ man gyɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ nɩn? Nɩɩ Pita kyɛ yɛ, Ayee, n man gyɛ.

eng: A servant of the high priest, a relative of the one whose ear Peter had cut off, said to him, Didnt I see you in the garden with him.
local: Nɩɩ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ ayaafɔlɛ mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ, ɔmʋ nɩɩ Pita ŋɛ mʋ kʋsʋ tɩn mʋ, mʋ kʋʋpʋ, taasɛ mʋ yɛ, N man wu fʋ maa bʋ mʋ asɛ kadɔɔ‐bi mʋ tɔ.

eng: Peter denied it again, and immediately a rooster crowed.
local: Nɩɩ Pita bwii kyɛ. Opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ kyaasɛ bʋn.

eng: The Jewish leaders led Jesus from Caiaphas to the Roman governors palace. It was early in the morning. So that they could eat the Passover, the Jewish leaders wouldnt enter the palace; entering the palace would have made them ritually impure.
local: Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ Yesu lɩɩ Kayifasi asɛ nyenyenfuu, kpee Ɔsʋwʋlɛ Ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ mʋ kɩkpaara tɔ Amaa Gyiwu abi mʋ ba man loo kɩkpaara mʋ tɔ, lɩɩ fɛɛ nɩɩ ba kan loo tʋtɔ mʋ, bee biti ba waa ineesi kɛ i tii bamʋ ɔkpa kɛ ba man nyɛ gyii Kesu kɔnɔ Kanyaŋɛ nbunɔ mʋ.

eng: So Pilate went out to them and asked, What charge do you bring against this man.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ Ɔsʋwʋlɛ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ Paleti lɩɩ kɩkpaara sʋ ba bamʋ asɛ, nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Mɩnɛ asɩn pwɩɩsa nɩɩ fan sʋ lɩɩ ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ sʋ.

eng: They answered, If he had done nothing wrong, we wouldnt have handed him over to you.
local: Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɔ daa ɔ man waa alibi akʋ nɩn, naafɔɔ a mɛɛ yɛ a taa mʋ ba waa fʋ abaa tɔ.

eng: Pilate responded, Take him yourselves and judge him according to your Law. The Jewish leaders replied, The Law doesnt allow us to kill anyone. .
local: Paleti tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan taa mʋ bwii kɛ fan naa taa fanɛ gbaa‐gbaa fanɛ nbara gyii mʋ asɩn. Amaa ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Fanɛ Roma abi fan man sa anɛ ɔkpa yɛ a mɔɔ ɔkʋ.

eng: This was so that Jesus word might be fulfilled when he indicated how he was going to die.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ waa nɩn kɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ lɩɩ mu lewu mʋ nɩɩ ee biti o wu mʋ kudubi ɩ ba tɔ.

eng: Pilate went back into the palace. He summoned Jesus and asked, Are you the king of the Jews.
local: Nɩɩ Paleti bwii loo mʋ kɩkpaara tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ Yesu ba yɩlɛ mu ansi tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Fʋ ɩ gyɛ Gyiwu abi Owura mʋ.

eng: Jesus answered, Do you say this on your own or have others spoken to you about me.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Asɩn mʋ nɩɩ fʋ taasɛ mɛ falɛ lɩɩ fʋ asɛ nɩn abɛɛ ɔsa kʋ ɩ tɔwɛ mɛ asɩn sa fʋ.

eng: Pilate responded, Im not a Jew, am I? Your nation and its chief priests handed you over to me. What have you done.
local: Paleti lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, N gyɛ Gyiwu obii nɩn? Fʋ gbaa‐gbaa fʋ asa yɛ fʋ aseepu bɩlɩsa ɩ taa fʋ waa mɛ kɩbaa tɔ. Mɩnɛ nɩɩ fʋ waa.

eng: Jesus replied, My kingdom doesnt originate from this world. If it did, my guards would fight so that I wouldnt have been arrested by the Jewish leaders. My kingdom isnt from here.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Mi kuwura‐gyii kɩ man gyɛ dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ lɛɛ. Nɩɩ n daa n gyɛ dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ lɛɛ nɩn, naafɔɔ mi abuupu ba dɩn kɔ sa mɛ, kɛ Gyiwu abi ba man nyɛ mɛ kɩtaa waa bamʋ abɩlɩsa abaa tɔ. Amaa mi kuwura‐gyii mʋ kɩ man gyɛ dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ lɛɛ.

eng: So you are a king? Pilate said. Jesus answered, You say that I am a king. I was born and came into the world for this reason: to testify to the truth. Whoever accepts the truth listens to my voice.
local: Nɩɩ Paleti lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Kanɩn bɩrɛ fʋ gyɛ owura nɩn! Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Fʋ maa tɔwɛ yɛ n gyɛ owura mʋ, ɩ gyɛ kesintin. Ɩnɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ ba kʋʋgɛ mɛ ba dulinyaa tɔ, kɛ n ba tɔwɛ lɩɩ kesintin mʋ sʋ. Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti kesintin mʋ ii nu mɛ asɛ.

eng: What is truth? Pilate asked. After Pilate said this, he returned to the Jewish leaders and said, I find no grounds for any charge against him.
local: Nɩɩ Paleti taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Mɩnɛ ɩ gyɛ kesintin? Maa taasɛ mʋ lʋwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ o bwii lɩɩ kpee Gyiwu abi mʋ asɛ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, N man wu asɩn mʋ nɩɩ n yɩlɛ sʋ kɛ n bun ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ kʋpwɛ.

eng: You have a custom that I release one prisoner for you at Passover. Do you want me to release for you the king of the Jews.
local: Amaa ɩ gyɛ fanɛ anaana tɔ nɩn fɛɛ Kesu kɔnɔ Kanyaŋɛ nbunɔ kensi‐gyii saŋa n yɛgɛ n lɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba tii bamʋ obu mʋ ɔkʋ mʋ nɩɩ fen biti mʋ, n sa fanɛ. Ɩmʋ sʋ fen biti fɛɛ n yɛgɛ fanɛ Gyiwu abi owura mʋ sa fanɛ abɛɛ.

eng: They shouted, Not this man! Give us Barabbas.
local: Nɩɩ ba kuusi tɔwɛ yɛ, Ayee! Man gyɛ mʋ, lɛɛ Barabasi sa anɛ. Barabasi ɩ kyɩna ɔ kʋsʋ yɩlɛ awura sʋ bɩya keluu.

eng: Then Pilate had Jesus taken and whipped.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Paleti taa Yesu sa, nɩɩ ba tɩŋɛ mʋ awulibi.

eng: The soldiers twisted together a crown of thorns and put it on his head, and dressed him in a purple robe.
local: Nɩɩ anaakɔpʋ mʋ ba taa iwii bɔɔ kuwura‐wʋrʋ bun mʋ, nɩɩ ba bun mʋ kɩrɩman‐kyu balɩmaasʋ fɛɛ owura.

eng: Over and over they went up to him and said, Greetings, king of the Jews! And they slapped him in the face.
local: Nɩɩ bɛɛ ba mʋ asɛ bɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Eee! Gyiwu abi owura, eee, owura! Nɩɩ bɛɛ kpee bɛɛ kpa mʋ ɩtan.

eng: Pilate came out of the palace again and said to the Jewish leaders, Look! Im bringing him out to you to let you know that I find no grounds for a charge against him.
local: Nɩɩ Paleti bɩla lɩɩ ba kewu Gyiwu abi mʋ asɛ ba tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan nu nfɩɩ, n sʋ mʋ n bɩya fanɛ, kɛ fan bɩɩ fɛɛ mɛɛ mɛ n man wu alibi akʋ mʋ sʋ, mʋ nɩɩ n taa bun mʋ kʋpwɛ.

eng: When Jesus came out, wearing the crown of thorns and the purple robe, Pilate said to them, Heres the man.
local: Yesu maa lɩɩ ba mʋ, o bun iwii kuwura‐wʋrʋ yɛ kɩrɩman‐kyu balɩmaasʋ, nɩɩ Paleti tɔwɛ asa mʋ yɛ, Fan kɩɩ, ɔnyɩn mʋ ndee.

eng: When the chief priests and their deputies saw him, they shouted out, Crucify, crucify! Pilate told them, You take him and crucify him. I dont find any grounds for a charge against him.
local: Aseepu bɩlɩsa yɛ Gyiwu anaakɔpʋ ba maa wu Yesu mʋ ba kuusi yɛ, Da mʋ mata oyu laasa sʋ mɔɔ. Da mʋ mata. Nɩɩ Paleti tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fanɛ gbaa‐gbaa fanɛ fan taa mʋ naa da mata oyu laasa sʋ. Mɛɛ mɛ n man wu alibi akʋ mʋ nɩɩ n taa bun mʋ kʋpwɛ.

eng: The Jewish leaders replied, We have a Law, and according to this Law he ought to die because he made himself out to be Gods Son.
local: Nɩɩ Gyiwu abi mʋ ba lɛɛ Paleti kɔnɔ yɛ, An sʋ nbara kʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ dagaa maa wu, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ tɩɩ mʋ n‐yɩɩ yɛ mʋ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Mu‐Bii.

eng: When Pilate heard this word, he was even more afraid.
local: Paleti maa nu ɩnɩmʋ mʋ kufuu kɩ kɩtaa mʋ tii sʋ.

eng: He went back into the residence and spoke to Jesus, Where are you from? Jesus didnt answer.
local: Nɩɩ o bwiiyaa Yesu loo kɩkpaara mʋ tɔ nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Fɩnɛ nɩɩ fʋ lɩɩ? Amaa Yesu man tʋwɛ mʋ.

eng: So Pilate said, You wont speak to me? Dont you know that I have authority to release you and also to crucify you.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ bɩla taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Fu kina mɛ asɛ kɩ tɔwɛ? Fʋ man nyi fɛɛ n sʋ ɔlʋn n taalɛ yɛgɛ fʋ natɛ kɩyan, abɛɛ ba da fʋ mata oyu laasa sʋ mɔɔ.

eng: Jesus replied, You would have no authority over me if it had not been given to you from above. Thats why the one who handed me over to you has the greater sin.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ mʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ daa ɔ man sa fʋ ɔlʋn mɛ sʋ nɩn, naafɔɔ fʋ mɛɛ taalɛ waa mɛ sɛhn. Amaa ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ mɛ sa fʋ mʋ, mu alibi ɩ kyɔ fʋ lɛɛ gaa.

eng: From that moment on, Pilate wanted to release Jesus. However, the Jewish leaders cried out, saying, If you release this man, you arent a friend of the emperor! Anyone who makes himself out to be a king opposes the emperor.
local: Paleti maa nu kanɩn mʋ, o biti ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bɔla yɛgɛ Yesu ɔ natɛ kɩyan. Amaa Gyiwu abi mʋ ba kuusi yɛ, Fʋ dan kan yɛgɛ nɩɩ ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ natɛ kɩyan bɩrɛ, fʋ man gyɛ Owura lala Kasaro mʋ kyɛmɩnɛ. Kpalɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ n‐yɩɩ yɛ ɔ gyɛ owura mʋ, gyɛ Owura lala Kasaro mʋ dʋn nɩn.

eng: When Pilate heard these words, he led Jesus out and seated him on the judges bench at the place called Stone Pavement .
local: Paleti maa nu kanɩn mʋ, ɔ taa Yesu lɩɩ ba kɩlandɛ sʋ, nɩɩ Paleti naa kyɩna kagya mʋ nɩɩ awura bɛɛ kyɩna kɩmʋ tɔ bɛɛ gyii asɩn mʋ. Bɛɛ tɩɩ tʋtɔ yɛ, Abuu ɔtagakpa. Gyiwu abi katɔwɛ tɔ bɛɛ tɩɩ kɩmʋ yɛ, Gabata.

eng: It was about noon on the Preparation Day for the Passover. Pilate said to the Jewish leaders, Heres your king.
local: Kanɩn kakɛ maŋa gyɛ kakɛ kʋlasa sa Kesu kɔnɔ Kanyaŋɛ nbunɔ kensi‐gyii mʋ. Lɩɩ ɩ maa fʋʋ kyɔwɛ amu nsana mʋ. Nɩɩ Paleti tɔwɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ yɛ, Fan kɩɩ fanɛ Owura.

eng: The Jewish leaders cried out, Take him away! Take him away! Crucify him! Pilate responded, What? Do you want me to crucify your king? We have no king except the emperor, the chief priests answered.
local: Nɩɩ Gyiwu abi ba kuusi yɛ, Mɔɔ mʋ, mɔɔ mʋ. Da mʋ mata oyu laasa sʋ mɔɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, N mɔɔ fanɛ owura mʋ abɛɛ? Nɩɩ aseepu bɩlɩsa mʋ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, A man sʋ owura kʋ tii Owura lala Kasaro sʋ.

eng: Then Pilate handed Jesus over to be crucified. The soldiers took Jesus prisoner.
local: Tʋtɔ maŋa nɩɩ Paleti taa mʋ sa bamʋ yɛ ba naa da mʋ mata oyu laasa sʋ mɔɔ. Ɩmʋ sʋ anaakɔpʋ mʋ ba taa Yesu kyʋn.

eng: Carrying his cross by himself, he went out to a place called Skull Place .
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɩɩ tʋtɔ ɔ kyɛ mʋ oyu laasa naa fʋʋ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ Kumu kawɛ opula. Nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ kɩmʋ Gyiwu abi katɔwɛ tɔ yɛ, Goligota mʋ.

eng: Thats where they crucified him—and two others with him, one on each side and Jesus in the middle.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba da mʋ anyenbi mata oyu laasa sʋ. Nɩɩ ba bɩla da asa nyɔ anyenbi mata iyu laasa sʋ, ɔkʋ bʋ mʋ gyisa sʋ nɩɩ ɔkʋ mɔɔ bʋ mʋ bɩna sʋ.

eng: Pilate had a public notice written and posted on the cross. It read Jesus the Nazarene, the king of the Jews.
local: Nɩɩ Paleti yɛgɛ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ asɩn gyaga kʋtɔ kʋ sʋ naa mata oyu laasa mʋ sʋ yɛ, YESU KANASARETI‐NYƖN, GYIWU ABI OWURA.

eng: Many of the Jews read this sign, for the place where Jesus was crucified was near the city and it was written in Aramaic, Latin, and Greek.
local: Ba kyʋrɔɔ ɩmʋ Gyiwu abi yɛ Roma abi yɛ Giriki abi ntɔwɛ tɔ, nɩɩ Gyiwu abi gaalaagaa ba kalɛ ɩmʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba da mʋ mata oyu laasa sʋ mʋ, man bʋ kata yɛ kadɛ mʋ.

eng: Therefore, the Jewish chief priests complained to Pilate, Dont write, The king of the Jews but This man said, I am the king of the Jews.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu bɩlɩsa mʋ ba ba tɔwɛ Paleti yɛ, Man kyʋrɔɔ yɛ Gyiwu abi Owura, amaa kyʋrɔɔ yɛ Ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ ɩ tɔwɛ yɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ Gyiwu abi owura. .

eng: Pilate answered, What Ive written, Ive written.
local: Nɩɩ Paleti tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ n dan kyʋrɔɔ mʋ kɩmʋ nɩɩ n kyʋrɔɔ.

eng: When the soldiers crucified Jesus, they took his clothes and his sandals, and divided them into four shares, one for each soldier. His shirt was seamless, woven as one piece from the top to the bottom.
local: Anaakɔpʋ mʋ ba maa da Yesu mata oyu laasa sʋ lʋwɛ mʋ, ba barɩgɛ mʋ atɔ bunsa mʋ tɔ ntun nna, nɩɩ bamʋ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ nyɛ katɩn kʋlʋn. Nɩɩ ɩ san mʋ kaalɛ bʋnswɩɩsa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ man sʋ ɔbaatakpa, nɩɩ ba lʋʋ kɩmʋ lɩɩ sʋsʋ ba yii kasɛ nɩn.

eng: They said to each other, Lets not tear it. Lets cast lots to see who will get it. This was to fulfill the scripture, They divided my clothes among themselves, and they cast lots for my clothing. Thats what the soldiers did.
local: Nɩɩ anaakɔpʋ mʋ ba tɔwɛ abaa yɛ, A man kyatɛ kɩmʋ barɩgɛ tɔ. Fan yɛgɛ a tʋʋ kɩmʋ sʋ nbii, kɛ a kɩɩ anɛ tɔ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ee biti o gyii kɩmʋ. Ba maa waa kanɩn mʋ, ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ ɩ ba tɔ kesintin. Ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, Ba barɩgɛ mɛ atɔ bunsa tɔ sa abaa, nɩɩ ba tʋʋ nbii mɛ kaalɛ bʋnswɩɩsa sʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ kanɩn nɩɩ anaakɔpʋ mʋ ba waa.

eng: Jesus mother and his mothers sister, Mary the wife of Clopas, and Mary Magdalene stood near the cross.
local: Yesu mu‐nyi yɛ mʋ nɩkarɛ yɛ Kilopa mʋ‐ka Mɛɛrɩ yɛ Mɛɛrɩ Magidalen ba yɩlɛ Yesu oyu laasa mʋ asɛ.

eng: When Jesus saw his mother and the disciple whom he loved standing nearby, he said to his mother, Woman, here is your son.
local: Yesu maa wu mu‐nyi yɛ mʋ ɔbɩɩlapʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ kebiti sa mʋ ba maa yɩlɛ tʋtɔ mʋ, ɔ tɔwɛ mu‐nyi yɛ, Ɔkyɩɩ, fu‐bii ndee.

eng: Then he said to the disciple, Here is your mother. And from that time on, this disciple took her into his home.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ ɔbɩɩlapʋ mʋ mɔɔ yɛ, Fɔɔ fʋ, fu‐nyi ndee. Lɩɩ kanɩn saŋa mʋ, nɩɩ ɔbɩɩlapʋ mʋ taa mʋ naa kyɩna mʋ asɛ.

eng: After this, knowing that everything was already completed, in order to fulfill the scripture, Jesus said, I am thirsty.
local: Asɩn nɩmʋ kpini kamaa mʋ, Yesu nyi fɛɛ nbɩɩnbɩɩ bɩrɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa kɩ lʋwɛ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Bʋlɩfɔ sʋ mɛ. Ɔ tɔwɛ ɩnɩmʋ nɩn kɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ ɩ nyɛ ba tɔ.

eng: A jar full of sour wine was nearby, so the soldiers soaked a sponge in it, placed it on a hyssop branch, and held it up to his lips.
local: Tʋtɔ mʋ, nta nyaŋɛsa ɩ bɔla kɔlɔ‐bi ɩ yɩlɛ, nɩɩ anaakɔpʋ mʋ ba taa ɔkyɛɛ ba nyɔ nta mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ba taa sɔgɩta hisopi oyu sʋ, nɩɩ ba kʋsaa kɩmʋ naa tii mʋ kɔnɔ.

eng: When he had received the sour wine, Jesus said, It is completed. Bowing his head, he gave up his life.
local: Yesu maa kyʋkyʋʋ nta mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Nbɩɩnbɩɩ bɩrɛ ɩ lʋwɛ. Nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ ɔŋɛɛ.

eng: It was the Preparation Day and the Jewish leaders didnt want the bodies to remain on the cross on the Sabbath, especially since that Sabbath was an important day. So they asked Pilate to have the legs of those crucified broken and the bodies taken down.
local: Kanɩn kakɛ maŋa kɩ gyɛ kakɛ kʋlasa akɛ sa Kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa. Kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa maŋa maa gyɛ Kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa lala mʋ, nɩɩ Gyiwu abɩlɩsa mʋ be mee biti fɛɛ asa mʋ ba baa ba saa iyu laasa mʋ sʋ naa fʋʋ kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa maŋa. Kanɩn sʋ Gyiwu abɩlɩsa mʋ ba naa kʋlɛ Paleti yɛ ɔ yɛgɛ ba naa gyayɩ‐gyayɩ bamʋ ayaa, kɛ ba wu bilen, kɛ ba nyɛ saatɛ bamʋ lɩɩ iyu laasa mʋ sʋ.

eng: Therefore, the soldiers came and broke the legs of the two men who were crucified with Jesus.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ anaakɔpʋ mʋ ba ba asa anyɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba da bamʋ mata iyu laasa sʋ tii Yesu sʋ mʋ. Nɩɩ ba gyayɩ ɔgyankpaasa mʋ ayaa yɛ ɔbanban mʋ gbaa lɛɛ.

eng: When they came to Jesus, they saw that he was already dead so they didnt break his legs.
local: Amaa ba maa fʋʋ Yesu asɛ mʋ, ba wu fɛɛ ɔ tɛɛ wʋla wu, kanɩn sʋ ba man gyayɩ mʋ ayaa.

eng: However, one of the soldiers pierced his side with a spear, and immediately blood and water came out.
local: Amaa anaakɔpʋ mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ taa kɩkpɛ yii Yesu kikelebi tɔ. Opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ nkalan yɛ nkyu ɩ lɩɩ wʋrɩgɛ.

eng: The one who saw this has testified, and his testimony is true. He knows that he speaks the truth, and he has testified so that you also can believe.
local: Ɔmʋ nɩɩ o wu ɩmʋ yɛ ansi ɩ maa waa mʋ, mʋ ii gyii ɩmʋ kesintin, kɛ fan nyɛ kɔɔlɛ gyii. Nɩɩ ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ kesintin, nɩɩ o nyi fɛɛ mʋ asɩn mʋ gyɛ kesintin.

eng: These things happened to fulfill the scripture, They wont break any of his bones .
local: Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ waa nɩn kaapʋ fɛɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ ɩ ba tɔ kesintin. Asɩn mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, Mu kuwuya kʋlʋn gbaa kɩ mɛɛ yɛ kɩ gya.

eng: And another scripture says, They will look at him whom they have pierced.
local: Nɩɩ bɩla kyʋrɔɔ yɩla katɩn kʋ gbaa tɔwɛ yɛ, Asa bee biti ba kɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ba taa kɩkpɛ yii mʋ mʋ.

eng: After this Joseph of Arimathea asked Pilate if he could take away the body of Jesus. Joseph was a disciple of Jesus, but a secret one because he feared the Jewish authorities. Pilate gave him permission, so he came and took the body away.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ kamaa tɔ mʋ, ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Gyosefu mʋ kpee Paleti asɛ naa kʋlɛ mʋ yɛ, ɔ sa mʋ ɔkpa kɛ ɔ naa saatɛ Yesu kibuni naa pula. Nɩɩ Paleti sa mʋ ɔkpa, nɩɩ o kpee naa taa Yesu kibuni mʋ. Gyosefu maŋa lɩɩ Arimatiya kadɛ tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ Yesu ɔbɩɩlapʋ mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ. Amaa maa sɩlɛ Gyiwu abɩlɩsa sʋ mʋ, ɔ man lɛɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ ifuli.

eng: Nicodemus, the one who at first had come to Jesus at night, was there too. He brought a mixture of myrrh and aloe, nearly seventy-five pounds in all.
local: Gyosefu maa Nikodemusi mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kyɩna kpee naa wu Yesu kagyanbwɛ kʋ mʋ i kpee. Nikodemusi maŋa bɩya tulale mʋ nɩɩ ba taa mɩɩrɛ yɛ kibondiri waa, nɩɩ ba sʋ ba sʋswa abuni mʋ. Ɩmʋ odu ɩ waa fɛɛ akyembee bɔɔta kʋlʋn.

eng: Following Jewish burial customs, they took Jesus body and wrapped it, with the spices, in linen cloths.
local: Anyɩn anyɔ mʋ ba taa Yesu kibuni mʋ, nɩɩ ba taa taafuli mili mʋ, nɩɩ ba bɔ mʋ tulale fɛɛ kanɩn mʋ nɩɩ Gyiwu abi bɛɛ waa bamʋ obuni, pɔyɩ nɩɩ bee pula mʋ.

eng: There was a garden in the place where Jesus was crucified, and in the garden was a new tomb in which no one had ever been laid.
local: Kadɔɔ‐bi kʋ bʋ tiri katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba da Yesu anyenbi mata oyu laasa sʋ mʋ. Kanɩn kadɔɔ‐bi maŋa tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ba lɔŋɔ kakyan kʋ kʋfʋlɛ tɔ yɩla, nɩɩ ba man pɩɩta pula ɔkʋ kɩmʋ tɔ.

eng: Because it was the Jewish Preparation Day and the tomb was nearby, they laid Jesus in it.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ kanɩn kakɛ maŋa maa gyɛ Gyiwu abi kakɛ kʋlasa sa Kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ kakyan mʋ maa tiri tʋtɔ sʋ mʋ, ba taa Yesu naa pula tʋtɔ.

eng: Early in the morning of the first day of the week, while it was still dark, Mary Magdalene came to the tomb and saw that the stone had been taken away from the tomb.
local: Kwasida akɛ kabwaayɩkɛ ɩ maa man pɩɩ ŋmaŋɛ tɔ mʋ, Mɛɛrɩ Magidalen kpee kakyan mʋ asɛ, nɩɩ o wu fɛɛ ba lɛɛ kubuu mʋ nɩɩ ba gbiliti ba tii kɩfʋlɛ tɔ kakyan mʋ kɔnɔ mʋ.

eng: She ran to Simon Peter and the other disciple, the one whom Jesus loved, and said, They have taken the Lord from the tomb, and we dont know where theyve put him.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔ sɩlɛ bwii kpee Simon Pita yɛ ɔbɩɩlapʋ banban mʋ nɩɩ Yesu su kebiti sa mʋ mʋ asɛ, naa tɔwɛ yɛ, Ba taa Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ lɩɩ kakyan mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ a man nyi katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba taa mʋ naa yɩla.

eng: Peter and the other disciple left to go to the tomb.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ Pita maa ɔbɩɩlapʋ banban mʋ ba lɩɩ bee kpee kakyan mʋ asɛ.

eng: They were running together, but the other disciple ran faster than Peter and was the first to arrive at the tomb.
local: Nɩɩ bamʋ anyɔ maa kpini ba sɩlɛ kyɛɛkyɛɛ bee kpee, amaa ɔbɩɩlapʋ banban mʋ sɩlɛ kyʋn Pita sʋ gyankpaa naa fʋʋ kakyan mʋ asɛ.

eng: Bending down to take a look, he saw the linen cloths lying there, but he didnt go in.
local: Nɩɩ o muŋa kɩɩ kakyan mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ o wu taafuli maa dɛ tɔ, amaa ɔ man loo to.

eng: Following him, Simon Peter entered the tomb and saw the linen cloths lying there.
local: Nɩɩ Simon Pita ba kamaa tɔ, nɩɩ o loo kakyan mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ o wu taafuli maa dɛ tʋtɔ.

eng: He also saw the face cloth that had been on Jesus head. It wasnt with the other clothes but was folded up in its own place.
local: yɛ kikyibi fufuli mʋ nɩɩ ba taa ŋmina mu kumu mʋ. Kikyibi mʋ kɩ bɔɔ kɩ dɛ kɩmʋ kʋkwɛ.

eng: Then the other disciple, the one who arrived at the tomb first, also went inside. He saw and believed.
local: Ɔbɩɩlapʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyankpaa fʋʋ tʋtɔ gbaa maa loo to nɩɩ o wu ɩmʋ mʋ, ɔ kɔɔlɛ gyii.

eng: They didnt yet understand the scripture that Jesus must rise from the dead.
local: Ba san ba man pɩɩta nu Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ kasɛ fɛɛ Yesu dagaa maa kyiŋi lɩɩ lewu tɔ.

eng: Then the disciples returned to the place where they were staying.
local: Nɩɩ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba bwii kpee bamʋ akpaara sʋ.

eng: Mary stood outside near the tomb, crying. As she cried, she bent down to look into the tomb.
local: Amaa Mɛɛrɩ bɩrɛ ɔ yɩlɛ kakyan mʋ asɛ ee su. Maa yɩlɛ ee su mʋ, nɩɩ o muŋa suŋa kɩɩ kakyan mʋ tɔ.

eng: She saw two angels dressed in white, seated where the body of Jesus had been, one at the head and one at the foot.
local: Nɩɩ o wu Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ anyɔ yɛgɛ ba bun atɔ fufuli, ba maa tɛ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba daa taa Yesu kibuni yɩla mʋ, ɔkʋ tɛ mu amu sʋ nɩɩ ɔkʋ mɔɔ tɛ mʋ ayaa kasɛ.

eng: The angels asked her, Woman, why are you crying? She replied, They have taken away my Lord, and I dont know where theyve put him.
local: Ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɔkyɩɩ, mɩnɛ waa nɩɩ fii su? Nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ba taa mɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ kyʋn, nɩɩ n man nyi katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba taa mʋ naa yɩla.

eng: As soon as she had said this, she turned around and saw Jesus standing there, but she didnt know it was Jesus.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ kamaa mʋ, ɔ gbɛɛ kɩɩ nɩɩ o wu Yesu maa yɩlɛ tʋtɔ, amaa ɔ man bɩɩ yɛ Yesu nɩn.

eng: Jesus said to her, Woman, why are you crying? Who are you looking for? Thinking he was the gardener, she replied, Sir, if you have carried him away, tell me where you have put him and I will get him.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɔkyɩɩ, mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fii su? Anɩmʋ nɩɩ fii biti? Mɛɛrɩ maa fɛ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ ɔ gyɛ kʋdɔɔ mʋ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ mʋ, ɔ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɔbɩlɩsa, nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ fʋ ɩ kan taa mʋ mʋ, tɔwɛ mɛ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ fʋ taa mʋ naa yɩla kɛ n naa taa mʋ.

eng: Jesus said to her, Mary. She turned and said to him in Aramaic, Rabbouni .
local: Yesu tɩɩ mʋ yɛ, Mɛɛrɩ! Maa biliŋi kɩɩ mʋ, o kuusi tɔwɛ Hibriwu katɔwɛ tɔ yɛ, Raboni! .

eng: Jesus said to her, Dont hold on to me, for I havent yet gone up to my Father. Go to my brothers and sisters and tell them, Im going up to my Father and your Father, to my God and your God.
local: Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Man mata mɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ n man pɩɩta bwii kpee Mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ asɛ. Amaa kpee mi abuupu mʋ asɛ naa tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, n bwii n kpee Mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ fanɛ‐Sɛ, yɛ mɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ fanɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ asɛ.

eng: Mary Magdalene left and announced to the disciples, Ive seen the Lord. Then she told them what he said to her.
local: Nɩɩ Mɛɛrɩ Magidalen bwii kpee abɩɩlapʋ mʋ asɛ naa tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, N wu Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ! Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ tɔwɛ mʋ mʋ.

eng: It was still the first day of the week. That evening, while the disciples were behind closed doors because they were afraid of the Jewish authorities, Jesus came and stood among them. He said, Peace be with you.
local: Kwasida maŋa kalaatɩpwɛ sʋ mʋ, abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba bʋ obu to, nɩɩ ba tii ɔpʋnʋ mʋ biri bamʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋ waa tɔ, lɩɩ ba maa sɩlɛ Gyiwu abɩlɩsa mʋ sʋ. Nɩɩ Yesu ba yɩlɛ bamʋ nsana, nɩɩ ɔ fwaala bamʋ yɛ, Kayɩɩ yuuli kɩ ba fanɛ asɛ.

eng: After he said this, he showed them his hands and his side. When the disciples saw the Lord, they were filled with joy.
local: Maa tɔwɛ ɩnɩmʋ lʋwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ abaa yɛ mu kikelebi kaapʋ bamʋ. Nɩɩ abɩɩlapʋ mu ansi i gyii fɛɛ ba maa wu Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ.

eng: Jesus said to them again, Peace be with you. As the Father sent me, so I am sending you.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu bɩla tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Kayɩɩ yuuli kɩ ba fanɛ asɛ. Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Mɛ‐Sɛ sun mɛ mʋ, kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ ngbaa mɛ n sun fanɛ.

eng: Then he breathed on them and said, Receive the Holy Spirit.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ fʋʋtɛ bun bamʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Fan kɔɔlɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ.

eng: If you forgive anyones sins, they are forgiven; if you dont forgive them, they arent forgiven.
local: Nɩɩ fan kan taa ɔkʋ alibi kɛɛ mʋ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ gbaa ii biti ɔ taa mu alibi kɛɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fan man kan taa mu alibi kɛɛ mʋ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ gbaa mɛɛ yɛ ɔ taa mu alibi kɛɛ mʋ.

eng: Thomas, the one called Didymus, one of the Twelve, wasnt with the disciples when Jesus came.
local: Tomasi mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ abɩɩlapʋ kudu anyɔ mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ, nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ Kabwaarabi mʋ, ɔ man bʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ asɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Yesu ba bamʋ asɛ mʋ.

eng: The other disciples told him, Weve seen the Lord! But he replied, Unless I see the nail marks in his hands, put my finger in the wounds left by the nails, and put my hand into his side, I wont believe.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ abɩɩlapʋ banban mʋ ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, An wu Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ. Amaa ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Nɩɩ n man kan wu anyenbi alɔpanɛ mʋ abaa tɔ, kɛ n taa mɛ kɩbaa‐bi yii ɩmʋ tɔ, kɛ n taa mɛ kɩbaa yii mʋ kikelebi tɔ mʋ, n mɛɛ yɛ n kɔɔlɛ gyii.

eng: After eight days his disciples were again in a house and Thomas was with them. Even though the doors were locked, Jesus entered and stood among them. He said, Peace be with you.
local: Ɩmʋ nkɛ nsunɔ kamaa mʋ, abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba bɩla ba bʋ kɩkpaara mʋ sʋ, yɛgɛ Tomasi bʋ bamʋ asɛ. Ba tii bamʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋ biri sʋ waa obu to. Ba ba kɩɩ mʋ, Yesu ba yɩlɛ bamʋ nsana, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Kayɩɩ yuula ɩ ba fanɛ sʋ.

eng: Then he said to Thomas, Put your finger here. Look at my hands. Put your hand into my side. No more disbelief. Believe.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ Tomasi yɛ, Taa fʋ kɩbaa‐bi yii nfɩɩ, kɛ fʋ kɩɩ mɛ abaa tɔ. Tɛɛŋɛ fʋ kɩbaa yii mɛ kikelebi tɔ. Yɛgɛ sika‐sika kɩwaa kɛ fʋ kɔɔlɛ gyii.

eng: Thomas responded to Jesus, My Lord and my God.
local: Nɩɩ Tomasi tɔwɛ yɛ, Fʋ ɩ gyɛ mɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ yɛ mɛ Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, Do you believe because you see me? Happy are those who dont see and yet believe.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Tomasi, fʋ maa wu mɛ sʋ nɩɩ fʋ kɔɔlɛ gyii? Kusee ɩ gyɛ sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba man wu mɛ nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ gyii.

eng: Then Jesus did many other miraculous signs in his disciples presence, signs that arent recorded in this scroll.
local: Yesu waa ɩsɩnkaala gaalaagaa abɩɩlapʋ mu ansi tɔ, mʋ nɩɩ ba man kyʋrɔɔ ɩmʋ waa ɔwʋlɛ nɩmʋ tɔ.

eng: But these things are written so that you will believe that Jesus is the Christ, Gods Son, and that believing, you will have life in his name.
local: Amaa ba kyʋrɔɔ ɩnɩmʋ nɩn, kɛ fan nyɛ kɔɔlɛ gyii fɛɛ Yesu ɩ gyɛ Krisito, Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ, nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ Mu‐Bii mʋ. Nɩɩ fan kan kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii mʋ, fen biti fan nyɛ nkpa.

eng: Later, Jesus himself appeared again to his disciples at the Sea of Tiberias. This is how it happened.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ kamaa mʋ, Yesu bɩla lɛɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ kaapʋ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ɔbʋn lala mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ Tiberiya mʋ kɔnɔ. Falɛ nɩɩ ɩ ba.

eng: Simon Peter, Thomas , Nathanael from Cana in Galilee, Zebedees sons, and two other disciples were together.
local: Kakaakʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ akʋ ba baa ba bʋ katɩn kʋlʋn. Bamʋ ɩ gyɛ Simon Pita maa Tomasi mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ Kabwaarabi mʋ, yɛ Nataneli mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ Kena, yɛ Sebedi mu‐bii ana anyɔ yɛ abɩɩlapʋ anyɔ akʋ.

eng: Simon Peter told them, Im going fishing. They said, Well go with you. They set out in a boat, but throughout the night they caught nothing.
local: Nɩɩ Simon Pita tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, N kpee n naa kɩtaa ɩkɩn. Nɩɩ ngbaa bamʋ ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, An biti a buu fʋ kpee. Nɩɩ bamʋ kpini ba naa loo ɔkʋlɩ tɔ, amaa kanɩn kagyanbwɛ maŋa ba man kɩtaa sɛhn.

eng: Early in the morning, Jesus stood on the shore, but the disciples didnt realize it was Jesus.
local: Kayɩ maa kɛ nyenyenfuu mʋ, Yesu ba nɩɩ ɔ yɩlɛ ɔlɩɩkaa, amaa abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba man bɩɩ yɛ mʋ nɩn.

eng: Jesus called to them, Children, have you caught anything to eat? They answered him, No.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ bamʋ yɛ, Mɛ kyɛmɩnɛ ana, fan man kɩtaa ɩkɩn? Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ mʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ayee, a man kɩtaa sɛhn.

eng: He said, Cast your net on the right side of the boat and you will find some. So they did, and there were so many fish that they couldnt haul in the net.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan tʋʋ fanɛ kisewu mʋ ɔkʋlɩ mʋ gyisa sʋ, fen biti fan nyɛ akʋ kɩtaa. Ba maa tʋʋ kisewu mʋ, ba kɩtaa ɩkɩn damantɛ, halɩɩ ba man taalɛ bɩɩtɛ kisewu mʋ waa ɔkʋlɩ mʋ tɔ.

eng: Then the disciple whom Jesus loved said to Peter, Its the Lord! When Simon Peter heard it was the Lord, he wrapped his coat around himself and jumped into the water.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔbɩɩlapʋ mʋ nɩɩ Yesu ii biti mʋ asɩn gaa mʋ tɔwɛ Pita yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ nɩn. Simon maa nu yɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ kaalɛ lala mʋ nɩɩ ɔ wʋla lɛɛ yɩla mʋ bun, nɩɩ ɔ kpɩlɩgɛ loo nkyu mʋ tɔ ba kidenbi sʋ.

eng: The other disciples followed in the boat, dragging the net full of fish, for they werent far from shore, only about one hundred yards.
local: Nɩɩ abɩɩlapʋ asansa mʋ ba palɛ ɔkʋlɩ mʋ buu mʋ, yɛgɛ bɛɛ bɩɩtɛ kisewu mʋ yɛ ɩkɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ kɩmʋ tɔ mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ba maa kebunɔ mʋ ba man bʋ kata. Ɩmʋ kata ɩ waa fɛɛ abaa nswɩɩ tɔ adunuu.

eng: When they landed, they saw a fire there, with fish on it, and some bread.
local: Ba maa dan mʋ, ba wu ɔkɩn maa gyan afʋlɔn‐gyi sʋ yɛgɛ bodobodo dɛ tʋtɔ.

eng: Jesus said to them, Bring some of the fish that youve just caught.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan taa ɩkɩn mʋ nɩɩ fan dan kɩtaa mʋ akʋ ba.

eng: Simon Peter got up and pulled the net to shore. It was full of large fish, one hundred fifty-three of them. Yet the net hadnt torn, even with so many fish.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Simon Pita loo ɔkʋlɩ mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ bɩɩtɛ kisewu mʋ ba kidenbi sʋ. Ɩkɩn lala mʋ nɩɩ ba bɔla kɩmʋ mʋ ba gyɛ kɩlɩfa kʋlʋn yɛ adunuu yɛ asa, amaa halɩɩ ɩkɩn damantɛ mʋ nɩɩ ba bɔla kɩmʋ falɛ gbaa mʋ kisewu mʋ kɩ man kyatɛ.

eng: Jesus said to them, Come and have breakfast. None of the disciples could bring themselves to ask him, Who are you? They knew it was the Lord.
local: Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan ba biri kɔnɔ. Amaa abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ɔkʋ man taalɛ nyɛ kɔkɔlɔ taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Fʋ ɩ gyɛ anɩmʋ? Yɛgɛ be nyi yɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ nɩn.

eng: Jesus came, took the bread, and gave it to them. He did the same with the fish.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu naa taa bodobodo mʋ sa bamʋ, nɩɩ kanɩn gbaa nɩɩ ɔ taa ɔkɩn mʋ sa bamʋ.

eng: This was now the third time Jesus appeared to his disciples after he was raised from the dead.
local: Yesu maa kyiŋi lɩɩ lewu tɔ mʋ, kɩnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ kɩsasapʋ maa lɛɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ kaapʋ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ.

eng: When they finished eating, Jesus asked Simon Peter, Simon son of John, do you love me more than these? Simon replied, Yes, Lord, you know I love you. Jesus said to him, Feed my lambs.
local: Ba maa biri kɔnɔ lʋwɛ mʋ, Yesu taasɛ Simon Pita yɛ, Simon, Gyɔn mu‐bii, fii biti mɛ fʋ kyɔ kanan mʋ nɩɩ banɩmʋ bee biti mɛ mʋ? Nɩɩ Pita lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɛɛhn Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, fu nyi n maa sʋ kebiti sa fʋ. Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Sa mɛ nbʋlʋpʋ‐bi mʋ agyitɔ.

eng: Jesus asked a second time, Simon son of John, do you love me? Simon replied, Yes, Lord, you know I love you. Jesus said to him, Take care of my sheep.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu bɩla taasɛ mʋ kɩnyɔsapʋ yɛ, Simon, Gyɔn mu‐bii, fʋ sʋ kebiti sa mɛ? Nɩɩ Simon lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, fu nyi n maa sʋ kebiti sa fʋ. Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Kɩɩ mɛ nbʋlʋpʋ sʋ.

eng: He asked a third time, Simon son of John, do you love me? Peter was sad that Jesus asked him a third time, Do you love me? He replied, Lord, you know everything; you know I love you. Jesus said to him, Feed my sheep.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ mʋ kɩlɩɩ sasapʋ yɛ, Simon, Gyɔn mu‐bii, fʋ sʋ kebiti sa mɛ? Nɩɩ ɩ tɔɔrɔɔ Pita fɛɛ Yesu maa taasɛ mʋ ɩlɩɩ ɩsa yɛ, Fʋ sʋ kebiti sa mɛ. Nɩɩ Pita tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, fu nyi kʋtɔ kʋmaa, fu nyi n maa sʋ kebiti sa fʋ. Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Sa mɛ nbʋlʋpʋ mʋ agyitɔ.

eng: I assure you that when you were younger you tied your own belt and walked around wherever you wanted. When you grow old, you will stretch out your hands and another will tie your belt and lead you where you dont want to go.
local: N tɔwɛ fʋ kesintin, saŋa mʋ nɩɩ fʋ gyɛ kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ, fii bun fʋ atɔ bunsa, nɩɩ fii kpee katɩn kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fii biti, amaa fʋ ba waa kɩnyɩn bɩlɩsa mʋ, fɩɩ tɛɛŋɛ fʋ abaa tɔ kɛ ɔkʋ o bun fʋ, kɛ ɔ taa fʋ kpee katɩn mʋ nɩɩ fii mee biti.

eng: He said this to show the kind of death by which Peter would glorify God. After saying this, Jesus said to Peter, Follow me.
local: Yesu tɔwɛ ɩnɩmʋ nɩn kaapʋ lewu mʋ nɩɩ Pita ii biti o be wu mu kedubi, kɛ ɩ lɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ bʋnyaa kaapʋ. Yesu maa tɔwɛ ɩnɩmʋ lʋwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Buu mɛ.

eng: Peter turned around and saw the disciple whom Jesus loved following them. This was the one who had leaned against Jesus at the meal and asked him, Lord, who is going to betray you.
local: Pita maa biliŋi mu ansi kɩɩ mʋ, o wu fɛɛ ɔbɩɩlapʋ mʋ nɩɩ Yesu sʋ kebiti sa mʋ mʋ, buu bamʋ. Mʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛ ɔ kpɛɛ Yesu saŋa mʋ nɩɩ bee gyii kalaatɩpwɛ sʋ agyitɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ Yesu yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, anɩmʋ ii biti ɔ lɛɛ fʋ sa mʋ.

eng: When Peter saw this disciple, he said to Jesus, Lord, what about him.
local: Pita maa wu mʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, ɔnɩmʋ mɔɔ de.

eng: Jesus replied, If I want him to remain until I come, what difference does that make to you? You must follow me.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Nɩɩ n kan n biti yɛ ɔ baa ɔ tɛ nkpa tɔ naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ n biti n bwii ba mʋ, ɩ gyɛ fʋ asɩn? Fɛɛ fʋ buu mɛ.

eng: Therefore, the word spread among the brothers and sisters that this disciple wouldnt die. However, Jesus didnt say he wouldnt die, but only, If I want him to remain until I come, what difference does that make to you.
local: Lɩɩ falɛ sʋ mʋ, asɩn nɩmʋ a yaasɛ tɔ loo kpee Krisito abuupu kpini asɛ fɛɛ, ɔbɩɩlapʋ nɩmʋ man bʋ tɔ o wu. Amaa Yesu man tɔwɛ yɛ ɔ mɛɛ yɛ o wu. Asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, Nɩɩ n kan n biti yɛ ɔ baa ɔ tɛ nkpa tɔ naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ mʋ nɩɩ n biti n bwii ba mʋ, ɩ gyɛ fʋ asɩn.

eng: This is the disciple who testifies concerning these things and who wrote them down. We know that his testimony is true.
local: Ɔbɩɩlapʋ nɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ mʋ nɩɩ ee gyii asɩn nɩmʋ kesintin, nɩɩ mʋ ɩ kyʋrɔɔ ɩmʋ yɩla. Nɩɩ an nyi fɛɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ gyɛ kesintin.

eng: Jesus did many other things as well. If all of them were recorded, I imagine the world itself wouldnt have enough room for the scrolls that would be written.
local: Yesu waa atɔ gaalaagaa tii sʋ. Nɩɩ ba daa kyʋrɔɔ ɩmʋ kpini yɩla nɩn, naafɔɔ ɩwʋlɛ mʋ ii biti i tiri dulinyaa tɔ, nɩɩ ɩ mɛɛ yɛ ɩ nyɛ opula yɩlakpa.

eng: When Jesus finished saying these things, he looked up to heaven and said, Father, the time has come. Glorify your Son, so that the Son can glorify you.
local: Yesu maa tɔwɛ ɩnɩmʋ lʋwɛ mʋ, ɔ kʋsaa mu kumu kɩɩ sʋsʋ nɩɩ ɔ kʋlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ, Nsɛ saŋa mʋ fʋʋ. Sa mɛ, Fu‐bii mʋ bʋnyaa, kɛ mɛ, Fu‐bii mʋ gbaa n sa fʋ bʋnyaa.

eng: You gave him authority over everyone so that he could give eternal life to everyone you gave him.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ fʋ sa mɛ ɔlʋn lɩɩ asa kpini sʋ, fɛɛ n sa asa mʋ nɩɩ fʋ taa bɔɔta mɛ mʋ kpini nkpa kakpaa.

eng: This is eternal life: to know you, the only true God, and Jesus Christ whom you sent.
local: Nkpa kakpaa mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ asa ba bɩɩ fʋ, kesintin Ɩbwaarɛ kʋlʋn mʋ, kɛ ba bɩɩ mɛ, Yesu Krisito mʋ nɩɩ fu sun mʋ.

eng: I have glorified you on earth by finishing the work you gave me to do.
local: N bɩya fʋ bʋnyaa kasɛ sʋ nfɩɩ, lɩɩ n maa lʋwɛ fu kusun mʋ nɩɩ fʋ sa mɛ yɛ n ba waa mʋ sʋ.

eng: Theophilus, the first scroll I wrote concerned everything Jesus did and taught from the beginning.
local: Ɔwʋlɛ nɩmʋ lɩɩ mɛ Luka asɛ kɩ ba fʋ Tiyofilosi asɛ. Mɛ ɔwʋlɛ gyankpaasa mʋ tɔ mʋ, n kyʋrɔɔ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ Yesu kaapʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa mʋ kpini, lɩɩ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ o yii mu kusun kasɛ mʋ.

eng: Now, Father, glorify me in your presence with the glory I shared with you before the world was created.
local: Mɛ‐Sɛ, sa mɛ bʋnyaa fu ansi tɔ nbɩɩnbɩɩ nɩmʋ, bʋnyaa mʋ nɩɩ n wʋla n sʋ fʋ asɛ pɔyɩ nɩɩ fʋ tʋʋ dulinyaa mʋ.

eng: right up to the day when he was taken up into heaven. Before he was taken up, working in the power of the Holy Spirit, Jesus instructed the apostles he had chosen.
local: naa fʋʋ kakɛ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ taa mʋ kyʋn sʋsʋ mʋ. Pɔyɩ kɛ ɔ taa mʋ kpee sʋsʋ mʋ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu bɔla Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ sʋ kaapʋ mu asunpu mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ bamʋ mʋ, atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba waa.

eng: I have revealed your name to the people you gave me from this world. They were yours and you gave them to me, and they have kept your word.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu kyaga sʋ yɛ, N lɛɛ fu kedubi kaapʋ bamʋ nɩɩ fʋ lɛɛ bamʋ lɩɩ dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ sa mɛ mʋ. Ba daa ba gyɛ fʋ lɛɛ nɩn, nɩɩ fʋ taa bamʋ sa mɛ. Nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ fʋ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba buu ɩmʋ.

eng: After his suffering, he showed them that he was alive with many convincing proofs. He appeared to them over a period of forty days, speaking to them about Gods kingdom.
local: Yesu kelewu kamaa nkɛ adɩna mʋ tɔ mʋ, ɔ lɛɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ kaapʋ mu asunpu fɛɛ ɔ tɛ nkpa tɔ, nɩɩ ɩ man sʋ kamɔɔrɛ‐gyii. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii mʋ asɩn.

eng: Now they know that everything you have given me comes from you.
local: Nbɩɩnbɩɩ bɩrɛ, ba bɩɩ fɛɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ n sʋ mʋ lɩɩ fʋ asɛ nɩn.

eng: While they were eating together, he ordered them not to leave Jerusalem but to wait for what the Father had promised. He said, This is what you heard from me.
local: Maa bamʋ ba maa nsarɛ bee gyii kakaakʋ mʋ, ɔ sa bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Fan man kan waa lɩɩ Gyerusalem, amaa fan gyoo fanɛ kɛkɛɛ mʋ nɩɩ n wʋla tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, Nsɛ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ yɛ ee biti ɔ sa fanɛ mʋ.

eng: This is because I gave them the words that you gave me, and they received them. They truly understood that I came from you, and they believed that you sent me.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ n tɔwɛ bamʋ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ fʋ sa mɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ. Ba bɩɩ kesintin tɔ fɛɛ n lɩɩ fʋ asɛ, nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ gyii fɛɛ fu i sun mɛ.

eng: John baptized with water, but in only a few days you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit.
local: Nkyu nɩɩ Gyɔn sʋ ee gyee fanɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee, amaa nkɛ gbɛrɛɛ mʋ nɩɩ ii biti ɩ ba falɛ tɔ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ taa mʋ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ gyee fanɛ.

eng: Im praying for them. Im not praying for the world but for those you gave me, because they are yours.
local: N kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ n sa bamʋ. N mɛɛ kʋlɛ n sa dulinyaa abi, amaa bamʋ nɩɩ fʋ taa sa mɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ba gyɛ fʋ lɛɛ mʋ.

eng: As a result, those who had gathered together asked Jesus, Lord, are you going to restore the kingdom to Israel now.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ asunpu mʋ ba maa Yesu ba bwii sarɛ mʋ, ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, nbɩɩnbɩɩ nɩɩ fii biti fu bwii taa kuwura‐gyii mʋ sa anɛ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi abɛɛ.

eng: Everything that is mine is yours and everything that is yours is mine; I have been glorified in them.
local: Asa mʋ nɩɩ ba gyɛ mɛ lɛɛ mʋ kpini, ba gyɛ fʋ lɛɛ, nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba gyɛ fʋ lɛɛ mʋ ba gyɛ mɛ lɛɛ. Lɩɩ bamʋ sʋ nɩɩ mɛ bʋnyaa mʋ lɩɩ ifuli.

eng: Jesus replied, It isnt for you to know the times or seasons that the Father has set by his own authority.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Saŋa abɛɛ kakɛ yɛ mʋ awaasa mʋ kpini mʋ, mɛ‐Sɛ ɩ yɩla ɩmʋ lɩɩ mʋ ɔlʋn tɔ sʋ, ɩmʋ sʋ ɩ man tiri fɛɛ fan bɩɩ saŋa mʋ.

eng: Im no longer in the world, but they are in the world, even as Im coming to you. Holy Father, watch over them in your name, the name you gave me, that they will be one just as we are one.
local: N man bɩla yɛ n kyɩna dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ, n ba fʋ asɛ nɩn. Amaa bɛɛ bamʋ bɩrɛ ba san ba bʋ dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ. Nsɛ Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ taa fʋ kɩtɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ fʋ sa mɛ ɔlʋn mʋ kun bamʋ, kɛ ba nyɛ waa kʋkʋlʋn fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ maa fʋ an gyɛ kʋkʋlʋn mʋ.

eng: Rather, you will receive power when the Holy Spirit has come upon you, and you will be my witnesses in Jerusalem, in all Judea and Samaria, and to the end of the earth.
local: Amaa nɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ kan ba fanɛ sʋ mʋ, fen biti fan nyɛ ɔlʋn kɛ fan naa tɔwɛ mɛ asɩn mʋ Gyerusalem tɔ yɛ Gyudeya kpini yɛ Samariya tɔ, taa naa fʋʋ dulinyaa ɔkaa.

eng: When I was with them, I watched over them in your name, the name you gave to me, and I kept them safe. None of them were lost, except the one who was destined for destruction, so that scripture would be fulfilled.
local: Maa kyɩna n bʋ bamʋ asɛ mʋ, kɩtɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ fʋ sa mɛ mʋ, kɩmʋ ɔlʋn tɔ nɩɩ n taa kɩɩ bamʋ sʋ, nɩɩ n kun bamʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ bamʋ tɔ ɔkʋ man fɔyɩ, amɔɔ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ dagaa fɛɛ ɔ fɔyɩ mʋ, kɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ ɩ ba tɔ.

eng: After Jesus said these things, as they were watching, he was lifted up and a cloud took him out of their sight.
local: Maa tɔwɛ ɩ nɩmʋ kamaa mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ taa mʋ kpee sʋsʋ bamʋ ansi tɔ, nɩɩ abwaarɛ‐wʋlɛ a bun mʋ sʋ lɩɩ bamʋ ansi sʋ.

eng: Now Im coming to you and I say these things while Im in the world so that they can share completely in my joy.
local: Nbɩɩnbɩɩ n ba fʋ asɛ nɩmʋ. Amaa maa san n bʋ dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ mʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ ɩnɩmʋ, kɛ ba nyɛ mɛ kensi‐gyii bamʋ tɔ danbɩrasa naa lʋwɛ.

eng: While he was going away and as they were staring toward heaven, suddenly two men in white robes stood next to them.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ sʋ mʋ ii kpee yɛgɛ bɛɛ kɩɩ sʋsʋ haa mʋ, opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ anyɩn anyɔ akʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba bun atɔ fufuli ba ba yɩlɛ bamʋ asɛ.

eng: I gave your word to them and the world hated them, because they dont belong to this world, just as I dont belong to this world.
local: N taa fʋ asɩn mʋ sa bamʋ, nɩɩ dulinyaa abi ba kisi bamʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ba man bɩla ba gyɛ dulinyaa tɔ abi, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ n man gyɛ dulinyaa tɔ obii mʋ.

eng: They said, Galileans, why are you standing here, looking toward heaven? This Jesus, who was taken up from you into heaven, will come in the same way that you saw him go into heaven.
local: nɩɩ ba taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Galile abi, mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fan yɩlɛ nfɩɩ fɛn kɩɩ abwaarɛ‐wʋlɛ tɔ kanɩn? Yesu mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ taa mʋ lɩɩ fanɛ asɛ kpee sʋsʋ mʋ, kanɩn nɩɩ ee biti o bwii ba fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fan kɩɩ mʋ maa kpee sʋsʋ falɛ.

eng: Im not asking that you take them out of this world but that you keep them safe from the evil one.
local: N mɛɛ kʋlɛ fʋ nɩn yɛ fʋ lɛɛ bamʋ lɩɩ dulinyaa tɔ, amaa n kʋlɛ nɩn yɛ fu kun bamʋ lɩɩ Sɩtaanɛ sʋ.

eng: Then they returned to Jerusalem from the Mount of Olives, which is near Jerusalem—a sabbath days journey away.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ asunpu mʋ ba lɩɩ Olifu kɩbɩɩ mʋ asɛ bwii ba Gyerusalem, ɩ man bʋ kata kpini‐kpini.

eng: They dont belong to this world, just as I dont belong to this world.
local: Ba man gyɛ dulinyaa nɩmʋ lɛɛ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ n man gyɛ kɩmʋ lɛɛ mʋ.

eng: When they entered the city, they went to the upstairs room where they were staying. Peter, John, James, and Andrew; Philip and Thomas; Bartholomew and Matthew; James, Alphaeus son; Simon the zealot; and Judas, James son.
local: Ba maa loo Gyerusalem kadɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ, ba dii naa loo abansʋrʋ obu, mʋ nɩɩ ba wʋla ba bʋ kɩmʋ tɔ mʋ. Asunpu mʋ ɩ gyɛ Pita maa Gyɔn, yɛ Gyemisi yɛ Andiruwu yɛ Filipu yɛ Tomasi yɛ Batolome yɛ Matiwu yɛ Alifeyo mu‐bii Gyemisi maa Simon Seloti mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba kɔ bee kun bamʋ ɔsʋwʋlɛ mʋ bamʋ ɔkʋ, yɛ Gyemisi mu‐bii Gyudasi.

eng: Make them holy in the truth; your word is truth.
local: Taa fʋ asɩn kesintin lɔŋɔ bamʋ yɩla, lɩɩ fɛɛ fʋ asɩn mʋ ɩ gyɛ kesintin.

eng: all were united in their devotion to prayer, along with some women, including Mary the mother of Jesus, and his brothers.
local: Bamʋ kpini ba taalɛ ba bʋga saŋa kʋmaa yɛ akyɩɩ akʋ yɛ Yesu mu‐nyi yɛ mu‐supu ana.

eng: As you sent me into the world, so I have sent them into the world.
local: Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fu sun mɛ dulinyaa tɔ mʋ, kanɩn nɩɩ ngbaa mɛ n sun bamʋ dulinyaa tɔ.

eng: During this time, the family of believers was a company of about one hundred twenty persons. Peter stood among them and said.
local: Nkɛ nfɩnɛ nkʋ kamaa tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ Krisito akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu bɛɛ waa fɛɛ asa kɩlɩfa yɛ adʋnyɔ ba ba sarɛ, nɩɩ Pita kʋsʋ yɩlɛ.

eng: I made myself holy on their behalf so that they also would be made holy in the truth.
local: Lɩɩ bamʋ sʋ nɩɩ n taa mɛ n‐yɩɩ sa fʋ, kɛ ngbaa bamʋ ba nyɛ taa bamʋ n‐yɩɩ sa fʋ kesintin tɔ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, the scripture that the Holy Spirit announced beforehand through David had to be fulfilled. This was the scripture concerning Judas, who became a guide for those who arrested Jesus.
local: nɩɩ o yii kasɛ yɛ, Mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana, asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ bɔla owura Dawidi sʋ wʋla tɔwɛ yɩla yɛ Gyudasi ii biti ɔ gyankpaa kɛ ba kɩtaa Yesu mʋ, ɩ dagaa ɩ ba kanɩn.

eng: Im not praying only for them but also for those who believe in me because of their word.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu bɩla kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ, Man gyɛ mɛ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ nkʋn nɩɩ n kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ n sa. N kʋlɛ n sa bamʋ nɩɩ bee biti ba be nu mɛ asɩn lɩɩ bamʋ asɛ kɛ ba kɔɔlɛ mɛ gyii mʋ gbaa.

eng: This happened even though he was one of us and received a share of this ministry. .
local: Gyudasi kyɩna ɔ gyɛ anɛ kʋbʋ kudu‐anyɔ mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ nɩn, nɩɩ ɔ sʋ ɔyɩlɛkpa kusun mʋ tɔ.

eng: I pray they will be one, Father, just as you are in me and I am in you. I pray that they also will be in us, so that the world will believe that you sent me.
local: Nsɛ, mɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɩ gyɛ yɛ bamʋ kpini ba waa kʋkʋlʋn, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fʋ bʋ mɛ tɔ nɩɩ ngbaa n bʋ fʋ tɔ mʋ. N kʋlɛ fʋ yɛ, ba baa ba bʋ anɛ tɔ, kɛ dulinyaa abi ba kɔɔlɛ gyii yɛ fu i sun mɛ.

eng: In fact, he bought a field with the payment he received for his injustice. Falling headfirst, he burst open in the middle and all his intestines spilled out.
local: Afulee mʋ nɩɩ Gyudasi nyɛ lɩɩ mu alibi mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa sʋ mʋ, ɔ sɔɔ kasɛ, katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɩyɛ ketulaamu nɩɩ o wu, ɔ patɩyɛ nɩɩ mʋ ipuni kpini ɩ patɩyɛ lɩɩ.

eng: Ive given them the glory that you gave me so that they can be one just as we are one.
local: N sa bamʋ bʋnyaa mʋ nɩɩ fʋ sa mɛ mʋ, kɛ ba nyɛ waa kʋkʋlʋn fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ an gyɛ kʋkʋlʋn mʋ.

eng: This became known to everyone living in Jerusalem, so they called that field in their own language Hakeldama, or Field of Blood.
local: Gyerusalem abi kpini ba nu asɩn nɩmʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ ba yɩla ɔsʋwʋlɛ mʋ kɩtɩɩ bamʋ katɔwɛ tɔ yɛ Akeledama, kasɛ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, Nkalan kasɛ. .

eng: Im in them and you are in me so that they will be made perfectly one. Then the world will know that you sent me and that you have loved them just as you loved me.
local: N ba n bʋ bamʋ tɔ yɛgɛ fʋ bʋ mɛ tɔ, kɛ ba bʋga waa kɔnɔ kʋlʋn naa lʋwɛ. Ɩnɩmʋ ii biti ɩ yɛgɛ dulinyaa abi ba bɩɩ fɛɛ fu i sun mɛ, nɩɩ fʋ sʋ kebiti sa bamʋ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fʋ sʋ kebiti sa mɛ mʋ.

eng: It is written in the Psalms scroll, Let his home become deserted and let there be no one living in it; and Give his position of leadership to another. .
local: Nɩɩ Pita tɔwɛ yɛ owura Dawidi wʋla kyʋrɔɔ Gyudasi asɩn waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ yɛ, Mʋ kɩkpaara ki biliŋi kɩtakpan, kɛ ɔkʋ ɔ man kyɩna kɩmʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ Dawidi bɩla tɔwɛ yɛ, Ba yɛgɛ kɛ ɔkʋ o yii mu kusun kɩbɩlɩsa mʋ ayaa tɔ.

eng: Father, I want those you gave me to be with me where I am. Then they can see my glory, which you gave me because you loved me before the creation of the world.
local: Nsɛ n biti fɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ fʋ taa sa mɛ mʋ, ba baa ba bʋ mɛ asɛ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ n bʋ mʋ. Kɛ ba wu mɛ bʋnyaa mʋ nɩɩ fʋ taa sa mɛ mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ fʋ sʋ kebiti sa mɛ pɔyɩ nɩɩ fʋ tʋʋ dulinyaa mʋ.

eng: Therefore, we must select one of those who have accompanied us during the whole time the Lord Jesus lived among us.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ a lɛɛ anɛ asa mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ ba tii anɛ sʋ. An biti ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ wʋla ɔ bʋ anɛ asɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ anaa Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu an dii an kpɩlɩgɛ mʋ.

eng: Righteous Father, even the world didnt know you, but Ive known you, and these believers know that you sent me.
local: Nsɛ, ɩkpa kyiigisa wuya, dulinyaa abi ba maa man nyi fʋ, amaa mɛɛ mɛ n nyi fʋ, nɩɩ mi abuupu mʋ be nyi fɛɛ fu i sun mɛ.

eng: beginning from the baptism of John until the day when Jesus was taken from us. This person must become along with us a witness to his resurrection.
