local: lɩɩ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Gyɔn okyugyeepu gyee asa Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee, naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ taa Yesu kpee sʋsʋ. Ɩ dagaa fɛɛ kanɩn asa maŋa tɔ ɔkʋ maa tii anɛ sʋ fɛɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu kelewu kyiŋi kesintin ogyipu.

eng: Ive made your name known to them and will continue to make it known so that your love for me will be in them, and I myself will be in them.
local: N lɛɛ fu kedubi kaapʋ bamʋ, nɩɩ n biti n kyaga sʋ lɛɛ fʋ kaapʋ bamʋ, kɛ fu kebiti mʋ nɩɩ fʋ sʋ sa mɛ mʋ kɩ baa kɩ bʋ bamʋ tɔ, kɛ ngbaa mɛ n baa n bʋ bamʋ tɔ.

eng: So they nominated two: Joseph called Barsabbas, who was also known as Justus, and Matthias.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ba lɛɛ asa anyɔ yɛ ba yɩlɛ. Bamʋ ɩ gyɛ Gyosefu mʋ nɩɩ bɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Basabasi, nɩɩ ba bɩla bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ Gyusitu, nɩɩ ɔbanban mʋ mɔɔ gyɛ Matiyasi.

eng: They prayed, Lord, you know everyones deepest thoughts and desires. Show us clearly which one you have chosen from among these two.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, fu nyi ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ. Ɩmʋ sʋ fii biti fʋ kaapʋ anɛ asa anyɔ anɩmʋ tɔ ɔmʋ nɩɩ fʋ lɛɛ.

eng: to take the place of this ministry and apostleship, from which Judas turned away to go to his own place.
local: Kɛ ɔ ba waa fu asunpu mʋ kusun mʋ nɩɩ Gyudasi yɛgɛ yɩla mʋ, kpee katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa mʋ mʋ.

eng: When they cast lots, the lot fell on Matthias. He was added to the eleven apostles.
local: Nɩɩ ba tʋʋ nbii biti ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ ba tii bamʋ sʋ, nɩɩ Matiyasi gyii, nɩɩ ba taa mʋ tii bamʋ asunpu kudu‐ɔkʋ mʋ sʋ.

eng: While Peter and John were speaking to the people, the priests, the captain of the temple guard, and the Sadducees confronted them.
local: Pita maa Gyɔn ba san ba maa sʋ bɛɛ tɔwɛ asa mʋ asɛ mʋ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu akʋ, yɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ akunpu mʋ ɔbɩlɩsa yɛ, Sadusi abi ba ba fʋʋ tʋtɔ.

eng: They were incensed that the apostles were teaching the people and announcing that the resurrection of the dead was happening because of Jesus.
local: Nɩɩ ba nyɛ agbʋ waa Pita maa Gyɔn fɛɛ ba maa kaapʋ bɛ tɔwɛ asa mʋ yɛ Yesu bwii kyiŋi ba nkpa tɔ, nɩɩ ɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ kelewu kyiŋi kɩ bʋ tɔ sa asa kpini.

eng: They seized Peter and John and put them in prison until the next day.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ba kɩtaa Pita maa Gyɔn naa tii bamʋ obu naa fʋʋ kayɩkɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ kyɔwɛ maa tɛɛ lʋwɛ sʋ mʋ ba tii bamʋ obu naa fʋʋ kayɩkɛ.

eng: Many who heard the word became believers, and their number grew to about five thousand.
local: Amaa asa gaalaagaa mʋ nɩɩ ba nu Yesu Krisito asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ, ba kɔɔlɛ gyii nɩɩ kʋbʋ mʋ kɩ nyɛ akyɔ bɛɛ waa fɛɛ anyɩn ngbɩn‐nnuu.

eng: The next day the leaders, elders, and legal experts gathered in Jerusalem.
local: Lɩɩkaakɛ mʋ Gyiwu abi agyankpaapʋ yɛ abɩlɩsa yɛ Mosesi nbara akaapʋpʋ ba naa sarɛ Gyerusalem.

eng: along with Annas the high priest, Caiaphas, John, Alexander, and others from the high priests family.
local: Bamʋ nɩɩ ba sarɛ tʋtɔ ɩ gyɛ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ Anasi yɛ Kefasi yɛ Gyɔn yɛ Alekisanda yɛ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ, mʋ kʋʋpʋ ana.

eng: They had Peter and John brought before them and asked, By what power or in what name did you do this.
local: Ba taa Pita maa Gyɔn ba ba yɩlɛ bamʋ ansi tɔ, nɩɩ ba taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Mɩnɛ ɔlʋn nɩɩ fan nyɛ abɛɛ anɩmʋ kɩtɩɩ nɩɩ fan sʋ fan waa ɩnɩmʋ falɛ.

eng: Then Peter, inspired by the Holy Spirit, answered, Leaders of the people and elders.
local: Tʋtɔ mʋ, Pita mʋ nɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ kɩ bɔla mʋ tɔ mʋ lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Agyankpaapʋ yɛ abɩlɩsa.

eng: are we being examined today because something good was done for a sick person, a good deed that healed him?.
local: fan taasɛ anɛ ndaga asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ nɩɩ an waa boobi nɩmʋ, nɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ alanfɩya.

eng: If so, then you and all the people of Israel need to know that this man stands healthy before you because of the name of Jesus Christ the Nazarene—whom you crucified but whom God raised from the dead.
local: Kanɩn sʋ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ fanɛ yɛ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi kpini fan bɩɩ fɛɛ ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ yɩlɛ nfɩɩ falɛ, Yesu Krisito mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ Nasareti, ɔmʋ nɩɩ fan da mʋ mata oyu laasa sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bwii kyiŋi mʋ ba nkpa tɔ mʋ, ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ alanfɩya.

eng: This Jesus is the stone you builders rejected; he has become the cornerstone!.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu ɩ gyɛ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ ɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, Kubuu mʋ nɩɩ fanɛ, apɔyɩpʋ fan kina yɛ kɩ man bʋrɔn mʋ, kanɩn kubuu maŋa ɩ ba biliŋi kubuu kparɛ ɩmʋ kpini tɔ.

eng: Salvation can be found in no one else. Throughout the whole world, no other name has been given among humans through which we must be saved.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ man bʋ tɔ nɩɩ ɛɛ taalɛ mɔlɩgɛ asa, amɔɔ Yesu nkʋn, lɩɩ fɛɛ kɩtɩɩ pʋpwɛ kʋ man bɩla ɔ bʋ dulinyaa tɔ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ taa sa yɛ kɩ mɔlɩgɛ anɛ.

eng: The council was caught by surprise by the confidence with which Peter and John spoke. After all, they understood that these apostles were uneducated and inexperienced. They also recognized that they had been followers of Jesus.
local: Ɩ waa abɩlɩsa mʋ kpini kanankʋ fɛɛ Pita maa Gyɔn ba maa yɩlɛ kɩnyɩn tɔ, nɩɩ ba bɩɩ fɛɛ ba man gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ be nyi ɩwʋlɛ. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba wu fɛɛ ba wʋla ba gyɛ Yesu abuupu nɩn.

eng: However, since the healed man was standing with Peter and John before their own eyes, they had no rebuttal.
local: Kpalɩ fɛɛ ba maa wu boobi mʋ nɩɩ ba kyɛ mʋ, maa yɩlɛ Pita maa Gyɔn asɛ mʋ, ba man taalɛ nyɛ asɩn kʋ ba tɔwɛ.

eng: After ordering them to wait outside, the council members began to confer with each other.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ba dan sii tɔwɛ bamʋ nɩn yɛ ba lɩɩ nsarɛ obu mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ba sii ba taa Pita maa Gyɔn sʋ nfɛɛrɛ.

eng: What should we do with these men? Everyone living in Jerusalem is aware of the sign performed through them. Its obvious to everyone and we cant deny it.
local: Nɩɩ ba taasɛ abaa yɛ, Nɛnɛ nɩɩ an biti a waa asa anɩmʋ? Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ Gyerusalem nyi yɛ bamʋ ɩ waa asɩn kyinkyinsa nɩmʋ, nɩɩ a mɛɛ taalɛ gyii ɩ mʋ ɩmɔɔrɛ.

eng: To keep it from spreading further among the people, we need to warn them not to speak to anyone in this name.
local: Amaa pɔyɩ kɛ a tii asɩn nɩmʋ kɛ ɩ man naa lɩɩ asa asɛ kata‐kata mʋ, a da bamʋ amu tɔ yɛ, ɩ nɩmʋ i gyii bamʋ ɔkaa, ba man bɩla taa Yesu kɩtɩɩ asɩn tɔwɛ sa asa.

eng: When they called Peter and John back, they demanded that they stop all speaking and teaching in the name of Jesus.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ ba bwii tɩɩ Pita maa Gyɔn ba lenbu mʋ tɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ kakaakʋ ba man bɩla lɩɩ tɔwɛ abɛɛ kaapʋ lɩɩ Yesu kɩtɩɩ tɔ.

eng: Peter and John responded, Its up to you to determine whether its right before God to obey you rather than God.
local: Amaa Pita maa Gyɔn ba lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ nɩn yɛ, Fanɛ gbaa‐gbaa fanɛ fan kɩɩ damu maŋa tɔ ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋrɔn lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ansi tɔ. An nu fanɛ asɛ abɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ?.

eng: As for us, we cant stop speaking about what we have seen and heard.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ a mɛɛ taalɛ yɛgɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ anɛ gbaa‐gbaa anɛ an wu nɩɩ an nu mʋ kɩtɔwɛ.

eng: They threatened them further, then released them. Because of public support for Peter and John, they couldnt find a way to punish them. Everyone was praising God for what had happened.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ abɩlɩsa mʋ ba lɔŋɔ da Pita maa Gyɔn amu tɔ kenken nɩɩ ba yɛgɛ bamʋ yɛ ba natɛ. Ba man nyɛ ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ ba yɩlɛ sʋ taa bɩɩtɛ bamʋ asʋ, kpalɩ fɛɛ asa kpini ba maa yɩn Ɩbwaarɛ lɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba waa mʋ sʋ.

eng: because the man who had experienced this sign of healing was over years old.
local: Ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba waa asɩn kyinkyinsa kyɛ mʋ mʋ, gyii ɔ kyɔ nsu adɩna.

eng: After their release, Peter and John returned to the brothers and sisters and reported everything the chief priests and elders had said.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba dan yɛgɛ Pita maa Gyɔn mʋ, ba bwii kpee bamʋ kyɛmɩnɛ ana akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ asɛ naa tɔwɛ bamʋ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ abɩlɩsa yɛ aseepu‐bɩlɩsa mʋ ba tɔwɛ.

eng: They listened, then lifted their voices in unison to God, Master, you are the one who created the heaven, the earth, the sea, and everything in them.
local: Akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ ba maa dan nu kanɩn mʋ, nɩɩ bamʋ kpini ba waa kɔnɔ kʋlʋn kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ, fʋ mʋ nɩɩ fʋ tʋʋ sʋsʋ yɛ kasɛ yɛ ɔpʋ yɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ ɩmʋ tɔ kpini.

eng: You are the one who spoke by the Holy Spirit through our ancestor David, your servant: Why did the Gentiles rage , and the peoples plot in vain? .
local: Fʋ bɔla Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ sʋ nɩɩ fʋ yɛgɛ anɛ naana Dawidi, fʋ kɩyaafɔlɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Mɩnɛ ɩ waa sʋ nɩɩ ndɛndɛmandɛ bɛɛ nyɛ agbʋ? Mɩnɛ ɩ waa sʋ nɩɩ asa anɩmʋ bɛɛ fɛ nfɛɛrɛ libi kanɩn?.

eng: The kings of the earth took their stand and the rulers gathered together as one against the Lord and against his Christ. .
local: Kasɛ sʋ awura bɛɛ kʋʋla bamʋ n‐yɩɩ kʋsʋ yɩlɛ, nɩɩ akɩɩsʋpʋ mɔɔ bɛɛ sarɛ bɛɛ kʋsʋ yɩlɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ maa Ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ mʋ sʋ.

eng: Indeed, both Herod and Pontius Pilate, with Gentiles and Israelites, did gather in this city against your holy servant Jesus, whom you anointed.
local: Kesintin, asɩn nɩmʋ a ba tɔ fɛɛ Herodi maa Pontiyu Paleti yɛ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi yɛ ndɛndɛmandɛ ba sarɛ kadɛ nɩmʋ tɔ, bee ŋmina agyina kʋsʋ yɩlɛ Yesu sʋ fʋ Kɩyaafɔlɛ Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ nɩɩ fʋ lɛɛ mʋ.

eng: They did what your power and plan had already determined would happen.
local: Kesintin ba sarɛ kanɩn nɩn ba ba waa kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ lɩɩ fʋ ɔlʋn sʋ, yɛ fʋ kɔkɔlɔ biti mʋ nɩɩ fʋ wʋla paa yɛ ɩ waa mʋ.

eng: Now, Lord, take note of their threats and enable your servants to speak your word with complete confidence.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, nbɩɩnbɩɩ bɩɩ aŋaala mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ ŋaala anɛ falɛ kɛ fʋ sa anɛ, fʋ ayaafɔlɛ kɔkɔlɔ ɔsɩɩ kɛ a taalɛ tɔwɛ fʋ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ.

eng: Stretch out your hand to bring healing and enable signs and wonders to be performed through the name of Jesus, your holy servant.
local: Tɛɛŋɛ fʋ abaa kɛ alɔpʋ ba nyɛ alanfɩya, kɛ fʋ waa adangana yɛ asɩn kyinkyinsa bɔla fʋ kɩyaafɔlɛ kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ Yesu kɩtɩɩ sʋ waa kaapʋ asa.

eng: After they prayed, the place where they were gathered was shaken. They were all filled with the Holy Spirit and began speaking Gods word with confidence.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ lʋwɛ mʋ, opula mʋ nɩɩ ba sarɛ mʋ gyingyen. Nɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ loo bɔla bamʋ kpini nɩɩ ba piili bɛ tɔwɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn mʋ yɛ kɔkɔlɔ ɔsɩɩ.

eng: The community of believers was one in heart and mind. None of them would say, This is mine! about any of their possessions, but held everything in common.
local: Akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu kʋbʋ mʋ kpini ba waa nfɛɛrɛ kʋlʋn yɛ kɔkɔlɔ kʋlʋn. Bamʋ ɔkʋ mɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ mʋ gyɛ mʋ lɛɛ. Kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ mʋ gyɛ bamʋ kpini lɛɛ.

eng: The apostles continued to bear powerful witness to the resurrection of the Lord Jesus, and an abundance of grace was at work among them all.
local: Asunpu mʋ ba tɔwɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu Krisito kelewu kyiŋi mʋ asɩn ba kaapʋ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔlʋn lala mʋ sʋ. Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ waa bamʋ kpini kusee.

eng: There were no needy persons among them. Those who owned properties or houses would sell them, bring the proceeds from the sales.
local: Ɔkʋ man bʋ bamʋ kʋbʋ mʋ tɔ nɩɩ kʋtɔ kʋ ki tiri mʋ kpaa. Lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ kasɛ abɛɛ akpaara mʋ, ba fɛ ɩmʋ yɛgɛ ba sʋ afulee mʋ ba ba.

eng: and place them in the care and under the authority of the apostles. Then it was distributed to anyone who was in need.
local: ba yɩla asunpu mʋ kagya kasɛ, nɩɩ ba barɩgɛ ɩmʋ tɔ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ i tiri ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ.

eng: Joseph, whom the apostles nicknamed Barnabas , was a Levite from Cyprus.
local: Kanɩn nɩɩ Gyosefu, mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ Lewi kebunɔ, nɩɩ ba kʋʋgɛ mʋ Sipuru, nɩɩ asunpu mʋ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Banabasi, .

eng: He owned a field, sold it, brought the money, and placed it in the care and under the authority of the apostles.
local: Mʋ gbaa mʋ fɛ mʋ kasɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taa afulee mʋ ba waa asunpu mʋ kagya kasɛ.

eng: However, a man named Ananias, along with his wife Sapphira, sold a piece of property.
local: Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn kʋ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Ananiyasi maa mʋ‐ka Safira ba fɛ bamʋ kasɛ kʋkʋ.

eng: With his wifes knowledge, he withheld some of the proceeds from the sale. He brought the rest and placed it in the care and under the authority of the apostles.
local: Nɩɩ Ananiyasi maa mʋ‐ka ba waa kɔnɔ nɩɩ ɔ barɩgɛ afulee mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ taa ɩkʋ yɩla, nɩɩ ɔ taa asansa mʋ kpiya asunpu mʋ kagya kasɛ.

eng: Peter asked, Ananias, how is it that Satan has influenced you to lie to the Holy Spirit by withholding some of the proceeds from the sale of your land?.
local: Nɩɩ Pita taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Ananiyasi, mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fʋ yɛgɛ nɩɩ Sɩtaanɛ i gyii fu kumu nɩɩ fʋ tɩn antɩŋɛsa sa Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ kanɩn? Mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fʋ fɛ fʋ kasɛ nyɛ afulee nɩɩ fʋ barɩgɛ tɔ taa ɩkʋ yɩla?.

eng: Wasnt that property yours to keep? After you sold it, wasnt the money yours to do with whatever you wanted? What made you think of such a thing? You havent lied to other people but to God.
local: Pɔyɩ kɛ fʋ fɛ kasɛ maŋa mʋ, kɩ gyɛ fʋ lɛɛ nɩn, nɩɩ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ fʋ fɛ lʋwɛ mʋ, afulee mʋ gbaa a gyɛ fʋ lɛɛ nɩn. Mɩnɛ ɩ waa sʋ nɩɩ fʋ waa kanɩn asɩn maŋa? Man gyɛ asa nɩɩ fʋ tɩn antɩŋɛsa sa, amaa Ɩbwaarɛ nɩɩ fʋ tɩn antɩŋɛsa sa.

eng: When Ananias heard these words, he dropped dead. Everyone who heard this conversation was terrified.
local: Ananiyasi maa dan nu kanɩn asɩn maŋa mʋ, tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɩ tɩn mʋ tʋʋ nɩɩ o wu. Nɩɩ ɩ waa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o nu kanɩn mʋ kufuu gaa.

eng: Some young men stood up, wrapped up his body, carried him out, and buried him.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ayaafɔlɛ ba loo bɔlɔɔ mʋ, kukuri lɩɩ naa pula.

eng: About three hours later, his wife entered, but she didnt know what had happened to her husband.
local: Ɩ maa waa gbɛrɛɛ mʋ, mʋ‐ka Safira gbaa loo ba ɔ man nyi kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ waa.

eng: Peter asked her, Tell me, did you and your husband receive this price for the field? She responded, Yes, thats the amount.
local: Nɩɩ Pita taasɛ, mʋ yɛ, Tɔwɛ mɛ kesintin, kasɛ afulee mʋ nɩɩ faa fu‐kuli fan fɛ mʋ kpini ndee? Nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Oo, ɩmʋ afulee kpini nɩn.

eng: He replied, How could you scheme with each other to challenge the Lords Spirit? Look! The feet of those who buried your husband are at the door. They will carry you out too.
local: Nɩɩ Pita taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Mɩnɛ ɩ waa sʋ nɩɩ faa fu‐kuli fan waa kɔnɔ kʋlʋn yɛ fan tʋʋ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Kayaayu mʋ tɔ kɩɩ. Kɩɩ! Ayaafɔlɛ mʋ nɩɩ ba naa pula fu‐kuli mʋ ba yɩlɛ kebunɔ tʋtɔ nbɩɩnbɩɩ, kanɩn nɩɩ bee biti ba kukuri ngbaa fʋ lɩɩ naa pula nɩn.

eng: At that very moment, she dropped dead at his feet. When the young men entered and found her dead, they carried her out and buried her with her husband.
local: Opula tɔ, nɩɩ ɩ tɩn mʋ tʋʋ Pita ayaa tɔ nɩɩ o wu. Nɩɩ ayaafɔlɛ mʋ ba be loo, nɩɩ ba wu yɛ o wu ɔ dɛ, nɩɩ ba bɔlɔɔ mʋ kukuri lɩɩ naa pula mata mu‐kuli.

eng: Trepidation and dread seized the whole church and all who heard what had happened.
local: Krisito abi kʋbʋ yɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ tʋtɔ mʋ kpini ba maa nu kanɩn mʋ, ɩ waa bamʋ kufuu gaa.

eng: The apostles performed many signs and wonders among the people. They would come together regularly at Solomons Porch.
local: Asunpu mʋ ba waa ɩsɩnkaala yɛ asɩn kyinkyinsa asa mu ansi tɔ. Akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ kpini bɛɛ taalɛ bɛɛ gyaŋɛ Ɩbwaarɛ osunkpa ayɛ mʋ opula mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ Solomon kasɔrɔga tɔ mʋ.

eng: No one from outside the church dared to join them, even though the people spoke highly of them.
local: Asa mʋ ba sʋ bɛɛrɛɛ bɛ sa bamʋ gaa, amaa bamʋ nɩɩ ba man kɔɔlɛ gyii mʋ ɔkʋ man taalɛ nyɛ kɔkɔlɔ naa tii bamʋ sʋ.

eng: Indeed, more and more believers in the Lord, large numbers of both men and women, were added to the church.
local: Amaa kamaa tɔ mʋ, anyɩn yɛ akyɩɩ gaalaagaa ba kɔɔlɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ gyii, nɩɩ ba naa tii bamʋ kʋbʋ mʋ sʋ.

eng: As a result, they would even bring the sick out into the main streets and lay them on cots and mats so that at least Peters shadow could fall on some of them as he passed by.
local: Lɩɩ kanɩn sʋ, asa ba sʋla alɔpʋ ba yɩla agbenbi ɔkaa yɛgɛ ba dɛ bamʋ mpaa yɛ ɩkalan sʋ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɩ waa kɛ Pita kan ɛɛ kyʋn mʋ, mʋ kayaayu kɩ natɛ bamʋ sʋ.

eng: Even large numbers of persons from towns around Jerusalem would gather, bringing the sick and those harassed by unclean spirits. Everyone was healed.
local: Ndɛ‐bii mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ kyaabɔɔ Gyerusalem mʋ asa ba taa bamʋ alɔpʋ yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi bɛɛ tɔɔrɔɔ bamʋ kpini ba, nɩɩ ba nyɛ alanfɩya.

eng: About that time, while the number of disciples continued to increase, a complaint arose. Greek-speaking disciples accused the Aramaic-speaking disciples because their widows were being overlooked in the daily food service.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Yesu abuupu mʋ ba nyɛ akyɔ mʋ, kamɔɔrɛ‐gyii kɩ ba Gyiwu ndɛ‐bii mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɔwɛ Giriki mʋ yɛ Gyiwu ndɛ‐bii mʋ nsana. Ba fabɩlɛ yɛ Gyiwu ndɛ‐bii mʋ ba mɛɛ sa bamʋ akulapu‐kyɩɩ atɔ barɩgɛsa kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa.

eng: The high priest, together with his allies, the Sadducees, was overcome with jealousy.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ, yɛ mʋ kamaa abi mʋ nɩɩ ba lɩɩ Sadusi kʋbʋ mʋ tɔ mʋ, ba nyɛ kayɩɩ basa lɩɩ asunpu mʋ sʋ.

eng: The Twelve called a meeting of all the disciples and said, It isnt right for us to set aside proclamation of Gods word in order to serve tables.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ mʋ, asunpu kudu‐anyɔ mʋ ba sarɛ Yesu abuupu mʋ kpini. Nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Ɩ man dagaa fɛɛ a yɛgɛ abwaarɛsɩn kɩtɔwɛ yɩla kɛ a ba kɩɩ atɔ kɩbarɩgɛ sʋ.

eng: They seized the apostles and made a public show of putting them in prison.
local: Ba kɩtaa asunpu mʋ naa tii katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba tii bamʋ asa obu mʋ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, carefully choose seven well-respected men from among you. They must be well-respected and endowed by the Spirit with exceptional wisdom. We will put them in charge of this concern.
local: Kanɩn sʋ anɛ‐daa ana yɛ anɛ‐supu ana, fan lɛɛ fanɛ tɔ anyɩn asunɔ mʋ nɩɩ bɛ sʋ bɛɛrɛɛ yɛ kanyaasɩn nɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ kɩ bɔla bamʋ tɔ mʋ, kɛ a taa kusun nɩmʋ waa bamʋ abaa tɔ.

eng: An angel from the Lord opened the prison doors during the night and led them out. The angel told them.
local: Amaa kanɩn kagyanbwɛ maŋa mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ ba tigi obu mʋ kebunɔ nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ bamʋ.

eng: As for us, we will devote ourselves to prayer and the service of proclaiming the word.
local: kɛ a nyɛ saŋa sa kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ yɛ abwaarɛsɩn kɩtɔwɛ mʋ.

eng: Go, take your place in the temple, and tell the people everything about this new life.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan kpee naa yɩlɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ kɛ fan tɔwɛ asa mʋ, nkpa pʋpwɛ nɩmʋ asɩn.

eng: This proposal pleased the entire community. They selected Stephen, a man endowed by the Holy Spirit with exceptional faith, Philip, Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and Nicolaus from Antioch, a convert to Judaism.
local: Kanɩn nfɛɛrɛ mʋ ɩ pɩrɛ bamʋ kpini. Nɩɩ Yesu abuupu mʋ ba lɛɛ Sitefen mʋ nɩɩ mʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii yɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ kɩ kyɔ mʋ tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ba lɛɛ Filipi yɛ Pokoro yɛ Nikano yɛ Timon yɛ Pamena maa Nikolasi mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ Antiyoki, nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ n‐yɩɩ biliŋi Gyiwu abi, nɩɩ ɔ ba kɔɔlɛ Yesu gyii mʋ.

eng: Early in the morning, they went into the temple as they had been told and began to teach. When the high priest and his colleagues gathered, they convened the Jerusalem Council, that is, the full assembly of Israels elders. They sent word to the prison to have the apostles brought before them.
local: Kayɩ kɩ maa kɛ mʋ Krisito asunpu mʋ ba kpee kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ katɔwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ba yii kasɛ bɛɛ tɔwɛ asa mʋ nkpa mʋ asɩn. Oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ maa mʋ kamaa abi, ba maa ba mʋ, ɔ tɩɩ Sanhediri mʋ nɩɩ ba gyɛ Gyiwu abi kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ abɩlɩsa mʋ kpini ba ba gyaŋɛ, nɩɩ o sun yɛ ba naa lɛɛ asunpu mʋ ba.

eng: The community presented these seven to the apostles, who prayed and laid their hands on them.
local: Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ bamʋ kaapʋ asunpu mʋ, nɩɩ asunpu mʋ ba taa bamʋ abaa gyaga bamʋ sʋ nɩɩ ba kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ sa bamʋ.

eng: However, the guards didnt find them in the prison. They returned and reported.
local: Apurusi mʋ nɩɩ ba sun mʋ, ba maa fʋʋ obu tiisa mʋ tɔ mʋ, ba man wu asunpu mʋ obu maŋa tɔ. Ɩmʋ sʋ ba bwii naa tɔwɛ abɩlɩsa mʋ nɩɩ bee gyoo bamʋ mʋ yɛ.

eng: Gods word continued to grow. The number of disciples in Jerusalem increased significantly. Even a large group of priests embraced the faith.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ abwaarɛsɩn mʋ ɩ yɛlɩgɛ. Yesu abuupu mʋ ba nyɛ akyɔ tii sʋ Gyerusalem ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ. Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu mʋ gaalaagaa gbaa ba kɔɔlɛ Yesu gyii.

eng: We found the prison locked and well-secured, with guards standing at the doors, but when we opened the doors we found no one inside.
local: An wu obu mʋ maa biri sʋ danbɩrasa yɛgɛ akɩɩsʋpʋ mʋ ba yɩlɛ nbunɔ, amaa a maa tigi nbunɔ mʋ a man wu ɔkʋ obu mʋ tɔ.

eng: Stephen, who stood out among the believers for the way Gods grace was at work in his life and for his exceptional endowment with divine power, was doing great wonders and signs among the people.
local: Sitefen mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ ɔlʋn nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bwaala mʋ mʋ, waa asɩn kyinkyinsa yɛ asɩnkaala asa mʋ tɔ.

eng: When they received this news, the captain of the temple guard and the chief priests were baffled and wondered what might be happening.
local: Ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ akɩɩsʋpʋ mʋ, yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ oseepu bɩlɩsa ba maa nu kanɩn mʋ i kyinkyin bamʋ, kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ɩ ba kanɩn.

eng: Opposition arose from some who belonged to the so-called Synagogue of Former Slaves. Members from Cyrene, Alexandria, Cilicia, and Asia entered into debate with Stephen.
local: Amaa Gyiwu abi mʋ nɩɩ ba lɩɩ Kirini yɛ Alekisanda kabwaarɛ kʋlɛkpa mʋ nɩɩ bɛ tɩɩ yɛ ba kɔɔlɛ bamʋ n‐yɩɩ, yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba lɩɩ Silisiya, Asiya ɩsʋwʋlɛ sʋ mʋ kpini ba gyii Sitefen ɩmɔɔrɛ.

eng: Just then, someone arrived and announced, Look! The people you put in prison are standing in the temple and teaching the people.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔkʋ ba tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan kɩɩ! Anyɩn mʋ nɩɩ fan tii bamʋ obu mʋ bɛɛ yɩlɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ kasɔrɔga tɔ bɛɛ kaapʋ asa.

eng: However, they couldnt resist the wisdom the Spirit gave him as he spoke.
local: Lɩɩ mʋ kanyaasɩn yɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ ɔkpa katɔwɛ tɔ sʋ mʋ, ba man taalɛ yɩlɛ mu ansi tɔ.

eng: Then the captain left with his guards and brought the apostles back. They didnt use force because they were afraid the people would stone them.
local: Ba maa nu kanɩn mʋ akɩɩsʋpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ yɛ mʋ ayaafɔlɛ ba kpee naa bɩya asunpu mʋ. Ba maa sɩlɛ kufuu fɛɛ asa mʋ, bee biti ba tʋʋ bamʋ abuu su mʋ, ba man taa bamʋ yɛ ɔlʋn sʋ.

eng: Then they secretly enticed some people to claim, We heard him insult Moses and God.
local: Ba lɩɩ asiiri tɔ naa wu asa akʋ yɛ ba ba tɩn atɩntɩnsa gyaga Sitefen sʋ, kɛ ba ba tɔwɛ yɛ, An nu Sitefen maa tɔwɛ nbusaa asɩn ɛɛ gyaga Mosesi maa Ɩbwaarɛ sʋ.

eng: The apostles were brought before the council where the high priest confronted them.
local: Nɩɩ ba taa bamʋ ba yɩlɛ abɩlɩsa mʋ nɩɩ ba sarɛ mu ansi tɔ. Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ taasɛ bamʋ yɛ.

eng: They stirred up the people, the elders, and the legal experts. They caught Stephen, dragged him away, and brought him before the Jerusalem Council.
local: Nɩɩ amɔɔrɛ‐gyipu ba tɔwɛ fun waa abɩlɩsa mʋ yɛ Mosesi nbara akaapʋpʋ yɛ asa mʋ kpini nkɔlɔ nɩɩ ba kɩtaa Sitefen ba Ɩsɩrayɩ kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ abɩlɩsa mu ansi tɔ.

eng: In no uncertain terms, we demanded that you not teach in this name. And look at you! You have filled Jerusalem with your teaching. And you are determined to hold us responsible for this mans death.
local: Yɛ an da fanɛ amu tɔ yɛ, fan man bɩla taa Yesu kɩtɩɩ tɔ kaapʋ? Amaa fan taa fanɛ kɩkaapʋ kyaabɔɔ Gyerusalem kpini. Fen biti fan taa ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ kelewu ba anɛ sʋ.

eng: Before the council, they presented false witnesses who testified, This man never stops speaking against this holy place and the Law.
local: Ba bɩya antɩŋɛsa wuya ana nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, Saŋa kʋmaa ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ ɩ tɔwɛ nbusaa asɩn ee yii kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ yɛ Mosesi nbara mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sa anɛ mʋ.

eng: Peter and the apostles replied, We must obey God rather than humans!.
local: Nɩɩ Pita maa asunpu banban mʋ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɩ dagaa fɛɛ a maa nu Ɩbwaarɛ kɔnɔ nɩn, man gyɛ nyɩmɩsa lɛɛ.

eng: In fact, we heard him say that this man Jesus of Nazareth will destroy this place and alter the customary practices Moses gave us.
local: Nɩɩ an bɩla nu maa tɔwɛ yɛ Yesu Kanasareti‐nyɩn mʋ biti o boyi Kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ, kɛ ɔ kyɛɛgɛ anɛ anaana atɔ mʋ nɩɩ anɛ naana Mosesi taa sa anɛ mʋ.

eng: The God of our ancestors raised Jesus from the dead—whom you killed by hanging him on a tree.
local: Anɛ naana ana bamʋ Ɩbwaarɛ i kyiŋi Yesu mʋ nɩɩ fan da mʋ oyu laasa mɔɔ mʋ, lɩɩ lewu tɔ.

eng: Everyone seated in the council stared at Stephen, and they saw that his face was radiant, just like an angels.
local: Ɩsɩrayɩ kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ abɩlɩsa mʋ kpini mʋ nɩɩ ba tɛ asɩn mʋ tɔ mʋ, ba kɩɩ Sitefen kyimm, nɩɩ ba wu mu ansi tɔ ɩ maa du fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ ansi tɔ mʋ.

eng: God has exalted Jesus to his right side as leader and savior so that he could enable Israel to change its heart and life and to find forgiveness for sins.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ diyaa mʋ naa kyɩna mʋ gyisa sʋ fɛɛ Ɔgyankpaapʋ yɛ Ɔmɔlɩgɛpʋ mʋ, nɩɩ o bugi ɔkpa sa Ɩsɩrayɩ abi kɛ ba yɛgɛ alibi kɩwaa, kɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ taa bamʋ alibi kɛɛ bamʋ.

eng: We are witnesses of such things, as is the Holy Spirit, whom God has given to those who obey him.
local: Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ taa sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba buu mʋ mʋ, yɛ anɛ asunpu mʋ ɩ gyɛ ɩmʋ kesintin agyipu ɩnɩmʋ kpini sʋ.

eng: Peter and John were going up to the temple at three oclock in the afternoon, the established prayer time.
local: Kakaakʋ ɩ waa fɛɛ kakyʋwapɛ kerefi asa nɩɩ Pita maa Gyɔn bee kpee kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa tɔ ba naa kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ.

eng: When the council members heard this, they became furious and wanted to kill the apostles.
local: Nɩɩ nbara abɩlɩsa kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ mʋ ba maa nu kanɩn mʋ, ɩ nyɛ bamʋ agbʋ gaa nɩɩ ba waa bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ ba mɔɔ asunpu mʋ.

eng: Meanwhile, a man crippled since birth was being carried in. Every day, people would place him at the temple gate known as the Beautiful Gate so he could ask for money from those entering the temple.
local: Kewusunɔ Danbɩrasa, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ kɩmʋ mʋ, tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba naa tu ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba kʋʋgɛ mʋ yɛgɛ ɔ gyɛ boobi maa tɛ. Kakɛ kʋmaa bɛɛ sʋla mʋ bee kpee naa yɩla tʋtɔ yɛgɛ ɛɛ kʋlɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ bee kpee kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ afulee.

eng: One council member, a Pharisee and teacher of the Law named Gamaliel, well-respected by all the people, stood up and ordered that the men be taken outside for a few moments.
local: Amaa bamʋ tɔ ɔnyɩn kʋ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Gamaliye, nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ Farasii obii yɛ Mosesi nbara ɔkaapʋpʋ mʋ nɩɩ asa kpini bɛɛ sa mʋ bɛɛrɛɛ mʋ, kʋsʋ yɩlɛ bamʋ nsana nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, ba yɛgɛ asunpu mʋ ba lɩɩ kewu gbɛrɛɛ.

eng: When he saw Peter and John about to enter, he began to ask them for a gift.
local: Maa wu Pita maa Gyɔn ba maa biti ba loo tʋtɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kʋlɛ bamʋ yɛ ba sa mu afulee.

eng: He said, Fellow Israelites, consider carefully what you intend to do to these people.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Ɩsɩrayɩ abi, fan lɔŋɔ fɛ nfɛɛrɛ mʋ nɩɩ fan taa lɩɩ anyɩn anɩmʋ sʋ danbɩrasa.

eng: Peter and John stared at him. Peter said, Look at us.
local: Nɩɩ Pita maa Gyɔn ba kɩɩ mʋ kyimm, nɩɩ Pita yɛ, Diyaa ansi kɩɩ anɛ danbɩrasa.

eng: Some time ago, Theudas appeared, claiming to be somebody, and some four hundred men joined him. After he was killed, all of his followers scattered, and nothing came of that.
local: Saŋa kʋ tɔ mʋ, Tewuda kʋ gbaa daa kyɩna kʋsʋ yɛ ɔ gyɛ ɔkʋ nɩn. Ɔ nyɛ kʋbʋ fɛɛ asa alɩfa ana nɩɩ ba buu mʋ. Amaa ba mɔɔ mʋ, nɩɩ mʋ kʋbʋ kɩ yaasɛ tɔ, nɩɩ mʋ awaasa kpini ɩ waa kɩyan.

eng: So the man gazed at them, expecting to receive something from them.
local: Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ kɩɩ bamʋ kyimm, nɩɩ ɔ waa nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ ee biti ɔ nyɛ kʋtɔ kʋ lɩɩ bamʋ asɛ.

eng: Afterward, at the time of the census, Judas the Galilean appeared and got some people to follow him in a revolt. He was killed too, and all his followers scattered far and wide.
local: Mʋ kamaa mʋ Gyudasi mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ Galile mʋ, asa kɩkalɛ saŋa mʋ ngbaa mʋ kʋsʋ yɩlɛ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ kʋbʋ lala kʋ nɩɩ ba buu mʋ. Amaa ba mɔɔ mʋ, nɩɩ mu abuupu gbaa ba yaasɛ tɔ.

eng: Peter said, I dont have any money, but I will give you what I do have. In the name of Jesus Christ the Nazarene, rise up and walk.
local: Amaa Pita tɔwɛ mʋ nɩn yɛ, Man sʋ afulee kpini‐kpini mɛ asɛ, amaa n biti n sa fʋ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ n sʋ. Lɩɩ Yesu Krisito Kanasareti‐nyɩn kɩtɩɩ tɔ sʋ mʋ n yɛ fʋ, kʋsʋ natɛ.

eng: Heres my recommendation in this case: Distance yourselves from these men. Let them go! If their plan or activity is of human origin, it will end in ruin.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ndaga ɩ ba falɛ, n biti n tɔwɛ fanɛ fɛɛ fɛn yɛgɛ asa anɩmʋ ba natɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ nɩɩ bamʋ kusun kɩnɩmʋ kɩ gyɛ nyɩmɩsa lɛɛ bɩrɛ kii biti ki nyita.

eng: Then he grasped the mans right hand and raised him up. At once his feet and ankles became strong.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Pita kɩtaa mʋ gyisa kɩbaa tɔ kyɛ mʋ tɔ nɩɩ ɔ kʋsʋ yɩlɛ. Opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ ayaa yɛ mu amulii ɩ waa ɔlʋn.

eng: If it originates with God, you wont be able to stop them. Instead, you would actually find yourselves fighting God! The council was convinced by his reasoning.
local: Amaa nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ bɩrɛ fɛn mɛɛ taalɛ tii asa anɩmʋ, nɩɩ ɩ waa kanɩn bɩrɛ fen biti fen wu fɛɛ fɛn kɔ yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: Jumping up, he began to walk around. He entered the temple with them, walking, leaping, and praising God.
local: nɩɩ o fugi yii mʋ ayaa sʋ, o piili ɔ naa. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ maa bamʋ ba loo obu mʋ kasɔrɔga tɔ, ɔ naa ee fugi yɛgɛ ɛɛ yɩn Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: After calling the apostles back, they had them beaten. They ordered them not to speak in the name of Jesus, then let them go.
local: Ba nu sa Gamaliye katɔwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ba tɩɩ asunpu mʋ tɩŋɛ bamʋ. Nɩɩ ba da bamʋ amu tɔ yɛ, ba man bɩla tɔwɛ Yesu kɩtɩɩ tɔ asɩn. Nɩɩ ba yɛgɛ bamʋ yɛ ba natɛ.

eng: All the people saw him walking and praising God.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ asa mʋ kpini ba wu mʋ maa naa ɛɛ yɩn Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ.

eng: The apostles left the council rejoicing because they had been regarded as worthy to suffer disgrace for the sake of the name.
local: Asunpu mʋ ba lɩɩ Ɩsɩrayɩ kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ mʋ tɔ kpini abɩlɩsa mu ansi tɔ, taa kensi‐gyii fɛɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ kalɛ bamʋ tii bamʋ nɩɩ ba wu awʋrʋfɔ lɩɩ Yesu kɩtɩɩ tɔ mʋ sʋ.

eng: They recognized him as the same one who used to sit at the temples Beautiful Gate asking for money. They were filled with amazement and surprise at what had happened to him.
local: nɩɩ ba bɩɩ fɛɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ boobi mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛ kewusunɔ Danbɩrasa ɛɛ kʋlɛ afulee mʋ, ɩ waa bamʋ kɩyan, nɩɩ ɩ waa bamʋ kanankʋ maa kʋsʋ ɔ naa mʋ.

eng: Every day they continued to teach and proclaim the good news that Jesus is the Christ, both in the temple and in houses.
local: Kakɛ kʋmaa nɩmʋ ba naa kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ nsɔrɔga tɔ yɛ asa akpaara sʋ. Ba man yɛgɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ kɩtɔwɛ yɛ Yesu ɩ gyɛ Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ mʋ.

eng: While the healed man clung to Peter and John, all the people rushed toward them at Solomons Porch, completely amazed.
local: Lɩɩ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ taa mʋ n‐yɩɩ ba Pita maa Gyɔn asɛ sʋ mʋ, ɩ waa asa mʋ kanankʋ nɩɩ ba sɩlɛ kpee bamʋ asɛ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛ tɩɩ yɛ Solomon lenbu mʋ.

eng: Seeing this, Peter addressed the people: You Israelites, why are you amazed at this? Why are you staring at us as if we made him walk by our own power or piety?.
local: Pita maa wu asa mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Ɩsɩrayɩ abi, mɩnɛ ɩ yɛgɛ sʋ nɩɩ asɩn nɩmʋ a waa fanɛ kanankʋ? Nɩɩ mɩnɛ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ fan kɩɩ anɛ gbaa fɛɛ anɛ gbaa‐gbaa anɛ ɔlʋn yɛ anɛ kabwaarɛ‐sun ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ kʋsʋ ɔ naa falɛ?.

eng: The God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob—the God of our ancestors—has glorified his servant Jesus. This is the one you handed over and denied in Pilates presence, even though he had already decided to release him.
local: Ɩ gyɛ anɛ naana Abraham maa Isaki maa Gyekobu bamʋ Ɩbwaarɛ, ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ anɛ naana ana kpini Ɩbwaarɛ, mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sa mʋ kɩyaafɔlɛ Yesu bʋnyaa kparɛ. Mʋ maŋa nɩɩ fan taa mʋ sa agyankpaapʋ, nɩɩ fan kina mʋ Paleti ansi tɔ, yɛgɛ Paleti waa mʋ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ ee biti ɔ yɛgɛ mʋ.

eng: You rejected the holy and righteous one, and asked that a murderer be released to you instead.
local: Ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋrɔn nɩɩ o du kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ, mʋ, nɩɩ fan kina, nɩɩ fan kpalɩ ɩmʋ sʋ tɔwɛ Paleti yɛ ɔ yɛgɛ asa ɔmɔɔpʋ mʋ sa fanɛ.

eng: You killed the author of life, the very one whom God raised from the dead. We are witnesses of this.
local: Fan taa Yesu mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ sa nkpa mʋ mɔɔ, amaa Ɩbwaarɛ kyiŋi mʋ ba nkpa tɔ. Anɛ ɩ gyɛ asɩn maŋa kesintin agyipu.

eng: His name itself has made this man strong. That is, because of faith in Jesus name, God has strengthened this man whom you see and know. The faith that comes through Jesus gave him complete health right before your eyes.
local: Lɩɩ Yesu Krisito kɩtɩɩ tɔ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ, nɩɩ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ɔnyɩn boobi mʋ nɩɩ fen nyi nɩɩ fen wu mʋ falɛ nyɛ alanfɩya. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔnyɩn mʋ maa kɔɔlɛ Yesu Krisito kɩtɩɩ gyii sʋ mʋ, ɔ kpaarɛ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fen wu mʋ falɛ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I know you acted in ignorance. So did your rulers.
local: Mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana, nbɩɩnbɩɩ n bɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ fanɛ yɛ fanɛ agyankpaapʋ ba waa Yesu falɛ, ɩ lɩɩ fanɛ kɩman‐nyi sʋ.

eng: But this is how God fulfilled what he foretold through all the prophets: that his Christ would suffer.
local: Amaa Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla bɔla mʋ atɔwɛpʋ mʋ kpini sʋ tɔwɛ asɩn nɩmʋ fɛɛ, Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ mʋ ii biti o wu awʋrʋfɔ gaa, nɩɩ ɩ ba kanɩn mɔɔ kesintin.

eng: Change your hearts and lives! Turn back to God so that your sins may be wiped away.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan yɛgɛ alibi kɩwaa fanɛ kakyɩna tɔ kɛ fɛn buu Ɩbwaarɛ, kɛ ɔ taa fanɛ alibi kɛɛ fanɛ.

eng: When Pentecost Day arrived, they were all together in one place.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Gyiwu abi Pentekosi kakɛ gyisa maa fʋʋ mʋ, Krisito akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ kpini ba ba sarɛ opula kʋlʋn.

eng: Then the Lord will provide a season of relief from the distress of this age and he will send Jesus, whom he handpicked to be your Christ.
local: Fɛn kan waa kanɩn mʋ, kayɩɩ yuuli kakyɩna ii biti ɩ lɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ asɛ, kɛ o sun Yesu Krisito mʋ nɩɩ ɔ wʋla lɛɛ mʋ sa fanɛ mʋ kɛ ɔ ba fanɛ asɛ.

eng: Suddenly a sound from heaven like the howling of a fierce wind filled the entire house where they were sitting.
local: Opula tɔ nɩɩ ɩlawʋ ɩkʋ ɩ lɩɩ abwaarɛ‐wʋlɛ tɔ a maa kpira fɛɛ afuu bʋlʋnsa akʋ a maa da ba loo kɩkpaara mʋ nɩɩ ba tɛ kɩmʋ sʋ mʋ kpini.

eng: Jesus must remain in heaven until the restoration of all things, about which God spoke long ago through his holy prophets.
local: Ɩ dagaa fɛɛ Yesu maa sii sʋsʋ naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ waa kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ ki biliŋi kɩpʋpwɛ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla bɔla mʋ atɔwɛpʋ kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ sʋ tɔwɛ asɩn nɩmʋ lɩɩ koo dɩdaa mʋ.

eng: They saw what seemed to be individual flames of fire alighting on each one of them.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba wu atɔ kʋ fɛɛ fʋlɔn anobi a maa yaasɛ ba gyaga ɔsa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ tʋtɔ mʋ sʋ.

eng: Moses said, The Lord your God will raise up from your own people a prophet like me. Listen to whatever he tells you. .
local: Kanɩn sʋ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Mosesi tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ lɛɛ fanɛ tɔ ɔtɔwɛpʋ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ o sun mɛ falɛ, nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ fanɛ gbaa‐gbaa fanɛ nyɩmɩsa. Nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ fɛn kan fanɛ asʋ, kɛ fen nu kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ tɔwɛ fanɛ.

eng: They were all filled with the Holy Spirit and began to speak in other languages as the Spirit enabled them to speak.
local: Nɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ bɔla bamʋ kpini tɔ, nɩɩ bamʋ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ yii kasɛ ɛɛ tɔwɛ katɔwɛ pʋpwɛ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ kɩ sa bamʋ yɛ ba tɔwɛ mʋ.

eng: Whoever doesnt listen to that prophet will be totally cut off from the people. .
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o mee nu kanɩn ɔtɔwɛpʋ maŋa asɛ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ lɛɛ mʋ lɩɩ mʋ asa tɔ kɛ o nyita mʋ.

eng: There were pious Jews from every nation under heaven living in Jerusalem.
local: Saŋa maŋa tɔ mʋ Gyiwu abi akʋ mʋ nɩɩ be sun Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ, ba lɩɩ dulinyaa tɔ ɩsʋwʋlɛ kpini sʋ ba baa ba bʋ Gyerusalem tʋtɔ.

eng: All the prophets who spoke—from Samuel forward—announced these days.
local: Lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Samuweli yɛ atɔwɛpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ kanɩn katɔwɛ maŋa yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba lɩɩ Samuweli kamaa ba mʋ kpini ba tɔwɛ kanɩn asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɩ waa ndaga anɛ kakyɩna kanɩmʋ tɔ falɛ.

eng: When they heard this sound, a crowd gathered. They were mystified because everyone heard them speaking in their native languages.
local: Ba maa kanan ɩlawʋ maŋa mʋ, nɩɩ kʋbʋ damantɛ kʋkʋ kɩ sarɛ. Ɩ waa ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ kɩyan, kpalɩ fɛɛ bamʋ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ nu akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ kpini ba maa tɔwɛ asa mʋ bamʋ tɔ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ kadɛ tɔwɛsa.

eng: You are the heirs of the prophets and the covenant that God made with your ancestors when he told Abraham, Through your descendants, all the families on earth will be blessed. .
local: Ɩbwaarɛ maa bɔla mʋ atɔwɛpʋ sʋ ɛɛ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ mʋ kpini ɩ dɛ sa fanɛ. Kɛ katɔwɛ kyula mʋ nɩɩ fanaa Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ fanɛ naana ana ba waa mʋ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ Abraham yɛ, Lɩɩ fʋ kɩnaana sʋ mʋ, n biti n waa kasɛ sʋ asa kpini kusee.

eng: They were surprised and amazed, saying, Look, arent all the people who are speaking Galileans, every one of them?.
local: Ɩ waa bamʋ kanankʋ, nɩɩ ba taasɛ yɛ, Asa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɔwɛ falɛ mʋ, ba man gyɛ Galile abi nɩn abɛɛ?.

eng: After God raised his servant, he sent him to you first—to bless you by enabling each of you to turn from your evil ways.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ mʋ kɩyaafɔlɛ Yesu nɩɩ ɔ gyankpaa sun mʋ fanɛ asɛ, kɛ fan yɛgɛ alibi kɩwaa ɔkpa kʋmaa sʋ kɛ ɔ waa fanɛ kusee.

eng: How then can each of us hear them speaking in our native language?.
local: Ɩ waa nɛnɛ sʋ nɩɩ anɛ kpini an nu ba maa tɔwɛ anɛ ntɔwɛ nusa?.

eng: Parthians, Medes, and Elamites; as well as residents of Mesopotamia, Judea, and Cappadocia, Pontus and Asia.
local: Anɛ kpini an lɩɩ‐lɩɩ ndɛ kʋkwɩɩ‐kʋkwɩɩ fɛɛ, Patiya yɛ Medeya yɛ Elam yɛ Mesopotamiya yɛ Gyudeya yɛ Kapadosiya yɛ Pontisi yɛ Asiya yɛ.

eng: Phrygia and Pamphylia, Egypt and the regions of Libya bordering Cyrene; and visitors from Rome.
local: Firigiya yɛ Panfiliya yɛ Egyipiti yɛ Libiya kɩbaafʋn mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ ki tiri Sereni, nɩɩ Gyiwu abi yɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba biliŋi Gyiwu abi, nɩɩ bamʋ kpini ba lɩɩ Roma mʋ.

eng: Cretans and Arabs—we hear them declaring the mighty works of God in our own languages.
local: Anɛ akʋ an lɩɩ Kereti nɩɩ anɛ akʋ mɔɔ an lɩɩ Arabiki. Anɛ kpini an nu asa anɩmʋ ntɔwɛ tɔ ba maa tɔwɛ asɩn kyinkyinsa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ waa.

eng: They were all surprised and bewildered. Some asked each other, What does this mean.
local: Ɩ maa waa bamʋ kɩyan nɩɩ ɩ kɩtaa bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ mʋ, nɩɩ bɛ taasɛ abaa yɛ, Mɩnɛ nɩɩ ɩnɩmʋ kasɛ ɩɩ kaapʋ.

eng: Others jeered at them, saying, Theyre full of new wine.
local: Amaa yɛgɛ akʋ mɔɔ be keesi akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Asa maŋa ba bʋʋ nɩn.

eng: Peter stood with the other eleven apostles. He raised his voice and declared, Judeans and everyone living in Jerusalem! Know this! Listen carefully to my words!.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Pita maa asunpu kudu‐ɔkʋ bambam mʋ, ba kʋsʋ yɩlɛ nɩɩ Pita diyaa mʋ ɔbʋlɛ sʋsʋ kenken ɔ tɔwɛ ɛɛ sa kʋbʋ mʋ yɛ, Mɛ tɔmaa ana Gyiwu abi yɛ ɔsa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛ Gyerusalem mʋ kpini, fan kan fanɛ asʋ kɛ n lɛɛ ɩnɩmʋ kasɛ sa fanɛ.

eng: These people arent drunk, as you suspect; after all, its only nine oclock in the morning!.
local: Asa anɩmʋ ba man bʋʋ nɩn, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fan fɛ bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ falɛ, ɩ dan san ɩ gyɛ kerefi akpanɔ nyanyan nɩmʋ.

eng: Rather, this is what was spoken through the prophet Joel.
local: Amaa ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ ba falɛ ɩ gyɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Gyoweli wʋla tɔwɛ ɩmʋ asɩn yɛ.

eng: In the last days, God says, I will pour out my Spirit on all people. Your sons and daughters will prophesy. Your young will see visions. Your elders will dream dreams. .
local: Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ yɩla yɛ nkɛ laalaalʋwɛ mʋ, n biti n yɛgɛ mɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ ɔ ba asa kpini sʋ. N biti n bɔla fanɛ nbii‐nyɩnsa yɛ akyɩɩsa sʋ kɛ n tɔwɛ asɩn. Fanɛ ayaafɔlɛ bee biti be wu ansi sʋ kɩlɛɛkaapʋ, kɛ fanɛ anyɩn bɩlɩsa bɛ kɔɔ idee.

eng: Even upon my servants, men and women, I will pour out my Spirit in those days, and they will prophesy. .
local: Kakɛ laalaalʋwɛ mʋ n biti n yɛgɛ mɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ ɔ sa fanɛ ayaafɔlɛ yɛ abite kɛ ba tɔwɛ mɛ asɩn.

eng: I will cause wonders to occur in the heavens above and signs on the earth below, blood and fire and a cloud of smoke. .
local: N biti n waa asɩn kyinkyinsa sʋsʋ, kɛ n waa ɩsɩnkaala kasɛ sʋ. Nkalan yɛ fʋlɔn yɛ idiisii ii biti ɩ waa kudu‐kudu.

eng: The sun will be changed into darkness, and the moon will be changed into blood, before the great and spectacular day of the Lord comes. .
local: Kyɔwɛ ii biti o biliŋi kitentenbiri, kɛ ɔbwaayɩ ɔ payɩ fɛɛ nkalan, pɔyɩ kɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ kakɛ bʋnyaa lala mʋ kɩ ba.

eng: And everyone who calls on the name of the Lord will be saved. .
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kɩtɩɩ mʋ, ee biti ɔ mɔlɩgɛ mʋ.

eng: Fellow Israelites, listen to these words! Jesus the Nazarene was a man whose credentials God proved to you through miracles, wonders, and signs, which God performed through him among you. You yourselves know this.
local: Mɛ asa Ɩsɩrayɩ abi fan bɩla nu asɩn nɩmʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ Yesu mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ Kanasareti‐nyɩn yɛgɛ nɩɩ ɔ waa asɩn kyinkyinsa yɛ ɩsɩnkaala yɛ adangana nɩɩ ɩ kaapʋ tɔ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ i sun mʋ. Nɩɩ fanɛ kpini fen nyi ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa falɛ.

eng: In accordance with Gods established plan and foreknowledge, he was betrayed. You, with the help of wicked men, had Jesus killed by nailing him to a cross.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ o nyi kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ kyula, nɩɩ o ŋmina yɩla fɛɛ bee biti ba lɛɛ Yesu sa fanɛ, nɩɩ ɩ ba kanɩn. Fan yɛgɛ nɩɩ asa libi ba da Yesu anyenbi mata oyu laasa sʋ nɩɩ ba mɔɔ mʋ.

eng: God raised him up! God freed him from deaths dreadful grip, since it was impossible for death to hang on to him.
local: Amaa Ɩbwaarɛ kyiŋi mʋ lɩɩ lewu tɔ, lɛɛ mʋ lɩɩ lewu awʋrʋfɔ tɔ, lɩɩ fɛɛ lewu man taalɛ tii mʋ yɩla.

eng: David says about him, I foresaw that the Lord was always with me; because he is at my right hand I wont be shaken. .
local: Dawidi wʋla tɔwɛ Yesu n‐yɩɩ sʋ asɩn yɛ, Saŋa kʋmaa nɩmʋ, n wu Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mi ansi sʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ o tii mɛ sʋ, ɩmʋ sʋ sɛhn mɛɛ tɔɔrɔɔ mɛ.

eng: Therefore, my heart was glad and my tongue rejoiced. Moreover, my body will live in hope, .
local: Ɩnɩmʋ sʋ mɛ kɔkɔlɔ ki fuuli mɛ, nɩɩ kensi‐gyii kɩ bɔla mɛ atɔwɛsa tɔ, nɩɩ halɩɩ n wu gbaa mʋ, n sʋ tamaa.

eng: because you wont abandon me to the grave, nor permit your holy one to experience decay. .
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ fʋ mɛɛ yɛ fʋ yɛgɛ mɛ kala o sii kamandɛ, nɩɩ fʋ mɛɛ yɛ fʋ yɛgɛ fʋ Ɔkyɩrɛkyɩrɛpʋ ɔ gyɔɔ nyita.

eng: You have shown me the paths of life; your presence will fill me with happiness. .
local: Fʋ kaapʋ mɛ nkpa ɔkpa, nɩɩ fʋ bʋ mɛ asɛ, ɩmʋ sʋ n nyɛ kensi‐gyii gaa.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I can speak confidently about the patriarch David. He died and was buried, and his tomb is with us to this very day.
local: Mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana, fɛn yɛgɛ n lɛɛ anɛ naana Dawidi nɩmʋ asɩn kaapʋ fanɛ. Dawidi mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ falɛ asɩn nɩmʋ gbaa wu nɩn, nɩɩ ba pula mʋ, nɩɩ mʋ kakyan kɛ san kɩ dɛ anɛ asɛ.

eng: Because he was a prophet, he knew that God promised him with a solemn pledge to seat one of his descendants on his throne.
local: Amaa ɔ dan ɔ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ nɩn, nɩɩ o nyi fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ bɔ ntan da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ yɛ ee biti ɔ yɛgɛ mʋ kɩnaana tɔ ɔkʋ ɔ ba kyɩna mʋ Dawidi kuwura‐gya sʋ.

eng: Having seen this beforehand, David spoke about the resurrection of Christ, that he wasnt abandoned to the grave, nor did his body experience decay .
local: Dawidi wʋla wu kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ waa, kanɩn sʋ ɔ tɔwɛ Krisito kiwu kyiŋi mʋ asɩn yɛ, Ɔ man yɛgɛ nɩɩ o sii abuni tɔ, nɩɩ mu kibuni man gyɔɔ kakyan tɔ.

eng: This Jesus God raised up. We are all witnesses to that fact.
local: Yesu nɩmʋ asɩn nɩɩ Dawidi tɔwɛ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ kyiŋi mʋ lɩɩ lewu tɔ. Anɛ kpini ɩ gyɛ ɩmʋ kesintin agyipu.

eng: He was exalted to Gods right side and received from the Father the promised Holy Spirit. He poured out this Spirit, and you are seeing and hearing the results of his having done so.
local: Nɩɩ mʋ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ diyaa mʋ yɩla mʋ gyisa sʋ, sa mʋ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ mʋ. Ɩmʋ nɩɩ fen nu, nɩɩ fen wu falɛ ɩ gyɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sa anɛ.

eng: David didnt ascend into heaven. Yet he says, The Lord said to my Lord, Sit at my right side, .
local: Dawidi gbaa‐gbaa man kpee sʋsʋ, amaa ɔ tɔwɛ fɛɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ tɔwɛ sa mɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ yɛ, Kyɩna mɛ gyisa sʋ.

eng: until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet. .
local: amɔɔ n taa fu akisipu kpini waa fʋ ayaa kasɛ. .

eng: Therefore, let all Israel know beyond question that God has made this Jesus, whom you crucified, both Lord and Christ.
local: Fanɛ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi kpini, fan nu danbɩrasa fɛɛ, Yesu nɩmʋ, mʋ nɩɩ fan da mʋ oyu laasa sʋ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ waa mʋ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ yɛ Krisito nɩɩ ɩɩ kaapʋ Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ.

eng: When the crowd heard this, they were deeply troubled. They said to Peter and the other apostles, Brothers, what should we do.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ asa mʋ ba nu ɩnɩmʋ kanɩn mʋ, i loo bamʋ nkɔlɔ tɔ, nɩɩ ba taasɛ Pita maa asunpu bambam mʋ yɛ, Anɛ‐daa ana, mɩnɛ nɩɩ an biti a waa.

eng: Peter replied, Change your hearts and lives. Each of you must be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of your sins. Then you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit.
local: Nɩɩ Pita tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fanɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ ɔ yɛgɛ alibi kɩwaa, kɛ ba gyee mʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee lɩɩ Yesu Krisito kɩtɩɩ tɔ, kɛ fan nyɛ alibi kɩtaakɛɛ, kɛ fan nyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kakɛɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ mʋ Kayaayu mʋ.

eng: This promise is for you, your children, and for all who are far away—as many as the Lord our God invites.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ sa fanaa fanɛ‐bii ana mʋ, yɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ kata‐kata mʋ kpini, bamʋ nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ anɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ tɩɩ ba mʋ asɛ mʋ.

eng: With many other words he testified to them and encouraged them, saying, Be saved from this perverse generation.
local: Nɩɩ Pita gyii bamʋ kesintin bwii tɔwɛ ntɔwɛ gaalaagaa waa bamʋ aniya tɔ yɛ, Fan lɛɛ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ lɩɩ ndaga asa libi nɩmʋ tɔ.

eng: Those who accepted Peters message were baptized. God brought about three thousand people into the community on that day.
local: Asa mʋ gaalaagaa ba kɔɔlɛ mʋ asɩn mʋ gyii nɩɩ ba gyee bamʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee. Asa mʋ nɩɩ ba be tii kʋbʋ mʋ sʋ kakɛ maŋa mʋ ba waa fɛɛ asa ngbɩn nsa.

eng: The believers devoted themselves to the apostles teaching, to the community, to their shared meals, and to their prayers.
local: Nɩɩ bamʋ kpini bɛ bɩɩla bɛɛ lɩɩ asunpu mʋ asɛ, ba sʋ kakyɛmɩnɛ ɔkɔn yɛ abaa, nɩɩ bee gyii Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ agyitɔ, nɩɩ bɛɛ bʋga bɛɛ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ.

eng: A sense of awe came over everyone. God performed many wonders and signs through the apostles.
local: Asunpu mʋ ba waa ɩsɩnkaala yɛ asɩn kyinkyinsa gaalaagaa, nɩɩ asɩn mʋ ɩ waa ɔkʋmaa anɩmʋ kɩyan.

eng: All the believers were united and shared everything.
local: Akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ kpini bɛɛ bʋga katɩn kʋlʋn, nɩɩ ba sʋ kɔnɔ kʋlʋn, yɛgɛ bamʋ kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ gyɛ bamʋ kpini lɛɛ.

eng: They would sell pieces of property and possessions and distribute the proceeds to everyone who needed them.
local: Bɛɛ fɛ bamʋ npatɩyɛ yɛ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ mʋ kpini, kɛ ba barɩgɛ afulee mʋ tɔ sa abaa kanan kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ i tiri ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ.

eng: Every day, they met together in the temple and ate in their homes. They shared food with gladness and simplicity.
local: Kakɛ kʋmaa nɩmʋ bɛɛ naa sarɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ kasɔrɔga tɔ fɛɛ kɩbʋlɛ kʋlʋn, bɛɛ bʋga nɩn bee gyii Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ agyitɔ bamʋ akpaara sʋ, nɩɩ bɛɛ bʋga bee gyii bamʋ gbaa‐gbaa bamʋ agyitɔ kensi‐gyii yɛ nkɔlɔ fuuli.

eng: They praised God and demonstrated Gods goodness to everyone. The Lord added daily to the community those who were being saved.
local: yɛgɛ bɛɛ yɩn Ɩbwaarɛ, nɩɩ bamʋ awaasa kpini a gyii asa ansi. Nɩɩ kakɛ kʋmaa nɩmʋ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ sʋ bamʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ mɔlɩgɛ mʋ, ee tii bamʋ Krisito abi kʋbʋ mʋ sʋ.

eng: The high priest asked, Are these accusations true.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ taasɛ Sitefen yɛ, Asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ lɩɩ fʋ sʋ falɛ kpini, ɩ gyɛ kesintin.

eng: Stephen responded, Brothers and fathers, listen to me. Our glorious God appeared to our ancestor Abraham while he was still in Mesopotamia, before he settled in Haran.
local: Sitefen lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Mɛ‐Sɛ ana yɛ mɛ‐daa ana, fen nu mɛ asɛ! Ɩbwaarɛ bʋnyaa wuya mʋ lɛɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ kaapʋ anɛ naana Abraham, saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ Mesopotamiya kasɛ sʋ, pɔyɩ nɩɩ ɔ kʋsʋ ba Haran mʋ.

eng: God told him, Leave your homeland and kin, and go to the land that I will show you.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Kʋsʋ lɩɩ fʋ ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ yɛ fʋ asa asɛ, kɛ fu kpee ɔsʋwʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ n biti n kaapʋ fʋ falɛ sʋ.

eng: So Abraham left the land of the Chaldeans and settled in Haran. After Abrahams father died, God had him resettle in this land where you now live.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ Abraham lɩɩ Kalidiya ɔsʋwʋlɛ mʋ sʋ nɩɩ ɔ naa kyɩna Haran. Mʋ‐sɛ lewu kamaa nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ tʋtɔ naa kyɩna ɔsʋwʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ ndaga fan tɛ sʋ falɛ.

eng: God didnt give him an inheritance here, not even a square foot of land. However, God did promise to give the land as his possession to him and to his descendants, even though Abraham had no child.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ man pɩɩta taa kasɛ mʋ ntɩn kʋ sa mʋ, halɩɩ kɩyaa tantan tɔ kasɛ gbaa man pɩɩta biliŋi mʋ lɛɛ. Amaa Ɩbwaarɛ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ tɔwɛ yɛ ɔsʋwʋlɛ mʋ ii biti o be biliŋi maa mʋ anaana‐bii lɛɛ nɩn. Yɛgɛ saŋa maa Abraham man pɩɩta kʋʋgɛ.

eng: God put it this way: His descendants will be strangers in a land that belongs to others, who will enslave them and abuse them for four hundred years. .
local: Asɩn nɩmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ Abraham yɛ, Fʋ anaana‐bii bee biti ba naa biliŋi afɔ ɔkʋ ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ, kɛ ba taa bamʋ biliŋi anyɛ kɛ ba nyanla bamʋ nsu alɩfa ana.

eng: And I will condemn the nation they serve as slaves, God said, and afterward they will leave that land and serve me in this place.
local: N biti n bɩɩtɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba biliŋaa bamʋ anyɛ mʋ asʋ, ɩmʋ kamaa mʋ, fʋ anaana‐bii mʋ bee biti ba lɩɩ kanɩn ɔsʋwʋlɛ maŋa sʋ kɛ ba be sun mɛ nfɩɩ.

eng: God gave him the covenant confirmed through circumcision. Accordingly, eight days after Isaacs birth, Abraham circumcised him. Isaac did the same with Jacob, and Jacob with the twelve patriarchs.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ maa Abraham ba nyɛ katɔwɛ kyula fɛɛ, mʋ yɛ mʋ kamaa tɔ kpini ba tɩŋɛ iduu. Ɩmʋ sʋ Abraham maa kʋʋgɛ Isaki mʋ ɔ tɩn mʋ oduu kakɛ buruwasapu, nɩɩ Isaki kʋʋgɛ Gyekobu nɩɩ ɔ tɩn mʋ oduu, nɩɩ Gyekobu kʋʋgɛ anɛ naana ana kudu‐anyɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɩŋɛ bamʋ kpini iduu.

eng: Because the patriarchs were jealous of Joseph, they sold him into slavery in Egypt. God was with him, however.
local: Gyosefu mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ kudu anyɔ mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ mʋ, mʋ daa ana ba nyɛ mʋ sʋ kayɩɩ basa, nɩɩ ba fɛ mʋ fɛɛ kɩnyɛ sa Egyipiti abi.

eng: and rescued him from all his troubles. The grace and wisdom he gave Joseph were recognized by Pharaoh, king of Egypt, who appointed him ruler over Egypt and over his whole palace.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ Gyosefu lɩɩ mʋ awʋrʋfɔ kpini tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ sa mʋ kanyaasɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ kebiti lɩɩ Egyipiti Owura Faro asɛ. Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ o yii mʋ kuwura fɛɛ ɔ kɩɩ mʋ owura‐kpa yɛ Egyipiti ɔsʋwʋlɛ kpini sʋ.

eng: A famine came upon all Egypt and Canaan, and great hardship came with it. Our ancestors had nothing to eat.
local: Nɩɩ akʋn damantɛ ɩ ba tɩyɛ Egyipiti yɛ Kaanan ɩsʋwʋlɛ sʋ, nɩɩ ɩ bɩya awʋrʋfɔ gaa. Nɩɩ anɛ naana ana ba man nyɛ agyitɔ.

eng: When Jacob heard there was grain in Egypt, he sent our ancestors there for the first time.
local: Bamʋ‐sɛ Gyekobu maa nu fɛɛ agyitɔ ɩ bʋ Egyipiti mʋ, o sun mu‐bii ana mʋ nɩɩ ba gyɛ anɛ naana ana kpee tʋtɔ kɩgyankpaasa ba naa sɔɔ agyitɔ.

eng: During their second visit, Joseph told his brothers who he was, and Pharaoh learned about Josephs family.
local: Ba maa bwii kpee Egyipiti kɩnyɔsapʋ ba naa sɔɔ agyitɔ mʋ, Gyosefu lɛɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ kaapʋ mʋ‐daa ana, nɩɩ Faro gbaa nu Gyosefu mʋ kʋʋpʋ ana sʋ.

eng: Joseph sent for his father Jacob and all his relatives—seventy-five in all—and invited them to live with him.
local: Kamaa tɔ mʋ Gyosefu sun yɛ ba naa taa mʋ‐sɛ Gyekobu yɛ mʋ kʋʋpʋ ana mʋ nɩɩ bɛ waa fɛɛ asa adusunɔ yɛ anu mʋ kpini ba mʋ asɛ.

eng: So Jacob went down to Egypt, where he and our ancestors died.
local: Nɩɩ Gyekobu kʋsʋ kpee Egyipiti, tʋtɔ nɩɩ maa mu‐bii ana ba naa wu.

eng: Their bodies were brought back to Shechem and placed in the tomb that Abraham had purchased for a certain sum of money from Hamors children, who lived in Shechem.
local: Nɩɩ ba bwiiyaa bamʋ ba Sekem ba pula kakyan mʋ nɩɩ Abraham sɔɔ lɩɩ Hamo mu‐bii ana asɛ mʋ tɔ.

eng: When it was time for God to keep the promise he made to Abraham, the number of our people in Egypt had greatly expanded.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kɔkɔlɔ kɩda sa Abraham maa tiri tɔ fɛɛ ɩ ba tɔ mʋ, anɛ asa mʋ ba nyɛ akyɔ Egyipiti ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ.

eng: But then another king rose to power over Egypt who didnt know anything about Joseph. .
local: Kanɩn saŋa maa nɩɩ owura pʋpwɛ kʋ ba kyɩna Egyipiti kagya sʋ, amaa ɔ man nyi Gyosefu asɩn.

eng: He exploited our people and abused our ancestors. He even forced them to abandon their newly born babies so they would die.
local: Nɩɩ owura pʋpwɛ nɩmʋ nyanla anɛ asa nɩɩ ɔ waa bamʋ awʋrʋfɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ nyan bamʋ yɛ ba lɛɛ bamʋ nbii pʋpwɛ kewu kɛ ɔ mɔɔ bamʋ.

eng: Thats when Moses was born. He was highly favored by God, and for three months his parents cared for him in their home.
local: Saŋa maŋa tɔ nɩɩ ba kʋʋgɛ Mosesi nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ kebii danbɩrasa Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ nɩɩ mʋ kɩbaabʋtɔ kwɩɩ. Nɩɩ ba baala mʋ, kɩɩ mʋ sʋ ɩbwaayɩ ɩsa mʋ‐sɛ kɩkpaara sʋ.

eng: After he was abandoned, Pharaohs daughter adopted and cared for him as though he were her own son.
local: Ɩmʋ kamaa tɔ mʋ, ba lɛɛ mʋ kewu nɩɩ Owura Faro mu‐bii kyɩɩsa taa mʋ naa bɩla nɩɩ o biliŋi mu‐bii.

eng: Moses learned everything Egyptian wisdom had to offer, and he was a man of powerful words and deeds.
local: Nɩɩ ba kaapʋ Mosesi, nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ Egyipiti abi kɩbɩɩla yɛ bamʋ kanyaasɩn, nɩɩ ɔ sʋ kinyi yɛ katɔwɛ yɛ awaasa tɔ.

eng: When Moses was years old, he decided to visit his family, the Israelites.
local: Mosesi maa gyii nsu adɩna kamaa mʋ, ɔ waa mʋ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ o kpee ɔ naa bɔɔ mʋ ayɛ abi Ɩsɩrayɩ abi sʋ.

eng: He saw one of them being wronged so he came to his rescue and evened the score by killing the Egyptian.
local: Tʋtɔ maa nɩɩ o wu Egyipiti ɔnyɩn kʋ maa nyanla Ɩsɩrayɩ ɔnyɩn kʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ Mosesi naa kɔɔlɛ mʋ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ɔ mɔɔ Egyipiti ɔnyɩn mʋ ka kʋkɔ.

eng: He expected his own kin to understand that God was using him to rescue them, but they didnt.
local: Mosesi nyi fɛɛ mʋ ayɛ abi bee biti ba bɩɩ fɛɛ, mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ biti ɔ bɔla sʋ mɔlɩgɛ bamʋ, amaa ba man bɩɩ.

eng: The next day he came upon some Israelites who were caught up in an argument. He tried to make peace between them by saying, You are brothers! Why are you harming each other.
local: Lɩɩkaakɛ mʋ, Mosesi ba tu Ɩsɩrayɩ abi asa anyɔ ba maa kɔ. Maa biti ɔ barɩgɛ bamʋ tɔ mʋ, ɔ taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan gyɛ asa kʋlʋn nɩn, mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fɛn nyanla abaa kanɩn.

eng: The one who started the fight against his neighbor pushed Moses aside and said, Who appointed you as our leader and judge? .
local: Nɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ nyanla mʋ bɩrɩsa mʋ nin Mosesi naa yɩlɛ ɩkaa sʋ nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ yɛ, Anɩmʋ ɩ yɩla fʋ yɛ fʋ kɩɩ anɛ sʋ kɛ fu gyii anɛ asɩn?.

eng: Are you planning to kill me like you killed that Egyptian yesterday? .
local: Fii biti fʋ mɔɔ mɛ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ndee fʋ mɔɔ Egyipiti ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩn.

eng: When Moses heard this, he fled to Midian, where he lived as an immigrant and had two sons.
local: Mosesi maa nu kanɩn mʋ ɔ sɩlɛ lɩɩ Egyipiti kpee Midiyan ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ, naa kyɩna fɛɛ ɔfɔ. Tʋtɔ maŋa nɩɩ ɔ kʋʋgɛ nbii nnyɔ.

eng: Forty years later, an angel appeared to Moses in the flame of a burning bush in the wilderness near Mount Sinai.
local: Mosesi maa kyɩna tʋtɔ nsu adɩna kamaa mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ lɛɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ kaapʋ mʋ, kɩpʋnfa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ dɛɛ ofuli sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɩ maa Sinayi kɩbɩɩ mʋ.

eng: Enthralled by the sight, Moses approached to get a closer look and he heard the Lords voice.
local: Maa wu falɛ atɔ mʋ, ɩ waa mʋ kanankʋ. Maa nin tiri kɛ ɔ kɩɩ danbɩrasa mʋ, o nu Ɩbwaarɛ maa tɔwɛ yɛ.

eng: I am the God of your ancestors, the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. Trembling with fear, Moses didnt dare to investigate any further.
local: Mɛ ɩ gyɛ fʋ naana ana Ɩbwaarɛ, Abraham yɛ Isaki yɛ Gyekobu Ɩbwaarɛ. Mosesi maa yɩnŋɛ nɩɩ kufuu kɩ kɩtaa mʋ gaa sʋ mʋ, ɔ man bɩla taalɛ kɩɩ kʋtɔ maŋa.

eng: The Lord continued, Remove the sandals from your feet, for the place where you are standing is holy ground. .
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Lɛɛ fʋ asɩbɩta, lɩɩ fɛɛ ntɩn mʋ nɩɩ fʋ yɩlɛ mʋ gyɛ kasɛ kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ nɩn.

eng: I have clearly seen the oppression my people have experienced in Egypt, and I have heard their groaning. I have come down to rescue them. Come! I am sending you to Egypt .
local: Kesintin, n wu kanan mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ nyanla mɛ asa Egyipiti tɔ. Nɩɩ n nu bamʋ kusu, nɩɩ n kpɩlɩgɛ ba n ba mɔlɩgɛ bamʋ. Kanɩn sʋ n sun fʋ kɛ fʋ bwii kpee Egyipiti tɔ.

eng: This is the same Moses whom they rejected when they asked, Who appointed you as our leader and judge? This is the Moses whom God sent as leader and deliverer. God did this with the help of the angel who appeared before him in the bush.
local: Mosesi kʋlʋn mʋ nɩɩ mʋ asa ba kina mʋ, tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Anɩmʋ ɩ yɩla fʋ anɛ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ yɛ asɩn ogyipu? mʋ, mʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bɩla bɔla mʋ kabɔɔ sʋ fʋlɔn nnɔ‐bi tɔ yɛ, ɔ naa waa bamʋ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ yɛ ɔmɔlɩgɛpʋ.

eng: This man led them out after he performed wonders and signs in Egypt at the Red Sea and for forty years in the wilderness.
local: Ɔ lɛɛ bamʋ lɩɩ Egyipiti nɩɩ ɔ waa asɩn kyinkyinsa yɛ adangana Egyipiti tɔ yɛ ɔpʋ pɩpɛɛ mʋ asɛ, nɩɩ ba gyii nsu adɩna ofuli mʋ sʋ.

eng: This is the Moses who told the Israelites, God will raise up for you a prophet like me from your own people. .
local: Mosesi nɩmʋ ɩ tɔwɛ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi yɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ lɛɛ mʋ ɔtɔwɛpʋ pʋpwɛ lɩɩ fanɛ tɔ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ mɛ mʋ.

eng: This is the one who was in the assembly in the wilderness with our ancestors and with the angel who spoke to him on Mount Sinai. He is the one who received life-giving words to give to us.
local: Mosesi nɩmʋ tii Ɩsɩrayɩ kʋbʋ mʋ sʋ lɩɩ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ ofuli sʋ mʋ. Mosesi maa anɛ naana ana yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ ba bʋ tʋtɔ. Sɩnayɩ kɩbɩɩ mʋ sʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ nkpa asɩn mʋ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ asɛ yɛ ɔ taa bɩya anɛ.

eng: Hes also the one whom our ancestors refused to obey. Instead, they pushed him aside and, in their thoughts and desires, returned to Egypt.
local: Ba waa kʋsʋ tɔ ɔlʋn nɩɩ ba man nu Mosesi asɛ, nɩɩ bamʋ nkɔlɔ tɔ mʋ, naafɔɔ ba daa nyɛ nɩn bee biti ba bwii kpee Egyipiti.

eng: They told Aaron, Make us gods that will lead us. As for this Moses who led us out of Egypt, we dont know whats happened to him! .
local: Nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ Aron yɛ, Mosesi mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ anɛ lɩɩ Egyipiti mʋ, a man nyi kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ waa mʋ kɩbɩɩ Sɩnayɩ sʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ waa ɩdakpa sa anɛ kɛ bɛɛ gyankpaa anɛ.

eng: Thats when they made an idol in the shape of a calf, offered a sacrifice to it, and began to celebrate what they had made with their own hands.
local: Kanɩn saŋa maa tɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba pɔyɩ kanaatɛ‐bi taa biliŋi bamʋ ɔdakpa. Nɩɩ ba lɔŋɔ ɔdakpa maŋa yɛgɛ bee gyii bamʋ ansi lɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba taa bamʋ abaa pɔyɩ mʋ sʋ.

eng: So God turned away from them and handed them over to worship the stars in the sky, just as it is written in the scroll of the Prophets: Did you bring sacrifices and offerings to me for forty years in the wilderness, house of Israel? .
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ Ɩbwaarɛ palɛ mʋ kamaa sa bamʋ yɛ ba kyaga sʋ be sun kyɔwɛ yɛ ɔbwaayɩ yɛ akyikpebi, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba wʋla kyʋrɔɔ waa Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ ɩwʋlɛ tɔ yɛ, Ɩsɩrayɩ abi! Man gyɛ mɛ nɩɩ fɛn daa fɛn lɔŋɔ atɔbwaaya fɛn sa nsu adɩna ofuli sʋ mʋ.

eng: No! Instead, you took the tent of Moloch with you, and the star of your god Rephan, the images that you made in order to worship them. Therefore, I will send you far away, farther than Babylon. .
local: Fanɛ nkɔlɔ ɩ gyan ɔdakpa Moleki yɛ ɔdakpa Refan mʋ nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ fanɛ kikyikpebi ɔdakpa, mʋ nɩɩ fan taa fanɛ abaa pɔyɩ nɩɩ fen sun mʋ sʋ nɩn. Ɩmʋ sʋ n biti n yɛgɛ fanɛ akiisipu ba taa fanɛ kpee kata‐kata kyʋn Babilon sʋ.

eng: The tent of testimony was with our ancestors in the wilderness. Moses built it just as he had been instructed by the one who spoke to him and according to the pattern he had seen.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ anɛ naana ana ba naa ba naa ofuli mʋ sʋ mʋ, ba sʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kubuu lala mʋ nɩɩ ɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ bʋ bamʋ asɛ. Ba waa kɩmʋ nɩn fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ kaapʋ Mosesi yɛ ɔ waa kɩmʋ mʋ.

eng: In time, when they had received the tent, our ancestors carried it with them when, under Joshuas leadership, they took possession of the land from the nations whom God expelled. This tent remained in the land until the time of David.
local: Ɩmʋ kamaa mʋ, anɛ naana ana ba nyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kubuu mʋ, lɩɩ bamʋ‐sɛ ana asɛ, nɩɩ Gyosuwa maa bamʋ ba sʋla Ɩbwaarɛ kubuu lala maŋa, naa fʋʋ ɩsʋwʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ gya asɛ lɩɩ ɩmʋ tɔ asa, nɩɩ ɔ yɛgɛ nɩɩ anɛ asa ba kyɩna tʋtɔ mʋ. Nɩɩ kubuu mʋ sii tʋtɔ naa fʋʋ halɩɩ Dawidi saŋa mʋ.

eng: God approved of David, who asked that he might provide a dwelling place for the God of Jacob.
local: Dawidi mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ansi gyii mʋ sʋ mʋ kʋlɛ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ yɛgɛ ɔ pɔyɩ osunkpa ayɛ sa Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ anɛ naana Gyekobu Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ.

eng: But it was Solomon who actually built a house for God.
local: Amaa mu‐bii Solomon ɩ gyɛ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ ba pɔyɩ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ.

eng: However, the Most High doesnt live in houses built by human hands. As the prophet says.
local: Amaa Ɩbwaarɛ Owura lala mɛɛ kyɩna akpaara mʋ nɩɩ asa ba taa bamʋ abaa pɔyɩ mʋ tɔ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bɔla mʋ ɔtɔwɛpʋ sʋ tɔwɛ yɛ.

eng: Heaven is my throne, and the earth is my footstool. What kind of house will you build for me, says the Lord, or where is my resting place? .
local: Sʋsʋ ɩ gyɛ mɛ kuwura gya, nɩɩ kasɛ mɔɔ ɩ gyɛ mɛ ayaa ɔgyagakpa. Mɩnɛ obu nɩɩ fen biti fan pɔyɩ sa mɛ? Abɛɛ fɩnɛ ɩ taalɛ waa mɛ ɔkyɩnakpa?.

eng: Didnt I make all these things with my own hand? .
local: Man gyɛ mɛ gbaa‐gbaa mɛ ɩ waa ɩnɩmʋ kpini.

eng: You stubborn people! In your thoughts and hearing, you are like those who have had no part in Gods covenant! You continuously set yourself against the Holy Spirit, just like your ancestors did.
local: Fan gyɛ asʋ tɔ ɔlʋn wuya ana, nɩɩ fan kina Ɩbwaarɛ fanɛ nkɔlɔ tɔ, nɩɩ fan waa akpawu sa abwaarɛsɩn mʋ. Fan dan fan du nɩn fɛɛ fanɛ naana ana, mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ kɔ bee tii Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ ɔkpa.

eng: Was there a single prophet your ancestors didnt harass? They even killed those who predicted the coming of the righteous one, and youve betrayed and murdered him!.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ mɔɔ ɩ kyɩna nɩɩ fanɛ naana ana ba man nyanla mʋ? Ba mɔɔ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ gaalaagaa mʋ nɩɩ dɩdaa ba wʋla tɔwɛ ɔkpa kyiigisa wuya mʋ kɩba asɩn. Nɩɩ nbɩɩnbɩɩ fan waa mʋ kadɛ‐boyi nɩɩ fan mɔɔ mʋ.

eng: You received the Law given by angels, but you havent kept it.
local: Fanɛ ɩ nyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ nbara mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ ba taa bɩya fanɛ mʋ, amaa fan kina ɩmʋ ki buu.

eng: Once the council members heard these words, they were enraged and began to grind their teeth at Stephen.
local: Ɩsɩrayɩ kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba gyaŋɛ mʋ ba maa nu Sitefen asɛ mʋ, ba nyɛ agbʋ nɩɩ ba duŋi bamʋ anobi.

eng: But Stephen, enabled by the Holy Spirit, stared into heaven and saw Gods majesty and Jesus standing at Gods right side.
local: Amaa Sitefen mʋ nɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ kɩ bɔla mʋ tɔ mʋ, diyaa mu ansi kɩɩ sʋsʋ nɩɩ o wu Ɩbwaarɛ bʋnyaa, nɩɩ o wu Yesu maa yɩlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gyisa sʋ.

eng: He exclaimed, Look! I can see heaven on display and the Human One standing at Gods right side.
local: Nɩɩ Sitefen yɛ, Fan kɩɩ! N wu sʋsʋ maa bugi nɩɩ n wu Yesu, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ maa yɩlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gyisa sʋ.

eng: At this, they shrieked and covered their ears. Together, they charged at him.
local: Maa tɔwɛ kanɩn kamaa tɔ mʋ ba tii bamʋ asʋ nɩɩ ba kuusi kenken. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ bamʋ kpini ba naa mili mʋ sʋ.

eng: threw him out of the city, and began to stone him. The witnesses placed their coats in the care of a young man named Saul.
local: Nɩɩ ba bɩɩtɛ mʋ lɩɩ kadɛ mʋ tɔ naa twɩɩ mʋ abuu. Nyamaanyɩlɛpʋ mʋ ba taa bamʋ ɩkaalɛ sa kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Sɔɔlɩ yɛ ɔ kɩɩ sʋ.

eng: As they battered him with stones, Stephen prayed, Lord Jesus, accept my life.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ twɩɩ mʋ abuu mʋ, Sitefen kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu, kɔɔlɛ mɛ kala.

eng: Falling to his knees, he shouted, Lord, dont hold this sin against them! Then he died.
local: Nɩɩ Sitefen muŋa mʋ ayaa muli sʋ tɔwɛ kenken yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu, man taa alibi mʋ nɩɩ ba waa falɛ ka bamʋ kʋkɔ. Maa tɔwɛ kanɩn mʋ nɩɩ o wu.

eng: Saul was in full agreement with Stephens murder. At that time, the church in Jerusalem began to be subjected to vicious harassment. Everyone except the apostles was scattered throughout the regions of Judea and Samaria.
local: Sɔɔlɩ gbaa bʋ tʋtɔ nɩɩ o kyula Sitefen kelewu mʋ. Kanɩn kakɛ maŋa nɩɩ kanyanla kɩ ba Krisito abi kʋbʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Gyerusalem mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ bamʋ kpini ba yaasɛ tɔ loo Gyudeya maa Samariya ɩsʋwʋlɛ sʋ. Amaa Krisito asunpu mʋ nkʋn ɩ man yaasɛ tɔ.

eng: Some pious men buried Stephen and deeply grieved over him.
local: Nɩɩ anyɩn mʋ nɩɩ bee sun Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ akʋ ɩ lɛɛ bamʋ n‐yɩɩ naa pula Sitefen, nɩɩ ba su mʋ keli.

eng: Saul began to wreak havoc against the church. Entering one house after another, he would drag off both men and women and throw them into prison.
local: Amaa Sɔɔlɩ bɩrɛ piili ee nyita Krisito abi kʋbʋ mʋ, yɛgɛ ɔ naa akpaara‐akpaara sʋ ɛɛ bɩɩtɛ anyɩn yɛ akyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ ba buu Yesu mʋ ee kpee ee tii ibu.

eng: Those who had been scattered moved on, preaching the good news along the way.
local: Nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba yaasɛ tɔ mʋ ba naa bɛɛ tɔwɛ Yesu asɩn danbɩrasa katɩn kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ba kpee mʋ.

eng: Philip went down to a city in Samaria and began to preach Christ to them.
local: Nɩɩ Filipu gbaa kpee Samariya kadɛ lala kʋ tɔ naa tɔwɛ Yesu asɩn.

eng: The crowds were united by what they heard Philip say and the signs they saw him perform, and they gave him their undivided attention.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ kʋbʋ damantɛ mʋ nu Filipu asɛ nɩɩ ba wu asɩn kyinkyinsa yɛ ɩsɩnkaala mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa mʋ, ba kan bamʋ asʋ ba nu mʋ katɔwɛ.

eng: With loud shrieks, unclean spirits came out of many people, and many who were paralyzed or crippled were healed.
local: Nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi ba kuusi kenken lɩɩ asa gaalaagaa tɔ, nɩɩ amʋgʋlɛ‐mʋgʋlɛ yɛ aboobi gaalaagaa ba nyɛ alanfɩya.

eng: There was great rejoicing in that city.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ kensi‐gyii lala kɩ ba kadɛ lala maŋa tɔ.

eng: Before Philips arrival, a certain man named Simon had practiced sorcery in that city and baffled the people of Samaria. He claimed to be a great person.
local: Kanɩn saŋa maŋa tɔ mʋ, ɔnyɩn kʋ bʋ tɔ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Simon, nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ Sɩtaanɛ adangana ɔwaapʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ Samariya kadɛ mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ mʋ awaasa mʋ ɩ waa Samariya abi kɩyan. Ɩmʋ sʋ ɛɛ kaapʋ mʋ n‐yɩɩ yɛ ɔ gyɛ nyɩmɩsa kparɛ.

eng: Everyone, from the least to the greatest, gave him their undivided attention and referred to him as the power of God called Great.
local: Lɩɩ falɛ sʋ mʋ asa kparɛ yɛ asansa mʋ kpini bɛɛ nu mʋ asɛ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔlʋn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Ɔlʋn lala mʋ.

eng: He had their attention because he had baffled them with sorcery for a long time.
local: Ɔ waa Sɩtaanɛ adangana ɩnɩmʋ kyɛɛrɛɛ, nɩɩ ɩ waa bamʋ kɩyan nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ asa bee nu mʋ asɛ.

eng: After they came to believe Philip, who preached the good news about Gods kingdom and the name of Jesus Christ, both men and women were baptized.
local: Amaa saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Filipu taa Yesu Krisito kɩtɩɩ tɔ tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii sʋ mʋ, asa mʋ ba kɔɔlɛ asɩn mʋ gyii. Nɩɩ ba gyee anyɩn yɛ akyɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee.

eng: Even Simon himself came to believe and was baptized. Afterward, he became one of Philips supporters. As he saw firsthand the signs and great miracles that were happening, he was astonished.
local: Nɩɩ Simon mʋ gbaa wu asɩn kyinkyinsa yɛ ɩsɩnkaala mʋ nɩɩ Filipu waa mʋ, ɩ waa mʋ kanankʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ gyii, nɩɩ ba gyee mʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee, nɩɩ o buu Filipu kpee katɩn kʋmaa.

eng: When word reached the apostles in Jerusalem that Samaria had accepted Gods word, they commissioned Peter and John to go to Samaria.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Krisito asunpu mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Gyerusalem ba nu yɛ Samariya abi ba kɔɔlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn mʋ gyii mʋ, ba sun Pita maa Gyɔn bamʋ asɛ.

eng: Peter and John went down to Samaria where they prayed that the new believers would receive the Holy Spirit. .
local: Ba maa fʋʋ bamʋ asɛ mʋ, ba kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ sa bamʋ yɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ sa bamʋ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ.

eng: This was because the Holy Spirit had not yet fallen on any of them; they had only been baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba taa Yesu kɩtɩɩ tɔ gyee bamʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee mʋ, ba man pɩɩta nyɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ.

eng: So Peter and John laid their hands on them, and they received the Holy Spirit.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Pita maa Gyɔn ba taa bamʋ abaa gyaga bamʋ sʋ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ nɩɩ ba nyɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ.

eng: When Simon perceived that the Spirit was given through the laying on of the apostles hands, he offered them money.
local: Simon mʋ maa wu fɛɛ Pita maa Gyɔn ba taa bamʋ abaa gyaga akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ sʋ nɩɩ ba nyɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ, Simon biti fɛɛ ɔ sa Pita maa Gyɔn afulee. Nɩɩ ɔ yɛ.

eng: He said, Give me this authority too so that anyone on whom I lay my hands will receive the Holy Spirit.
local: Fan sa mɛ kanɩn ɔlʋn maŋa, nɩɩ saŋa kʋmaa nɩɩ ngbaa mɛ n kan taa mɛ abaa gyaga ɔkʋmaa sʋ mʋ, kɛ ɔ nyɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ maŋa.

eng: Peter responded, May your money be condemned to hell along with you because you believed you could buy Gods gift with money!.
local: Amaa Pita tɔwɛ Simon yɛ, Faa fʋ afulee a biliŋi kɩyan. Lɩɩ fɛɛ fʋ fɛ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ fɩɩ taalɛ taa afulee sɔɔ Ɩbwaarɛ kakɛɛ.

eng: You can have no part or share in Gods word because your heart isnt right with God.
local: Fʋ man tii sʋ abɛɛ fʋ man sʋ ɔ lɛɛ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kakɛɛ kusun nɩmʋ tɔ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ fʋ kɔkɔlɔ kɩ man dɛ Ɩbwaarɛ sʋ.

eng: Therefore, change your heart and life! Turn from your wickedness! Plead with the Lord in the hope that your wicked intent can be forgiven.
local: Kanɩn sʋ kyɛɛgɛ fʋ nfɛɛrɛ libi kɛ fʋ kʋlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ. Akʋtɔ fɛɛ nfɛɛrɛ mʋ nɩɩ fʋ fɛ fʋ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ mʋ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ taa kɛɛ fʋ.

eng: for I see that your bitterness has poisoned you and evil has you in chains.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ n wu fɛɛ kayɩɩ basa kɩ bʋ fʋ tɔ gaa, nɩɩ alibi ɩ bɔla fʋ tɔ.

eng: Simon replied, All of you, please, plead to the Lord for me so that nothing of what you have said will happen to me.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Simon lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Kʋlɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ sa mɛ kɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ fʋ tɔwɛ falɛ ɩ man ba mɛ sʋ.

eng: After the apostles had testified and proclaimed the Lords word, they returned to Jerusalem, preaching the good news to many Samaritan villages along the way.
local: Pita maa Gyɔn ba gyii Yesu asɩn mʋ kesintin, nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ asɩn mʋ gaa sa bamʋ. Kamaa tɔ mʋ, ba bwii kpee Gyerusalem. Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ bee bwii bee kpee mʋ, ba tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ Samariya ndɛ‐bii gaalaagaa tɔ.

eng: An angel from the Lord spoke to Philip, At noon, take the road that leads from Jerusalem to Gaza.
local: Nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ kabɔɔ tɔwɛ Filipu yɛ, Kʋsʋ kpee akpɩlɩgɛ ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ dɛ ofuli sʋ mʋ. Kanɩn ɔkpa maŋa kɩ dɛ lɩɩ Gyerusalem naa fʋʋ Gasa kadɛ sʋ.

eng: So he did. Meanwhile, an Ethiopian man was on his way home from Jerusalem, where he had come to worship. He was a eunuch and an official responsible for the entire treasury of Candace.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ Filipu kʋsʋ yii ɔkpa sʋ ee kpee. Maa kpee mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ naa gyaŋa Etiyopiya ɔwʋlɛ nyipu kparɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kɩɩ Etiyopiya owura kyɩɩsa afulee sʋ. Ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ kpee Gyerusalem naa sun Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: He was reading the prophet Isaiah while sitting in his carriage.
local: Saŋa maŋa tɔ mʋ, ɔ tɛ mʋ ɩgbaŋɛ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ bɩɩtɛ asa ɔkyɩnakpa sʋ ee bwii ee kpewu yɛgɛ ɛɛ kalɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Isaya ɔwʋlɛ.

eng: The Spirit told Philip, Approach this carriage and stay with it.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu tɔwɛ Filipu yɛ, Kpe kanɩn ɩgbaŋɛ nɩmʋ asɛ naa yɩlɛ tiri mʋ.

eng: Running up to the carriage, Philip heard the man reading the prophet Isaiah. He asked, Do you really understand what you are reading.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Filipu sɩlɛ kpee Etiyopiya‐nyɩn mʋ asɛ, nɩɩ o nu ɔnyɩn mʋ maa kalɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Isaya ɔwʋlɛ. Nɩɩ Filipu taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɩmʋ nɩɩ fɩɩ kalɛ falɛ fii nu kasɛ.

eng: The man replied, Without someone to guide me, how could I? Then he invited Philip to climb up and sit with him.
local: Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Nɛnɛ nɩɩ n waa nu kasɛ amɔɔ n nyɛ ɔkʋ kɛ ɔ lɛɛ kasɛ sa mɛ. Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔ tɩɩ Filipu yɛ ɔ ba kyɩna tiri mʋ tɔ.

eng: Meanwhile, Saul was still spewing out murderous threats against the Lords disciples. He went to the high priest.
local: Saŋa maŋa tɔ mʋ, Sɔɔlɩ dan san ɔ yɩlɛ sʋ, ɔ sʋ kanyanla yɛ kɩmɔɔ aŋala lɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu abuupu mʋ sʋ. Kanɩn sʋ o kpee Ɩbwaarɛ Oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ asɛ.

eng: This was the passage of scripture he was reading: Like a sheep he was led to the slaughter and like a lamb before its shearer is silent so he didnt open his mouth. .
local: Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ ɩɩ kalɛ ɛɛ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlɛsa mʋ tɔ yɛ, O du fɛɛ kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ mʋ ba naa mɔɔ mʋ, abɛɛ kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ nɩɩ be kyiigi mʋ ifuu, yɛgɛ ɔ man bugi mʋ kɔnɔ halɩɩ ee su.

eng: seeking letters to the synagogues in Damascus. If he found persons who belonged to the Way, whether men or women, these letters would authorize him to take them as prisoners to Jerusalem.
local: nɩɩ ɔ naa tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ ɔ sa mʋ ɩwʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ sa ɔkpa fɛɛ o kpee kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɩgyaŋɛkpa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ Damasiko kadɛ mʋ tɔ, kɛ ɔ naa kɩtaa anyɩn yɛ akyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ bee buu Yesu kɩkaapʋ mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ kan nyɛ kanɩn abuupu maŋa kɛ ɔ kɩtaa bamʋ bwii ba Gyerusalem ba tii obu.

eng: In his humiliation justice was taken away from him. Who can tell the story of his descendants because his life was taken from the earth? .
local: Nɩɩ ba gyila mʋ ipeeli, nɩɩ ba man gyii kesintin sa mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔkʋ man taalɛ tɔwɛ kaapʋ mʋ kɩnaana, lɩɩ fɛɛ ba tɩn mʋ nkpa sʋ kasɛ sʋ.

eng: During the journey, as he approached Damascus, suddenly a light from heaven encircled him.
local: Nɩɩ Sɔɔlɩ maa naa ee kpee mʋ, ɔ naa tiri Damasiko kadɛ lala mʋ. Opula tɔ nɩɩ kʋtɔ kʋ kɩ lɩɩ sʋsʋ ba tɩn mu ansi sʋ fɛɛ bʋlayɩ maa wɛɛ ɩsɛɛ mʋ.

eng: The eunuch asked Philip, Tell me, about whom does the prophet say this? Is he talking about himself or someone else.
local: Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ taasɛ Filipu yɛ, Ɔbɩlɩsa, n kʋlɛ fʋ lɛɛ kasɛ sa mɛ. Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ mʋ, ɩ tɔwɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋ asɩn nɩn abɛɛ ɔkʋ lɛɛ nɩɩ ɛɛ tɔwɛ.

eng: He fell to the ground and heard a voice asking him, Saul, Saul, why are you harassing me.
local: Nɩɩ ɩ tɩn mʋ tʋʋ kasɛ nɩɩ o nu ɔbʋlɛ kʋ maa taasɛ yɛ, Sɔɔlɩ, Sɔɔlɩ, mɩnɛ waa nɩɩ fii kisi mɛ kanɩn.

eng: Starting with that passage, Philip proclaimed the good news about Jesus to him.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Filipu piili ɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kalɛ lɩɩ Isaya ɔwʋlɛ tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ Yesu n‐yɩɩ sʋ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ sa mʋ.

eng: Saul asked, Who are you, Lord? I am Jesus, whom you are harassing, came the reply.
local: Nɩɩ Sɔɔlɩ taasɛ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, fʋ anɩmʋ nɩn? Nɩɩ ɔbʋlɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɩ gyɛ mɛ Yesu mʋ nɩɩ fii kisi mʋ nɩn.

eng: As they went down the road, they came to some water. The eunuch said, Look! Water! What would keep me from being baptized.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba naa ɔkpa mʋ tɔ bee kpee mʋ, ba naa tu nkyu kʋ ɩ maa dɛ. Nɩɩ Etiyopiya‐nyɩn mʋ tɔwɛ Filipu yɛ, Kɩɩ nkyu ndee, mɩnɛ i tii mɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee ɔkpa.

eng: Now get up and enter the city. You will be told what you must do.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ kʋsʋ kpee Damasiko kadɛ lala mʋ tɔ, tʋtɔ nɩɩ bee biti ba tɔwɛ fʋ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ fʋ waa.

eng: He ordered that the carriage halt. Both Philip and the eunuch went down to the water, where Philip baptized him.
local: Nɩɩ Etiyopiya‐nyɩn mʋ yɛgɛ mʋ gbaŋɛ mʋ yɩla. Nɩɩ maa Filipu kpini ba kpɩlɩgɛ loo nkyu mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ Filipu gyee mʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyu gyee.

eng: Those traveling with him stood there speechless; they heard the voice but saw no one.
local: Nɩɩ anyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba maa Sɔɔlɩ ba naa mʋ, ba nu katɔwɛ mʋ, amaa ba man wu ɔkʋ. Nɩɩ kufuu kɩ kɩtaa bamʋ, nɩɩ be sii ba yɩlɛ ba man bɩla bugi bamʋ nnɔ.

eng: When they came up out of the water, the Lords Spirit suddenly took Philip away. The eunuch never saw him again but went on his way rejoicing.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba lɩɩ nkyu mʋ tɔ mʋ, opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Kayaayu taa Filipu lɩɩ ɔnyɩn mu ansi sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ man bɩla wu mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ taa kensi‐gyii kyaga mʋ ɔkpa sʋ ee kpewu.

eng: After they picked Saul up from the ground, he opened his eyes but he couldnt see. So they led him by the hand into Damascus.
local: Nɩɩ Sɔɔlɩ kʋsʋ maa yɩlɛ nɩɩ o bugi mu ansi mʋ, ɔ man bɩla ɔ kɩɩ. Ɩmʋ sʋ ba sii kɩtaa mʋ kɩbaa tɔ nɩn taa mʋ kpee Damasiko.

eng: Philip found himself in Azotus. He traveled through that area, preaching the good news in all the cities until he reached Caesarea.
local: Nɩɩ ɩ taa Filipu naa lɩɩ Asotusi kadɛ tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ natɛ ndɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ tʋtɔ mʋ kpini, ɛɛ tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa halɩɩ ɔ naa fʋʋ Kasariya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ.

eng: For three days he was blind and neither ate nor drank anything.
local: Nɩɩ Sɔɔlɩ biliŋi gyaatanapʋ nkɛ nsa, o mee gyii yɛgɛ o mee nu.

eng: In Damascus there was a certain disciple named Ananias. The Lord spoke to him in a vision, Ananias! He answered, Yes, Lord.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu ɔbɩɩlapʋ kʋ bʋ Damasiko kadɛ tɔ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Ananiyasi. Nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu lɛɛ ansi sʋ atɔ kaapʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ, Ananiyasi. Nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ n kyula.

eng: The Lord instructed him, Go to Judas house on Straight Street and ask for a man from Tarsus named Saul. He is praying.
local: Nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ tɔwɛ Ananiyasi yɛ, Kʋsʋ kpee Gyudasi kɩkpaara mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ kigbenbi kyiigisa mʋ sʋ, naa taasɛ ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ Tasusi kadɛ tɔ nɩɩ bɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Sɔɔlɩ mʋ. Ɔ bʋ tʋtɔ ɛɛ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ.

eng: In a vision he has seen a man named Ananias enter and put his hands on him to restore his sight.
local: Nɩɩ Sɔɔlɩ gbaa wu ansi sʋ kɩlɛɛkaapʋ mʋ tɔ, fɛɛ ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Ananiyasi maa ba kɛ ɔ ba taa mʋ abaa gyaga mʋ sʋ kɛ mu ansi i bugi.

eng: Ananias countered, Lord, I have heard many reports about this man. People say he has done horrible things to your holy people in Jerusalem.
local: Amaa Ananiyasi lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, n nu asɩn damantɛ lɩɩ ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ sʋ, kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ nyanla fʋ asa kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Gyerusalem mʋ.

eng: Hes here with authority from the chief priests to arrest everyone who calls on your name.
local: An nu yɛ Sɔɔlɩ nɩmʋ nyɛ ɔlʋn lɩɩ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ ba Damasiko nfɩɩ, ɔ ba kɩtaa asa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ fʋ Yesu kɩtɩɩ mʋ.

eng: The Lord replied, Go! This man is the agent I have chosen to carry my name before Gentiles, kings, and Israelites.
local: Amaa Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu tɔwɛ Ananiyasi yɛ, Kʋsʋ kpee! Lɩɩ fɛɛ mɛ ɩ lɛɛ ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ fɛɛ mɛ kɩbaa tɔ nyɩmɩsa, kɛ ɔ taa mɛ asɩn mʋ naa tɔwɛ bamʋ nɩɩ be mee sun Ɩbwaarɛ, yɛ ba maa bamʋ awura, yɛ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi.

eng: I will show him how much he must suffer for the sake of my name.
local: Nɩɩ mɛ gbaa‐gbaa mɛ n biti n kaapʋ mʋ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ee biti o gyii awʋrʋfɔ lɩɩ mɛ kɩtɩɩ sʋ.

eng: Ananias went to the house. He placed his hands on Saul and said, Brother Saul, the Lord sent me—Jesus, who appeared to you on the way as you were coming here. He sent me so that you could see again and be filled with the Holy Spirit.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Ananiyasi kpee kɩkpaara mʋ sʋ nɩɩ ɔ naa taa mʋ abaa gyaga Sɔɔlɩ sʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Mɛ bɩrɩsa Sɔɔlɩ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ kaapʋ fʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ fɩɩ ba nfɩɩ mʋ, i sun mɛ fʋ asɛ kɛ fu ansi i bwii bugi, kɛ fʋ nyɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ.

eng: Instantly, flakes fell from Sauls eyes and he could see again. He got up and was baptized.
local: Nɩɩ opula tɔ atɔ kʋ fɛɛ ɔkɩn agyafɔyɩ ɩ lɩɩ Sɔɔlɩ ansi tɔ tɩyɛ, nɩɩ mu ansi i bugi. Nɩɩ ɔ kʋsʋ nɩɩ ba naa gyee mʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee.

eng: After eating, he regained his strength. He stayed with the disciples in Damascus for several days.
local: Maa gyii agyitɔ kamaa tɔ mʋ, ɔ nyɛ ɔlʋn. Nɩɩ Sɔɔlɩ gyii nkɛ nkʋ Damasiko Yesu abuupu mʋ asɛ.

eng: Right away, he began to preach about Jesus in the synagogues. He is Gods Son, he declared.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ kpee Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɩgyaŋɛkpa naa tɔwɛ Yesu asɩn yɛ, Yesu ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mu‐bii.
