eng: Everyone who heard him was baffled. They questioned each other, Isnt he the one who was wreaking havoc among those in Jerusalem who called on this name? Hadnt he come here to take those same people as prisoners to the chief priests.
local: Asa mʋ nɩɩ ba nu Sɔɔlɩ katɔwɛ mʋ, ɩ waa bamʋ kɩyan nɩɩ ba taasɛ yɛ, Man gyɛ ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ ɩ kyɩna ɛɛ nyanla asa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Gyerusalem bɛɛ tɔwɛ asɩn Yesu kɩtɩɩ tɔ mʋ? Nɩɩ man gyɛ kanɩn sʋ nɩɩ o bwii ba, kɛ ɔ ba kɩtaa bamʋ naa sa Ɩbwaarɛ oseepu bɩlɩsa o tii obu.

eng: But Saul grew stronger and stronger. He confused the Jews who lived in Damascus by proving that Jesus is the Christ.
local: Amaa Sɔɔlɩ nyɛ ɔlʋn tii sʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ tɔwɛ, ɛɛ kaapʋ tɔ yɛ Yesu ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋnɩ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ yɛ ɔ ba mɔlɩgɛ asa. Nɩɩ i kyinkyin Gyiwu abi mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Damasiko kadɛ tɔ mʋ.

eng: After this had gone on for some time, the Jews hatched a plot to kill Saul.
local: Nɩɩ ɩmʋ kamaa tɔ ɩ maa gyii nkɛ nkʋ mʋ Gyiwu abi ba ŋmina yɛ ba mɔɔ Sɔɔlɩ.

eng: However, he found out about their scheme. They were keeping watch at the city gates around the clock so they could assassinate him.
local: Amaa Sɔɔlɩ bɩɩ bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ lɩɩ mʋ kɩmɔɔ mʋ sʋ lɩɩ fɛɛ ba sii ba tɛ bɛɛ kɩɩ kadɛ mʋ nwusunɔ mʋ sʋ kakyʋwapɛ yɛ kagyanbwɛ kʋmaa kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba nyɛ mʋ mɔɔ.

eng: But his disciples took him by night and lowered him in a basket through an opening in the city wall.
local: Amaa Yesu abuupu ba taa mʋ waa kɩlantan tɔ kakyanbwɛ, baala mʋ taa mʋ bʋlɛ kɩbandɩyɛ ɔbɔ tɔ kpɩlɩgaa mʋ yii.

eng: When Saul arrived in Jerusalem, he tried to join the disciples, but they were all afraid of him. They didnt believe he was really a disciple.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Sɔɔlɩ kpee Gyerusalem mʋ, o biti fɛɛ ɔ taa mʋ n‐yɩɩ naa tii abɩɩlapʋ mʋ sʋ, amaa ba waa kufuu yɛ mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ba man kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii yɛ o tii Yesu abuupu mʋ sʋ.

eng: Then Barnabas brought Saul to the apostles and told them the story about how Saul saw the Lord on the way and that the Lord had spoken to Saul. He also told them about the confidence with which Saul had preached in the name of Jesus in Damascus.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Banabasi taa mʋ kpee asunpu mʋ asɛ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Sɔɔlɩ wu Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ Damasiko ɔkpa tɔ, yɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ tɔwɛ Sɔɔlɩ mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ bɩla tɔwɛ bamʋ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Sɔɔlɩ man waa kufuu, nɩɩ ɔ yɩlɛ tɔwɛ lɩɩ Yesu Krisito kɩtɩɩ tɔ Damasiko kadɛ tɔ.

eng: After this, Saul moved freely among the disciples in Jerusalem and was speaking with confidence in the name of the Lord.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ Sɔɔlɩ kyɩna bamʋ asɛ Gyerusalem yɛgɛ ɔ sʋ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ kɩtɩɩ tɔ ɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛgɛ ɔ man sʋ kufuu.

eng: He got into debates with the Greek-speaking Jews as well, but they tried to kill him.
local: Nɩɩ Sɔɔlɩ maa Giriki abi mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɔwɛ Gyiwu katɔwɛ mʋ, ba gyii abaa ɩmɔɔrɛ, amaa yɛgɛ ba biti mʋ kɩmɔɔ ɔkpa.

eng: When the family of believers learned about this, they escorted him down to Caesarea and sent him off to Tarsus.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ mʋ tɔɔmaa ana ba bɩɩ kanɩn mʋ, ba taa mʋ kpee Kasariya kadɛ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ naa kyaga sʋ kpee mʋ kadɛ tɔ Tasusi.

eng: Then the church throughout Judea, Galilee, and Samaria enjoyed a time of peace. God strengthened the church, and its life was marked by reverence for the Lord. Encouraged by the Holy Spirit, the church continued to grow in numbers.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ Krisito abi kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ Gyudeya yɛ Galile yɛ Samariya kpini ba nyɛ kayɩɩ yuuli. Nɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ waa bamʋ aniya tɔ, nɩɩ ba nyɛ akyɔ bɛɛ kpan bamʋ n‐yɩɩ, yɛgɛ ba sɩlɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ.

eng: As Peter toured the whole region, he went to visit Gods holy people in Lydda.
local: Nɩɩ Pita kʋsʋ ɔ naa ɔ naa ɩsʋwʋlɛ mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ naa bɔɔ akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Lida mʋ sʋ.

eng: There he found a man named Aeneas who was paralyzed and had been confined to his bed for eight years.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔ naa wu ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ mʋ n‐yɩɩ i wu, nɩɩ ɔ dɛ nsu nburuwa nɩɩ bɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Aneyasi.

eng: Peter said to him, Aeneas, Jesus Christ heals you! Get up and make your bed. At once he got up.
local: Nɩɩ Pita tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Aneyasi, kʋsʋ taa fʋ kalan, Yesu Krisito kyɛ fʋ. Opula tɔ nɩɩ ɔ kʋsʋ.

eng: Everyone who lived in Lydda and Sharon saw him and turned to the Lord.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Lida yɛ Saron mʋ kpini ba maa wu kanɩn mʋ, nɩɩ ba kyɛɛgɛ bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ biliŋi ba Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ asɛ.

eng: In Joppa there was a disciple named Tabitha . Her life overflowed with good works and compassionate acts on behalf of those in need.
local: Nɩɩ Gyopa kadɛ tɔ ɔkyɩɩ kɔɔlɛ‐gyipu kʋ bʋ tʋtɔ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Tabita. Tabita ɩmʋ Giriki katɔwɛ ɩ gyɛ yɛ Dokasi. Dokasi maŋa ɩ waa adanbɩrasa yɛgɛ ɛɛ kyɛ atiripu tɔ saŋa kʋmaa.

eng: About that time, though, she became so ill that she died. After they washed her body, they laid her in an upstairs room.
local: Kanɩn saŋa maa mʋ kɩlɔ kɩ daa ɔkyɩɩ mʋ, nɩɩ o wu. Nɩɩ ba gyee mʋ kubuni naa dɩɩla abansʋrʋ obu to.

eng: Since Lydda was near Joppa, when the disciples heard that Peter was there, they sent two people to Peter. They urged, Please come right away.
local: Lida kadɛ kɩ man bʋ kata yɛ Gyopa kadɛ. Ɩmʋ sʋ Yesu abuupu mʋ ba maa nu fɛɛ Pita bʋ Lida mʋ, nɩɩ ba sun anyɩn anyɔ Pita asɛ naa tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Waa bilen kɛ fʋ ba anɛ asɛ.

eng: Peter went with them. Upon his arrival, he was taken to the upstairs room. All the widows stood beside him, crying as they showed the tunics and other clothing Dorcas made when she was alive.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ Pita maa bamʋ nɩɩ ba sun mʋ asɛ mʋ, ba bwii kpee Gyopa. Nɩɩ ba taa Pita kpee abansʋrʋ obu mʋ tɔ, ntɩn mʋ nɩɩ obuni mʋ dɛ mʋ. Nɩɩ akulapu‐kyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ obu mʋ tɔ mʋ kpini, ba kyaabɔɔ Pita bɛ sawʋ yɛgɛ ba sʋ atɔ bunsa mʋ nɩɩ Dokasi baata sa bamʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛ nkpa tɔ mʋ bɛɛ kaapʋ Pita.

eng: Peter sent everyone out of the room, then knelt and prayed. He turned to the body and said, Tabitha, get up! She opened her eyes, saw Peter, and sat up.
local: Amaa nɩɩ Pita tɔwɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ obu mʋ tɔ kpini yɛ, ba lɩɩ kewu. Nɩɩ Pita muŋa mʋ ayaa muuli sʋ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ, nɩɩ o biliŋi mu ansi kaapʋ obuni mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Tabita, kʋsʋ. Nɩɩ o bugi mʋ ansi wu Pita, nɩɩ ɔ kʋsʋ kyɩna.

eng: He gave her his hand and raised her up. Then he called Gods holy people, including the widows, and presented her alive to them.
local: Nɩɩ Pita kɩtaa mʋ kɩbaa tɔ kʋsaa mʋ yɩlɛ. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu yɛ akulapu‐kyɩɩ mʋ taa ɔkyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ o kyiŋi mʋ sa bamʋ.

eng: The news spread throughout Joppa, and many put their faith in the Lord.
local: Falɛ asɩn nɩmʋ ɩ yaasɛ loo Gyopa kadɛ tɔ kpini. Nɩɩ asa gaalaagaa ba kɔɔlɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu mʋ gyii.

eng: Peter stayed for some time in Joppa with a certain tanner named Simon.
local: Nɩɩ Pita kyɩna Gyopa kadɛ tɔ, ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ Simon nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ gbansaba mʋ asɛ nkɛ nnyɔ kʋ.

eng: There was a man in Caesarea named Cornelius, a centurion in the Italian Company.
local: Ɔbɩlɩsa kʋ bʋ tɔ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Koneliyo. Nɩɩ ɔ tɛ Kasariya kadɛ tɔ. Ɔ kyɩna ɔ gyɛ anaakɔpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa sa Roma anaakɔpʋ kʋbʋ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ kɩmʋ yɛ Itali abi kʋbʋ mʋ.

eng: He and his whole household were pious, Gentile God-worshippers. He gave generously to those in need among the Jewish people and prayed to God constantly.
local: Maa mʋ kʋʋpʋ ana ba taa bamʋ n‐yɩɩ sa Ɩbwaarɛ nɩɩ ba sɩlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ. Ɔ gyɛ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kɛɛ atiripu atɔ nɩɩ ɔ bɩla ɔ sʋ saŋa sa kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ.

eng: One day at nearly three oclock in the afternoon, he clearly saw an angel from God in a vision. The angel came to him and said, Cornelius.
local: Kanyɩn nɩmʋ nyɛ kɩlɛɛkaapʋ kakɛ kʋ kakyʋwapɛ ɩ waa fɛɛ kerefi asa saŋa tɔ. O wu Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ maa ba mʋ asɛ nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ, Koneliyo.

eng: Startled, he stared at the angel and replied, What is it, Lord? The angel said, Your prayers and your compassionate acts are like a memorial offering to God.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ kɩɩ mʋ yɛ kufuu nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mɩnɛ nɩn? Nɩɩ kabɔɔ mʋ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ ansi i gyii fʋ kakɛɛ yɛ fʋ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ee biti o kyula fʋ.

eng: Send messengers to Joppa at once and summon a certain Simon, the one known as Peter.
local: Nbɩɩnbɩɩ, sun asa kʋ kpee Gyopa ba naa tɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Simon Pita mʋ ba.

eng: He is a guest of Simon the tanner, whose house is near the seacoast.
local: Ɔ bʋ Simon mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ gbansaba mʋ ayɛ nɩn, nɩɩ kɩ bʋ kɩ maa ɔpʋ mʋ.

eng: When the angel who was speaking to him had gone, Cornelius summoned two of his household servants along with a pious soldier from his personal staff.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ ɩ kyʋn mʋ, nɩɩ Koneliyo tɩɩ mʋ ayaafɔlɛ anyɔ yɛ mʋ anaakɔpʋ mʋ tɔ ɔkʋlʋn mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ waa kʋtɔ kʋmaa ɛɛ sa mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ n‐yɩɩ sa Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ.

eng: He explained everything to them, then sent them to Joppa.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o wu nɩɩ o sun bamʋ Gyopa.

eng: At noon on the following day, as their journey brought them close to the city, Peter went up on the roof to pray.
local: Lɩɩkaakɛ mʋ Koneliyo nbɔɔ ba maa tiri Gyopa mʋ Pita mɔɔ dii kpee abansʋrʋ sʋsʋ ɔ naa kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ kakyʋwapɛ maŋa.

eng: He became hungry and wanted to eat. While others were preparing the meal, he had a visionary experience.
local: Akʋn a kɩtaa mʋ, nɩɩ ee biti kʋtɔ kʋ o gyii. Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa agyitɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ ansi sʋ kɩlɛɛkaapʋ.

eng: He saw heaven opened up and something like a large linen sheet being lowered to the earth by its four corners.
local: O wu sʋsʋ maa bugi nɩɩ o wu kʋtɔ kʋ, maa lɩɩ sʋsʋ fɛɛ kɩtaa lala kɩɩ kpɩlɩgɛ kɩɩ ba kasɛ. Kɩ maa kɩmʋ anobi ana.

eng: Inside the sheet were all kinds of four-legged animals, reptiles, and wild birds.
local: Kɩtaa mʋ sʋ mʋ, o wu atɔbwaaya kʋmaa yɛ nbwiibi yɛ atɔbwaaya mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ kpɩɩ ɩtɔ sʋ kpini.

eng: A voice told him, Get up, Peter! Kill and eat.
local: Nɩɩ o nu ɔbʋlɛ kʋ maa tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Pita! Kʋsʋ mɔɔ kɛ fu gyii.

eng: Peter exclaimed, Absolutely not, Lord! I have never eaten anything impure or unclean.
local: Nɩɩ Pita tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ ayee, n man pɩɩta gyii falɛ ineesi atɔ abɛɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ anɛ Gyiwu abi ikisii.

eng: The voice spoke a second time, Never consider unclean what God has made pure.
local: Nɩɩ ɔbʋlɛ mʋ bɩla tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Man bɩla tɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ lɔŋɔ kɩmʋ mʋ, yɛ ineesi kʋtɔ.

eng: This happened three times, then the object was suddenly pulled back into heaven.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ waa kanɩn ɩlɩɩ ɩsa, nɩɩ ɩ taa kɩtaa mʋ yɛ atɔ mʋ bwii kyʋn sʋsʋ.

eng: Peter was bewildered about the meaning of the vision. Just then, the messengers sent by Cornelius discovered the whereabouts of Simons house and arrived at the gate.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Pita san ɛɛ fɛ ɩmʋ nfɛɛrɛ mʋ, nɩɩ asa mʋ nɩɩ Koneliyo sun mʋ ba bɩɩ Simon kɩkpaara mʋ, nɩɩ ba ba yɩlɛ kɩmʋ kewusunɔ.

eng: Calling out, they inquired whether the Simon known as Peter was a guest there.
local: Nɩɩ ba tɩɩ ɔkʋ taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɔnyɩn kʋ bʋ nfɩɩ nɩɩ bɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Simon Pita.

eng: While Peter was brooding over the vision, the Spirit interrupted him, Look! Three people are looking for you.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Pita san ɛɛ fɛ mʋ kɩlɛɛkaapʋ mʋ nfɛɛrɛ mʋ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Anyɩn asa akʋ bee biti fʋ.

eng: Go downstairs. Dont ask questions; just go with them because I have sent them.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ kpɩlɩgɛ kpee bamʋ asɛ, man kan kina katɩn kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ faa bamʋ fen biti fan kpee, lɩɩ fɛɛ mɛ ɩ sun bamʋ fʋ asɛ.

eng: So Peter went downstairs and told them, Im the one you are looking for. Why have you come.
local: Nɩɩ Pita kpɩlɩgɛ ba kasɛ tɔ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ anyɩn mʋ yɛ, Mɛ ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ fen biti mʋ. Mɩnɛ ɩ waa.

eng: They replied, Weve come on behalf of Cornelius, a centurion and righteous man, a God-worshipper who is well-respected by all Jewish people. A holy angel directed him to summon you to his house and to hear what you have to say.
local: Nɩɩ anyɩn mʋ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ Anaakɔpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Koneliyo mʋ i sun anɛ. Ɔ gyɛ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ mʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi nɩɩ ɔ sɩlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ, nɩɩ Gyiwu abi kpini bɛ sa mʋ bɛɛrɛɛ. Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ ɩ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ ɔ tɩɩ fʋ ba mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ, kɛ o nu kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ fʋ sʋ sa mʋ.

eng: Peter invited them into the house as his guests. The next day he got up and went with them, together with some of the believers from Joppa.
local: Nɩɩ Pita taa anyɩn mʋ loo kɩkpaara mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ bamʋ, kɩtaa bamʋ kɩfɔ. Lɩɩkaakɛ mʋ, nɩɩ Pita maa Yesu abɩɩlapʋ akʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba lɩɩ Gyopa mʋ ba buu bamʋ.

eng: They arrived in Caesarea the following day. Anticipating their arrival, Cornelius had gathered his relatives and close friends.
local: Ɩmʋ lɩɩkaakɛ nɩɩ ba fʋʋ Kasariya. Yɛgɛ Koneliyo maa mʋ kʋʋpʋ ana yɛ mʋ kyɛmɩnɛ ana ba sarɛ bee gyoo mʋ.

eng: As Peter entered the house, Cornelius met him and fell at his feet in order to honor him.
local: Pita maa biti o loo kɩkpaara mʋ sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ Koneliyo gyaŋa mʋ, nɩɩ o muŋa o sun mʋ.

eng: But Peter lifted him up, saying, Get up! Like you, Im just a human.
local: Amaa Pita kɩtaa mʋ kɩbaa tɔ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ Kʋsʋ yɩlɛ! Ngbaa mɛ n dan n gyɛ nyɩmɩsa nɩn fɛɛ fʋ falɛ.

eng: As they continued to talk, Peter went inside and found a large gathering of people.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ maa Pita bɛɛ waa kiligyima bee loo kɩkpaara mʋ sʋ mʋ, ɔ naa tu asa ba maa sarɛ ba tɛ.

eng: He said to them, You all realize that it is forbidden for a Jew to associate or visit with outsiders. However, God has shown me that I should never call a person impure or unclean.
local: Nɩɩ Pita tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fanɛ gbaa‐gbaa fanɛ fan dan fen nyi fɛɛ naafɔɔ ɩ man dɛ ɔkpa fɛɛ Gyiwu nyɩmɩsa maa bʋga ɔmʋ nɩɩ o mee sun Ɩbwaarɛ abɛɛ ɔ bɔɔ mʋ sʋ. Amaa Ɩbwaarɛ kaapʋ mɛ fɛɛ n man tɔwɛ ɔkʋ yɛ ɔ man du kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ.

eng: For this reason, when you sent for me, I came without objection. I want to know, then, why you sent for me.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan maa sun tɩɩ mɛ mʋ n man kina, kanɩn sʋ fan tɔwɛ mɛ kʋtɔ mʋ sʋ nɩɩ fan tɩɩ mɛ.

eng: Cornelius answered, Four days ago at this same time, three oclock in the afternoon, I was praying at home. Suddenly a man in radiant clothing stood before me.
local: Nɩɩ Koneliyo lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ndaga nkɛ nna nɩmʋ n maa kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ kerefi asa kakyʋwapɛ saŋa mʋ. Opula tɔ nɩɩ n wu ɔnyɩn kʋ maa bun kaalɛ fufuli ba yɩlɛ mi ansi tɔ.

eng: He said, Cornelius, God has heard your prayers, and your compassionate acts are like a memorial offering to him.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, Koneliyo Ɩbwaarɛ nu fʋ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ, nɩɩ o wu fʋ kakɛɛ mʋ nɩɩ fɩɩ kɛɛ atiripu mʋ.

eng: Therefore, send someone to Joppa and summon Simon, who is known as Peter. He is a guest in the home of Simon the tanner, located near the seacoast.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ sun asa kpee Gyopa kɛ ba naa tɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Simon Pita mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ ɔfɔ ɔ bʋ Simon mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ gbansaba nɩɩ mʋ kɩkpaara bʋ mata ɔpʋ mʋ.

eng: I sent for you right away, and you were kind enough to come. Now, here we are, gathered in the presence of God to listen to everything the Lord has directed you to say.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ n sun tɩɩ fʋ bilen, nɩɩ ɩ bʋrɔn fɛɛ fʋ maa ba. Nbɩɩnbɩɩ an gyaŋɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ansi tɔ an nu fʋ asɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ taa sa fʋ yɛ fʋ ba tɔwɛ anɛ mʋ.

eng: Peter said, I really am learning that God doesnt show partiality to one group of people over another.
local: Nɩɩ Pita tɔwɛ yɛ, Nbɩɩnbɩɩ bɩrɛ n bɩɩ fɛɛ ɩ gyɛ kesintin Ɩbwaarɛ mɛɛ kɩɩ nyɩmɩsa ansi tɔ pɔyɩ nɩɩ ɛɛ waa kʋtɔ kʋ.

eng: Rather, in every nation, whoever worships him and does what is right is acceptable to him.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ kɔɔlɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ ndɛndɛmandɛ ɔ sɩlɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɛɛ waa ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa mʋ.

eng: This is the message of peace he sent to the Israelites by proclaiming the good news through Jesus Christ: He is Lord of all!.
local: Fanɛ gbaa‐gbaa fan nu asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ taa bɩya Gyiwu abi nɩɩ ɔ bɔla Yesu Krisito mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ dulinyaa tɔ atɔ kpini Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ sʋ taa kayɩɩ yuuli asɩn danbɩrasa sa bamʋ.

eng: You know what happened throughout Judea, beginning in Galilee after the baptism John preached.
local: Nɩɩ fan nyi asɩn kparɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ waa Gyiwu ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ nɩɩ i piili lɩɩ Galile mʋ. Ɩ waa saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Gyɔn tɔwɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee asɩn mʋ kamaa.

eng: You know about Jesus of Nazareth, whom God anointed with the Holy Spirit and endowed with power. Jesus traveled around doing good and healing everyone oppressed by the devil because God was with him.
local: Fan nu Yesu Kanasareti‐nyɩn mʋ asɩn, kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ taa mʋ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ yɛ ɔlʋn bɔla mʋ tɔ mʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ tii mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ katɩn kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o kpee mʋ ɛɛ waa adanbɩrasa yɛgɛ ɛɛ kyɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ Sɩtaanɛ bʋ mʋ tɔ.

eng: We are witnesses of everything he did, both in Judea and in Jerusalem. They killed him by hanging him on a tree.
local: Kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa Gyerusalem yɛ Ɩsɩrayɩ kpini‐kpini mʋ, anɛ asunpu nɩmʋ an gyɛ ɩmʋ kesintin agyipu. Ba da mʋ oyu laasa sʋ mɔɔ mʋ.

eng: but God raised him up on the third day and allowed him to be seen.
local: Amaa nkɛ nsa kamaa tɔ mʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kyiŋi mʋ lɩɩ lewu tɔ. Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ kaapʋ.

eng: not by everyone but by us. We are witnesses whom God chose beforehand, who ate and drank with him after God raised him from the dead.
local: Man gyɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ kaapʋ amɔɔ anɛ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ yɛ an baa an gyɛ mʋ kesintin agyipu mʋ maa wu kyiŋi kamaa tɔ mʋ ana mʋ a bʋga gyii nɩɩ an nu.

eng: He commanded us to preach to the people and to testify that he is the one whom God appointed as judge of the living and the dead.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu sa anɛ kɔnɔ yɛ a taa tɔwɛ Abwaarɛsɩn mʋ sa Gyiwu kasɛ sʋ asa kpini. Kɛ a lɛɛ kesintin mʋ kaapʋ fɛɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ asa kɩɩsa yɛ alewupu kpini ɔtalɛpʋ.

eng: All the prophets testify about him that everyone who believes in him receives forgiveness of sins through his name.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ mʋ kpini ba tɔwɛ mʋ asɩn mʋ kaapʋ tɔ yɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii mʋ, lɩɩ mʋ kɩtɩɩ tɔ sʋ mʋ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ taa mu alibi kɛɛ mʋ.

eng: While Peter was still speaking, the Holy Spirit fell on everyone who heard the word.
local: Pita maa san ɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ, Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ ba loo bamʋ nɩɩ bee nu asɩn mʋ kpini tɔ.

eng: The circumcised believers who had come with Peter were astonished that the gift of the Holy Spirit had been poured out even on the Gentiles.
local: I kyinkyin Gyiwu akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ nɩɩ ba buu Pita lɩɩ Gyopa ba mʋ gaa, fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ maa taa mʋ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi.

eng: They heard them speaking in other languages and praising God. Peter asked.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ba nu ba maa tɔwɛ ntɔwɛ pʋpwɛ yɛgɛ bɛɛ yɩn Ɩbwaarɛ. Nɩɩ Pita taasɛ yɛ.

eng: These people have received the Holy Spirit just as we have. Surely no one can stop them from being baptized with water, can they.
local: Ba maa nyɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ an nyɛ falɛ mʋ, ɔkʋ ɩ taalɛ tii ɔkpa fɛɛ ba man gyee bamʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee.

eng: He directed that they be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ. Then they invited Peter to stay for several days.
local: Nɩɩ Pita sa kɔnɔ yɛ ba gyee bamʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee lɩɩ Yesu Krisito kɩtɩɩ tɔ. Nɩɩ ba kʋlɛ Pita yɛ ɔ kyɩna bamʋ asɛ nkɛ nyɔ nkʋ.

eng: The apostles and the brothers and sisters throughout Judea heard that even the Gentiles had welcomed Gods word.
local: Krisito asunpu mʋ yɛ akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Gyudeya mʋ kpini ba nu yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ gbaa ba kɔɔlɛ abwaarɛsɩn mʋ.

eng: When Peter went up to Jerusalem, the circumcised believers criticized him.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Pita bwii kpee Gyerusalem mʋ, akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ nɩɩ ba yɩlɛ tɔ sa iduu kɩtɩn mʋ.

eng: They accused him, You went into the home of the uncircumcised and ate with them.
local: ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Fu loo bamʋ nɩɩ ba man tɩn iduu mʋ akpaara sʋ nɩɩ faa bamʋ fan bʋga gyii.

eng: Step-by-step, Peter explained what had happened.
local: Nɩɩ Pita lɛɛ kasɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ waa kʋkʋlʋn‐kʋkʋlʋn sa bamʋ tɔwɛ yɛ.

eng: I was in the city of Joppa praying when I had a visionary experience. In my vision, I saw something like a large linen sheet being lowered from heaven by its four corners. It came all the way down to me.
local: Kakaakʋ n bʋ Gyopa kadɛ tɔ n kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ kʋlɛ. Nɩɩ n nyɛ ansi sʋ kɩlɛɛkaapʋ wu sʋsʋ maa bugi. Nɩɩ n wu kʋtɔ kʋ maa lɩɩ sʋsʋ fɛɛ kɩtaa lʋʋsa kɩlala, nɩɩ ba sʋ kɩmʋ anobi ana mʋ tɔ ba kasɛ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ n bʋ.

eng: As I stared at it, wondering what it was, I saw four-legged animals—including wild beasts—as well as reptiles and wild birds.
local: N maa kɩɩ kɩmʋ tɔ mʋ, n wu atɔbwaaya yɛ nbwiibi yɛ atɔbwaaya mʋ nɩɩ bɛ kpɩɩ ɩtɔ sʋ.

eng: I heard a voice say, Get up, Peter! Kill and eat.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ n nu ɔbʋlɛ kʋ maa tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, Pita kʋsʋ mɔɔ kɛ fu gyii.

eng: I responded, Absolutely not, Lord! Nothing impure or unclean has ever entered my mouth.
local: Nɩɩ n lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ ayee, falɛ ineesi atɔ abɛɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ anɛ Gyiwu abi ii kisi ɩ man pɩɩta loo mɛ kɔnɔ tɔ.

eng: The voice from heaven spoke a second time, Never consider unclean what God has made pure.
local: Nɩɩ ɔbʋlɛ maŋa bɩla tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ man bɩla tɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ lɔŋɔ kɩmʋ kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ yɛ ineesi kʋtɔ.

eng: This happened three times, then everything was pulled back into heaven.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ waa kanɩn ɩlɩɩ ɩsa, nɩɩ ɩ taa kɩtaa mʋ yɛ atɔ mʋ bwii kyʋn sʋsʋ.

eng: At that moment three men who had been sent to me from Caesarea arrived at the house where we were staying.
local: Saŋa maŋa tɔ anyɩn asa mʋ nɩɩ ba sun bamʋ lɩɩ Kasariya ba ba mɛ asɛ mʋ, ba ba yɩlɛ kɩkpaara mʋ nɩɩ n bʋ kɩmʋ sʋ mʋ.

eng: The Spirit told me to go with them even though they were Gentiles. These six brothers also went with me, and we entered that mans house.
local: Nɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ man kan gyii ɩmɔɔrɛ, faa bamʋ fan kpee. Akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu asiye anɩmʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba lɩɩ Gyopa mʋ i sun mɛ kpee naa loo, ɔnyɩn mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ.

eng: He reported to us how he had seen an angel standing in his house and saying, Send to Joppa and summon Simon, who is known as Peter.
local: Ɔ tɔwɛ anɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ o wu Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ maa ba yɩlɛ mu obu to, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Sun asa akʋ kpee Gyopa ba naa tɩɩ Simon mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Pita mʋ.

eng: He will tell you how you and your entire household can be saved.
local: Ee biti ɔ tɔwɛ fanɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ faa fʋ kɩkpaara sʋ abi kpini fan nyɛ kamɔlɩgɛ.

eng: When I began to speak, the Holy Spirit fell on them, just as the Spirit fell on us in the beginning.
local: N maa piili n tɔwɛ bamʋ abwaarɛsɩn mʋ, Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ ba bamʋ tɔ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ o loo anɛ tɔ npiili asɛ mʋ.

eng: I remembered the Lords words: John baptized with water, but you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ n nyiŋi asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu wʋla tɔwɛ yɛ, Gyɔn taa nkyu nɩn gyee asa Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee. Amaa fanɛɛ fanɛ bɩrɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ taa mʋ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ gyee fanɛ.

eng: If God gave them the same gift he gave us who believed in the Lord Jesus Christ, then who am I? Could I stand in Gods way.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ fɛɛ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sa bamʋ kakɛɛ kʋlʋn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sa anɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ an kɔɔlɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu gyii mʋ, mɛ ɩ gyɛ anɩmʋ kɛ n taalɛ tii Ɩbwaarɛ ɔkpa.

eng: Once the apostles and other believers heard this, they calmed down. They praised God and concluded, So then God has enabled Gentiles to change their hearts and lives so that they might have new life.
local: Krisito abuupu mʋ maa nu kanɩn mʋ, ba man bɩla taalɛ tɔwɛ sɛhn, nɩɩ ba yɩn Ɩbwaarɛ. Nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ gbaa ɔkpa fɛɛ ba yɛgɛ alibi kɩwaa kɛ ba nyɛ nkpa.

eng: Now those who were scattered as a result of the trouble that occurred because of Stephen traveled as far as Phoenicia, Cyprus, and Antioch. They proclaimed the word only to Jews.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba twɩɩ abuu mɔɔ Sitefen mʋ, kanyanla kɩ ba Yesu akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ akʋ ba yaasɛ tɔ loo Fonesiya yɛ Sipuru yɛ Antiyoki kasɛ sʋ yɛgɛ bɛɛ tɔwɛ Yesu asɩn mʋ bɛɛ sa Gyiwu abi nkʋn.

eng: Among them were some people from Cyprus and Cyrene. They entered Antioch and began to proclaim the good news about the Lord Jesus also to Gentiles.
local: Yesu akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu akʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba lɩɩ Sipuru yɛ Siren ba Antiyoki mʋ, ba tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa lɩɩ Yesu Krisito sʋ sa Giriki abi gbaa.

eng: The Lords power was with them, and a large number came to believe and turned to the Lord.
local: Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ kɩbaa kɩmaa gyan bamʋ sʋ sʋ mʋ, asa damantɛ ba kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii nɩɩ ba be buu Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ.

eng: When the church in Jerusalem heard about this, they sent Barnabas to Antioch.
local: Krisito abi kʋbʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Gyerusalem mʋ ba maa nu kanɩn asɩn nɩmʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ba sun Banabasi kpee Antiyoki.

eng: When he arrived and saw evidence of Gods grace, he was overjoyed and encouraged everyone to remain fully committed to the Lord.
local: Maa naa fʋʋ tʋtɔ mʋ, nɩɩ o wu kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bwaala bamʋ mʋ, mu ansi i gyii nɩɩ ɔ waa bamʋ aniya tɔ yɛ ba nyɛ kɔkɔlɔ kʋlʋn Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii mʋ tɔ.

eng: Barnabas responded in this way because he was a good man, whom the Holy Spirit had endowed with exceptional faith. A considerable number of people were added to the Lord.
local: Banabasi kyɩna ɔ gyɛ nyɩmɩsa danbɩrasa nɩɩ ɔ sʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii kɩkyɔ nɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ bɔla mʋ tɔ lɩɩ kanɩn sʋ asa damantɛ ba ba kɔɔlɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ gyii.

eng: Barnabas went to Tarsus in search of Saul.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Banabasi kpee Tasusi kadɛ tɔ ɔ naa biti Sɔɔlɩ.

eng: When he found him, he brought him to Antioch. They were there for a whole year, meeting with the church and teaching large numbers of people. It was in Antioch where the disciples were first labeled Christians.
local: Maa naa wu mʋ mʋ, o bwiiyaa mʋ ba Antiyoki. Nɩɩ maa mʋ kpini ba sii Yesu abɩɩlapʋ mʋ asɛ kesu laalaamuu yɛgɛ bɛ kaapʋ asa damantɛ. Antiyoki tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba piili kasɛ bɛɛ tɩɩ Yesu abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ Krisito abi.

eng: About that time, some prophets came down from Jerusalem to Antioch.
local: Kanɩn saŋa maŋa tɔ mʋ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ akʋ ba lɩɩ Gyerusalem kpee Antiyoki.

eng: One of them, Agabus, stood up and, inspired by the Spirit, predicted that a severe famine would overtake the entire Roman world.
local: Bamʋ tɔ ɔkʋ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ Agabu mʋ, kʋsʋ yɩlɛ lɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ ɔlʋn tɔ sʋ tɔwɛ yɩla yɛ, akʋn lala a biti a tɛ Roma Ɩsʋwʋlɛ kpini sʋ.

eng: The disciples decided they would send support to the brothers and sisters in Judea, with everyone contributing to this ministry according to each persons abundance.
local: Kanɩn sʋ Yesu abɩɩlapʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Antiyoki mʋ, ba taa nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ ɔ taa kɩkyɛtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ taalɛ nyɛ, kɛ ba taa naa sa Yesu abɩɩlapʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Gyudeya mʋ.

eng: They sent Barnabas and Saul to take this gift to the elders.
local: Nɩɩ ba taa bamʋ nkyɛtɔ mʋ sa Banabasi maa Sɔɔlɩ yɛ ba taa naa sa Krisito abɩɩlapʋ abɩlɩsa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Gyerusalem mʋ.

eng: About that time King Herod began to harass some who belonged to the church.
local: Saŋa maŋa tɔ nɩɩ Owura Herodi yɛgɛ nɩɩ ba naa kɩtaa Krisito abɩɩlapʋ akʋ, nɩɩ ee biti fɛɛ ɔ nyanla bamʋ.

eng: He had James, Johns brother, killed with a sword.
local: Ɔ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ba tɩn Gyɔn mʋ‐daa Gyemisi kumu.

eng: When he saw that this pleased the Jews, he arrested Peter as well. This happened during the Festival of Unleavened Bread.
local: Maa wu fɛɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa mʋ maa pɩrɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ, ɔ kyaga sʋ naa kɩtaa Pita gbaa. Ɩnɩmʋ kpini ɩ waa saŋa mʋ nɩɩ bee gyii Kesu kɔnɔ kɔlɔŋɔ bodobodo mʋ nɩɩ kɩ man sʋ kɩfɛ tinsa mʋ.

eng: He put Peter in prison, handing him over to four squads of soldiers, sixteen in all, who guarded him. He planned to charge him publicly after the Passover.
local: Maa kɩtaa mʋ mʋ, ɔ taa mʋ tii obu nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ naa waa anaakɔpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba yɩlɛ ana ntun nna mʋ abaa tɔ yɛ bɛ kɩɩ obu tiisa mʋ sʋ. Herodi waa mʋ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ, nɩɩ ba kan gyii Kanyaŋɛ nbunɔ Kesu kɔnɔ kɔlɔŋɔ kyʋn lʋwɛ mʋ, ee biti ɔ lɛɛ Pita ifuli kɛ ba gyii mʋ asɩn.

eng: While Peter was held in prison, the church offered earnest prayer to God for him.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ Pita san o tii obu, amaa Krisito abi kʋbʋ mʋ ba payɩ kinsi bɛɛ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ bɛ sa mʋ.

eng: The night before Herod was going to bring Peters case forward, Peter was asleep between two soldiers and bound with two chains, with soldiers guarding the prison entrance.
local: Ɩmʋ kagyanbwɛ pɔyɩ kayɩ kɩ kɛ, kɛ Owura Herodi lɛɛ mʋ o be gyii mʋ asɩn mʋ, ba da mʋ ɩgbɛɛ yɛgɛ ɔ dɛ anaakɔpʋ anyɔ nsana yɛgɛ anaakɔpʋ akʋ ba bɩla ba yɩlɛ kebunɔ.

eng: Suddenly an angel from the Lord appeared and a light shone in the prison cell. After nudging Peter on his side to awaken him, the angel raised him up and said, Quick! Get up! The chains fell from his wrists.
local: Opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ ba obu tiisa mʋ tɔ nɩɩ ɩ ŋmaŋɛ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ da Pita kyiŋi, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Kʋsʋ yɩlɛ bilen. Nɩɩ agbeeleebi mʋ nɩɩ ba taa da mʋ ɩgbɛɛ mʋ ɩ saŋɛ ɩ lɩɩ tɩyɛ.

eng: The angel continued, Get dressed. Put on your sandals. Peter did as he was told. The angel said, Put on your coat and follow me.
local: Nɩɩ kabɔɔ mʋ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Bun fʋ atɔ kɛ fʋ waa asɩbɩta, kɛ fu bun fʋ balɩmaasʋ kɛ fʋ buu mɛ.

eng: Following the angel, Peter left the prison. However, he didnt realize the angel had actually done all this. He thought he was seeing a vision.
local: Nɩɩ Pita buu kabɔɔ mʋ lɩɩ abu tiisa mʋ tɔ, amaa Pita man nyi fɛɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kabɔɔ mʋ ɩ waa mʋ gyɛ kesintin. Ɩ waa mʋ nɩn fɛɛ ɩ gyɛ ansi sʋ kɩlɛɛkaapʋ nɩn.

eng: They passed the first and second guards and came to the iron gate leading to the city. It opened for them by itself. After leaving the prison, they proceeded the length of one street, when abruptly the angel was gone.
local: Ba kyʋn nbunɔ akɩɩsʋpʋ gyankpaasa kɩnyɔsapʋ mʋ sʋ naa fʋʋ kebunɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba taa abɩlɩtɔ waa mʋ, nɩɩ lɩɩ kɩmʋ tɔ taa kpee kadɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ. Ba maa fʋʋ tʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kebunɔ mʋ gbaa‐gbaa tigi nɩɩ ba lɩɩ. Ba maa natɛ ɔkpa mʋ sʋ kpee ansi tɔ gbɛrɛɛ mʋ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ kɩ nyaa mu ansi sʋ.

eng: At that, Peter came to his senses and remarked, Now Im certain that the Lord sent his angel and rescued me from Herod and from everything the Jewish people expected.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɩ ŋmaŋɛ Pita sʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɩ gyɛ kesintin nɩn fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ sun mʋ kabɔɔ yɛ ɔ ba kɔɔlɛ mɛ lɩɩ Owura Herodi abaa tɔ yɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Gyiwu abi ba da ŋmina yɛ ba waa mɛ mʋ.

eng: Realizing this, he made his way to Marys house. Many believers had gathered there and were praying.
local: Maa fɛ ɩnɩmʋ kpini nfɛɛrɛ mʋ o kpee Maki Gyɔn mu‐nyi Mɛɛrɩ kɩkpaara sʋ, opula mʋ nɩɩ asa kʋkyɔ, ba gyaŋɛ bɛɛ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ mʋ.

eng: When Peter knocked at the outer gate, a female servant named Rhoda went to answer.
local: Nɩɩ Pita da kewusunɔ ɔpʋnʋ, nɩɩ kibite mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Roda mʋ ba suŋa kɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ daa.

eng: She was so overcome with joy when she recognized Peters voice that she didnt open the gate. Instead, she ran back in and announced that Peter was standing at the gate.
local: Kebite mʋ maa bɩɩ yɛ ɩ gyɛ Pita ɔbʋlɛ mʋ, ɩ maa waa mʋ ɔkɔn gaa sʋ o man tigi ɔpʋnʋ mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ sɩlɛ naa tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ Pita ɩ yɩlɛ kewusunɔ.

eng: Youve lost your mind! they responded. She stuck by her story with such determination that they began to say, It must be his guardian angel.
local: Roda maa tɔwɛ kanɩn mʋ, nɩɩ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Fɩɩ lɔ ɩbʋn nɩn? Maa dan san ɔ yɩlɛ sʋ ɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ ɩ gyɛ kesintin mʋ, nɩɩ ba yɛ, Falɛ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kɩɩ Pita sʋ nɩn.

eng: Meanwhile, Peter remained outside, knocking at the gate. They finally opened the gate and saw him there, and they were astounded.
local: Amaa Pita dan san ɔ yɩlɛ kewusunɔ ɛɛ daa ɔpʋnʋ mʋ. Ba maa tigi ɔpʋnʋ mʋ, ba wu Pita, nɩɩ ɩ waa bamʋ kɩyan.

eng: He gestured with his hand to quiet them down, then recounted how the Lord led him out of prison. He said, Tell this to James and the brothers and sisters. Then he left for another place.
local: Nɩɩ Pita nyɛɛ bamʋ kɩbaa yɛ ba bun bamʋ nnɔ sʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ lɛɛ mʋ lɩɩ obu tiisa mʋ tɔ mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ yɛ, Fan naa tɔwɛ sa Gyɔn maa akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ nɩɩ ba san mʋ, kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ waa. Pita maa tɔwɛ lʋwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ bamʋ asɛ kpee katɩnkʋ.

eng: The next morning the soldiers were flustered about what had happened to Peter.
local: Ɩmʋ nyenyenfuu mʋ, nfɛɛrɛ ɩ kɩtaa anaakɔpʋ mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ba man nyi kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ waa Pita.

eng: Herod called for a thorough search. When Peter didnt turn up, Herod interrogated the guards and had them executed. Afterward, Herod left Judea in order to spend some time in Caesarea.
local: Owura Herodi sa kɔnɔ yɛ ba naa biti Pita ba. Amaa ba biti mʋ kpɩnɛ nɩɩ ba man wu mʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ Owura mʋ taasɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ tɔ asɩn kpɩnɛ, nɩɩ ɔ sa kɔnɔ yɛ ba mɔɔ bamʋ kpini. Nɩɩ Owura Herodi lɩɩ Gyudeya kpee Kasariya naa kyɩna tʋtɔ gyii nkɛ gbɛrɛɛ.

eng: Herod had been furious with the people of Tyre and Sidon for some time. They made a pact to approach him together, since their region depended on the kings realm for its food supply. They persuaded Blastus, the kings personal attendant, to join their cause, then appealed for an end to hostilities.
local: Owura Herodi nyɛ agbʋ lɩɩ Tayi yɛ Sidon abi sʋ. Nɩɩ ba waa kɔnɔ kʋlʋn sun kpee Herodi asɛ. Ba bɔla Owura Herodi kɩyaafɔlɛ bɩlɩsa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Bilasitu mʋ sʋ, sun ɩmata Herodi asɛ lɩɩ fɛɛ mʋ ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ nyɛ bamʋ agyitɔ bɛɛ lɩɩ.

eng: On the scheduled day Herod dressed himself in royal attire, seated himself on the throne, and gave a speech to the people.
local: Kakɛ mʋ nɩɩ ba yɩla maa fʋʋ mʋ, Owura Herodi bun mʋ awura tɔ naa kyɩna mʋ kuwura‐gya sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ asɩn mʋ tɔwɛ sa bamʋ.

eng: Those assembled kept shouting, over and over, This is a gods voice, not the voice of a mere human.
local: Nɩɩ asa mʋ ba kuusi nɩɩ ba gyaabii mʋ yɛ, Ɩnɩmʋ bɩrɛ ɩ man gyɛ nyɩmɩsa ɔbʋlɛ nɩn, amaa Ɩbwaarɛ kʋ ɔbʋlɛ nɩn.

eng: Immediately an angel from the Lord struck Herod down, because he didnt give the honor to God. He was eaten by worms and died.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Owura Herodi maa man taa kanɩn bʋnyaa sa Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ. Opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ yɛgɛ nɩɩ kɩlɔ kɩ da mʋ, nɩɩ ɔtɔ tɔ asʋnswɛɛ ba wɛ mʋ mɔɔ.

eng: Gods word continued to grow and increase.
local: Amaa abwaarɛsɩn mʋ ɩ dan san ɩ kyaga sʋ ɩ yɛlɩgɛ i tii sʋ i kpee ansi tɔ.

eng: Barnabas and Saul returned to Antioch from Jerusalem after completing their mission, bringing with them John, who was also known as Mark.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Banabasi maa Sɔɔlɩ ba lʋwɛ bamʋ kusun mʋ, ba lɩɩ Gyerusalem bwii kpee Antiyoki, nɩɩ ba taa Gyɔn mʋ nɩɩ ba bɩla bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Maki mʋ tii bamʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋ.

eng: The church at Antioch included prophets and teachers: Barnabas, Simeon , Lucius from Cyrene, Manaen , and Saul.
local: Kanɩn saŋa maŋa mʋ Krisito abi kʋbʋ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ Antiyoki mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ yɛ akaapʋpʋ akʋ ba bʋ tʋtɔ. Nɩɩ bamʋ ɩ gyɛ Banabasi, yɛ Simeyon mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Nigya, yɛ Lusiyu mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ Siren, yɛ Sɔɔlɩ maa Maniya mʋ nɩɩ ba bɩla maa Herodi bʋga mʋ.

eng: As they were worshipping the Lord and fasting, the Holy Spirit said, Appoint Barnabas and Saul to the work I have called them to undertake.
local: Ba maa kyaga sʋ bee sun Ɩbwaarɛ nɩɩ bee ŋmina kɔnɔ mʋ, Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ ba tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan lɛɛ Banabasi maa Sɔɔlɩ kɛ ba naa waa kusun mʋ nɩɩ n tɩɩ bamʋ yɛ ba ba waa mʋ.

eng: After they fasted and prayed, they laid their hands on these two and sent them off.
local: Nɩɩ ba bwii ŋmina kɔnɔ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ taa bamʋ abaa gyaga bamʋ sʋ sa bamʋ ɔkpa.

eng: After the Holy Spirit sent them on their way, they went down to Seleucia. From there they sailed to Cyprus.
local: Nɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ maŋa ɩ kaapʋ bamʋ, nɩɩ ɔ gyankpaa bamʋ, nɩɩ ba lɩɩ tʋtɔ kpee Selusiya, nɩɩ ba bɔla nkyu sʋ kpee Sipuru kifintin sʋ.

eng: In Salamis they proclaimed Gods word in the Jewish synagogues. John was with them as their assistant.
local: Ba maa fʋʋ Salami mʋ ba tɔwɛ abwaarɛsɩn mʋ Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa, nɩɩ Gyɔn Maki gbaa kyɛ bamʋ tɔ.

eng: They traveled throughout the island until they arrived at Paphos. There they found a certain man named Bar-Jesus, a Jew who was a false prophet and practiced sorcery.
local: Ɩmʋ kamaa ba natɛ kyaabɔɔ kifintin mʋ sʋ kpini naa fʋʋ Pafosi. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba tu Kagyiwu‐nyɩn kʋ mu nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Baa‐Yesu. Ɔ sʋ nbwɩɩ libi ɛɛ waa adangana nɩɩ ɔ waa mʋ n‐yɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ.

eng: He kept company with the governor of that province, an intelligent man named Sergius Paulus. The governor sent for Barnabas and Saul since he wanted to hear Gods word.
local: Kifintin mʋ sʋ owura mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ Segusi Polusi kyɩna ɔ gyɛ Baa Yesu mʋ kyɛmɩnɛ nɩn. Owura mʋ sʋ kanyaasɩn gaa. O sun tɩɩ Banabasi maa Pɔɔlɩ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ee biti o nu abwaarɛsɩn lɩɩ bamʋ asɛ.

eng: But Elymas the sorcerer opposed them, trying to steer the governor away from the faith.
local: Amaa nbwɩɩ libi wuya Baa‐Yesu, nɩɩ mʋ Giriki katɔwɛ tɔ ɩ gyɛ Elimasi mʋ, mee biti fɛɛ owura mʋ o nu kɛ ɔ kɔɔlɛ gyii.

eng: Empowered by the Holy Spirit, Saul, also known as Paul, glared at Bar-Jesus and.
local: Nɩɩ Sɔɔlɩ mʋ nɩɩ bɛ bɩla bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ Pɔɔlɩ, nɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ bɔla mʋ tɔ mʋ, kɩɩ Elimasi kyiim nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ.

eng: said, You are a deceiver and trickster! You devil! You attack anything that is right! Will you never stop twisting the straight ways of the Lord into crooked paths?.
local: Fʋ gyɛ Sɩtaanɛ obii nɩn nɩɩ fʋ gyɛ ɔdʋn sa kesintin kpini. Fʋ naa alibi ɔkpa sʋ fɩɩ pɩna asa, nɩɩ fii biti fu biliŋi Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ asɩn mʋ yɛ ɩ man waa kesintin.

eng: Listen! The Lords power is set against you. You will be blind for a while, unable even to see the daylight. At once, Bar-Jesus eyes were darkened, and he began to grope about for someone to lead him around by the hand.
local: Kɩɩ, Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ mʋ kɩbaa fʋ sʋ. Fii biti fu biliŋi gyaatanapʋ kɛ fʋ man bɩla wu kyɔwɛ naa fʋʋ saŋa kʋ. Opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ ɩ waa mu ansi sʋ kitentenbiri. Nɩɩ o sii ɛɛ kyaabɔɔ ee biti ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɩtaa mʋ kɩbaa tɔ kaapʋ mʋ ɔkpa.

eng: When the governor saw what had taken place, he came to believe, for he was astonished by the teaching about the Lord.
local: Owura mʋ maa wu kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ waa mʋ, yɛ abwaarɛsɩn kɩkaapʋ mʋ, ɩ waa mʋ kɩyan nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ asɩn mʋ gyii.

eng: Paul and his companions sailed from Paphos to Perga in Pamphylia. John deserted them there and returned to Jerusalem.
local: Pɔɔlɩ maa mʋ kʋbʋ ba lɩɩ Pafosi bɔla nkyu sʋ ba Pega kadɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ Panfiliya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ mʋ. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba maa Gyɔn Maki ba barɩgɛ abaa nɩɩ Gyɔn bwii kpee Gyerusalem.

eng: They went on from Perga and arrived at Antioch in Pisidia. On the Sabbath, they entered and found seats in the synagogue there.
local: Amaa Banabasi maa Pɔɔlɩ ba lɩɩ Pega kpee Antiyoki mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ Pisidiya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ mʋ. Kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ mʋ, ba kpee Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa tɔ naa kyɩna.

eng: After the reading of the Law and the Prophets, the synagogue leaders invited them, Brothers, if one of you has a sermon for the people, please speak.
local: Asa mʋ ɔkʋ maa kalɛ lɩɩ Mosesi nbara ɩwʋlɛ yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ ɩwʋlɛ lʋwɛ mʋ, tʋtɔ alʋlʋnpʋ ba taa tʋʋ Pɔɔlɩ maa Banabasi ansi tɔ yɛ, Anɛ bɩrɩsa ana, nɩɩ fan sʋ asɩn tɔwɛ waa asa mʋ, fan tɔwɛ anɛ.

eng: Standing up, Paul gestured with his hand and said, Fellow Israelites and Gentile God-worshippers, please listen to me.
local: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ kʋsʋ yɩlɛ nɩɩ ɔ kʋsaa mʋ kɩbaa nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Gyiwu abi yɛ fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fan man gyɛ Gyiwu abi, nɩɩ fen sun Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ, fan nu mɛ asɛ.

eng: The God of this people Israel chose our ancestors. God made them a great people while they lived as strangers in the land of Egypt. With his great power, he led them out of that country.
local: Anɛ Gyiwu abi anɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ, lɛɛ anɛ naana ana nɩɩ ɔ sa bamʋ kanyɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Egyipiti ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ ɔlʋn lɛɛ bamʋ lɩɩ ɔsʋwʋlɛ maŋa tɔ.

eng: For about forty years, God put up with them in the wilderness.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kɩɩ bamʋ sʋ nsu adɩna saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ ofuli sʋ mʋ.

eng: God conquered seven nations in the land of Canaan and gave the Israelites their land as an inheritance.
local: Ɩsʋwʋlɛ isunɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ Kena kasɛ sʋ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ gya bamʋ lɩɩ tʋtɔ, nɩɩ ɔ taa kasɛ mʋ sa Ɩsɩrayɩ abi nɩɩ ki biliŋi bamʋ kapatɩyɛ.

eng: This happened over a period of about four hundred fifty years. After this, he gave them judges until the time of the prophet Samuel.
local: Ɩmʋ kpini ɩ waa nsu alɩfa ana yɛ adunuu. Kamaa mʋ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sa bamʋ atalapʋ naa fʋʋ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Samuweli sʋ.

eng: The Israelites requested a king, so God gave them Saul, Kishs son, from the tribe of Benjamin, and he served as their king for forty years.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi ba tɔwɛ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ ɔ lɛɛ owura sa bamʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ Kiisi mu‐bii Sɔɔlɩ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ Bengyamin kanaan tɔ, nɩɩ o gyii kuwura nsu adɩna.

eng: After God removed him, he raised up David to be their king. God testified concerning him, I have found David, Jesses son, a man who shares my desires. Whatever my will is, he will do.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ maa kʋsaa Sɔɔlɩ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taa Dawidi yii Ɩsɩrayɩ kuwura. Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ, N wu Dawidi. Gyeese mu‐bii mʋ nɩɩ mɛ kɔkɔlɔ ki biti, nɩɩ mʋ ii biti ɔ waa mɛ kɔkɔlɔ biti.

eng: From this mans descendants, God brought to Israel a savior, Jesus, just as he promised.
local: Dawidi kanaan tɔ nɩɩ Gyiwu abi Ɔmɔlɩgɛpʋ Yesu mʋ lɩɩ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ tɔwɛ mʋ.

eng: Before Jesus appearance, John proclaimed to all the Israelites a baptism to show they were changing their hearts and lives.
local: Pɔyɩ Ɔmɔlɩgɛpʋ Yesu ɔ ba mʋ, Gyɔn wʋla gyankpaa ba tɔwɛ Gyiwu abi yɛ ba yɛgɛ alibi kɩwaa, kɛ ba nyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee.

eng: As John was completing his mission, he said, Who do you think I am? Im not the one you think I am, but he is coming after me. Im not worthy to loosen his sandals.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Gyɔn ii biti ɔ lʋwɛ mu kusun mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ, Fen nyi fɛɛ mɛ ɩ gyɛ Ɔmɔlɩgɛpʋ mʋ? N man gyɛ mɛ ɩ gyɛ Ɔmɔlɩgɛpʋ mʋ, mɛɛ mʋ ɔ bʋ mɛ kamaa ɛɛ ba. N man dagaa fɛɛ n saŋɛ mu asɩbɩta gbaa.

eng: Brothers, children of Abrahams family, and you Gentile God-worshippers, the message about this salvation has been sent to us.
local: Mɛ tɔmaa ana Abraham anaana abi yɛ fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fan man gyɛ Gyiwu abi nɩɩ fen sun Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ, anɛ sʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sa kamɔlɩgɛ asɩn mʋ.

eng: The people in Jerusalem and their leaders didnt recognize Jesus. By condemning him they fulfilled the words of the prophets that are read every Sabbath.
local: Nɩɩ Gyerusalem abi yɛ bamʋ abɩlɩsa kpini ba man bɩɩ fɛɛ Yesu ɩ gyɛ Ɔmɔlɩgɛpʋ mʋ. Yɛgɛ bee nu mʋ asɩn mʋ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ ɔwʋlɛ tɔ mʋ kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa kʋmaa, amaa ba man nu kasɛ. Ba bun mʋ kʋpwɛ nɩɩ ɩ yɛgɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ mʋ ba wʋla tɔwɛ yɩla mʋ ɩ ba tɔ.

eng: Even though they didnt find a single legal basis for the death penalty, they asked Pilate to have him executed.
local: Yɛgɛ ba man taalɛ wu mu alibi mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa nɩɩ ɔ dagaa lewu, nɩɩ ba yɛgɛ nɩɩ Ɔbɩlɩsa Paleti mɔɔ mʋ.

eng: When they finished doing everything that had been written about him, they took him down from the cross and laid him in a tomb.
local: Ba maa waa ɩmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ ba wʋla kyʋrɔɔ yɩla lɩɩ mʋ sʋ lʋwɛ mʋ, ba kpɩlɩgaa mʋ lɩɩ oyu laasa mʋ sʋ naa waa kakyan tɔ.

eng: But God raised him from the dead!.
local: Amaa Ɩbwaarɛ kyiŋi mʋ lɩɩ lewu tɔ ba nkpa tɔ.

eng: He appeared over many days to those who had traveled with him from Galilee to Jerusalem. They are now his witnesses to the people.
local: Nɩɩ nkɛ gaalaagaa mʋ tɔ mʋ, ɔ lɛɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ kaapʋ bamʋ nɩɩ maa bamʋ ba daa bɛɛ natɛ lɩɩ Galile bee kpee Gyerusalem mʋ. Bamʋ ɩ gyɛ mu kuwu kyiŋi kesintin agyipu bɛɛ sa Gyiwu abi.

eng: We proclaim to you the good news. What God promised to our ancestors.
local: Nɩɩ an tɔwɛ falɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ sa anɛ naana ana mʋ sa fanɛ.

eng: he has fulfilled for us, their children, by raising up Jesus. As it was written in the second psalm, You are my son; today I have become your father. .
local: Kanɩn asɩn maŋa nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ɩ ba tɔ sa anɛ, mʋ nɩɩ an gyɛ bamʋ‐bii ana mʋ, kpalɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kyiŋi Yesu lɩɩ lewu tɔ sʋ. Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ waa Dawidi ɔlʋn nyɔsapʋ mʋ tɔ yɛ, Ndaga n lɛɛ kaapʋ fɛɛ fʋ ɩ gyɛ mi‐bii.

eng: God raised Jesus from the dead, never again to be subjected to deaths decay. Therefore, God said, I will give to you the holy and firm promises I made to David. .
local: Kikyiŋi mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kyiŋi Yesu lɩɩ kelewu tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ gyɔɔ kakaakʋ kɩɩ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ ɩmʋ asɩn gbaa yɛ, N biti n waa fanɛ kusee sa fanɛ atɔ kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ n da mɛ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ tɔwɛ Dawidi mʋ.

eng: In another place it is said, You will not let your holy one experience deaths decay. .
local: Nɩɩ Dawidi tɔwɛ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ, Fʋ mɛɛ yɛgɛ kɛ fʋ Ɔkyɩrɛkyɩrɛpʋ mʋ ɔ gyɔɔ nyita.

eng: David served Gods purpose in his own generation, then he died and was buried with his ancestors. He experienced deaths decay.
local: Asɩn nɩmʋ man gyɛ Dawidi asɩn, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ natɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kebiti sʋ, mʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kyɩna, nɩɩ o wu, nɩɩ ba pula mʋ naa tii mʋ naana ana sʋ nɩɩ mʋ n‐yɩɩ ɩ gyɔɔ.

eng: but the one whom God has raised up didnt experience deaths decay.
local: Amaa Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kyiŋi mʋ lɩɩ lewu tɔ mʋ bɩrɛ man gyɔɔ.

eng: Therefore, brothers and sisters, know this: Through Jesus we proclaim forgiveness of sins to you. From all those sins from which you couldnt be put in right relationship with God through Moses Law.
local: Kanan sʋ mɛ bɩrɩsa ana Gyiwu abi, lɩɩ Yesu kɩtɩɩ tɔ sʋ nɩɩ an tɔwɛ alibi kɩtaa kɛɛ asɩn an sa fanɛ falɛ.

eng: through Jesus everyone who believes is put in right relationship with God.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ man bʋ tɔ ɔ taalɛ gyii lɩɩ Mosesi nbara mʋ kpini sʋ, amaa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ Yesu gyii bɩrɛ, ee biti o gyii lɩɩ.

eng: Take care that the prophets words dont apply to you.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan kɩɩ sʋ danbɩrasa kɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ ba tɔwɛ yɩla mʋ, ɩ man ba fanɛ sʋ. Asɩn mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ.

eng: Look, you scoffers , marvel and die . Im going to do work in your day — a work you wont believe even if someone told you .
local: Fanɛ ahin‐sɩn wuya ana, ii biti ɩ waa fanɛ kɩyan fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ n biti n mɔɔ fanɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ fanɛ saŋa nɩmʋ tɔ n biti n waa kʋtɔ kʋ nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan ba tɔwɛ gbaa, fan mɛɛ yɛ fan kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii.

eng: As Paul and Barnabas were leaving the synagogue, the people urged them to speak about these things again on the next Sabbath.
local: Pɔɔlɩ maa Banabasi ba maa lɩɩ Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ asa mʋ ba tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ ba bɩla bwii ba kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ, kɛ ba ba tɔwɛ bamʋ asɩn nɩmʋ tii sʋ.

eng: When the people in the synagogue were dismissed, many Jews and devout converts to Judaism accompanied Paul and Barnabas, who urged them to remain faithful to the message of Gods grace.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ asa mʋ ba lɩɩ ɔgyaŋɛkpa nɩɩ ba yaasɛ tɔ mʋ, Gyiwu abi mʋ gaalaagaa, yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi, nɩɩ ba kyɛɛgɛ bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ ba Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa tɔ mʋ, ba buu Pɔɔlɩ maa Banabasi. Nɩɩ ba waa asa mʋ ɔlʋn tɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ ba kyɩna Ɩbwaarɛ kabwaala mʋ tɔ.

eng: On the next Sabbath, almost everyone in the city gathered to hear the Lords word.
local: Nɩɩ kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa pʋpwɛ mʋ maa fʋʋ mʋ, kadɛ mʋ tɔ asa kɩbaafʋn mʋ nɩɩ ba kpee mʋ ɩ kyɔ gaa nɩɩ ba naa gyaŋɛ ba nu abwaarɛsɩn.

eng: When the Jews saw the crowds, they were overcome with jealousy. They argued against what Paul was saying by slandering him.
local: Amaa Gyiwu abɩlɩsa ba maa wu asa damantɛ ba maa nu Banabasi maa Pɔɔlɩ abwaarɛsɩn mʋ, ba nyɛ bamʋ kayɩɩ basa yɛgɛ bɛɛ tɔwɛ nbusaa asɩn bee nyita asɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ.

eng: Speaking courageously, Paul and Barnabas said, We had to speak Gods word to you first. Since you reject it and show that you are unworthy to receive eternal life, we will turn to the Gentiles.
local: Pɔɔlɩ maa Banabasi ba yɩlɛ kɩnyɩn tɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fanɛ Gyiwu abi nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ a maa gyankpaa tɔwɛ abwaarɛsɩn sa pɔyɩ. Amaa fan maa man kɔɔlɛ ɩmʋ gyii nɩɩ fan kɩɩ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ fɛɛ fan man dagaa sa nkpa kakpaa mʋ, an biti a taa asɩn mʋ naa sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi.

eng: This is what the Lord commanded us: I have made you a light for the Gentiles, so that you could bring salvation to the end of the earth .
local: Ɩnɩmʋ nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ sa kɔnɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, N taa fanɛ waa bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ fɩtɩla. Kɛ fan taa kamɔlɩgɛ mʋ naa sa asa dulinyaa katɩn kʋmaa.

eng: When the Gentiles heard this, they rejoiced and honored the Lords word. Everyone who was appointed for eternal life believed.
local: Nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ ba maa nu falɛ mʋ, ɩ waa bamʋ ɔkɔn gaa, nɩɩ ba taa bʋnyaa sa Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn mʋ. Nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ bamʋ fɛɛ ba ba nyɛ nkpa kakpaa mʋ ba kɔɔlɛ gyii.

eng: and the Lords word was broadcast throughout the entire region.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn mʋ ɩ yɛlɩgɛ kanɩn ɔsʋwʋlɛ maŋa sʋ kpini.

eng: However, the Jews provoked the prominent women among the Gentile God-worshippers, as well as the citys leaders. They instigated others to harass Paul and Barnabas, and threw them out of their district.
local: Nɩɩ Gyiwu abi ba fun waa akyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ ba sɩlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ nɩɩ ba sʋ bɛɛrɛɛ, yɛ anyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba gyɛ Antiyoki kadɛ mʋ tɔ agyankpaapʋ. Nɩɩ bamʋ kpini ba kʋsʋ yɩlɛ Pɔɔlɩ maa Banabasi sʋ bɛɛ nyanla bamʋ, nɩɩ ba gya bamʋ lɩɩ kanɩn ofuli maŋa sʋ.

eng: Paul and Barnabas shook the dust from their feet and went to Iconium.
local: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ maa Banabasi ba kpɩkpaa bamʋ ayaa ɩsɛ, nɩɩ ba waa bamʋ ahee. Nɩɩ ba lɩɩ tʋtɔ kpee Ikoniyum kadɛ mʋ tɔ.

eng: Because of the abundant presence of the Holy Spirit in their lives, the disciples were overflowing with happiness.
local: Yesu abɩɩlapʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Antiyoki tʋtɔ mʋ kpini ba nyɛ kensi‐gyii gaa. Nɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ bɔla bamʋ.

eng: The same thing happened in Iconium. Paul and Barnabas entered the Jewish synagogue and spoke as they had before. As a result, a huge number of Jews and Greeks believed.
local: Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ maa Banabasi ba wʋla bɛɛ waa mʋ, ba kpee Ikoniyum Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa. Nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ abwaarɛsɩn mʋ, nɩɩ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ Gyiwu abi yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ gaalaagaa ba kɔɔlɛ gyii.

eng: However, the Jews who rejected the faith stirred up the Gentiles, poisoning their minds against the brothers.
local: Amaa Gyiwu abi mʋ nɩɩ ba man kɔɔlɛ asɩn mʋ gyii mʋ, ba fun waa bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ, nyita bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ nɩɩ ba nyɛ nfɛɛrɛ libi lɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ maa Banabasi sʋ.

eng: Nevertheless, Paul and Barnabas stayed there for quite some time, confidently speaking about the Lord. And the Lord confirmed the word about his grace by the signs and wonders he enabled them to perform.
local: Pɔɔlɩ maa Banabasi ba gyii nkɛ gbɛrɛɛ tʋtɔ. Ba yɩlɛ kɩnyɩn tɔ bɛɛ tɔwɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ kabwaala asɩn. Nɩɩ ngbaa mʋ yɩlɛ bamʋ kamaa nɩɩ ba waa asɩn kyinkyinsa yɛ ɩsɩnkaala.

eng: The people of the city were divided—some siding with the Jews, others with the Lords messengers.
local: Nɩɩ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ kadɛ mʋ tɔ asa mʋ ba barɩgɛ tɔ ntun nnyɔ, akʋ ba buu Gyiwu abi nɩɩ akʋ mɔɔ ba buu Yesu asunpu mʋ.

eng: Then some Gentiles and Jews, including their leaders, hatched a plot to mistreat and stone Paul and Barnabas.
local: Saŋa maŋa mʋ, bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ akʋ yɛ Gyiwu abi, taa tii bamʋ agyankpaapʋ sʋ mʋ ba waa kɔnɔ kʋlʋn fɛɛ ba tɔɔrɔɔ bamʋ kɛ ba tʋʋ bamʋ abuu mɔɔ.

eng: When they learned of it, these two messengers fled to the Lycaonian cities of Lystra and Derbe and the surrounding area.
local: Amaa Pɔɔlɩ maa Banabasi ba maa bɩɩ bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ba sɩlɛ kpee Lisitira yɛ Debe ndɛ yɛ ndɛ‐bii mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ Likoniya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ kpini.

eng: where they continued to proclaim the good news.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba kyaga sʋ tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ.

eng: In Lystra there was a certain man who lacked strength in his legs. He had been crippled since birth and had never walked. Sitting there, he.
local: Ɔnyɩn kʋ tɛ Lisitira tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba kʋʋgɛ mʋ ayaa yɛ abaa wusa wuya ana, nɩɩ ɔ man natɛ kakaakʋ.

eng: heard Paul speaking. Paul stared at him and saw that he believed he could be healed.
local: Ɔ tɛ tʋtɔ ee nu Pɔɔlɩ maa tɔwɛ. Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ kɩɩ mʋ kyimm wu fɛɛ ɔnyɩn mʋ sʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii, nɩɩ ɔ taalɛ nyɛ alanfɩya.

eng: Raising his voice, Paul said, Stand up straight on your feet! He jumped up and began to walk.
local: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ pʋntɛ yɛ, Kʋsʋ yɩlɛ fʋ ayaa sʋ. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ fugi yɩlɛ mʋ ayaa sʋ nɩɩ o piili ɔ naa.

eng: Seeing what Paul had done, the crowd shouted in the Lycaonian language, The gods have taken human form and come down to visit us.
local: Sakyɔ mʋ maa wu kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ waa mʋ, ba kuusi Likoniya katɔwɛ tɔ yɛ, Anɛ ɩdakpa mʋ ba biliŋi asa ba anɛ asɛ.

eng: They referred to Barnabas as Zeus and to Paul as Hermes, since Paul was the main speaker.
local: Nɩɩ ba sa Banabasi kɩtɩɩ yɛ Sewu, nɩɩ ba sa Pɔɔlɩ gbaa kɩtɩɩ yɛ Hemesi lɩɩ fɛɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ asɩn mʋ ɔtɔwɛpʋ.

eng: The priest of Zeus, whose temple was located just outside the city, brought bulls and wreaths to the city gates. Along with the crowds, he wanted to offer sacrifices to them.
local: Nɩɩ ɔdakpa Sewu oseepu obu yɩlɛ kadɛ mʋ ɩkaa sʋ. Nɩɩ kɩmʋ oseepu taa ɩnaa‐nyɩn yɛ ntʋtʋlɛ ba kadɛ mʋ kewusunɔ, lɩɩ fɛɛ maa sakyɔ mʋ bee biti ba waa kɔlɔŋɔ sa Krisito asunpu mʋ.

eng: When the Lords messengers Barnabas and Paul found out about this, they tore their clothes in protest and rushed out into the crowd. They shouted.
local: Amaa ba maa nu kanɩn mʋ, ba kɩtaa bamʋ atɔ bunsa kyatɛ sɩlɛ loo sakyɔ mʋ tɔ, bee kuusi yɛ.

eng: People, what are you doing? We are humans too, just like you! We are proclaiming the good news to you: turn to the living God and away from such worthless things. He made the heaven, the earth, the sea, and everything in them. .
local: Anyɩn, mɩnɛ nɩɩ fɛn waa falɛ? Anɛ gbaa anɛ an gyɛ asa nɩn fɛɛ fanɛ falɛ. An sʋ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ an bɩya fanɛ nɩn. An tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ fan lɛɛ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ lɩɩ atɔ gyaga sʋ, kɛ fan bwii ba Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛ nkpa kakpaa tɔ mʋ asɛ. Mʋ ɩ tʋʋ sʋsʋ yɛ kasɛ yɛ ɔpʋ yɛ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ ɩmʋ kpini tɔ mʋ.

eng: In the past, he permitted every nation to go its own way.
local: Dɩdaa mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ mu ansi yɛgɛ nɩɩ dulinyaa tɔ asa kpini ba natɛ waa bamʋ gbaa‐gbaa bamʋ nkɔlɔ biti.

eng: Nevertheless, he hasnt left himself without a witness. He has blessed you by giving you rain from above as well as seasonal harvests, and satisfying you with food and happiness.
local: Amaa Ɩbwaarɛ waa adanbɩrasa kaapʋ asa fɛɛ maa bʋ tɔ, fɛɛ maa sa bʋlayɩ nɩɩ atɔ duusa a waa saŋa kyɛɛkyɛɛ tɔ. Nɩɩ ɛɛ sa fanɛ agyitɔ kɩkyɔ nɩɩ ɛɛ yɛgɛ fanɛ ansi i gyii.

eng: Even with these words, they barely kept the crowds from sacrificing to them.
local: Halɩɩ ba maa tɔwɛ ɩnɩmʋ kamaa gbaa mʋ, ɩ waa ɔlʋn pɔyɩ nɩɩ ba taalɛ gya sakyɔ mʋ yɛ ba man waa kɔlɔŋɔ mʋ.

eng: Jews from Antioch and Iconium arrived and won the crowds over. They stoned Paul and dragged him out of the city, supposing he was dead.
local: Tʋtɔ mʋ Gyiwu abi mʋ nɩɩ ba lɩɩ Antiyoki yɛ Ikoniyum mʋ ba ba tɔwɛ kyɛɛgɛ sakyɔ mʋ nfɛɛrɛ. Nɩɩ ba tʋʋ Pɔɔlɩ abuu waa nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ o wu nɩn, nɩɩ ba kpɩɩ mʋ lɩɩ kadɛ mʋ tɔ.

eng: When the disciples surrounded him, he got up and entered the city again. The following day he left with Barnabas for Derbe.
local: Amaa saŋa mʋ nɩɩ akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ ba ba kyaabɔɔ Pɔɔlɩ mʋ, ɔ kʋsʋ nɩɩ o bwii kpee kadɛ mʋ tɔ. Ɩmʋ lɩɩkaakɛ mʋ Pɔɔlɩ maa Banabasi ba lɩɩ tʋtɔ kpee Debe.

eng: Paul and Barnabas proclaimed the good news to the people in Derbe and made many disciples. Then they returned to Lystra, Iconium, and Antioch, where.
local: Nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ Debe kadɛ tɔ, nɩɩ ɩ yɛgɛ asa kɩkyɔ ba be buu Yesu. Nɩɩ ba bwii kpee Lisitira yɛ Ikoniyum ndɛ yɛ Antiyoki mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ Pisidiya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ mʋ.

eng: they strengthened the disciples and urged them to remain firm in the faith. They told them, If we are to enter Gods kingdom, we must pass through many troubles.
local: Ba tɔwɛ waa Yesu abɩɩlapʋ mʋ, waa bamʋ ɔlʋn tɔ fɛɛ ba yɩlɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ bɩla tɔwɛ yɛ, pɔyɩ kɛ ba nyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ loo mʋ, ɩ dagaa ba maa gyii awʋrʋfɔ.

eng: They appointed elders for each church. With prayer and fasting, they committed these elders to the Lord, in whom they had placed their trust.
local: Krisito abi kʋbʋ kʋmaa tɔ mʋ, ba lɛɛ abɩlɩsa sa bamʋ. Nɩɩ ba ŋmina kɔnɔ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ kʋlɛ taa bamʋ waa Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii mʋ abaa tɔ.

eng: After Paul and Barnabas traveled through Pisidia, they came to Pamphylia.
local: Ɩmʋ kamaa tɔ mʋ, ba bɔla lɩɩ Pisidiya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ taa ba fʋʋ Panfiliya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ.

eng: They proclaimed the word in Perga, then went down to Attalia.
local: Nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ Pega kadɛ tɔ kyaga sʋ kpee Ataliya kadɛ tɔ.

eng: From there they sailed to Antioch, where they had been entrusted by Gods grace to the work they had now completed.
local: Ba lɩɩ Ataliya bɔla nkyu sʋ kpee Antiyoki mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ Siriya. Antiyoki tʋtɔ nɩɩ Krisito abi kʋbʋ mʋ ba wʋla kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ taa bamʋ waa Ɩbwaarɛ kɩbaa tɔ, fɛɛ ɔ bwaala bamʋ kɛ ba taalɛ waa kusun mʋ nɩɩ ba waa lʋwɛ falɛ.

eng: On their arrival, they gathered the church together and reported everything that God had accomplished through their activity, and how God had opened a door of faith for the Gentiles.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba fʋʋ Antiyoki mʋ, ba tɩɩ Krisito abi kʋbʋ mʋ ba sarɛ katɩn kʋlʋn. Nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ bamʋ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bɔla bamʋ sʋ waa, yɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ o bugi ɔkpa sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi, nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ Yesu asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ gyii mʋ.

eng: They stayed with the disciples a long time.
local: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ maa Banabasi ba kyɩna Yesu abɩɩlapʋ mʋ asɛ kyɛɛrɛɛ.

eng: Some people came down from Judea teaching the family of believers, Unless you are circumcised according to the custom weve received from Moses, you cant be saved.
local: Amaa asa akʋ ba lɩɩ Gyudeya ba Antiyoki, nɩɩ ba ba bɛɛ kaapʋ akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ yɛ, nɩɩ ba kan man tin bamʋ iduu fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Mosesi nbara ɩ kaapʋ mʋ, ba mɛɛ yɛ ba nyɛ kamɔlɩgɛ.

eng: Paul and Barnabas took sides against these Judeans and argued strongly against their position. The church at Antioch appointed Paul, Barnabas, and several others from Antioch to go up to Jerusalem to set this question before the apostles and the elders.
local: Nɩɩ ɩnɩmʋ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ bɩɩtɛ yɛ kamɔɔrɛ‐gyii kɩ ba Pɔɔlɩ maa Banabasi yɛ asa mʋ nsana. Ɩmʋ sʋ ba lɛɛ Pɔɔlɩ maa Banabasi yɛ akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ akʋ fɛɛ ba kpee Gyerusalem naa wu Krisito asunpu yɛ abɩlɩsa lɩɩ kamɔɔrɛ‐gyii mʋ sʋ.

eng: The church sent this delegation on their way. They traveled through Phoenicia and Samaria, telling stories about the conversion of the Gentiles to everyone. Their reports thrilled the brothers and sisters.
local: Nɩɩ Krisito abi kʋbʋ mʋ ba lɛɛ bamʋ ɔkpa. Ba maa kpee mʋ ba bɔla lɩɩ Fonesiya yɛ Samariya. Yɛgɛ bɛɛ tɔwɛ bamʋ kanan mʋ nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ ba kɔɔlɛ Krisito gyii mʋ. Nɩɩ ɩ waa akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ tʋtɔ mʋ ɔkɔn.

eng: When they arrived in Jerusalem, the church, the apostles, and the elders all welcomed them. They gave a full report of what God had accomplished through their activity.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba fʋʋ Gyerusalem mʋ, Krisito abi kʋbʋ yɛ Krisito asunpu yɛ kʋbʋ mʋ bamʋ abɩlɩsa mʋ ba waa bamʋ nsa‐nsa. Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ maa Banabasi ba lɛɛ tɔwɛ bamʋ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bɔla bamʋ sʋ waa mʋ.

eng: Some believers from among the Pharisees stood up and claimed, The Gentiles must be circumcised. They must be required to keep the Law from Moses.
local: Amaa Farasii kʋbʋ mʋ tɔ akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ akʋ ba kʋsʋ yɩlɛ tɔ yɛ, Ɩ dagaa fɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi, nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ Krisito gyii mʋ, ba maa tɩŋɛ iduu fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Mosesi nbara mʋ ɩ kaapʋ tɔ mʋ.

eng: The apostles and the elders gathered to consider this matter.
local: Kanɩn sʋ Krisito asunpu mʋ yɛ abɩlɩsa mʋ ba gyaŋɛ kɛ ba taa ɩmʋ nfɛɛrɛ.

eng: After much debate, Peter stood and addressed them, Fellow believers, you know that, early on, God chose me from among you as the one through whom the Gentiles would hear the word of the gospel and come to believe.
local: Ba maa kyɩna asɩn mʋ sʋ gyii abaa ɩmɔɔrɛ kyɛɛrɛɛ mʋ, Pita kʋsʋ yɩlɛ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Mɛ daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana, fan nyi fɛɛ nsu nkʋ tɔ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ maa lɛɛ mɛ lɩɩ fanɛ tɔ yɛ n tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ ba nu kɛ ba kɔɔlɛ gyii.

eng: God, who knows peoples deepest thoughts and desires, confirmed this by giving them the Holy Spirit, just as he did to us.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ o nyi nyɩmɩsa kɔkɔlɔ tɔ asɩn mʋ, waa nɩɩ ɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ ɔ kɔɔlɛ bamʋ, fɛɛ ɔ maa sa bamʋ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sa anɛ gbaa anɛ mʋ.

eng: He made no distinction between us and them, but purified their deepest thoughts and desires through faith.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ man sʋ nbarɩgɛ tɔ yɛ anaa bamʋ nsana tɔ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ, lɩɩ bamʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ ɔ lɔŋɔ bamʋ nkɔlɔ tɔ.

eng: Why then are you now challenging God by placing a burden on the shoulders of these disciples that neither we nor our ancestors could bear?.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fen biti fan tʋʋ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔ kɩɩ, nɩɩ fɛn taa ɩsʋla budusa fɛn sʋla Yesu abuupu mʋ nɩɩ anɛ yɛ anɛ naana ana gbaa an man taalɛ sʋla?.

eng: On the contrary, we believe that we and they are saved in the same way, by the grace of the Lord Jesus.
local: An kɔɔlɛ gyii fɛɛ, lɩɩ anɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Yesu kabwaala mʋ sʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ mɔlɩgɛ anɛ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ bamʋ gbaa bamʋ ba nyɛ mʋ.

eng: The entire assembly fell quiet as they listened to Barnabas and Paul describe all the signs and wonders God did among the Gentiles through their activity.
local: Nɩɩ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba sarɛ tʋtɔ mʋ kpini ba laatɔ bee nu Banabasi yɛ Pɔɔlɩ ba maa tɔwɛ ɩsɩnkaala yɛ asɩn kyinkyinsa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bɔla bamʋ sʋ waa bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ tɔ.

eng: When Barnabas and Paul also fell silent, James responded, Fellow believers, listen to me.
local: Ba maa tɔwɛ lʋwɛ mʋ, Gyemisi kʋsʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, Mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana, fan nu mɛ asɛ.

eng: Simon reported how, in his kindness, God came to the Gentiles in the first place, to raise up from them a people of God.
local: Simon Pita tɔwɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ gyankpaa waa, nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ kaapʋ fɛɛ ee biti bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi gbaa, nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ bamʋ tɔ akʋ biliŋi mʋ lɛɛ.

eng: The prophets words agree with this; as it is written.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ asɩn nɩmʋ yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ ɩ waa kʋkʋlʋn.

eng: After this I will return, and I will rebuild Davids fallen tent; I will rebuild what has been torn down. I will restore it .
local: Asɩn mʋ ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ, Ɩnɩmʋ kamaa n biti n bwii kɛ n pɔyɩ Dawidi kɩkpaara mʋ nɩɩ kɩ lɩɩ tɩyɛ mʋ. N biti n lɔŋɔ‐lɔŋɔ ɩmʋ atɔ nyitasa mʋ kɛ n bwii kʋsaa kɩmʋ yɩlɛ.

eng: so that the rest of humanity will seek the Lord, even all the Gentiles who belong to me. The Lord says this, the one who does these things .
local: Kɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba san mʋ yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi, nɩɩ mɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kɩtɩɩ kɩ gyan bamʋ sʋ mʋ, kɛ ba kɩɩ biti mɛ.

eng: known from earliest times.
local: Mɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ n wʋla lɛɛ ɩnɩmʋ kaapʋ lɩɩ koo dɩdaa mʋ, ɩ tɔwɛ kanɩn.

eng: Therefore, I conclude that we shouldnt create problems for Gentiles who turn to God.
local: Kanɩn sʋ mɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ n sʋ n tɔwɛ ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ, an man nyan bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi nɩɩ ba kyɛɛgɛ ba Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ mʋ.

eng: Instead, we should write a letter, telling them to avoid the pollution associated with idols, sexual immorality, eating meat from strangled animals, and consuming blood.
local: Amaa ɩ dagaa a maa kyʋrɔɔ ɔwʋlɛ naa tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, ba lɛɛ ɩdakpa agyitɔ kigyii yɛ kagyɩɩ abɛɛ kanyɩn‐biti. Kɛ ba man gyii atɔbwaaya mʋ nɩɩ ba man tɩn bamʋ amu nɩɩ ba wu abɛɛ nkalan.

eng: After all, Moses has been proclaimed in every city for a long time, and is read aloud every Sabbath in every synagogue.
local: Nɩɩ koo dɩdaa mʋ, ba wʋla bɛɛ kaapʋ Mosesi nbara mʋ ndɛ kʋmaa tɔ, nɩɩ bɛɛ kalɛ ɩmʋ Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɩgyaŋɛkpa kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa kʋmaa.

eng: The apostles and the elders, along with the entire church, agreed to send some delegates chosen from among themselves to Antioch, together with Paul and Barnabas. They selected Judas Barsabbas and Silas, who were leaders among the brothers and sisters.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Krisito asunpu mʋ yɛ abɩlɩsa mʋ yɛ Krisito abi kʋbʋ mʋ kpini ba tɔwɛ kyula yɛ, ba lɛɛ bamʋ tɔ anyɩn akʋ, kɛ ba sun ba maa Pɔɔlɩ yɛ Banabasi kɛ ba kpee Antiyoki. Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ Gyudasi mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ Basabasi yɛ Silasi. Bamʋ anyɔ ba gyɛ Krisito abi agyankpaapʋ mʋ akʋ.

eng: They were to carry this letter: The apostles and the elders, to the Gentile brothers and sisters in Antioch, Syria, and Cilicia. Greetings!.
local: Nɩɩ ba taa falɛ ɔwʋlɛ nɩmʋ waa bamʋ abaa tɔ. Nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ ɔwʋlɛ mʋ falɛ. Ɩ gyɛ anɛ Krisito asunpu yɛ abɩlɩsa yɛ Krisito abi mʋ kpini. An kyʋrɔɔ ɔwʋlɛ nɩmʋ an sa fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fan man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ. Nɩɩ fan kɔɔlɛ Yesu gyii mʋ, nɩɩ fan bʋ Antiyoki yɛ Siriya yɛ Sisiliya mʋ kpini. Anɛ fanɛ bɩrɩsa ana an fwaala fanɛ.

eng: Weve heard that some of our number have disturbed you with unsettling words we didnt authorize.
local: An nu fɛɛ anɛ tɔ asa akʋ ba ba fanɛ asɛ, ba sʋ asɩn akʋ ba nyan fanɛ, yɛgɛ bee nyita fanɛ nfɛɛrɛ. Yɛgɛ an man sa bamʋ ɔkpa fɛɛ ba waa kanɩn, nɩɩ an man nyi bamʋ sʋ.

eng: We reached a united decision to select some delegates and send them to you along with our dear friends Barnabas and Paul.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ anɛ kpini a tɔwɛ kyula fɛɛ a lɛɛ asa akʋ kɛ ba maa anɛ kyɛmɩnɛ ana Banabasi maa Pɔɔlɩ, an sun bamʋ ba fanɛ asɛ.

eng: These people have devoted their lives to the name of our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Bamʋ ɩ gyɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba taga apʋ lɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu Krisito kɩtɩɩ tɔ sʋ.

eng: Therefore, we are sending Judas and Silas. They will confirm what we have written.
local: Kanɩn sʋ an bɩla sun Gyudasi maa Silasi fanɛ asɛ an tii sʋ. Kɛ ba naa taa bamʋ kɔnɔ tɔ tɔwɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ an kyʋrɔɔ mʋ.

eng: The Holy Spirit has led us to the decision that no burden should be placed on you other than these essentials.
local: Nɩɩ ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋrɔn sa anɛ yɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ, a mee biti fɛɛ a taa kasʋla budusa kʋ sʋla fanɛ tii sʋ, lɩɩ ɩmʋ nɩɩ an biti an tɔwɛ fanɛ falɛ.

eng: refuse food offered to idols, blood, the meat from strangled animals, and sexual immorality. You will do well to avoid such things. Farewell.
local: Ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ fan lɛɛ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ lɩɩ agyitɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba taa lɔŋɔ sa ɩdakpa yɛ atɔbwaaya nkalan yɛ atɔbwaaya mʋ nɩɩ ba man tɩn bamʋ amu nɩɩ ba wu mʋ, kɛ fan bɩla lɛɛ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ lɩɩ kagyɩɩ abɛɛ kanyɩn‐biti tɔ. Ii biti ɩ waa ɔdan sa fanɛ fɛɛ fan maa lɛɛ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ lɩɩ ɩnɩmʋ tɔ. An kala fanɛ.

eng: When Barnabas, Paul, and the delegates were sent on their way, they went down to Antioch. They gathered the believers and delivered the letter.
local: Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ bamʋ, nɩɩ ba sun bamʋ ɔkpa. Nɩɩ nbɔɔ mʋ ba maa naa fʋʋ Antiyoki mʋ, ba tɩɩ Krisito abi mʋ sarɛ, nɩɩ ba lɛɛ ɔwʋlɛ mʋ sa bamʋ.

eng: The people read it, delighted with its encouraging message.
local: Ba maa kalɛ ɔwʋlɛ mʋ, katɔwɛ waa nkyɛtɔ asɩn mʋ, ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ bamʋ ansi i gyii.

eng: Judas and Silas were prophets, and they said many things that encouraged and strengthened the brothers and sisters.
local: Gyudasi maa Silasi mʋ nɩɩ ba gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ mʋ ba tɔwɛ waa Krisito abi kʋbʋ mʋ ɔlʋn tɔ.

eng: Judas and Silas stayed there awhile, then were sent back with a blessing of peace from the brothers and sisters to those who first sent them.
local: Nɩɩ ba maa gyii nkɛ nkʋ bamʋ asɛ mʋ, ba lɛɛ bamʋ ɔkpa kayɩɩ yuuli tɔ. Nɩɩ ba bwii naa fʋʋ bamʋ nɩɩ ba sun bamʋ mʋ asɛ.

eng: Paul and Barnabas stayed in Antioch, where, together with many others, they taught and proclaimed the good news of the Lords word.
local: Amaa Pɔɔlɩ maa Banabasi ba sii Antiyoki. Nɩɩ ba maa asa gaalaagaa bɛɛ tɔwɛ bɛɛ kaapʋ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ asɩn mʋ.

eng: Some time later, Paul said to Barnabas, Lets go back and visit all the brothers and sisters in every city where we preached the Lords word. Lets see how they are doing.
local: Ɩmʋ kamaa tɔ mʋ, Pɔɔlɩ tɔwɛ Banabasi yɛ, Yɛgɛ a bwii kpee naa bɔɔ Krisito abi mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ ndɛ‐ndɛ sʋ mʋ, katɩn mʋ nɩɩ an wʋla tɔwɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ asɩn mʋ. Kɛ a kpee naa wu kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba du.

eng: Barnabas wanted to take John Mark with them.
local: Nɩɩ Banabasi ii biti fɛɛ ɔ taa Gyɔn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ Maki mʋ o tii bamʋ sʋ.

eng: Paul insisted that they shouldnt take him along, since he had deserted them in Pamphylia and hadnt continued with them in their work.
local: Amaa Pɔɔlɩ man kyula fɛɛ ba taa Maki buu bamʋ n‐yɩɩ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ lɛɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ lɩɩ bamʋ tɔ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Panfiliya bɛɛ waa kusun mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ bamʋ asɛ.

eng: Their argument became so intense that they went their separate ways. Barnabas took Mark and sailed to Cyprus.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ bɩɩtɛ tɩyɛ Pɔɔlɩ maa Banabasi nsana, nɩɩ ba barɩgɛ tɔ. Nɩɩ Banabasi taa Maki buu mʋ n‐yɩɩ bɔla nkyu sʋ kpee Sipuru.

eng: Paul chose Silas and left, entrusted by the brothers and sisters to the Lords grace.
local: Amaa Pɔɔlɩ bɩrɛ taa Silasi tii mʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋ, nɩɩ Krisito abi mʋ ba kʋlɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ fɛɛ ɔ bwaala bamʋ, nɩɩ ba kyʋn.

eng: He traveled through Syria and Cilicia, strengthening the churches.
local: Nɩɩ ba bɔla lɩɩ Siriya yɛ Silisiya nɩɩ ba waa Krisito abi kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ mʋ ɔlʋn tɔ.

eng: Paul reached Derbe, and then Lystra, where there was a disciple named Timothy. He was the son of a believing Jewish woman and a Greek father.
local: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ maa Silasi ba ba Debe, nɩɩ ba lɩɩ tʋtɔ kpee Lisitira. Tʋtɔ maa nɩɩ ba wu Yesu obuupu kʋ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Timoti. Nɩɩ mu‐nyi gyɛ Gyiwu obii mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ Krisito gyii, amaa mʋ‐sɛ bɩrɛ gyɛ Giriki obii nɩn.

eng: The brothers and sisters in Lystra and Iconium spoke well of him.
local: Krisito akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Lisitira yɛ Ikoniyum mʋ, ba tɔwɛ Timoti n‐yɩɩ sʋ asɩn danbɩrasa.

eng: Paul wanted to take Timothy with him, so he circumcised him. This was because of the Jews who lived in those areas, for they all knew Timothys father was Greek.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ Pɔɔlɩ ii biti fɛɛ ɔ taa Timoti buu mʋ n‐yɩɩ kpee ɔkpa. Nɩɩ Gyiwu abi mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ tʋtɔ mʋ sʋ mʋ, ɔ tɩn Timoti oduu, lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ kpini be nyi fɛɛ Timoti mʋ‐sɛ maa gyɛ Giriki obii nɩn.

eng: As Paul and his companions traveled through the cities, they instructed Gentile believers to keep the regulations put in place by the apostles and elders in Jerusalem.
local: Ba maa naa ndɛ sʋ mʋ, bɛɛ kaapʋ bamʋ kɔnɔsa mʋ nɩɩ Krisito asunpu mʋ yɛ abɩlɩsa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Gyerusalem ba sa bamʋ fɛɛ ɔsa kʋmaa o buu ɩmʋ.

eng: So the churches were strengthened in the faith and every day their numbers flourished.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ Krisito abi ba nyɛ ɔlʋn kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii tɔ, yɛgɛ kakɛ kʋmaa bɛɛ nyɛ akyɔ bee tii sʋ.

eng: Paul and Silas journeyed through Amphipolis and Apollonia, then came to Thessalonica, where there was a Jewish synagogue.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ maa Silasi ba bɔla lɩɩ Amfipolisi yɛ Apoloniya ndɛ tɔ mʋ, ba naa fʋʋ Tesalonika kadɛ tɔ, katɩn mʋ nɩɩ Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa bʋ mʋ.

eng: Paul and his companions traveled throughout the regions of Phrygia and Galatia because the Holy Spirit kept them from speaking the word in the province of Asia.
local: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ maa mʋ bɩrɩsa ana ba bɔla Firigiya maa Galati ifuli sʋ kyʋn, lɩɩ fɛɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ man sa bamʋ ɔkpa fɛɛ ba tɔwɛ abwaarɛsɩn Asiya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ.

eng: As was Pauls custom, he entered the synagogue and for three Sabbaths interacted with them on the basis of the scriptures.
local: Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ wʋla ɛɛ waa mʋ, Pɔɔlɩ kpee ɔgyaŋɛkpa mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ abwaarɛsɩn sa bamʋ nkɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ kanɩn ɩlɩɩ ɩsa.

eng: When they approached the province of Mysia, they tried to enter the province of Bithynia, but the Spirit of Jesus wouldnt let them.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba fʋʋ Misiya ngyaŋɛ mʋ, ba kpan bamʋ n‐yɩɩ fɛɛ ba faa loo Bitiniya, amaa Yesu Kayaayu mʋ man sa bamʋ ɔkpa.

eng: Through his interpretation of the scriptures, he demonstrated that the Christ had to suffer and rise from the dead. He declared, This Jesus whom I proclaim to you is the Christ.
local: Pɔɔlɩ lɛɛ kaapʋ tɔ fɛɛ, Ɩ dagaa fɛɛ Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ Wʋla lɛɛ mʋ o gyii awʋrʋfɔ, kɛ o wu kɛ ɔ bɩla kyiŋi. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Yesu mʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ fanɛ mʋ asɩn falɛ ɩ gyɛ Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ Wʋla lɛɛ mʋ.

eng: Passing by Mysia, they went down to Troas instead.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ba lɩɩ Misiya kpee Trowa kadɛ tɔ.

eng: Some were convinced and joined Paul and Silas, including a larger number of Greek God-worshippers and quite a few prominent women.
local: Gyiwu abi mʋ akʋ yɛ Giriki abi mʋ nɩɩ ba sɩlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ, yɛ akyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ bɛɛrɛɛ kadɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ kpini ba kɔɔlɛ Pɔɔlɩ asɩn mʋ kyula yɛ ɩ gyɛ kesintin nɩɩ ba buu bamʋ.

eng: A vision of a man from Macedonia came to Paul during the night. He stood urging Paul, Come over to Macedonia and help us.
local: Ɩmʋ kagyanbwɛ mʋ, Pɔɔlɩ nyɛ ansi sʋ kɩlɛɛkaapʋ. O wu Masedoniya ɔnyɩn kʋ maa yɩlɛ ɛɛ kʋlɛ mʋ yɛ, Faa ba Masedoniya, ba kyɛ anɛ tɔ.

eng: But the Jews became jealous and brought along some thugs who were hanging out in the marketplace. They formed a mob and started a riot in the city. They attacked Jasons house, intending to bring Paul and Silas before the people.
local: Amaa Gyiwu abi mʋ nɩɩ ba san mʋ ba nyɛ kayɩɩ basa. Nɩɩ ba kpee kɩgya tɔ naa biti asa gyaga. Nɩɩ ba be tii bamʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋ, nɩɩ ba piili bɛɛ waa ɩlawʋ kadɛ mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ ba kpala kpee Yasin ayɛ ba naa biti Pɔɔlɩ maa Silasi, kɛ ba kɩtaa bamʋ ba sakyɔ mʋ tɔ.

eng: Immediately after he saw the vision, we prepared to leave for the province of Macedonia, concluding that God had called us to proclaim the good news to them.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ wu ansi sʋ kɩlɛɛkaapʋ mʋ, opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ an kʋʋla anɛ n‐yɩɩ an biti a kpee Masedoniya. Lɩɩ fɛɛ an kɔɔlɛ kyula fɛɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ tɩɩ anɛ yɛ a naa tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ sa bamʋ.

eng: When they didnt find them, they dragged Jason and some believers before the city officials. They were shouting, These people who have been disturbing the peace throughout the empire have also come here.
local: Amaa ba maa man wu bamʋ mʋ, ba kɩtaa Yasin maa Yesu akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu akʋ bɩya bamʋ kadɛ mʋ abɩlɩsa ansi tɔ. Nɩɩ ba kuusi yɛ, Ba nɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba biliŋaa dulinyaa bee bun mʋ, ba ba nfɩɩ.

eng: We sailed from Troas straight for Samothrace and came to Neapolis the following day.
local: An lɩɩ Trowa kyɩna nkyu sʋ yii sʋ kpee Samotiri. Ɩmʋ lɩɩkaakɛ mʋ, nɩɩ an fʋʋ Neyapoli.

eng: What is more, Jason has welcomed them into his home. Every one of them does what is contrary to Caesars decrees by naming someone else as king: Jesus.
local: Nɩɩ Yasin kɔɔlɛ bamʋ waa mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ. Bamʋ kpini ba fɔyɩ Owura lala Kasaro kɔnɔsa nbara, nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ yɛ Owura pʋpwɛ kʋ bʋ tɔ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Yesu.

eng: From there we went to Philippi, a city of Macedonias first district and a Roman colony. We stayed in that city several days.
local: Nɩɩ an lɩɩ tʋtɔ kyaga sʋ natɛ kpee Filipi mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ Masedoniya kabaafʋn kʋ kadɛ lala, nɩɩ tʋtɔ bʋ Roma abi kasɛ. An gyii tʋtɔ nkɛ nkʋ.

eng: This provoked the crowd and the city officials even more.
local: Sakyɔ mʋ yɛ kadɛ abɩlɩsa mʋ ba maa nu falɛ asɩn mʋ, ɩ tɔɔrɔɔ bamʋ gaa.

eng: On the Sabbath we went outside the city gate to the riverbank, where we thought there might be a place for prayer. We sat down and began to talk with the women who had gathered.
local: Nɩɩ kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ mʋ an lɩɩ kadɛ mʋ tɔ kpee ɔlɩɩkaa. An waa nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ an biti an wu kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ opula. A maa wu kʋkʋ mʋ, an kyɩna piili an tɔwɛ an sa akyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ ba ba gyaŋɛ tʋtɔ mʋ.

eng: After Jason and the others posted bail, they released them.
local: Nɩɩ ba yɛgɛ Yasin maa mʋ tɔmaa ana ba ka afulee mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa, nɩɩ ba yɛgɛ bamʋ.

eng: One of those women was Lydia, a Gentile God-worshipper from the city of Thyatira, a dealer in purple cloth. As she listened, the Lord enabled her to embrace Pauls message.
local: Akyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ bee nu asɩn mʋ bɛɛ tɩɩ bamʋ tɔ ɔkʋ yɛ Lidiya, nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ ataa kɩrɩman‐kyu ɔfɛpʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ Tatira kadɛ lala tɔ nɩɩ ɔ sɩlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ. Nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ bugi mʋ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ Pɔɔlɩ asɩn mʋ gyii.

eng: As soon as it was dark, the brothers and sisters sent Paul and Silas on to Beroea. When they arrived, they went to the Jewish synagogue.
local: Kanɩn kakɛ maŋa kagyanbwɛ, nɩɩ Yesu akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu ba sun Pɔɔlɩ maa Silasi kpee Bereya kadɛ tɔ. Ba maa fʋʋ tʋtɔ mʋ ba kpee Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa.

eng: Once she and her household were baptized, she urged, Now that you have decided that I am a believer in the Lord, come and stay in my house. And she persuaded us.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ an gyee maa mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ abi kpini Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee mʋ, ɔ tɩɩ anɛ ba mʋ ayɛ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ anɛ yɛ, Nɩɩ fan kɩɩ mɛ fɛɛ n kɔɔlɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ gyii bɩrɛ, fan ba kyɩna mɛ kɩkpaara sʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ dan mata sʋ halɩɩ an kyula naa kyɩna mʋ asɛ.

eng: The Beroean Jews were more honorable than those in Thessalonica. This was evident in the great eagerness with which they accepted the word and examined the scriptures each day to see whether Paul and Silas teaching was true.
local: Asa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Bereya mʋ nfɛɛrɛ ɩ dɛ kasɛ ɩ kyɔ bamʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Tesalonika. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ba taa bamʋ nkɔlɔ kpini kɔɔlɛ asɩn mʋ yɛgɛ bɛɛ bɩla bɛɛ kɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ, kakɛ kʋmaa bɛɛ kɩɩ yɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ tɔwɛ mʋ ɩ bʋ tɔ kanɩn.

eng: One day, when we were on the way to the place for prayer, we met a slave woman. She had a spirit that enabled her to predict the future. She made a lot of money for her owners through fortune-telling.
local: Kakaakʋ an maa kpee kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ opula mʋ, an gyaŋa kɩnyɛ kibite mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ kabwɩɩ libi. Nɩɩ ɛɛ kpalɩ ee wu asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ii biti ɩ ba, yɛgɛ ɛɛ nyɛ afulee gaa ɛɛ sa mʋ wuya ana.

eng: Many came to believe, including a number of reputable Greek women and many Greek men.
local: Kanɩn sʋ Gyiwu abi damantɛ yɛ Giriki akyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ bɛɛrɛɛ yɛ anyɩn gaalaagaa ba kɔɔlɛ asɩn mʋ gyii.

eng: She began following Paul and us, shouting, These people are servants of the Most High God! They are proclaiming a way of salvation to you.
local: Nɩɩ kebii kyɩɩsa‐bi nɩmʋ buu anaa Pɔɔlɩ, yɛgɛ ee kuusi ɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Asa anɩmʋ ba gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ lala mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ sʋsʋ mʋ ayaafɔlɛ. Nɩɩ bɛɛ tɔwɛ fanɛ ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ fen biti fan bɔla sʋ nyɛ kamɔlɩgɛ.

eng: The Jews from Thessalonica learned that Paul also proclaimed Gods word in Beroea, so they went there too and were upsetting and disturbing the crowds.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Gyiwu abi mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Tesalonika ba nu yɛ Pɔɔlɩ bʋ Bereya ɛɛ tɔwɛ abwaarɛsɩn mʋ, ba ba tʋtɔ, bee fun bɛɛ waa asa mʋ.

eng: She did this for many days. This annoyed Paul so much that he finally turned and said to the spirit, In the name of Jesus Christ, I command you to leave her! It left her at that very moment.
local: Ɔ maa sʋ ɛɛ tɔwɛ asɩn nɩmʋ kakɛ kʋmaa. Amaa kamaa tɔ mʋ kibite mʋ asɩn mʋ i fii Pɔɔlɩ, nɩɩ o biliŋi tɔwɛ kabwɩɩ libi mʋ yɛ, N tɔwɛ fʋ yɛ, lɩɩ Yesu Krisito kɩtɩɩ tɔ sʋ mʋ, lɩɩ mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ opula tɔ nɩɩ kabwɩɩ libi mʋ lɩɩ mʋ tɔ.

eng: The brothers and sisters sent Paul away to the seacoast at once, but Silas and Timothy remained at Beroea.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ ba waa bilen taa Pɔɔlɩ kpee kebunɔ, amaa Silasi maa Timoti ba sii Bereya kadɛ tɔ.

eng: Her owners realized that their hope for making money was gone. They grabbed Paul and Silas and dragged them before the officials in the city center.
local: Amaa saŋa mʋ nɩɩ kebii kyɩɩsa‐bi mʋ wuya ana, ba wu fɛɛ ba man bɩla ba sʋ tamaa mʋ nɩɩ ba nyɛ afulee lɩɩ mʋ sʋ mʋ, ɩmʋ sʋ ba kɩtaa Pɔɔlɩ maa Silasi nɩɩ ba bɩɩtɛ bamʋ kpee asɩn ogyikpa alʋnpʋ mu ansi tɔ.

eng: Those who escorted Paul led him as far as Athens, then returned with instructions for Silas and Timothy to come to him as quickly as possible.
local: Nɩɩ anyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba sun Pɔɔlɩ ɔkpa mʋ, ba sun mʋ ɔkpa kpee Antensi. Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ kala bamʋ fɛɛ Silasi maa Timoti ba waa bilen ba tu mʋ Antensi.

eng: When her owners approached the legal authorities, they said, These people are causing an uproar in our city. They are Jews.
local: Nɩɩ ba taa bamʋ ba asɩn agyipu abɩlɩsa asɛ nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, Anyɩn anɩmʋ ba gyɛ Gyiwu abi nɩn nɩɩ bee biti ba boyi anɛ kadɛ.

eng: While Paul waited for them in Athens, he was deeply distressed to find that the city was flooded with idols.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ gyoo Silasi maa Timoti Antensi mʋ, maa wu fɛɛ ɩdakpa ɩ bɔla kadɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ɩ tɔɔrɔɔ mʋ gaa.

eng: who promote customs that we Romans cant accept or practice.
local: yɛgɛ bɛɛ kaapʋ anɛ atɔ pʋpwɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ man dagaa anɛ Roma abi fɛɛ a buu abɛɛ a waa.

eng: He began to interact with the Jews and Gentile God-worshippers in the synagogue. He also addressed whoever happened to be in the marketplace each day.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ saŋa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o kpee Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa mʋ, maa Gyiwu abi yɛ Giriki abi mʋ nɩɩ ba sɩlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ bee gyii abwaarɛsɩn mʋ ɩmɔɔrɛ. Kanɩn sʋ nɩɩ ee kpee kɩgya tɔ kakɛ kʋmaa yɛgɛ maa asa bee gyii ɩmɔɔrɛ.
