eng: The crowd joined in the attacks against Paul and Silas, so the authorities ordered that they be stripped of their clothes and beaten with a rod.
local: Nɩɩ sakyɔ mʋ nyaŋɛ tɔ ba tii Pɔɔlɩ maa Silasi waa tɔ, nɩɩ asɩn agyipu mʋ ba sa ɔkpa fɛɛ ba bugi bamʋ ɩkaalɛ mʋ kɛ ba tɩŋɛ bamʋ.

eng: Certain Epicurean and Stoic philosophers engaged him in discussion too. Some said, What an amateur! Whats he trying to say? Others remarked, He seems to be a proclaimer of foreign gods.
local: Nɩɩ Epikuriyan ɩwʋlɛ anyipu kʋbʋ yɛ Sitoki ɩwʋlɛ anyipu kʋbʋ bee piili bee gyii Pɔɔlɩ ɩmɔɔrɛ. Mʋ katɔwɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ, Pɔɔlɩ ɩ tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa lɩɩ Yesu maa mu kiwu kyiŋi asɩn. Nɩɩ bamʋ akʋ ba taasɛ yɛ, Mɩnɛ nɩɩ nyɩmɩsa gyaga nɩmʋ ii biti ɔ kaapʋ? Nɩɩ akʋ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, I du fɛɛ ɛɛ kaapʋ akʋ ɩdakpa asɩn nɩn.

eng: When Paul and Silas had been severely beaten, the authorities threw them into prison and ordered the jailer to secure them with great care.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba tɩŋɛ bamʋ lʋwɛ mʋ, ba taa bamʋ naa tii obu. Nɩɩ ba sa ɔmʋ nɩɩ ee kun obu mʋ sʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, ɔ kɩɩ bamʋ sʋ danbɩrasa.

eng: They took him into custody and brought him to the council on Mars Hill. What is this new teaching? Can we learn what you are talking about?.
local: Nɩɩ ba kɩtaa Pɔɔlɩ taa mʋ kpee Aropago ɔgyaŋɛkpa abɩlɩsa ansi tɔ. Nɩɩ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Fʋ taalɛ tɔwɛ anɛ kɩkaapʋ pʋpwɛ mʋ nɩɩ fʋ bɩya mʋ?.

eng: When he received these instructions, he threw them into the innermost cell and secured their feet in stocks.
local: Ba maa sa mʋ kɔnɔ lʋwɛ mʋ, ɔ taa bamʋ naa tii nsana tɔ obu mʋ tɔ, taa agbeeleebi ŋmina bamʋ ayaa kyaga kigbilin.

eng: Youve told us some strange things and we want to know what they mean. .
local: Asɩn mʋ nɩɩ fʋ sʋ fʋ bɩya anɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ anɛ asʋ tɔ asɩn pʋpwɛ. Ɩmʋ sʋ ba kaapʋ anɛ ɩmʋ kasɛ.

eng: Around midnight Paul and Silas were praying and singing hymns to God, and the other prisoners were listening to them.
local: Amaa kiyiridi nsana mʋ, Pɔɔlɩ maa Silasi bɛɛ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ yɛgɛ bɛɛ bɩɩ ɩlʋn bɛɛ yɩn Ɩbwaarɛ, yɛgɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba tii obu mʋ gbaa bee nu bamʋ asɛ.

eng: They said this because all Athenians as well as the foreigners who live in Athens used to spend their time doing nothing but talking about or listening to the newest thing.
local: Antensi abi yɛ afɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ kadɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ gaalaagaa ba mɛɛ waa sɛhn tii asɩn pʋpwɛ mʋ kunu sʋ yɛ ɩmʋ katɔwɛ sʋ.

eng: All at once there was such a violent earthquake that it shook the prisons foundations. The doors flew open and everyones chains came loose.
local: Nɩɩ opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ kasɛ ki gyingyen nɩɩ obu mʋ gyingyen naa lɩɩ halɩɩ kɩmʋ kasɛ. Nɩɩ saŋa maŋa tɔ obu mʋ ɩpʋnʋ i tigi nɩɩ agbeeleebi mʋ nɩɩ ba taa ŋmina bamʋ ɔkʋmaa mʋ ɩ saŋɛ.

eng: Paul stood up in the middle of the council on Mars Hill and said, People of Athens, I see that you are very religious in every way.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ kʋsʋ yɩlɛ Aropago ngyaŋɛ mʋ tɔ, tɔwɛ yɛ, Antensi anyɩn, n wu fɛɛ ɔkpa kʋmaa sʋ mʋ, kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fen sun mʋ, fɛn sʋ kensi payɩ fen sun mʋ.

eng: When the jailer awoke and saw the open doors of the prison, he thought the prisoners had escaped, so he drew his sword and was about to kill himself.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ obu tiipu okunpu kyiŋi wu fɛɛ ɩpʋnʋ mʋ i tigi mʋ ɔ bɩɩtɛ deebi lɛɛ nɩɩ ee biti fɛɛ ɔ mɔɔ mʋ n‐yɩɩ, o nyi fɛɛ obu atiipu mʋ ba lɩɩ sɩlɛ nɩn.

eng: As I was walking through town and carefully observing your objects of worship, I even found an altar with this inscription: To an unknown God. What you worship as unknown, I now proclaim to you.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ n maa naa kadɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ, n wu atɔ mʋ nɩɩ fen muŋa fen sun. Nɩɩ n wu saraga kaswɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ bɛ kyʋrɔɔ kɩmʋ sʋ yɛ, ƖBWAARƖ MƲ NƖƖ AN MAN NYI MƲ MƲ LƐƐ. Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔmʋ nɩɩ fen sun, nɩɩ fen mee nyi mʋ mʋ, mʋ asɩn nɩɩ n biti n tɔwɛ fanɛ.

eng: But Paul shouted loudly, Dont harm yourself! Were all here.
local: Amaa Pɔɔlɩ pʋntɛ mʋ sʋ yɛ, Man waa fʋ n‐yɩɩ sɛhn, anɛ kpini a bʋ tɔ.

eng: God, who made the world and everything in it, is Lord of heaven and earth. He doesnt live in temples made with human hands.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tʋʋ dulinyaa yɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ kɩmʋ tɔ mʋ, mʋ ɩ gyɛ sʋsʋ yɛ kasɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ. Ɔ mɛɛ kyɩna kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba taa abaa pɔyɩ mʋ tɔ.

eng: The jailer called for some lights, rushed in, and fell trembling before Paul and Silas.
local: Nɩɩ okunpu mʋ biti fɩtɩla, sɩlɛ taa kufuu yɛ kɩkyɩkya ba tɩyɛ Pɔɔlɩ maa Silasi ayaa tɔ.

eng: Nor is God served by human hands, as though he needed something, since he is the one who gives life, breath, and everything else.
local: Kʋtɔ kʋ kɩ man bʋ tɔ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ mɛɛ taalɛ waa, nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ nyɩmɩsa ɔ waa sa mʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ mʋ ɩ sa nyɩmɩsa nkpa yɛ ɔ fʋʋtɛ yɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ.

eng: He led them outside and asked, Honorable masters, what must I do to be rescued.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ bamʋ ba kewu taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Anyɩn, mɩnɛ ɩ dagaa n waa kɛ n nyɛ kamɔlɩgɛ.

eng: From one person God created every human nation to live on the whole earth, having determined their appointed times and the boundaries of their lands.
local: Ɔ tʋʋ nyɩmɩsa kʋlʋn nɩɩ ɔ bɔla mʋ sʋ nɩɩ ɔ tʋʋ ndɛndɛmandɛ kpini, nɩɩ ɔ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ba kyɩna dulinyaa ɩsʋwʋlɛ mʋ sʋ kpini. Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla ŋmina yɩla opula mʋ nɩɩ asa ba kyɩna yɛ ɩmʋ saŋa.

eng: They replied, Believe in the Lord Jesus, and you will be saved—you and your entire household.
local: Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Kɔɔlɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu gyii kɛ faa fʋ kɩkpaara sʋ abi fan nyɛ kamɔlɩgɛ.

eng: God made the nations so they would seek him, perhaps even reach out to him and find him. In fact, God isnt far away from any of us.
local: Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ waa ɩnɩmʋ nɩn, lɩɩ fɛɛ ee biti fɛɛ asa ba kɩɩ biti mʋ kɛ ba wu mʋ. Amaa yɛgɛ ɔ man bʋ kata yɛ anɛ tɔ ɔkʋ.

eng: They spoke the Lords word to him and everyone else in his house.
local: Nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ asɩn sa mʋ yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ kpini.

eng: In God we live, move, and exist. As some of your own poets said, We are his offspring.
local: Nɩɩ mʋ sʋ nɩɩ an sʋ nkpa, nɩɩ an taalɛ an naa yɛgɛ an bʋ tɔ falɛ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fanɛ ɔlʋn‐bɩɩpʋ kʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, Anɛ gbaa anɛ an gyɛ mu‐bii ana nɩn.

eng: Right then, in the middle of the night, the jailer welcomed them and washed their wounds. He and everyone in his household were immediately baptized.
local: Kanɩn kagyanbwɛ saŋa maŋa tɔ nɩɩ okunpu mʋ taa Pɔɔlɩ maa Silasi lɩɩ naa nyɛɛ bamʋ ɩlɔ. Nɩɩ kanɩn opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ ba gyee maa mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ abi kpini Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee.

eng: Therefore, as Gods offspring, we have no need to imagine that the divine being is like a gold, silver, or stone image made by human skill and thought.
local: Nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ kesintin fɛɛ anɛ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mu‐bii ana bɩrɛ mʋ, an man kan fɛ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ keduli du fɛɛ suwa abɛɛ gbiti abɛɛ kubuu nɩɩ nyɩmɩsa taalɛ taa mʋ kanyaasɩn yɛ adawʋ pɔyɩ mʋ fɛɛ ɔdakpa.

eng: He brought them into his home and gave them a meal. He was overjoyed because he and everyone in his household had come to believe in God.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ obu atiipu okunpu mʋ taa bamʋ kpee mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ naa dɩŋa agyitɔ sa bamʋ. Nɩɩ maa mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ abi kpini ansi i gyii lɩɩ ba maa kɔɔlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gyii mʋ sʋ.

eng: God overlooks ignorance of these things in times past, but now directs everyone everywhere to change their hearts and lives.
local: Dɩdaa mʋ Ɩbwaarɛ man taa asa kimennyi waa sɛhn, amaa nbɩɩnbɩɩ ɔ sa asa kpini kɔnɔsa katɩn kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ fɛɛ ba yɛgɛ alibi kɩwaa.

eng: The next morning the legal authorities sent the police to the jailer with the order Release those people.
local: Amaa ɩ maa ba ŋmaŋɛ tɔ mʋ, asɩn agyipu mʋ ba sun Apurusi yɛ ba naa tɔwɛ okunpu mʋ yɛ, O tigi anyɩn mʋ kɛ ba natɛ.

eng: This is because God has set a day when he intends to judge the world justly by a man he has appointed. God has given proof of this to everyone by raising him from the dead.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ lɛɛ kakɛ yɩla mʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ bɔla ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ wʋla lɛɛ mʋ kɛ ɔ talɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ kesintin ɔkpa sʋ. O kyiŋi kanɩn nyɩmɩsa maŋa lɩɩ lewu tɔ, waa kanɩn kaapʋ sa asa kpini yɛ ɩnɩmʋ ii biti ɩ waa.

eng: So the jailer reported this to Paul, informing him, The authorities sent word that you both are to be released. You can leave now. Go in peace.
local: Nɩɩ okunpu mʋ tɔwɛ Pɔɔlɩ yɛ, Asɩn agyipu mʋ ba sa kɔnɔ yɛ, n tigi faa Silasi. Ɩmʋ sʋ fɛn taalɛ natɛ kayɩɩ yuuli tɔ.

eng: When they heard about the resurrection from the dead, some began to ridicule Paul. However, others said, Well hear from you about this again.
local: Ba maa nu Pɔɔlɩ asɛ lɩɩ kelewu kyiŋi asɩn mʋ, akʋ ba waa mʋ ahin‐sɩn, nɩɩ akʋ mɔɔ ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, An biti fɛɛ fu bwii tɔwɛ anɛ kanɩn asɩn nɩmʋ.

eng: Paul told the police, Even though we are Roman citizens, they beat us publicly without first finding us guilty of a crime, and they threw us into prison. And now they want to send us away secretly? No way! They themselves will have to come and escort us out.
local: Amaa nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ tɔwɛ Apurusi mʋ yɛ, Ba kɩtaa anɛ ba man gyii anɛ asɩn nɩɩ ba dayɩ anɛ sakyɔ tɔ, yɛgɛ an gyɛ Roma abi nɩɩ ba kɩtaa anɛ tii. Nɩɩ bee biti ba lɛɛ anɛ lɩɩ nfɩɩ asiri tɔ? Ayee! Kanɩn sʋ yɛgɛ bamʋ gbaa‐gbaa bamʋ ba ba lɛɛ anɛ.

eng: At that, Paul left the council.
local: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ lɩɩ ɔgyaŋɛkpa mʋ.

eng: The police reported this to the legal authorities, who were alarmed to learn that Paul and Silas were Roman citizens.
local: Nɩɩ Apurusi mʋ ba kpee naa tɔwɛ asɩn agyipu mʋ. Nɩɩ ba maa nu fɛɛ Pɔɔlɩ maa Silasi ba gyɛ Roma abi mʋ, kufuu kɩ kɩtaa bamʋ.

eng: Some people joined him and came to believe, including Dionysius, a member of the council on Mars Hill, a woman named Damaris, and several others.
local: Nɩɩ anyɩn akʋ ba buu mʋ nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ Yesu gyii. Bamʋ akʋ atɩɩ ɩ gyɛ Diyonisi mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ Aropago kʋbʋ mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ, yɛ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Damarisi, yɛ asa mʋ akʋ.

eng: They came and consoled Paul and Silas, escorting them out of prison and begging them to leave the city.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ asɩn agyipu mʋ ba ba kɩtaa bamʋ ayaa tɔ. Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ bamʋ lɩɩ obu tiisa mʋ tɔ, kʋlɛ bamʋ yɛ ba lɩɩ kadɛ mʋ tɔ.

eng: Paul and Silas left the prison and made their way to Lydias house where they encouraged the brothers and sisters. Then they left Philippi.
local: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ maa Silasi ba maa lɩɩ obu tiisa mʋ tɔ mʋ, ba kpee Lidiya kɩkpaara sʋ. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba naa tu Krisito abi mʋ, nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ waa bamʋ. Nɩɩ ba lɩɩ tʋtɔ kyʋn.

eng: After this, Paul left Athens and went to Corinth.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ kamaa mʋ Pɔɔlɩ lɩɩ Antensi kpee Korinto kadɛ tɔ.

eng: There he found a Jew named Aquila, a native of Pontus. He had recently come from Italy with his wife Priscilla because Claudius had ordered all Jews to leave Rome. Paul visited with them.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔ naa tu Gyiwu obii kʋ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Akwila, nɩɩ ba kʋʋgɛ mʋ Pontu kadɛ tɔ, maa mʋ‐ka Prisila. Ba lɩɩ Itali ba Korinto ɩ man pɩɩ kyɛɛrɛɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ Owura lala Kiladiyo maa gya Gyiwu abi kpini lɩɩ Roma kadɛ tɔ mʋ sʋ. Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ kpee ɔ naa kɩɩ bamʋ.

eng: Because they practiced the same trade, he stayed and worked with them. They all worked with leather.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ maa bamʋ kpini ba gyɛ tantɩ awaapʋ sʋ mʋ, ɔ kyɩna bamʋ asɛ nɩɩ maa bamʋ ba bʋga waa kusun.

eng: Every Sabbath he interacted with people in the synagogue, trying to convince both Jews and Greeks.
local: Kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa kʋmaa nɩmʋ, Pɔɔlɩ ii kpee Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa, ɛɛ tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ ɛɛ sa Gyiwu abi yɛ Giriki abi kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ gyii.

eng: Once Silas and Timothy arrived from Macedonia, Paul devoted himself fully to the word, testifying to the Jews that Jesus was the Christ.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Silasi maa Timoti ba lɩɩ Masedoniya ba mʋ asɛ Korinto mʋ, Pɔɔlɩ nyɛ saŋa sa abwaarɛsɩn kɩtɔwɛ, yɛgɛ ɛɛ lɛɛ asɩn mʋ kesintin ɛɛ kaapʋ tɔ ɛɛ sa Gyiwu abi mʋ yɛ Yesu ɩ gyɛ Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ mʋ.

eng: When they opposed and slandered him, he shook the dust from his clothes in protest and said to them, You are responsible for your own fates! Im innocent! From now on Ill go to the Gentiles.
local: Amaa Gyiwu abi ba maa kina asɩn mʋ, nɩɩ ba saalɛ mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ fwɩɩ mʋ abaa yɛ mʋ ayaa nɩɩ ɔ yɛ, Fanɛ lewu i bwii fanɛ gbaa‐gbaa fanɛ amu sʋ. Mɛɛ mɛ bɩrɛ ɩ man gyɛ mɛ asɩn. Lɩɩ ndaga ɩ sʋ ii kpee mʋ, n biti n kpee bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ asɛ.

eng: He left the synagogue and went next door to the home of Titius Justus, a Gentile God-worshipper.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ Pɔɔlɩ lɩɩ Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa mʋ, nɩɩ o kpee ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Titusi Gyusitu mʋ asɛ. Ɔ man gyɛ Gyiwu obii, amaa ee sun Ɩbwaarɛ, nɩɩ mʋ kɩkpaara kɩ man bʋ kata yɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa mʋ.

eng: Crispus, the synagogue leader, and his entire household came to believe in the Lord. Many Corinthians believed and were baptized after listening to Paul.
local: Kirisipu mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ yɛ mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ abi kpini ba kɔɔlɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu gyii. Nɩɩ Korinto abi gaalaagaa mʋ nɩɩ ba nu abwaarɛsɩn mʋ ba kɔɔlɛ gyii, nɩɩ ba gyee bamʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee.

eng: One night the Lord said to Paul in a vision, Dont be afraid. Continue speaking. Dont be silent.
local: Kakaakʋ kagyanbwɛ nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ nyɛ ansi sʋ kɩlɛɛkaapʋ, nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Man waa kufuu, man laatɔ, kyaga sʋ tɔwɛ.

eng: Im with you and no one who attacks you will harm you, for I have many people in this city.
local: N tii fʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔkʋ mɛɛ taalɛ mata fʋ abɛɛ ɔ waa fʋ kʋtɔ kʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ kadɛ kɩnɩmʋ tɔ asa gaalaagaa ba gyɛ mɛ lɛɛ.

eng: So he stayed there for eighteen months, teaching Gods word among them.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ Pɔɔlɩ kyɩna tʋtɔ kesu kʋlʋn yɛ kɩdɩyɛ, ɛɛ kaapʋ abwaarɛsɩn ɛɛ sa bamʋ.

eng: Now when Gallio was the governor of the province of Achaia, the Jews united in their opposition against Paul and brought him before the court.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Galiyo gyɛ Akiya kadɛ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ mʋ, Gyiwu abi akʋ ba bʋga kʋsʋ naa tii Pɔɔlɩ, taa mʋ kpee asɩn ogyikpa.

eng: This man is persuading others to worship God unlawfully, they declared.
local: Nɩɩ ba pwɩɩ mʋ fɛɛ, Ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ ɩɩ kaapʋ asa Kabwaarɛ‐sun kʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɩmaa anɛ nbara mʋ ɩ man gyɛ kʋkʋlʋn.

eng: Just as Paul was about to speak, Gallio said to the Jews, If there had been some sort of injury or criminal behavior, I would have reason to accept your complaint.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ piili yɛ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ, kadɛ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ Galiyo tɔwɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ pwɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ mʋ yɛ, Ɩ daa ɩ gyɛ alibi akʋ abɛɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ man dɛ ɔkpa nɩn, naafɔɔ n daa nu fanɛ asɛ.

eng: However, since these are squabbles about a message, names, and your own Law, deal with them yourselves. I have no desire to sit in judgment over such things.
local: Amaa ɩ maa gyɛ ntɔwɛ kɩtaasɛ yɛ atɩɩ yɛ fanɛ Gyiwu abi nbara falɛ bɩrɛ, fanɛ gbaa‐gbaa fan naa gyii asɩn mʋ. Man gyɛ mɛ ɩ gyɛ kanɩn asɩn nɩmʋ ɔtalɛpʋ.

eng: He expelled them from the court.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ gya bamʋ lɩɩ asɩn ogyikpa mʋ.

eng: but everyone seized Sosthenes, the synagogue leader, and gave him a beating in the presence of the governor. None of this mattered to Gallio.
local: Nɩɩ ba kɩtaa Sotene mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ, dayɩ mu asɩn ogyikpa ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ Galiyo mu ansi tɔ. Amaa ɔ man tɔwɛ sɛhn.

eng: After Paul stayed in Corinth for some time, he said good-bye to the brothers and sisters. At the Corinthian seaport of Cenchreae he had his head shaved, since he had made a solemn promise. Then, accompanied by Priscilla and Aquila, he sailed away to Syria.
local: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ kyɩna akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Korinto mʋ asɛ nkɛ nkʋ, nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ bamʋ asɛ kpee Siriya. Maa lɩɩ tʋtɔ ee kpee mʋ, Prisila maa Akwila ba buu mʋ kpee. Pɔyɩ kɛ ba faa mʋ, Pɔɔlɩ sɩyɛ mu inyini Kenkera kadɛ tɔ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bɔ ntan mʋ.

eng: After they arrived in Ephesus, he left Priscilla and Aquila and entered the synagogue and interacted with the Jews.
local: Ba maa fʋʋ Efeso mʋ, Pɔɔlɩ yɛgɛ Prisila maa Akwila, nɩɩ o kpee Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa, kɛ maa Gyiwu abi ba naa gyii Yesu asɩn mʋ ɩmɔɔrɛ.

eng: They asked him to stay longer, but he declined.
local: Ba maa kʋlɛ mʋ yɛ ɔ kyɩna bamʋ asɛ nkɛ nnyɔ kʋ mʋ, nɩɩ o kina.

eng: As he said farewell to them, though, he added, God willing, I will return. Then he sailed off from Ephesus.
local: Amaa maa kpee mʋ, ɔ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kan kyula, n biti n bwii ba. Nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ Efeso bɔla nkyu sʋ kyʋn.

eng: He arrived in Caesarea, went up to Jerusalem and greeted the church, and then went down to Antioch.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ naa dan Kasariya mʋ, ɔ naa fwaala Krisito abi kʋbʋ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ Gyerusalem mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ kyaga sʋ kpee Antiyoki.

eng: After some time there he left and traveled from place to place in the region of Galatia and the district of Phrygia, strengthening all the disciples.
local: Pɔɔlɩ maa gyii nkɛ nkʋ Antiyoki mʋ, ɔ lɩɩ tʋtɔ ɔ naa ɛɛ bɔɔ akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ o kpee Galati yɛ Firigiya yɛgɛ ɛɛ tɔwɛ ɛɛ waa bamʋ ɔlʋn tɔ.

eng: Meanwhile, a certain Jew named Apollos arrived in Ephesus. He was a native of Alexandria and was well-educated and effective in his use of the scriptures.
local: Saŋa maa mʋ, Gyiwu ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Apolosi nɩɩ ba kʋʋgɛ mʋ Alekisanda kadɛ tɔ mʋ ba Efeso. Ɔ gyɛ ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ kinyi katɔwɛ tɔ, nɩɩ o nyi Ɩbwaarɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ danbɩrasa.

eng: He had been instructed in the way of the Lord and spoke as one stirred up by the Spirit. He taught accurately the things about Jesus, even though he was aware only of the baptism John proclaimed and practiced.
local: Ba kaapʋ mʋ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ ɔkpa mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ yɩlɛ kɩnyɩn tɔ ɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛgɛ ɛɛ kaapʋ lɩɩ Yesu sʋ kyɛɛkyɛɛ sʋ. Amaa Gyɔn kekyu gyee nkʋn nɩɩ o nyi.

eng: He began speaking with confidence in the synagogue. When Priscilla and Aquila heard him, they received him into their circle of friends and explained to him Gods way more accurately.
local: Prisila maa Akwila ba maa nu Apolosi maa tɔwɛ Yesu asɩn Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa yɛ kɩnyɩn tɔ mʋ, ba tɩɩ mʋ kpee bamʋ kɩkpaara sʋ, nɩɩ ba naa lɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔkpa mʋ kaapʋ mʋ kyɛɛkyɛɛ sʋ tii sʋ.

eng: When he wanted to travel to Achaia, the brothers and sisters encouraged him and wrote to the disciples so they would open their homes to him. Once he arrived, he was of great help to those who had come to believe through grace.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Apolosi waa mʋ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ ee biti o kpee Akiya mʋ, akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Efeso mʋ ba waa mʋ ɔlʋn tɔ. Nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ ɔwʋlɛ naa sa akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Akiya mʋ yɛ ba kɔɔlɛ mʋ. Mu kikpe tʋtɔ mʋ, ɩ kyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabwaala mʋ sʋ nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ gyii mʋ tɔ.

eng: He would vigorously defeat Jewish arguments in public debate, using the scriptures to prove that Jesus was the Christ.
local: Nɩɩ Apolosi yɩlɛ kɩnyɩn tɔ gyii biri Gyiwu abi mʋ sakyɔ tɔ. Ɔ taa Ɩbwaarɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ kaapʋ bamʋ fɛɛ Yesu ɩ gyɛ Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ mʋ.

eng: While Apollos was in Corinth, Paul took a route through the interior and came to Ephesus, where he found some disciples.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Apolosi bʋ Korinto mʋ, Pɔɔlɩ taa kasɛ sʋ halɩɩ ɔ ba fʋʋ Efeso. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔ naa wu akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu akʋ.

eng: He asked them, Did you receive the Holy Spirit when you came to believe? They replied, Weve not even heard that there is a Holy Spirit.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan maa kɔɔlɛ gyii mʋ, fan nyɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ? Nɩɩ ba yɛ, Ayee, an man pɩɩta nu gbaa yɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ kʋ bʋ tɔ.

eng: Then he said, What baptism did you receive, then? They answered, Johns baptism.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ Pɔɔlɩ bɩla taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, Kanɩn bɩrɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee mɔmɔ nɩɩ fan nyɛ? Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Gyɔn kekyugyee mʋ nɩɩ an nyɛ.

eng: Paul explained, John baptized with a baptism by which people showed they were changing their hearts and lives. It was a baptism that told people about the one who was coming after him. This is the one in whom they were to believe. This one is Jesus.
local: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Gyɔn kekyugyee kɩ gyɛ nfɛɛrɛ kɩkyɛɛgɛ lɩɩ alibi tɔ ba Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ nɩn. Kanɩn sʋ Gyɔn tɔwɛ asa yɛ, ba kɔɔlɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ mʋ kamaa tɔ ɛɛ ba mʋ gyii, mʋ ɩ gyɛ Yesu.

eng: After they listened to Paul, they were baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
local: Ba maa nu kanɩn mʋ, Pɔɔlɩ gyee bamʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee lɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu kɩtɩɩ tɔ.

eng: When Paul placed his hands on them, the Holy Spirit came on them, and they began speaking in other languages and prophesying.
local: Pɔɔlɩ maa taa mʋ abaa gyaga bamʋ sʋ mʋ, Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ kɩ ba bɔla bamʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ ntɔwɛ pʋpwɛ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bɔla bamʋ sʋ tɔwɛ asɩn.

eng: Altogether, there were about twelve people.
local: Asa mʋ ba gyɛ anyɩn kudu‐anyɔ nɩn.

eng: Paul went to the synagogue and spoke confidently for the next three months. He interacted with those present and offered convincing arguments concerning the nature of Gods kingdom.
local: Ɩbwaayɩ ɩsa mʋ tɔ mʋ, Pɔɔlɩ i kpee Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa, ɔ yɩlɛ kɩnyɩn tɔ ɛɛ tɔwɛ bamʋ kɛ ba kɔɔlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ asɩn gyii.

eng: Some people had closed their minds, though. They refused to believe and publicly slandered the Way. As a result, Paul left them, took the disciples with him, and continued his daily interactions in Tyrannus lecture hall.
local: Amaa akʋ ba waa nkɔlɔ ɔsɩɩ kina, ba man kɔɔlɛ Yesu asɩn mʋ gyii. Nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ asɩn libi yii Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɔkpa mʋ sakyɔ maŋa tɔ. Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ lɩɩ ɔgyaŋɛkpa mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taa akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ buu mʋ n‐yɩɩ. Kakɛ kʋmaa nɩmʋ maa akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ bee kpee Tiranu ɔkaapʋkpa nɩɩ ɛɛ kaapʋ bamʋ.

eng: This went on for two years, so that everyone living in the province of Asia—both Jews and Greeks—heard the Lords word.
local: Ɔ kaapʋ kanɩn nsu nnyɔ nɩɩ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ Gyiwu abi yɛ Giriki abi mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Asiya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ kpini ba nu Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu asɩn mʋ.

eng: God was doing unusual miracles through Paul.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bɔla Pɔɔlɩ sʋ waa asɩn kyinkyinsa gaa.

eng: Even the small towels and aprons that had touched his skin were taken to the sick, and their diseases were cured and the evil spirits left them.
local: Nɩɩ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ asa ba taa atɔ fɛɛ bɔrɩdɩba‐bi yɛ ntaa‐bi mʋ nɩɩ ɩ wʋla mata Pɔɔlɩ n‐yɩɩ mʋ, ba taa naa mata alɔpʋ nɩɩ ba nyɛ alanfɩya, nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi mɔɔ nɩn, kɛ ba lɩɩ.

eng: There were some Jews who traveled around throwing out evil spirits. They tried to use the power of the name of the Lord Jesus against some people with evil spirits. They said, In the name of the Jesus whom Paul preaches, I command you.
local: Gyiwu abi akʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba naa bɛɛ gya nbwɩɩ libi mʋ, ba waa kɔkɔlɔ ɔsɩɩ taa Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu kɩtɩɩ fɛɛ ba taalɛ gya nbwɩɩ libi lɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ bɛɛ tɔwɛ nbwɩɩ libi yɛ, Lɩɩ Yesu kɩtɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ tɔwɛ mʋ asɩn mʋ sʋ mʋ, n sa fanɛ kɔnɔ yɛ fan lɩɩ.

eng: The seven sons of Sceva, a Jewish chief priest, were doing this.
local: Ɩ gyɛ Gyiwu oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Sikefa mu‐bii anaa anyɩnsa asunɔ ɩ waa kanɩn mʋ.

eng: The evil spirit replied, I know Jesus and Im familiar with Paul, but who are you.
local: Kakaakʋ ba maa kpee ba naa waa kanɩn mʋ, nɩɩ kabwɩɩ libi mʋ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, N nyi Yesu, nɩɩ n bɩla n nyi Pɔɔlɩ, amaa anɩmʋ ana ɩ gyɛ fanɛ.

eng: The person who had an evil spirit jumped on them and overpowered them all with such force that they ran out of that house naked and wounded.
local: Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ kabwɩɩ libi mʋ bʋ mʋ tɔ mʋ, tɩyɛ bamʋ sʋ, taalɛ bamʋ kpini. Nɩɩ ɔ dayɩ bamʋ larɩgɛ, nɩɩ ba taa kubolonkpan sɩlɛ lɩɩ kɩkpaara mʋ sʋ.

eng: This became known to the Jews and Greeks living in Ephesus. Everyone was seized with fear and they held the name of the Lord Jesus in the highest regard.
local: Gyiwu abi yɛ Giriki abi mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Efeso mʋ, ba maa nu kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ waa mʋ, ɩ kɩtaa bamʋ kufuu. Nɩɩ ba taa bɛɛrɛɛ taa sa Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu kɩtɩɩ mʋ.

eng: Many of those who had come to believe came, confessing their past practices.
local: Nɩɩ asa damantɛ mʋ nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ Yesu gyii mʋ, ba lɛɛ bamʋ nnɔ tɔ alibi mʋ nɩɩ ba waa tɔwɛ sakyɔ tɔ.

eng: This included a number of people who practiced sorcery. They collected their sorcery texts and burned them publicly. The value of those materials was calculated at more than someone might make if they worked for one hundred sixty-five years.
local: Bamʋ adamantɛ mʋ nɩɩ ba gyɛ Sɩtaanɛ adangana awaapʋ mʋ, ba taa bamʋ ɩwʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ kaapʋ bamʋ afasɩn mʋ, ba bʋga tɔ nɩɩ ba tuwa ɩmʋ sakyɔ mʋ tɔ. Ba maa waa ɩwʋlɛ mʋ kpini afulee kɔnɔ mʋ, ɩ waa fɛɛ nderebi ngbɩn adunuu.

eng: In this way the Lords word grew abundantly and strengthened powerfully.
local: Kanɩn sʋ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu asɩn mʋ ɩ yɩlɛ nɩɩ ɩ nyɛ ɔlʋn.

eng: Once these things had come to an end, Paul, guided by the Spirit, decided to return to Jerusalem, taking a route that would carry him through the provinces of Macedonia and Achaia. He said, After I have been there, I must visit Rome as well.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ kpini kamaa mʋ, Pɔɔlɩ waa nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ ee biti ɔ bɔla lɩɩ Masedoniya yɛ Akiya kɛ o kpee Gyerusalem. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Nɩɩ n kan kpee tʋtɔ kamaa mʋ, ɩ dagaa maa bɔɔ Roma gbaa sʋ.

eng: He sent two of his assistants, Timothy and Erastus, to Macedonia, while he remained awhile in the province of Asia.
local: Nɩɩ o sun mʋ akyɛtɔpʋ Timoti maa Erasitu kpee Masedoniya. Nɩɩ mɛɛ mʋ sii Asiya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ gbɛrɛɛ.

eng: At that time a great disturbance erupted about the Way.
local: Kanɩn saŋa maŋa tɔ mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɔkpa mʋ sʋ mʋ, tɔɔrɔɔ damantɛ tɩyɛ Efeso kadɛ tɔ.

eng: There was a silversmith named Demetrius. He made silver models of Artemis temple, and his business generated a lot of profit for the craftspeople.
local: Gbiti ɔŋalagɛpʋ kʋ bʋ Efeso tʋtɔ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Demetirisu. Ɔ sʋ gbiti ɛɛ bɩlɛ ibu‐pipi fɛɛ ɔdakpa kyɩɩsa Atemisi obu. Nɩɩ mʋ yɛ abuupu mʋ kpini ba sʋ kusun mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ nyɛ kɩmʋ afulee.

eng: He called a meeting with these craftspeople and others working in related trades and said, Friends, you know that we make an easy living from this business.
local: Nɩɩ Demetirisu tɩɩ abuupu mʋ kpini yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ɩ maa bamʋ kusun kɩ gyɛ kʋkʋlʋn mʋ gyaŋɛ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Mɛ tɔɔmaa ana fen nyi fɛɛ an nyɛ anɛ kigyii an lɩɩ kusun nɩmʋ sʋ.

eng: And you can see and hear that this Paul has convinced and misled a lot of people, not only in Ephesus but also throughout most of the province of Asia. He says that gods made by human hands arent really gods.
local: Yɛ fanɛ gbaa‐gbaa fan wu nɩɩ fan nu, fɛɛ Pɔɔlɩ nɩmʋ naa ee nyita asa damantɛ nfɛɛrɛ. Nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ Efeso nfɩɩ nkʋn, amaa Asiya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ kpini. Nɩɩ ɔ naa ɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Anyɩrɩpɛ mʋ nɩɩ ba taa abaa pɔyɩ mʋ, man gyɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ gbaa kpini‐kpini.

eng: This poses a danger not only by discrediting our trade but also by completely dishonoring the great goddess Artemis. The whole province of Asia—indeed, the entire civilized world—worships her, but her splendor will soon be extinguished.
local: Man gyɛ anɛ kusun wʋlɛ nkʋn ii biti i nyita. Amaa anɛ ɔdakpa kyɩɩsa obu yɛ mʋ kɩtɩɩ Atemisi mʋ, ee biti o wu. Nɩɩ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Asiya ɔsʋwʋlɛ yɛ dulinyaa tɔ kpini, mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ bɛɛrɛɛ bɛɛ sa ɔdakpa lala nɩmʋ mʋ, ii biti i biliŋi kɩyan.

eng: Once they heard this, they were beside themselves with anger and began to shout, Great is Artemis of the Ephesians.
local: Ba maa nu falɛ mʋ, ɩ nyɛ bamʋ agbʋ. Nɩɩ ba piili bee kuusi yɛ, Anɛ Efeso abi, anɛ ɔdakpa Atemisi mʋ, ɩ gyɛ ɔdakpa lala.

eng: The city was thrown into turmoil. They rushed as one into the theater. They seized Gaius and Aristarchus, Pauls traveling companions from the province of Macedonia.
local: Opula tɔ nɩɩ kadɛ mʋ tɔ kyaŋɛ ɔlanba. Nɩɩ asa mʋ ba naa kɩtaa Gayusi maa Arisitako mʋ nɩɩ ba gyɛ Masedoniya abi, nɩɩ bee buu Pɔɔlɩ mʋ. Nɩɩ ba bʋga bamʋ kpee asa ɔgyaŋɛkpa.

eng: Paul wanted to appear before the assembly, but the disciples wouldnt allow him.
local: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ daa ee biti fɛɛ o kpee sakyɔ mʋ tɔ, amaa akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ ba man sa mʋ ɔkpa.

eng: Even some officials of the province of Asia, who were Pauls friends, sent word to him, urging him not to risk going into the theater.
local: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ mʋ kyɛmɩnɛ ana gbaa mʋ nɩɩ ba gyɛ Asiya abɩlɩsa akʋ ba sa kɔnɔ kʋlɛ mʋ fɛɛ ɔ man waa kɛ o kpee sakyɔ mʋ tɔ.

eng: Meanwhile, the assembly was in a state of confusion. Some shouted one thing, others shouted something else, and most of the crowd didnt know why they had gathered.
local: Nɩɩ sakyɔ mʋ be mee nu abaa kasɛ. Akʋ bee kuusi yɛ falɛ, nɩɩ banɩmʋ mɔɔ ba yɛ bamʋ lɛɛ ndee. Nɩɩ akʋ mɔɔ ba bʋ tʋtɔ yɛgɛ ba man nyi kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kii kpee sʋ tʋtɔ.

eng: The Jews sent Alexander to the front, and some of the crowd directed their words toward him. He gestured that he wanted to offer a defense before the assembly.
local: Nɩɩ Gyiwu abi mʋ ba nin Alekisanda kyʋŋɛ kʋbʋ mu ansi tɔ ɔ yɩlɛ bamʋ ayaa tɔ tɔwɛ sa bamʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ nyɛɛ bamʋ kɩbaa yɛ ba laatɔ kɛ ɔ lɛɛ asa mʋ kɔnɔ sa bamʋ.

eng: but when they realized he was a Jew, they all shouted in unison, Great is Artemis of the Ephesians! This continued for about two hours.
local: Amaa asa mʋ ba maa bɩɩ fɛɛ Alekisanda gyɛ Gyiwu obii mʋ, ba kuusi kyɛɛrɛɛ fɛɛ kerefi anyɔ bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Anɛ Efeso abi, anɛ ɔdakpa Atemisi mʋ, ɩ gyɛ ɔdakpa lala.

eng: The city manager brought order to the crowd and said, People of Ephesus, doesnt everyone know that the city of Ephesus is guardian of the temple of the great Artemis and of her image, which fell from heaven?.
local: Nɩɩ kadɛ mʋ tɔ ɔwʋlɛ kyanpʋ yɛgɛ nɩɩ sakyɔ mʋ yɛgɛ ɔlanba mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ yɛ, Efeso abi, ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ nyi fɛɛ anɛ ɔdakpa lala Atemisi, yɛ ɩmʋ kubuu mʋ nɩɩ kɩ lɩɩ sʋsʋ ba tɩyɛ mʋ, ɩmʋ kumu ɩ gyɛ Efeso kadɛ nɩmʋ.

eng: Therefore, since these facts are undeniable, you must calm down. Dont be reckless.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ɩnɩmʋ ɩ man sʋ kamɔɔrɛ‐gyii, fan laatɔ kɛ fan man kpala.

eng: The men you brought here have neither robbed the temple nor slandered our goddess.
local: Asa mʋ nɩɩ fan bɩya nfɩɩ falɛ, ba man yuuri ɔdakpa kʋtɔ kʋkʋ, nɩɩ ba man tɔwɛ da anɛ ɔdakpa kyɩɩsa mʋ sʋ.

eng: Therefore, if Demetrius and the craftspeople with him have a charge against anyone, the courts are in session and governors are available. They can press charges against each other there.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ Demetirisu maa mʋ tɔɔmaa ana be nyi fɛɛ ba sʋ asɩn akʋ ba pwɩɩ bamʋ bɩrɛ, an sʋ kakɛ mʋ nɩɩ an gyii asɩn kɩbanɛ abɩlɩsa ansi tɔ. Kanɩn sʋ bɛɛ taalɛ ba pwɩɩ bamʋ asɩn ogyikpa tʋtɔ.

eng: Additional disputes can be resolved in a legal assembly.
local: Nɩɩ fan san fan sʋ asɩn akʋ mʋ, kanɩn bɩrɛ fan naa gyii fanɛ asɩn ogyikpa fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ nbara mʋ ɩ kaapʋ mʋ.

eng: As for us, we are in danger of being charged with rioting today, since we cant justify this unruly gathering.
local: Nɩɩ an man bɩɩ mʋ, an biti an nyɛ ndaga ɔlanba nɩmʋ kɩpwɩɩ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ nɩɩ ba naa taasɛ anɛ ɩmʋ asɩn mʋ, an man sʋ asɩn akʋ nɩɩ an taalɛ taa kaapʋ tɔ lɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ sʋ nɩɩ ɔlanba mʋ waa.

eng: After he said this, he dismissed the assembly.
local: Nɩɩ kadɛ mʋ tɔ ɔwʋlɛ kyanpʋ mʋ maa tɔwɛ ɩnɩmʋ lʋwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ yɛgɛ sakyɔ mʋ yaasɛ tɔ.

eng: When the riot was over, Paul sent for the disciples, encouraged them, said good-bye, and left for the province of Macedonia.
local: Ɔlanba mʋ kamaa mʋ, Pɔɔlɩ sun tɩɩ Krisito akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ waa bamʋ, kala bamʋ, nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ tʋtɔ kpee Masedoniya.

eng: He traveled through that region with a message of encouragement. When he came to Greece.
local: Ɔ natɛ tʋtɔ kyaabɔɔ, ɛɛ tɔwɛ abwaarɛsɩn yɛgɛ ɛɛ tɔwɛ ɛɛ waa asa, nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ tʋtɔ kpee Akiya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ.

eng: he stayed for three months. Because the Jews hatched a plot against Paul as he was about to sail for Syria, he decided instead to return through Macedonia.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ kyɩna tʋtɔ ɩbwaayɩ ɩsa. Amaa saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa mʋ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ ee biti o kpee Siriya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ bɩɩ fɛɛ Gyiwu abi ba ŋmina asɩn lɩɩ mʋ sʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔ kyɛɛgɛ mʋ nfɛɛrɛ bwii kamaa naa bɔla lɩɩ Masedoniya.

eng: He was accompanied by Sopater, Pyrrhus son from Beroea, Aristarchus and Secundus from Thessalonica, Gaius from Derbe, Timothy, and Tychicus and Trophimus from the province of Asia.
local: Asa mʋ nɩɩ ba buu mʋ kpee ɩ gyɛ Piru mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ Bereya mu‐bii‐nyɩnsa Sofata, maa Arisitako maa Sekundu mʋ nɩɩ ba lɩɩ Tesalonika, yɛ Gayusi mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ Debe, yɛ Timoti, taa tii Tikiku maa Tirofimu mʋ nɩɩ ba lɩɩ Asiya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ mʋ.

eng: They went on ahead and waited for us in Troas.
local: Asa anɩmʋ ɩ gyankpaa naa gyoo anɛ Trowa kadɛ tɔ.

eng: We sailed from Philippi after the Festival of Unleavened Bread and met them five days later in Troas, where we stayed for a week.
local: Amaa Kesu kɔnɔ kɔlɔŋɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bodobodo mʋ nɩɩ kɩ man sʋ kɩfɛ tinsa kamaa tɔ mʋ, an bɔla nkyu sʋ lɩɩ Filipi. Lɩɩ nkɛ nnuu kamaa mʋ, an naa tu bamʋ Trowa. Nɩɩ an gyii nkɛ nsunɔ tʋtɔ.

eng: On the first day of the week, as we gathered together for a meal, Paul was holding a discussion with them. Since he was leaving the next day, he continued talking until midnight.
local: Nkɛ nsunɔ tɔ kakɛ gyankpaasa mʋ, an ba gyaŋɛ kɛ an gyii Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ agyitɔ mʋ. Pɔɔlɩ maa biti fɛɛ lɩɩkaakɛ ɔ lɩɩ Krisito abi kʋbʋ mʋ asɛ sʋ mʋ, ɔ kyaga sʋ tɔwɛ abwaarɛsɩn mʋ naa fʋʋ halɩɩ kiyiridi nsana.

eng: There were many lamps in the upstairs room where we had gathered.
local: Ɩfɩtɩla ɩ kyɔ abansʋrʋ obu mʋ nɩɩ an gyaŋɛ kɩmʋ tɔ mʋ.

eng: A young man named Eutychus was sitting in the window. He was sinking into a deep sleep as Paul talked on and on. When he was sound asleep, he fell from the third floor and died.
local: Nɩɩ kɩyaafɔlɛ kʋ bʋ tʋtɔ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Yutiku. Ɔ tɛ nfansɩrɛ sʋ ee nu asɩn mʋ yɛgɛ ɛɛ gyɔɔsɛ. Kpalɩ fɛɛ Pɔɔlɩ maa tɔwɛ kyɛɛrɛɛ sʋ mʋ, ɔ taa dɛ nsɩɩrɛ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ abansʋrʋ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyan sʋ kɩsasapʋ mʋ sʋ ba tɩyɛ kasɛ, nɩɩ o wu.

eng: Paul went down, fell on him and embraced him, then said, Dont be alarmed. Hes alive.
local: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ kpɩlɩgɛ ba mʋ asɛ, ba bun mʋ sʋ, taa mʋ abaa kyaabɔɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Fan man yɛgɛ ɩ tɔɔrɔɔ fanɛ, ɔ san ɛɛ fʋʋtɛ.

eng: Then Paul went back upstairs and ate. He talked for a long time—right up until daybreak—then he left.
local: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ bwii dii kpee abansʋrʋ mʋ sʋ, naa kpɛɛ bodobodo wɛ. Nɩɩ o bwii kyaga mʋ katɔwɛ sʋ naa fʋʋ kayɩkɛ, nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ tʋtɔ kyʋn.

eng: They took the young man away alive, and they were greatly comforted.
local: Nɩɩ asa mʋ ba taa kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ san ɔ tɛ nkpa tɔ mʋ kpee kewu. Nɩɩ asa mʋ nkɔlɔ i fuuli bamʋ.

eng: We went on to the ship and sailed for Assos, where we intended to take Paul on board. Paul had arranged this, since he intended to make his way there by land.
local: An gyankpaa naa taa ɔkʋlɩ lala fɛɛ pantuu bɔla nkyu sʋ kpee Asosi. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ an kpee a naa taa Pɔɔlɩ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ wʋla tɔwɛ yɛ ee biti ɔ taa ayaa tɔ naa fʋʋ tʋtɔ.

eng: When he met us at Assos, we took him aboard and went on to Mitylene.
local: An naa mʋ a naa gyaŋɛ Asosi mʋ, an taa mʋ, nɩɩ an ba Mitilene.

eng: The next day we sailed from there and arrived opposite Chios. On the day after, we sailed to Samos, and on the following day we came to Miletus.
local: Ɩmʋ lɩɩkaakɛ mʋ, an lɩɩ tʋtɔ bɔla nkyu sʋ ba Kiyo. Nɩɩ ɩmʋ kayɩ maa bɩla kɛ mʋ, an faa nkyu kpee Samosi, nɩɩ kakɛ sasapʋ mʋ, an naa fʋʋ Miletu.

eng: Paul had decided to sail past Ephesus so that he wouldnt need to spend too much time in the province of Asia. He was hurrying to reach Jerusalem, if possible, by Pentecost Day.
local: Pɔɔlɩ waa mʋ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ ɔ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ yɩlɛ Efeso, kɛ ɔ man nyɛ ee nyita saŋa Asiya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ee biti bilen, fɛɛ nɩɩ ɔ taalɛ mʋ, o loo Gyerusalem Pentekosi kensi‐gyii kakɛ.

eng: From Miletus he sent a message to Ephesus calling for the churchs elders to meet him.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ an fʋʋ Miletu mʋ, Pɔɔlɩ sun tɩɩ Efeso Krisito abi kʋbʋ abɩlɩsa mʋ.

eng: When they arrived, he said to them, You know how I lived among you the whole time I was with you, beginning with the first day I arrived in the province of Asia.
local: Ba maa ba mʋ asɛ mʋ, ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fanɛ gbaa‐gbaa fanɛ fen nyi kanan mʋ nɩɩ n kyɩna yɛ fanɛ, lɩɩ kakɛ gyankpaasa mʋ nɩɩ n ba fanɛ asɛ Asiya ofuli sʋ nfɩɩ.

eng: I served the Lord with great humility and with tears in the midst of trials that came upon me because of the Jews schemes.
local: Nɩɩ n bɩya mɛ n‐yɩɩ kasɛ taa ansi‐kyu, yɛ awʋrʋfɔ mʋ nɩɩ n gyii lɩɩ ɩkpa libi mʋ nɩɩ Gyiwu abi ba biti mɛ mʋ sʋ kpini sun Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ kusun.

eng: You know I held back nothing that would be helpful so that I could proclaim to you and teach you both publicly and privately in your homes.
local: Nɩɩ fan nyi fɛɛ n man taa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kii biti kɩ kyɛ fanɛ tɔ mʋ baala fanɛ sʋ. Amaa n tɔwɛ abwaarɛsɩn nɩɩ n kaapʋ fanɛ alandɛ sʋ yɛ akpaara sʋ.

eng: You know I have testified to both Jews and Greeks that they must change their hearts and lives as they turn to God and have faith in our Lord Jesus.
local: N lɛɛ asɩn mʋ kesintin tɔwɛ Gyiwu abi yɛ Giriki abi fɛɛ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ ba kyɛɛgɛ bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ, kɛ ba bwii ba Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ, kɛ ba kɔɔlɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu gyii.

eng: Now, compelled by the Spirit, Im going to Jerusalem. I dont know what will happen to me there.
local: Nbɩɩnbɩɩ bɩrɛ, ɩ fʋʋ fɛɛ n kpee Gyerusalem fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ tɔwɛ waa mɛ mʋ, amaa n mee nyi kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ki biti kɩ waa mɛ tʋtɔ.

eng: What I do know is that the Holy Spirit testifies to me from city to city that prisons and troubles await me.
local: Kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ n nyi ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ, kadɛ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ n kpee mʋ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ ɩ da mi kumu tɔ fɛɛ awʋrʋfɔ yɛ obu kitii ɩ dɛ i gyoo mɛ.

eng: But nothing, not even my life, is more important than my completing my mission. This is nothing other than the ministry I received from the Lord Jesus: to testify about the good news of Gods grace.
local: Amaa man taa mɛ nkpa tɩɩ sɛhn, nɩɩ n mɛɛ kɩɩ mɛ n‐yɩɩ fɛɛ n gyɛ kʋtɔ kʋ. Amɔɔ n kyaga mɛ kɩnatɛ sʋ, kɛ n waa kusun mʋ nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu taa sa mɛ mʋ lʋwɛ. Ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ n lɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kabwaala Asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ kesintin kaapʋ.

eng: I know that none of you will see me again—you among whom I traveled and proclaimed the kingdom.
local: Nɩɩ nbɩɩnbɩɩ n nyi fɛɛ fanɛ tɔ bamʋ nɩɩ n natɛ tɔwɛ sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii asɩn sa mʋ, fanɛ ɔkʋ man bɩla yɛ o wu mi ansi tɔ.

eng: Therefore, today I testify to you that Im not responsible for anyones fate.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ndaga, n lɛɛ n tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin yɛ nɩɩ fanɛ ɔkʋ kan ɔ mɛɛ nyɛ nkpa kakpaa mʋ, ɩ man lɩɩ mɛ asɛ.

eng: I havent avoided proclaiming the entire plan of God to you.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ n man taa Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn kʋ baala fanɛ sʋ, amaa n lɛɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa tɔwɛ fanɛ.

eng: Watch yourselves and the whole flock, in which the Holy Spirit has placed you as supervisors, to shepherd Gods church, which he obtained with the death of his own Son.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ ɩɩ kɩɩ mʋ nbʋlʋpʋ sʋ mʋ, fan kɩɩ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ yɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ taa waa fanɛ abaa tɔ, yɛ fan kɩɩ sʋ mʋ danbɩrasa. Fan bɩla Krisito abi kʋbʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ gbaa‐gbaa nkalan sɔɔ mʋ.

eng: I know that, after my departure, savage wolves will come in among you and wont spare the flock.
local: Nɩɩ n nyi fɛɛ nɩɩ n kan kpee kamaa tɔ mʋ, akuntun bee biti ba be loo fanɛ tɔ, nɩɩ ba mɛɛ yɛ ba yɛgɛ nbʋlʋpʋ mʋ.

eng: Some of your own people will distort the word in order to lure followers after them.
local: Saŋa kʋ ii biti ɔ ba nɩɩ fanɛ tɔ akʋ bee biti ba kʋsʋ yɩlɛ tɩn atɩntɩnsa biliŋaa asɩn mʋ, kɛ ba bɩɩtɛ akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ ba be buu bamʋ.

eng: Stay alert! Remember that for three years I constantly and tearfully warned each one of you. I never stopped warning you!.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan kɩɩ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ danbɩrasa, kɛ fan nyiŋi fɛɛ nsu nsa mʋ nɩɩ n maa fanɛ an kyɩna mʋ, n sʋ ansikyu n da fanɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ kumu tɔ kakyʋwapɛ yɛ kagyanbwɛ.

eng: Now I entrust you to God and the message of his grace, which is able to build you up and give you an inheritance among all whom God has made holy.
local: Nbɩɩnbɩɩ n sʋ fanɛ n waa Ɩbwaarɛ kɩbaa tɔ yɛ mʋ kabwaala asɩn tɔ. Mʋ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ fan dan, kɛ ɔ sa fanɛ mu kusee yɛ mʋ asa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɔŋɔ yɩla kpini.

eng: I havent craved anyones silver, gold, or clothing.
local: Nɩɩ mi ansi ɩ man payɩ ɔkʋ suwa abɛɛ mu gbiti abɛɛ mʋ kaalɛ sʋ.

eng: You yourselves know that I have provided for my own needs and for those of my companions with my own hands.
local: Fanɛ gbaa‐gbaa fanɛ fan nyi fɛɛ mɛ abaa nɩmʋ nɩɩ n sʋ n sun n nyɛ maa mɛ tɔɔmaa ana atɔ mʋ nɩɩ i tiri anɛ.

eng: In everything I have shown you that, by working hard, we must help the weak. In this way we remember the Lord Jesus words: It is more blessed to give than to receive.
local: Kʋtɔ kʋmaa tɔ mʋ, n kaapʋ fanɛ fɛɛ ɩ dagaa fan waa kusun ɔlʋn sʋ kanɩn kyɛ atiripu tɔ. Kɛ fan nyiŋi Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu kɔnɔ tɔ asɩn mʋ yɛ, Kakɛɛ sʋ kusee kɩ kyɔ kɩkɔɔlɛ. .

eng: After he said these things, he knelt down with all of them to pray.
local: Maa tɔwɛ kanɩn lʋwɛ mʋ, maa bamʋ kpini ba muŋa amulii sʋ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ.

eng: They cried uncontrollably as everyone embraced and kissed Paul.
local: Nɩɩ bamʋ kpini ba taa ansikyu biri mʋ tɔ fwaala mʋ.

eng: They were especially grieved by his statement that they would never see him again. Then they accompanied him to the ship.
local: Kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ku duŋi bamʋ ɩbʋlɛ tɔ gaa mʋ, ɩ gyɛ maa tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, ba man bɩla yɛ ba wu abaa. Nɩɩ ba sun mʋ ɔkpa kpee ɔkʋlɩ lala mʋ nɩɩ ku du fɛɛ pantuu mʋ asɛ.

eng: After we tore ourselves away from them, we set sail on a straight course to Cos, reaching Rhodes the next day, and then Patara.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ an barɩgɛ tɔ mʋ, an yii sʋ bɔla ɔpʋ sʋ kpee Kosi kadɛ tɔ. Nɩɩ kɩmʋ lɩɩkaakɛ mʋ, an kpee Rodo kadɛ tɔ, nɩɩ an lɩɩ tʋtɔ kpee Patara kadɛ tɔ.

eng: We found a ship crossing over to Phoenicia, boarded, and put out to sea.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ an nyɛ ɔkʋlɩ lala mʋ nɩɩ kii kpee Fonesiya ofuli sʋ, nɩɩ an loo kɩmʋ tɔ naa kyʋn.

eng: We spotted Cyprus, but passed by it on our left. We sailed on to the province of Syria and landed in Tyre, where the ship was to unload its cargo.
local: Anɛ ansi ɩ maa kɩɩ Kiporo kifintin mʋ, nɩɩ an bwii anɛ bɩna sʋ naa fʋʋ Siriya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ. Nɩɩ an yɩlɛ Trowa kadɛ tɔ, tʋtɔ maa nɩɩ bee biti bɛ lɛɛ ɩsʋla ɔkʋlɩ maa tɔ.

eng: We found the disciples there and stayed with them for a week. Compelled by the Spirit, they kept telling Paul not to go to Jerusalem.
local: An maa kpɩlɩgɛ tʋtɔ mʋ, an wu akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ kadɛ mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ an kyɩna bamʋ asɛ nkɛ nsunɔ. Nɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ lɛɛ kaapʋ bamʋ, nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ Pɔɔlɩ yɛ ɔ man kpee Gyerusalem.

eng: When our time had come to an end, we departed. All of them, including women and children, accompanied us out of town where we knelt on the beach and prayed.
local: Amaa anɛ saŋa maa fʋʋ mʋ, nɩɩ an bwii kyaga anɛ ɔkpa sʋ. Nɩɩ ba maa bamʋ‐ka ana yɛ bamʋ‐bii ana kpini ba sun anɛ ɔkpa lɩɩ kadɛ mʋ tɔ ba kebunɔ. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ an muŋa kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ.

eng: We said good-bye to each other, then we boarded the ship and they returned to their homes.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ an kala abaa lʋwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ an loo ɔkʋlɩ lala mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ba bwii kyʋn kewu.

eng: Continuing our voyage, we sailed from Tyre and arrived in Ptolemais. We greeted the brothers and sisters there and spent a day with them.
local: An kyaga anɛ ɔkpa sʋ lɩɩ Tayi naa fʋʋ Polimesi. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ an fwaala akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ, nɩɩ an kyɩna bamʋ asɛ kakɛ kʋlʋn.

eng: The next day we left and came to Caesarea. We went to the house of Philip the evangelist, one of the Seven, and stayed with him.
local: Lɩɩkaakɛ mʋ, an kpee Kasariya naa loo Filipu mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ abwaarɛsɩn ɔtɔwɛpʋ mʋ ayɛ. Nɩɩ ɔ bɩla ɔ gyɛ asa asunɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba kyɩna lɛɛ bamʋ Gyerusalem yɛ ba barɩgɛ agyitɔ mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ.

eng: He had four unmarried daughters who were involved in the work of prophecy.
local: Ɔ sʋ nbii‐kyɩɩsa‐bii ana mʋ nɩɩ ba man pɩɩta bɩɩ anyɩn, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ bɔla bamʋ sʋ ɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ asɩn.

eng: After staying there for several days, a prophet named Agabus came down from Judea.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ an san an bʋ tʋtɔ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ Ɔtɔwɛpʋ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Agabu lɩɩ Gyudeya ba.

eng: He came to us, took Pauls belt, tied his own feet and hands, and said, This is what the Holy Spirit says: In Jerusalem the Jews will bind the man who owns this belt, and they will hand him over to the Gentiles.
local: Maa ba anɛ asɛ mʋ, ɔ taa Pɔɔlɩ kɩrɩsʋ mʋ ŋmina mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mʋ abaa yɛ mʋ ayaa. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ yɛ, Falɛ maa nɩɩ Gyiwu abi mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Gyerusalem mʋ, bee biti ba ŋmina kɩrɩsʋ nɩmʋ wuya, kɛ ba taa mʋ waa bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ abaa tɔ. .

eng: When we heard this, we and the local believers urged Paul not to go up to Jerusalem.
local: Amaa an maa nu falɛ asɩn nɩmʋ mʋ, anaa bamʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Kasariya kadɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ, an kʋlɛ Pɔɔlɩ yɛ ɔ man kpee Gyerusalem.

eng: Paul replied, Why are you doing this? Why are you weeping and breaking my heart? Im ready not only to be arrested but even to die in Jerusalem for the sake of the name of the Lord Jesus.
local: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, Mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fen su, nɩɩ fɛn yaagɛ mɛ kɔkɔlɔ kanɩn? Man gyɛ kiŋmina mʋ nkʋn, amaa n kʋʋla mɛ n‐yɩɩ fɛɛ n wu Gyerusalem lɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu kɩtɩɩ mʋ sʋ.

eng: Since we couldnt talk him out of it, the only thing we could say was, The Lords will be done.
local: A maa bɩɩ fɛɛ an pʋpɔlɛ mʋ kpɩnɛ mʋ, nɩɩ an tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ kebiti nɩn kɩ waa.

eng: After this, we got ready and made our way up to Jerusalem.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ kamaa mʋ an kʋsʋ kʋʋla kpee Gyerusalem.

eng: Some of the disciples from Caesarea accompanied us and led us to Mnasons home, where we were guests. He was from Cyprus and had been a disciple a long time.
local: Akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Kasariya mʋ akʋ ba sun anɛ kpee Gyerusalem. Nɩɩ ba taa anɛ kpee Kasiporo‐nyɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Manson mʋ ayɛ nɩɩ a naa kyɩna tʋtɔ. Ɔ gyɛ akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu agyankpaapʋ mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ.

eng: When we arrived in Jerusalem, the brothers and sisters welcomed us warmly.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ an fʋʋ Gyerusalem mʋ, anɛ tɔɔmaa ana ba taa kensi‐gyii kʋlɛ anɛ.

eng: On the next day Paul and the rest of us went to see James. All of the elders were present.
local: Lɩɩkaakɛ mʋ, anaa Pɔɔlɩ an kpee a naa wu Gyemisi, nɩɩ Krisito abi kʋbʋ abɩlɩsa mʋ gbaa ba bʋ tʋtɔ.

eng: After greeting them, he gave them a detailed report of what God had done among the Gentiles through his ministry.
local: Pɔɔlɩ fwaala bamʋ nɩɩ ɔ kalɛ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bɔla mu kusun sʋ waa sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ tɔ kaapʋ bamʋ kʋkʋlʋn‐kʋkʋlʋn.

eng: Those who heard this praised God. Then they said to him, Brother, you see how many thousands of Jews have become believers, and all of them keep the Law passionately.
local: Ba maa nu falɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ba taa bʋnyaa sa Ɩbwaarɛ. Nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ Pɔɔlɩ yɛ, Anɛ bɩrɩsa, yɛ fʋ gbaa‐gbaa fʋ, fu wu kanan mʋ nɩɩ Gyiwu abi ngbɩn‐ngbɩn ba kɔɔlɛ gyii ba, nɩɩ ba bɩla ba sʋ Mosesi nbara mʋ tɔ kenken.

eng: They have been informed that you teach all the Jews who live among the Gentiles to reject Moses, telling them not to circumcise their children nor to live according to our customs.
local: Gyiwu abi akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Gyerusalem nfɩɩ mʋ, ba nu yɛ fɩɩ kaapʋ Gyiwu abi mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ ndɛndɛmandɛ sʋ mʋ, yɛ ba man buu Mosesi nbara kɛ ba man tɩŋɛ bamʋ‐bii ana iduu, kɛ ba yɛgɛ Gyiwu abi anaana tɔ mʋ kpini.

eng: What about this? Without a doubt, they will hear that you have arrived.
local: Mɩnɛ nɩɩ an biti an waa? Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɩ waa nɛnɛ bee biti ba nu fɛɛ fʋ ba.

eng: You must therefore do what we tell you. Four men among us have made a solemn promise.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ anɛ nfɛɛrɛ mʋ nɩɩ an sʋ an sa fʋ ndee. Asa ana akʋ ba bʋ anɛ asɛ nfɩɩ nɩɩ ba bɔ ntan sa Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: Take them with you, go through the purification ritual with them, and pay the cost of having their heads shaved. Everyone will know there is nothing to those reports about you but that you too live a life in keeping with the Law.
local: Taa bamʋ kɛ faa bamʋ fan kpee, naa lɛɛ fanɛ ineesi, kɛ fʋ ka bamʋ amu su akɔ sa bamʋ, kɛ ba sɩyɛ bamʋ amu. Kɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ ɔ bɩɩ fɛɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ lɩɩ fʋ sʋ mʋ, ɩ man gyɛ kesintin. Lɩɩ fɛɛ fʋ gbaa‐gbaa fʋ, fu buu Mosesi nbara mʋ kyɛɛkyɛɛ.

eng: As for the Gentile believers, we wrote a letter about what we decided, that they avoid food offered to idols, blood, the meat from strangled animals, and sexual immorality.
local: Amaa akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ bɩrɛ, an tɔwɛ kyula nɩɩ an kyʋrɔɔ ɔwʋlɛ naa sa bamʋ yɛ, ba man gyii agyitɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ba dɩla sa ɔdakpa abɛɛ nkalan kʋmaa, kɛ ba man gyii atɔbwaaya mʋ nɩɩ ba man tɩn bamʋ amu nɩɩ ba wu kibelinboni, kɛ ba lɛɛ bamʋ n‐yɩɩ lɩɩ kakyɩɩ abɛɛ kanyɩn‐biti tɔ.

eng: The following day Paul took the men with him and went through the purification ritual with them. He entered the temple and publicly announced the completion of the days of purification, when the offering would be presented for each one of them.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ lɩɩkaakɛ mʋ, Pɔɔlɩ taa asa ana mʋ tii mʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋ nɩɩ ba naa fwɩɩ bamʋ amu tɔ. Nɩɩ o kpee kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ naa yɩla nkɛ mʋ nɩɩ amu tɔ kɩfwɩɩ mʋ ii biti ɩ lʋwɛ, yɛ saraga mʋ nɩɩ bee biti bɛ lɛɛ sa ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ.

eng: When the seven days of purification were almost over, the Jews from the province of Asia saw Paul in the temple. Grabbing him, they threw the whole crowd into confusion by shouting.
local: Nkɛ nsunɔ mʋ ɩ maa san gbɛrɛɛ kɛ ɩ lʋwɛ mʋ, Gyiwu abi akʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba lɩɩ Asiya kɩbaafʋn kʋ sʋ mʋ, ba maa wu Pɔɔlɩ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ. Nɩɩ ba fun waa sakyɔ mʋ lɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ sʋ nɩɩ ba kɩtaa mʋ.

eng: Fellow Israelites! Help! This is the man who teaches everyone everywhere against our people, the Law, and this place. Not only that, he has even brought Greeks into the temple and defiled this holy place. .
local: Nɩɩ bee kuusi bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɩsɩrayɩ abi fan kyɛ tɔ. Ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ naa katɩn kʋmaa, ɛɛ kaapʋ asa kpini, yɛgɛ ɛɛ tɔwɛ ee yii anɛ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi yɛ anɛ nbara mʋ yɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ. Nɩɩ ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ bɩla i tii sʋ mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, ɔ taa bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi ba anɛ obu kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ lala nɩmʋ tɔ ba waa kɩmʋ ineesi.

eng: They said this because they had seen Trophimus the Ephesian in the city with him earlier, and they assumed Paul had brought him into the temple.
local: Ba maa wʋla wu Efeso ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Tirofimu maa Pɔɔlɩ ba maa naa kadɛ lala mʋ tɔ mʋ, ba fɛ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ Pɔɔlɩ taa mʋ kpee kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ.

eng: The entire city was stirred up. The people came rushing, seized Paul, and dragged him out of the temple. Immediately the gates were closed.
local: Nɩɩ kadɛ mʋ kpini asa ba maa nu Pɔɔlɩ asɩn mʋ, ba sɩlɛ lɩɩ kadɛ mʋ tɔ abaafʋn kʋmaa ba. Ba kɩtaa mʋ bɩɩtɛ lɩɩ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ, opula tɔ nɩɩ ba tii nbunɔ mʋ.

eng: While they were trying to kill him, a report reached the commander of a company of soldiers that all Jerusalem was in a state of confusion.
local: Ba maa dayɩ Pɔɔlɩ bee biti mʋ kɩmɔɔ mʋ, asɩn mʋ a naa tu Roma abi anaakɔpʋ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa fɛɛ ɩlawʋ ɩ kʋsʋ Gyerusalem kadɛ mʋ kpini tɔ.

eng: Without a moments hesitation, he took some soldiers and officers and ran down to the mob. When the mob saw the commander and his soldiers, they stopped beating Paul.
local: Ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ taa mʋ kamaa tɔ abi yɛ anaakɔpʋ mʋ akʋ kpee sakyɔ mʋ tɔ. Amaa asa mʋ ba maa wu ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ yɛ anaakɔpʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ba yɛgɛ Pɔɔlɩ kɩdayɛ.

eng: When the commander arrived, he arrested Paul and ordered him to be bound with two chains. Only then did he begin to ask who Paul was and what he had done.
local: Nɩɩ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ kpee naa kɩtaa Pɔɔlɩ, nɩɩ ɔ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ba taa agbeeleebi anyɔ ŋmina mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ asa mʋ yɛ, Anɩmʋ nɩn, nɩɩ mɩnɛ nɩɩ ɔ waa.

eng: Some in the crowd shouted one thing, others shouted something else. Because of the commotion, he couldnt learn the truth, so he ordered that Paul be taken to the military headquarters.
local: Nɩɩ asa mʋ akʋ ba yɛ falɛ, nɩɩ banɩmʋ mɔɔ ba yɛ bamʋ lɛɛ ndee. Bamʋ nnɔ ɩ maa kwɩɩ‐kwɩɩ sʋ mʋ, ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ man nu asɩn mʋ kasɛ. Nɩɩ ɔ sa kɔnɔ yɛ ba taa Pɔɔlɩ kpee anaakɔpʋ opula.

eng: When Paul reached the steps, he had to be carried by the soldiers in order to protect him from the violence of the crowd.
local: Ba maa taa Pɔɔlɩ naa fʋʋ anaakɔpʋ opula mʋ, nɩɩ bee biti ba dii loo tʋtɔ mʋ, lɩɩ asa mʋ ɔlanba sʋ mʋ, anaakɔpʋ mʋ ba kukuri Pɔɔlɩ kɛ asa mʋ ba man nyɛ dayɩ mʋ.

eng: The mob that followed kept screaming, Away with him.
local: Nɩɩ sakyɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba buu bamʋ mʋ, bee kuusi yɛ, Fan mɔɔ mʋ.

eng: Brothers and fathers, listen now to my defense.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Nsɛ ana yɛ mɛ‐daa ana, fan nu mɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ n biti n lɛɛ kɔnɔ fanɛ asɛ falɛ.

eng: As Paul was about to be taken into the military headquarters, he asked the commander, May I speak with you? He answered, Do you know Greek?.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ anaakɔpʋ ba sʋ Pɔɔlɩ bee loo tʋtɔ mʋ, ɔ tɔwɛ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ yɛ, Naafɔɔ n biti fɛɛ n tɔwɛ fʋ asɛ. Nɩɩ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Fii nu Giriki katɔwɛ.

eng: When they heard him address them in Aramaic, they became even more quiet.
local: Nɩɩ ba maa nu fɛɛ Gyiwu abi katɔwɛ tɔ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ ɛɛ tɔwɛ bamʋ asɛ mʋ, bamʋ kpini ba laatɔ diin. Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ tɔwɛ yɛ.

eng: Arent you the Egyptian who started a revolt and led four thousand terrorists into the desert some time ago.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ bɩla bwii taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Man gyɛ fʋ ɩ gyɛ Egyipiti kanyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kʋsʋ yɩlɛ ɛɛ kɔ yɛ anɛ, nɩɩ ɔ taa amɔɔpʋ ngbɩn nna kpee ofuli sʋ mʋ.

eng: Paul continued, Im a Jew, born in Tarsus in Cilicia but raised in this city. Under Gamaliels instruction, I was trained in the strict interpretation of our ancestral Law. I am passionately loyal to God, just like you who are gathered here today.
local: N gyɛ Gyiwu obii nɩn, nɩɩ ba kʋʋgɛ mɛ Taasu, Silisiya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ. Amaa ba bɩla mɛ Gyerusalem kadɛ lala nɩmʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ Gamaliye ɩ kaapʋ mɛ anɛ naana ana nbara mʋ nɩɩ ba buu mʋ. Nɩɩ n kpan mɛ n‐yɩɩ n sun Ɩbwaarɛ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fan sʋ tɔ falɛ.

eng: Paul replied, Im a Jew from Tarsus in Cilicia, a citizen of an important city. Please, let me speak to the people.
local: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ayee, n gyɛ Gyiwu obii nɩn, nɩɩ n lɩɩ Taasu kadɛ kparɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ Silisiya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ asa be nyi kɩmʋ mʋ. N kʋlɛ fʋ nɩn, yɛgɛ n tɔwɛ asa mʋ asɛ.

eng: I harassed those who followed this Way to their death, arresting and delivering both men and women into prison.
local: Nɩɩ n nyanla bamʋ nɩɩ ba buu Yesu ɔkpa mʋ naa fʋʋ bamʋ lewu tɔ. Nɩɩ n kɩtaa anyɩn yɛ akyɩɩ gbaa naa tii obu.

eng: With the commanders permission, Paul stood on the steps and gestured to the people. When they were quiet, he addressed them in Aramaic.
local: Ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ maa sa mʋ ɔkpa mʋ, Pɔɔlɩ kʋsʋ yɩlɛ obu adenbi diisa sʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ nyɛɛ bamʋ kɩbaa yɛ ba laatɔ. Ba maa laatɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ Gyiwu abi katɔwɛ.

eng: The high priest and the whole Jerusalem Council can testify about me. I received letters from them, addressed to our associates in Damascus, then went there to bring those who were arrested to Jerusalem so they could be punished.
local: Nɩɩ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ yɛ Gyiwu kʋbʋ abɩlɩsa mʋ gbaa bɛɛ taalɛ gyii falɛ asɩn nɩmʋ kesintin. Nɩɩ n bɩla n nyɛ ɩwʋlɛ n lɩɩ bamʋ asɛ fɛɛ n taa naa sa bamʋ bɩrɩsa ana Gyiwu abi mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Damasiko kadɛ tɔ. Kɛ ɩ sa mɛ ɔkpa n naa kɩtaa asa anɩmʋ, ŋmina bamʋ taa bamʋ ba Gyerusalem kɛ ba bɩɩtɛ bamʋ asʋ.

eng: During that journey, about noon, as I approached Damascus, suddenly a bright light from heaven encircled me.
local: N maa naa n kpee nɩɩ n naa tiri Damasiko kakyʋwapɛ asɛ mʋ, opula tɔ nɩɩ ɩ ŋalɩgɛ mi ansi sʋ fɛɛ bʋlayɩ maa wɛɛ ɩsɛɛ mʋ lɩɩ sʋsʋ ba kyaabɔɔ mɛ.

eng: I fell to the ground and heard a voice asking me, Saul, Saul, why are you harassing me.
local: Nɩɩ n lɩɩ tɩyɛ kasɛ nu ɔbʋlɛ kʋ maa taasɛ mɛ yɛ, Sɔɔlɩ! Sɔɔlɩ! Mɩnɛ waa nɩɩ fɩɩ nyanla mɛ kanɩn.

eng: I answered, Who are you, Lord? I am Jesus the Nazarene, whom you are harassing, he replied.
local: Nɩɩ n taasɛ yɛ, Fʋ anɩmʋ nɩn, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ? Nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Mɛ Yesu mʋ nɩɩ n gyɛ Ganasareti‐nyɩn nɩɩ fɩɩ nyanla mʋ nɩn.

eng: My traveling companions saw the light, but they didnt hear the voice of the one who spoke to me.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ mɛ asɛ mʋ, ba wu ɩŋalɩgɛsa mʋ, amaa ba man bɩɩ ɔbʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ tɔwɛ mɛ asɛ mʋ.

eng: I asked, What should I do, Lord? Get up, the Lord replied, and go into Damascus. There you will be told everything you have been appointed to do.
local: Nɩɩ n taasɛ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, nɛnɛ nɩɩ n waa? Nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, Kʋsʋ, kɛ fu kpee Damasiko. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔkʋ ii biti ɔ ba tɔwɛ fʋ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ yɩla yɛ fʋ waa.

eng: I couldnt see because of the brightness of that light, so my companions led me by the hand into Damascus.
local: Lɩɩ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɩ ŋalɩgɛ mi ansi sʋ mʋ, ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ n biliŋi gyaatanapʋ. Nɩɩ mɛ bɩrɩsa ana mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ mɛ asɛ mʋ ba kɩtaa mɛ kɩbaa tɔ kpee Damasiko.

eng: There was a certain man named Ananias. According to the standards of the Law, he was a pious man who enjoyed the respect of all the Jews living there.
local: Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn kʋ bʋ tʋtɔ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Ananiyasi. Nɩɩ ee sun Ɩbwaarɛ ee buu Gyiwu abi nbara mʋ kyɛɛkyɛɛ sʋ, nɩɩ Gyiwu abi mʋ nɩɩ ba tɛ tʋtɔ mʋ, ba sʋ bɛɛrɛɛ ba sa mʋ.

eng: He came and stood beside me. Brother Saul, receive your sight! he said. Instantly, I regained my sight and I could see him.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ ba mɛ asɛ ba yɩlɛ tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, Mɛ bɩrɩsa Sɔɔlɩ, fu ansi i bugi. Nɩɩ opula tɔ nɩɩ mi ansi a bugi nɩɩ n wu mʋ.

eng: He said, The God of our ancestors has selected you to know his will, to see the righteous one, and to hear his voice.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, Anɛ naana ana Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ lɛɛ fʋ yɩla fɛɛ fʋ bɩɩ mu kebiti, kɛ fu wu mʋ kɩyaafɔlɛ kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ Yesu mʋ, kɛ fu nu mʋ asɩn mʋ lɩɩ mʋ kɔnɔ tɔ.

eng: You will be his witness to everyone concerning what you have seen and heard.
local: Kɛ fʋ baa fʋ gyɛ mʋ kesintin ogyipu sa asa kpini, lɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ fu wu nɩɩ fu nu mʋ sʋ.

eng: What are you waiting for? Get up, be baptized, and wash away your sins as you call on his name.
local: Nɩɩ mɩnɛ nɩɩ fʋ bɩla fu gyoo? Kʋsʋ yɩlɛ kɛ fʋ yɛgɛ ba gyee fʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee kɛ ɔ taa fʋ alibi kɛɛ fʋ, kɛ fʋ tɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ kɩtɩɩ.

eng: When I returned to Jerusalem and was praying in the temple, I had a visionary experience.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ n bwii kpee Gyerusalem nɩɩ n kpee n kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ, n nyɛ ansi sʋ kɩlɛɛkaapʋ.

eng: I saw the Lord speaking to me. Hurry! he said. Leave Jerusalem at once because they wont accept your testimony about me.
local: Nɩɩ n wu Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, Waa bilen lɩɩ Gyerusalem, lɩɩ fɛɛ fu kesintin asɩn mʋ nɩɩ fii biti fu gyii lɩɩ mɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ mʋ, ba mɛɛ yɛ ba kɔɔlɛ gyii.

eng: I responded, Lord, these people know I used to go from one synagogue to the next, beating those who believe in you and throwing them into prison.
local: Nɩɩ n tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, bamʋ gbaa‐gbaa be nyi kanan mʋ nɩɩ n loo bamʋ kabwaarɛ kʋlɛkpa ibu ana tɔ, n dayɩ yɛgɛ n kɩtaa bamʋ nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ fu gyii mʋ n kpee n tii obu.

eng: When Stephen your witness was being killed, I stood there giving my approval, even watching the clothes that belonged to those who were killing him.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ mɔɔ fʋ kesintin ogyipu Sitefen mʋ, n yɩlɛ tʋtɔ kyula mu kelewu mʋ, nɩɩ mɛ ɩ kɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba mɔɔ mʋ mʋ ɩkaalɛ sʋ.

eng: Then the Lord said to me, Go! I will send you far away to the Gentiles.
local: Nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, natɛ, n biti n sun fʋ lɩɩ nfɩɩ kpee kata‐kata bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ asɛ. .

eng: The crowd listened to Paul until he said this. Then they shouted, Away with this man! Hes not fit to live.
local: Nɩɩ asa mʋ ba nu Pɔɔlɩ katɔwɛ naa fʋʋ mʋ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ falɛ sʋ. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba diyaa bamʋ ɩbʋlɛ kuusi yɛ, Fan lɛɛ ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ lɩɩ kasɛ sʋ, ɔ man dagaa maa bɩla kyɩna nkpa tɔ.

eng: As they were screaming, throwing off their garments, and flinging dust into the air.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ bee kuusi bɛɛ lɛɛ bamʋ ɩkaalɛ bee fugiti, yɛgɛ bɛɛ waa apurutiti mʋ.

eng: the commander directed that Paul be taken into the military headquarters. He ordered that Paul be questioned under the whip so that he could find out why they were shouting at him like this.
local: nɩɩ anaakɔpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ sa kɔnɔ yɛ ba taa Pɔɔlɩ loo bamʋ kɩkpaara sʋ. Kɛ ba tɩŋɛ mʋ taasɛ mʋ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa, nɩɩ bee kuusi bɛɛ wʋrɩgɛ mʋ sʋ kanɩn.

eng: As they were stretching him out and tying him down with straps, Paul said to the centurion standing there, Can you legally whip a Roman citizen who hasnt been found guilty in court.
local: Amaa saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba ŋmina Pɔɔlɩ kɛ ba bun mʋ tɩŋɛ mʋ mʋ, ɔ taasɛ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kɩɩ anaakɔpʋ kɩlɩfa sʋ mʋ yɛ, Ɩ dɛ ɔkpa fɛɛ ba tɩŋɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ Roma obii ba kɔɔlɛ mʋ fɛɛ odibii, nɩɩ ba man pɩɩta gyii mʋ asɩn kɛ ba bun mʋ kʋpwɛ.

eng: When the centurion heard this, he went to the commander and reported it. He asked, What are you about to do? This man is a Roman citizen.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kɩɩ anaakɔpʋ kɩlɩfa sʋ mʋ nu kanɩn mʋ, ɔ naa kasɛ asɩn mʋ sa anaakɔpʋ kpini ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Roma abi ba kɔɔlɛ ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ fɛɛ odibii nɩn, amaa kɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ an biti an waa.

eng: The commander went to Paul and demanded, Tell me! Are you a Roman citizen? He said, Yes.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ anaakɔpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ naa taasɛ Pɔɔlɩ yɛ, Tɔwɛ mɛ, Roma abi ba kɔɔlɛ fʋ fɛɛ odibii? Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɛɛhn, n gyɛ.

eng: The commander replied, It cost me a lot of money to buy my citizenship. Paul said, Im a citizen by birth.
local: Nɩɩ anaakɔpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ yɛ, N nyita afulee gaa pɔyɩ nɩɩ Roma abi ba kɔɔlɛ mɛ fɛɛ odibii. Amaa Pɔɔlɩ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Mɛɛ mɛ bɩrɛ ba kʋʋgɛ mɛ nɩn fɛɛ Roma odibii.

eng: At once those who were about to examine him stepped away. The commander was alarmed when he realized he had bound a Roman citizen.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ bamʋ nɩɩ ba daa bee biti fɛɛ ba taasɛ Pɔɔlɩ kɔnɔ tɔ asɩn mʋ, ba bwii kamaa bilen. Nɩɩ ɩ waa anaakɔpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ gbaa kufuu fɛɛ maa yɛgɛ nɩɩ ba ŋmina Pɔɔlɩ yɛgɛ ɔ gyɛ Roma obii.

eng: The commander still wanted to know the truth about why Paul was being accused by the Jews. Therefore, the next day he ordered the chief priests and the entire Jerusalem Council to assemble. Then he took Paul out of prison and had him stand before them.
local: Anaakɔpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ maa biti o wu kʋtɔ kʋlʋn mʋ nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ dan waa sʋ nɩɩ Gyiwu abi bɛɛ pwɩɩ mʋ mʋ, ɩmʋ sʋ lɩɩkaakɛ mʋ, ɔ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ba saŋɛ Pɔɔlɩ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ Gyiwu abi aseepu bɩlɩsa yɛ bamʋ kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ abɩlɩsa mʋ ba sarɛ. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔ bɩya Pɔɔlɩ ba yɩlɛ bamʋ ansi tɔ.

eng: Paul stared at the council and said, Brothers, I have lived my life with an altogether clear conscience right up to this very day.
local: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ kɩɩ kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ abɩlɩsa mʋ kyimm, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Mɛ tɔɔmaa ana, n nyi mɛ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ fɛɛ lɩɩ mi kusun mʋ nɩɩ n sun sa Ɩbwaarɛ ba fʋʋ ndaga, ɩ bʋrɔn Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ.

eng: The high priest Ananias ordered those standing beside Paul to strike him in the mouth.
local: Opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ Ananiyasi mʋ, sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba yɩlɛ tiri Pɔɔlɩ mʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, ba da mʋ kɔnɔ sʋ.

eng: Then Paul said to him, God is about to strike you, you whitewashed wall! You sit and judge me according to the Law, yet disobey the Law by ordering that I be struck.
local: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Kɩda nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ da fʋ gbaa fʋ, fʋ kebunbun ansi wuya. Fʋ kyɩna tʋtɔ bɔla nbara sʋ talɛ mɛ, amaa yɛgɛ fʋ gbaa‐gbaa fʋ, fʋ fɔyɩ nbara, lɩɩ fɛɛ fʋ sa kɔnɔ yɛ ba da mɛ kɔnɔ sʋ.

eng: Those standing near him asked, You dare to insult Gods high priest.
local: Bamʋ nɩɩ ba yɩlɛ tiri Pɔɔlɩ mʋ, ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ nɩɩ fʋ saalɛ falɛ.

eng: Paul replied, Brothers, I wasnt aware that he was the high priest. It is written, You will not speak evil about a ruler of your people .
local: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Mɛ tɔmaa ana, n man nyi fɛɛ ɔ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ nɩn. Kpalɩ fɛɛ ba kyʋrɔɔ yɩla yɛ, Man tɔwɛ asɩn libi gyaga ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kɩɩ fʋ asa sʋ. .

eng: Knowing that some of them were Sadducees and the others Pharisees, Paul exclaimed in the council, Brothers, Im a Pharisee and a descendant of Pharisees. I am on trial because of my hope in the resurrection of the dead.
local: Pɔɔlɩ maa ba bɩɩ fɛɛ asa mʋ akʋ ba gyɛ Sadusi nɩɩ akʋ mɔɔ ba gyɛ Farasii abi mʋ, ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Mɛ tɔmaa ana, ngbaa mɛ n gyɛ Farasii nɩn, mɛ‐sɛ gbaa gyɛ Farasii obii nɩn. Kelewu kyiŋi tamaa mʋ nɩɩ n sʋ sʋ, nɩɩ bee gyii mɛ asɩn falɛ.

eng: These words aroused a dispute between the Pharisees and Sadducees, and the assembly was divided.
local: Maa tɔwɛ kanɩn asɩn mʋ, nɩɩ ɩmɔɔrɛ ɩ tɩyɛ Sadusi yɛ Farasii abi mʋ nsana. Nɩɩ kʋbʋ mʋ kɩ barɩgɛ tɔ anyɔ.

eng: Council members were shouting loudly. Some Pharisees who were legal experts stood up and insisted forcefully, We find nothing wrong with this man! What if a spirit or angel has spoken to him.
local: Nɩɩ ɔlanba mʋ waa ɔlʋn. Nɩɩ nbara akaapʋpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba gyɛ Farasii abi mʋ, ba kʋsʋ yɩlɛ bee gyii ɩmɔɔrɛ ɔlʋn sʋ yɛ, An man wu alibi akʋ lɩɩ ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ sʋ. Akʋtɔ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu abɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ kʋ ɩ tɔwɛ mʋ asɛ.

eng: The dispute became so heated that the commander feared they might tear Paul to pieces. He ordered soldiers to go down and remove him by force from their midst. Then they took him back to the military headquarters.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔlanba mʋ nyɛ akyɔ gaa mʋ, kufuu kɩ kɩtaa anaakɔpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ, fɛɛ bɛ kan bɩɩtɛ Pɔɔlɩ kyatɛ tɔ. Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔ sa mʋ anaakɔpʋ kɔnɔ fɛɛ, ba kpɩlɩgɛ loo sakyɔ mʋ tɔ naa gbebi Pɔɔlɩ kɔɔlɛ lɩɩ bamʋ asɛ, kɛ ba taa mʋ kpee anaakɔpʋ opula.

eng: The following night the Lord stood near Paul and said, Be encouraged! Just as you have testified about me in Jerusalem, so too you must testify in Rome.
local: Lɩɩkaakɛ kagyanbwɛ mʋ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu ba yɩlɛ mʋ asɛ, tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Nyɛ kɔkɔlɔ! Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fʋ lɛɛ mɛ kesintin mʋ kaapʋ Gyerusalem mʋ, kanɩn maa nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ fʋ bɩla lɛɛ mɛ asɩn mʋ kaapʋ Roma kadɛ gbaa.

eng: The next morning some Jewish leaders formulated a plot and solemnly promised that they wouldnt eat or drink until they had killed Paul.
local: Kayɩ maa kɛ mʋ, Gyiwu abi akʋ ba waa kɔnɔ bɔ ntan yɛ, ba man dan mɔɔ Pɔɔlɩ mʋ, ba mɛɛ yɛ be gyii nɩɩ ba mɛɛ yɛ be nuu.

eng: More than forty people were involved in the conspiracy.
local: Bamʋ nɩɩ ba waa kanɩn kɔnɔ mʋ ba kyɔ asa adɩna.

eng: They went to the chief priests and elders and said, We have solemnly promised to eat nothing until we have killed Paul.
local: Nɩɩ ba kpee bamʋ aseepu bɩlɩsa yɛ abɩlɩsa mʋ asɛ naa tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, An bɔ ntan yɛ an man dan mɔɔ Pɔɔlɩ, an mɛɛ yɛ i gyii.

eng: You and the council must explain to the commander that you need Paul brought down to you. Pretend that you want to examine his case more closely. Were prepared to kill him before he arrives.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ nbɩɩnbɩɩ fʋ yɛ kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ abɩlɩsa mʋ, fen sun naa tɔwɛ anaakɔpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ yɛ, o bwiiyaa Pɔɔlɩ ba fanɛ ansi tɔ. Kɛ fan waa fɛɛ fen biti fan lɔŋɔ nu mʋ asɩn mʋ tɔ danbɩrasa. Ɩmʋ sʋ an kʋʋla anɛ n‐yɩɩ fɛɛ, nɩɩ ba kan ba sʋ mʋ bɛɛ ba mʋ, pɔyɩ kɛ ɔ fʋʋ nfɩɩ mʋ an biti a mɔɔ mʋ ɔkpa tɔ.

eng: Pauls sister had a son who heard about the ambush and he came to the military headquarters and reported it to Paul.
local: Amaa Pɔɔlɩ kawʋpa‐bii maa nu kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba ŋmina mʋ, nɩɩ o kpee naa loo anaakɔpʋ mʋ ayɛ, naa tɔwɛ Pɔɔlɩ.

eng: Paul called for one of the centurions and said, Take this young man to the commander because he has something to report to him.
local: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ tɩɩ anaakɔpʋ abɩlɩsa mʋ ɔkʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Taa kɩyaafɔlɛ nɩmʋ kpee fanɛ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa asɛ. Ɔ sʋ asɩn kparɛ kʋ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ.

eng: He took him to the commander and said, The prisoner Paul asked me to bring this young man to you. He has something to tell you.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔ taa mʋ kpee ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ asɛ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Pɔɔlɩ mʋ nɩɩ an taa ba tii obu mʋ, ɩ tɩɩ mɛ. Nɩɩ ɔ yɛ n taa kɩyaafɔlɛ nɩmʋ ba fʋ asɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ sʋ asɩn akʋ ɔ tɔwɛ fʋ.

eng: The commander took him by the hand and withdrew to a place where they could speak privately. He asked, What do you have to report to me.
local: Nɩɩ Anaakɔpʋ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ, kɩtaa kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ kɩbaa tɔ lɩɩ ɩkaa sʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Mɩnɛ asɩn nɩɩ fʋ sʋ fʋ tɔwɛ mɛ.

eng: He replied, The Jewish leaders have conspired to ask that you bring Paul down to the council tomorrow. They will pretend that they want to investigate his case more closely.
local: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ kawʋpa‐bii mʋ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Gyiwu abi abɩlɩsa mʋ ba tɔwɛ kyula yɛ, bee biti ba kʋlɛ fʋ fɛɛ fʋ taa Pɔɔlɩ ba kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ abɩlɩsa mʋ asɛ ɔkɛ. Kɛ ba pɩna fʋ yɛ bee biti ba lɔŋɔ nu mʋ asɩn mʋ tɔ danbɩrasa.

eng: Dont fall for it! More than forty of them are waiting to ambush him. They have solemnly promised not to eat or drink until they have killed him. They are ready now, awaiting your consent.
local: Amaa man kyula sa bamʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ asa mʋ tɔ akʋ ba kyɔ asa adɩna be bun afitiri tɔ bee gyoo mʋ. Nɩɩ ba bɔ ntan fɛɛ ba man dan mɔɔ mʋ mʋ, ba mɛɛ yɛ be gyii nɩɩ ba mɛɛ yɛ be nuu. Ɩmʋ sʋ ba kʋʋla bamʋ n‐yɩɩ bee gyoo ba nu lɩɩ fʋ asɛ kɛ ba waa.

eng: The commander dismissed the young man, ordering him, Dont tell anyone that you brought this to my attention.
local: Nɩɩ anaakɔpʋ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa da kɩyaafɔlɛ mu kumu yɛ, Man kan tɔwɛ ɔkʋ fɛɛ fʋ tɛɛ ba tɔwɛ falɛ asɩn nɩmʋ sa mɛ. Nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ mʋ ɔkpa.

eng: The commander called two centurions and said, Prepare two hundred soldiers, seventy horsemen, and two hundred spearmen to leave for Caesarea at nine oclock tonight.
local: Tʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ anaakɔpʋ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ, tɩɩ mʋ kamaa tɔ abɩlɩsa anyɔ sa bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Fan lɛɛ anaakɔpʋ anyɔ, yɛ ɩgbaŋɛ adiipu adusunɔ, yɛ akpɛ atʋʋpʋ alɩfa anyɔ. Kɛ fan kʋʋla fanɛ n‐yɩɩ kɛ fan lɩɩ nfɩɩ ndaga kagyanbwɛ kerefi akpanɔ kpee Kasariya.

eng: Have horses ready for Paul to ride, so they may take him safely to Governor Felix.
local: Fan sa Pɔɔlɩ gbaa ɩgbaŋɛ akʋ, kɛ fan kɩɩ mʋ sʋ naa fʋʋ ɔsʋwʋlɛ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ Felisi asɛ.

eng: He wrote the following letter.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ kyʋrɔɔ ɔwʋlɛ naa sa Felisi. Ɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ.

eng: Claudius Lysias, to the most honorable Governor Felix: Greetings.
local: Mɛ anaakɔpʋ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa Kilodiyo Lisiya ɩ kyʋrɔɔ falɛ ɔwʋlɛ nɩmʋ n sa fʋ Ɔsʋwʋlɛ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ kparɛ Felisi. N fwaala fʋ.

eng: This man was seized by the Jews and was almost killed by them. I was nearby with a unit of soldiers, and I rescued him when I discovered that he was a Roman citizen.
local: Gyiwu abi akʋ ɩ kɩtaa ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ yɛ bee biti ba mɔɔ mʋ. N maa bɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ gyɛ Roma odibii mʋ, nɩɩ maa mɛ ayaafɔlɛ an kɔɔlɛ mʋ yɛgɛ.

eng: I wanted to find out why they were accusing him, so I brought him to their council.
local: Nɩɩ n maa biti n bɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa nɩɩ bɛɛ pwɩɩ mʋ mʋ, n taa mʋ ba bamʋ kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ abɩlɩsa mu ansi tɔ.

eng: I discovered that they were accusing him about questions related to their Law. I found no charge deserving of death or imprisonment.
local: N bɩɩ fɛɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ bɛ pwɩɩ mʋ mʋ, ɩ gyɛ bamʋ Gyiwu abi nbara asɩn nɩn. Amaa n man wu alibi mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ ba mɔɔ mʋ abɛɛ ba tii mʋ obu.

eng: When I was informed of a conspiracy against his life, I sent him to you at once and ordered his accusers to bring their case against him before you.
local: N maa nu fɛɛ Gyiwu abi akʋ ba ŋmina fɛɛ ba mɔɔ mʋ mʋ, n waa bilen taa mʋ bɩya fʋ. Nɩɩ n tɔwɛ mʋ apwɩɩpʋ mʋ yɛ ba ba pwɩɩ mʋ fu ansi tɔ.

eng: Following their orders, the soldiers took Paul during the night and brought him to Antipatris.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ anaakɔpʋ mʋ ba waa kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba sa bamʋ kɔnɔ mʋ. Kagyanbwɛ maŋa nɩɩ ba taa Pɔɔlɩ kpee naa loo Antipatirisi.

eng: The following day they let the horsemen continue on with Paul while they returned to the military headquarters in Jerusalem.
local: Lɩɩkaakɛ mʋ anaakɔpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba naa ayaa tɔ mʋ, ba bwii kpee bamʋ anaakɔpʋ opula. Nɩɩ ɩgbɛŋɛ adiipu mʋ ba taa mʋ kpee Kasariya.

eng: The horsemen entered Caesarea, delivered the letter to the governor, and brought Paul before him.
local: Anaakɔpʋ mʋ ba maa fʋʋ Kasariya mʋ, ba taa ɔwʋlɛ mʋ sa Ɔsʋwʋlɛ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ba taa Pɔɔlɩ waa mʋ abaa tɔ.

eng: After he read the letter, he asked Paul about his home province. When he learned that he was from Cilicia.
local: Maa kalɛ ɔwʋlɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ Pɔɔlɩ ɔsʋwʋlɛ kʋlʋn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ sʋ. Maa bɩɩ fɛɛ Pɔɔlɩ lɩɩ Silisiya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ mʋ.

eng: the governor said, I will hear your case when your accusers arrive. Then he ordered that Paul be kept in custody in Herods palace.
local: nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ Pɔɔlɩ yɛ, Nɩɩ fʋ apwɩɩpʋ ba kan ba mʋ, n biti n nu fʋ asɛ. Nɩɩ ɔ sa kɔnɔ fɛɛ ba naa kɩɩ mʋ sʋ Owura lala Herodi lenbu tɔ.

eng: Five days later the high priest Ananias came down with some elders and a lawyer named Tertullus. They pressed charges against Paul before the governor.
local: Pɔɔlɩ maa fʋʋ Kasariya nkɛ nnuu kamaa tɔ mʋ, oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ Ananiyasi yɛ abɩlɩsa akʋ yɛ lɔya mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Tetulu ba ba. Nɩɩ ba taa bamʋ asɩn pwɩɩsa ba pwɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ ɔsʋwʋlɛ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ Felisi ansi tɔ.

eng: After the governor summoned Paul, Tertullus began to make his case against him. He declared, Under your leadership, we have experienced substantial peace, and your administration has brought reforms to our nation.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba tɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ ba loo tʋtɔ mʋ, nɩɩ Tetulu taa asɩn tʋʋ Ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ Felisi ansi tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ yɛ, Ɔsʋwʋlɛ Ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ kparɛ, fʋ ɔsʋwʋlɛ kɩkɩta danbɩrasa sʋ mʋ, an nyɛ kayɩɩ yuuli gaa, nɩɩ lɩɩ fʋ kanyaasɩn sʋ mʋ, fʋ yɛgɛ nɩɩ atɔ damantɛ a lɔŋɔ sa anɛ ɔsʋwʋlɛ nɩmʋ sʋ.

eng: Always and everywhere, most honorable Felix, we acknowledge this with deep gratitude.
local: Katɩn kʋmaa yɛ ɔkpa kʋmaa nɩmʋ sʋ mʋ, an taa kensi‐gyii kɔɔlɛ fʋ awaasa mʋ, nɩɩ an fwaala fʋ gaa.

eng: I dont want to take too much of your time, so I ask that you listen with your usual courtesy to our brief statement of the facts.
local: Amaa kanan mʋ nɩɩ n man nyita fʋ saŋa sʋ mʋ, n kʋlɛ fʋ fɛɛ fu nu anɛ asɛ gbɛrɛɛ lɩɩ ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ asɩn sʋ.

eng: We have found this man to be a troublemaker who stirs up riots among all the Jews throughout the empire. Hes a ringleader of the Nazarene faction.
local: An wu ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ maa gyɛ alibi wuya, nɩɩ ɔ sʋ kamɔɔrɛ‐gyii ɛɛ kɔla Gyiwu abi dulinyaa tɔ kpini. Nɩɩ ɔ bɩla ɔ gyɛ kʋbʋ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ kɩmʋ yɛ Nasarene mʋ ɔgyankpaapʋ.

eng: and even tried to defile the temple. Thats when we arrested him.
local: Nɩɩ naafɔɔ o biti fɛɛ ɔ taa ineesi waa kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ, nɩɩ an kɩtaa mʋ. Naafɔɔ an daa biti fɛɛ i gyii mʋ asɩn fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ anɛ nbara ɩ kaapʋ mʋ.

eng: By examining him yourself, you will be able to verify the allegations we are bringing against him.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ sa kɔnɔ yɛ mʋ apwɩɩpʋ ba ba fu ansi tɔ. Nɩɩ fʋ gbaa‐gbaa fʋ tʋʋ mʋ tɔ kɩɩ mʋ, fii biti fu wu fɛɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ an taa an pwɩɩ mʋ mʋ, ɩ gyɛ kesintin.

eng: The Jews reinforced the action against Paul, affirming the truth of these accusations.
local: Nɩɩ Gyiwu abi mʋ ba yɩlɛ kyula yɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba taa pwɩɩ mʋ mʋ, kpini ɩ bʋ tɔ kanɩn.

eng: The governor nodded at Paul, giving him permission to speak. He responded, I know that you have been judge over this nation for many years, so I gladly offer my own defense.
local: Ɔsʋwʋlɛ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ maa sa Pɔɔlɩ ɔkpa yɛ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, N nyi fɛɛ fʋ ɩ gyɛ asɩn ogyipu danbɩrasa lɩɩ Gyiwu ɔsʋwʋlɛ nɩmʋ sʋ nsu damantɛ. Ɩmʋ sʋ ɩ waa mɛ ɔkɔn fɛɛ fu ansi tɔ nɩɩ n biti n tɔwɛ mɛ asɩn.

eng: You can verify that I went up to worship in Jerusalem no more than twelve days ago.
local: Fɩɩ taalɛ taasɛ kɛ fu wu fɛɛ ɩ waa fɛɛ nkɛ kudu anyɔ nɩmʋ nɩɩ n kpee Gyerusalem n naa sun Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: They didnt find me arguing with anyone in the temple or stirring up a crowd, whether in the synagogue or anywhere else in the city.
local: Nɩɩ mɛ apwɩɩpʋ banɩmʋ ba man wu n maa gyii ɩmɔɔrɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ, abɛɛ n maa fun n waa asa kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɩgyaŋɛkpa, kadɛ mʋ tɔ katɩn kʋ.

eng: Nor can they prove to you the allegations they are now bringing against me.
local: Nɩɩ ba mɛɛ taalɛ kaapʋ tɔ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba tɩn gyaga mɛ sʋ falɛ, fɛɛ ɩ gyɛ kesintin.

eng: I do admit this to you, that I am a follower of the Way, which they call a faction. Accordingly, I worship the God of our ancestors and believe everything set out in the Law and written in the Prophets.
local: Amaa n kyula fɛɛ Yesu Ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ ba kɩɩ fɛɛ ɩ gyɛ alibi ɔkpa mʋ, kɩmʋ kɩkaapʋ nɩɩ n sʋ n sun anɛ naana ana Ɩbwaarɛ. Nɩɩ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ waa Mosesi nbara ɩwʋlɛ tɔ, yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Atɔwɛpʋ ɩwʋlɛ tɔ mʋ n kɔɔlɛ ɩmʋ kpini gyii.

eng: The hope I have in God I also share with my accusers, that there will be a resurrection of both the righteous and the unrighteous.
local: Nɩɩ n sʋ tamaa Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ anyɩn banɩmʋ gbaa ba sʋ mʋ, yɛ saŋa kʋ ii biti ɔ ba nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti o kyiŋi bamʋ nɩɩ bamʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bamʋ ɩkpa ɩ man kyiigi lɩɩ lewu tɔ.

eng: On account of this, I have committed myself to maintaining a clear conscience before God and with all people.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ n kpan mɛ n‐yɩɩ, n waa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋrɔn Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ nyɩmɩsa asɛ.

eng: After an absence of several years, I came to Jerusalem to bring gifts for the poor of my nation and to offer sacrifices.
local: N maa lɩɩ Gyerusalem nsu damantɛ kamaa mʋ, n bwii kpee tʋtɔ, taa afulee ba yɛ sa mɛ asa, kɛ n taa kɛkɛɛ sa Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: When they found me in the temple, I was ritually pure. There was no crowd and no disturbance.
local: N maa dan lʋwɛ mɛ ineesi kɩfwɩɩ mʋ, nɩɩ n waa ɩnɩmʋ mʋ, nɩɩ mɛ apwɩɩpʋ mʋ ba wu mɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ. Amaa ba man wu ɔkʋ mɛ asɛ, nɩɩ n man waa ɩlawʋ ɩkʋ.

eng: But there were some Jews from the province of Asia. They should be here making their accusations, if indeed they have something against me.
local: Amaa Gyiwu abi akʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba lɩɩ Asiya kɩbaafʋn ɩ kyɩna ba bʋ tʋtɔ. Nɩɩ naafɔɔ n waa alibi akʋ mʋ, bamʋ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ ba ba pwɩɩ mɛ fu ansi tɔ.

eng: In their absence, have these people who are here declare what crime they found when I stood before the Jerusalem Council.
local: Abɛɛ yɛgɛ asa anɩmʋ ba tɔwɛ alibi mʋ nɩɩ ba wu lɩɩ mɛ sʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ n kpee naa yɩlɛ kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ abɩlɩsa mʋ asɛ mʋ.

eng: Perhaps it concerns this one statement that I blurted out when I was with them: I am on trial before you today because of the resurrection of the dead.
local: Amaa saŋa mʋ nɩɩ n yɩlɛ bamʋ ansi tɔ mʋ, kʋtɔ kʋlʋn nkʋn nɩɩ n kuusi tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Kelewu kyiŋi asɩn mʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ nɩɩ ndaga ba pwɩɩ mɛ fu ansi tɔ falɛ. .

eng: Felix, who had an accurate understanding of the Way, adjourned the meeting. He said, When Lysias the commander arrives from Jerusalem, I will decide this case.
local: Nɩɩ Ɔsʋwʋlɛ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ Felisi mʋ nɩɩ ɔ wʋla o nyi Yesu Ɔkpa asɩn mʋ sʋ mʋ, o nin asɩn mʋ naa yɩla kakɛ pʋpwɛ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Anaakɔpʋ Ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ Lisiya kan ba mʋ, n biti n kɩɩ fʋ asɩn mʋ tɔ.

eng: He arranged for a centurion to guard Paul. He was to give Paul some freedom, and his friends were not to be hindered in their efforts to provide for him.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ sa anaakɔpʋ mʋ ɔbɩlɩsa kɔnɔ yɛ, ɔ taa Pɔɔlɩ naa kɩɩ mʋ sʋ danbɩrasa. Kɛ ɔ man yɛgɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ ɩ nyan mʋ, kɛ ɔ sa ɔkpa kɛ mʋ asa ba ba bɔɔ mʋ sʋ kɛ ba kyɛ mʋ tɔ.

eng: After several days, Felix came with his wife Drusilla, who was Jewish, and summoned Paul. He listened to him talk about faith in Christ Jesus.
local: Nkɛ kalɩsa nkʋ kamaa mʋ, Felisi maa mʋ‐ka Durusila mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ Gyiwu‐kyɩɩ mʋ ba ba. Nɩɩ o sun tɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ, nɩɩ ɔ yɛgɛ nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ tɔwɛ Yesu Krisito kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii mʋ tɔ asɩn sa mʋ.

eng: When he spoke about upright behavior, self-control, and the coming judgment, Felix became fearful and said, Go away for now! When I have time, Ill send for you.
local: Pɔɔlɩ maa máá sʋ ɛɛ tɔwɛ nbara kyiigisa asɩn yɛ n‐yɩɩ kɩkɩta yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ katalɛ mʋ nɩɩ kii biti kɩ ba mʋ, ɩ waa Felisi kufuu. Nɩɩ ɔ yɛ, Ɩ kʋkwɛ kanɩn, natɛ. Nɩɩ n kan nyɛ saŋa mʋ n biti n tɩɩ fʋ.

eng: At the same time, he was hoping that Paul would offer him some money, so he often sent for him and talked with him.
local: Tʋtɔ mʋ yɛgɛ ɛɛ waa tamaa fɛɛ Pɔɔlɩ ii biti ɔ sa mu afulee kɛ ɔ yɛgɛ mʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ saŋa kʋmaa mʋ, ee sun ɛɛ tɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ nɩɩ bee biri aligyima.

eng: When two years had passed, Felix was succeeded by Porcius Festus. Since Felix wanted to grant a favor to the Jews, he left Paul in prison.
local: Nsu nyɔ kamaa mʋ ba lɛɛ Felisi nɩɩ ba taa Pokiyu Fesitu ba yii mʋ ayaa tɔ. Amaa Felisi maa biti fɛɛ ɔ waa kʋtɔ kʋ bɔla Gyiwu abi ansi mʋ, ɔ yɛgɛ nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ san o tii obu.

eng: Three days after arriving in the province, Festus went up to Jerusalem from Caesarea.
local: Nɩɩ Fesitu maa ba Kasariya fɛɛ ɔsʋwʋlɛ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ mʋ, ɩmʋ nkɛ nsa kamaa mʋ, ɔ lɩɩ tʋtɔ kpee Gyerusalem.

eng: The chief priests and Jewish leaders presented their case against Paul. Appealing to him.
local: Nɩɩ aseepu bɩlɩsa yɛ Gyiwu abi abɩlɩsa ba taa Pɔɔlɩ asɩn mʋ naa tʋʋ mu ansi tɔ.

eng: they asked as a favor from Festus that he summon Paul to Jerusalem. They were planning to ambush and kill him along the way.
local: Nɩɩ ba kʋlɛ Fesitu gaa fɛɛ ɔ waa bamʋ kɔkɔlɔ biti sa bamʋ. Ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ ɔ yɛgɛ Pɔɔlɩ ɔ lɩɩ Kasariya ba Gyerusalem. Kpalɩ fɛɛ ba waa bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ yɛ ba naa bun ɔkpa tɔ, kɛ ba nyɛ mɔɔ Pɔɔlɩ.

eng: But Festus responded by keeping Paul in Caesarea, since he was to return there very soon himself.
