local: Nɩɩ Fesitu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Pɔɔlɩ tii obu Kasariya, nɩɩ ɩ mɛɛ yɛ ɩ kyɛɛrɛɛ mɛ gbaa‐gbaa mɛ n biti n kpee tʋtɔ.

eng: Some of your leaders can come down with me, he said. If hes done anything wrong, they can bring charges against him.
local: Fan lɛɛ fanɛ abɩlɩsa akʋ kɛ maa bamʋ a kpee Kasariya. Nɩɩ fan kan fan sʋ ɔnyɩn mʋ asɩn libi akʋ mʋ, kɛ fan naa mʋ fan tɔwɛ.

eng: He stayed with them for no more than eight or ten days, then went down to Caesarea. The following day he took his seat in the court and ordered that Paul be brought in.
local: Fesitu maa kyɩna bamʋ asɛ fɛɛ nkɛ nburuwa abɛɛ nkɛ kudu kamaa tɔ mʋ, ɔ lɩɩ tʋtɔ kpee Kasariya. Kayɩ kɩ maa kɛ mʋ, ɔ naa kyɩna asɩn ogyikpa kagya sʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ sa kɔnɔ yɛ ba taa Pɔɔlɩ ba mu ansi tɔ.

eng: When he arrived, many Jews who had come down from Jerusalem surrounded him. They brought serious charges against him, but they couldnt prove them.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ ba tʋtɔ mʋ, Gyiwu abi abɩlɩsa mʋ nɩɩ ba lɩɩ Gyerusalem ba tʋtɔ mʋ, ba yɩlɛ kyaabɔɔ mʋ. Nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩn atɩntɩnsa bɛɛ gyaga mʋ sʋ, amaa yɛgɛ ba mɛɛ taalɛ kaapʋ ɩmʋ kasɛ.

eng: In his own defense, Paul said, Ive done nothing wrong against the Jewish Law, against the temple, or against Caesar.
local: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ lɛɛ asɩn mʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, N man waa alibi akʋ lɩɩ Gyiwu abi nbara sʋ, abɛɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ, abɛɛ Roma Owura lala Kayisa.

eng: Festus, wanting to put the Jews in his debt, asked Paul, Are you willing to go up to Jerusalem to stand trial before me concerning these things.
local: Amaa Fesitu maa biti fɛɛ ɔ waa Gyiwu abi kɔkɔlɔ biti sʋ mʋ, ɔ taasɛ Pɔɔlɩ yɛ, Fii biti fɛɛ n kpiya fʋ Gyerusalem, kɛ n naa gyii fʋ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ pwɩɩ fʋ falɛ sʋ mi ansi tɔ abɛɛ.

eng: Paul replied, Im standing before Caesars court. I ought to be tried here. I have done nothing wrong to the Jews, as you well know.
local: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Nbɩɩnbɩɩ n yɩlɛ opula mʋ nɩɩ Roma Owura lala Kayisa ee gyii asɩn mʋ, nɩɩ ɩ dagaa be gyii mɛ asɩn. Amaa Gyiwu abi bɩrɛ n man waa bamʋ alibi akʋ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fʋ gbaa‐gbaa fʋ, fu nyi falɛ.

eng: If Im guilty and have done something that deserves death, then I wont try to avoid death. But if there is nothing to their accusations against me, no one has the authority to hand me over to them. I appeal to Caesar.
local: Fɛɛ nɩɩ mɛ asɩn akʋ a bun mɛ kʋpwɛ, nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ n wu bɩrɛ, man kina kɩmʋ. Amaa asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba tɩn gyaga mɛ sʋ falɛ, nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ kesintin mʋ, ɔkʋ mɛɛ taalɛ lɛɛ mɛ sa bamʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ n biti n taa mɛ asɩn mʋ naa tʋʋ Kayisa ansi tɔ.

eng: After Festus conferred with his advisors, he responded, You have appealed to Caesar. To Caesar you will go.
local: Nɩɩ Fesitu maa mʋ atɔwɛ kyatɔpʋ ba maa tɔwɛ lʋwɛ kamaa mʋ, ba tɔwɛ Pɔɔlɩ yɛ, Fʋ maa tɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ fʋ taa fʋ asɩn kpee Kayisa ansi tɔ mʋ, Kayisa ansi tɔ nɩɩ fii kpee.

eng: After several days had passed, King Agrippa and Bernice arrived in Caesarea to welcome Festus.
local: Ɩ maa gyii nkɛ nkʋ kamaa tɔ mʋ, Owura Agiripa yɛ mʋ pɩɩkyɩɩ Benisi ba kpee Kasariya ba naa fwaala Ɔsʋwʋlɛ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ Fesitu.

eng: Since they were staying there for many days, Festus discussed the case against Paul with the king. He said, There is a man whom Felix left in prison.
local: Ba maa kyɩna tʋtɔ nkɛ nkʋ mʋ, Fesitu tɔwɛ Pɔɔlɩ n‐yɩɩ sʋ asɩn sa owura Agiripa yɛ, Ɔnyɩn kʋ bʋ tɔ nɩɩ Felisi yɛgɛ nɩɩ o tii obu.

eng: When I was in Jerusalem, the Jewish chief priests and elders brought charges against him and requested a guilty verdict in his case.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ n kpee Gyerusalem mʋ, aseepu bɩlɩsa yɛ Gyiwu abi abɩlɩsa ba bɩya Pɔɔlɩ asɩn pwɩɩsa mi ansi tɔ, nɩɩ bee biti fɛɛ n bun mʋ kʋpwɛ.

eng: I told them it is contrary to Roman practice to hand someone over before they have faced their accusers and had opportunity to offer a defense against the charges.
local: Nɩɩ n tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, ɩ man gyɛ anɛ Roma abi adɩdaa‐sɩn fɛɛ a bun ɔkʋ kʋpwɛ, amɔɔ maa bamʋ nɩɩ ba pwɩɩ mʋ mʋ, ba gyaŋɛ kɛ ba gyii asɩn mʋ pɔyɩ kɛ ba bun mʋ kʋpwɛ.

eng: When they came here, I didnt put them off. The very next day I took my seat in the court and ordered that the man be brought before me.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ bamʋ abɩlɩsa ba buu mɛ ba nfɩɩ mʋ, n man taa asɩn mʋ kpɩɩ kasɛ. Amaa ɩmʋ lɩɩkaakɛ mʋ, n kpee naa kyɩna asɩn mʋ ogyikpa, nɩɩ n sa kɔnɔ yɛ ba taa ɔnyɩn mʋ ba tʋtɔ.

eng: When the accusers took the floor, they didnt charge him with any of the crimes I had expected.
local: Amaa saŋa mʋ nɩɩ mʋ apwɩɩpʋ ba kʋsʋ yɩlɛ mʋ, ba man taalɛ kaapʋ tɔ asɩn libi mʋ nɩɩ n da fɛ nfɛɛrɛ mʋ ɩkʋ lɩɩ mʋ sʋ.

eng: Instead, they quibbled with him about their own religion and about some dead man named Jesus, who Paul claimed was alive.
local: Amaa asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba nyɛ lɩɩ mʋ sʋ ɩ gyɛ maa bamʋ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa kamɔɔrɛ‐gyii, yɛ ɔnyɩn kʋ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ Yesu nɩɩ o wu mʋ, nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ ɩ tɔwɛ fɛɛ ɔ san ɔ tɛ nkpa tɔ.

eng: Since I had no idea how to investigate these matters, I asked if he would be willing to go to Jerusalem to stand trial there on these issues.
local: Nɩɩ asɩn nɩmʋ kɩmɛɛgɛ tɔ ɩ kɩtaa mɛ nfɛɛrɛ. Ɩmʋ sʋ n taasɛ mʋ yɛ, Fii biti fɛɛ fu kpee Gyerusalem kɛ ba naa gyii fʋ asɩn nɩmʋ tʋtɔ abɛɛ?.

eng: However, Paul appealed that he be held in custody pending a decision from His Majesty the emperor, so I ordered that he be held until I could send him to Caesar.
local: Amaa Pɔɔlɩ maa kʋlɛ fɛɛ ee biti ɔ baa o tii obu mʋ, naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Roma Owura lala Kayisa ii biti o gyii mʋ asɩn mʋ, kanɩn sʋ n kɩtaa mʋ n yɩla naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ n biti n taa mʋ naa sa owura lala Kayisa. .

eng: Agrippa said to Festus, I want to hear the man myself. Tomorrow, Festus replied, you will hear him.
local: Nɩɩ Owura Agiripa tɔwɛ Fesitu yɛ, Mɛ gbaa‐gbaa mɛ n biti fɛɛ n nu lɩɩ ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ asɛ. Nɩɩ Fesitu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɔkɛ fii biti fu nu lɩɩ mʋ asɛ.

eng: The next day Agrippa and Bernice came with great fanfare. They entered the auditorium with the military commanders and the citys most prominent men. Festus then ordered that Paul be brought in.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ lɩɩkaakɛ mʋ Owura Agiripa maa mʋ‐pɩɩkyɩɩ Benisi ba suu‐tɔ, nɩɩ asa ba taa bɛɛrɛɛ sa bamʋ, nɩɩ anaakɔpʋ abɩlɩsa lala yɛ kadɛ mʋ tɔ abɩlɩsa ba naa gyaŋɛ asɩn ogyikpa mʋ. Nɩɩ Fesitu sa kɔnɔ nɩɩ ba naa bɩya Pɔɔlɩ.

eng: Festus said, King Agrippa and everyone present with us: You see this man! The entire Jewish community, both here and in Jerusalem, has appealed to me concerning him. Theyve been calling for his immediate death.
local: Nɩɩ Fesitu kʋsʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, Owura Agiripa yɛ fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fan gyaŋɛ anɛ asɛ nfɩɩ kpini fan nu asɩn nɩmʋ. Ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ nɩɩ Gyiwu abi kpini ba kʋlɛ mɛ lɩɩ Gyerusalem yɛ Kasariya nfɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ man dagaa maa bɩla kyɩna nkpa tɔ.

eng: Ive found that he has done nothing deserving death. When he appealed to His Majesty, I decided to send him to Rome.
local: N wu fɛɛ ɔ man waa alibi mʋ nɩɩ ɔ dagaa lewu. Amaa lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ maa taa mʋ asɩn naa sa Owura lala Kayisa sʋ mʋ, n waa mɛ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ n kpiya mʋ Roma.

eng: I have nothing definite to write to our lord emperor. Therefore, Ive brought him before all of you, and especially before you, King Agrippa, so that after this investigation, I might have something to write.
local: Amaa n man nyɛ asɩn kitii mʋ nɩɩ n kyʋrɔɔ naa sa mɛ Owura lala Kayisa. Ɩmʋ sʋ n taa mʋ ba fanɛ ansi tɔ, fɔnfɔn fʋ owura Agiripa, fɛɛ an kan kɩɩ mʋ asɩn mʋ tɔ mʋ, n nyɛ asɩn kʋ lɩɩ mʋ sʋ kɛ n kyʋrɔɔ.

eng: After all, it would be foolish to send a prisoner without specifying the charges against him.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɩ man sʋ kasɛ fɛɛ n lɛɛ obu tiipu kyasɛ Owura Kayisa, yɛgɛ n man kaapʋ tɔ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ sʋ ba pwɩɩ mʋ.

eng: Agrippa said to Paul, You may speak for yourself. So Paul gestured with his hand and began his defense.
local: Nɩɩ Owura Agiripa tɔwɛ Pɔɔlɩ yɛ, Nbɩɩnbɩɩ ɔkpa dɛ sa fʋ fɛɛ fʋ tɔwɛ fʋ asɩn. Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ kʋsaa mʋ kɩbaa piili ɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ.

eng: King Agrippa, I consider myself especially fortunate that I stand before you today as I offer my defense concerning all the accusations the Jews have brought against me.
local: Owura Agiripa, ɩ gyɛ kumu ɔkɔn sa mɛ fɛɛ ndaga n maa yɩlɛ fu ansi tɔ n biti n lɛɛ kɔnɔ lɩɩ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Gyiwu abi ba tɩn gyaga mɛ sʋ falɛ kpini.

eng: This is because you understand well all the Jewish customs and controversies. Therefore, I ask you to listen to me patiently.
local: Fɔnfɔn fɛɛ fʋ bɩrɛ fu nyi Gyiwu abi anaana tɔ kpini yɛ bamʋ ɩmɔɔrɛ‐libi. Ɩmʋ sʋ n kʋlɛ fʋ fɛɛ fʋ nyɛ kɔkɔlɔ kɛ fu nu mɛ asɛ.

eng: Every Jew knows the way of life I have followed since my youth because, from the beginning, I was among my people and in Jerusalem.
local: Gyiwu abi kpini be nyi mɛ kakyɩna tɔ lɩɩ koo mi kibi tɔ. Nɩɩ be nyi kanan mʋ nɩɩ n du mɛ gbaa‐gbaa mɛ ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ yɛ Gyerusalem tɔ.

eng: They have known me for a long time. If they wanted to, they could testify that I followed the way of life set out by the most exacting group of our religion. I am a Pharisee.
local: Nɩɩ bee biti ba gyii kesintin mʋ, ba wʋla be nyi mɛ koo dɩdaa fɛɛ, n kyɩna n gyɛ Farasii kʋbʋ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ anɛ kabwaarɛ‐sun, nɩɩ kɩ sʋ anɛ nbara kyɛɛkyɛɛ mʋ ɔkʋ.

eng: Today I am standing trial because of the hope in the promise God gave our ancestors.
local: Ndaga n yɩlɛ nfɩɩ nɩɩ bɛɛ talɛ mɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ n sʋ tamaa lɩɩ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ sa anɛ naana ana mʋ sʋ.

eng: This is the promise our twelve tribes hope to receive as they earnestly worship night and day. The Jews are accusing me, King Agrippa, because of this hope!.
local: Falɛ kɔkɔlɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ da mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi nbunɔ kudu anyɔ ba sʋ tamaa yɛ bee biti ba nyɛ sʋ, nɩɩ bee sun Ɩbwaarɛ kakyʋwapɛ yɛ kagyanbwɛ. Owura danbɩrasa, falɛ tamaa nɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ Gyiwu abi mʋ bɛɛ pwɩɩ mɛ.

eng: Why is it inconceivable to you that God raises the dead?.
local: Mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ ɩ bʋlʋn sa fanɛ Gyiwu abi nɩɩ fan mɛɛ kɔɔlɛ fen gyii fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ i kyiŋi alewupu?.

eng: I really thought that I ought to oppose the name of Jesus the Nazarene in every way possible.
local: Mɛ gbaa‐gbaa mɛ n daa kɔɔlɛ gyii fɛɛ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ n waa kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ n taalɛ waa kun Yesu Kanasareti‐nyɩn mʋ kɩtɩɩ.

eng: And thats exactly what I did in Jerusalem. I locked up many of Gods holy people in prison under the authority of the chief priests. When they were condemned to death, I voted against them.
local: Nɩɩ kanɩn gbaa nɩɩ n waa Gyerusalem kadɛ tɔ. N nyɛ ɔlʋn lɩɩ aseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ n naa n kɩtaa Ɩbwaarɛ asa tii obu. Nɩɩ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba mɔɔ bamʋ mʋ, mɛ kɔnɔ ki tii sʋ.

eng: In one synagogue after another—indeed, in all the synagogues—I would often torture them, compelling them to slander God. My rage bordered on the hysterical as I pursued them, even to foreign cities.
local: Saŋa kʋmaa n kpee Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɩgyaŋɛkpa, nɩɩ n yɛgɛ nɩɩ bɛɛ nyanla bamʋ. Nɩɩ n nyan bamʋ ba tɔwɛ nbusaa asɩn gyaga Yesu sʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ n kisi bamʋ sʋ mʋ, n kpee asa ndɛ sʋ n naa nyanla bamʋ.

eng: On one such journey, I was going to Damascus with the full authority of the chief priests.
local: Kanɩn asɩn nɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ n kyɩna naa kɔɔlɛ ɔwʋlɛ yɛ ɔlʋn lɩɩ aseepu bɩlɩsa mʋ asɛ, nɩɩ n kpee Damasiko.

eng: While on the road at midday, King Agrippa, I saw a light from heaven shining around me and my traveling companions. That light was brighter than the sun.
local: Mɛ Owura danbɩrasa, n maa kpee nɩɩ ɩ naa fʋʋ kakyʋwapɛ asɛ mʋ, nɩɩ n wu fɩtɩla kʋ lɩɩ sʋsʋ kɩ ŋalɩgɛ kɩ kyɔ kyɔwɛ ba kyaabɔɔ maa bamʋ nɩɩ anaa mʋ.

eng: We all fell to the ground, and I heard a voice that said to me in Aramaic, Saul, Saul, why are you harassing me? Its hard for you to kick against a spear.
local: Nɩɩ anɛ kpini an tɩyɛ kasɛ, nɩɩ n nu ɔbʋlɛ kʋ maa tɔwɛ Hibriwu katɔwɛ tɔ yɛ, Sɔɔlɩ, Sɔɔlɩ, mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fɩɩ nyanla mɛ kanɩn? Ɩ bʋlʋn sa fʋ fɛɛ fʋ maa kɔɔ yɛ mi kebiti.

eng: Then I said, Who are you, Lord? The Lord replied, I am Jesus, whom you are harassing.
local: Nɩɩ n taasɛ yɛ, Fʋ anɩmʋ nɩn, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ? Nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Mɛ Yesu mʋ nɩɩ fɩɩ nyanla mʋ nɩn.

eng: Get up! Stand on your feet! I have appeared to you for this purpose: to appoint you as my servant and witness of what you have seen and what I will show you.
local: Amaa kʋsʋ yɩlɛ fʋ ayaa sʋ. N lɛɛ mɛ n‐yɩɩ kaapʋ fʋ, kɛ n taa fʋ waa mɛ kɩyaafɔlɛ yɛ mi kesintin ogyipu. Nɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ fu wu lɩɩ mɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ, yɛ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ n biti n lɛɛ kaapʋ fʋ mʋ, kɛ fʋ taa tɔwɛ asa.

eng: I will rescue you from your own people and from the Gentiles. I am sending you.
local: N biti n sun fu kpee fʋ gbaa‐gbaa fʋ asa yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ asɛ, kɛ n mɔlɩgɛ fʋ lɩɩ bamʋ abaa tɔ.

eng: to open their eyes. Then they can turn from darkness to light and from the power of Satan to God, and receive forgiveness of sins and a place among those who are made holy by faith in me.
local: Kɛ fʋ naa bugi bamʋ ansi, kɛ fʋ lɛɛ bamʋ lɩɩ kitentenbiri tɔ ba fɩtɩla tɔ. Kɛ fʋ lɛɛ bamʋ lɩɩ Sɩtaanɛ ɔlʋn tɔ ba Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ. Kɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bee biti ba kɔɔlɛ gyii mʋ, ba nyɛ alibi kɩtaakɛɛ, kɛ ba nyɛ ɔkyɩnakpa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ lɔŋɔ yɩla sa mʋ asa mʋ.

eng: So, King Agrippa, I wasnt disobedient to that heavenly vision.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ, Owura Agiripa, n man waa kʋsʋ tɔ ɔlʋn sa kɩlɛɛkaapʋ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ lɩɩ sʋsʋ mʋ.

eng: Instead, I proclaimed first to those in Damascus and Jerusalem, then to the whole region of Judea and to the Gentiles. My message was that they should change their hearts and lives and turn to God, and that they should demonstrate this change in their behavior.
local: Kanɩn sʋ n gyankpaa tɔwɛ asɩn nɩmʋ sa Damasiko abi, yɛ Gyerusalem abi. N bɩla tɔwɛ sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Gyudeya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ kpini, yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi. N naa tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ ba kyɛɛgɛ bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ lɩɩ alibi tɔ, kɛ ba ba Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ, kɛ ba waa ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ ba yɛgɛ libi kɩwaa.

eng: Because of this, some Jews seized me in the temple and tried to murder me.
local: Lɩɩ ɩnɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ Gyiwu abi ba kɩtaa mɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ bee biti ba mɔɔ mɛ.

eng: God has helped me up to this very day. Therefore, I stand here and bear witness to the lowly and the great. Im saying nothing more than what the Prophets and Moses declared would happen.
local: Amaa Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ kyɛ mɛ tɔ ba fʋʋ ndaga kakɛ nɩmʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ n yɩlɛ nfɩɩ n lɛɛ Yesu kesintin asɩn n kaapʋ asa kpini. Nɩɩ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ yɛ Mosesi tɔwɛ yɩla yɛ ii biti ɩ ba mʋ, ɩmʋ asɩn maŋa nɩɩ n tɔwɛ falɛ.

eng: that the Christ would suffer and that, as the first to rise from the dead, he would proclaim light both to my people and to the Gentiles.
local: Asɩn mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, ɩ dagaa fɛɛ Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ mʋ maa gyii awʋrʋfɔ, kɛ ɔ baa ɔ gyɛ kelewu kyiŋi tɔ ɔgyankpaapʋ, kɛ ɔ tɔwɛ fɩtɩla mʋ asɩn sa Gyiwu abi yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi kpini.

eng: At this point in Pauls defense, Festus declared with a loud voice, Youve lost your mind, Paul! Too much learning is driving you mad.
local: Pɔɔlɩ maa lɛɛ mʋ asɩn mʋ kasɛ kanɩn mʋ, nɩɩ Fesitu tɩn mʋ sʋ pʋntɛ yɛ, Pɔɔlɩ, fɩɩ lɔɔ ɩbʋn nɩn? Fʋ ɔwʋlɛ kinyi kɩbansa ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ fʋ nfɛɛrɛ i nyita.

eng: But Paul replied, Im not mad, most honorable Festus! Im speaking what is sound and true.
local: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɔsʋwʋlɛ Ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ Fesitu, n mɛɛ lɔɔ ɩbʋn nɩn, n tɔwɛ kesintin asɩn mʋ nɩɩ kanyaasɩn bʋ ɩmʋ tɔ.

eng: King Agrippa knows about these things, and I have been speaking openly to him. Im certain that none of these things have escaped his attention. This didnt happen secretly or in some out-of-the-way place.
local: Owura Agiripa, n kɔɔlɛ gyii fɛɛ fʋ gbaa fu nyi asɩn nɩmʋ sʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ n yɩlɛ kɩnyɩn tɔ, n tɔwɛ falɛ. Nɩɩ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ falɛ ɩmʋ ɩkʋ ɩ man baala fʋ sʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ba man waa ɩkʋ asiiri tɔ.

eng: King Agrippa, do you believe the prophets? I know you do.
local: Owura Agiripa, fʋ kɔɔlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ mʋ asɩn gyii? N nyi fɛɛ fʋ maa kɔɔlɛ gyii.

eng: Agrippa said to Paul, Are you trying to convince me that, in such a short time, youve made me a Christian.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ owura Agiripa taasɛ Pɔɔlɩ yɛ, Fu nyi fɛɛ fɩɩ taalɛ taa saŋa dɛɛ nɩmʋ tɔ kyɛɛgɛ mɛ nfɛɛrɛ, kɛ n ba biliŋi Krisito obii abɛɛ.

eng: Paul responded, Whether it is a short or a long time, I pray to God that not only you but also all who are listening to me today will become like me, except for these chains.
local: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɩ gyɛ saŋa dɛɛ nɩmʋ tɔ nɩn o, ii biti ɩ kyɛɛrɛɛ nɩn o, ɩmʋ nɩɩ n kʋlɛ n lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, ɔ yɛgɛ fʋ yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba sarɛ bee nu mɛ asɛ nfɩɩ mʋ kpini ba biliŋi fɛɛ mɛ, amaa ba man taa agbeeleebi ŋmina bamʋ fɛɛ mɛ.

eng: The king stood up, as did the governor, Bernice, and those sitting with them.
local: Saŋa maa tɔ nɩɩ Owura Agiripa maa mʋ pɩɩkyɩɩ Benisi yɛ Ɔsʋwʋlɛ Ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ Fesitu yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba tɛ tʋtɔ mʋ kpini ba kʋsʋ lɩɩ.

eng: As they left, they were saying to each other, This man is doing nothing that deserves death or imprisonment.
local: Ba maa lɩɩ obu mʋ tɔ mʋ, bee biri asɩn mʋ kiligyima, nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ abaa yɛ, Ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ man waa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ ba mɔɔ mʋ, abɛɛ ba tii mʋ obu.

eng: Agrippa said to Festus, This man could have been released if he hadnt appealed to Caesar.
local: Nɩɩ Agiripa tɔwɛ Fesitu yɛ, Ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ man dɩn tɔwɛ, yɛ ee biti ɔ taa mʋ asɩn naa sa Owura lala Kayisa nɩn, naafɔɔ an taalɛ yɛgɛ mʋ.

eng: When it was determined that we were to sail to Italy, Paul and some other prisoners were placed in the custody of a centurion named Julius of the Imperial Company.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba taa nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ a kpee Roma kadɛ tɔ mʋ, ba taa Pɔɔlɩ maa bamʋ nɩɩ ba tii bamʋ obu mʋ akʋ, waa anaakɔpʋ kɩlɩfa ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ ɔbɩlɩsa Yuriyo abaa tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ Owura lala Kayisa anaakɔpʋ kʋbʋ mʋ tɔ.

eng: We boarded a ship from Adramyttium that was about to sail for ports along the coast of the province of Asia. So we put out to sea. Aristarchus, a Macedonian from Thessalonica, came with us.
local: Nɩɩ an taa ɔkʋlɩ lala mʋ nɩɩ kɩ lɩɩ Adaramitiyo kadɛ tɔ, nɩɩ kii biti ki kpee Asiya ɔsʋwʋlɛ kabaafʋn kʋkʋ sʋ kebunɔ. Nɩɩ Kamasedoniya‐nyɩn Arisitako mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ Tesalonika mʋ gbaa tii anɛ sʋ.

eng: The next day we landed in Sidon. Julius treated Paul kindly and permitted him to go to some friends so they could take care of him.
local: Ɩmʋ lɩɩkaakɛ mʋ, a naa dan Sidon kadɛ tɔ. Nɩɩ anaakɔpʋ kɩlɩfa Yuriyo waa Pɔɔlɩ adanbɩrasa, sa mʋ ɔkpa fɛɛ ɔ naa wu mʋ kyɛmɩnɛ ana, kɛ ɔ nyɛ nkyɛtɔ lɩɩ bamʋ asɛ.

eng: From there we sailed off. We passed Cyprus, using the island to shelter us from the headwinds.
local: Nɩɩ an lɩɩ tʋtɔ bɔla ɔpʋ sʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ afuu mʋ ɩmaa daa ɩ gyaŋa anɛ sʋ mʋ, an bɔla kpee Sipuru kifintin mʋ asɛ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ afuu mʋ a man bʋlʋn.

eng: We sailed across the open sea off the coast of Cilicia and Pamphylia, and landed in Myra in Lycia.
local: A maa bɔla ɔpʋ sʋ naa kara Kilikiya yɛ Panfiliya, nɩɩ a naa dan Mira mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ Lisiya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ mʋ.

eng: There the centurion found an Alexandrian ship headed for Italy and put us on board.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ anaakɔpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ wu ɔkʋlɩ lala kʋ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ lɩɩ Alekisandiya kadɛ tɔ kii kpee Itali. Nɩɩ ɔ taa anɛ naa waa kɩmʋ tɔ.

eng: After many days of slow and difficult sailing, we arrived off the coast of Cnidus. The wind wouldnt allow us to go farther, so we sailed under the shelter of Crete off Salmone.
local: Nɩɩ an natɛ nkyu sʋ bwahn‐bwahn gyii nkɛ nkʋ nɩɩ ɩ waa ɔlʋn sa anɛ, pɔyɩ nɩɩ an naa fʋʋ Kinido. Lɩɩ fɛɛ afuu mʋ ɩ maa man sa anɛ ɔkpa sʋ mʋ, an bɔla naa tiri Kereti kefintin mʋ, nɩɩ an kara Salimone kadɛ tɔ.

eng: We sailed along the coast only with difficulty until we came to a place called Good Harbors, near the city of Lasea.
local: A maa naa an kara an kyʋn nbunɔ‐nbunɔ sʋ mʋ, an kpɩnɛ pɔyɩ nɩɩ an ba fʋʋ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ Ɩkʋlɩ lala Ɔdankpa Danbɩrasa, nɩɩ kɩ bʋ tiri Laseya kadɛ.

eng: Much time had been lost, and the voyage was now dangerous since the Day of Reconciliation had already passed. Paul warned them.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ an maa kyɛɛrɛɛ ɔkpa tɔ gaa sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ kɔnɔ‐ŋmina saŋa maa kyʋn mʋ, nkyu sʋ kɩbɔla waa kufuu. Ɩmʋ sʋ Pɔɔlɩ da bamʋ amu tɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ.

eng: Men, I see that our voyage will suffer damage and great loss, not only for the cargo and ship but also for our lives.
local: Anyɩn, n wu fɛɛ anɛ ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ an kpee falɛ, ii biti ɩ bɩya kumu libi, yɛ gyerebi lɩɩ ɔkʋlɩ lala mʋ yɛ kɩmʋ ɩsʋla. Nɩɩ anɛ gbaa anɛ an man lɩɩ.

eng: But the centurion was persuaded more by the ships pilot and captain than by Pauls advice.
local: Amaa anaakɔpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ ɔ man nu Pɔɔlɩ asɩn mʋ, ɔkʋlɩ wuya mʋ yɛ kɩmʋ ɔkpa ɔkaapʋpʋ asɛ nɩɩ ee nu.

eng: Since the harbor was unsuitable for spending the winter, the majority supported a plan to put out to sea from there. They thought they might reach Phoenix in Crete and spend the winter in its harbor, which faced southwest and northwest.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɩkʋlɩ ɔdankpa maŋa man bʋrɔn sa awɔɔ saŋa. Nɩɩ bamʋ gaalaagaa ba tɔwɛ kyula yɛ ba kpan bamʋ n‐yɩɩ kɛ ba fʋʋ Fonike. Tʋtɔ ɩkʋlɩ ɔdankpa mʋ ɩ bʋrɔn sa awɔɔ saŋa. Fonike gyɛ ɔdankpa nɩɩ kɩ dɛ Kereti kefintin, mʋ nɩɩ kɩ kɩɩ akpɩlɩgɛ kɩtɩyɛ yɛ adii kɩtɩyɛ nsana tɔ.

eng: When a gentle south wind began to blow, they thought they could carry out their plan. They pulled up anchor and sailed closely along the coast of Crete.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ afuu pii ɩ daa ɩ lɩɩ akpɩlɩgɛ mʋ, asa mʋ be nyi fɛɛ ba nyɛ afuu mʋ nɩɩ bee biti. Ɩmʋ sʋ ba lɛɛ kɩbɩlɩtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ kɩtaa ɔkʋlɩ lala kɩ yɩla, nɩɩ ba kyaga bamʋ ɔkpa sʋ ba naa Kereti ɩkaa sʋ ɩkaa sʋ.

eng: Before long, a hurricane-strength wind known as a northeaster swept down from Crete.
local: Amaa ɩ man kyɛɛrɛɛ nɩɩ afuu bʋlʋnsa akʋ, ɩ daa apaa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ Adɩɩ yɛ Kyɔwɛ kɩlɩɩ afuu. Nɩɩ ɩ daa lɩɩ kefintin mʋ sʋ.

eng: The ship was caught in the storm and couldnt be turned into the wind. So we gave in to it, and it carried us along.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ anɛ ɔkʋlɩ ɩ gyaŋa afuu mʋ, ɔkʋlɩ mʋ man bɩla ɛɛ taalɛ ee kpee ansi tɔ. Ɩmʋ sʋ an yɛgɛ anɛ n‐yɩɩ sa afuu mʋ, nɩɩ i sii ɩ sʋ anɛ ɩ naa.

eng: After sailing under the shelter of an island called Cauda, we were able to control the lifeboat only with difficulty.
local: Nɩɩ ɩ sʋla anɛ naa lɩɩ kifintin pii mʋ nɩɩ bɛ tɩɩ yɛ Kuda, nɩɩ kii kun afuu mʋ gbɛrɛɛ. Nɩɩ ɩ waa ɔlʋn sa anɛ pɔyɩ nɩɩ an taalɛ kɩtaa ɔkʋlɩ pii mʋ nɩɩ kɩlala mʋ kɩ bɩɩtɛ mʋ.

eng: They brought the lifeboat aboard, then began to wrap the ship with cables to hold it together. Fearing they might run aground on the sandbars of the Gulf of Syrtis, they lowered the anchor and let the ship be carried along.
local: Anyɩn mʋ ba maa nyɛ ɔkʋlɩ‐pii mʋ, ba taa kɩmʋ waa kɩlala mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ba taa ɩfɛ ŋmina kyaabɔɔ ɔkʋlɩ lala mʋ kinkin. Nɩɩ ɩ waa bamʋ kufuu fɛɛ afuu mʋ ii biti ɩ sʋla bamʋ naa wɔtɛ Libiya kɩkyafa. Ɩmʋ sʋ ba bɔɔ abaala mʋ, nɩɩ ba yɛgɛ nɩɩ afuu mʋ i sii ɩ sʋ ɔkʋlɩ mʋ ɩ naa.

eng: We were so battered by the violent storm that the next day the men began throwing cargo overboard.
local: Afuu mʋ ɩ maa nyanla anɛ kanɩn mʋ, kayɩ kɩ maa kɛ mʋ, ba piili bɛɛ lɛɛ ɔkʋlɩ mʋ tɔ ɩsʋla bɛɛ tʋʋ nkyu mʋ tɔ.

eng: On the third day, they picked up the ships gear and hurled it into the sea.
local: Ɩmʋ kakɛ sasapʋ mʋ, ba taa bamʋ abaa bɛɛ lɛɛ ɔkʋlɩ mʋ gbaa‐gbaa tɔ atɔ bɛɛ tʋʋ nkyu mʋ tɔ.

eng: When neither the sun nor the moon appeared for many days and the raging storm continued to pound us, all hope of our being saved from this peril faded.
local: An gyii nkɛ gaalaagaa yɛgɛ a mee wu kyɔwɛ abɛɛ akyikpebi, yɛgɛ afuu mʋ mɔɔ ɩ san ɩ daa kanɩn. Ɩmʋ sʋ anɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ man bɩla ɔ sʋ tamaa fɛɛ ɔ sʋ nkpa.

eng: For a long time no one had eaten. Paul stood up among them and said, Men, you should have complied with my instructions not to sail from Crete. Then we would have avoided this damage and loss.
local: A maa kyɩna akʋn nkɛ gaalaagaa nɩmʋ yɛgɛ a mee gyii sɛhn mʋ, nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ kʋsʋ yɩlɛ bamʋ nsana tɔwɛ yɛ, Anyɩn, fan daa nu mɛ katɔwɛ yɛ an man lɩɩ Kereti mʋ, naafɔɔ falɛ awʋrʋfɔ yɛ gyerebi nɩmʋ man dɩn ba anɛ sʋ.

eng: Now I urge you to be encouraged. Not one of your lives will be lost, though we will lose the ship.
local: Amaa n tɔwɛ fanɛ fɛɛ fan nyɛ kɔkɔlɔ, fanɛ ɔkʋ mɛɛ yɛ o sii tɔ, amɔɔ ɔkʋlɩ mʋ nkʋn ii biti o nyita.

eng: Last night an angel from the God to whom I belong and whom I worship stood beside me.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ndee kagyanbwɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ n gyɛ mʋ lɛɛ, nɩɩ n sun mɛ mʋ, mʋ kabɔɔ ba yɩlɛ mɛ asɛ.

eng: The angel said, Dont be afraid, Paul! You must stand before Caesar! Indeed, God has also graciously given you everyone sailing with you.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Pɔɔlɩ, man waa kufuu. Ɩ dan ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ fʋ naa yɩlɛ Owura lala Kayisa ansi tɔ. Nɩɩ fʋ sʋ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ taa bamʋ nɩɩ be tii fʋ sʋ falɛ kpini nkpa sa bamʋ.

eng: Be encouraged, men! I have faith in God that it will be exactly as he told me.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ anyɩn, fan nyɛ kɔkɔlɔ. N kɔɔlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gyii fɛɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mɛ mʋ, kanɩn nɩɩ ee biti ɔ waa.

eng: However, we must run aground on some island.
local: Amaa ɩ waa kanan kʋmaa afuu mʋ ii biti ɩ sʋla anɛ naa dan kefintin kʋ sʋ.

eng: On the fourteenth night, we were being carried across the Adriatic Sea. Around midnight the sailors began to suspect that land was near.
local: Nɩɩ a maa gyii nkɛ kudu ana, yɛgɛ ɩ san ɩ sʋ anɛ ɩɩ kyaa ɩɩ bɔɔ Adiratiki ɔpʋ mʋ sʋ. Kiyiridi saŋa mʋ, ɔkʋlɩ mʋ tɔ kusun awaapʋ mʋ, ɩ waa bamʋ fɛɛ bee nin bee tiri kidenbi.

eng: They dropped a weighted line to take soundings and found the water to be about one hundred twenty feet deep. After proceeding a little farther, we took soundings again and found the water to be about ninety feet deep.
local: Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ ɔfɛ kalasa tʋʋ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba gyan nkyu mʋ sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ba wu yɛ tʋtɔ ɔnyɛ bunkyin gyɛ abaa nswɩɩ tɔ adʋnyɔ. Ba maa bɩla nin ba ansi tɔ gbɛrɛɛ mʋ, ba bɩla tʋʋ ɔfɛ mʋ wu fɛɛ tʋtɔ ɔnyɛ gyɛ abaa nswɩɩ tɔ kudu anu.

eng: Afraid that we might run aground somewhere on the rocks, they hurled out four anchors from the stern and began to pray for daylight.
local: Nɩɩ ɩ waa anɛ kufuu fɛɛ akʋtɔ an biti a naa wɔtɛ afʋlɛ‐buu fɛɛ. Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ ba lɛɛ abɩlɩtɔ ana mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ kɩtaa ɔkʋlɩ ɩɩ yɩlɛ mʋ, tʋʋ nkyu mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ba kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ fɛɛ kayɩ kɩ kɛ bamʋ.

eng: The sailors tried to abandon the ship by lowering the lifeboat into the sea, pretending they were going to lower anchors from the bow.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋlɩ mʋ tɔ kusun awaapʋ mʋ ba maa paa fɛɛ ba sɩlɛ lɩɩ ɔkʋlɩ lala mʋ tɔ. Ɩmʋ sʋ ba lɛɛ ɔkʋlɩ‐pii mʋ gyaga ɔpʋ sʋ, bɛɛ pɩna fɛɛ bee biti ba naa lɛɛ abɩlɩtɔ mʋ akʋ ɔkʋlɩ mu ansi tɔ.

eng: Paul said to the centurion and his soldiers, Unless they stay in the ship, you cant be saved from peril.
local: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ tɔwɛ anaakɔpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ maa anaakɔpʋ mʋ yɛ, fɛɛ nɩɩ anyɩn anɩmʋ ba man kyɩna ɔkʋlɩ lala mʋ tɔ bɩrɛ, fan mɛɛ yɛ fan nyɛ nkpa.

eng: The soldiers then cut the ropes to the lifeboat and let it drift away.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ anaakɔpʋ mʋ ba kɩtaa ɩfɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ sʋ ɔkʋlɩ‐pii mʋ tɔ mʋ tɩŋɛ tɔ. Nɩɩ ba yɛgɛ kɩmʋ tɔ nɩɩ kɩ kyʋn.

eng: Just before daybreak, Paul urged everyone to eat. He said, This is the fourteenth day youve lived in suspense, and youve not had even a bite to eat.
local: Nɩɩ ɩmʋ kɩbwayɩkɛ asɛ mʋ, Pɔɔlɩ tʋtɔɔ bamʋ asɛ yɛ be gyii agyitɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fanɛ nkɛ kudu ana nɩmʋ nɩɩ fan tɛ yɛ kafʋn, yɛgɛ sɛhn mee loo fanɛ nnɔ tɔ, nɩɩ fan tɛ yɛ akʋn.

eng: I urge you to take some food. Your health depends on it. None of you will lose a single hair from his head.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ n kʋlɛ fanɛ nɩn fan kʋsʋ gyii agyitɔ, kɛ fan nyɛ ɔlʋn. Fanɛ tɔ ɔkʋlʋn kinyinibi kʋlʋn gbaa mɛɛ yɛ kɩ fɔyɩ.

eng: After he said these things, he took bread, gave thanks to God in front of them all, then broke it and began to eat.
local: Pɔɔlɩ maa tɔwɛ ɩnɩmʋ kamaa mʋ, ɔ taa bodobodo nɩɩ ɔ taa afwaala sa Ɩbwaarɛ bamʋ ansi tɔ. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔ gya bodobodo mʋ tɔ piili ɛɛ wɛ.

eng: Everyone was encouraged and took some food. .
local: Nɩɩ bamʋ kpini nkɔlɔ n tɩyɛ bamʋ, nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ gyii agyitɔ.

eng: In all, there were two hundred seventy-six of us on the ship.
local: Nɩɩ anɛ mʋ nɩɩ an bʋ ɔkʋlɩ lala mʋ tɔ mʋ, an gyɛ asa alafa anyɔ yɛ adusunɔ yɛ asiye .

eng: When they had eaten as much as they wanted, they lightened the ship by throwing the grain into the sea.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba gyii ŋmɛ mʋ, ba lɛɛ ɩbɔɔta tɔ agyitɔ mʋ tʋʋ ɔpʋ tɔ, kɛ ɔkʋlɩ mʋ ɔ kʋsaa oduu.

eng: In the morning light they saw a bay with a sandy beach. They didnt know what land it was, but they thought they might possibly be able to run the ship aground.
local: Kayɩ maa kɛ mʋ, ba man taalɛ bɩɩ kebunɔ mʋ. Amaa ba wu kidenbi kʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ kɩkyafa. Nɩɩ ba waa bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ nɩɩ bɛɛ taalɛ mʋ, ba taa ɔkʋlɩ lala mʋ naa dan tʋtɔ.

eng: They cut the anchors loose and left them in the sea. At the same time, they untied the ropes that ran back to the rudders. They raised the foresail to catch the wind and made for the beach.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ba tɩŋɛ ɩfɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ sʋ abɩlɩtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ kɩtaa ɔkʋlɩ lala mʋ tɔ ɩ yɩlɛ mʋ waa ɔpʋ mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ opula maŋa tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ba saŋɛ ɩfɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ sʋ iyu mʋ nɩɩ bɛ sʋ bɛ kaapʋ ɔkpa. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba ŋmina kɩtaa lala mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ kɩmʋ yɛ abaala ɔkʋlɩ mʋ kɔnɔ asɛ sa afuu, kɛ afuu mʋ a sʋla bamʋ i kpee kebunɔ.

eng: But they struck a sandbar and the ship ran aground. The bow was stuck and wouldnt move, and the stern was broken into pieces by the force of the waves.
local: Amaa ɔkʋlɩ lala mʋ kasɛ tɔ naa wɔtɛ kɩkyafa. Nɩɩ kɩmʋ ansi tɔ mʋ kɩ man bɩla kɩ taalɛ ki biliŋi. Nɩɩ apaa a daa kɩmʋ kamaa tɔ mʋ gyɛ‐gyɛ tɔ.

eng: The soldiers decided to kill the prisoners to keep them from swimming to shore and escaping.
local: Nɩɩ anaakɔpʋ mʋ ba waa bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ ba mɔɔ obu atiipu mʋ, kɛ ba man nyɛ tɩyɛ nkyu tɔ sɩlɛ.

eng: However, the centurion wanted to save Paul, so he stopped them from carrying out their plan. He ordered those who could swim to jump overboard first and head for land.
local: Amaa anaakɔpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ, biti fɛɛ ɔ mɔlɩgɛ Pɔɔlɩ. Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔ man sa bamʋ ɔkpa kɛ ba waa bamʋ nkɔlɔ biti. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ nɩɩ be nyi nkyu kɩtɩyɛ mʋ yɛ, ba gyankpaa fugi tɩyɛ tɔ kɛ ba tɩyɛ lɩɩ kpee ofuli sʋ.

eng: He ordered the rest to grab hold of planks or debris from the ship. In this way, everyone reached land safely.
local: Nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba san mʋ, ba taa bamʋ n‐yɩɩ bun‐bun itaabo dɩyɛ‐dɩyɛ sʋ. Nɩɩ akʋ mɔɔ ba taa bamʋ n‐yɩɩ bun ɔkʋlɩ iyu boyisa mʋ sʋ. Kanɩn nɩɩ ɩ sʋla ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ naa dan yɛ alanfɩya.

eng: After reaching land safely, we learned that the island was called Malta.
local: A maa fʋʋ kifintin mʋ sʋ yɛ alanfɩya nɩɩ, an bɩɩ fɛɛ bɛɛ tɩɩ kɩmʋ yɛ Malita.

eng: The islanders showed us extraordinary kindness. Because it was rainy and cold, they built a fire and welcomed all of us.
local: Kifintin mʋ sʋ ndɛ abi ba waa anɛ nsa‐nsa kɔɔlɛ anɛ abaa anyɔ. Bʋlayɩ maa da nɩɩ awɔɔ a bʋ tɔ sʋ mʋ, ba dɛɛla fʋlɔn sa anɛ yɛ a wʋlɛ.

eng: Paul gathered a bunch of dry sticks and put them on the fire. As he did, a poisonous snake, driven out by the heat, latched on to his hand.
local: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ naa kʋʋla ngya ɔ ba waa fʋlɔn mʋ tɔ. Maa taa ngya mʋ ɛɛ waa fʋlɔn mʋ tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ fʋlɔn mʋ awulawula ɩ maa wiili kɩnansɛ mʋ, nɩɩ o dun Pɔɔlɩ kɩbaa yɛgɛ ɔ kyɔlɛ sʋ.

eng: When the islanders saw the snake hanging from his hand, they said to each other, This man must be a murderer! He was rescued from the sea, but the goddess Justice hasnt let him live.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ kifintin mʋ sʋ ndɛ abi ba wu kɩnansɛ mʋ maa kyɔlɛ mʋ kɩbaa sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ abaa asɛ yɛ, Ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ biti ɔ baa ɔ gyɛ ɔmɔɔpʋ nɩn. Lɩɩ fɛɛ maa nyɛ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ ɔpʋ mʋ tɔ gbaa mʋ, ɔmʋnɩ ɛɛ ka alibi akɔ mʋ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ yɛgɛ ɔ nyɛ nkpa.

eng: Paul shook the snake into the fire and suffered no harm.
local: Amaa Pɔɔlɩ maa ŋmanyan kɩnansɛ mʋ tʋʋ fʋlɔn mʋ tɔ mʋ, sɛhn man waa mʋ.

eng: They expected him to swell up with fever or suddenly drop dead. After waiting a long time and seeing nothing unusual happen to him, they changed their minds and began to claim that he was a god.
local: Nɩɩ asa mʋ ba fɛ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ Pɔɔlɩ ii biti o puŋa abɛɛ ɔ tɩyɛ opula maŋa tɔ kɛ o wu. Amaa ba maa gyoo naa fʋʋ saŋa kʋ nɩɩ ba wu fɛɛ sɛhn man waa Pɔɔlɩ mʋ, ba kyɛɛgɛ bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ, nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, ɔ gyɛ ɔdakpa kʋ nɩn.

eng: Publius, the islands most prominent person, owned a large estate in that area. He welcomed us warmly into his home as his guests for three days.
local: Kanɩn kifintin maŋa sʋ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Pubilo sʋ kasɛ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ tiri katɩn mʋ nɩɩ an yɩlɛ mʋ. Ɔnyɩn mʋ waa anɛ nsa‐nsa, nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ anɛ mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ, kɩtaa anɛ kɩfɔ nkɛ nsa.

eng: Publius father was bedridden, sick with a fever and dysentery. Paul went to see him and prayed. He placed his hand on him and healed him.
local: Saŋa maa Pubilo mʋ‐sɛ dɛ ɛɛ lɔ kibirima yɛgɛ ɛɛ kyaalɛ. Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ naa loo taa mʋ abaa gyaga mʋ sʋ, kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ, nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ alanfɩya.

eng: Once this happened, the rest of the sick on the island came to him and were healed.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ maa waa kanɩn mʋ, nɩɩ kifintin mʋ sʋ alɔpʋ mʋ kpini ba ba Pɔɔlɩ asɛ nɩɩ ba nyɛ alanfɩya.

eng: They honored us in many ways. When we were getting ready to sail again, they supplied us with what we needed.
local: Lɩɩ kanɩn sʋ ba taa bɛɛrɛɛ sa anɛ ɔkpa kʋmaa nɩmʋ sʋ. Nɩɩ saŋa maa fʋʋ fɛɛ a lɩɩ tʋtɔ mʋ, ba kɛɛ anɛ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ i tiri sa anɛ.

eng: After three months we put out to sea in a ship that had spent the winter at the island. It was an Alexandrian ship with carvings of the twin gods Castor and Pollux as its figurehead.
local: A maa kyɩna tʋtɔ ɩbwaayɩ ɩsa kamaa mʋ, a naa loo ɔkʋlɩ lala mʋ nɩɩ kɩ lɩɩ Alekisanda nɩɩ kɩ ba sii tʋtɔ awɔɔ saŋa mʋ. Ba kyan ɩdakpa nbwaara‐bii mʋ gyaga kɩmʋ sʋ.

eng: We landed in Syracuse where we stayed three days.
local: A maa lɩɩ tʋtɔ mʋ, an naa yɩlɛ Sirakusi kadɛ tɔ, nɩɩ an gyii tʋtɔ nkɛ nsa.

eng: From there we sailed to Rhegium. After one day a south wind came up, and we arrived on the second day in Puteoli.
local: Lɩɩ tʋtɔ mʋ, an naa fʋʋ Regiyo kadɛ tɔ. Lɩɩkaakɛ mʋ afuu a da lɩɩ akpɩlɩgɛ, ɩmʋ sʋ kakɛ nyɔsapʋ mʋ, an naa fʋʋ Putoli kadɛ tɔ.

eng: There we found brothers and sisters who urged us to stay with them for a week. In this way we came to Rome.
local: Tʋtɔ maŋa nɩɩ an wu akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu akʋ, nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ anɛ yɛ a ba kyɩna bamʋ asɛ nkɛ nsunɔ. Falɛ nɩɩ an natɛ naa fʋʋ Roma.

eng: When the brothers and sisters there heard about us, they came as far as the Forum of Appius and the Three Taverns to meet us. When Paul saw them, he gave thanks to God and was encouraged.
local: Akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Roma ba maa nu fɛɛ an ba mʋ, ba kʋsʋ ba gyaŋa anɛ. Akʋ ba natɛ kanɩn ba gyaŋa anɛ Apiya Kɩgya opula, nɩɩ akʋ mɔɔ ba ba gyaŋa anɛ ɔfʋʋtɛkpa asa opula mʋ tɔ. Pɔɔlɩ maa wu bamʋ kanɩn mʋ, ɔ fwaala Ɩbwaarɛ, nɩɩ mʋ kɔkɔlɔ kɩ tɩyɛ mʋ.

eng: When we entered Rome, Paul was permitted to live by himself, with a soldier guarding him.
local: An maa fʋʋ Roma mʋ, ba sa Pɔɔlɩ ɔkpa fɛɛ maa ɔnaakɔpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kɩɩ mʋ sʋ mʋ ba naa kyɩna ntɩn mʋ nɩɩ mʋ Pɔɔlɩ ii biti.

eng: Three days later, Paul called the Jewish leaders together. When they gathered, he said, Brothers, although I have done nothing against our people or the customs of our ancestors, Im a prisoner from Jerusalem. They handed me over to the Romans.
local: Nkɛ nsa kamaa mʋ, Pɔɔlɩ tɩɩ Gyiwu abɩlɩsa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ tʋtɔ mʋ ba sarɛ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Mɛ tɔɔmaa ana Gyiwu abi, amaa n maa man waa anɛ asa alibi akʋ gbaa, nɩɩ man nyita anɛ naana ana nbara mʋ, ba kɩtaa mɛ naa tii Gyerusalem, nɩɩ ba taa mɛ waa Roma abi abaa tɔ.

eng: who intended to release me after they examined me, because they couldnt find any reason for putting me to death.
local: Amaa Roma abi ba maa gyii mɛ asɩn mʋ, ba waa nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ ba yɛgɛ mɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ba man wu alibi mʋ nɩɩ n waa nɩɩ ɩ dagaa ba mɔɔ mɛ.

eng: When the Jews objected, I was forced to appeal to Caesar. Dont think I appealed to Caesar because I had any reason to bring charges against my nation.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Gyiwu abi ba kina fɛɛ ba man yɛgɛ mɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɩ nyan mɛ, nɩɩ n taa asɩn mʋ ba Owura lala Kayisa asɛ. Amaa ɩ man gyɛ fɛɛ n biti n ba tɩɩ mɛ asa ntalɛ nɩn.

eng: This is why I asked to see you and speak with you: its because of the hope of Israel that I am bound with this chain.
local: Falɛ asɩn nɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ n tɩɩ fanɛ, kɛ n lɛɛ asɩn mʋ kasɛ sa fanɛ. Lɩɩ Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi kpini ba sʋ tamaa mʋ sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ndaga ba ŋmina mɛ agbeeleebi.

eng: They responded, We havent received any letters about you from Judea, nor have any of our brothers come and reported or said anything bad about you.
local: Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, An man nyɛ ɔwʋlɛ lɩɩ Gyudeya lɩɩ fʋ sʋ. Nɩɩ anɛ bɩrɩsa ana mʋ nɩɩ ba lɩɩ tʋtɔ mʋ ɔkʋ man ba waa adiboyi‐sɩn, nɩɩ ba man tɔwɛ asɩn libi kʋ lɩɩ fʋ sʋ.

eng: But we think its important to hear what you think, for we know that people everywhere are speaking against this faction.
local: Amaa anɛ Gyiwu abi an biti an wu fʋ nfɛɛrɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ an nyi fɛɛ kʋbʋ mʋ nɩɩ fʋ bʋ kɩmʋ tɔ falɛ, katɩn kʋmaa nɩmʋ asa bɛɛ tɔwɛ bee yii kɩmʋ.

eng: On the day scheduled for this purpose, many people came to the place where he was staying. From morning until evening, he explained and testified concerning Gods kingdom and tried to convince them about Jesus through appealing to the Law from Moses and the Prophets.
local: Gyiwu abi mʋ ba yɩla kakɛ kʋ, kɛ ba maa Pɔɔlɩ ba gyaŋɛ. Kanɩn kakɛ maŋa maa fʋʋ mʋ, bamʋ asa damantɛ ba kpee katɩn mʋ nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ tɛ mʋ. Lɩɩ nyenyenfuu naa fʋʋ kalaatɩpwɛ mʋ, ɔ kpan mʋ n‐yɩɩ tɔwɛ bamʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ asɩn. Ɔ bɔla Mosesi nbara yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ ɩwʋlɛ mʋ sʋ kaapʋ bamʋ lɩɩ Yesu sʋ yɛ, ba kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii.

eng: Some were persuaded by what he said, but others refused to believe.
local: Nɩɩ akʋ ba kɔɔlɛ asɩn mʋ gyii, amaa akʋ mɔɔ ba man kɔɔlɛ gyii.

eng: They disagreed with each other and were starting to leave when Paul made one more statement: The Holy Spirit spoke correctly when he said to your ancestors through Isaiah the prophet.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ tɔwɛ asɩn mʋ laalaalʋwɛ mʋ, asa mʋ ba gyii abaa ɩmɔɔrɛ, nɩɩ bamʋ kpini ba yaasɛ tɔ. Asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ tɔwɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ, Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ bɔla Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Isaya sʋ tɔwɛ kesintin sa fanɛ naana ana yɛ.

eng: Go to this people and say: You will hear, to be sure, but never understand; and you will certainly see but never recognize what you are seeing. .
local: Kpe asa anɩmʋ asɛ naa tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fen biti fan nu asɩn, amaa fan mɛɛ yɛ fan nu kasɛ. Fen biti fan kɩɩ kɛ fan kɩɩ, amaa fɛn mɛɛ yɛ fen wu.

eng: This peoples senses have become calloused, and theyve become hard of hearing, and theyve shut their eyes so that they wont see with their eyes or hear with their ears or understand with their minds, and change their hearts and lives that I may heal them. .
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ asa anɩmʋ nkɔlɔ i budu, nɩɩ bamʋ asʋ a bʋlʋn, nɩɩ ba kɩtaa bamʋ ansi bun sʋ. Nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ kanɩn sʋ mʋ, naafɔɔ bamʋ ansi ii biti i wu, kɛ bamʋ asʋ a nu, kɛ ba bɩya bamʋ nkɔlɔ kasɛ, kɛ ba biliŋi ba mɛ asɛ kɛ n kyɛ bamʋ. .

eng: Therefore, be certain of this: Gods salvation has been sent to the Gentiles. They will listen.
local: Kanɩn sʋ n biti fan bɩɩ fɛɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ taa mʋ kamɔlɩgɛ mʋ naa sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi, nɩɩ bee biti be nu mʋ.

eng: Paul lived in his own rented quarters for two full years and welcomed everyone who came to see him.
local: Pɔɔlɩ kyɩna kɩkpaara mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ nɩɩ ɛɛ ka kɩmʋ sʋ kʋkɔ nsu nnyɔ laalaamuu. Nɩɩ ɛɛ kɔɔlɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ ba ɛɛ bɔɔ mʋ sʋ mʋ.

eng: Unhindered and with complete confidence, he continued to preach Gods kingdom and to teach about the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Ɔ yɩlɛ kɩnyɩn tɔ ɛɛ tɔwɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ asɩn, nɩɩ ɛɛ kaapʋ asɩn lɩɩ Yesu Krisito sʋ, yɛgɛ sɛhn man tii mʋ ɔkpa.

eng: From Paul, a slave of Christ Jesus, called to be an apostle and set apart for Gods good news.
local: Mɛ, Pɔɔlɩ ɩ kyʋrɔɔ ɔwʋlɛ nɩmʋ n sa fanɛ. N gyɛ Yesu Krisito kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ mɛ yɛ n ba waa mu osunpu, nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ mɛ yɩla yɛ n ba tɔwɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ.

eng: God promised this good news about his Son ahead of time through his prophets in the holy scriptures. His Son was descended from David.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ, bɔla mʋ atɔwɛpʋ sʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ asɩn danbɩrasa nɩmʋ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ tɔ.

eng: God promised this good news about his Son ahead of time through his prophets in the holy scriptures. His Son was descended from David.
local: Asɩn mʋ ɩ tɔwɛ ɩ lɩɩ anɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Yesu Krisito sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mu‐bii, nɩɩ ba kʋʋgɛ mʋ nyɩmɩsa lɩɩ Owura Dawidi kɩnaana tɔ.

eng: He was publicly identified as Gods Son with power through his resurrection from the dead, which was based on the Spirit of holiness. This Son is Jesus Christ our Lord.
local: Anɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Yesu Krisito mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ ɔlʋn mʋ kyiŋi mʋ lɩɩ lewu tɔ, lɛɛ mʋ kaapʋ fɛɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mu‐bii mʋ.

eng: Through him we have received Gods grace and our appointment to be apostles. This was to bring all Gentiles to faithful obedience for his names sake.
local: Lɩɩ mʋ yɛ mʋ kɩtɩɩ sʋ Ɩbwaarɛ sa anɛ kabwaala, nɩɩ ɔ waa anɛ Krisito kusun asunpu, kɛ a tɔwɛ kaapʋ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi, kɛ ba nu asɩn mʋ kasɛ kɛ ba kɔɔlɛ Krisito mʋ gyii.

eng: You who are called by Jesus Christ are also included among these Gentiles.
local: Nɩɩ fanɛ gbaa fanɛ fan tii bamʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tɩɩ yɛ ba be biliŋi Yesu Krisito lɛɛ mʋ sʋ.

eng: To those in Rome who are dearly loved by God and called to be Gods people. Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ n kyʋrɔɔ ɔwʋlɛ nɩmʋ n sa fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fan bʋ Roma, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩɩ sʋ kebiti sa, nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ fanɛ yɛ fan ba waa mʋ lɛɛ mʋ. N fwaala fanɛ, nɩɩ n kʋlɛ kabwaala yɛ kayɩɩ‐yuuli mʋ nɩɩ kɩ lɩɩ anɛ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ anɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Yesu Krisito asɛ mʋ kɩ ba fanɛ sʋ.

eng: First of all, I thank my God through Jesus Christ for all of you, because the news about your faithfulness is being spread throughout the whole world.
local: Kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyankpaa mʋ ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ, fanɛ kpini sʋ mʋ, n bɔla Yesu Krisito sʋ n sʋ afwaala n sa mɛ Ɩbwaarɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ asa bɛɛ tɔwɛ fanɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii nɩmʋ asɩn dulinyaa tɔ katɩn kʋmaa.

eng: I serve God in my spirit by preaching the good news about Gods Son, and God is my witness that I continually mention you.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ n taa mɛ kɔkɔlɔ kpini n sun mʋ, nɩɩ n tɔwɛ Mu‐Bii asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ, ɩ gyɛ mi kesintin ogyipu fɛɛ saŋa kʋmaa n nyiŋi fanɛ sʋ, mɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ tɔ.

eng: in all my prayers. Im always asking that somehow, by Gods will, I might succeed in visiting you at last.
local: Nɩɩ n kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ saŋa kʋmaa nɩmʋ fɛɛ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kan kyula mʋ, o bugi ɔkpa sa mɛ nbɩɩnbɩɩ kɛ n ba bɔɔ fanɛ sʋ.

eng: I really want to see you to pass along some spiritual gift to you so that you can be strengthened.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ mi ansi ɩ dɛ fanɛ sʋ fɛɛ n ba wu fanɛ, kɛ n taa Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu tɔ kakɛɛ ba sa fanɛ, kɛ ɩ waa fanɛ ɔlʋn fanɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii tɔ.

eng: What I mean is that we can mutually encourage each other while I am with you. We can be encouraged by the faithfulness we find in each other, both your faithfulness and mine.
local: Ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ a kpan anɛ n‐yɩɩ kɛ a taa kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii mʋ nɩɩ an bʋ kɩmʋ tɔ falɛ kyɛ abaa tɔ.

eng: I want you to know, brothers and sisters, that I planned to visit you many times, although I have been prevented from coming until now. I want to harvest some fruit among you, just as I have done among the other Gentiles.
local: Mɛ bɩrɩsa ana akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu, n biti fan bɩɩ fɛɛ n ŋmina ɩlɩɩ fɩnɛ kpini fɛɛ n ba fanɛ asɛ. Amaa ba lɩɩ halɩɩ ndaga kʋtɔ kʋ kii tii mɛ ɔkpa. N biti kanan mʋ nɩɩ n waa kɛ n nyɛ akʋ lɩɩ fanɛ asɛ tʋtɔ tɔ ba ba kɔɔlɛ Yesu gyii, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ n nyɛ akʋ lɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ tɔ mʋ.

eng: I have a responsibility both to Greeks and to those who dont speak Greek, both to the wise and to the foolish.
local: Nɩɩ ɩ dɛ sa mɛ fɛɛ n tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ sa bamʋ nɩɩ be nyi ɩwʋlɛ yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man nyi ɩwʋlɛ, bamʋ nɩɩ ba gyɛ anyaasɩnpʋ yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba gyɛ amelensipu.

eng: Thats why Im ready to preach the gospel also to you who are in Rome.
local: Kanɩn sʋ nɩɩ mi ansi ɩ payɩ fɛɛ n ba tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ sa fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fan bʋ Roma mʋ gbaa.

eng: Im not ashamed of the gospel: it is Gods own power for salvation to all who have faith in God, to the Jew first and also to the Greek.
local: Ɩ man sʋ mɛ ipeeli lɩɩ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ sʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔlʋn mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ sa ɔsa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ gyii mʋ kamɔlɩgɛ. Gyiwu abi mʋ ɩ gyankpaa kɔɔlɛ gyii, yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ gbaa.

eng: Gods righteousness is being revealed in the gospel, from faithfulness for faith, as it is written, The righteous person will live by faith. .
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ tɔ mʋ, ɩ lɛɛ kaapʋ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ anɛ ɩkpa i kyiigi mu ansi tɔ. Ɩkpa kyiigisa nɩmʋ npiili yɛ ɩmʋ laalaalʋwɛ kpini ɩ yɩlɛ sa kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ yɩla yɛ, Kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii tɔ nɩɩ ɩkpa kyiigisa wuya ii biti ɔ nyɛ nkpa.

eng: Gods wrath is being revealed from heaven against all the ungodly behavior and the injustice of human beings who silence the truth with injustice.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ mʋ agbʋ lɩɩ sʋsʋ kaapʋ asa mʋ nɩɩ bamʋ ɩkpa ɩ man kyiigi yɛ alibi awaapʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba taa bamʋ alibi tii kesintin mʋ ɔkpa.

eng: This is because what is known about God should be plain to them because God made it plain to them.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bɩɩtɛ bamʋ asʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba taalɛ bɩɩ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sʋ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ gbaa‐gbaa ɩ lɛɛ ɩmʋ ifuli kaapʋ bamʋ.

eng: Ever since the creation of the world, Gods invisible qualities—Gods eternal power and divine nature—have been clearly seen, because they are understood through the things God has made. So humans are without excuse.
local: Lɩɩ koo saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tʋʋ dulinyaa mʋ, ɔ bɔla mʋ atɔ tʋʋsa mʋ sʋ lɛɛ mu keduli mʋ nɩɩ a mɛɛ taalɛ taa ansi wu mʋ kaapʋ nyɩmɩsa. Ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ mʋ ɔlʋn mʋ nɩɩ ɩ man sʋ ɔkaa, yɛ mu keduli mʋ nɩɩ kɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kesintin mʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ man kɔɔlɛ gyii mʋ, man sʋ kɔnɔ ɔ lɛɛ.

eng: Although they knew God, they didnt honor God as God or thank him. Instead, their reasoning became pointless, and their foolish hearts were darkened.
local: Ba nyi Ɩbwaarɛ maa bʋ tɔ, amaa ba man sʋ bʋnyaa abɛɛ afwaala bɛɛ sa mʋ. Amaa nfɛɛrɛ danbɩrasa kʋ ɩ man bɩla ɩ bʋ bamʋ tɔ, nɩɩ bamʋ nkɔlɔ tɔ i biliŋi kitentenbiri.

eng: While they were claiming to be wise, they made fools of themselves.
local: Ba yɛ bamʋ ɩ gyɛ anyaasɩnpʋ, amaa ba gyɛ amelensipu nɩn.

eng: They exchanged the glory of the immortal God for images that look like mortal humans: birds, animals, and reptiles.
local: Nɩɩ ba biliŋaa Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ tɔ kakpaa mʋ bʋnyaa ba sʋ bɛɛ sa atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba pɔyɩ ɩmʋ fɛɛ anyɩmɩsa, nbwiibi yɛ atɔbwaaya yɛ atɔbwaaya kpɩɩsa.

eng: So God abandoned them to their hearts desires, which led to the moral corruption of degrading their own bodies with each other.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ mu ansi yɛgɛ bamʋ, nɩɩ ba taa kɩgyagaa bɛɛ waa bamʋ nkɔlɔ biti, nɩɩ bɛɛ waa kɩgyagaa libi maŋa bɛɛ ŋaabaa abaa.

eng: They traded Gods truth for a lie, and they worshipped and served the creation instead of the creator, who is blessed forever. Amen.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ba taa Ɩbwaarɛ kesintin mʋ kyɛrɛ antɩŋɛsa, nɩɩ bee sun atɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tʋʋ mʋ, lan yɛgɛ mʋ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tʋʋ ɩmʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ a yɩn mʋ saŋa kʋmaa mʋ. Amen!.

eng: Thats why God abandoned them to degrading lust. Their females traded natural sexual relations for unnatural sexual relations.
local: Lɩɩ ɩnɩmʋ sʋ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ mu ansi yɛgɛ bamʋ sa ba maa bamʋ kɩgyagaa ipeeli, akyɩɩ ba yɛgɛ anyɩn asɛ kadɛ nɩɩ ba sii bɛɛ dɛ yɛ bamʋ bɩrɩsa ana akyɩɩ yɛgɛ ɩ man gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔkpa sʋ.

eng: Also, in the same way, the males traded natural sexual relations with females, and burned with lust for each other. Males performed shameful actions with males, and they were paid back with the penalty they deserved for their mistake in their own bodies.
local: Kanɩn ɔkpa maŋa sʋ nɩɩ anyɩn gbaa ba biti ba yɛgɛ kadɛ yɛ akyɩɩ, nɩɩ kɩdɛ yɛ bamʋ bɩrɩsa ana anyɩn ɩ sʋ bamʋ ɔbʋlɛ, nɩɩ bɛɛ waa apeeli‐sɩn bɛ dɛ yɛ abaa, nɩɩ ba nyɛ kɩgyagaa mʋ kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ lɩɩ bamʋ gbaa‐gbaa bamʋ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ tɔ.

eng: Since they didnt think it was worthwhile to acknowledge God, God abandoned them to a defective mind to do inappropriate things.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba kina Ɩbwaarɛ kɩbɩɩ lɩɩ bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ tɔ mʋ, ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛgɛ bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ bɔrɔbɔrɔ sa bamʋ, kɛ ba waa atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ man dagaa yɛ ba waa.

eng: So they were filled with all injustice, wicked behavior, greed, and evil behavior. They are full of jealousy, murder, fighting, deception, and malice. They are gossips.
local: Nɩɩ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ba kyaga sʋ bɛɛ waa asun libi, kensi payɩ libi, kikisi libi, kɩmɔɔ, kamɔɔrɛ‐gyii, npɩna, kɩkɩɩ ansi waa asɩn libi yɛ asa kukuli.

eng: they slander people, and they hate God. They are rude and proud, and they brag. They invent ways to be evil, and they are disobedient to their parents.
local: kɩtɩn antɩŋɛsa gyaga asa sʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ akisipu, asa kɩsaalɛ, n‐yɩɩ kɩkaapʋ, alala‐sɩn, ɔkpa pʋpwɛ sʋ kɩtaa waa alibi, bɛɛ waa kʋsʋ tɔ ɔlʋn bɛɛ sa bamʋ‐nyi ana yɛ bamʋ‐sɛ ana.

eng: They are without understanding, disloyal, without affection, and without mercy.
local: Ba man sʋ nfɛɛrɛ, ba man sʋ kesintin, ba man sʋ kebiti sa asa, be mee su asa sʋ.

eng: Though they know Gods decision that those who persist in such practices deserve death, they not only keep doing these things but also approve others who practice them.
local: Yɛgɛ be nyi fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ nbara mʋ ɩɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ waa falɛ alibi mʋ, dagaa lewu gbaa mʋ, amaa man gyɛ falɛ atɔ nɩmʋ nkʋn nɩɩ bɛɛ waa, ba bɩla bɛɛ tɔwɛ bɛɛ yɩlɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa kanɩn mʋ kamaa.

eng: So every single one of you who judge others is without any excuse. You condemn yourself when you judge another person because the one who is judging is doing the same things.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ fʋ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fii bun fʋ bɩrɩsa kʋpwɛ mʋ, fʋ man sʋ kɔnɔ fʋ lɛɛ. Nɩɩ fʋ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fu bun fʋ bɩrɩsa kʋpwɛ mʋ, fu bun fʋ n‐yɩɩ gbaa kʋpwɛ nɩmʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ngbaa fʋ fɩɩ waa alibi kʋlʋn maŋa.

eng: We know that Gods judgment agrees with the truth, and his judgment is against those who do these kinds of things.
local: Amaa an nyi fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kʋpwɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ sa bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa kanɩn atɔ libi mʋ, ɩ dɛ kesintin ɔkpa sʋ.

eng: If you judge those who do these kinds of things while you do the same things yourself, think about this: Do you believe that you will escape Gods judgment?.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ fɛɛ fʋ nyɩmɩsa, fʋ kan fii bun fʋ bɩrɩsa ana kʋpwɛ, yɛgɛ ngbaa fʋ fɩɩ waa alibi kʋlʋn maŋa mʋ, fɩɩ fɛ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ fɩɩ taalɛ sɩlɛ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kʋpwɛ kibun mʋ tɔ abɛɛ?.

eng: Or do you have contempt for the riches of Gods generosity, tolerance, and patience? Dont you realize that Gods kindness is supposed to lead you to change your heart and life?.
local: Abɛɛ fu bun ansi lɩɩ kɩdanbɩrasa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ waa ɛɛ sa fʋ fɛɛ maa nyɛ kɔkɔlɔ ɛɛ sa fʋ yɛ maa sʋ kenyiita sa fʋ? Fu man nyi fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ waa adanbɩrasa anɩmʋ kpini ɛɛ sa fʋ kɛ ɩ kyɛ fʋ tɔ kɛ fʋ yɛgɛ alibi kɩwaa?.

eng: You are storing up wrath for yourself because of your stubbornness and your heart that refuses to change. Gods just judgment will be revealed on the day of wrath.
local: Amaa lɩɩ fʋ kʋsʋ tɔ ɔlʋn yɛ alibi kɩ mɛɛ yɛgɛ kɩwaa sʋ mʋ, fʋ kʋʋla kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ sa fʋ n‐yɩɩ fu gyoo Ɩbwaarɛ agbʋ lala mʋ kakɛ mʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ lɛɛ kaapʋ alibi awaapʋ mʋ kesintin katalɛ mʋ tɔ.

eng: God will repay everyone based on their works .
local: Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ ka ɔsa kʋmaa nɩmʋ kʋkɔ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa mʋ lɩɩ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa mʋ sʋ.

eng: On the one hand, he will give eternal life to those who look for glory, honor, and immortality based on their patient good work.
local: Nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ kenyiita bɛɛ waa kɩdanbɩrasa, bee biti Ɩbwaarɛ bʋnyaa yɛ bɛɛrɛɛ yɛ nkpa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ man sʋ ɔkaa mʋ, bamʋ ii biti ba nyɛ nkpa kakpaa.

eng: But on the other hand, there will be wrath and anger for those who obey wickedness instead of the truth because they are acting out of selfishness and disobedience.
local: Nɩɩ asa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ kɩɩ bamʋ nkʋn lɛɛ, nɩɩ ba kina kesintin asɩn mʋ kunu nɩɩ bɛɛ waa asun libi mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ agbʋ ii biti ɩ ba bamʋ sʋ kɛ ɔ man su bamʋ kʋwɛɛ.

eng: There will be trouble and distress for every human being who does evil, for the Jew first and also for the Greek.
local: Nɩɩ awʋrʋfɔ yɛ kayɩyɛɛ ii biti ɩ ba asa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa alibi mʋ kpini sʋ. Ii biti ɩ gyankpaa ba Gyiwu abi sʋ, yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ gbaa sʋ.

eng: But there will be glory, honor, and peace for everyone who does what is good, for the Jew first and also for the Greek.
local: Amaa bʋnyaa yɛ bɛɛrɛɛ yɛ kayɩɩ yuuli ii biti ɩ ba sa asa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa adanbɩrasa mʋ kpini, nɩɩ Gyiwu abi mʋ ii biti ba gyankpaa yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ gbaa.

eng: God does not have favorites.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ man sʋ nmɛɛgɛ tɔ.

eng: Those who have sinned outside the Law will also die outside the Law, and those who have sinned under the Law will be judged by the Law.
local: Bamʋ nɩɩ ba man nyi Mosesi nbara, nɩɩ ba waa alibi mʋ, bee biti ba wu, nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ yɛ ba maa kina Mosesi nbara mʋ kibuu sʋ. Nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ be nyi Mosesi nbara mʋ, nɩɩ ba lɩɩ ɩmʋ tɔ waa alibi mʋ, kanɩn nbara maŋa nɩɩ bee biti ba taa bun bamʋ kʋpwɛ.

eng: It isnt the ones who hear the Law who are righteous in Gods eyes. It is the ones who do what the Law says who will be treated as righteous.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ man gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ be nyi Mosesi nbara mʋ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩɩ kɩɩ fɛɛ ba man gyii kʋpwɛ, amaa bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa nbara mʋ awaasa mʋ, bamʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii wu fɛɛ bamʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi.

eng: Gentiles dont have the Law. But when they instinctively do what the Law requires they are a Law in themselves, though they dont have the Law.
local: Nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi nɩɩ ba man sʋ Mosesi nbara, amaa bɛɛ waa atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ kyɩna nbara mʋ sʋ mʋ, ba man sʋ nbara mʋ bɩrɛ, amaa nbara kesintin ɩ bʋ bamʋ tɔ.

eng: They show the proof of the Law written on their hearts, and their consciences affirm it. Their conflicting thoughts will accuse them, or even make a defense for them.
local: Ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ bamʋ awaasa ɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ bamʋ nkɔlɔ tɔ mʋ, be nyi nbara mʋ. Nɩɩ bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ tɔ gbaa i gyii bamʋ kesintin, lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ tɔ ɩ kaapʋ yɛ ɩnɩmʋ ɩ bʋrɔn nɩɩ ɩnɩmʋ falɛ ɩ man bʋrɔn.

eng: on the day when, according to my gospel, God will judge the hidden truth about human beings through Christ Jesus.
local: Nɩɩ kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ ii biti ɩ ba waa kakɛ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ bɔla Yesu Krisito sʋ taa nyɩmɩsa asiiri tɔ awaasa gyii bamʋ asɩn, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ mʋ ɩ kaapʋ mʋ.

eng: But, if you call yourself a Jew; if you rely on the Law; if you brag about your relationship to God.
local: Nɩɩ fɩɩ tɔwɛ yɛ fʋ gyɛ Gyiwu nyɩmɩsa, nɩɩ fʋ taa fʋ n‐yɩɩ tʋʋ Mosesi nbara mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ fɩɩ kaapʋ fʋ n‐yɩɩ fɛɛ fʋ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ.

eng: if you know the will of God; if you are taught by the Law so that you can figure out the things that really matter.
local: Nɩɩ fɩɩ tɔwɛ yɛ fu nyi Ɩbwaarɛ kebiti, lɩɩ fɛɛ fʋ bɩɩla Mosesi nbara mʋ, nɩɩ fu nyi atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋrɔn.

eng: if you have persuaded yourself that you are: a guide for the blind; a light to those who are in darkness.
local: Nɩɩ fɩɩ tɔwɛ yɛ fʋ ɩ gyɛ agyaatanapʋ ɩkpa ɔkaapʋpʋ, nɩɩ fʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kaapʋ bamʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ kitentenbiri tɔ mʋ fɩtɩla.

eng: an educator of the foolish; a teacher of infants.
local: Nɩɩ fɩɩ tɔwɛ yɛ fʋ ɩ gyɛ amelensipu ɔtɔwɛwaapʋ, yɛ nbii wuribi ɔkaapʋpʋ. Nɩɩ fu nyi Mosesi nbara mʋ, nɩɩ ɩmʋ tɔ nɩɩ kinyi yɛ kesintin mʋ bʋ.

eng: then why dont you who are teaching others teach yourself? If you preach, No stealing, do you steal?.
local: Nɩɩ fɩɩ tɔwɛ yɛ fɩɩ kaapʋ fʋ bɩrɩsa ana asansa, nɩɩ mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fɩɩ mɛɛ kaapʋ fʋ n‐yɩɩ? Fʋ tɔwɛ abwaarɛsɩn yɛ, keyu man bʋrɔn, yɛgɛ fɔɔ fʋ fii yuuri.

eng: If you say, No adultery, do you commit adultery? If you hate idols, do you rob temples?.
local: Nɩɩ fɩɩ tɔwɛ yɛ asa ba man lɩɩ bamʋ‐ka ana abɛɛ bamʋ‐kuli ana kamaa mʋ, nɩɩ fɔɔ fʋ fɩɩ mɛɛ lɩɩ fʋ lɛɛ kamaa? Fii kisi ɩdakpa kusun, yɛgɛ fɔɔ fʋ fii yuuri ɩdakpa atɔ fɩɩ lɩɩ ɩdakpa isunkpa ibu to.

eng: If you brag about the Law, do you shame God by breaking the Law?.
local: Fʋ mʋ nɩɩ fɩɩ kaapʋ fʋ n‐yɩɩ yɛ fii nyi Mosesi nbara mʋ, man gyɛ fʋ i gyila Ɩbwaarɛ ipeeli fɛɛ fʋ maa nyita nbara mʋ?.

eng: As it is written: The name of God is discredited by the Gentiles because of you .
local: Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ ɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, Lɩɩ fanɛ Gyiwu abi sʋ mʋ, bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ bɛɛ tɔwɛ asɩn bee nyita Ɩbwaarɛ kɩtɩɩ.

eng: Circumcision is an advantage if you do what the Law says. But if you are a person who breaks the Law, your status of being circumcised has changed into not being circumcised.
local: Fɛɛ nɩɩ fɛn kan buu Mosesi nbara mʋ, kanɩn bɩrɛ fu oduu katɩn mʋ sʋ tɔnɔ. Amaa nɩɩ fʋ tɩn fu oduu nɩɩ fu mee buu nbara mʋ bɩrɛ, i du nɩn fɛɛ fʋ maa man tɩn oduu mʋ.

eng: So if the person who isnt circumcised keeps the Law, wont his status of not being circumcised be counted as if he were circumcised?.
local: Nɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ man tɩn oduu nɩɩ ɛɛ waa kʋtɔ kʋmaa kyɛɛkyɛɛ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Mosesi nbara mʋ ɩ kaapʋ mʋ, mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ taa mʋ fɛɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɩn oduu?.

eng: The one who isnt physically circumcised but keeps the Law will judge you. You became a lawbreaker after you had the written Law and circumcision.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔ man tɩn mu oduu, yɛgɛ ɛɛ waa kʋtɔ kʋmaa kyɛɛkyɛɛ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Mosesi nbara mʋ ɩɩ kaapʋ mʋ, ee biti o bun fʋ mʋ nɩɩ fʋ gyɛ Gyiwu obii, nɩɩ fʋ tɩn fu oduu, nɩɩ fu nyita nbara mʋ kʋpwɛ.

eng: It isnt the Jew who maintains outward appearances who will receive praise from God, and it isnt people who are outwardly circumcised on their bodies.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɩ man gyɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ tɔ atɔ awaasa ɩ waa ɔkʋ Gyiwu obii gbaa‐gbaa, nɩɩ oduu katɩn mʋ, man gyɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ tɔ atɔ waasa nɩn.

eng: Instead, it is the person who is a Jew inside, who is circumcised in spirit, not literally. That persons praise doesnt come from people but from God.
local: Amaa ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ Gyiwu obii kesintin mʋ, mʋ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ ɩ kaapʋ, nɩɩ oduu katɩn mʋ ɩ gyɛ kɔkɔlɔ awaasa mʋ nɩɩ Kayaayu mʋ ɩɩ waa, man gyɛ Mosesi nbara mʋ ɩɩ waa. Kanɩn ɔsa maŋa ɩɩ nyɛ kɩyɩn lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ man gyɛ nyɩmɩsa asɛ.

eng: So whats the advantage of being a Jew? Or whats the benefit of circumcision?.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan tɔwɛ yɛ ɔ gyɛ Gyiwu obii mʋ, mɩnɛ tɔnɔ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ lɩɩ tɔ? Abɛɛ mɩnɛ tɔnɔ ɩ bʋ oduu katɩn tɔ?.

eng: Plenty in every way. First of all, the Jews were trusted with Gods revelations.
local: Ɔkpa kʋmaa sʋ tɔnɔ bʋ tɔ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ tɔnɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyankpaa ɩ gyɛ yɛ, Gyiwu abi mʋ abaa tɔ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ taa mʋ kɔnɔ tɔ asɩn mʋ waa.

eng: What does it matter, then, if some werent faithful? Their lack of faith wont cancel Gods faithfulness, will it?.
local: Nɩɩ ɩ kan ba fɛɛ akʋ ba man sʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii mʋ, bamʋ kɩman kɔɔlɛ gyii mʋ, ɩ taalɛ yɛgɛ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ man waa kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɩla mʋ abɛɛ?.

eng: Absolutely not! God must be true, even if every human being is a liar, as it is written: So that it can show that you are right in your words; and you will triumph when you are judged. .
local: Ayee! Ɩ mɛɛ ba kanɩn. Amaa nɩɩ nyɩmɩsa kʋmaa gyɛ antɩŋɛsa wuya mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ bɩrɛ san ɔ gyɛ mʋ kesintin wuya, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ yɛ, Ɩ dagaa fɛɛ fʋ Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn tɔwɛsa ɩ maa lɛɛ fu kesintin kaapʋ, nɩɩ ba kan pwɩɩ fʋ mʋ kɛ fu gyii asʋ.

eng: But if our lack of righteousness confirms Gods justice, what will we say? That God, who brings wrath upon us, isnt just ?.
local: A taa fɛɛ anɛ awaasa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ man bʋrɔn, nɩɩ ɩ lɛɛ kaapʋ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ awaasa ɩ dɛ ɔkpa mʋ, ɩmɛɛ mʋ bɩrɛ, nɩɩ ɔ bɩɩtɛ anɛ kʋsʋ mʋ, an biti a tɔwɛ yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ man waa asɩn mʋ kyɛɛkyɛɛ? .

eng: Absolutely not! If God werent just, how could he judge the world?.
local: Ayee! Nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ kanɩn mʋ, nɛnɛ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ waa kɛ ɔ talɛ dulinyaa?.

eng: But if Gods truth is demonstrated by my lie and it increases his glory, why am I still judged as a sinner?.
local: Ɔkʋ ii biti o gyii ɩmɔɔrɛ yɛ, fɛɛ nɩɩ mɛ antɩŋɛsa ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kesintin kɩ lɔŋɔ lɩɩ ifuli mʋ, nɩɩ mʋ bʋnyaa mʋ tii sʋ mʋ, mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ ee bun mɛ kʋpwɛ fɛɛ n gyɛ alibi ɔwaapʋ?.

eng: Why not say, Lets do evil things so that good things will come out of it?.
local: Lɩɩ falɛ asɩn sʋ mʋ, akʋ bee biti ba tɔwɛ yɛ, Fan yɛgɛ a waa alibi, kɛ asa ba wu Ɩbwaarɛ adanbɩrasa. Asa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɔwɛ falɛ asɩn nɩmʋ bɛɛ saalɛ mɛ yɛ mɛ gbaa mɛ n tɔwɛ kanɩn mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti o bun bamʋ kʋpwɛ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa.

eng: So what are we saying? Are we better off? Not at all. We have already stated the charge: both Jews and Greeks are all under the power of sin.
local: Nɛnɛ nɩɩ an biti a tɔwɛ ɩnɩmʋ? Anɛ Gyiwu abi an gyɛ asa kparɛ an kyɔ bamʋ nɩɩ ba san mʋ abɛɛ? Ayee! N tɛ wʋla tɔwɛ fɛɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ kpini alibi i gyii bamʋ sʋ ɔlʋn.

eng: As it is written, There is no righteous person, not even one. .
local: Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ ɩ tɔwɛ katɩn kʋ yɛ, Ɔkʋ man bʋ tɔ nɩɩ mʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi, halɩɩ ɔkʋlʋn gbaa.

eng: There is no one who understands. There is no one who looks for God. .
local: Ɔkʋ man bʋ tɔ nɩɩ ee nu Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn kasɛ, nɩɩ ɔkʋ man bʋ tɔ ɔ naa ee biti Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: They all turned away. They have become worthless together. There is no one who shows kindness. There is not even one. .
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ kpeli mu ansi lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sʋ, nɩɩ bamʋ kpini ba man sʋ kyaarɛ, nɩɩ ɔkʋ man bʋ tɔ nɩɩ ɛɛ waa kɩdanbɩrasa halɩɩ ɔkʋlʋn gbaa.

eng: Their throat is a grave that has been opened. They are deceitful with their tongues, and the poison of vipers is under their lips. .
local: Bamʋ nnɔ tɔ asɩn tɔwɛsa ɩ mɔɔ fɛɛ lewu. Antɩŋɛsa nkʋn ɩ bɔla bamʋ nnandʋlʋ sʋ. Bamʋ nnɔ tɔ asɩn i du fɛɛ kɩnansɛ kʋbwɛ.

eng: Their mouths are full of cursing and bitterness. .
local: Bɛɛ tɔwɛ asa nnɔ, nɩɩ bamʋ nnɔ tɔ asɩn tɔwɛsa ɩ bʋlɩlɩ.

eng: Their feet are quick to shed blood; .
local: Nyɩmɩsa kɩmɔɔ man bʋ bamʋ ɔsɩn.

eng: destruction and misery are in their ways; .
local: Katɩn kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ba kpee mʋ ba yɛgɛ nɩɩ asa bee gyii gyerebi nɩɩ bee wu awʋrʋfɔ.

eng: and they dont know the way of peace. .
local: Nɩɩ ba mɛɛ yɛgɛ nɩɩ kayɩɩ yuuli kɛ ba asa tɔ.

eng: There is no fear of God in their view of the world. .
local: Nɩɩ ba man sɩlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kpini‐kpini.

eng: Now we know that whatever the Law says, it speaks to those who are under the Law, in order to shut every mouth and make it so the whole world has to answer to God.
local: To, an nyi fɛɛ asɩn kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ nbara mʋ ɩɩ kaapʋ mʋ, ɩɩ tɔwɛ ɩɩ sa bamʋ nɩɩ bee buu nbara mʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔkʋ mɛɛ nyɛ tɔwɛ sɛhn yɛ mɛɛ mʋ ɔ man waa alibi akʋ. Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ dulinyaa tɔ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti o bun mʋ kʋpwɛ lɩɩ mu alibi sʋ.

eng: It follows that no human being will be treated as righteous in his presence by doing what the Law says, because the knowledge of sin comes through the Law.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋ mɛɛ taalɛ buu nbara mʋ lʋwɛ, kɛ Ɩbwaarɛ o wu fɛɛ mʋ awaasa ɩ bʋrɔn mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ. Nbara mʋ ɩ dɛ sa nɩn kɛ a wu anɛ alibi.

eng: But now Gods righteousness has been revealed apart from the Law, which is confirmed by the Law and the Prophets.
local: Amaa nbɩɩnbɩɩ bɩrɛ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ nyɩmɩsa ɔ bɔla sʋ kɛ mʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi mʋ asɛ mʋ kaapʋ, nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ nbara kibuu. Halɩɩ ɩmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ yɛ nbara mʋ kpini i gyii ɩmʋ kesintin Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ.

eng: Gods righteousness comes through the faithfulness of Jesus Christ for all who have faith in him. Theres no distinction.
local: Ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ bɔla sʋ, kɛ o wu fɛɛ asa ɩkpa ii kyiigi mʋ asɛ mʋ, ɩ gyɛ ba maa kɔɔlɛ Yesu Krisito gyii. Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ lɛɛ man kwɩɩ.

eng: All have sinned and fall short of Gods glory.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ waa alibi nɩɩ ɔ pan Ɩbwaarɛ bʋnyaa mʋ.

eng: but all are treated as righteous freely by his grace because of a ransom that was paid by Christ Jesus.
local: Lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabwaala kakɛɛ sʋ mʋ, ɔ bɔla Yesu Krisito kɩkɔɔlɛ asa yɛgɛ mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ o kyula sa bamʋ fɛɛ bamʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi mʋ asɛ.

eng: Through his faithfulness, God displayed Jesus as the place of sacrifice where mercy is found by means of his blood. He did this to demonstrate his righteousness in passing over sins that happened before.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ Yesu Krisito yɛ o be wu lɩɩ asa alibi sʋ. Mʋ nkalan kɩkyɛɛgɛ wʋrɩgɛ sʋ mʋ, asa mʋ nɩɩ bee biti ba kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii mʋ, kɛ ba maa Ɩbwaarɛ nsana ɩ lɔŋɔ. Nɩɩ kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ bɩɩtɛ anɛ mʋ, Yesu yɩlɛ anɛ ayaa tɔ kɔɔlɛ ɩmʋ. Ɔ waa ɩnɩmʋ nɩn kɛ ɔ lɛɛ mʋ kesintin kaapʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba waa alibi dɩdaa mʋ, ɔ man bɩɩtɛ bamʋ asʋ.

eng: during the time of Gods patient tolerance. He also did this to demonstrate that he is righteous in the present time, and to treat the one who has faith in Jesus as righteous.
local: Ɔ nyɛ kenyiita gyoo ba fʋʋ Yesu Krisito kelewu mʋ nɩɩ o wu sa alibi mʋ. Ɔ bɩla lɛɛ kaapʋ fɛɛ mʋ awaasa ɩ dɛ ɔkpa, nɩɩ ɛɛ yɛgɛ nɩɩ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ Yesu gyii mʋ bamʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi mʋ asɛ.

eng: What happens to our bragging? Its thrown out. With which law? With what we have accomplished under the Law?.
local: Mɩnɛ kʋtɔ sʋ nɩɩ an kaapʋ anɛ n‐yɩɩ? Sɛhn man bʋ tɔ. Mɩnɛ ɩ waa sʋ nɩɩ a mɛɛ taalɛ kaapʋ anɛ n‐yɩɩ? Lɩɩ fɛɛ man gyɛ nbara mʋ kibuu ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ i wu fɛɛ nyɩmɩsa ɩkpa i kyiigi, amaa ɩ lɩɩ anɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii mʋ sʋ.

eng: No, not at all, but through the law of faith. We consider that a person is treated as righteous by faith, apart from what is accomplished under the Law.
local: Kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ an nu kasɛ ɩ gyɛ yɛ nyɩmɩsa kɩkɔɔlɛ Yesu Krisito gyii sʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ i wu fɛɛ mʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi mʋ asɛ, amaa man gyɛ nbara mʋ kibuu sʋ.

eng: Or is God the God of Jews only? Isnt God the God of Gentiles also? Yes, God is also the God of Gentiles.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ gyɛ Gyiwu abi nkʋn Ɩbwaarɛ nɩn? Ɔ man gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi gbaa Ɩbwaarɛ nɩn abɛɛ? Ɛɛhn! Ɔ gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi gbaa Ɩbwaarɛ nɩn.

eng: Since God is one, then the one who makes the circumcised righteous by faith will also make the one who isnt circumcised righteous through faith.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ gyɛ ɔkʋlʋn nɩn, ɩmʋ sʋ lɩɩ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ nɩɩ ee biti o wu Gyiwu abi mʋ fɛɛ bamʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi, nɩɩ lɩɩ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ nɩɩ ee biti o wu bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi gbaa mʋ fɛɛ bamʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi mʋ asɛ.

eng: Do we then cancel the Law through this faith? Absolutely not! Instead, we confirm the Law.
local: Kanɩn sʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii kɩnɩmʋ kɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ nbara mʋ i biliŋi kɩyan abɛɛ? Ayee! Ɩ man dɛ kanɩn. Amaa kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ an buu nbara mʋ sʋ kyɛɛkyɛɛ.

eng: So what are we going to say? Are we going to find that Abraham is our ancestor on the basis of genealogy?.
local: Mɩnɛ asɩn nɩɩ an biti a tɔwɛ lɩɩ anɛ naana Abraham sʋ? Mɩnɛ ɩ waa Abraham asɩn mʋ tɔ?.

eng: Because if Abraham was made righteous because of his actions, he would have had a reason to brag, but not in front of God.
local: Nɩɩ ɩ kan ɩ gyɛ Abraham awaasa sʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wu mʋ fɛɛ mʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi mʋ, naafɔɔ ɔ sʋ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa kaapʋ mʋ n‐yɩɩ, amaa man gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ansi tɔ.

eng: What does the scripture say? Abraham had faith in God, and it was credited to him as righteousness .
local: Mɩnɛ nɩɩ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ ɩ tɔwɛ? Abraham kɔɔlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gyii, lɩɩ mʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii maŋa sʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kyula yɛ mʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi.

eng: Workers salaries arent credited to them on the basis of an employers grace but rather on the basis of what they deserve.
local: Nɩɩ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ee sun kusun mʋ bɛɛ ka mʋ kʋkɔ nɩn, mʋ kʋkɔka mʋ kɩ man gyɛ kakɛɛ nɩn sa mʋ, amaa ɩ gyɛ mu kusun mʋ kʋkɔ nɩɩ bɛɛ ka mʋ.

eng: But faith is credited as righteousness to those who dont work, because they have faith in God who makes the ungodly righteous.
local: Nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ ɔkʋ kusun mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ waa sʋ, amaa maa kɔɔlɛ gyii fɛɛ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ alibi ɔwaapʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ Yesu gyii mʋ bamʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi mʋ, bamʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii mʋ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ kɔɔlɛ bamʋ kyula fɛɛ bamʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi.

eng: In the same way, David also pronounces a blessing on the person to whom God credits righteousness apart from actions.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ nɩɩ Dawidi gbaa wʋla tɔwɛ yɛ kumu ɔkɔn wuya ɩ gyɛ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kyula fɛɛ mʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ mʋ awaasa sʋ. Nɩɩ Dawidi tɔwɛ yɛ.

eng: Happy are those whose actions outside the Law are forgiven, and whose sins are covered. .
local: Kumu ɔkɔn wuya ana ɩ gyɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ taa bamʋ alibi kɛɛ bamʋ, nɩɩ ɔ fwɩɩ bamʋ alibi lɩɩ bamʋ sʋ.

eng: Happy are those whose sin isnt counted against them by the Lord .
local: Kumu ɔkɔn wuya ɩ gyɛ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ man bɩla yɛ o nyiŋi mu alibi sʋ.

eng: Is this state of happiness only for the circumcised or is it also for those who arent circumcised? We say, Faith was credited to Abraham as righteousness.
local: Kumu ɔkɔn mʋ nɩɩ Dawidi tɔwɛ mʋ asɩn mʋ, ɩ gyɛ sa Gyiwu abi nkʋn nɩn, abɛɛ yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi gbaa? Nɩɩ an wʋla tɔwɛ yɛ, Abraham kɔɔlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gyii, nɩɩ mʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii maŋa sʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kyula yɛ mʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi.

eng: So how was it credited? When he was circumcised, or when he wasnt circumcised? In fact, it was credited while he still wasnt circumcised, not after he was circumcised.
local: Saŋa mɔɔ tɔ nɩɩ ɩnɩmʋ ɩ waa sa mʋ? Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɩn oduu mʋ abɛɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man pɩɩta tɩn oduu mʋ? Ɩ gyɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man pɩɩta tɩn.

eng: He received the sign of circumcision as a seal of the righteousness that comes from the faith he had while he still wasnt circumcised. It happened this way so that Abraham could be the ancestor of all those people who arent circumcised, who have faith in God, and so are counted as righteous.
local: Abraham oduu katɩn mʋ gyɛ sɩnkaala nɩn, nɩɩ ɩ kaapʋ tɔ fɛɛ mʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ nɩɩ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man pɩɩta tɩn mu oduu mʋ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kyula yɛ mʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi. Kanɩn sʋ mʋ ɩ gyɛ asa kpini mʋ nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ gyii nɩɩ ba man pɩɩta tɩn bamʋ iduu mʋ kpini bamʋ‐sɛ, nɩɩ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kyula yɛ bamʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi.

eng: He could also be the ancestor of those circumcised people, who arent only circumcised but who also walk in the path of faith, like our ancestor Abraham did while he wasnt circumcised.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ bɩla ɔ gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba tɩn iduu mʋ bamʋ‐sɛ, man gyɛ ba maa tɩn iduu sʋ, amaa lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ gbaa bamʋ, ba maa naa kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii ɔkpa kʋlʋn mʋ nɩɩ anɛ naana Abraham natɛ sʋ pɔyɩ nɩɩ ɔ tɩn oduu mʋ.

eng: The promise to Abraham and to his descendants, that he would inherit the world, didnt come through the Law but through the righteousness that comes from faith.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ sa Abraham maa mʋ anaana‐bii yɛ bee biti ba baa ba gyɛ dulinyaa tɔ kapatɩyɛ agyipu. Falɛ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ kɩda mʋ man gyɛ nbara mʋ kibuu sʋ, amaa lɩɩ mʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kyula yɛ mʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi mʋ asɛ.

eng: If they inherit because of the Law, then faith has no effect and the promise has been canceled.
local: Nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba kan bee buu nbara mʋ, ɩ gyɛ dulinyaa kapatɩyɛ agyipu bɩrɛ, kanɩn bɩrɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii mʋ kɩ gyɛ kɩyan, nɩɩ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ kɩda mʋ man sʋ kyaarɛ.

eng: The Law brings about wrath. But when there isnt any law, there isnt any violation of the law.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba fɔyɩ nbara mʋ, bɛɛ nyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ. Amaa nɩɩ nbara ɩ man bʋ tɔ mʋ, nbara kɩfɔyɩ gbaa man bʋ tɔ.

eng: Thats why the inheritance comes through faith, so that it will be on the basis of Gods grace. In that way, the promise is secure for all of Abrahams descendants, not just for those who are related by Law but also for those who are related by the faith of Abraham, who is the father of all of us.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ lɩɩ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ nɩɩ asa bɛɛ nyɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ sa bamʋ mʋ. Ɩ gyɛ kabwaala kakɛɛ sa Abraham kɩnaana tɔ. Ɩ man gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bee buu nbara mʋ nkʋn, amaa yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ gyii gbaa fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Abraham kɔɔlɛ gyii mʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ anɛ kpini anɛ‐sɛ.

eng: As it is written: I have appointed you to be the father of many nations. So Abraham is our father in the eyes of God in whom he had faith, the God who gives life to the dead and calls things that dont exist into existence.
local: Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ yɩla mʋ yɛ, N waa fʋ ɔsɛ sa ndɛndɛmandɛ kpini. Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ mʋ Abraham ɩ gyɛ ɔsɛ sa anɛ kpini. Abraham kɔɔlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ee kyiŋi alewupu ɛɛ ba nkpa tɔ mʋ gyii, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɛɛ sa alewupu nkpa. Nɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ man wʋla kɩ bʋ tɔ mʋ, mʋ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ kɩɩ ba.

eng: When it was beyond hope, he had faith in the hope that he would become the father of many nations, in keeping with the promise God spoke to him: Thats how many descendants you will have. .
local: Ɩ maa du fɛɛ tamaa man bʋ tɔ gbaa mʋ, Abraham kɔɔlɛ gyii nɩɩ ɔ sʋ tamaa. Nɩɩ ɔ ba waa ɔsɛ sa ndɛndɛmandɛ gaalaagaa fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Kanɩn nɩɩ fʋ anaana‐bii bee biti ba ba nyɛ akyɔ fɛɛ akyikpebi.

eng: Without losing faith, Abraham, who was nearly years old, took into account his own body, which was as good as dead, and Sarahs womb, which was dead.
local: Abraham gyii fɛɛ nsu kɩlɩfa nɩɩ o nyi mʋ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ kɩ maa pɔɔ. Nɩɩ mʋ‐ka Sara gbaa bɩlɛ nɩɩ ɔ man kʋʋgɛ kɩɩ. Lɩɩ ɩnɩmʋ kpini sʋ Abraham kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii kɩ man yɔɔ.

eng: He didnt hesitate with a lack of faith in Gods promise, but he grew strong in faith and gave glory to God.
local: Nɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ sa mʋ mʋ, ɔ man gyii ɩmʋ ɩmɔɔrɛ, amaa ɔ nyɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii tɔ ɔlʋn nɩɩ ɔ taa bʋnyaa sa Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: He was fully convinced that God was able to do what he promised.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ kyula fɛɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ yɛ ee biti ɔ waa sa mʋ mʋ, ee biti ɔ taalɛ waa.

eng: Therefore, it was credited to him as righteousness.
local: Falɛ sʋ nɩɩ, Ɩbwaarɛ kyula yɛ mʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi.

eng: But the scripture that says it was credited to him wasnt written only for Abrahams sake.
local: Ɩmʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ yɛ Abraham ɩkpa i kyiigi Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ mʋ, man gyɛ mʋ nkʋn sʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ kanɩn.

eng: It was written also for our sake, because it is going to be credited to us too. It will be credited to those of us who have faith in the one who raised Jesus our Lord from the dead.
local: Amaa anɛ kpini sʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ kanɩn. Kanɩn sʋ anɛ gbaa anɛ mʋ nɩɩ an kɔɔlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ o kyiŋi anɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Yesu lɩɩ lewu tɔ gyii mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ kɔɔlɛ anɛ fɛɛ anɛ ɩkpa i kyiigi.

eng: He was handed over because of our mistakes, and he was raised to meet the requirements of righteousness for us.
local: Kpalɩ fɛɛ anɛ alibi sʋ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ mʋ sa fɛɛ o wu, nɩɩ o kyiŋi mʋ ba nkpa tɔ nɩɩ lɩɩ mʋ sʋ mʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kɔɔlɛ anɛ fɛɛ anɛ ɩkpa i kyiigi.

eng: Therefore, since we have been made righteous through his faithfulness, we have peace with God through our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Kanɩn sʋ, lɩɩ anɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ kyula yɛ anɛ ɩkpa i kyiigi mʋ asɛ, nɩɩ lɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu Krisito sʋ mʋ, kayɩɩ yuuli kɩ bʋ anaa Ɩbwaarɛ nsana.

eng: We have access by faith into this grace in which we stand through him, and we boast in the hope of Gods glory.
local: Lɩɩ mʋ sʋ nɩɩ an taa anɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii nyɛ kabwaala mʋ nɩɩ nbɩɩnbɩɩ an yɩlɛ kɩmʋ tɔ falɛ. Nɩɩ anɛ ansi i gyii lɩɩ fɛɛ an sʋ tamaa fɛɛ an biti an nyɛ bʋnyaa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ sa anɛ sʋsʋ mʋ.

eng: But not only that! We even take pride in our problems, because we know that trouble produces endurance.
local: Nɩɩ man gyɛ kanɩn nkʋn sʋ, amaa awʋrʋfɔ mʋ nɩɩ an gyii falɛ gbaa mʋ, anɛ ansi i gyii, lɩɩ fɛɛ awʋrʋfɔ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ asa ba nyɛ aniya yɩlɛ tɔ.

eng: endurance produces character, and character produces hope.
local: Nɩɩ aniya kɩyɩlɛ tɔ mɔɔ ɩ bɩya kɩbɩɩlabɩɩ, nɩɩ kɩbɩɩlabɩɩ kɩ bɩya tamaa.

eng: This hope doesnt put us to shame, because the love of God has been poured out in our hearts through the Holy Spirit, who has been given to us.
local: Nɩɩ tamaa mɛɛ waa kɩyan, lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ bɔla Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa sa anɛ mʋ sʋ kyɛɛgɛ mu kebiti mʋ bɔla anɛ nkɔlɔ tɔ.

eng: While we were still weak, at the right moment, Christ died for ungodly people.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ anɛ anyɩmɩsa a mɛɛ bɩla taalɛ waa sɛhn mʋ, kanɩn saŋa maŋa tɔ kyɛɛkyɛɛ nɩɩ Krisito ba wu sa anɛ alibi awaapʋ.

eng: It isnt often that someone will die for a righteous person, though maybe someone might dare to die for a good person.
local: Ii biti ɩ waa ɔlʋn sa nyɩmɩsa fɛɛ o wu sa ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ mʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi gbaa. Amaa ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ adanbɩrasa ɔwaapʋ bɩrɛ akʋtɔ fɛɛ ɔkʋ ii biti ɔ waa kɔkɔlɔ ɔsɩɩ yɛ ee biti o wu sa mʋ.

eng: But God shows his love for us, because while we were still sinners Christ died for us.
local: Amaa Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ mu kebiti mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ sa anɛ mʋ kaapʋ lɩɩ ɩnɩmʋ sʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ a maa san an gyɛ alibi awaapʋ gbaa mʋ, nɩɩ Krisito ba wu sa anɛ.

eng: So, now that we have been made righteous by his blood, we can be even more certain that we will be saved from Gods wrath through him.
local: Lɩɩ Yesu Krisito nkalan sʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kɔɔlɛ kyula fɛɛ anɛ ɩkpa i kyiigi mʋ asɛ. Kanɩn maŋa sʋ lɩɩ Krisito sʋ mʋ, ee biti ɔ mɔlɩgɛ anɛ lɩɩ mʋ kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ mʋ tɔ.

eng: If we were reconciled to God through the death of his Son while we were still enemies, now that we have been reconciled, how much more certain is it that we will be saved by his life?.
local: Halɩɩ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ an gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ akisipu mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ yɛgɛ mu‐bii Yesu ba wu nɩɩ ɩ lɔŋɔ anaa mʋ nsana. Lɩɩ fɛɛ Yesu maa tɛ nkpa tɔ sʋ mʋ, ee biti ɔ mɔlɩgɛ anɛ lɩɩ kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ kakpaa mʋ tɔ.

eng: And not only that: we even take pride in God through our Lord Jesus Christ, the one through whom we now have a restored relationship with God.
local: Nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ ɩnɩmʋ nkʋn, amaa ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ bɩla i tii sʋ ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ anɛ ansi i gyii lɩɩ kɔlɔŋɔ mʋ nɩɩ anɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Yesu Krisito ba lɔŋɔ anaa Ɩbwaarɛ nsana mʋ sʋ.

eng: Just as through one human being sin came into the world, and death came through sin, so death has come to everyone, since everyone has sinned.
local: Nɩɩ lɩɩ nyɩmɩsa kʋlʋn awaasa sʋ nɩɩ alibi ɩ ba dulinyaa, nɩɩ lɩɩ alibi sʋ nɩɩ lewu ba dulinyaa tɔ. Nɩɩ falɛ sʋ nɩɩ lewu nyɛ ɔkpa asa kpini sʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ waa alibi.

eng: Although sin was in the world, since there was no Law, it wasnt taken into account until the Law came.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ pɔyɩ kɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ bɩya nbara mʋ, alibi ɩ wʋla ɩ bʋ dulinyaa tɔ. Amaa saŋa mʋ nɩɩ nbara mʋ ɩ man bʋ tɔ mʋ, ba mɛɛ taa alibi bɛɛ tɩɩ sɛhn.

eng: But death ruled from Adam until Moses, even over those who didnt sin in the same way Adam did—Adam was a type of the one who was coming.
local: Amaa lɩɩ alibi sʋ mʋ, lewu gyii nyɩmɩsa sʋ kuwura lɩɩ Adam sʋ ba fʋʋ Mosesi saŋa sʋ, halɩɩ ba maa man nyita Ɩbwaarɛ kɔnɔsa mʋ fɛɛ Adam lɛɛ mʋ gbaa. Ɔkpa kʋ sʋ mʋ Adam ɩ yɩlɛ sa Yesu mʋ nɩɩ ɔ ba ba kamaa tɔ mʋ.

eng: But the free gift of Christ isnt like Adams failure. If many people died through what one person did wrong, Gods grace is multiplied even more for many people with the gift—of the one person Jesus Christ—that comes through grace.
local: Amaa Ɩbwaarɛ kabwaala kakɛɛ mʋ man du fɛɛ Adam alibi mʋ. Kpalɩ fɛɛ Adam kʋlʋn alibi mʋ sʋ mʋ, ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ wu. Amaa Ɩbwaarɛ kabwaala mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa kɛɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ lɩɩ Yesu Krisito kʋlʋn sʋ mʋ, kɩ gyɛ kʋtɔ kparɛ.

eng: The gift isnt like the consequences of one persons sin. The judgment that came from one persons sin led to punishment, but the free gift that came out of many failures led to the verdict of acquittal.
local: Kanɩn maŋa gbaa nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kakɛɛ mʋ kɩ man du fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Adam nyɩmɩsa kʋlʋn alibi ɩ ba waa mʋ. Nyɩmɩsa kʋlʋn alibi katalɛ sʋ mʋ, i bun ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ kʋpwɛ. Amaa lɩɩ asa alibi gaalaagaa kɩwaa kamaa mʋ, ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ yɛgɛ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩɩ taalɛ kyula fɛɛ nyɩmɩsa ɩkpa i kyiigi mʋ asɛ mʋ, ɩ ba.

eng: If death ruled because of one persons failure, those who receive the multiplied grace and the gift of righteousness will even more certainly rule in life through the one person Jesus Christ.
local: Nyɩmɩsa kʋlʋn alibi sʋ nɩɩ lewu gyii asa sʋ ɔlʋn lɩɩ kanɩn nyɩmɩsa kʋlʋn maŋa sʋ. Nɩɩ Yesu Krisito nyɩmɩsa kʋlʋn gbaa sʋ mʋ, anyɩmɩsa ba nyɛ kabwaala nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii kyula fɛɛ bamʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi mʋ asɛ, nɩɩ ba nyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ kakyɩna kakpaa. Ɩmʋ sʋ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Yesu Krisito waa mʋ, kɩ kyɔ lewu ɔlʋn mʋ nɩɩ kɩ da gyii anɛ sʋ mʋ.

eng: So now the righteous requirements necessary for life are met for everyone through the righteous act of one person, just as judgment fell on everyone through the failure of one person.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ nyɩmɩsa kʋlʋn alibi sʋ nɩɩ asa kpini ba gyii kʋpwɛ mʋ, kanɩn maŋa sʋ nɩɩ nyɩmɩsa kʋlʋn ɩkpa kikyiigi sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii kyula yɛ asa bamʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi, nɩɩ ba nyɛ nkpa.

eng: Many people were made righteous through the obedience of one person, just as many people were made sinners through the disobedience of one person.
