local: Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Adam kʋlʋn waa kʋsʋ tɔ ɔlʋn nɩɩ ɔ man nu Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ, nɩɩ asa kpini ba biliŋi alibi awaapʋ mʋ, kanɩn maŋa sʋ nɩɩ Yesu Krisito kʋlʋn bɩya mʋ asʋ kasɛ nu Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ nɩɩ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kyula asa gaalaagaa fɛɛ bamʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi mʋ asɛ.

eng: The Law stepped in to amplify the failure, but where sin increased, grace multiplied even more.
local: Mosesi nbara mʋ ɩ ba nɩn, kɛ ɩ yɛgɛ asa ba wu alibi mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa mʋ kʋkyɔ. Amaa alibi kɩwaa kɩ maa nyɛ akyɔ mʋ, yɛgɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kabwaala mʋ kɩ nyɛ akyɔ ki tii sʋ.

eng: The result is that grace will rule through Gods righteousness, leading to eternal life through Jesus Christ our Lord, just as sin ruled in death.
local: Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ alibi nyɛ ɔlʋn nyɩmɩsa sʋ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ lewu mʋ, kanɩn maŋa sʋ nɩɩ asa ba nyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kabwaala fɛɛ maa kyula fɛɛ asa ɩkpa i kyiigi mʋ asɛ. Ɩmʋ sʋ lɩɩ anɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Yesu Krisito sʋ mʋ, asa ba nyɛ nkpa kakpaa.

eng: So what are we going to say? Should we continue sinning so grace will multiply?.
local: Kanɩn sʋ, mɩnɛ nɩɩ an biti a tɔwɛ? A kyaga sʋ an waa alibi kɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kabwaala mʋ kɩ nyɛ akyɔ ki tii sʋ abɛɛ?.

eng: Absolutely not! All of us died to sin. How can we still live in it?.
local: Ayee! Alibi ɩ man bɩla sʋ anɛ sʋ ɔlʋn, lɩɩ fɛɛ i du nɩn fɛɛ a maa wu mʋ, nɛnɛ nɩɩ an bɩla taalɛ kyɩna alibi mʋ tɔ?.

eng: Or dont you know that all who were baptized into Christ Jesus were baptized into his death?.
local: Abɛɛ fan man nyi fɛɛ anɛ kpini mʋ nɩɩ ba gyee anɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee mʋ waa Yesu Krisito tɔ mʋ, ɩ waa fɛɛ anaa mʋ an waa kʋkʋlʋn mu lewu tɔ abɛɛ?.

eng: Therefore, we were buried together with him through baptism into his death, so that just as Christ was raised from the dead through the glory of the Father, we too can walk in newness of life.
local: Nɩɩ lɩɩ anɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee mʋ sʋ mʋ, anaa mʋ an wu, nɩɩ ba pula anaa mʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɔsɛ Ɩbwaarɛ taa mʋ bʋnyaa kyiŋi mʋ lɩɩ lewu tɔ mʋ, anɛ gbaa anɛ an biti a kyɩna kakyɩna pʋpwɛ tɔ.

eng: If we were united together in a death like his, we will also be united together in a resurrection like his.
local: Fɛɛ anaa mʋ a maa gyɛ kʋkʋlʋn lɩɩ mʋ kelewu mʋ sʋ mʋ, kanɩn maŋa sʋ nɩɩ anaa mʋ an biti a ba an gyɛ kʋlʋn lɩɩ mu kikyiŋi bwii ba nkpa tɔ mʋ.

eng: This is what we know: the person that we used to be was crucified with him in order to get rid of the corpse that had been controlled by sin. That way we wouldnt be slaves to sin anymore.
local: Nɩɩ an nyi fɛɛ ba da anɛ alibi n‐yɩɩ dɩdaa mʋ yɛ mʋ mata oyu laasa sʋ mɔɔ, nɩɩ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ anɛ alibi n‐yɩɩ dɩdaa man bɩla kɩ sʋ ɔlʋn anɛ sʋ, kɛ a man bɩla taa anɛ n‐yɩɩ waa anyɛ sa alibi.

eng: because a person who has died has been freed from sins power.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o wu mʋ, ɔ nyɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ lɩɩ alibi kɩwaa sʋ.

eng: But if we died with Christ, we have faith that we will also live with him.
local: Fɛɛ anaa Krisito a maa wu falɛ mʋ, an kɔɔlɛ gyii fɛɛ anaa mʋ an biti a bʋga kyɩna.

eng: We know that Christ has been raised from the dead and he will never die again. Death no longer has power over him.
local: Nɩɩ an nyi fɛɛ Krisito maa kyiŋi lɩɩ lewu tɔ falɛ mʋ, ɔ mɛɛ bɩla yɛ o wu, nɩɩ lewu man bɩla ɔ sʋ mʋ sʋ ɔlʋn.

eng: He died to sin once and for all with his death, but he lives for God with his life.
local: Nɩɩ kelewu mʋ nɩɩ o wu mʋ, o wu sa anɛ alibi kɩlɩɩ kʋlʋn kakpaa, amaa nkpa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛ kɩmʋ tɔ mʋ, ɔ tɛ sa Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: In the same way, you also should consider yourselves dead to sin but alive for God in Christ Jesus.
local: Kanɩn ɔkpa maŋa sʋ, fan kɩɩ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ fɛɛ fan wu, nɩɩ alibi kɩwaa kɩ man bɩla kɩ sʋ fanɛ sʋ ɔlʋn, amaa fan tɛ sa Ɩbwaarɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ fan naa Yesu Krisito fan waa kʋkʋlʋn.

eng: So then, dont let sin rule your body, so that you do what it wants.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan man yɛgɛ alibi i gyii kuwura fanɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ sʋ, kɛ fan man buu atɔ libi mʋ nɩɩ fanɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ ii biti ɩ waa mʋ.

eng: Dont offer parts of your body to sin, to be used as weapons to do wrong. Instead, present yourselves to God as people who have been brought back to life from the dead, and offer all the parts of your body to God to be used as weapons to do right.
local: Fan man taa fanɛ n‐yɩɩ katɩn kʋ sa alibi kɛ ɩ taa waa asun libi, amaa fan taa fanɛ n‐yɩɩ sa Ɩbwaarɛ fɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ bamʋ lɩɩ lewu tɔ ba nkpa tɔ mʋ, kɛ fan taa fanɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ kpini taa sa mʋ, kɛ ɔ taa fanɛ waa ɩkpa kyiigisa atɔ.

eng: Sin will have no power over you, because you arent under Law but under grace.
local: Nɩɩ fan man bɩla yɛgɛ alibi i gyii fanɛ sʋ ɔlʋn, lɩɩ fɛɛ fan man bʋ Mosesi nbara mʋ kasɛ, amaa Ɩbwaarɛ kabwaala mʋ tɔ nɩɩ fan bʋ.

eng: So what? Should we sin because we arent under Law but under grace? Absolutely not!.
local: Kanɩn sʋ mɩnɛ nɩɩ an biti a tɔwɛ? Fɛɛ a maa man bɩla an bʋ nbara mʋ kasɛ, nɩɩ an nyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kabwaala mʋ, a kyaga sʋ an waa alibi? Ayee!.

eng: Dont you know that if you offer yourselves to someone as obedient slaves, that you are slaves of the one whom you obey? Thats true whether you serve as slaves of sin, which leads to death, or as slaves of the kind of obedience that leads to righteousness.
local: Fan man nyi fɛɛ nɩɩ fan kan taa fanɛ n‐yɩɩ sa ɔkʋ nɩɩ fan nu mʋ asɛ mʋ, fan gyɛ anyɛ sa ɔsa maŋa. Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ fan kan buu alibi mʋ, fan gyɛ anyɛ sa ɩmʋ, nɩɩ ɩɩ sʋ fanɛ i kpee lewu tɔ, amaa nɩɩ fan kan buu Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ, ɩmʋ ɩ sʋ fanɛ i kpee ɩkpa kyiigisa tɔ.

eng: But thank God that although you used to be slaves of sin, you gave wholehearted obedience to the teaching that was handed down to you, which provides a pattern.
local: Fan kyɩna fan gyɛ anyɛ sa alibi, amaa n fwaala Ɩbwaarɛ fɛɛ nbɩɩnbɩɩ bɩrɛ fan taa fanɛ nkɔlɔ kpini buu kesintin kɩkaapʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba taa kaapʋ fanɛ mʋ.

eng: Now that you have been set free from sin, you have become slaves of righteousness.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ kɔɔlɛ fanɛ lɩɩ alibi tɔ, nɩɩ fan ba biliŋi anyɛ sa ɩkpa kyiigisa awaasa.

eng: Once, you offered the parts of your body to be used as slaves to impurity and to lawless behavior that leads to still more lawless behavior. Now, you should present the parts of your body as slaves to righteousness, which makes your lives holy.
local: Fanɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii kɩ maa man pɩɩta waa ɔlʋn sʋ, nɩɩ n taa kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ tɔ asɩn n kala n sa fanɛ falɛ. Dɩdaa mʋ fan kyɩna taa fanɛ n‐yɩɩ biliŋi anyɛ alibi tɔ, nɩɩ fan waa alibi kɩkyɔ tii sʋ. Kanɩn sʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ nbɩɩnbɩɩ fan taa fanɛ n‐yɩɩ kpini fɛɛ anyɛ sa ɩkpa kyiigisa awaasa, kɛ fan ba biliŋi Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ.

eng: When you were slaves of sin, you were free from the control of righteousness.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ fan gyɛ anyɛ sa alibi mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ ɩkpa kyiigisa asɩn mʋ, fan man taa ɩmʋ waa sɛhn.

eng: What consequences did you get from doing things that you are now ashamed of? The outcome of those things is death.
local: Mɩnɛ tɔnɔ nɩɩ fan nyɛ lɩɩ kanɩn saŋa maŋa, lɩɩ kanɩn atɔ maŋa mʋ nɩɩ ndaga ɩ gyɛ ipeeli‐sɩn sa fanɛ falɛ? Kanɩn atɔ maŋa ɩ bɩya lewu.

eng: But now that you have been set free from sin and become slaves to God, you have the consequence of a holy life, and the outcome is eternal life.
local: Amaa nbɩɩnbɩɩ fan maa man bɩla fan gyɛ anyɛ sa alibi falɛ, nɩɩ fan biliŋi anyɛ sa Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ, tɔnɔ mʋ nɩɩ fen biti fan nyɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ fan maa biliŋi Ɩbwaarɛ asa, nɩɩ ɩ bɩya fanɛ nkpa kakpaa.

eng: The wages that sin pays are death, but Gods gift is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord.
local: Nɩɩ alibi kʋkɔka ɩ gyɛ lewu, amaa Ɩbwaarɛ kakɛɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bɔla anɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Yesu Krisito sʋ sa anɛ ɩ gyɛ nkpa kakpaa.

eng: Brothers and sisters, Im talking to you as people who know the Law. Dont you know that the Law has power over someone only as long as he or she lives?.
local: Mɛ bɩrɩsa ana akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu, fanɛ kpini fan nyi fɛɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ nyɩmɩsa san ɔ tɛ nkpa tɔ mʋ, saŋa maa nkʋn nɩɩ nbara mʋ ɩ sʋ ɔlʋn mʋ sʋ.

eng: A married woman is united with her husband under the Law while he is alive. But if her husband dies, she is released from the Law concerning her husband.
local: I du nɩn fɛɛ ɔkyɩɩ kilipu. Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ mu‐kuli san ɔ tɛ nkpa tɔ mʋ, kekili nbara mʋ i ŋmina mʋ sa mu‐kuli. Amaa nɩɩ mu‐kuli mʋ kan wu bɩrɛ ɔ nyɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ lɩɩ kekili nbara mʋ tɔ.

eng: So then, if she lives with another man while her husband is alive, shes committing adultery. But if her husband dies, shes free from the Law, so she wont be committing adultery if she marries someone else.
local: Kanɩn sʋ nɩɩ ɔ kan kili ɔnyɩn pʋpwɛ yɛgɛ mu‐kuli san ɔ tɛ nkpa tɔ mʋ, ɔ lɩɩ mu‐kuli kamaa nɩmʋ. Amaa nɩɩ mu‐kuli mʋ kan wu bɩrɛ ɔ nyɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ lɩɩ kekili nbara mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɛɛ bɩla taalɛ kili ɔnyɩn pʋpwɛ nɩɩ ɩ mɛɛ kaapʋ fɛɛ ɔ lɩɩ mu‐kuli kamaa nɩn.

eng: Therefore, my brothers and sisters, you also died with respect to the Law through the body of Christ, so that you could be united with someone else. You are united with the one who was raised from the dead so that we can bear fruit for God.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana, kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ fanɛ gbaa fanɛ fan maa kɔɔlɛ Yesu Krisito gyii mʋ, fan bɔla mʋ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ sʋ wu nɩn. Ɩmʋ sʋ Mosesi nbara mʋ ɩ man bɩla ɩ sʋ fanɛ sʋ ɔlʋn. Ɩmʋ sʋ nbɩɩnbɩɩ bɩrɛ fan gyɛ sa Krisito mʋ nɩɩ o wu kyiigi lɩɩ lewu tɔ mʋ, kɛ a nyɛ waa adanbɩrasa sa Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: When we were self-centered, the sinful passions aroused through the Law were at work in all the parts of our body, so that we bore fruit for death.
local: Fɛɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ an bʋ anɛ gbaa‐gbaa kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ kebiti tɔ mʋ, nbara mʋ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ kebiti mʋ nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ alibi mʋ, ɩ waa kusun anɛ n‐yɩɩ tɔ, nɩɩ an sun asun libi nɩɩ ɩ sʋ anɛ ii kpee lewu tɔ.

eng: But now we have been released from the Law. We have died with respect to the thing that controlled us, so that we can be slaves in the new life under the Spirit, not in the old life under the written Law.
local: Amaa nbɩɩnbɩɩ an nyɛ anɛ n‐yɩɩ lɩɩ nbara mʋ nɩɩ ɩ daa kyɩna ŋmina anɛ mʋ tɔ, lɩɩ fɛɛ i du nɩn fɛɛ anaa Krisito a maa wu. Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ a man bɩla an sun nbara dɩdaa mʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ yɩla mʋ, amaa an sun Ɩbwaarɛ ɔkpa pʋpwɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bɔla Kayaayu mʋ sʋ.

eng: So what are we going to say? That the Law is sin? Absolutely not! But I wouldnt have known sin except through the Law. I wouldnt have known the desire for what others have if the Law had not said, Dont desire to take what others have .
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ mɩnɛ nɩɩ an biti a tɔwɛ? Nbara mʋ ɩ gyɛ alibi nɩn abɛɛ? Ayee! Ɩ man gyɛ alibi nɩn. Nbara mʋ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ n bɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ alibi ɩ gyɛ. Nɩɩ nbara mʋ ɩ man dɩn tɔwɛ yɛ, Man yɛgɛ fu ansi ɩ payɩ ɔkʋ atɔ sʋ mʋ naafɔɔ n mɛɛ yɛ n bɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ onsi‐payɩ gyɛ.

eng: But sin seized the opportunity and used this commandment to produce all kinds of desires in me. Sin is dead without the Law.
local: Nɩɩ nbara ɩnɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ alibi a bɔla nyɛ ɔkpa mɛ tɔ, nɩɩ ɩɩ sʋ mɛ ɔbʋlɛ nɩɩ n biti alibi kʋmaa kɩwaa tii sʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ nɩɩ nbara ɩ man bʋ tɔ mʋ, alibi mʋ kɔnɔ ki wu.

eng: I used to be alive without the Law, but when the commandment came, sin sprang to life.
local: Saŋa kʋ tɔ mʋ n daa kyɩna yɛgɛ n man nyi Mosesi nbara mʋ. Amaa saŋa mʋ nɩɩ n ba bɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kɔnɔsa mʋ, nɩɩ n lan bɩɩ fɛɛ alibi tɔ nɩɩ n tɛ, nɩɩ n wu.

eng: and I died. So the commandment that was intended to give life brought death.
local: Nɩɩ n lan bɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kɔnɔsa mʋ nɩɩ naafɔɔ ɩ bɩya mɛ nkpa mʋ, ɩ taa mɛ kpee lewu tɔ.

eng: Sin seized the opportunity through the commandment, deceived me, and killed me.
local: Nɩɩ alibi ɩ bɔla Ɩbwaarɛ kɔnɔsa mʋ sʋ nyɛ ɔkpa pɩna mɛ, nɩɩ ɩ bɔla ɩmʋ sʋ mɔɔ mɛ.

eng: So the Law itself is holy, and the commandment is holy, righteous, and good.
local: Mosesi nbara mʋ i du kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kɔnɔsa mʋ gbaa i du kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ, nɩɩ i du kyɛɛkyɛɛ nɩɩ ɩ bʋrɔn.

eng: So did something good bring death to me? Absolutely not! But sin caused my death through something good so that sin would be exposed as sin. That way sin would become even more thoroughly sinful through the commandment.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ falɛ kʋtɔ danbɩrasa nɩmʋ ɩ bɩya mɛ lewu nɩn? Ayee! Amaa alibi a bɔla kʋtɔ danbɩrasa nɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ n wu lewu. Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ waa nɩn kɛ alibi ɩ lɩɩ ifuli kaapʋ kanan mʋ nɩɩ i du. Nɩɩ kɔnɔsa mʋ kɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ an taalɛ wu kesintin fɛɛ alibi ɩ gyɛ alibi ɩ man bʋrɔn nɩn kpini‐kpini.

eng: We know that the Law is spiritual, but Im made of flesh and blood, and Im sold as a slave to sin.
local: An nyi fɛɛ nbara mʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu tɔ atɔ nɩn, amaa mɛɛ mɛ bɩrɛ n gyɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ nyɩmɩsa nɩn, nɩɩ alibi i gyii mɛ sʋ nɩɩ ɩ waa mɛ fɛɛ kɩnyɛ.

eng: I dont know what Im doing, because I dont do what I want to do. Instead, I do the thing that I hate.
local: N mee nu mɛ awaasa mʋ kasɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ n biti fɛɛ n waa mʋ, mɛɛ taalɛ n waa, amaa ɩmʋ nɩɩ n mee biti mʋ, ɩmʋ nɩɩ n waa.

eng: But if Im doing the thing that I dont want to do, Im agreeing that the Law is right.
local: Nɩɩ fɛɛ nɩɩ n kan waa ɩmʋ nɩɩ n mee biti fɛɛ n waa bɩrɛ, n kyula fɛɛ nbara mʋ ɩ bʋrɔn.

eng: But now Im not the one doing it anymore. Instead, its sin that lives in me.
local: Kanɩn bɩrɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ i du mʋ, man gyɛ mɛ gbaa‐gbaa mɛ ɩ waa atɔ waasa libi nɩmʋ, amaa alibi mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ mɛ tɔ mʋ ɩ waa ɩmʋ.

eng: I know that good doesnt live in me—that is, in my body. The desire to do good is inside of me, but I cant do it.
local: Nɩɩ n nyi fɛɛ mɛ gbaa‐gbaa mɛ tɔ nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ mɛ kɩbaabʋtɔ dɩdaa mʋ, kʋtɔ danbɩrasa kʋ kɩ man bʋ mɛ tɔ. Ɩ gyɛ mɛ kɩpɩrɛ fɛɛ n waa adanbɩrasa, amaa n mɛɛ taalɛ n waa.

eng: I dont do the good that I want to do, but I do the evil that I dont want to do.
local: Adanbɩrasa mʋ nɩɩ n biti fɛɛ n waa mʋ, n mɛɛ waa ɩmʋ, amaa alibi mʋ nɩɩ n mee biti fɛɛ n waa mʋ, ɩmʋ nɩɩ n waa.

eng: But if I do the very thing that I dont want to do, then Im not the one doing it anymore. Instead, it is sin that lives in me that is doing it.
local: Fɛɛ nɩɩ n kan waa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ man gyɛ mi kebiti fɛɛ n waa mʋ, man gyɛ mɛ ɩ waa ɩmʋ kanɩn, amaa alibi mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ mɛ tɔ mʋ, ɩmʋ ɩ waa kanɩn.

eng: So I find that, as a rule, when I want to do what is good, evil is right there with me.
local: N wu fɛɛ saŋa kʋmaa mʋ, kanɩn nɩɩ ɩ waa mɛ nɩn. Lɩɩ fɛɛ saŋa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ n biti fɛɛ n waa adanbɩrasa mʋ, alibi nɩɩ ɩ sʋla mɛ naa waa.

eng: I gladly agree with the Law on the inside.
local: Nɩɩ mɛ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ mʋ, n kɔɔlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ nbara mʋ kyula fɛɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ n biti n buu.

eng: but I see a different law at work in my body. It wages a war against the law of my mind and takes me prisoner with the law of sin that is in my body.
local: Amaa n wu nbara kʋ maa bʋ mɛ tɔ ɩɩ waa kusun, nɩɩ ɩ kɔ yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ nbara mʋ nɩɩ n kɔɔlɛ kyula mɛ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ mʋ. Nbara nɩmʋ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ alibi gyii mi kumu nɩɩ i loo mɛ n‐yɩɩ tɔ kpini.

eng: Im a miserable human being. Who will deliver me from this dead corpse?.
local: Mɛ awʋrʋfɔ wuya nɩmʋ! Anɩmʋ ii biti ɔ mɔlɩgɛ mɛ lɩɩ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ awaasa libi mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ sʋ mɛ kii kpee lewu tɔ falɛ?.

eng: Thank God through Jesus Christ our Lord! So then Im a slave to Gods Law in my mind, but Im a slave to sins law in my body.
local: N afwaala Ɩbwaarɛ fɛɛ ɔ bɔla anɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Yesu Krisito sʋ mɔlɩgɛ mɛ. Mɛ gbaa‐gbaa mɛ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ mʋ, n buu Ɩbwaarɛ nbara mʋ, amaa mɛ kɩbaabʋtɔ dɩdaa mʋ, kii biti alibi kɩwaa.

eng: So now there isnt any condemnation for those who are in Christ Jesus.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ, nbɩɩnbɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kʋpwɛ kubun man bʋ tɔ sa anɛ mʋ nɩɩ anaa Yesu Krisito an waa kʋkʋlʋn mʋ.

eng: The law of the Spirit of life in Christ Jesus has set you free from the law of sin and death.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ a maa bɔla Yesu Krisito sʋ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu nbara pʋpwɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ sa nyɩmɩsa nkpa mʋ, ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ n nyɛ mɛ n‐yɩɩ lɩɩ nbara mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, nɩɩ n kan waa mʋ alibi n biti n wu mʋ.

eng: God has done what was impossible for the Law, since it was weak because of selfishness. God condemned sin in the body by sending his own Son to deal with sin in the same body as humans, who are controlled by sin.
local: Kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Mosesi nbara mʋ ɩ man taalɛ waa, kpalɩ fɛɛ anɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ mʋ maa man bɩla kɩ bʋlʋn ki buu nbara mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ taalɛ waa fɛɛ maa sun mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mu‐bii fɛɛ nyɩmɩsa alibi awaapʋ keduli. Nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ n‐yɩɩ waa saraga sa anɛ alibi, ba bun alibi mʋ nɩɩ i gyii nyɩmɩsa kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ sʋ mʋ kʋpwɛ.

eng: He did this so that the righteous requirement of the Law might be fulfilled in us. Now the way we live is based on the Spirit, not based on selfishness.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ waa falɛ nɩn kɛ a taalɛ waa kesintin ɩkpa danbɩrasa mʋ nɩɩ nbara ii biti mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ an man bɩla an buu anɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ dɩdaa mʋ, amaa an buu Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu kebiti mʋ.

eng: People whose lives are based on selfishness think about selfish things, but people whose lives are based on the Spirit think about things that are related to the Spirit.
local: Nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba naa kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ dɩdaa tɔ mʋ, bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ ɩ dɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ kebiti sʋ nɩn, amaa bamʋ nɩɩ ba naa Kayaayu tɔ mʋ, bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ ɩ dɛ Kayaayu kebiti tɔ nɩn.

eng: The attitude that comes from selfishness leads to death, but the attitude that comes from the Spirit leads to life and peace.
local: Fɛɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ kebiti kan gyii ɔkʋ nfɛɛrɛ sʋ mʋ, ɩ bɩya mʋ lewu nɩn, amaa ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu kebiti gyii mʋ nfɛɛrɛ sʋ mʋ, ɩ bɩya mʋ nkpa yɛ keyuula.

eng: So the attitude that comes from selfishness is hostile to God. It doesnt submit to Gods Law, because it cant.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan taa mʋ nfɛɛrɛ tʋʋ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ kebiti sʋ mʋ, ɔ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ okisipu. Nɩɩ o mee buu Ɩbwaarɛ nbara, nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ taalɛ buu sʋ gbaa.

eng: People who are self-centered arent able to please God.
local: Nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa bamʋ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ kebiti mʋ, ba mɛɛ bɔla Ɩbwaarɛ ansi.

eng: But you arent self-centered. Instead you are in the Spirit, if in fact Gods Spirit lives in you. If anyone doesnt have the Spirit of Christ, they dont belong to him.
local: Amaa fanɛ bɩrɛ fɛn mɛɛ waa fanɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ kebiti, amaa Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ fanɛ tɔ mʋ kebiti nɩɩ fɛn waa. Nɩɩ ɔsa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man sʋ Krisito Kayaayu mʋ, ɔ man gyɛ Krisito lɛɛ.

eng: If Christ is in you, the Spirit is your life because of Gods righteousness, but the body is dead because of sin.
local: Amaa fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ Krisito bʋ fanɛ nkɔlɔ tɔ mʋ bɩrɛ, fanɛ n‐yaayu mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ fanɛ tɔ mʋ ɩ sʋ nkpa. Lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ yɛ fanɛ ɩkpa i kyiigi mʋ asɛ, amaa lɩɩ fanɛ alibi sʋ mʋ fanɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ kii biti ki wu.

eng: If the Spirit of the one who raised Jesus from the dead lives in you, the one who raised Christ from the dead will give life to your human bodies also, through his Spirit that lives in you.
local: Nɩɩ ɩ kan ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ o kyiŋi Yesu lɩɩ lewu tɔ mʋ Kayaayu bʋ fanɛ tɔ bɩrɛ, kanɩn Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ o kyiŋi Yesu lɩɩ lewu tɔ mʋ, ii biti ɔ sa fanɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ nkpa, lɩɩ mʋ Kayaayu mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ fanɛ tɔ falɛ sʋ.

eng: So then, brothers and sisters, we have an obligation, but it isnt an obligation to ourselves to live our lives on the basis of selfishness.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana, nbɩɩnbɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ dagaa ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ a man taa anɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ kebiti waa anɛ kakyɩna tɔ.

eng: If you live on the basis of selfishness, you are going to die. But if by the Spirit you put to death the actions of the body, you will live.
local: Nɩɩ fan kan taa kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ asun libi waa fanɛ kakyɩna tɔ bɩrɛ fen biti fen wu, amaa nɩɩ fan bɔla Kayaayu mʋ sʋ kina nɩɩ fan yɛgɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ asun libi mʋ bɩrɛ, fen biti fan nyɛ nkpa.

eng: All who are led by Gods Spirit are Gods sons and daughters.
local: Bamʋ nɩɩ ba buu Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu ɔkpa kaapʋsa bamʋ mʋ, bamʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mu‐bii ana.

eng: You didnt receive a spirit of slavery to lead you back again into fear, but you received a Spirit that shows you are adopted as his children. With this Spirit, we cry, Abba, Father.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Kayaayu mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sa fanɛ mʋ, mɛɛ waa fanɛ anyɛ kɛ fan baa fan sʋ kufuu. Amaa fan nyɛ Kayaayu mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ waa fanɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mu‐bii ana. Ɩmʋ ɔlʋn tɔ nɩɩ an taalɛ tɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ, Abaa! Anɛ‐Sɛ.

eng: The same Spirit agrees with our spirit, that we are Gods children.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu mʋ gbaa‐gbaa ɩ lɛɛ ɩ kaapʋ anɛ nkɔlɔ tɔ kesintin fɛɛ an gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mu‐bii ana.

eng: But if we are children, we are also heirs. We are Gods heirs and fellow heirs with Christ, if we really suffer with him so that we can also be glorified with him.
local: Nɩɩ a maa gyɛ mu‐bii ana falɛ sʋ mʋ, an biti a nyɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ yɛ ee biti ɔ sa anɛ mʋ. Nɩɩ anaa Krisito an biti a bʋga nyɛ kanɩn kʋtɔ maŋa. Amɔɔ ana mʋ an bʋga gyii mʋ awʋrʋfɔ mʋ, pɔyɩ kɛ ana mʋ a bʋga gyii mʋ bʋnyaa mʋ.

eng: I believe that the present suffering is nothing compared to the coming glory that is going to be revealed to us.
local: Nɩɩ n wu fɛɛ nbɩɩnbɩɩ awʋrʋfɔ mʋ nɩɩ an gyii falɛ mʋ, ɔkʋ mɛɛ taalɛ taa kaala bʋnyaa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ lɛɛ kaapʋ anɛ mʋ.

eng: The whole creation waits breathless with anticipation for the revelation of Gods sons and daughters.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ atɔ tʋʋsa mʋ kpini bɛɛ gyʋgyʋʋ ba gyoo Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ ba lɛɛ mu‐bii ana mʋ kaapʋ.

eng: Creation was subjected to frustration, not by its own choice—it was the choice of the one who subjected it—but in the hope.
local: Nɩɩ atɔ tʋʋsa mʋ kpini ɩ ba waa atɔ kɩyan mʋ nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ ɩmʋ kebiti sʋ, amaa Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ waa ɩmʋ kanɩn kɛ ɔ sa ɩmʋ tamaa.

eng: that the creation itself will be set free from slavery to decay and brought into the glorious freedom of Gods children.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ atɔ tʋʋsa mʋ kpini bee biti ba kɔɔlɛ bamʋ n‐yɩɩ lɩɩ lewu yɛ kinyita tɔ, kɛ ba ba nyɛ bamʋ n‐yɩɩ yɛ bʋnyaa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dɛ sa Ɩbwaarɛ mu‐bii ana mʋ.

eng: We know that the whole creation is groaning together and suffering labor pains up until now.
local: Nɩɩ an nyi fɛɛ atɔ tʋʋsa mʋ kpini bɛɛ yɩyɛɛ ba fʋʋ halɩɩ ndaga, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ ii duŋi mʋ.

eng: And its not only the creation. We ourselves who have the Spirit as the first crop of the harvest also groan inside as we wait to be adopted and for our bodies to be set free.
local: Nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ ɩmʋ nkʋn ɩɩ yɩyɛɛ, amaa anɛ gbaa‐gbaa mʋ nɩɩ an nyɛ Kayaayu mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kakɛɛ gyankpaasa mʋ gbaa an yɩyɛɛ anɛ nkɔlɔ tɔ. An yɩyɛɛ an gyoo kakɛ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ taa anɛ waa mu‐bii ana, kɛ ɔ kɔɔlɛ anɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ yɛgɛ mʋ.

eng: We were saved in hope. If we see what we hope for, that isnt hope. Who hopes for what they already see?.
local: Lɩɩ tamaa mʋ nɩɩ an sʋ mʋ sʋ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ mɔlɩgɛ anɛ. Amaa nɩɩ ɔkʋ sʋ tamaa lɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛɛ wʋla wu mʋ, man gyɛ tamaa kpini‐kpini. Nɩɩ anɩmʋ ɩ sʋ tamaa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛɛ wʋla ɔ sʋ mʋ sʋ?.

eng: But if we hope for what we dont see, we wait for it with patience.
local: Amaa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ an man pɩɩta nyɛ nɩɩ an sʋ ɩmʋ sʋ tamaa mʋ an nyɛ kenyiita nɩn an gyoo kɩmʋ.

eng: In the same way, the Spirit comes to help our weakness. We dont know what we should pray, but the Spirit himself pleads our case with unexpressed groans.
local: Kanɩn ɔkpa kʋlʋn sʋ nɩɩ Kayaayu mʋ ɩ kyɛ anɛ tɔ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ anɛ n‐yɩɩ ɩ yɔɔ anɛ mʋ. Kpalɩ fɛɛ a maa man nyi kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ a kʋlɛ, nɩɩ a man nyi kanan mʋ nɩɩ a tɔwɛ anɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ tɔ mʋ, Kayaayu mʋ gbaa‐gbaa ii muusi ɛɛ tɔwɛ atɔwɛ‐bi mʋ nɩɩ ɔkʋ mɛɛ taalɛ taa kɔnɔ tɔwɛ nɩɩ ɔ yɩlɛ tɔ ɛɛ kʋlɛ ɛɛ kyɛ anɛ tɔ.

eng: The one who searches hearts knows how the Spirit thinks, because he pleads for the saints, consistent with Gods will.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ o nyi anɛ nkɔlɔ tɔ mʋ, nyi nfɛɛrɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ Kayaayu mʋ tɔ, lɩɩ fɛɛ Kayaayu mʋ ɩ yɩlɛ tɔ ɛɛ kʋlɛ ɛɛ sa akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kebiti sʋ.

eng: We know that God works all things together for good for the ones who love God, for those who are called according to his purpose.
local: Nɩɩ an nyi fɛɛ atɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ ba mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ sʋ ɩmʋ kpini ɛɛ waa kusun, kɛ i kpee naa lʋwɛ danbɩrasa sa bamʋ nɩɩ bee biti mʋ asɩn mʋ. Bamʋ ɩ gyɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla tɩɩ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ wʋla tɔwɛ yɩla mʋ.

eng: We know this because God knew them in advance, and he decided in advance that they would be conformed to the image of his Son. That way his Son would be the first of many brothers and sisters.
local: Nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla o nyi mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ bamʋ yɩla kɛ ba ba waa fɛɛ mu‐bii keduli mʋ, kɛ mu‐bii mʋ ɔ ba waa nbii damantɛ mʋ tɔ obii gyankpaasa.

eng: Those who God decided in advance would be conformed to his Son, he also called. Those whom he called, he also made righteous. Those whom he made righteous, he also glorified.
local: Nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ yɩla mʋ, ɔ tɩɩ bamʋ, nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ mʋ, ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ bamʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi mʋ asɛ, nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ bamʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi mʋ asɛ mʋ, ɔ taa mʋ bʋnyaa mʋ sa bamʋ.

eng: So what are we going to say about these things? If God is for us, who is against us?.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ kpini kamaa mʋ, mɩnɛ nɩɩ an biti a tɔwɛ? Fɛɛ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kan ɔ yɩlɛ anɛ kamaa mʋ, anɩmʋ ɩ taalɛ kɔ yɛ anɛ?.

eng: He didnt spare his own Son but gave him up for us all. Wont he also freely give us all things with him?.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man kina mu‐bii kɩtaa sa, nɩɩ lɩɩ anɛ alibi sʋ mʋ, ɔ yɛgɛ nɩɩ o be wu sa anɛ mʋ, mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ taa atɔ kpini tii mʋ sʋ bwaala anɛ?.

eng: Who will bring a charge against Gods elect people? It is God who acquits them.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ gbaa‐gbaa maa tɔwɛ yɛ an gyii asʋ mʋ, anɩmʋ ii biti ɔ taalɛ pwɩɩ anɛ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ yɩla sa mʋ n‐yɩɩ mʋ?.

eng: Who is going to convict them? It is Christ Jesus who died, even more, who was raised, and who also is at Gods right side. It is Christ Jesus who also pleads our case for us.
local: Anɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋnɩ o bun anɛ kʋpwɛ? Yesu Krisito abɛɛ. Ayee! Mʋ i wu sa anɛ nɩɩ o kyiŋi lɩɩ lewu tɔ sa anɛ, nɩɩ ɔ tɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gyisa sʋ ɔ yɩlɛ tɔ ɛɛ kʋlɛ ɛɛ kyɛ anɛ tɔ.

eng: Who will separate us from Christs love? Will we be separated by trouble, or distress, or harassment, or famine, or nakedness, or danger, or sword?.
local: Mɩnɛ ɩ taalɛ barɩgɛ anɛ lɩɩ Krisito kebiti mʋ tɔ? Kayɩyɛɛ, abɛɛ kɩkpɩnɛ, abɛɛ kanyanla, abɛɛ akʋn, abɛɛ kitiri, atɔ mʋ nɩɩ a sʋ kufuu, abɛɛ kɩmɔmɔ? Ɩnɩmʋ kpini ɩ mɛɛ taalɛ barɩgɛ anɛ lɩɩ Yesu kebiti mʋ tɔ.

eng: As it is written, We are being put to death all day long for your sake. We are treated like sheep for slaughter. .
local: N tɔwɛ kanɩn lɩɩ fɛɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ ɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, O! Ɩbwaarɛ, kpalɩ fʋ sʋ bɛɛ mɔɔ anɛ kakɛ kʋmaa, nɩɩ ba taa anɛ waa fɛɛ nbʋlʋpʋ mʋ nɩɩ bee biti ba naa mɔɔ bamʋ mʋ.

eng: But in all these things we win a sweeping victory through the one who loved us.
local: Amaa lɩɩ Yesu Krisito kebiti mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ sa anɛ sʋ mʋ, an kɔ an lɩɩ ɩmʋ kpini‐kpini tɔ.

eng: Im convinced that nothing can separate us from Gods love in Christ Jesus our Lord: not death or life, not angels or rulers, not present things or future things, not powers.
local: Nɩɩ n nyi ɩ maa gyɛ kesintin fɛɛ lewu nɩn o, nkpa nɩn o, Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ nɩn o, nbwɩɩ libi nɩn o, atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ tɔ nbɩɩnbɩɩ nɩn o, atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ii biti ɩ ba nɩn o, kasɛ sʋ atɔ nɩn o, ɔlʋn nɩn o.

eng: or height or depth, or any other thing that is created.
local: sʋsʋ nɩn o, kasɛ tɔ kasɛ tɔ atɔ nɩn o, atɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tʋʋ mʋ, kʋkʋ mɛɛ taalɛ barɩgɛ kebiti mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ an naa mʋ nsana lɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu Krisito sʋ.

eng: Im speaking the truth in Christ—Im not lying, as my conscience assures me with the Holy Spirit.
local: Lɩɩ maa Krisito a maa gyɛ kʋkʋlʋn sʋ mʋ, asɩn mʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ falɛ ɩ gyɛ kesintin, n mɛɛ tɩn antɩŋɛsa. Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ yɛ mɛ nfɛɛrɛ tɔ ii gyii mɛ kesintin, fɛɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ falɛ ɩ man gyɛ antɩŋɛsa.

eng: I have great sadness and constant pain in my heart.
local: Mɛ ɔtɔ tɔ ɩ fɔyɩ mɛ nɩɩ mɛ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ ki nyita sa mɛ asa.

eng: I wish I could be cursed, cut off from Christ if it helped my brothers and sisters, who are my flesh-and-blood relatives.
local: Naafɔɔ nɩɩ ii biti ɩ waa mʋ, n biti n taa mɛ n‐yɩɩ sa kɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ tɔwɛ mɛ nnɔ, kɛ ɩ barɩgɛ maa Yesu nsana, kɛ ɩ yɛgɛ mɛ asa Ɩsɩrayɩ abi ba nyɛ nkpa.

eng: They are Israelites. The adoption as Gods children, the glory, the covenants, the giving of the Law, the worship, and the promises belong to them.
local: Ɩsɩrayɩ abi ba gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asa. Bamʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ nɩɩ ɔ taa bamʋ waa mʋ asa, bamʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ mʋ bʋnyaa kparɛ mʋ kaapʋ, nɩɩ maa bamʋ ba waa katɔwɛ kyula, nɩɩ ɔ sa bamʋ mʋ Nbara mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kaapʋ bamʋ ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ ba taa sʋ sun mʋ, nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ɔ da kɔkɔlɔ sʋ sa.

eng: The Jewish ancestors are theirs, and the Christ descended from those ancestors. He is the one who rules over all things, who is God, and who is blessed forever. Amen.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi banɩmʋ tɔ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asa kparɛ‐kparɛ mʋ ba lɩɩ, nɩɩ Krisito maa ba fɛɛ nyɩmɩsa kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ tɔ mʋ, ɔ lɩɩ kanɩn kɩnaana maŋa tɔ. Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋnɩ kʋtɔ kʋmaa kɩ bʋ mʋ kasɛ, nɩɩ ayɩn‐bi a gyɛ mʋ lɛɛ kakpaa. Amen!.

eng: But its not as though Gods word has failed. Not all who are descended from Israel are part of Israel.
local: Amaa ɩ man gyɛ yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ man waa asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ tɔwɛ yɩla mʋ tɔ nɩn. Kpalɩ fɛɛ man gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba lɩɩ Ɩsɩrayɩ kɩnaana mʋ tɔ kpini ɩ gyɛ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi kesintin.

eng: Not all of Abrahams children are called Abrahams descendants, but instead your descendants will be named through Isaac. .
local: Abɛɛ ɩ man gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba lɩɩ Abraham kɩnaana mʋ tɔ kpini ɩ gyɛ Abraham anaana‐bii. Lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ sa Abraham yɛ, Lɩɩ Isaki kanan tɔ nɩɩ fii biti fʋ nyɛ anaana‐bii.

eng: That means it isnt the natural children who are Gods children, but it is the children from the promise who are counted as descendants.
local: Asɩn mʋ kasɛ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, man gyɛ nbii mʋ nɩɩ Abraham kʋʋgɛ mʋ kpini ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mu‐bii ana. Amɔɔ bamʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ sa Abraham mʋ nkʋn ɩ gyɛ mʋ anaana‐bii.

eng: The words in the promise were: A year from now I will return, and Sarah will have a son .
local: Kpalɩ fɛɛ falɛ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ tɔwɛ Abraham, Kesu kakʋ falɛ saŋa kɛ n ba bwii ba mʋ, fʋ‐ka Sara ii biti ɔ kʋʋgɛ kebii‐nyɩnsa‐bii.

eng: Not only that, but also Rebecca conceived children with one man, our ancestor Isaac.
local: Man gyɛ ɩmʋ nkʋn. Amaa ɔkyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ ba tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Rebeka mʋ ɔ kʋʋgɛ nbwaara‐bii mʋ nɩɩ bamʋ‐sɛ gyɛ anɛ naana Isaki.

eng: When they hadnt been born yet and when they hadnt yet done anything good or bad, it was shown that Gods purpose would continue because it was based on his choice.
local: Amaa ba maa man pɩɩta kʋʋgɛ bamʋ gbaa kɛ ba waa asɩn danbɩrasa abɛɛ alibi mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ kaapʋ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ lɛɛ ɔkʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ mʋ, ɩ man bɔla ɔsa maa awaasa sʋ.

eng: It wasnt because of what was done but because of Gods call. This was said to her: The older child will be a slave to the younger one .
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ pɔyɩ ba kʋʋgɛ nbii mʋ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ Rebeka yɛ, Ɔbɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ ii biti o be sun kebii asɛ mʋ.

eng: As it is written, I loved Jacob, but I hated Esau .
local: Kanɩn nɩɩ ɔ wʋla tɔwɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ yɛ, N biti Gyekobu, amaa n kisi Esawu.

eng: So what are we going to say? Isnt this unfair on Gods part? Absolutely not!.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ nɛnɛ nɩɩ an biti a tɔwɛ? A tɔwɛ yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mɛɛ waa mʋ asɩn kyɛɛkyɛɛ abɛɛ? Ayee, ɩ man gyɛ kanɩn.

eng: He says to Moses, Ill have mercy on whomever I choose to have mercy, and Ill show compassion to whomever I choose to show compassion .
local: Kpalɩ fɛɛ ɔ tɔwɛ Mosesi yɛ, Ɔmʋ nɩɩ n biti n wu kʋwɛɛ mʋ, mʋ nɩɩ n biti n wu kʋwɛɛ, nɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ mɛ kɔkɔlɔ kɩɩ kyɔɔ mʋ sʋ mʋ, mʋ nɩɩ mɛ kɔkɔlɔ kii biti kɩ kyɔɔ mʋ sʋ.

eng: So then, it doesnt depend on a persons desire or effort. It depends entirely on God, who shows mercy.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ɩ man gyɛ nyɩmɩsa kɩpɩrɛ sʋ abɛɛ maa kpan mʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋ, amaa ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kʋwɛɛ kuwu nɩn.

eng: Scripture says to Pharaoh, I have put you in this position for this very thing: so I can show my power in you and so that my name can be spread through the entire earth. .
local: Kpalɩ fɛɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ mʋ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ Faro yɛ, Ɩnɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ n bun fu kuwura kɛ n bɔla fʋ sʋ lɛɛ mɛ ɔlʋn kaapʋ, kɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ dulinyaa kpini tɔ ba yɩn mɛ kɩtɩɩ.

eng: So then, God has mercy on whomever he wants to, but he makes resistant whomever he wants to.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ kpini ɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti o wu kʋwɛɛ mʋ, bamʋ nɩɩ ee wu kʋwɛɛ, nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ee biti ba waa mʋ kʋsʋ tɔ ɔlʋn mʋ, ɛɛ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ba waa kʋsʋ tɔ ɔlʋn.

eng: So you are going to say to me, Then why does he still blame people? Who has ever resisted his will.
local: Fanɛ tɔ ɔkʋ ii biti ɔ taasɛ mɛ yɛ, Nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ kanɩn bɩrɛ, ɔkʋ mɛɛ taalɛ kina Ɩbwaarɛ kebiti mʋ, mɩnɛ ɩ waa sʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ san ɛɛ pwɩɩ anɛ kʋsʋ tɔ ɔlʋn sʋ.

eng: You are only a human being. Who do you think you are to talk back to God? Does the clay say to the potter, Why did you make me like this? .
local: Amaa nyɩmɩsa, fʋ ɩ gyɛ anɩmʋ, nɩɩ fɩɩ taalɛ tɔwɛ gyaŋa Ɩbwaarɛ? Kʋtɔ pɔyɩsa ɩ taalɛ tɔwɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ pɔyɩ kɩmʋ mʋ yɛ, Mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fʋ pɔyɩ mɛ falɛ.

eng: Doesnt the potter have the power over the clay to make one pot for special purposes and another for garbage from the same lump of clay?.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔpɔyɩpʋ sʋ ɔkpa fɛɛ ɔ barɩgɛ kabɔlɛ kuli tɔ kɩnyɔ, kɛ ɔ taa kʋkʋ pɔyɩ lapɛ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ yɛgɛ bɛɛ yɩla kensi‐gyii akɛ, kɛ ɔ taa kʋkʋ pɔyɩ lapɛ mʋ nɩɩ bee gyii tɔ kakɛ kʋmaa.

eng: What if God very patiently puts up with pots made for wrath that were designed for destruction, because he wanted to show his wrath and to make his power known?.
local: Kanɩn ɔkpa kʋlʋn sʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti fɛɛ ɔ lɛɛ mʋ agbʋ kaapʋ asa, kɛ ba wu mʋ ɔlʋn mʋ, amaa bamʋ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ agbʋ bamʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ o nyita bamʋ mʋ, ɔ nyɛ kenyiita gaa sa bamʋ.

eng: What if he did this to make the wealth of his glory known toward pots made for mercy, which he prepared in advance for glory?.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ bɩla ee biti fɛɛ ɔ lɛɛ mʋ bʋnyaa kparɛ mʋ kaapʋ mʋ asa mʋ nɩɩ ee wu bamʋ kʋwɛɛ, nɩɩ ba gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɔŋɔ bamʋ yɩla sa mʋ bʋnyaa mʋ.

eng: We are the ones God has called. We dont come only from the Jews but we also come from the Gentiles.
local: Anɛ ɩ gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ mʋ, nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ tɔ nkʋn, amaa ɔ tɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ tɔ gbaa.

eng: As it says also in Hosea, I will call my people those who arent my people, and the one who isnt well loved, I will call loved one. .
local: Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Hoseya ɔwʋlɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ yɛ, Asa mʋ nɩɩ naafɔɔ ba man gyɛ mɛ asa mʋ, n biti n tɩɩ bamʋ mɛ asa. Nɩɩ ɔsʋwʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ n man sʋ kebiti sa bamʋ mʋ, n biti n biti bamʋ.

eng: And in the place where it was said to them, You arent my people, there they will be called the living Gods children .
local: Nɩɩ ii biti ɩ ba fɛɛ opula mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan man gyɛ mɛ asa mʋ, kanɩn opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ bee biti ba tɩɩ bamʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ nkpa wuya mu‐bii ana. .

eng: But Isaiah cries out for Israel, Though the number of Israels children will be like the sand of the sea, only a remaining part will be saved, .
local: Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Isaya gbaa kuusi tɔwɛ lɩɩ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi sʋ yɛ, Halɩɩ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi ba maa kyɔ fɛɛ ɔpʋ kɔnɔ kɩkyafa falɛ gbaa mʋ, bamʋ tɔ akalɩsa akʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ ba mɔlɩgɛ.

eng: because the Lord does what he says completely and quickly. .
local: Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti o gyii mʋ asɩn kasɛ sʋ bilen‐bilen kɛ ɔ lʋwɛ ɩmʋ kɛ ɔ tɩn ɩmʋ kɩdɩyɛ.

eng: As Isaiah prophesied, If the Lord of the heavenly forces had not left descendants for us, we would have been like Sodom, and we would have become like Gomorrah. .
local: I du nɩn fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Isaya wʋla tɔwɛ yɩla mʋ yɛ, Fɛɛ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ Ɔlʋn kpini wuya man dɩn dan yɛgɛ anɛ odu tɔ sa akʋ yɩla mʋ, naafɔɔ an dɩn muuri fɛɛ Sodom yɛ Gomora abi mʋ.

eng: So what are we going to say? Gentiles who werent striving for righteousness achieved righteousness, the righteousness that comes from faith.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ mɩnɛ nɩɩ an biti a tɔwɛ? An biti an tɔwɛ yɛ, bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi, nɩɩ bamʋ ansi an man payɩ bee biti ɩkpa kyiigisa mʋ, lɩɩ bamʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ Yesu gyii sʋ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ kɔɔlɛ bamʋ fɛɛ bamʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi mʋ asɛ.

eng: But though Israel was striving for a Law of righteousness, they didnt arrive.
local: Amaa Ɩsɩrayɩ abi mʋ bɩrɛ, ba payɩ ansi bee buu nbara mʋ bee biti fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ kɔɔlɛ bamʋ yɛ bamʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi mʋ asɛ, yɛgɛ ba man nyɛ ɩmʋ.

eng: Why? Its because they didnt go for it by faith but they went for it as if it could be reached by doing something. They have tripped over a stumbling block.
local: Mɩnɛ maa waa sʋ? Lɩɩ fɛɛ ba man taa kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ bee biti, amaa ba sʋ ba naa yɛ awaasa sʋ nɩn. Nɩɩ ba siti Kubuu sitisa mʋ lɩɩ tɩyɛ.

eng: As it is written: Look! Im putting a stumbling block in Zion, which is a rock that offends people. And the one who has faith in him will not be put to shame. .
local: fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ lɩɩ Yesu mʋ nɩɩ ɔ yɩlɛ sa kubuu nɩmʋ asɩn Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ yɛ, Fan kɩɩ! N taa kubuu mʋ nɩɩ kɩ yɛgɛ asa ba siti mʋ yɩla Siyon kadɛ tɔ. Kɛ kanɩn kɩfʋlɛ‐buu maŋa kɩ yɛgɛ asa ba lɩɩ bɛɛ tɩyɛ. Amaa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii mʋ, mɛɛ yɛ o gyii ipeeli.

eng: Brothers and sisters, my hearts desire is for Israels salvation. Thats my prayer to God for them.
local: Mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana, mɛ kɔkɔlɔ biti yɛ mɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɩ gyɛ yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ mɔlɩgɛ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi.

eng: I can vouch for them: they are enthusiastic about God. However, it isnt informed by knowledge.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ n taalɛ gyii kesintin lɩɩ bamʋ sʋ kaapʋ fɛɛ bɛɛ payɩ ansi bee buu Ɩbwaarɛ bɩrɛ, amaa bamʋ kensi payɩ man yɩlɛ kinyi sʋ.

eng: They dont submit to Gods righteousness because they dont understand his righteousness, and they try to establish their own righteousness.
local: Nɩɩ ba man nyi kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩɩ kɔɔlɛ asa fɛɛ bamʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi mʋ asɛ. Ɩmʋ sʋ bɛɛ kɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ gbaa‐gbaa bamʋ awaasa ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ bamʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi. Ɩmʋ sʋ ba man bɩya bamʋ n‐yɩɩ kasɛ ba buu Ɩbwaarɛ ɩkpa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ yɛgɛ o kyula fɛɛ bamʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi mʋ asɛ.

eng: Christ is the goal of the Law, which leads to righteousness for all who have faith in God.
local: Ɩmʋ kesintin ɩ gyɛ yɛ Krisito ɩ waa nbara mu kusun mʋ lʋwɛ, nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ mʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi mʋ asɛ.

eng: Moses writes about the righteousness that comes from the Law: The person who does these things will live by them. .
local: Mosesi kaapʋ asa kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba buu nbara mʋ, kɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ kɔɔlɛ bamʋ fɛɛ bamʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi mʋ asɛ. Nɩɩ ɔ kyʋrɔɔ yɛ, Ɔsa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o buu ɩnɩmʋ mʋ ee biti ɔ nyɛ nkpa lɩɩ ɩmʋ sʋ.

eng: But the righteousness that comes from faith talks like this: Dont say in your heart , Who will go up into heaven? .
local: Amaa fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ ɩ tɔwɛ lɩɩ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii mʋ nɩɩ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ kɔɔlɛ asa yɛ bamʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi mʋ asɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ, Man kan tɔwɛ fʋ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ yɛ, anɩmʋ ii biti o dii kpee sʋsʋ? .

eng: or Who will go down into the region below? .
local: abɛɛ man tɔwɛ yɛ, anɩmʋ ii biti ɔ kpɩlɩgɛ kpee kasɛ‐kasɛ tɔ? .

eng: But what does it say? The word is near you, in your mouth and in your heart .
local: Amaa nɛnɛ nɩɩ ɩ kyaga sʋ ɩ tɔwɛ? Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn mʋ i tiri fʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɩ bʋ fʋ kɔnɔ tɔ yɛ fʋ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ. Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii asɩn mʋ nɩɩ an tɔwɛ mʋ.

eng: Because if you confess with your mouth Jesus is Lord and in your heart you have faith that God raised him from the dead, you will be saved.
local: Nɩɩ fʋ kan taa fʋ kɔnɔ lɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Yesu ɩ gyɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ, nɩɩ fʋ kɔɔlɛ gyii fʋ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kyiŋi mʋ lɩɩ lewu tɔ mʋ, fii biti fʋ nyɛ kamɔlɩgɛ.

eng: Trusting with the heart leads to righteousness, and confessing with the mouth leads to salvation.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ nɩɩ nyɩmɩsa ɩ kɔɔlɛ ee gyii, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kɔɔlɛ mʋ fɛɛ mʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi. Nɩɩ ɔ kan lɛɛ kɔnɔ tɔwɛ yɛ Yesu ɩ gyɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ, ee biti ɔ nyɛ kamɔlɩgɛ.

eng: The scripture says, All who have faith in him wont be put to shame .
local: Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ ɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii mʋ, ɔ mɛɛ yɛ o gyii ipeeli kpaa.

eng: There is no distinction between Jew and Greek, because the same Lord is Lord of all, who gives richly to all who call on him.
local: Gyiwu abi ba man kwɩɩ yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ tɔ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ kʋlʋn ɩ gyɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ sa asa kpini. Nɩɩ ɔ sʋ kʋsee kʋkyɔ ɛɛ sa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ kɩtɩɩ mʋ.

eng: All who call on the Lords name will be saved. .
local: fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ ɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ kɩtɩɩ mʋ, ee biti ɔ nyɛ kamɔlɩgɛ.

eng: So how can they call on someone they dont have faith in? And how can they have faith in someone they havent heard of? And how can they hear without a preacher?.
local: Nɛnɛ nɩɩ bee biti ba waa kɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man pɩɩta kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii mʋ, ba taalɛ tɩɩ mʋ kɩtɩɩ? Nɩɩ ba man kan nu mʋ asɩn mʋ, nɛnɛ nɩɩ bee biti ba waa kɛ ba kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii? Nɩɩ ɔkʋ man pɩɩta tɔwɛ bamʋ abwaarɛsɩn mʋ, nɛnɛ nɩɩ bee biti ba waa kɛ ba nu?.

eng: And how can they preach unless they are sent? As it is written, How beautiful are the feet of those who announce the good news. .
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ man kan sun bamʋ yɛ ba naa tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ, nɛnɛ nɩɩ bee biti ba waa kɛ ba naa tɔwɛ asɩn mʋ? Ɩbwaarɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ ɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, Bamʋ nɩɩ ba naa bɛɛ tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ, bamʋ kɩnatɛ mʋ kɩ bʋrɔn gaa.

eng: But everyone hasnt obeyed the good news. As Isaiah says, Lord, who has had faith in our message? .
local: Amaa man gyɛ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi kpini ɩ kɔɔlɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ gyii. Lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Isaya tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ, anɩmʋ ɩ kɔɔlɛ anɛ asɩn tɔwɛsa mʋ gyii.

eng: So, faith comes from listening, but its listening by means of Christs message.
local: Kanɩn bɩrɛ lɩɩ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ kɩtɔwɛ lɩɩ Yesu Krisito sʋ nɩɩ asa bee nu asɩn mʋ. Nɩɩ lɩɩ asɩn mʋ kinu sʋ, nɩɩ asa bɛɛ kɔɔlɛ bee gyii.

eng: But I ask you, didnt they hear it? Definitely! Their voice has gone out into the entire earth, and their message has gone out to the corners of the inhabited world. .
local: Amaa n taasɛ yɛ, Gyiwu abi mʋ nɩɩ ba man kɔɔlɛ gyii mʋ, ba man nu asɩn mʋ nɩn? Ba nu yɛ bamʋ asʋ. Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ ɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, Asɩn danbɩrasa kɩtɔwɛ mʋ, ii kpee dulinyaa tɔ kpini, nɩɩ asɩn tɔwɛsa nɩmʋ i kpee ɩsʋwʋlɛ kpini sʋ.

eng: But I ask you again, didnt Israel understand? First, Moses says, I will make you jealous of those who arent a people, of a people without understanding. .
local: N bɩla taasɛ yɛ, Ɩsɩrayɩ abi ba man nu kasɛ nɩn? Ba nu kasɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ Mosesi wʋla gyankpaa tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ, N biti n taa bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ, nɩɩ ba man gyɛ kʋbʋ fɛɛ fanɛ mʋ, waa fanɛ kensi payɩ. N biti n taa asa mʋ nɩɩ ba man nyi mɛ mʋ waa fanɛ agbʋ.

eng: And Isaiah even dares to say, I was found by those who didnt look for me; I revealed myself to those who didnt ask for me. .
local: Nɩɩ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Isaya yɩlɛ kɩnyɩntɔ kyaga sʋ tɔwɛ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ, Bamʋ nɩɩ ba man kɩɩ biti mɛ gbaa mʋ, bamʋ nɩɩ n yɛgɛ nɩɩ ba wu mɛ. Nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man taasɛ mɛ sʋ mʋ, n lɛɛ mɛ n‐yɩɩ n kaapʋ bamʋ.

eng: But he says about Israel, All day long I stretched out my hands to a disobedient and contrary people. .
local: Amaa lɩɩ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi mʋ sʋ bɩrɛ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Halɩɩ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi ba maa bamʋ kʋsʋ tɔ ɔlʋn, yɛ bamʋ kikina mɛ sʋ mʋ, n san n da kɩbaa n tɩɩ bamʋ kakɛ kʋmaa yɛ ba ba mɛ asɛ. .

eng: So I ask you, has God rejected his people? Absolutely not! Im an Israelite, a descendant of Abraham, from the tribe of Benjamin.
local: Nɩɩ n taasɛ yɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ kina mʋ asa Ɩsɩrayɩ abi nɩn abɛɛ? Ayee! Ngbaa mɛ n gyɛ Ɩsɩrayɩ obii nɩn. Nɩɩ n gyɛ Abraham kanaana‐bii nɩɩ n lɩɩ Bengyamin kebunɔ.

eng: God hasnt rejected his people, whom he knew in advance. Or dont you know what the scripture says in the case of Elijah, when he pleads with God against Israel?.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ man kina mʋ asa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ wʋla o nyi bamʋ mʋ. Nɩɩ fan man nyiŋi asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Eligya tɔwɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ mʋ? Eligya maa fabɩlɛ ɛɛ sa Ɩbwaarɛ lɩɩ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi sʋ yɛ.

eng: Lord, they have killed your prophets, and they have torn down your altars. Im the only one left, and they are trying to take my life. .
local: Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ, ba mɔɔ fʋ atɔwɛpʋ kpini, nɩɩ ba boyi fʋ saraga nswɩɩ kpini. Nɩɩ mɛ nkʋn i sii nɩɩ bee biti ba mɔɔ mɛ.

eng: But what is Gods reply to him? I have kept for myself seven thousand people who havent bowed their knees to Baal. .
local: Amaa Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ mʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, N lɛɛ anyɩn ngbɩn nsunɔ yɩla sa mɛ n‐yɩɩ, nɩɩ bamʋ ɔkʋ gbaa man muŋa ɔdakpa Baali.

eng: So also in the present time there is a remaining group by the choice of Gods grace.
local: Kanɩn sʋ anɛ saŋa nɩmʋ tɔ gbaa, Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ mʋ asa kalɩsa akʋ lɩɩ mʋ kabwaala sʋ.

eng: But if it is by grace, it isnt by whats done anymore. If it were, Gods grace wouldnt be grace.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kɩlɛɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ kabwaala sʋ bɩrɛ, kanɩn bɩrɛ ɩ man bɩla ɩ gyɛ kusun mʋ nɩɩ nyɩmɩsa sun. Nɩɩ ɩ kan ɩ gyɛ kusun sʋ bɩrɛ, kabwaala man bɩla kɩ gyɛ kabwaala.

eng: So what? Israel didnt find what it was looking for. Those who were chosen found it, but the others were resistant.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ mɩnɛ? Kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi bɛɛ kpan bamʋ n‐yɩɩ bee biti mʋ ba man nyɛ kɩmʋ, amɔɔ bamʋ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ bamʋ mʋ ɩ nyɛ. Amaa bamʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba san mʋ nkɔlɔ ɩ bʋlʋn.

eng: As it is written, God gave them a dull spirit, so that their eyes would not see and their ears not hear, right up until the present day. .
local: Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Isaya tɔwɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ yɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ waa bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ tɔ fɛɛ amelensipu, nɩɩ ba mɛɛ taalɛ taa bamʋ ansi wu, nɩɩ ba mɛɛ taalɛ taa bamʋ asʋ gbaa nu ba fʋʋ ndaga kakɛ nɩmʋ.

eng: And David says, Their table should become a pitfall and a trap, a stumbling block and payback to them for what they have done. .
local: Nɩɩ Dawidi gbaa tɔwɛ lɩɩ Ɩlʋn ɩwʋlɛ mʋ tɔ yɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ yɛgɛ bamʋ ansi ogyikpa i biliŋi ɔdʋʋ da bamʋ, kɛ ɩ bɩla yɛgɛ ba lɩɩ tɩyɛ, kɛ ɩ baa ɩ gyɛ bamʋ kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ.

eng: Their eyes should be darkened so they cant see, and their backs always bent. .
local: Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ yɛgɛ bamʋ ansi i bun kɛ ba man bɩla wu, kɛ ɔ yɛgɛ awʋrʋfɔ i nu bamʋ kɛ ɩ yɛgɛ ba nyɔn tɔ kakpaa.

eng: So Im asking you: They havent stumbled so that theyve fallen permanently, have they? Absolutely not! But salvation has come to the Gentiles by their failure, in order to make Israel jealous.
local: Nɩɩ n bɩla taasɛ yɛ, Ɩsɩrayɩ abi ba maa siti tɩyɛ mʋ, ba mɛɛ bɩla taalɛ kʋsʋ? Ayee! Amaa lɩɩ bamʋ alibi sʋ mʋ, bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi mʋ bee biti ba ba nyɛ kamɔlɩgɛ kɛ ɩ yɛgɛ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi ba nyɛ kayɩɩ basa.

eng: But if their failure brings riches to the world, and their defeat brings riches to the Gentiles, how much more will come from the completion of their number!.
local: Nɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi alibi ɩnɩmʋ ɩ yɛgɛ dulinyaa tɔ asa kpini ba nyɛ atɔ kparɛ, nɩɩ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi kɩtɩyɛ kɩnɩmʋ kɩ yɛgɛ kɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi ba nyɛ tɔnɔ gaa mʋ, mɩnɛ tɔnɔ nɩɩ dulinyaa tɔ asa ba mɛɛ yɛ ba nyɛ nɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi mʋ ba kan bwii kɔɔlɛ gyii mʋ?.

eng: Im speaking to you Gentiles. Considering that Im an apostle to the Gentiles, I publicize my own ministry.
local: Nbɩɩnbɩɩ n tɔwɛ n sa fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fan man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ nɩn. Fɛɛ n maa gyɛ Krisito osunpu mʋ nɩɩ o sun mɛ fanɛ asɛ mʋ, kusun mʋ ki gyii mi ansi.

eng: in the hope that somehow I might make my own people jealous and save some of them.
local: N sʋ tamaa fɛɛ akʋtɔ mi kusun mʋ kii biti kɩ taalɛ yɛgɛ mɛ gbaa‐gbaa mɛ asa ba nyɛ kayɩɩ basa kɛ bamʋ akʋ ba nyɛ kamɔlɩgɛ.

eng: If their rejection has brought about a close relationship between God and the world, how can their acceptance mean anything less than life from the dead?.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kina Ɩsɩrayɩ abi mʋ, ɩmʋ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ dulinyaa abi ba bwii ba Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ. Kanɩn sʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bwii kɔɔlɛ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi mʋ, ii biti ɩ baa i du nɩn fɛɛ alewupu ii bwii kyiŋi ba nkpa tɔ.

eng: But if part of a batch of dough is offered to God as holy, the whole batch of dough is holy too. If a root is holy, the branches will be holy too.
local: Fɛɛ nɩɩ ba kan lɛɛ atɔ kʋ tɔ ɩkʋ waa saraga sa Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ, ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ san mʋ gbaa ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ nɩn. Fɛɛ nɩɩ ba taa oyu ilin waa saraga sa Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ, kanɩn bɩrɛ n‐yaabi mʋ kpini ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ.

eng: If some of the branches were broken off, and you were a wild olive branch, and you were grafted in among the other branches and shared the root that produces the rich oil of the olive tree.
local: Ɩsɩrayɩ abi ba du nɩn fɛɛ olifu oyu mʋ nɩɩ ba duu kɩmʋ kewu tɔ, nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ, ba du fɛɛ olifu oyu mʋ nɩɩ kɩ kɔyɩ afitiri tɔ mʋ. I du nɩn fɛɛ ba maa ŋɛ kewu tɔ oyu duusa mʋ kɩyaa‐bi kʋ fɔyɩ, kɛ ba taa afitiri tɔ lɛɛ mʋ kɩyaa‐bi ba ŋmina kyaga kuduusa mʋ sʋ. Nɩɩ ɩ kan ba kanɩn mʋ n‐yaabi mʋ nɩɩ ba ŋmina kyaga mʋ, ɩɩ nyɛ nkyu danbɩrasa ɩɩ lɩɩ oyu duusa mʋ ilin tɔ. Nɩɩ fan kɩɩ ɩnɩmʋ mʋ, fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fan man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ, fanɛ ɩ gyɛ n‐yaabi mʋ nɩɩ ba taa lɩɩ afitiri tɔ mʋ.

eng: then dont brag like youre better than the other branches. If you do brag, be careful: its not you that sustains the root, but its the root that sustains you.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan man kaapʋ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ fɛɛ fan kyɔ kewu tɔ oyu duusa mʋ n‐yaabi mʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ man gyɛ fanɛ ɩɩ sa ilin mʋ nkpa, amaa ilin mʋ ɩɩ sa fanɛ nkpa.

eng: You will say then, Branches were broken off so that I could be grafted in.
local: Akʋtɔ fii biti fʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, Ba ŋɛ n‐yaabi fɔyɩ kɛ ba nyɛ taa mɛ ŋmina kyaga sʋ.

eng: Fine. They were broken off because they werent faithful, but you stand only by your faithfulness. So dont think in a proud way; instead be afraid.
local: Ɩ bʋ tɔ kanɩn. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ba maa man kɔɔlɛ gyii sʋ nɩɩ ba ŋɛ bamʋ fɔyɩ. Nɩɩ fanɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ nɩɩ fan ba yii bamʋ ayaa tɔ. Kanɩn sʋ fan man kan kʋsaa fanɛ n‐yɩɩ, amaa fan baa fan daa fanɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ.

eng: If God didnt spare the natural branches, he wont spare you either.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ fan baa fen nyi fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ maa man yɛgɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ nɩɩ ba yɩlɛ sa oyu duusa mʋ kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ mʋ, kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ yɛgɛ fanɛ gbaa.

eng: So look at Gods kindness and harshness. Its harshness toward those who fell, but its Gods kindness for you, provided you continue in his kindness; otherwise, you could be cut off too.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan kɩɩ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wu asa kʋwɛɛ yɛ mʋ katalɛ ɔkpa sʋ. Bamʋ nɩɩ ba waa mʋ kʋsʋ ɔlʋn mʋ, ɛɛ talɛ bamʋ ɔkpa sʋ, amaa fanɛɛ fanɛ bɩrɛ ee su fanɛ kʋwɛɛ. Nɩɩ fan kan kyaga sʋ kɔɔlɛ mʋ kʋwɛɛ kuwu gyii mʋ, ee biti ɔ kyaga sʋ ee wu fanɛ kʋwɛɛ. Nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ kanɩn mʋ, ee biti ɔ ŋɛ fanɛ tʋʋ.

eng: And even those who were cut off will be grafted back in if they dont continue to be unfaithful, because God is able to graft them in again.
local: Nɩɩ Gyiwu abi bɩrɛ, nɩɩ ba kan yɛgɛ bamʋ kɩman kɔɔlɛ gyii mʋ bɩrɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti o bwiiyaa bamʋ ba kyaga oyu duusa mʋ sʋ. Ɩbwaarɛ sʋ ɔlʋn ɔ taalɛ waa kanɩn.

eng: If you were naturally part of a wild olive tree and you were cut off from it, and then, contrary to nature, you were grafted into the cultivated olive tree, wont these natural branches stand an even better chance of being grafted back onto their own olive tree?.
local: Fan kɩɩ, fanɛ gbaa‐gbaa fanɛ ɩ gyɛ afitiri tɔ olifu n‐yaabi, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ŋɛ fanɛ lɩɩ tʋtɔ ba kyaga kewu tɔ olifu oyu duusa mʋ sʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ ɩ mɛɛ yɛ ɩ ba ɩ bʋlʋn sa Ɩbwaarɛ fɛɛ o bwiiyaa kewu oyu duusa mʋ n‐yaabi ba kyaga kɩmʋ gbaa‐gbaa sʋ.

eng: I dont want you to be unaware of this secret, brothers and sisters. That way you wont think too highly of yourselves. A part of Israel has become resistant until the full number of the Gentiles comes in.
local: Mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana, n biti fɛɛ fan baa fen nyi falɛ asɩn baalasa nɩmʋ sʋ, kɛ fanɛ tɔ ɔkʋ ɔ man kʋsaa mʋ n‐yɩɩ. Gyiwu abi akʋ ba nyɛ kɔkɔlɔ ɔsɩɩ naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ gaalaagaa ba ba kɔɔlɛ Yesu gyii.

eng: In this way, all Israel will be saved, as it is written: The deliverer will come from Zion. He will remove ungodly behavior from Jacob. .
local: Lɩɩ falɛ ɔkpa sʋ nɩɩ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi kpini bee biti ba nyɛ kamɔlɩgɛ. Kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ yɛ, Siyon kadɛ tɔ nɩɩ Ɔmɔlɩgɛpʋ mʋ ii biti ɔ lɩɩ. Ee biti ɔ ba yɛgɛ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi kpini ba yɛgɛ asɩn gyaga kɩwaa.

eng: This is my covenant with them, when I take away their sins .
local: Nɩɩ n kan taa bamʋ alibi kɛɛ bamʋ mʋ, ɩmʋ ii biti ɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ maa bamʋ katɔwɛ kyula mʋ kɩ ba tɔ.

eng: According to the gospel, they are enemies for your sake, but according to Gods choice, they are loved for the sake of their ancestors.
local: Lɩɩ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ kekina sʋ mʋ, ba biliŋi Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ‐dʋn ana. Amaa bamʋ kekina mʋ ɩ yɛgɛ fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fan man gyɛ Gyiwu abi fan nyɛ tɔnɔ. Amaa lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kɩlɛɛ tɔ mʋ, ba gyɛ mʋ asa bitisa lɩɩ bamʋ naana ana sʋ.

eng: Gods gifts and calling cant be taken back.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kan kɛɛ ɔkʋ kakɛɛ abɛɛ nɩɩ ɔ kan tɩɩ ɔkʋ mʋ, ɔ man bɩla yɛ ɔ kyɛɛgɛ mʋ nfɛɛrɛ.

eng: Once you were disobedient to God, but now you have mercy because they were disobedient.
local: Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fan man gyɛ Gyiwu abi fan kyɩna waa Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ kʋsʋ tɔ ɔlʋn, nɩɩ nbɩɩnbɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wu fanɛ kʋwɛɛ, kpalɩ fɛɛ Gyiwu abi ba maa waa kʋsʋ tɔ ɔlʋn sʋ.

eng: In the same way, they have also been disobedient because of the mercy that you received, so now they can receive mercy too.
local: lɩɩ kanɩn maŋa sʋ nɩɩ nbɩɩnbɩɩ ngbaa bamʋ bɛɛ waa kʋsʋ tɔ ɔlʋn, kɛ ɩ ba fɛɛ ngbaa bamʋ Ɩbwaarɛ o wu bamʋ kʋwɛɛ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ o wu fanɛ gbaa fanɛ kʋwɛɛ mʋ.

eng: God has locked up all people in disobedience, in order to have mercy on all of them.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛgɛ nɩɩ asa kpini kʋsʋ tɔ ɔlʋn gyii bamʋ sʋ, kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɩ waa kɛ o wu bamʋ kʋwɛɛ.

eng: Gods riches, wisdom, and knowledge are so deep! They are as mysterious as his judgments, and they are as hard to track as his paths!.
local: Fan kɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔ kparɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ mʋ. Ɩbwaarɛ kanyaasɩn yɛ mʋ kinyi man sʋ ɔkaa. Anɩmʋ ɩ taalɛ bɩɩ mʋ ntalɛ tɔ kyɛɛkyɛɛ? Nɩɩ anɩmʋ ɩ taalɛ kɩɩ biti wu mʋ ɩkpa naa lʋwɛ?.

eng: Who has known the Lords mind? Or who has been his mentor? .
local: Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ yɩla Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ yɛ, Nɩɩ anɩmʋ i nyi Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ nfɛɛrɛ abɛɛ anɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ mʋ ɔtɔwɛwaapʋ.

eng: Or who has given him a gift and has been paid back by him? .
local: Nɩɩ anɩmʋ ɩ tɛɛ sa Ɩbwaarɛ atɔ kʋ, kɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ ba ka ɩmʋ kʋkɔ.

eng: All things are from him and through him and for him. May the glory be to him forever. Amen.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ atɔ kpini ɩ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ, nɩɩ mʋ ɔlʋn sʋ nɩɩ ɩmʋ kpini ɩ bʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɩmʋ kpini ɩ gyɛ mʋ lɛɛ. Mʋ nɩɩ bʋnyaa gyɛ mʋ lɛɛ kakpaa. Ɩ ba kanɩn.

eng: So, brothers and sisters, because of Gods mercies, I encourage you to present your bodies as a living sacrifice that is holy and pleasing to God. This is your appropriate priestly service.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana, n sʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kʋwɛɛ kuwu mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ sa anɛ mʋ, n kʋlɛ fanɛ fɛɛ fan taa fanɛ n‐yɩɩ kpini sa Ɩbwaarɛ, kɛ ɩ waa fɛɛ saraga kɔlɔŋɔ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ tɛ nkpa tɔ, nɩɩ i du kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ, nɩɩ ɩ bɔla Ɩbwaarɛ ansi. Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ fanɛ kabwaarɛ‐sun mʋ nɩɩ ɩ lɩɩ fanɛ nkɔlɔ tɔ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa.

eng: Dont be conformed to the patterns of this world, but be transformed by the renewing of your minds so that you can figure out what Gods will is—what is good and pleasing and mature.
local: Fan man kasɛ dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ asɩn waasa, amaa fan kyɛɛgɛ fanɛ kɩbaabʋtɔ, kɛ fan nyɛ nfɛɛrɛ pʋpwɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋrɔn. Ɩmʋ ii biti ɩ yɛgɛ fan taalɛ kaala bɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kebiti yɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋrɔn yɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ i gyii mu ansi yɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ dɛ kyɛɛkyɛɛ naa fʋʋ ɔkaa.

eng: Because of the grace that God gave me, I can say to each one of you: dont think of yourself more highly than you ought to think. Instead, be reasonable since God has measured out a portion of faith to each one of you.
local: N bɔla Ɩbwaarɛ bwaala kɛkɛɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa sa mɛ mʋ sʋ, n tɔwɛ n sa fanɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ yɛ, ɔ man kɩɩ mʋ n‐yɩɩ ɔ kyɔ kanan mʋ nɩɩ o du, amaa ɔ bɩya mʋ nfɛɛrɛ kasɛ lɩɩ mʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kaala sa mʋ sʋ mʋ.

eng: We have many parts in one body, but the parts dont all have the same function.
local: Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ anɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ sʋ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ kʋlʋn yɛ ɩkyagakpa kʋkwɩɩ‐kʋkwɩɩ, nɩɩ kʋkʋmaa nɩmʋ sʋ kɩmʋ kusun sunsa mʋ.

eng: In the same way, though there are many of us, we are one body in Christ, and individually we belong to each other.
local: kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ a maa kyɔ gbaa mʋ, an bʋga waa kʋbʋ kʋlʋn Krisito tɔ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ sʋ ɩkyagakpa kʋkwɩɩ‐kʋkwɩɩ, nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ kayɩɩ kʋlʋn mʋ.

eng: We have different gifts that are consistent with Gods grace that has been given to us. If your gift is prophecy, you should prophesy in proportion to your faith.
local: Nɩɩ an sʋ kabwaala kakɛɛ kʋkwɩɩ‐kʋkwɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ taa sa anɛ. Nɩɩ ɔkʋ lɛɛ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ maa bɔla mʋ sʋ ɛɛ tɔwɛ asɩn mʋ, ɔ taa waa Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ kusun fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ mʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii kɩ kaapʋ mʋ.

eng: If your gift is service, devote yourself to serving. If your gift is teaching, devote yourself to teaching.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ lɛɛ kan ɔ gyɛ ɔ taa sun mʋ tɔɔmaa ana mʋ, ɔ taa sun bamʋ. Nɩɩ ɔkʋ lɛɛ kan ɔ gyɛ kɩkaapʋ mʋ, ɔ taa kaapʋ asa.

eng: If your gift is encouragement, devote yourself to encouraging. The one giving should do it with no strings attached. The leader should lead with passion. The one showing mercy should be cheerful.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ lɛɛ kan ɔ gyɛ kɩtɔwɛ waa mʋ, ɔ tɔwɛ waa. Nɩɩ ɔkʋ lɛɛ kan ɔ gyɛ kɛkɛɛ sa asa mʋ nɩɩ ii tiri bamʋ mʋ, ɔ lɛɛ mʋ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ sa. Nɩɩ ɔkʋ lɛɛ kan ɔ gyɛ kɩbɩlɩsa mʋ, ɔ kɩɩ sʋ kpan mʋ n‐yɩɩ waa. Nɩɩ ɔkʋ mɔɔ lɛɛ kan ɔ gyɛ kʋwɛɛ kuwu mʋ, o bugi mʋ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ su asa sʋ.

eng: Love should be shown without pretending. Hate evil, and hold on to what is good.
local: Fan biti abaa lɩɩ fanɛ nkɔlɔ tɔ. Fan yɛgɛ alibi ɩ waa fanɛ kʋtɔ kisisa, kɛ fan kɩtaa kɩdanbɩrasa tɔ kinkin.

eng: Love each other like the members of your family. Be the best at showing honor to each other.
local: Fan biti fanɛ bɩrɩsa ana Krisito abi fɛɛ kinyi‐bii. Kɛ fan taa bɛɛrɛɛ sa abaa fan kyɔ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ.

eng: Dont hesitate to be enthusiastic—be on fire in the Spirit as you serve the Lord!.
local: Fan kpan fanɛ n‐yɩɩ, kɛ fan man waa wʋlʋgyɔ, amaa fan taa fanɛ ɔlʋn kpini sun Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ.

eng: Be happy in your hope, stand your ground when youre in trouble, and devote yourselves to prayer.
local: Fan yɛgɛ fanɛ ansi i gyii lɩɩ fanɛ tamaa mʋ nɩɩ fan sʋ mʋ sʋ. Nɩɩ fɛn kan fen gyii awʋrʋfɔ mʋ, fan nyɛ kɔkɔlɔ yɩlɛ tɔ, kɛ fan kyaga sʋ fɛn kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ saŋa kʋmaa.

eng: Contribute to the needs of Gods people, and welcome strangers into your home.
local: Nɩɩ fanɛ bɩrɩsa ana Krisito abuupu mʋ nɩɩ atɔ kʋ i tiri bamʋ mʋ, fɛn kyɛ bamʋ tɔ, kɛ fan waa aniya kɩtaa afɔ fanɛ akpaara sʋ.

eng: Bless people who harass you—bless and dont curse them.
local: Fan kʋlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ waa bamʋ nɩɩ bee kisi fanɛ gbaa mʋ kusee. Ɛɛhn! Fan kʋlɛ mʋ ɔ waa bamʋ kusee, kɛ fan man tɔwɛ bamʋ nnɔ.

eng: Be happy with those who are happy, and cry with those who are crying.
local: Bamʋ nɩɩ bee gyii ansi mʋ, fan naa bamʋ fan bʋga gyii ansi, nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ bɔta bee su mʋ, fan naa bamʋ fɛn bɔta tɔ su.

eng: Consider everyone as equal, and dont think that youre better than anyone else. Instead, associate with people who have no status. Dont think that youre so smart.
local: Fan waa kɔnɔ kʋlʋn tɔwɛ kyula abaa. Fan man kaapʋ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ, amaa fan taa fanɛ n‐yɩɩ bʋga asa mʋ nɩɩ ba man bʋ sɛhn tɔ mʋ. Fan man fɛ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ fan nyi fan kyɔ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ.

eng: Dont pay back anyone for their evil actions with evil actions, but show respect for what everyone else believes is good.
local: Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa fanɛ alibi mʋ, fan man taa alibi ka mʋ kʋkɔ. Fan kɩɩ yɛ ɔdan kɛ fan waa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa ii biti o wu fɛɛ ɩ bʋrɔn.

eng: If possible, to the best of your ability, live at peace with all people.
local: Fan waa fanɛ abaa ɔkaa lɛɛ, kɛ fan naa ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ fan kyɩna kayɩɩ yuuli tɔ.

eng: Dont try to get revenge for yourselves, my dear friends, but leave room for Gods wrath. It is written, Revenge belongs to me; I will pay it back, says the Lord .
local: Mɛ kyɛmɩnɛ ana, fan man taa alibi ka ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa fanɛ alibi mʋ kʋkɔ. Fan yɛgɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa sa Ɩbwaarɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ ɩ tɔwɛ yɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Mɛ ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ n tɛɛla asa, nɩɩ n ka alibi kʋkɔ.

eng: Instead, If your enemy is hungry, feed him; if he is thirsty, give him a drink. By doing this, you will pile burning coals of fire upon his head .
local: Nɩɩ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa ɩ bɩla tɔwɛ yɛ, Fɛɛ nɩɩ akʋn ɩ kan ɩ sʋ fu okisipu mʋ, sa mʋ agyitɔ kɛ o gyii, nɩɩ bʋlɩfɔ kan ɔ sʋ mʋ mʋ, sa mʋ nkyu kɛ o nuu. Fʋ nyɛ nɩɩ fʋ waa kanɩn mʋ, i du nɩn fɛɛ fʋ maa kʋʋla afʋlɔn‐gyi gyaga mu kumu nsana.

eng: Dont be defeated by evil, but defeat evil with good.
local: Fan man yɛgɛ alibi i gyii fanɛ sʋ, amaa fan taa fanɛ adanbɩrasa gyii alibi sʋ.

eng: Every person should place themselves under the authority of the government. There isnt any authority unless it comes from God, and the authorities that are there have been put in place by God.
local: Ɩ dagaa fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ maa bɩya mʋ n‐yɩɩ kasɛ sa kɩbanɛ alʋlʋnpʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔlʋnpʋ kʋ man bʋ tɔ, amɔɔ ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ yɩla mʋ. Nɩɩ alʋlʋnpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ tɔ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ i yii bamʋ.

eng: So anyone who opposes the authority is standing against what God has established. People who take this kind of stand will get punished.
local: Kanɩn sʋ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kʋsʋ ɛɛ yɩlɛ ɔlʋnpʋ kʋ sʋ mʋ, ɛɛ kʋsʋ ɛɛ yɩlɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ yɩla mʋ sʋ. Nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa kanɩn mʋ, bee biti ba bɩya kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ sa bamʋ n‐yɩɩ.

eng: The authorities dont frighten people who are doing the right thing. Rather, they frighten people who are doing wrong. Would you rather not be afraid of authority? Do whats right, and you will receive its approval.
local: Kpalɩ fɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa adanbɩrasa mʋ ba man sɩlɛ ɩsʋwʋlɛ akɩɩsʋpʋ, amɔɔ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa alibi nkʋn ɩ sɩlɛ bamʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ fen mee biti fan baa fan sɩlɛ alʋlʋnpʋ mʋ, kanɩn bɩrɛ fan baa fɛn waa adanbɩrasa, kɛ ɩ yɛgɛ ba yɩn fanɛ.

eng: It is Gods servant given for your benefit. But if you do whats wrong, be afraid because it doesnt have weapons to enforce the law for nothing. It is Gods servant put in place to carry out his punishment on those who do what is wrong.
local: Kpalɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ lɛɛ bamʋ waa akɩɩsʋpʋ kɛ ɩ kyɛ fanɛ tɔ kɛ ɩ waa ɔdan sa fanɛ. Amaa nɩɩ fan waa alibi bɩrɛ, fan sɩlɛ bamʋ kpalɩ fɛɛ man gyɛ kɩyan sʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ ɔlʋn fanɛ sʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ba gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ayaafɔlɛ mʋ nɩɩ ba yɩlɛ mʋ ayaa tɔ ba bɩɩtɛ alibi awaapʋ asʋ.

eng: That is why it is necessary to place yourself under the governments authority, not only to avoid Gods punishment but also for the sake of your conscience.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ fan bɩya fanɛ n‐yɩɩ kasɛ sa alʋlʋnpʋ mʋ. Man gyɛ kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ nkʋn sʋ, amaa fen nyi fanɛ nfɛɛrɛ tɔ fɛɛ ɩmʋ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ fan waa.

eng: You should also pay taxes for the same reason, because the authorities are Gods assistants, concerned with this very thing.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ kpini sʋ mʋ fan ka fanɛ tʋʋtʋʋ sa kɩbanɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ alʋlʋnpʋ mʋ ba gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ayaafɔlɛ nɩn, nɩɩ ba sʋ bamʋ saŋa kpini bɛɛ waa bamʋ kusun.

eng: So pay everyone what you owe them. Pay the taxes you owe, pay the duties you are charged, give respect to those you should respect, and honor those you should honor.
local: Fan ka ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ kʋkɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa mʋ. Nɩɩ ɩ kan ɩ gyɛ tʋʋtʋʋ mʋ fan ka tʋʋtʋʋ. Nɩɩ ɩ kan ɩ gyɛ lenpoo mʋ, fan ka lenpoo. Nɩɩ ɩ kan ɩ gyɛ n‐yɩɩ kɩbɩya kasɛ mʋ, fan bɩya n‐yɩɩ kasɛ. Nɩɩ ɩ kan ɩ gyɛ bɛɛrɛɛ mɔɔ mʋ, fan taa bɛɛrɛɛ sa.

eng: Dont be in debt to anyone, except for the obligation to love each other. Whoever loves another person has fulfilled the Law.
local: Fan man yɛgɛ ɔkʋ kʋkɔ ki sii fanɛ sʋ, amaa kʋkɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ dagaa kɩ dɛ fanɛ sʋ mʋ, ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ fan biti abaa. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ kebiti sa mʋ bɩrɩsa mʋ, mʋ ii buu Mosesi nbara mʋ sʋ kyɛɛkyɛɛ.

eng: The commandments, Dont commit adultery, dont murder, dont steal, dont desire what others have, and any other commandments, are all summed up in one word: You must love your neighbor as yourself. .
local: Mosesi nbara mʋ ɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, Man lɩɩ fʋ‐ka abɛɛ fu‐kuli kamaa, man mɔɔ nyɩmɩsa, man kan yuuri, man kan yɛgɛ fu ansi ɩ payɩ ɔkʋ atɔ sʋ. Nbara mʋ yɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ bɩla i tii sʋ mʋ, ɩmʋ kpini kumu ɩ gyɛ nbara mʋ nɩɩ ɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, Biti fʋ bɩrɩsa fɛɛ fʋ n‐yɩɩ.

eng: Love doesnt do anything wrong to a neighbor; therefore, love is what fulfills the Law.
local: Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ kebiti sa mʋ bɩrɩsa mʋ, mɛɛ waa mʋ alibi. Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ fʋ kan fii biti fʋ bɩrɩsa ana mʋ, fu buu nbara mʋ nɩmʋ.

eng: As you do all this, you know what time it is. The hour has already come for you to wake up from your sleep. Now our salvation is nearer than when we first had faith.
local: Kanɩn sʋ fan buu nbara nɩmʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ fen nyi nbɩɩnbɩɩ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ an bʋ kɩmʋ tɔ falɛ. Saŋa mʋ fʋʋ fɛɛ fan kyiŋi lɩɩ fanɛ nsɩɩrɛ tɔ, lɩɩ fɛɛ anɛ kamɔlɩgɛ saŋa mʋ tiri tɔ nbɩɩnbɩɩ ɩ kyɔ kakɛ mʋ nɩɩ an kɔɔlɛ Yesu gyii mʋ.

eng: The night is almost over, and the day is near. So lets get rid of the actions that belong to the darkness and put on the weapons of light.
local: Kagyanbwɛ kɩ tɛɛ kɩɩ lʋwɛ, nɩɩ kayɩkɛ mɔɔ kii tiri tɔ. Ɩmʋ sʋ fan yɛgɛ a lɛɛ kitentenbiri awaasa mʋ tʋʋ, kɛ a taa fɩtɩla atɔ mʋ nɩɩ an taa kɔ kitentenbiri tɔ awaasa mʋ taa bun.

eng: Lets behave appropriately as people who live in the day, not in partying and getting drunk, not in sleeping around and obscene behavior, not in fighting and obsession.
local: Fan yɛgɛ anɛ kɩbaabʋtɔ kɩ baa kɩ bʋrɔn yɛgɛ kɩ kɩɩ tɔ fɛɛ kakyʋwapɛ, kɛ an man nyita saŋa iseeli libi abɛɛ kata‐bʋʋ tɔ, abɛɛ anɛ tɔ ɔkʋ ɔ man lɩɩ mʋ‐ka abɛɛ mu‐kuli kamaa, an man waa ineesi atɔ, kɛ a man kɔ nluu kɛ a man waa kensi payɩ libi yɛ abaa.

eng: Instead, dress yourself with the Lord Jesus Christ, and dont plan to indulge your selfish desires.
local: Amaa fan taa Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu Krisito waa fɛɛ fanɛ ɩkaalɛ bunsa. Kɛ fan man fɛ nfɛɛrɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fanɛ alibi kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ mʋ kɩ taa waa kɩmʋ kebiti.

eng: Welcome the person who is weak in faith—but not in order to argue about differences of opinion.
local: Nɩɩ fanɛ akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii kɩ man bʋlʋn mʋ, fan kɔɔlɛ mʋ, amaa fan man gyii mʋ ɩmɔɔrɛ.

eng: One person believes in eating everything, while the weak person eats only vegetables.
local: A taa fɛɛ ɔkʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii kɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ɛɛ taalɛ gyii kʋtɔ kʋmaa, amaa ɔkʋ mʋ nɩɩ mʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii kɩ man bʋlʋn mʋ, ee gyii afatɛɛ ɩpʋ nkʋn.

eng: Those who eat must not look down on the ones who dont, and the ones who dont eat must not judge the ones who do, because God has accepted them.
local: Ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ee gyii kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ mʋ, ɔ man kɩɩ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ o mee gyii ɩmʋ mʋ kɩyan. Nɩɩ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ o mee gyii kanɩn atɔ mʋ, ɔ man pwɩɩ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ee gyii kanɩn atɔ mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kyula sa mʋ.

eng: Who are you to judge someone elses servants? They stand or fall before their own Lord .
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ, fʋ ɩ gyɛ anɩmʋ nɩɩ fɩɩ pwɩɩ ɔkʋ kɩyaafɔlɛ? Mʋ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ gbaa‐gbaa ɩ taalɛ wu fɛɛ ɔ yɩlɛ tɔ abɛɛ ɔ waa tɩyɛ. Nɩɩ ee biti ɔ taalɛ yɩlɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ yɛgɛ ɔ taalɛ yɩlɛ.

eng: One person considers some days to be more sacred than others, while another person considers all days to be the same. Each person must have their own convictions.
local: Ɔkʋ sʋ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ kakɛ kʋ ki tiri kɩ kyɔ nkɛ nkʋ, nɩɩ ɔkʋ mɔɔ ɩ kɩɩ fɛɛ nkɛ mʋ kpini ɩ gyɛ kyɛɛkyɛɛ. Fanɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ nfɛɛrɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ fɛ yɛ ɩ bʋrɔn mʋ, ɔ yɩlɛ ɩmʋ tɔ.

eng: Someone who thinks that a day is sacred, thinks that way for the Lord. Those who eat, eat for the Lord, because they thank God. And those who dont eat, dont eat for the Lord, and they thank the Lord too.
local: Ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kɩɩ fɛɛ kakɛ kʋ ki tiri mʋ, ɛɛ waa kanɩn ɛɛ sa Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ. Nɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ee gyii kunun mʋ, ee gyii yɛgɛ ɛɛ taa bɛɛrɛɛ ɛɛ sa Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ sʋ afwaala ɛɛ sa Ɩbwaarɛ. Nɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ o mee gyii mɔɔ mʋ, ɛɛ waa kanɩn ɔ sʋ bɛɛrɛɛ ɛɛ sa Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ, kɛ mʋ gbaa mʋ ɔ taa afwaala sa Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: We dont live for ourselves and we dont die for ourselves.
local: Nɩɩ anɛ ɔkʋ man bʋ tɔ nɩɩ ɔ tɛ sa mʋ n‐yɩɩ, nɩɩ kanɩn gbaa nɩɩ fu wu mʋ, fʋ man wu sa fʋ n‐yɩɩ.

eng: If we live, we live for the Lord, and if we die, we die for the Lord. Therefore, whether we live or die, we belong to God.
local: Nɩɩ an kan a tɛ nkpa tɔ, an tɛ sa Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ nɩn. Nɩɩ an kan wu gbaa mʋ, an san an gyɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ lɛɛ. Ɩmʋ sʋ an tɛ nkpa tɔ nɩn oo, an wu nɩn oo, an gyɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ lɛɛ.

eng: This is why Christ died and lived: so that he might be Lord of both the dead and the living.
local: Krisito wu nɩɩ o bwii kyiŋi ba nkpa tɔ, lɩɩ falɛ sʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba tɛ nkpa tɔ yɛ alewupu.

eng: But why do you judge your brother or sister? Or why do you look down on your brother or sister? We all will stand in front of the judgment seat of God.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fii bun fʋ bɩrɩsa kʋpwɛ, abɛɛ nɩɩ fɩɩ kɩɩ fʋ bɩrɩsa kɩyan? Lɩɩ fɛɛ anɛ kpini an biti an naa yɩlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ansi tɔ, kɛ o gyii anɛ asɩn.

eng: Because it is written, As I live, says the Lord, every knee will bow to me, and every tongue will give praise to God. .
local: Kpalɩ fɛɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ Isaya Asɩnkyan yɩlasa ɔwʋlɛ mʋ tɔ yɛ, N maa dan n bʋ tɔ falɛ, ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ ii biti o muŋa mɛ, kɛ nyɩmɩsa kʋmaa nɩmʋ o bugi kɔnɔ tɔwɛ yɛ mɛ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: So then, each of us will give an account of ourselves to God.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ anɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ ii biti ɔ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ lɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa mʋ sa Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: So stop judging each other. Instead, this is what you should decide: never put a stumbling block or obstacle in the way of your brother or sister.
local: Kanɩn sʋ, fan yɛgɛ kɛ a yɛgɛ abaa kʋpwɛ kubun. Amaa fan man waa atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ii biti ɩ yɛgɛ fanɛ bɩrɩsa ana ba siti abɛɛ ba waa alibi.

eng: I know and Im convinced in the Lord Jesus that nothing is wrong to eat in itself. But if someone thinks something is wrong to eat, it becomes wrong for that person.
local: Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu maa lɛɛ kaapʋ mɛ, nɩɩ n kɔɔlɛ gyii fɛɛ kʋtɔ kʋ kɩ man bʋ tɔ nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ kʋtɔ kisisa kɩmʋ kɩbaa sʋ. Amaa nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan kɩɩ fɛɛ kʋtɔ kʋ kɩ gyɛ ikisii bɩrɛ, kɩ gyɛ ikisii sa mʋ.

eng: If your brother or sister is upset by your food, you are no longer walking in love. Dont let your food destroy someone for whom Christ died.
local: Fɛɛ nɩɩ ɩ kan ɩ gyɛ fu agyitɔ gyisa sʋ, nɩɩ fʋ bɩrɩsa kɔkɔlɔ ki nyita mʋ mʋ, kanɩn bɩrɛ fʋ awaasa ɩ mɛɛ kaapʋ kebiti. Man taa fu agyitɔ gyisa nyita fʋ bɩrɩsa mʋ nɩɩ Krisito wu sa mʋ mʋ.

eng: And dont let something you consider to be good be criticized as wrong.
local: Man yɛgɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ fii nyi fɛɛ kɩ gyɛ kʋtɔ danbɩrasa mʋ, ki biliŋi kʋtɔ libi asa asɛ.

eng: Gods kingdom isnt about eating food and drinking but about righteousness, peace, and joy in the Holy Spirit.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii tɔ kakyɩna mʋ tɔ mʋ, ɩ man gyɛ kegyi yɛ kunuu asɩn nɩn, amaa ɩ gyɛ awaasa danbɩrasa yɛ kayɩɩ yuuli yɛ kensi‐gyii mʋ nɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ kɩɩ sa mʋ.

eng: Whoever serves Christ this way pleases God and gets human approval.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ee sun Krisito falɛ ɔkpa sʋ mʋ, ɩɩ bɔla Ɩbwaarɛ ansi, nɩɩ asa gbaa bee wu yɛ ɩ bʋrɔn.

eng: So lets strive for the things that bring peace and the things that build each other up.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan yɛgɛ a kpan anɛ n‐yɩɩ waa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ bɩya kayɩɩ yuuli, kɛ a kyɛ abaa tɔ kɛ a dan anɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii tɔ.

eng: Dont destroy what God has done because of food. All food is acceptable, but its a bad thing if it trips someone else.
local: Fan man kpalɩ agyitɔ sʋ nyita Ɩbwaarɛ kusun. Agyitɔ kpini ɩ bʋrɔn, amaa ɩ man dɛ ɔkpa fɛɛ fu gyii agyitɔ kʋ kɛ ɩ yɛgɛ fʋ bɩrɩsa ɔ waa alibi.

eng: Its a good thing not to eat meat or drink wine or to do anything that trips your brother or sister.
local: Ɩ bʋrɔn fɛɛ fu man gyii kunun kɛ fu man nuu nta abɛɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ kii biti kɩ yɛgɛ fʋ bɩrɩsa ɔ tɩyɛ.

eng: Keep the belief that you have to yourself—its between you and God. People are blessed who dont convict themselves by the things they approve.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fʋ kɔɔlɛ gyii lɩɩ atɔ nɩmʋ sʋ mʋ, yɛgɛ ɩ baa ɩ bʋ faa Ɩbwaarɛ nsana. Kumu ɔkɔn wuya ɩ gyɛ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man bun mʋ n‐yɩɩ kʋpwɛ lɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɩɩ fɛɛ kɩ bʋrɔn sa mʋ mʋ sʋ.

eng: But those who have doubts are convicted if they go ahead and eat, because they arent acting on the basis of faith. Everything that isnt based on faith is sin.
local: Amaa ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ mʋ nfɛɛrɛ i du sika‐sika lɩɩ kʋtɔ kigyii sʋ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ee gyii kɩmʋ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ wʋla bun mu kʋpwɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ mʋ kigyii mʋ man lɩɩ mʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii tɔ. Nɩɩ awaasa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ man lɩɩ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii tɔ mʋ gyɛ alibi nɩn.

eng: We who are powerful need to be patient with the weakness of those who dont have power, and not please ourselves.
local: Anɛ mʋ nɩɩ an yɩlɛ Yesu Krisito kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii tɔ yɛ ɔlʋn mʋ, ɩ dagaa fɛɛ an maa kyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bamʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii kɩ man bʋlʋn mʋ tɔ, kɛ a man taa waa anɛ nkɔlɔ biti.

eng: Each of us should please our neighbors for their good in order to build them up.
local: Fan yɛgɛ anɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ ɔ waa kɩdanbɩrasa kyɛ mʋ bɩrɩsa tɔ, kɛ ɔ nyɛ ɔlʋn yɩlɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii tɔ.

eng: Christ didnt please himself, but, as it is written, The insults of those who insulted you fell on me. .
local: Kpalɩ fɛɛ Krisito gbaa man waa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bɔla mʋ nkʋn ansi, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ lɩɩ Asɩnkya yɩlasa mʋ tɔ yɛ, Asebi kpini mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ saalɛ fʋ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ, mɛ nɩɩ bɛɛ saalɛ.

eng: Whatever was written in the past was written for our instruction so that we could have hope through endurance and through the encouragement of the scriptures.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ yɩla Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ dɩdaa mʋ, ba kyʋrɔɔ nɩn yɛ ɩ ba kaapʋ anɛ asɩn. Ɩnɩmʋ ii biti ɩ yɛgɛ ɩ waa anɛ aniya tɔ, kɛ ɩ sa anɛ kenyiita, yɛgɛ an sʋ tamaa.

eng: May the God of endurance and encouragement give you the same attitude toward each other, similar to Christ Jesus attitude.
local: N kʋlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ sa asa kenyiita nɩɩ ɛɛ waa bamʋ aniya tɔ mʋ, fɛɛ fan maa buu Yesu Krisito falɛ mʋ, ɔ sa fanɛ kɔnɔ kʋlʋn.

eng: That way you can glorify the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ together with one voice.
local: Saamʋ lɩɩ fanɛ kɔkɔlɔ kʋlʋn yɛ kɔnɔ kʋlʋn sʋ mʋ, fen biti fan bʋga yɩn anɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Yesu Krisito Mʋ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: So welcome each other, in the same way that Christ also welcomed you, for Gods glory.
local: Fan taa kɔkɔlɔ fuuli kɔɔlɛ abaa fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Krisito kɔɔlɛ fanɛ mʋ, kɛ ɩ yɛgɛ ɩ bɩya ayɩn‐bi sa Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: Im saying that Christ became a servant of those who are circumcised for the sake of Gods truth, in order to confirm the promises given to the ancestors.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ fɛɛ Krisito ɩ ba biliŋi Gyiwu abi kɩyaafɔlɛ, kɛ ɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ gyɛ kesintin wuya, nɩɩ ɔ yɛgɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ sa adɩdaapʋ mʋ ɩ ba tɔ.

eng: and so that the Gentiles could glorify God for his mercy. As it is written, Because of this I will confess you among the Gentiles, and I will sing praises to your name .
local: Nɩɩ ɩ bɩla yɛgɛ nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ, ba taa bʋnyaa sa Ɩbwaarɛ lɩɩ mʋ kʋwɛɛ kuwu sʋ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa ɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɩmʋ sʋ n biti n yɩn fʋ, bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ tɔ, n biti n bɩɩ ɩlʋn yɩn fʋ kɩtɩɩ.

eng: And again, it says, Rejoice, Gentiles, with his people .
local: Nɩɩ ɩ bɩla tɔwɛ yɛ, Fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fan man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ fanaa Ɩbwaarɛ asa ansi i gyii.

eng: And again, Praise the Lord, all you Gentiles, and all the people should sing his praises .
local: Nɩɩ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ ɩ bɩla tɔwɛ yɛ, Fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fan man gyii Gyiwu abi mʋ kpini fan yɩn Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ, kɛ ndɛndɛmandɛ kpini fan bɩɩ ayɩn‐bi ɩlʋn sa mʋ.

eng: And again, Isaiah says, There will be a root of Jesse, who will also rise to rule the Gentiles. The Gentiles will place their hope in him .
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Isaya bɩla tɔwɛ yɛ, Gyeese kanan tɔ mʋ ɔkʋ ii biti ɔ kʋsʋ be gyii kuwura bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ kpini sʋ. Mʋ tɔ nɩɩ ba sʋ tamaa.

eng: May the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace in faith so that you overflow with hope by the power of the Holy Spirit.
local: N kʋlɛ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ sa asa tamaa mʋ, fɛɛ fan maa kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii falɛ mʋ, ɔ sa fanɛ kensi‐gyii yɛ keyuula. Kɛ mʋ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ ɔlʋn mʋ ɩ yɛgɛ fanɛ tamaa ɔ nyɛ akyɔ.

eng: My brothers and sisters, I myself am convinced that you yourselves are full of goodness, filled with all knowledge, and are able to teach each other.
local: Mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana, mɛ gbaa‐gbaa mɛ n kɔɔlɛ gyii fɛɛ kɩdanbɩrasa kɩ bʋ fanɛ tɔ, nɩɩ fan sʋ kinyi gaa nɩɩ fan taalɛ kaapʋ abaa.

eng: But Ive written to you in a sort of daring way, partly to remind you of what you already know. Im writing to you in this way because of the grace that was given to me by God.
local: Lɩɩ mɛ ɔwʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ n kyʋrɔɔ fanɛ falɛ sʋ mʋ, n bugi mɛ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ n nyiŋi fanɛ asɩn akʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kabwaala mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa sa mɛ mʋ sʋ.

eng: It helps me to be a minister of Christ Jesus to the Gentiles. Im working as a priest of Gods gospel so that the offering of the Gentiles can be acceptable and made holy by the Holy Spirit.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ mɛ waa Yesu Krisito osunpu sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ. Nɩɩ n sun fɛɛ mu‐seepu, kɛ n tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ, kɛ ɩ yɛgɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ, nɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ lɔŋɔ bamʋ yɩla mʋ, ba ba waa fɛɛ saraga mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bɔla Ɩbwaarɛ ansi.

eng: So in Christ Jesus I brag about things that have to do with God.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ n taalɛ kaapʋ mɛ n‐yɩɩ Yesu Krisito sʋ lɩɩ kusun mʋ nɩɩ n sun sa Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ sʋ.

eng: I dont dare speak about anything except what Christ has done through me to bring about the obedience of the Gentiles. He did it by what Ive said and what Ive done.
local: N waa mɛ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ n mɛɛ yɛ n tɔwɛ kʋtɔ kʋ, amɔɔ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Krisito bɔla mɛ atɔwɛsa yɛ mɛ awaasa sʋ bɩya bamʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ ba be buu Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: by the power of signs and wonders, and by the power of Gods Spirit. So Ive completed the circuit of preaching Christs gospel from Jerusalem all the way around to Illyricum.
local: Lɩɩ ɔlʋn mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sa mɛ ɩ gyɛ sɩnkaala yɛ adangana lɩɩ mʋ Kayaayu mʋ ɔlʋn tɔ sʋ, kanɩn sʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ Krisito asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa lɩɩ Gyerusalem, kyaabɔɔ ndɛ‐bii sʋ naa fʋʋ Ilirikum.

eng: In this way, I have a goal to preach the gospel where they havent heard of Christ yet, so that I wont be building on someone elses foundation.
local: Mi kebiti ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ n tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ ntɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba man pɩɩta nu Krisito sʋ, saamʋ kɛ n man kyaga ɔkʋ kusun piilisa sʋ.

eng: Instead, as its written, Those who hadnt been told about him will see, and those who hadnt heard will understand .
local: Amaa fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ ɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, Asa mʋ nɩɩ ba man pɩɩta tɔwɛ bamʋ mʋ asɩn mʋ, bee biti ba wu mʋ. Kɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man pɩɩta nu mʋ, bee biti ba nu kasɛ.

eng: Thats why Ive been stopped so many times from coming to see you.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ n kpan mɛ n‐yɩɩ saŋa kʋmaa fɛɛ n ba bɔɔ fanɛ sʋ, amaa i tii mɛ ɔkpa fanɛ asɛ kɩba.

eng: But now, since I dont have any place to work in these regions anymore, and since Ive wanted to come to see you for many years.
local: Amaa nbɩɩnbɩɩ opula kʋ man bɩla ɔ bʋ kɩbaafʋn kɩnɩmʋ katɩn kʋ mʋ nɩɩ n maa waa kusun, nɩɩ n bɩla n kpan mɛ n‐yɩɩ nsu damantɛ fɛɛ n ba bɔɔ fanɛ sʋ.

eng: Ill visit you when I go to Spain. I hope to see you while Im passing through. And I hope you will send me on my way there, after I have first been reenergized by some time in your company.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ n waa nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ n kan kpee Sipen mʋ, n biti n bɔla fanɛ asɛ. N sʋ tamaa fɛɛ n ba bɔɔ fanɛ sʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ n biti n bɔla kyʋn mʋ, kɛ maa fanɛ an bʋga gyii ansi, kɛ fan kyɛ mɛ tɔ kɛ n naa fʋʋ tʋtɔ.

eng: But now Im going to Jerusalem, to serve Gods people.
local: Amaa nbɩɩnbɩɩ bɩrɛ n bʋ ɔkpa sʋ n kpee Gyerusalem n naa kyɛ Krisito abuupu tɔ.

eng: Macedonia and Achaia have been happy to make a contribution for the poor among Gods people in Jerusalem.
local: Masedoniya yɛ Akyɩya akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu bɩrɛ ba taa kensi‐gyii waa tʋʋtʋʋ sa Krisito abuupu mʋ tɔ atiripu mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Gyerusalem mʋ.

eng: They were happy to do this, and they are actually in debt to Gods people in Jerusalem. If the Gentiles got a share of the Jewish peoples spiritual resources, they ought to minister to them with material resources.
local: Masedoniya yɛ Akyɩya akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu bamʋ ansi i gyii fɛɛ ba maa waa kanɩn sa Gyiwu abi akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ. Nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ ba maa waa kanɩn kɛ ba taa ka bamʋ kʋkɔ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ, ba nyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ Kayaayu tɔ nkyɛtɔ atɔ, ɩmʋ sʋ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ ba baraga bamʋ kakyɩna tɔ atɔ sa Gyiwu abi mʋ.

eng: So then after I have finished this job and have safely delivered the final amount of the Gentiles offering to them, I will leave for Spain, visiting you on the way.
local: Nɩɩ n kan taa tʋʋtʋʋ nɩmʋ waa bamʋ abaa tɔ lʋwɛ mʋ, n biti n bɔla fanɛ asɛ kpee Sipen kadɛ tɔ.

eng: And I know that when I come to you I will come with the fullest blessing of Christ.
