local: N nyi fɛɛ mɛ kɩba mʋ nɩɩ n ba fanɛ asɛ mʋ, n sʋ Krisito kabwaala lala mʋ n bɩya fanɛ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I urge you, through our Lord Jesus Christ and through the love of the Spirit, to join me in my struggles in your prayers to God for me.
local: Mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana, n sʋ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu Krisito yɛ kebiti mʋ nɩɩ kɩ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu tɔ fɛɛ fan kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔlʋn kyɛ mɛ tɔ.

eng: Pray that I will be rescued from the people in Judea who dont believe. Also, pray that my service for Jerusalem will be acceptable to Gods people there.
local: Fan kʋlɛ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ lɛɛ mɛ lɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man kɔɔlɛ gyii Gyudeya ifuli sʋ mʋ abaa tɔ. Kɛ fan bɩla kʋlɛ fɛɛ Krisito abuupu mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Gyerusalem mʋ ba kɔɔlɛ nkyɛtɔ mʋ abaa anyɔ.

eng: so that I can come to you with joy by Gods will and be reenergized with your company.
local: Lɩɩ kanɩn sʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kan kyula mʋ n biti n taa kensi‐gyii lala ba fanɛ asɛ, kɛ ɩ waa maa fanɛ aniya tɔ.

eng: May the God of peace be with you all. Amen.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ keyuula wuya ɔ baa ɔ bʋ fanɛ kpini asɛ. Amen!.

eng: Im introducing our sister Phoebe to you, who is a servant of the church in Cenchreae.
local: N biti n lɛɛ anɛ pɩɩkyɩɩ Fobe mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ ɔwʋlɛ nɩmʋ ɛɛ bɩya fanɛ falɛ n kaapʋ fanɛ. Mʋ ɩ gyɛ Krisito abuupu mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Kenkireya, nɩɩ ɛɛ kyɛ bamʋ tɔ mʋ.

eng: Welcome her in the Lord in a way that is worthy of Gods people, and give her whatever she needs from you, because she herself has been a sponsor of many people, myself included.
local: N kʋlɛ fanɛ, fan kɔɔlɛ mʋ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ kɩtɩɩ tɔ sʋ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ Krisito abuupu ba kɩtaa abaa mʋ, kɛ fan sa mʋ nkyɛtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ i tiri mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ kyɛ asa gaalaagaa tɔ nɩɩ ngbaa mɛ n tii sʋ.

eng: Say hello to Prisca and Aquila, my coworkers in Christ Jesus.
local: Fan fwaala Prisila maa Akwila, mʋ nɩɩ n maa bamʋ an waa Yesu Krisito kusun mʋ.

eng: who risked their own necks for my life. Im not the only one who thanks God for them, but all the churches of the Gentiles do the same.
local: Nɩɩ ba taga apʋ lɩɩ mɛ sʋ. Ɩ man gyɛ mɛ nkʋn ɩ fwaala bamʋ, amaa yɛ Krisito abi kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ kpini bɛɛ fwaala bamʋ nsa yɛ kusun.

eng: Also say hello to the church that meets in their house. Say hello to Epaenetus, my dear friend, who was the first convert in Asia for Christ.
local: Fan fwaala Krisito kʋbʋ mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ gyaŋɛ Prisila ana kɩkpaara sʋ mʋ. Fan fwaala mɛ kyɛmɩnɛ danbɩrasa Epanetu. Mʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ da kɩkɔɔlɛ Yesu Krisito gyii Asiya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ kpini.

eng: Say hello to Mary, who has worked very hard for you.
local: Fan fwaala Mɛɛrɩ mʋ nɩɩ o sun kusun gaa sa fanɛ mʋ.

eng: Say hello to Andronicus and Junia, my relatives and my fellow prisoners. They are prominent among the apostles, and they were in Christ before me.
local: Fan fwaala Androniko maa Gyuniya, mɛ kʋʋpʋ ana mʋ nɩɩ ba tii maa bamʋ obu mʋ. Nɩɩ asa gaalaagaa be nyi bamʋ yɛ ɔdan Krisito asunpu mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ba gyankpaa mɛ kɔɔlɛ Yesu Krisito gyii.

eng: Say hello to Ampliatus, my dear friend in the Lord.
local: Fan fwaala Ampiliyatu mʋ nɩɩ n sʋ kebiti sa mʋ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ tɔ.

eng: Say hello to Urbanus, our coworker in Christ, and my dear friend Stachys.
local: Fan fwaala Ubano, anɛ bɩrɩsa kusun ɔwaapʋ Krisito tɔ, yɛ mɛ kyɛmɩnɛ danbɩrasa Sitakusi.

eng: Say hello to Apelles, who is tried and true in Christ. Say hello to the members of the household of Aristobulus.
local: Fan fwaala Apelesi mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ kaapʋ kesintin yɛ ɔ taa mʋ n‐yɩɩ sa Krisito. Fan fwaala bamʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Arisitobulu kɩkpaara sʋ kpini.

eng: Say hello to my relative Herodion. Say hello to the members of the household of Narcissus who are in the Lord.
local: Fan fwaala mɛ kʋʋpʋ Herodiyon. Fan fwaala Nasiso kɩkpaara sʋ abi kpini mʋ nɩɩ ba gyɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ lɛɛ mʋ.

eng: Say hello to Tryphaena and Tryphosa, who are workers for the Lord. Say hello to my dear friend Persis, who has worked hard in the Lord.
local: Fan fwaala akyɩɩ nɩmʋ, Trifina maa Trifosa mʋ nɩɩ ba kpan bamʋ n‐yɩɩ sun Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ kusun mʋ. Fan fwaala mɛ kyɛmɩnɛ danbɩrasa Pesi, nɩɩ ngbaa mʋ ɔ gyɛ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kpan mʋ n‐yɩɩ waa Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ kusun mʋ.

eng: Say hello to Rufus, who is an outstanding believer, along with his mother and mine.
local: Fan fwaala Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ kɩyaafɔlɛ Rufusi, yɛ mu‐nyi, mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa mɛ waa mu‐bii kʋʋgɛsa.

eng: Say hello to Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermes, Patrobas, Hermas, and the brothers and sisters who are with them.
local: Fan fwaala Asinkrito, yɛ Felegon yɛ Hemesi yɛ Patrobasi yɛ Hemesi yɛ Krisito abuupu mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ bamʋ asɛ mʋ.

eng: Say hello to Philologus and Julia, Nereus and his sister, and Olympas, and all the saints who are with them.
local: Fan fwaala Filologo yɛ Gyuliya yɛ Neresu maa mʋ pɩɩkyɩɩ yɛ Olipasi yɛ Krisito abuupu mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ bamʋ asɛ mʋ kpini.

eng: Say hello to each other with a holy kiss. All the churches of Christ say hello to you.
local: Fan taa kinyi‐bii afwaala fwaala abaa. Krisito kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ mʋ kpini bɛɛ fwaala fanɛ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I urge you to watch out for people who create divisions and problems against the teaching that you learned. Keep away from them.
local: Mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana, n kʋlɛ fanɛ nɩn, kɛ fan kɩɩ danbɩrasa lɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ bee biti ba bɩya nbarɩgɛ fanɛ tɔ, nɩɩ bɛɛ kpakyarɛ kɩbɩɩla mʋ nɩɩ ba kaapʋ fanɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɩ yɛgɛ asa bee tutu bamʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii tɔ. Fan lɛɛ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ lɩɩ bamʋ tɔ.

eng: People like that arent serving the Lord. They are serving their own feelings. They deceive the hearts of innocent people with smooth talk and flattery.
local: Kpalɩ fɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa kanɩn mʋ, be mee sun anɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Yesu Krisito, amaa bamʋ kɩpɩrɛ lɛɛ nɩɩ bee biti. Lɩɩ bamʋ ntɔwɛ danbɩrasa yɛ kɩgyaa‐bi sʋ mʋ, ba sʋ bɛɛ pɩna akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ nɩɩ ba man pɩɩ dan kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii tɔ.

eng: The news of your obedience has reached everybody, so Im happy for you. But I want you to be wise about whats good, and innocent about whats evil.
local: Ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ nu kanan mʋ nɩɩ fan taa fanɛ n‐yɩɩ sa Krisito mʋ. Kanɩn sʋ mi ansi i gyii fanɛ sʋ gaa. Amaa n biti fɛɛ fan baa fan gyɛ anyaasɩnpʋ lɩɩ kɩdanbɩrasa kɩwaa sʋ, kɛ fan lɛɛ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ lɩɩ alibi kɩwaa tɔ.

eng: The God of peace will soon crush Satan under your feet. The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
local: Ɩ mɛɛ yɛ ɩ kyɛɛrɛɛ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ keyuula wuya mʋ, ii biti ɔ yɛgɛ kɛ fan gyii Sɩtaanɛ sʋ. Anɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Yesu kabwaala mʋ kɩ baa kɩ bʋ fanɛ sʋ.

eng: Timothy my coworker says hello to you, and Lucius, Jason, and Sosipater, my relatives.
local: Mɛ bɩrɩsa osunsunpu Timoti ɩ fwaala fanɛ. Kanɩn maa nɩɩ mɛ kʋʋpʋ ana Lukiyo yɛ Gyason yɛ Sosipata gbaa bɛɛ fwaala fanɛ.

eng: Im Tertius, and Im writing this letter to you in the Lord—hello!.
local: Mɛ Tetiyo mʋ nɩɩ n kyʋrɔɔ Pɔɔlɩ ɔwʋlɛ nɩmʋ, n fwaala fanɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ kɩtɩɩ tɔ sʋ.

eng: Gaius, who is host to me and to the whole church, says hello to you. Erastus the city treasurer says hello to you, along with our brother Quartus.
local: Gayusi mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ mɛ kɩkpaara wuya, nɩɩ Krisito abi gbaa bɛɛ gyaŋɛ mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ mʋ, ɩ fwaala fanɛ. Erasitu mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ kadɛ mʋ tɔ afulee ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ yɛ anɛ‐supu Kwatu bɛɛ fwaala fanɛ.

eng: May the glory be to God who can strengthen you with my good news and the message that I preach about Jesus Christ. He can strengthen you with the announcement of the secret that was kept quiet for a long time.
local: Fan yɛgɛ a taa bʋnyaa sa Ɩbwaarɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ mʋ ɩ taalɛ yɛgɛ fan nyɛ ɔlʋn yɩlɛ fanɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii mʋ tɔ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ Asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ lɩɩ Yesu Krisito sʋ mʋ. Kanɩn asɩn mʋ ɩ gyɛ kesintin asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɩ baala lɩɩ koo dɩdaa mʋ.

eng: Now that secret is revealed through what the prophets wrote. It is made known to the Gentiles in order to lead to their faithful obedience based on the command of the eternal God.
local: Nɩɩ nbɩɩnbɩɩ ɩ bɔla Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ sʋ lɛɛ kaapʋ. Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛ kakpaa mʋ sa kɔnɔ, yɛ ba tɔwɛ kanɩn kesintin asɩn baalasa sa ndɛndɛmandɛ kpini, kɛ ba kɔɔlɛ Yesu Krisito gyii kɛ ba nu mʋ asɛ.

eng: May the glory be to God, who alone is wise! May the glory be to him through Jesus Christ forever! Amen.
local: Lɩɩ Yesu Krisito sʋ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ kanyaasɩn wuya mʋ nkʋn nɩɩ bʋnyaa gyɛ mʋ lɛɛ kakpaa. Amen!.

eng: From Paul, called by Gods will to be an apostle of Jesus Christ, and from Sosthenes our brother.
local: Ɔwʋlɛ nɩmʋ lɩɩ mɛ, Pɔɔlɩ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bɔla mu kebiti sʋ tɩɩ mɛ, fɛɛ n ba waa Yesu Krisito osunpu, yɛ anɛ bɩrɩsa Sositini asɛ.

eng: To Gods church that is in Corinth: To those who have been made holy to God in Christ Jesus, who are called to be Gods people. Together with all those who call upon the name of our Lord Jesus Christ in every place—hes their Lord and ours!.
local: An kyʋrɔɔ kɩmʋ an sa fanɛ Krisito kʋbʋ mʋ nɩɩ fan bʋ Korinto kadɛ tɔ, fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ Yesu tɩɩ ba waa mʋ lɛɛ, nɩɩ fan ba biliŋi Ɩbwaarɛ asa, taa bʋga yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ katɩn kʋmaa nɩmʋ bee sun anɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Yesu Krisito, mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ anaa bamʋ kpini Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ.

eng: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Anɛ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ anɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Yesu Krisito ba bwaala fanɛ kɛ ba sa fanɛ kayɩɩ yuuli.

eng: I thank my God always for you, because of Gods grace that was given to you in Christ Jesus.
local: Saŋa kʋmaa nɩmʋ n fwaala Ɩbwaarɛ n sa fanɛ lɩɩ kabwaala mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bɔla Yesu Krisito sʋ taa sa fanɛ mʋ.

eng: That is, you were made rich through him in everything: in all your communication and every kind of knowledge.
local: Kpalɩ fɛɛ fan maa bʋ Yesu Krisito tɔ sʋ mʋ, fan dan kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ tɔ lɩɩ fanɛ katɔwɛ yɛ fanɛ kinyi tɔ.

eng: in the same way that the testimony about Christ was confirmed with you.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Yesu Krisito asɩn mʋ nɩɩ an lɛɛ tɔwɛ fanɛ mʋ ɩ kyɩna fanɛ tɔ danbɩrasa.

eng: The result is that you arent missing any spiritual gift while you wait for our Lord Jesus Christ to be revealed.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan man pan Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu kakɛɛ mʋ kʋkʋ, fɛɛ fan maa tɛ fan gyoo saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ ba lɛɛ anɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Yesu Krisito kaapʋ mʋ.

eng: He will also confirm your testimony about Christ until the end so that you will be blameless on the day of our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Mʋ ii biti ɔ waa fanɛ ɔlʋn tɔ kanɩn naa fʋʋ ɔkaa, kɛ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ ɩ man nyɛ alibi akʋ, kakɛ mʋ nɩɩ anɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Yesu Krisito ii biti o bwii ba mʋ.

eng: God is faithful, and you were called by him to partnership with his Son, Jesus Christ our Lord.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ fanɛ yɛ fan naa mu‐bii Yesu Krisito mʋ fan ba waa kɩkyɛmɩnɛ ɔkɔn mʋ, mʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ tɔwɛ nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ lɩɩ ɩmʋ kamaa.

eng: Now I encourage you, brothers and sisters, in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ: Agree with each other and dont be divided into rival groups. Instead, be restored with the same mind and the same purpose.
local: Mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana, n sʋ anɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Yesu Krisito kɩtɩɩ, n kʋlɛ fanɛ fɛɛ, fanɛ kpini fan tɔwɛ kyula abaa, kɛ nbarɩgɛ tɔ kʋ ɩ man ba fanɛ tɔ. Kɛ fan waa kɔnɔ kʋlʋn fanɛ nfɛɛrɛ tɔ yɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ tɔ.

eng: My brothers and sisters, Chloes people gave me some information about you, that youre fighting with each other.
local: Mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana, Kilowe kɩkpaara sʋ abi akʋ ba ba tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, nluu ɩ bʋ fanɛ nsana.

eng: What I mean is this: that each one of you says, I belong to Paul, I belong to Apollos, I belong to Cephas, I belong to Christ.
local: Kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ fanɛ tɔ ɔkʋ ɩɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, N buu Pɔɔlɩ. Nɩɩ ɔkʋ mɔɔ yɛ, Mɛɛ mɛ n buu Apolosi, nɩɩ ɔnɩmʋ mɔɔ yɛ, Mɛɛ mɛ n buu Pita. Nɩɩ ɔkʋ gbaa yɛ, Mɛɛ mɛ n buu Krisito nɩn.

eng: Has Christ been divided? Was Paul crucified for you, or were you baptized in Pauls name?.
local: Ba barɩgɛ Krisito tɔ waa kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ nɩn abɛɛ? Pɔɔlɩ nɩɩ ba da oyu laasa sʋ sa fanɛ abɛɛ? Abɛɛ Pɔɔlɩ kɩtɩɩ tɔ nɩɩ ba gyee fanɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee?.

eng: Thank God that I didnt baptize any of you, except Crispus and Gaius.
local: N sʋ afwaala n sa Ɩbwaarɛ fɛɛ n maa man gyee fanɛ ɔkʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee, amɔɔ Krisipu maa Gayusi nkʋn nɩɩ n gyee.

eng: so that nobody can say that you were baptized in my name!.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔkʋ mɛɛ taalɛ tɔwɛ yɛ mɛ kɩtɩɩ tɔ nɩɩ ba gyee mʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee.

eng: Oh, I baptized the house of Stephanas too. Otherwise, I dont know if I baptized anyone else.
local: N bɩla n nyiŋi fɛɛ n maa gyee Sitefen maa mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ abi Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee. Ɩnɩmʋ kamaa mʋ, n mɛɛ bɩla nyiŋi fɛɛ n gyee ɔkʋ.

eng: Christ didnt send me to baptize but to preach the good news. And Christ didnt send me to preach the good news with clever words so that Christs cross wont be emptied of its meaning.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Krisito man sun mɛ yɛ n ba gyee asa Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee. Amaa o sun mɛ nɩn yɛ n ba tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ sa asa, nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ nyɩmɩsa kanyaasɩn nɩɩ n sʋ n tɔwɛ, kɛ ɩ naa ba fɛɛ Krisito kelewu mʋ nɩɩ o wu oyu laasa sʋ asɩn mʋ ɩ pan ɩmʋ ɔlʋn.

eng: The message of the cross is foolishness to those who are being destroyed. But it is the power of God for those of us who are being saved.
local: Krisito oyu laasa mʋ sʋ kelewu asɩn mʋ, ɩ gyɛ amelensi‐sɩn sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ kemuuri ɔkpa sʋ mʋ. Amaa anɛ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩɩ mɔlɩgɛ mʋ, asɩn maŋa ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔlʋn mʋ.

eng: It is written in scripture: I will destroy the wisdom of the wise, and I will reject the intelligence of the intelligent .
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ yɛ, N biti n nyita anyaasɩnpʋ kanyaasɩn, kɛ n yɛgɛ anyipu kinyi ɩ waa kɩyan.

eng: Where are the wise? Where are the legal experts? Where are todays debaters? Hasnt God made the wisdom of the world foolish?.
local: Nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ kanan bɩrɛ mʋ dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ ɔnyaasɩnpʋ bʋ fɩnɛ? Nɩɩ ɔwʋlɛ‐nyipu bʋ fɩnɛ? Abɛɛ ɔkaapʋpʋ bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ bʋ fɩnɛ? Ɩbwaarɛ man kaapʋ fɛɛ dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ kanyaasɩn gyɛ amelensi‐sɩn mʋ asɛ nɩn abɛɛ?.

eng: In Gods wisdom, he determined that the world wouldnt come to know him through its wisdom. Instead, God was pleased to save those who believe through the foolishness of preaching.
local: Lɩɩ mʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kanyaasɩn mʋ tɔ mʋ, o ŋmina yɩla fɛɛ, dulinyaa asa ba mɛɛ taalɛ bɔla bamʋ kanyaasɩn sʋ bɩɩ mʋ. Amaa ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kɩpɩrɛ sʋ fɛɛ, ɔ bɔla asɩn mʋ nɩɩ asa bɛɛ kɩɩ fɛɛ ɩ gyɛ amelensi‐sɩn mʋ nɩɩ an tɔwɛ falɛ mʋ sʋ mɔlɩgɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ kɔɔlɛ bee gyii mʋ.

eng: Jews ask for signs, and Greeks look for wisdom.
local: Asɩn mʋ ɩ gyɛ amelensi‐sɩn sa bamʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ Gyiwu abi bɛɛ kɩɩ bee biti asɩn kyinkyinsa, nɩɩ Giriki abi mɔɔ bɛɛ kɩɩ bee biti kanyaasɩn.

eng: but we preach Christ crucified, which is a scandal to Jews and foolishness to Gentiles.
local: Amaa anɛɛnɛ bɩrɛ an tɔwɛ Yesu Krisito kelewu oyu laasa mʋ sʋ asɩn, nɩɩ ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ii tii Gyiwu abi ɔkpa, nɩɩ ba mɛɛ kɔɔlɛ bee gyii, nɩɩ ɩ bɩla ɩ gyɛ amelensi‐sɩn sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ.

eng: But to those who are called—both Jews and Greeks—Christ is Gods power and Gods wisdom.
local: Amaa bamʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tɩɩ ba kamɔlɩgɛ mʋ tɔ bɩrɛ, bɛ gyɛ Gyiwu abi nɩn oo, ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi nɩn oo, asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ tɔ ɩ gyɛ yɛ Krisito ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔlʋn yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kanyaasɩn.

eng: This is because the foolishness of God is wiser than human wisdom, and the weakness of God is stronger than human strength.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ amelensi‐sɩn mʋ ɩ kyɔ nyɩmɩsa kanyaasɩn, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kapɔɔsɛ ɩ sʋ ɔlʋn ɩ kyɔ nyɩmɩsa ɔlʋn.

eng: Look at your situation when you were called, brothers and sisters! By ordinary human standards not many were wise, not many were powerful, not many were from the upper class.
local: Mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana, fan nyiŋi kanan mʋ nɩɩ fan daa fan du pɔyɩ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tɩɩ fanɛ ba kamɔlɩgɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ. Kanan saŋa maŋa nyɩmɩsa ansi sʋ mʋ, fanɛ tɔ gbɛrɛɛ kʋ ɩ gyɛ anyaasɩnpʋ, nɩɩ gbɛrɛɛ kʋ ɩ gyɛ alʋnpʋ, nɩɩ gbɛrɛɛ kʋ ɩ lɩɩ kuwura‐gyii kebunɔ.

eng: But God chose what the world considers foolish to shame the wise. God chose what the world considers weak to shame the strong.
local: Amaa atɔ mʋ nɩɩ dulinyaa asa bɛɛ kɩɩ yɛ ɩ gyɛ amelensi‐sɩn mʋ, ɩmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ taa gyila dulinyaa tɔ anyaasɩnpʋ ipeeli. Nɩɩ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ man bʋlʋn dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ mʋ, ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɔ bɩla taa gyila ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋlʋn mʋ ipeeli.

eng: And God chose what the world considers low-class and low-life—what is considered to be nothing—to reduce what is considered to be something to nothing.
local: Nɩɩ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ dulinyaa asa bɛɛ kɩɩ kɩyan, nɩɩ ba man taa tɩɩ sɛhn, nɩɩ bɛɛ kɩɩ yɛ ɩ man sʋ tɔnɔ mʋ, ɩmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ taa ŋaabaa atɔ mʋ nɩɩ dulinyaa asa bɛɛ kɩɩ fɛɛ ɩ gyɛ atɔ kparɛ mʋ.

eng: So no human being can brag in Gods presence.
local: kɛ nyɩmɩsa kʋ ɔ man nyɛ kaapʋ mʋ n‐yɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ.

eng: It is because of God that you are in Christ Jesus. He became wisdom from God for us. This means that he made us righteous and holy, and he delivered us.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ anaa Yesu Krisito an waa kʋkʋlʋn, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ waa Krisito nɩɩ ɔ baa ɔ gyɛ anɛ kanyaasɩn wuya. Nɩɩ mʋ sʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wu fɛɛ anɛ ɩkpa i kyiigi, nɩɩ anɛ ɩ gyɛ mʋ asa kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ, nɩɩ a nyɛ kamɔlɩgɛ.

eng: This is consistent with what was written: The one who brags should brag in the Lord! .
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ mʋ yɛ, Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ kaapʋ mʋ n‐yɩɩ mʋ, ɔ kaapʋ mʋ n‐yɩɩ lɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ waa mʋ sʋ.

eng: When I came to you, brothers and sisters, I didnt come preaching Gods secrets to you like I was an expert in speech or wisdom.
local: Mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana, n maa ba fanɛ asɛ ba lɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kesintin asɩn mʋ tɔwɛ fanɛ mʋ, n man taa kɔnɔ tɔ ɔkɔn abɛɛ kanyaasɩn kɩbansa nɩn ba tɔwɛ fanɛ.

eng: I had made up my mind not to think about anything while I was with you except Jesus Christ, and to preach him as crucified.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ n waa mɛ nfɛɛrɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ n bʋ fanɛ asɛ mʋ fɛɛ, n mee biti n bɩla bɩɩ asɩn akʋ tɔwɛ tii Yesu Krisito, yɛ mʋ kelewu mʋ nɩɩ o wu oyu laasa sʋ mʋ nkʋn.

eng: I stood in front of you with weakness, fear, and a lot of shaking.
local: N maa ba fanɛ asɛ mʋ, n taa kɩpɔɔsɛ yɛ kufuu yɛ kɩkyɩkya gaa nɩn ba.

eng: My message and my preaching werent presented with convincing wise words but with a demonstration of the Spirit and of power.
local: Mɛ asɩn tɔwɛsa yɛ mɛ abwaarɛsɩn kɩtɔwɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ, n man taa nyɩmɩsa kanyaasɩn pʋpɔlɛ fanɛ, amaa Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu mʋ ɩ lɛɛ mʋ ɔlʋn mʋ kaapʋ fanɛ.

eng: I did this so that your faith might not depend on the wisdom of people but on the power of God.
local: Saamʋ, fanɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii kɩ mɛɛ yɛ kɩ baa kɩ gyan nyɩmɩsa kanyaasɩn sʋ, amaa kɩ baa kɩ gyan Ɩbwaarɛ ɔlʋn sʋ.

eng: What we say is wisdom to people who are mature. It isnt a wisdom that comes from the present day or from todays leaders who are being reduced to nothing.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ kpini gbaa mʋ, an tɔwɛ kanyaasɩn asɩn an sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba tɛɛ dan kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii mʋ tɔ mʋ, man gyɛ dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ kanyaasɩn, abɛɛ kɩmʋ alʋnpʋ kanyaasɩn mʋ nɩɩ bamʋ ɔlʋn mʋ ii biti ɩ ba fʋʋ ɔkaa mʋ.

eng: We talk about Gods wisdom, which has been hidden as a secret. God determined this wisdom in advance, before time began, for our glory.
local: Amaa kanyaasɩn asɩn mʋ nɩɩ an tɔwɛ fanɛ falɛ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kanyaasɩn baalasa, mʋ nɩɩ ɔ wʋla ŋmina yɩla yɛ ee biti ɔ taa kanyaasɩn maŋa bʋnyaa anɛ, pɔyɩ nɩɩ o piili dulinyaa mʋ.

eng: It is a wisdom that none of the present-day rulers have understood, because if they did understand it, they would never have crucified the Lord of glory!.
local: Nɩɩ dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ alʋnpʋ mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ man taalɛ bɩɩ kanɩn kanyaasɩn maŋa, nɩɩ ba daa bɩɩ nɩn, naafɔɔ ba man dɩn da Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ bʋnyaa wuya mʋ oyu laasa sʋ.

eng: But this is precisely what is written: God has prepared things for those who love him that no eye has seen, or ear has heard, or that havent crossed the mind of any human being .
local: Amaa i du nɩn fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ mʋ yɛ, Kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔkʋ ansi ɩ man pɩɩ wu, nɩɩ asʋ ɩ man pɩɩ nu, nɩɩ ɔkʋ man pɩɩ fɛ ɩmʋ nfɛɛrɛ mʋ, ɩ gyɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ lɔŋɔ yɩla sa bamʋ nɩɩ bee biti mʋ mʋ.

eng: God has revealed these things to us through the Spirit. The Spirit searches everything, including the depths of God.
local: Amaa Ɩbwaarɛ bɔla mʋ Kayaayu sʋ lɛɛ mʋ kanyaasɩn baalasa mʋ kaapʋ anɛ. Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu mʋ ɩɩ mɛɛgɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ tɔ ɛɛ kɩɩ, halɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ mʋ ɔtɔ tɔ asɩn nɩɩ ɩ baala mʋ gbaa.

eng: Who knows a persons depths except their own spirit that lives in them? In the same way, no one has known the depths of God except Gods Spirit.
local: Ɔkʋ mɛɛ taalɛ bɩɩ nfɛɛrɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔkʋ ɩɩ fɛ, amɔɔ mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mʋ. Kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ ɔkʋ mɛɛ taalɛ bɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ nfɛɛrɛ, amɔɔ mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mʋ Kayaayu mʋ nkʋn.

eng: We havent received the worlds spirit but Gods Spirit so that we can know the things given to us by God.
local: Man gyɛ dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ kayaayu nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ taa sa anɛ, amaa Kayaayu mʋ nɩɩ kɩ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ mʋ, nɩɩ an nyɛ, kɛ a taalɛ nu atɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ taa kɛɛ anɛ mʋ kasɛ.

eng: These are the things we are talking about—not with words taught by human wisdom but with words taught by the Spirit—we are interpreting spiritual things to spiritual people.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ man gyɛ nyɩmɩsa kanyaasɩn asɩn kaapʋsa nɩɩ an sʋ an tɔwɛ fanɛ. Amaa ɩ gyɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Kayaayu mʋ kaapʋ anɛ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ an lɛɛ Kayaayu tɔ asɩn mʋ kasɛ an kaapʋ bamʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ Kayaayu mʋ.

eng: But people who are unspiritual dont accept the things from Gods Spirit. They are foolishness to them and cant be understood, because they can only be comprehended in a spiritual way.
local: Amaa ɔsa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man sʋ Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu mʋ, mɛɛ yɛ ɔ taalɛ kɔɔlɛ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu mʋ asɛ mʋ kyula. Asɩn mʋ ɩ gyɛ amelensi‐sɩn sa ɔsa mʋ, ɔ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ taalɛ nu ɩmʋ kasɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu mʋ nkʋn ɩɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ asa bee nu ɩmʋ kasɛ.

eng: Spiritual people comprehend everything, but they themselves arent understood by anyone.
local: Nɩɩ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu mʋ bɩrɛ, ɛɛ taalɛ mɛɛgɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa tɔ wu ɩmʋ tɔ, amaa mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mʋ lɛɛ bɩrɛ, ɔkʋ mɛɛ taalɛ mɛɛgɛ ɩmʋ tɔ wu sɛhn.

eng: Who has known the mind of the Lord, who will advise him? But we have the mind of Christ.
local: I du nɩn fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ mʋ yɛ, Anɩmʋ ɩ taalɛ bɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ nfɛɛrɛ? Nɩɩ anɩmʋ ɩ taalɛ tɔwɛ waa mʋ? Amaa anɛɛnɛ bɩrɛ an fɛ nfɛɛrɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Krisito ɩɩ fɛ mʋ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I couldnt talk to you like spiritual people but like unspiritual people, like babies in Christ.
local: Mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana, n man taalɛ tɔwɛ fanɛ asɛ fɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu mʋ. Amaa n tɔwɛ fanɛ fɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ ɩ gyan dulinyaa atɔ sʋ, abɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man pɩɩta dan Krisito kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii mʋ tɔ.

eng: I gave you milk to drink instead of solid food, because you werent up to it yet.
local: Kanɩn sʋ man gyɛ agyitɔ budusa nɩɩ n taa sa fanɛ, amaa kɩnyapʋ‐kyu nɩɩ n taa sa fanɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ kanɩn saŋa maŋa mʋ, fan man pɩɩta dan fʋʋ agyitɔ maŋa kigyii, nɩɩ halɩɩ ba lɩɩ ndaga gbaa mʋ fan man pɩɩta fʋʋ ɩmʋ kigyii.

eng: Now you are still not up to it because you are still unspiritual. When jealousy and fighting exist between you, arent you unspiritual and living by human standards?.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ fanɛ nfɛɛrɛ ɩ san ɩ gyan dulinyaa atɔ sʋ. Kayɩɩ‐basa kɩ maa bʋ fanɛ tɔ, nɩɩ fɛn kɔ yɛ abaa mʋ, ɩɩ mɛɛ kaapʋ nɩn fɛɛ fanɛ nfɛɛrɛ ɩ san ɩ gyan dulinyaa atɔ sʋ abɛɛ? Ɩnɩmʋ ɩɩ kaapʋ nɩn fɛɛ fɛn san fɛn waa fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ dulinyaa asa bɛɛ waa mʋ.

eng: When someone says, I belong to Paul, and someone else says, I belong to Apollos, arent you acting like people without the Spirit?.
local: Nɩɩ fanɛ tɔ ɔkʋ ɩɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, N buu Pɔɔlɩ. Nɩɩ ɔkʋ mɔɔ ɩɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, N buu Apolosi nɩn. Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ mɛɛ kaapʋ nɩn fɛɛ fɛn san fen du fɛɛ dulinyaa asa nɩn abɛɛ?.

eng: After all, what is Apollos? What is Paul? They are servants who helped you to believe. Each one had a role given to them by the Lord.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ kpini mʋ, anɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ Apolosi? Nɩɩ mɛ, Pɔɔlɩ ɩ gyɛ anɩmʋ? An gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ayaafɔlɛ nɩn, nɩɩ fan bɔla anɛ sʋ ba kɔɔlɛ Yesu gyii. Anɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ waa mu kusun fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu taa sa mʋ yɛ ɔ waa mʋ.

eng: I planted, Apollos watered, but God made it grow.
local: Mɛ i duu, nɩɩ Apolosi mɔɔ ɩ wʋrɩgɛ sʋ nkyu, amaa Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ kɩ kɔyɩ dan.

eng: Because of this, neither the one who plants nor the one who waters is anything, but the only one who is anything is God who makes it grow.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ee duu, yɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ wʋrɩgɛ sʋ nkyu mʋ, bamʋ asɩn man tiri, amaa Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn i tiri, lɩɩ fɛɛ mʋ ɩɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ atɔ duusa mʋ ɩɩ dan.

eng: The one who plants and the one who waters work together, but each one will receive their own reward for their own labor.
local: Ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ee duu yɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ wʋrɩgɛ sʋ nkyu mʋ kpini bɛɛ waa kusun kʋlʋn nɩn. Nɩɩ bamʋ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ ii biti ɔ nyɛ mʋ kʋkɔka, lɩɩ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa mu kusun mʋ sʋ.

eng: We are Gods coworkers, and you are Gods field, Gods building.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ anɛ kpini an bʋga nɩn an waa Ɩbwaarɛ kusun. Nɩɩ fen du nɩn fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kʋdɔɔ, nɩɩ fɛn bɩla fen du fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ obu.

eng: I laid a foundation like a wise master builder according to Gods grace that was given to me, but someone else is building on top of it. Each person needs to pay attention to the way they build on it.
local: Lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabwaala mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa bwaala mɛ sʋ mʋ, n du nɩn fɛɛ ɔpɔyɩpʋ onyipu mʋ nɩɩ o yii obu kasɛ, nɩɩ ɔkʋ ɩɩ pɔyɩ ɛɛ gyaga kɩmʋ sʋ. Amaa ɔkʋmaa ɔ baa ɔ da sʋ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ pɔyɩ ɛɛ gyaga sʋ.

eng: No one can lay any other foundation besides the one that is already laid, which is Jesus Christ.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋ mɛɛ bɩla taalɛ yii ɔpɔrɛ gyankpaasa, tii kɩmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla yii mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ kɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ Yesu Krisito.

eng: So, whether someone builds on top of the foundation with gold, silver, precious stones, wood, grass, or hay.
local: Akʋ bee biti ba taa suwa, abɛɛ gbiti abɛɛ abuu mʋ nɩɩ ɩ sʋ ɔgyalɩn abɛɛ iyu abɛɛ afitiri abɛɛ afatɛɛ pɔyɩ gyaga ɔpɔrɛ gyankpaasa mʋ sʋ.

eng: each ones work will be clearly shown. The day will make it clear, because it will be revealed with fire—the fire will test the quality of each ones work.
local: Ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ kusun mʋ nɩɩ o sun sa Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ, ii biti ɩ lɩɩ ifuli. Lɩɩ fɛɛ Krisito kakɛ lala mʋ nɩɩ ee biti o bwii ba mʋ, ii biti ɩ lɛɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ kusun kudubi kaapʋ. Fʋlɔn ii biti ɔ ba lɛɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ kusun kaapʋ, kɛ ɩ kaala kusun mʋ nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ waa sa Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ, ɔ dan kɩɩ.

eng: If anyones work survives, theyll get a reward.
local: Nɩɩ fʋlɔn mʋ man kan taalɛ nyita ɔkʋ kapɔyɩ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ pɔyɩ gyaga sʋ mʋ, mʋ ii biti ɔ nyɛ kʋkɔka.

eng: But if anyones work goes up in flames, theyll lose it. However, they themselves will be saved as if they had gone through a fire.
local: Amaa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fʋlɔn mʋ dɛɛ mu kusun mʋ bɩrɛ, o sun fɔyɩ nɩmʋ. Nɩɩ mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mʋ bɩrɛ ee biti ɔ nyɛ kamɔlɩgɛ, amaa ii biti ɩ baa i du nɩn fɛɛ maa sɩlɛ lɩɩ fʋlɔn tɔ.

eng: Dont you know that you are Gods temple and Gods Spirit lives in you?.
local: Abɛɛ fan man nyi yɛ fanɛ gbaa‐gbaa fanɛ ɩ gyɛ Kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu mʋ tɛ fanɛ tɔ abɛɛ?.

eng: If someone destroys Gods temple, God will destroy that person, because Gods temple is holy, which is what you are.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan nyita Kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ gbaa ii biti o nyita mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ Kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ mʋ, kɩ gyɛ kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ nɩn, nɩɩ fanɛ ɩ gyɛ kanɩn Kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ maŋa.

eng: Dont fool yourself. If some of you think they are worldly-wise, then they should become foolish so that they can become wise.
local: Ɔkʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔkʋ ɔ man pɩna mʋ n‐yɩɩ. Nɩɩ fanɛ tɔ ɔkʋ kan ɛɛ fɛ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ ɔ gyɛ ɔnyaasɩnpʋ dulinyaa asa ansi sʋ mʋ, ɩ dagaa maa waa mʋ n‐yɩɩ fɛɛ omelensipu, kɛ ɔ nyɛ biliŋi ɔnyaasɩnpʋ gbaa‐gbaa.

eng: This worlds wisdom is foolishness to God. As its written, He catches the wise in their cleverness. .
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ kanyaasɩn gyɛ amelensi‐sɩn Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ. I du nɩn fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ yɛ, Anyaasɩnpʋ mʋ, bamʋ kanyaasɩn awaasa mʋ tɔ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sʋ ɛɛ kɩtaa bamʋ.

eng: And also, The Lord knows that the thoughts of the wise are silly .
local: Nɩɩ ba bɩla kyʋrɔɔ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ ntɩn kʋ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ nyi fɛɛ anyaasɩnpʋ mʋ nfɛɛrɛ ɩ maa gyɛ kɩyan.

eng: So then, no one should brag about human beings. Everything belongs to you.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔkʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔkʋ, ɔ man taa mʋ n‐yɩɩ tʋʋ nyɩmɩsa sʋ kɛ ɔ kaapʋ mʋ n‐yɩɩ, lɩɩ fɛɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ gyɛ fanɛ lɛɛ nɩn.

eng: Paul, Apollos, Cephas, the world, life, death, things in the present, things in the future—everything belongs to you.
local: Nɩɩ mɛ Pɔɔlɩ nɩn‐oo, abɛɛ Apolosi nɩn‐oo, abɛɛ Pita nɩn‐oo, abɛɛ dulinyaa nɩmʋ nɩn‐oo, abɛɛ nkpa nɩn‐oo, abɛɛ lewu nɩn‐oo, abɛɛ nbɩɩnbɩɩ nɩn‐oo, abɛɛ ɔkɛ asɩn nɩn‐oo, ɩmʋ kpini ɩ gyɛ fanɛ lɛɛ nɩn.

eng: but you belong to Christ, and Christ belongs to God.
local: Nɩɩ fanɛ gbaa fanɛ fan gyɛ Krisito lɛɛ nɩn, nɩɩ Krisito mɔɔ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ.

eng: So a person should think about us this way—as servants of Christ and managers of Gods secrets.
local: Kanɩn sʋ ɩ dagaa fan maa kɩɩ anɛ fɛɛ an gyɛ Krisito ayaafɔlɛ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ taa mʋ kesintin asɩn baalasa mʋ waa anɛ abaa tɔ yɛ a lɛɛ ɩmʋ kaapʋ.

eng: In this kind of situation, what is expected of a manager is that they prove to be faithful.
local: Nɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ dagaa sa ɔmʋ nɩɩ ba taa asɩn waa mʋ abaa mʋ ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ, o buu mʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ mʋ asɩn mʋ sʋ.

eng: I couldnt care less if Im judged by you or by any human court; I dont even judge myself.
local: Amaa mɛɛmɛ asɛ bɩrɛ, nɩɩ fanɛ abɛɛ asɩn ogyipu kʋ gyii mɛ asɩn mʋ, ɩ man tiri mɛ, mɛ gbaa‐gbaa n mee gyii mɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ asɩn.

eng: Im not aware of anything against me, but that doesnt make me innocent, because the Lord is the one who judges me.
local: Mɛ nfɛɛrɛ tɔ mʋ, n man wu asɩn pwɩɩsa kʋ mɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ, amaa ɩ mɛɛ kaapʋ fɛɛ n gyii asʋ. Amɔɔ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ nkʋn ɩ gyɛ mɛ asɩn ogyipu.

eng: So dont judge anything before the right time—wait until the Lord comes. He will bring things that are hidden in the dark to light, and he will make peoples motivations public. Then there will be recognition for each person from God.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan man kan gyii ɔkʋ asɩn, bun mʋ kʋpwɛ, pɔyɩ kɛ kakɛ mʋ ɔ fʋʋ. Fan gyoo Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ ɔ ba pɔyɩ. Ee biti ɔ ba lɛɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ baala kitentenbiri tɔ mʋ ba fɩtɩla tɔ, kɛ ɔ lɛɛ asa nkɔlɔ tɔ asɩn baalasa ifuli. Kanɩn saŋa maŋa nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa ii biti ɔ nyɛ kɩyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa mʋ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I have applied these things to myself and Apollos for your benefit. Ive done this so that you can learn what it means not to go beyond what has been written and so none of you will become arrogant by supporting one of us against the other.
local: Mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana, lɩɩ fanɛ sʋ nɩɩ n taa n maa Apolosi waa sɩnkaala sa fanɛ, kɛ fan bɩɩla asɩn tɔwɛsa mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, Fan man waa asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ yɩla mʋ kyʋn sʋ. Ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, fan man kaapʋ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ naa yɩlɛ ɔkʋ kamaa kɛ fan kina ɔkʋ.

eng: Who says that you are better than anyone else? What do you have that you didnt receive? And if you received it, then why are you bragging as if you didnt receive it?.
local: Mɩnɛ nɩɩ fan sʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ fan kyɔ asansa? Mɩnɛ nɩɩ fan sʋ nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ sa fanɛ? Nɩɩ ɩ kan ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ sa fanɛ ɩmʋ mʋ, mɩnɛ ɩ waa sʋ nɩɩ fɛn kaapʋ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ, fɛɛ man gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ sa fanɛ?.

eng: Youve been filled already! Youve become rich already! You rule like kings without us! I wish you did rule so that we could be kings with you!.
local: Fan tɛɛ nyɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fen biti nɩn abɛɛ? Fan tɛɛ ba waa afulee wuya ana nɩn abɛɛ? Fan tɛɛ ba biliŋi awura yɛgɛ a man gyɛ nɩn abɛɛ? Nɩɩ fan daa fan gyɛ awura kesintin tɔ mʋ, naafɔɔ ɩ bʋrɔn gaa kɛ anaa fanɛ a ba bʋga gyii kuwura.

eng: I suppose that God has shown that we apostles are at the end of the line. We are like prisoners sentenced to death, because we have become a spectacle in the world, both to angels and to humans.
local: Mɛ asɛ mʋ, i du nɩn fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ maa taa anɛ mʋ nɩɩ an gyɛ Yesu asunpu mʋ, waa asii‐amaapʋ fɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba bun bamʋ kʋpwɛ, nɩɩ bee biti ba naa mɔɔ bamʋ asa nsana mʋ. Nɩɩ i du fɛɛ an biliŋi sɩnkɩɩ sa kasɛ sʋ asa yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ yɛ dulinyaa kpini.

eng: We are fools for Christ, but you are wise through Christ! We are weak, but you are strong! You are honored, but we are dishonored!.
local: Krisito sʋ an gyɛ amelensipu, amaa fanɛɛ fanɛ fan gyɛ anyaasɩnpʋ lɩɩ Krisito sʋ. Anɛɛnɛ bɩrɛ a man bʋlʋn, amaa fanɛɛ fanɛ bɩrɛ fan bʋlʋn. Asa bɛɛ sa fanɛ bɛɛrɛɛ, nɩɩ anɛɛnɛ bɩrɛ ba man taa anɛ tɩɩ sɛhn.

eng: Up to this very moment we are hungry, thirsty, wearing rags, abused, and homeless.
local: Taa ba fʋʋ ndaga mʋ, an naa akʋn yɛ bʋlɩfɔ, nɩɩ an bun akyiibi, nɩɩ an nyɛ kɩdayɛ libi‐libi, nɩɩ an man sʋ anɛ gbaa‐gbaa kewu‐kyɩnasa.

eng: We work hard with our own hands. When we are insulted, we respond with a blessing; when we are harassed, we put up with it.
local: Anɛ gbaa‐gbaa anɛ abaa nɩɩ an sʋ an sun kusun. Nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan tɔwɛ anɛ nnɔ mʋ, an waa mʋ kusee. Nɩɩ ba kan nyanla anɛ mʋ, an nyɛ kenyiita an yɩlɛ tɔ.

eng: when our reputation is attacked, we are encouraging. We have become the scum of the earth, the waste that runs off everything, up to the present time.
local: Nɩɩ ba maa saalɛ anɛ mʋ, an nyɛ kenyiita an lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ danbɩrasa. An biliŋi dulinyaa kpini akpaa nɩɩ asa bɛɛ kɩɩ anɛ fɛɛ kɩgyaakpa ba lɩɩ ndaga.

eng: Im not writing these things to make you ashamed but to warn you, since you are my loved children.
local: N man kyʋrɔɔ ɩnɩmʋ n sa fanɛ n gyila fanɛ ipeeli. Amaa fan maa gyɛ mɛ nbii bitisa sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ n da fanɛ amu tɔ.

eng: You may have ten thousand mentors in Christ, but you dont have many fathers. I gave birth to you in Christ Jesus through the gospel.
local: Halɩɩ nɩɩ fan kan fan sʋ asa fɛɛ ngbɩn kudu nɩɩ bɛɛ kaapʋ fanɛ Krisito asɩn mʋ gbaa mʋ, fanɛ‐sɛ ana ba man gyɛ adamantɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ, lɩɩ Yesu Krisito sʋ mʋ n ba biliŋi fanɛ‐sɛ, lɩɩ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ sa fanɛ mʋ sʋ.

eng: so I encourage you to follow my example.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ n kʋlɛ fanɛ nɩn yɛ fan kasɛ mɛ tɔ.

eng: This is why Ive sent Timothy to you; hes my loved and trusted child in the Lord; hell remind you about my way of life in Christ Jesus. Hell teach the same way as I teach everywhere in every church.
local: Lɩɩ falɛ sʋ nɩɩ n sun Timoti fanɛ asɛ. Nɩɩ mʋ ɩ gyɛ mi‐bii mʋ nɩɩ n biti mʋ asɩn gaa, nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ kesintin ogyipu Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ mʋ ii biti o be nyiŋi fanɛ mɛ ɩkpa mʋ nɩɩ n taa n buu Krisito Yesu, nɩɩ ɩmaa ɩmʋ nɩɩ n kaapʋ Krisito kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ katɩn kʋmaa nɩmʋ mʋ, ɩ waa kʋkʋlʋn.

eng: Some have become arrogant as if Im not coming to see you.
local: Fanɛ akʋ fɛn kʋsaa fanɛ n‐yɩɩ fɛn fɛ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ, akʋtɔ n mɛɛ bɩla yɛ n ba bɔɔ fanɛ sʋ.

eng: But, if the Lord is willing, Ill come to you soon. Then I wont focus on what these arrogant people say, but Ill find out what power they possess.
local: Amaa nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ kan kyula bɩrɛ, ɩ mɛɛ yɛ ɩ kyɛɛrɛɛ n biti n ba fanɛ asɛ kɛ mɛ gbaa‐gbaa mɛ n ba wu ɔlʋn mʋ nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ kaapʋ bamʋ n‐yɩɩ mʋ ba sʋ, man gyɛ bamʋ ntɔwɛ nkʋn.

eng: Gods kingdom isnt about words but about power.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ man gyɛ asɩn kɩtɔwɛ, amaa kɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔlʋn mʋ tɔ atɔ waasa nɩn.

eng: Which do you want? Should I come to you with a big stick to punish you, or with love and a gentle spirit?.
local: Kɩmɔmɔ nɩɩ fen biti n taa ba fanɛ asɛ? Fen biti fɛɛ n taa kʋkyʋkyʋʋ nɩn n ba, abɛɛ n taa kebiti yɛ keyuula ba fanɛ asɛ?.

eng: Everyone has heard that there is sexual immorality among you. This is a type of immorality that isnt even heard of among the Gentiles—a man is having sex with his fathers wife!.
local: Kesintin tɔ, n nu asɩn lɩɩ fanɛ sʋ fɛɛ, kakyɩɩ abɛɛ kanyɩn biti agyaga‐sɩn kɩbansa ɩ maa bʋ fanɛ tɔ. Nɩɩ halɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ be mee sun Ɩbwaarɛ gbaa, ba mɛɛ waa kanɩn agyaga‐sɩn maŋa. Ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ fanɛ tɔ ɔkʋ ɩɩ dɛ yɛ mʋ‐sɛ mʋ‐ka.

eng: And youre proud of yourselves instead of being so upset that the one who did this thing is expelled from your community.
local: Mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ ɩnɩmʋ ɩ bʋ fanɛ tɔ, nɩɩ fɛn kʋsaa fanɛ n‐yɩɩ? Ɩ dagaa fan maa bɔɔta tɔ, kɛ fan lɛɛ ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ o sun falɛ kusun mʋ lɩɩ fanɛ kʋbʋ mʋ tɔ.

eng: Though Im absent physically, Im present in the spirit and Ive already judged the man who did this as if I were present.
local: Halɩɩ n maa man bʋ fanɛ asɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ tɔ gbaa mʋ, n bʋ fanɛ asɛ kayaayu tɔ. Nɩɩ n tɛɛ wʋla bun ɔnyɩn maŋa kʋpwɛ fɛɛ n maa bʋ fanɛ asɛ tʋtɔ mʋ.

eng: When you meet together in the name of our Lord Jesus, Ill be present in spirit with the power of our Lord Jesus.
local: Nɩɩ fan kan gyaŋɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu Krisito kɩtɩɩ tɔ, nɩɩ n bʋ fanɛ asɛ kayaayu tɔ, nɩɩ anɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Yesu Krisito ɔlʋn mʋ bʋ fanɛ asɛ mʋ.

eng: At that time we need to hand this man over to Satan to destroy his human weakness so that his spirit might be saved on the day of the Lord.
local: fan lɛɛ falɛ ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ lɩɩ fanɛ asɛ sa Sɩtaanɛ, o nyita mu alibi kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ mʋ, kɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ mɔlɩgɛ mʋ kala kakɛ mʋ nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu ii biti ɔ ba mʋ.

eng: Your bragging isnt good! Dont you know that a tiny grain of yeast makes a whole batch of dough rise?.
local: Fanɛ n‐yɩɩ kɩkaapʋ kɩ man bʋrɔn. Fan man nyi fɛɛ kɩfɛ tinsa pii ɩɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ bodobodo ii tin?.

eng: Clean out the old yeast so you can be a new batch of dough, given that youre supposed to be unleavened bread. Christ our Passover lamb has been sacrificed.
local: Fan lɛɛ kɩfɛ tinsa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ alibi mʋ lɩɩ fanɛ tɔ, yɛgɛ fan man sʋ ineesi ɩkʋ. Kɛ fan baa fan du fɛɛ bodobodo pʋpwɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ man sʋ kɩfɛ tinsa, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ n nyi fan maa du mʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ Yesu Krisito mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ Kanyaŋɛ nbunɔ kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ tɛɛ taa mʋ waa kɔlɔŋɔ sa anɛ.

eng: so lets celebrate the feast with the unleavened bread of honesty and truth, not with old yeast or with the yeast of evil and wickedness.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan yɛgɛ a gyii Kanyaŋɛ nbunɔ kesu kɔnɔ, kɛ a lɛɛ alibi yɛ kumu tɔ ɔlʋn mʋ nɩɩ i du fɛɛ bodobodo mʋ nɩɩ kɩ sʋ kɩfɛ tinsa dɩdaa mʋ. Amaa fan yɛgɛ i gyii Kesu kɔnɔ mʋ, fɛɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ kɩdanbɩrasa yɛ kesintin mʋ fɛɛ bodobodo mʋ nɩɩ kɩ man sʋ kɩfɛ tinsa mʋ.

eng: I wrote to you in my earlier letter not to associate with sexually immoral people.
local: N wʋla kyʋrɔɔ ɔwʋlɛ sa fanɛ yɛ, fan man taa fanɛ n‐yɩɩ bʋga asa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ kakyɩɩ abɛɛ kanyɩn biti kɩgyagaa mʋ tɔ.

eng: But I wasnt talking about the sexually immoral people in the outside world by any means—or the greedy, or the swindlers, or people who worship false gods—otherwise, you would have to leave the world entirely!.
local: N maa tɔwɛ kanɩn mʋ, man gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ be mee sun Ɩbwaarɛ, nɩɩ bɛɛ waa kakyɩɩ abɛɛ kanyɩn biti kɩgyagaa, yɛ onsi‐payɩ libi abɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bee yuuri abɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ sun ɩdakpa, nɩɩ n dɩyɛ. Naafɔɔ nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ yɛ fan man taa fanɛ n‐yɩɩ bʋga yɛ kanɩn asa maŋa bɩrɛ, amɔɔ fan lɩɩ dulinyaa tɔ kpini‐kpini.

eng: But now Im writing to you not to associate with anyone who calls themselves brother or sister who is sexually immoral, greedy, someone who worships false gods, an abusive person, a drunk, or a swindler. Dont even eat with anyone like this.
local: Amaa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ n kyʋrɔɔ n sa fanɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, fan man taa fanɛ n‐yɩɩ bʋga yɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ n‐yɩɩ yɛ Krisito obuupu, yɛgɛ ɔ gyɛ akyɩɩ abɛɛ anyɩn bitipu, yɛ onsi‐payɩ libi wuya, abɛɛ ɔdakpa osunpu, abɛɛ asa atɩɩ onyitapu, abɛɛ ɔtabʋʋpʋ, abɛɛ oyu. Fan naa ɔsa maŋa fan man bʋga gyii gbaa.

eng: What do I care about judging outsiders? Isnt it your job to judge insiders?.
local: Ɩ man gyɛ mi kusun fɛɛ n gyii bamʋ nɩɩ ba man bʋ Krisito kʋbʋ mʋ tɔ asɩn, amaa bamʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Krisito kʋbʋ mʋ tɔ mʋ, ɩ dagaa n maa gyii bamʋ asɩn.

eng: God will judge outsiders. Expel the evil one from among you! .
local: Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti o gyii bamʋ nɩɩ ba man tii Krisito kʋbʋ mʋ sʋ asɩn. Amaa fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ mʋ, Fan gya alibi ɔwaapʋ lɩɩ fanɛ tɔ.

eng: Now, about what you wrote: Its good for a man not to have sex with a woman.
local: Nɩɩ lɩɩ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ fan kyʋrɔɔ ɔwʋlɛ bɩya mɛ mʋ sʋ mʋ, n biti n lɛɛ ɩmʋ kɔnɔ. Ɩ bʋrɔn fɛɛ ɔnyɩn maa kyɩna yɛgɛ ɔ man mata ɔkyɩɩ.

eng: Each man should have his own wife, and each woman should have her own husband because of sexual immorality.
local: Amaa lɩɩ kakyɩɩ abɛɛ kanyɩn biti kɩgyagaa sʋ mʋ, ɩ dagaa ɔnyɩn kʋmaa nɩmʋ maa nyɛ mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mʋ‐ka, kɛ ɔkyɩɩ kʋmaa gbaa ɔ nyɛ mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mu‐kuli.

eng: The husband should meet his wifes sexual needs, and the wife should do the same for her husband.
local: Ɔnyɩn kilipu o kyula maa mʋ‐ka ba bʋga ɔdɛkpa, kɛ ɔkyɩɩ kilipu gbaa o kyula maa mu‐kuli ba bʋga ɔdɛkpa.

eng: The wife doesnt have authority over her own body, but the husband does. Likewise, the husband doesnt have authority over his own body, but the wife does.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkyɩɩ kilipu kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ man bɩla kɩ gyɛ mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mʋ nkʋn lɛɛ nɩn, amaa kɩ gyɛ mu‐kuli gbaa lɛɛ nɩn. Kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ ɔnyɩn kilipu gbaa kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ man gyɛ mʋ nkʋn lɛɛ, amaa kɩ gyɛ mʋ‐ka gbaa lɛɛ nɩn.

eng: Dont refuse to meet each others needs unless you both agree for a short period of time to devote yourselves to prayer. Then come back together again so that Satan might not tempt you because of your lack of self-control.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔnyɩn maa mʋ‐ka ba man kina abaa asɛ kɩdɛ kɩnyɩn yɛ kɩkyɩɩ. Amɔɔ fan tɔwɛ kyula abaa yɛ fan mɛɛ yɛ fan dɛ yɛ abaa saŋa gbɛrɛɛ, kɛ fan nyɛ‐nyɛ saŋa sa kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ. Amaa ɩmʋ kamaa mʋ, fan bwii bʋga dɛ kɩnyɩn yɛ kɩkyɩɩ, saamʋ Sɩtaanɛ mɛɛ nyɛ tʋʋ fanɛ tɔ kɩɩ, lɩɩ fan maa mɛɛ taalɛ kɩtaa fanɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ.

eng: Im saying this to give you permission; its not a command.
local: N maa tɔwɛ yɛ ɔkʋmaa ɔ nyɛ mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mʋ‐ka abɛɛ mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mu‐kuli mʋ, man gyɛ yɛ n nyan fanɛ nɩn, nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ nbara nɩn yɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ o kili.

eng: I wish all people were like me, but each has a particular gift from God: one has this gift, and another has that one.
local: Naafɔɔ n biti fɛɛ ɔnyɩn kʋmaa nɩmʋ ɔ baa o du fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ n du falɛ. Amaa nyɩmɩsa kʋmaa nɩmʋ sʋ mʋ gbaa‐gbaa kakɛɛ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ taa sa mʋ, ɔnɩmʋ lɛɛ kwɩɩ, nɩɩ ɔnɩmʋ lɛɛ gbaa kwɩɩ.

eng: Im telling those who are single and widows that its good for them to stay single like me.
local: Nbɩɩnbɩɩ lɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man pɩɩta kili yɛ akulapu‐kyɩɩ mʋ, n tɔwɛ bamʋ fɛɛ ɩ bʋrɔn sa bamʋ fɛɛ ba maa man kili fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ n man kili mʋ.

eng: But if they cant control themselves, they should get married, because its better to marry than to burn with passion.
local: Amaa nɩɩ ba mɛɛ yɛ ba kan taalɛ kɩtaa bamʋ n‐yɩɩ bɩrɛ, ɩ dagaa ba maa kili. Lɩɩ fɛɛ nɩɩ ba kili mʋ, ɩ bʋrɔn sa bamʋ ɩ kyɔ kakyɩɩ abɛɛ kanyɩn biti kayalaga kɩ maa bʋ bamʋ tɔ.

eng: Im passing on the Lords command to those who are married: A wife shouldnt leave her husband.
local: Lɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba kili mʋ mɔɔ, n sa fanɛ kɔnɔ, nɩɩ kanɩn kɔnɔsa maŋa man lɩɩ mɛ asɛ, ɩ lɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu Krisito asɛ nɩn. Ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ ɔkyɩɩ kilipu ɔ man kina mu‐kuli.

eng: but if she does leave him, then she should stay single or be reconciled to her husband. And a man shouldnt divorce his wife.
local: Amaa nɩɩ ɔ kan kina mu‐kuli mʋ, ɔ man bɩla kili, amɔɔ o bwii kpee, kɛ maa mu‐kuli ba naa lɔŋɔ abaa. Nɩɩ ɔkpa kʋlʋn maŋa sʋ ɔnyɩn gbaa ɔ man kina mʋ‐ka.

eng: Im telling everyone else : If a believer has a wife who doesnt believe, and she agrees to live with him, then he shouldnt divorce her.
local: Nɩɩ asansa mʋ mɔɔ mɛ gbaa‐gbaa mɛ ɩ tɔwɛ ɩnɩmʋ, man gyɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ. Nɩɩ fanɛ Krisito abuupu ɔkʋ kan ɔ sʋ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man gyɛ ɔkɔɔlɛ‐gyipu, nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ kyula yɛ maa ɔnyɩn mʋ ba kyɩna mʋ, ɔnyɩn mʋ ɔ man kina mʋ.

eng: If a woman has a husband who doesnt believe and he agrees to live with her, then she shouldnt divorce him.
local: Nɩɩ fanɛ Krisito obuupu ɔkyɩɩ kan ɔ sʋ ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man gyɛ ɔkɔɔlɛ‐gyipu, nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ kyula yɛ maa ɔkyɩɩ mʋ ba kyɩna mʋ, ɔkyɩɩ mʋ ɔ man kina ɔnyɩn mʋ.

eng: The husband who doesnt believe belongs to God because of his wife, and the wife who doesnt believe belongs to God because of her husband. Otherwise, your children would be contaminated by the world, but now they are spiritually set apart.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ maa gyɛ ɔkɔɔlɛ‐gyipu sʋ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ wu fɛɛ ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man gyɛ ɔkɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ, n‐yɩɩ sʋ ɩ man bɩla ɩ sʋ ineesi. Kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ ɔkɔɔlɛ‐gyipu sʋ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ wu fɛɛ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man gyɛ ɔkɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ, mʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋ ɩ man bɩla ɩ sʋ ineesi. Nɩɩ man gyɛ kanɩn mʋ bamʋ‐bii ana bee biti ba baa ba gyɛ ineesi nbii, amaa ɩ maa ba falɛ sʋ mʋ Ɩbwaarɛ wu fɛɛ ba man sʋ ineesi.

eng: But if a spouse who doesnt believe chooses to leave, then let them leave. The brother or sister isnt tied down in these circumstances. God has called you to peace.
local: Amaa Krisito obuupu mʋ nɩɩ mʋ‐ka abɛɛ mu‐kuli mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man gyɛ Krisito obuupu, nɩɩ ee biti fɛɛ o kina mʋ bɩrɛ mʋ, ɔ yɛgɛ mʋ ɔ natɛ. Nɩɩ ɩ kan ba kanɩn mʋ, kekili nbara ɩ man ŋmina mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ tɩɩ anɛ nɩn yɛ a ba kyɩna kayɩɩ yuuli tɔ.

eng: How do you know as a wife if you will save your husband? Or how do you know as a husband if you will save your wife?.
local: Fʋ Krisito obuupu ɔkyɩɩ, akʋtɔ fʋ sʋ nɩɩ fu‐kuli ii biti ɔ bɔla nyɛ kamɔlɩgɛ? Nɩɩ fʋ Krisito obuupu ɔnyɩn, akʋtɔ fɛɛ fʋ sʋ nɩɩ fʋ‐ka ii biti ɔ bɔla nyɛ kamɔlɩgɛ?.

eng: Nevertheless, each person should live the kind of life that the Lord assigned when he called each one. This is what I teach in all the churches.
local: Ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ ɔ kyaga sʋ kyɩna kakyɩna mʋ nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ taa sa mʋ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ wʋla o du pɔyɩ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ o be sun mʋ mʋ. Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ kɔnɔsa mʋ nɩɩ n kaapʋ Krisito kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ mʋ kpini tɔ.

eng: If someone was circumcised when called, he shouldnt try to reverse it. If someone wasnt circumcised when he was called, he shouldnt be circumcised.
local: Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɩn mʋ oduu pɔyɩ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tɩɩ mʋ mʋ, ɔ man bun mʋ oduu kɩlɔpanɛ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man kan tɩn oduu, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tɩɩ mʋ mʋ, ɔ man tɩn mʋ oduu.

eng: Circumcision is nothing; not being circumcised is nothing. What matters is keeping Gods commandments.
local: Fʋ tɩn oduu oo, fʋ man tɩn oduu oo, man gyɛ ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ki tiri. Amaa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ki tiri mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ fan buu Ɩbwaarɛ nbara mʋ sʋ.

eng: Each person should stay in the situation they were in when they were called.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ ɔ baa o du fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ wʋla o du pɔyɩ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ o be sun mʋ mʋ.

eng: If you were a slave when you were called, dont let it bother you. But if you are actually able to be free, take advantage of the opportunity.
local: A taa fɛɛ nɩɩ fʋ kan fʋ gyɛ kɩnyɛ pɔyɩ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tɩɩ fʋ yɛ fʋ ba sun mʋ mʋ man yɛgɛ ɩ tɔɔrɔɔ fʋ. Amaa nɩɩ fʋ nyɛ ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ fʋ taa kɔɔlɛ fʋ n‐yɩɩ bɩrɛ, waa kanɩn.

eng: Anyone who was a slave when they were called by the Lord has the status of being the Lords free person. In the same way, anyone who was a free person when they were called is Christs slave.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ fanɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ kɩnyɛ nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ tɩɩ mʋ, nɩɩ o be biliŋi Krisito obuupu mʋ, Krisito ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ. Nɩɩ ɔkpa kʋlʋn maŋa sʋ, fanɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ ɔ ba waa Krisito obuupu mʋ, gyɛ Krisito kɩnyɛ.

eng: You were bought and paid for. Dont become slaves of people.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Krisito sɔɔ fanɛ kpini ɔgyalɩn, ɩmʋ sʋ fan man gyɛ anyɛ sa asa.

eng: So then, brothers and sisters, each of you should stay with God in the situation you were in when you were called.
local: Mɛ bɩrɩsa ana Krisito abuupu, kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ wʋla o du pɔyɩ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tɩɩ mʋ mʋ, ɔ kyɩna kanɩn yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: I dont have a command from the Lord about people who have never been married, but Ill give you my opinion as someone you can trust because of the Lords mercy.
local: Nbɩɩnbɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man pɩɩta kili mʋ bɩrɛ, n man nyɛ nbara kʋ lɩɩ anɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Yesu asɛ lɩɩ bamʋ sʋ. Amaa mɛ Pɔɔlɩ gbaa‐gbaa n tɔwɛ n sa bamʋ lɩɩ mɛ nfɛɛrɛ tɔ, fɛɛ n maa gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ wu kʋwɛɛ sʋ mʋ, fɛn taalɛ kɔɔlɛ mɛ gyii fɛɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ fanɛ falɛ, n mɛɛ pɩna fanɛ nɩn.

eng: So I think this advice is good because of the present crisis: Stay as you are.
local: Mɛ asɛ mʋ, ɩ bʋrɔn fɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man pɩɩ kili mʋ, ba kyɩna kanɩn, lɩɩ fɛɛ nbɩɩnbɩɩ kakyɩna kanɩmʋ tɔ waa ɔlʋn.

eng: If you are married, dont get a divorce. If you are divorced, dont try to find a spouse.
local: Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ o tee kili mʋ, ɔ man kina mʋ‐ka. Amaa ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man pɩɩta kili mʋ, ɔ man mata ɔkyɩɩ.

eng: But if you do marry, you havent sinned; and if someone who hasnt been married gets married, they havent sinned. But married people will have a hard time, and Im trying to spare you that.
local: Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn kan kili mʋ, ɔ man waa alibi. Nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man pɩɩta kili nɩɩ ɔ kan kili mʋ, ɔ man waa alibi. Amaa asa mʋ nɩɩ ba kili mʋ, awʋrʋfɔ mʋ nɩɩ bee biti be wu kakyɩna kanɩmʋ tɔ mʋ, ɩmʋ nɩɩ n mee biti fan maa wu.

eng: This is what Im saying, brothers and sisters: The time has drawn short. From now on, those who have wives should be like people who dont have them.
local: Mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana Krisito abuupu, kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ n kaapʋ mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, saŋa man bɩla ɔ kyɔ kɛ dulinyaa kɩ ba laalaalʋwɛ, ɩmʋ sʋ lɩɩ ndaga ɩɩ sʋ ii kpee mʋ, anyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba kili mʋ ba kyɩna fɛɛ anyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba man sʋ akyɩɩ.

eng: Those who are sad should be like people who arent crying. Those who are happy should be like people who arent happy. Those who buy something should be like people who dont have possessions.
local: Kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba bɔɔta tɔ mʋ, ba waa fɛɛ ɩ maa man tiri bamʋ, nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ bamʋ ansi i gyii mʋ mɔɔ, ba waa fɛɛ bamʋ ansi ii mee gyii, nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba sɔɔ mʋ, ba waa fɛɛ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba sɔɔ mʋ, ɩ man gyɛ bamʋ lɛɛ.

eng: Those who use the world should be like people who arent preoccupied with it, because this world in its present form is passing away.
local: Bamʋ nɩɩ ba taa dulinyaa atɔ waasa bee sun kusun mʋ, ba waa fɛɛ ɩ man tiri bamʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ dulinyaa du nbɩɩnbɩɩ nɩmʋ mʋ, kɩɩ kyʋn nɩmʋ.

eng: I want you to be free from concerns. A man who isnt married is concerned about the Lords concerns—how he can please the Lord.
local: N biti fɛɛ fan lɛɛ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ lɩɩ nfɛɛrɛ kɩbansa tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man sʋ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mu kusun i tiri mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɛɛ kpan mʋ n‐yɩɩ ɛɛ waa kusun kanan mʋ nɩɩ i gyii Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ ansi.

eng: But a married man is concerned about the worlds concerns—how he can please his wife.
local: Amaa ɔnyɩn kilipu bɩrɛ, mʋ nfɛɛrɛ ɩ gyan dulinyaa asɩn sʋ nɩn, kanan mʋ nɩɩ ee biti o gyii mʋ‐ka ansi.

eng: His attention is divided. A woman who isnt married or who is a virgin is concerned about the Lords concerns so that she can be dedicated to God in both body and spirit. But a married woman is concerned about the worlds concerns—how she can please her husband.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ nfɛɛrɛ nnyɔ‐nnyɔ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ. Nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man kili mʋ mɔɔ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mu kusun i tiri mʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ ɔ taa mʋ n‐yɩɩ yɛ mʋ kala kpini sa Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ. Amaa ɔkyɩɩ‐kilipu bɩrɛ mʋ nfɛɛrɛ ɩ gyan dulinyaa asɩn sʋ nɩn, yɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ waa kɛ o gyii mu‐kuli ansi.

eng: Im saying this for your own advantage. Its not to restrict you but rather to promote effective and consistent service to the Lord without distraction.
local: Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɩ kyɛ fanɛ tɔ sʋ, nɩɩ n tɔwɛ fanɛ ɩnɩmʋ n man gyɛ yɛ n nyan fanɛ nɩn. Amaa n biti fan waa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ dɛ ɔkpa yɛgɛ kɩ bʋrɔn, kɛ fan nyɛ taa fanɛ n‐yɩɩ kpini sa Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ naa lʋwɛ.

eng: If someone thinks he is acting inappropriately toward an unmarried woman whom he knows, and if he has strong feelings and it seems like the right thing to do, he should do what he wants—hes not sinning—they should get married.
local: Nɩɩ ɩ kan ba fɛɛ ɔnyɩn maa ɔkyɩɩ ba ŋmina fɛɛ ba kili, nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ mɛɛ taalɛ kɩtaa mʋ n‐yɩɩ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ mɔɔ dan fʋʋ kekili mʋ, ɔnyɩn mʋ ɔ waa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ dagaa, kɛ ba kili abaa. Ɩ man gyɛ alibi nɩn.

eng: But if a man stands firm in his decision, and doesnt feel the pressure, but has his own will under control, he does right if he decides in his own heart not to marry the woman.
local: Amaa nɩɩ ɔnyɩn kan fɛ nfɛɛrɛ yɛ ɔ mɛɛ yɛ o kili, nɩɩ ɔ yɩlɛ sʋ kanɩn mʋ, ɔkʋ ɔ man nyan mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taalɛ kɩtaa mʋ n‐yɩɩ mʋ ɩ bʋrɔn.

eng: Therefore, the one who marries the unmarried woman does right, and the one who doesnt get married will do even better.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ o kili ɔkyɩɩ mʋ ɩ bʋrɔn, nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man kili ɔkyɩɩ mɔɔ mʋ ɩ bʋrɔn gaa.

eng: A woman is obligated to stay in her marriage as long as her husband is alive. But if her husband dies, she is free to marry whomever she wants, only it should be a believer in the Lord.
local: Ɔkyɩɩ kilipu mʋ nɩɩ mu‐kuli san ɔ tɛ nkpa tɔ mʋ, ɔ mɛɛ taalɛ kina mu‐kuli kɛ ɔ naa kili ɔnyɩn pʋpwɛ. Amaa nɩɩ mu‐kuli mʋ wu bɩrɛ, ɔ sʋ ɔkpa ɔ taalɛ kili ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ mʋ kɔkɔlɔ kii biti, amaa ɔ baa ɔ gyɛ Krisito obuupu.

eng: But in my opinion, she will be happier if she stays the way she is. And I think that I have Gods Spirit too.
local: Mɛ asɛ mʋ, ɔkyɩɩ kyɩna mʋ nkʋn mʋ, ii biti ɩ waa ɔdan sa mʋ, ɩ kyɔ maa kili. Nɩɩ n kɔɔlɛ gyii fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu mʋ kɩ tɛ mɛ tɔ.

eng: Now concerning meat that has been sacrificed to a false god: We know that we all have knowledge. Knowledge makes people arrogant, but love builds people up.
local: Nbɩɩnbɩɩ, taa lɩɩ agyitɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba taa lɔŋɔ sa ɩdakpa mʋ, an nyi fɛɛ anɛ kpini an sʋ kinyi fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fɛn taalɛ fɛn tɔwɛ mʋ. Kinyi ɩɩ bɩya n‐yɩɩ kɩkʋsaa, amaa kebiti kɩɩ kyɛ asa tɔ.

eng: If anyone thinks they know something, they dont yet know as much as they should know.
local: Ɔsa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ fɛ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ o nyi kʋtɔ kʋ mʋ, o mee nyi naa fʋʋ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ ɔ baa o nyi mʋ.

eng: But if someone loves God, then they are known by God.
local: Amaa ɔmʋ nɩɩ ee biti Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ, mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ nyi.

eng: So concerning the actual food involved in these sacrifices to false gods, we know that a false god isnt anything in this world, and that there is no God except for the one God.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ lɩɩ agyitɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba taa lɔŋɔ sa ɩdakpa mʋ kigyii mʋ, an nyi fɛɛ ɔdakpa kʋ man bʋ tɔ nɩɩ kɩ sʋ nkpa, nɩɩ an nyi fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kʋlʋn nkʋn ɩ bʋ tɔ.

eng: Granted, there are so-called gods, in heaven and on the earth, as there are many gods and many lords.
local: Halɩɩ ɩdakpa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ bamʋ yɛ anyɩrɩpɛ nɩɩ ba bʋ sʋsʋ abɛɛ kasɛ sʋ, nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ kesintin fɛɛ ɩdakpa yɛ anyɩrɩpɛ gaalaagaa ba bʋ tɔ gbaa mʋ.

eng: However, for us believers, There is one God the Father. All things come from him, and we belong to him. And there is one Lord Jesus Christ. All things exist through him, and we live through him.
local: anɛɛnɛ bɩrɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ kʋlʋn nɩɩ an nyi maa bʋ tɔ, nɩɩ mʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɔsɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tʋʋ kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ, nɩɩ an bʋ tɔ sa mʋ, nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ kʋlʋn ɩ bʋ tɔ, mʋ ɩ gyɛ Yesu Krisito mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bɔla mʋ sʋ tʋʋ kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ, nɩɩ mʋ sʋ nɩɩ an bʋ tɔ.

eng: But not everybody knows this. Some are eating this food as though it really is food sacrificed to a real idol, because they were used to idol worship until now. Their conscience is weak because it has been damaged.
local: Amaa man gyɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ i nyi falɛ asɩn nɩmʋ. Ɩdakpa asɩn mʋ ɩ wʋla dɩn akʋ, ɩmʋ sʋ ba lɩɩ ndaga nɩɩ ba kan gyii kanɩn agyitɔ mʋ, bɛɛ fɛ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ agyitɔ mʋ ɩ gyɛ ɩdakpa lɛɛ nɩn. Ba maa man pɩɩ bɩɩ abwaarɛsɩn mʋ tɔ gaa sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ba kan gyii mʋ bɛɛ fɛ nfɛɛrɛ nɩn fɛɛ ba waa bamʋ n‐yɩɩ ineesi.

eng: Food wont bring us close to God. Were not missing out if we dont eat, and we dont have any advantage if we do eat.
local: Amaa agyitɔ mɛɛ taalɛ kyɛ anɛ tɔ kɛ a nin tiri Ɩbwaarɛ. Nɩɩ a man kan gyii mʋ, a mɛɛ yɛ a pan sɛhn, nɩɩ an kan gyii mɔɔ mʋ, a mɛɛ yɛ a nyɛ sɛhn.

eng: But watch out or else this freedom of yours might be a problem for those who are weak.
local: Amaa fan baa fan da sʋ lɩɩ ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ fan nyɛ mʋ sʋ, kɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man pɩɩ bɩɩ abwaarɛsɩn mʋ tɔ gaa mʋ, ba man tɩyɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii tɔ.

eng: Suppose someone sees you eating in an idols temple. Wont the person with a weak conscience be encouraged to eat the meat sacrificed to false gods?.
local: Nɩɩ ɩ kan ba fɛɛ ɔkʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man pɩɩ bɩɩ abwaarɛsɩn mʋ tɔ gaa, nɩɩ o wu fʋ mʋ nɩɩ fu nyi abwaarɛsɩn mʋ tɔ gaa, fʋ maa tɛ ɔdakpa tɔ fii gyii mʋ, ɩnɩmʋ ɩ mɛɛ yɛgɛ, kɛ ngbaa mʋ ɔ naa gyii agyitɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba taa lɔŋɔ sa ɔdakpa mʋ abɛɛ?.

eng: The weak brother or sister for whom Christ died is destroyed by your knowledge.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ fʋ bɩrɩsa mʋ nɩɩ Krisito wu sa mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ man pɩɩ bɩɩ abwaarɛsɩn mʋ tɔ gaa mʋ, ee biti o nyita lɩɩ fʋ kinyi gaa mʋ sʋ.

eng: You sin against Christ if you sin against your brothers and sisters and hurt their weak consciences this way.
local: Nɩɩ fʋ kan waa fʋ bɩrɩsa alibi falɛ ɔkpa sʋ mʋ, fɩɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ mʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii kɩ lɛɛ sʋ, nɩɩ fʋ waa Krisito alibi.

eng: This is why, if food causes the downfall of my brother or sister, I wont eat meat ever again, or else I may cause my brother or sister to fall.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ agyitɔ mʋ nɩɩ n gyii, ii biti ɩ yɛgɛ mɛ bɩrɩsa ɔ tɩyɛ alibi tɔ bɩrɛ, n mɛɛ bɩla yɛ n gyii kanɩn agyitɔ maŋa, kɛ n yɛgɛ ɔ lɩɩ tɩyɛ.

eng: Am I not free? Am I not an apostle? Havent I seen Jesus our Lord? Arent you my work in the Lord?.
local: N man bʋ mɛ n‐yɩɩ nɩn abɛɛ? N man gyɛ Krisito osunpu nɩn abɛɛ? N man wu Yesu anɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ nɩn abɛɛ? Man gyɛ fanɛ ɩ gyɛ kusun mʋ nɩɩ n waa sa Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ tɔnɔ?.

eng: If Im not an apostle to others, at least I am to you! You are the seal that shows Im an apostle.
local: Halɩɩ nɩɩ akʋ ba man kɩɩ mɛ fɛɛ n gyɛ Krisito osunpu gbaa mʋ, fanɛɛ fanɛ bɩrɛ n gyɛ Krisito osunpu sa fanɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ fanɛ gbaa‐gbaa fanɛ kakyɩna Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ, ɩmʋ ii duligi fɛɛ n gyɛ Krisito osunpu nɩn.

eng: This is my defense against those who criticize me.
local: Falɛ asɩn nɩmʋ nɩɩ n sʋ n lɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ n man gyɛ Krisito osunpu mʋ kɔnɔ.

eng: Dont we have the right to eat and drink?.
local: N maa Banabasi a man sʋ ɔkpa fɛɛ i gyii kɛ anuu lɩɩ kusun mʋ nɩɩ an sun mʋ sʋ abɛɛ?.

eng: Dont we have the right to travel with a wife who believes like the rest of the apostles, the Lords brothers, and Cephas?.
local: A man sʋ ɔkpa fɛɛ a kili Krisito abuupu akyɩɩ, kɛ a taa bamʋ buu anɛ n‐yɩɩ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Krisito asunpu akʋ, yɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu mu‐supu ana yɛ Pita bɛɛ waa mʋ abɛɛ?.

eng: Or is it only I and Barnabas who dont have the right to not work for our living?.
local: Abɛɛ anɛ nkʋn ɩ dagaa fɛɛ a taa anɛ abaa waa kusun biti anɛ agyitɔ‐gyisa?.

eng: Who joins the army and pays their own way? Who plants a vineyard and doesnt eat its fruit? Who shepherds a flock and doesnt drink its milk?.
local: Ɔnaakɔpʋ mɔɔ ii sun kusun, nɩɩ ɛɛ ka mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mʋ n‐yɩɩ kʋkɔ? Ɔdɔɔpʋ mɔɔ ɩ bʋ tɔ, nɩɩ o mee gyii mʋ ndɔɔ tɔ agyitɔ? Atɔbwaayaa ɔkpapʋ mɔɔ ɩ bʋ tɔ, nɩɩ o mee nuu mʋ atɔbwaaya mʋ kɩnyapʋ‐kyu.

eng: Im not saying these things just based on common sense, am I? Doesnt the Law itself say these things?.
local: Man gyɛ nyɩmɩsa nfɛɛrɛ nɩɩ n sʋ n tɔwɛ falɛ asɩn nɩmʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɩ bʋ Ɩbwaarɛ nbara mʋ tɔ gbaa.

eng: In Moses Law its written: You will not muzzle the ox when it is threshing . Is God worried about oxen.
local: Ba kyʋrɔɔ yɩla Mosesi Nbara mʋ tɔ yɛ, Man ŋmina naatɛ‐nyɩnsa kɔnɔ, saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ waa agyitɔ tɔ kusun mʋ. Amɔɔ fu nyi fɛɛ ɩnaatɛ‐nyɩnsa asɩn nkʋn nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ falɛ?.

eng: or did he say this entirely for our sake? It was written for our sake because the one who plows and the one who threshes should each do so with the hope of sharing the produce.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ maa tɔwɛ kanɩn mʋ, man gyɛ anɛ nɩɩ ɛɛ tɔwɛ ɛɛ sa abɛɛ? Ɛɛhn! Kesintin, anɛ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ ɩmʋ sa. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ baŋɛ ɩfɔlɛ, yɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ tɩŋɛ ayu mʋ, bamʋ kpini bɛɛ waa kanɩn yɛgɛ ba sʋ tamaa fɛɛ bɛɛ nyɛ bamʋ agyitɔ‐gyisa lɩɩ ɩmʋ tɔ.

eng: If we sowed spiritual things in you, is it so much to ask to harvest some material things from you?.
local: Nɩɩ an maa duu kayaayu tɔ atɔ duusa fanɛ tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ a nyɛ kakyɩna nɩmʋ tɔ atɔ tirisa lɩɩ fanɛ asɛ mʋ, ii kisi nɩn?.

eng: If others have these rights over you, dont we deserve them all the more? However, we havent made use of this right, but we put up with everything so we dont put any obstacle in the way of the gospel of Christ.
local: Nɩɩ akʋ ba sʋ ɔkpa fɛɛ ba kɔɔlɛ falɛ atɔ tirisa lɩɩ fanɛ asɛ mʋ, a man sʋ ɔkpa an kyɔ bamʋ? Halɩɩ a maa sʋ kanɩn ɔkpa gbaa mʋ, a man taa ɩmʋ waa sɛhn. Amaa an nyɛ kenyiita yɩlɛ tɔ, kɛ ɩ man nyɛ tii Asɩn danbɩrasa lɩɩ Krisito sʋ mʋ kɩ yɛlɩgɛ.

eng: Dont you know that those who serve in the temple get to eat food from the temple, and those who serve at the altar share part of what is sacrificed on the altar?.
local: Fan man nyi fɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bee sun kusun kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ, bɛɛ nyɛ bamʋ agyitɔ bɛɛ lɩɩ tʋtɔ nɩn? Nɩɩ fan man nyi fɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ lɛɛ saraga Ɩbwaarɛ kaswɩɩ mʋ sʋ gbaa, bɛɛ nyɛ bamʋ lɛɛ lɩɩ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba taa ba lɛɛ saraga sa Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ.

eng: In the same way, the Lord commanded that those who preach the gospel should get their living from the gospel.
local: Kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu sa kɔnɔ yɩla yɛ, bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ, bee biti ba nyɛ bamʋ agyitɔ gyisa lɩɩ bamʋ asɩn danbɩrasa kɩtɔwɛ mʋ tɔ.

eng: But I havent taken advantage of this. And Im not writing this so that it will be done for me. Its better for me to die than to lose my right to brag about this!.
local: Amaa n man taa falɛ ɩkpa ɩnɩmʋ tɔ ɔkʋ waa kusun. Nɩɩ n man kyʋrɔɔ ɩnɩmʋ nɩn kɛ ɩ yɛgɛ fan waa kanɩn atɔ sa mɛ. Nɩɩ n kan wu gbaa mʋ, ɩ bɔ fɛɛ ɔkʋ ɔ maa yɛgɛ nɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩmʋ sʋ n kaapʋ mɛ n‐yɩɩ mʋ ɩ maa waa kɩyan.

eng: If I preach the gospel, I have no reason to brag, since Im obligated to do it. Im in trouble if I dont preach the gospel.
local: Nɩɩ n kan tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ, n man sʋ ɔkpa fɛɛ n kaapʋ mɛ n‐yɩɩ. Ɩ dan ɩ gyɛ nɩn fɛɛ, n tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ n kan n man tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ, mɛ laakʋ.

eng: If I do this voluntarily, I get rewarded for it. But if Im forced to do it, then Ive been charged with a responsibility.
local: Nɩɩ n kan n waa kusun mʋ mɛ kɩpɩrɛ tɔ mʋ, n biti n nyɛ kʋkɔka lala. Nɩɩ n man kan sun kusun mʋ lɩɩ mɛ kɩpɩrɛ tɔ bɩrɛ, n dan waa kusun mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ taa sa mɛ yɛ n waa mʋ nɩn dee.

eng: What reward do I get? That when I preach, I offer the good news free of charge. Thats why I dont use the rights to which Im entitled through the gospel.
local: Nɩɩ mɩnɛ ɩ gyɛ mɛ kʋkɔka? Ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ n tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ yɛgɛ n man kɔɔlɛ ɩmʋ sɛhn, nɩɩ n mɛɛ taa ɩkpa mʋ nɩɩ n sʋ lɩɩ asɩn danbɩrasa kɩtɔwɛ mʋ sʋ taa nyɛ mɛ atɔ tirisa.

eng: Although Im free from all people, I make myself a slave to all people, to recruit more of them.
local: N bʋ mɛ n‐yɩɩ nɩn, n man gyɛ ɔkʋ kɩnyɛ, amaa n waa mɛ n‐yɩɩ kɩnyɛ sa ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ, kɛ n nyɛ‐nyɛ asa kʋkyɔ ba be biliŋi Krisito lɛɛ.

eng: I act like a Jew to the Jews, so I can recruit Jews. I act like Im under the Law to those under the Law, so I can recruit those who are under the Law .
local: Nɩɩ n maa bʋ Gyiwu abi asɛ mʋ, n waa mɛ n‐yɩɩ nɩn fɛɛ Gyiwu obii, kɛ n nyɛ‐nyɛ bamʋ ba be biliŋi Krisito lɛɛ. Nɩɩ n maa bʋ bamʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Mosesi nbara mʋ kasɛ mʋ, yɛgɛ mɛɛ mɛ n man bʋ nbara mʋ kasɛ mʋ, n waa mɛ n‐yɩɩ nɩn fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba du mʋ, kɛ n nyɛ‐nyɛ bamʋ ba ba biliŋi Krisito lɛɛ.

eng: I act like Im outside the Law to those who are outside the Law, so I can recruit those outside the Law .
local: Kanɩn ɔkpa kʋlʋn sʋ nɩɩ n maa bʋ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man bʋ Mosesi nbara mʋ kasɛ mʋ, n waa mɛ n‐yɩɩ fɛɛ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man bʋ nbara mʋ kasɛ, kɛ n nyɛ‐nyɛ bamʋ kɛ ba be buu Krisito. Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ mɛɛ kaapʋ nɩn fɛɛ n mee buu Ɩbwaarɛ nbara mʋ, n buu Krisito nbara mʋ nɩn.

eng: I act weak to the weak, so I can recruit the weak. I have become all things to all people, so I could save some by all possible means.
local: Nɩɩ n maa bʋ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man pɩɩ bɩɩ abwaarɛsɩn mʋ tɔ gaa asɛ mʋ, n waa mɛ n‐yɩɩ nɩn fɛɛ ngbaa mɛ n man pɩɩ bɩɩ abwaarɛsɩn mʋ tɔ gaa, kɛ n nyɛ‐nyɛ bamʋ ba be buu Krisito. N taa mɛ n‐yɩɩ waa kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ sa asa kpini, kɛ n nyɛ‐nyɛ ɔkpa kʋmaa nɩmʋ sʋ yɛgɛ bamʋ akʋ ba nyɛ kamɔlɩgɛ.

eng: All the things I do are for the sake of the gospel, so I can be a partner with it.
local: Asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ sʋ nɩɩ n waa ɩnɩmʋ kpini, kɛ n nyɛ‐nyɛ kusee mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ tɔ mʋ.

eng: Dont you know that all the runners in the stadium run, but only one gets the prize? So run to win.
local: Fan man nyi fɛɛ asɩlɛpʋ kʋkyɔ bɛɛ waa kadandan abaa kɩsɩlɛ tɔ mʋ, bamʋ tɔ ɔkʋlʋn ii biti o gyii sʋ kɛ ɔ nyɛ kakɛɛ. Ɩmʋ sʋ fan payɩ ansi sɩlɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fan nyɛ gyii sʋ, kɛ fan nyɛ ɩmʋ kakɛɛ.

eng: Everyone who competes practices self-discipline in everything. The runners do this to get a crown of leaves that shrivel up and die, but we do it to receive a crown that never dies.
local: Ɔsa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ kadandan abaa ɔsɩlɛpʋ mʋ, ɛɛ payɩ mu ansi ɛɛ kʋʋla mʋ n‐yɩɩ danbɩrasa. Bamɛɛ bamʋ bɩrɛ, bɛɛ waa kanɩn bee biti kakɛɛ mʋ nɩɩ kii biti ki be nyita. Amaa anɛɛnɛ bɩrɛ, an payɩ ansi an sun an biti kakɛɛ mʋ nɩɩ ki mee nyita kpini‐kpini.

eng: So now this is how I run—not without a clear goal in sight. I fight like a boxer in the ring, not like someone who is shadowboxing.
local: Kanɩn sʋ n mɛɛ sɩlɛ fɛɛ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ sɩlɛ kɩyan, nɩɩ n mɛɛ kɔ kolu fɛɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ tʋʋ akɔtɔɔ afuu tɔ.

eng: Rather, Im landing punches on my own body and subduing it like a slave. I do this to be sure that I myself wont be disqualified after preaching to others.
local: N nyan mɛ n‐yɩɩ, nɩɩ n yɛgɛ nɩɩ kii nu mɛ asɛ, kɛ ɩ man naa ba fɛɛ, nɩɩ n kan tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa sa asa mʋ, kɛ mɛɛmɛ n pan kakɛɛ mʋ.

eng: Follow my example, just like I follow Christs.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan kasɛ mɛ tɔ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ n kasɛ Krisito tɔ mʋ.

eng: I praise you because you remember all my instructions, and you hold on to the traditions exactly as I handed them on to you.
local: N yɩn fanɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ fen nyiŋi mɛ sʋ lɩɩ kʋtɔ kʋmaa kɩnɩmʋ tɔ, nɩɩ fen buu kɩkaapʋ mʋ nɩɩ n taa waa fanɛ abaa tɔ mʋ.

eng: Now I want you to know that the head of every man is Christ, and the head of the woman is the man, and the head of Christ is God.
local: Amaa n biti fan bɩɩ fɛɛ ɔnyɩn kʋmaa kumu ɩ gyɛ Krisito, nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ‐kilipu kumu ɩ gyɛ mu‐kuli, nɩɩ Krisito kumu ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: Every man who prays or prophesies with his head covered shames his head.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔnyɩn kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ, abɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩɩ bɔla mʋ sʋ ɛɛ tɔwɛ, yɛgɛ o bun mu kumu sʋ mʋ, ee gyila mu kumu mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ Krisito mʋ ipeeli nɩmʋ.

eng: Every woman who prays or prophesies with her head uncovered disgraces her head. It is the same thing as having her head shaved.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ abɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩɩ bɔla mʋ sʋ ɛɛ tɔwɛ, nɩɩ ɔ man bun mu kumu sʋ mʋ, ee gyila mu kumu mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ mu‐kuli ipeeli nɩmʋ, ɩ dan i du nɩn fɛɛ maa kpaatɛ mu kumu mʋ.

eng: If a woman doesnt cover her head, then she should have her hair cut off. If it is disgraceful for a woman to have short hair or to be shaved, then she should keep her head covered.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man kan bun mu kumu sʋ bɩrɛ, ɩ dagaa maa kpaatɛ mu kumu. Nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ ipeeli‐sɩn sa ɔkyɩɩ maa kpaatɛ mu kumu, abɛɛ maa gyatɛ mu inyini sʋ mʋ, kanɩn bɩrɛ o bun mu kumu sʋ.

eng: A man shouldnt have his head covered, because he is the image and glory of God; but the woman is mans glory.
local: Ɔnyɩn bɩrɛ man dagaa maa bun mu kumu sʋ, saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ, abɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩɩ bɔla mʋ sʋ ɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔnyɩn mʋ tɔ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ keduli yɛ mʋ bʋnyaa ɩɩ lɛɛ ɩɩ kaapʋ. Amaa ɔkyɩɩ mʋ ɩɩ lɛɛ ɔnyɩn bʋnyaa ɩɩ kaapʋ.

eng: Man didnt have his origin from woman, but woman from man.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ man tʋʋ ɔnyɩn lɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ tɔ, amaa ɔ tʋʋ ɔkyɩɩ nɩn lɩɩ ɔnyɩn tɔ.

eng: and man wasnt created for the sake of the woman, but the woman for the sake of the man.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ man tʋʋ ɔnyɩn nɩn sa ɔkyɩɩ, amaa ɔ tʋʋ ɔkyɩɩ nɩn sa ɔnyɩn.

eng: Because of this a woman should have authority over her head, because of the angels.
local: Lɩɩ ɩnɩmʋ sʋ yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ sʋ mʋ, ɩ dagaa ɔkyɩɩ maa bun mu kumu sʋ, kɛ ɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ ɔ bʋ mu‐kuli kasɛ.

eng: However, woman isnt independent from man, and man isnt independent from woman in the Lord.
local: Amaa ɩnɩmʋ kpini, lɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ ɩkpa sʋ mʋ, ɔkyɩɩ tiri sa ɔnyɩn, nɩɩ ɔnyɩn gbaa tiri sa ɔkyɩɩ.

eng: As woman came from man so also man comes from woman. But everything comes from God.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkyɩɩ lɩɩ ɔnyɩn tɔ nɩn, nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mɔɔ ɩ kʋʋgɛ ɔnyɩn. Amaa kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ nɩn.

eng: Judge for yourselves: Is it appropriate for a woman to pray to God with her head uncovered?.
local: Fanɛ gbaa‐gbaa fanɛ fan kɩɩ, nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ maa kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ, yɛgɛ ɔ man bun mu kumu sʋ mʋ, ɩ bʋrɔn kanɩn abɛɛ?.

eng: Doesnt nature itself teach you that if a man has long hair, it is a disgrace to him.
local: Ɩ wʋla ɩ dɛ fɛɛ ɔnyɩn kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ mu inyini ɩ waa nswɩɩ ɩ kyɔlɛ mʋ, ɩ gyɛ ipeeli sa mʋ.

eng: but if a woman has long hair, it is her glory? This is because her long hair is given to her for a covering.
local: Amaa ɔkyɩɩ bɩrɛ, nɩɩ ɔ kan ɔ sʋ inyini bʋnswɩɩsa mʋ, ɩ gyɛ bʋnyaa nɩn sa mʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ sa ɔkyɩɩ inyini bʋnswɩɩsa nɩn, kɛ i bun mʋ sʋ.

eng: But if someone wants to argue about this, we dont have such a custom, nor do Gods churches.
local: Amaa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti o gyii ɩnɩmʋ ɩmɔɔrɛ mʋ, kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ n sʋ n tɔwɛ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, a man buu atɔ waasa kʋ Krisito tɔ tii ɩnɩmʋ sʋ. Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ kʋmaa nɩmʋ tɔ gbaa kanɩn nɩɩ i du.

eng: Now I dont praise you as I give the following instruction because when you meet together, it does more harm than good.
local: Nbɩɩnbɩɩ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ n biti n ba tɔwɛ fanɛ falɛ bɩrɛ n mɛɛ yɩn fanɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ fanɛ kɩgyaŋɛ kʋmaa nɩmʋ tɔ mʋ, ɩ mɛɛ bɩya adanbɩrasa, amɔɔ ii nyita nɩn ii tii sʋ.

eng: First of all, when you meet together as a church, I hear that there are divisions among you, and I partly believe it.
local: Ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ gyankpaa mʋ, n nu yɛ saŋa kʋmaa nɩɩ fanɛ kʋbʋ fɛn gyaŋɛ mʋ, nbarɩgɛ ɩ bʋ fanɛ tɔ, nɩɩ n kɔɔlɛ ɩmʋ ɩkʋ gyii.

eng: Its necessary that there are groups among you, to make it clear who is genuine.
local: Kesintin tɔ, ɩ dagaa nbarɩgɛ ɩ maa bʋ fanɛ tɔ, saamʋ kɛ ɩ yɛgɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ ɔ bɩɩ fanɛ tɔ bamʋ nɩɩ bee sun Ɩbwaarɛ kesintin tɔ.

eng: So when you get together in one place, it isnt to eat the Lords meal.
local: Nɩɩ fan maa gyaŋɛ mʋ, man gyɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ agyitɔ mʋ, nɩɩ fen gyii.

eng: Each of you goes ahead and eats a private meal. One person goes hungry while another is drunk.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ fan maa gyii mʋ, ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ ii gyii mʋ lɛɛ nɩn, nɩɩ fen mee gyoo abaa, kanɩn sʋ akʋn ɩɩ mɔɔ akʋ, yɛgɛ akʋ mɔɔ bɛɛ bʋʋ.

eng: Dont you have houses to eat and drink in? Or do you look down on Gods churches and humiliate those who have nothing? What can I say to you? Will I praise you? No, I dont praise you in this.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan man sʋ akpaara mʋ nɩɩ fen gyii kɛ fen nu ɩmʋ sʋ nɩn? Abɛɛ fen biti fan ŋaabaa Ɩbwaarɛ kʋbʋ mʋ nɩn, kɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man sʋ sɛhn mʋ ba gyii ipeeli? Mɩnɛ nɩɩ fen biti n tɔwɛ fanɛ? N yɩn fanɛ ɩnɩmʋ sʋ abɛɛ? N mɛɛ yɛ n yɩn fanɛ.

eng: I received a tradition from the Lord, which I also handed on to you: on the night on which he was betrayed, the Lord Jesus took bread.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ n nyɛ lɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu asɛ mʋ nɩɩ n taa kaapʋ fanɛ. Ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ, kagyanbwɛ, pɔyɩ kɛ ba lɛɛ anɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Yesu sa mʋ, ɔ taa bodobodo.

eng: After giving thanks, he broke it and said, This is my body, which is for you; do this to remember me.
local: nɩɩ ɔ fwaala Ɩbwaarɛ, nɩɩ ɔ gya bodobodo mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ mɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ, mʋ nɩɩ n taa sa fanɛ. Fan waa falɛ taa nyiŋi mɛ.

eng: He did the same thing with the cup, after they had eaten, saying, This cup is the new covenant in my blood. Every time you drink it, do this to remember me.
local: Ba maa gyii lʋwɛ mʋ, ɔkpa kʋlʋn maŋa sʋ ɔ taa kɔɔpʋ yɛ nta mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Kɔɔpʋ nɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ mɛ nkalan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ taa waa katɔwɛ kyula pʋpwɛ maa asa nsana. Fan waa falɛ, saŋa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fan nuu ɩmʋ mʋ, taa nyiŋi mʋ.

eng: Every time you eat this bread and drink this cup, you broadcast the death of the Lord until he comes.
local: Kanɩn sʋ saŋa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fen gyii bodobodo nɩmʋ, nɩɩ fen nuu lɩɩ kɔɔpʋ nɩmʋ tɔ mʋ, ɩ kaapʋ nɩn fɛɛ fɛn da Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu kelewu mʋ kibonbon naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti o bwii ba mʋ.

eng: This is why those who eat the bread or drink the cup of the Lord inappropriately will be guilty of the Lords body and blood.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ee gyii Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu bodobodo abɛɛ ee nuu lɩɩ kɔɔpʋ mʋ tɔ, ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ man dagaa sʋ mʋ, o gyii kʋpwɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ man buu Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ n‐yɩɩ yɛ mʋ nkalan mʋ.

eng: Each individual should test himself or herself, and eat from the bread and drink from the cup in that way.
local: Kanɩn sʋ, ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ dagaa maa kɩɩ mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mʋ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ, pɔyɩ kɛ ɔ lan gyii bodobodo mʋ, kɛ o nuu lɩɩ kɔɔpʋ mʋ tɔ.

eng: Those who eat and drink without correctly understanding the body are eating and drinking their own judgment.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ee gyii bodobodo mʋ, nɩɩ ee nuu lɩɩ kɔɔpʋ mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ bɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ n‐yɩɩ mʋ kasɛ mʋ, o gyii kʋpwɛ.

eng: Because of this, many of you are weak and sick, and quite a few have died.
local: Lɩɩ falɛ sʋ nɩɩ fanɛ tɔ gaalaagaa n‐yɩɩ ɩ yɔlagɛ fanɛ, nɩɩ fɛn lɔ, nɩɩ akʋ mɔɔ ba wu.

eng: But if we had judged ourselves, we wouldnt be judged.
local: Amaa nɩɩ an kan gyankpaa kɩɩ anɛ gbaa‐gbaa nkɔlɔ tɔ pɔyɩ mʋ, naafɔɔ Ɩbwaarɛ mɛɛ yɛ o bun anɛ kʋpwɛ.

eng: However, we are disciplined by the Lord when we are judged so that we wont be judged and condemned along with the whole world.
local: Amaa nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kan bun anɛ kʋpwɛ nɩɩ ɔ bɩɩtɛ anɛ kʋsʋ mʋ, ɛɛ waa kanɩn nɩn, kɛ ɔ man taa anɛ tii dulinyaa asa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti o muuri mʋ sʋ.

eng: For these reasons, my brothers and sisters, when you get together to eat, wait for each other.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana, nɩɩ fan kan gyaŋɛ kɛ fan gyii Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ agyitɔ mʋ, fan gyoo abaa.

eng: If some of you are hungry, they should eat at home so that getting together doesnt lead to judgment. I will give directions about the other things when I come.
local: Nɩɩ akʋn a kan a mɔɔ ɔkʋ mʋ, ɔ gyankpaa gyii mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ, saamʋ, nɩɩ fan gyaŋɛ mʋ ɩ mɛɛ yɛ ɩ bɩya Ɩbwaarɛ kʋpwɛ kubun. Nɩɩ lɩɩ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɩ san mʋ bɩrɛ, n kan ba mʋ, n biti n lɔŋɔ ɩmʋ sa fanɛ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I want you to be sure of the fact that our ancestors were all under the cloud and they all went through the sea.
local: Mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana, n biti fɛɛ fan nyiŋi kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ kyɩna waa anɛ naana ana mʋ. Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba kʋsʋ lɩɩ Egyipiti ba buu Mosesi bee kpee mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ kabwaarɛ‐wʋlɛ kun bamʋ kpini, nɩɩ ɔpʋ baŋɛ tɔ kɩnyɔ nɩɩ bamʋ kpini ba natɛ kɩmʋ tɔ bʋlɛ lɩɩ.

eng: All were baptized into Moses in the cloud and in the sea.
local: Lɩɩ kabwaarɛ‐wʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ ki kun bamʋ yɛ ɔpʋ mʋ tɔ kɩnatɛ faa mʋ sʋ mʋ, ba nyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee biliŋi Mosesi abuupu.

eng: All ate the same spiritual food.
local: Bamʋ kpini ba gyii agyitɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ mʋ.

eng: and all drank the same spiritual drink. They drank from a spiritual rock that followed them, and the rock was Christ.
local: Nɩɩ bamʋ kpini ba nuu nkyu mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ɩ lɩɩ kɩfʋlɛ tɔ mʋ. Ba nuu lɩɩ kɩfʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩmaa bamʋ ba naa mʋ, nɩɩ kanɩn kɩfʋlɛ maŋa ɩ gyɛ Krisito.

eng: However, God was unhappy with most of them, and they were struck down in the wilderness.
local: Amaa ɩmaa ɩmʋ kpini gbaa mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ ansi ɩ man gyii bamʋ gaalaagaa sʋ, nɩɩ ba wu‐wu yaasɛ ba dɛ ofuli mʋ sʋ.

eng: These things were examples for us, so we wont crave evil things like they did.
local: Nbɩɩnbɩɩ asɩn nɩmʋ kpini ɩ waa nɩn kaapʋ ɩ da anɛ amu tɔ, kɛ ɩ man yɛgɛ anɛ nfɛɛrɛ ɩ gyaga alibi kɩwaa sʋ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ adɩdaapʋ mʋ ba waa mʋ.

eng: Dont worship false gods like some of them did, as it is written, The people sat down to eat and drink and they got up to play .
local: Fan man sun ɩdakpa fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ bamʋ tɔ akʋ ba kyɩna waa mʋ. Ba kyʋrɔɔ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ lɩɩ bamʋ sʋ yɛ, Asa mʋ ba kyɩna nɩɩ ba gyii, nɩɩ ba nuu, nɩɩ ba kʋsʋ yɩlɛ kyaa.

eng: Lets not practice sexual immorality, like some of them did, and twenty-three thousand died in one day.
local: A man waa kakyɩɩ abɛɛ kanyɩn biti kɩgyagaa fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ bamʋ tɔ akʋ ba daa kyɩna waa mʋ, nɩɩ kakɛ kʋlʋn tɔ bamʋ asa ngbɩn adʋnyɔ yɛ asa ba wu mʋ.

eng: Lets not test Christ, like some of them did, and were killed by the snakes.
local: Fan man yɛgɛ a tʋʋ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔ kɩɩ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ bamʋ akʋ ba daa kyɩna waa mʋ, nɩɩ anansɛ ba be duŋi bamʋ mɔɔ mʋ.

eng: Lets not grumble, like some of them did, and were killed by the destroyer.
local: Fan man kɔlɩtɛ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ bamʋ akʋ ba kyɩna kɔlɩtɛ mʋ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sun mʋ kabɔɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ mɔɔ asa, nɩɩ ɔ ba mɔɔ bamʋ mʋ.

eng: These things happened to them as an example and were written as a warning for us to whom the end of time has come.
local: Asɩn nɩmʋ kpini i tu asa mʋ, nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ sɩnkaala sa asa. Ba kyʋrɔɔ ɩmʋ yɩla fɛɛ ɩ da anɛ amu tɔ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ an bʋ dulinyaa tɔ falɛ mʋ, ii kpee laalaalʋwɛ nɩmʋ.

eng: So those who think they are standing need to watch out or else they may fall.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o nyi yɛ ɔ yɩlɛ mʋ ayaa sʋ kenken mʋ, ɔ baa ɔ da sʋ kɛ ɔ man lɩɩ tɩyɛ.

eng: No temptation has seized you that isnt common for people. But God is faithful. He wont allow you to be tempted beyond your abilities. Instead, with the temptation, God will also supply a way out so that you will be able to endure it.
local: Kʋtʋʋ tɔ kɩɩ kʋ man ba fanɛ sʋ yɛgɛ kɩ man pɩɩta ba nyɩmɩsa sʋ. Ɩbwaarɛ gyɛ kesintin wuya, nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ yɛgɛ kʋtʋʋ tɔ kɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ fɛn mɛɛ yɛ fan taalɛ yɩlɛ tɔ mʋ kɩ ba fanɛ sʋ. Amaa nɩɩ kʋtʋʋ tɔ kɩɩ kan ba fanɛ sʋ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ kaapʋ fanɛ ɔkpa kɛ fan taa lɩɩ tɔ, kɛ fan taalɛ yɩlɛ tɔ.

eng: So then, my dear friends, run away from the worship of false gods!.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ mɛ bɩrɩsa ana danbɩrasa, fan lɛɛ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ lɩɩ ɩdakpa kusun tɔ.

eng: Im talking to you like you are sensible people. Think about what Im saying.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ asɛ fɛɛ anyaasɩnpʋ, ɩmʋ sʋ fanɛ gbaa‐gbaa fanɛ fan fɛ nfɛɛrɛ lɩɩ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ falɛ tɔ kɩɩ.

eng: Isnt the cup of blessing that we bless a sharing in the blood of Christ? Isnt the loaf of bread that we break a sharing in the body of Christ?.
local: Anɛ Krisito abuupu a maa gyii Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ agyitɔ mʋ, kʋʋpʋ yɛ nta mʋ nɩɩ an taa afwaala an sa Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ, an kan nuu kɔɔpʋ mʋ tɔ mʋ, ɩ kaapʋ nɩn fɛɛ anaa Krisito an bʋga waa kʋkʋlʋn lɩɩ nkalan mʋ sʋ. Nɩɩ an kpɛɛ bodobodo tɔ mʋ, ɩɩ waa nɩn fɛɛ anaa Krisito an bʋga waa kʋkʋlʋn mʋ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ tɔ nɩn.

eng: Since there is one loaf of bread, we who are many are one body, because we all share the one loaf of bread.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ bodobodo kʋlʋn ɩ bʋ tɔ, halɩɩ a maa kyɔ falɛ gbaa mʋ, an gyɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ kʋlʋn nɩn, lɩɩ fɛɛ anɛ kpini an gyii bodobodo kʋlʋn maŋa.

eng: Look at the people of Israel. Dont those who eat the sacrifices share from the altar?.
local: A taa Ɩsɩrayɩ abi asɩn kaala kɩɩ. Bamʋ nɩɩ ba taa kʋtɔbwaaya lɔŋɔ sa Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ba gyii ɩmʋ mʋ, ɩ kaapʋ nɩn fɛɛ ba maa Ɩbwaarɛ ba waa kʋkʋlʋn mʋ kɔsɔrɩga kaswɩɩ mʋ sʋ.

eng: What am I saying then? That food sacrificed to a false god is anything, or that a false god is anything?.
local: Nbɩɩnbɩɩ mɩnɛ nɩɩ n biti n tɔwɛ? Ɩ kaapʋ nɩn fɛɛ atɔbwaaya mʋ nɩɩ ba taa lɔŋɔ sa mʋ ɩ gyɛ kʋtɔ kʋ, abɛɛ ɩdakpa mʋ ɩ gyɛ kʋtɔkʋ?.

eng: No, but this kind of sacrifice is sacrificed to demons and not to God. I dont want you to be sharing in demons.
local: Ayee, amaa n biti n kaapʋ nɩn fɛɛ, ɩdakpa asunpu kɔlɔŋɔ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ lɔŋɔ mʋ, bɛɛ lɔŋɔ bɛɛ sa nbwɩɩ libi nɩn, man gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ. N mee biti fan naa nbwɩɩ libi fan maa bʋga.

eng: You cant drink the cup of the Lord and the cup of demons; you cant participate in the table of the Lord and the table of demons.
local: Fan mɛɛ taalɛ nuu Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu kɔɔpʋ mʋ tɔ, kɛ fan bɩla naa nuu nbwɩɩ libi kɔɔpʋ tɔ. Fan mɛɛ taalɛ gyii Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu agyitɔ, kɛ fan bɩla naa gyii nbwɩɩ libi agyitɔ.

eng: Or should we make the Lord jealous? We arent stronger than he is, are we?.
local: Nɩɩ an kan waa kanɩn mʋ, an biti Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ nyɛ agbʋ nɩn abɛɛ? An bʋlʋn an kyɔ Ɩbwaarɛ nɩn abɛɛ?.

eng: Everything is permitted, but everything isnt beneficial. Everything is permitted, but everything doesnt build others up.
local: Fanɛ tɔ ɔkʋ ɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, N sʋ ɔkpa fɛɛ n waa kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ. Ɩ gyɛ kesintin, amaa man gyɛ ɩmʋ kpini ɩ bʋrɔn sa mɛ. Fɛn tɔwɛ yɛ, N sʋ ɔkpa fɛɛ n waa kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ. Amaa man gyɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ ɩɩ kyɛ tɔ.

eng: No one should look out for their own advantage, but they should look out for each other.
local: Ɔkʋ ɔ man kɩɩ biti kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋrɔn sa mʋ wʋlɛ nkʋn, amaa ɔ kɩɩ biti kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋrɔn sa mʋ bɩrɩsa ana.
