eng: Eat everything that is sold in the marketplace, without asking questions about it because of your conscience.
local: Fɛn taalɛ gyii kunun kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ fɛ kunun ɔfɛkpa, amaa fan man taasɛ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ kɩ lɩɩ, kɛ i wuliti ɔkʋ nfɛɛrɛ tɔ.

eng: The earth and all that is in it belong to the Lord .
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ba kyʋrɔɔ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ yɛ, Kasɛ yɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ kɩmʋ sʋ mʋ kpini gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ nɩn.

eng: If an unbeliever invites you to eat with them and you want to go, eat whatever is served, without asking questions because of your conscience.
local: Nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Krisito abuupu mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ kan da kɩbaa tɩɩ fanɛ yɛ, fan ba kɛ fan bʋga gyii, nɩɩ fan waa nfɛɛrɛ yɛ fen kpee mʋ, fen gyii agyitɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ba taa be yii fanɛ ansi tɔ, kɛ fan man taasɛ sɛhn, kɛ i wuliti ɔkʋ nfɛɛrɛ tɔ.

eng: But if someone says to you, This meat was sacrificed in a temple, then dont eat it for the sake of the one who told you and for the sake of conscience.
local: Amaa nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, ba taa agyitɔ nɩmʋ sa ɔdakpa mʋ, lɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ asa nfɛɛrɛ sʋ mʋ, fan man gyii.

eng: Now when I say conscience I dont mean yours but the other persons. Why should my freedom be judged by someone elses conscience?.
local: Man gyɛ fanɛ nfɛɛrɛ sʋ nɩn n tɔwɛ kanɩn, amaa ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ fanɛ mʋ nfɛɛrɛ sʋ. Fanɛ ɔkʋ ii biti ɔ taasɛ yɛ, Nɩɩ n kan sʋ ɔkpa n gyii atɔ nɩmʋ mʋ, mɩnɛ ɩɩ waa nɩɩ ɔkʋ nfɛɛrɛ sʋ mʋ, n mɛɛ taalɛ gyii?.

eng: If I participate with gratitude, why should I be blamed for food I thank God for?.
local: Nɩɩ n kan taa afwaala sa Ɩbwaarɛ pɔyɩ nɩɩ n gyii agyitɔ mʋ, mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ ɔkʋ ɩ pwɩɩ mɛ yɛgɛ n taa afwaala sa Ɩbwaarɛ pɔyɩ nɩɩ n gyii.

eng: So, whether you eat or drink or whatever you do, you should do it all for Gods glory.
local: To! Kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fɛn waa nɩn‐oo, fen gyii nɩn‐oo, fen nuu nɩn‐oo, fan waa ɩmʋ kpini kanan mʋ nɩɩ ii biti ɩ taa bʋnyaa sa Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: Dont offend either Jews or Greeks, or Gods church.
local: Amaa fan man kan waa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kii biti kɩ yɛgɛ asa ba siti tɩyɛ bamʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii tɔ, ba gyɛ Gyiwu abi nɩn‐oo, bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi nɩn‐oo, halɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba gyɛ Krisito akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu gbaa.

eng: This is the same thing that I do. I please everyone in everything I do. I dont look out for my own advantage, but I look out for many people so that they can be saved.
local: Kanɩn nɩɩ ngbaa mɛ n waa. N waa kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ kii biti ki gyii ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ ansi ɔkpa kʋmaa nɩmʋ sʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ n mɛɛ waa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋrɔn sa mɛ wʋlɛ nkʋn, amaa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋrɔn sa asa gaalaagaa, kɛ ba nyɛ kamɔlɩgɛ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I dont want you to be ignorant about spiritual gifts.
local: Mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana, lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kayaayu tɔ nkɛɛ asɩn mʋ bɩrɛ, n mee biti fɛɛ fan kyɩna kitentenbiri tɔ.

eng: You know that when you were Gentiles you were often misled by false gods that cant even speak.
local: Sɩtaanɛ pɩna fanɛ, nɩɩ fan fɔyɩ, nɩɩ fan naa sun ɩdakpa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ mɛɛ tɔwɛ.

eng: So I want to make it clear to you that no one says, Jesus is cursed! when speaking by Gods Spirit, and no one can say, Jesus is Lord, except by the Holy Spirit.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ n biti fan bɩɩ fɛɛ, ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu bʋ mʋ tɔ mʋ, mɛɛ taalɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Nbusaa asɩn ɩ gyan Yesu sʋ. Nɩɩ ɔkʋ mɛɛ taalɛ lɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Yesu ɩ gyɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, amɔɔ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ bʋ mʋ tɔ.

eng: There are different spiritual gifts but the same Spirit.
local: Kayaayu tɔ nkɛɛ mʋ ɩ kwɩɩ ɩ kwɩɩ, amaa Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu kʋlʋn maŋa ɩɩ kɛɛ ɩmʋ kpini.

eng: and there are different ministries and the same Lord.
local: Kusun kʋkwɩɩ‐kʋkwɩɩ kɩ bʋ kabwaarɛ‐sun tɔ, amaa Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ kʋlʋn maŋa nɩɩ an sun.

eng: and there are different activities but the same God who produces all of them in everyone.
local: Nɩɩ Kusun sunsa gbaa bʋ tɔ kʋkwɩɩ‐kʋkwɩɩ, amaa Ɩbwaarɛ kʋlʋn maŋa ɩɩ sa ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ ɔlʋn, nɩɩ ɔ sʋ ee sun kusun kʋmaa nɩmʋ.

eng: A demonstration of the Spirit is given to each person for the common good.
local: Amaa Kayaayu mʋ lɛɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ kaapʋ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ sa ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ nkyɛtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ nyɛ kyɛ kʋbʋ mʋ tɔ.

eng: A word of wisdom is given by the Spirit to one person, a word of knowledge to another according to the same Spirit.
local: Kayaayu maŋa ɩɩ sa ɔkʋ kanyaasɩn katɔwɛ, nɩɩ ɛɛ sa ɔkʋ mɔɔ kinyi katɔwɛ.

eng: faith to still another by the same Spirit, gifts of healing to another in the one Spirit.
local: Nɩɩ Kayaayu kʋlʋn maŋa ɩɩ sa ɔkʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii lala, nɩɩ ɛɛ sa ɔkʋ mɔɔ ɔlʋn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa kyɛ alɔ.

eng: performance of miracles to another, prophecy to another, the ability to tell spirits apart to another, different kinds of tongues to another, and the interpretation of the tongues to another.
local: Kayaayu mʋ ɩɩ sa ɔkʋ ɔlʋn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa asɩn kyinkyinsa, nɩɩ ɔkʋ mɔɔ Ɩbwaarɛ bɔla mʋ sʋ ɛɛ tɔwɛ, nɩɩ ɔ sa ɔkʋ mɔɔ ɔ bɩɩ n‐yaayu danbɩrasa yɛ alibi. Nɩɩ ɔ sa ɔkʋ mɔɔ katɔwɛ mʋ nɩɩ o mee nu kasɛ, nɩɩ ɔ sa ɔkʋ katɔwɛ mʋ nɩɩ o mee nu mʋ kasɛ kɩlɛɛ.

eng: All these things are produced by the one and same Spirit who gives what he wants to each person.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ kpini ɩ gyɛ Kayaayu kʋlʋn maŋa kusun sunsa, nɩɩ ɔ sʋ ɩmʋ ɛɛ sa ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ee biti.

eng: Christ is just like the human body—a body is a unit and has many parts; and all the parts of the body are one body, even though there are many.
local: Nyɩmɩsa kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ sʋ abaafʋn‐abaafʋn kʋkyɔ, halɩɩ ɩ maa kwɩɩ ɩ kwɩɩ gbaa mʋ, ɩ san ɩ gyɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ kʋlʋn. Kanɩn nɩɩ Krisito kʋbʋ gbaa ki du nɩn.

eng: We were all baptized by one Spirit into one body, whether Jew or Greek, or slave or free, and we all were given one Spirit to drink.
local: Kayaayu kʋlʋn maŋa ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ba gyee anɛ kpini Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee, ba waa Krisito kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ kʋlʋn. Halɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba gyɛ Gyiwu abi, yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi, yɛ anyɛ, yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ anyɛ gbaa mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ sa Kayaayu kʋlʋn maŋa nɩɩ kɩɩ waa kusun anɛ nkɔlɔ tɔ.

eng: Certainly the body isnt one part but many.
local: Nyɩmɩsa kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ sʋ abaafʋn kʋkwɩɩ‐kʋkwɩɩ kʋkyɔ, man gyɛ kɩbaafʋn kʋlʋn.

eng: If the foot says, Im not part of the body because Im not a hand, does that mean its not part of the body?.
local: Nɩɩ kɩyaa kan tɔwɛ yɛ, N man gyɛ kɩbaa, ɩmʋ sʋ n man tii kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ mʋ sʋ mʋ, halɩɩ nɩɩ kɩ kan tɔwɛ kanɩn gbaa mʋ, kɩ san ki tii n‐yɩɩ mʋ sʋ.

eng: If the ear says, Im not part of the body because Im not an eye, does that mean its not part of the body?.
local: Nɩɩ kʋsʋ kan tɔwɛ yɛ, N man gyɛ kinsi, ɩmʋ sʋ n man tii kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ mʋ sʋ mʋ, halɩɩ nɩɩ kɩ kan tɔwɛ kanɩn gbaa mʋ, kɩ san ki tii n‐yɩɩ mʋ sʋ.

eng: If the whole body were an eye, what would happen to the hearing? And if the whole body were an ear, what would happen to the sense of smell?.
local: Nɩɩ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ kpini kɩ daa kɩ gyɛ kinsi nkʋn nɩn, nɛnɛ nɩɩ kii biti kɩ waa nu asɩn? Nɩɩ kɩ daa kɩ gyɛ kʋsʋ nkʋn nɩn, nɛnɛ nɩɩ kii biti kɩ waa nu ɔsɔn?.

eng: But as it is, God has placed each one of the parts in the body just like he wanted.
local: Amaa Ɩbwaarɛ taa nyɩmɩsa abaafʋn mʋ kʋkʋmaa nɩmʋ yii kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ mʋ sʋ opula mʋ nɩɩ ee biti.

eng: If all were one and the same body part, what would happen to the body?.
local: Nɩɩ abaafʋn mʋ kpini ɩ daa ɩ gyɛ kɩbaafʋn kʋlʋn mʋ, naafɔɔ nɛnɛ nɩɩ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ mʋ ii biti ɔ baa o du?.

eng: But as it is, there are many parts but one body.
local: Amaa kanan mʋ nɩɩ i du mʋ, abaafʋn‐abaafʋn mʋ ɩ kyɔ, amaa ɩ gyɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ kʋlʋn nɩn.

eng: So the eye cant say to the hand, I dont need you, or in turn, the head cant say to the feet, I dont need you.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ kinsi kɩ mɛɛ taalɛ tɔwɛ kɩbaa yɛ, Fʋ nkyɛtɔ man tiri mɛ. Nɩɩ kumu kɩ mɛɛ taalɛ tɔwɛ ayaa yɛ, Fanɛ nkyɛtɔ man tiri mɛ.

eng: Instead, the parts of the body that people think are the weakest are the most necessary.
local: Nɩɩ fan kɩɩ mʋ, nyɩmɩsa kɩbaafʋn mʋ nɩɩ fan kɩɩ yɛ kɩ man bʋlʋn mʋ, kɩmʋ asɩn i tiri gaa.

eng: If I speak in tongues of human beings and of angels but I dont have love, Im a clanging gong or a clashing cymbal.
local: Nɩɩ n kan n taalɛ n tɔwɛ asa abɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ ndɛ tɔwɛsa, nɩɩ n man sʋ kebiti sa mɛ bɩrɩsa mʋ, i du nɩn fɛɛ agongon mʋ nɩɩ kɩ sʋ ɔlanba mʋ, abɛɛ kidulonbi mʋ nɩɩ kɩ sʋ ɩlawʋ mʋ.

eng: The parts of the body that we think are less honorable are the ones we honor the most. The private parts of our body that arent presentable are the ones that are given the most dignity.
local: Kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ abaafʋn‐abaafʋn mʋ nɩɩ ɩ man sʋ bɛɛrɛɛ gaa mʋ, ɩmʋ maŋa nɩɩ an munkura. Nɩɩ ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ man bʋrɔn ansi sʋ mʋ, ɩmʋ nɩɩ an kɩɩ sʋ danbɩrasa.

eng: If I have the gift of prophecy and I know all the mysteries and everything else, and if I have such complete faith that I can move mountains but I dont have love, Im nothing.
local: Nɩɩ n kan n sʋ kakɛɛ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩɩ bɔla mɛ sʋ ɛɛ tɔwɛ, nɩɩ n nyi Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn baalasa kpini, nɩɩ n sʋ kinyi, nɩɩ n sʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii mʋ nɩɩ n taalɛ kʋsaa abɩɩ gbaa, nɩɩ n man sʋ kebiti bɩrɛ, n man gyɛ sɛhn.

eng: The parts of our body that are presentable dont need this. But God has put the body together, giving greater honor to the part with less honor.
local: Amaa anɛ abaafʋn mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋrɔn ansi sʋ mʋ, ɩ man tiri fɛɛ a kɩɩ ɩmʋ sʋ yɛ ɔdan. Ɩbwaarɛ taa kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ abaafʋn mʋ nɩn kyaga abaa sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taa bʋnyaa sa ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ man sʋ bʋnyaa ɩmʋ gbaa‐gbaa ɩmʋ kɩbaa sʋ mʋ.

eng: If I give away everything that I have and hand over my own body to feel good about what Ive done but I dont have love, I receive no benefit whatsoever.
local: Nɩɩ n kan taa mɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ n sʋ kɛɛ atiripu, nɩɩ n kan taa mɛ n‐yɩɩ sa yɛ ba tuwa gbaa, nɩɩ n man sʋ kebiti mʋ, n mɛɛ yɛ n nyɛ tɔnɔ kʋ.

eng: so that there wont be division in the body and so the parts might have mutual concern for each other.
local: kɛ nbarɩgɛ tɔ kʋ ɩ man ba kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ mʋ tɔ, amaa ɩmʋ kpini ɩɩ kɩɩ abaa sʋ kyɛɛkyɛɛ.

eng: Love is patient, love is kind, it isnt jealous, it doesnt brag, it isnt arrogant.
local: Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ kebiti mʋ, ɔ sʋ kenyiita, nɩɩ ɛɛ waa kɩdanbɩrasa, nɩɩ ɔ man sʋ kayɩɩ‐basa, nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ waa alala‐sɩn, nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ kaapʋ mʋ n‐yɩɩ.

eng: If one part suffers, all the parts suffer with it; if one part gets the glory, all the parts celebrate with it.
local: Nɩɩ kɩbaafʋn kʋlʋn ii gyii awʋrʋfɔ mʋ abaafʋn mʋ kpini ii gyii awʋrʋfɔ, nɩɩ kɩbaafʋn kʋlʋn kan nyɛ bʋnyaa mʋ, abaafʋn mʋ kpini ii gyii ɔkɔn.

eng: it isnt rude, it doesnt seek its own advantage, it isnt irritable, it doesnt keep a record of complaints.
local: Ɔ mɛɛ waa kɩmʋ nɩɩ kɩ man dagaa, nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ kɩɩ ee biti mʋ nkʋn lɛɛ, nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ nyɛ agbʋ bilen‐bilen, nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ taa ɔkʋ asɩn ɛɛ waa mʋ ɔtɔ tɔ.

eng: You are the body of Christ and parts of each other.
local: Nbɩɩnbɩɩ fanɛ kpini ɩ bʋga waa kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ Krisito lɛɛ mʋ. Nɩɩ fanɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ gyɛ kɩmʋ kɩbaafʋn kʋ nɩn.

eng: it isnt happy with injustice, but it is happy with the truth.
local: Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ kebiti mʋ, alibi kɩwaa ɩ mɛɛ waa mʋ ɔkɔn, amaa mu ansi i gyii kesintin sʋ.

eng: In the church, God has appointed first apostles, second prophets, third teachers, then miracles, then gifts of healing, the ability to help others, leadership skills, different kinds of tongues.
local: Nɩɩ lɩɩ Krisito kʋbʋ mʋ tɔ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ akʋ, nɩɩ ɔ sa bamʋ asunsunsa. Kɩgyankpaasa mʋ ɩ gyɛ Krisito asunpu, nɩɩ kɩnyɔsapʋ mʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ, nɩɩ kɩsasapʋ mʋ ɩ gyɛ abwaarɛsɩn akaapʋpʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ bɩla lɛɛ adangana awaapʋ, yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ kyɛ alɔ, yɛ asa akyɛtɔpʋ, yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ kɩɩ Krisito kʋbʋ mʋ asɩn tɔ, yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɔwɛ ntɔwɛ mʋ nɩɩ be mee nu ɩmʋ kasɛ.

eng: Love puts up with all things, trusts in all things, hopes for all things, endures all things.
local: Saŋa kʋmaa ɛɛ yɩlɛ tɔ naa fʋʋ ɔkaa, nɩɩ ɔ sʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii, yɛ kenyiita, yɛ tamaa kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ tɔ.

eng: All arent apostles, are they? All arent prophets, are they? All arent teachers, are they? All dont perform miracles, do they?.
local: Bamʋ kpini ba gyɛ Krisito asunpu nɩn abɛɛ? Bamʋ kpini ba gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ nɩn abɛɛ? Bamʋ kpini ba gyɛ abwaarɛsɩn akaapʋpʋ nɩn abɛɛ? Bamʋ kpini ba gyɛ adangana awaapʋ nɩn abɛɛ?.

eng: Love never fails. As for prophecies, they will be brought to an end. As for tongues, they will stop. As for knowledge, it will be brought to an end.
local: Kebiti kɩ man sʋ ɔkaa. Amaa Ɩbwaarɛ kɩbɔla sʋ ɛɛ tɔwɛ, yɛ katɔwɛ mʋ nɩɩ be mee nu kasɛ, yɛ kinyi, ɩmʋ kpini ii biti ɩ ba fʋʋ ɔkaa.

eng: All dont have gifts of healing, do they? All dont speak in different tongues, do they? All dont interpret, do they?.
local: Bamʋ kpini ba sʋ kakɛɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ kyɛ alɔ nɩn abɛɛ? Bamʋ kpini bɛɛ tɔwɛ ntɔwɛ mʋ nɩɩ be mee nu ɩmʋ kasɛ abɛɛ? Bamʋ kpini ba sʋ kakɛɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ lɛɛ katɔwɛ mʋ nɩɩ be mee nu kasɛ mʋ nɩn abɛɛ? Ayee!.

eng: We know in part and we prophesy in part.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛgɛ nɩɩ an nyi, yɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ bɔla anɛ sʋ ɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ gbɛrɛɛ nɩn.

eng: Use your ambition to try to get the greater gifts. And Im going to show you an even better way.
local: Amaa fan taa fanɛ nkɔlɔ kpini biti Kayaayu nkɛɛ mʋ nɩɩ i tiri gaa mʋ. Nɩɩ nbɩɩnbɩɩ n biti n kaapʋ fanɛ ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ ki tiri kɩ kyɔ ɩnɩmʋ kpini.

eng: but when the perfect comes, what is partial will be brought to an end.
local: Amaa nɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ki du kyɛɛkyɛɛ lʋwɛ kan ba mʋ, kɩmʋ nɩɩ kɩ man du kyɛɛkyɛɛ mʋ ii biti ɩ kyʋn.

eng: When I was a child, I used to speak like a child, reason like a child, think like a child. But now that I have become a man, Ive put an end to childish things.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ n gyɛ kebii mʋ, n tɔwɛ fɛɛ kebii, nɩɩ n fɛ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ kebii, nɩɩ n gyii ɩmɔɔrɛ fɛɛ kebii. Amaa n maa ba dan mʋ n yɛgɛ abii‐sɩn mʋ.

eng: Now we see a reflection in a mirror; then we will see face-to-face. Now I know partially, but then I will know completely in the same way that I have been completely known.
local: Nbɩɩnbɩɩ bɩrɛ, an wu atɔ nɩn fuu anɛ ansi sʋ, fɛɛ a maa kɩɩ digi mʋ nɩɩ kɩ man kɩɩ tɔ mʋ tɔ, amaa saŋa kʋ an biti a wu kʋtɔ kʋmaa anɛ ansi sʋ danbɩrasa. Nbɩɩnbɩɩ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ n nyi mʋ ɩ gyɛ gbɛrɛɛ, amaa saŋa kʋ n biti n bɩɩ kʋtɔ kʋmaa tɔ lʋwɛ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ nyi mɛ mʋ.

eng: Now faith, hope, and love remain—these three things—and the greatest of these is love.
local: Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ i du mʋ, atɔ asá anɩmʋ ɩ bʋ tɔ. Ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii, yɛ tamaa yɛ kebiti. Amaa kebiti ɩ kyɔ ɩmʋ kpini.

eng: Pursue love, and use your ambition to try to get spiritual gifts but especially so that you might prophesy.
local: Fan payɩ ansi buu kebiti ɩkpa, kɛ fan taa fanɛ nkɔlɔ kpini biti Kayaayu tɔ nkɛɛ mʋ, fɔnfɔn Ɩbwaarɛ kɩbɔla nyɩmɩsa sʋ ɛɛ tɔwɛ kakɛɛ mʋ.

eng: This is because those who speak in a tongue dont speak to people but to God; no one understands it—they speak mysteries by the Spirit.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ ɩɩ tɔwɛ katɔwɛ mʋ nɩɩ asa be mee nu ɩmʋ kasɛ mʋ, ɔ mɛɛ tɔwɛ ɛɛ sa asa, amaa ɛɛ tɔwɛ ɛɛ sa Ɩbwaarɛ nɩn, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋ mee nu kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ kasɛ. Lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu mʋ ɔlʋn tɔ mʋ, ɛɛ tɔwɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn baalasa.

eng: Those who prophesy speak to people, building them up, and giving them encouragement and comfort.
local: Amaa ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩɩ bɔla mʋ sʋ ɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ, ɛɛ tɔwɛ ɛɛ waa asa ɔlʋn tɔ, yɛ aniya tɔ, nɩɩ ee yuula bamʋ nkɔlɔ.

eng: People who speak in a tongue build up themselves; those who prophesy build up the church.
local: Ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ tɔwɛ katɔwɛ mʋ nɩɩ asa be mee nu kasɛ mʋ, ɩɩ kyɛ mʋ wʋlɛ nkʋn tɔ nɩn, amaa ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩɩ bɔla mʋ sʋ ɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ, ɩɩ kyɛ Krisito kʋbʋ mʋ kpini tɔ nɩn.

eng: I wish that all of you spoke in tongues, but Id rather you could prophesy. Those who prophesy are more important than those who speak in tongues, unless they are able to interpret them so that the church might be built up.
local: Naafɔɔ n biti fɛɛ fanɛ kpini fan tɔwɛ ntɔwɛ mʋ nɩɩ fen mee nu ɩmʋ kasɛ mʋ, amaa kɩmʋ nɩɩ n biti ɩ gyɛ yɛ fan lɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn tɔwɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩɩ bɔla mʋ sʋ ɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ, kyɔ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ tɔwɛ katɔwɛ mʋ nɩɩ asa be mee nu ɩmʋ kasɛ mʋ, amɔɔ ɔkʋ bʋ tɔ nɩɩ ɛɛ taalɛ lɛɛ ɩmʋ kasɛ, kɛ ɩ nyɛ kyɛ Krisito kʋbʋ mʋ tɔ.

eng: After all, brothers and sisters, if I come to you speaking in tongues, how will I help you unless I speak to you with a revelation, some knowledge, a prophecy, or a teaching?.
local: Mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana, nɩɩ n kan ba fanɛ asɛ n tɔwɛ katɔwɛ mʋ nɩɩ fen mee nu ɩmʋ kasɛ mʋ, mɩnɛ tɔnɔ nɩɩ fen biti fan nyɛ lɩɩ ɩmʋ sʋ? Amaa nɩɩ n bɩya fanɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn kɩlɛɛkaapʋ, abɛɛ kanyaasɩn, abɛɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bɔla mɛ sʋ tɔwɛ, abɛɛ asɩn kaapʋsa mʋ, ɩnɩmʋ ii biti ɩ kyɛ fanɛ tɔ.

eng: Likewise, things that arent alive like a harp or a lyre can make a sound, but if there arent different notes in the sounds they make, how will the tune from the harp or the lyre be recognized?.
local: Fan yɛgɛ a taa atɔ lansa fɛɛ nleebi yɛ adʋnlɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ man sʋ nkpa waa sɩnkaala. Nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan ɛɛ fʋlɛ keleebi abɛɛ ɛɛ lan kɩdʋnlɛ, nɩɩ ii mee su kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa mʋ, nɛnɛ nɩɩ bee biti ba waa bɩɩ ɩmʋ asubi mʋ ɔkɔn?.

eng: And if a trumpet call is unrecognizable, then who will prepare for battle?.
local: Nɩɩ kɩnaa kabɩlɛ ɔfʋlɛpʋ mʋ man kan fʋlɛ kabɩlɛ tɩɩ kyɛɛkyɛɛ mʋ, nɛnɛ nɩɩ bee biti ba waa bɩɩ fɛɛ ba kʋʋla kpee kɩnaa?.

eng: Its the same way with you: If you dont use language that is easy to understand when you speak in a tongue, then how will anyone understand what is said?.
local: Kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ ɩ dɛ sa fanɛ. Nɩɩ fan kan fɛn tɔwɛ katɔwɛ mʋ nɩɩ asa be mee nu ɩmʋ kasɛ mʋ, nɛnɛ nɩɩ ɔkʋ ii biti ɔ waa bɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ fɛn tɔwɛ? I du nɩn fɛɛ fɛn maa tɔwɛ fɛn sa afuu mʋ.

eng: It will be as if you are speaking into the air! There are probably many language families in the world, and none of them are without meaning.
local: Ntɔwɛ kʋkwɩɩ‐kʋkwɩɩ gaalaagaa ɩ bʋ dulinyaa tɔ, amaa ɩmʋ kpini ɩ sʋ ɩmʋ kasɛ.

eng: So if I dont know the meaning of the language, then I will be like a foreigner to those who speak it, and they will be like foreigners to me.
local: Nɩɩ n mɛɛ kan n nu katɔwɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔkʋ ɩɩ tɔwɛ mɛ asɛ mʋ, ɔ gyɛ ɔfɔ kyilim mɛ asɛ, nɩɩ ngbaa mɛ n gyɛ ɔfɔ kyilim mʋ asɛ.

eng: The same holds true for you: since you are ambitious for spiritual gifts, use your ambition to try to work toward being the best at building up the church.
local: Kanɩn nɩɩ fanɛ gbaa fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fɛn taa fanɛ nkɔlɔ kpini biti Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu tɔ nkɛɛ mʋ, fan kpan fanɛ n‐yɩɩ biti nkɛɛ mʋ nɩɩ ii biti ɩ yɛgɛ Krisito kʋbʋ mʋ kɩ nyɛ ɔlʋn dan tii sʋ mʋ.

eng: Therefore, those who speak in a tongue should pray to be able to interpret.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ tɔwɛ katɔwɛ mʋ nɩɩ be mee nu ɩmʋ kasɛ mʋ, ɩ dagaa maa kʋlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kɛ ɔ sa mʋ kakɛɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ taalɛ lɛɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ kasɛ.

eng: If I pray in a tongue, my spirit prays but my mind isnt productive.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ nɩɩ n kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ kʋlɛ katɔwɛ mʋ nɩɩ be mee nu ɩmʋ kasɛ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ mɛ tɔ mʋ ɩɩ kʋlɛ, amaa mɛ nfɛɛrɛ bɩrɛ ɩ man tii sʋ.

eng: What should I do? Ill pray in the Spirit, but Ill pray with my mind too; Ill sing a psalm in the Spirit, but Ill sing the psalm with my mind too.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ mɩnɛ nɩɩ n biti n waa? N biti n taa Kayaayu mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ mɛ tɔ mʋ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ, nɩɩ n biti n taa mɛ nfɛɛrɛ gbaa kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ. N biti n taa Kayaayu mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ mɛ tɔ mʋ bɩɩ ɔlʋn, kɛ n taa mɛ nfɛɛrɛ gbaa bɩɩ ɔlʋn.

eng: After all, if you praise God in the Spirit, how will the people who arent trained in that language say Amen! to your thanksgiving, when they dont know what you are saying?.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan taa Kayaayu mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ mʋ tɔ ɛɛ fwaala Ɩbwaarɛ katɔwɛ mʋ nɩɩ be mee nu ɩmʋ kasɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ, nɛnɛ nɩɩ asa pʋpwɛ mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ bamʋ tɔ mʋ bɛɛ waa kyula sʋ yɛ, Ɩ waa kanɩn, yɛgɛ be mee nu asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ kasɛ?.

eng: You may offer a beautiful prayer of thanksgiving, but the other person is not being built up.
local: Halɩɩ nɩɩ fʋ afwaala mʋ nɩɩ fʋ sʋ fɩɩ sa Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ ɩ baa ɩ bʋrɔn nɛnɛ, ɩ mɛɛ yɛ ɩ kyɛ fʋ bɩrɩsa ana mʋ tɔ.

eng: I thank God that I speak in tongues more than all of you.
local: N fwaala Ɩbwaarɛ fɛɛ n maa tɔwɛ ntɔwɛ mʋ nɩɩ asa be mee nu ɩmʋ kasɛ mʋ n kyɔ fanɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ.

eng: But in the church Id rather speak five words in my right mind than speak thousands of words in a tongue so that I can teach others.
local: Amaa nɩɩ n biti n tɔwɛ Krisito kʋbʋ tɔ mʋ, n tɔwɛ ntɔwɛ mʋ nɩɩ asa bee nu kasɛ n sʋ n kaapʋ bamʋ, ɩ kyɔ fɛɛ n maa tɔwɛ ntɔwɛ ngbɩn‐du mʋ nɩɩ asa be mee nu ɩmʋ kasɛ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, dont be like children in the way you think. Well, be babies when it comes to evil, but be adults in your thinking.
local: Mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana, fan yɛgɛ nfɛɛrɛ kɩ fɛ fɛɛ nbii. Lɩɩ alibi ɩkpa kʋkwɩɩ‐kʋkwɩɩ sʋ mʋ, fan baa fan du fɛɛ nbii, amaa fanɛ nfɛɛrɛ tɔ mʋ, fan kaapʋ fɛɛ fan dan.

eng: In the Law it is written: I will speak to this people with foreign languages and foreigners lips, but they will not even listen to me this way , says the Lord.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ mʋ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, N biti n bɔla asa mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ ntɔwɛ kʋkwɩɩ‐kʋkwɩɩ mʋ sʋ, yɛ afɔ kyilim ntɔwɛ sʋ n tɔwɛ sa mɛ asa. Halɩɩ ɩmʋ kpini gbaa mʋ, ba mɛɛ yɛ be nu mɛ asɛ.

eng: So then, tongues are a sign for those who dont believe, not for those who believe. But prophecy is a sign for believers, not for those who dont believe.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ katɔwɛ mʋ nɩɩ asa be mee nu ɩmʋ kasɛ mʋ, ɩ man gyɛ sɩnkaala sa akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu, amaa asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bɔla nyɩmɩsa sʋ tɔwɛ mʋ, gyɛ sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba gyɛ akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu, man gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man kɔɔlɛ gyii.

eng: So suppose that the whole church is meeting and everyone is speaking in tongues. If people come in who are outsiders or unbelievers, wont they say that you are out of your minds?.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ fanɛ Krisito kʋbʋ mʋ kpini kan gyaŋɛ, nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ ɩɩ tɔwɛ katɔwɛ mʋ nɩɩ asa be mee nu kasɛ, nɩɩ asa pʋpwɛ yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man kɔɔlɛ gyii kan ba bʋ tʋtɔ mʋ, ba mɛɛ yɛ ba tɔwɛ yɛ fanɛ kpini fɛn lɔ ɩbʋn nɩn?.

eng: But if everyone is prophesying when an unbeliever or outsider comes in, they are tested by all and called to account by all.
local: Amaa nɩɩ ɩ kan ba fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩɩ bɔla fanɛ kpini sʋ ɛɛ tɔwɛ asɩn, nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man kɔɔlɛ gyii yɛ asa pʋpwɛ ba ba fanɛ ngyaŋɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ, ii biti ɩ yɛgɛ ba wu bamʋ alibi mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa. Kɛ fanɛ asɩn tɔwɛsa mʋ ii biti i bun bamʋ kʋpwɛ.

eng: The secrets of their hearts are brought to light. When that happens, they will fall on their faces and worship God, proclaiming out loud that truly God is among you!.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ ii biti ɩ yɛgɛ bamʋ nkɔlɔ tɔ asiri asɩn libi mʋ ɩ lɩɩ ifuli, kɛ ba muŋa sun Ɩbwaarɛ, bugi bamʋ nnɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ bʋ fanɛ asɛ kesintin.

eng: What is the outcome of this, brothers and sisters? When you meet together, each one has a psalm, a teaching, a revelation, a tongue, or an interpretation. All these things must be done to build up the church.
local: Mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana, mɩnɛ nɩɩ n biti n kaapʋ? Kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ n kaapʋ mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, nɩɩ fan kan gyaŋɛ fen sun Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ, fan waa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kii biti kɩ kyɛ fanɛ kpini tɔ. Akʋtɔ fanɛ tɔ ɔkʋ sʋ kalʋnbɩɩ, ɔkʋ mɔɔ sʋ kɩkaapʋ, ɔkʋ mɔɔ sʋ kɩlɛɛkaapʋ, nɩɩ ɔkʋ sʋ katɔwɛ mʋ nɩɩ asa be mee nu ɩmʋ kasɛ, nɩɩ ɔkʋ mɔɔ sʋ katɔwɛ mʋ kasɛ kɩlɛɛ. Ɩ dagaa fan waa nɩmʋ kpini kɛ ɩ nyɛ kyɛ Krisito kʋbʋ mʋ tɔ.

eng: If some speak in a tongue, then let two or at most three speak, one at a time, and someone must interpret.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan ɛɛ tɔwɛ katɔwɛ mʋ nɩɩ asa be mee nu ɩmʋ kasɛ mʋ, ɩ dagaa ba maa gyɛ asa nyɔ abɛɛ asa asá, kɛ ba tɔwɛ kʋkʋlʋn‐kʋkʋlʋn, nɩɩ i tiri fɛɛ ɔkʋ maa lɛɛ asɩn mʋ kasɛ.

eng: However, if there is no interpreter, then they should keep quiet in the meeting. They should speak privately to themselves and to God.
local: Amaa nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan ɔ man bʋ tɔ ɔ lɛɛ ɩmʋ kasɛ bɩrɛ, ɔtɔwɛpʋ mʋ, ɔ laatɔ ngyaŋɛ mʋ tɔ, kɛ ɔ tɔwɛ sa mʋ n‐yɩɩ yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: In the case of prophets, let two or three speak and have the rest evaluate what is said.
local: Nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bɔla bamʋ sʋ ɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ, ɩ dagaa ba maa gyɛ asa anyɔ abɛɛ asa asá, kɛ asansa mʋ ba nu kɛ ba kɩɩ ɩmʋ tɔ yɛ ɔdan.

eng: And if some revelation comes to someone else who is sitting down, the first one should be quiet.
local: Amaa nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan ɔ tɛ ngyaŋɛ mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kɩlɛɛkaapʋ mʋ, ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ yɩlɛ ɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ, ɔ laatɔ pɔyɩ.

eng: You can all prophesy one at a time so that everyone can learn and be encouraged.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ fanɛ kpini fɛn taalɛ tɔwɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩɩ bɔla fanɛ sʋ kɛ fan tɔwɛ mʋ, kɛ ɩ yɛgɛ fanɛ kpini fan bɩɩla kɛ ɩ kyɛ fanɛ tɔ.

eng: The spirits of prophets are under the control of the prophets.
local: Ɔsa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bɔla mʋ sʋ ɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ, ɛɛ taalɛ kɩtaa mʋ n‐yɩɩ lɩɩ mʋ katɔwɛ mʋ sʋ.

eng: God isnt a God of disorder but of peace. Like in all the churches of Gods people.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ mee biti atɔ i suguti tɔ, amaa ee biti kakyɩna tɔ ɩ baa ɩ sʋ kpee kyɛɛkyɛɛ kayɩɩ‐yuuli tɔ. Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ i du Krisito kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ mʋ tɔ mʋ.

eng: the women should be quiet during the meeting. They are not allowed to talk. Instead, they need to get under control, just as the Law says.
local: akyɩɩ ba bun bamʋ nnɔ sʋ kʋbʋ kʋmaa tɔ. Ɩ man dɛ ɔkpa fɛɛ ba tɔwɛ. Amaa ba bɩya bamʋ n‐yɩɩ kasɛ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ nbara mʋ ɩɩ kaapʋ mʋ.

eng: If they want to learn something, they should ask their husbands at home. It is disgraceful for a woman to talk during the meeting.
local: Nɩɩ ba kan ba sʋ asɩn taasɛsa ɩkʋ mʋ, ba tasɛ bamʋ‐kuli ana bamʋ akpaara sʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɩ gyɛ ipeeli‐sɩn fɛɛ ɔkyɩɩ maa tɔwɛ Krisito kʋbʋ tɔ.

eng: Did the word of God originate with you? Has it come only to you?.
local: Fanɛ Korinto abi, fen nyi fɛɛ fanɛ asɛ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn mʋ i piili abɛɛ? Abɛɛ fan nyi yɛ fanɛ wʋlɛ nkʋn asɛ nɩɩ ɩ ba?.

eng: If anyone thinks that they are prophets or spiritual people, then let them recognize that what Im writing to you is the Lords command.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan fɛ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ ɔ gyɛ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩɩ bɔla mʋ sʋ ɛɛ tɔwɛ, abɛɛ ɔ sʋ Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu tɔ kakɛɛ mʋ, ɩ dagaa maa taalɛ bɩɩ fɛɛ, ɩmʋ nɩɩ n kyʋrɔɔ n sa fanɛ falɛ gyɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ kɔnɔsa.

eng: If someone doesnt recognize this, they arent recognized.
local: Amaa nɩɩ ɔsa maŋa kan kina yɛ o mee nu falɛ asɩn mʋ, fanɛ gbaa fan kina mʋ.

eng: So then, brothers and sisters, use your ambition to try to get the gift of prophecy, but dont prevent speaking in tongues.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana, fan taa fanɛ nkɔlɔ kpini biti kakɛɛ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩɩ bɔla fanɛ sʋ ɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ. Amaa fan man tii ɔkpa yɛ ɔkʋ ɔ man nyɛ tɔwɛ katɔwɛ mʋ nɩɩ asa be mee nu ɩmʋ kasɛ mʋ.

eng: Everything should be done with dignity and in proper order.
local: Amaa fan waa kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ kyɛɛkyɛɛ yɛ kɩmʋ ɔkpa sʋ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I want to call your attention to the good news that I preached to you, which you also received and in which you stand.
local: Mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana, nbɩɩnbɩɩ n biti fɛɛ n nyiŋi fanɛ asɩn danbɩrasa lɩɩ Yesu mʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ mʋ asɩn sa fanɛ, nɩɩ fan kɔɔlɛ kyula, nɩɩ fan san fan yɩlɛ ɩmʋ tɔ mʋ.

eng: You are being saved through it if you hold on to the message I preached to you, unless somehow you believed it for nothing.
local: Nɩɩ fan kan kɩtaa asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ sa fanɛ mʋ tɔ kenken mʋ, ɩɩ bɩya fanɛ kamɔlɩgɛ. Nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ kanɩn bɩrɛ, fanɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii kɩ gyɛ kɩyan.

eng: I passed on to you as most important what I also received: Christ died for our sins in line with the scriptures.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ n nyɛ, nɩɩ ngbaa mɛ n sʋ n sa fanɛ nɩɩ i tiri ɩ kyɔ ɩmʋ kpini mʋ, ɩ gyɛ yɛ Krisito ba wu lɩɩ anɛ alibi sʋ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ mʋ.

eng: he was buried, and he rose on the third day in line with the scriptures.
local: Nɩɩ ba pula mʋ, nɩɩ ɩmʋ kakɛ sasapʋ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ kyiŋi mʋ ba nkpa tɔ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ mʋ.

eng: He appeared to Cephas, then to the Twelve.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ kaapʋ Pita, nɩɩ o bwii lɛɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ kaapʋ asunpu kudu anyɔ mʋ.

eng: and then he appeared to more than five hundred brothers and sisters at once—most of them are still alive to this day, though some have died.
local: Ɩmʋ kamaa mʋ ɔ lɛɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ kaapʋ mu abuupu mʋ nɩɩ ba kyɔ alɩfa anuu saŋa kʋlʋn tɔ. Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ n kyʋrɔɔ ɩnɩmʋ mʋ bamʋ gaalaagaa ba san ba tɛ nkpa tɔ, nɩɩ bamʋ akʋ mɔɔ ba wu.

eng: Then he appeared to James, then to all the apostles.
local: Ɩmʋ kamaa mʋ ɔ lɛɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ kaapʋ mu‐supu Gyemisi, pɔyɩ nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ kaapʋ mu asunpu mʋ kpini.

eng: and last of all he appeared to me, as if I were born at the wrong time.
local: Ɩmʋ kpini laalaalʋwɛ mʋ, ɔ lɛɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ kaapʋ ngbaa mɛ, mʋ nɩɩ n du fɛɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ man fʋʋ kɩkʋʋgɛ nɩɩ ba kʋʋgɛ mʋ mʋ.

eng: Im the least important of the apostles. I dont deserve to be called an apostle, because I harassed Gods church.
local: Krisito asunpu mʋ kpini tɔ mʋ, mɛ ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ man taa bamʋ tɔ, nɩɩ n man dagaa gbaa ba tɩɩ mɛ Krisito osunpu, lɩɩ fɛɛ n kyɩna nyanla Krisito abuupu mʋ.

eng: I am what I am by Gods grace, and Gods grace hasnt been for nothing. In fact, I have worked harder than all the others—that is, it wasnt me but the grace of God that is with me.
local: Amaa lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabwaala mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ n du fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ n du falɛ, nɩɩ mʋ kabwaala mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ sa mɛ mʋ, man gyɛ kɩyan. Nɩɩ n ba payɩ ansi waa kusun gaa n kyɔ asunpu mʋ kpini, nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ mɛ ɔlʋn sʋ nɩɩ n taalɛ waa kusun mʋ, amaa Ɩbwaarɛ ɔlʋn mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ mɛ tɔ mʋ ɩ waa.

eng: So then, whether you heard the message from me or them, this is what we preach and this is what you have believed.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ, nɩɩ mɛ asɛ nɩɩ fan nu asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ nɩn‐oo, nɩɩ bamʋ asɛ nɩɩ fan nu nɩn‐oo, ɩmʋ kpini ɩ gyɛ asɩn kʋlʋn mʋ nɩɩ an tɔwɛ sa fanɛ, nɩɩ fan kɔɔlɛ ɩmʋ gyii mʋ.

eng: So if the message that is preached says that Christ has been raised from the dead, then how can some of you say, Theres no resurrection of the dead?.
local: Nɩɩ a maa tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ Krisito kyiŋi lɩɩ lewu tɔ mʋ, mɩnɛ ɩ waa sʋ nɩɩ fanɛ akʋ bee gyii ɩmɔɔrɛ, yɛ kelewu‐kyiŋi man bʋ tɔ?.

eng: If theres no resurrection of the dead, then Christ hasnt been raised either.
local: Nɩɩ kelewu kyiŋi daa man bʋ tɔ mʋ, kanɩn bɩrɛ Krisito gbaa man kyiŋi ba nkpa tɔ.

eng: If Christ hasnt been raised, then our preaching is useless and your faith is useless.
local: Nɩɩ Krisito man dɩn kyiŋi ba nkpa tɔ mʋ, kanɩn bɩrɛ anɛ abwaarɛsɩn mʋ nɩɩ an tɔwɛ mʋ, man sʋ kyaarɛ, nɩɩ fanɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii mʋ gbaa man sʋ kyaarɛ.

eng: We are found to be false witnesses about God, because we testified against God that he raised Christ, when he didnt raise him if its the case that the dead arent raised.
local: Naafɔɔ nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ kanɩn bɩrɛ mʋ, ii biti ɩ ba fɛɛ an tɩn antɩŋɛsa gyaga Ɩbwaarɛ sʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ an tɔwɛ kaapʋ tɔ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kyiŋi Krisito lɩɩ lewu tɔ. Amaa nɩɩ kelewu kyiŋi man bʋ tɔ mʋ, kanɩn bɩrɛ Ɩbwaarɛ man kyiŋi Yesu lɩɩ lewu tɔ.

eng: If the dead arent raised, then Christ hasnt been raised either.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ nɩɩ kelewu kyiŋi man bʋ tɔ mʋ, kanɩn bɩrɛ Krisito gbaa man dɩn kyiŋi ba nkpa tɔ.

eng: If Christ hasnt been raised, then your faith is worthless; you are still in your sins.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ man dɩn kyiŋi Krisito ba nkpa tɔ mʋ, kanɩn bɩrɛ fanɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii kɩ gyɛ kɩyan, nɩɩ fan san fan bʋ fanɛ alibi tɔ.

eng: and whats more, those who have died in Christ are gone forever.
local: Nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ kanɩn mʋ, ɩɩ kaapʋ nɩn fɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ Krisito gyii nɩɩ ba wu mʋ, ba wu muuri.

eng: If we have a hope in Christ only in this life, then we deserve to be pitied more than anyone else.
local: Nɩɩ ɩ kan ɩ gyɛ kasɛ sʋ nfɩɩ nkʋn nɩɩ an sʋ tamaa Krisito tɔ bɩrɛ, anɛ asɩn ɩ sʋ kʋwɛɛ gaa ɩ kyɔ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ.

eng: But in fact Christ has been raised from the dead. Hes the first crop of the harvest of those who have died.
local: Amaa ɩmʋ kesintin ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ kyiŋi Krisito lɩɩ lewu tɔ ba nkpa tɔ. Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ dan ɩ gyɛ kesintin nɩn fɛɛ, Krisito ɩ gyɛ ɔgyankpaapʋ sa alewupu mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti o kyiŋi bamʋ lɩɩ lewu tɔ ba nkpa tɔ mʋ.

eng: Since death came through a human being, the resurrection of the dead came through one too.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ lewu bɔla nyɩmɩsa kʋlʋn sʋ ba dulinyaa tɔ mʋ, kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ kelewu kyiŋi ba nkpa gbaa tɔ mʋ bɔla nyɩmɩsa kʋlʋn sʋ ba.

eng: In the same way that everyone dies in Adam, so also everyone will be given life in Christ.
local: Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ asa kpini ba lɩɩ Adam tɔ, nɩɩ Adam alibi sʋ asa ba wu mʋ, kanɩn nɩɩ asa kpini mʋ nɩɩ ba gyɛ Krisito lɛɛ mʋ bee biti ba nyɛ nkpa Krisito lewu mʋ sʋ.

eng: Each event will happen in the right order: Christ, the first crop of the harvest, then those who belong to Christ at his coming.
local: Amaa falɛ nɩɩ kelewu kyiŋi mʋ ii biti ɩ waa. Krisito ɩ wʋla gyankpaa kyiŋi lɩɩ lewu tɔ ba nkpa tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ kan bwii ba mʋ, ee biti o kyiŋi bamʋ nɩɩ ba gyɛ mʋ lɛɛ mʋ.

eng: and then the end, when Christ hands over the kingdom to God the Father, when he brings every form of rule, every authority and power to an end.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ kamaa mʋ, kɛ laalaalʋwɛ mʋ ɩ ba. Kanɩn saŋa mʋ, Krisito ii biti o nyita kɩbɩlɩsa‐kigyii kʋmaa nɩmʋ yɛ alʋlʋnpʋ kʋmaa nɩmʋ yɛ ɔlʋn kʋmaa nɩmʋ, kɛ ɔ taa kuwura‐gyii mʋ waa mʋ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kɩbaa tɔ.

eng: It is necessary for him to rule until he puts all enemies under his feet .
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɩ dagaa Krisito maa gyii kuwura naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ taa mʋ dʋn ana kpini waa mʋ ayaa kasɛ.

eng: Death is the last enemy to be brought to an end.
local: Laalaalʋwɛ ɔdʋn mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ nyita mʋ ɩ gyɛ lewu.

eng: since he has brought everything under control under his feet. When it says that everything has been brought under his control, this clearly means everything except for the one who placed everything under his control.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ba kyʋrɔɔ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ yɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ taa kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ waa mʋ kasɛ. Nɩɩ maa tɔwɛ kanɩn mʋ, ɩ dɛ ɔkpa fɛɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ gbaa‐gbaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ waa Krisito kasɛ mʋ man tii sʋ.

eng: But when all things have been brought under his control, then the Son himself will also be under the control of the one who gave him control over everything so that God may be all in all.
local: Nɩɩ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kan ba taa kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ waa Krisito kasɛ mʋ, Obii mʋ gbaa‐gbaa ii biti ɔ taa mʋ n‐yɩɩ waa mʋ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa kʋtɔ kʋmaa waa mʋ kasɛ mʋ, abaa tɔ. Kɛ Ɩbwaarɛ o be gyii kuwura kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ sʋ.

eng: Otherwise, what are those who are getting baptized for the dead doing? If the dead arent raised, then why are they being baptized for them?.
local: Nɩɩ kelewu kyiŋi man bʋ tɔ mʋ, mɩnɛ ɩ waa sʋ nɩɩ akʋ bee biti fɛɛ be gyee bamʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba tee wu mʋ? Nɩɩ alewupu ba mɛɛ yɛ be kyiŋi ba nkpa tɔ mʋ, ɩ waa nɛnɛ sʋ nɩɩ akʋ bee biti fɛɛ be gyee bamʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba wu mʋ?.

eng: And what about us? Why are we in danger all day every day?.
local: Nɩɩ anɛɛnɛ bɩrɛ, mɩnɛ ɩ waa sʋ nɩɩ an taga apʋ saŋa kʋmaa kanɩn?.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I swear by the pride I have in you in Christ Jesus our Lord, Im facing death every day.
local: Mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana, n tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin tɔ fɛɛ n gyaŋɛ lewu asɩn saŋa kʋmaa. Nɩɩ n kaapʋ mɛ n‐yɩɩ fanɛ sʋ, fɛɛ fan maa gyɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu Krisito abuupu mʋ sʋ mʋ, ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ kanɩn.

eng: From a human point of view, what good does it do me if I fought wild animals in Ephesus? If the dead arent raised, lets eat and drink because tomorrow well die .
local: Nɩɩ n kan n kɔ yɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba du fɛɛ ndʋnbwɛ Efeso Ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ, lɩɩ nyɩmɩsa nfɛɛrɛ sʋ mʋ, mɩnɛ nɩɩ n biti n nyɛ lɩɩ ɩmʋ tɔ? Nɩɩ alewupu ba mɛɛ yɛ be kyiŋi ba nkpa tɔ bɩrɛ, fan yɛgɛ a buu asɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Fan yɛgɛ i gyii kɛ a nuu, kɛ ɔkɛ a wu.

eng: Dont be deceived, bad company corrupts good character.
local: Fan man kan yɛgɛ ba pɩna fanɛ. Asa libi tɔ kɩbʋga, ii nyita nyɩmɩsa danbɩrasa nfɛɛrɛ.

eng: Sober up by acting like you should and dont sin. Some of you are ignorant about God—I say this because you should be ashamed of yourselves!.
local: Fan bwii fɛ nfɛɛrɛ danbɩrasa, kɛ fan yɛgɛ alibi kɩwaa. N tɔwɛ fanɛ ɩnɩmʋ nɩn n gyila fanɛ ipeeli fɛɛ, akʋ ba bʋ fanɛ tɔ yɛgɛ ba man nyi Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: But someone will say, How are the dead raised? What kind of body will they have when they come back.
local: Amaa ɔkʋ ii biti ɔ taasɛ yɛ, Nɛnɛ nɩɩ alewupu bee biti ba kyiŋi ba nkpa tɔ? Nɩɩ mɩnɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ nɩɩ bee biti ba baa ba sʋ.

eng: Look, fool! When you put a seed into the ground, it doesnt come back to life unless it dies.
local: Mɩnɛ kimelensi asɩn taasɛsa ndee? Fu mee nyi fɛɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ fu duu kasɛ tɔ mʋ, kɩ mɛɛ yɛ kɩ kɔyɩ, amɔɔ ki wu pɔyɩ.

eng: What you put in the ground doesnt have the shape that it will have, but its a bare grain of wheat or some other seed.
local: Nɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ fii duu mʋ, man gyɛ kɩmʋ oyu mʋ nɩɩ kɩ tɛɛ dan lʋwɛ mʋ gbaa‐gbaa nɩɩ fɩɩ sʋ fii duu, amaa kɩ gyɛ oyu mʋ kibii nɩn fɛɛ kuyu kibi abɛɛ kikyenbee kibi.

eng: God gives it the sort of shape that he chooses, and he gives each of the seeds its own shape.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ sa oyu kʋmaa nɩmʋ kɩmʋ n‐yɩɩ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ee biti. Nɩɩ ɔ sa kibi kʋmaa nɩmʋ kɩmʋ gbaa‐gbaa kɩmʋ n‐yɩɩ kudubi.

eng: All flesh isnt alike. Humans have one kind of flesh, animals have another kind of flesh, birds have another kind of flesh, and fish have another kind.
local: N‐yɩɩ wʋlɛ kpini man gyɛ kʋkʋlʋn. Nyɩmɩsa kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ kwɩɩ nɩɩ atɔbwaaya lɛɛ gbaa kwɩɩ. Nbwiibi lɛɛ kwɩɩ nɩɩ ɩkɩn lɛɛ gbaa kwɩɩ.

eng: There are heavenly bodies and earthly bodies. The heavenly bodies have one kind of glory, and the earthly bodies have another kind of glory.
local: Nɩɩ sʋsʋ n‐yɩɩ wʋlɛ atɔ ɩ bʋ tɔ, nɩɩ kasɛ sʋ n‐yɩɩ wʋlɛ atɔ gbaa bʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ sʋsʋ n‐yɩɩ wʋlɛ bʋnyaa mʋ kwɩɩ, nɩɩ kasɛ sʋ n‐yɩɩ wʋlɛ bʋnyaa gbaa kwɩɩ.

eng: The sun has one kind of glory, the moon has another kind of glory, and the stars have another kind of glory .
local: Kyɔwɛ kɩkɩɩ tɔ kwɩɩ, nɩɩ ɔbwaayɩ kɩkɩɩ tɔ kwɩɩ, nɩɩ akyikpebi gbaa kɩkɩɩ tɔ kwɩɩ. Nɩɩ kikyikpebi kʋmaa nɩmʋ sʋ kɩmʋ gbaa‐gbaa kɩkɩɩ tɔ.

eng: Its the same with the resurrection of the dead: a rotting body is put into the ground, but what is raised wont ever decay.
local: Kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ kelewu kyiŋi mʋ ii biti ɩ ba waa. N‐yɩɩ wʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ bee pula mʋ ɩɩ gyɔɔ nɩn, amaa n‐yɩɩ pʋpwɛ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti o kyiŋi lɩɩ lewu tɔ mʋ bɩrɛ mɛɛ yɛ ɩ gyɔɔ.

eng: Its degraded when its put into the ground, but its raised in glory. Its weak when its put into the ground, but its raised in power.
local: Kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ bee pula mʋ, gyɛ kʋtɔ bɔrɔbɔrɔ nɩɩ kɩ man sʋ ɔlʋn, amaa nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kan kyiŋi kɩmʋ bɩrɛ mʋ, kii biti kɩ baa kɩ sʋ bʋnyaa yɛ ɔlʋn.

eng: Its a physical body when its put into the ground, but its raised as a spiritual body. If theres a physical body, theres also a spiritual body.
local: Nɩɩ ba kan pula kɩmʋ mʋ, kɩ gyɛ dulinyaa tɔ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ nɩn, amaa nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kyiŋi kɩmʋ mʋ, kɩ gyɛ kayaayu tɔ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ nɩn. Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ dulinyaa tɔ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ bʋ tɔ mʋ, kanɩn maŋa gbaa nɩɩ kayaayu tɔ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ bʋ tɔ.

eng: So it is also written, The first human, Adam, became a living person, and the last Adam became a spirit that gives life.
local: Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ ɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, Adam gyankpaasa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tʋʋ mʋ ba biliŋi nyɩmɩsa kɩɩsa. Amaa Yesu Krisito mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ Adam laalaalʋwɛ mʋ, gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ sa nkpa.

eng: But the physical body comes first, not the spiritual one—the spiritual body comes afterward.
local: Man gyɛ kayaayu tɔ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ ɩ gyankpaa ba. Amaa dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ ɩ gyankpaa ba, pɔyɩ nɩɩ kayaayu tɔ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ ba.

eng: The first human was from the earth made from dust; the second human is from heaven.
local: Adam gyankpaasa mʋ lɩɩ kasɛ sʋ nɩn, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɩsɛ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ taa pɔyɩ mʋ. Amaa Adam nyɔsapʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ Krisito mʋ, lɩɩ sʋsʋ nɩn.

eng: The nature of the person made of dust is shared by people who are made of dust, and the nature of the heavenly person is shared by heavenly people.
local: Kanɩn sʋ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Adam mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ kasɛ sʋ du mʋ, kanɩn nɩɩ kasɛ sʋ asa gbaa ba du. Nɩɩ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Adam mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ sʋsʋ du mʋ, kanɩn nɩɩ mʋ asa gbaa ba du nɩn.

eng: We will look like the heavenly person in the same way as we have looked like the person made from dust.
local: Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ an duli kasɛ sʋ nyɩmɩsa mʋ, kanɩn sʋ an biti an baa an duli nyɩmɩsa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ sʋsʋ mʋ gbaa.

eng: This is what Im saying, brothers and sisters: Flesh and blood cant inherit Gods kingdom. Something that rots cant inherit something that doesnt decay.
local: Mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana asɩn mʋ nɩɩ n biti fɛn bɩɩ mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ sʋ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ yɛ nkalan mʋ, mɛɛ taalɛ nyɛ loo Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ anɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ gyɔɔ mʋ, mɛɛ taalɛ kyɩna kakpaa.

eng: Listen, Im telling you a secret: All of us wont die, but we will all be changed.
local: Fan nu asɩn baalasa nɩmʋ danbɩrasa. Man gyɛ anɛ kpini ii biti a wu. Amaa saŋa kʋ tɔ mʋ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ kyɛɛgɛ anɛ Krisito abuupu mʋ keduli yɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ.

eng: in an instant, in the blink of an eye, at the final trumpet. The trumpet will blast, and the dead will be raised with bodies that wont decay, and we will be changed.
local: Nɩɩ ɩnɩmʋ kpini ii biti ɩ waa opula tɔ fɛɛ ansi sʋ kɩŋabɛɛ, saŋa mʋ nɩɩ kabɩlɛ laalaalʋwɛ mʋ kan fʋlɛ mʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ kabɩlɛ mʋ ii biti o su, nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba wu mʋ, bee biti ba kyiŋi ba nkpa tɔ, nɩɩ ba mɛɛ yɛ ba bɩla wu. Nɩɩ anɛ mʋ nɩɩ an san an tɛ nkpa tɔ mʋ, an biti a kyɛɛgɛ.

eng: Its necessary for this rotting body to be clothed with what cant decay, and for the body that is dying to be clothed in what cant die.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ gyɔɔ mʋ, dagaa kɩ maa kyɛɛgɛ waa kɩmʋ nɩɩ kɩ mɛɛ gyɔɔ. Nɩɩ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ kii wu mʋ, dagaa kɩ maa kyɛɛgɛ waa kɩmʋ nɩɩ ki mee wu.

eng: And when the rotting body has been clothed in what cant decay, and the dying body has been clothed in what cant die, then this statement in scripture will happen: Death has been swallowed up by a victory .
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ ɩ kan ba fɛɛ, kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ gyɔɔ mʋ, kyɛɛgɛ waa kɩmʋ nɩɩ kɩ mɛɛ gyɔɔ, nɩɩ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ kii wu mʋ, kyɛɛgɛ waa kɩmʋ nɩɩ ki mee wu mʋ, tʋtɔ nɩɩ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ ii biti ɩ ba kesintin tɔ. Ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, Ba kɔ gyii lewu sʋ muuri mʋ.

eng: Where is your victory, Death? Where is your sting, Death? .
local: Lewu, fʋ ɔlʋn mʋ nɩɩ fʋ sʋ fii gyii asa sʋ mʋ bʋ fɩnɛ? Lewu, fʋ ɔgyʋʋ nɩɩ fʋ sʋ fii yii asa mʋ bʋ fɩnɛ.

eng: Deaths sting is sin, and the power of sin is the Law.
local: Ɔgyʋʋ mʋ nɩɩ lewu sʋ ee yii mʋ ɩ gyɛ alibi, nɩɩ alibi mɔɔ nyɛ ɩmʋ ɔlʋn lɩɩ nbara tɔ.

eng: Thanks be to God, who gives us this victory through our Lord Jesus Christ!.
local: Amaa afwaala ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ, fɛɛ maa bɔla anɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Yesu Krisito sʋ nɩɩ ɔ yɛgɛ nɩɩ an kɔ lɩɩ lewu ɔlʋn tɔ.

eng: As a result of all this, my loved brothers and sisters, you must stand firm, unshakable, excelling in the work of the Lord as always, because you know that your labor isnt going to be for nothing in the Lord.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu, fan yɩla tɔ kenken, kɛ fan man yɛgɛ kʋtɔ kʋ ki gyingyen fanɛ. Fan payɩ ansi waa Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ kusun mʋ saŋa kʋmaa. Lɩɩ fɛɛ fen nyi fɛɛ kusun mʋ nɩɩ fen sun Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ tɔ mʋ, ɩ mɛɛ yɛ ɩ waa kɩyan.

eng: When someone in your assembly has a legal case against another member, do they dare to take it to court to be judged by people who arent just, instead of by Gods people?.
local: Nɩɩ fanɛ tɔ ɔkʋ maa mʋ bɩrɩsa, Krisito obuupu ba kan nyɛ asɩn mʋ, mɩnɛ ɩ waa sʋ nɩɩ ee kpee naa samaa mʋ asɩn agyipu mʋ nɩɩ ba man nyi Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ asɛ, nɩɩ ɔ man taa mʋ kpee Krisito abuupu mʋ asɛ kɛ ba lɔŋɔ bamʋ?.

eng: Or dont you know that Gods people will judge the world? If the world is to be judged by you, are you incompetent to judge trivial cases?.
local: Fan man nyi yɛ anɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ asa ii biti a talɛ dulinyaa abi? Nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ anɛ ii biti a talɛ dulinyaa abi asɩn bɩrɛ, mɩnɛ ɩ waa sʋ nɩɩ fɛn mɛɛ taalɛ lɔŋɔ fanɛ nsana tɔ asɩn wuribi?.

eng: Dont you know that we will judge angels? Why not ordinary things?.
local: Fan man nyi fɛɛ anɛ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ yɛgɛ a talɛ mʋ nbɔɔ mʋ? Ɩmʋ sʋ mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fɛn mɛɛ taalɛ lɔŋɔ abaa ndaga kakyɩna nɩmʋ tɔ asɩn?.

eng: So then if you have ordinary lawsuits, do you appoint people as judges who arent respected by the church?.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ fan kan fan sʋ falɛ asɩn fanɛ nsana mʋ, mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fan sʋ fen kpiya asɩn agyipu mʋ nɩɩ Krisito kʋbʋ mʋ ba man sʋ bɛɛrɛɛ sa bamʋ mʋ, yɛ ba gyii fanɛ asɩn?.

eng: Im saying this because you should be ashamed of yourselves! Isnt there one person among you who is wise enough to pass judgment between believers?.
local: Ɩ man sʋ fanɛ ipeeli? Amɔɔ fan man sʋ ɔnyaasɩnpʋ kʋlʋn fanɛ tɔ, mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ taalɛ lɔŋɔ fanɛ wʋlɛ‐wʋlɛ nsana?.

eng: But instead, does a brother or sister have a lawsuit against another brother or sister, and do they do this in front of unbelievers?.
local: Amaa fanɛɛ fanɛ bɩrɛ, Krisito obuupu ɩɩ taa mʋ bɩrɩsa Krisito obuupu asɩn ee kpee ɛɛ sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba man kɔɔlɛ Yesu gyii mʋ asɛ.

eng: The fact that you have lawsuits against each other means that youve already lost your case. Why not be wronged instead? Why not be cheated?.
local: Fan maa nyɛ asɩn nɩɩ fɛn samaa abaa mʋ, ɩ kaapʋ nɩn fɛɛ fan tɛɛ wʋla gyii tɩyɛ fanɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii tɔ nɩn. Mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ odunwura mʋ mee kyula yɛ mʋ asɩn ɩ man bʋrɔn? Mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ yɛgɛ kɛ ba sisii mʋ?.

eng: But instead you are doing wrong and cheating—and youre doing it to your own brothers and sisters.
local: Amaa fanɛ wʋlɛ‐wʋlɛ Krisito abi fen siisi abaa, nɩɩ fɛn waa fanɛ bɩrɩsa ana kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ man bʋrɔn.

eng: Dont you know that people who are unjust wont inherit Gods kingdom? Dont be deceived. Those who are sexually immoral, those who worship false gods, adulterers, both participants in same-sex intercourse.
local: Fan man nyi yɛ alibi awaapʋ mʋ, ba mɛɛ yɛ ba nyɛ loo Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ? Fan man yɛgɛ ɔkʋ ɔ pɩna fanɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa kakyɩɩ abɛɛ kanyɩn biti kɩgyagaa, yɛ ɩdakpa asunpu, abɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ lɩɩ bamʋ‐kuli ana abɛɛ bamʋ‐ka ana kamaa, yɛ anyɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ dɛ yɛ bamʋ bɩrɩsa ana anyɩn.

eng: thieves, the greedy, drunks, abusive people, and swindlers wont inherit Gods kingdom.
local: yɛ ayuu, yɛ onsi‐payɩ libi wuya ana, yɛ atabʋʋpʋ, yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bee nyita asa atɩɩ, yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ pɩna asa bee gyii mʋ, falɛ asa anɩmʋ ɔkʋ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ nyɛ loo Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ.

eng: That is what some of you used to be! But you were washed clean, you were made holy to God, and you were made right with God in the name of the Lord Jesus Christ and in the Spirit of our God.
local: Falɛ nɩɩ fanɛ akʋ fan kyɩna fan du, amaa nbɩɩnbɩɩ bɩrɛ Ɩbwaarɛ fwɩɩ fanɛ alibi, nɩɩ ɔ lɔŋɔ fanɛ yɩla sa mʋ n‐yɩɩ, nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ fanɛ kyula yɛ fanɛ ɩkpa i kyiigi mʋ asɛ lɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu Krisito yɛ anɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu mʋ sʋ.

eng: I have the freedom to do anything, but not everything is helpful. I have the freedom to do anything, but I wont be controlled by anything.
local: Fanɛ tɔ ɔkʋ ɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, N sʋ ɔkpa fɛɛ n waa kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ. Ɩ gyɛ kesintin, amaa man gyɛ ɩmʋ kpini ɩ bʋrɔn sa mʋ. Ngbaa mɛ n taalɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, n sʋ ɔkpa fɛɛ n waa kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ, amaa n mɛɛ yɛ n waa mɛ n‐yɩɩ kɩnyɛ sa kʋtɔ kʋ.

eng: Food is for the stomach and the stomach is for food, and yet God will do away with both. The body isnt for sexual immorality but for the Lord, and the Lord is for the body.
local: Fanɛ tɔ ɔkʋ ɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, Agyitɔ ɩ bʋ tɔ sa ɔtɔ, nɩɩ ɔtɔ gbaa bʋ tɔ sa agyitɔ. Ɩ gyɛ kesintin, amaa Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti o nyita ɩmʋ kpini. Nyɩmɩsa kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ kɩ man bʋ tɔ sa kakyɩɩ yɛ kanyɩn biti kɩgyagaa, amaa kɩ gyɛ sa Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ nɩn, nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mɔɔ bʋ tɔ sa kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ.

eng: God has raised the Lord and will raise us through his power.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ taa mʋ ɔlʋn kyiŋi Yesu lɩɩ lewu tɔ, kanɩn nɩɩ ee biti o kyiŋi anɛ gbaa anɛ lɩɩ lewu tɔ.

eng: Dont you know that your bodies are parts of Christ? So then, should I take parts of Christ and make them a part of someone who is sleeping around? No way!.
local: Fan man nyi yɛ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ ɩ gyɛ Yesu kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ abaafʋn mʋ abɛɛ? Ɩmʋ sʋ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ n taa mɛ n‐yɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ Krisito kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ kɩbaafʋn mʋ naa dɛ yɛ ɔbʋrʋkɔɔnɛ‐kyɩɩ abɛɛ? Afuloo! Ɩ mɛɛ yɛ ɩ ba kanɩn.

eng: Dont you know that anyone who is joined to someone who is sleeping around is one body with that person? The scripture says, The two will become one flesh .
local: Fan man nyi fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ naa bʋga yɛ ɔbʋrʋkɔɔnɛ‐kyɩɩ mʋ, maa mʋ ba biliŋi kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ kʋlʋn nɩn? Lɩɩ fɛɛ ba kyʋrɔɔ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ yɛ, Bamʋ anyɔ ba biliŋi kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ kʋlʋn nɩn.

eng: The one who is joined to the Lord is one spirit with him.
local: Amaa ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ n‐yɩɩ bʋga yɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ, maa mʋ ba biliŋi kʋkʋlʋn nɩn kayaayu tɔ.

eng: Avoid sexual immorality! Every sin that a person can do is committed outside the body, except those who engage in sexual immorality commit sin against their own bodies.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan sɩlɛ lɩɩ kakyɩɩ abɛɛ kanyɩn biti kɩgyagaa tɔ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ alibi kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ nyɩmɩsa ɩ waa mʋ, ɩ mɛɛ waa mʋ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ ineesi, amaa nyɩmɩsa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa kanyɩn abɛɛ kakyɩɩ biti kɩgyagaa mʋ, ɔ waa mʋ gbaa‐gbaa kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ alibi nɩn.

eng: Or dont you know that your body is a temple of the Holy Spirit who is in you? Dont you know that you have the Holy Spirit from God, and you dont belong to yourselves?.
local: Fan man nyi yɛ fanɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ ɩ gyɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ? Fan man nyi yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ sa fanɛ Kayaayu maŋa, nɩɩ ɔ tɛ fanɛ tɔ? Fan man tɛ fanɛ kɩbaa sʋ.

eng: You have been bought and paid for, so honor God with your body.
local: lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ sɔɔ fanɛ ɔgyalɩn. Ɩmʋ sʋ fan taa fanɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ waa atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ sa Ɩbwaarɛ bʋnyaa.

eng: Concerning the collection of money for Gods people: You should do what I have directed the churches in Galatia to do.
local: Nbɩɩnbɩɩ lɩɩ afulee mʋ nɩɩ fɛn tʋʋ fan naa sa Krisito abi mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Gyerusalem mʋ, fan waa fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ Krisito kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ Galati ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ mʋ.

eng: On the first day of the week, each of you should set aside whatever you can afford from what you earn so that the collection wont be delayed until I come.
local: Ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, Gyiwu abi kakɛ gyankpaasa kʋmaa mʋ, fanɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ ɔ lɛɛ mu afulee mʋ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ mʋ tɔ ɩkʋ yɩla, fan man gyoo naa fʋʋ kakɛ mʋ nɩɩ n biti n ba pɔyɩ kɛ fan lan lɛɛ.

eng: Then when I get there, Ill send whomever you approve to Jerusalem with letters of recommendation to bring your gift.
local: Nɩɩ n ba mʋ, kɛ fan taa tʋʋtʋʋ mʋ sa asa mʋ nɩɩ fan lɛɛ mʋ, kɛ n kyʋrɔɔ ɔwʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ kaapʋ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba gyɛ, kɛ n sun bamʋ, kɛ ba taa fanɛ tʋʋtʋʋ mʋ kpee Gyerusalem kadɛ tɔ.

eng: If it seems right for me to go too, theyll travel with me.
local: Amaa nɩɩ ɩ kan ba fɛɛ ngbaa mɛ n kpee mʋ, kɛ ba buu mɛ kɛ a kpee.

eng: Ill come to you after I go through Macedonia, and because Im going through Macedonia.
local: N kan lɩɩ Masedoniya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ mʋ, kɛ n ba fanɛ asɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ n waa nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ n biti n bɔla tʋtɔ pɔyɩ kɛ n ba fanɛ asɛ.

eng: I may stay with you or even spend the winter there in Corinth so that you can send me on my way to wherever Im off to next.
local: Akʋtɔ n biti n kyɩna fanɛ asɛ gbɛrɛɛ, abɛɛ gbangban ii biti o tu mɛ fanɛ asɛ, kɛ fɛn taalɛ sun mɛ ɔkpa kpee katɩn kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ n biti n kpee.

eng: I dont want to make a quick visit to you, since I hope to spend some time with you if the Lord lets it happen.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ bɩrɛ, n mee biti fɛɛ n dan wu fanɛ kɛ n ŋmayɩ sʋ kyʋn. Nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kan kyula mʋ, n biti fɛɛ n gyii nkɛ gbɛrɛɛ kʋ fanɛ asɛ.

eng: Ill stay here in Ephesus until the Festival of Pentecost.
local: Amaa n san n biti n baa n bʋ Efeso kadɛ nfɩɩ, naa fʋʋ Pentekosi kesu kɔnɔ ngyaŋɛ mʋ saŋa.

eng: In spite of the fact that there are many opponents, a big and productive opportunity has opened up for my mission here.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ bugi ɔkpa lala kʋ sa mɛ, kɛ n nyɛ waa kusun mʋ, amaa bamʋ nɩɩ bee kisi mɛ mʋ ba kyɔ nfɩɩ.

eng: If Timothy comes to you, be sure that he has no reason to be afraid while hes with you, because he does the work of the Lord just like I do.
local: Nɩɩ Timoti kan ba fanɛ asɛ mʋ, fan kɩtaa mʋ danbɩrasa kɛ mu ansi i gyii. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ee sun Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu kusun mʋ nɩn, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ngbaa mɛ n sun falɛ.

eng: So dont let anyone disrespect him, but send him on in peace so he can join me. Im waiting for him along with the brothers and sisters.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ fanɛ tɔ ɔkʋ ɔ man kan ŋmanyan mʋ, amaa fan sun mʋ ɔkpa kayɩɩ yuuli tɔ, kɛ o bwii ba tu mɛ nfɩɩ. N kɩɩ mʋ yɛ anɛ bɩrɩsa ana Krisito abuupu mʋ ɔkpa.

eng: Concerning Apollos our brother: I strongly encouraged him to visit you with the brothers and sisters, but he didnt want to go now. Hell come when he has an opportunity.
local: Nbɩɩnbɩɩ lɩɩ anɛ bɩrɩsa Apolosi sʋ mʋ, n payɩ ansi waa mʋ aniya tɔ fɛɛ, o tii anɛ bɩrɩsa ana Krisito abuupu mʋ sʋ, kɛ ba ba bɔɔ fanɛ sʋ. Ɔ man pɩɩ yɩlɛ kyula yɛ ee biti ɔ ba nbɩɩnbɩɩ, amaa nɩɩ ɔ kan nyɛ ɔkpa mʋ, ee biti ɔ ba.

eng: Stay awake, stand firm in your faith, be brave, be strong.
local: Fan baa fan da sʋ, kɛ fan yɩlɛ fanɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii mʋ tɔ kenken, kɛ fan yɩlɛ kɩnyɩn tɔ, kɛ fan nyɛ ɔlʋn.

eng: Everything should be done in love.
local: Fan taa kebiti sun fanɛ kusun kʋmaa nɩmʋ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I encourage you to do something else. You know that the people in Stephanas household were the first crop of the harvest to come from the mission to Achaia. They have dedicated themselves to the service of Gods people.
local: Fen nyi fɛɛ Sitefana maa mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ abi, ɩ gyɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba gyankpaa kɔɔlɛ Krisito gyii Akiya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ, nɩɩ ba taa bamʋ n‐yɩɩ sa bɛɛ kyɛ abuupu mʋ tɔ.

eng: So accept the authority of people like them and of anyone who cooperates and works hard.
local: Mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana, n kʋlɛ fanɛ nɩn fɛɛ fan bɩya fanɛ n‐yɩɩ kasɛ sa kanɩn asa maŋa, yɛ ɔsa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ waa kanɩn kusun maŋa mʋ.

eng: Im so happy that Stephanas, Fortunatus, and Achaicus have arrived; theyve made up for my missing you.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Sitefana maa Fotunatu yɛ Akayiku ba ba mɛ asɛ mʋ, mi ansi i gyii. Lɩɩ fɛɛ fan maa man bʋ mi asɛ mʋ, bamʋ ɩ gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba be yii fanɛ ayaa tɔ waa ɩmʋ nɩɩ fan daa yɛ fan waa mʋ.

eng: Indeed theyve provided my spirit and yours with a much-needed rest. Therefore, give them proper recognition.
local: Ba yɛgɛ nɩɩ mɛ kɔkɔlɔ ki yuuli mɛ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba waa sa fanɛ gbaa fanɛ mʋ. Falɛ asa ɩ dagaa fan maa lɛɛ bamʋ kɩtɩɩ.

eng: The churches in the province of Asia greet you. Aquila and Prisca greet you warmly in the Lord, together with the church that meets in their house.
local: Krisito kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ Asiya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ mʋ kpini bɛɛ fwaala fanɛ. Akwila maa Prisila yɛ Krisito kʋbʋ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ gyaŋɛ bamʋ kɩkpaara sʋ mʋ, bɛɛ fwaala fanɛ gaa lɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ kɩtɩɩ tɔ sʋ.

eng: All the brothers and sisters greet you. You in turn should greet each other with a holy kiss.
local: Anɛ bɩrɩsa ana akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ nfɩɩ mʋ kpini, bɛɛ fwaala fanɛ. Fan taa kibiri abaa tɔ fwaala abaa lɩɩ fanɛ nkɔlɔ tɔ.

eng: Here is my greeting in my own handwriting—Paul.
local: Mɛ Pɔɔlɩ gbaa‐gbaa ɩ taa mɛ kɩbaa kyʋrɔɔ afwaala ɩnɩmʋ n sa fanɛ.

eng: A curse on anyone who doesnt love the Lord. Come, Lord!.
local: Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o mee biti anɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Yesu Krisito mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ kɔnɔ. Maranata kɩmʋ kasɛ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, Anɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ, ba.

eng: The grace of the Lord Jesus be with you.
local: Anɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Yesu Krisito ɔ bwaala fanɛ.

eng: My love is with all of you in Christ Jesus.
local: Lɩɩ Yesu Krisito kɩtɩɩ tɔ sʋ mʋ, mi kebiti kɩ baa kɩ bʋ fanɛ kpini asɛ. Amen!.

eng: From Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by Gods will, and Timothy our brother. To Gods church that is in Corinth, along with all of Gods people throughout Achaia.
local: Ɔwʋlɛ nɩmʋ lɩɩ mɛ, Pɔɔlɩ mʋ nɩɩ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kebiti sʋ nɩɩ n ba waa Krisito Yesu Osunpu mʋ, yɛ anɛ bɩrɩsa danbɩrasa Timoti asɛ. An kyʋrɔɔ an sʋ an sa fanɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kʋbʋ mʋ nɩɩ fan bʋ Korinto kadɛ tɔ, yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɔŋɔ bamʋ yɩla sa mʋ n‐yɩɩ, nɩɩ ba bʋ Akiya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ mʋ kpini.

eng: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and from our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Anɛ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ anɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Yesu Krisito ba bwaala fanɛ, kɛ ba sa fanɛ kayɩɩ yuuli.

eng: May the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ be blessed! He is the compassionate Father and God of all comfort.
local: Fan yɛgɛ a fwaala Ɩbwaarɛ, anɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Yesu Krisito mʋ‐Sɛ. Nɩɩ mʋ ɩ gyɛ kʋwɛɛ kuwu kumu yɛ kɔkɔlɔ yuuli wuya.

eng: Hes the one who comforts us in all our trouble so that we can comfort other people who are in every kind of trouble. We offer the same comfort that we ourselves received from God.
local: Nɩɩ ee yuula anɛ nkɔlɔ lɩɩ anɛ awʋrʋfɔ tɔ, kɛ anɛ gbaa anɛ a taalɛ taa kɔkɔlɔ yuuli mʋ nɩɩ an nyɛ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ mʋ yuula bamʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ awʋrʋfɔ kʋmaa tɔ mʋ nkɔlɔ.

eng: That is because we receive so much comfort through Christ in the same way that we share so many of Christs sufferings.
local: Lɩɩ kanan mʋ nɩɩ an gyii Yesu Krisito awʋrʋfɔ damantɛ gbaa mʋ, kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ kɔkɔlɔ yuuli damantɛ gbaa bɔla Krisito sʋ ba anɛ sʋ.

eng: So if we have trouble, it is to bring you comfort and salvation. If we are comforted, it is to bring you comfort from the experience of endurance while you go through the same sufferings that we also suffer.
local: Nɩɩ an kan gyii awʋrʋfɔ mʋ, ɩɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ fan nyɛ nkɔlɔ yuuli yɛ kamɔlɩgɛ. Nɩɩ an kan nyɛ kɔkɔlɔ yuuli lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ mʋ, fanɛ gbaa fanɛ fɛn nyɛ kɔkɔlɔ yuuli mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ bɩya fanɛ ɔlʋn, yɛ kenyiita kɛ fan taalɛ yɩlɛ tɔ gyii awʋrʋfɔ kʋlʋn mʋ nɩɩ anɛ gbaa anɛ an gyii mʋ.

eng: Our hope for you is certain, because we know that as you are partners in suffering, so also you are partners in comfort.
local: Anɛ tamaa mʋ nɩɩ an sʋ fanɛ sʋ mʋ bʋlʋn, nɩɩ an nyi fɛɛ fan maa gyii awʋrʋfɔ mʋ nɩɩ an gyii mʋ, kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ fen biti fan nyɛ kɔkɔlɔ yuuli fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ an nyɛ mʋ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we dont want you to be unaware of the troubles that we went through in Asia. We were weighed down with a load of suffering that was so far beyond our strength that we were afraid we might not survive.
local: Mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana, an mɛɛ yɛ a taa awʋrʋfɔ mʋ nɩɩ an gyii Asiya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ baala fanɛ sʋ. Awʋrʋfɔ mʋ nɩɩ an wu mʋ ɩ nyan anɛ naa ban sʋ halɩɩ an man bɩla an sʋ tamaa anɛ nkpa sʋ.

eng: It certainly seemed to us as if we had gotten the death penalty. This was so that we would have confidence in God, who raises the dead, instead of ourselves.
local: Kesintin tɔ, anɛ nkɔlɔ tɔ mʋ, an nyi fɛɛ ba bun anɛ lewu kʋpwɛ. Amaa ɩnɩmʋ ɩ waa nɩn kɛ a man taa anɛ n‐yɩɩ tʋʋ anɛ gbaa‐gbaa anɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ, amɔɔ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ee kyiŋi alewupu mʋ.

eng: God rescued us from a terrible death, and he will rescue us. We have set our hope on him that he will rescue us again.
local: Mʋ ɩ lɛɛ anɛ lɩɩ falɛ alewu‐sɩn nɩmʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ maa sʋ ɛɛ lɛɛ anɛ, nɩɩ an sʋ tamaa fɛɛ ee biti ɔ kyaga sʋ lɛɛ anɛ.

eng: since you are helping with your prayer for us. Then many people can thank God on our behalf for the gift that was given to us through the prayers of many people.
local: fɛɛ fan maa kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ fɛn kyɛ anɛ tɔ falɛ. Kɛ ɩ yɛgɛ Ɩbwaarɛ o nu kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ asa gaalaagaa bɛɛ kʋlɛ bɛɛ sa anɛ mʋ, kɛ ɔ waa anɛ kusee, kɛ ba taa afwaala sa mʋ lɩɩ anɛ sʋ.

eng: We have conducted ourselves with godly sincerity and pure motives in the world, and especially toward you. This is why we are confident, and our conscience confirms this. We didnt act with human wisdom but we relied on the grace of God.
local: Kʋtɔ kʋlʋn mʋ sʋ nɩɩ an kaapʋ anɛ n‐yɩɩ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, anɛ nfɛɛrɛ ɩɩ kaapʋ anɛ yɛ, kesintin yɛ kɔkɔlɔ fufuli mʋ nɩɩ ɩ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ, nɩɩ an taa kyɩna dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ, fɔnfɔn lɩɩ anaa fanɛ kakyɩna tɔ. Man gyɛ dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ kanyaasɩn nɩɩ an taa waa ɩnɩmʋ, amaa Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ taa bwaala anɛ.

eng: We dont write anything to you except what you can read and also understand. I hope that you will understand totally.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ n mɛɛ kyʋrɔɔ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ fɛn mɛɛ taalɛ karagɛ abɛɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ fɛn mɛɛ taalɛ nu kɩmʋ kasɛ. Nɩɩ n sʋ tamaa.

eng: since you have already understood us partly. Understand that in the day of our Lord Jesus, we will make you proud as you will also make us proud.
local: fɛɛ fan maa nu asɩn mʋ kasɛ gbɛrɛɛ lɩɩ anɛ asɛ falɛ mʋ, fen biti fan ba nu ɩmʋ kasɛ lʋwɛ, kɛ fan kaapʋ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ lɩɩ anɛ sʋ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ an biti a kaapʋ anɛ n‐yɩɩ lɩɩ fanɛ sʋ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu kakɛ mʋ nɩɩ ee biti o bwii ba mʋ.

eng: Because I was sure of this, I wanted to visit you first so that you could have a second opportunity to see me.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ n maa sʋ ɩnɩmʋ kpini tamaa sʋ mʋ, n ŋmina yɩla fɛɛ n biti n daa kɩba bɔɔ fanɛ sʋ, saamʋ kɛ fan nyɛ tɔnɔ ɩlɩɩ ɩnyɔ.

eng: I wanted to visit you on my way to Macedonia, and then come to you again on my way back from Macedonia, at which point I was hoping you would help me on my way to Judea.
local: N ŋmina yɩla yɛ n kan n kpee Masedoniya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ mʋ, n biti n bɔɔ fanɛ sʋ, nɩɩ n kan bɩla lɩɩ tʋtɔ n ba kyʋn mʋ, kɛ n bɩla bɔɔ fanɛ sʋ, kɛ fan kyɛ mɛ tɔ kɛ n taalɛ naa fʋʋ Gyudeya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ.

eng: So I wasnt unreliable when I planned to do this, was I? Or do I make decisions with a substandard human process so that I say Yes, yes and No, no at the same time?.
local: N maa ŋmina yɩla yɛ n biti n ba fanɛ asɛ, nɩɩ n man taalɛ ba mʋ, ɩɩ kaapʋ nɩn fɛɛ n gyɛ dulinyaa tɔ nnɔ nnyɔ‐nnyɔ wuya ana mʋ nɩɩ bee kyula yɛ Ɛɛhn, kɛ opula maŋa tɔ ba bwii kyɛɛgɛ kɔnɔ tɔwɛ yɛ ayee abɛɛ?.

eng: But as God is faithful, our message to you isnt both yes and no.
local: Amaa fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ gyɛ kesintin wuya falɛ mʋ, kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ anɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ an tɔwɛ an sa fanɛ mʋ, man gyɛ nnɔ nnyɔ‐nnyɔ asɩn.

eng: Gods Son, Jesus Christ, is the one who was preached among you by us—through me, Silvanus, and Timothy—he wasnt yes and no. In him it is always yes.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mu‐Bii Yesu Krisito asɩn mʋ nɩɩ n maa Silasi yɛ Timoti an tɔwɛ sa fanɛ mʋ, asɩn mʋ ɩ man kyɩna ɩ gyɛ nnɔ nnyɔ‐nnyɔ, amaa mʋ asɩn kpini ɩ gyɛ kʋkʋlʋn nɩn.

eng: All of Gods promises have their yes in him. That is why we say Amen through him to the glory of God.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ sa mʋ kpini ɩ ba tɔ lɩɩ Yesu Krisito sʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ an bʋnyaa Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ, an bɔla Yesu sʋ an kyula yɛ Amen.

eng: God is the one who establishes us with you in Christ and who anointed us.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ yɛgɛ nɩɩ anaa fanɛ kpini an nyɛ ɔlʋn yɩlɛ Krisito tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ anɛ yɩla sa mʋ n‐yɩɩ.

eng: God also sealed us and gave the Spirit as a down payment in our hearts.
local: nɩɩ ɔ taa mu keduligi gyaga anɛ sʋ fɛɛ mʋ ɩ bʋ anɛ, nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ sa anɛ, mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa yɩla kasɛ sa atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa yɩla sa anɛ mʋ.

eng: I call on God as my witness—I didnt come again to Corinth because I wanted to spare you.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ gyɛ mi kesintin ogyipu fɛɛ, n maa mee biti fɛɛ n ba bɩɩtɛ fanɛ asʋ sʋ mʋ, kanɩn sʋ nɩɩ n man bɩla ba fanɛ asɛ Korinto kadɛ tɔ.

eng: It isnt that we are trying to control your faith, but we are working with you for your happiness, because you stand firm in your faith.
local: Ɩ man gyɛ yɛ an tɔwɛ an nyan fanɛ lɩɩ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fan kɔɔlɛ gyii sʋ, fɛɛ a maa gyɛ abɩlɩsa sʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ an nyi fan maa yɩlɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii mʋ tɔ kenken. Amaa kanan mʋ nɩɩ a waa kɛ ɩ kyɛ fanɛ tɔ kɛ fan nyɛ kɔkɔlɔ fuuli sʋ nɩɩ an waa kanɩn.

eng: So I decided that, for my own sake, I wouldnt visit you again while I was upset.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ n waa mɛ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ, n man bɩla yɛ n bwii ba fanɛ asɛ, kɛ n nyita fanɛ nkɔlɔ.

eng: If I make you sad, who will be there to make me glad when you are sad because of me?.
local: Nɩɩ n kan yɛgɛ nɩɩ fanɛ nkɔlɔ i nyita fanɛ mʋ, anɩmʋ ii biti ɔ yɛgɛ mi ansi i gyii? Amɔɔ man gyɛ fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ n nyita fanɛ nkɔlɔ mʋ abɛɛ?.

eng: Thats why I wrote this very thing to you, so that when I came I wouldnt be made sad by the ones who ought to make me happy. I have confidence in you, that my happiness means your happiness.
local: Kanɩn sʋ nɩɩ n daa kyʋrɔɔ ɔwʋlɛ maŋa ba sa fanɛ. N mee biti n maa ba fanɛ asɛ, kɛ fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ naafɔɔ fen biti fan yɛgɛ mi ansi i gyii mʋ, fan nyita mɛ kɔkɔlɔ. Nɩɩ n kɔɔlɛ fanɛ kpini gyii mɛ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ fɛɛ, nɩɩ mi ansi i gyii mʋ, fanɛ gbaa fanɛ ansi ii biti i gyii.

eng: I wrote to you in tears, with a very troubled and anxious heart. I didnt write to make you sad but so you would know the overwhelming love that I have for you.
local: Saŋa maŋa, tɔɔrɔɔ lala yɛ kɔkɔlɔ nyita yɛ ansikyu nɩɩ n taa kyʋrɔɔ fanɛ ɔwʋlɛ mʋ. Man gyɛ yɛ n biti fɛɛ n nyita fanɛ nkɔlɔ nɩn. Amaa n biti yɛ fan bɩɩ mi kebiti lala mʋ nɩɩ n sʋ sa fanɛ mʋ.

eng: But if someone has made anyone sad, that person hasnt hurt me but all of you to some degree .
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan nyita ɔsa kʋ kɔkɔlɔ mʋ, man gyɛ mɛ kɔkɔlɔ nɩɩ o nyita, ɔkpa kʋ sʋ mʋ fanɛ kpini nkɔlɔ. Nɩɩ n mee biti n maa tɔwɛ asɩn mʋ kyʋn sʋ.

eng: The punishment handed out by the majority is enough for this person.
local: Kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ lɩɩ fanɛ gaalaagaa asɛ ba mʋ sʋ mʋ, kʋkwɛ mʋ.

eng: This is why you should try your best to forgive and to comfort this person now instead, so that this person isnt overwhelmed by too much sorrow.
local: Nbɩɩnbɩɩ mʋ, fan taa mu alibi kɛɛ mʋ, kɛ fan tɔwɛ yuula mʋ kɔkɔlɔ, kɛ mʋ kɔkɔlɔ o man nyita mʋ kyʋn sʋ.

eng: So I encourage you to show your love for this person.
local: N kʋlɛ fanɛ nɩn, fan yɛgɛ ɔ bɩɩ fɛɛ fan san fan sʋ kebiti sa mʋ.

eng: This is another reason why I wrote you. I wanted to test you and see if you are obedient in everything.
local: Kʋtɔ mʋ sʋ nɩɩ n daa kyʋrɔɔ fanɛ ɔwʋlɛ maŋa ɩ gyɛ yɛ, n waa fanɛ nɩn kɩɩ, kɛ n wu kanan mʋ nɩɩ fen nu fɛn sa mɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa tɔ.

eng: If you forgive anyone for anything, I do too. And whatever Ive forgiven , I did it for you in the presence of Christ.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan waa alibi, nɩɩ fan taa kɛɛ mʋ mʋ, ngbaa mɛ n taa kɛɛ mʋ. Nɩɩ kʋtɔ kʋ kɩ kyɩna kɩ bʋ tɔ nɩɩ i tiri fɛɛ n taa kɛɛ gbaa mʋ, n waa kanɩn Krisito ansi tɔ lɩɩ fanɛ sʋ.

eng: This is so that we wont be taken advantage of by Satan, because we are well aware of his schemes.
local: Saamʋ Sɩtaanɛ mɛɛ nyɛ ɔkpa taalɛ anɛ tɔ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ an nyi mʋ ɩkpa golon‐golon mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ ɔ naa mʋ.

eng: When I came to Troas to preach Christs gospel, the Lord gave me an opportunity to preach.
local: N maa kpee Trowa kadɛ tɔ, naa tɔwɛ Krisito asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ, n wu fɛɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ bugi ɔkpa sa mɛ.

eng: But I was worried because I couldnt find my brother Titus there. So I said good-bye to them and went on to Macedonia.
local: Amaa mɛ nfɛɛrɛ ɩ man dɛ mɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ n man wu mɛ bɩrɩsa Titusi tʋtɔ. Ɩmʋ sʋ n kala bamʋ, nɩɩ n yii sʋ kpee Masedoniya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ.

eng: But thank God, who is always leading us around through Christ as if we were in a parade. He releases the fragrance of the knowledge of him everywhere through us.
local: Amaa an fwaala Ɩbwaarɛ. Lɩɩ a maa waa kʋkʋlʋn yɛ Krisito sʋ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ gyankpaa anɛ saŋa kʋmaa fɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba kɔ nyɛ sa Krisito. Nɩɩ ɔ bɔla anɛ sʋ yaasɛ Krisito kinyi mʋ kpee katɩn kʋmaa fɛɛ tuulaale liifensa.

eng: We smell like the aroma of Christs offering to God, both to those who are being saved and to those who are on the road to destruction.
local: An du nɩn fɛɛ atɔ liifensa mʋ nɩɩ Krisito taa lɛɛ saraga sa Ɩbwaarɛ. Nɩɩ ɩɩ yaasɛ loo bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ nyɛ kamɔlɩgɛ yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ fɔyɩ mʋ tɔ.

eng: We smell like a contagious dead person to those who are dying, but we smell like the fountain of life to those who are being saved. Who is qualified for this kind of ministry?.
local: Bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ fɔyɩ asɛ mʋ, Krisito kinyi mʋ gyɛ ɔsɔn mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ bɩya lewu, nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ nyɛ kamɔlɩgɛ mʋ, kɩ gyɛ ofen mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ bɩya nkpa. Nɩɩ anɩmʋ ɩ dagaa kusun nɩmʋ kɩwaa?.

eng: We arent like so many people who hustle the word of God to make a profit. We are speaking through Christ in the presence of God, as those who are sincere and as those who are sent from God.
local: A man du fɛɛ asa gaalaagaa mʋ nɩɩ ba man sʋ bɛɛrɛɛ sa asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ, nɩɩ bɛɛ sʋ bee gyii yawʋ bee biti tɔnɔ. Amaa lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ maa sun anɛ sʋ mʋ, an taa kɔkɔlɔ fuuli tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ fɛɛ Krisito ayaafɔlɛ.

eng: Are we starting to commend ourselves again? We dont need letters of introduction to you or from you like other people, do we?.
local: An bɩla an biti a lɛɛ anɛ n‐yɩɩ kaapʋ fanɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ an du nɩn abɛɛ? Abɛɛ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ anɛ gbaa anɛ, a nyɛ ɔwʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ lɛɛ anɛ ɩɩ kaapʋ kanan mʋ nɩɩ an du bɩya fanɛ, abɛɛ a nyɛ lɩɩ fanɛ asɛ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ asa akʋ bɛɛ waa mʋ?.

eng: You are our letter, written on our hearts, known and read by everyone.
local: Fanɛ gbaa‐gbaa fanɛ i du fɛɛ ɔwʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ lɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ an du mʋ kɩɩ kaapʋ, nɩɩ Kayaayu mʋ kyʋrɔɔ gyaga fanɛ nkɔlɔ sʋ, kɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ o wu kɛ ɔ bɩɩ anɛ‐daa.

eng: You show that you are Christs letter, delivered by us. You werent written with ink but with the Spirit of the living God. You werent written on tablets of stone but on tablets of human hearts.
local: Kesintin tɔ mʋ, fanɛ kɩbaabʋtɔ kɩɩ lɛɛ kɩɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ, lɩɩ anɛ kusun sʋ mʋ, fan gyɛ ɔwʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ Krisito kyʋrɔɔ. Nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ inki nɩɩ ba taa kyʋrɔɔ ɔwʋlɛ mʋ, amaa Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ nkpa wuya mʋ Kayaayu nɩɩ ba taa kyʋrɔɔ kɩmʋ. Nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ abuu patɩla‐patɩla sʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ gyaga, amaa nyɩmɩsa kɔkɔlɔ sʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ gyaga.

eng: This is the confidence that we have through Christ in the presence of God.
local: A maa taalɛ tɔwɛ ɩnɩmʋ bɔla Krisito sʋ mʋ, an taa anɛ n‐yɩɩ tʋʋ Ɩbwaarɛ sʋ.

eng: It isnt that we ourselves are qualified to claim that anything came from us. No, our qualification is from God.
local: A mɛɛ tɔwɛ nɩn yɛ anɛ gbaa‐gbaa anɛ ɔlʋn nɩɩ an sʋ an waa kusun mʋ, amaa Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ an taalɛ waa kusun mʋ.

eng: He has qualified us as ministers of a new covenant, not based on what is written but on the Spirit, because what is written kills, but the Spirit gives life.
local: Mʋ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ an dagaa mʋ katɔwɛ kyula pʋpwɛ mʋ kusun asunpu. Nɩɩ kɩ man gyɛ Nbara kyʋrɔɔsa lɛɛ, amaa kɩ gyɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ lɛɛ. Nbara kyʋrɔɔsa mʋ ɩɩ bɩya lewu nɩn, amaa Kayaayu mʋ kɩɩ sa nkpa nɩn.

eng: The ministry that brought death was carved in letters on stone tablets. It came with such glory that the Israelites couldnt look for long at Moses face because his face was shining with glory, even though it was a fading glory.
local: Nbara mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ kyʋrɔɔ gyaga abuu patɩla‐patɩla sʋ sa Mosesi. Saŋa maa mʋ, Nbara mʋ ba yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ bʋnyaa ŋalɩgɛsa, nɩɩ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi ba man taalɛ kɩɩ mu ansi tɔ, lɩɩ fɛɛ mu ansi sʋ ɩɩ ŋalɩgɛ gaa, halɩɩ ɩ maa kpa sʋ gbaa mʋ. Nɩɩ Nbara mʋ nɩɩ ɩmʋ kusun bɩya lewu, nɩɩ kɩ sʋ falɛ bʋnyaa mʋ.

eng: Wont the ministry of the Spirit be much more glorious?.
local: mɩnɛ ɩ waa sʋ nɩɩ Kayaayu mʋ kusun mɛɛ yɛ ɔ baa ɔ sʋ bʋnyaa kʋkyɔ?.

eng: If the ministry that brought condemnation has glory, how much more glorious is the ministry that brings righteousness?.
local: Nɩɩ kusun mʋ nɩɩ kii bun nyɩmɩsa kʋpwɛ, kɩ kan kɩ sʋ bʋnyaa mʋ, mɩnɛ ɩ waa sʋ nɩɩ kusun mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ bɩya ɩkpa kikyiigi sa asa mʋ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ bɩya bʋnyaa kʋkyɔ?.

eng: In fact, what was glorious isnt glorious now, because of the glory that is brighter.
local: Nɩɩ an taa nbɩɩnbɩɩ bʋnyaa kparɛ mʋ kaala yɛ bʋnyaa gyankpaasa mʋ, bʋnyaa gyankpaasa mʋ man gyɛ sɛhn.

eng: If the glory that fades away was glorious, how much more glorious is the one that lasts!.
local: Nɩɩ kɩmʋ nɩɩ kɩ ba nɩɩ kɩ man kyɛɛrɛɛ mʋ kɩ ba yɛ bʋnyaa mʋ, mɩnɛ ɩ waa sʋ nɩɩ kɩmʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ tɔ kakpaa mɛɛ yɛ kɩ baa kɩ sʋ bʋnyaa kɩkyɔ?.

eng: So, since we have such a hope, we act with great confidence.
local: Kanɩn sʋ, a maa sʋ falɛ tamaa sʋ mʋ, an yɩlɛ kɩnyɩn tɔ anɛ atɔwɛsa tɔ.

eng: We arent like Moses, who used to put a veil over his face so that the Israelites couldnt watch the end of what was fading away.
local: A man du fɛɛ Mosesi mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa kɩtaa‐bi bun mu ansi sʋ, kɛ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi ba man nyɛ wu kanan mʋ nɩɩ bʋnyaa mʋ ɩɩ kpa sʋ mu ansi sʋ mʋ.

eng: But their minds were closed. Right up to the present day the same veil remains when the old covenant is read. The veil is not removed because it is taken away by Christ.
local: Amaa bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ ɩ man nu asɩn mʋ kasɛ. Ba lɩɩ halɩɩ ndaga nɩɩ bɛɛ kalɛ katɔwɛ kyula dɩdaa ɩwʋlɛ mʋ, i du nɩn fɛɛ kʋtɔ kʋ kɩ san ki bun bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ sʋ, amɔɔ nyɩmɩsa waa kʋkʋlʋn yɛ Krisito, pɔyɩ nɩɩ kii bugi mʋ nfɛɛrɛ sʋ.

eng: Even today, whenever Moses is read, a veil lies over their hearts.
local: Halɩɩ ba lɩɩ ndaga, saŋa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ kalɛ Mosesi nbara ɔwʋlɛ mʋ, i du nɩn fɛɛ kʋtɔ bunsa mʋ san o bun bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ sʋ.

eng: But whenever someone turns back to the Lord, the veil is removed.
local: Amaa nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan biliŋi ba Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ asɛ mʋ, mʋ kʋtɔ bunsa mʋ kii bugi.

eng: The Lord is the Spirit, and where the Lords Spirit is, there is freedom.
local: Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ Kayaayu, nɩɩ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Kayaayu mʋ bʋ mʋ, n‐yɩɩ kɩnyɛ bʋ tʋtɔ.

eng: All of us are looking with unveiled faces at the glory of the Lord as if we were looking in a mirror. We are being transformed into that same image from one degree of glory to the next degree of glory. This comes from the Lord, who is the Spirit.
local: Nɩɩ anɛ kpini mʋ nɩɩ kɩtaa‐bi man bun anɛ nfɛɛrɛ sʋ mʋ, an lɛɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ bʋnyaa mʋ kaapʋ. Anɛ kɩbaabʋtɔ kii biliŋi waa fɛɛ mu keduli, nɩɩ saŋa kʋmaa an nyɛ mʋ bʋnyaa mʋ an tii sʋ, nɩɩ ɩnɩmʋ ɩ lɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Kayaayu mʋ asɛ.

eng: This is why we dont get discouraged, given that we received this ministry in the same way that we received Gods mercy.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ Ɩbwaarɛ maa wu anɛ kʋwɛɛ sʋ mʋ, ɔ taa asɩn danbɩrasa kɩtɔwɛ kusun nɩmʋ waa anɛ abaa tɔ, nɩɩ anɛ abaa mɛɛ yɛ ɩ yɔɔ.

eng: Instead, we reject secrecy and shameful actions. We dont use deception, and we dont tamper with Gods word. Instead, we commend ourselves to everyones conscience in the sight of God by the public announcement of the truth.
local: An kina ipeeli‐sɩn yɛ atɔ baala waasa, nɩɩ a mɛɛ taa kɔnɔ tɔ kɔn an pɩna asa, nɩɩ a mee biliŋaa Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn mʋ. Amaa anɛɛnɛ bɩrɛ, an tɔwɛ kesintin asɩn mʋ sakyɔ tɔ, kɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ ɔ bɩɩ mʋ nfɛɛrɛ tɔ kanan mʋ nɩɩ an du Ɩbwaarɛ ansi tɔ.

eng: And even if our gospel is veiled, it is veiled to those who are on the road to destruction.
local: Nɩɩ kʋtɔ kʋ kan ki bun anɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ nɩɩ an tɔwɛ falɛ gbaa mʋ, ii bun ɩɩ sa bamʋ nɩɩ bee muuri mʋ nkʋn nɩn.

eng: The god of this age has blinded the minds of those who dont have faith so they couldnt see the light of the gospel that reveals Christs glory. Christ is the image of God.
local: Bɛɛ mɛɛ kɔɔlɛ bee gyii lɩɩ fɛɛ Sɩtaanɛ mʋ nɩɩ ee gyii kuwura kasɛ sʋ mʋ, bun bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ sʋ, kɛ ba man taalɛ wu asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ fɩtɩla mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ Krisito bʋnyaa mʋ, nɩɩ mʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ keduli gbaa‐gbaa mʋ.

eng: We dont preach about ourselves. Instead, we preach about Jesus Christ as Lord, and we describe ourselves as your slaves for Jesus sake.
local: Man gyɛ anɛ gbaa‐gbaa anɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ asɩn nɩɩ an tɔwɛ, amaa Yesu Krisito n‐yɩɩ sʋ asɩn nɩɩ an tɔwɛ, mʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, nɩɩ lɩɩ mʋ sʋ mʋ an gyɛ fanɛ ayaafɔlɛ.

eng: God said that light should shine out of the darkness. He is the same one who shone in our hearts to give us the light of the knowledge of Gods glory in the face of Jesus Christ.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ yɛ, Ɩ ŋmaŋɛ kitentenbiri tɔ mʋ, mʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kʋlʋn maŋa ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ɩ ŋmaŋɛ anɛ nkɔlɔ tɔ, kɛ a nyɛ bɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bʋnyaa mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ ŋalɩgɛ Krisito ansi tɔ mʋ.

eng: But we have this treasure in clay pots so that the awesome power belongs to God and doesnt come from us.
local: Asɩn nɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ kapatɩyɛ kparɛ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ taa waa anɛ tɔ, amaa an du nɩn fɛɛ nlobi mʋ nɩɩ kapatɩyɛ kparɛ mʋ ɩ bʋ ɩmʋ tɔ mʋ, kɛ ɩɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ ɔlʋn kparɛ mʋ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ, man gyɛ anɛ asɛ.

eng: We are experiencing all kinds of trouble, but we arent crushed. We are confused, but we arent depressed.
local: Saŋa kʋmaa, ba nyan anɛ, amaa a man patɩyɛ, i wuliti anɛ tɔ, amaa anɛ abaa ɩ man yɔɔ.

eng: We are harassed, but we arent abandoned. We are knocked down, but we arent knocked out.
local: ba nyanla anɛ, amaa Ɩbwaarɛ man yɛgɛ nɩɩ ba da anɛ tʋʋ lewu‐lewu, amaa a man wu.

eng: We always carry Jesus death around in our bodies so that Jesus life can also be seen in our bodies.
local: Saŋa kʋmaa an sʋ Yesu kelewu mʋ anɛ n‐yɩɩ tɔ nɩn, kɛ Yesu nkpa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ mʋ kɩ nyɛ lɩɩ anɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ tɔ kaapʋ.

eng: We who are alive are always being handed over to death for Jesus sake so that Jesus life can also be seen in our bodies that are dying.
local: Kpalɩ fɛɛ anɛ mʋ nɩɩ an san an tɛ nkpa tɔ falɛ mʋ, lɩɩ Yesu sʋ mʋ saŋa kʋmaa an tɛ lewu kɔnɔ, kɛ mʋ nkpa mʋ ɩ nyɛ lɩɩ ifuli anɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ kii biti ki wu mʋ tɔ.

eng: So death is at work in us, but life is at work in you.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ ɩɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ an gyaŋa alewu‐sɩn, amaa ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ fen biti fɛn nyɛ nkpa kakpaa.

eng: We have the same faithful spirit as what is written in scripture: I had faith, and so I spoke. We also have faith, and so we also speak.
local: Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ mʋ, ɔkʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, N kɔɔlɛ gyii, ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ. Kanɩn kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii kʋlʋn maŋa nɩɩ anɛ gbaa anɛ an sʋ, ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ an tɔwɛ kanɩn.

eng: We do this because we know that the one who raised the Lord Jesus will also raise us with Jesus, and he will bring us into his presence along with you.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ an nyi fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ o kyiŋi Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu lɩɩ lewu tɔ ba nkpa tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ lɩɩ anaa Yesu kɩwaa kʋkʋlʋn sʋ mʋ ee biti o kyiŋi anɛ, kɛ ɔ taa anaa fanɛ bʋga kpee mʋ asɛ.

eng: All these things are for your benefit. As grace increases to benefit more and more people, it will cause gratitude to increase, which results in Gods glory.
local: Fanɛ sʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ waa ɩnɩmʋ kpini kanɩn, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ mʋ kabwaala mʋ kɩ kyɔ, nɩɩ asa gaalaagaa bɛɛ nyɛ kɩmʋ mʋ, ɩɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ bɛɛ fwaala Ɩbwaarɛ nɩɩ bɛɛ bʋnyaa mʋ.

eng: So we arent depressed. But even if our bodies are breaking down on the outside, the person that we are on the inside is being renewed every day.
local: Kpalɩ ɩnɩmʋ sʋ mʋ, anɛ abaa ɩ mɛɛ yɔɔ. Halɩɩ anɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ kɩ maa pɔɔ kii tii sʋ gbaa mʋ, anɛ kakyɩna pʋpwɛ mʋ ɩɩ nyɛ ɔlʋn ii tii sʋ kakɛ kʋmaa.

eng: Our temporary minor problems are producing an eternal stockpile of glory for us that is beyond all comparison.
local: Nɩɩ anɛ awʋrʋfɔ wuribi mʋ nɩɩ an gyii nɩɩ ɩ mɛɛ kyɛɛrɛɛ falɛ mʋ, ɩɩ bɩya anɛ bʋnyaa lala mʋ nɩɩ ɩ man sʋ ɔkaa, nɩɩ ɩ kyɔ awʋrʋfɔ mʋ nɩɩ an gyii falɛ gaa mʋ.

eng: We dont focus on the things that can be seen but on the things that cant be seen. The things that can be seen dont last, but the things that cant be seen are eternal.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ a man sʋ anɛ ansi an gyaga atɔ mʋ nɩɩ nyɩmɩsa sʋ mu ansi ee wu mʋ sʋ, amaa ɩmʋ nɩɩ nyɩmɩsa mee wu yɛ ansi mʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ nyɩmɩsa sʋ mu ansi ee wu mʋ mɛɛ kyɛɛrɛɛ, amaa ɩmʋ nɩɩ bee mɛɛ wu yɛ ansi mʋ bɩrɛ ɩ bʋ tɔ nɩn kakpaa.

eng: We know that if the tent that we live in on earth is torn down, we have a building from God. Its a house that isnt handmade, which is eternal and located in heaven.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ an dan an nyi fɛɛ anɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ ki du fɛɛ tɛntɩ abu mʋ nɩɩ anɛ kala ana ba tɛ ɩmʋ tɔ kasɛ sʋ nfɩɩ ɩ kan wu mʋ, an biti an nyɛ ibu kyɩna tɔ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ, ibu mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ tɔ kakpaa sʋsʋ, nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ nyɩmɩsa kɩbaa nɩɩ ba taa pɔyɩ.

eng: We groan while we live in this residence. We really want to dress ourselves with our building from heaven.
local: Lɩɩ falɛ sʋ an yɩyɛɛ nɩɩ an kpaala, an biti fɛɛ naafɔɔ Ɩbwaarɛ o bun anɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ mʋ sʋ fɛɛ ɩkaalɛ.

eng: since we assume that when we take off this tent, we wont find out that we are naked.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ nɩɩ ɩ kan bun anɛ sʋ mʋ, ɩ mɛɛ bɩla i du fɛɛ an naa kibulunkpan.

eng: Yes, while we are in this tent we groan, because we are weighed down. We want to be dressed not undressed, so that what is dying can be swallowed up by life.
local: A maa bʋ tɛntɩ kubuu mʋ tɔ kasɛ sʋ nfɩɩ mʋ, an yɩyɛɛ fɛɛ kasʋla maa nyɩyɛ anɛ, man gyɛ fɛɛ an biti nɩn fɛɛ a lɩɩ kasɛ sʋ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ mʋ tɔ, amaa an biti fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ o bun anɛ kɩmʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ sʋsʋ lɛɛ mʋ, kɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ ii wu mʋ ɩ kyɛɛgɛ waa ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ sʋ nkpa kakpaa.

eng: Now the one who prepared us for this very thing is God, and God gave us the Spirit as a down payment for our home.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɔŋɔ anɛ yɩla sa falɛ kakyɛɛgɛ nɩmʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ Kayaayu mʋ sa anɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa yɩla kasɛ sa atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa yɩla yɛ ee biti ɔ sa anɛ mʋ.

eng: So we are always confident, because we know that while we are living in the body, we are away from our home with the Lord.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ an nyɛ nkɔlɔ yɩlɛ tɔ saŋa kʋmaa. Nɩɩ an nyi fɛɛ a maa san an tɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ tɔ nfɩɩ mʋ, ɩɩ kaapʋ nɩn fɛɛ an bʋ kata yɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu.

eng: We live by faith and not by sight.
