local: Anɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ Yesu gyii mʋ nɩɩ an sʋ an naa mʋ, man gyɛ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ an wu yɛ anɛ ansi.

eng: We are confident, and we would prefer to leave the body and to be at home with the Lord.
local: An yɩlɛ tɔ yɛ tamaa. Nɩɩ an biti yɛ an lɩɩ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ nɩmʋ tɔ kɛ a naa kyɩna anɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Yesu mʋ asɛ.

eng: So our goal is to be acceptable to him, whether we are at home or away from home.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ, nɩɩ an san an tɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ tɔ kasɛ sʋ nɩn‐oo, abɛɛ an lɩɩ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ tɔ nɩn‐oo, kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ anɛ ansi ɩ payɩ sʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ a waa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ bɔla Yesu ansi.

eng: We all must appear before Christ in court so that each person can be paid back for the things that were done while in the body, whether they were good or bad.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɩ dan ɩ dɛ nɩn yɛ, anɛ kpini an biti an naa yɩlɛ Krisito kuwura‐gyii asɩn ogyikpa, kɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ ɔ nyɛ katalɛ lɩɩ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ tɔ mʋ. Alibi nɩn‐oo, adanbɩrasa nɩn‐oo, ee biti ɔ nyɛ kʋkɔka mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa mʋ.

eng: So we try to persuade people, since we know what it means to fear the Lord. We are well known by God, and I hope that in your heart we are well known by you as well.
local: Fɛɛ a maa nyi kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kasɩlɛ mʋ, an kpan anɛ n‐yɩɩ an tɔwɛ an pʋpɔlɛ asa kɛ baa kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii. Ɩbwaarɛ i nyi anɛ danbɩrasa, asa mʋ nɩɩ an gyɛ, nɩɩ n tamaa fɛɛ fanɛ gbaa fanɛ fen nyi anɛ fanɛ nfɛɛrɛ tɔ.

eng: We arent trying to commend ourselves to you again. Instead, we are giving you an opportunity to be proud of us so that you could answer those who take pride in superficial appearance, and not in what is in the heart.
local: Man gyɛ yɛ an bɩla an biti a kaapʋ fanɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ an du, amaa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ an waa mʋ ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ an sa fanɛ ɔkpa, kɛ fan nyɛ kaapʋ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ lɩɩ anɛ sʋ, kɛ fan taalɛ lɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ kaapʋ bamʋ n‐yɩɩ mʋ kɔnɔ, lɩɩ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ bee wu yɛ ansi mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ lɩɩ asa nkɔlɔ tɔ.

eng: If we are crazy, its for Gods sake. If we are rational, its for your sake.
local: Nɩɩ anɛ katɔwɛ nɩmʋ ɩ kan ɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ anɛ amu ii nyita mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ sʋ nɩɩ an du kanɩn. Amaa nɩɩ anɛ amu tɔ ɩ kan i du kyɛɛkyɛɛ bɩrɛ, fanɛ sʋ nɩɩ i du kanɩn.

eng: The love of Christ controls us, because we have concluded this: one died for the sake of all; therefore, all died.
local: Krisito kebiti mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ sa anɛ mʋ, gyii anɛ amu. Lɩɩ fɛɛ an kɔɔlɛ gyii fɛɛ ɔsa kʋlʋn wu sa ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ, ɩmʋ sʋ i du nɩn fɛɛ anɛ gbaa anɛ an maa wu tii mʋ sʋ mʋ.

eng: He died for the sake of all so that those who are alive should live not for themselves but for the one who died for them and was raised.
local: O wu sa asa kpini, saamʋ bamʋ nɩɩ ba tɛ nkpa tɔ mʋ ba man bɩla ba tɛ sa bamʋ n‐yɩɩ, amaa ba tɛ sa ɔmʋ nɩɩ o wu, nɩɩ o bwii kyiŋi ba nkpa tɔ sa bamʋ mʋ.

eng: So then, from this point on we wont recognize people by human standards. Even though we used to know Christ by human standards, that isnt how we know him now.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ nbɩɩnbɩɩ man gyɛ nyɩmɩsa ɩkpa sʋ nɩɩ an sʋ an kaala kanan mʋ nɩɩ nyɩmɩsa du. Saŋa kʋ tɔ mʋ kanɩn nɩɩ an daa kaala Krisito nɩn, amaa nbɩɩnbɩɩ bɩrɛ a man bɩla an kaala mʋ kanɩn.

eng: So then, if anyone is in Christ, that person is part of the new creation. The old things have gone away, and look, new things have arrived!.
local: Kanɩn sʋ, nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan ba ɔ gyɛ Krisito lɛɛ mʋ, o biliŋi nyɩmɩsa pʋpwɛ. Kakyɩna dɩdaa mʋ kɩ kyʋn, nɩɩ kakyɩna pʋpwɛ kɩ ba.

eng: All of these new things are from God, who reconciled us to himself through Christ and who gave us the ministry of reconciliation.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ kpini ɩ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ nɩn, mʋ ɩ bɔla Krisito sʋ lɔŋɔ ana mʋ nsana, nɩɩ ɔ taa maa asa nsana kɔlɔŋɔ kusun mʋ waa anɛ abaa tɔ.

eng: In other words, God was reconciling the world to himself through Christ, by not counting peoples sins against them. He has trusted us with this message of reconciliation.
local: Ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ bɔla Krisito sʋ ɛɛ lɔŋɔ maa dulinyaa asa nsana, yɛgɛ ɔ mɛɛ kɩɩ asa alibi mʋ nɩɩ ba waa sʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ taa maa asa nsana kɔlɔŋɔ asɩn mʋ waa anɛ abaa tɔ yɛ an tɔwɛ.

eng: So we are ambassadors who represent Christ. God is negotiating with you through us. We beg you as Christs representatives, Be reconciled to God.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ an gyɛ Krisito ayaa tɔ ayɩlɛpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba sun fanɛ asɛ, nɩɩ i du fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩɩ bɔla anɛ sʋ nɩn ɛɛ kʋlɛ fanɛ. Ɩmʋ sʋ an yɩlɛ Krisito ayaa tɔ an kʋlɛ fanɛ yɛ fanaa Ɩbwaarɛ nsana ɩ lɔŋɔ.

eng: God caused the one who didnt know sin to be sin for our sake so that through him we could become the righteousness of God.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ yɛgɛ Krisito mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man waa alibi kɩɩ mʋ, ba waa anɛ alibi ɔsʋlapʋ, nɩɩ lɩɩ mʋ sʋ mʋ kɛ anɛ ɩkpa i kyiigi Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ.

eng: Since we work together with him, we are also begging you not to receive the grace of God in vain.
local: Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ maa anɛ an sun mu kusun mʋ, an kʋlɛ fanɛ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kabwaala mʋ nɩɩ fan nyɛ mʋ, fan man yɛgɛ ɩ waa kɩyan.

eng: He says, I listened to you at the right time, and I helped you on the day of salvation. Look, now is the right time! Look, now is the day of salvation!.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Mɛ saŋa mʋ kyɛɛkyɛɛ tɔ maa fʋʋ mʋ, n nu fanɛ asɛ, nɩɩ mɛ kakɛ mɔlɩgɛsa maa fʋʋ mʋ, n kyɛ fanɛ tɔ. Nɩɩ mɛ, Pɔɔlɩ n tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, nbɩɩnbɩɩ saŋa nɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ saŋa kyɛɛkyɛɛ tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ndaga kakɛ kanɩmʋ kɩ gyɛ kamɔlɩgɛ kakɛ mʋ.

eng: We dont give anyone any reason to be offended about anything so that our ministry wont be criticized.
local: A maa mee biti ɔkʋ o wu asɩn libi ɩkʋ anɛ kusun mʋ tɔ sʋ mʋ, a mee biti a maa waa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kii biti ki tii ɔkʋ ɔkpa.

eng: Instead, we commend ourselves as ministers of God in every way. We did this with our great endurance through problems, disasters, and stressful situations.
local: Amaa kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ an waa mʋ, an waa nɩn an kaapʋ fɛɛ an gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ayaafɔlɛ. An nyɛ kenyiita yɩlɛ awʋrʋfɔ tɔ, yɛ kayɩɩ kɩtaa tɔ, yɛ tɔɔrɔɔ tɔ.

eng: We went through beatings, imprisonments, and riots. We experienced hard work, sleepless nights, and hunger.
local: Asa ba dayɩ anɛ, nɩɩ ba taa anɛ tii ibu, nɩɩ ba bɩɩtɛ nin anɛ sakyɔ tɔ. Nɩɩ an waa kusun ɔlʋn sʋ, nɩɩ a mɛɛ nyɛ an dɛ nsɩɩrɛ, nɩɩ an naa yɛ akʋn.

eng: We displayed purity, knowledge, patience, and generosity. We served with the Holy Spirit, genuine love.
local: yɛgɛ anɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ ɩ maa man sʋ ineesi ɩkʋ, nɩɩ an sʋ kanyaasɩn, yɛ kenyiita, yɛ kɩdanbɩrasa kɩwaa, yɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ ɔlʋn yɛ kebiti abaa gaa.

eng: telling the truth, and Gods power. We carried the weapons of righteousness in our right hand and our left hand.
local: An tɔwɛ kesintin asɩn mʋ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔlʋn mʋ waa kusun anɛ tɔ. An sʋ ɩkpa kyiigisa fɛɛ adʋntɔ, kɛ a taa kun kɛ a taa kɔ.

eng: We were treated with honor and dishonor and with verbal abuse and good evaluation. We were seen as both fake and real.
local: Asa akʋ ba sa anɛ bɛɛrɛɛ, nɩɩ akʋ mɔɔ ba ŋaabaa anɛ, akʋ ba saalɛ anɛ, nɩɩ akʋ ba yɩn anɛ. Ba kɩɩ anɛ fɛɛ asa apɩnapʋ, yɛgɛ anɛɛnɛ an gyɛ kesintin wuya ana.

eng: as unknown and well known, as dying—and look, we are alive! We were seen as punished but not killed.
local: Ba waa fɛɛ ba man bɩɩ anɛ, yɛgɛ katɩn kʋmaa asa be nyi anɛ, ba kɩɩ anɛ fɛɛ a maa yɛ a wu, amaa yɛgɛ an san an tɛ nkpa tɔ, ba bɩɩtɛ anɛ asʋ, yɛgɛ a man wu.

eng: as going through pain but always happy, as poor but making many rich, and as having nothing but owning everything.
local: Ba kɩɩ anɛ fɛɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ bamʋ asɩn ɩ sʋ kʋwɛɛ, yɛgɛ saŋa kʋmaa anɛ ansi i gyii, ba kɩɩ anɛ atiripu, amaa yɛgɛ an waa asa gaalaagaa atɔ wuya ana, ba kɩɩ fɛɛ a man sʋ sɛhn yɛgɛ anɛ ɩ sʋ kʋtɔ kʋmaa.

eng: Corinthians, we have spoken openly to you, and our hearts are wide open.
local: Oo Korinto abi, an tɔwɛ tigi fanɛ, nɩɩ an bugi anɛ nkɔlɔ tɔ sa fanɛ.

eng: There are no limits to the affection that we feel for you. You are the ones who placed boundaries on your affection for us.
local: Anɛɛnɛ bɩrɛ, an man kɩtaa anɛ n‐yɩɩ yɛ fanɛ, amaa fanɛ ɩ kɩtaa fanɛ n‐yɩɩ yɛ anɛ.

eng: But as a fair trade—Im talking to you like you are children—open your hearts wide too.
local: Mɛ Pɔɔlɩ, n tɔwɛ n sa fanɛ fɛɛ mi‐bii ana. Fen bugi fanɛ nkɔlɔ tɔ sa anɛ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ an bugi anɛ lɛɛ sa fanɛ mʋ.

eng: Dont be tied up as equal partners with people who dont believe. What does righteousness share with that which is outside the Law? What relationship does light have with darkness?.
local: Fan man waa kʋkʋlʋn yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ. Kpalɩ fɛɛ, mɩnɛ ɩ bʋ ɩkpa kikyiigi yɛ nbara kikina nsana? Abɛɛ nɛnɛ nɩɩ kitentenbiri maa ɩŋmaŋɛsa ɩ nyɛ waa kʋlʋn?.

eng: What harmony does Christ have with Satan? What does a believer have in common with someone who doesnt believe?.
local: Nɛnɛ sʋ nɩɩ Krisito maa Sɩtaanɛ ba taalɛ waa kɔnɔ kʋlʋn? Mɩnɛ ɩ bʋ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ gyii yɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ man kɔɔlɛ gyii nsana?.

eng: What agreement can there be between Gods temple and idols? Because we are the temple of the living God. Just as God said, I live with them, and I will move among them. I will be their God, and they will be my people. .
local: Mɩnɛ kɩtɔwɛ kyula ɩ bʋ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ yɛ ɩdakpa nsana? Lɩɩ fɛɛ anɛ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛ nkpa tɔ mʋ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ. Mʋ ɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, N biti n kyɩna bamʋ tɔ, kɛ n natɛ bamʋ tɔ, n biti n baa n gyɛ bamʋ Ɩbwaarɛ, kɛ ba baa ba gyɛ mɛ asa.

eng: Therefore, come out from among them and be separated, says the Lord. Dont touch what is unclean. Then I will welcome you. .
local: Ɩnɩmʋ sʋ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ bɩla tɔwɛ yɛ, Fan tɩn fanɛ n‐yɩɩ lɩɩ bamʋ asɛ, kɛ fan barɩgɛ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ lɩɩ bamʋ tɔ. Fan man taa fanɛ n‐yɩɩ mata ineesi kʋtɔ kʋ, saamʋ kɛ n kɔɔlɛ fanɛ.

eng: I will be a father to you, and you will be my sons and daughters, says the Lord Almighty .
local: N biti n baa n gyɛ fanɛ‐Sɛ, nɩɩ fanɛ ii biti fan baa fan gyɛ mi‐bii ana anyɩnsa yɛ akyɩɩsa, mɛ, Ɔlʋnpʋ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ, ɩ tɔwɛ kanɩn.

eng: My dear friends, since we have these promises, lets cleanse ourselves from anything that contaminates our body or spirit so that we make our holiness complete in the fear of God.
local: Mɛ‐bɩrɩsa ana, Ɩbwaarɛ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ lɩɩ falɛ atɔ nɩmʋ kpini sʋ sa anɛ. Kanɩn sʋ fan yɛgɛ a fwɩɩ anɛ n‐yɩɩ lɩɩ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ waa kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ yɛ kayaayu ineesi mʋ fɔyɩ. Fan yɛgɛ a sɩlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kɛ a payɩ ansi waa anɛ n‐yɩɩ kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ yɩla sa mʋ.

eng: Make room in your hearts for us. We didnt do anything wrong to anyone. We didnt ruin anyone. We didnt take advantage of anyone.
local: Fan bugi fanɛ nkɔlɔ tɔ sa anɛ. Nɩɩ an man waa ɔkʋ kilibi, nɩɩ an man nyita ɔkʋ, a man sisii ɔkʋ.

eng: Im not saying this to make you feel guilty. Ive already said that you are in our hearts so that we die and live together with you.
local: N mɛɛ tɔwɛ ɩnɩmʋ n bun fanɛ kʋpwɛ, n wʋla tɔwɛ fanɛ dɩdaa yɛ fan bʋ anɛ nkɔlɔ tɔ, ɩmʋ sʋ an tɛ nkpa tɔ nɩn oo, an wu nɩn oo, anaa fanɛ nɩn.

eng: I have every confidence in you. Im terribly proud of you. Im filled with encouragement. Im overwhelmed with happiness while in the middle of our problems.
local: N sʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii fanɛ tɔ, nɩɩ fanɛ sʋ mʋ n kaapʋ mɛ n‐yɩɩ, nɩɩ ɩ waa mɛ aniya anɛ tɔɔrɔɔ kpini tɔ, nɩɩ mi ansi i gyii naa kyʋn sʋ.

eng: Even after we arrived in Macedonia, we couldnt rest physically. We were surrounded by problems. There was external conflict, and there were internal fears.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ a maa ba Masedoniya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ mʋ, anɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ kɩ man nyɛ ɔfʋʋtɛ. Asa ba tɔɔrɔɔ anɛ, nɩɩ ba gyii anɛ ɩmɔɔrɛ katɩn kʋmaa nɩmʋ, nɩɩ kufuu kɩ kɩtaa anɛ.

eng: However, God comforts people who are discouraged, and he comforted us by Titus arrival.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ee yuula asa mʋ nɩɩ bamʋ abaa ɩɩ yɔɔ nkɔlɔ mʋ, mʋ maŋa bɔla Titusi mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ fanɛ asɛ ba mʋ sʋ yuula anɛ nkɔlɔ.

eng: We werent comforted only by his arrival but also by the comfort he had received from you. He told us about your desire to see me, how you were sorry, and about your concern for me, so that I was even happier.
local: Nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ mʋ kɩba mʋ nkʋn sʋ, amaa kanan mʋ nɩɩ fan yɛgɛ nɩɩ mʋ kɔkɔlɔ ki yuuli mʋ mʋ. Ɔ tɔwɛ anɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fɛn gyʋgyʋʋ fɛɛ fen nyɛ wu mɛ mʋ, yɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fan nu fanɛ n‐yɩɩ, yɛ fanɛ kebiti kparɛ mʋ nɩɩ fan sʋ sa mɛ mʋ. Lɩɩ ɩnɩmʋ kpini sʋ mʋ, ɩ waa mɛ ɔkɔn naa kyʋn sʋ.

eng: Even though my letter hurt you, I dont regret it. Well—I did regret it just a bit because I see that that letter made you sad, though only for a short time.
local: Halɩɩ mɛ ɔwʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ n kyʋrɔɔ sa fanɛ mʋ nyita fanɛ nkɔlɔ gbaa mʋ, n man nu mɛ n‐yɩɩ. Nɩɩ n daa bɩɩ fɛɛ ii biti ɩ kɔ fanɛ halɩɩ ɩ maa man kyɛɛrɛɛ gbaa mʋ, naafɔɔ n dɛ nu mɛ n‐yɩɩ.

eng: Now Im glad—not because you were sad but because you were made sad enough to change your hearts and lives. You felt godly sadness so that no one was harmed by us in any way.
local: Amaa nbɩɩnbɩɩ ɩ waa mɛ ɔkɔn, nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ yɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ i nyita fanɛ nkɔlɔ sʋ nɩɩ ɩ waa mɛ ɔkɔn, amaa fanɛ nkɔlɔ kinyita mʋ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ fan nyɛ nfɛɛrɛ kɩkyɛɛgɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ fanɛ nkɔlɔ kinyita mʋ gyɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ yɩla kɩmʋ kanɩn. Ɩmʋ sʋ an nyi fɛɛ a man waa fanɛ alibi akʋ.

eng: Godly sadness produces a changed heart and life that leads to salvation and leaves no regrets, but sorrow under the influence of the world produces death.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ kɔkɔlɔ nyita mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ yɩla kɩmʋ mʋ, ɩɩ bɩya nfɛɛrɛ kɩkyɛɛgɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ mɔlɩgɛ asa, nɩɩ kunu n‐yɩɩ man bʋ ɩmʋ tɔ, amaa dulinyaa tɔ kɔkɔlɔ nyita bɩrɛ ɩɩ bɩya lewu nɩn.

eng: Look at what this very experience of godly sadness has produced in you: such enthusiasm, what a desire to clear yourselves of blame, such indignation, what fear, what purpose, such concern, what justice! In everything you have shown yourselves to be innocent in the matter.
local: Fan kɩɩ kɔkɔlɔ nyita mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ yɩla mʋ, kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bɩya fanɛ. Ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ fan yɩlɛ tɔ lɛɛ alibi mʋ lɩɩ fanɛ tɔ, nɩɩ fan waa bilen lɛɛ fanɛ kɔnɔ, nɩɩ fan nyɛ agbʋ lala lɩɩ alibi ɔwaapʋ mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ fanɛ nkɔlɔ ɩ ŋmaa fanɛ, nɩɩ fen biti fɛɛ fan wu mɛ bilen, nɩɩ fanɛ ansi ɩ payɩ fɛɛ fan lɔŋɔ fanɛ nsana, nɩɩ fen biti fɛɛ fan bɩɩtɛ alibi ɔwaapʋ mʋ kʋsʋ. Ɔkpa kʋmaa nɩmʋ sʋ mʋ, fan lɛɛ kaapʋ fɛɛ alibi akʋ ɩ man maa fanɛ lɩɩ asɩn nɩmʋ tɔ.

eng: So although I wrote to you, it wasnt for the sake of the one who did wrong, or for the sake of the one who was wronged, but to show you your own enthusiasm for us in the sight of God.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ halɩɩ n maa kyʋrɔɔ ɔwʋlɛ mʋ sa fanɛ gbaa mʋ, man gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa alibi mʋ sʋ, abɛɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ba waa mʋ alibi mʋ sʋ, amaa n kyʋrɔɔ ɔwʋlɛ maa nɩn, kɛ ɩ yɛgɛ kɛ fanɛ gbaa‐gbaa fen wu kanan mʋ nɩɩ fanɛ nkɔlɔ ɩ gyan anɛ sʋ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ.

eng: Because of this we have been encouraged. And in addition to our own encouragement, we were even more pleased at how happy Titus was. His mind has been put at rest by all of you.
local: Lɩɩ ɩnɩmʋ sʋ mʋ ɩ waa anɛ aniya tɔ. Kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bɩla ki tii sʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, fan yɛgɛ nɩɩ anɛ ansi i gyii, lɩɩ Titusi kensi‐gyii mʋ sʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ fanɛ kpini fan yɛgɛ nɩɩ mʋ kɔkɔlɔ ki yuuli mʋ.

eng: If Ive bragged about you to him in any way, I havent been embarrassed. Instead, our bragging to Titus has also been proven to be true, just like everything we said to you was true.
local: Pɔyɩ kɛ ɔ ba fanɛ asɛ mʋ, n kaapʋ mɛ n‐yɩɩ fanɛ sʋ sa mʋ, nɩɩ fan man gyila mɛ ipeeli mʋ asɛ. Fɛɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ an tɔwɛ sa fanɛ maa gyɛ kesintin mʋ, kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ kanan mʋ nɩɩ an kaapʋ anɛ n‐yɩɩ fanɛ sʋ sa Titusi mʋ, ɩ waa kesintin.

eng: His devotion to you is growing even more as he remembers how all of you were obedient when you welcomed him with fear and trembling.
local: Mu kebiti mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ sa fanɛ mʋ lɔŋɔ tii sʋ, fɛɛ maa nyiŋi kanan mʋ nɩɩ fanɛ kpini fan nu mʋ asɛ, nɩɩ fan taa kufuu yɛ kɩkyɩkya kɔɔlɛ mʋ mʋ.

eng: Im happy, because I can completely depend on you.
local: Mɛɛmɛ bɩrɛ, ɩ waa mɛ ɔkɔn fɛɛ n maa taalɛ taa mɛ n‐yɩɩ tʋʋ fanɛ sʋ kʋtɔ kʋmaa tɔ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we want to let you know about the grace of God that was given to the churches of Macedonia.
local: Mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana, an biti fɛɛ fan bɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabwaala mʋ waa Krisito kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ Masedoniya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ mʋ tɔ.

eng: While they were being tested by many problems, their extra amount of happiness and their extreme poverty resulted in a surplus of rich generosity.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ bee gyii awʋrʋfɔ, nɩɩ asɩn wuribi ii tu bamʋ, nɩɩ halɩɩ ba maa bamʋ kitiri gbaa mʋ, lɩɩ kensi‐gyii lala mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ bamʋ tɔ sʋ mʋ, bee bugi bamʋ nkɔlɔ tɔ bɛɛ kɛɛ .

eng: I assure you that they gave what they could afford and even more than they could afford, and they did it voluntarily.
local: N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin yɛ ba kɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ taalɛ kɛɛ, halɩɩ naa kyʋn bamʋ ɔlʋn sʋ. Nɩɩ lɩɩ bamʋ gbaa‐gbaa bamʋ kɩpɩrɛ sʋ mʋ.

eng: They urgently begged us for the privilege of sharing in this service for the saints.
local: ba payɩ ansi kʋlɛ anɛ fɛɛ bamʋ gbaa bamʋ ba nyɛ ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ ba taalɛ taa kyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Gyudeya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ mʋ tɔ.

eng: They even exceeded our expectations, because they gave themselves to the Lord first and to us, consistent with Gods will.
local: Nɩɩ ba waa kyʋn sʋ kanan mʋ nɩɩ an waa tamaa yɛ bɛ taalɛ mʋ, amaa ba gyankpaa taa bamʋ n‐yɩɩ naa sa Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu, nɩɩ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kebiti tɔ sʋ mʋ, ba taa bamʋ n‐yɩɩ sa anɛ gbaa anɛ.

eng: As a result, we challenged Titus to finish this work of grace with you the way he had started it.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ an kʋlɛ Titusi mʋ nɩɩ ɔ wʋla piili kɛkɛɛ kusun kɩnɩmʋ kasɛ mʋ, fɛɛ ɔ kyaga sʋ waa fanɛ aniya tɔ, kɛ fan tiisi waa fanɛ kɛkɛɛ kusun mʋ naa lʋwɛ.

eng: Be the best in this work of grace in the same way that you are the best in everything, such as faith, speech, knowledge, total commitment, and the love we inspired in you.
local: N man wu kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ fan man waa kyʋn sʋ. Lɩɩ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii tɔ nɩn oo, katɔwɛ tɔ nɩn oo, kinyi tɔ nɩn oo, kɩ payɩ ansi kyɛ tɔ nɩn oo, kebiti mʋ nɩɩ fan sʋ sa anɛ nɩn oo, fan waa ɩmʋ kpini naa kyʋn sʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ an biti fɛɛ fan waa kyʋn sʋ lɩɩ fanɛ kɛkɛɛ kusun nɩmʋ gbaa tɔ.

eng: Im not giving an order, but by mentioning the commitment of others, Im trying to prove the authenticity of your love also.
local: Man gyɛ yɛ n yɩla kɔnɔsa kʋ n sa fanɛ nɩn, amaa n biti fɛɛ n nyɛ taa fanɛ kebiti mʋ taa kaala yɛ akʋ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ payɩ ansi bɛɛ waa kanɩn kɩdanbɩrasa mʋ lɛɛ, saamʋ n nyɛ wu kanan mʋ nɩɩ fanɛ kebiti mʋ gyɛ kesintin.

eng: You know the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ. Although he was rich, he became poor for your sakes, so that you could become rich through his poverty.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ fen nyi anɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Yesu Krisito kabwaala mʋ, fɛɛ maa gyɛ atɔ wuya gbaa mʋ, kpalɩ fanɛ sʋ mʋ o be biliŋi otiripu, kɛ lɩɩ mʋ kitiri mʋ sʋ mʋ, fan ba biliŋi atɔ wuya ana.

eng: Im giving you my opinion about this. Its to your advantage to do this, since you not only started to do it last year but you wanted to do it too.
local: Mɛ nfɛɛrɛ lɩɩ asɩn nɩmʋ sʋ sa fanɛ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, ɩ bʋrɔn fɛɛ fan maa lʋwɛ kusun mʋ nɩɩ fan piili sun‐tɔ mʋ. Fanɛ ɩ gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba gyankpaa ba sa nkɛɛ mʋ, nɩɩ fɛn bɩla fɛn sa lɩɩ fanɛ kɩpɩrɛ tɔ.

eng: Now finish the job as well so that you finish it with as much enthusiasm as you started, given what you can afford.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fen yii kusun mʋ kasɛ mʋ, fan lʋwɛ kɩmʋ. Fan payɩ ansi waa kusun mʋ naa lʋwɛ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fan payɩ ansi ŋmina yɩla mʋ, kɛ fan waa fanɛ abaa ɔkaa.

eng: A gift is appreciated because of what a person can afford, not because of what that person cant afford, if its apparent that its done willingly.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan ɛɛ kɛɛ mʋ kɩpɩrɛ sʋ lɩɩ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ mʋ sʋ mʋ, ɩmʋ ɩɩ bɔla Ɩbwaarɛ ansi, man gyɛ lɩɩ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man sʋ mʋ sʋ.

eng: It isnt that we want others to have financial ease and you financial difficulties, but its a matter of equality.
local: Man gyɛ yɛ an biti an taa akʋ kitiri ba sʋla fanɛ kɛ fɛn kɛɛ kyʋn sʋ, amaa an biti fɛɛ fanɛ kpini kakyɩna tɔ kɩ waa kyɛɛkyɛɛ.

eng: At the present moment, your surplus can fill their deficit so that in the future their surplus can fill your deficit. In this way there is equality.
local: Nbɩɩnbɩɩ bɩrɛ fanɛ ɩ sʋ gaa, nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ fɛn kyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ii tiri bamʋ mʋ tɔ. Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ kan ii tiri fanɛ mɔɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ba sʋ gaa mʋ, kɛ ba kyɛ fanɛ gbaa fanɛ tɔ. Saamʋ fanɛ kpini kakyɩna tɔ kɩ waa kyɛɛkyɛɛ.

eng: As it is written, The one who gathered more didnt have too much, and the one who gathered less didnt have too little. .
local: Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ yɛ, Ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kʋʋla kʋkyɔ mʋ, man nyɛ naa ban sʋ, nɩɩ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kʋʋla pii mʋ gbaa, ɩ kʋkwɛ mʋ kyɛɛkyɛɛ.

eng: But thank God, who put the same commitment that I have for you in Titus heart.
local: N fwaala Ɩbwaarɛ gaa fɛɛ maa yɛgɛ nɩɩ Titusi payɩ ansi ɛɛ kyɛ fanɛ tɔ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ngbaa mɛ n kyɛ fanɛ tɔ mʋ.

eng: Not only has he accepted our challenge but hes on his way to see you voluntarily, and hes excited.
local: Man gyɛ yɛ a maa tɔwɛ an kʋlɛ mʋ sʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ ba fanɛ asɛ, amaa lɩɩ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fanɛ asɩn i tiri mʋ sʋ mʋ, ɔ taa mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mʋ kɩpɩrɛ tɔ nɩn ɛɛ ba fanɛ asɛ.

eng: We are sending the brother who is famous in all the churches because of his work for the gospel along with him.
local: An sun anɛ bɩrɩsa kʋ fɛɛ o buu mʋ ba fanɛ asɛ. Mʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ Krisito kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ mʋ kpini bɛɛ yɩn mʋ, lɩɩ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ kɩtɔwɛ mʋ sʋ.

eng: In addition to this, he is chosen by the churches to be our traveling companion in this work of grace, which we are taking care of for the sake of the glory of the Lord himself, and to show our desire to help.
local: Kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bɩla ki tii sʋ mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, Krisito kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ mʋ ɩ lɛɛ mʋ yɛ, a maa sʋ nkɛɛ nɩmʋ kusun mʋ, o tii anɛ sʋ. Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ kusun mʋ nɩɩ an waa an sʋ bɛɛrɛɛ an sa Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu, kɛ ɩ kaapʋ kanan mʋ nɩɩ an payɩ ansi an kyɛ tɔ.

eng: We are trying to avoid being blamed by anyone for the way we take care of this large amount of money.
local: An da anɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ kɛ ɔkʋ ɔ man pwɩɩ anɛ lɩɩ nkɛɛ damantɛ mʋ nɩɩ ba taa waa anɛ abaa tɔ yɛ a kɩɩ sʋ mʋ.

eng: We care about doing the right thing, not only in the Lords eyes but also in the eyes of other people.
local: Kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ an biti mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, a waa ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ dɛ ɔkpa. Man gyɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ wʋlɛ ansi tɔ nkʋn, amaa asa gbaa ansi tɔ.

eng: We are sending our brother with them. We have tested his commitment in many ways and many times. Now hes even more committed, because he has so much confidence in you.
local: Kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ an bɩla sun anɛ bɩrɩsa kʋ tii bamʋ sʋ. Mʋ nɩɩ an wu fɛɛ ɩkpa gaalaagaa sʋ mʋ, ɔ sʋ kensi payɩ ɛɛ kyɛ tɔ. Nbɩɩnbɩɩ mʋ maa sʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii fanɛ tɔ sʋ mʋ, ɔ payɩ ansi gaa fɛɛ ɔ ba kyɛ tɔ.

eng: If there is any question about Titus, he is my partner and coworker among you. If there is any question about our brothers, they are the churches apostles and an honor to Christ.
local: Titusi bɩrɛ, ɔ gyɛ mɛ bɩrɩsa kusun osunpu ɔmʋ nɩɩ n maa mʋ an kyɛ fanɛ tɔ. Nɩɩ anɛ bɩrɩsa ana asansa mʋ nɩɩ ba maa mʋ bee kpee mʋ bɩrɛ, ba yɩlɛ Krisito kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ mʋ ayaa tɔ nɩn kɛ ɩ taa bɛɛrɛɛ bɩya Krisito.

eng: So show them the proof of your love and the reason we are so proud of you, in such a way that the churches can see it.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan lɛɛ fanɛ kebiti mʋ kaapʋ bamʋ, kɛ Krisito kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ mʋ ba wu kɩmʋ kesintin, kɛ ba wu kʋtɔ mʋ sʋ nɩɩ an kaapʋ anɛ n‐yɩɩ fanɛ sʋ mʋ.

eng: Its unnecessary for me to write to you about this service for Gods people.
local: Kesintin sʋ, ɩ man tiri fɛɛ n bɩla kyʋrɔɔ ɔwʋlɛ sa fanɛ, lɩɩ afulee kɛkɛɛ kusun sa Ɩbwaarɛ asa, mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Gyudeya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ mʋ.

eng: I know about your willingness to help. I brag about you to the Macedonians, saying, Greece has been ready since last year, and your enthusiasm has motivated most of them.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ n nyi kanan mʋ nɩɩ fen biti fan kyɛ bamʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ n kaapʋ mɛ n‐yɩɩ fanɛ sʋ n sa Masedoniya abi. N tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ lɩɩ koo sun‐tɔ nɩɩ fanɛ Akiya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ abi fan kʋʋla n‐yɩɩ yɛ fan sa afulee. Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ kʋsaa asa gaalaagaa nɩɩ bamʋ gbaa bɛɛ kʋʋla bamʋ n‐yɩɩ kanɩn.

eng: But Im sending the brothers so that our bragging about you in this case wont be empty words, and so that you can be prepared, just as I keep telling them you will be.
local: Amaa n sun anɛ bɩrɩsa ana banɩmʋ fanɛ asɛ, saamʋ kanan mʋ nɩɩ n kaapʋ mɛ n‐yɩɩ lɩɩ fanɛ sʋ n sa Masedoniya abi mʋ, ɩ man ba waa kɩyan. Amaa fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ bamʋ mʋ, n nyi fan maa kʋʋla fanɛ n‐yɩɩ fɛn waa kanɩn.

eng: If some Macedonians should come with me and find out that you arent ready, we would be embarrassed as far as this project goes.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ nɩɩ Masedoniya asa kʋ ba kan buu mɛ ba, nɩɩ ba be wu fɛɛ fan man kʋʋla fanɛ n‐yɩɩ mʋ, anɛ ipeeli ɩ gyɛ kawɛ bɔla, kɛ fanɔɔ fanɛ lɛɛ kɩlantan bɔla, fɛɛ a maa taa anɛ n‐yɩɩ tʋʋ fanɛ sʋ mʋ.

eng: This is why I thought it was necessary to encourage the brothers to go to you ahead of time and arrange in advance the generous gift you have already promised. I want it to be a real gift from you. I dont want you to feel like you are being forced to give anything.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ n wu yɛ i tiri fɛɛ n kʋlɛ mɛ bɩrɩsa ana yɛ ba gyankpaa mɛ ba fanɛ asɛ, kɛ ba ba kʋʋla afulee tʋʋtʋʋ mʋ nɩɩ fan da fanɛ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ fɛɛ fen biti fɛn sa mʋ. Amaa ɩ baa ɩ gyɛ tʋʋtʋʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ lɩɩ nkɔlɔ tɔ, man gyɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ nyan fanɛ.

eng: What I mean is this: the one who sows a small number of seeds will also reap a small crop, and the one who sows a generous amount of seeds will also reap a generous crop.
local: Fan nyiŋi fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o duu atɔ duusa gbɛrɛɛ mʋ, ɛɛ nyɛ adɔɔ‐gyitɔ gbɛrɛɛ, nɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ o duu atɔ duusa gaa mʋ, ɛɛ nyɛ adɔɔ‐gyitɔ gaa.

eng: Everyone should give whatever they have decided in their heart. They shouldnt give with hesitation or because of pressure. God loves a cheerful giver.
local: Fanɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ ɔ sa kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ fɛ nfɛɛrɛ lɩɩ mʋ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ yɛ ee biti ɔ sa mʋ, kɛ ɩ man ba waa fɛɛ ɩɩ kɔ mʋ, abɛɛ ɔkʋ ɩɩ nyan mʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ sa kɔkɔlɔ fuuli tɔ.

eng: God has the power to provide you with more than enough of every kind of grace. That way, you will have everything you need always and in everything to provide more than enough for every kind of good work.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ ɩɩ taalɛ yɛgɛ kɛ fan nyɛ kabwaala kyʋn sʋ, saamʋ fen biti fan nyɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ kii tiri fanɛ, kɛ fan nyɛ kyʋn sʋ, kɛ fan kyɛ kusun danbɩrasa kʋmaa tɔ.

eng: As it is written, He scattered everywhere; he gave to the needy; his righteousness remains forever. .
local: Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ yɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ ii bugi mʋ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ, ɛɛ kɛɛ atiripu atɔ, nɩɩ mʋ kɩdanbɩrasa kɩ man sʋ ɔkaa.

eng: The one who supplies seed for planting and bread for eating will supply and multiply your seed and will increase your crop, which is righteousness.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ sa oduupu atɔ duusa yɛ agyitɔ‐gyisa mʋ, mʋ kʋlʋn maŋa ii biti ɔ sa fanɛ atɔ duusa mʋ, kɛ ɩ kɔyɩ, kɛ ɩ sɔyɩ, kɛ fan tɩŋɛ kʋkyɔ, kɩdanbɩrasa sʋ.

eng: You will be made rich in every way so that you can be generous in every way. Such generosity produces thanksgiving to God through us.
local: Ee biti ɔ yɛgɛ fan biliŋi atɔ wuya ana ɔkpa kʋmaa nɩmʋ sʋ, kɛ fan taalɛ waa kɩdanbɩrasa saŋa kʋmaa nɩmʋ. Kɛ lɩɩ afulee mʋ nɩɩ fan bɔla anɛ sʋ kɛɛ mʋ, ɩ yɛgɛ asa kpini ba taa afwaala sa Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: Your ministry of this service to Gods people isnt only fully meeting their needs but it is also multiplying in many expressions of thanksgiving to God.
local: Kɛkɛɛ kusun mʋ nɩɩ fɛn waa falɛ, man gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asa wʋlɛ nkʋn tɔ nɩɩ ɩɩ kyɛ, amaa ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ asa gaalaagaa ba sʋ afwaala bɛɛ sa Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: They will give honor to God for your obedience to your confession of Christs gospel. They will do this because this service provides evidence of your obedience, and because of your generosity in sharing with them and with everyone.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ, fanɛ kusun danbɩrasa maa kaapʋ asa kanan mʋ nɩɩ fan du mʋ, bee biti ba taa bʋnyaa sa Ɩbwaarɛ, lɩɩ Krisito asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ nɩɩ fan kɔɔlɛ gyii, yɛ kɩlɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ afulee kɛkɛɛ mʋ nɩɩ fɛn kyɛ bamʋ tɔ yɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ mʋ.

eng: They will also pray for you, and they will care deeply for you because of the outstanding grace that God has given to you.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ bamʋ gbaa bamʋ nkɔlɔ ɩ gyan fanɛ sʋ, nɩɩ bee biti ba baa bɛɛ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ bɛɛ sa fanɛ, lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabwaala kʋkyɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sa fanɛ sʋ mʋ.

eng: Thank God for his gift that words cant describe!.
local: Fan yɛgɛ a taa afwaala sa Ɩbwaarɛ, lɩɩ kɛkɛɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ man sʋ kudubi mʋ sʋ.

eng: I, Paul, make a personal request to you with the gentleness and kindness of Christ. Im shy when Im with you, but Im bossy when Im away from you!.
local: Lɩɩ Krisito n‐yɩɩ kasɛ kɩbɩya yɛ mʋ kɩbwahn sʋ mʋ, n kʋlɛ fanɛ. Mɛ, Pɔɔlɩ mʋ nɩɩ fanɛ tɔ akʋ bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, n kan n bʋ fanɛ asɛ mʋ, n bɩya mɛ n‐yɩɩ kasɛ katɔwɛ tɔ, amaa nɩɩ n man kan n bʋ fanɛ asɛ mʋ, n yɩlɛ kɩnyɩn tɔ n tɔwɛ mʋ.

eng: I beg you that when Im with you in person, I wont have to boss you around. Im afraid that I may have to use that kind of behavior with those people who think we live by human standards.
local: n kʋlɛ fanɛ fɛɛ n kan ba fanɛ asɛ mʋ, fan man yɛgɛ n ba yɩlɛ kɩnyɩn tɔ tɔwɛ sa fanɛ. Amaa n kan ba mʋ, n biti n ba yɩlɛ kɩnyɩn tɔ tɔwɛ sa bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ fɛ nfɛɛrɛ yɛ dulinyaa atɔ danbɩrasa kebiti sʋ nɩɩ an waa Ɩbwaarɛ kusun mʋ.

eng: Although we live in the world, we dont fight our battles with human methods.
local: Ɩ gyɛ kesintin fɛɛ an bʋ dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ bɩrɛ, amaa man gyɛ dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ kɩnaa nɩɩ an kɔ.

eng: Our weapons that we fight with arent human, but instead they are powered by God for the destruction of fortresses. They destroy arguments.
local: Adʋntɔ mʋ nɩɩ an sʋ an kɔ mʋ, man gyɛ dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ adʋntɔ kudubi, amaa ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ adʋntɔ bʋlʋnsa mʋ nɩɩ ii nyita ɔlʋn kumu kʋmaa nɩmʋ.

eng: and every defense that is raised up to oppose the knowledge of God. They capture every thought to make it obedient to Christ.
local: An sʋ ɩmʋ an nyita nfɛɛrɛ kʋmaa, yɛ nfɛɛrɛ tɔ alala‐sɩn kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ii tii nyɩmɩsa kɩnyɛ bɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ, nɩɩ an kɔ gyii nfɛɛrɛ kʋmaa nɩmʋ sʋ fɛɛ ɔdʋn, kɛ ɔ ba nyɛ kʋsʋ‐nu sa Krisito.

eng: Once your obedience is complete, we are ready to punish any disobedience.
local: Nɩɩ fan kan nyɛ kʋsʋ‐nu naa lʋwɛ mʋ, an biti a ba bɩɩtɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba waa kʋsʋ tɔ ɔlʋn mʋ asʋ.

eng: Look at what is right in front of you! If anyone is sure about belonging to Christ, that person should think again. We belong to Christ just like that person.
local: Fanɛ fanɛ bɩrɛ sʋsʋ‐sʋsʋ wʋlɛ nkʋn nɩɩ fɛn kɩɩ atɔ. Nɩɩ fanɛ tɔ ɔkʋ kan kɔɔlɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ gyii fɛɛ ɔ gyɛ Krisito lɛɛ mʋ, ɩ dagaa maa wu fɛɛ anɛ gbaa anɛ an gyɛ Krisito lɛɛ nɩn, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ mʋ gbaa mʋ gyɛ mʋ.

eng: Even if I went on to brag about our authority, I wouldnt be ashamed of it. The Lord gave us that authority to build you up and not to destroy you.
local: Halɩɩ nɩɩ n kan n kaapʋ mɛ n‐yɩɩ kyʋn sʋ gbaa lɩɩ ɔlʋn mʋ nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ sa anɛ yɛ a taa kyɛ fanɛ tɔ kɛ i kpiya fanɛ ansi tɔ, nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ yɛ an bɩɩtɛ fanɛ tʋʋ kasɛ sʋ mʋ, ipeeli ɩ mɛɛ yɛ i gyii mɛ lɩɩ ɩmʋ sʋ.

eng: I dont want it to seem like Im trying to intimidate you with my letters.
local: Nɩɩ n mee biti ɩ naa ba fɛɛ, n kyʋrɔɔ mɛ ɩwʋlɛ n sʋ n ŋaala fanɛ.

eng: I know what some people are saying: His letters are severe and powerful, but in person he is weak and his speech is worth nothing.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ akʋ bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Pɔɔlɩ ɩwʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kyʋrɔɔ mʋ, sʋ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋlʋn nɩɩ i budu. Amaa nɩɩ ɔ bʋ anɛ asɛ bɩrɛ, ɔ pɔɔ nɩɩ mʋ asɩn tɔwɛsa ɩ mɛɛ kaapʋ sɛhn.

eng: These people need to think about this—that when we are with you, our actions will show that we are the same as the words we wrote when we were away from you.
local: Kanɩn asa mʋ ɩ dagaa ba maa bɩɩ fɛɛ, asɩn kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ an man bʋ fanɛ asɛ nɩɩ an kyʋrɔɔ anɛ ɩwʋlɛ tɔ sa fanɛ mʋ, ɩmʋ maŋa nɩɩ an bʋ fanɛ asɛ gbaa, an biti a tɔwɛ fanɛ.

eng: We wont dare to place ourselves in the same league or to compare ourselves with some of those who are promoting themselves. When they measure themselves by themselves, and compare themselves with themselves, they have no understanding.
local: Kesintin tɔ mʋ, ɩ sʋ anɛ kufuu gbaa fɛɛ a taa anɛ n‐yɩɩ an biri, abɛɛ an kaala yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ lɛɛ bamʋ n‐yɩɩ bɛɛ kaapʋ fɛɛ ba gyɛ asa kparɛ mʋ. Fɛɛ ba maa sʋ bamʋ n‐yɩɩ bee biri abaa abɛɛ bɛɛ kaala abaa mʋ, man gyɛ amelensi‐sɩn ndee abɛɛ?.

eng: We wont take pride in anything more than what is appropriate. Lets look at the boundaries of our work area that God has assigned to us. Its an area that includes you.
local: Nɩɩ anɛɛnɛ bɩrɛ, a mɛɛ yɛ a kaapʋ anɛ n‐yɩɩ naa kyʋn anɛ ɩkaa sʋ. Amaa an biti a kaapʋ anɛ n‐yɩɩ lɩɩ kusun mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ taa waa anɛ abaa tɔ, halɩɩ an waa kɩmʋ ba fʋʋ fanɛ asɛ mʋ.

eng: We arent going out of bounds, as if our work area doesnt extend as far as you. We were the first ones to travel as far as Corinth with the gospel of Christ.
local: Nɩɩ a mɛɛ kaapʋ anɛ n‐yɩɩ lɩɩ fanɛ sʋ an kyʋn sʋ, naafɔɔ a man dɩn fʋʋ fanɛ asɛ mʋ, amaa anɛ ɩ gyankpaa taa Krisito asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ kɩtɔwɛ ba fʋʋ fanɛ asɛ.

eng: We dont take pride in what other people do outside of our boundaries. We hope that our work will be extended even more by you as your faith grows, until it expands fully .
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ a mɛɛ kaapʋ anɛ n‐yɩɩ lɩɩ kusun mʋ nɩɩ akʋ ba waa mʋ sʋ, fɛɛ anɛ ɩ waa ɩmʋ mʋ. Amaa anɛ tamaa ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ fanɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii mʋ nɩɩ fan sʋ mʋ, kɩ nyɛ akyɔ tii sʋ, kɛ anɛ kusun mʋ nɩɩ an sun fanɛ asɛ mʋ, kɩ nyɛ yɛlɩgɛ tii sʋ naa fʋʋ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ yɩla mʋ ɔkaa.

eng: We hope that our work grows even to the point of the gospel being preached in places beyond Corinth, without bragging about what has already been done in another persons work area.
local: Kɛ a nyɛ tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ sa ɩsʋwʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ fanɛ ansi tɔ mʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ an mee biti a maa kaapʋ anɛ n‐yɩɩ lɩɩ kusun mʋ nɩɩ ɔkʋ tɛɛ wʋla waa kasɛ kʋ sʋ.

eng: But, the one who brags should brag in the Lord. .
local: Amaa ɩ dagaa fɛɛ a maa waa fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ mʋ. Ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ kaapʋ mʋ n‐yɩɩ mʋ, ɔ kaapʋ mʋ n‐yɩɩ lɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ waa mʋ sʋ.

eng: It isnt the person who promotes himself or herself who is approved but the person whom the Lord commends.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ man gyɛ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ kaapʋ kanan mʋ nɩɩ o du mʋ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ansi ii gyii mʋ sʋ, amɔɔ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ kaapʋ kanan mʋ nɩɩ o du mʋ.

eng: I hope that you will put up with me while I act like a fool. Well, in fact, you are putting up with me!.
local: Nɩɩ fan kan nyɛ kenyiita gbɛrɛɛ sa mɛ lɩɩ mɛ amelensi‐sɩn nɩmʋ sʋ mʋ, naafɔɔ ɩ bʋrɔn. Nɩɩ n nyi fɛɛ fen biti fɛn waa kanɩn.

eng: Im deeply concerned about you with the same concern that God has. As your father, I promised you in marriage to one husband. I promised to present you as an innocent virgin to Christ himself.
local: Krisito ayaa tɔ nɩɩ n yɩlɛ n kɔ fanɛ sʋ kɔkʋrʋkɔ falɛ. Fen du nɩn fɛɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man nyi ɔnyɩn nɩɩ ɔ man sʋ ineesi ɩkʋ, nɩɩ n sʋ n sa ɔnyɩn kekili, nɩɩ ɔnyɩn maŋa ɩ gyɛ Krisito.

eng: But Im afraid that your minds might be seduced in the same way as the snake deceived Eve with his devious tricks. You might be unable to focus completely on a genuine and innocent commitment to Christ.
local: Amaa n sʋ kufuu fɛɛ ɔkʋ ii biti o be nyita fanɛ nfɛɛrɛ lɩɩ Krisito mʋ nɩɩ fan taa fanɛ nkɔlɔ kpini buu mʋ mʋ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ kɩnansɛ taa mʋ kɔnɔ ɔkɔn pɩna Hawa mʋ.

eng: If a person comes and preaches some other Jesus than the one we preached, or if you receive a different Spirit than the one you had received, or a different gospel than the one you embraced, you put up with it so easily!.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ ba tɔwɛ asɩn lɩɩ Yesu kʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kwɩɩ yɛ Yesu mʋ nɩɩ an tɔwɛ mʋ asɩn mʋ, abɛɛ nɩɩ fan kan nyɛ kayaayu kʋ, nɩɩ kɩ kwɩɩ yɛ kayaayu mʋ nɩɩ fan daa kɩnyɛ mʋ, abɛɛ nɩɩ fan nu asɩn danbɩrasa kʋ, nɩɩ ɩ kwɩɩ lɩɩ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ nɩɩ fan kɔɔlɛ gyii mʋ, ɩɩ kaapʋ nɩn fɛɛ fɛn mɛɛ kɛrɛ kanɩn atɔ kɩkɔɔlɛ kyula.

eng: I dont consider myself as second-rate in any way compared to the super-apostles.
local: Amaa n mɛɛ fɛ nfɛɛrɛ yɛ n bʋ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa bamʋ n‐yɩɩ asunpu kparɛ mʋ kasɛ pii falɛ gbaa.

eng: But even if Im uneducated in public speaking, Im not uneducated in knowledge. We have shown this to you in every way and in everything we have done.
local: Nɩɩ n kan n gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ man nyi katɔwɛ gbaa mʋ, n sʋ kinyi lɩɩ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ an kaapʋ fanɛ ɔkpa kʋmaa sʋ yɛ saŋa kʋmaa, nɩɩ kanɩn nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ.

eng: Did I commit a sin by humbling myself to give you an advantage because I preached the gospel of God to you free of charge?.
local: N maa tɔwɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn danbɩrasa sa fanɛ mʋ, n man kɔɔlɛ fanɛ sɛhn, nɩɩ bɩya mɛ n‐yɩɩ kasɛ, kɛ ɩ nyɛ waa fanɛ asa kparɛ mʋ, n waa alibi nɩn abɛɛ?.

eng: I robbed other churches by taking a salary from them in order to serve you!.
local: N taa kɩkpɩnɛ tʋʋ Krisito kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ mʋ ɩkʋ sʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ n kɔɔlɛ nkyɛtɔ lɩɩ bamʋ asɛ, kɛ n nyɛ ba kyɛ fanɛ tɔ.

eng: While I was with you, I didnt burden any of you even though I needed things. The believers who came from Macedonia gave me everything I needed. I kept myself from being a financial drain on you in any way, and I will continue to keep myself from being a burden.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ n kyɩna n bʋ fanɛ asɛ nɩɩ i tiri mɛ mʋ, n man yɛgɛ nɩɩ ɩ waa kasʋla fanɛ sʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ mɛ bɩrɩsa ana mʋ nɩɩ ba lɩɩ Masedoniya mʋ ba bɩya mɛ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ii tiri mɛ. N kɩtaa mɛ n‐yɩɩ ɔkpa kʋmaa nɩmʋ sʋ kanan mʋ nɩɩ n man ba waa kasʋla fanɛ sʋ, nɩɩ n biti n kyaga sʋ waa kanɩn.

eng: Since Christs truth is in me, I wont stop telling the entire area of Greece that Im proud of what I did.
local: Fɛɛ Krisito kesintin mʋ maa bʋ mɛ tɔ falɛ mʋ, n tɔwɛ yɛ ɔkʋ man bʋ Akiya kɩbaafʋn nɩmʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔkʋ mɛɛ taalɛ tii mɛ n‐yɩɩ kɩkaapʋ nɩmʋ sʋ.

eng: Why? Is it because I dont love you? God knows that I do!.
local: N maa tɔwɛ falɛ mʋ, ɩ kaapʋ nɩn fɛɛ n mee biti fanɛ asɩn abɛɛ? Ayee! Ɩbwaarɛ nyi fɛɛ n biti fanɛ asɩn.

eng: But Im going to continue to do what Im doing. I want to contradict the claims of the people who want to be treated like they are the same as us because of what they brag about.
local: Nɩɩ ɩmʋ nɩɩ n waa mʋ n biti n kyaga sʋ n waa, kɛ ɩ nyɛ tii bamʋ nɩɩ bee biti ɩkpa ba kʋsaa bamʋ n‐yɩɩ mʋ ɔkpa. Lɩɩ fɛɛ bɛɛ kaapʋ yɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa kusun mʋ, anaa bamʋ lɛɛ gyɛ kʋlʋn nɩn.

eng: Such people are false apostles and dishonest workers who disguise themselves as apostles of Christ.
local: Kanɩn asa maŋa ba gyɛ antɩŋɛsa asunpu mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ pɩna asa lɩɩ kanan mʋ nɩɩ bee sun kusun mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ba baala bamʋ daa bɛɛ waa fɛɛ ba gyɛ Krisito asunpu.

eng: And no wonder! Even Satan disguises himself as an angel of light.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ man gyɛ asɩn kyinkyinsa, lɩɩ fɛɛ Sɩtaanɛ gbaa‐gbaa ɩɩ taalɛ baala mʋ daa ɛɛ ŋalɩgɛ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ.

eng: It is no great surprise then that his servants also disguise themselves as servants of righteousness. Their end will be what their actions deserve.
local: Kanɩn gbaa nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ asɩn kyinkyinsa fɛɛ Sɩtaanɛ ayaafɔlɛ gbaa ba baala bamʋ daa, nɩɩ ba waa bamʋ n‐yɩɩ fɛɛ nbɔɔ mʋ nɩɩ bamʋ ɩkpa ii kyiigi mʋ. Laalaalʋwɛ mʋ bee biti ba nyɛ bamʋ asun libi kʋkɔka.

eng: I repeat, no one should take me for a fool. But if you do, then allow me to be a fool so that I can brag like a fool for a bit.
local: N pɩla tɔ n tɔwɛ yɛ, ɔkʋ ɔ man fɛ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ n gyɛ omelensipu. Amaa nɩɩ fɛn kan fɛ nfɛɛrɛ kanɩn mʋ, ɩmʋ bɩrɛ fɛn taa mɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fen biti fan taa omelensipu mʋ, kɛ n nyɛ kaapʋ mɛ n‐yɩɩ gbɛrɛɛ.

eng: Im not saying what Im saying because the Lord tells me to. Im saying it like Im a fool. Im putting my confidence in this business of bragging.
local: Ɩmʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ falɛ, man gyɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ ii biti fɛɛ n tɔwɛ, amaa n‐yɩɩ kɩkaapʋ asɩn nɩmʋ tɔ mʋ, n tɔwɛ nɩn fɛɛ omelensipu.

eng: Since so many people are bragging based on human standards, that is how Im going to brag too.
local: Fɛɛ asa gaalaagaa ba maa kaapʋ bamʋ n‐yɩɩ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ dulinyaa abi bɛɛ waa mʋ, ngbaa mɛ n biti n kaapʋ mɛ n‐yɩɩ.

eng: Because you, who are so wise, are happy to put up with fools.
local: Fan maa yɛ fan sʋ kanyaasɩn gaa sʋ mʋ, fɛn nyɛ kenyiita fen nu amelensipu asɛ.

eng: You put up with it if someone enslaves you, if someone exploits you, if someone takes advantage of you, if someone places themselves over you, or if someone hits you in the face.
local: Kesintin tɔ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa fanɛ fɛɛ anyɛ, abɛɛ ba sʋ fanɛ bee biliŋi sʋ, abɛɛ bee gyii fanɛ, abɛɛ bɛɛ kɩɩ fanɛ kɩyan, abɛɛ bɛɛ kpaalɛ fanɛ ɩtan gbaa mʋ, fɛn taalɛ fɛn nyɛ kenyiita fen sa bamʋ.

eng: Im ashamed to say that we have been weak in comparison! But in whatever they challenge me, I challenge them .
local: Ɩ sʋ mɛ ipeeli nɩɩ n kɔɔlɛ kyula fɛɛ, atɔ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa falɛ bɩrɛ, an gyɛ apɔɔsɩpʋ ɩmʋ tɔ gaa. Amaa nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan ɔ man sʋ kufuu fɛɛ ee biti ɔ kaapʋ mʋ n‐yɩɩ kʋtɔ kʋ sʋ mʋ, n maa tɔwɛ fɛɛ omelensipu falɛ mʋ, ngbaa mɛ n mɛɛ yɛ n waa kufuu fɛɛ n kaapʋ mɛ n‐yɩɩ ɩmʋ sʋ.

eng: Are they Hebrews? So am I. Are they Israelites? So am I. Are they descendants of Abraham? So am I.
local: Asa maŋa ba gyɛ Hibriwu abi nɩn abɛɛ? Kanɩn nɩɩ ngbaa mɛ n gyɛ. Ba gyɛ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi nɩn abɛɛ? Kanɩn nɩɩ ngbaa mɛ n gyɛ. Ba gyɛ Abraham anaana‐bii nɩn abɛɛ? Kanɩn nɩɩ ngbaa mɛ n gyɛ.

eng: Are they ministers of Christ? Im speaking like a crazy person. What Ive done goes well beyond what theyve done. Ive worked much harder. Ive been imprisoned much more often. Ive been beaten more times than I can count. Ive faced death many times.
local: Ba gyɛ Krisito ayaafɔlɛ nɩn abɛɛ? N nyi mɛ katɔwɛ nɩmʋ maa duli ɩbʋn bɩrɛ, amaa n gyɛ kɩyaafɔlɛ kparɛ n kyɔ bamʋ. N sun kusun ɔlʋn sʋ n kyɔ bamʋ, nɩɩ ba tii mɛ ibu saŋa kʋmaa nɩmʋ ɩ kyɔ bamʋ, ba taa awulibi tɩŋɛ mɛ libi‐libi ɩ kyɔ bamʋ, nɩɩ n gyaŋa lewu saŋa kʋkyɔ.

eng: I received the forty lashes minus one from the Jews five times.
local: Gyiwu abi ba taa awulibi tɩŋɛ mɛ adɩsa yɛ akpanɔ kanɩn ɩlɩɩ inuu.

eng: I was beaten with rods three times. I was stoned once. I was shipwrecked three times. I spent a day and a night on the open sea.
local: Nɩɩ Roma abi gbaa ba taa abɩlɩtɔ kpekpesen da mɛ ɩlɩɩ ɩsa, nɩɩ saŋa kʋ asa ba twɩɩ mɛ abuu, nɩɩ ɔkʋlɩ lala taa mɛ naa watɛ ɩlɩɩ ɩsa, nɩɩ kakaakʋ n dɛ ɔpʋ sʋ kalayɩ tɩyɛ kyɔwɛ.

eng: Ive been on many journeys. I faced dangers from rivers, robbers, my people, and Gentiles. I faced dangers in the city, in the desert, on the sea, and from false brothers and sisters.
local: Mɛ kɩnatɛ tɔ mʋ, n gyaŋa kinsi‐payɩ gaalaagaa. N nyɛ kinsi‐payɩ lɩɩ ɩbʋn kɩbɔla sʋ, yɛ ayu mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ nyan bɛɛ kɔɔlɛ atɔ abaa tɔ, yɛ mɛ asa Gyiwu abi, yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ abaa tɔ. Nɩɩ n nyɛ kinsi‐payɩ ndɛ lala tɔ, yɛ ɩsʋwʋlɛ sʋ, yɛ ɔpʋ sʋ. Nɩɩ n nyɛ kinsi‐payɩ lɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ pɩna yɛ ba gyɛ anɛ Krisito abuupu mʋ asɛ.

eng: I faced these dangers with hard work and heavy labor, many sleepless nights, hunger and thirst, often without food, and in the cold without enough clothes.
local: N sun kusun kenken nɩɩ n kpɩnɛ, halɩɩ nkɛ damantɛ n mɛɛ nyɛ nsɩɩrɛ n dɛ. Akʋn yɛ bʋlɩfɔ nɩɩ n naa, nɩɩ saŋa gaalaagaa sɛhn man loo mɛ kɔnɔ tɔ. Nɩɩ n pan atɔ bunsa nɩɩ n dɛ awɔɔ.

eng: Besides all the other things I could mention, theres my daily stress because Im concerned about all the churches.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ kpini ɩ naa dɛ, ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ nyan mɛ kakɛ kʋmaa mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ n fɛ Krisito kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ mʋ sʋ.

eng: Who is weak without me being weak? Who is led astray without me being furious about it?.
local: Anɩmʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii ɩɩ kɔɔgɛ sʋ, nɩɩ n mee su n sa mʋ? Anɩmʋ nɩɩ ba pɩna mʋ ba waa alibi tɔ, nɩɩ mɛ ɔtɔ tɔ mɛɛ fɔyɩ mɛ?.

eng: If its necessary to brag, Ill brag about my weaknesses.
local: Nɩɩ n kan i tiri fɛɛ n kaapʋ mɛ n‐yɩɩ mʋ, n biti n kaapʋ mɛ n‐yɩɩ lɩɩ mɛ kɩpɔɔsɛ sʋ nɩn.

eng: The God and Father of the Lord Jesus, the one who is blessed forever, knows that Im not lying.
local: Anɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ anɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Yesu Mʋ‐Sɛ, nɩɩ ayɩn‐bi ɩ gyɛ mʋ lɛɛ kakpaa mʋ, i nyi yɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ falɛ man gyɛ antɩŋɛsa.

eng: At Damascus the governor under King Aretas was guarding the city of Damascus in order to capture me.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ n bʋ Damasiko kadɛ tɔ mʋ, Owura Aretasi mʋ ɔsʋwʋlɛ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ mʋ lɛɛ akunpu yɛ ba kun nbunɔ mʋ nɩɩ ii loo Damasiko kadɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩbandɩyɛ kɩ kyaabɔɔ kɩmʋ mʋ, kɛ ba nyɛ kɩtaa mɛ.

eng: but I got away from him by being lowered in a basket through a window in the city wall.
local: Amaa akʋ ba taa mɛ waa kɩlantan tɔ, bʋlɛ kɩbandɩyɛ mʋ ɔbɔ tɔ, kpɩlɩgaa mɛ yii, nɩɩ n nyɛ sɩlɛ lɩɩ mʋ abaa tɔ.

eng: It is necessary to brag, not that it does any good. Ill move on to visions and revelations from the Lord.
local: Ɩ dagaa n maa kyaga sʋ kaapʋ mɛ n‐yɩɩ. Halɩɩ ɩ maa man sʋ tɔnɔ gbaa mʋ, nbɩɩnbɩɩ bɩrɛ n biti n tɔwɛ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛgɛ nɩɩ n wu fɛɛ idee, yɛ asɩnlɛɛ kaapʋsa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa sa mɛ.

eng: I know a man in Christ who was caught up into the third heaven fourteen years ago. I dont know whether it was in the body or out of the body. God knows.
local: Ɔnyɩn kʋ kyɩna ɔ bʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ Krisito obuupu. Nsu kudu ana ndee nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ taa mʋ kpee sʋsʋ sasapʋ mʋ tɔ. N man nyi yɛ ɩnɩmʋ ɩ waa kanɩn nɩn kesintin, abɛɛ ansi sʋ atɔ nɩɩ o wu fɛɛ idee, amɔɔ Ɩbwaarɛ nkʋn i nyi.

eng: I know that this man was caught up into paradise and that he heard unspeakable words that were things no one is allowed to repeat. I dont know whether it was in the body or apart from the body. God knows.
local: N bwii n tɔwɛ yɛ, ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ mʋ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ kpini nɩɩ ɩ taa kpee sʋsʋ nɩn o, mʋ kala nkʋn nɩɩ ɩ taa kpee nɩn o, n man nyi, amɔɔ Ɩbwaarɛ nkʋn i nyi.

eng: I know that this man was caught up into paradise and that he heard unspeakable words that were things no one is allowed to repeat. I dont know whether it was in the body or apart from the body. God knows.
local: Amaa Ɩbwaarɛ maa taa mʋ kpee paradise mʋ, o nu asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba mɛɛ taalɛ tɔwɛ, yɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ nyɩmɩsa man sʋ ɔkpa yɛ ɔ tɔwɛ.

eng: Ill brag about this man, but I wont brag about myself, except to brag about my weaknesses.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ n biti n kaapʋ mɛ n‐yɩɩ mʋ, kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ ba ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ n biti n kaapʋ n‐yɩɩ. N mɛɛ yɛ n kaapʋ mɛ n‐yɩɩ lɩɩ mɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ, amɔɔ mɛ kapɔɔsɛ sʋ nkʋn.

eng: If I did want to brag, I wouldnt make a fool of myself because Id tell the truth. Im holding back from bragging so that no one will give me any more credit than what anyone sees or hears about me.
local: Halɩɩ nɩɩ n kan n kaapʋ mɛ n‐yɩɩ gbaa mʋ, n mɛɛ yɛ n waa omelensipu. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ kesintin. Amaa n lɛɛ mɛ n‐yɩɩ lɩɩ n‐yɩɩ kɩkaapʋ tɔ, kɛ ɔkʋ ɔ man nyɛ kaala mɛ kyʋn kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ o wu mɛ maa waa sʋ, abɛɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ sʋ.

eng: I was given a thorn in my body because of the outstanding revelations Ive received so that I wouldnt be conceited. Its a messenger from Satan sent to torment me so that I wouldnt be conceited.
local: Kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɩ waa kɛ n man taa Ɩbwaarɛ kɩlɛɛkaapʋ damantɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa sa mɛ mʋ waa n‐yɩɩ alala‐sɩn sʋ mʋ, ɔ yɛgɛ nɩɩ Sɩtaanɛ sun kʋlɔ‐libi kʋ waa mɛ n‐yɩɩ tɔ fɛɛ owii, nɩɩ kii yii mɛ. Nɩɩ kʋlɔ kɩɩ nyanla mɛ, nɩɩ kii tii mɛ ɔkpa, kɛ n man nyɛ n waa mɛ n‐yɩɩ alala‐sɩn tɔ.

eng: I pleaded with the Lord three times for it to leave me alone.
local: N kʋlɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu ɩlɩɩ ɩsa fɛɛ ɔ lɛɛ ɩmʋ lɩɩ mɛ sʋ.

eng: He said to me, My grace is enough for you, because power is made perfect in weakness. So Ill gladly spend my time bragging about my weaknesses so that Christs power can rest on me.
local: Amaa ɔ lɛɛ mɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, Mɛ kabwaala mʋ nɩɩ n sʋ sa fʋ mʋ, ɩ kʋkwɛ fʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ fʋ kɩpɔɔsɛ mʋ tɔ nɩɩ mɛ ɔlʋn mʋ ɩɩ lɩɩ ifuli. Ɩmʋ sʋ n biti n kaapʋ mɛ n‐yɩɩ kensi‐gyii tɔ gaa lɩɩ mɛ kɩpɔɔsɛ tɔ, kɛ Krisito ɔlʋn mʋ kɩ ba bun mɛ sʋ.

eng: Therefore, Im all right with weaknesses, insults, disasters, harassments, and stressful situations for the sake of Christ, because when Im weak, then Im strong.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ lɩɩ Krisito sʋ mʋ, mi ansi i gyii lɩɩ mɛ kɩpɔɔsɛ tɔ, yɛ asebi tɔ, yɛ kayɩɩ kɩtaa tɔ, yɛ kanyanla tɔ, yɛ awʋrʋfɔ tɔ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ n pɔɔ mʋ, saŋa maa nɩɩ n sʋ ɔlʋn.

eng: Ive become a fool! You made me do it. Actually, I should have been commended by you. Im not inferior to the super-apostles in any way, even though Im a nonentity.
local: N waa fɛɛ omelensipu, amaa fanɛ i nin mɛ nɩɩ n waa kanɩn. Naafɔɔ fanɛ ɩ dagaa fan maa lɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ n du mʋ kaapʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ halɩɩ n maa man gyɛ sɛhn gbaa mʋ, bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa bamʋ n‐yɩɩ yɛ ba gyɛ Krisito asunpu kparɛ ba man kyɔ mɛ pii falɛ gbaa.

eng: The signs of an apostle were performed among you with continuous endurance through signs, wonders, and miracles.
local: Nɩɩ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ lɛɛ ɩɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ n gyɛ Krisito osunpu mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, n nyɛ kɔkɔlɔ yɩlɛ tɔ, nɩɩ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ n waa ɩsɩnkaala yɛ adangana yɛ asɩn kyinkyinsa fanɛ asɛ.

eng: How were you treated worse than the other churches, except that I myself wasnt a financial burden on you? Forgive me for this wrong!.
local: Mɩnɛ atɔ nɩɩ Krisito kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ asansa mʋ ba nyɛ nɩɩ fanɛ bɩrɛ fan man nyɛ? Amɔɔ afulee asɩn nɩɩ n man taa ba sʋla fanɛ. Nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ kanɩn bɩrɛ fan taa mɛ alibi kɛɛ mɛ.

eng: Look, Im ready to visit you a third time, and I wont be a burden on you. I dont want your things; I want you. It isnt the childrens responsibility to save up for their parents but parents for children.
local: Nbɩɩnbɩɩ n kʋʋla lʋwɛ n ba bɔɔ fanɛ sʋ ɩlɩɩ sasapʋ. Nɩɩ mee biti fɛɛ n maa biliŋi kasʋla sa fanɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ man gyɛ fanɛ atɔ nɩɩ n biti, fanɛ gbaa‐gbaa nɩɩ n biti. Nɩɩ man gyɛ nbii ii biti atɔ bɛɛ yɩla bamʋ akʋʋgɛpʋ, amɔɔ akʋʋgɛpʋ ii biti bɛɛ yɩla bamʋ‐bii ana.

eng: I will very gladly spend and be spent for your sake. If I love you more, will you love me less?.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ n biti n kpɩnɛ kɛ n nyita mɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa fanɛ sʋ, kɛ n taa mɛ n‐yɩɩ gbaa tii sʋ. Fɛɛ n maa sʋ kebiti lala falɛ sa fanɛ mʋ, fen biti fan nyɛ kebiti pii sa mɛ nɩn?.

eng: We all know that I didnt place a burden on you, but in spite of that you think Im a con artist who fooled you with a trick.
local: N nyi fan maa kɔɔlɛ kyula fɛɛ n man biliŋi kasʋla fanɛ sʋ. Amaa akʋ bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ n taa mɛ kanyaasɩn ɔkpa sʋ n nyɛ fanɛ.

eng: I havent taken advantage of you through any of the people I sent to you, have I?.
local: Nɛnɛ sʋ? Asa mʋ nɩɩ n sun bamʋ fanɛ asɛ mʋ, n bɔla bamʋ sʋ gyii fanɛ nɩn abɛɛ?.

eng: I strongly encouraged Titus to go to you and sent the brother with him. Titus didnt take advantage of you, did he? Didnt we live by the same Spirit? Didnt we walk in the same footsteps?.
local: Mɛ ɩ kʋlɛ Titusi yɛ o kpee fanɛ asɛ, nɩɩ n sun anɛ bɩrɩsa kʋ yɛ o buu mʋ kpee. Abɛɛ fɛn fɛ nfɛɛrɛ yɛ Titusi ba pɩna fanɛ gyii nɩn? Man gyɛ nfɛɛrɛ kʋlʋn yɛ ɔkpa kʋlʋn sʋ nɩɩ anaa mʋ an naa?.

eng: Have you been thinking up to now that we are defending ourselves to you? Actually, we are speaking in the sight of God and in Christ. Dear friends, everything is meant to build you up.
local: Akʋtɔ fen fɛ nfɛɛrɛ nɩn fɛɛ an tɔwɛ an lɛɛ anɛ kɔnɔ nɩn. Ayee! Man gyɛ kanɩn nɩn. Amaa an tɔwɛ nɩn fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Krisito ii biti fɛɛ a tɔwɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ mʋ. Mɛ kyɛmɩnɛ ana danbɩrasa, kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ an waa mʋ, an waa nɩn kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɩ kyɛ fanɛ tɔ.

eng: Im afraid that maybe when I come you will be different from the way I want you to be, and that Ill be different from the way you want me to be. Im afraid that there might be fighting, obsession, losing your temper, competitive opposition, backstabbing, gossip, conceit, and disorderly conduct.
local: Ɩ sʋ mɛ kufuu fɛɛ n kan ba fanɛ asɛ mʋ, n mɛɛ yɛ n ba wu fanɛ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ n biti fɛɛ n ba wu fanɛ mʋ, nɩɩ fanɛ gbaa fɛn mɛɛ yɛ fen wu mɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fen biti fan wu mɛ mʋ. Ɩ sʋ mɛ kufuu fɛɛ n biti n ba wu nluu yɛ kayɩɩ‐basa yɛ agbʋ lala yɛ kigyi‐lɛɛ yɛ asa kɩsaalɛ yɛ asa asɩn kɩtɔwɛ yɛ alala‐sɩn yɛ nbarɩgɛ tɔ mʋ, ɩ maa bʋ fanɛ tɔ.

eng: Im afraid that when I come again, my God may embarrass me in front of you. I might have to go into mourning over all the people who have sinned before and havent changed their hearts and lives from what they used to practice: moral corruption, sexual immorality, and doing whatever feels good.
local: Ɩ sʋ mɛ kufuu fɛɛ nɩɩ n kan bwii ba fanɛ asɛ mʋ, mɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ bɩya mɛ kasɛ fanɛ ansi tɔ, kɛ mɛ kɔkɔlɔ ki nyita mɛ lɩɩ asa gaalaagaa mʋ nɩɩ ba wʋla waa alibi, nɩɩ ba man kyɛɛgɛ bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ lɩɩ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ ineesi, yɛ kakyɩɩ yɛ kanyɩn biti kɩgyagaa, yɛ agyaga‐sɩn kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ i tii sʋ mʋ.

eng: This is the third time that Im coming to visit you. Every matter is settled on the evidence of two or three witnesses.
local: Kɩnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ mɛ ɩlɩɩ sasapʋ mʋ nɩɩ n biti n ba bɔɔ fanɛ sʋ. Ba kyʋrɔɔ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ yɛ, Pɔyɩ kɛ ba kɔɔlɛ asɩn kʋ kyula mʋ, ɩ dagaa asa anyɔ abɛɛ asa asá ba maa gyii ɩmʋ kesintin.

eng: When I was with you on my second visit, I already warned those who continued to sin. Now Im repeating that warning to all the rest of you while Im at a safe distance: if I come again, I wont spare anyone.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ n ba bɔɔ fanɛ sʋ kɩnyɔsapʋ mʋ, n da bamʋ nɩɩ ba wʋla waa alibi yɛ asansa mʋ amu tɔ. Nɩɩ nbɩɩnbɩɩ n maa man bʋ fanɛ asɛ falɛ mʋ, n bɩla n nyiŋi fanɛ yɛ, nɩɩ n kan ba mʋ, n mɛɛ yɛ n yɛgɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba san bɛɛ waa alibi mʋ.

eng: Since you are demanding proof that Christ speaks through me, Christ isnt weak in dealing with you but shows his power among you.
local: Lɩɩ fan maa biti fɛɛ n kaapʋ fanɛ fɛɛ Krisito ɩɩ bɔla mɛ sʋ ɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ, fen biti fan wu. Lɩɩ fɛɛ man gyɛ yɛ o yuuli yɛ fanɛ nɩn, amaa ɔ gyɛ ɔlʋnpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ fanɛ tɔ.

eng: Certainly he was crucified because of weakness, but he lives by the power of God. Certainly we also are weak in him, but we will live together with him, because of Gods power that is directed toward you.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɩ gyɛ kesintin fɛɛ mu keyuuli sʋ nɩɩ ba da mʋ mata oyu laasa sʋ, amaa lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔlʋn tɔ sʋ mʋ ɔ tɛ nkpa tɔ. Kanɩn nɩɩ anɛ gbaa anɛ an yuuli fɛɛ mʋ, amaa lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔlʋn tɔ sʋ mʋ, an tii Krisito sʋ kɛ a bʋga kyɩna sun fanɛ.

eng: Examine yourselves to see if you are in the faith. Test yourselves. Dont you understand that Jesus Christ is in you? Unless, of course, you fail the test.
local: Fan kɩɩ fanɛ kakyɩna tɔ kɛ fan kɩɩ yɛ ɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ fan sʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii abɛɛ? Fan man nyi fɛɛ Krisito Yesu tɛ fanɛ tɔ? Ɔ tɛ fanɛ tɔ, amɔɔ kanɩn bɩrɛ fan tɩyɛ fanɛ kakyɩna tɔ nɩn.

eng: But I hope that you will realize that we dont fail the test.
local: Nɩɩ n sʋ tamaa fɛɛ fen biti fan bɩɩ yɛ anɛɛnɛ bɩrɛ a man tɩyɛ.

eng: We pray to God that you dont do anything wrong, not because we want to appear to pass the test but so that you might do the right thing, even if we appear to fail.
local: An kʋlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ fɛɛ fan man kan waa alibi. Ɩ man gyɛ yɛ asa ba wu anɛ yɛ an waa atɔ, amaa fan waa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ dɛ ɔkpa, halɩɩ nɩɩ i du fɛɛ an waa tɩyɛ gbaa.

eng: We cant do anything against the truth but only to help the truth.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ a mɛɛ taalɛ waa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kii tii Krisito kesintin mʋ ɔkpa, amɔɔ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ kyɛ kesintin mʋ tɔ nkʋn.

eng: We are happy when we are weak but you are strong. We pray for this: that you will be made complete.
local: Nɩɩ fan kan fan bʋlʋn, nɩɩ anɛɛnɛ an pɔɔ mʋ, ɩɩ waa anɛ ɔkɔn. Nɩɩ anɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ fan dan fanɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii tɔ naa lʋwɛ kyɛɛkyɛɛ.

eng: This is why Im writing these things while Im away. Im writing so that I wont need to act harshly when Im with you by using the authority that the Lord gave me. He gave it to me so that I could build you up, not tear you down.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ fan wu fɛɛ n maa man bʋ fanɛ asɛ falɛ mʋ, n kyʋrɔɔ fanɛ ɩnɩmʋ. Saamʋ nɩɩ n kan ba fanɛ asɛ mʋ, n mee biti fɛɛ n taa ɔlʋn mʋ nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu taa sa mɛ mʋ tɔwɛ ŋmanyan fanɛ. Ɔlʋn maŋa nɩɩ ɔ taa sa mɛ yɛ n kyɛ fanɛ tɔ, kɛ fan dan fanɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii tɔ, man gyɛ yɛ a taa ɔlʋn mʋ nin fanɛ tʋʋ.

eng: Finally, brothers and sisters, good-bye. Put things in order, respond to my encouragement, be in harmony with each other, and live in peace—and the God of love and peace will be with you.
local: Mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana, mɛ katɔwɛ mʋ laalaalʋwɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, fan kyɩna danbɩrasa, fan payɩ fanɛ anɛ ansi kɛ fanɛ kakyɩna tɔ kɩ waa ɔdan kyɛɛkyɛɛ, fan nu mɛ asɛ, fan tɔwɛ kyula abaa, kɛ fan kyɩna kayɩɩ‐yuuli tɔ. N kʋlɛ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ kebiti wuya yɛ kayɩɩ yuuli wuya mʋ, ɔ baa ɔ bʋ fanɛ asɛ.

eng: Say hello to each other with a holy kiss. All of Gods people say hello to you.
local: Fan taa kibiri abaa tɔ fwaala abaa.

eng: The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ, the love of God, and the fellowship of the Holy Spirit be with you all.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ asa mʋ kpini bɛɛ fwaala fanɛ.

eng: From Paul, an apostle who is not sent from human authority or commissioned through human agency, but sent through Jesus Christ and God the Father who raised him from the dead.
local: Mɛ Pɔɔlɩ mʋ nɩɩ n gyɛ Krisito osunpu, nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ asa kʋbʋ kʋ, abɛɛ ɔsa kʋ ɩ lɛɛ mɛ. Amaa Yesu Krisito yɛ Ɔsɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ o kyiŋi mʋ lɩɩ lewu tɔ mʋ ɩ lɛɛ mɛ waa osunpu.

eng: and from all the brothers and sisters with me. To the churches in Galatia.
local: N kyʋrɔɔ ɔwʋlɛ n kyʋŋɛ fanɛ Krisito kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ mʋ nɩɩ fan bʋ Galati ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ, nɩɩ akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ nfɩɩ mʋ kpini ba tii mɛ sʋ nɩɩ an fwaala fanɛ.

eng: Grace and peace to you from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Anɛ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ maa anɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Yesu Krisito ba bwaala fanɛ kɛ ba sa fanɛ kayɩɩ‐yuuli.

eng: He gave himself for our sins, so he could deliver us from this present evil age, according to the will of our God and Father.
local: Lɩɩ anɛ alibi sʋ mʋ, Yesu kyula taa mʋ n‐yɩɩ lɛɛ saraga wu sa anɛ, kɛ ɔ nyɛ lɛɛ anɛ lɩɩ ndaga kakyɩna libi ɔlʋn mʋ tɔ. Anɛ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kebiti tɔ nɩɩ o kyula nɩɩ ɩnɩmʋ kpini ɩ waa kanɩn.

eng: To God be the glory forever and always! Amen.
local: Bʋnyaa ɔ baa ɔ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ kakpaa! Amen.

eng: Im amazed that you are so quickly deserting the one who called you by the grace of Christ to follow another gospel.
local: I kyinkyin mɛ gaa fɛɛ fan maa tee biliŋi fanɛ kamaa sa Ɩbwaarɛ bilen, yɛgɛ mʋ ɩ bɔla Krisito kabwaala mʋ sʋ tɩɩ fanɛ. Nɩɩ fan biliŋi kpee fan buu asɩn kʋkwɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ akʋ bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ.

eng: Its not really another gospel, but certain people are confusing you and they want to change the gospel of Christ.
local: Kesintin yɛgɛ asɩn danbɩrasa kʋ man bɩla ɩ bʋ tɔ. Amaa n tɔwɛ fanɛ ɩnɩmʋ nɩn, lɩɩ fɛɛ asa akʋ bɛɛ wuliti fanɛ tɔ, bee biti fɛɛ ba kyɛɛgɛ Krisito asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ.

eng: However, even if we ourselves or a heavenly angel should ever preach anything different from what we preached to you, they should be under a curse.
local: Amaa, halɩɩ anɛ abɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ, kan lɩɩ sʋsʋ ba tɔwɛ fanɛ asɩn danbɩrasa kʋ, nɩɩ ɩ kwɩɩ yɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ an tɔwɛ sa fanɛ mʋ, mʋ laakʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ bɩɩtɛ mʋ kʋsʋ kakpaa.

eng: Im repeating what weve said before: if anyone preaches something different from what you received, they should be under a curse!.
local: Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ an wʋla tɔwɛ mʋ, n bɩla n pɩla tɔ yɛ, nɩɩ ɔsa kʋ kan ɛɛ tɔwɛ fanɛ asɩn danbɩrasa kʋ, nɩɩ ɩ kwɩɩ yɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ fan kɔɔlɛ waa fanɛ lɛɛ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ bɩɩtɛ mʋ kʋsʋ kakpaa.

eng: Am I trying to win over human beings or God? Or am I trying to please people? If I were still trying to please people, I wouldnt be Christs slave.
local: Nɩɩ ɩmʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ falɛ mʋ, ɩ kaapʋ nɩn fɛɛ n biti nyɩmɩsa ɔ kɔɔlɛ mɛ kyula nɩn abɛɛ? Ayee! N biti Ɩbwaarɛ nkʋn ɔ kɔɔlɛ mɛ kyula. N biti nyɩmɩsa ansi i gyii mɛ sʋ nɩn abɛɛ? Nɩɩ n kan n biti fɛɛ nyɩmɩsa ansi i gyii mɛ sʋ mʋ, naafɔɔ n mɛɛ yɛ n bɩla n gyɛ Krisito kɩyaafɔlɛ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I want you to know that the gospel I preached isnt human in origin.
local: Mɛ bɩrɩsa ana akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu, n biti fan bɩɩ fɛɛ, asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ mʋ, ɩ man gyɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɩ lɩɩ daadimaadi asɛ.

eng: I didnt receive it or learn it from a human. It came through a revelation from Jesus Christ.
local: Nɩɩ man gyɛ nyɩmɩsa kʋ asɛ nɩɩ n nyɛ ɩmʋ, abɛɛ ɔsa kʋ ɩ kaapʋ mɛ ɩmʋ. Amaa Yesu Krisito ɩ lɛɛ ɩmʋ kaapʋ mɛ.

eng: You heard about my previous life in Judaism, how severely I harassed Gods church and tried to destroy it.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ fan daa nu mɛ kakyɩna mʋ nɩɩ n bʋ kɩmʋ tɔ dɩdaa saŋa mʋ nɩɩ n daa n bʋ Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐sun mʋ tɔ mʋ. Nɩɩ fan nu kanan mʋ nɩɩ n daa taa kumu tɔ ɔlʋn nyanla Krisito abi mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ mʋ, nɩɩ n kpan mɛ n‐yɩɩ fɛɛ n nyita Krisito kabwaarɛ‐sun mʋ.

eng: I advanced in Judaism beyond many of my peers, because I was much more militant about the traditions of my ancestors.
local: Nɩɩ mɛ sara sʋ Gyiwu abi adamantɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ, n gyankpaa bamʋ anɛ Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐sun mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ anɛ anaana‐tɔ awaasa mʋ n yɛgɛ nɩɩ i gyii mi kumu, nɩɩ n kpan mɛ n‐yɩɩ yɛ ɩ yɩlɛ.

eng: But God had set me apart from birth and called me through his grace. He was pleased.
local: Amaa lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabwaala sʋ mʋ, ɔ wʋla lɛɛ mɛ yɩla pɔyɩ nɩɩ ba kʋʋgɛ mɛ, nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ mɛ yɛ n ba sun mʋ.

eng: to reveal his Son to me, so that I might preach about him to the Gentiles. I didnt immediately consult with any human being.
local: Nɩɩ ɩ bɔla Ɩbwaarɛ ansi fɛɛ ɔ lɛɛ Mu‐Bii mʋ kaapʋ mɛ, kɛ n tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ lɩɩ Mu‐Bii mʋ sʋ, sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ, nɩɩ n man taasɛ biti kɩkaapʋ lɩɩ ɔsa kʋ asɛ.

eng: I didnt go up to Jerusalem to see the men who were apostles before me either, but I went away into Arabia and I returned again to Damascus.
local: Nɩɩ n man kpee Gyerusalem naa wu bamʋ nɩɩ ba gyɛ Krisito asunpu mʋ pɔyɩ nɩɩ ngbaa mɛ n ba waa osunpu mʋ. Amaa n dan kʋsʋ opula maŋa tɔ nɩn, kpee Arabiya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ, nɩɩ kamaa tɔ n lɩɩ tʋtɔ bwii ba Damasiko kadɛ tɔ.

eng: Then after three years I went up to Jerusalem to visit Cephas and stayed with him fifteen days.
local: Nsu nsa kamaa tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ n kpee Gyerusalem naa wu Pita. Nɩɩ n kyɩna mʋ asɛ nkɛ kudu anu.

eng: But I didnt see any other of the apostles except James the brother of the Lord.
local: N man wu Krisito asunpu mʋ ɔkʋ tii sʋ, amɔɔ anɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Krisito mu‐supu Gyemisi nkʋn.

eng: Before God, Im not lying about the things that Im writing to you!.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ sʋ, kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ n kyʋrɔɔ n sa fanɛ falɛ gyɛ kesintin, n mɛɛ tɩn antɩŋɛsa.

eng: Then I went into the regions of Syria and Cilicia.
local: Ɩmʋ kamaa tɔ mʋ n kpee Siriya yɛ Kilisiya ɩsʋwʋlɛ sʋ.

eng: but I wasnt known personally by the Christian churches in Judea.
local: Kanɩn saŋa maŋa tɔ mʋ, Krisito abi kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ Gyudeya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ mʋ, ba man pɩɩ wu mɛ yɛ ansi.

eng: They only heard a report about me: The man who used to harass us now preaches the faith that he once tried to destroy.
local: Amaa kʋtɔ kʋlʋn mʋ nɩɩ ba nu lɩɩ asa asɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, Ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ daa ɛɛ nyanla anɛ mʋ, nbɩɩnbɩɩ bɩrɛ ɛɛ tɔwɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ Yesu gyii asɩn mʋ nɩɩ naafɔɔ ɔ daa kpan mʋ n‐yɩɩ fɛɛ o nyita mʋ.

eng: So they were glorifying God because of me.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ba yɩn Ɩbwaarɛ lɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba nu lɩɩ mɛ sʋ.

eng: You irrational Galatians! Who put a spell on you? Jesus Christ was put on display as crucified before your eyes!.
local: Fanɛ Galati abi nfɛɛrɛ ɩ man fʋʋ fanɛ! Anɩmʋ ɩ waa fanɛ biila kanɩn? Fanɛ gbaa‐gbaa fanɛ ansi tɔ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ bugi tɔ Yesu Krisito kelewu oyu laasa mʋ sʋ asɩn sa fanɛ.

eng: I just want to know this from you: Did you receive the Spirit by doing the works of the Law or by believing what you heard?.
local: Kʋtɔ kʋlʋn nɩɩ n biti n taasɛ fanɛ. Fan maa daa nyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu mʋ, Mosesi nbara kibuu mʋ sʋ nɩɩ fan nyɛ Kayaayu mʋ, abɛɛ fan maa nu asɩn danbɩrasa nɩɩ fan kɔɔlɛ gyii mʋ sʋ nɩɩ fan nyɛ kɩmʋ?.

eng: Are you so irrational? After you started with the Spirit, are you now finishing up with your own human effort?.
local: Mɩnɛ nɩɩ nfɛɛrɛ ɩ man fʋʋ fanɛ kanɩn? Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu nɩɩ fan taa piili fanɛ Krisito kusun mʋ, amaa nbɩɩnbɩɩ mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ fen biti fan taa fanɛ gbaa‐gbaa ɔlʋn taa lʋwɛ?.

eng: Did you experience so much for nothing? I wonder if it really was for nothing.
local: Awʋrʋfɔ mʋ nɩɩ fan wu falɛ kpini mʋ ɩ waa kɩyan nɩn abɛɛ? N kɔɔlɛ gyii fɛɛ ɩ man waa kɩyan.

eng: So does the one providing you with the Spirit and working miracles among you do this by you doing the works of the Law or by you believing what you heard?.
local: Fan nyi fɛɛ fan maa buu Mosesi nbara mʋ sʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sa fanɛ mʋ Kayaayu nɩɩ ɛɛ waa ɩsɩnkaala ɛɛ sa fanɛ mʋ, abɛɛ lɩɩ fan maa nu asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ, nɩɩ fan kɔɔlɛ gyii sʋ?.

eng: Understand that in the same way that Abraham believed God and it was credited to him as righteousness, .
local: Ba kyʋrɔɔ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ yɛ, Abraham kɩkɔɔlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gyii sʋ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kɔɔlɛ mʋ kyula fɛɛ mʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi.

eng: those who believe are the children of Abraham.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan bɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gyii mʋ, ɩ gyɛ Abraham anaana‐bii gbaa‐gbaa.

eng: But when it saw ahead of time that God would make the Gentiles righteous on the basis of faith, scripture preached the gospel in advance to Abraham: All the Gentiles will be blessed in you .
local: Ba gyankpaa kyʋrɔɔ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ fɛɛ lɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ kɔɔlɛ bamʋ kyula fɛɛ bamʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi. Kanɩn sʋ ɔ wʋla tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ sa Abraham yɛ, Fʋ sʋ nɩɩ n biti n bɔla waa dulinyaa asa kpini kusee.

eng: Therefore, those who believe are blessed together with Abraham who believed.
local: Abraham maa kɔɔlɛ gyii sʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ waa mʋ kusee. Ɩmʋ sʋ bamʋ nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ gyii mʋ kpini, Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ waa bamʋ kusee tii Abraham sʋ.

eng: All those who rely on the works of the Law are under a curse, because it is written, Everyone is cursed who does not keep on doing all the things that have been written in the Law scroll .
local: Nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba taa bamʋ n‐yɩɩ kpini gyaga nbara mʋ kibuu sʋ kɛ ba bɔla Ɩbwaarɛ ansi mʋ, ba bʋ Ɩbwaarɛ nnɔ kɩtɔwɛ mʋ tɔ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ba kyʋrɔɔ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ yɛ, Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man kyaga sʋ ee buu kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ waa Mosesi nbara ɔwʋlɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ nnɔ kɩtɔwɛ mʋ gyan bamʋ sʋ.

eng: But since no one is made righteous by the Law as far as God is concerned, it is clear that the righteous one will live on the basis of faith .
local: I bugi tɔ kaapʋ kesintin fɛɛ, ɔkʋ man bʋ tɔ nɩɩ ɛɛ bɔla nbara kibuu mʋ sʋ, kɛ Ɩbwaarɛ o wu mʋ fɛɛ mʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi mʋ asɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ, ba kyʋrɔɔ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ yɛ, Kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii tɔ nɩɩ ɩkpa kyiigisa wuya ii biti ɔ nyɛ nkpa.

eng: The Law isnt based on faith; rather, the one doing these things will live by them .
local: Nbara mʋ kibuu man gyan kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ yɛ, Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ waa kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ nbara mʋ ɩ kaapʋ mʋ, ee biti ɔ nyɛ nkpa lɩɩ ɩmʋ kibuu sʋ.

eng: Christ redeemed us from the curse of the Law by becoming a curse for us—because it is written, Everyone who is hung on a tree is cursed .
local: Amaa nnɔ kɩtɔwɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ gyan anɛ sʋ fɛɛ a maa man buu nbara mʋ, Krisito ka ɩmʋ kʋkɔ. Maa wu oyu laasa sʋ mʋ, ɔ kɔɔlɛ kɔnɔ tɔwɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyan anɛ sʋ mʋ sʋla. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ba kyʋrɔɔ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ yɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ nnɔ waa ɔsa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ saa oyu sʋ mʋ.

eng: He redeemed us so that the blessing of Abraham would come to the Gentiles through Christ Jesus, and that we would receive the promise of the Spirit through faith.
local: Krisito ka anɛ alibi kʋkɔ, kɛ kusee mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ waa Abraham mʋ, ɩ nyɛ bɔla Krisito Yesu sʋ taa ba sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ gbaa, taa lɩɩ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ mʋ, a nyɛ Kayaayu mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ yɛ ee biti ɔ sa mʋ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, Ill use an example from human experience. No one ignores or makes additions to a validated will.
local: Mɛ bɩrɩsa ana Krisito abuupu, n biti n taa dulinyaa kakyɩna nɩmʋ tɔ asɩn kaala asɩn nɩmʋ. Nɩɩ asa kʋ ba kan bɔ ntan yɩla bamʋ nsana mʋ, ɔkʋ mɛɛ taalɛ nyita ɩmʋ abɛɛ ɔ taa kʋtɔ kʋ tii sʋ.

eng: The promises were made to Abraham and to his descendant. It doesnt say, and to the descendants, as if referring to many rather than just one. It says, and to your descendant, who is Christ.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ maa da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ sa Abraham maa mʋ kanaana‐bii mʋ, ba man kyʋrɔɔ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ yɛ, fʋ anaana‐bii, nɩɩ ɩmʋ ii biti ɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ asa mʋ ba kyɔ. Amaa asɩn mʋ ɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, fʋ kanaana‐bii, nɩɩ ɩmʋ kasɛ ɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ mʋ kanaana‐bii kʋlʋn, nɩɩ obii maŋa ɩ gyɛ Krisito.

eng: Im saying this: the Law, which came four hundred thirty years later, doesnt invalidate the agreement that was previously validated by God so that it cancels the promise.
local: Kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ n kaapʋ mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ sa Abraham. Nɩɩ nsu ana yɛ adɩsa kamaa tɔ pɔyɩ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sa nbara mʋ, amaa nbara mʋ ɩ man taalɛ nyita abɛɛ kpayɩ katɔwɛ kyula mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ waa yɩla mʋ.

eng: If the inheritance were based upon the Law, it would no longer be from the promise. But God has given it graciously to Abraham through a promise.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ ɩ kan ɩ gyɛ nbara mʋ kibuu sʋ nɩɩ nyɩmɩsa ii biti ɔ nyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kusee mʋ, kanɩn bɩrɛ, ɩ man gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ kɩda mʋ sʋ nɩɩ ɩ bɩla ɩ gyan. Amaa lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabwaala mʋ sʋ mʋ, ɔ bɔla mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ kɩda mʋ sʋ taa kusee sa Abraham.

eng: So why was the Law given? It was added because of offenses, until the descendant would come to whom the promise had been made. It was put in place through angels by the hand of a mediator.
local: Nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ kanɩn bɩrɛ, mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sa nbara mʋ? Ɩbwaarɛ daa taa nbara mʋ nɩn tii sʋ kɛ ɩ kaapʋ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ alibi ɩ gyɛ. Ɩ dagaa nbara mʋ maa kyɩna naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Abraham kanaana‐bii mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ yɩla sa mʋ mʋ, ɔ ba. Ɩbwaarɛ taa nbara mʋ bɔla mʋ nbɔɔ sʋ taa sa Mosesi mʋ nɩɩ ɔ yɩlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ maa asa nsana mʋ abaa tɔ.

eng: Now the mediator does not take one side; but God is one.
local: Amaa nɩɩ ɩ dan ɩ gyɛ nyɩmɩsa kʋlʋn mʋ, nsana ɔyɩlɛpʋ man tiri. Ɩbwaarɛ bɩrɛ gyɛ ɔkʋlʋn nɩn.

eng: So, is the Law against the promises of God? Absolutely not! If a Law had been given that was able to give life, then righteousness would in fact have come from the Law.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ɩɩ kaapʋ nɩn fɛɛ, nbara mʋ yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ kɩda mʋ, mɛɛ kyɩna abaa sʋ nɩn abɛɛ? Ayee, man gyɛ kanɩn nɩn kpini‐kpini! Lɩɩ fɛɛ nɩɩ nyɩmɩsa daa nyɛ nbara kʋ nɩɩ ɩ taalɛ sa nyɩmɩsa nkpa mʋ, naafɔɔ kanɩn nbara maŋa kibuu ɩ taalɛ yɛgɛ nyɩmɩsa ɩkpa i kyiigi Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ.

eng: But scripture locked up all things under sin, so that the promise based on the faithfulness of Jesus Christ might be given to those who have faith.
local: Amaa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ ɩɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, dulinyaa asa kpini ba bʋ alibi kɩbaa tɔ nɩn. Ɩmʋ sʋ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ yɛ ee biti ɔ sa mʋ, gyan Yesu Krisito kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ, nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ gyii mʋ, nɩɩ ee biti ɔ taa sa.

eng: Before faith came, we were guarded under the Law, locked up until faith that was coming would be revealed.
local: Pɔyɩ kɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii mʋ kɩ ba mʋ, nbara mʋ ɩ dan nyan anɛ waa tɔ nɩn fɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba tii bamʋ obu biri sʋ, naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii mʋ kɩ ba lɩɩ ifuli.

eng: so that the Law became our custodian until Christ so that we might be made righteous by faith.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ nbara mʋ ɩ kyɩna ɩ gyɛ anɛ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Krisito ba. Saamʋ, bɔla lɩɩ anɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti o wu fɛɛ anɛ ɩkpa i kyiigi mʋ asɛ.

eng: But now that faith has come, we are no longer under a custodian.
local: Nbɩɩnbɩɩ kɩkɔɔlɛ Krisito gyii maa ba falɛ bɩrɛ, a man bɩla an bʋ nbara mʋ nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ anɛ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ mʋ kasɛ.

eng: You are all Gods children through faith in Christ Jesus.
local: Kpalɩ fɛɛ fan maa kɔɔlɛ Krisito Yesu gyii sʋ mʋ, fanɛ kpini fan gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mu‐bii ana.

eng: All of you who were baptized into Christ have clothed yourselves with Christ.
local: Nɩɩ fanɛ kpini mʋ nɩɩ ba gyee fanɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee waa Krisito lɛɛ mʋ, ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ Krisito kudubi mʋ bʋ fanɛ tɔ.

eng: There is neither Jew nor Greek; there is neither slave nor free; nor is there male and female, for you are all one in Christ Jesus.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ bɩrɛ, ɩ man bɩla ɩ bʋ tɔ fɛɛ akʋ ba gyɛ Gyiwu abi abɛɛ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi, abɛɛ akʋ ba gyɛ anyɛ abɛɛ akʋ ba gyɛ ndɛ abi, abɛɛ akʋ ba gyɛ anyɩn abɛɛ ba gyɛ akyɩɩ, lɩɩ fɛɛ fanɛ kpini fan gyɛ kʋkʋlʋn Krisito Yesu tɔ.

eng: Now if you belong to Christ, then indeed you are Abrahams descendants, heirs according to the promise.
local: Nɩɩ fan kan fan gyɛ Krisito lɛɛ mʋ, kanɩn bɩrɛ fan gyɛ Abraham anaana‐bii, nɩɩ fen biti fan nyɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ sa Abraham mʋ.

eng: Then after fourteen years I went up to Jerusalem again with Barnabas, and I took Titus along also.
local: Nsu kudu ana kamaa tɔ mʋ, n bwii kpee Gyerusalem. N maa Banabasi i kpee nɩɩ n taa Titusi tii sʋ kpee.

eng: I went there because of a revelation, and I laid out the gospel that I preach to the Gentiles for them. But I did it privately with the influential leaders to make sure that I wouldnt be working or that I hadnt worked for nothing.
local: N kpee tʋtɔ nɩn, lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ lɛɛ kaapʋ mɛ fɛɛ n kpee. N maa abɩlɩsa mʋ an gyaŋɛ anɛ nkʋn, nɩɩ n lɛɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ n sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ kasɛ kaapʋ bamʋ. N waa kanɩn nɩn kɛ ɩ man naa ba fɛɛ kusun mʋ nɩɩ n waa yɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ n san n waa falɛ mʋ, ɩ man ba biliŋi kɩyan.

eng: However, not even Titus, who was with me and who was a Greek, was required to be circumcised.
local: Amaa Titusi mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ mɛ asɛ, yɛgɛ be nyi maa gyɛ Giriki nyɩmɩsa gbaa mʋ, ɔkʋ man nyan mʋ yɛ ɔ tɩn mʋ oduu.

eng: But false brothers and sisters, who were brought in secretly, slipped in to spy on our freedom, which we have in Christ Jesus, and to make us slaves.
local: Amaa falɛ asɩn nɩmʋ ɩ ba, lɩɩ fɛɛ asa kʋ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ pɩna yɛ ba gyɛ akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu fɛɛ anɛ mʋ ba be loo anɛ tɔ, kɛ ba ba kɩɩ anɛ n‐yɩɩ kɩnyɛ Krisito Yesu tɔ, kɛ ba nyɛ taa anɛ biliŋi Gyiwu nbara mʋ anyɛ.

eng: We didnt give in and submit to them for a single moment, so that the truth of the gospel would continue to be with you.
local: Amaa a man daa yɛgɛ anɛ n‐yɩɩ sa bamʋ. An waa kanɩn nɩn kɛ kesintin asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ ɩ nyɛ sii fanɛ tɔ kanɩn.

eng: The influential leaders didnt add anything to what I was preaching—and whatever they were makes no difference to me, because God doesnt show favoritism.
local: Nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba daa ba gyɛ Krisito kʋbʋ mʋ agyankpaapʋ mʋ, ba man taa sɛhn tii mɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ nɩɩ n kaapʋ mʋ sʋ. Halɩɩ ba maa gyɛ agyankpaapʋ gbaa mʋ, ɩ man tiri mɛ. Lɛɛ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ bɩrɛ mɛɛ kɩɩ nyɩmɩsa ansi sʋ.

eng: But on the contrary, they saw that I had been given the responsibility to preach the gospel to the people who arent circumcised, just as Peter had been to the circumcised.
local: Amaa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ agyankpaapʋ banɩmʋ ba wu ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ taa asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ waa mɛ abaa tɔ yɛ n tɔwɛ ɩmʋ sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa asɩn kʋlʋn maŋa waa Pita abaa tɔ yɛ ɔ tɔwɛ sa Gyiwu abi mʋ.

eng: The one who empowered Peter to become an apostle to the circumcised empowered me also to be one to the Gentiles.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ mʋ ɔlʋn bɔla Pita, mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ Krisito osunpu sa Gyiwu abi, nɩɩ ɔlʋn mʋ ii sun kusun bamʋ tɔ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ maŋa ɔlʋn ɩ bɔla mɛ, Pɔɔlɩ gbaa, mʋ nɩɩ n gyɛ Krisito osunpu sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ, nɩɩ ɔlʋn maŋa ii sun kusun bamʋ tɔ.

eng: James, Cephas, and John, who are considered to be key leaders, shook hands with me and Barnabas as equals when they recognized the grace that was given to me. So it was agreed that we would go to the Gentiles, while they continue to go to the people who were circumcised.
local: Gyemisi maa Pita yɛ Gyɔn nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ nyi ba maa gyɛ kʋbʋ mʋ abɩlɩsa gbaa‐gbaa mʋ, ba bɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ bwaala ngbaa mɛ. Ɩmʋ sʋ ba gyɔɔ n maa Banabasi abaa tɔ kaapʋ fɛɛ anaa bamʋ kpini an sun kusun kʋlʋn. Nɩɩ an kyula yɛ n maa Banabasi a waa kusun bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ tɔ, kɛ bamʋ mɔɔ ba waa kusun mʋ Gyiwu abi tɔ.

eng: They asked only that we would remember the poor, which was certainly something I was willing to do.
local: Kʋtɔ kʋlʋn mʋ nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ anɛ tii sʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, a nyiŋi atiripu mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ bamʋ tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ n wʋla payɩ mi ansi n waa.

eng: But when Cephas came to Antioch, I opposed him to his face, because he was wrong.
local: Saŋa kʋ Pita maa kyɩna ba Antiyoki mʋ, n tɔwɛ tigi mʋ yɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa mʋ man dɛ ɔkpa.

eng: He had been eating with the Gentiles before certain people came from James. But when they came, he began to back out and separate himself, because he was afraid of the people who promoted circumcision.
local: Asɩn mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, pɔyɩ kɛ Gyemisi o sun anyɩn akʋ kpee Antiyoki mʋ, naafɔɔ Pita maa Krisito abuupu mʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ ɩ bʋga bee gyii lapɛ kʋlʋn tɔ. Amaa saŋa mʋ nɩɩ anyɩn mʋ ba naa fʋʋ tʋtɔ mʋ, Pita tɩn mʋ n‐yɩɩ lɩɩ Krisito abuupu mʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ asɛ kigyii, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ sɩlɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi gbaa ba tɩn oduu mʋ.

eng: And the rest of the Jews also joined him in this hypocrisy so that even Barnabas got carried away with them in their hypocrisy.
local: Kanɩn gbaa nɩn, nɩɩ Gyiwu abi asansa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ tʋtɔ mʋ ba gyɛ nnɔ nnyɔ‐nnyɔ wuya ana fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Pita waa mʋ, halɩɩ ɩ yɛgɛ Banabasi gbaa fɔyɩ ɔkpa o buu bamʋ.

eng: But when I saw that they werent acting consistently with the truth of the gospel, I said to Cephas in front of everyone, If you, though youre a Jew, live like a Gentile and not like a Jew, how can you require the Gentiles to live like Jews.
local: N maa wu fɛɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa mʋ, man buu asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ kesintin ɔkpa sʋ mʋ, n tɔwɛ Pita Krisito abuupu mʋ kpini ansi tɔ yɛ, Gyiwu obii ɩ gyɛ fʋ, yɛgɛ fʋ kakyɩna tɔ du fɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi. Mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ fɩɩ nyan Krisito abuupu mʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ yɛ ba kyɩna fɛɛ Gyiwu abi.

eng: We are born Jews—were not Gentile sinners.
local: Kakʋʋgɛ tɔ mʋ, maa fʋ an gyɛ Gyiwu abi gbaa‐gbaa, a man gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi, nɩɩ ba man buu Gyiwu nbara mʋ.

eng: However, we know that a person isnt made righteous by the works of the Law but rather through the faithfulness of Jesus Christ. We ourselves believed in Christ Jesus so that we could be made righteous by the faithfulness of Christ and not by the works of the Law—because no one will be made righteous by the works of the Law.
local: Amaa an nyi fɛɛ ɩ man gyɛ Mosesi nbara mʋ kibuu sʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ taa nyɩmɩsa fɛɛ, mʋ ɩkpa ii kyiigi, amɔɔ ɔ kɔɔlɛ Yesu Krisito gyii. Ɩmʋ sʋ, amɔɔ anɛ gbaa anɛ an kɔɔlɛ Krisito Yesu gyii, kɛ Ɩbwaarɛ o wu anɛ fɛɛ anɛ ɩkpa ii kyiigi lɩɩ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii mʋ nɩɩ an sʋ Krisito tɔ, nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ nbara mʋ kibuu sʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋ man bʋ tɔ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ i wu fɛɛ maa buu nbara mʋ sʋ nɩɩ mʋ ɩkpa ii kyiigi.

eng: But if it is discovered that we ourselves are sinners while we are trying to be made righteous in Christ, then is Christ a servant of sin? Absolutely not!.
local: Nɩɩ ɩ kan ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ anɛ kɩbɔla Krisito sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ i wu fɛɛ anɛ ɩkpa i kyiigi mʋ asɛ, nɩɩ ɩnɩmʋ ɩɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ an wu fɛɛ anɛ gbaa anɛ an gyɛ alibi awaapʋ fɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ, ɩnɩmʋ ɩɩ kaapʋ nɩn fɛɛ Krisito sa alibi kɩwaa ɔlʋn tii sʋ nɩn abɛɛ? Ayee! Ɩ man gyɛ kanɩn!.

eng: If I rebuild the very things that I tore down, I show that I myself am breaking the Law.
local: Nɩɩ n kan kina nbara mʋ, nɩɩ n bwii n kyiŋi ɩmʋ mʋ, kanɩn bɩrɛ n tɩyɛ alibi tɔ.

eng: I died to the Law through the Law, so that I could live for God.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ n maa buu nbara mʋ, n wu fɛɛ ɩ mɛɛ sa nkpa. Ɩmʋ sʋ n wu sa nbara mʋ, kɛ n nyɛ kyɩna sa Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: I have been crucified with Christ and I no longer live, but Christ lives in me. And the life that I now live in my body, I live by faith, indeed, by the faithfulness of Gods Son, who loved me and gave himself for me.
local: Nɩɩ ɩ waa nɩn fɛɛ n maa Krisito nɩɩ ba daa mata oyu laasa sʋ. Nɩɩ n man bɩla n tɛ sa mɛ n‐yɩɩ, amaa Krisito Kayaayu mʋ kɩ bʋ mɛ tɔ. Nɩɩ kakyɩna mʋ nɩɩ n tɛ kɩmʋ tɔ nbɩɩnbɩɩ falɛ mʋ, kɩkɔɔlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mu‐Bii gyii mʋ tɔ nɩɩ n tɛ, mʋ ii biti mɛ asɩn, nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ nkpa yɩla sa mɛ.

eng: I dont ignore the grace of God, because if we become righteous through the Law, then Christ died for no purpose.
local: Nɩɩ n man taa Ɩbwaarɛ kabwaala mʋ yɩla ɩkaa sʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ nɩɩ ɩ kan ɩ gyɛ nbara mʋ kɩbɔla sʋ nɩɩ nyɩmɩsa ɩkpa ii biti i kyiigi Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ mʋ, kanɩn bɩrɛ ɩ kaapʋ nɩn fɛɛ Krisito kelewu mʋ man tiri.

eng: Im saying that as long as the heirs are minors, they are no different from slaves, though they really are the owners of everything.
local: Kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, kebii‐pii mʋ nɩɩ ee biti o gyii mʋ‐sɛ kapatɩyɛ nɩɩ ɔ man pɩɩ dan, nɩɩ halɩɩ kʋtɔ kʋmaa maa gyɛ mʋ lɛɛ gbaa mʋ, ɔ man kwɩɩ yɛ kɩnyɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ kɩkpaara mʋ sʋ mʋ.

eng: However, they are placed under trustees and guardians until the date set by the parents.
