local: Lɩɩ ɩnɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa maa lɛɛ mʋ gbaa‐gbaa n‐yɩɩ sʋ alibi saraga fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ lɛɛ asa mʋ alibi saraga mʋ.

eng: No one takes this honor for themselves but takes it only when they are called by God, just like Aaron.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ mɛɛ taalɛ taa bɛɛrɛɛ sa mʋ n‐yɩɩ fɛɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ, amɔɔ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ lɛɛ mʋ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kyɩna lɛɛ Aron mʋ.

eng: In the same way Christ also didnt promote himself to become high priest. Instead, it was the one who said to him, You are my Son. Today I have become your Father .
local: Kanɩn ɔkpa maŋa sʋ nɩɩ Krisito gbaa man sa mʋ n‐yɩɩ bɛɛrɛɛ fɛɛ ɔ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ. Amaa Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ mʋ nɩn tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Fʋ ɩ gyɛ Mi‐bii, Lɩɩ ndaga n gyɛ Fʋ‐Sɛ.

eng: as he also says in another place, You are a priest forever, according to the order of Melchizedek. .
local: Nɩɩ ɔ bɩla tɔwɛ katɩn kʋ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ yɛ, Fʋ gyɛ oseepu kakpaa fɛɛ ɔkpa kʋlʋn mʋ nɩɩ Mekisedeki kyɩna ɔ gyɛ mʋ.

eng: During his days on earth, Christ offered prayers and requests with loud cries and tears as his sacrifices to the one who was able to save him from death. He was heard because of his godly devotion.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Yesu tɛ nkpa tɔ kasɛ sʋ mʋ, ɔ sawʋ kenken yɛ ansikyu kʋlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ taalɛ lɛɛ mʋ lɩɩ lewu tɔ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ maa bɩya mʋ n‐yɩɩ kasɛ sa Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ, o nu Yesu asɛ.

eng: Although he was a Son, he learned obedience from what he suffered.
local: Halɩɩ maa gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mu‐Bii gbaa mʋ, ɔ bɩɩla fɛɛ kunu Ɩbwaarɛ kadɛ ɩ bɩya awʋrʋfɔ kigyii.

eng: After he had been made perfect, he became the source of eternal salvation for everyone who obeys him.
local: Maa taalɛ waa Ɩbwaarɛ kɔkɔlɔ biti lʋwɛ kyɛɛkyɛɛ sʋ mʋ, mʋ o be biliŋi nkpa kamɔlɩgɛ mu kumu, nɩɩ ɛɛ sa bamʋ nɩɩ bee nu mʋ asɛ mʋ nkpa kakpaa.

eng: He was appointed by God to be a high priest according to the order of Melchizedek.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ lɛɛ mʋ waa mu oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Mekisedeki kyɩna ɔ gyɛ mʋ.

eng: We have a lot to say about this topic, and its difficult to explain, because you have been lazy and you havent been listening.
local: Naafɔɔ n sʋ asɩn damantɛ n tɔwɛ fanɛ lɩɩ ɩnɩmʋ sʋ, amaa fan maa mɛɛ nu asɩn kasɛ bilen sʋ mʋ, ɩ bʋlʋn gaa sa fɛɛ n lɛɛ ɩmʋ kasɛ sa fanɛ.

eng: Although you should have been teachers by now, you need someone to teach you an introduction to the basics about Gods message. You have come to the place where you need milk instead of solid food.
local: Naafɔɔ halɩɩ nbɩɩnbɩɩ nɩmʋ gbaa, ɩ dagaa fɛɛ fan maa tɛɛ fan gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn mʋ akaapʋpʋ. Amaa ɩ maa san i tiri sa fanɛ fɛɛ fan nyɛ ɔsa kʋ, kɛ ɔ bɩla lɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn gyankpaasa mʋ kaapʋ fanɛ mʋ, fan du nɩn fɛɛ nbii pʋpwɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩnyapʋ tiri sa bamʋ, nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ agyitɔ budusa.

eng: Everyone who lives on milk is not used to the word of righteousness, because they are babies.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ san ɛɛ nyapʋ kɩnyapʋ mʋ, ɔ man pɩɩta dan. Ɔ san ɔ gyɛ kebii nɩɩ o mee nu Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ nyɩmɩsa ɩkpa i kyiigi mʋ kasɛ.

eng: But solid food is for the mature, whose senses are trained by practice to distinguish between good and evil.
local: Amaa agyitɔ budusa ɩ gyɛ sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba bɩɩla nɩɩ ba dan nɩɩ ba nu Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn, nɩɩ ba bɩɩ adanbɩrasa yɛ alibi mʋ.

eng: Therefore, since the promise that we can enter into rest is still open, lets be careful so that none of you will appear to miss it.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ maa da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ lɩɩ mu opula fʋʋtɛkpa kuloo mʋ, nɩɩ kɩ maa san kɩ dɛ sa anɛ sʋ mʋ, fan yɛgɛ a baa an da sʋ yɛ ɔdan, kɛ ɩ man ba fɛɛ fanɛ tɔ ɔkʋ ɔ fɔyɩ kɛ ɔ man nyɛ opula fʋʋtɛkpa mʋ tɔ loo.

eng: We also had the good news preached to us, just as the Israelites did. However, the message they heard didnt help them because they werent united in faith with the ones who listened to it.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ anɛ gbaa anɛ an nyɛ nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ sa anɛ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ sa ngbaa bamʋ mʋ. Amaa ba man nyɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba nu mʋ tɔnɔ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ba maa nu waa bamʋ asʋ tɔ mʋ, ba man kɔɔlɛ gyii bamʋ nkɔlɔ tɔ.

eng: We who have faith are entering the rest. As God said, And because of my anger I swore: They will never enter into my rest! And yet Gods works were completed at the foundation of the world.
local: Kanɩn sʋ nbɩɩnbɩɩ anɛ mʋ nɩɩ an kɔɔlɛ gyii mʋ, anɛ ii biti a loo mu opula fʋʋtɛkpa mʋ tɔ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, N nyɛ agbʋ bɔ ntan yɛ, Ba mɛɛ yɛ ba nyɛ loo mi opula fʋʋtɛkpa mʋ tɔ. Halɩɩ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ kanɩn mʋ, ɔ tɛɛ wʋla lʋwɛ dulinyaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tʋʋ mʋ kusun.

eng: Then somewhere he said this about the seventh day of creation: God rested on the seventh day from all his works. .
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ ɩ tɔwɛ katɩn kʋ lɩɩ kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa mʋ sʋ yɛ, Kakɛ sunɔsapʋ mʋ tɔ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ fʋʋtɛ lɩɩ kusun mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa mʋ kpini tɔ.

eng: But again, in the passage above, God said, They will never enter my rest! .
local: Nɩɩ ɩ bɩla tɔwɛ lɩɩ asɩn nɩmʋ sʋ yɛ, Ba mɛɛ yɛ ba nyɛ loo mi opula fʋʋtɛkpa mʋ tɔ.

eng: Therefore, its left open for some to enter it, and the ones who had the good news preached to them before didnt enter because of disobedience.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ mʋ kɩ san kɩ dɛ fɛɛ akʋ bee biti ba nyɛ loo opula fʋʋtɛkpa mʋ tɔ. Amaa bamʋ nɩɩ ba gyankpaa nu asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ, ba waa kʋsʋ tɔ ɔlʋn, nɩɩ ba man nyɛ loo kɩmʋ tɔ.

eng: Just as it says in the passage above, God designates a certain day as today, when he says through David much later, Today, if you hear his voice, dont have stubborn hearts. .
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ Ɩbwaarɛ bɩla yɩla kakɛ kʋ, nɩɩ ɛɛ tɩɩ kɩmʋ yɛ, Ndaga. Nɩɩ ɩ maa kyɛɛrɛɛ kamaa tɔ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ bɔla Dawidi sʋ tɔwɛ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ wʋla tɔwɛ asɩn maŋa mʋ yɛ, Ndaga, nɩɩ fan kan nu mʋ kɔnɔ tɔ asɩn mʋ, fan man kan waa kɔkɔlɔ ɔsɩɩ yɛ fɛn mɛɛ yɛ fan nu.

eng: If Joshua gave the Israelites rest, God wouldnt have spoken about another day later on.
local: Fɛɛ nɩɩ Gyosuwa dɩn sa bamʋ ɔfʋʋtɛ nɩn, naafɔɔ kamaa tɔ Ɩbwaarɛ man dɩn bɩla tɔwɛ kakɛ pʋpwɛ kʋ asɩn.

eng: So you see that a sabbath rest is left open for Gods people.
local: Kanɩn bɩrɛ ɔfʋʋtɛ fɛɛ kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa mʋ kɩ san kɩ dɛ sa Ɩbwaarɛ asa, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ fʋʋtɛ kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ mʋ.

eng: The one who entered Gods rest also rested from his works, just as God rested from his own.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ loo Ɩbwaarɛ opula fʋʋtɛkpa mʋ tɔ mʋ, ɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ ɔ fʋʋtɛ lɩɩ mʋ gbaa‐gbaa kusun waasa sʋ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ gbaa fʋʋtɛ lɩɩ mu kusun sʋ mʋ.

eng: Therefore, lets make every effort to enter that rest so that no one will fall by following the same example of disobedience.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan yɛgɛ a kpan anɛ n‐yɩɩ ɔkpa kʋmaa sʋ, kɛ a loo kanɩn opula fʋʋtɛkpa mʋ tɔ, kɛ ɩ man nyɛ ba fɛɛ fanɛ tɔ ɔkʋ ɔ waa kʋsʋ tɔ ɔlʋn, kɛ ɔ fɔyɩ ɔkpa naa lɩɩ tɩyɛ fɛɛ bamʋ mʋ.

eng: because Gods word is living, active, and sharper than any two-edged sword. It penetrates to the point that it separates the soul from the spirit and the joints from the marrow. Its able to judge the hearts thoughts and intentions.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn mʋ ɩ sʋ nkpa, nɩɩ ii sun kusun. Nɩɩ asɩn mʋ ɩ bʋyɛ ɩ kyɔ sakunde gyimba anyɔ, nɩɩ ɩ taalɛ nyɛ loo nyɩmɩsa kɔkɔlɔ tɔ yɛ mʋ nfɛɛrɛ tɔ, nɩɩ ɩɩ barɩgɛ nyɩmɩsa ɩkyagakpa yɛ awiya tɔ npu. Nɩɩ kɩ bɩla kɩ talɛ nyɩmɩsa kɔkɔlɔ yɛ mʋ nfɛɛrɛ tɔ.

eng: No creature is hidden from it, but rather everything is naked and exposed to the eyes of the one to whom we have to give an answer.
local: Nɩɩ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tʋʋ mʋ kʋkʋ man bʋ tɔ, nɩɩ kɩ baala mu ansi sʋ. Amaa kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ kɩ dɛ tentengilen mu ansi sʋ. Nɩɩ anɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ ii biti ɔ yɩlɛ lɛɛ mʋ kɔnɔ mu ansi tɔ.

eng: Also, lets hold on to the confession since we have a great high priest who passed through the heavens, who is Jesus, Gods Son.
local: Lɩɩ kanɩn sʋ fan yɛgɛ a yɩlɛ anɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii mʋ nɩɩ an sʋ mʋ tɔ kenken, lɩɩ fɛɛ an sʋ Ɩbwaarɛ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ kparɛ. Mʋ ɩ gyɛ Yesu, Ɩbwaarɛ mu‐bii mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kyʋn sʋsʋ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ mʋ.

eng: because we dont have a high priest who cant sympathize with our weaknesses but instead one who was tempted in every way that we are, except without sin.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ anɛ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ man gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ taalɛ wu anɛ kʋwɛɛ lɩɩ ntɩn mʋ nɩɩ a mɛɛ taalɛ an kɩtaa anɛ n‐yɩɩ mʋ. Amaa an sʋ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ kʋtʋʋ tɔ kɩɩ atɔ kpini tɔ fɛɛ anɛ, nɩɩ mɛɛ mʋ man waa alibi kʋ.

eng: Finally, lets draw near to the throne of favor with confidence so that we can receive mercy and find grace when we need help.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan yɛgɛ a nyɛ kɔkɔlɔ yɩlɛ tɔ, ba Ɩbwaarɛ ansi tɔ mu kuwura‐gya mʋ asɛ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ kabwaala kɩ bʋ, kɛ o wu anɛ kʋwɛɛ kɛ a nyɛ kabwaala mʋ nkyɛtɔ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ i tiri san anɛ mʋ.

eng: Therefore, brothers and sisters who are partners in the heavenly calling, think about Jesus, the apostle and high priest of our confession.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana, Ɩbwaarɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ fanɛ mʋ, fan yɛgɛ fanɛ nfɛɛrɛ ɩ baa ɩ gyan Yesu sʋ. Mʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ osunpu yɛ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ nɩɩ an kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii nɩɩ an lɛɛ an tɔwɛ mʋ.

eng: Jesus was faithful to the one who appointed him just like Moses was faithful in Gods house.
local: Nɩɩ ee nu Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ mʋ mʋ asɛ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Mosesi nu Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa tɔ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ayɛ asa kɩkɩɩ sʋ mʋ.

eng: But he deserves greater glory than Moses in the same way that the builder of the house deserves more honor than the house itself.
local: Yesu gyɛ nyɩmɩsa kparɛ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ bɛɛrɛɛ lala ɔ kyɔ Mosesi, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ kɩkpaara ɔpɔyɩpʋ sʋ bɛɛrɛɛ lala ɔ kyɔ kɩkpaara mʋ gbaa‐gbaa.

eng: Every house is built by someone, but God is the builder of everything.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ kɩkpaara kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ba pɔyɩ mʋ, ɔkʋ ɩ pɔyɩ kɩmʋ, amaa Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ gyɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ ɔpɔyɩpʋ.

eng: Moses was faithful in all Gods house as a servant in order to affirm the things that would be spoken later.
local: Nɩɩ Mosesi gyɛ kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kɩkpaara mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ee gyii kesintin kʋtɔ kʋmaa tɔ. Nɩɩ ɛɛ tɔwɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ tɔwɛ kakaakʋ.

eng: But Jesus was faithful over Gods house as a Son. We are his house if we hold on to the confidence and the pride that our hope gives us.
local: Amaa Krisito ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Mu‐Bii nɩɩ ɛɛ kɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kɩkpaara asa sʋ yɛ kesintin. Nɩɩ an kan taa kɔkɔlɔ ɔsɩɩ, yɩlɛ tamaa mʋ tɔ kɩnyɩn tɔ mʋ, ɩmʋ ɩɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ anɛ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kɩkpaara mʋ sʋ asa.

eng: So, as the Holy Spirit says, Today, if you hear his voice, .
local: Kanɩn sʋ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, Nɩɩ fan kan nu mʋ kɔnɔ tɔ asɩn ndaga mʋ.

eng: dont have stubborn hearts as they did in the rebellion, on the day when they tested me in the desert. .
local: fan man waa nkɔlɔ ɔsɩɩ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ anɛ naana ana ba daa waa, nɩɩ ba kʋsʋ yɩlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ba tʋʋ mʋ tɔ kɩɩ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ ofuli sʋ mʋ.

eng: That is where your ancestors challenged and tested me, though they had seen my work for forty years. .
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ fanɛ naana ana ba waa mʋ, nɩɩ ba tʋʋ mʋ tɔ kɩɩ, nɩɩ ba wu atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa nsu adɩna mʋ tɔ mʋ.

eng: So I was angry with them. I said, Their hearts always go off course, and they dont know my ways. .
local: Kanɩn sʋ nɩɩ ɩ waa mʋ agbʋ sa kanɩn kanaana maŋa asa, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Saŋa kʋmaa bamʋ nkɔlɔ ɩ man gyan mi kebiti sʋ, nɩɩ ii be kina mɛ nbara kibuu.

eng: Because of my anger I swore: They will never enter my rest! .
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ n nyɛ agbʋ bɔ ntan yɛ, N mɛɛ yɛ n bɩla sa bamʋ opula fʋʋtɛkpa mʋ nɩɩ n daa n biti n sa bamʋ mʋ. .

eng: Watch out, brothers and sisters, so that none of you have an evil, unfaithful heart that abandons the living God.
local: Kanɩn sʋ mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana Ɩbwaarɛ asa, fan baa fan da sʋ kɛ fanɛ tɔ ɔkʋ ɔ man fɛ nfɛɛrɛ libi, yɛgɛ mʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii tɔ, kɛ ɩ man lɛɛ mʋ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛ nkpa tɔ kakpaa mʋ asɛ.

eng: Instead, encourage each other every day, as long as its called today, so that none of you become insensitive to God because of sins deception.
local: Amaa kakɛ kʋmaa fan waa abaa aniya tɔ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fan san fan tɛ nkpa tɔ ndaga falɛ, kɛ alibi ɩ man pɩna fanɛ tɔ ɔkʋ kɛ ɔ waa kɔkɔlɔ ɔsɩɩ.

eng: We are partners with Christ, but only if we hold on to the confidence we had in the beginning until the end.
local: Nɩɩ an kan yɩlɛ tɔ kinkin naa fʋʋ laalaalʋwɛ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ an kɔɔlɛ gyii npiili asɛ mʋ, anaa Krisito an biti a bʋga barɩgɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ mʋ lɛɛ mʋ tɔ.

eng: When it says, Today, if you hear his voice, dont have stubborn hearts as they did in the rebellion. .
local: Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ ɩ wʋla tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Nɩɩ fan kan nu mʋ ɔbʋlɛ ndaga mʋ, fan man waa nkɔlɔ ɔsɩɩ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fanɛ naana ana ba daa kʋsʋ yɩlɛ mʋ sʋ saŋa mʋ dɩdaa mʋ.

eng: Who was it who rebelled when they heard his voice? Wasnt it all of those who were brought out of Egypt by Moses?.
local: Anɩmʋ ana ɩ gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba nu Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn nɩɩ ba kʋsʋ yɩlɛ tɔ mʋ? Man gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ Mosesi lɛɛ lɩɩ Egyipiti mʋ kpini?.

eng: And with whom was God angry for forty years? Wasnt it with the ones who sinned, whose bodies fell in the desert?.
local: Nɩɩ anɩmʋ ana nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ agbʋ waa nsu adɩna mʋ? Man gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba waa alibi nɩɩ ba wu ba dɛ‐dɛ ofuli sʋ mʋ?.

eng: And against whom did he swear that they would never enter his rest, if not against the ones who were disobedient?.
local: Anɩmʋ ana nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bɔ ntan yɛ, ba mɛɛ yɛ ba nyɛ loo mu opula fʋʋtɛkpa tɔ mʋ? Man gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba waa kʋsʋ tɔ ɔlʋn mʋ?.

eng: We see that they couldnt enter because of their lack of faith.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ an wu fɛɛ, lɩɩ bamʋ kɩman‐kɔɔlɛ‐gyi sʋ mʋ, ba man taalɛ loo mu opula fʋʋtɛkpa mʋ tɔ.

eng: So lets press on to maturity, by moving on from the basics about Christs word. Lets not lay a foundation of turning away from dead works, of faith in God.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan yɛgɛ a yɛgɛ Krisito kusun asɩn gyankpaasa mʋ nɩɩ an bɩɩla mʋ kanɩn, kɛ a dan kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii mʋ tɔ kpee anɛ ansi tɔ. Ɩ man dagaa a maa bɩla bwii naa bɩɩla npiili asɛ asɩn mʋ, fɛɛ nfɛɛrɛ kɩkyɛɛgɛ lɩɩ atɔ gyaga tɔ, yɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gyii.

eng: of teaching about ritual ways to wash with water, laying on of hands, the resurrection from the dead, and eternal judgment—all over again.
local: yɛ kɩkaapʋ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee sʋ, yɛ abaa kɩtaa gyaga asa sʋ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ kʋlɛ, yɛ alewupu kikyiŋi, yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ katalɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ dɛ kakpaa.

eng: Were going to press on, if God allows it.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kan kyula mʋ, falɛ nɩɩ an biti a waa kɛ a dan.

eng: Because its impossible to restore people to changed hearts and lives who turn away once they have seen the light, tasted the heavenly gift, become partners with the Holy Spirit.
local: Ii biti ɩ baa ɩ bʋlʋn sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba yɛgɛ bamʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii tɔ, fɛɛ ba bɩla taalɛ kyɛɛgɛ bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ lɩɩ bamʋ alibi tɔ bwii ba Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla ŋmaŋɛ bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ tɔ saŋa kʋ, nɩɩ ba daa nyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kakɛɛ kɩɩ, nɩɩ ba maa akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ ba bʋga nyɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ.

eng: and tasted Gods good word and the powers of the coming age.
local: Nɩɩ ba nu Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn mʋ, nɩɩ ba wu fɛɛ ɩ bʋrɔn, nɩɩ ba bɩɩ kakyɩna pʋpwɛ mʋ nɩɩ kii biti kɩ ba mʋ ɔlʋn.

eng: They are crucifying Gods Son all over again and exposing him to public shame.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ ba kan fɔyɩ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔkpa mʋ sʋ mʋ, ii biti ɩ baa ɩ bʋlʋn sa bamʋ yɛ ba bɩla bwii kyɛɛgɛ bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ lɩɩ bamʋ ɩkpa tɔ ba Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ i du nɩn fɛɛ ba maa bɩla bɛ da Ɩbwaarɛ mu‐Bii mʋ oyu laasa sʋ bee gyila mʋ ipeeli sakyɔ tɔ.

eng: The ground receives a blessing from God when it drinks up the rain that regularly comes and falls on it and yields a useful crop for those people for whom it is being farmed.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ kasɛ mʋ nɩɩ bʋlayɩ ɩ da kɩmʋ sʋ saŋa kʋmaa, nɩɩ kɩ sʋ kii nuu mʋ, ɩ waa adɔɔ‐gyitɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋrɔn ɩ sa bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ dɔɔ kɩmʋ sʋ mʋ, kasɛ mʋ ɩ nyɛ kusee Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ.

eng: But if it produces thorns and thistles, its useless and close to being cursed. It ends up being burned.
local: Amaa kasɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ kɔyɩ iwii yɛ afitiri mʋ bɩrɛ man sʋ kyaarɛ, nɩɩ kɩ man bʋ kata yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ tɔwɛ kɩmʋ nnɔ. Nɩɩ ɩmʋ laalaalʋwɛ mʋ fʋlɔn ii biti ɔ dɛɛ kɩmʋ.

eng: But we are convinced of better things in your case, brothers and sisters, even though we are talking this way—things that go together with salvation.
local: Mɛ tɔmaa ana kinyi‐bii ɔkɔn, halɩɩ a maa tɔwɛ falɛ gbaa mʋ, fanɛ bɩrɛ a man sʋ kufuu sa fanɛ, an nyi fan maa sʋ atɔ danbɩrasa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ lɩɩ fanɛ kamɔlɩgɛ mʋ sʋ.

eng: God isnt unjust so that he forgets your efforts and the love you have shown for his names sake when you served and continue to serve Gods holy people.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ gyɛ kesintin ogyipu. Ɔ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ tan fanɛ kusun mʋ nɩɩ fan waa yɛ kebiti mʋ nɩɩ fan sʋ sa mʋ mʋ sʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ fan kyɛ mʋ asa tɔ, nɩɩ fɛn san kyaga sʋ fɛn kyɛ bamʋ tɔ.

eng: But we desperately want each of you to show the same effort to make your hope sure until the end.
local: Atɔ mʋ nɩɩ i tiri anɛ lɩɩ fanɛ asɛ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ ɔ kpan mʋ n‐yɩɩ waa kanɩn adanbɩrasa naa fʋʋ ɔkaa, kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɩ waa kɛ fanɛ tamaa mʋ nɩɩ fan sʋ mʋ ɩ ba tɔ kesintin.

eng: This is so you wont be lazy but follow the example of the ones who inherit the promises through faith and patience.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ a mee biti fɛɛ fan ba biliŋi awʋlɛgyɔpʋ kabwaarɛ‐sun tɔ, amaa fan kasɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii yɛ kenyiita mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ bee biti ba nyɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ sa bamʋ mʋ.

eng: When God gave Abraham his promise, he swore by himself since he couldnt swear by anyone greater.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ sa Abraham mʋ, ɔ taa mʋ gbaa‐gbaa kɩtɩɩ bɔ ntan, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋ man bʋ tɔ ɔ kyɔ mʋ.

eng: He said, I will certainly bless you and multiply your descendants. .
local: nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, N tɔwɛ fʋ kesintin tɔ yɛ, n biti n waa fʋ kusee, kɛ n yɛgɛ fʋ kɩnaana ba nyɛ akyɔ.

eng: This Melchizedek, who was king of Salem and priest of the Most High God, met Abraham as he returned from the defeat of the kings, and Melchizedek blessed him.
local: Mekisedeki nɩmʋ kyɩna gyii Salem kadɛ kuwura, nɩɩ ɔ bɩla ɔ gyɛ Ɔlʋnpʋ Ɩbwaarɛ Owura lala mʋ Oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ. Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Abraham naa kɔ taalɛ awura akʋ, nɩɩ o bwii ɛɛ ba mʋ, Mekisedeki gyaŋa mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ waa Abraham kusee.

eng: So Abraham obtained the promise by showing patience.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ Abraham nyɛ kenyiita gyoo nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ sa mʋ mʋ.

eng: Abraham gave a tenth of everything to him. His name means first king of righteousness, and then king of Salem, that is, king of peace.
local: Nɩɩ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ Abraham naa nyɛ ɩkɔ mʋ tɔ mʋ, ɔ barɩgɛ ɩmʋ tɔ ntun kudu, nɩɩ ɔ taa ketun kʋlʋn sa Mekisedeki. Mekisedeki kɩtɩɩ mʋ kasɛ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, Ɩkpa‐kyiigisa Owura. Nɩɩ ɔ bɩla ɔ gyɛ Salem owura, nɩɩ Salem kasɛ ɩ gyɛ yɛ Kayɩɩ yuuli.

eng: People pledge by something greater than themselves. A solemn pledge guarantees what they say and shuts down any argument.
local: Nɩɩ asa bɛ kan bee biti ba bɔ ntan mʋ, bɛɛ taa ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kyɔ bamʋ gbaa‐gbaa bamʋ kɩtɩɩ bɔ ntan. Ntan kɩbɔ mʋ ɩɩ kaapʋ kesintin, nɩɩ ɩ yɛgɛ ntan mʋ nɩɩ ba bɔ mʋ ɩ tɩn kamɔɔrɛ‐gyii mʋ tɔ.

eng: He is without father or mother or any family. He has no beginning or end of life, but hes like Gods Son and remains a priest for all time.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ man nyi sɛhn lɩɩ mʋ‐sɛ abɛɛ mu‐nyi sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ man lɩɩ kɩnaana kʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔkʋ man nyi mʋ kɩkʋʋgɛ abɛɛ mu lewu asɩn. Nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ kakpaa fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mu‐Bii mʋ.

eng: When God wanted to further demonstrate to the heirs of the promise that his purpose doesnt change, he guaranteed it with a solemn pledge.
local: Lɩɩ kanɩn maŋa sʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ maa biti fɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ɔ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ sa bamʋ ba wu fɛɛ ɔ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ kyɛɛgɛ mʋ nfɛɛrɛ mʋ, ɔ yɩlɛ ɩmʋ sʋ bɔ ntan.

eng: See how great Melchizedek was! Abraham, the father of the people, gave him a tenth of everything he captured.
local: Fan kɩɩ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Mekisedeki gyɛ nyɩmɩsa kparɛ. Halɩɩ Abraham mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ anɛ naana ana tɔ ɔgyankpaasa mʋ, atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ naa kɔ ɩkɔ nyɛ mʋ, ɔ barɩgɛ ɩmʋ tɔ ntun kudu nɩɩ ɔ taa ketun kʋlʋn sa mʋ.

eng: So these are two things that dont change, because its impossible for God to lie. He did this so that we, who have taken refuge in him, can be encouraged to grasp the hope that is lying in front of us.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ waa falɛ atɔ anyɔ anɩmʋ. Ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ kɩda yɛ mʋ ntan kɩbɔ, mʋ nɩɩ ɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mɛɛ kyɛɛgɛ, abɛɛ ɔ mɛɛ taalɛ tɩn antɩŋɛsa. Kanɩn sʋ anɛ mʋ nɩɩ an sɩlɛ naa loo mʋ mʋ, a kpan anɛ n‐yɩɩ gaa kɛ a kɩtaa tamaa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ dɛ anɛ ansi tɔ mʋ kenken.

eng: The descendants of Levi who receive the office of priest have a commandment under the Law to collect a tenth of everything from the people who are their brothers and sisters, though they also are descended from Abraham.
local: Nɩɩ Mosesi nbara mʋ ɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ bamʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba gyɛ Lewi kɩnaana mʋ, nɩɩ bee gyii Ɩbwaarɛ kiseepu mʋ ɩ sʋ ɔkpa ba kɔɔlɛ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba barɩgɛ tɔ ntun kudu tɔ mʋ ketun kʋlʋn bamʋ asa asɛ, yɛgɛ bamʋ asa gbaa ba gyɛ Abraham kɩnaana tɔ.

eng: This hope, which is a safe and secure anchor for our whole being, enters the sanctuary behind the curtain.
local: Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ kɩbɩlɩtɔ kparɛ mʋ nɩɩ ki budu nɩɩ kɩtaa ɔkʋlɩ lala kɩ yɩlɛ katɩn kʋlʋn mʋ, kanɩn nɩɩ anɛ tamaa ɩ kɩtaa anɛ yɩlɛ katɩn kʋlʋn anɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii tɔ. Kanɩn tamaa nɩmʋ ɩ bɔla loo Ɩbwaarɛ osunkpa opula kyɩrɛkyɩrɛkpa kɩkyɩrɛkyɩrɛ sʋsʋ mʋ tɔ.

eng: But Melchizedek, who isnt related to them, received a tenth of everything from Abraham and blessed the one who had received the promises.
local: Mekisedeki man gyɛ Lewi kanan tɔ mʋ nɩɩ bee gyii Ɩbwaarɛ kiseepu mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ, amaa ɔ kɔɔlɛ nbarɩgɛ tɔ ntun kudu mʋ tɔ ketun kʋlʋn lɩɩ Abraham asɛ nɩɩ ɔ waa mʋ kusee, nɩɩ Abraham mɔɔ ɩ gyɛ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ sa mʋ.

eng: Thats where Jesus went in advance and entered for us, since he became a high priest according to the order of Melchizedek.
local: Tʋtɔ ɩ gyɛ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ Yesu yɩlɛ anɛ ayaa tɔ gyankpaa anɛ naa loo. Mʋ ɩ ba waa Ɩbwaarɛ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ kakpaa fɛɛ ɔkpa kʋlʋn mʋ nɩɩ Mekisedeki kyɩna ɔ gyɛ mʋ.

eng: Without question, the less important person is blessed by the more important person.
local: Nɩɩ ɩ man sʋ kamɔɔrɛ‐gyii fɛɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ waa kusee mʋ, mʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔbɩlɩsa ɔ kyɔ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kɔɔlɛ kusee mʋ.

eng: In addition, in one case a tenth is received by people who die, and in the other case, the tenth is received by someone who continues to live, according to the record.
local: Lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kiseepu mʋ asɩn tɔ mʋ, aseepu mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ kɔɔlɛ nbarɩgɛ tɔ atɔ ntun kudu tɔ ketun kʋlʋn mʋ bee wu. Amaa lɩɩ Mekisedeki mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ ntun kudu tɔ ketun kʋlʋn mʋ bɩrɛ, ba lɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ ɔ tɛ nkpa tɔ.

eng: It could be said that Levi, who received a tenth, paid a tenth through Abraham.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ gbaa ɩ taalɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Lewi kebunɔ abii tɔ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ kɔɔlɛ ntun kudu tɔ ketun kʋlʋn gbaa mʋ ba gyɛ Abraham anaana‐bii nɩn. Ɩmʋ ɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ Abraham maa ka ntun kudu tɔ ketun kʋlʋn mʋ, bamʋ gbaa ba ka kanɩn.

eng: because he was still in his ancestors body when Abraham paid the tenth to Melchizedek.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Mekisedeki gyaŋa Abraham mʋ, ba man pɩɩta kʋʋgɛ Lewi kebunɔ abii mʋ.

eng: So if perfection came through the levitical office of priest , why was there still a need to speak about raising up another priest according to the order of Melchizedek rather than one according to the order of Aaron?.
local: Nɩɩ Lewi kɩnaana kiseepu kigyii mʋ nɩɩ ki buu Mosesi Nbara mʋ, kɩ daa taalɛ waa nyɩmɩsa kyɛɛkyɛɛ nɩn, naafɔɔ mɩnɛ waa sʋ nɩɩ ɩ san i tiri fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ oseepu pʋpwɛ kʋ ɔ bɩla be gyii kiseepu fɛɛ ɔkpa kʋlʋn mʋ nɩɩ Mekisedeki kyɩna ɔ gyɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ Lewi kɩnaana‐bii Aron lɛɛ mʋ?.

eng: When the order of the priest changes, there has to be a change in the Law as well.
local: Fɛɛ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kiseepu kigyii ɩ maa kyɛɛgɛ mʋ, kanɩn gbaa nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ Nbara mʋ gbaa ɩ maa kyɛɛgɛ.

eng: The person we are talking about belongs to another tribe, and no one ever served at the altar from that tribe.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu mʋ nɩɩ asɩn nɩmʋ ɩ tɔwɛ ɩ lɩɩ mʋ sʋ falɛ mʋ, lɩɩ kebunɔ kʋ tɔ nɩn. Nɩɩ kebunɔ maŋa tɔ ɔkʋ man pɩɩta gyii Ɩbwaarɛ kiseepu kɛ o sun kiseepu kusun.

eng: Its clear that our Lord came from the tribe of Judah, but Moses never said anything about priests from that tribe.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɩ gyɛ kesintin fɛɛ anɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Yesu mʋ lɩɩ Gyuda kebunɔ tɔ nɩn, amaa kanɩn kebunɔ maŋa bɩrɛ, Mosesi man tɔwɛ yɛ bee biti ba be gyii Ɩbwaarɛ kiseepu.

eng: And its even clearer if another priest appears who is like Melchizedek.
local: Nɩɩ asɩn mʋ ɩ lɛɛ kaapʋ tɔ danbɩrasa. Ɩbwaarɛ oseepu pʋpwɛ kʋ ba, nɩɩ o du fɛɛ Mekisedeki.

eng: He has become a priest by the power of a life that cant be destroyed, rather than a legal requirement about physical descent.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ ba waa Ɩbwaarɛ oseepu yɛgɛ ɔ man lɩɩ Lewi kɩnaana mʋ tɔ, amaa Ɩbwaarɛ waa mʋ oseepu bɔla mʋ nkpa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ sʋ ɔlʋn nɩɩ ɩ man sʋ ɔkaa.

eng: This is confirmed: You are a priest forever, according to the order of Melchizedek. .
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlɛsa mʋ ɩ tɔwɛ lɩɩ mʋ sʋ yɛ, Fʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ oseepu kakpaa, fɛɛ ɔkpa kʋlʋn mʋ nɩɩ Mekisedeki kyɩna ɔ gyɛ mʋ.

eng: On the one hand, an earlier command is set aside because it was weak and useless.
local: Kanɩn sʋ dɩdaa nbara mʋ nɩɩ ba buu mʋ bɩrɛ, ba yɛgɛ ɩmʋ yɩla ɩkaa sʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɩ man bʋlʋn nɩɩ ɩ man sʋ tɔnɔ.

eng: . On the other hand, a better hope is introduced, through which we draw near to God.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Mosesi nbara man taalɛ waa kʋtɔ kʋmaa lʋwɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kebiti sʋ kyɛɛkyɛɛ. Amaa nbɩɩnbɩɩ an sʋ tamaa danbɩrasa mʋ nɩɩ an bɔla sʋ an nin an tiri Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: And this was not done without a solemn pledge! The others have become priests without a solemn pledge.
local: Kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bɩla ki tii sʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ bɔ ntan kiseepu pʋpwɛ nɩmʋ sʋ. Yɛgɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba ba waa Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu mʋ kpini, Ɩbwaarɛ man bɔ ntan sa bamʋ.

eng: but this priest was affirmed with a solemn pledge by the one who said, The Lord has made a solemn pledge and will not change his mind: You are a priest forever. .
local: Amaa Yesu bɩrɛ maa ba waa Ɩbwaarɛ oseepu mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ bɔ ntan tii sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Mɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ n bɔ ntan, nɩɩ n mɛɛ yɛ n kyɛɛgɛ mɛ nfɛɛrɛ, fʋ ɩ gyɛ oseepu kakpaa.

eng: As a result, Jesus has become the guarantee of a better covenant.
local: Lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ntan nɩmʋ sʋ mʋ, Yesu ɩ ba waa ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ yɩlɛ katɔwɛ kyula mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ waa anaa mʋ nsana mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ kɩ bʋrɔn kɩ kyɔ kɩdɩdaa mʋ.

eng: The others who became priests are numerous because death prevented them from continuing to serve.
local: Kɩmʋ nɩɩ kɩ bɩla kɩ tii sʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, bamʋ nɩɩ ba gyankpaa gyii Ɩbwaarɛ kiseepu mʋ ba kyɔ gaa. Lɩɩ fɛɛ nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan wu yɛgɛ mu kusun mʋ, ɔpʋpwɛ ɩɩ ba ee yii mʋ ayaa tɔ.

eng: In contrast, he holds the office of priest permanently because he continues to serve forever.
local: Amaa lɩɩ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Yesu maa tɛ nkpa tɔ kakpaa sʋ mʋ, ee gyii Ɩbwaarɛ kiseepu mʋ nɩɩ kɩ man sʋ ɔkaa.

eng: This is why he can completely save those who are approaching God through him, because he always lives to speak with God for them.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ Yesu ɩ taalɛ mɔlɩgɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba bɔla mʋ sʋ ba Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ mʋ kakpaa, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ tɛ nkpa tɔ kakpaa nɩɩ ɔ yɩlɛ tɔ ɛɛ kʋlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɛɛ sa bamʋ.

eng: Its appropriate for us to have this kind of high priest: holy, innocent, incorrupt, separate from sinners, and raised high above the heavens.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ dagaa sa anɛ a maa nyɛ. Nɩɩ mʋ ɩ bɔla Ɩbwaarɛ ansi, nɩɩ mʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋ man sʋ alibi kʋ, nɩɩ ɔ man sʋ ineesi kʋ, nɩɩ ɔ man du fɛɛ alibi awaapʋ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ diyaa mʋ sʋsʋ ɔ kyɔ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ mʋ ayɛ.

eng: He doesnt need to offer sacrifices every day like the other high priests, first for their own sins and then for the sins of the people. He did this once for all when he offered himself.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu man du fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Aseepu abɩlɩsa banban mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ gyankpaa bɛɛ lɛɛ bamʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋ alibi saraga kakɛ kʋmaa, pɔyɩ nɩɩ bɛɛ lɛɛ asa n‐yɩɩ sʋ alibi saraga mʋ. Amaa Yesu bɩrɛ man waa kanɩn, ɔ taa mʋ mʋ n‐yɩɩ lɛɛ saraga sa alibi awaapʋ kɩlɩɩ kʋlʋn.

eng: The Law appoints people who are prone to weakness as high priests, but the content of the solemn pledge, which came after the Law, appointed a Son who has been made perfect forever.
local: Mosesi nbara kɩkaapʋ mʋ sʋ mʋ, asa mʋ nɩɩ ba be gyii Ɩbwaarɛ kiseepu bɩlɩsa mʋ kpini ba gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man taalɛ kɩtaa bamʋ n‐yɩɩ kyɛɛkyɛɛ. Amaa Mosesi nbara mʋ kamaa mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ bɔ ntan lɛɛ mu‐Bii ba waa Oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ nɩɩ o du kyɛɛkyɛɛ naa fʋʋ ɔkaa.

eng: Now the main point of what we are saying is this: We have this kind of high priest. He sat down at the right side of the throne of the majesty in the heavens.
local: Nbɩɩnbɩɩ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ an tɔwɛ falɛ kpini mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, an sʋ Ɩbwaarɛ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gya lala mʋ gyisa sʋ sʋsʋ.

eng: Hes serving as a priest in the holy place, which is the true meeting tent that God, not any human being, set up.
local: Nɩɩ ee sun kiseepu kusun opula kyɩrɛkyɩrɛkpa kɩkyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ tantɩ kubuu gbaa‐gbaa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ sʋsʋ mʋ. Nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gbaa‐gbaa i yii kɩmʋ, man gyɛ nyɩmɩsa.

eng: Every high priest is appointed to offer gifts and sacrifices. So its necessary for this high priest also to have something to offer.
local: Bɛɛ lɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ kʋmaa nɩmʋ yɛ ɔ baa ɔ sʋ nkɛɛ yɛ atɔbwaaya kɔlɔŋɔ ɛɛ sa Ɩbwaarɛ. Ɩmʋ sʋ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ anɛ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ Krisito nɩmʋ gbaa maa nyɛ kʋtɔ kʋ taa lɔŋɔ sa Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: If he was located on earth, he wouldnt be a priest because there are already others who offer gifts based on the Law.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ dɩn ɔ bʋ kasɛ sʋ nɩn, naafɔɔ ɔ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ baa ɔ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ oseepu kpini‐kpini. Lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bee gyii Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu mʋ, ba wʋla ba bʋ tɔ bɛɛ lɛɛ saraga fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Mosesi nbara mʋ ɩ kaapʋ mʋ.

eng: They serve in a place that is a copy and shadow of the heavenly meeting tent. This is indicated when Moses was warned by God when he was about to set up the meeting tent: See that you follow the pattern that I showed you on the mountain in every detail .
local: Nɩɩ kasɛ sʋ Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu kusun mʋ gyɛ sʋsʋ lɛɛ mʋ keduli nɩn, man gyɛ kɩmʋ gbaa‐gbaa nɩn. Kanɩn sʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Mosesi ii biti o yii Ɩbwaarɛ tantɩ kubuu mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ da mu kumu tɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Kɩɩ sʋ, kɛ fu yii mi osunkpa tantɩ kubuu mʋ kyɛɛkyɛɛ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ n kaapʋ fʋ kɩbɩɩ mʋ sʋ mʋ.

eng: But now, Jesus has received a superior priestly service just as he arranged a better covenant that is enacted with better promises.
local: Amaa Yesu gyii kiseepu kusun mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋrɔn gaa kɩ kyɔ Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu banban mʋ lɛɛ. Nɩɩ katɔwɛ kyula pʋpwɛ mʋ nɩɩ Yesu waa yɩla anaa Ɩbwaarɛ nsana mʋ gbaa, kɩ bʋrɔn kɩ kyɔ kɩdɩdaa mʋ, nɩɩ kɩ bɩla kɩ sʋ kɔkɔlɔ kɩda danbɩrasa kɩ kyɔ kɩdɩdaa mʋ.

eng: If the first covenant had been without fault, it wouldnt have made sense to expect a second.
local: Fɛɛ nɩɩ katɔwɛ kyula dɩdaa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ waa maa asa nsana, nɩɩ kɩ daa ki du kyɛɛkyɛɛ nɩn, naafɔɔ ɩ man tiri sa Ɩbwaarɛ fɛɛ ɔ bɩla waa kɩnyɔsapʋ mʋ.

eng: But God did find fault with them, since he says, Look, the days are coming, says the Lord, when I will make a covenant with the house of Israel, and I will make a new covenant with the house of Judah. .
local: Amaa Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ wu alibi lɩɩ asa mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Saŋa kʋ ii biti ɔ ba kɛ mɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ n biti n waa katɔwɛ kyula pʋpwɛ maa Ɩsɩrayɩ abi yɛ Gyiwu abi nsana.

eng: It will not be like the covenant that I made with their ancestors on the day I took them by the hand to lead them out of the land of Egypt, because they did not continue to keep my covenant, and I lost interest in them, says the Lord. .
local: Nɩɩ kɩ mɛɛ yɛ kɩ baa ki du fɛɛ katɔwɛ kyula mʋ nɩɩ n maa bamʋ naana ana a kyɩna waa mʋ, saŋa mʋ nɩɩ n kɩtaa bamʋ abaa tɔ, gyankpaa bamʋ lɛɛ bamʋ lɩɩ Egyipiti mʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ba maa man gyii kesintin lɩɩ mɛ katɔwɛ kyula mʋ tɔ sʋ mʋ, n palɛ mɛ kamaa sa bamʋ.

eng: This is the covenant that I will make with the house of Israel after those days, says the Lord. I will place my laws in their minds, and write them on their hearts. I will be their God, and they will be my people. .
local: Nkɛ mʋ nɩɩ ii biti ɩ ba falɛ tɔ mʋ, ɩnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ katɔwɛ kyula mʋ nɩɩ n biti n waa maa Ɩsɩrayɩ abi nsana. N biti n taa mɛ nbara waa bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ tɔ, kɛ n kyʋrɔɔ ɩmʋ waa bamʋ nkɔlɔ tɔ. N biti n baa n gyɛ bamʋ Ɩbwaarɛ, kɛ ba baa ba gyɛ mɛ asa.

eng: And each person wont ever teach a neighbor or their brother or sister, saying, Know the Lord, because they will all know me, from the least important of them to the most important; .
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔkʋ man bɩla yɛ ɔ kaapʋ mʋ tɔmaa abɛɛ mu‐nyi mu‐bii tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Bɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ ii biti ɔ ba bɩɩ mɛ lɩɩ nbii sʋ naa fʋʋ abɩlɩsa sʋ.

eng: because I will be lenient toward their unjust actions, and I wont remember their sins anymore. .
local: N biti n taa bamʋ alibi kɛɛ bamʋ, kɛ n man bɩla nyiŋi bamʋ alibi kakɛ akʋ. Mɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ n tɔwɛ kanɩn.

eng: When it says new, it makes the first obsolete. And if something is old and outdated, its close to disappearing.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ maa tɔwɛ lɩɩ katɔwɛ kyula pʋpwɛ mʋ sʋ mʋ, ɩɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ katɔwɛ kyula gyankpaasa mʋ kɩ waa kɩdɩdaa. Nɩɩ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ ba waa kɩdɩdaa, nɩɩ kɩ man sʋ kyaarɛ mʋ, kii biti kɩ ba fɔyɩ.

eng: The Law is a shadow of the good things that are coming, not the real things themselves. It never can perfect the ones who are trying to draw near to God through the same sacrifices that are offered continually every year.
local: Mosesi nbara mʋ man gyɛ kesintin atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ kamaa ɩɩ ba mʋ gbaa‐gbaa, amaa ɩ gyɛ ɩmʋ keduli nɩn. Lɩɩ falɛ sʋ mʋ atɔbwaaya saraga kɔlɔŋɔ kʋlʋn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa kesu‐kesu kanan mʋ nɩɩ nbara mʋ ɩɩ kaapʋ mʋ, mɛɛ taalɛ waa asa mʋ nɩɩ bee sun Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ kyɛɛkyɛɛ naa lʋwɛ.

eng: Otherwise, wouldnt they have stopped being offered? If the people carrying out their religious duties had been completely cleansed once, no one would have been aware of sin anymore.
local: Fɛɛ nɩɩ atɔbwaaya saraga kɔlɔŋɔ ɩ dɩn taalɛ fwɩɩ asa mʋ nɩɩ bee sun Ɩbwaarɛ, lɩɩ bamʋ alibi tɔ kɩlɩɩ kʋlʋn mʋ, naafɔɔ ba dɩn yɛgɛ saraga kɩlɛɛ, yɛgɛ ba man bɩla fɛ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ ba gyii alibi kʋpwɛ.

eng: Instead, these sacrifices are a reminder of sin every year.
local: Amaa kanɩn atɔbwaaya saraga kɔlɔŋɔ maŋa ii nyiŋi asa mʋ kesu kʋmaa bamʋ alibi mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa.

eng: because its impossible for the blood of bulls and goats to take away sins.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ anaa‐nyɩn yɛ nbwɛ nkalan ɩ mɛɛ taalɛ lɛɛ alibi.

eng: Therefore, when he comes into the world he says, You didnt want a sacrifice or an offering, but you prepared a body for me; .
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Krisito ɩɩ ba dulinyaa tɔ mʋ ɔ tɔwɛ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ, Fu mee biti atɔbwaaya saraga kɩlɛɛ yɛ atɔ kɛkɛɛ, amaa fʋ sa mɛ nyɩmɩsa kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ yɛ n ba taa lɛɛ saraga.

eng: you werent pleased with entirely burned offerings or a sin offering. .
local: Nɩɩ atɔbwaaya mʋ nɩɩ bee tuwa fʋ kaswɩɩ sʋ bɛɛ lɛɛ saraga, yɛ alibi kɩtaa kɛɛ saraga kɩlɛɛ mʋ bɩrɛ, ɩ mɛɛ bɔla fu ansi.

eng: So then I said, Look, Ive come to do your will, God. This has been written about me in the scroll. .
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ, mɛ ndee! N ba nɩn kɛ n ba waa fu kebiti fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ mɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ asɩn waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ mʋ.

eng: He says above, You didnt want and you werent pleased with a sacrifice or an offering or with entirely burned offerings or a purification offering, which are offered because the Law requires them.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ daa kɩ tɔwɛ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ, Fu mee biti atɔbwaaya saraga kɩlɛɛ yɛ atɔ kɛkɛɛ, ɩmaa atɔbwaaya mʋ nɩɩ bee tuwa fʋ kaswɩɩ sʋ bɛɛ lɛɛ saraga, yɛ alibi kɩtaa kɛɛ saraga kɩlɛɛ mʋ, ɩ mɛɛ bɔla fu ansi .

eng: Then he said, Look, Ive come to do your will . He puts an end to the first to establish the second.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Krisito bɩla bwii tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ mɛ ndee! N ba nɩn kɛ n ba waa fu kebiti. Ɩmʋ sʋ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛgɛ atɔbwaaya saraga kɩlɛɛ mʋ tʋʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taa Krisito saraga kɩlɛɛ mʋ ba yii ayaa tɔ.

eng: We have been made holy by Gods will through the offering of Jesus Christs body once for all.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Yesu Krisito maa waa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kebiti sʋ mʋ, fɛɛ maa taa mʋ gbaa‐gbaa n‐yɩɩ lɛɛ saraga kɩlɩɩ kʋlʋn kakpaa sʋ mʋ, ɔ lɔŋɔ anɛ kpini yɩla sa Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: Every priest stands every day serving and offering the same sacrifices over and over, sacrifices that can never take away sins.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ Oseepu kʋmaa nɩmʋ yɩlɛ ɛɛ waa mʋ kabwaarɛ‐sun asun kakɛ kʋmaa, nɩɩ saŋa kʋmaa ɛɛ lɛɛ ɩsaraga kʋlʋn maŋa, yɛgɛ ɩ mɛɛ taalɛ lɛɛ asa alibi.

eng: But when this priest offered one sacrifice for sins for all time, he sat down at the right side of God.
local: Amaa Krisito bɩrɛ, taa mʋ n‐yɩɩ lɛɛ saraga kɩlɩɩ kʋlʋn sa alibi kakpaa, nɩɩ ɔ naa kyɩna Ɩbwaarɛ kɩbaa gyisa sʋ.

eng: Since then, hes waiting until his enemies are made into a footstool for his feet.
local: Taa lɩɩ kanɩn saŋa maŋa mʋ, o sii ɔ tɛ o gyoo fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ taa mʋ dʋn ana waa mʋ ayaa kasɛ.

eng: because he perfected the people who are being made holy with one offering for all time.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ saraga kʋlʋn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ n‐yɩɩ lɛɛ sʋ mʋ, ɔ lɔŋɔ bamʋ nɩɩ ɔ fwɩɩ bamʋ lɩɩ alibi tɔ mʋ yɩla sa Ɩbwaarɛ kyɛɛkyɛɛ kakpaa.

eng: The Holy Spirit affirms this when saying.
local: Nɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ gbaa ii gyii asɩn nɩmʋ kesintin kaapʋ anɛ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ gyankpaa tɔwɛ anɛ yɛ.

eng: This is the covenant that I will make with them. After these days, says the Lord, I will place my laws in their hearts and write them on their minds. .
local: Nkɛ mʋ nɩɩ ii biti ɩ ba mʋ, katɔwɛ kyula nɩmʋ nɩɩ n biti n waa maa bamʋ nsana. Mɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ tɔwɛ kanɩn. N biti n taa mɛ nbara waa bamʋ nkɔlɔ tɔ kɛ n kyʋrɔɔ ɩmʋ waa bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ tɔ.

eng: And I wont remember their sins and their lawless behavior anymore. .
local: Nɩɩ o bwii kyaga sʋ yɛ, N mɛɛ bɩla yɛ n nyiŋi bamʋ alibi mʋ nɩɩ ba waa yɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba fɔyɩ ɔkpa nbara mʋ tɔ mʋ kakaakʋ.

eng: When there is forgiveness for these things, there is no longer an offering for sin.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ Ɩbwaarɛ maa taa asa alibi kɛɛ bamʋ falɛ bɩrɛ, ɩ man bɩla i tiri fɛɛ ba kpalɩ alibi sʋ lɛɛ saraga. Fan yɛgɛ an nyɛ nkɔlɔ yɩlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ansi tɔ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we have confidence that we can enter the holy of holies by means of Jesus blood.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ mi‐nyi mu‐bii ana akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu, lɩɩ Yesu nkalan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kyɛɛgɛ wʋrɩgɛ mʋ sʋ mʋ, an sʋ ɔkpa fɛɛ an taalɛ loo opula Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛkpa Kɩkyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ tɔ Ɩbwaarɛ ansi tɔ.

eng: through a new and living way that he opened up for us through the curtain, which is his body.
local: An taalɛ faa kɩtaa kunsa mʋ sʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ Yesu ɩ taa mʋ gbaa‐gbaa kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ bugi ɔkpa pʋpwɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ sa nkpa mʋ, sa anɛ.

eng: and we have a great high priest over Gods house.
local: Nɩɩ a maa sʋ Ɩbwaarɛ Oseepu kparɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kɩkpaara asa sʋ mʋ.

eng: Therefore, lets draw near with a genuine heart with the certainty that our faith gives us, since our hearts are sprinkled clean from an evil conscience and our bodies are washed with pure water.
local: fan yɛgɛ a nin tiri Ɩbwaarɛ. Fan yɛgɛ a taa kɔkɔlɔ fuuli yɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii mʋ nɩɩ kɩ man sʋ kamɔɔrɛ‐gyii, yɛ nfɛɛrɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ kaapʋ yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ taa anɛ alibi kɛɛ anɛ, yɛ anɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ ba taa nkyu kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ fwɩɩ mʋ, ba Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ.

eng: Lets hold on to the confession of our hope without wavering, because the one who made the promises is reliable.
local: Fan yɛgɛ a kɩtaa tamaa asɩn mʋ nɩɩ an yɩlɛ sʋ an tɔwɛ mʋ tɔ kenken, lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ mʋ, ɩɩ waa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ ee biti ɔ waa mʋ.

eng: And let us consider each other carefully for the purpose of sparking love and good deeds.
local: Fan yɛgɛ a fɛ nfɛɛrɛ lɩɩ kanan mʋ nɩɩ a tɔwɛ an waa abaa, kɛ a kyɛ abaa tɔ, kɛ a waa adanbɩrasa sa abaa.

eng: Dont stop meeting together with other believers, which some people have gotten into the habit of doing. Instead, encourage each other, especially as you see the day drawing near.
local: Fan man yɛgɛ kɛ a yɛgɛ kɩgyaŋɛ bʋga, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ akʋ ba mɛɛ gyaŋɛ bɛɛ bʋga mʋ, amaa fan yɛgɛ a waa abaa aniya tɔ, fɛɛ fan maa kɩɩ wu fɛɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu Kakɛ lala mʋ kii biti kɩ ba mʋ ki tiri tɔ.

eng: If we make the decision to sin after we receive the knowledge of the truth, there isnt a sacrifice for sins left any longer.
local: Fɛɛ a maa wʋla bɩɩ kesintin asɩn nɩmʋ, nɩɩ a kɩɩ ansi an san an waa alibi bɩrɛ, saraga kɩlɛɛ sa alibi kʋ man bɩla kɩ bʋ tɔ.

eng: Theres only a scary expectation of judgment and of a burning fire thats going to devour Gods opponents.
local: Amaa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ san ɩ gyɛ yɛ, kɔkɔlɔ ŋmaa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ dɛ kii gyoo Ɩbwaarɛ kʋpwɛ kubun yɛ fʋlɔn yen‐yen mʋ nɩɩ kii biti kɩ ba dɩyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ dʋn ana mʋ.

eng: When someone rejected the Law from Moses, they were put to death without mercy on the basis of the testimony of two or three witnesses.
local: Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o kina Mosesi nbara mʋ, nɩɩ asa anyɔ abɛɛ asa asá be gyii kesintin yɛ ɔsa mʋ kina kanɩn nbara mʋ bɩrɛ, be mee wu mʋ kʋwɛɛ bɛɛ mɔɔ mʋ nɩn.

eng: How much worse punishment do you think is deserved by the person who walks all over Gods Son, who acts as if the blood of the covenant that made us holy is just ordinary blood, and who insults the Spirit of grace?.
local: Nɩɩ kanɩn bɩrɛ, bamʋ nɩɩ ba tʋʋ kɩyaa saalɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mu‐bii mɔɔ de? Kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ lala kɩɩ dɛ sa bamʋ, fɛɛ ba maa man taa Ɩbwaarɛ katɔwɛ kyula nkalan mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ fwɩɩ bamʋ lɩɩ alibi tɔ mʋ tɩɩ sɛhn, nɩɩ ba bɩla bwii saalɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ bwaala asa mʋ?.

eng: We know the one who said, Judgment is mine; I will pay people back. And he also said, The Lord will judge his people. .
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ an nyi Ɩbwaarɛ maa tɔwɛ yɛ, Mɛ ɩ tɛɛla alibi awaapʋ, Nɩɩ mɛ ii biti n bɩɩtɛ bamʋ asʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ bɩla bwii tɔwɛ yɛ, Mɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ n biti n gyii mɛ asa asɩn.

eng: Its scary to fall into the hands of the living God!.
local: Kanɩn sʋ ii biti ɩ baa ɩ gyɛ kufuu fɛɛ ɔkʋ maa tɩyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ tɔ kakpaa mʋ kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ tɔ.

eng: But remember the earlier days, after you saw the light. You stood your ground while you were suffering from an enormous amount of pressure.
local: Fan nyiŋi fanɛ nkɛ gyankpaasa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bugi fanɛ ansi, nɩɩ fan wu mʋ ɔkpa, nɩɩ fan yɩlɛ tɔ gyii awʋrʋfɔ, yɛgɛ fanɛ abaa ɩ man yɔɔ mʋ.

eng: Sometimes you were exposed to insults and abuse in public. Other times you became partners with those who were treated that way.
local: Saŋa kʋ tɔ mɔɔ mʋ, fan nyɛ asebi yɛ kanyanla asa nsana tɔ, ɩkʋ mɔɔ mʋ, fan kyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba nyɛ kanɩn awʋrʋfɔ mʋ tɔ.

eng: You even showed sympathy toward people in prison and accepted the confiscation of your possessions with joy, since you knew that you had better and lasting possessions.
local: Fan su, nɩɩ fan yɩyɛɛ, nɩɩ fan sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba kɩtaa bamʋ tii obu mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ba maa kɔɔlɛ fanɛ npatɩyɛ gbaa mʋ, ɩ man kɔ fanɛ, amaa fanɛ ansi i gyii nɩn. Lɩɩ fɛɛ fanɛ gbaa‐gbaa fanɛ fen nyi fɛɛ fan maa sʋ npatɩyɛ danbɩrasa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ tɔ kakpaa sʋsʋ.

eng: So dont throw away your confidence—it brings a great reward.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan man yɛgɛ fanɛ abaa ɩ yɔɔ, fan yɩlɛ kɩnyɩn tɔ kenken. Lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ ka fanɛ kʋkɔ lala.

eng: You need to endure so that you can receive the promises after you do Gods will.
local: Fan nyɛ kenyiita yɩlɛ tɔ kɛ fan taalɛ waa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti, kɛ ɩ yɛgɛ fan nyɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ yɛ ee biti ɔ sa fanɛ mʋ.

eng: In a little while longer, the one who is coming will come and wont delay; .
local: Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ ɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɩ san gbɛrɛɛ kɛ Ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ ba mʋ, ɔ ba. Ɩ man bɩla yɛ ɩ kyɛɛrɛɛ.

eng: but my righteous one will live by faith, and my whole being wont be pleased with anyone who shrinks back. .
local: Nɩɩ mɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ bamʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi lɩɩ bamʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ mʋ, bee biti ba nyɛ nkpa. Amaa ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ kan bwii kamaa bɩrɛ, ɔ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ bɔla mi ansi.

eng: But we arent the sort of people who timidly draw back and end up being destroyed. Were the sort of people who have faith so that our whole beings are preserved.
local: Nɩɩ an man gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bee bwii kamaa nɩɩ ba mɛɛ nyɛ kamɔlɩgɛ mʋ, amaa an gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ an kɔɔlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gyii, nɩɩ an nyɛ kamɔlɩgɛ mʋ.

eng: Faith is the reality of what we hope for, the proof of what we dont see.
local: Mɩnɛ ɩ gyɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii? Kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii ɩ gyɛ yɛ an nyi fɛɛ an biti a nyɛ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ an sʋ ɩmʋ tamaa, nɩɩ a mee wu ɩmʋ yɛ anɛ ansi yɛgɛ ɩ bʋ tɔ kesintin mɔɔ.

eng: The elders in the past were approved because they showed faith.
local: Kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii nɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ansi i gyii adɩdaapʋ mʋ sʋ.

eng: By faith we understand that the universe has been created by a word from God so that the visible came into existence from the invisible.
local: Lɩɩ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ nɩɩ an nu kasɛ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ kɔnɔsa nɩɩ ɔ taa tʋʋ dulinyaa. Ɩmʋ sʋ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ a mee wu yɛ ansi mʋ, ɩmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ taa waa atɔ mʋ nɩɩ an wu falɛ.

eng: By faith Abel offered a better sacrifice to God than Cain, which showed that he was righteous, since God gave approval to him for his gift. Though he died, hes still speaking through faith.
local: Lɩɩ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ, nɩɩ Abeli lɛɛ saraga danbɩrasa sa Ɩbwaarɛ, nɩɩ ɩ bʋrɔn ɩ kyɔ mʋ‐daa Ken lɛɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa mʋ. Nɩɩ lɩɩ mʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wu mʋ fɛɛ mʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi, lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gbaa‐gbaa mu ansi i gyii mʋ saraga mʋ sʋ. Lɩɩ mʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii nɩmʋ sʋ mʋ, halɩɩ maa wu gbaa mʋ, mʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii mʋ kɩ san kɩɩ tɔwɛ anɛ yɛ anɛ gbaa anɛ a kɔɔlɛ gyii kanɩn.

eng: By faith Enoch was taken up so that he didnt see death, and he wasnt found because God took him up. He was given approval for having pleased God before he was taken up.
local: Enoki kɩkɔɔlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gyii sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ taa mʋ kpee sʋsʋ yɛgɛ ɔ man wu. Nɩɩ asa ba man bɩla wu mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ taa mʋ lɩɩ bamʋ ansi sʋ kyʋn. Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ ɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, pɔyɩ kɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ taa Enoki kpee sʋsʋ mʋ, ɔ bɔla Ɩbwaarɛ ansi.

eng: Its impossible to please God without faith because the one who draws near to God must believe that he exists and that he rewards people who try to find him.
local: Amaa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ kɔɔlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ee gyii mʋ, ɔ mɛɛ taalɛ bɔla mu ansi. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ ba Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ mʋ, ɩ dagaa ɔ kɔɔlɛ gyii fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ bʋ tɔ, nɩɩ mʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ ka bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ payɩ ansi bɛɛ kɩɩ bee biti mʋ mʋ kʋkɔ.

eng: By faith Noah responded with godly fear when he was warned about events he hadnt seen yet. He built an ark to deliver his household. With his faith, he criticized the world and became an heir of the righteousness that comes from faith.
local: Nowa kɩkɔɔlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gyii sʋ mʋ, saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ da mu kumu tɔ lɩɩ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ man pɩɩta waa mʋ sʋ mʋ, o nu sa Ɩbwaarɛ. Nɩɩ ɔ salɛ ɔkʋlɩ lala mʋ nɩɩ kɩ mɔlɩgɛ maa mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ abi kpini. Lɩɩ mʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii nɩmʋ sʋ mʋ, o bun dulinyaa kpini kʋpwɛ, nɩɩ lɩɩ mʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ ɩkpa kyiigisa wuya ana mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ.

eng: By faith Abraham obeyed when he was called to go out to a place that he was going to receive as an inheritance. He went out without knowing where he was going.
local: Abraham kɩkɔɔlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gyii sʋ mʋ, saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ ɔ kʋsʋ lɩɩ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ mʋ kpee kasɛ mʋ nɩɩ kii biti kɩ ba biliŋi mʋ kapatɩyɛ kakaakʋ mʋ, o nu sa Ɩbwaarɛ. Nɩɩ ɔ kʋsʋ ee kpee, yɛgɛ ɔ man nyi katɩn mʋ ee kpee.

eng: By faith he lived in the land he had been promised as a stranger. He lived in tents along with Isaac and Jacob, who were coheirs of the same promise.
local: Lɩɩ mʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ mʋ, maa fʋʋ kasɛ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ sa mʋ mʋ, o yii ɩtantɩ abu kyɩna tɔ fɛɛ ɔfɔ. Nɩɩ kanɩn maŋa sʋ nɩɩ mu‐bii Isaki, yɛ mʋ kanaana‐bii Gyekobu mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ sa bamʋ mʋ, gbaa ba kyɩna.

eng: He was looking forward to a city that has foundations, whose architect and builder is God.
local: Abraham waa kanɩn lɩɩ fɛɛ mu ansi ɩɩ gyan kadɛ lala mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ gbaa‐gbaa ɩ kyʋrɔɔ kɩmʋ, nɩɩ o yii kɩmʋ kasɛ, nɩɩ ɔ pɔyɩ kɩmʋ yɩla kakpaa mʋ.

eng: By faith even Sarah received the ability to have a child, though she herself was barren and past the age for having children, because she believed that the one who promised was faithful.
local: Sara kɩkɔɔlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gyii taa tii mu‐kuli Abraham lɛɛ sʋ, nɩɩ ba kʋʋgɛ kebii. Bamʋ kpini ba bɩlɛ, nɩɩ Sara gyɛ ogbentipu. Amaa ba kɔɔlɛ gyii fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ sa bamʋ fɛɛ ee biti ɔ sa bamʋ kebii mʋ, gyɛ kesintin wuya.

eng: So descendants were born from one man . They were as many as the number of the stars in the sky and as countless as the grains of sand on the seashore.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ Abraham mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bɩlɛ kyʋn sʋ, halɩɩ nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ bɩla taalɛ kʋʋgɛ gbaa mʋ, kʋʋgɛ obii‐nyɩnsa, nɩɩ mʋ kɩnaana kɩ nyɛ akyɔ fɛɛ sʋsʋ akyikpebi abɛɛ ɔpʋ kɔnɔ kɩkyafa, nɩɩ ɔkʋ mɛɛ taalɛ kalɛ bamʋ.

eng: All these people died in faith without receiving the promises, but they saw the promises from a distance and welcomed them. They confessed that they were strangers and immigrants on earth.
local: Asa nɩmʋ kpini ba sʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba tɛ nkpa tɔ halɩɩ naa fʋʋ bamʋ lewu. Nɩɩ ba man nyɛ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ yɛ ee biti ɔ sa bamʋ mʋ. Ba wu ɩmʋ kata‐kata, nɩɩ ɩ waa bamʋ ɔkɔn. Nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ kyula nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, ba gyɛ afɔ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ kasɛ kanɩmʋ sʋ asa.

eng: People who say this kind of thing make it clear that they are looking for a homeland.
local: Nɩɩ asa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɔwɛ kanɩn asɩn mʋ, ɩ kaapʋ nɩn fɛɛ bɛɛ kɩɩ bee biti bamʋ gbaa‐gbaa bamʋ ayɛ.

eng: If they had been thinking about the country that they had left, they would have had the opportunity to return to it.
local: Nɩɩ bɛɛ daa bɛɛ fɛ nfɛɛrɛ lɩɩ ɔsʋwʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ ba kʋsʋ kɩmʋ sʋ mʋ, naafɔɔ ba dɩn nyɛ ɔkpa bwii kpee tʋtɔ.

eng: But at this point in time, they are longing for a better country, that is, a heavenly one. Therefore, God isnt ashamed to be called their God—he has prepared a city for them.
local: Amaa bɛɛ kɩɩ bee biti kadɛ lala mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋrɔn kɩ kyɔ kasɛ sʋ lɛɛ nɩmʋ. Kɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ kɩmʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ sʋsʋ mʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ ɩ man sʋ Ɩbwaarɛ ipeeli sa bamʋ fɛɛ ba maa tɩɩ mʋ yɛ bamʋ Ɩbwaarɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ lɔŋɔ sʋsʋ kadɛ lala yɩla sa bamʋ.

eng: By faith Abraham offered Isaac when he was tested. The one who received the promises was offering his only son.
local: Lɩɩ Abraham kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ mʋ, saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tʋʋ mʋ tɔ kɩɩ mʋ, ɔ taa mu‐bii Isaki yɛ ɔ naa lɛɛ saraga sa Ɩbwaarɛ. Abraham ɩ gyɛ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ sa lɩɩ Isaki sʋ mʋ, amaa nɩɩ o kyula fɛɛ ee biti ɔ taa mu‐bii kʋlʋn kpen nɩmʋ naa lɛɛ saraga.

eng: He had been told concerning him, Your legitimate descendants will come from Isaac. .
local: Amaa Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla tɔwɛ Abraham yɛ, Taa bɔla fu‐bii Isaki sʋ nɩɩ fii biti fʋ nyɛ anaana‐bii mʋ nɩɩ n da mɛ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ sa fʋ mʋ.

eng: He figured that God could even raise him from the dead. So in a way he did receive him back from the dead.
local: Abraham dan o nyi fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ bɩla taalɛ kyiŋi Isaki lɩɩ lewu tɔ. Nɩɩ fʋ kɩɩ mʋ, i du nɩn fɛɛ o wu nɩn, nɩɩ Abraham bwii nyɛ mʋ, mʋ abaa tɔ.

eng: By faith Isaac also blessed Jacob and Esau concerning their future.
local: Lɩɩ Isaki kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ mʋ, ɔ waa Gyekobu maa Esawu kʋsee lɩɩ bamʋ kakaakʋ kakyɩna tɔ sʋ.

eng: By faith Jacob blessed each of Josephs sons as he was dying and bowed in worship over the head of his staff. .
local: Lɩɩ Gyekobu kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ mʋ, saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti o wu mʋ, ɔ waa Gyosefu mu‐bii ana anyɔ kusee. Ɔ taa mʋ n‐yɩɩ kpasa mu kikpebi nɩɩ ɔ yɩn Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: By faith Joseph recalled the exodus of the Israelites at the end of his life, and gave instructions about burying his bones.
local: Lɩɩ Gyosefu kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ mʋ, saŋa mʋ nɩɩ mu lewu tiri tɔ mʋ, ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ asa Ɩsɩrayɩ abi yɛ saŋa kʋ ɩ ba bee biti ba lɩɩ Egyipiti tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ sa bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, bɛɛ kan bɛɛ lɩɩ mʋ, ba nyiŋi mʋ awiya sʋ kɛ ba taa.

eng: By faith Moses was hidden by his parents for three months when he was born, because they saw that the child was beautiful and they werent afraid of the kings orders.
local: Lɩɩ Mosesi akʋʋgɛpʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ mʋ, saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba kʋʋgɛ Mosesi mʋ, ba taa mʋ baala ɩbwaayɩ ɩsa, lɩɩ fɛɛ ba wu Mosesi maa pɔyɩ ɔdan, yɛgɛ ba man buu Owura kɔnɔsa mʋ gbaa mʋ ɩ man sʋ bamʋ kufuu.

eng: By faith Moses refused to be called the son of Pharaohs daughter when he was grown up.
local: Lɩɩ Mosesi kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ mʋ, saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kʋsʋ dan mʋ o mee biti ba tɩɩ mʋ yɛ ɔ gyɛ Egyipiti Owura Faro mu‐bii kyɩɩsa mu‐bii.

eng: He chose to be mistreated with Gods people instead of having the temporary pleasures of sin.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ waa mʋ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ maa Ɩbwaarɛ asa ba bʋga gyii awʋrʋfɔ mʋ, ɩ kyɔ fɛɛ o gyii ɔkɔn kakyɩna libi tɔ saŋa gbɛrɛɛ tɔ.

eng: He thought that the abuses he suffered for Christ were more valuable than the treasures of Egypt, since he was looking forward to the reward.
local: Mosesi wu fɛɛ ɩ dagaa maa gyii ipeeli lɩɩ Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ mʋ sʋ, ɩ kyɔ Egyipiti npatɩyɛ kparɛ kpini. Lɩɩ fɛɛ mu ansi ɩ gyan kʋkɔka mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ ka mʋ kakɛ kakʋ mʋ sʋ.

eng: By faith he left Egypt without being afraid of the kings anger. He kept on going as if he could see what is invisible.
local: Lɩɩ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ ɔ kʋsʋ lɩɩ Egyipiti tɔ, yɛgɛ ɔ man sʋ owura agbʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ mʋ kufuu. Ɔ kpan mʋ n‐yɩɩ yɩlɛ tɔ lɩɩ fɛɛ o wu Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔkʋ mee wu mʋ mʋ.

eng: By faith he kept the Passover and the sprinkling of blood, in order that the destroyer could not touch their firstborn children.
local: Lɩɩ mʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ mʋ, o yii Kanyaŋɛ Nbunɔ kigyii kakɛ mʋ. Kanɩn kakɛ maŋa tɔ nɩɩ ɔ yɛgɛ nɩɩ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi ba taa atɔbwaaya nkalan ŋmatɛ‐ŋmatɛ bamʋ nbunɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ɩ yɛgɛ nbii gyankpaasa ɔmɔɔpʋ mʋ man nyɛ mata bamʋ.

eng: By faith they crossed the Red Sea as if they were on dry land, but when the Egyptians tried it, they were drowned.
local: Lɩɩ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ nɩɩ ba natɛ Ɔpʋ Pɩpɛɛ mʋ tɔ faa fɛɛ kasɛ wʋlɛsa sʋ. Amaa Egyipiti abi ba maa kpan bamʋ n‐yɩɩ yɛ ba waa kanɩn mʋ, nkyu mʋ i gyii bamʋ.

eng: By faith Jerichos walls fell after the people marched around them for seven days.
local: Lɩɩ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ nɩɩ ba natɛ kyaabɔɔ Gyeriko kadɛ nkɛ nsunɔ nɩɩ kɩmʋ ɩpɔrɛ lala mʋ nɩɩ ɩ kyaabɔɔ kadɛ mʋ ɩ lɩɩ tɩyɛ.

eng: By faith Rahab the prostitute wasnt killed with the disobedient because she welcomed the spies in peace.
local: Lɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ gyaga Rahabu kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ mʋ, Ɩsɩrayɩ abi mʋ nɩɩ ba sun bamʋ ba naa baala kɩɩ Gyeriko kadɛ mʋ, ɔ kɩtaa bamʋ kɩfɔ. Ɩmʋ sʋ mɛɛ mʋ bɩrɛ ba man kyɩna mɔɔ mʋ bʋga bamʋ nɩɩ ba waa kʋsʋ tɔ ɔlʋn sa Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ.

eng: What more can I say? I would run out of time if I told you about Gideon, Barak, Samson, Jephthah, David, Samuel, and the prophets.
local: Mɩnɛ asɩn nɩɩ n bɩla yɛ n tɔwɛ tii sʋ? N mɛɛ yɛ n nyɛ saŋa tɔwɛ adɩdaapʋ asa fɛɛ Gideyon maa Baraki yɛ Samson yɛ Gyefeta yɛ Dawidi yɛ Samuweli yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ mʋ asɩn tii sʋ.

eng: Through faith they conquered kingdoms, brought about justice, realized promises, shut the mouths of lions.
local: Lɩɩ bamʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ mʋ, bamʋ akʋ ba kɔ gyii awuragyi lala sʋ, nɩɩ bamʋ akʋ ba gyii kesintin natɛ kɩmʋ ɔkpa sʋ, nɩɩ bamʋ akʋ ba nyɛ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ sa bamʋ mʋ, nɩɩ bamʋ akʋ mɔɔ ba tii bɩranɛ nnɔ yɛgɛ ba man taalɛ waa sɛhn.

eng: put out raging fires, escaped from the edge of the sword, found strength in weakness, were mighty in war, and routed foreign armies.
local: Lɩɩ bamʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ mʋ, bamʋ akʋ ba dunla fʋlɔn yen‐yen, nɩɩ bamʋ akʋ ba lɩɩ isakunde lewu tɔ. Bamʋ akʋ kapɔɔsɛ ki biliŋi ɔlʋn, nɩɩ ba waa alʋlʋnpʋ ɩkɔ tɔ gyii bamʋ dun ana ndɛndɛmandɛ anaakɔpʋ sʋ.

eng: Women received back their dead by resurrection. Others were tortured and refused to be released so they could gain a better resurrection.
local: Lɩɩ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ mʋ, akyɩɩ akʋ ba nyɛ bamʋ‐nyi mu‐bii ana mʋ nɩɩ ba wu, nɩɩ ba bwii kyiŋi ba nkpa tɔ. Akʋ ba nyɛ kanyanla nɩɩ ba yɩlɛ tɔ kina, fɛɛ ba mɛɛ lɩɩ bamʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii kamaa kɛ ba nyɛ sɩlɛ lɩɩ lewu tɔ, amaa ba kyula fɛɛ bee biti ba wu, kɛ ba nyɛ kelewu kyiŋi yɛ nkpa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋrɔn.

eng: But others experienced public shame by being taunted and whipped; they were even put in chains and in prison.
local: Lɩɩ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ mʋ, asa ba yooli akʋ nɩɩ ba tɩŋɛ akʋ, nɩɩ ba da akʋ mɔɔ ɩgbɛɛ taa bamʋ naa tii ibu to.

eng: They were stoned to death, they were cut in two, and they died by being murdered with swords. They went around wearing the skins of sheep and goats, needy, oppressed, and mistreated.
local: Lɩɩ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ mʋ, asa ba twɩɩ akʋ abuu mɔɔ bamʋ, nɩɩ akʋ mɔɔ ba baŋɩ bamʋ tɔ anyɔ‐anyɔ, nɩɩ akʋ mɔɔ ba taa isakunde mɔɔ bamʋ. Akʋ ba taa nbʋlʋpʋ yɛ nbwɛ ɩwʋlɛ ba bun, yɛgɛ ba naa ba naa kitiri tɔ yɛ awʋrʋfɔ yɛ kanyanla tɔ.

eng: The world didnt deserve them. They wandered around in deserts, mountains, caves, and holes in the ground.
local: Dulinya nɩmʋ tɔ asa libi ba man bɩla dagaa fɛɛ ba maa kanɩn asa maŋa ba kyɩna bʋga. Bamʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii kɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ba sʋ ba naa bɛɛ yaalɛ ifuli sʋ yɛ abɩɩ sʋ, nɩɩ bɛɛ baala afʋlɛ ɩbɔ tɔ yɛ kasɛ ɩbɔ lala tɔ.

eng: All these people didnt receive what was promised, though they were given approval for their faith.
local: Asa banɩmʋ kpini ba nyɛ kɩyɩn lɩɩ bamʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ, yɛgɛ bamʋ ɔkʋ man nyɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ sa bamʋ mʋ.

eng: God provided something better for us so they wouldnt be made perfect without us.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ ŋmina atɔ danbɩrasa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ kyɔ yɩla anɛ, nɩɩ ngbaa bamʋ bee biti ba nyɛ, amaa ba man nyɛ bamʋ kusun danbɩrasa mʋ kʋkɔka, amɔɔ naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ an biti a lʋwɛ anɛ kusun pɔyɩ kɛ anaa bamʋ kpini a naa bʋga nyɛ kʋkɔka mʋ kyɛɛkyɛɛ.

eng: Keep loving each other like family.
local: Fan kyaga sʋ fen biti abaa fɛɛ kinyi‐bii Krisito tɔ.

eng: Dont neglect to open up your homes to guests, because by doing this some have been hosts to angels without knowing it.
local: Fan nyiŋi kɛ fan kɩtaa afɔ fanɛ akpaara sʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ akʋ ba maa waa kanɩn mʋ, ba kɩtaa Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ kɩfɔ yɛgɛ ba man da sʋ.

eng: Remember prisoners as if you were in prison with them, and people who are mistreated as if you were in their place.
local: Fan nyiŋi Krisito abii mʋ nɩɩ ba tii bamʋ obu mʋ, fɛɛ fan maa tii bamʋ sʋ obu otiikpa mʋ. Kɛ fan nyiŋi bamʋ nɩɩ bee gyii awʋrʋfɔ mʋ, fɛɛ fanɛ ii gyii kanɩn awʋrʋfɔ maŋa.

eng: Marriage must be honored in every respect, with no cheating on the relationship, because God will judge the sexually immoral person and the person who commits adultery.
local: Ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ dagaa maa taa bɛɛrɛɛ yɛ kesintin sa kekili, kɛ ɔkʋ ɔ man lɩɩ mʋ‐ka abɛɛ mu‐kuli kamaa naa dɛ ɔkʋ asɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ waa kanyɩn yɛ kakyɩɩ biti kɩgyagaa, yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ lɩɩ mʋ‐ka ana abɛɛ bamʋ‐kuli ana kamaa mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ talɛ bamʋ.

eng: Your way of life should be free from the love of money, and you should be content with what you have. After all, he has said, I will never leave you or abandon you .
local: Fan man yɛgɛ kufulee biti bansa kɩ baa kɩ bʋ fanɛ kakyɩna tɔ, nɩɩ ɩmʋ nɩɩ fan sʋ mʋ ɩ kʋkwɛ fanɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Kakaakʋ n mɛɛ yɛ n yɛgɛ fanɛ, nɩɩ kakaakʋ n mɛɛ yɛ n kina fan.

eng: This is why we can confidently say, The Lord is my helper, and I wont be afraid. What can people do to me? .
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ an taalɛ a tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ gyɛ mɛ ɔkyɛtɔpʋ, n mɛɛ yɛ n baa n sʋ kufuu. Mɩnɛ nɩɩ nyɩmɩsa ɩ taalɛ waa mɛ.

eng: Remember your leaders who spoke Gods word to you. Imitate their faith as you consider the way their lives turned out.
local: Fan nyiŋi fanɛ agyankpaapʋ dɩdaa mʋ nɩɩ ba da tɔwɛ fanɛ abwaarɛsɩn mʋ. Fan yɛgɛ fanɛ nfɛɛrɛ ɩ baa ɩ gyan kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba kyɩna kakyɩna danbɩrasa mʋ, kɛ fan kasɛ bamʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii mʋ tɔ.

eng: Jesus Christ is the same yesterday, today, and forever!.
local: Kanan mʋ nɩɩ Yesu Krisito du ndee mʋ, kanɩn nɩɩ o du ndaga, nɩɩ kanɩn nɩɩ ee biti ɔ baa o du kakpaa.

eng: Dont be misled by the many strange teachings out there. Its a good thing for the heart to be strengthened by grace rather than by food. Food doesnt help those who live in this context.
local: Fan man yɛgɛ akaapʋ‐bi kʋkwɩɩ‐kʋkwɩɩ ɩkʋ ɩ lɛɛ fanɛ lɩɩ kesintin ɔkpa mʋ sʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɩ bʋrɔn sa anɛ fɛɛ anɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kayaayu mʋ kɩ sa anɛ nkɔlɔ tɔ ɔlʋn, nɩɩ ɩ man waa fɛɛ nbara mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dɛ sa agyitɔ kigyii, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ akʋ ba gyii ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ man sʋ tɔnɔ sa bamʋ mʋ.

eng: We have an altar, and those who serve as priests in the meeting tent dont have the right to eat from it.
local: Saŋa mʋ dɩdaa mʋ, anɛ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi an sʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kaswɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ lɛɛ saraga kɩmʋ sʋ, yɛgɛ Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ tantɩ kubuu lala mʋ tɔ bɛɛ lɛɛ saraga mʋ, ba man sʋ ɔkpa be gyii ɩmʋ atɔ.

eng: The blood of the animals is carried into the holy of holies by the high priest as an offering for sin, and their bodies are burned outside the camp.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ ɩɩ taa atɔbwaaya nkalan ee loo Opula Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛkpa Kɩkyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ tɔ ɛɛ lɛɛ alibi kɩtaakɛɛ saraga, amaa atɔbwaaya mʋ inun bɩrɛ bee tuwa ɩmʋ kanɩn kadɛ mʋ nbwama nɩn.

eng: And so Jesus also suffered outside the city gate to make the people holy with his own blood.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ Yesu gbaa gyii awʋrʋfɔ kadɛ nbwama, nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ gbaa‐gbaa nkalan fwɩɩ asa lɩɩ bamʋ alibi tɔ.

eng: So now, lets go to him outside the camp, bearing his shame.
local: Kanɩn sʋ fan yɛgɛ a lɩɩ kpee mʋ asɛ kadɛ mʋ nbwama naa gyii ipeeli mʋ nɩɩ o gyii mʋ.

eng: We dont have a permanent city here, but rather we are looking for the city that is still to come.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ mʋ, a man sʋ kadɛ mʋ nɩɩ a biti a kyɩna kɩmʋ tɔ kakpaa, amaa an kɩɩ kadɛ mʋ nɩɩ kii biti kɩ ba mʋ ɔkpa nɩn.

eng: So lets continually offer up a sacrifice of praise through him, which is the fruit from our lips that confess his name.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan yɛgɛ a bɔla Yesu sʋ lɛɛ saraga yɩn Ɩbwaarɛ saŋa kʋmaa, nɩɩ kanɩn saraga maŋa ɩ gyɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ an sʋ anɛ asɩn tɔwɛsa an lɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kɩtɩɩ.

eng: Dont forget to do good and to share what you have because God is pleased with these kinds of sacrifices.
local: Amaa fan man tan adanbɩrasa kɩwaa sʋ, kɛ fan baa fɛn kyɛ abaa tɔ, lɩɩ fɛɛ falɛ ɩsaraga ɩnɩmʋ ɩ bɔla Ɩbwaarɛ ansi.

eng: Rely on your leaders and defer to them, because they watch over your whole being as people who are going to be held responsible for you. They need to be able to do this with pleasure and not with complaints about you, because that wouldnt help you.
local: Fan nu bamʋ nɩɩ ba gyankpaa fanɛ kabwaarɛ‐sun mʋ tɔ asɛ, kɛ fan bɩya fanɛ n‐yɩɩ kasɛ sa bamʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ ɩɩ kɩɩ fanɛ kala ana sʋ kakɛ kʋmaa, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ taasɛ bamʋ lɩɩ fanɛ ana sʋ. Nɩɩ fan kan nu bamʋ asɛ mʋ, ɩɩ yɛgɛ ba sun kusun mʋ yɛ kensi‐gyii, nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ kanɩn, bee biti ba taa kɔkɔlɔ nyita waa kusun mʋ, ɩmɛɛ mʋ ɩ mɛɛ yɛ ɩ kyɛ fanɛ tɔ.

eng: Pray for us. Were sure that we have a good conscience, and we want to do the right thing in every way.
local: Fan kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ kʋlɛ fɛn sa anɛ. Nɩɩ an kɔɔlɛ gyii fɛɛ an sʋ nfɛɛrɛ danbɩrasa, lɩɩ fɛɛ an biti fɛɛ an waa atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋrɔn saŋa kʋmaa sa Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: Im particularly asking you to do this so that I can be returned to you quickly.
local: N kʋlɛ fanɛ gaa fɛɛ fan kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ kʋlɛ fɔnfɔn sa mɛ, kɛ n nyɛ ɔkpa bwii ba fanɛ asɛ bilen.

eng: May the God of peace, who brought back the great shepherd of the sheep, our Lord Jesus, from the dead by the blood of the eternal covenant.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ gyɛ kayɩɩ‐yuuli wuya, mʋ ɩ bɔla katɔwɛ kyula mʋ nɩɩ kɩ man sʋ ɔkaa mʋ nkalan sʋ kyiŋi anɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Yesu Krisito lɩɩ lewu tɔ. Yesu ɩ gyɛ nbʋlʋpʋ Ɔkpapʋ kparɛ mʋ.

eng: equip you with every good thing to do his will, by developing in us what pleases him through Jesus Christ. To him be the glory forever and always. Amen.
local: Nɩɩ nbɩɩnbɩɩ bɩrɛ n kʋlɛ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ sa fanɛ atɔ danbɩrasa mʋ nɩɩ ii tiri fanɛ, kɛ fɛn taalɛ waa mu kebiti. Kɛ ɔ bɔla Yesu Krisito sʋ taa anɛ waa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ bɔla mu ansi. Yesu Krisito nɩɩ bʋnyaa gyɛ mʋ lɛɛ kakpaa. Amen.

eng: I urge you, brothers and sisters, to put up with this message of encouragement, since Ive only written a short letter to you!.
local: Mɛ tɔmaa ana Krisito abuupu, n kʋlɛ fanɛ gaa fɛɛ fen nu mɛ asɩn tɔwɛ waasa anɩmʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔwʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ n kyʋrɔɔ n sa fanɛ falɛ man bʋnswɩɩ.

eng: You should know that our brother Timothy has been set free. If he comes soon, we will travel together to see you.
local: N biti fɛɛ fɛn bɩɩ fɛɛ ba tigi anɛ bɩrɩsa Timoti mʋ nɩɩ ba daa kɩtaa tii obu mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ kan ba bilen mʋ, maa mʋ an biti a ba fanɛ asɛ.

eng: Greet your leaders and all of Gods holy people. The group from Italy greets you.
local: Fan fwaala fanɛ agyankpaapʋ mʋ yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asa mʋ kpini. Krisito abi mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Itali mʋ gbaa bɛɛ fwaala fanɛ.

eng: May grace be with all of you.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ kabwaala kɩ baa kɩ bʋ fanɛ kpini asɛ. Amen.

eng: So then, with endurance, lets also run the race that is laid out in front of us, since we have such a great cloud of witnesses surrounding us. Lets throw off any extra baggage, get rid of the sin that trips us up.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ fɛɛ asa damantɛ mʋ nɩɩ ba lɛɛ bamʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii ifuli kaapʋ, nɩɩ ba lɛɛ bamʋ ansi bɛɛ kɩɩ anɛ falɛ sʋ mʋ, fan yɛgɛ a lɛɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ kii tii anɛ ɔkpa, yɛ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ an tɩyɛ alibi tɔ bilen‐bilen mʋ fɔyɩ, kɛ a payɩ ansi sɩlɛ kɩsɩlɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ dɛ anɛ ansi tɔ mʋ naa fʋʋ ɔkaa.

eng: and fix our eyes on Jesus, faiths pioneer and perfecter. He endured the cross, ignoring the shame, for the sake of the joy that was laid out in front of him, and sat down at the right side of Gods throne.
local: Fan yɛgɛ an baa an kɩɩ Yesu. Mʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ anɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii mʋ kɩ gyan mʋ sʋ lɩɩ npiili asɛ, nɩɩ mʋ ii biti ɔ yɛgɛ a taalɛ yɩlɛ tɔ naa fʋʋ ɔkaa. Nɩɩ o nyi ɔkɔn mʋ nɩɩ kɩ dɛ kii gyoo mʋ sʋ mʋ, ɔ man taa oyu laasa lewu yɛ ɩmʋ ipeeli mʋ tɩɩ sɛhn. Nɩɩ nbɩɩnbɩɩ ɔ tɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gya mʋ gyisa sʋ.

eng: Think about the one who endured such opposition from sinners so that you wont be discouraged and you wont give up.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan kɩɩ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Yesu yɩlɛ tɔ gyii awʋrʋfɔ alibi awaapʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba gyɛ mʋ akisipu mʋ abaa tɔ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɩ waa kɛ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ ɩ man yɔɔ fanɛ kɛ fanɛ nkɔlɔ ɩ man nyita fanɛ.

eng: In your struggle against sin, you havent resisted yet to the point of shedding blood.
local: Halɩɩ fan maa nyaŋɛ ansi fɛn kɔ fen tii alibi kɩwaa falɛ gbaa mʋ, fan man pɩɩta kɔ naa fʋʋ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ii biti ɩ sʋla fanɛ kpee lewu tɔ.

eng: and you have forgotten the encouragement that addresses you as sons and daughters: My child, dont make light of the Lords discipline or give up when you are corrected by him, .
local: Fɛn tan asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ waa fanɛ fɛɛ mu‐bii ana mʋ sʋ? Ba kyʋrɔɔ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ yɛ, Mi‐bii, nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kan ɛɛ bɩla fʋ mʋ, man taa ɩmʋ waa ɩwɔgya asɩn. Nɩɩ ɛɛ kan ɛɛ bɩɩtɛ fʋ kʋsʋ mʋ, man yɛgɛ fʋ abaa tɔ ɩ yɔɔ.

eng: because the Lord disciplines whomever he loves, and he punishes every son or daughter whom he accepts. .
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti bamʋ asɩn mʋ, bamʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ bɩla, nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ mʋ fɛɛ mu‐bii mʋ, ɛɛ bɩɩtɛ mʋ kʋsʋ.

eng: Bear hardship for the sake of discipline. God is treating you like sons and daughters! What child isnt disciplined by his or her father?.
local: Fan nyɛ nkɔlɔ yɩlɛ awʋrʋfɔ tɔ fɛɛ kɩbɩla, Ɩbwaarɛ ɩɩ waa fanɛ kanɩn fɛɛ mu‐bii ana. Lɩɩ fɛɛ kebii mɔɔ nɩɩ mʋ‐sɛ mɛɛ bɩla mʋ?.

eng: But if you dont experience discipline, which happens to all children, then you are illegitimate and not real sons and daughters.
local: Amaa nɩɩ fan man kan nyɛ kɩbɩla fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ mu‐bii kʋmaa nɩmʋ ɩɩ nyɛ mʋ, kanɩn bɩrɛ fan man gyɛ mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mu‐bii ana, ɔkʋ mu‐bii ana ɩ gyɛ fanɛ.

eng: Whats more, we had human parents who disciplined us, and we respected them for it. How much more should we submit to the Father of spirits and live?.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ kpini gbaa mʋ, an sʋ kasɛ sʋ nfɩɩ asɛ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ bɩla anɛ, yɛgɛ an sʋ bɛɛrɛɛ an sa bamʋ. Nɩɩ mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ a mɛɛ bɩya anɛ n‐yɩɩ kasɛ gaa sa Ɩbwaarɛ Anɛ‐Sɛ, mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ sʋsʋ, kɛ a nyɛ nkpa?.

eng: Our human parents disciplined us for a little while, as it seemed best to them, but God does it for our benefit so that we can share his holiness.
local: Nɩɩ anɛ kasɛ sʋ asɛ bɛɛ bɩla anɛ saŋa gbɛrɛɛ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ kɩɩ fɛɛ ɩ bʋrɔn sa bamʋ mʋ. Amaa anɛ kɩdanbɩrasa sʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩɩ bɩla anɛ, kɛ a nyɛ‐nyɛ mʋ kɩbaabʋtɔ kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ.

eng: No discipline is fun while it lasts, but it seems painful at the time. Later, however, it yields the peaceful fruit of righteousness for those who have been trained by it.
local: Kɩbɩla kʋ man bʋ tɔ nɩɩ kɩ bʋkɔn. Opula maŋa tɔ mʋ ɩɩ kɔɔ nɩn. Amaa bamʋ nɩɩ ba nu sa kɩbɩla mʋ, ɩɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ bamʋ kakyɩna tɔ ɩkpa i kyiigi, nɩɩ ɩɩ sa bamʋ kayɩɩ yuuli.

eng: So strengthen your drooping hands and weak knees!.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan man yɛgɛ fanɛ abaa a yɔɔ fanɛ, amaa fan kʋsʋ yɩlɛ kɩtaa tɔ kinkin.

eng: Make straight paths for your feet so that if any part is lame, it will be healed rather than injured more seriously.
local: Fan yɛgɛ fanɛ ɩkpa mʋ nɩɩ fɛn naa sʋ mʋ ɩ waa ntintin, kɛ ayaa yɛ abaa wusa wuya ana ba man fɔyɩ ɔkpa, amaa ba nyɛ ɔlʋn.

eng: Pursue the goal of peace along with everyone—and holiness as well, because no one will see the Lord without it.
local: Fan kpan fanɛ n‐yɩɩ ɔkpa kʋmaa sʋ, kɛ fanaa asa kpini fan taalɛ kyɩna kayɩɩ yuuli tɔ, kɛ fan kyɩna kakyɩna mʋ nɩɩ kɩ man sʋ ineesi. Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ ineesi mʋ, ɔ mɛɛ yɛ o wu Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: Make sure that no one misses out on Gods grace. Make sure that no root of bitterness grows up that might cause trouble and pollute many people.
local: Fan baa fan da sʋ kɛ fanɛ tɔ ɔkʋ ɔ man pan Ɩbwaarɛ bwaala, kɛ ɔkʋ ɔ man ba waa fɛɛ oyu bʋlɩlɩsa mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ dan nɩɩ kɩɩ bɩya asɩn nɩɩ kii nyita asa gaalaagaa mʋ.

eng: Make sure that no one becomes sexually immoral or ungodly like Esau. He sold his inheritance as the oldest son for one meal.
local: Fan kɩɩ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ yɛ ɔdan, kɛ fanɛ tɔ ɔkʋ ɔ man taa kanyɩn abɛɛ kakyɩɩ biti kɩgyagaa waa mʋ n‐yɩɩ tɔ. Fan man yɛgɛ fanɛ tɔ ɔkʋ ɔ taa Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn mʋ tɩɩ kɩyan fɛɛ Esawu mʋ nɩɩ lɩɩ agyitɔ kʋlʋn sʋ, ɔ fɛ mʋ kɩbɩlɩsa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ kɩmʋ kapatɩyɛ ogyipu mʋ sa mu‐supu.

eng: You know that afterward, when he wanted to inherit the blessing, he was rejected because he couldnt find a way to change his heart and life, though he looked for it with tears.
local: Ɩmʋ kamaa tɔ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fen nyi mʋ, saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti fɛɛ mʋ‐sɛ ɔ waa mʋ kʋsee mʋ, ɔ pan kɩmʋ ɔ man nyɛ kusee mʋ. O sii taa ansikyu nɩn ee biti fɛɛ naafɔɔ ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɔ wʋla waa yɩla mʋ ɩ kyɛɛgɛ, amaa ɔ man nyɛ ɩmʋ kanɩn.

eng: You havent drawn near to something that can be touched: a burning fire, darkness, shadow, a whirlwind.
local: Fan nyiŋi fɛɛ fan man ba Sinayi kɩbɩɩ mʋ asɛ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩsɩrayɩ adɩdaapʋ mʋ ba nin tiri kɩmʋ, ɩmaa kɩmʋ fʋlɔn mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ dɛɛ yen‐yen, yɛgɛ ɩɩ waa kitentenbiri tumm, nɩɩ afuu lala ɩ maa da mʋ.

eng: a blast of a trumpet, and a sound of words that made the ones who heard it beg that there wouldnt be one more word.
local: Nɩɩ ba nu kabɩlɛ lala kʋ kusu yɛ ɔbɩlɛ kʋ ɩlawʋ maa tɔwɛ bamʋ asɩn, nɩɩ kufuu kɩ kɩtaa bamʋ, nɩɩ ba kʋlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ fɛɛ ɔ yɛgɛ katɔwɛ.

eng: They couldnt stand the command, If even a wild animal touches the mountain, it must be stoned. .
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ asa mʋ ba man taalɛ yɩlɛ kɔnɔsa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sa bamʋ mʋ. Kɔnɔsa mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, Halɩɩ nɩɩ kʋtɔbwaaya gbaa kan mata kɩbɩɩ mʋ, ba tʋʋ abuu mɔɔ mʋ.

eng: The sight was so frightening that Moses said, Im terrified and shaking.
local: Kesintin tɔ mʋ, asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɩ waa tʋtɔ mʋ ɩ waa kufuu gaa, halɩɩ Mosesi gbaa tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɩ waa mɛ kufuu nɩɩ mɛ n‐yɩɩ ɩ kyɩkyaa mɛ.

eng: But you have drawn near to Mount Zion, the city of the living God, heavenly Jerusalem, to countless angels in a festival gathering.
local: Amaa fanɛɛ fanɛ bɩrɛ fan nyɛ ɔkpa ba Siyon kɩbɩɩ mʋ asɛ nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ sʋsʋ Gyerusalem, Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ tɔ kakpaa mʋ kadɛ lala. Fan ba opula mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ ngbɩn‐ngbɩn kensi‐gyii ngyaŋɛ mʋ tɔ.

eng: So then the first covenant had regulations for the priests service and the holy place on earth.
local: Kanɩn sʋ katɔwɛ kyula gyankpaasa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ waa maa asa nsana mʋ, kɩ sʋ ɩmʋ kabwaarɛ‐sun nbara yɛ tantɩ kubuu opula kyɩrɛkyɩrɛkpa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ kasɛ sʋ mʋ.

eng: to the assembly of Gods firstborn children who are registered in heaven, to God the judge of all, to the spirits of the righteous who have been made perfect.
local: Nɩɩ fan ba Ɩbwaarɛ nbii gyankpaasa mʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ bamʋ atɩɩ sʋsʋ. Nɩɩ fan ba Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ asa kpini asɩn ogyipu mʋ asɛ. Nɩɩ fan ba asa mʋ nɩɩ bamʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛgɛ nɩɩ bamʋ kala ana ba ba waa kyɛɛkyɛɛ mʋ asɛ.

eng: They pitched the first tent called the holy place. It contained the lampstand, the table, and the loaves of bread presented to God.
local: Nɩɩ ba taa kɩtaa lala sɩɩtasa barɩgɛ kɩmʋ tɔ ipula ɩnyɔ. Opula gyankpaasa mʋ tɔ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ Opula Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ nɩɩ kɩmʋ tɔ nɩɩ fɩtɩla ɔgyagakpa yɛ teeburi yɛ bodobodo mʋ nɩɩ ba taa sa Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ bʋ.

eng: to Jesus the mediator of the new covenant, and to the sprinkled blood that speaks better than Abels blood.
local: Nɩɩ fan ba Yesu asɛ, mʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ yɩlɛ asa yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ nsana sa katɔwɛ kyula pʋpwɛ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ waa mʋ, nɩɩ mʋ ɩ taa mʋ nkalan ŋmatɛ, nɩɩ mʋ nkalan mʋ ɩɩ tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa ɩ kyɔ Abeli lɛɛ mʋ.

eng: There was a tent behind the second curtain called the holy of holies.
local: Nɩɩ kɩtaa mʋ kamaa ɩ gyɛ opula nyɔsapʋ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ Opula Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛkpa Kɩkyɩrɛkyɩrɛ.

eng: See to it that you dont resist the one who is speaking. If the people didnt escape when they refused to listen to the one who warned them on earth, how will we escape if we reject the one who is warning from heaven?.
local: Fan kɩɩ yɛ ɔdan kɛ fan man kina Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ tɔwɛ fanɛ mʋ asɩn mʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba kina ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ abwaarɛsɩn kasɛ sʋ nfɩɩ gbaa mʋ, ba man taalɛ sɩlɛ lɩɩ kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ mʋ tɔ. Kanɩn bɩrɛ, nɩɩ an kan kina ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ tɔwɛ ɛɛ lɩɩ sʋsʋ ɛɛ sa anɛ asɛ kunu mɔɔ de, nɛnɛ nɩɩ an taalɛ sɩlɛ lɩɩ kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ mʋ tɔ?.

eng: It had the gold altar for incense and the chest containing the covenant, which was covered with gold on all sides. In the chest there was a gold jar containing manna, Aarons rod that budded, and the stone tablets of the covenant.
local: Nɩɩ tʋtɔ nɩɩ suwa kaswɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ bee tuwa atɔ liifensa kɩmʋ sʋ, yɛ katɔwɛ kyula kɩdakaa mʋ nɩɩ ba taa suwa nyalɛ kɩmʋ mʋ bʋ. Kɩdakaa maŋa tɔ nɩɩ suwa bonburon mʋ nɩɩ kɩ sʋ Mana agyitɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sa bamʋ naana ana ofuli sʋ mʋ bʋ. Nɩɩ Aron kikpebi mʋ nɩɩ kɩ pɔrɔgɛ afatɛɛ bunboli, yɛ abuu panpasan anyɔ mʋ nɩɩ Mosesi kyʋrɔɔ Ɩbwaarɛ nbara kudu mʋ gyaga sʋ mʋ bʋ.

eng: His voice shook the earth then, but now he has made a promise: Still once more I will shake not only the earth but heaven also .
local: Kanɩn saŋa maŋa tɔ Ɩbwaarɛ maa tɔwɛ mʋ, kasɛ ki gyingyen. Amaa nbɩɩnbɩɩ bɩrɛ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ yɩla yɛ, Man gyɛ kasɛ nkʋn nɩɩ n biti n bɩla gyingyen, amaa sʋsʋ gbaa tii sʋ.

eng: Above the chest there were magnificent winged creatures casting their shadow over the seat of the chest, where sin is taken care of. Right now we cant talk about these things in detail.
local: Nɩɩ ba waa atɔ anyɔ akʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ atɛɛ ba gyan kɩdakaa mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɩɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ bʋ tʋtɔ. Ba yaasɛ bamʋ atɛɛ bun kɩdakaa mʋ obunkpa nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ tʋtɔ yɛ Opula mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sʋ alibi ɛɛ kɛɛ. Amaa an mɛɛ taalɛ lɛɛ asɩn nɩmʋ kpini kasɛ sa fanɛ nbɩɩnbɩɩ nɩmʋ kʋkʋlʋn‐kʋlʋn.

eng: The words still once more reveal the removal of what is shaken—the things that are part of this creation—so that what isnt shaken will remain.
local: Asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ N biti n bɩla mʋ, Ɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ atɔ tʋʋsa mʋ ii biti i gyingyen kɛ ɩ lɩɩ kyʋn, kɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ mɛɛ taalɛ gyingyen mʋ i sii.

eng: When these things have been prepared in this way, priests enter the first tent all the time as they perform their service.
local: Falɛ nɩɩ ba waa kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ yɩla, kɛ Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu mʋ bee loo tantɩ opula kyɩrɛkyɩrɛkpa mʋ tɔ kakɛ kʋmaa bɛɛ waa bamʋ kiseepu kusun awaasa.

eng: Therefore, since we are receiving a kingdom that cant be shaken, lets continue to express our gratitude. With this gratitude, lets serve in a way that is pleasing to God with respect and awe.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan yɛgɛ a taa afwaala lala sa Ɩbwaarɛ fɛɛ a maa nyɛ mʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ nɩɩ kɩ mɛɛ taalɛ gyingyen. Kɛ a taa kabwaarɛ sɩlɛ yɛ bɛɛrɛɛ sun Ɩbwaarɛ, ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bɔla mu ansi.

eng: But only the high priest enters the second tent once a year. He never does this without blood, which he offers for himself and for the sins the people committed in ignorance.
local: Amaa, amɔɔ Ɩbwaarɛ Oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ nkʋn ii loo Opula Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛkpa Kɩkyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ kesu kʋmaa tɔ mʋ, ee loo tʋtɔ kɩlɩɩ kʋlʋn nɩn. Nɩɩ atɔbwaaya nkalan nɩɩ ɔ sʋ ee loo tʋtɔ, nɩɩ ɛɛ taa kʋlɛ mʋ gbaa‐gbaa kumu sʋ alibi yɛ alibi mʋ nɩɩ mʋ asa ba waa yɛgɛ ba man nyi yɛ ɩ gyɛ alibi mʋ.

eng: because our God really is a consuming fire.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ anɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gyɛ fʋlɔn mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ dɛɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa ɛɛ lʋwɛ.

eng: With this, the Holy Spirit is showing that the way into the holy place hadnt been revealed yet while the first tent was standing.
local: Bɔla ɩnɩmʋ sʋ mʋ, Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ ɩ lɛɛ kaapʋ fɛɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ tantɩ kubuu lala gyankpaasa mʋ maa san ɔ yɩlɛ falɛ bɩrɛ, ɔkpa mʋ man pɩɩta bugi kpee Opula kyɩrɛkyɩrɛkpa Kɩkyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ tɔ.

eng: This is a symbol for the present time. It shows that the gifts and sacrifices that are being offered cant perfect the conscience of the one who is serving.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ asɩn kaapʋsa sa anɛ mʋ nɩɩ an tɛ ndaga kakyɩna nɩmʋ tɔ. Ɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ nkɛɛ mʋ nɩɩ ba taa bɩya Ɩbwaarɛ, yɛ atɔbwaaya mʋ nɩɩ ba taa lɔŋɔ sa Ɩbwaarɛ gbaa mʋ, ɩ mɛɛ taalɛ yɛgɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ bee sun mʋ mʋ, bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ ɩ bɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ taa bamʋ alibi kɛɛ bamʋ.

eng: These are superficial regulations that are only about food, drink, and various ritual ways to wash with water. They are regulations that have been imposed until the time of the new order.
local: Ɩmʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa mʋ kpini mʋ ɩ gyɛ ikisii lɩɩ agyitɔ kigyii yɛ atɔ nuusa sʋ yɛ atɔ kɩfwɩɩ yɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ kigyee. Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ ansi sʋ awaasa nbara mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bɩya kabwaarɛ‐sun pʋpwɛ katɔwɛ kyula pʋpwɛ mʋ tɔ.

eng: But Christ has appeared as the high priest of the good things that have happened. He passed through the greater and more perfect meeting tent, which isnt made by human hands .
local: Amaa Krisito ba fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ sa atɔ danbɩrasa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ tɔ nbɩɩnbɩɩ falɛ. Nɩɩ o loo tantɩ kubuu lala mʋ nɩɩ kɩ dagaa, nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ kɩkparɛ, nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ nyɩmɩsa i yii kɩmʋ, nɩɩ kɩ man gyɛ dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ lɛɛ mʋ.

eng: He entered the holy of holies once for all by his own blood, not by the blood of goats or calves, securing our deliverance for all time.
local: Krisito maa loo tantɩ kubuu Opula Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛkpa Kɩkyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ kɩlɩɩ kʋlʋn kakpaa mʋ, ɔ man taa nbwɛ yɛ ɩnaatɛ nkalan loo naa waa kɔlɔŋɔ. Amaa ɔ taa mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mʋ nkalan loo nɩn naa ka alibi kʋkɔ kɛ a nyɛ kamɔlɩgɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ sʋ nkpa kakpaa.

eng: If the blood of goats and bulls and the sprinkled ashes of cows made spiritually contaminated people holy and clean.
local: Dɩdaa lɛɛ mʋ bɩrɛ nbwɛ yɛ ɩnaatɛ nkalan, yɛ naatɛ kadanba mʋ nɩɩ ba tuwa mʋ nsɔ nɩɩ ba sʋ bɛɛ ŋmatɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba waa ineesi mʋ sʋ, kɛ ɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ ba nyɛ bamʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋ kɩfwɩɩ. Nɩɩ ɩnɩmʋ ɩ kan taalɛ fwɩɩ bamʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋ ineesi mʋ.

eng: how much more will the blood of Jesus wash our consciences clean from dead works in order to serve the living God? He offered himself to God through the eternal Spirit as a sacrifice without any flaw.
local: kanɩn bɩrɛ, Krisito nkalan mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bɔla Kayaayu mʋ nɩɩ kɩ sʋ nkpa kakpaa sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ gbaa‐gbaa n‐yɩɩ waa kɔlɔŋɔ kparɛ sa Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ, nɛnɛ sʋ nɩɩ mʋ nkalan maŋa ɩ mɛɛ taalɛ fwɩɩ anɛ nfɛɛrɛ lɩɩ awaasa libi mʋ nɩɩ ɩ sʋ anɛ ii kpee lewu tɔ mʋ? Ɩnɩmʋ ii biti ɩ yɛgɛ kɛ a nyɛ sun Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛ nkpa tɔ mʋ kakpaa.

eng: This is why hes the mediator of a new covenant : so that those who are called might receive the promise of the eternal inheritance on the basis of his death. His death occurred to set them free from the offenses committed under the first covenant.
local: Kpalɩ ɩnɩmʋ sʋ mʋ, Krisito ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ yɩlɛ asa yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ nsana sa katɔwɛ kyula pʋpwɛ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ waa mʋ, kɛ ɩ yɛgɛ bamʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tɩɩ bamʋ mʋ, ba ba nyɛ mʋ kusee mʋ nɩɩ ɔ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ sa bamʋ mʋ kakpaa. Ɩnɩmʋ ii biti ɩ waa nɩn, lɩɩ fɛɛ Krisito taa mʋ n‐yɩɩ sa lewu nɩɩ ɔ ka bamʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba waa alibi katɔwɛ kyula gyankpaasa mʋ tɔ mʋ kʋkɔ.

eng: When there is a will, you need to confirm the death of the one who made the will.
