eng: After all, you say, Im rich, and Ive grown wealthy, and I dont need a thing. You dont realize that you are miserable, pathetic, poor, blind, and naked.
local: Fɛn tɔwɛ yɛ, an gyɛ atɔ wuya ana, nɩɩ kʋtɔ kʋmaa kɩ waa ɔdan ɩ sa anɛ, nɩɩ sɛhn man tiri anɛ. Amaa fan man nyi fɛɛ fan gyɛ awʋrʋfɔ agyipu, nɩɩ fanɛ asɩn a sʋ kʋwɛɛ, nɩɩ fan gyɛ atiripu, yɛ agyaatanapʋ nɩɩ fan naa kubolonkpan.

eng: My advice is that you buy gold from me that has been purified by fire so that you may be rich, and white clothing to wear so that your nakedness wont be shamefully exposed, and ointment to put on your eyes so that you may see.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ n tɔwɛ n waa fanɛ fɛɛ fan ba mɛ asɛ ba sɔɔ suwa kparɛ, kɛ fan ba waa atɔ wuya ana. Kɛ fan ba sɔɔ atɔ fufuli mɛ asɛ naa bun fanɛ ipeeli kubolonkpan mʋ sʋ. Kɛ fan bɩla sɔɔ kɩfɛ waa fanɛ ansi tɔ kɛ fan taalɛ nyɛ wu.

eng: I correct and discipline those whom I love. So be earnest and change your hearts and lives.
local: Nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ n biti bamʋ asɩn mʋ, n gyii kadɛ sa bamʋ nɩɩ n bɩɩtɛ bamʋ asʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ fan payɩ fanɛ ansi kɛ fan yɛgɛ alibi kɩwaa.

eng: Look! Im standing at the door and knocking. If any hear my voice and open the door, I will come in to be with them, and will have dinner with them, and they will have dinner with me.
local: Fan nu mɛ asɛ! N yɩlɛ kebunɔ n ŋɛ ɔpʋnʋ mʋ. Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o nu mɛ ɔbʋlɛ, nɩɩ o tigi ɔpʋnʋ mʋ, n biti n loo to ba, kɛ maa mʋ a bʋga gyii.

eng: As for those who emerge victorious, I will allow them to sit with me on my throne, just as I emerged victorious and sat down with my Father on his throne.
local: Ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔ lɩɩ mʋ, n biti n sa mʋ ɔkpa ɔ ba kyɩna kuwura‐gya sʋ mata mɛ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ngbaa mɛ n kɔ lɩɩ nɩɩ n kyɩna mata Mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ kuwura‐gya mʋ sʋ.

eng: If you can hear, listen to what the Spirit is saying to the churches.
local: Ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ asʋ mʋ, o nu kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu mʋ ɩɩ tɔwɛ Krisito kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ mʋ.

eng: After this I looked and there was a door that had been opened in heaven. The first voice that I had heard, which sounded like a trumpet, said to me, Come up here, and I will show you what must take place after this.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ kamaa mʋ, n kɩɩ nɩɩ n wu sʋsʋ Ɩbwaarɛ ayɛ ɔpʋnʋ maa tigi kɩ dɛ. Nɩɩ n nu ɔbʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ daa tɔwɛ mɛ asɛ fɛɛ kabɩlɛ mʋ bɩla tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, Ba sʋsʋ nfɩɩ, kɛ n kaapʋ fʋ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ ɩ maa waa ɩnɩmʋ kamaa.

eng: At once I was in a Spirit-inspired trance and I saw a throne in heaven, and someone was seated on the throne.
local: Opula tɔ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu mʋ taa mɛ. Nɩɩ n ba kɩɩ mʋ, n wu kuwura‐gya mʋ kɩ maa bʋ sʋsʋ Ɩbwaarɛ ayɛ yɛgɛ ɔkʋ tɛ kɩmʋ sʋ.

eng: The one seated there looked like jasper and carnelian, and surrounding the throne was a rainbow that looked like an emerald.
local: Nɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛ kɩmʋ sʋ mʋ, ɩɩ ŋalɩgɛ pɩlɛpɩlɛ fɛɛ abuu ŋalɩgɛsa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ sʋ ɔgyalɩn, ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ gyasipa nɩɩ ii fuuli yɛ kaneliyan nɩɩ ɩɩ payɩ mʋ. Nɩɩ okyimale mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ ŋalɩgɛ fɛɛ ɔgyalɩn kubuu mʋ nɩɩ ki du fɛɛ kɩfatɛɛ bunboli, nɩɩ kɩ kyaabɔɔ kuwura‐gya mʋ.

eng: Twenty-four thrones, with twenty-four elders seated upon them, surrounded the throne. The elders were dressed in white clothing and had gold crowns on their heads.
local: Nwuragya adʋnyɔ yɛ ana ɩkʋ ɩ yɩlɛ kyaabɔɔ kuwura‐gya mʋ yɛgɛ abɩlɩsa adʋnyɔ yɛ ana ba tɛ ɩmʋ sʋ. Ba bun atɔ fufuli nɩɩ ba bun suwa awura‐wʋrʋ.

eng: From the throne came lightning, voices, and thunder. In front of the throne were seven flaming torches, which are the seven spirits of God.
local: Nɩɩ bʋlayɩ ɩɩ wɛɛ ɩsɛɛ yɛgɛ ɛɛ pʋntɛ lɩɩ kuwura‐gya mʋ asɛ. Ɩfɩtɩla isunɔ ɩ bʋ kuwura‐gya mʋ asɛ ɩ dɩyɛ. Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ N‐yaayu nsunɔ mʋ.

eng: Something like a glass sea, like crystal, was in front of the throne. In the center, by the throne, were four living creatures encircling the throne. These creatures were covered with eyes on the front and on the back.
local: Kuwura‐gya mu ansi tɔ mʋ, n bɩla wu kʋtɔ kʋ fɛɛ ɔpʋ, nɩɩ ki du fɛɛ kɩlasɛ, nɩɩ kɩ bɩla ki du kɩlɩn‐kɩlɩn. Nsana tɔ mʋ atɔ kɩsa ana ba kyaabɔɔ kuwura‐gya mʋ, yɛgɛ ba sʋ ansi bamʋ ansi tɔ yɛ bamʋ kamaa yɛ katɩn kʋmaa.

eng: The first living creature was like a lion. The second living creature was like an ox. The third living creature had a face like a human being. And the fourth living creature was like an eagle in flight.
local: Kʋtɔ kɩɩsa gyankpaasa mʋ ki du nɩn fɛɛ barɩnɛ, nɩɩ ɔnyɔsapʋ du fɛɛ naatɛ gbelu, nɩɩ ɔsasapʋ mu ansi tɔ i du fɛɛ nyɩmɩsa, nɩɩ ɔnasapʋ du fɛɛ kpaakpaatɛ mʋ nɩɩ ee fugi.

eng: Each of the four living creatures had six wings, and each was covered all around and on the inside with eyes. They never rest day or night, but keep on saying, Holy, holy, holy is the Lord God Almighty, who was and is and is coming.
local: Atɔbwaaya kɩɩsa ana maŋa ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ sʋ abanbantɛ asiye, nɩɩ ba sʋ ansi bamʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋ katɩn kʋmaa nɩmʋ kyaabɔɔ halɩɩ bamʋ abanbantɛ tɔ. Ba mɛɛ yɛgɛ kɩtɔwɛ kakyʋwapɛ yɛ kagyanbwɛ yɛ, Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ, kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ, kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ, ɩ gyɛ anɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Owura lala, mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ wʋla ɔ bʋ tɔ pɔyɩ nɩɩ atɔ i piili, nɩɩ ee biti ɔ baa ɔ bʋ tɔ kakpaa mʋ.

eng: Whenever the living creatures give glory, honor, and thanks to the one seated on the throne, who lives forever and always.
local: Saŋa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ atɔbwaaya kɩɩsa mʋ bɛɛ bɩɩ ɩlʋn bɛɛ taa bʋnyaa yɛ bɛɛrɛɛ yɛ afwaala bɛɛ sa ɔmʋnɩ ɔ tɛ kuwura‐gya mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɛ nkpa tɔ kakpaa mʋ.

eng: the twenty-four elders fall before the one seated on the throne. They worship the one who lives forever and always. They throw down their crowns before the throne and say.
local: Nɩɩ abɩlɩsa adʋnyɔ yɛ ana mʋ bee muŋa ɔmʋnɩ ɔ tɛ kuwura‐gya mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɛ nkpa tɔ kakpaa mu ansi tɔ, bee sun mʋ. Nɩɩ bee bugi bamʋ awura‐wʋrʋ bɛɛ yɩla kuwura‐gya mʋ asɛ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ.

eng: You are worthy, our Lord and God, to receive glory and honor and power, because you created all things. It is by your will that they existed and were created.
local: Anɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ yɛ anɛ Ɩbwaarɛ, fʋ dagaa fɛɛ ba sa fʋ bʋnyaa yɛ bɛɛrɛɛ, nɩɩ fʋ ɔlʋn dagaa fʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ fʋ ɩ tʋʋ kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ, nɩɩ fʋ kebiti tɔ nɩɩ fʋ tʋʋ ɩnɩmʋ kpini, nɩɩ bamʋ kpini ba bʋ tɔ nɩɩ ba sʋ nkpa falɛ.

eng: Then I saw a scroll in the right hand of the one seated on the throne. It had writing on the front and the back, and it was sealed with seven seals.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ n wu ɔwʋlɛ bɔɔsa kʋ maa bʋ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛ kuwura‐gya mʋ sʋ kɩbaa gyisa tɔ. Nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ kɩmʋ ansi tɔ yɛ kamaa kpini, nɩɩ ba taa atɔ tarɩsa asunɔ tarɩ ɔwʋlɛ bɔɔsa mʋ kɔnɔ sʋ.

eng: I saw a powerful angel, who proclaimed in a loud voice, Who is worthy to open the scroll and break its seals.
local: Nɩɩ n wu Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ ɔlʋnpʋ maa taa ɔbʋlɛ lala ee kuusi ɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Anɩmʋ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ ɔ kyatɛ kʋtɔ tarɩsa mʋ, kɛ o bugi ɔwʋlɛ bɔɔsa mʋ tɔ.

eng: But no one in heaven or on earth or under the earth could open the scroll or look inside it.
local: Amaa ɔkʋ man bʋ sʋsʋ abɛɛ kasɛ sʋ abɛɛ kasɛ tɔ, nɩɩ ɛɛ taalɛ bugi ɔwʋlɛ bɔɔsa mʋ tɔ abɛɛ ɔ taalɛ kɩɩ kɩmʋ tɔ.

eng: So I began to weep and weep, because no one was found worthy to open the scroll or to look inside it.
local: Nɩɩ n su gaa, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋ man bʋ tɔ nɩɩ ɔ dagaa fɛɛ o bugi ɔwʋlɛ bɔɔsa mʋ tɔ abɛɛ ɔ kɩɩ kɩmʋ tɔ.

eng: Then one of the elders said to me, Dont weep. Look! The Lion of the tribe of Judah, the Root of David, has emerged victorious so that he can open the scroll and its seven seals.
local: Nɩɩ abɩlɩsa mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, Man bɩla su! Kɩɩ, Gyuda kebunɔ nyɩmɩsa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Bɩranɛ, nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ Dawidi kanaana kparɛ tɔ mʋ, ɔ kɔ gyii sʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ ɛɛ taalɛ kyatɛ atɔ tarɩsa asunɔ mʋ, kɛ o bugi ɔwʋlɛ bɔɔsa mʋ.

eng: Then, in between the throne and the four living creatures and among the elders, I saw a Lamb, standing as if it had been slain. It had seven horns and seven eyes, which are Gods seven spirits, sent out into the whole earth.
local: Nɩɩ n maa kɩɩ mʋ, n wu Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ nɩɩ mʋ ɔbʋlɛ tɔ sʋ kɩlɔpanɛ fɛɛ ba maa taa mʋ waa saraga, maa yɩlɛ kuwura‐gya mʋ yɛ atɔ kɩɩsa ana mʋ yɛ abɩlɩsa mʋ nsana. Ɔ sʋ agyakpaa asunɔ yɛ ansi asunɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ N‐yaayu nsunɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sun bamʋ kpee dulinyaa tɔ katɩn kʋmaa mʋ.

eng: He came forward and took the scroll from the right hand of the one seated on the throne.
local: Nɩɩ Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ ba kɔɔlɛ ɔwʋlɛ bɔɔsa mʋ lɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛ kuwura‐gya mʋ sʋ mʋ gyisa kɩbaa tɔ.

eng: When he took the scroll, the four living creatures and the twenty-four elders fell down before the Lamb. Each held a harp and gold bowls full of incense, which are the prayers of the saints.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ ɔwʋlɛ bɔɔsa mʋ, atɔ kɩɩsa ana mʋ yɛ abɩlɩsa adʋnyɔ yɛ ana mʋ ba muŋa Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ. Nɩɩ bamʋ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ sʋ adʋnlɛ yɛ suwa kalapɛ‐bi mʋ nɩɩ nlaa liifensa ɩ bɔla ɩmʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɩ yɩlɛ sa Ɩbwaarɛ asa mʋ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ.

eng: They took up a new song, saying, You are worthy to take the scroll and open its seals, because you were slain, and by your blood you purchased for God persons from every tribe, language, people, and nation.
local: Nɩɩ atɔ kɩɩsa ana mʋ yɛ abɩlɩsa mʋ ba bɩɩ ɔlʋn pʋpwɛ sa Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ yɛ, Fʋ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ fʋ taa ɔwʋlɛ bɔɔsa mʋ, kɛ fʋ kyatɛ ɩmʋ ɩtarɛsa mʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ba mɔɔ fʋ, nɩɩ fʋ taa fʋ nkalan mʋ sɔɔ asa sa Ɩbwaarɛ. Fʋ sɔɔ bamʋ lɩɩ nbunɔ kʋmaa tɔ yɛ ntɔwɛ kʋmaa tɔ, yɛ asa kʋmaa tɔ yɛ ndɛndɛmandɛ kʋmaa tɔ.

eng: You made them a kingdom and priests to our God, and they will rule on earth.
local: Nɩɩ fʋ taa bamʋ ba loo fu kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ, waa bamʋ aseepu kɛ be sun anɛ Ɩbwaarɛ, nɩɩ bee biti ba gyii kuwura kasɛ sʋ.

eng: Then I looked, and I heard the sound of many angels surrounding the throne, the living creatures, and the elders. They numbered in the millions—thousands upon thousands.
local: Nɩɩ n bɩla kɩɩ, nɩɩ n nu Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ ngbɩn‐ngbɩn kudu ɩbʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ nyɩmɩsa mɛɛ taalɛ bɩɩ bamʋ kɔnɔ ba maa kyaabɔɔ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gya mʋ yɛ atɔ kɩɩsa ana mʋ yɛ abɩlɩsa mʋ.

eng: They said in a loud voice, Worthy is the slaughtered Lamb to receive power, wealth, wisdom, and might, and honor, glory, and blessing.
local: Nɩɩ ba taa ɔbʋlɛ lala bɩɩ ɔlʋn yɛ, Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba mɔɔ mʋ, mʋ nɩɩ ɔlʋn yɛ atɔ kparɛ yɛ kanyaasɩn yɛ kuwura tɔ ɔlʋn yɛ bɛɛrɛɛ yɛ bʋnyaa yɛ ayɩn‐bi ɩ dagaa mʋ.

eng: And I heard every creature in heaven and on earth and under the earth and in the sea—I heard everything everywhere say, Blessing, honor, glory, and power belong to the one seated on the throne and to the Lamb forever and always.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ n nu atɔ tʋʋsa kpini mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ sʋsʋ yɛ kasɛ sʋ yɛ kasɛ tɔ yɛ ɔpʋ tɔ, yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ ɩmʋ kpini tɔ bɛɛ bɩɩ yɛ, Ayɩn‐bi yɛ bɛɛrɛɛ yɛ bʋnyaa yɛ ɔlʋn, ɩ gyɛ sa ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛ kuwura‐gya mʋ sʋ, yɛ Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ kakpaa.

eng: Then the four living creatures said, Amen, and the elders fell down and worshipped.
local: Nɩɩ atɔ kɩɩsa ana mʋ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, Amen. Nɩɩ abɩlɩsa mʋ ba muŋa sun Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ.

eng: After this I saw four angels standing at the four corners of the earth. They held back the earths four winds so that no wind would blow against the earth, the sea, or any tree.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ kamaa mʋ, n wu Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ ana, ba maa yɩlɛ kasɛ sʋ abaafʋn ntun nna ba kɩtaa abaafʋn ana afuu, kɛ ɩ man nyɛ funti kasɛ sʋ abɛɛ ɔpʋ sʋ abɛɛ iyu.

eng: I saw another angel coming up from the east, holding the seal of the living God. He cried out with a loud voice to the four angels who had been given the power to damage the earth and sea.
local: Nɩɩ n bɩla wu kabɔɔ kʋ maa lɩɩ kyɔwɛ ɔlɩɩkpa ɛɛ ba sʋsʋ, yɛgɛ ɔ sʋ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ tɔ kakpaa mʋ kʋtɔ duligisa. Nɩɩ ɔ taa ɔbʋlɛ lala kuusi tɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ ana mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sa bamʋ ɔlʋn yɛ ba nyita kasɛ yɛ ɔpʋ mʋ.

eng: He said, Dont damage the earth, the sea, or the trees until we have put a seal on the foreheads of those who serve our God.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan man nyita kasɛ abɛɛ ɔpʋ abɛɛ iyu, amɔɔ an duligi anɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asunpu bamʋ asɩkpɩɩ sʋ pɔyɩ.

eng: Then I heard the number of those who were sealed: one hundred forty-four thousand, sealed from every tribe of the Israelites.
local: Nɩɩ n nu asa mʋ nɩɩ ba duligi bamʋ mʋ kɔnɔ. Ba bʋ ngbɩn kɩlɩfa yɛ adɩna yɛ ana lɩɩ Ɩsɩrayɩ nbunɔ kudu anyɔ mʋ kpini tɔ.

eng: From the tribe of Judah, twelve thousand were sealed; from the tribe of Reuben, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Gad, twelve thousand.
local: Gyuda kebunɔ tɔ asa ba bʋ ngbɩn kudu anyɔ. Nɩɩ Ruben kebunɔ tɔ asa ba bʋ ngbɩn kudu anyɔ. Nɩɩ Gadi kebunɔ tɔ asa ba bʋ ngbɩn kudu anyɔ.

eng: from the tribe of Asher, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Naphtali, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Manasseh, twelve thousand.
local: Nɩɩ Asa kebunɔ tɔ asa ba bʋ ngbɩn kudu anyɔ. Nafitali kebunɔ tɔ asa ba bʋ ngbɩn kudu anyɔ. Manase kebunɔ tɔ asa ba bʋ ngbɩn kudu anyɔ.

eng: from the tribe of Simeon, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Levi, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Issachar, twelve thousand.
local: Simeyon kebunɔ tɔ asa ba bʋ ngbɩn kudu anyɔ. Lewi kebunɔ tɔ asa ba bʋ ngbɩn kudu anyɔ. Isaki kebunɔ tɔ asa ba bʋ ngbɩn kudu anyɔ.

eng: from the tribe of Zebulun, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Joseph, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Benjamin, twelve thousand were sealed.
local: Sebulun kebunɔ tɔ ba bʋ asa ngbɩn kudu anyɔ. Gyosefu kebunɔ tɔ ba bʋ asa ngbɩn kudu anyɔ. Bengyamin kebunɔ tɔ ba bʋ asa ngbɩn kudu anyɔ.

eng: After this I looked, and there was a great crowd that no one could number. They were from every nation, tribe, people, and language. They were standing before the throne and before the Lamb. They wore white robes and held palm branches in their hands.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ kamaa mʋ n wu sakyɔ damantɛ kʋ, nɩɩ ɔkʋ mɛɛ taalɛ karagɛ bamʋ. Ba lɩɩ ndɛndɛmandɛ yɛ nbunɔ kʋkwɩɩ‐kʋkwɩɩ yɛ asa kʋkwɩɩ‐kʋkwɩɩ yɛ ntɔwɛ kʋkwɩɩ‐kʋkwɩɩ ba yɩlɛ kuwura‐gya mʋ yɛ Kabʋlʋpʋ mu ansi tɔ. Ba bun ɩbalɩmasʋ fufuli yɛgɛ ba sʋ abalaafa bamʋ abaa tɔ.

eng: They cried out with a loud voice: Victory belongs to our God who sits on the throne, and to the Lamb.
local: Nɩɩ ba taa ɔbʋlɛ lala bee kuusi bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Kamɔlɩgɛ gyɛ anɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛ kuwura‐gya sʋ mʋ yɛ Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ lɛɛ.

eng: All the angels stood in a circle around the throne, and around the elders and the four living creatures. They fell facedown before the throne and worshipped God.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ mʋ kpini ba yɩlɛ kyaabɔɔ kuwura‐gya mʋ yɛ abɩlɩsa mʋ yɛ atɔ‐kɩɩsa ana mʋ. Nɩɩ ba muŋa taa bamʋ ansi bun kasɛ kuwura‐gya mu ansi tɔ, nɩɩ ba sun Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: saying, Amen! Blessing and glory and wisdom and thanksgiving and honor and power and might be to our God forever and always. Amen.
local: bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Amen! Ayɩn‐bi yɛ bʋnyaa yɛ kanyaasɩn yɛ afwaala yɛ bɛɛrɛɛ yɛ kɩtɩɩ kɩnyɛ yɛ ɔlʋn ɩ gyɛ anɛ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ kakpaa‐kakpaa! Amen.

eng: Then one of the elders said to me, Who are these people wearing white robes, and where did they come from.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ abɩlɩsa mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ taasɛ mɛ yɛ, Nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba bun ɩbalɩmasʋ fufuli nɩmʋ mʋ, anɩmʋ ana nɩn? Nɩɩ fɩnɛ nɩɩ ba lɩɩ.

eng: I said to him, Sir, you know. Then he said to me, These people have come out of great hardship. They have washed their robes and made them white in the Lambs blood.
local: Nɩɩ n lɛɛ mʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, Ɔbɩlɩsa, fu i nyi. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, Bamʋ ɩ gyɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba lɩɩ awʋrʋfɔ lala mʋ kigyii tɔ, nɩɩ ba fwɩɩ bamʋ ɩkaalɛ Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ nkalan tɔ, nɩɩ i fuuli mʋ.

eng: This is the reason they are before Gods throne. They worship him day and night in his temple, and the one seated on the throne will shelter them.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ ba yɩlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gya mu ansi tɔ, nɩɩ bee sun mʋ kakyʋwapɛ yɛ kagyanbwɛ mʋ osunkpa obu lala mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛ kuwura‐gya mʋ sʋ mʋ, ee biti ɔ baa ɔ bʋ bamʋ asɛ kɛ o kun bamʋ.

eng: They wont hunger or thirst anymore. No sun or scorching heat will beat down on them.
local: Nɩɩ akʋn man bɩla yɛ ɔ kɩtaa bamʋ, nɩɩ bʋlɩfɔ man bɩla yɛ ɔ kɩtaa bamʋ. Nɩɩ kyɔwɛ man bɩla yɛ ɔ da bamʋ, nɩɩ awulawula man bɩla yɛ ii wiili bamʋ.

eng: because the Lamb who is in the midst of the throne will shepherd them. He will lead them to the springs of life-giving water, and God will wipe away every tear from their eyes.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Kabʋlʋpʋ‐bi mʋ nɩɩ ɔ yɩlɛ kuwura‐gya mʋ nsana mʋ ii biti ɔ baa ɔ gyɛ bamʋ ɔkpapʋ. Kɛ o kpiya bamʋ ɔbʋn kumu mʋ nɩɩ kɩ sʋ nkpa nkyu mʋ asɛ. Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ kpayɛ ansikyu kʋmaa lɩɩ bamʋ ansi tɔ.

eng: Then I looked on as the Lamb opened one of the seven seals. I heard one of the four living creatures say in a voice like thunder, Come.
local: N wu Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ maa kyatɛ atɔ tarɩsa asunɔ mʋ tɔ kɩgyankpaasa mʋ. Nɩɩ n nu atɔ kɩɩsa ana mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ ɔbʋlɛ maa pʋntɛ fɛɛ bʋlayɩ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, Ba.

eng: So I looked, and there was a white horse. Its rider held a bow and was given a crown. And he went forth from victory to victory.
local: N maa kɩɩ mʋ n wu gbaŋɛ fufuli! Nɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ee dii mʋ mʋ sʋ kɩtaa, nɩɩ ba sa mʋ kuwura‐wʋrʋ, nɩɩ o dii fɛɛ ɔkɔ‐gyisapu mʋ nɩɩ ee kpee ɔ naa kɔ gyii sʋ.

eng: When the Lamb opened the second seal, I heard the second living creature say, Come.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ kyatɛ kʋtɔ tarɩsa nyɔsapʋ mʋ, n nu kʋtɔ kɩɩsa nyɔsapʋ mʋ maa tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, Ba.

eng: Out came another horse, fiery red. Its rider was allowed to take peace from the earth so that people would kill each other. He was given a large sword.
local: Nɩɩ gbaŋɛ kʋ bɩla lɩɩ ba, ɔ gyɛ ɔpɩpɛɛ nɩn. Nɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ee dii mʋ mʋ, ba sa mʋ ɔlʋn yɛ ɔ lɛɛ kayɩɩ yuuli lɩɩ kasɛ sʋ, kɛ asa ba mɔɔ abaa. Nɩɩ ba sa mʋ sakunde lala kʋ.

eng: When he opened the third seal, I heard the third living creature say, Come! So I looked, and there was a black horse. Its rider held a balance for weighing in his hand.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ kyatɛ kʋtɔ tarɩsa sasapʋ mʋ, n nu kʋtɔ kɩɩsa sasapʋ mʋ maa tɔwɛ yɛ, Ba! N maa kɩɩ tʋtɔ mʋ n wu gbaŋɛ lenbiri maa yɩlɛ, nɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ee dii mʋ mʋ sʋ kɩbɩlɩtɔ kaalasa mʋ kɩbaa tɔ.

eng: I heard what sounded like a voice from among the four living creatures. It said, A quart of wheat for a denarion, and three quarts of barley for a denarion, but dont damage the olive oil and the wine.
local: Nɩɩ n nu ɔbʋlɛ kʋ maa tɔwɛ lɩɩ atɔ kɩɩsa ana mʋ asɛ yɛ, Ayu mudu ii biti ɩ waa ɔgyalɩn, kɛ ɩmʋ kɩgya ii biti ɩ waa kakɛ kʋlʋn kusun kʋkɔka nɩɩ akyembee mɔɔ imuduu ɩsa kɩgya ii biti ɩ waa kakɛ kʋlʋn kusun kʋkɔka, amaa fan man kan nyita nfɔ yɛ nta.

eng: When he opened the fourth seal, I heard the voice of the fourth living creature say, Come.
local: Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ maa kyatɛ kʋtɔ tarɩsa nasapʋ mʋ, n nu kʋtɔ kɩɩsa nasapʋ mʋ ɔbʋlɛ maa tɔwɛ yɛ, Ba.

eng: So I looked, and there was a pale green horse. Its riders name was Death, and the Grave was following right behind. They were given authority over a fourth of the earth, to kill by sword, famine, disease, and the wild animals of the earth.
local: N maa kɩɩ tʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ n wu gbaŋɛ mʋ nɩɩ mʋ ifuu ɩ du fɛɛ ndaara mʋ, maa yɩlɛ, nɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ee dii mʋ mʋ, bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Lewu, yɛgɛ kamandɛ mɔɔ buu mʋ kamaa. Ba sa bamʋ ɔlʋn yɛ ba mɔɔ kasɛ sʋ asa nbarɩgɛ tɔ ntun nna mʋ tɔ kʋkʋlʋn. Ɩkɔ yɛ akʋn yɛ alɔ yɛ kasɛ sʋ ndʋnbwɛ nɩɩ bee biti ba taa mɔɔ bamʋ.

eng: When he opened the fifth seal, I saw under the altar those who had been slaughtered on account of the word of God and the witness they had given.
local: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ kyatɛ kʋtɔ tarɩsa nusapʋ mʋ, asa mʋ nɩɩ ba mɔɔ bamʋ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn mʋ katɔwɛ sʋ, yɛ kɩtɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ lɩɩ Yesu Krisito sʋ mʋ, n wu bamʋ kala ana ba maa bʋ Ɩbwaarɛ saraga kaswɩɩ mʋ ɔpɩɩ tɔ.

eng: They cried out with a loud voice, Holy and true Master, how long will you wait before you pass judgment? How long before you require justice for our blood, which was shed by those who live on earth.
local: Nɩɩ ba kuusi kenken tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Owura lala, Ɔkyɩrɛkyɩrɛpʋ yɛ kesintin wuya, saŋa mɔɔ nɩɩ fii biti fu gyoo naa fʋʋ, pɔyɩ kɛ fʋ talɛ, kɛ fʋ bɩɩtɛ kasɛ sʋ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba mɔɔ anɛ mʋ asʋ.

eng: Each of them was given a white robe, and they were told to rest a little longer, until their fellow servants and brothers and sisters—who were about to be killed as they were—were finished.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sa akaala mʋ ɩbalɩmasʋ fufuli, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan nyɛ kenyiita saŋa gbɛrɛɛ tii sʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ, fanɛ bɩrɩsa ana Krisito kusun awaapʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ asa ba mɔɔ bamʋ fɛɛ fanɛ mʋ, kɔnɔ ɔ fʋʋ pɔyɩ.

eng: I looked on as he opened the sixth seal, and there was a great earthquake. The sun became black as funeral clothing, and the entire moon turned red as blood.
local: N maa kɩɩ mʋ Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ kyatɛ kʋtɔ tarɩsa siyesapʋ mʋ. Nɩɩ kasɛ ki gyingyen ɔlʋn sʋ, nɩɩ kyɔwɛ biliŋi kitentenbiri tumm fɛɛ kɩtaa lenbiri. Nɩɩ ɔbwaayɩ payɩ kɔɔ fɛɛ nkalan.

eng: The stars of the sky fell to the earth as a fig tree drops its fruit when shaken by a strong wind.
local: Nɩɩ akyikpebi a lɩɩ sʋsʋ tɩyɛ kasɛ fɛɛ afuu bʋlʋnsa ɩ da nɩɩ ɩ yɩyaa figi abi ɩ lɩɩ tɩyɛ mʋ.

eng: The sky disappeared like a scroll being rolled up, and every mountain and island was moved from its place.
local: Nɩɩ sʋsʋ nyaa fɛɛ kalan nɩɩ ba bɔɔ mʋ. Nɩɩ abɩɩ yɛ afintin kpini ɩ bɩɩtɛ lɩɩ ɩmʋ ipula.

eng: Then the kings of the earth, the officials and the generals, the rich and the powerful, and everyone, slave and free, hid themselves in caves and in the rocks of the mountains.
local: Nɩɩ kasɛ sʋ awura yɛ asa kparɛ, yɛ anaakɔpʋ abɩlɩsa yɛ afulee wuya ana, yɛ alʋnpʋ yɛ anyɛ kpini, yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba tɛ bamʋ abaa sʋ kpini, ba sɩlɛ naa baala afʋlɛ ɩbɔ tɔ, yɛ afʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ abɩɩ sʋ mʋ tɔ.

eng: They called to the mountains and the rocks, Fall on us and hide us from the face of the one seated on the throne and from the Lambs wrath!.
local: Nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ abɩɩ yɛ afʋlɛ mʋ yɛ, Fan tɩyɛ anɛ sʋ baala anɛ lɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛ kuwura‐gya mʋ sʋ ansi sʋ yɛ Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ agbʋ lala sʋ.

eng: The great day of their wrath has come, and who is able to stand.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ agbʋ kakɛ lala mʋ kɩ fʋʋ, nɩɩ anɩmʋ ɩ taalɛ yɩlɛ?.

eng: Then the fifth angel blew his trumpet, and I saw a star that had fallen from heaven to earth, and he was given the key to the shaft of the abyss.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ nusapʋ mʋ fʋlɛ mʋ kabɩlɛ, nɩɩ n wu kikyikpebi mʋ nɩɩ kɩ lɩɩ sʋsʋ ba tɩyɛ kasɛ sʋ mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔkʋ taa ɔbɔ bunkyinsa lala mʋ nɩɩ kɩ man sʋ ɔkaa mʋ kebunɔ saafɩɩ sa mʋ.

eng: He opened the shaft of the abyss; and smoke rose up from the shaft, like smoke from a huge furnace. The sun and air were darkened by the smoke from the shaft.
local: Maa tigi ɔbɔ bunkyinsa lala mʋ, idiisii ɩ lɩɩ kɩmʋ tɔ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɩ lɩɩ bodobodo obu lala tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ idiisii ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ kyɔwɛ yɛ sʋsʋ waa kitentenbiri.

eng: Then locusts came forth from the smoke and onto the earth. They were given power like the power that scorpions have on the earth.
local: Nɩɩ ntuntunbi ba lɩɩ idiisii mʋ tɔ ba kasɛ sʋ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sa bamʋ ɔlʋn mʋ nɩɩ ba taa dun fɛɛ kasɛ sʋ kyankpatɩ lɛɛ mʋ.

eng: They were told not to hurt the grass of the earth or any green plant or any tree. They could only hurt the people who didnt have the seal of God on their foreheads.
local: Ba tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ ba man kan mata kasɛ sʋ afitiri abɛɛ atɔ duusa abɛɛ iyu, amaa ba nyanla bamʋ nɩɩ ba man sʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kakyʋlɛ bamʋ asɩkpɩɩ sʋ mʋ.

eng: The locusts werent allowed to kill them, but only to make them suffer for five months—and the suffering they inflict is like that of a scorpion when it strikes a person.
local: Ba man sa ntuntunbi mʋ ɔkpa yɛ ba mɔɔ asa, amaa ba sa ba mʋ ɔkpa yɛ ba nyanla bamʋ ɩbwaayɩ inuu. Nɩɩ ntuntunbi mʋ kudun ɩɩ kɔ fɛɛ kyankpatɩ maa dun nyɩmɩsa mʋ.

eng: In those days people will seek death, but they wont find it. They will want to die, but death will run away from them.
local: Kanɩn ɩbwaayɩ maŋa tɔ mʋ asa maŋa bee biti ba biti lewu, amaa ba mɛɛ yɛ ba nyɛ lewu mʋ. Bee biti fɛɛ ba wu gbaa, amaa ba mɛɛ yɛ ba wu.

eng: The locusts looked like horses ready for battle. On their heads were what seemed to be gold crowns. Their faces were like human faces.
local: Ntuntunbi mʋ ba du nɩn fɛɛ ɩgbaŋɛ mʋ nɩɩ ba kʋʋla bamʋ bee kpee ɩkɔ. Ba bun atɔ kʋ fɛɛ suwa awura‐wʋrʋ bamʋ amu tɔ nɩɩ bamʋ ansi tɔ i du fɛɛ asa ansi.

eng: their hair was like womens hair, and their teeth were like lions teeth.
local: Bamʋ inyini i du nɩn fɛɛ akyɩɩ inyini, nɩɩ bamʋ anyi i du fɛɛ barɩnɛ anyi mʋ.

eng: In front they had what seemed to be iron armor upon their chests, and the sound of their wings was like the sound of many chariots and horses racing into battle.
local: Ba sʋ atɔ kʋ fɛɛ abɩlɩtɔ panpasan kun bamʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ, nɩɩ ba maa fugi mʋ bamʋ abanbantɛ tɔ mʋ ɩlawʋ i du fɛɛ abɩlɩtɔ itoroko damantɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩgbaŋɛ bɛɛ bɩɩtɛ ba sɩlɛ bee kpee ɩkɔ tɔ.

eng: They also have tails with stingers, just like scorpions; and in their tails is their power to hurt people for five months.
local: Ba sʋ ɩpɩɩ nɩɩ bee duŋi fɛɛ ɩkyankpatɛ, nɩɩ bamʋ ɩpɩɩ mʋ tɔ nɩɩ ba sʋ ɔlʋn bɛɛ nyanla asa ɩbwaayɩ inuu mʋ.

eng: Their king is an angel from the abyss, whose Hebrew name is Abaddon, and whose Greek name is Apollyon.
local: Ba sʋ owura mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kɩɩ bamʋ sʋ, nɩɩ mʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔbɔ bunkyinsa lala mʋ nɩɩ kɩ man sʋ ɔkaa mʋ kabɔɔ. Nɩɩ Hibriwu katɔwɛ tɔ mʋ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Abadon, nɩɩ Giriki katɔwɛ tɔ mʋ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Apoliyon. Mʋ kɩtɩɩ mʋ kasɛ ɩ gyɛ onyitapu.

eng: The first horror has passed. Look! Two horrors are still coming after this.
local: Gyerebi gyankpaasa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ sʋ kufuu mʋ kyʋn. Amaa gyerebi ntun nnyɔ sʋ ɩ bʋ ɔkpa ɩɩ ba.

eng: Then the sixth angel blew his trumpet, and I heard a voice from the four horns of the gold altar that is before God.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ siyesapʋ fʋlɛ mʋ kabɩlɛ, nɩɩ n nu ɔbʋlɛ kʋ maa lɩɩ suwa kaswɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ yɩlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ansi tɔ nbɔntɔnbʋlɔn ana tɔ.

eng: It said to the sixth angel, who had the trumpet, Release the four angels who are bound at the great river Euphrates.
local: Nɩɩ ɔbʋlɛ mʋ tɔwɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ siyesapʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ kabɩlɛ mʋ yɛ, Saŋɛ nbɔɔ nna mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ŋmina bamʋ yɩla ɔbʋn lala Gyufereti asɛ mʋ.

eng: Then the four angels who had been made ready for that hour, day, month, and year were released to kill a third of humankind.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ saŋɛ nbɔɔ nna maŋa, mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ lɔŋɔ bamʋ yɩla sa saŋa nɩmʋ yɛ kakɛ nɩmʋ yɛ ɔbwaayɩ nɩmʋ yɛ kesu nɩmʋ, yɛ ba mɔɔ kasɛ sʋ asa nbarɩgɛ tɔ ntun nsa mʋ tɔ ketun kʋlʋn.

eng: The number of cavalry troops was two hundred million. I heard their number.
local: Nɩɩ n nu anaakɔpʋ mʋ kɔnɔ. Ba gyɛ ngbɩn kudu‐kudu kanɩn kudu kanɩn anyɔ .

eng: And this is the way I saw the horses and their riders in the vision: they had breastplates that were fiery red, dark blue, and yellow as sulfur. The horses heads were like lions heads, and out of their mouths came fire, smoke, and sulfur.
local: Mɛ ansi sʋ kɩlɛɛkaapʋ mʋ tɔ mʋ, n wu ɩgbaŋɛ yɛ bamʋ adiipu. Nɩɩ adiipu mʋ ba sʋ abɩlɩtɔ panpasan kunsa bamʋ nkɔlɔ sʋ. Nɩɩ ɩɩ payɩ fɛɛ fʋlɔn, nɩɩ i du fɛɛ buluu, nɩɩ kɩ bɩla ki du fɛɛ kɩkyʋn‐nyufo. Ɩgbɛŋɛ mʋ amu i du nɩn fɛɛ abɩranɛ amu, nɩɩ fʋlɔn yɛ idiisii yɛ ferinwuta ɩɩ lɩɩ bamʋ nnɔ tɔ.

eng: By these three plagues a third of humankind was killed: by the fire, smoke, and sulfur coming out of their mouths.
local: Kanɩn gyerebi atɔ asá mʋ nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ fʋlɔn yɛ idiisii yɛ ferinwuta nɩmʋ ɩ mɔɔ asa nbarɩgɛ tɔ ntun nsa mʋ tɔ ketun kʋlʋn.

eng: The horses power is in their mouths and their tails, for their tails are like snakes with heads that inflict injuries.
local: Ɩgbaŋɛ mʋ ɔlʋn bʋ bamʋ nnɔ tɔ yɛ bamʋ ndufa tɔ. Bamʋ ndufa i du nɩn fɛɛ anansɛ, nɩɩ ɩ sʋ amu nɩɩ ba sʋ bee gyii gyerebi.

eng: The rest of humankind, who werent killed by these plagues, didnt change their hearts and lives and turn from their handiwork. They didnt stop worshipping demons and idols made of gold, silver, bronze, stone, and wood—idols that cant see or hear or walk.
local: Asa mʋ nɩɩ ba san kasɛ sʋ, nɩɩ gyerebi atɔ nɩmʋ gbaa ɩ man mɔɔ bamʋ mʋ, ba man yɛgɛ alibi kɩwaa. Nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi abɛɛ ɩdakpa mʋ nɩɩ ba taa suwa abɛɛ gbiti abɛɛ kɩtasan abɛɛ abuu abɛɛ iyu mʋ, ba man yɛgɛ ɩmʋ kusun. Bamʋ ɩdakpa maŋa ɩ man kɩɩ, nɩɩ i mee nu, nɩɩ ɩ mɛɛ natɛ.

eng: They didnt turn away from their murders, their spells and drugs, their sexual immorality, or their stealing.
local: Nɩɩ ba man yɛgɛ asa kɩmɔɔ, yɛ afɛ kʋtʋʋ yɛ akpa‐sɩn, yɛ kɩgyagaa kakyɩɩ yɛ kanyɩn biti yɛ keyu tɔ.

eng: Then, when the Lamb opened the seventh seal, there was silence in heaven for about half an hour.
local: Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ maa kyatɛ kʋtɔ tarɩsa sunɔsapʋ mʋ, nɩɩ sʋsʋ waa surum fɛɛ kerefi kʋlʋn kɩbaafʋn.

eng: Then I saw the seven angels who stand before God, and seven trumpets were given to them.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ n wu Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ asunɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba yɩlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ansi tɔ, nɩɩ ba taa nbɩlɛ nsunɔ ba sa bamʋ.

eng: Another angel came and stood at the altar, and he held a gold bowl for burning incense. He was given a large amount of incense, in order to offer it on behalf of the prayers of all the saints on the gold altar in front of the throne.
local: Nɩɩ n wu Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ kʋ maa sʋ suwa kalapɛ‐bi nɩɩ ɔ ba yɩlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kaswɩɩ asɛ. Nɩɩ ba taa atɔ liifensa kʋkyɔ sa mʋ kɛ ɔ taa bʋga yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asa mʋ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ sʋ, taa ba suwa kaswɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ yɩlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gya mu ansi tɔ mʋ, kɛ o tuwa sa Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: The smoke of the incense offered for the prayers of the saints rose up before God from the angels hand.
local: Kɛ atɔ liifensa mʋ nɩɩ ba tuwa mʋ idiisii, taa bʋga yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asa mʋ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ mʋ, ɩ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ abaa tɔ kpee sʋsʋ Ɩbwaarɛ ansi tɔ.

eng: Then the angel took the incense container and filled it with fire from the altar. He threw it down to the earth, and there were thunder, voices, lightning, and an earthquake.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ taa suwa kalapɛ‐bi mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ nyɔ fʋlɔn lɩɩ suwa kaswɩɩ mʋ sʋ bɔla suwa kalapɛ‐bi mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ tʋʋ ɩmʋ wʋrɩgɛ kasɛ sʋ, nɩɩ bʋlayɩ kpira nɩɩ o kpira, ɔlanba ɩ waa, nɩɩ bʋlayɩ wɛɛ ɩsɛɛ nɩɩ kasɛ gyingyen.

eng: Then the seven angels who held the seven trumpets got ready to blow them.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ asunɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ nbɩlɛ mʋ ba kʋʋla bamʋ n‐yɩɩ ba fʋlɛ nbɩlɛ mʋ.

eng: The first angel blew his trumpet, and hail and fire mixed with blood appeared, and was thrown down to the earth. A third of the earth was burned up. A third of the trees were burned up. All the green grass was burned up.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ gyankpaasa mʋ fʋlɛ mʋ kabɩlɛ. Nɩɩ n wu bʋlayɩ abuu yɛ fʋlɔn ɩ maa kyagɩlɛ yɛ nkalan lɩɩ sʋsʋ ba wʋrɩgɛ kasɛ sʋ. Nɩɩ kasɛ nbarɩgɛ tɔ ntun nsa ɩmʋ tɔ ketun kʋlʋn kɩ dɩyɛ, nɩɩ iyu gbaa nbarɩgɛ tɔ ntun nsa mʋ tɔ ketun kʋlʋn gbaa dɩyɛ, nɩɩ afitiri bunboli mʋ kpini gbaa ɩ dɩyɛ.

eng: Then the second angel blew his trumpet, and something like a huge mountain burning with fire was thrown down into the sea. A third of the sea became blood.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ nyɔsapʋ mʋ fʋlɛ mʋ kabɩlɛ. Nɩɩ ba tʋʋ kʋtɔ kʋ fɛɛ kɩbɩɩ lala mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ dɩyɛ yen‐yen waa ɔpʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔpʋ mʋ tɔ nbarɩgɛ tɔ ntun nsa ɩmʋ tɔ ketun kʋlʋn biliŋi nkalan.

eng: a third of the creatures living in the sea died, and a third of the ships were destroyed.
local: Nɩɩ atɔ tʋʋsa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ ɔpʋ mʋ tɔ nbarɩgɛ tɔ ntun nsa mʋ tɔ ketun kʋlʋn ba wu, nɩɩ ɩkʋlɩ lala mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ ɔpʋ mʋ tɔ nbarɩgɛ tɔ ntun nsa mʋ tɔ ketun kʋlʋn i nyita.

eng: Then the third angel blew his trumpet, and a great star, burning like a torch, fell from heaven. It fell on a third of the rivers and springs of water.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ sasapʋ mʋ fʋlɛ mʋ kabɩlɛ. Nɩɩ kikyikpebi lala mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ dɩyɛ fɛɛ ɔgyaa ba tɩyɛ ɩbʋn amu‐amu yɛ atiribo mʋ tɔ nbarɩgɛ tɔ ntun nsa ketun kʋlʋn tɔ.

eng: The stars name is Wormwood, and a third of the waters became wormwood, and many people died from the water, because it became so bitter.
local: Kikyikpebi mʋ kɩtɩɩ ɩ gyɛ yɛ Ɔlɩlɩ. Nɩɩ nkyu mʋ nbarɩgɛ tɔ ntun nsa mʋ tɔ ketun kʋlʋn ɩ bʋlɩlɩ. Nɩɩ asa gaalaagaa ba nuu nkyu mʋ, nɩɩ ba wu‐wu, lɩɩ fɛɛ nkyu mʋ ɩ bʋlɩlɩ.

eng: Then the fourth angel blew his trumpet, and a third of the sun was struck, and a third of the moon, and a third of the stars so that a third of them became dark. The day lost a third of its light, and the night lost a third of its light too.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ nasapʋ mʋ fʋlɛ mʋ kabɩlɛ. Nɩɩ kanan mʋ nɩɩ kyɔwɛ daa ɔ kɩɩ tɔ mʋ, kakɛ tɔ nbarɩgɛ tɔ ntun nsa tɔ mʋ, ketun kʋlʋn ki biliŋi kitentenbiri. Nɩɩ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔbwaayɩ yɛ akyikpebi ɩ daa ɩ kɩɩ tɔ mʋ, kagyanbwɛ nbarɩgɛ tɔ ntun nsa tɔ mʋ, ketun kʋlʋn biliŋi kitentenbiri.

eng: Then I looked and I heard an eagle flying high overhead. It said with a loud voice, Horror, horror, oh! The horror for those who live on earth because of the blasts of the remaining trumpets that the three angels are about to blow.
local: Nɩɩ n maa kɩɩ mʋ, n nu kpaakpaatɛ maa fugi sʋsʋ afuu tɔ ee su kenken yɛ, Asa mʋ nɩɩ bee biti ba sii kasɛ sʋ, naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ nsa mʋ nɩɩ ba san mʋ, ba ba fʋlɛ bamʋ nbɩlɛ mʋ, bamʋ laakʋ ɩlɩɩ ɩsa.

eng: Then I saw another powerful angel coming down from heaven. He was robed with a cloud, with a rainbow over his head. His face was like the sun, and his feet were like fiery pillars.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ n bɩla wu Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ ɔlʋlʋnpʋ kʋ maa kpɩlɩgɛ lɩɩ sʋsʋ ɛɛ ba kasɛ sʋ. Nɩɩ abwaarɛ‐wʋlɛ a bun mʋ fɛɛ atɔ bunsa, nɩɩ okyimale kyaabɔɔ mu kumu. Nɩɩ mu ansi i du fɛɛ kyɔwɛ, nɩɩ mʋ ayaa i du fɛɛ sabarɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ dɛɛ fʋlɔn.

eng: He held an open scroll in his hand. He put his right foot on the sea and his left foot on the land.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ sʋ kawʋlɛ‐bi mʋ nɩɩ ki bugi kɩ bʋ mʋ kɩbaa tɔ. Ɔ taa mʋ gyisa kɩyaa yii ɔpʋ sʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ bɩna kɩyaa yii kasɛ sʋ.

eng: He called out with a loud voice like a lion roaring, and when he called out, the seven thunders raised their voices.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ pʋntɛ kenken fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ bɩranɛ muusi mʋ. Maa pʋntɛ kanɩn mʋ bʋlayɩ kikpira asunɔ mʋ kyula sʋ.

eng: When the seven thunders spoke, I was about to write, but I heard a voice from heaven say, Seal up what the seven thunders have said, and dont write it down.
local: Bʋlayɩ kikpira asunɔ maa tɔwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ n biti n kyʋrɔɔ. Amaa n nu ɔbʋlɛ kʋ maa tɔwɛ lɩɩ sʋsʋ yɛ, Taa asɩn mʋ nɩɩ fu nu lɩɩ bʋlayɩ kikpira asunɔ mʋ tɔ mʋ waa asiiri tɔ, man kyʋrɔɔ asɩn mʋ yɩla.

eng: Then the angel I saw standing on the sea and on the land raised his right hand to heaven.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ nɩɩ n wu maa yɩlɛ ɔpʋ sʋ yɛ kasɛ sʋ mʋ, kʋsaa mʋ gyisa kɩbaa sʋsʋ.

eng: He swore by the one who lives forever and always, who created heaven and what is in it, the earth and what is in it, and the sea and what is in it, and said, The time is up.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ taa Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ tɔ kakpaa, nɩɩ ɔ tʋʋ sʋsʋ yɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ kɩmʋ tɔ, yɛ kasɛ yɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ kɩmʋ tɔ, yɛ ɔpʋ yɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ kɩmʋ tɔ, bɔ ntan tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ man bɩla yɛ o gyoo.

eng: In the days when the seventh angel blows his trumpet, Gods mysterious purpose will be accomplished, fulfilling the good news he gave to his servants the prophets.
local: Amaa saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ sunɔsapʋ mʋ ii biti ɔ fʋlɛ mʋ kabɩlɛ mʋ, kanɩn saŋa maŋa nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn baalasa mʋ ii biti ɩ ba tɔ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ wʋla tɔwɛ sa mʋ atɔwɛpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba gyɛ mʋ ayaafɔlɛ mʋ.

eng: Then the voice I heard from heaven spoke to me again and said, Go, take the opened scroll from the hand of the angel who stands on the sea and on the land.
local: Nɩɩ ɔbʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ n nu lɩɩ sʋsʋ mʋ bɩla tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, Kpe naa kɔɔlɛ kawʋlɛ‐bi bɔɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ki bugi mʋ, lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ yɩlɛ ɔpʋ sʋ yɛ kasɛ sʋ mʋ kɩbaa gyisa tɔ.

eng: So I went to the angel and told him to give me the scroll. He said to me, Take it and eat it. It will make you sick to your stomach, but sweet as honey in your mouth.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ n kpee Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ asɛ naa tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Taa kawʋlɛ‐bi mʋ nɩɩ fʋ sʋ mʋ sa mɛ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, Kɔɔlɛ, kɛ fʋ wɛ kɩmʋ. Kii biti kɩ baa kɩ bʋkɔn fʋ kɔnɔ tɔ fɛɛ ŋmasan, amaa fʋ ɔtɔ tɔ bɩrɛ kii biti kɩ baa kɩ bʋlɩlɩ kɩtɛ‐kɩtɛ.

eng: So I took the scroll from the angels hand and ate it. And it was sweet as honey in my mouth, but when I swallowed it, it made my stomach churn.
local: Nɩɩ n kɔɔlɛ kawʋlɛ‐bi mʋ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ kɩba tɔ, nɩɩ n wɛ kɩmʋ. Kɩ waa mɛ kɔnɔ tɔ ɔkɔn fɛɛ ŋmasan mʋ, amaa n maa wɛ kɩmʋ lʋwɛ mʋ, mɛ ɔtɔ tɔ waa mɛ ɔlɩlɩ.

eng: I was told, You must prophesy again about many peoples, nations, languages, and kings.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, Ɩ dagaa fʋ maa kyaga sʋ fɩɩ tɔwɛ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ii biti ɩ ba asa gaalaagaa sʋ yɛ ndɛndɛmandɛ sʋ yɛ ntɔwɛ kʋkwɩɩ‐kʋkwɩɩ sʋ yɛ awura sʋ mʋ.

eng: Then I was given a measuring rod, which was like a pole. And I was told, Get up and measure Gods temple, the altar, and those who worship there.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔkʋ taa oyu kaalasa nɩɩ ki du fɛɛ kikpebi mʋ sa mɛ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, Kʋsʋ kɛ fʋ naa kaala mɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ mi osunkpa ayɛ mʋ yɛ mɛ kaswɩɩ mʋ, kɛ fʋ kalɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ kɩmʋ tɔ bee sun mɛ mʋ kpini.

eng: But dont measure the court outside the temple. Leave that out, because it has been given to the nations, and they will trample the holy city underfoot for forty-two months.
local: Amaa yɛgɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ kasɔrɔga opula mʋ yɩla, man kaala tʋtɔ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ mɛ ɩ taa tʋtɔ sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ mɛ asa. Nɩɩ bee biti ba ba kɔ kɔɔlɛ mɛ kadɛ mʋ ɩbwaayɩ adɩna yɛ anyɔ.

eng: And I will allow my two witnesses to prophesy for one thousand two hundred sixty days, wearing mourning clothes.
local: Nɩɩ n biti n sa mɛ kesintin agyipu anyɔ mʋ ɔlʋn, kɛ ba taa nkɛ kagbɩn kʋlʋn yɛ anyɔ yɛ adisiye tɔwɛ mɛ asɩn, yɛgɛ ba bun kensi payɩ atɔ bunsa, mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ asɩn ɩ basa bamʋ.

eng: These are the two olive trees and the two lampstands that stand before the Lord of the earth.
local: Falɛ kesintin agyipu anyɔ anɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ olifu iyu ɩnyɔ yɛ ɩfɩtɩla ɩgyagakpa ɩnyɔ mʋ. Anyɩn anyɔ banɩmʋ ɩ yɩlɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kasɛ wuya ansi tɔ.

eng: If anyone wants to hurt them, fire comes out of their mouth and burns up their enemies. So if anyone wants to hurt them, they have to be killed in this way.
local: Nɩɩ ɔdʋn kʋ kan ii biti ɔ tɔɔrɔɔ Ɩbwaarɛ kesintin agyipu anyɔ banɩmʋ mʋ, fʋlɔn ii biti ɔ lɩɩ bamʋ nnɔ tɔ ba dɩyɛ mʋ. Falɛ nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ tɔɔrɔɔ bamʋ mʋ, ii biti o wu.

eng: They have the power to close up the sky so that no rain will fall for as long as they prophesy. They also have power over the waters, to turn them into blood, and to strike the earth with any plague, as often as they wish.
local: Anyɩn anyɔ banɩmʋ ba sʋ ɔlʋn ba taalɛ kɩtaa bʋlayɩ sʋsʋ, kɛ ɔ man bɩla da saŋa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɔwɛ abwaarɛsɩn mʋ. Nɩɩ ba sʋ ɔlʋn ba taalɛ yɛgɛ nkyu ii biliŋi nkalan, nɩɩ ba bɩla ba sʋ ɔlʋn ba tɩɩ gyerebi ba tʋʋ kasɛ sʋ saŋa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ bee biti.

eng: When they have finished their witnessing, the beast that comes up from the abyss will make war on them, gain victory over them, and kill them.
local: Nɩɩ ba kan lʋwɛ bamʋ abwaarɛsɩn kɩtɔwɛ mʋ, kadʋnbwɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ lɩɩ ɔbɔ bunkyinsa lala mʋ nɩɩ kɩ man sʋ ɔkaa mʋ tɔ mʋ, ee biti ɔ ba kɔ yɛ bamʋ, taalɛ bamʋ kɛ ɔ mɔɔ bamʋ.

eng: Their dead bodies will lie on the street of the great city that is spiritually called Sodom and Egypt, where also their Lord was crucified.
local: Kɛ bamʋ abuni ɩ baa ɩ dɛ kadɛ lala mʋ kigbenbi sʋ, katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba da bamʋ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Yesu oyu laasa sʋ mʋ. Kɩŋasan tɔ mʋ bɛɛ tɩɩ kadɛ lala nɩmʋ yɛ Sodom abɛɛ Egyipiti.

eng: And for three and a half days, members of the peoples, tribes, languages, and nations will look at their dead bodies, but they wont let their dead bodies be put in a tomb.
local: Asa kʋkwɩɩ‐kʋkwɩɩ yɛ ndɛndɛmandɛ yɛ ntɔwɛ kʋkwɩɩ‐kʋkwɩɩ yɛ ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ asa kpini bee biti ba ba yɩlɛ kɩɩ bamʋ abuni mʋ nkɛ nsa yɛ kɩdɩyɛ, nɩɩ ba mɛɛ yɛ be kyula kɛ ɔkʋ o pula bamʋ.

eng: Those who live on earth will rejoice over them. They will celebrate and give each other gifts, because these two prophets had brought such pain to those who live on earth.
local: Atɔwɛpʋ anyɔ maŋa lewu ii biti ɔ waa asa mʋ nɩɩ ba tɛ kasɛ sʋ mʋ kpini ɔkɔn, kɛ ba gyii bamʋ ansi kɛ ba kɛɛ abaa atɔ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ kanɩn Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ anyɔ maŋa ba yɛgɛ nɩɩ asa ba gyii awʋrʋfɔ kasɛ sʋ gaa.

eng: But after three and a half days, the breath of life from God entered them, and they stood on their feet. Great fear came over those who saw them.
local: Amaa nkɛ nsa yɛ kɩdɩyɛ kamaa mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ nkpa ɔfʋʋtɛ ba loo bamʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ba kyiŋi kʋsʋ yɩlɛ bamʋ ayaa sʋ. Tʋtɔ mʋ, kufuu kɩ kɩtaa asa mʋ nɩɩ ba wu bamʋ mʋ.

eng: Then they heard a loud voice from heaven say to them, Come up here. And they went up to heaven in a cloud, while their enemies watched them.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ anyɔ mʋ ba nu ɔbʋlɛ lala kʋ lɩɩ sʋsʋ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, Fan dii ba sʋsʋ nfɩɩ. Nɩɩ ba bɔla abwaarɛ‐wʋlɛ tɔ dii kpee sʋsʋ yɛgɛ bamʋ dʋn ana ba kɩɩ bamʋ.

eng: At that hour there was a great earthquake, and a tenth of the city fell. Seven thousand people were killed by the earthquake, and the rest were afraid and gave glory to the God of heaven.
local: Kanɩn opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ kasɛ ki gyingyen, nɩɩ kadɛ mʋ tɔ nbarɩgɛ tɔ kudu mʋ tɔ ketun kʋlʋn boyi tɩyɛ. Nɩɩ kanɩn kasɛ kigyingyen maŋa mɔɔ asa ngbɩn nsunɔ. Nɩɩ kufuu kɩ kɩtaa bamʋ nɩɩ ba nyɛ nkpa mʋ, nɩɩ ba taa bʋnyaa sa Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ sʋsʋ mʋ.

eng: The second horror is over. The third horror is coming soon.
local: Gyerebi nyɔsapʋ mʋ kyʋn nɩɩ gyerebi sasapʋ mɔɔ bʋ ɔkpa sʋ ɛɛ ba bilen.

eng: Then the seventh angel blew his trumpet, and there were loud voices in heaven saying, The kingdom of the world has become the kingdom of our Lord and his Christ, and he will rule forever and always.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ sunɔsapʋ mʋ fʋlɛ mʋ kabɩlɛ, nɩɩ ɩbʋlɛ damantɛ kʋ ii tɔwɛ kenken lɩɩ sʋsʋ yɛ, Kasɛ sʋ kuwura‐gyii ki biliŋi anɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ Ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ wʋla lɛɛ mʋ lɛɛ, nɩɩ ee biti o gyii kuwura kakpaa.

eng: Then the twenty-four elders, who were seated on their thrones before God, fell on their faces and worshipped God.
local: Abɩlɩsa adʋnyɔ yɛ ana mʋ nɩɩ ba tɛ bamʋ awuragya sʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ ansi tɔ mʋ kpini ba muŋa, taa bamʋ ansi bun kasɛ sun Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: They said, We give thanks to you, Lord God Almighty, who is and was, for you have taken your great power and enforced your rule.
local: bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ, Ɔlʋlʋnpʋ, an sʋ afwaala an sa fʋ, fɛɛ fʋ maa taa fʋ ɔlʋn lala mʋ nɩɩ fu piili fii gyii kuwura.

eng: The nations were enraged, but your wrath came. The time came for the dead to be judged. The time came to reward your servants, the prophets and saints, and those who fear your name, both small and great, and to destroy those who destroy the earth.
local: Kasɛ sʋ asa mʋ nɩɩ be mee sun fʋ mʋ ba nyɛ agbʋ, amaa nbɩɩnbɩɩ fʋ agbʋ mʋ ba. Saŋa fʋ fɛɛ fʋ talɛ alewupu, kɛ fʋ ka fʋ atɔwɛpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba gyɛ fʋ ayaafɔlɛ kʋkɔ, yɛ fʋ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba sɩlɛ fʋ kɩtɩɩ mʋ, asa kparɛ yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba san kpini. Saŋa mʋ fʋʋ fɛɛ fu nyita bamʋ nɩɩ bee nyita kasɛ mʋ.

eng: Then Gods temple in heaven was opened, and the chest containing his covenant appeared in his temple. There were lightning, voices, thunder, an earthquake, and large hail.
local: Nɩɩ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ sʋsʋ mʋ ki bugi, nɩɩ n wu Ɩbwaarɛ maa asa nsana katɔwɛ kyula kɩdakaa mʋ maa bʋ obu lala mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ n wu bʋlayɩ maa wɛɛ ɩsɛɛ nɩɩ ɩ sʋ ɔlanba yɛgɛ ɩɩ pʋntɛ, nɩɩ kasɛ ki gyingyen, nɩɩ bʋlayɩ abuu ɩ lɩɩ tɩyɛ gaa.

eng: Then a great sign appeared in heaven: a woman clothed with the sun, with the moon under her feet and a crown of twelve stars on her head.
local: N wu sɩnkaala yɛ adangana lala kʋ maa lɛɛ ɩmʋ n‐yɩɩ kaapʋ sʋsʋ. N wu ɔkyɩɩ maa bun kyɔwɛ fɛɛ atɔ bunsa, nɩɩ ɔbwaayɩ bʋ mʋ ayaa kasɛ, nɩɩ o bun kuwura‐wʋrʋ mʋ nɩɩ akyikpebi kudu anyɔ ɩɩ maa kɩmʋ sʋ.

eng: She was pregnant, and she cried out because she was in labor, in pain from giving birth.
local: Ɔ sʋ ɔtɔ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ kʋʋgɛ. Nɩɩ keduŋidi mʋ maa kɔ mʋ sʋ mʋ, ɔ sawʋ kenken.

eng: Then another sign appeared in heaven: it was a great fiery red dragon, with seven heads and ten horns, and seven royal crowns on his heads.
local: Nɩɩ n bɩla bwii wu sɩnkaala kʋ maa lɛɛ ɩmʋ n‐yɩɩ kaapʋ sʋsʋ. N wu kɩnansɛ pɩpɛɛ dansa kʋ, nɩɩ ɔ sʋ amu asunɔ yɛ agyakpaa kudu nɩɩ o bun awura‐wʋrʋ asunɔ amu mʋ sʋ.

eng: His tail swept down a third of heavens stars and threw them to the earth. The dragon stood in front of the woman who was about to give birth so that when she gave birth, he might devour her child.
local: Nɩɩ mʋ ɔpɩɩ bɩɩtɛ akyikpebi mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ sʋsʋ mʋ nbarɩgɛ tɔ ntun nsa mʋ ketun kʋlʋn ba tʋʋ kasɛ sʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ ba yɩlɛ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ kʋʋgɛ mu ansi tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ kan kʋʋgɛ mʋ kɛ ɔ nyɛ taa kebii mʋ mɩyɛ bilen.

eng: She gave birth to a son, a male child who is to rule all the nations with an iron rod. Her child was snatched up to God and his throne.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ kʋʋgɛ kebii‐nyɩnsa‐bii mʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ taa kɩbɩlɩtɔ kuwura‐kpebi gyii ndɛndɛmandɛ kpini sʋ kuwura. Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ ba taa kebii mʋ kpee Ɩbwaarɛ maa mʋ kuwura‐gya mʋ asɛ.

eng: Then the woman fled into the desert, where God has prepared a place for her. There she will be taken care of for one thousand two hundred sixty days.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ sɩlɛ kpee keperi sʋ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ lɔŋɔ yɩla sa mʋ, katɩn mʋ nɩɩ bee biti ba kɩɩ mʋ sʋ nkɛ kagbɩn kʋlʋn yɛ alɩfa anyɔ yɛ adisiye . Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ bɩlɩsa asɛ kɔ yɛ kɩnansɛ pɩpɛɛ dansa mʋ.

eng: Then there was war in heaven: Michael and his angels fought the dragon. The dragon and his angels fought back.
local: Nɩɩ kɩnaa kɩtɩyɛ sʋsʋ. Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ bɩlɩsa asɛ Maakɛlɩ maa mʋ nbɔɔ ba kɔ yɛ kɩnansɛ dansa mʋ, nɩɩ kanansɛ dansa mʋ maa mʋ nbɔɔ gbaa ba kɔ yɛ bamʋ.

eng: but they did not prevail, and there was no longer any place for them in heaven.
local: Amaa kɩnansɛ dansa maa mʋ nbɔɔ ba kɔ tɩyɛ, nɩɩ ba man bɩla nyɛ ɔkyɩnakpa sʋsʋ.

eng: So the great dragon was thrown down. The old snake, who is called the devil and Satan, the deceiver of the whole world, was thrown down to the earth; and his angels were thrown down with him.
local: Nɩɩ ba ŋɛ kanɩn kɩnansɛ dansa maŋa kyʋŋɛ kasɛ sʋ. Mʋ ɩ gyɛ dɩdaa kanansɛ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ okisipu, abɛɛ Sɩtaanɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ pɩna dulinyaa tɔ asa kpini mʋ. Ba ŋɛ maa mʋ nbɔɔ mʋ kyʋŋɛ kasɛ sʋ.

eng: Then I heard a loud voice in heaven say, Now the salvation and power and kingdom of our God, and the authority of his Christ have come. The accuser of our brothers and sisters, who accuses them day and night before our God, has been thrown down.
local: Nɩɩ n nu ɔbʋlɛ lala kʋ sʋsʋ maa tɔwɛ yɛ, Nbɩɩnbɩɩ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kamɔlɩgɛ kɩ ba. Nbɩɩnbɩɩ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kaapʋ mʋ ɔlʋn fɛɛ Owura. Nbɩɩnbɩɩ nɩɩ Krisito mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ Wʋla lɛɛ mʋ, kɩbɩlɩsa mʋ kɩ lɩɩ ifuli. Lɩɩ fɛɛ Sɩtaanɛ, anɛ tɔɔmaa ana ɔpwɩɩpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ yɩlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ansi tɔ ɛɛ pwɩɩ bamʋ kakyʋwapɛ yɛ kagyanbwɛ mʋ, ba ŋɛ mʋ lɩɩ sʋsʋ fɔyɩ.

eng: They gained the victory over him on account of the blood of the Lamb and the word of their witness. Love for their own lives didnt make them afraid to die.
local: Nɩɩ anɛ tɔɔmaa ana ba taa Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ nkalan, yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn mʋ kesintin kɩlɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ gyii Sɩtaanɛ sʋ, nɩɩ ba man taa bamʋ n‐yɩɩ tɩɩ sɛhn, amaa ba taa bamʋ nkpa yɩlɛ tɔ sa lewu.

eng: Therefore, rejoice, you heavens and you who dwell in them. But oh! The horror for the earth and sea! The devil has come down to you with great rage, for he knows that he only has a short time.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ, sʋsʋ yɛ fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fan tɛ kɩmʋ tɔ mʋ, fan yɛgɛ fanɛ ansi i gyii. Amaa kasɛ yɛ ɔpʋ bɩrɛ, laakʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ Sɩtaanɛ kpɩlɩgɛ ba kasɛ sʋ fanɛ asɛ, nɩɩ agbʋ a bɔla mʋ tɔ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ o nyi mʋ saŋa maa man bɩla ɔ kyɔ.

eng: When the dragon saw that he had been thrown down to the earth, he chased the woman who had given birth to the male child.
local: Kɩnansɛ pɩpɛɛ dansa mʋ maa wu fɛɛ ba ŋɛ mʋ kyʋŋɛ kasɛ sʋ mʋ, ɔ kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ ɔ gya ɔkyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kʋʋgɛ obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ.

eng: But the woman was given the two wings of the great eagle so that she could fly to her place in the desert. There she would be taken care of—out of the snakes reach—for a time and times and half a time.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sa ɔkyɩɩ mʋ kpaakpaatɛ lala abanbantɛ anyɔ, kɛ ɔ taalɛ fugi kpee kepiri sʋ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba lɔŋɔ yɩla sa mʋ. Kɛ ba kɩɩ mʋ sʋ naa fʋʋ nsu nsa yɛ kɩdɩyɛ, kɛ kɩnansɛ pɩpɛɛ dansa mʋ ɔ man nyɛ mʋ.

eng: Then from his mouth the snake poured a river of water after the woman so that the river would sweep her away.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ kɩnansɛ mʋ gbʋʋ nkyu lɩɩ mʋ kɔnɔ tɔ fɛɛ ɔbʋn mʋ, kɛ kɩmʋ ɔgyalɛ o gyii ɔkyɩɩ mʋ.

eng: But the earth helped the woman. The earth opened its mouth and swallowed the river that the dragon poured out of his mouth.
local: Amaa kasɛ kɩ kyɛ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ tɔ, fɛɛ kɩ maa bugi kɩmʋ kɔnɔ mɩyɛ nkyu mʋ nɩɩ kɩnansɛ mʋ gbʋʋ lɩɩ mʋ kɔnɔ tɔ mʋ.

eng: So the dragon was furious with the woman, and he went off to make war on the rest of her children, on those who keep Gods commandments and hold firmly to the witness of Jesus.
local: Nɩɩ kɩnansɛ mʋ nyɛ agbʋ lɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kyʋn ɔ naa kɔ yɛ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ mu‐bii ana asansa mʋ kpini. Bamʋ ɩ gyɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba buu Ɩbwaarɛ nbara, nɩɩ ba yɩlɛ kenken bee gyii Yesu asɩn mʋ kesintin mʋ.

eng: Then the dragon stood on the seashore.
local: Nɩɩ kɩnansɛ pɩpɛɛ dansa mʋ naa sii ɔ yɩlɛ ɔpʋ kɔnɔ.

eng: and I saw a beast coming up out of the sea. It had ten horns and seven heads. Each of its horns was decorated with a royal crown, and on its heads were blasphemous names.
local: N wu kadʋnbwɛ kʋ maa lɩɩ ɔpʋ tɔ ɛɛ ba. Ɔ sʋ agyakpaa kudu yɛ amu asunɔ yɛgɛ awura‐wʋrʋ kudu ɩɩ bun agyakpaa mʋ sʋ, yɛgɛ atɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ saalɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ gyan mu kumu kʋmaa nɩmʋ sʋ.

eng: The beast I saw was like a leopard. Its feet were like a bears, and its mouth was like a lions mouth. The dragon gave it his power, throne, and great authority.
local: Kadʋnbwɛ mʋ nɩɩ n wu mʋ du fɛɛ ɔpɩya, nɩɩ mʋ ayaa i du nɩn fɛɛ ɔkyʋkyʋrakyʋrɛ ayaa, nɩɩ mʋ kɔnɔ ki du fɛɛ bɩranɛ kɔnɔ. Nɩɩ kɩnansɛ pɩpɛɛ dansa mʋ taa mʋ ɔlʋn yɛ mʋ kuwura‐gya yɛ kɩmʋ kɩbɩlɩsa mʋ kpini sa kadʋnbwɛ mʋ.

eng: One of its heads appeared to have been slain and killed, but its deadly wound was healed. So the whole earth was amazed and followed the beast.
local: Mu amu mʋ tɔ kʋkʋ du nɩn fɛɛ kɩ maa nyɛ lewu ɔlɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ taalɛ mɔɔ, amaa ki wu. Nɩɩ i kyinkyin dulinyaa tɔ asa kpini nɩɩ ba buu mʋ.

eng: They worshipped the dragon because it had given the beast its authority. They worshipped the beast and said, Who is like the beast, and who can fight against it.
local: Nɩɩ asa ba sun kɩnansɛ pɩpɛɛ dansa mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ sa kadʋnbwɛ mʋ ɔlʋn nɩɩ ba bɩla ba sun kadʋnbwɛ mʋ. Nɩɩ bɛɛ taasɛ yɛ, Anɩmʋ ɩ fʋʋ kadʋnbwɛ nɩmʋ? Anɩmʋ ɩ taalɛ kɔ yɛ mʋ.

eng: The beast was given a mouth that spoke boastful and blasphemous things, and it was given authority to act for forty-two months.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ sa kadʋnbwɛ mʋ ɔkpa yɛ ɔ tɔwɛ alala‐sɩn kɛ ɔ saalɛ mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ sa mʋ ɔlʋn yɛ o gyii kɩbɩlɩsa ɩbwaayɩ adɩna yɛ anyɔ.

eng: It opened its mouth to speak blasphemies against God. It blasphemed Gods name and his dwelling place .
local: Nɩɩ o bugi mʋ kɔnɔ tɔwɛ nbusaa asɩn ee yii Ɩbwaarɛ, ee nyita mʋ kɩtɩɩ yɛgɛ ɛɛ saalɛ mʋ ɔkyɩnakpa yɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ mʋ asɛ sʋsʋ mʋ kpini.

eng: It was also allowed to make war on the saints and to gain victory over them. It was given authority over every tribe, people, language, and nation.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ bɩla sa mʋ ɔkpa yɛ ɔ kɔ mʋ asa, kɛ o gyii bamʋ sʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ bɩla sa mʋ ɔkpa yɛ o gyii kuwura nbunɔ kʋmaa yɛ asa kʋmaa yɛ ntɔwɛ kʋmaa yɛ ndɛndɛmandɛ kpini sʋ.

eng: All who live on earth worshipped it, all whose names hadnt been written—from the time the earth was made—in the scroll of life of the Lamb who was slain.
local: Kasɛ sʋ asa kpini bee biti ba sun kadʋnbwɛ mʋ. Amɔɔ bamʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ bamʋ atɩɩ waa nkpa ɔwʋlɛ mʋ tɔ lɩɩ dulinyaa npiili asɛ mʋ nkʋn. Nkpa ɔwʋlɛ nɩmʋ gyɛ Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba mɔɔ mʋ lɛɛ.

eng: Whoever has ears must listen.
local: Nɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ asʋ mʋ o nu.

eng: If any are to be taken captive, then into captivity they will go. If any are to be killed by the sword, then by the sword they will be killed. This calls for endurance and faithfulness on the part of the saints.
local: Nɩɩ ɩ kan ɩ dɛ sa fɛɛ bee biti ba tii ɔkʋ obu mʋ, bee biti ba kɩtaa mʋ naa tii. Nɩɩ ɩ kan ɩ dɛ fɛɛ sakunde nɩɩ bee biti ba taa mɔɔ ɔkʋ mʋ, sakunde lewu nɩɩ ee biti o wu. Asɩn nɩmʋ falɛ sʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asa ba maa nyɛ kenyiita, kɛ ba nyɛ nkɔlɔ yɩlɛ bamʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii tɔ.

eng: Then I saw another beast coming up from the earth. It had two horns like a lamb, but it was speaking like a dragon.
local: Nɩɩ n bɩla wu kadʋnbwɛ kʋ maa paatɛ lɩɩ kasɛ tɔ. Ɔ sʋ agyakpaa anyɔ fɛɛ kabʋlʋpʋ, amaa ɛɛ tɔwɛ fɛɛ kɩnansɛ pɩpɛɛ dansa mʋ.

eng: It exercises all the authority of the first beast in its presence. It also makes the earth and those who live in it worship the first beast, whose fatal wound was healed.
local: Ɔ sʋ kadʋnbwɛ gyankpaasa mʋ kɩbɩlɩsa, nɩɩ ɔ yɩlɛ mʋ ayaa tɔ sun kusun. Nɩɩ ɔ nyan kasɛ yɛ kɩmʋ sʋ asa kpini, yɛ ba sun kadʋnbwɛ gyankpaasa mʋ nɩɩ mu lewu ɔlɔ mʋ wu mʋ.

eng: It does great signs so that it even makes fire come down from heaven to earth in the presence of the people.
local: Nɩɩ kadʋnbwɛ nyɔsapʋ nɩmʋ waa asɩn kyinkyinsa lala, halɩɩ ɔ yɛgɛ nɩɩ fʋlɔn lɩɩ sʋsʋ ba kasɛ sʋ nɩɩ asa ba wu yɛ bamʋ ansi.

eng: It deceives those who live on earth by the signs that it was allowed to do in the presence of the beast. It told those who live on earth to make an image for the beast who had been wounded by the sword and yet came to life again.
local: Lɩɩ ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sa mʋ sʋ mʋ, ɔ yɩlɛ kadʋnbwɛ gyankpaasa mʋ ayaa tɔ waa asɩn kyinkyinsa, ɔ sʋ ɛɛ pɩna dulinyaa tɔ asa kpini. Nɩɩ ɔ nyan dulinyaa tɔ asa yɛ ba pɔyɩ ɔdakpa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ yɩlɛ sa kadʋnbwɛ gyankpaasa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ lewu ɔlɔ, nɩɩ o bwii kpaarɛ mʋ.

eng: It was allowed to give breath to the beasts image so that the beasts image would even speak and cause anyone who didnt worship the beasts image to be put to death.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ sa kadʋnbwɛ nyɔsapʋ mʋ ɔkpa, fɛɛ ɔ fʋʋtɛ nkpa ɔfʋʋtɛ sa ɔdakpa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ yɩlɛ sa kadʋnbwɛ gyankpaasa mʋ. Kɛ kɩ taalɛ tɔwɛ, kɛ kɩ taalɛ mɔɔ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o kina yɛ ɔ mɛɛ yɛ o sun mʋ mʋ.

eng: It forces everyone—the small and great, the rich and poor, the free and slaves—to have a mark put on their right hand or on their forehead.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ bɩla nyan ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ, abʋlʋpʋ yɛ alʋlʋnpʋ, afulee wuya ana yɛ atiripu, bamʋ nɩɩ bee gyii bamʋ kɩbaa sʋ yɛ anyɛ yɛ ba nyɛ kakyʋlɛ bamʋ abaa gyisa sʋ abɛɛ bamʋ asɩkpɩɩ sʋ.

eng: It will not allow anyone to make a purchase or sell anything unless the person has the mark with the beasts name or the number of its name.
local: Saamʋ, ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man sʋ kadʋnbwɛ mʋ kakyʋlɛ mʋ, ɔ mɛɛ taalɛ sɔɔ abɛɛ ɔ fɛ. Kakyʋlɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ kadʋnbwɛ mʋ kɩtɩɩ, abɛɛ mʋ kɩtɩɩ mʋ akaragɛ‐bi.

eng: This calls for wisdom. Let the one who understands calculate the beasts number, for its a human beings number. Its number is six hundred sixty-six.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa ɔkʋmaa maa nyɛ kanyaasɩn. Ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ kanyaasɩn mʋ, ɔ taalɛ bɩɩ kadʋnbwɛ mʋ akaragɛ‐bi mʋ kasɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ akaragɛ‐bi nɩmʋ yɩla sa nyɩmɩsa nɩn. Akaragɛ‐bi nɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ alɩfa asiye yɛ adisiye yɛ asiye .

eng: Then I looked, and there was the Lamb, standing on Mount Zion. With him were one hundred forty-four thousand who had his name and his Fathers name written on their foreheads.
local: N maa bɩla kɩɩ mʋ, n wu Kabʋlʋpʋ kʋ maa yɩlɛ Siyon kɩbɩɩ mʋ sʋ. Nɩɩ asa ngbɩn kɩlɩfa yɛ adɩna yɛ ana mʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ kɩtɩɩ yɛ Mʋ‐Sɛ kɩtɩɩ gyaga bamʋ asɩkpɩɩ sʋ mʋ ba maa yɩlɛ mʋ asɛ.

eng: I heard a sound from heaven that was like the sound of rushing water and loud thunder. The sound I heard was like that of harpists playing their harps.
local: Nɩɩ n nu ɩlawʋ kʋ lɩɩ sʋsʋ fɛɛ ɔbʋn maa sʋ ɔgyalɛ abɛɛ bʋlayɩ maa pʋntɛ mʋ. Ɩlawʋ mʋ nɩɩ n nu mʋ, ii su nɩn fɛɛ adʋnlɛ alanpʋ kʋkyɔ ba maa lan bamʋ adʋnlɛ mʋ.

eng: They sing a new song in front of the throne, the four living creatures, and the elders. And no one could learn the song except the one hundred forty-four thousand who had been purchased from the earth.
local: Nɩɩ asa ngbɩn kɩlɩfa yɛ adɩna yɛ ana ba yɩlɛ kuwura‐gya mʋ asɛ yɛ atɔ kɩɩsa ana mʋ yɛ abɩlɩsa adʋnyɔ yɛ ana mu ansi tɔ, nɩɩ ba bɩɩ ɔlʋn pʋpwɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔkʋ man taalɛ bɩɩla, amɔɔ asa ngbɩn kɩlɩfa yɛ adɩna yɛ ana mʋ nɩɩ Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ mɔlɩgɛ lɩɩ kasɛ sʋ mʋ nkʋn.

eng: They werent defiled with women, for these people who follow the Lamb wherever he goes are virgins. They were purchased from among humankind as early produce for God and the Lamb.
local: Kanɩn asa maŋa ɩ gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man taa kakyɩɩ‐biti waa bamʋ n‐yɩɩ ineesi, nɩɩ be du nɩn fɛɛ anyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba man nyi akyɩɩ. Nɩɩ ba buu Kabʋlʋpʋ Yesu, mʋ katɩn kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ee kpee. Nɩɩ ɔ ka bamʋ amu sʋ kʋkɔ lɩɩ asa tɔ, ba du nɩn fɛɛ abi gyankpaasa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ Kabʋlʋpʋ Yesu mʋ lɛɛ.

eng: No lie came from their mouths; they are blameless.
local: Atɩntɩnsa kʋ ɩ man pɩɩ lɩɩ bamʋ nnɔ tɔ, nɩɩ bamʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋ ɩ man sʋ asɩn libi akʋ.

eng: Then I saw another angel flying high overhead with eternal good news to proclaim to those who live on earth, and to every nation, tribe, language, and people.
local: Nɩɩ n wu Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ kʋ maa fugi ɛɛ kyaabɔɔ sʋsʋ. Ɔ sʋ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ tɔ kakpaa nɩɩ ɛɛ tɔwɛ ɛɛ sa ɔsʋwʋlɛ kʋmaa yɛ kebunɔ kʋmaa yɛ katɔwɛ kʋmaa yɛ asa kpini.

eng: He said in a loud voice, Fear God and give him glory, for the hour of his judgment has come. Worship the one who made heaven and earth, the sea and springs of water.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ taa ɔbʋlɛ lala kuusi tɔwɛ yɛ, Fan sɩlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kɛ fan taa bʋnyaa sa mʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ mʋ katalɛ saŋa mʋ fʋʋ. Fan sun ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa sʋsʋ yɛ kasɛ, ɔpʋ yɛ ɩbʋn.

eng: Another angel, a second one, followed and said, Fallen, fallen is Babylon the great! She made all the nations drink the wine of her lustful passion.
local: Nɩɩ kabɔɔ nyɔsapʋ kʋ buu ɔbanban mʋ lɩɩ sʋsʋ ɛɛ pʋntɛ kenken yɛ, Babilon tɩyɛ, kadɛ kparɛ mʋ tɩyɛ. Mʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ɩsʋwʋlɛ sʋ asa kpini ba waa kɩgyagaa fɛɛ mʋ. Nɩɩ i du fɛɛ maa sa bamʋ nta bʋlʋnsa nɩɩ ba nuu mʋ. Ɩnɩmʋ ii biti ɩ yɛgɛ kɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ bɩɩtɛ bamʋ asʋ.

eng: Then another angel, a third one, followed them and said in a loud voice, If any worship the beast and its image, and receive a mark on their foreheads or their hands.
local: Nɩɩ kabɔɔ sasapʋ kʋ gbaa buu abanban mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ sʋ ɔbʋlɛ lala ɛɛ pʋntɛ yɛ, Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o sun kadʋnbwɛ mʋ yɛ mʋ apɔyɩtɔ ɔdakpa, nɩɩ ɔ sʋ mʋ kakyʋlɛ mʋ kɩsɩkpɩɩ sʋ abɛɛ mʋ kɩbaa sʋ mʋ.

eng: they themselves will also drink the wine of Gods passionate anger, poured full strength into the cup of his wrath. They will suffer the pain of fire and sulfur in the presence of the holy angels and the Lamb.
local: kanɩn ɔsa maŋa gbaa mʋ ii biti o nuu Ɩbwaarɛ agbʋ nta mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋlʋn nɩɩ ɔ kyɛɛgɛ waa mʋ agbʋ kawɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ. Bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa ɩnɩmʋ kpini mʋ bee biti ba nyɛ kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ ferinwuta fʋlɔn tɔ Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ yɛ mʋ Kabʋlʋpʋ mu ansi tɔ.

eng: The smoke of their painful suffering goes up forever and always. There is no rest day or night for those who worship the beast and its image, and those who receive the mark of its name.
local: Asa mʋ nɩɩ ba sun kadʋnbwɛ mʋ yɛ mʋ apɔyɩtɔ ɔdakpa mʋ, abɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ mʋ kakyʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ mʋ kɩtɩɩ mʋ, fʋlɔn mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ dɩyɛ bamʋ mʋ, ii biti dɩyɛ kakpaa, nɩɩ ba mɛɛ yɛ ba nyɛ ɔfʋʋtɛ kakyʋwapɛ yɛ kagyanbwɛ.

eng: This calls for the endurance of the saints, who keep Gods commandments and keep faith with Jesus.
local: Asɩn nɩmʋ falɛ sʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asa ba nyɛ kenyiita. Bamʋ ɩ gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba buu mʋ nbara, nɩɩ ba nyɛ nkɔlɔ yɩlɛ Yesu Krisito kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii mʋ tɔ.

eng: And I heard a voice from heaven say, Write this: Favored are the dead who die in the Lord from now on. Yes, says the Spirit, so they can rest from their labors, because their deeds follow them.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ n nu ɔbʋlɛ kʋ maa tɔwɛ lɩɩ sʋsʋ yɛ, Kyʋrɔɔ ɩnɩmʋ yɩla! Lɩɩ nbɩɩnbɩɩ nɩmʋ ɩ sʋ ii kpee mʋ, kusee ɩ gyɛ sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba san ba sʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu tɔ nɩɩ ba wu mʋ. Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu mʋ gbaa tɔwɛ yɛ, Kesintin nɩn, bee biti ba nyɛ ɔfʋʋtɛ lɩɩ bamʋ kɩkpɩnɛ sʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ awaasa kʋkɔka ii biti i buu bamʋ.

eng: Then I looked, and there was a white cloud. On the cloud was seated someone who looked like the Human One. He had a gold crown on his head and a sharp sickle in his hand.
local: N maa kɩɩ mʋ, n wu kabwaarɛ wʋlɛ fufuli kʋ, nɩɩ n wu ɔkʋ maa tɛ kɩmʋ sʋ fɛɛ nyɩmɩsa. O bun suwa kuwura‐wʋrʋ nɩɩ loogyi bʋyɛsa bʋ mʋ kɩbaa tɔ.

eng: Another angel came out of the temple, calling in a loud voice to the one seated on the cloud: Use your sickle to reap the harvest, for the time to harvest has come, and the harvest of the earth is ripe.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ kʋ gbaa lɩɩ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ taa ɔbʋlɛ lala kuusi tɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛ abwaarɛ‐wʋlɛ mʋ sʋ yɛ, Taa fu loogyi sɔɔ adɔɔ‐gyitɔ mʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ kasɛ sʋ atɔ duusa mʋ a payɩ, nɩɩ ɩmʋ kɩtɩŋɛ saŋa fʋʋ.

eng: So the one seated on the cloud swung his sickle over the earth, and the earth was harvested.
local: Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔmʋnɩ ɔ tɛ abwaarɛ‐wʋlɛ sʋ mʋ, taa mʋ loogyi mʋ sɔɔ atɔ duusa mʋ lɩɩ kasɛ sʋ kpini.

eng: Then another angel came out of the temple in heaven, and he also had a sharp sickle.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ kʋ bɩla lɩɩ sʋsʋ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ, yɛgɛ ngbaa mʋ sʋ loogyi bʋyɛsa.

eng: Still another angel, who has power over fire, came out from the altar. He said in a loud voice to the one who had the sharp sickle, Use your sharp sickle to cut the clusters in the vineyard of the earth, because its grapes are ripe.
local: Biyo, Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kɩɩ fʋlɔn sʋ mʋ, lɩɩ saraga kaswɩɩ mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ taa ɔbʋlɛ lala pʋntɛ tɔ tɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ loogyi bʋyɛsa mʋ yɛ, Taa fu loogyi bʋyɛsa mʋ kʋʋla oyu abi akyaa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ sʋ bɛɛ waa wayɩn nta mʋ lɩɩ kasɛ sʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɩmʋ abi mʋ ɩɩ payɩ.

eng: So the angel swung his sickle into the earth, and cut the vineyard of the earth, and he put what he reaped into the great winepress of Gods passionate anger.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ taa mu loogyi mʋ sɔɔ oyu abi mʋ kasɛ sʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ kʋʋla ɩmʋ naa waa katɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ nyan oyu abi mʋ bɛɛ lɛɛ nkyu. Opula mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ nyan maŋa mʋ, ɩ yɩlɛ sa Ɩbwaarɛ agbʋ lala mʋ.

eng: Then the winepress was trampled outside the city, and the blood came out of the winepress as high as the horses bridles for almost two hundred miles.
local: Kanɩn opula mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ nyan oyu abi bɛɛ lɛɛ ɩmʋ nkyu mʋ, bʋ kadɛ mʋ ɩkaa sʋ nɩn. Nɩɩ ba kan nyan abi mʋ, nkalan ii biti ɩ lɩɩ ɩmʋ tɔ ba ɩ kyʋn fɛɛ okyukpa mʋ nɩɩ ɩmʋ nswɩɩ ɩ waa fɛɛ ɩmayɩlɩ alɩfa anyɔ, nɩɩ ɩmʋ ɔnyɛ ɩ waa fɛɛ ayaa tantan anuu.

eng: Then I saw another great and awe-inspiring sign in heaven. There were seven angels with seven plagues—and these are the last, for with them Gods anger is brought to an end.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ kamaa mʋ, n bɩla wu sɩnkaala yɛ asɩn kyinkyinsa kʋ sʋsʋ. Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ nsunɔ, nɩɩ ba sʋ laalaalʋwɛ igyerebi nsunɔ bɛɛ ba. Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ igyerebi laalaalʋwɛ mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɩnɩmʋ kamaa mʋ Ɩbwaarɛ agbʋ mʋ ɩ fʋʋ ɔkaa.

eng: Then I saw what appeared to be a sea of glass mixed with fire. Those who gained victory over the beast, its image, and the number of its name were standing by the glass sea, holding harps from God.
local: Nɩɩ n wu kʋtɔ kʋ fɛɛ ɔpʋ nkyu mʋ nɩɩ ki du fɛɛ digi nɩɩ kɩ bʋga yɛ fʋlɔn. Nɩɩ n wu bamʋ nɩɩ ba kɔ lɩɩ kadʋnbwɛ mʋ, yɛ mʋ apɔyɩtɔ ɔdakpa mʋ, yɛ mʋ kɩtɩɩ akaragɛ‐bi mʋ. N wu ba maa yɩlɛ ɔpʋ mʋ kɔnɔ, yɛgɛ ba sʋ adʋnlɛ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sa bamʋ mʋ.

eng: They sing the song of Moses, Gods servant, and the song of the Lamb, saying, Great and awe-inspiring are your works, Lord God Almighty. Just and true are your ways, king of the nations.
local: Nɩɩ ba bɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kɩyaafɔlɛ Mosesi yɛ Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ ɔlʋn yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɔlʋnpʋ Ɩbwaarɛ, fʋ awaasa kparɛ mʋ ɩ kyɔ nɩɩ ii kyinkyin. Fʋ ɩ gyɛ kasɛ sʋ kpini owura, fʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ kesintin.

eng: Who wont fear you, Lord, and glorify your name? You alone are holy. All nations will come and fall down in worship before you, for your acts of justice have been revealed.
local: O Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, anɩmʋ ɩ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ sɩlɛ fʋ, nɩɩ anɩmʋ ɩ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ taa bʋnyaa sa fʋ kɩtɩɩ? Lɩɩ fɛɛ fʋ nkʋn ii du kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ kasɛ sʋ. Kasɛ sʋ asa kpini bee biti ba be muŋa fu ansi tɔ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ fʋ ɩkpa kyiigisa mʋ ɩ lɩɩ ifuli.

eng: After this I looked, and the temple in heaven—that is, the tent of witness—was opened.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ kamaa mʋ, n kɩɩ nɩɩ n wu ba maa tigi opula kyɩrɛkyɩrɛkpa kɩkyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ tantɩ kubuu mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ sʋsʋ mʋ tɔ yɩla.

eng: The seven angels, who have the seven plagues, came out of the temple. They were clothed in pure bright linen and had gold sashes around their waists.
local: Tantɩ kubuu maŋa tɔ nɩɩ nbɔɔ asunɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ igyerebi asunɔ mʋ ba lɩɩ, yɛgɛ ba bun ɩkaalɛ fufuli parɛparɛ yɛgɛ ɩɩ ŋalɩgɛ sʋ, nɩɩ ba taa suwa ɩkɩrɩsʋ ŋmina bamʋ nkɔlɔ tɔ.

eng: Then one of the four living creatures gave the seven angels seven gold bowls full of the anger of the God who lives forever and always.
local: Nɩɩ atɔ kɩɩsa ana mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ sa Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ nsunɔ mʋ, suwa ɩlapɛ isunɔ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛ nkpa tɔ kakpaa mʋ agbʋ a bɔla ɩmʋ tɔ.

eng: The temple was filled with smoke from Gods glory and power, and no one could go into the temple until the seven plagues of the seven last angels were brought to an end.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bʋnyaa yɛ mʋ ɔlʋn ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ idiisii ɩ ba bɔla tantɩ kubuu mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔkʋ mɛɛ taalɛ loo tʋtɔ, amɔɔ Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ nsunɔ mʋ ba lʋwɛ igyerebi isunʋ mʋ pɔyɩ.

eng: Then I heard a loud voice from the temple say to the seven angels, Go and pour out the seven bowls of Gods anger on the earth.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ n nu ɔbʋlɛ lala kʋ lɩɩ tantɩ kubuu mʋ tɔ, kɩ maa tɔwɛ kɩɩ sa Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ asunɔ mʋ yɛ, Fan kpee naa kyɛɛgɛ Ɩbwaarɛ agbʋ ɩlapɛ isunɔ mʋ wʋrɩgɛ kasɛ sʋ.

eng: Then one of the seven angels who had the seven bowls spoke with me. Come, he said, I will show you the judgment upon the great prostitute, who is seated on deep waters.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ asunɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ ɩlapɛ isunɔ mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ ba tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, Ba, kɛ n kaapʋ fʋ kanan mʋ nɩɩ bʋrʋkɔɔnɛ‐kyɩɩ libi, mʋ nɩɩ ɔ yɩlɛ sa kadɛ lala, mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ tiri ɩbʋn lala mʋ, kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ kii biti kɩ baa kɩmʋ sʋ.

eng: So the first angel poured his bowl on the earth, and a nasty and terrible sore appeared on the people who had the beasts mark and worshipped its image.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ gyankpaasa mʋ naa kyɛɛgɛ agbʋ lapɛ mʋ wʋrɩgɛ kasɛ sʋ, nɩɩ ɩlɔ libi mʋ nɩɩ ɩ gyɔɔ nɩɩ ɩɩ kɔ mʋ ɩ yaasɛ loo asa mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ kadʋnbwɛ mʋ kakyʋlɛ nɩɩ bee sun mʋ apɔyɩtɔ ɔdakpa mʋ.

eng: The kings of the earth have committed sexual immorality with her, and those who live on earth have become drunk with the wine of her whoring.
local: Kadɛ lala nɩmʋ tɔ nɩɩ kasɛ sʋ awura bee dii bɛɛ kpɩlɩgɛ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ anyɩn bee dii bɛɛ kpɩlɩgɛ ɔkyɩɩ‐kyagaa sʋ mʋ. Kasɛ sʋ asa kpini ba nuu mʋ kɩgyagaa nta mʋ bʋʋ.

eng: The second angel poured his bowl into the sea, and the sea turned into blood, like the blood of a corpse, and every living thing in the sea died.
local: Nɩɩ kabɔɔ nyɔsapʋ mʋ kyɛɛgɛ mʋ lapɛ mʋ waa ɔpʋ tɔ, nɩɩ i biliŋi nkalan fɛɛ nyɩmɩsa wusa nkalan, nɩɩ atɔ kɩɩsa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ ɔpʋ tɔ mʋ ba wu.

eng: Then he brought me in a Spirit-inspired trance to a desert. There I saw a woman seated on a scarlet beast that was covered with blasphemous names. It had seven heads and ten horns.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ taa mɛ kayaayu tɔ kpee ifuli sʋ. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ n wu ɔkyɩɩ maa tɛ kadʋnbwɛ pɩpɛɛ sʋ, nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ atɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ saalɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gyaga kadʋnbwɛ mʋ sʋ, yɛgɛ ɔ sʋ amu asunɔ yɛ agbakpaa kudu.

eng: The third angel poured his bowl into the rivers and springs of water, and they turned into blood.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ sasapʋ mʋ kyɛɛgɛ mʋ lapɛ mʋ waa ɩbʋn yɛ ɩbʋn amu tɔ, nɩɩ nkyu mʋ i biliŋi nkalan.

eng: The woman wore purple and scarlet clothing, and she glittered with gold and jewels and pearls. In her hand she held a gold cup full of the vile and impure things that came from her activity as a prostitute.
local: Ɔkyɩɩ mʋ bun atɔ pɩpɛɛ yɛ kɩrɩman‐kyu mʋ nɩɩ ɩ sʋ ɔgyalɩn, yɛ suwa yɛ abuu mʋ nɩɩ ɩ sʋ ɔgyalɩn yɛ agyibi kparɛ. Nɩɩ ɔ sʋ suwa kɔɔpʋ mʋ kɩbaa tɔ, yɛgɛ atɔ kisisa yɛ ineesi atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ lɩɩ mʋ kɩgyagaa sʋ mʋ ɩ bɔla kɩmʋ tɔ.

eng: Then I heard the angel of the waters say, You are just, holy one, who is and was, because you have given these judgments.
local: Nɩɩ n nu Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kɩɩ nkyu sʋ mʋ maa tɔwɛ yɛ, O Ɔkyɩrɛkyɩrɛpʋ, Fʋ mʋ nɩɩ fʋ bʋ tɔ, nɩɩ fʋ wʋla fʋ bʋ tɔ, Fu gyii asɩn mʋ, ɩmʋ ɔkpa sʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ fu gyii asɩn mʋ kesintin.

eng: A name—a mystery—was written on her forehead: Babylon the great, the mother of prostitutes and the vile things of the earth.
local: Nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ asiiri aŋasantɩɩ gyaga mʋ kɩsɩkpɩɩ sʋ yɛ, ASƖN KPARƐ BABILON KADƐ KPARƐ ABƲRƲKƆƆNƐ BAMƲ‐NYI, YƐ KASƐ SƲ INEESI KPINI ƖMƲ NYI.

eng: They poured out the blood of saints and prophets, and you have given them blood to drink. They deserve it.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ ɩ mɔɔ fʋ asa yɛ fʋ atɔwɛpʋ, nɩɩ bamʋ nkalan ɩ kyɛɛgɛ wʋrɩgɛ, ɩmʋ sʋ fʋ sa bamʋ nkalan yɛ ba nuu kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa bamʋ.

eng: I saw that the woman was drunk on the blood of the saints and the blood of Jesus witnesses. I was completely stunned when I saw her.
local: N wu fɛɛ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ nuu Ɩbwaarɛ asa nkalan, bamʋ nɩɩ ba yɩlɛ tɔ bee gyii kesintin bɛɛ sa Yesu mʋ, nɩɩ ɩɩ bʋʋ mʋ fɛɛ nta mʋ. N maa wu kanɩn mʋ, i kyinkyin mɛ gaa.

eng: And I heard the altar say, Yes, Lord God Almighty, your judgments are true and just.
local: Nɩɩ n nu Ɩbwaarɛ saraga kaswɩɩ mʋ maa kyula sʋ yɛ, Ɛɛhn, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ ɔlʋnpʋ Ɩbwaarɛ, fʋ katalɛ kɩ gyɛ kesintin nɩɩ kɩ dɛ ɔkpa.

eng: Then the angel said to me, Why are you amazed? I will tell you the mystery of the woman and the seven-headed, ten-horned beast that carries her.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ taasɛ mɛ yɛ, Mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ i kyinkyin fʋ? Ɔkyɩɩ mʋ yɛ kadʋnbwɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ amu asunɔ yɛ agbakpaa kudu, nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ dii mʋ, n biti n lɛɛ ɩmʋ kɩŋasan tɔ kaapʋ fʋ.

eng: The fourth angel poured his bowl on the sun, and it was allowed to burn people with fire.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ nasapʋ kyɛɛgɛ mʋ lapɛ mʋ wʋrɩgɛ kyɔwɛ sʋ, nɩɩ kyɔwɛ mʋ nyɛ ɔlʋn kɛ o fugya asa fɛɛ fʋlɔn.

eng: The beast that you saw was and is not, and is about to come up out of the abyss and go to destruction. Those who live on earth, whose names havent been written in the scroll of life from the time the earth was made, will be amazed when they see the beast, because it was and is not and will again be present.
local: Kadʋnbwɛ mʋ nɩɩ fʋ daa wu nɩɩ ɔ bʋ tɔ mʋ, nbɩɩnbɩɩ ɔ man bɩla ɔ bʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ ee biti ɔ lɩɩ ɔbɔ bunkyinsa lala mʋ nɩɩ kɩ man sʋ ɔkaa mʋ tɔ ba, kɛ ɔ ba gyaŋa mu lewu. Nɩɩ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba tɛ kasɛ sʋ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ man kyʋrɔɔ bamʋ atɩɩ waa nkpa ɔwʋlɛ mʋ tɔ, lɩɩ dulinyaa npiili asɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ba kan wu kadʋnbwɛ mʋ, ii biti i kyinkyin bamʋ fɛɛ kadʋnbwɛ mʋ maa kyɩna ɔ bʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ man bɩla ba ɔ bʋ tɔ, amaa nɩɩ ee biti ɔ bɩla bwii ba.

eng: The people were burned by intense heat, and they cursed the name of the God who had power over these plagues. But they didnt change their hearts and lives and give him glory.
local: Nɩɩ kyɔwɛ mʋ awulawula i fugya bamʋ gaa, nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ yii Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ igyerebi ɩnɩmʋ sʋ ɔlʋn mʋ. Amaa ba kina ba man yɛgɛ alibi kɩwaa, kɛ ba taa bʋnyaa sa mʋ.

eng: This calls for an understanding mind. The seven heads are seven mountains on which the woman is seated. They are also seven kings.
local: Asɩn nɩmʋ falɛ sʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asa ba maa nyɛ nfɛɛrɛ yɛ kanyaasɩn. Amu asunɔ mʋ ɩ gyɛ abɩɩ asunɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ tɛ ɩmʋ sʋ. Nɩɩ ɩ bɩla ɩ gyɛ awura asunɔ.

eng: The fifth angel poured his bowl over the beasts throne, and darkness covered its kingdom. People bit their tongues because of their pain.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ nusapʋ mʋ kyɛɛgɛ mʋ lapɛ mʋ wʋrɩgɛ kadʋnbwɛ mʋ kuwura‐gya sʋ, nɩɩ mu kuwura‐gyii tɔ kpini ki biliŋi kitentenbiri. Nɩɩ ɩmʋ kpini kʋkɔ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ asa ba duŋi bamʋ anobi.

eng: Five kings have fallen, the one is, and the other hasnt yet come. When that king comes, he must remain for only a short time.
local: Awura mʋ tɔ mʋ anuu ba tɩyɛ, nɩɩ ɔkʋlʋn san ee gyii kuwura, nɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ san mʋ mɔɔ man pɩɩta ba. Nɩɩ ɔ kan ba mʋ, ee biti o gyii kuwura nkɛ gbɛrɛɛ kʋ.

eng: and they cursed the God of heaven because of their pains and sores; but they didnt turn away from what they had done.
local: Nɩɩ lɩɩ bamʋ kayɩɩ‐kɔ yɛ ɩlɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba nyɛ sʋ mʋ, ba tɔwɛ yii sʋsʋ Ɩbwaarɛ. Amaa ba kina ba man yɛgɛ alibi kɩwaa kɛ ba ba Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ.

eng: As for the beast that was and is not, it is itself an eighth king that belongs to the seven, and it is going to destruction.
local: Kadʋnbwɛ mʋ nɩɩ naafɔɔ ɔ kyɩna ɔ bʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ man bɩla ɔ bʋ tɔ mʋ, mʋ ɩ gyɛ owura buruwasapu mʋ. Nɩɩ o tii awura gyankpaasa asunɔ mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ee biti ɔ ba gyaŋa mu lewu.

eng: Then the sixth angel poured his bowl on the great river Euphrates. Its water was dried up so that the way was ready for the kings from the east.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ siyesapʋ mʋ kyɛɛgɛ mʋ lapɛ mʋ wʋrɩgɛ ɔbʋn lala Gyufereti mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ kɩmʋ nkyu ɩ nyaa, kɛ ɩ nyɛ lɔŋɔ ɔkpa sa awura mʋ nɩɩ bee biti ba lɩɩ kyɔwɛ kɩlɩɩ ba mʋ.

eng: The ten horns that you saw are ten kings, who havent yet received royal power. But they will receive royal authority for an hour, along with the beast.
local: Agyakpaa kudu mʋ nɩɩ fu wu mʋ yɩlɛ sa awura kudu mʋ nɩɩ ba man pɩɩ nyɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ. Amaa bee biti ba nyɛ kɩbɩlɩsa kɛ ba maa kadʋnbwɛ mʋ ba gyii kuwura kerefi kʋlʋn.

eng: Then I saw three unclean spirits, like frogs, come from the dragons mouth, the beasts mouth, and the mouth of the false prophet.
local: Nɩɩ n wu nbwɩɩ libi asa mʋ nɩɩ ba du fɛɛ apulo. Ba lɩɩ kɩnansɛ pɩpɛɛ dansa mʋ yɛ kadʋnbwɛ mʋ yɛ antɩŋɛsa atɔwɛpʋ mʋ nnɔ tɔ.

eng: These kings will be of one mind, and they will give their power and authority to the beast.
local: Awura kudu mʋ ba sʋ nfɛɛrɛ kʋlʋn, nɩɩ bee biti ba taa bamʋ ɔlʋn kɩbɩlɩsa sa kadʋnbwɛ mʋ.

eng: These are demonic spirits that do signs. They go out to the kings of the whole world, to gather them for battle on the great day of God the Almighty. .
local: Bamʋ ɩ gyɛ Sɩtaanɛ n‐yaayu mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa adangana. Nbwɩɩ libi asa nɩmʋ bee kpee dulinyaa tɔ awura kpini asɛ, naa kʋʋla bamʋ ba naa kɔ kɩnaa, Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ Ɔlʋlʋnpʋ mʋ kakɛ lala mʋ akɛ.

eng: They will make war on the Lamb, but the Lamb will emerge victorious, for he is Lord of lords and King of kings. Those with him are called, chosen, and faithful.
local: Bee biti ba kɔ yɛ Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ, amaa Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ ii biti ɔ taalɛ bamʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ anyɩrɩpɛ tɔ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, yɛ awura tɔ Owura. Nɩɩ mʋ asa ɩ gyɛ, bamʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ, yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ, yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bee buu mʋ kesintin tɔ mʋ.

eng: Look! Im coming like a thief! Favored are those who stay awake and clothed so that they dont go around naked and exposed to shame.
local: Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ wʋla tɔwɛ yɛ, Fan nyiŋi! N biti n ba fɛɛ oyu. Kusee ɩ gyɛ sa ɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ daa mʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋ nɩɩ ɔ kʋʋla o gyoo mɛ, nɩɩ ɔ man dɩlɩgɛ mʋ atɔ bunsa, kɛ ɔ natɛ kibulunkpan kɛ o gyii ipeeli.

eng: Then he said to me, The waters that you saw, where the prostitute is seated, are peoples, crowds, nations, and languages.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, Ɩbʋn mʋ nɩɩ fu wu nɩɩ kabʋrʋkɔɔnɛ‐kyɩɩ mʋ tɛ o tiri ɩmʋ mʋ, ɩ yɩlɛ sa asa damantɛ yɛ ɩsʋwʋlɛ yɛ ntɔwɛ kʋkwɩɩ‐kʋkwɩɩ.

eng: The spirits gathered them at the place that is called in Hebrew, Harmagedon.
local: Nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi mʋ ba kʋʋla awura naa bʋga opula kʋlʋn, katɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ Hibriwu katɔwɛ tɔ yɛ Amagadon mʋ.

eng: As for the ten horns that you saw, they and the beast will hate the prostitute. They will destroy her and strip her bare. They will devour her flesh and burn her with fire.
local: Kadʋnbwɛ yɛ agyakpaa kudu mʋ nɩɩ fu wu mʋ, bee biti ba kisi kabʋrʋkɔɔnɛ‐kyɩɩ mʋ. Nɩɩ bee biti ba kɔɔlɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ lɩɩ mʋ asɛ, kɛ ba yɛgɛ mʋ ɔ natɛ kibulunkpan. Bee biti ba wɛ mʋ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ mʋ, kɛ ba taa ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ san mʋ tʋʋ fʋlɔn tɔ.

eng: Then the seventh angel poured his bowl into the air, and a loud voice came out from the temple, from the throne, saying, It is done.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ sunɔsapʋ kyɛɛgɛ mʋ agbʋ lapɛ mʋ waa afuu tɔ. Nɩɩ n nu ɔbʋlɛ lala kɔ maa lɩɩ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ, kuwura‐gya mʋ asɛ yɛ, Ɩ waa lʋwɛ.

eng: because God moved them to carry out his purposes. That is why they will be of one mind and give their royal power to the beast, until Gods words have been accomplished.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ taa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ee biti fɛɛ ba waa mʋ waa bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ tɔ, kɛ ba nyɛ waa mu kebiti. Kanɩn sʋ nɩɩ awura kudu mʋ ba kyula taa bamʋ ɔlʋn mʋ sa kadʋnbwɛ mʋ, kɛ o gyii kuwura naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɩla mʋ ɩ ba tɔ.

eng: There were lightning strikes, voices, and thunder, and a great earthquake occurred. The earthquake was greater than any that have occurred since there have been people on earth.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɩ wɛɛ ɩsɛɛ nɩɩ bʋlayɩ kpira nɩɩ ɩ pʋntɛ, nɩɩ kasɛ ki gyingyen ɔlʋn sʋ. Nɩɩ lɩɩ koo saŋa mʋ nɩɩ nyɩmɩsa bʋ kasɛ sʋ mʋ, kasɛ man pɩɩta gyingyen falɛ kudubi.

eng: The woman whom you saw is the great city that rules over the kings of the earth.
local: Ɔkyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ fu wu mʋ, yɩlɛ sa kadɛ kparɛ mʋ nɩɩ kii gyii kuwura kasɛ sʋ awura kpini sʋ.

eng: The great city split into three parts, and the cities of the nations fell. God remembered Babylon the great so that he gave her the wine cup of his furious anger.
local: Nɩɩ Babilon kadɛ lala mʋ kɩ barɩgɛ tɔ ntun nsa, nɩɩ ndɛ lala mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ kasɛ sʋ mʋ kpini i nyita. Ɩbwaarɛ nyiŋi Babilon kadɛ lala mʋ alibi, nɩɩ ɔ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ba nuu mʋ agbʋ nta mʋ lɩɩ mʋ kawɛ tɔ.

eng: Every island fled, and the mountains disappeared.
local: Nɩɩ kifintin kʋmaa nɩmʋ nyaa, nɩɩ ɔkʋ man bɩla wu abɩɩ kasɛ sʋ.

eng: Huge hailstones weighing about one hundred pounds came down from heaven on the people. They cursed God for the plague of hail, because the plague was so terrible.
local: Nɩɩ bʋlayɩ abuu mʋ nɩɩ i du fɛɛ afʋlɛ‐buu, nɩɩ ɩmʋ kʋkʋlʋn oduu ɩ waa fɛɛ ayu bɔɔta oduu mʋ, ɩ lɩɩ sʋsʋ ba tɩyɛ asa sʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ asa mʋ ba tɔwɛ yii Ɩbwaarɛ, fɛɛ maa yɛgɛ nɩɩ bʋlayɩ abuu gyerebi mʋ ba bamʋ sʋ mʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ tɔɔrɔɔ mʋ ban sʋ.

eng: After this I saw another angel coming down from heaven. He had great authority, and the earth was filled with light because of his glory.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ kamaa mʋ n wu Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ kʋ mɔɔ maa lɩɩ sʋsʋ ɛɛ ba. Ɔ sʋ ɔlʋn lala, nɩɩ mʋ kɩŋalagɛ kɩɩ ŋmaŋɛ kasɛ sʋ kpini.

eng: He called out with a loud voice, saying, Fallen, fallen is Babylon the great! She has become a home for demons and a lair for every unclean spirit. She is a lair for every unclean bird, and a lair for every unclean and disgusting beast.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ taa ɔbʋlɛ lala kuusi yɛ, Babilon kadɛ kparɛ mʋ lɩɩ tɩyɛ. Kadɛ kparɛ mʋ ki boyi biliŋi kɩtaakpan. Kɩ ba biliŋi nbwɩɩ libi yɛ n‐yaayu libi, yɛ nbwiibi libi yɛ nbwiibi kisisa ɔbaalakpa.

eng: because all the nations have fallen due to the wine of her lustful passion. The kings of the earth committed sexual immorality with her, and the merchants of the earth became rich from the power of her loose and extravagant ways.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɩsʋwʋlɛ sʋ kpini asa ba nuu kadɛ mʋ kabʋrʋkɔɔnɛ nta bʋlʋnsa mʋ. Nɩɩ kasɛ sʋ awura kpini bee dii bɛɛ kpɩlɩgɛ bɛɛ waa kanyɩn yɛ kakyɩɩ biti kɩgyagaa kadɛ mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ kasɛ sʋ ayawʋ‐gyipu kpini ba nyɛ afulee yɛ atɔ tii sʋ lɩɩ kadɛ mʋ ɔfʋʋtɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ nyɛ fʋʋ mʋ sʋ.

eng: Then I heard another voice from heaven say, Come out of her, my people, so that you dont take part in her sins and dont receive any of her plagues.
local: Nɩɩ n bɩla nu ɔbʋlɛ kʋ mɔɔ maa lɩɩ sʋsʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, Mɛ asa, fan lɩɩ kadɛ mʋ tɔ, kɛ fan man nyɛ waa alibi mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ waa mʋ, kɛ ɩ man naa ba fɛɛ fan nyɛ mʋ kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ mʋ tɔ ɩkʋ.

eng: Her sins have piled up as high as heaven, and God remembered her unjust acts.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ mu alibi kpini ɩ kʋʋla ɩ gyaga abaa sʋ naa fʋʋ sʋsʋ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ nyiŋi mu alibi kpini yɛ ɩmʋ kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ sʋ.

eng: Give her what she has given to others. Give her back twice as much for what she has done. In the cup that she has poured, pour her twice as much.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Fan waa kadɛ mʋ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ kɩ waa fanɛ mʋ, kɛ kɩmʋ alibi mʋ nɩɩ kɩ waa mʋ, fan ka kɩmʋ kʋkɔ ɩlɩɩ ɩnyɔ sʋ. Nta bʋlʋnsa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ taa sa asa nɩɩ ba nuu mʋ, fan waa ɩmʋ ɔlʋn kanɩn ɩlɩɩ ɩnyɔ sʋ sa kɩmʋ kɛ ki nuu.

eng: To the extent that she glorified herself and indulged her loose and extravagant ways, give her pain and grief. In her heart she says, I sit like a queen! Im not a widow. Ill never see grief.
local: Fan yɛgɛ ki gyii awʋrʋfɔ yɛ kanyanla, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ kɩ kyɩna nyɛ bʋnyaa nɩɩ ki gyii ɔkɔn, nɩɩ kɩ kyɩna kʋsaa mʋ n‐yɩɩ tɔwɛ mʋ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ yɛ, N gyɛ owura‐kyɩɩsa, n man gyɛ okulapu‐kyɩɩ nɩɩ n mɛɛ yɛ n bɔɔta tɔ kakaakʋ.

eng: This is why her plagues will come in a single day—deadly disease, grief, and hunger. She will be consumed by fire because the Lord God who judges her is powerful.
