local: Lɩɩ kanɩn sʋ, kakɛ kʋlʋn, gyerebi ii biti i gyii kɩmʋ, ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ lewu yɛ kɩbɔɔta tɔ yɛ akʋn lala. Nɩɩ fʋlɔn ii biti ɔ dɩyɛ mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ talɛ kɩmʋ mʋ, ɩ gyɛ Ɔlʋnpʋ.

eng: The kings of the earth, who committed sexual immorality with her and shared her loose and extravagant ways, will weep and mourn over her when they see the smoke from her burning.
local: Kasɛ sʋ awura kpini mʋ nɩɩ bee dii bɛɛ kpɩlɩgɛ, bɛɛ waa kanyɩn yɛ kakyɩɩ biti kɩmʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ba gyii ɔkɔn mʋ, ba kan wu fʋlɔn mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dɩyɛ kɩmʋ idiisii mʋ, bee biti ba su kɛ ba bɔɔta tɔ sa kɩmʋ.

eng: They will stand a long way off because they are afraid of the pain she suffers, and they will say, Oh, the horror! Babylon, you great city, you powerful city! In a single hour your judgment has come.
local: Kɩmʋ awʋrʋfɔ mʋ ii biti i waa bamʋ kufuu, ɩmʋ sʋ bee biti ba yɩlɛ kata‐kata kɛ ba su mʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, O! Kadɛ kparɛ Babilon, laakʋ. Ɔlʋn kadɛ lala Babilon, laakʋ. Fʋ nyɛ kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ opula tɔ.

eng: The merchants of the earth will weep and mourn over her, for no one buys their cargoes anymore.
local: Kasɛ sʋ ayawʋ‐gyipu bee biti ba su kɛ ba bɔɔta tɔ lɩɩ kɩmʋ sʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋ man bɩla ɛɛ sɔɔ bamʋ atɔ fɛsa mʋ.

eng: cargoes of gold, silver, jewels, and pearls; fine linen, purple, silk, and scarlet; all those things made of scented wood, ivory, fine wood, bronze, iron, and marble.
local: Ɔkʋ man bɩla ɛɛ sɔɔ suwa yɛ gbiti atɔ waasa yɛ ɔgyalɩn abuu ŋalɩgɛsa yɛ ɔgyalɩn agyibi yɛ akalala kparɛ yɛ agoo yɛ ataa lʋʋsa yɛ ɔgyalɩn ataa pɩpɛɛ yɛ abondiri yɛ kʋntɩ anyi atɔ yɛ ɔgyalɩn iyu salɛsa yɛ mɛɛtɔ yɛ abɩlɩtɔ yɛ afʋlɛ fufuli danbɩrasa.

eng: cinnamon, incense, fragrant ointment, and frankincense; wine, oil, fine flour, and wheat; cattle, sheep, horses, and carriages; and slaves, even human lives.
local: Ɔkʋ man bɩla ɛɛ sɔɔ bamʋ atɔ gyisa liifensa yɛ nfɔ sʋswasa liifensa yɛ mɩɩrɛ liifensa yɛ atɔ bɔsa yɛ atuwasa yɛ nta yɛ olifu nfɔ yɛ nnyifo kyɩkyɛɛsa yɛ ayu yɛ ɩnaatɛ yɛ nbʋlʋpʋ yɛ ɩgbaŋɛ yɛ itoroko mʋ nɩɩ atɔbwaaya bɛɛ bɩɩtɛ yɛ anyɛ yɛ asa kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ abaafʋn mʋ nɩɩ asa bɛɛ fɛ mʋ.

eng: The fruit your whole being craved has gone from you. All your glitter and glamour are lost to you, never ever to be found again.
local: Ayawʋ‐gyipu mʋ bee biti ba tɔwɛ yɛ, Babilon, atɔ danbɩrasa mʋ nɩɩ fu ansi ɩ payɩ fɛɛ fii biti fʋ nyɛ mʋ, ɩ nyaa yɛgɛ fʋ. Fʋ kanyɛ yɛ fʋ kɩtɩɩ kparɛ mʋ kpini ɩ kyʋn. Nɩɩ fʋ man bɩla yɛ fʋ nyɛ ɩmʋ.

eng: The merchants who sold these things, and got so rich by her, will stand a long way off because they fear the pain she suffers. They will weep and mourn, and say.
local: Ayawʋ‐gyipu mʋ nɩɩ ba fɛ ɩnɩmʋ, nɩɩ ba nyɛ tɔnɔ lɩɩ kadɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ, lɩɩ kufuu sʋ bee biti ba yɩlɛ kata bɔɔta tɔ, kɛ ba su lɩɩ mʋ gyerebi mʋ sʋ.

eng: Oh, the horror! The great city that wore fine linen, purple, and scarlet, who glittered with gold, jewels, and pearls.
local: Kɛ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, O! Kadɛ kparɛ Babilon laakʋ. Fʋ mʋ nɩɩ fʋ asa ba kyɩna bee bun atɔ danbɩrasa, fɛɛ akalala kparɛ yɛ ɔgyalɩn ataa pɩpɛɛ yɛ agoo. Naafɔɔ bamʋ n‐yɩɩ ɩɩ ŋalɩgɛ suwa atɔ yɛ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba taa ɔgyalɩn abuu waa yɛ ɔgyalɩn agyibi.

eng: in just one hour such great wealth was destroyed. Every sea captain, every seafarer, sailors, and all who make their living on the sea stood a long way off.
local: Amaa opula tɔ mʋ, fʋ kanɩn npatɩyɛ damantɛ maŋa kpini i muuri. Ɔpʋ sʋ ɩkʋlɩ lala akɩɩsʋpʋ yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ kyɩna ɩkʋlɩ lala tɔ bee kpee ɩkpa, yɛ ɩmʋ apalɩpʋ yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa ɩmʋ tɔ kusun mʋ kpini, bee biti ba yɩlɛ kata‐kata.

eng: They cried out as they saw the smoke from her burning and said, What city was ever like the great city.
local: Nɩɩ ba kan wu fʋlɔn mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dɩyɛ kɩmʋ idiisii mʋ, bee biti ba su kuusi yɛ, Kadɛ lala kʋ daa ɔ bʋ tɔ nɩɩ ku du fɛɛ kɩnɩmʋ dubi.

eng: They threw dust on their heads, and they cried out, weeping and mourning. They said, Oh, the horror! The great city, where all who have ships at sea became so rich by her prosperity—in just one hour she was destroyed.
local: Bee biti ba paata bamʋ amu yɛgɛ bɛɛ bɔɔta tɔ, nɩɩ bee su bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, O! Kadɛ kparɛ Babilon, laakʋ. Lɩɩ fʋ sʋ nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ ɩkʋlɩ lala bɛɛ waa kusun ɔpʋ sʋ mʋ, ba biliŋi atɔ wuya ana. Amaa opula tɔ mʋ, atɔ mʋ nɩɩ fʋ sʋ mʋ kpini i muuri.

eng: Rejoice over her, heaven—you saints, apostles, and prophets—because God has condemned her as she condemned you.
local: Nɩɩ ɔbʋlɛ kʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, Fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fan bʋ sʋsʋ mʋ, fan yɛgɛ fanɛ ansi i gyii! Ɩbwaarɛ asa yɛ Krisito asunpu yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ, fan yɛgɛ fanɛ ansi i gyii. Ɩbwaarɛ bɩɩtɛ kɩmʋ kʋsʋ, lɩɩ kanan mʋ nɩɩ kɩ waa fanɛ sʋ mʋ. .

eng: Then a powerful angel picked up a stone that was like a huge millstone and threw it into the sea, saying, With such violent force the great city of Babylon will be thrown down, and it wont be found anymore.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ lʋnpʋ kʋ kukuri kubuu lala kʋ fɛɛ kubuu kɔyɩsa tʋʋ waa ɔpʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, Falɛ nɩɩ bee biti ba muuri Babilon kadɛ lala ɔlʋn sʋ waa ɔpʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ ba man bɩla yɛ ba wu kɩmʋ kakaakʋ.

eng: The sound of harpists and musicians, of pipers and trumpeters, will never be heard among you again. No craftsman of any kind will ever be found among you again. The sound of the hand mill will never be heard among you again.
local: Babilon kadɛ lala, ɔkʋ man bɩla yɛ o nu adʋwʋlɛ alanpʋ yɛ ɩlʋn bɩɩpʋ yɛ nleebi yɛ nbɩlɛ afʋlɛpʋ kpini sʋ kakaakʋ fʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔkʋ man bɩla yɛ o wu abaa tɔ kusun asunpu kʋ, nɩɩ ɔkʋ man bɩla yɛ o nu kubuu kɔyɩsa ɩlawʋ fʋ tɔ.

eng: The light of a lamp will never shine among you again. The sound of a bridegroom and bride will never be heard among you again because your merchants ran the world, because all the nations were deceived by the spell you cast, and because.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ man bɩla yɛ o wu fɩtɩla fʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔkʋ man bɩla yɛ o nu ɔkyɩɩfɔ abɛɛ mu‐kuli ɔbʋlɛ fʋ tɔ. Fʋ ayawʋ‐gyipu ɩ kyɩna ba gyɛ asa kparɛ kasɛ sʋ, nɩɩ fʋ taa Sɩtaanɛ adangana pɩna kasɛ sʋ asa kpini.

eng: the blood of prophets, of saints, and of all who have been slaughtered on the earth was found among you.
local: Kʋtɔ mʋ sʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bɩɩtɛ Babilon kadɛ kʋsʋ mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, kɩmʋ tɔ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wu mʋ atɔwɛpʋ yɛ mʋ asa yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba mɔɔ bamʋ kasɛ sʋ kpini, mʋ nkalan.

eng: After this I heard what sounded like a huge crowd in heaven. They said, Hallelujah! The salvation and glory and power of our God!.
local: Ɩnɩmʋ kamaa mʋ, n nu ɩlawʋ lala kʋ fɛɛ sakyɔ kʋbʋ lala kʋ maa bʋ sʋsʋ nɩɩ bee kuusi yɛ, Haleluya! Fan yɩn Ɩbwaarɛ. Kamɔlɩgɛ yɛ bʋnyaa yɛ ɔlʋn gyɛ anɛ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ.

eng: His judgments are true and just, because he judged the great prostitute, who ruined the earth by her whoring, and he exacted the penalty for the blood of his servants from her hand.
local: Lɩɩ fɛɛ mʋ katalɛ ɩ gyɛ kesintin nɩɩ kɩ dɛ ɔkpa. O bun bʋrʋkɔɔnɛ‐kyɩɩ lala mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ kɩbʋrʋkɔɔnɛ nyita kasɛ sʋ mʋ kʋpwɛ. Ɩbwaarɛ bɩɩtɛ mʋ kʋsʋ lɩɩ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ mɔɔ mʋ ayaafɔlɛ mʋ sʋ.

eng: Then they said a second time, Hallelujah! Smoke goes up from her forever and always.
local: Nɩɩ ba bwii kuusi yɛ, Haleluya! Fan yɩn Ɩbwaarɛ. Kadɛ mʋ idiisii ii kpee sʋsʋ kakpaa.

eng: The twenty-four elders and the four living creatures fell down and worshipped God, who is seated on the throne, and they said, Amen. Hallelujah.
local: Nɩɩ abɩlɩsa adʋnyɔ yɛ ana yɛ atɔ‐kɩɩsa ana mʋ ba muŋa sun Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛ kuwura‐gya sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, Amen! Fan yɩn Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: Then a voice went out from the throne and said, Praise our God, all you his servants, and you who fear him, both small and great.
local: Nɩɩ ɔbʋlɛ kʋ lɩɩ kuwura‐gya mʋ asɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Fan yɩn anɛ Ɩbwaarɛ. Fanɛ mʋ ayaafɔlɛ kpini yɛ fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fan sɩlɛ mʋ mʋ, abɩlɩsa yɛ nbii kpini Fan yɩn anɛ Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: And I heard something that sounded like a huge crowd, like rushing water and powerful thunder. They said, Hallelujah! The Lord our God, the Almighty, exercised his royal power!.
local: Nɩɩ n nu ɩlawʋ kʋ fɛɛ sakyɔ maa kuusi mʋ, yɛ nkyu damantɛ maa kyʋn mʋ ɩlawʋ, nɩɩ i du fɛɛ bʋlayɩ kɩpʋntɛ ɩlawʋ ɩɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, Haleluya! Fan yɩn Ɩbwaarɛ. Anɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Ɔlʋnpʋ Ɩbwaarɛ ii gyii kuwura.

eng: Let us rejoice and celebrate, and give him the glory, for the wedding day of the Lamb has come, and his bride has made herself ready.
local: Fan yɛgɛ ɩ waa anɛ ɔkɔn kɛ an gyii anɛ ansi kɛ a taa bɛɛrɛɛ sa mʋ Lɩɩ fɛɛ saŋa fʋʋ fɛɛ Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ ɔ kpɩɩ mʋ ɔkyɩɩfɔ, nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩfɔ mʋ kʋʋla mʋ n‐yɩɩ o gyoo.

eng: She was given fine, pure white linen to wear, for the fine linen is the saints acts of justice.
local: Nɩɩ ba sa mʋ kɩkyɩɩfɔ kɩtaa fufuli parɛparɛ ŋalɩgɛsa yɛ ɔ dɩla. .

eng: Then the angel said to me, Write this: Favored are those who have been invited to the wedding banquet of the Lamb. He said to me, These are the true words of God.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, Kusee ɩ gyɛ sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba tɩɩ bamʋ ba Ɩbwaarɛ kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ kɩkyɩɩfɔ tɔ agyitɔ kigyii mʋ. Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ bwii tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kɔnɔ tɔ asɩn kesintin. .

eng: Then I fell at his feet to worship him. But he said, Dont do that! Im a servant just like you and your brothers and sisters who hold firmly to the witness of Jesus. Worship God! The witness of Jesus is the spirit of prophecy.
local: Nɩɩ n muŋa mʋ ayaa tɔ kɛ n sun mʋ. Amaa nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, Man waa kanɩn! Ngbaa mɛ n gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ osunpu nɩn fɛɛ fʋ, yɛ fʋ tɔɔmaa ana mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ Yesu asɩn mʋ tɔ kesintin mʋ. Muŋa sun Ɩbwaarɛ! Lɩɩ fɛɛ kesintin asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kaapʋ mʋ, ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ Kayaayu yɛgɛ nɩɩ mʋ atɔwɛpʋ mʋ bɛɛ tɔwɛ.

eng: Then I saw heaven opened, and there was a white horse. Its rider was called Faithful and True, and he judges and makes war justly.
local: Nɩɩ n wu sʋsʋ maa bugi tɔ nɩɩ n wu gbaŋɛ fufuli maa yɩlɛ. Gbaŋɛ mʋ odiipu kɩtɩɩ ɩ gyɛ Kesintin gyipu yɛ Kesintin wuya. Ee gyii asɩn ɩmʋ ɔkpa sʋ, nɩɩ ɛɛ kɔ ɩkɔ ɩmʋ ɔkpa sʋ.

eng: His eyes were like a fiery flame, and on his head were many royal crowns. He has a name written on him that no one knows but he himself.
local: Nɩɩ mu ansi i du fɛɛ fʋlɔn anobi, nɩɩ awura‐wʋrʋ i bun i bun mu kumu sʋ. Ɔ sʋ kɩtɩɩ kʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ gyaga mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔkʋ man nyi kɩmʋ kasɛ amɔɔ mʋ nkʋn.

eng: He wore a robe dyed with blood, and his name was called the Word of God.
local: Nɩɩ o bun balɩmaasʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba taa nyɔ nkalan tɔ, nɩɩ mʋ kɩtɩɩ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn.

eng: Heavens armies, wearing fine linen that was white and pure, were following him on white horses.
local: Nɩɩ sʋsʋ anaakɔpʋ ba buu mʋ, yɛgɛ bee dii ɩgbaŋɛ fufuli nɩɩ ba bun akalala parɛparɛ.

eng: From his mouth comes a sharp sword that he will use to strike down the nations. He is the one who will rule them with an iron rod. And he is the one who will trample the winepress of the Almighty Gods passionate anger.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ sʋ sakunde bʋyɛsa kʋ lɩɩ mʋ kɔnɔ tɔ, kɩmʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ taa kɔ gyii ndɛndɛmandɛ sʋ. Ee biti ɔ taa ɔlʋn fɛɛ kɩbɩlɩtɔ mʋ gyii kuwura bamʋ sʋ. Ee biti ɔ taa Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa sʋ ɔlʋnpʋ mʋ agbʋ lala mʋ taa nyan bamʋ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ nyan oyu abi bɛɛ lɛɛ nkyu mʋ.

eng: He has a name written on his robe and on his thigh: King of kings and Lord of lords.
local: Nɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ o dii gbaŋɛ fufuli mʋ sʋ kɩtɩɩ kʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ gyaga mʋ balɩmaasʋ yɛ mʋ ɔgyan sʋ yɛ, AWURA TƆ OWURA YƐ ANYƖRƖPƐ TƆ ƆNYƖRƖPƐ.

eng: Then I saw an angel standing in the sun, and he called out with a loud voice and said to all the birds flying high overhead, Come and gather for Gods great supper.
local: Nɩɩ n wu Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ kʋ maa yɩlɛ kyɔwɛ tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ pʋntɛ kenken tɩɩ nbwiibi mʋ nɩɩ bee fugi afuu tɔ mʋ yɛ, Fan ba! Fan ba bʋga kɛ fen gyii agyitɔ damantɛ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ waa sa fanɛ mʋ.

eng: Come and eat the flesh of kings, the flesh of generals, the flesh of the powerful, and the flesh of horses and their riders. Come and eat the flesh of all, both free and slave, both small and great.
local: Fan ba gyii awura yɛ anaakɔpʋ abɩlɩsa yɛ alʋlʋnpʋ yɛ ɩgbaŋɛ yɛ bamʋ adiipu yɛ asa kpini yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bee gyii bamʋ abaa sʋ yɛ anyɛ yɛ asa kparɛ yɛ asansa n‐yɩɩ inun.

eng: Then I saw that the beast and the kings of the earth and their armies had gathered to make war against the rider on the horse and his army.
local: Nɩɩ n wu kadʋnbwɛ yɛ kasɛ sʋ awura yɛ bamʋ anaakɔpʋ ba maa naa bʋga ba kɔ yɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ee dii gbaŋɛ fufuli mʋ maa mʋ ɔnaakɔpʋ mʋ ɩkɔ.

eng: But the beast was seized, along with the false prophet who had done signs in the beasts presence. The two of them were thrown alive into the fiery lake that burns with sulfur.
local: Amaa ba kɩtaa kadʋnbwɛ mʋ yɛ antɩŋɛsa ɔtɔwɛpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ yɩlɛ kadʋnbwɛ ayaa tɔ waa asɩn kyinkyinsa mʋ. Mʋ asɩn kyinkyinsa maŋa nɩɩ ɔ sʋ ɛɛ pɩna, bamʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ kadʋnbwɛ mʋ kakyʋlɛ, nɩɩ ba bɩla bee sun mʋ apɔyɩtɔ ɔdakpa mʋ. Ba taa bamʋ anyɔ maŋa yɛgɛ ba kɩɩ, tʋʋ waa ferinwuta fʋlɔn mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ yɩlɛ kɩɩ dɩyɛ mʋ tɔ.

eng: The rest were killed by the sword that comes from the mouth of the rider on the horse, and all the birds ate their fill of their flesh.
local: Nɩɩ gbaŋɛ odiipu mʋ, taa sakunde bʋyɛsa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ lɩɩ mʋ kɔnɔ tɔ mʋ, mɔɔ bamʋ anaakɔpʋ mʋ. Nɩɩ nbwiibi mʋ kpini ba gyii bamʋ inun mʋ naa fʋʋ bamʋ ɔkaa.

eng: Then I saw an angel coming down from heaven, holding in his hand the key to the abyss and a huge chain.
local: Nɩɩ n bɩla wu Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ kʋ maa kpɩlɩgɛ ɛɛ lɩɩ sʋsʋ ɛɛ ba kasɛ, yɛgɛ ɔ sʋ ɔbɔ bunkyinsa lala mʋ nɩɩ kɩ man sʋ ɔkaa mʋ safɩɩ, yɛ agbeeleebi lala mʋ kɩbaa tɔ.

eng: He seized the dragon, the old snake, who is the devil and Satan, and bound him for a thousand years.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ naa kɩtaa kɩnansɛ pɩpɛɛ dansa, mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ kɩnansɛ dɩdaa mʋ. Mʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ okisipu abɛɛ Sɩtaanɛ mʋ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ ŋmina mʋ yɩla nsu kagbɩn kʋlʋn.

eng: He threw him into the abyss, then locked and sealed it over him. This was to keep him from continuing to deceive the nations until the thousand years were over. After this he must be released for a little while.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ taa mʋ tʋʋ waa ɔbɔ bunkyinsa lala mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ o biri mʋ sʋ, tarɩ sʋ kɛ ɔ man bɩla nyɛ pɩna asa kʋkwɩɩ‐kʋkwɩɩ kasɛ sʋ, naa fʋʋ nsu kagbɩn mʋ ɔkaa. Ɩmʋ kamaa mʋ ɩ dagaa maa saŋɛ mʋ saŋa gbɛrɛɛ.

eng: Then I saw thrones, and people took their seats on them, and judgment was given in their favor. They were the ones who had been beheaded for their witness to Jesus and Gods word, and those who hadnt worshipped the beast or its image, who hadnt received the mark on their forehead or hand. They came to life and ruled with Christ for one thousand years.
local: Nɩɩ n wu nwuragya, nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba tɛ ɩmʋ sʋ mʋ ɩ gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ taa kasɛ sʋ asɩn kigyii mʋ ɔlʋn waa bamʋ abaa tɔ. Nɩɩ n bɩla wu asa mʋ nɩɩ ba lɛɛ Yesu asɩn mʋ kaapʋ tɔ mʋ, yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn nɩɩ ba tɩŋɛ bamʋ amu mʋ, bamʋ kala ana. Asa maŋa ba man sun kadʋnbwɛ mʋ abɛɛ mʋ apɔyɩtɔ ɔdakpa mʋ, nɩɩ ba man kɔɔlɛ mʋ kakyʋlɛ mʋ bamʋ asɩkpɩɩ sʋ abɛɛ bamʋ abaa sʋ. Nɩɩ falɛ asa nɩmʋ ba bwii ba kyɩna nkpa tɔ nɩɩ ba maa Yesu ba gyii kuwura nsu kagbɩn.

eng: The rest of the dead didnt come to life until the thousand years were over. This is the first resurrection.
local: Kelewu kyiŋi nɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ kɩgyankpaasa.

eng: Favored and holy are those who have a share in the first resurrection. The second death has no power over them, but they will be priests of God and of Christ, and will rule with him for one thousand years.
local: Kensi‐gyii yɛ kumu ɔkɔn lala wuya ana ɩ gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba tii kelewu kyiŋi gyankpaasa mʋ sʋ. Nɩɩ lewu nyɔsapʋ mʋ man bɩla ɔ sʋ ɔlʋn bamʋ sʋ. Amaa bee biti ba baa, ba gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ Krisito aseepu, kɛ ba maa mʋ be gyii kuwura nsu kagbɩn.

eng: When the thousand years are over, Satan will be released from his prison.
local: Nɩɩ nsu kagbɩn mʋ kan fʋʋ ɔkaa mʋ, bee biti ba tigi Sɩtaanɛ mʋ lɩɩ mu obu tiisa mʋ tɔ.

eng: He will go out to deceive the nations that are at the four corners of the earth—Gog and Magog. He will gather them for battle. Their number is like the sand of the sea.
local: Kɛ o kpee naa pɩna asa kʋkwɩɩ‐kʋkwɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ kasɛ sʋ mʋ kpini. Ɩsʋwʋlɛ nɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ Gogi maa Magogi. Sɩtaanɛ ii biti ɔ taa bamʋ kpini ba bʋga tɔ, kɛ ba naa kɔ ɩkɔ. Nɩɩ ba kyɔ fɛɛ ɔpʋ kɔnɔ kɩkyafa.

eng: They came up across the whole earth and surrounded the saints camp, the city that God loves. But fire came down from heaven and consumed them.
local: Ba yaasɛ kasɛ sʋ kpini bun kyaabɔɔ Ɩbwaarɛ asa, yɛ kadɛ lala mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti mʋ. Amaa fʋlɔn lɩɩ sʋsʋ ba kasɛ sʋ ba dɩyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba kyaabɔɔ Ɩbwaarɛ asa mʋ.

eng: Then the devil, who had deceived them, was thrown into the lake of fire and sulfur, where the beast and the false prophet also were. There painful suffering will be inflicted upon them day and night, forever and always.
local: Nɩɩ Sɩtaanɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ pɩna bamʋ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ taa mʋ tʋʋ waa ferinwuta fʋlɔn mʋ tɔ. Katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ wʋla taa kadʋnbwɛ yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɔwɛ bɛɛ pɩna asa mʋ tʋʋ waa mʋ. Bee biti ba gyii awʋrʋfɔ kayɩɩ‐kɔ tɔ, kakyʋwapɛ yɛ kagyanbwɛ kakpaa.

eng: Then I saw a great white throne and the one who is seated on it. Before his face both earth and heaven fled away, and no place was found for them.
local: Nɩɩ n wu kuwura‐gya fufuli lala yɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛ kɩmʋ sʋ. Sʋsʋ yɛ kasɛ ɩ lɩɩ mu ansi sʋ. Nɩɩ ɔkʋ man bɩla wu ɩmʋ.

eng: I saw the dead, the great and the small, standing before the throne, and scrolls were opened. Another scroll was opened too; this is the scroll of life. And the dead were judged on the basis of what was written in the scrolls about what they had done.
local: Nɩɩ n wu alewupu mʋ nɩɩ ba gyɛ asa kparɛ yɛ asansa, ba maa yɩlɛ kuwura‐gya mu ansi tɔ. Nɩɩ ba bugi ɩwʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kyʋrɔɔ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ awaasa waa tɔ mʋ. Nɩɩ ba bɩla bugi ɔwʋlɛ pʋpwɛ kʋ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ nkpa ɔwʋlɛ mʋ. Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ talɛ alewupu mʋ, taa bɔla lɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba waa nɩɩ ɔ kyʋrɔɔ ɩmʋ waa ɩwʋlɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ.

eng: The sea gave up the dead that were in it, and Death and the Grave gave up the dead that were in them, and people were judged by what they had done.
local: Nɩɩ ɔpʋ lɛɛ alewupu mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ kɩmʋ tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ lewu yɛ kamandɛ gbaa ɩ lɛɛ alewupu mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ ɩmʋ tɔ mʋ. Nɩɩ ɩbwaarɛ talɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ lɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa sʋ.

eng: Then Death and the Grave were thrown into the fiery lake. This, the fiery lake, is the second death.
local: Nɩɩ ba tʋʋ lewu yɛ kamandɛ waa fʋlɔn yen‐yen mʋ tɔ. Fʋlɔn yen‐yen nɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ lewu nyɔsapʋ mʋ.

eng: Then anyone whose name wasnt found written in the scroll of life was thrown into the fiery lake.
local: Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ba man kyʋrɔɔ mʋ kɩtɩɩ waa nkpa ɔwʋlɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ, ba tʋʋ mʋ waa fʋlɔn yen‐yen mʋ tɔ.

eng: Then I saw a new heaven and a new earth, for the former heaven and the former earth had passed away, and the sea was no more.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ n wu sʋsʋ pʋpwɛ yɛ kasɛ pʋpwɛ. Sʋsʋ gyankpaasa yɛ kasɛ gyankpaasa mʋ, ɩ nyaa kyʋn nɩɩ ɔpʋ man bɩla ɔ bʋ tɔ.

eng: I saw the holy city, New Jerusalem, coming down out of heaven from God, made ready as a bride beautifully dressed for her husband.
local: Nɩɩ n wu Kadɛ Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ Gyerusalem pʋpwɛ mʋ, kɩ maa kpɩlɩgɛ kɩ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ sʋsʋ kɩɩ ba kasɛ. Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ lɔŋɔ kɩmʋ yɩla fɛɛ ɔkyɩɩfɔ maa lɔŋɔ mʋ n‐yɩɩ nɩɩ ɔ bʋrɔn o gyoo mu‐kuli mʋ.

eng: I heard a loud voice from the throne say, Look! Gods dwelling is here with humankind. He will dwell with them, and they will be his peoples. God himself will be with them as their God.
local: Nɩɩ n nu ɔbʋlɛ kʋ maa kuusi ɛɛ tɔwɛ lɩɩ kuwura‐gya mʋ sʋ yɛ, Nbɩɩnbɩɩ bɩrɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ ɔkyɩnakpa bʋ asa asɛ, nɩɩ ee biti ɔ kyɩna bamʋ asɛ. Nɩɩ bee biti ba baa ba gyɛ mʋ asa. Ɩbwaarɛ gbaa‐gbaa ii biti ɔ baa ɔ bʋ bamʋ asɛ, nɩɩ mʋ ii biti ɔ baa ɔ gyɛ bamʋ Ɩbwaarɛ.

eng: He will wipe away every tear from their eyes. Death will be no more. There will be no mourning, crying, or pain anymore, for the former things have passed away.
local: Mʋ ii biti ɔ kpayɛ bamʋ ansikyu kpini lɩɩ bamʋ ansi sʋ. Nɩɩ lewu mɛɛ yɛ ɔ baa ɔ bʋ tɔ, abɛɛ kɩbɔɔta tɔ abɛɛ kusu abɛɛ kayɩɩ‐kɔ ɩ mɛɛ yɛ ɩ baa ɩ bʋ tɔ, lɩɩ fɛɛ atɔ dɩdaa mʋ kpini ɩ kyʋn.

eng: Then the one seated on the throne said, Look! Im making all things new. He also said, Write this down, for these words are trustworthy and true.
local: Nɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛ kuwura‐gya mʋ sʋ mʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, N waa kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ kɩpʋpwɛ! Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, Kyʋrɔɔ ɩnɩmʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ falɛ asɩn nɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ kesintin, nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ ɩ taalɛ taa mʋ n‐yɩɩ tʋʋ ɩmʋ sʋ.

eng: Then he said to me, All is done. I am the Alpha and the Omega, the beginning and the end. To the thirsty I will freely give water from the life-giving spring.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ bɩla tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, Ɩ lʋwɛ! Mɛ ɩ gyɛ Alɩfa yɛ Omega mʋ. Ɩmʋ kasɛ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, Npiili asɛ yɛ laalaalʋwɛ. Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ bʋlɩfɔ sʋ mʋ mʋ, n biti n sa mʋ nkpa nkyu mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ lɩɩ ɔbʋn kumu tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ ka sɛhn.

eng: Those who emerge victorious will inherit these things. I will be their God, and they will be my sons and daughters.
local: Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔ gyii lɩɩ mʋ, ee biti ɔ nyɛ kusee ɩnɩmʋ lɩɩ mɛ asɛ. Nɩɩ n biti n baa n gyɛ mʋ Ɩbwaarɛ, kɛ ɔ baa ɔ gyɛ mi‐bii.

eng: But for the cowardly, the faithless, the vile, the murderers, those who commit sexual immorality, those who use drugs and cast spells, the idolaters and all liars—their share will be in the lake that burns with fire and sulfur. This is the second death.
local: Amaa afuupu yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man kɔɔlɛ gyii naa fʋʋ ɔkaa, yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa ineesi yɛ asa amɔɔpʋ, yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa kanyɩn yɛ kakyɩɩ biti kɩgyagaa yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa Sɩtaanɛ adangana yɛ ɩdakpa asunpu yɛ antɩŋɛsa wuya ana mʋ kpini, bamʋ opula ɩ gyɛ ferinwuta fʋlɔn mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ dɩyɛ yen‐yen mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ kɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ lewu nyɔsapʋ.

eng: Then one of the seven angels who had the seven bowls full of the seven last plagues spoke with me. Come, he said, I will show you the bride, the Lambs wife.
local: Nɩɩ n wu Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ asunɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ ɩlapɛ isunɔ, nɩɩ laalaalʋwɛ igyerebi isunɔ ɩ bɔla ɩmʋ tɔ mʋ, bamʋ tɔ ɔkʋ ba mɛ asɛ ba tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, Ba, kɛ n kaapʋ fʋ Ɔkyɩɩfɔ mʋ. Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ Yesu mʋ, mʋ‐ka ɩ gyɛ Ɔkyɩɩfɔ mʋ.

eng: He took me in a Spirit-inspired trance to a great, high mountain, and he showed me the holy city, Jerusalem, coming down out of heaven from God.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ taa mɛ kayaayu tɔ, dii kpee kɩbɩɩ lala kʋ kumu tɔ legelege. Nɩɩ ɔ kaapʋ mɛ kadɛ kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ Gyerusalem mʋ, kɩ maa kpɩlɩgɛ kɩɩ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ sʋsʋ kɩɩ ba kasɛ.

eng: The city had Gods glory. Its brilliance was like a priceless jewel, like jasper that was as clear as crystal.
local: Ɩbwaarɛ bʋnyaa mʋ yɛgɛ nɩɩ kadɛ lala mʋ kɩɩ ŋalɩgɛ sʋ pɩlɛpɩlɛ. Nɩɩ kɩmʋ kɩŋalagɛ sʋ du fɛɛ kubuu kparɛ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ gyasipa, nɩɩ kɩ bɩla kɩ kɩɩ tɔ fɛɛ kɩlasɛ mʋ.

eng: It had a great high wall with twelve gates. By the gates were twelve angels, and on the gates were written the names of the twelve tribes of Israels sons.
local: Nɩɩ kɩ sʋ kɩbandɩyɛ lala, nɩɩ kɩ bʋ nswɩɩ, yɛgɛ kɩ sʋ nbunɔ kudu anyɔ. Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ kudu anyɔ akʋ ba tɛ ba tɛ nbunɔ mʋ nnɔ asɛ. Nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ Ɩsɩrayɩ nbunɔ kudu anyɔ mʋ atɩɩ kʋkʋlʋn‐kʋlʋn gyaga kebunɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ sʋ kɩmaa kɩmʋ kɩtɩɩ.

eng: There were three gates on the east, three gates on the north, three gates on the south, and three gates on the west.
local: Nɩɩ nbunɔ asa bʋ kyɔwɛ kɩlɩɩ, nɩɩ asa ɩ bʋ kyɔwɛ ɔtɩyɛkpa, nɩɩ asa ɩ bʋ adii sʋ, nɩɩ asa ɩ bʋ akpɩlɩgɛ sʋ.

eng: The city wall had twelve foundations, and on them were the twelve names of the Lambs twelve apostles.
local: Nɩɩ kadɛ mʋ kɩbandɩyɛ gyan abuu kudu anyɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba taa yii kasɛ mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ Kabʋlʋpʋ asunpu kudu anyɔ mʋ atɩɩ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ gyaga abuu kudu anyɔ mʋ sʋ.

eng: The angel who spoke to me had a gold measuring rod with which to measure the city, its gates, and its wall.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mɛ asɛ mʋ, sʋ suwa kʋtɔ kaalasa, mʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ taa kala kadɛ lala mʋ yɛ kɩmʋ nbunɔ yɛ kɩmʋ abandɩyɛ.

eng: Now the city was laid out as a square. Its length was the same as its width. He measured the city with the rod, and it was fifteen hundred miles. Its length and width and height were equal.
local: Kadɛ lala mʋ npɛɛ tɔ yɛ kɩmʋ kasɛ nswɩɩ tɔ mʋ kpini gyɛ kyɛɛkyɛɛ. Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ maa taa kʋtɔ kaalasa kaala kadɛ lala mʋ, ɩ waa mayɩlɩ kagbɩn kʋlʋn yɛ alɩfa anuu sʋsʋ nswɩɩ tɔ. Nɩɩ kadɛ mʋ kasɛ tɔ nswɩɩ abɛɛ npɛɛ tɔ gbaa mʋ ɩ gyɛ kagbɩn kʋlʋn yɛ alɩfa anuu kanɩn. Katɩn kʋ man kyɔ katɩn kʋ.

eng: He also measured the thickness of its wall. It was two hundred sixteen feet thick, as a person—or rather, an angel—measures things.
local: Nɩɩ ɔ kaala kɩbandɩyɛ mʋ kɩsɩɩta nɩɩ ɩ waa ayaa tantan anyɔ yɛ kudu asiye. Nyɩmɩsa akaala‐bi nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ taa kaala kanɩn.

eng: The wall was built of jasper, and the city was pure gold, like pure glass.
local: Nɩɩ gyasipa abuu kparɛ nɩɩ ba taa pɔyɩ kɩbandɩyɛ mʋ, nɩɩ suwa nɩɩ ba taa pɔyɩ kadɛ mʋ, yɛgɛ kɩɩ ŋalɩgɛ tɔ fɛɛ kɩlasɛ.

eng: The city walls foundations were decorated with every kind of jewel. The first foundation was jasper, the second was sapphire, the third was chalcedony, and the fourth was emerald.
local: Nɩɩ ba taa abuu ŋalɩgɛsa kparɛ kudu anyɔ yii kɩbandɩyɛ mʋ kasɛ. Kubuu gyankpaasa mʋ bɛɛ tɩɩ kɩmʋ yɛ gyasipa, kɩnyɔsapʋ mʋ bɛɛ tɩɩ kɩmʋ yɛ safire, kɩsasapʋ mʋ bɛɛ tɩɩ kɩmʋ yɛ agate, kɩnasapʋ mʋ bɛɛ tɩɩ kɩmʋ yɛ emeradi.

eng: The fifth was sardonyx, the sixth was carnelian, the seventh was chrysolite, and the eighth was beryl. The ninth was topaz, the tenth was chrysoprase, the eleventh was jacinth, and the twelfth was amethyst.
local: nɩɩ kunusapʋ mʋ bɛɛ tɩɩ kɩmʋ yɛ oniki, kisiyesapʋ mʋ bɛɛ tɩɩ kɩmʋ yɛ kaneliyan, kusunɔsapʋ mʋ bɛɛ tɩɩ kɩmʋ yɛ kirisoliti, nɩɩ kiburuwasapʋ mʋ bɛɛ tɩɩ kɩmʋ yɛ berili, nɩɩ kɩkpanɔsapʋ mʋ bɛɛ tɩɩ kɩmʋ yɛ topasi, nɩɩ kudusapu mʋ bɛɛ tɩɩ kɩmʋ yɛ kirisopasi, nɩɩ kudu ɔkʋsapʋ mʋ bɛɛ tɩɩ kɩmʋ yɛ yasinti, nɩɩ kudu anyɔsapʋ mʋ mɔɔ bɛɛ tɩɩ kɩmʋ yɛ ametisi.

eng: The twelve gates were twelve pearls; each one of the gates was made from a single pearl. And the citys main street was pure gold, as transparent as glass.
local: Nɩɩ ɔgyalɩn agyibi kparɛ kudu anyɔ nɩɩ ba taa waa nbunɔ kudu anyɔ mʋ. Kʋkʋmaa nɩmʋ maa kɩmʋ kigyibi. Nɩɩ suwa nɩɩ ba taa waa kigbenbi mʋ nɩɩ kɩ dɛ kadɛ lala mʋ tɔ, kɩɩ ŋalɩgɛ tɔ fɛɛ kɩlasɛ.

eng: I didnt see a temple in the city, because its temple is the Lord God Almighty and the Lamb.
local: Nɩɩ n man wu kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ kʋ kadɛ lala mʋ tɔ, lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɔlʋnpʋ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ maa Kabʋlʋpʋ Yesu mʋ, bamʋ ɩ bʋ tʋtɔ nɩɩ asa ba sun bamʋ.

eng: The city doesnt need the sun or the moon to shine on it, because Gods glory is its light, and its lamp is the Lamb.
local: Nɩɩ ɩ man tiri fɛɛ kyɔwɛ abɛɛ ɔbwaayɩ ɔ lɩɩ kadɛ lala mʋ sʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ bʋnyaa mʋ ɩɩ ŋalɩgɛ kɩmʋ sʋ. Nɩɩ Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ ɩ gyɛ kɩmʋ fɩtɩla.

eng: The nations will walk by its light, and the kings of the earth will bring their glory into it.
local: Nɩɩ dulinyaa tɔ asa kʋkwɩɩ‐kʋkwɩɩ bee biti ba natɛ fɩtɩla mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ kasɛ sʋ awura bee biti ba taa bamʋ bʋnyaa ba kadɛ mʋ tɔ.

eng: Its gates will never be shut by day, and there will be no night there.
local: Nɩɩ kadɛ lala mʋ nbunɔ ɩ mɛɛ yɛ i tii kakaakʋ kpaa, lɩɩ fɛɛ kagyanbwɛ kɩ man bɩla kɩ bʋ tʋtɔ.

eng: They will bring the glory and honor of the nations into it.
local: Nɩɩ dulinyaa tɔ asa kʋkwɩɩ‐kʋkwɩɩ mʋ, bee biti ba taa bamʋ bʋnyaa yɛ bɛɛrɛɛ ba kɩmʋ tɔ.

eng: Nothing unclean will ever enter it, nor anyone who does what is vile and deceitful, but only those who are registered in the Lambs scroll of life.
local: Amaa ineesi atɔ akʋ abɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ waa ipeeli‐sɩn abɛɛ ɛɛ tɩn atɩŋɛsa, mɛɛ yɛ ɔ nyɛ loo tʋtɔ. Amɔɔ bamʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ bamʋ atɩɩ waa Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ nkpa ɔwʋlɛ mʋ tɔ nkʋn ii biti ba nyɛ loo tʋtɔ.

eng: Then the angel showed me the river of life-giving water, shining like crystal, flowing from the throne of God and the Lamb.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ kaapʋ mɛ ɔbʋn mʋ nɩɩ kɩmʋ nkyu ɩ sa nkpa, nɩɩ ɩɩ ŋalɩgɛ fɛɛ kɩlasɛ mʋ. Nɩɩ ɩ lɩɩ ɩ kyʋn Ɩbwaarɛ maa mʋ Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ kuwura‐gya mʋ kasɛ.

eng: through the middle of the citys main street. On each side of the river is the tree of life, which produces twelve crops of fruit, bearing its fruit each month. The trees leaves are for the healing of the nations.
local: Ɔbʋn mʋ bɔla kigbenbi lala mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ kadɛ mʋ nsana‐nsana mʋ sʋ. Nɩɩ nkpa oyu mʋ yɩlɛ ɔbʋn mʋ benbe yɛ benbe. Nɩɩ kɩɩ sɔyɩ abi kʋkwɩɩ‐kʋkwɩɩ kudu anyɔ kesu tɔ, nɩɩ ɔbwaayɩ kʋmaa nɩmʋ kɩ maa kɩmʋ abi sɔyɩsa. Nɩɩ oyu maŋa afatɛɛ ɩɩ kyɛ sa ndɛndɛmandɛ alobi kpini.

eng: There will no longer be any curse. The throne of God and the Lamb will be in it, and his servants will worship him.
local: Nɩɩ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ kɩmʋ nnɔ mʋ, man bɩla yɛ kɩ baa kɩ bʋ kadɛ lala maŋa tɔ. Amaa Ɩbwaarɛ maa mʋ Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ kuwura‐gya mʋ ii biti ɩ baa ɩ bʋ tʋtɔ, kɛ mʋ ayaafɔlɛ ba sun mʋ.

eng: They will see his face, and his name will be on their foreheads.
local: Bee biti ba wu mu ansi tɔ, kɛ mʋ kɩtɩɩ kɩ baa kɩ gyan bamʋ asɩkpɩɩ sʋ.

eng: Night will be no more. They wont need the light of a lamp or the light of the sun, for the Lord God will shine on them, and they will rule forever and always.
local: Nɩɩ kagyanbwɛ man bɩla ɔ bʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ba man bɩla yɛ bee biti fɩtɩla abɛɛ kyɔwɛ ɩ baa ɩ kɩɩ tɔ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ yɛgɛ ɩ baa ɩ kɩɩ tɔ sa bamʋ. Kɛ be gyii kuwura kakpaa.

eng: Then he said to me, These words are trustworthy and true. The Lord, the God of the spirits of the prophets, sent his angel to show his servants what must soon take place.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, Asɩn mʋ nɩɩ fu nu falɛ, ɩ gyɛ kesintin nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa ɩ taalɛ taa mʋ n‐yɩɩ tʋʋ ɩmʋ sʋ. Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ taa mʋ Kayaayu ɛɛ sa atɔwɛpʋ mʋ, i sun mʋ kabɔɔ yɛ ɔ ba kaapʋ mʋ ayaafɔlɛ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa ɩ ba waa bilen.

eng: Look! Im coming soon. Favored is the one who keeps the words of the prophecy contained in this scroll.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ yɛ, Fan nu! N biti n ba bilen. Bamʋ nɩɩ bee buu Ɩbwaarɛ asɩnlɛɛ kaapʋsa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ ɔwʋlɛ nɩmʋ tɔ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ waa bamʋ kusee.

eng: I, John, am the one who heard and saw these things. When I heard and saw them, I fell down to worship at the feet of the angel who had shown them to me.
local: Mɛ, Gyɔn, ɩ gyɛ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ n nu, nɩɩ n wu falɛ atɔ nɩmʋ kpini. Nɩɩ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ n nu nɩɩ n wu ɩnɩmʋ kpini lʋwɛ mʋ, n muŋa Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ ɩnɩmʋ ɛɛ kaapʋ mɛ mʋ ayaa tɔ, fɛɛ n sun mʋ.

eng: But he said to me, Dont do that! Im a servant just like you and your brothers and sisters, the prophets, and those who keep the words of this scroll. Worship God.
local: Amaa ɔ tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, Man waa kanɩn! Maa fʋ, yɛ fʋ tɔmaa ana Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ mʋ yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba buu asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ ɔwʋlɛ nɩmʋ tɔ mʋ, anɛ kpini an gyɛ mu kusun asunpu nɩn. Muŋa sun Ɩbwaarɛ nkʋn.

eng: Then he said to me, Dont seal up the words of the prophecy contained in this scroll, because the time is near.
local: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, Man taa Ɩbwaarɛ asɩnlɛɛ kaapʋsa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ ɔwʋlɛ nɩmʋ tɔ mʋ baala, lɩɩ fɛɛ falɛ atɔ nɩmʋ ii biti ɩ waa bilen.

eng: Let those who do wrong keep doing what is wrong. Let the filthy still be filthy. Let those who are righteous keep doing what is right. Let those who are holy still be holy.
local: Fan yɛgɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ waa alibi mʋ, ɔ kyaga sʋ ɛɛ waa alibi. Nɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ waa ineesi atɔ mʋ, ɔ kyaga sʋ ɛɛ waa ineesi atɔ. Nɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ waa adanbɩrasa mʋ, ɔ kyaga sʋ ɛɛ waa adanbɩrasa. Nɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ o du kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ, ɔ kyaga sʋ o du kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ.

eng: Look! Im coming soon. My reward is with me, to repay all people as their actions deserve.
local: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ yɛ, Kɩɩ! N biti n ba bilen, n biti n taa mɛ kʋkɔka ba. Kɛ n ba ka ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ lɩɩ kusun mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa mʋ sʋ.

eng: I am the alpha and the omega, the first and the last, the beginning and the end.
local: Mɛ ɩ gyɛ Alɩfa yɛ Omega, Ɔgyankpaapʋ yɛ Kamaa wuya, Npiili asɛ wuya yɛ Laalaalʋwɛ wuya.

eng: Favored are those who wash their robes so that they may have the right of access to the tree of life and may enter the city by the gates.
local: Bamʋ nɩɩ ba fwɩɩ bamʋ atɔ bunsa, kɛ ba nyɛ nkpa oyu mʋ abi gyii, kɛ ba bɩla nyɛ ɔkpa bɔla kewusunɔ mʋ loo kadɛ lala mʋ tɔ mʋ, bamʋ ii gyii ɔkɔn.

eng: Outside are the dogs, the drug users and spell-casters, those who commit sexual immorality, the murderers, the idolaters, and all who love and practice deception.
local: Bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa ipeeli‐sɩn yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa Sɩtaanɛ adangana yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ waa kanyɩn yɛ kakyɩɩ biti kɩgyagaa, yɛ asa amɔɔpʋ yɛ ɩdakpa asunpu yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bee biti antɩŋɛsa kɩtɩn mʋ, bamʋ ii biti ba sii kadɛ mʋ nbwama mʋ.

eng: I, Jesus, have sent my angel to bear witness to all of you about these things for the churches. Im the root and descendant of David, the bright morning star.
local: Mɛ, Yesu, i sun mɛ kabɔɔ yɛ ɔ ba lɛɛ falɛ asɩn nɩmʋ tɔwɛ fanɛ, mʋ nɩɩ fan gyɛ mɛ kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ mʋ. N lɩɩ Owura Dawidi kanan tɔ, nɩɩ Owura Dawidi kanaana‐bii ɩ gyɛ mɛ. Nɩɩ n gyɛ Nyenyenfuu Kikyikpebi mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ ŋmaŋɛ tɔ mʋ.

eng: The Spirit and the bride say, Come! Let the one who hears say, Come! And let the one who is thirsty come! Let the one who wishes receive life-giving water as a gift.
local: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu mʋ maa Ɔkyɩɩfɔ mʋ ba yɛ, Ba! Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ee nu falɛ asɩn nɩmʋ gbaa ɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, Ba! Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ bʋlɩfɔ ɩɩ mɔɔ mʋ mʋ, ɔ ba. Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti mʋ, ɔ ba kɔɔlɛ nkpa nkyu mʋ, ɔ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ ka sɛhn.

eng: Now I bear witness to everyone who hears the words of the prophecy contained in this scroll: If anyone adds to them, God will add to that person the plagues that are written in this scroll.
local: Mɛ Gyɔn, n da ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ kumu tɔ yɛ, ɔmʋ nɩɩ o nu Ɩbwaarɛ asɩnlɛɛ kaapʋsa lɩɩ ɔwʋlɛ nɩmʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan taa asɩn akʋ tii ɩmʋ sʋ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ taa igyerebi mʋ nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ ɩmʋ asɩn ɔwʋlɛ nɩmʋ tɔ mʋ, tii mʋ kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ sʋ.

eng: If anyone takes away from the words of this scroll of prophecy, God will take away that persons share in the tree of life and the holy city, which are described in this scroll.
local: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan lɛɛ asɩn akʋ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asɩnlɛɛ kaapʋsa ɔwʋlɛ nɩmʋ tɔ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ lɛɛ mʋ ogyikpa lɩɩ nkpa oyu mʋ asɛ yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kadɛ mʋ nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ kɩmʋ asɩn ɔwʋlɛ nɩmʋ tɔ mʋ.

eng: The one who bears witness to these things says, Yes, Im coming soon. Amen. Come, Lord Jesus!.
local: Ɔmʋ nɩɩ ee gyii asɩn nɩmʋ kpini kesintin mʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɩ gyɛ kesintin! N biti n ba bilen. Nɩɩ n tɔwɛ yɛ, ɩ waa kanɩn. Ba, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu!.

eng: The grace of the Lord Jesus be with all.
local: Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu kabwaala mʋ kɩ baa kɩ bʋ Ɩbwaarɛ asa kpini asɛ. Ɩ waa kanɩn.
